title: 3 ingredient choco bar recipe | nutty chocobar ice cream | 3 ways choco bar
ingredients: 2 cup cream, 0.5 cup powdered sugar, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 4 grams dark chocolate, 0.25 cup oil, 0.5 cup crushed peanuts
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup fresh cream, ½ cup powdered sugar and 1 tsp vanilla extract. 2. beat on medium speed. you can also use a hand whisk. 3. after 2 minutes, the cream starts to thicken forming soft peaks. 4. transfer the ice cream base into the popsicle mould. 5. cover and freeze for 8 hours or until it sets completely. 6. to prepare outer coating, take 400 grams dark chocolate, ¼ cup oil in a large bowl. 7. using double boiler method, melt the chocolate completely. 8. silky smooth chocolate sauce is ready. 9. cool slightly, and transfer to a tall glass. 10. now unmould the ice cream and dip into prepared chocoalte sauce. 11. allow it to dry up without touching. plain choco bar ice cream ready. 12. to prepare crunchy choco bar, immediately after dipping into chocolate sauce, rollover crushed peanuts. 13. to prepare nutty choco bar, add 3 tbsp crushed peanuts into the chocolate sauce. 14. and then dip the ice cream popsicle into the peanut chocolate sauce. 15. allow it to dry up without touching. nutty choco bar ice cream ready. 16. finally, enjoy 3 ways choco bar. you can freeze and enjoy for up to a month.

title: 7 Cup Barfi Recipe | 7 Cup Cake | Seven Cup Burfi | Seven Cup Burfi
ingredients: 1 cup besan, 1 cup coconut, 1 cup cashew powder, 2 cup sugar, 1 cup ghee, 1 cup milk, pinch saffron food colour, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large kadai take 1 cup besan, 1 cup coconut, 1 cup cashew powder, 2 cup sugar, 1 cup ghee and 1 cup milk. 2. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 3. Now turn on the flame and start cooking on medium flame. 4. Stir until the mixture starts to thicken. 5. Now add a pinch saffron food colour and mix well. 6. Cook until the mixture starts to separate the pan and holds shape. 7. Add ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix until the mixture starts to turn frothy. 8. Transfer to the tray and level up uniformly. 9. Rest for 30 minutes and cut into pieces. 10. Finally, enjoy 7 Cup Burfi stored in an airtight container for a month.

title: 7 cup burfi recipe | 7 cup cake | 7 cups sweet
ingredients: 0.5 cup besan, 0.5 cup coconut, 0.5 cup milk, 0.5 cup ghee, 1 cup sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Grease a tray or an aluminium foil to pour the mixture. You can also use a parchment paper. Set this aside. 2. You can also add tiny chunks of cashews or pistachios to this, if you like to use, chop them and keep them ready. 3. Measure ½ cup ghee and melt it in a heavy bottom pan. Set aside. We will need hot ghee later. 4. Add ½ cup besan to a heavy bottom pan and begin to roast on a medium low flame. Keep stirring and roast it until fragrant. Do not let it brown. Keep stirring constantly. You don’t need to brown or roast it a lot like we do for besan ladoo. This step will take about 4 to 5 mins. Once it begins to smell good and aromatic, move to the next step. 5. Add ½ cup coconut or cashew powder and mix well. You can use fresh or desiccated coconut. 6. Tip: To make cashew powder you can just pulse ½ cup cashews in a mixer to a coarse powder. Measure the powder and add it. Do not blend at a stretch otherwise it will release oil. 7. Lower the flame and roast for 2 to 3 minutes. Again do not let the flour discolor. 8. Add 1 cup sugar. 9. Pour ½ cup milk. 10. Pour ¼ cup ghee from the melted ghee. 11. Mix well. Put on the flame to medium. The sugar melts and the mixture turns gooey. Keep stirring from here to the end without interrupting else the mixture gets burnt. 12. It takes around 10 to 12 minutes for the mixture to reach the final stage, but do not go by the time. Just for an approximation I mentioned it. 13. After few minutes, you will see the mixture begins to bubble. Continue stirring. 14. Add cardamom powder. Continue to stir and cook until the mixture thickens. 15. When you see the consistency of the mixture thickens, add ¼ cup hot ghee. Preferably hot this time to get a porous texture. If the ghee you heated up is not hot, heat it up again before adding. If you want a soft burfi then you can add ghee just as it is. Stir well again for the mixture to absorb the ghee. 16. You will find the mixture begins to leave the sides of the pan. But still quite runny. Continue to cook. Be attentive as it is going to reach the final stage. Adjust the flame if needed. 17. The mixture thickens and you can see lot of pores (bubbles) in the mixture. The mixture completely leaves the sides now and becomes heavy. It becomes a mass. If you cook past this stage, burfi will break or become powder.

title: aam papad recipe | mango papad | aam ka pappad recipe
ingredients: 2 cup mango, 2 tbsp sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 0.5 tsp ghee clarified butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in the blender take 2 cup mango and 2 tbsp sugar. 2. blend to smooth paste without adding any water. 3. transfer the mango puree to the kadai and begin to cook. 4. stir continuously and cook on medium flame until it thickens well. 5. now add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 6. further, brush ½ tsp ghee on a steel plate. 7. transfer the puree onto the plate and spread uniformly. 8. sundry for 2 days or a week, until it turns translucent. 9. scrape the sides using the knife. 10. peel gently without damaging the aam papad. 11. finally, cut into the desired shape and roll aam papad.

title: Achari Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 2 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1 tablespoon cumin seeds, 1.25 tablespoon fennel seeds, 0.25 tablespoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon fenugreek seeds, 2 red chilies, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 1.5 lbs chicken, 3.5 tablespoons oil, 0.625 teaspoon kalonji, 1 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 4 chili peppers, 0.3333333333333333 cup yogurt, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.75 teaspoon amchur, 1.5 tablespoons mango achar pickle, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger
instructions: 1. To a small spice grinder, add coriander seeds, red chilies, cumin, fennel seeds, methi seeds, mustard seeds, turmeric, salt and red chili powder. Make a fine powder. 2. Marinate chicken with 1 tablespoon of the prepared achari masala and half of the chaat masala or amchur. If it is too dry, add a splash of oil. Cover and rest aside until you prepare the rest. 3. On a medium flame, heat oil in a pan/pot and add kalonji. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and saute on a medium heat, until golden for 8 to 9 mins. 4. Stir in the ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute, until aromatic. Reduce the flame and stir in the achari masala. Saute for 1 to 2 mins, until aromatic. (If the pan is dry, add a tablespoon of oil.) 5. Add the tomatoes and saute till the masala becomes thick. 6. Lower the flame and incorporate the yogurt into the pan. Increase the heat to medium and saute for 5 to 7 mins, until the fats begin to appear on top. 7. If using chili peppers to stuff, reserve 2 tablespoons of this cooked masala in a small bowl. 8. Add the marinated chicken to the pan and saute on a medium high heat for 5 to 6 mins. Pour hot water (start with lower amount). Mix well and cook covered for 10 mins. 9. While the chicken cooks, slit each chili pepper lengthwise, keeping the stalk & the edges intact. Deseed for low heat. Stir in rest of the amchur or chaat masala to the reserved achari onion tomato masala. Taste test to adjust salt. Stuff this into the chili peppers. Heat a tablespoon of oil in a small pan and fry them until blistered, for 4 to 5 mins. 10. When the chicken is half cooked, add the stuffed chili peppers and push them slightly into the curry. Cover and cook until the chicken is fully cooked through and the chilies wilt/ soften completely. (It takes me 20 mins to cook the bone-in chicken) 11. Turn off and taste test to adjust salt or add sugar. If you feel the need sprinkle more chaat masala or amchur or mango achar over the achari chicken. Garnish with ginger juliennes. 12. Sprinkle coriander leaves before serving. Serve achari chicken with basmati rice, parathas, naan or tandoori roti.

title: Achari Paneer Recipe
ingredients: 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 2 red chilies, 1 tablespoon fennel seeds, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 pinch methi seeds, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.25 cup green, 2 tablespoon mustard oil, 0.5 teaspoon kalonji, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.5 cup onions, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.3333333333333333 cup curd, 0.5 cup water, 2 grams paneer, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 inch ginger slice, 1 teaspoon achar pickle of choice
instructions: 1. Add red chilies, coriander seeds, fennel seeds, cumin, mustard and methi seeds to a spice jar. Make a fine powder. Your spice blend is ready, Keep this aside. Puree tomatoes and capsicum. Set this aside as well. 2. Heat oil in a pan and add kalonji seeds. When they splutter add hing, followed by finely chopped onions. Saute until golden. 3. Add ginger garlic paste and saute till the raw smell goes away, for a minute. Lower the flame and stir in the achari spice blend. Saute for a minute or 2, on a low heat until aromatic. Do not burn. 4. Pour the tomato capsicum puree and cook until the mixture turns thick & aromatic. Stir in the chili powder, salt, turmeric and sugar. Saute for 2 mins. 5. Turn down the flame to low and stir in half of the curd. Mix well and stir in the rest until well incorporated. Cook until the masala turns thick and leaves the pan. 6. Stir in the water and cook until the gravy becomes thickens and traces of oil are visible on top. Stir in the paneer and ginger juliennes. Cover and cook on a low heat for 2 mins. Taste test and adjust salt, if you want you may stir in the achar or lemon juice. 7. Transfer achari paneer masala to a serving bowl. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve hot with Basmati rice, Tandoori roti or with Jeera rice.

title: achari paneer tikka recipe | easy achari paneer tikka on tawa
ingredients: 1 cup thick curd yogurt, 1 tsp mustard powder rai powder, 1.5 tsp kashmiri red chili powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp fennel powder saunf powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp ginger - garlic paste, 0.25 tsp methi powder fenugreek powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 2 tbsp mustard oil any cooking oil, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 1 tbsp lemon juice, salt to taste, 1 capsicum, 0.5 medium sized onion, 9 cubes paneer cottage cheese, 3 tsp oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take curd. 2. further add mustard powder, chili powder, turmeric, fennel powder, cumin powder, garam masala, methi powder, ajwain and kasuri methi leaves. 3. also add ginger garlic paste. 4. additionally add 2 tbsp mustard oil or any cooking oil. 5. now add lemon juice. 6. furthermore add salt to taste. 7. whisk smooth making sure the spices are mixed well with the curd. 8. now add capsicum, onions and paneer / cottage cheese. 9. mix gently without breaking paneer. 10. further, cover and refrigerate to marinate for 1 hour or more. 11. place the marinated paneer, capsicum and onions over tawa and pan fry. 12. roast the tikkas on medium flame on both side with oil as required. 13. now insert the marinated paneer, capsicum and onions into wooden skewers. 14. also garnish with leftover tikka sauce. 15. finally, serve achari paneer tikka with green chuntey and few salads.

title: Adai Recipe | Adai Dosa
ingredients: 0.75 cup rice, 0.25 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup toor dal, 2 tablespoons moong dal, 2 tablespoons urad dal, 0.875 inch ginger, 4.5 red chilies, 0.875 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.125 teaspoon Hing, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup water, 2 tablespoons Oil, 1 onion, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. To a large bowl, add all the lentils and rice. Rinse them very well thrice. Add fresh water and red chilies. Soak them for at least 2 to 4 hours. (overnight is okay) 2. Drain the water and add them to a grinder jar along with cumin and ginger. Pour fresh water. Blend to a slightly coarse thick batter, adding more water only as needed. 3. The batter has to be thick yet of spreading consistency. The batter is ready to use now alternately you may rest for 2 to 4 hours at room temperature. 4. Heat a pan on a medium heat and add few drops of oil. Smear it all over the pan with a kitchen tissue. 5. While the pan heats, add chopped onions, salt, hing, coriander leaves to the batter. Mix everything well. Taste test and adjust salt. 6. To make thick adai, keep the consistency of batter thick else add more water as needed for a slightly thin batter. (see photos in the post for consistency). 7. Regulate the flame to medium. Make sure the pan is hot and pour a ladle full of batter. Spread it quickly like a dosa, thick or thin as per your liking. 8. Add ½ tsp oil or ghee across the edges. Cook on a medium heat until the edges loosen from the pan. Turn it and cook on the other side as well. Make sure it is cooked on both the sides until crisp. 9. To make the next one, be sure to heat the pan well. It should be hot but not smoking hot. 10. Serve adai dosa with coconut chutney or any other chutney.

title: Ajwain Leaves Benefits & Uses | Doddapatre Kashaya Drink
ingredients: 5 ajwain leaf doddapatre karpooravalli vamu aku, 3 cup water, 1 tsp pepper, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp cloves
instructions: 1. Firstly, heat the 5 ajwain leaf directly on fire to release the juice. You can squeeze the juice directly and give it to kids for instant relief. 2. Take 3 cup water in a saucepan. 3. Cut the 5 doddapatre leaves into pieces. 4. Also add 1 tsp pepper, 1 inch cinnamon and ½ tsp cloves. 5. Stir and boil for 5 minutes or until the flavours are infused. 6. Finally, strain the water and enjoy Doddapatre Kashaya hot.

title: Akki Roti
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour, 1 onion, 3 tablespoons coconut, 0.5 carrot, 2 green chilli, 2.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon Salt, 0.5 teaspoon ginger grated, water, 1 tablespoon oil, 2.5 tablespoons sabsige soppu, 1 tablespoon chana dal
instructions: 1. Wash and chop very finely 1 small onions, 2 green chilies and handful of coriander leaves. Grate ½ carrot & ½ teaspoon ginger. If using sabsige, rinse well and chop finely. 2. Add 1 cup flour to a large mixing bowl along with 1/3 teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon cumin, chopped onions, chilies, coriander leaves and carrots. Mix everything well. You can also add about 2 to 3 tablespoons grated coconut & 2 to 3 tablespoons dill leaves. 3. Pour water little by little and make a soft dough. The dough must be smooth and moist. Divide the dough to 4 equal balls. Roll to smooth. Keep the bowl covered to prevent drying up. 4. You can make akki roti on a greased banana leaf, moist handkerchief, foil or parchment paper. 5. Place a ball on the moist cloth or foil or greased banana leaf. 6. Begin to spread it lightly with your fingers to make a round roti. If the edges of akki roti crack, then dip your fingers in water and join the cracks. Then continue to spread it. 7. It can be made thick or thin to suit your taste. If making thick, make 3 finger sized wholes with one and half inches apart. 8. Heat the greased tawa on a medium high flame. Lift the banana leaf or parchment paper and reverse it on the hot tawa, the roti facing the tawa. Gently peel off the leaf. 9. When the base firms up & golden, flip it. Toast it on both the sides. After flipping it sprinkle some oil. Press down with a spatula to cook evenly. 10. Remove akki roti when you see brown spots. Do not over cook it may turn hard. While you toast the akki roti, make another one on the leaf or cloth. Transfer to the tawa after the first one is done. 11. If you are making directly on the tawa then you will need 2 tawas to use them alternately. Or cool the tawa completely and then spread the next roti. 12. Lightly grease a cool tawa & keep it on a kitchen counter. Place the ball on the tawa and spread it directly to a thin akki roti. Place the tawa now on the stove and cook until the base firms. Then flip & cook on the other side until done. Press down with a spatula to cook evenly. 13. To make the next akki roti either use another cool tawa or wait until the first one cools completely. If you try to spread the dough on the hot tawa it will get stuck and not spread at all. Serve akki roti with chutney.

title: Akki Roti Recipe | Masala Akki Rotti - Karnataka Style
ingredients: 2 cup rice flour, 1 carrot, 0.5 cucumber, 0.5 onion, 4 tbsp dill leaves sabsige soppu, 2 tbsp coriander, 3 chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp salt, warm water, oil
instructions: 1. Take rice flour in a large bowl. 2. Add carrot, cucumber, onion, dill leaves, coriander, ginger, chilli and curry leaves. 3. Add cumin and salt. 4. Squeeze and mix well to release moisture from the vegetables. 5. Add warm water little by little and prepare a soft dough. 6. Grease butter paper with oil. You can alternatively use a banana leaf for traditional flavour. 7. Take a ball sized dough. Place on the butter paper and start patting gently. 8. Grease your hands with oil for easy patting. 9. Spread evenly to make a thin roti. 10. Place the butter paper upside down on hot tawa. 11. Press slightly and cook for few seconds. 12. Slowly remove the butter paper. 13. Spread little oil for uniform roasting. 14. Flip and roast both sides till they are cooked well. 15. Finally, Akki Rotti Recipe is ready to enjoy with spicy chutney.

title: Alasanda Vada (Black Eyed Pea Fritters)
ingredients: 1 cup black eyed peas, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 tablespoon rice flour, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 2 green chilies, 1 teaspoon cumin, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.25 cup onions, 0.625 cup Oil, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Wash & soak alasandalu for 4 hours. Rinse and drain water thoroughly. 2. Add them to a grinder jar along with salt and ginger garlic. 3. Grind to a coarse paste, splashing little water only if required. Test by shaping some batter to a vada. If it does not bind or hold shape, add a few tablespoons of the batter to the grinder and make a smooth paste and mix it well with the rest. 4. Make sure there are no whole lentils left without getting crushed as they can burst while frying the vadas. 5. Transfer this batter to a mixing bowl. Add all the other ingredients - onions, coriander leaves, rice flour (optional), green chilies and cumin seeds. Mix together well. Taste test and add more salt if required. 6. Pour oil to a deep pan and heat it on a medium flame until hot. 7. Grease a cling wrap or a plastic sheet or a parchment paper. You can also grease your hands and shape them in your palms directly. 8. Take small portions of the batter and place them on the greased sheet or hand. Shape to balls and flatten them to thick vadas/ patties. 9. Test if the oil is hot by dropping a small portion of the batter to the hot oil. It should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning brown. If the batter sinks, it means the oil is not ready to fry yet. So heat it a little longer. 10. Gently transfer the vadas to the hot oil. Do not touch them for 3 mins, let them fry on a medium heat. Do not crowd the pan, fry only as many as your kadai and oil can accommodate. 11. Later turn them to the other side and fry until golden and crisp. Remove to a cooling rack. Make sure the oil is hot but not smoking hot before you add the next batch of alasanda vada. 12. Place the shaped vadas in the air fryer basket or rack. Air fry for 10 mins at 180 C or 350 F. 13. Turn them to the other side and air fry for another 6 to 7 mins. Adjust the time as needed depending on your air fryer. They will turn hard if you over fry them. Once done brush the hot air fried alasandalu vada with oil or ghee.

title: Allam Pachadi (Ginger Pickle)
ingredients: 25 grams Ginger, 0.25 tsp turmeric powder, 1 grams tamarind, 62.5 grams jaggery, 6 tbsp Red chili powder, Salt, 1 tbsp fenugreek seeds, 2 sprig curry leaves, 1 tsp mustard, 9 ml oil, 2 red chilies, garlic, 1 Pinch hing
instructions: 1. Wash ginger under running water to remove mud and dirt. Peel off the skin if desired. Dry it for some time or overnight on an absorbent paper or cloth. 2. Soak tamarind in boiled and cooled water till we need it. Do not use lot of water, it must be just enough to soak the tamarind, so that we get a thick paste. 3. Dry roast 1 tbsp. Fenugreek seeds and powder them to fine powder. Set aside only 1 tbsp. powder to use. 4. Chop ginger to bite sized pieces and fry them on a medium flame till they turn slightly golden in little oil, for 3 to 4 minutes. Cool these completely 5. Blend it in a mixer to a smooth paste without adding water. Do not let the blender heat up. 6. Squeeze filter the tamarind pulp to remove impurities. We need thick tamarind paste, if it is thin, then boil the filtered tamarind to thicken. 7. All the ingredients need to be cool at this stage. Add the tamarind pulp, red chili powder, turmeric and salt to the ginger mix. Blend well again. 8. Add jaggery and blend again. Do not add the sweetener before you blend all the other ingredients well since this will thicken the mix and the mixer becomes loaded. Check the taste of the pickle now, it has to taste sour, sweet, hot and spicy. Adjust any taste as needed. 9. To finish off add methi powder and mix. 10. Heat the rest of the oil in a pan, add mustard and garlic. Add curry leaves, red chilies and hing. fry till the leaves turn crisp. Off the stove. Add the ginger pickle to the seasoning and mix well in the hot pan. 11. Cool completely and store allam pachadi in a glass bottle.

title: Almond Jaggery Burfi Sweet
ingredients: 1.5 cups almonds, 1.25 cups jaggery, 0.25 cup water, 0.125 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon almond oil, 1 teaspoon sesame seeds
instructions: 1. Grease a tray, square pan or plate. I use a 8 inch brownie pan. 2. Add almonds and cardamoms to a mixer jar. Grind them to a powder. Do not overgrind to the extent they begin to release oils. 3. Dissolve jaggery with water in a pan and strain the liquid to remove any impurities. Rinse the pan and return the syrup back to the pan. 4. Boil the jaggery syrup till it reaches soft ball consistency. 5. To test add ½ tsp of the syrup to a bowl of water, You should be able to make a soft ball. 6. Add the ground almond powder and switch off the heat. Stir well until the almond meal blends well. Pour it to the greased tray and sprinkle sesame seeds or chopped nuts. 7. Leave it undisturbed for 5 to 6 hours and cut it to squares. If it does not set, it means the jaggery syrup wasn’t cooked well. You can return the entire almond jaggery mixture to the pan and cook for a little longer. When it is done you will be able to make a non-sticky ball.

title: Almond milk powder (Badam milk powder)
ingredients: 1 cup almonds, 2.5 cups water
instructions: 1. Bring 2 to 3 cups of water to a rolling boil. Pour the almonds and leave them for 1 to 2 mins. 2. Drain them to a colander and rinse under cold running water. Drain completely. 3. Spread them on a clean cotton cloth. The skin of almonds loosens. 4. Peel off the skin. Pat them dry on a clean dry cloth. 5. Roast these almonds in a heavy bottom pan on a low flame until completely dry & moisture free. You can also do this in your oven on the lowest heat (60 C or 140 F) setting for 20 mins, stirring after 10 mins. 6. Do not brown the almonds but they must turn crunchy after roasting. Cool them completely. 7. Add them to a dry grinder like a spice jar or coffee grinder. Grind in intervals of 15 to 20 seconds on a minimum speed until you get a fine powder. Do not blend them for too long at a stretch as they begin to release oil making the power lumpy. 8. If your grinder isn't powerful enough, then sieve it to separate the coarse nuts. Grind the coarse nuts again. 9. Store almond powder in an air tight container. Stays good for several months at room temperature. If you live in a humid weather, then refrigerate it. 10. To make the milk, add 2 to 3 tablespoons of the almond powder to a cup. Pour ¼ cup boiling hot milk or water. Add your favorite flavorings like ⅛ teaspoon cardamom powder or 4 strands of crushed saffron. You can also stir in your sweetener. Let it sit for 10 mins, then top with more milk or water as desired. 11. Top your cereals: Sprinkle the almond powder on your grown-up or toddler’s cereals. This is a good way of serving almonds to toddlers who cannot still chew nuts. 12. You can also add this to desserts, ladoos, cookies and milkshakes.

title: Almond yogurt
ingredients: 1 cup almonds, yogurt starter
instructions: 1. Heat 2 cups water in a pot. Add almonds and leave them for a minute or two. 2. Drain them to a colander and rinse under running water. 3. Then spread them on a clean cloth. You will see the skins loosen. 4. You can easily peel them. 5. Then add these peeled almonds to the pot and cover them with clean water. Soak for 6 hours. do not cover the bowl or pot. If you want cover with a thin cloth so air circulates. These are the almonds after soaking. 6. Rinse the soaked almonds and then transfer to a grinder. Pour water little by little and begin to grind until you get a smooth paste. Keep adding water as you grind the mixture. 7. The puree has to be smooth. You won’t get a very smooth consistency like flour mixture though. If it is not smooth you will feel the grits in your yogurt. Transfer this to a steel bowl. 8. Heat 1 cup water in a small pot. Turn off the heat. Then place the bowl in the water and keep stirring till the mixture becomes warm. Do not let the mixture become hot as this alters the flavor completely. When it is warm, transfer this to a clean glass or ceramic jar. Add your yogurt starter. If using powdered starter, you may dissolve it in 1 tbsp water and pour it here. I used 1 teaspoon regular homemade yogurt here. 9. Cover this and keep in a warm place for 3 to 8 hours depending on the temperature. When the almond yogurt is ready you will get a faint smell of yogurt. However if it is the first time you are making this then you need to be careful and keep checking after 3 hours. If you leave it too long it will ferment and rise which we don’t want. Serve almond yogurt as is or use it to make lassi, chaas, smoothie, raita or curry.

title: Aloo Anday Recipe (Potato Egg Curry)
ingredients: 4 eggs, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1.5 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 3.5 potatoes, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a hot pan. Add ½ teaspoon cumin seeds & 3 green cardamoms. If you don’t have green cardamoms you may skip but we love the flavors. 2. When the spices begin to sizzle, add 1½ cup finely chopped onions and saute until golden. 3. Add 1½ teaspoon ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell goes off, for about a minute. 4. Add ¾ cup chopped tomatoes, ⅓ teaspoon salt and ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. 5. Saute until tomatoes turn soft and blend well with onions. Add ½ to ¾ red chili powder and 1 teaspoon garam masala powder. If you want spicy curry, you may add more red chilli powder. 6. Mix and fry until the raw smell of chili powder goes off. This takes about 1 to 2 minutes. 7. While the tomatoes fry, you can wash and peel 3 to 4 aloo / potato. Cube them to desired size. Add them to the pan. 8. Mix and fry for about 2 to 3 minutes. 9. Add water enough to partially cover the potatoes. 10. Cover and cook on a low flame till the potatoes are fork tender. Keep stirring in between. A slow cooked curry tastes better, so if you have time cook on a low flame. 11. When the potatoes are cooked, add boiled eggs and stir. You can also fry the eggs in a teaspoon of hot ghee. When the eggs are blistered add them to the curry. But make sure you pat dry and prick the eggs with a fork before you fry to prevent splatters. 12. Add coriander leaves or kasuri methi. Taste test and adjust salt and add more garam masala if you prefer. I prefer to add a little here. This is optional, but makes the curry flavorful. 13. Stir well. Cover and simmer till aloo anday reaches a desired consistency. To make more gravy, you may need to add more water and simmer. You can also add little thick coconut milk and switch off. To make a dry curry, just evaporate the water by cooking further. Serve with plain rice, roti or any flavored rice.

title: Aloo Baingan Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup onions, 25 grams potatoes, 25 grams eggplants, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1 green chilli, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.125 teaspoon garam masala, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 cup water for semi dry, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Peel 250 grams (2-3 medium) potatoes. Slice them lengthwise of ½-inch thickness (for faster cooking). Keep them immersed in the water until ready to cook. 2. Rinse 250gms of eggplant (use 3-4 small eggplants or 1 medium globe eggplant ). Slice the eggplants lengthwise of 1-inch thickness. Add the slices to a bowl of water & stir in ¼ teaspoon salt. 3. Prep the other ingredients: 4. Fine chop or puree 2 medium tomatoes (150gms) in a blender or grinder. 5. Fine chop 1 large onion (110 to 120 grams) to make 1 cup 6. Chop the green chili (Indian/ Thai/Serrano) 7. Press Saute button on your Instant pot and pour oil to the steel insert. When the oil turns slightly hot (not super-hot), add the cumin seeds. 8. Soon they will sizzle, add the fine chopped onions and green chilies. Saute until golden, you don’t need to brown them. 9. Stir in the ginger garlic paste and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. Stir in all the ground spices – turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder and salt. 10. Quickly add the potatoes and egg plants. Saute for 1 to 2 minutes. Immediately pour the tomato puree. 11. Mix well to deglaze and press cancel. Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position the steam release valve to sealing. 12. Press the pressure cook button and set the timer to 2 minutes. When the Instant pot pressure cooking is done, let wait for the pressure to release for 3 to 4 mins. 13. Release the rest manually by carefully moving the steam valve from sealing to venting. Open the lid and bring all the veggies to the center so all of the liquid moves to the sides. Press saute button. 14. Let cook on saute mode without stirring or mixing, until most of the moisture is evaporated. It only takes a few minutes. To thicken the curry faster, you may mash down some of the potatoes. 15. When the aloo baingan looks almost dry, mix and saute for 1 to 2 mins. Sprinkle coriander leaves and adjust salt to taste.

title: Aloo Baingan Sabji Recipe | Potato Eggplant curry
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, pinch hing asafoetida, 3 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, 1 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 4 potato aloo, 0.75 tsp salt, 0.25 cup water, 1 tomato, 4 grams brinjal eggplant baingan, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute spices. 2. now add 1 onion and saute until they turn golden brown. 3. further, add spice powder and saute on low flame. 4. add 4 potato, ½ tsp salt and saute on a low flame. 5. add 2 tbsp water, cover and cook until the aloo is almost cooked. 6. now add 1 tomato and saute for a minute. 7. further, add 400 grams brinjal, ¼ tsp salt and mix gently until the spices are combined well. 8. cover and cook or until brinjal is cooked well. 9. finally, add coriander and enjoy aloo baingan recipe with roti.

title: Aloo Bhindi Recipe (Okra With Potatoes)
ingredients: 1.1 lb bhindi, 0.75 lb potatoes, 2 tomatoes, 4 medium garlic cloves, 3 tablespoons oil, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.125 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse okra, potatoes and tomatoes under running water. Pat dry the okra or leave aside to air dry to remove any surface moisture. 2. Peel and dice/chop potatoes to ¾ inch pieces and add them to a bowl of water as you dice. 3. Pour half of the oil to a hot pan. Remove the potatoes from water and add them to the hot pan. 4. Spread them to a single layer and let them cook on a medium heat. Keep stirring every few minutes to fry/cook them evenly, until fork tender but not mushy. 5. While the potatoes fry, chop okra to ¾ to 1 inch pieces and slice the garlic cloves. Keep aside. 6. When the potatoes are fork tender, remove them to a plate. 7. Pour the rest of the oil to the pan and add the sliced garlic. Fry for about 30 seconds and add the okra. Spread them in the pan and let cook on a medium heat until all of the slime has vanished & they are almost cooked. 8. While they fry, stir them every few minutes so they cook evenly. Overdoing this can make them more slimy. Optionally- add the quartered tomatoes to a small grinder/blender and puree them. 9. When the bhindi is almost cooked, add the potatoes, red chili powder, turmeric, salt and garam masala (or cumin & coriander powder). Stir fry for 2 to 3 minutes on a medium heat. 10. If using tomatoes, move the veggies aside & pour the puree to the base of the pan in the center. Increase the heat to medium high and let cook without stirring, until most of the moisture from tomatoes is gone. This step takes about 3 minutes. 11. Mix the tomatoes well with the rest of the veggies and continue to saute for a few more minutes until the raw smell of tomatoes has gone completely, for about 4 to 5 minutes. 12. Taste test and add more salt or chili or garam masala at this stage to suit your liking. Add kasuri methi and amchur (if not using tomatoes) and mix well. 13. Serve Aloo Bhindi with rice, quinoa, roti or with any flatbreads of choice.

title: aloo bhindi recipe | bhindi aloo ki sabji | aloo bhindi fry
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 2 aloo, 1 bhindi okra, 3 tsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp kasuri methi
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and roast 2 potato. 2. roast on medium flame until it turns golden and crisp. 3. keep the roasted potato aside. 4. now in the same oil, add in bhindi. 5. roast on medium flame without burning. 6. now keep the crispy bhindi aside. 7. in the same kadai heat 3 tsp oil and saute 1 tsp cumin until it turns aromatic. 8. add in ½ onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute well. 9. saute until it turns golden brown. 10. keeping the flame on low, add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp aamchur, ¼ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp salt. 11. saute on low flame until it turns aromatic. 12. add in roasted aloo and bhindi. 13. mix well making sure all the spices are well coated. 14. now add 1 tsp kasuri methi and mix gently. 15. finally, aloo bhindi recipe tastes great as a side dish with roti or rice.

title: Aloo bhujia
ingredients: 25 grams boiled potatoes, 2 cups besan, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon mint powder, 0.25 teaspoon kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper powder, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 teaspoon ginger powder, 0.5 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.1875 teaspoon hing, 2.25 cups oil, 0.25 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper powder, 0.25 teaspoon mint powder
instructions: 1. Sieve flour to a large bowl and set aside. 2. Grate boiled potatoes with a fine grater. Break down any crumbs of potatoes left in the gratings. 3. Add all of the spice powders and salt. Mix well. 4. Measure and spread 1½ cup of flour over the potato mixture. 5. Heat 1 tablespoon oil until very hot in a frying pan. Remove it before the oil begins to smoke. Pour it over the flour. 6. Carefully using a spoon mix the oil with the flour. Mix everything well with your hand to make a non-sticky dough. 7. If the dough is sticky, then add 1 to 2 tbsps more besan each time and mix until you get a stiff & non-sticky dough. (check pictures above). 8. Taste test and add more salt or any other spice you prefer. Cover and keep this aside. 9. Pour oil to a frying pan and heat it on a medium flame. 10. While the oil heats, grease your sev maker and fit the plate/attachment with the smallest holes. If you want to make them slightly thicker, you can also use the next size. 11. Divide the dough to 2 to 3 parts and shape them to logs. (check pictures in the post) 12. Fill the mould with a log and keep the sev maker ready. 13. Check if the oil is hot enough to fry by dropping a small portion of dough. It has to come up immediately without browning. This is the right temperature. 14. Gently press down the sev maker over the hot oil, moving your hand in a circular motion. Since aloo bhujia dough is stiff and sticky, you will need to break down the ends using a spoon. 15. Fry aloo bhujia on a medium flame. They take only 2 to 3 mins to get fried. Invert them once they become firm. 16. Soon they become firm, crisp and light golden. Do not brown them. Remove them to a steel colander. 17. Continue to make the next batch. Ensure the oil is hot enough and not extremely hot as aloo bhujia will burn/brown. As soon as they cool, transfer them to a air tight container. 18. Crush them gently to break down. Sprinkle chaat masala, black pepper and mint powder over aloo bhujia.

title: aloo bhujia recipe | aloo ki bhujia | haldiram alu bhujia | namkeen bhujia
ingredients: 1 potato aloo, 1.5 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl grate 1 potato finely. 2. add 1½ cup besan and ¼ cup rice flour. 3. also add 1 tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp aamchur, ¾ tsp salt and 2 tsp oil. 4. mix well making sure everything is combined well. 5. knead the dough adding water if required to a smooth and soft dough. 6. the dough will be slightly sticky, grease 1 tsp oil and knead to smooth, soft and non-sticky dough. 7. grease the small holed mould with some oil and stuff dough into it. 8. further, press and spread the bhujiya in hot oil forming a circle in the oil and make sure you do not overlap. 9. after a minute, flip over and fry the other side till it turns golden and crisp. 10. drain over kitchen paper to absorb oil. 11. finally, break aloo bhujia into pieces and enjoy tea time snack.

title: Aloo Biryani (Dum Potato Biryani)
ingredients: 1 cup onions, 2 green chilies, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 3 tablespoons mint leaves, 8 strands saffron, 3 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 2 green cardamoms, 2 inch cinnamon, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 star anise, 4 grams potatoes, 0.75 cup yogurt, 0.5 tablespoons Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 tablespoons Ginger garlic paste, 0.625 tablespoons biryani masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon oil, 1.5 cups basmathi, 1 bay leaf, 2 inch cinnamon, 3 green cardamom, 0.25 teaspoon shahi jeera, 0.75 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Add 3 tbsps of ghee or oil in a pan and fry potatoes till they are almost cooked. It takes around 10 mins. Set these aside and fry the onions until golden like fried onions. Set these aside as well. You can also par boil the potatoes and use, if they are from cold storage, otherwise they will be uncooked in the biryani. 2. Pour 3/4 cup curd to a mixing bowl. Add red chili powder, turmeric, ginger garlic paste, half of the fried onions, salt and biryani masala. Mix this very well and check the spice and salt. If needed add more. 3. Next add the cooked or fried potatoes to the marination ingredients. Marinate for at least 30 mins or until the rice gets cooked. Keep this aside. 4. Wash and soak rice for 20 to 30 minutes. 5. Add bay leaf, shahi jeera, cardamoms, cinnamon, salt and oil to 4 cups of boiling water. When the water begins to boil rapidly, add the drained rice. Cook until it is done al dente or 90% cooked, not mushy. Drain off the rice to colander and set aside. 6. Pour ghee to the biryani pot or pan. You may use the same ghee you used for frying onions. Add green chilies, bay leaf, cardamoms, cinnamon, cloves, shahi jeera and star anise. 7. When the spices begin to splutter, add the marinated potatoes along with the yogurt, cook till the oil begins to separate and potatoes are tender, just cooked and not mushy. At the end a thick gravy should be remaining. Lower the flame. 8. On another burner, heat a tawa for dum process. 9. Sprinkle coriander and mint leaves to the cooked mixture. Layer rice evenly, then the remaining fried onions, again the coriander and mint leaves, pour the saffron milk evenly all over. 10. Seal the rim with an airtight lid or foil, for the steam to be trapped inside. 11. Transfer the potato biryani pot to the hot tawa. Cook for 10 to 12 mins on a medium to low flame. Switch off the stove. Leave the pan covered for another 10 to 20 minutes for the flavors to enhance. 12. Serve aloo dum biryani with a raita.

title: Aloo Bonda Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 cup potatoes - 3 medium sized, 1 onion, 1.5 tbsps Oil, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 tsp mustard, 0.25 tsp jeera, 1 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp ginger, Salt, 2 green chilies, 0.25 tsp coriander powder, 2 pinches saunf, 0.25 tsp garam masala, Salt, lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.5 cup besan, 2.5 tbsp rice flour, water, Salt, 2 pinches chili powder, 1 pinch turmeric, Oil
instructions: 1. Steam or boil potatoes without making them mushy. You can boil in a pot or cooker. 2. Pour oil to a hot pan. Add dal, mustard and cumin. Fry until the dal turns golden. 3. Saute ginger just for 30 secs. 4. Next add onions,salt, curry leaves and green chilies. Fry them. 5. When the onions turn golden, add the turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder and saunf. 6. Add potatoes and mash them a bit without making them mushy. 7. Mix well. Sprinkle lemon juice and coriander leaves. 8. Cool this and make 6 to 8 balls. 9. Add besan, rice flour, salt, chili powder and turmeric to a large mixing bowl. 10. Pour enough water little by little and mix it well to make a batter that is neither too thin or nor too thick. The batter must be moderately thick and smooth. 11. Beat the batter well. 12. Heat oil in a deep fry pan or kadai. 13. When the oil is hot enough, do the heat test by dropping a drop of batter. It must raise in the oil slowly and should not sink or turn brown quickly. 14. With the help of a tbsp dip each ball in the batter, swipe off the excess and drop in the hot oil. 15. Each batch you can fry about 3 to 4 balls depending on the size of your pan and oil used. 16. After a minute begin to stir and fry until golden. 17. Repeat making all until all the balls are done. 18. Drain the golden fried aloo bonda on to a kitchen tissue. 19. Serve aloo bonda hot or warm with chai or chutney.

title: aloo bonda recipe | batata vada recipe | potato bonda | bonda recipe
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard seeds rai, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin seeds, 0.5 tsp coriander seeds, pinch of hing asafoetida, few curry leaves, 6 cashews, 1 sized onion, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 green chilli, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, salt to taste, 0.25 tsp garam masala powder, 3 medium sized potato, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, salt to taste, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, water as required, 1 tsp oil, 0.25 tsp baking soda, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat oil and add mustard seeds, jeera, crushed coriander seeds, hing and few curry leaves. 2. allow to splutter. 3. further add broken cashews and roast to golden. 4. additionally saute chopped onions, ginger and chilli. 5. furthermore add in turmeric, chilli powder, garam masala powder and salt. saute. 6. now add in boiled and mashed potato mix well. 7. additionally, add in coriander leaves and lemon juice. 8. prepare the besan batter. 9. also make a ball from the prepared aloo masala. 10. further, dip into besan batter and deep fry in hot oil. 11. fry till the bonda turns golden and crisp. 12. finally, serve aloo bonda along with chutney or tomato sauce.

title: Aloo Capsicum | Aloo Shimla Mirch
ingredients: 1.5 teaspoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 onion, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chilli, 2 potatoes cubed to inch pieces, 0.5 cup capsicum chopped, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 teaspoon amchur powder, 1 Pinch turmeric, Salt, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala powder
instructions: 1. Wash and peel potatoes. Cube them to ¾ inch size and keep them immersed in a bowl of water. 2. Add oil to a hot pan. When the oil turns hot add cumin seeds & fry them till they begin to splutter. Next add in onions and green chilies. Fry until transparent stirring often. 3. Then add ginger and fry till a nice smell begins to come out. Drain the water from potatoes and add them to the pan. Saute for 2 minutes. Cook covered on a low to medium heat until fork tender or soft. If needed sprinkle little water while cooking. 4. Lastly add capsicum, kasuri methi, turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala and then salt as needed. Stir well and fry until the capsicum is done yet crunchy. 5. Adjust salt and chilli powder if needed. Serve aloo capsicum with rice, bread or roti. Sprinkle some lemon juice if you desire.

title: Aloo Chaat Recipe (Potato Chaat)
ingredients: 2 potatoes, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.125 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala powder, 0.375 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 3 tablespoons nylon sev, 2 tablespoon pomegranate arils, 0.625 teaspoon lemon juice, 0.375 cup coriander leaves, 5 mint leaves, 1 garlic, 0.125 inch ginger, 1 teaspoon fried gram, 1 tablespoon water, 0.25 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Wash, peel and boil the potatoes in whatever way you prefer. They must be just cooked al dente and not turn mushy. I usually cube them and steam in a cooker just to make sure they don’t over cook. If you prefer to deep fry the potatoes then just parboil them for a while until 80% cooked meaning they are slightly undercooked. 2. While the aloo boils, add all the ingredients mentioned to make green chutney to a mini blender jar. 3. The quantity to make the chutney mentioned in the recipe card is too less and may be hard to blend in a normal jar unless using a mini jar. So you can check this post on green chutney to make more quantity. 4. Add 1 tablespoon oil to the pan. Add 1 teaspoon minced ginger or garlic and 1 chopped green chili. 5. Optional for deep frying: or Alternately If you prefer deep fried crisp potatoes, then parboil potatoes. Air dry them for a while so there is not moisture over them. Heat oil in a kadai. Fry the potatoes in the hot oil until crisp and cooked thoroughly. Then skip to step 6. But I am just showing pan fried method here. 6. Add the potatoes and saute on a high flame stirring often. 7. They will turn golden in few minutes. They will also turn slightly crisp if potato is boiled al dente. Turn off the stove. 8. Add chaat masala powder as required and very little salt. There is salt also in the chutney so make sure you don’t add more here. You can add about ¼ to ½ tsp chaat masala. 9. Also add red chili powder as required and jeera or cumin powder as required. Add about ¼ to ½ tsp red chilli powder and ½ tsp cumin powder.

title: aloo chaat recipe | how to make spicy alu chaat | potato chaat recipe
ingredients: 2 potatoes, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 sized onion, 0.5 tsp kashmiri chili powder lal mirch powder, salt to taste, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 2 tbsp green chutney, 2 tbsp sweet tamarind chutney imli-chutney, 0.25 cup sev, 2 tsp curd yogurt, 1 tsp coriander leaves, 2 tsp pomegranate seeds
instructions: 1. firstly, pressure cook the potatoes for 2 whistle and peel the skin. 2. furthermore, cut the potatoes into cubes. 3. and pan fry in 2 tbsp of oil. add more oil if you are not using nonstick pan. 4. do not over mix, as the potatoes will break apart. 5. saute the potatoes till they turn golden brown from all the sides. 6. transfer the fried potatoes into bowl. 7. add finely chopped onions. adding onions is optional. 8. also add chili powder, roasted cumin powder, chaat masala and salt. 9. additionally add green chutney and sweet tamarind chutney / imli-chutney. 10. mix gently without breaking the aloo / potato pieces. 11. transfer the chaat into a serving plate. 12. top with generous amount of sev. 13. and also spread a tsp of curd and coriander leaves. 14. finally, garnish with pomegranate seeds and serve with cutting chai.

title: Aloo Chaat Recipe | Potato Chaat - Street Style
ingredients: 3 potato, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp green chutney, 2 tbsp tamarind chutney, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp kala namak, 2 tbsp coriander, sev, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, peel the skin of 3 potato and cube them 2. Rinse the potato in the water to remove the starch 3. Pat dry and fry in hot oil. 4. Stir occasionally, and fry till the potato turns golden brown and crispy. 5. Drain off and sprinkle ½ tsp salt. Mix well. 6. Now add 2 tbsp green chutney and 2 tbsp tamarind chutney. 7. Also add 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp capsicum and 2 tbsp tomato. 8. Add the following ingredients: ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp chaat masala, ¼ tsp kala namak, and 2 tbsp coriander. 9. Mix well, making sure the chutney is well combined. 10. Now serve the chaat in a plate, garnishing with some sev and onion. 11. Finally, enjoy Crispy Aloo Chaat Recipe with chai.

title: Aloo Chana (Kurma Style Chickpea Potato Curry)
ingredients: 2 cup boiled chickpeas, 1 potatoes, 3 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onions, 1.5 green chilies, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 2.5 tablespoons mint leaves, 0.3333333333333333 cup yogurt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 cup water stock, 0.5 cup water, 0.3333333333333333 cup raw coconut, 1 cashew nuts, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.5 cup water, 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 1 strand mace, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 3 green cardamoms, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 3 inch cinnamon piece
instructions: 1. Add coconut, cashews, fennel seeds and water to a small grinder and make a smooth paste. Keep aside. 2. Heat oil a pan or instant pot (on sauté mode) and add the whole spices. When they begin to sizzle, add curry leaves and onions. 3. Saute until they turn golden. Add ginger garlic and green chili. Saute for 1 minute and add tomatoes. Cook until the tomatoes break down to a mush. 4. Stir in the salt, turmeric, chili powder and garam masala. Add mint leaves and saute until aromatic for 1 to 2 mins. 5. Add yogurt and cook down for 2 to 3 mins. Mix in boiled chickpeas, potatoes, stock/water and coconut. (Note use boiled potatoes if using a normal pan/pot & use raw potatoes only to cook in IP). 6. Stovetop: Bring it to a gentle boil. Cover and simmer for 6 to 8 mins, until thick and aromatic. If it turns too thick, add a splash of hot water. 7. Instant pot: Pour half cup water. Cover the Instant pot and pressure cook for 5 mins. The IP beeps when it is done, press cancel and wait for 2 mins. Gently release the pressure manually. 8. Aloo chana is done when the chickpeas and potatoes are tender and very soft. The gravy is slightly runny at this stage but turns thick after cooling. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala or kasuri methi if you want. 9. Garnish Aloo Chana with chopped coriander leaves. Serve with plain rice, ghee rice, pulao, chapati, roti or dosa.

title: Aloo Cheese Toast Sandwich Recipe | Potato Cheese Toast Sandwich
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp mustard, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.5 onion, 0.5 turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 capsicum, 3 tbsp peas, 3 boiled potato, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 2 tbsp coriander, bread, butter, green chutney, cheese, tomato sauce
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. Splutter 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp mustard, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 2. Add 2 chilli, ½ inch ginger and saute well. 3. Also, add ½ onion and saute until the onions soften slightly. 4. Further add ½ turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. Add in ½ capsicum, 3 tbsp peas and saute for a minute. 6. Add 3 boiled potatoes, ½ tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp cumin powder and mix well. 7. add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. Aloo masala stuffing is ready. 8. To prepare the sandwiches, spread butter on 2 slices of bread. 9. Also, spread green chutney on one side of bread. 10. Top with aloo stuffing and cheese. 11. Now add tomato sauce and cover with the bread slice. 12. Toast the sandwich on the pan till the sandwich turns golden and crispy. 13. Flip over and toast both sides to golden brown. 14. Finally, enjoy Aloo Cheese Sandwich Recipe with tomato sauce.

title: Aloo Chop Recipe | Alur Chop | Aloor Chop
ingredients: 1 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, 3 red chilli, 1.5 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 3 boiled potato, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 2 boiled potato, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp kala namak
instructions: 1. Firstly, to prepare the masala for chop, dry roast 1 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp coriander seeds and 3 dried red chilli. 2. Roast on low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 3. Cool completely, and grind to a fine powder. Keep aside. 4. In a bowl take 1½ cup besan, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder and ½ tsp salt. 5. Add water as required and prepare a smooth batter. Keep aside. 6. To prepare the stuffing, mash 3 boiled potato and keep aside. 7. In a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. Add ½ tsp cumin and pinch hing. 8. Also add 2 chilli, ½ onion,1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute well. 9. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 2 boiled potato, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 10. Add prepared masala powder, 2 tbsp coriander, ½ tsp kala namak and mix well. 11. The stuffing is ready. Allow to cool completely. 12. Once the stuffing is cooled, prepare small patties-shaped mixture. Keep aside. 13. To the besan batter add ¼ tsp baking soda and mix well just before frying. 14. Dip the aloo mixture in besan batter coating the batter uniformly. 15. Deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 16. Stir occasionally, and fry on both sides. 17. Fry until the vada turns crispy brown and golden. Drain off. 18. Finally, enjoy Aloo Chop Recipe with chutney.

title: aloo fry recipe | alu fry recipe | potato masala fry 3 ways | potato fry crispy
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 4 potato aloo, 0.75 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, few curry leaves, 4 aloo potato, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 red chilli, pinch hing, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 4 aloo potato, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp water, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. 2. add 4 potato and mix well making sure the oil is coated well. make sure to cut the aloo on equal sizes and rinse well. 3. stir occasionally, and fry on medium flame. 4. spread the aloo over the kadai making sure the base is roasted well. 5. once the base turns golden brown, give a stir. 6. fry on medium flame until the aloo turns golden brown and crisp. 7. now add ¾ tsp chilli powder and ½ tsp salt. mix well. 8. finally, enjoy crispy aloo fry with hot steamed rice or as a side dish with rasam. 9. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. 10. add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli and few curry leaves. 11. splutter the tempering without burning. 12. add 4 aloo, ½ tsp salt and fry for a minute. make sure to cut the aloo on equal sizes and rinse well. 13. now add ¼ tsp turmeric and mix well. 14. cover and cook on low flame for 10 minutes or until the aloo is cooked well. 15. oven the kadai and roast for a minute, giving a crunchy texture. 16. also, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 17. finally, enjoy chatpate aloo fry with roti or as a side dish with rasam. 18. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. 19. add 1 tsp cumin, 1 dried red chilli, pinch hing and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 20. now add ½ onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until the onions shrink. 21. further, add 4 aloo and ½ tsp salt. 22. saute for a minute making sure the aloo is coated well with oil. 23. cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 24. keep stirring in between to prevent from burning. 25. now sprinkle 2 tbsp water. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the aloo is cooked well. 26. further, add ¼ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp aamchur. 27. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 28. also, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 29. finally, enjoy masaledar aloo fry with roti or as a side dish with rasam.

title: aloo gobi dry recipe | aloo gobhi ki sabji | aloo gobi masala dry
ingredients: 4 tbsp oil, 15 florets gobi cauliflower, 2 potato aloo, 3 tsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 tomato, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai, heat 3 tsp oil and saute 1 tsp cumin and 1 tsp kasuri methi until they turn aromatic. 2. further, add 1 onion and saute well. 3. now add 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli and saute for a minute. 4. keeping the flame on low add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder and ¾ tsp salt. 5. saute on low flame, until the spices turn aromatic. 6. further, add 1 tomato and saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 7. now add fried aloo and gobi. 8. mix gently without breaking them. if you prefer gravy, then add ½ cup water. 9. furthermore, add ½ tsp aamchur, ¼ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp coriander leaves. mix well. 10. finally, aloo gobi dry recipe is ready to serve with roti or rice.

title: Aloo Gobi Recipe (Cauliflower Potato Curry)
ingredients: 1.5 cup potatoes, 2 cups cauliflower florets, 0.875 cup onion, 0.875 cup tomatoes, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.5 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2.5 tablespoons oil, lemon juice to serve, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.75 teaspoon amchur
instructions: 1. Chop cauliflower florets to 1½ inch in size. Add them to slightly hot water and set aside for 3 to 4 mins. 2. Drain the water and rinse them well. Drain them completely. 3. Cube potatoes to ¾ by ¾ inch. or you can also slice them to ¾ inch. Keep them immersed in a bowl of water until used. Keeping them in water helps the potatoes to cook faster as they soak up some moisture. 4. Mince ginger and garlic. Keep this aside. 5. Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. When they sizzle, add ginger garlic & saute for 30 seconds. 6. Then add the onions & stir fry until transparent. 7. Add the potatoes and green chilli. Stir fry for 2 to 3 mins. Cover and cook on a low to medium heat until they are half done. If required you can splash some water so they cook faster. 8. Keep stirring in between and cook covered. 9. When they are slightly tender but still under cooked, add the cauliflower & stir fry for about 3 mins. 10. Add red chilli powder, turmeric, roasted cumin powder, garam masala and coriander powder. 11. Mix well. Sprinkle 2 to 3 tablespoons water all over the aloo gobi or across the sides of the pan & mix well. This step helps keep the aloo gobi moist without drying out. 12. Cook covered, stirring every few minutes, until both aloo & gobi are almost fork tender. Add salt and continue to cook covered on a low flame until the potatoes are soft & fully cooked. 13. The veggies will release moisture at this stage and cook quickly. So keep an eye not to over cook gobi at this stage. It must remain slightly crunchy yet cooked. 14. Next add the tomatoes and kasuri methi. Fry on a medium to high flame until the raw smell of tomatoes is gone. 15. If needed add little oil at this stage. It takes about 2 to 4 mins for the raw smell of tomatoes to go away. 16. Sprinkle amchur if using. Finally garnish aloo gobi with coriander leaves. Sprinkle some lemon juice if you want. Serve with rice, roti or any bread. 17. Option 1 – Oven – Preheat the oven to 450 F / 220 C for 15 mins. Add diced potatoes to a prepared baking tray. Cauliflower to another tray. Mix together chili powder, salt and garam masala in a small bowl and sprinkle half of it over the potatoes and the other half over the cauliflower. Add one teaspoon oil each to potatoes and cauliflower, mix them well and spread on the tray. 18. Place the aloo in the lower rack and the gobi in the middle rack of your oven. Bake for 20 to 22 mins, until fully done and golden. 19. Option 2 – You can also steam or air fry or stir fry the potatoes and cauliflower separately in batches using 2 tablespoons oil. 20. Heat a pan with oil and add cumin and cardamoms. When they sizzle, add onions and saute on a medium flame until golden, for 7 minutes. Add ginger garlic and saute for 40 to 50 seconds. 21. Add the tomatoes and cook until they break down and turn mushy. 22. Stir in the chili powder, turmeric, cumin powder, garam masala and coriander powder. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes until you begin to smell the aroma of spices. 23. Pour in water and cashew cream. Cook covered for 8 to 10 minutes, until the sauce is thick and creamy. 24. Stir in the cauliflower and potatoes and simmer just for 2 to 3 mins. If your sauce is too thick add little hot water. 25. Add salt, amchur and crushed kasuri methi. Taste test to adjust salt or garam masala. Garnish aloo gobi with chopped coriander leaves and squeeze some lemon juice if you want. Serve it over Basmati rice or with flatbreads.

title: aloo idli recipe | aalu ki idli | aloo suji ki idli | aloo rava idli
ingredients: 1 potato, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 tsp chana dal, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, 1 cup rava suji, 0.5 cup curd, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 cup water, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 1 potato and ¼ cup water. make sure to peel the skin and roughly chop. 2. blend to smooth paste. keep aside. 3. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, splutter ½ tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, 1 tsp chana dal, pinch hing, few curry leaves and 2 chilli. 4. now add 1 cup rava and roast on low to medium flame. roast until the rava turns aromatic. 5. transfer to the bowl and cool completely. 6. add in a prepared potato puree, ½ cup curd, ½ tsp salt and 2 tbsp coriander. 7. mix well-combining everything well. 8. rest for 15 minutes or until the rava absorbs well. 9. now add ¼ cup water adjusting consistency as required. 10. just before steaming add ½ tsp eno and mix gently. 11. pour the batter in greased idli plate. 12. and steam the idli for 15 minutes. 13. finally, enjoy aloo idli with coriander chutney.

title: aloo laccha pakora recipe | crispy potato lachha pakoda | aloo pakoda
ingredients: 4 aloo potato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp salt, pinch hing, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 cup maida, 0.5 cup cornflour, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, peel the skin of potato. make sure to take a large and fresh potato. 2. grate using a grater. make sure to grate in one side. do not go to a fro, just grate one direction. 3. take a long grated potato into a large bowl and rinse with cold water to get rid of excess starch. 4. drain off the potato and squeeze off the water. 5. now add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp salt and pinch hing. 6. also, add 1 chilli and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 7. further, add ½ cup maida and ½ cup cornflour. you can replace corn flour with rice flour. 8. mix gently, coating all the aloo well with flour. do not squeeze, as the aloo will then form a lump and not be crispy. 9. now take a small ball sized mixture and deep fry in hot oil. 10. keep stirring in between and fry on medium flame. 11. fry until the aloo pakoda turns golden brown and crisp. 12. drain off the aloo over the kitchen towel to remove excess oil. 13. finally, aloo laccha pakora recipe tastes great with dahi chutney.

title: aloo lachha namkeen | aloo lacha chivda | potato lachha | potato sticks
ingredients: 3 potato aloo, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.25 cup peanuts, 0.25 cup cashew kaju, 2 tbsp dry coconut kopra, few curry leaves, 2 red chilli, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, peel and grate 3 potato. use fresh potatoes, else namkeen will not be crispy. 2. now rinse the grated raw potato well to remove of starch. 3. drain off the potato and squeeze out water completely. 4. pat dry the potatoes to remove any water if present. 5. transfer the grated potato into a large bowl. 6. add 2 tbsp corn flour and mix well. corn flour helps to make namkeen more crispy. 7. deep fry the potato by spreading into hot oil. 8. using the chopstick fry the potato uniformly. 9. fry on medium flame, until the potato turns golden and crisp. 10. drain off the potato over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 11. now in the same oil fry ¼ cup peanuts, ¼ cup cashew, 2 tbsp dry coconut, few curry leaves and 2 dried red chilli. 12. also add ½ tsp pepper, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp aamchur and ½ tsp salt. 13. mix gently making sure all the spices are coated well. 14. finally, enjoy aloo lachha namkeen for a month when stored in an airtight container.

title: aloo manchurian recipe | potato manchurian | alu manchurian
ingredients: 4 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 2 potato aloo, 0.5 cup maida plain flour, 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 cup water, oil, 4 tsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tsp corn flour, 0.25 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large vessel boil 4 cup water with 1 tsp salt. 2. add 2 cubed potato and boil for 8 minutes or until the potatoes are half cooked. 3. drain off the aloo and keep aside. 4. in the kadai heat 4 tsp oil and saute 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp spring onion. 5. also saute ½ capsicum and ½ onion till they shrink slightly. 6. further add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ¼ tsp salt. 7. stir fry on high flame until the sauces are well combined. 8. now prepare corn flour slurry by mixing 1 tsp corn flour with ¼ cup water. 9. pour in corn flour slurry and mix until the sauce thickens and turns glossy. 10. add in fried aloo and mix gently. 11. finally, serve aloo manchurian serve garnished with spring onion.

title: Aloo Masala (Potato Masala Recipe)
ingredients: 2 cups potatoes, 1 medium onions, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.625 teaspoon Salt, 2 tablespoon oil, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 Pinch hing, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Boil potatoes either in a pot or pressure cooker. 2. If cooking in a pot, bring water to a boil and add the diced potatoes. Cook them just until done or fork tender and not mushy. OR If cooking in a pressure cooker, add the potatoes to a bowl and pour little water. Cover and pressure cook for 3 whistles on a medium flame. 3. Ensure potatoes are just cooked and not very soft or mushy. 4. Heat oil in a pan and add mustard and cumin. 5. When they begin to splutter, add chana dal and urad dal. Fry until golden and then add hing. 6. Then add ginger and saute for 30 to 60 seconds until aromatic. 7. Optional step to soften the dal - Pour 2 to 3 tablespoons water to the pan and cook until the dal turns soft. This is an optional step done to soften the dal. Ensure you evaporate all of the water before going to the next step. 8. Add onions, green chili and curry leaves. Saute till the onions turn transparent. 9. Once the onions turn transparent, add potatoes, turmeric and salt.. 10. Mash slightly and add very little water or potato boiled stock to make the entire potato masala moist. Stir and cook for one to 2 mins. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 11. Add coriander leaves. Stir. Serve potato masala with dosa, puri, chapati, bread or rice. 12. Press SAUTE button and pour oil to the inner pot of the instant pot. 13. Add mustard, cumin, chana dal & urad dal. Fry all of these until the dals turn golden. 14. Then add curry leaves, onions and green chilies. 15. Saute onions well until they turn transparent or pink. Add ginger and fry for 30 seconds. 16. Transfer the diced potatoes (1 inch cubes), add salt, turmeric and pour ⅓ cup (80 ml) water. 17. Press CANCEL button. Deglaze the pot by scrubbing the bottom with a spatula to release bits of food stuck. 18. Secure the instant pot with the lid. Position the steam release handle or vent to sealing. 19. Next press PRESSURE COOK button and set the timer to 4 mins (high pressure). 20. When the instant pot is done, press cancel. Release the pressure manually by moving the steam release handle from sealing to venting. 21. If there is any excess moisture left, evaporate it in the saute mode first. 22. Give a good stir and lightly mash only after the excess moisture has evaporated. Add more salt if needed. Garnish with coriander leaves. 23. Serve potato masala with dosa, sandwiches, roti or even in wraps.

title: aloo masala grilled sandwich recipe | potato grilled sandwich recipe
ingredients: 2 sized potato, 0.5 onion, 0.5 cup paneer cottage cheese, salt to taste, 0.5 tsp kashmiri chilli powder, 0.25 tsp chaat masala, butter to grill, 0.25 tsp mixed herbs, 4 bread slices, 2 tsp green chutney, 2 tsp tomato sauce
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl mash boiled potatoes. 2. also add in chopped onions. 3. furthermore, add crumbled paneer. 4. also add chilli powder, chaat masala, mixed herbs and salt. 5. mix and mash the potatoes well. 6. further take bread slices and spread green chutney and tomato sauce. 7. spread the generous amount of prepared potato stuffing. 8. cover with the other bread slice and press gently. 9. then grill or toast on tawa adding some butter. 10. spread butter on both the outer sides of bread to make sandwich more crispy and golden. 11. furthermore, close the griller and grill till the cheese melts and bread turn golden brown. 12. now cut the bread into half and serve. 13. finally, serve hot or pack for kids lunch box.

title: Aloo Matar Chaat Recipe | Delhi Famous Matar Chaat | Potato Peas Chaat
ingredients: 1 cup white peas, 3 potato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 12 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 tomato, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp sugar, 1 tbsp chilli powder, 0.5 lemon, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 onion, 2 tbsp garlic chatni, 2 tomato, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 4 drops red food colour, 1 tsp chaat masala, 2 tbsp coriander, papdi, green chutney, tamarind chutney, garlic chatni, onion, tomato, cucumber, sev, coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup white peas (soaked overnight). 2. add 3 potato, ½ tsp turmeric and ½ tsp salt. 3. also, add 2 cup water and pressure cook for 3 whistles. keep aside. 4. in a mixi jar take 12 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger and 1 tomato. 5. add ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp sugar, 1 tbsp chilli powder, ½ lemon and ½ tsp salt. 6. grind to smooth paste adding water if required. spicy garlic chutney is ready. 7. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi and saute until it turns aromatic. 8. add 1 onion and saute until the onions change colour. 9. further, add 2 tbsp prepared garlic chatni and cook well. 10. also, add 2 tomato and saute until the tomato turns soft and mushy. 11. keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp salt. 12. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 13. mash the boiled potato and add to the kadai. 14. cook until all the spices are well combined. 15. also, add cooked peas and mix well. 16. to get a bright colour, add 4 drops red food colour and water to adjust the consistency. 17. mix well making sure to adjust consistency as required. 18. cover and boil for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 19. now add 1 tsp chaat masala and 2 tbsp coriander. 20. mix well, and aloo matar ragda is ready to prepare the chaat. 21. firstly, crush the papdi into the plate. 22. pour in prepared ragda generously. 23. top with green chutney, tamarind chutney and garlic chutney. 24. further add onion, tomato, cucumber, sev and coriander. 25. finally, enjoy aloo matar chaat with more papdi if required.

title: Aloo Matar Recipe | Aloo Mutter
ingredients: 25 grams potatoes, 0.625 cup green peas, 1 grams onions, 2 grams tomatoes, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.75 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon kasuri methi, 3 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse and chop onions and tomatoes. Puree tomatoes if you do not like them chopped. 2. Also wash and peel potatoes. Cube them to 1 inch pieces. Keep them immersed in water until used. 3. Heat oil in a cooker, pan or pot. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. 4. Add chopped onions, green chili and fry until lightly golden or pink. 5. Next stir in the ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw smell disappears, for about a minute. 6. Add tomato puree and saute for 2 mins. (If using tomatoes, let them cook until they break down to a mushy texture) 7. Next add chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, turmeric and salt. 8. Saute until oil begins to separate from the masala. 9. Add potatoes and peas. Saute for 2 mins. 10. Pour hot water just enough to cover the potatoes, about a cup. Bring it to a rolling boil on a high heat and reduce the heat. Cook covered on a low to medium heat. 11. Stir in between a few times, cook until the potatoes turn soft. You can adjust the consistency by adding more water as needed. 12. If making in pressure cooker then, allow to whistle twice on a medium heat. 13. Add kasuri methi and mix well. Adjust salt if needed. Serve aloo matar with rice or roti. 14. Chop the potatoes to 1 inch pieces. Press saute button on the instant pot and pour oil to the steel insert. 15. When the oil turns hot, add cumin seeds. As soon as they begin to splutter, add chopped onions and saute until light golden. 16. Then stir in the ginger garlic and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. Then add tomato puree and mix well. 17. Add all the spice powders - red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder and salt. 18. Saute for a few minutes until the masala smells aromatic. (For a more authentic version you may saute this till the masala turns thick and aromatic. For this you have to switch over to saute low and saute.) 19. Then pour 1 cup of water for 2 cups of cubed potatoes. After pouring water scrape the bottom of the steel insert with a spatula to deglaze. Add peas. (If using frozen peas that are too tender, add them later once the curry is done. Simmer for a few minutes.) 20. Secure the Instant pot lid with the steam release handle set to sealing. 21. Press pressure cook button (high pressure) & set the timer to 4 minutes. When the IP is done, do a quick release by moving the steam release valve from sealing to venting. 22. Add kasuri methi. Stir and taste test. Add more garam masala and salt if you prefer. For a thicker consistency you can also cook it on a saute mode for 1 minute. 23. Garnish aloo matar with coriander leaves. Serve aloo matar with rice or roti. 24. If you are a beginner check the video on my potato curry post.

title: aloo matar recipe | aloo mutter recipe | alu matar recipe
ingredients: 4 tsp oil, 0.5 inch cinnamon dalchini, 2 pods cardamom elachi, 1 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 to mato, 2 potato aloo, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 cup peas matar, 1 cup water, 1 green chilli, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker heat 4 tsp oil and saute ½ inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom, 1 tsp kasuri methi, ½ tsp cumin till it turns aromatic. 2. now add in ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder and ½ tsp coriander powder. saute on low flame for 30 seconds. 3. further add 1 onion and saute well. 4. additionally add 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute well. 5. now add 2 tomato and saute till the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 6. furthermore, add 2 potato cubed and 1 tsp salt. 7. saute for a minute or more. 8. further add ½ cup peas, 1 cup water and 1 green chilli. 9. mix well and pressure cook for 3 whistles or till potato gets cooked well. 10. once the pressure gets settled down, mash slightly adjusting consistency. 11. add in ½ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp coriander leaves. mix well. 12. finally, serve aloo matar recipe with roti, kulcha or naan.

title: Aloo Methi Paratha Recipe - Perfect Lunchbox Paratha
ingredients: 1 bunch methi fenugreek leaves, 0.25 cup curd, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp sesame, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 potato, 2 cup wheat flour, water, 2 tsp oil
