instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 bunch of finely chopped methi. 2. add ¼ cup curd, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp turmeric and ¾ tsp garam masala. 3. also add ¼ tsp ajwain, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp sesame and ½ tsp salt. 4. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 5. now add 2 potato, 2 cup wheat flour and knead the dough. 6. add water as required and knead to a smooth and soft dough. 7. further, add 2 tsp oil and grease the dough well. 8. cover and rest for 10 minutes. 9. after 10 minutes, knead the dough again. pinch a ball-sized dough. 10. dust with wheat flour and roll uniformly. 11. further, roll to a slightly thick thickness like paratha. 12. now on a hot tawa place the rolled paratha and cook for a minute. 13. furthermore, when the base is partly cooked, flip the aloo methi paratha. 14. also, spread ½ tsp olive oil / ghee and cook both sides. 15. finally, enjoy aloo methi paratha with raita and pickle.

title: Aloo Methi Recipe | Potato Fenugreek Leaves Curry
ingredients: 1 bunch methi fenugreek, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 1 onion, 3 potatoes, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp salt, 2 tomato, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp kala namak
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tsp cumin, 10 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli and saute well. 2. Also, add 1 onion and sauté until the onion changes colour. 3. Add 3 potatoes and sauté for a minute. 4. Cover and cook for 5 minutes, or until the potatoes are cooked well. 5. Now add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp salt and saute till the spices turn aromatic. 6. Further add 2 tomato and cook till tomato softens. 7. Add 1 bunch of chopped methi and cook on a low flame until the methi is well-cooked. 8. Now add ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp kala namak and mix well. 9. Finally, squeeze some lemon juice and enjoy the Aloo Methi Recipe with roti.

title: Aloo Methi Recipe | Potato Fenugreek Leaves Fry
ingredients: 25 grams potatoes, 1.5 cups methi leaves, 2 cloves garlic, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 green chilies, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 pinch hing, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. Pluck methi leaves (only the leaves and not stalks). Add them to a large bowl. 2. Sprinkle one tsp salt and vinegar over the leaves and set aside for 10 minutes. This helps to get rid of the mud and pesticide residue from the leaves. 3. Pour water and rinse the leaves well a few times. Drain the leaves to a colander and set aside. 4. Wash and peel potatoes. Cube them to 1 inch pieces. Keep them immersed in a bowl of water to prevent potatoes from getting discolored. This also helps the potatoes to stir fry or cook faster. 5. Add oil to a pan and heat it. Add cumin seeds. When they begin to crackle, add garlic and green chilli. Fry till garlic begins to smell good. Sprinkle hing. 6. Drain water from the potatoes. 7. Transfer potatoes to the pan and sprinkle turmeric. Fry for one to 2 minutes. 8. Cover and cook on a low flame until they turn soft. Keep stirring in between. If there is no moisture in the pan sprinkle little water and continue to cook or saute them. 9. While the aloo cooks, chop the methi leaves roughly. 10. When the potatoes become fork tender, add garam masala and salt. Stir and fry for a minute. 11. Add chopped methi leaves. Fry until the leaves wilt completely and a nice aroma comes out. Adjust salt if needed. 12. Serve aloo methi with rice or roti.

title: Aloo Mushroom Curry (Potato Mushroom Gravy)
ingredients: 2.5 potatoes, 2 grams white button mushrooms, 2.5 tbsps oil, 1 bay leaves, 0.625 tsp cumin, 1 cup onions, 1.5 green chilies, 1.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 2.5 tomatoes, 1 tsp red chili powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, Salt, 9 cashews, 0.75 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 Handful coriander leaves, water
instructions: 1. Add cubed tomatoes to a jar along with cashews. Make a fine puree. Keep this aside. 2. Wash and chop onions very finely, slit green chilies. 3. Rinse mushrooms and cut them to desired sizes. 4. Add 1 tbsp oil to a pan and heat it. 5. Toss these mushrooms in the oil on a medium flame until they wilt. This takes about 3 to 4 mins. 6. Set these mushrooms aside. 7. Add another 2 tbsps oil to the same pan. 8. Add jeera and bay leaf. 9. When the jeera begins to sizzle add chopped onions and green chilies. 10. Fry them until golden on a medium heat. 11. While the onions fry, wash, peel and cube potatoes to 1 inch sizes. 12. When the onions turn golden, add the ginger garlic paste and fry for a minute. 13. Then add in tomato puree and cook until the moisture evaporates completely. 14. Add red chili powder, turmeric, salt and garam masala. Mix all these and fry for 2 to 3 mins. 15. Add the cubed potatoes and saute for 2 to 3 mins. 16. Pour water just enough to make a gravy. Mix well. Cover and cook until the potatoes are done. 17. Add mushrooms & kasuri methi (optional). Mix and cook covered until the mushrooms are done to your liking. 18. Check the salt and spice, adjust as needed. Cook until the gravy reaches a thick consistency. 19. Sprinkle coriander leaves and keep covered until served. 20. Serve aloo mushroom curry with rice or roti.

title: Aloo Pakora Recipe (Potato Pakora, Aloo Bajji)
ingredients: 2 medium potatoes, 0.5 cup besan, 2 tablespoons rice flour, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon carom seeds, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, oil
instructions: 1. Wash, peel and slice the potato to moderately thin slices (not very thin slices). (refer picture in the step by step section) 2. Keep them immersed in water until used. Apart from getting rid of the excess starch, this step helps to cook the potatoes easily. 3. Add gram flour, rice flour, carom seeds, salt, chili powder, ginger garlic & turmeric to a mixing bowl. 4. Pour water and make a moderately thick batter. The batter must be of a medium consistency, neither too thick nor too runny. (check the pictures in the step by step section) 5. If it is too runny you may adjust the consistency by adding little more flour. Taste the batter and add more salt or spice if needed. 6. Heat oil in a fry pan on a medium heat. 7. While the oil heats, drain all of the water from the potatoes completely. 8. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a little portion of the batter to the oil. The batter must rise without browning a lot. This is the right temperature. 9. If the batter sinks it means the oil is not hot enough. 10. Dip each slice of potato in batter and carefully slide it to the hot oil. If you are unable to coat the potatoes well, sprinkle little water and slightly thin down the batter. 11. Repeat coating the potato slices in batter and gently slide them to hot oil. Do not disturb them for 2 minutes until they firm up. 12. Fry them on a medium heat, turning them to the other side as required. Keep stirring in between for even frying. 13. When the aloo pakora turns crisp and golden, remove them on to a cooling rack or a steel colander. 14. Repeat making the next batch. Ensure the oil is hot but not too hot. 15. Sprinkle some chaat masala over aloo pakora or serve it with this mint chutney.

title: Aloo Pakora Recipe | Aloo Bajji | Potato Pakoda - Street Style
ingredients: 2 potato, 1 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, pinch hing, 0.5 tsp salt, water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 2 tbsp coriander, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, peel the skin of the potato and slice it thinly. 2. Soak the slices in the water to remove the excess starch. 3. Remove the potato slices from the water and rub with a cloth to remove excess water. Keep aside. 4. In a bowl, take 1 cup besan, 2 tbsp rice flour, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 5. Now add water slowly and mix using a whisk to prevent any lumps. 6. Add ¼ tsp baking soda, 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. Pakora batter is ready to prepare pakoda. 7. Dip the potato slice in batter, and deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 8. Stir occasionally, until the pakoda turns golden brown and crispy 9. Finally, drain off and enjoy Aloo Pakora Recipe with green chutney.

title: Aloo Palak Recipe (Spinach and Potato Recipe)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 cloves garlic, 1 pinch asafoetida, 2 green chilies, 1 medium onion, 2 cups potatoes, 4 cups spinach, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds, 0.75 teaspoon kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Rinse the spinach in lots of water a few times and drain them to a colander. 2. Peel & cube the potatoes to bite sized pieces. optional - Keep them immersed in a bowl of water to avoid discoloration. 3. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan. 4. When the oil turns hot add ¾ teaspoon cumin and let them splutter. 5. Then add 2 cloves of garlic finely chopped and 1 to 2 chopped green chilies. Fry them for a minute. Sprinkle hing. 6. Next add medium sized chopped or sliced onion and fry until light golden. 7. Drain the water from potatoes completely. Then add them to the pan & fry for 3 to 4 minutes. 8. You can just stir fry until the potatoes are fork tender or Pour 3 tablespoons water and cook covered until tender but not mushy. 9. Then sprinkle 1/8 teaspoon turmeric, ¼ teaspoon salt, ¼ teaspoon chili powder (optional), kasuri methi & garam masala (optional). Saute for a minute or two until the raw smell of chili powder goes away. Taste test and add more salt or spice at this level. 10. Next add 4 cups of chopped palak. Saute on a medium high flame for about 3 minutes or until the leaves wilt. 11. When the moisture evaporates turn off the stove. You can sprinkle 1 tsp of toasted sesame seeds before finishing 12. Sprinkle lemon juice before serving. Serve aloo palak with roti, chapati, paratha or rice.

title: Aloo Palak Recipe | Aalu Palak Ki Sabji | Potato Spinach Dry Curry
ingredients: 0.25 cup oil, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 red chilli, 5 cloves garlic, 2 inch ginger, 2 onion, 3 potato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp water, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tomato, 1 bunch palak, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp spring onion
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat ¼ cup oil. saute 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing and 1 dried red chilli until they turn aromatic. 2. add 5 cloves garlic, 2 inch ginger and saute well. 3. further, add 2 onion and saute until the onion turns golden brown. 4. now add 3 potato, ½ tsp turmeric and ½ tsp salt. 5. stir fry for 2 minutes making sure the turmeric is well combined. 6. add 2 tbsp water, cover and simmer for 10 minutes or until the aloo is cooked well. 7. now add 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and ½ tsp salt. 8. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 9. further, add 3 tomato and saute for 2 minutes. 10. also, add 1 bunch palak. do not mix the palak, as the leaves will not take much time to mix. 11. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until palak and aloo are cooked completely. 12. add ½ tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp kasuri methi and 2 tbsp spring onion. mix well. 13. finally, enjoy Aloo Palak Recipe with roti or jeera rice.

title: aloo paneer tikki recipe | paneer potato cutlet | paneer alu tikkis
ingredients: 2 potato aloo, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 cup cornflour, 0.5 tsp salt, 1.25 cup paneer cottage cheese, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp mint pudina, 0.25 tsp salt, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 potato boiled & mashed potato. 2. add 1 chilli, ½ tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 tbsp coriander, ¼ cup cornflour and ½ tsp salt. 3. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. now in another bowl take 1¼ cup paneer. 5. add ½ tsp ginger garlic paste, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp chaat masala. 6. also, add 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp mint and ¼ tsp salt. 7. mix well making sure everything is well combined. paneer stuffing is ready. 8. grease your hand with oil and pinch a ball sized aloo mixture. 9. flatten slightly making sure there is enough space. 10. now place a small ball sized prepared paneer stuffing in the centre. 11. wrap and seal the stuffing well. 12. now flatten slightly forming tikki. 13. heat oil in a pan and shallow fry on medium flame. 14. flip over once the base turns golden brown. 15. fry both sides until it turns golden brown and crisp. 16. finally, enjoy aloo paneer tikki with tomato sauce and green chutney.

title: aloo paratha recipe | aalu ka paratha | aloo ka paratha | potato paratha
ingredients: 2 cup wheat flour, 0.5 tsp salt, water, 2 tbsp oil, 3 potato aloo, 1 tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp coriander seeds, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.75 tsp aamchur, 0.5 tsp salt, wheat flour, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup wheat flour and ½ tsp salt. mix well. 2. add water as required and start to knead. 3. now add 2 tbsp oil and start to knead well. 4. knead to a smooth and soft dough. 5. grease oil and rest for 20 minutes. 6. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 potato, 1 tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander and ½ tsp coriander seeds. 7. also, add ¼ tsp ajwain, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ¾ tsp aamchur and ½ tsp salt. 8. mix well making sure all the ingredients are well combined. 9. aloo stuffing is ready. 10. after the dough has rested for 20 minutes, knead slightly. 11. now pinch a ball sized dough and roll about 4 inches in diameter. 12. place a ball sized prepared aloo stuffing in the centre. 13. take the edge and start pleating bringing it to the centre. 14. also join the pleats together and secure tight pinching off excess dough. 15. sprinkle some wheat flour and roll slightly thick. 16. on a hot tawa place the rolled paratha and cook for a minute. 17. furthermore, flip the paratha when the base is partly cooked (after a minute). 18. also, brush oil / ghee and press slightly. flip again once or twice till both the sides are cooked properly. 19. finally, serve hot aloo paratha with sauce, raita or pickle.

title: Aloo Paratha Recipe | Potato Stuffed Paratha
ingredients: 2 cups whole wheat flour, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon oil, water, 35 grams potatoes, 1 green chilli, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 teaspoon ajwain, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds powder, 4 tablespoons ghee
instructions: 1. Boil potatoes just until fork tender without making them mushy. To pressure cook in traditional cooker, cook for 3 to 4 whistles depending on the size of potatoes. In instant pot I pressure cooked for 5 mins since I used medium sized potatoes. 2. While the potatoes boil, make the dough. Add flour,oil and salt to a bowl. Pour half cup water and begin to mix to form a dough. Pour more water as needed and make a soft yet non-sticky dough. 3. Knead it well until soft & pliable. If you press down the dough with your finger, it should dent easily meaning it is soft enough. Cover and rest until the filling is ready. 4. When the potatoes are done, cool them slightly and peel while still warm. Ensure your boiled potatoes are not mushy or soggy. 5. Grate or mash them well until no bits of potatoes remain. Using your fingers break any chunks of potatoes left in the mash otherwise they will tear the parathas. Also do not over mash it as the potatoes will turn pasty and sticky. 6. Add ginger, green chilies, coriander leaves, salt, red chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder, kasuri methi and chaat masala. Crush the kasuri methi before adding. 7. Optional: Also add the optional ingredients - ajwain and fennel powder. 8. Mix all of the ingredients gently and taste test it. Add more salt or spices as per your taste. 9. Divide the aloo stuffing to 8 parts. Also divide the dough to 8 parts. Keep the dough covered. 10. Roll a dough ball in your palms to smooth. Lightly sprinkle some flour on the rolling area. Flatten the dough ball and dip it in some flour. 11. Method 1- Dust off the excess & roll it to a 4 inch disc or roti. Place a portion of aloo stuffing in the center. Gently bring the sides of the roti up shaping like a cup. Repeat the steps of pushing the stuffing inside with your finger and bringing the dough up until the edges come over the stuffing. (Refer video). 12. If the stuffing is sticky, dip your fingers in flour. Bring the edges together over the stuffing and join them to seal carefully. (Refer step-by-step photo or video). 13. Method 2 - Roll a 5 inch roti and place the stuffing in the center, hold the edges and make small pleats. Bring all of them on top and join them together to seal the stuffing. Remove the excess dough on top. 14. Dip the stuffed ball in flour and flatten it gently to spread the stuffing evenly. This ensures the stuffing does not come out at all. Dust off the excess. 15. Sprinkle some flour on the rolling area . 16. Place the paratha disc on the rolling area with the sealed side down. Begin to roll evenly all over without putting pressure. 17. Do not roll the aloo paratha continuously. Instead rotate in clock-wise direction a little after every 4 to 6 rolls to ensure it does not tear or get stuck. Sprinkle flour as needed and avoid using excess. If you see the stuffing coming out, gently apply some flour to seal it. 18. Roll the aloo paratha as thin or thick you like without tearing them. I roll mine to 8 to 9 inches. 19. Transfer the rolled ones to a dry area. Once you are done with 3 you can begin to fry them. 20. Method 3 - With this method, you will be able to make only 6 large parathas. So divide the dough to 12 and stuffing to 6 parts. Roll 2 rotis of 6 inches each. Spread the aloo mixture on one of the roti. Wet the edges with little water. Place the other roti on top of it. Press down gently on the sides to seal both the rotis. Then sprinkle flour and roll it evenly as thin as you want. 21. Heat a pan or griddle to cook the aloo parathas. The griddle should be hot enough otherwise the parathas will turn hard, 22. When the pan is hot, gently transfer a rolled aloo paratha to it. 23. With in 2 to 3 mins, you will see bubbles over it. Then flip it to the other side and cook. 24. You will see the aloo paratha puffs and cooks well. Drizzle ghee or oil all over and flip it to the other side and cook. 25. Smear some ghee on this side as well and press down the edges to cook thoroughly. 26. Soon you will see golden to light brown spots on the aloo parathas. Remove to a plate and stack them. This keeps them soft. 27. Top aloo paratha with some butter and serve with pickle or yogurt. This coriander chutney or mint chutney also go well.

title: aloo pizza recipe | aloo tikki pizza on tawa | aalu ka pizza
ingredients: 2 potato, 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp salt, oil, pizza sauce, onion, capsicum, tomato, sweet corn, olives, cheese, chilli flakes, mixed herbs
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 potato and ¼ cup corn flour. corn flour helps to absorb moisture from potato and holds shape while frying. 2. also add ½ tsp chilli flakes, ¼ tsp pepper powder, ½ tsp mixed herbs and ½ tsp salt. 3. mix well making a soft dough. 4. now grease hands with oil and pinch a ball sized dough. 5. shape to tikki and shallow fry in hot oil. 6. flip over once the base turns golden brown. 7. fry the tikki on both sides until it turns golden brown and crisp. 8. drain off the aloo tikki, and keep aside. 9. to prepare pizza, place the tikki in another pan with less oil. 10. spread 1 tsp pizza sauce on each tikki. 11. also top with onion, capsicum, tomato, sweet corn, olives and cheese. 12. further, sprinkle chilli flakes and mixed herbs. 13. cover and simmer for 2 minutes or until the cheese melt completely. 14. finally, enjoy crispy aloo tikki pizza.

title: Aloo Puri Recipe | Masala Potato Poori
ingredients: 3 aloo potato, 2 cup wheat flour atta, 2 tbsp rava semolina, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 0.5 tsp salt, water, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup of wheat flour and 2 tbsp rava. 2. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ¼ tsp ajwain, 1 tsp kasuri methi and ½ tsp salt. 3. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 4. further, add mashed 3 potatoes and mix well. 5. add water and start to knead the dough. 6. make sure to knead the dough adding water as required. also note that aloo will release the moisture, so add water accordingly. 7. knead to a smooth and tight dough. 8. also, add 2 tbsp oil and knead the dough. 9. now take a ball and roll using a rolling pin. 10. roll to slightly thick thickness. 11. drop the rolled dough into the hot oil. 12. press until the puri puffs up and splash oil to puff up fully. 13. flip over and fry until it turns golden brown. 14. finally, drain off the aloo poori and is ready to enjoy with sabzi or pickle.

title: aloo roti recipe | potato roti recipe | how to make alu roti
ingredients: 2 potato aloo, 2 cup wheat flour atta, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 0.5 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp lemon juice, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp kasuri methi, water to knead, 1 tsp oil, oil for roasting
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take all the ingridents and knead to a smooth and soft dough. 2. pinch a ball sized dough and flatten slightly. 3. dust with wheat flour and roll gently. 4. now on a hot tawa place the rolled roti and cook for a minute. 5. furthermore, when the base is partly cooked, flip the aloo roti. 6. also, spread ½ tsp oil / ghee and press slightly both the sides. 7. flip again once or twice till both the sides are cooked properly. 8. finally, serve aloo roti with mix veg sabzi, raita and pickle.

title: aloo tikki burger recipe | mcaloo tikki recipe | burger tikki recipe
ingredients: 2 potatoes, 0.25 cup peas matar, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 cup bread crumbs, 0.5 tsp kashmiri chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, oil for deep frying, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.25 tsp pepper, salt to taste, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup thick poha beaten rice aval, 3 tbsp maida plain flour all purpose flour, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 cup water, 3 tbsp eggless mayonnaise, 3 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 4 burger bun, few lettuce leaves, 1 to mato, 1 onion
instructions: 1. firstly, take 2 boiled and mashed potatoes, ¼ cup boiled peas and spices. 2. combine everything and prepare patties. 3. dip into maida paste and roll in bread crumbs. 4. deep fry in hot oil or bake at 180 degree celsius for 20 minutes or till it turns golden and crisp. 5. cut half the burger bun and spread a tsp of prepared burger sauce on both sides. 6. on bottom side of bun place few lettuce followed by prepared aloo patties. 7. again spread a tsp of burger sauce. 8. place 2 slice of tomato and 2 rings of onion. 9. cover with burger bun and press slightly. 10. finally, serve aloo tikki burger with french fries or potato chips.

title: Aloo Tikki Recipe
ingredients: 4 medium potatoes, 1 tablespoon ginger, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 tablespoon ghee optional, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoon corn flour or, 4 tablespoon bread crumbs, 0.75 teaspoon Salt, 2 tablespoon cashews, 5 tablespoon Oil, 1 teaspoon oil, 2 tablespoons cashews, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1 green chilli, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 cup peas, 1.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.75 cup paneer
instructions: 1. Boil potatoes just until cooked. Do not overcook them. They should be firm and not mushy or soggy. (check the tips above in intro for details). 2. Cooker: Add them to a cooker and pour water to partially cover them. Cook for 3 to 5 whistles depending on the size of your cooker. Instant pot: Pressure cook them for 7 to 8 minutes and let the pressure release naturally. 3. Heat oil in a small pan and fry the cashews until golden. Fry ginger and green chilis for 40 to 50 seconds. Lower the heat and stir in red chilli powder, garam masala, salt and chaat masala. 4. Add the peas and cook covered on a low heat till tender. If the pan is dry pour 3 to 4 tablespoons water. When they are soft cooked, mash them gently and turn off the heat. 5. Crush kasuri methi in your palms and add it along with coriander leaves and crumbled paneer. Mix well taste test and adjust salt as required. Keep this aside to cool. 6. Cool the boiled potatoes completely and remove the skin. Grate or mash them in a mixing bowl. Make sure there are no chunks of potatoes. 7. Add cashews, ginger paste, garam masala, chaat masala, salt and red chili powder. 8. Heat 1 tbsp ghee till very hot in a kadai. Pour that over the red chili powder to remove the raw flavor. Add coriander leaves and green chilies. 9. Make sure the potatoes are cooled first before adding the flour and bread crumbs. Begin to mix and make a firm and non sticky ball. If required you can add more bread crumbs or flour to absorb moisture. 10. The mixture must be firm and non-sticky. If it is sticky, it means it is too moist. So add more bread crumbs or flour. 11. Divide the mixture to 6 equal portions to make stuffed tikki or to 8 equal portions to make plain tikki. Roll them to smooth balls keep covered. 12. Plain Aloo Tikki: Flatten the ball to a thin tikki. Place them in a tray and set aside. 13. Stuffed Tikki: To make suffed tikki, shape the dough to a cup (check pictures in the post). Place 2 tablespoons of the paneer peas mixture inside the cup and begin to press down inside and bring the sides up and seal the aloo tikki. Make all the tikkis and place them in a tray. 14. Heat 4 to 6 tbsps oil in a medium sized pan. To test if the oil is hot, drop a small portion of dough to the oil. It has to sizzle and come up to the surface without turning brown. 15. Gently transfer the aloo tikkis one after the other to the oil. Do not disturb them until the base of the tikki firms up, for at least 2 to 3 mins. 16. Turn them to the other side gently and fry until golden & crisp on both the sides. 17. Remove them to cooling rack. For extra crisp tikkis, you can refry them in the oil again. 18. OR Bake in oven Alternately brush the patties with oil & place on a greased wire rack over a tray. Bake in a preheated oven for 18 to 20 mins at 390 F (200 C) or until crisp. Flip them to the other side after 10 to 12 mins. Adjust the timing based on your oven. 19. Air frying: Brush the tikkis & place them in the air fryer wired rack. Air fry at 360 F (180 C) for 8 to 10 mins on each side. Keep check and adjust the timing as needed. If your air fryer needs preheating, preheat it first before air frying. 20. Serve aloo tikki hot with coriander chutney, mint chutney or serve them inside your burger. (to make check the tips in intro)

title: Aloo Tikki Recipe | Multi Purpose Aloo Patties
ingredients: 3 potato aloo, 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp mint pudina, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp corn flour, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 3 boiled and grated potato. make sure to pressure cook potatoes for 4-5 whistles and drain off the water as soon as pressure releases. 2. add 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, ¼ tsp turmeric and ½ tsp chilli powder. 3. also add ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp aamchur, ½ tsp chaat masala and ½ tsp salt. 4. further add 2 tbsp mint and 2 tbsp coriander. 5. now add 2 tbsp cornflour and mix well alternatively use poha powder or rice flour as it helps to absorb moisture. 6. mix well forming a soft dough. 7. grease hand with oil and prepare ball-sized tikki. 8. shallow fry in hot oil. you can alternatively roast or deep fry. 9. roast on low flame until it turns golden brown. 10. flip over and cook on both sides. 11. finally, enjoy aloo tikki with green chutney, tamarind chutney or prepare aloo tikki chaat.

title: Ambur Star Biryani
ingredients: 5 grams Chicken, 1.5 cups seeraga samba rice, 3 tbsp oil, 4 inch cinnamon sticks, 9 cloves 8 to 10 cloves, 9 green cardamoms, 2 tbsp sliced onions, 1 tbsp garlic paste, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 Handful coriander leaves, 7 red chilies, 0.5 cup onions, 0.25 cup tomato, 1.5 tbsp curd, 1 tbsp Lemon juice, 1 Handful mint, 1 Handful coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash and soak rice for 10 mins. This is optional. Drain and set aside. 2. Deseed & soak red chilies for about an hour. Make a fine paste with little water used for soaking. 3. Heat oil in a pan and saute cinnamon, cloves and cardamoms 4. Add 2 tbsps onions and saute for 1 to 2 mins. 5. Saute garlic paste for a minute and then ginger paste as well for a minute. 6. Add coriander leaves and mint. Saute until it begins to smell good. This may take about 2 mins. 7. Add the red chilli paste and saute everything together until oil begins to separate. 8. Add half cup onions and fry for 2 to 3 mins until they turn transparent. 9. Add tomatoes and fry until they turn very soft and mushy. 10. Add chicken and fry on a low to medium heat until the chicken turns pale or white. This may take about 2 to 3 mins. 11. Add curd and salt. 12. Cover the pot and cook for 5 mins on a very low heat. Chicken begins to ooze out moisture. 13. Mix it and continue to cook until chicken turns completely soft. 14. While the chicken cooks, bring water to a boil in a large pot with salt. 15. Add drained rice and cook until 70% done. At this stage rice is still under cooked and you will very well know that it is undone if you bite it. The grain will still be hard in the center. 16. Make sure chicken is soft cooked before adding rice. Add coriander and mint leaves. Mix and level the mixture. At this stage you will see lot of moisture that has been released while cooking chicken. 17. Drain the rice completely and add it to the chicken pot. Add the lemon juice all over the rice. 18. Gently stir for the rice to get mixed with the moisture from chicken. 19. Cover and cook on the lowest heat until the rice has absorbed all the moisture. You can also use a old dosa tawa/ griddle for dum process. To do that heat a griddle first, then place this biryani pot over it. Cook until rice is done. 20. Open the lid and mix gently again. 21. If the rice is under cooked and there is no moisture you may sprinkle some warm water. Stir and cook covered. However I did not use any water at this stage. 22. Once the rice is done, fluff up well and cover it again. Cook for another 2 mins. Switch off and rest for about 5 to 20 mins before serving. Fluff up with a fork before serving. 23. Serve Ambur biryani with ennai kathirikai and raita.

title: Amla Juice Recipe (Gooseberry Juice)
ingredients: 5.5 amla, 1.5 cups liquid, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.375 inch ginger, 1 tablespoon honey, 1 pinch pink salt, 17.5 mint leaves, 13.5 holy basil, 0.25 teaspoon chaat masala
instructions: 1. Rinse amla well under running water. Soak them in a bowl of salted water for 5 to 10 mins and scrub them with a soft vegetable brush to get rid of any dirt and deposits. 2. Rinse well again and chop them to pieces. Add them to a grinder/blender jar with little liquid of choice (water/ coconut water/juice) & ginger, mint, tulsi if using. 3. Grind to a smooth paste. Strain/filter this to a pitcher, pressing down with a spoon and pour the rest of the liquid. Stir in pepper, honey, salt or chaat masala as per your choice. Serve immediately. 4. To make Shots: You will just grind only the amla and ginger (optional) without any liquid. The mixture will look slightly dry but will release all of the juices as you grind. 5. Run your slow juicer and add up to 3 Indian gooseberries (deseeded) per serving to your juicer chute. You can also add ginger or any other fruit of choice like watermelon, apple or Asian pears.

title: Amla Pickle Recipe
ingredients: 1 amla, 2 teaspoons mustard, 1 pinch methi seeds, 1 teaspoon salt, 4 tablespoons oil, 1 lemon, 3.5 teaspoons red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 garlic clove
instructions: 1. Wash and dry amla well. Wipe them with a clean tissue or cloth. Make few incisions with knife randomly. You can also chop them and use. 2. Powder 2 tsps mustard and generous pinch of methi seeds in a small blender jar. It has to be a fine powder. 3. Fry amla in oil until partially cooked. They turn light golden in color when they are done. 4. Add garlic and switch off. Allow to cool down a bit. 5. When still hot, add other ingredients except lemon juice. 6. Once cooled squeeze lemon juice. 7. Mix well and taste it. Add more salt if needed. Store in a glass jar. Rest for at least 2 to 3 days before using. Mix well with a dry spoon. Check salt and sourness. If needed add more salt and lemon juice. 8. Serve amla pickle with rice, dosa or paratha. 9. Wash amlas under running water and drain water completely. 10. Dry them on a clean cloth to keep them moisture free. 11. Boil 3 cups of water until it comes to a rolling boil. Turn off the heat and cool completely. 12. Deseed the tamarind and remove the fibers and shells if any. 13. Spread tamarind on a wide plate and sun dry for at least 2 hours just to ensure it is moisture free. 14. Sun dry crystal salt along with tamarind. 15. Spread red chili powder on a wide plate and sun dry for at least 30 mins. You can skip this step if using freshly milled red chilli powder. 16. Keep the garlic flakes in the sun for sometime just to ensure they are moisture free. 17. Ensure blender jars, mixing bowls and ladles all are free from moisture. I suggest sun drying them for a while. 18. Place the tamarind in a dry bowl and pour about 1 and a half cups water. Rinse well with this water. 19. Pour the rest of the water just as needed to soak the tamarind. The water must be just enough to soak it. Do not use excess water. The tamarind must turn very soft and must be moist. So if it looks dry, pour in more water. 20. Soak this for 6 hours. You can keep this for up to 12 hours. This can be done the previous night. 21. Roast methi seeds in half tbsp oil on a medium flame. Keep stirring fry until the seeds turn golden and light brown. 22. Cool these completely and make a fine powder. 23. Make 3 to 4 incisions on the amlas with a knife. 24. Heat oil for frying amlas. 25. When the oil is hot, fry them for 2 to 3 mins. You can fry them in batches. 26. Transfer to a plate and Cool these completely. 27. Add crystal salt and garlic to a blender and make a coarse paste. 28. Set this aside to a bowl. 29. Use a dry and clean hand to remove any fibers from the tamarind. 30. Add this tamarind to the blender jar and make a fine paste. 31. Transfer this to the garlic and mix both of them. 32. Blend this mixture to a smooth paste. 33. Add red chili powder and methi powder. Mix everything welll. 34. Add in the amla and pour the lemon juice. Taste the pickle and add in more salt or chili if needed. 35. Mix everything well. 36. Heat oil for seasoning. 37. When the oil turns hot, add mustard, garlic and red chilies. 38. When the garlic turns slightly golden, add in curry leaves. 39. When the curry leaves turn crisp, turn off the heat. Add the hing. 40. Pour this to the amla pickle along with the oil used for frying amla. 41. Mix everything well and allow to cool down completely. 42. Transfer to a glass or ceramic jar or bharani. 43. Refrigerate andhra amla pickle and use upto an year.

title: Amritsari Fish Fry (Tawa Fish Fry)
ingredients: 3 grams fish, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon ajwain, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon amchur powder, 2 tablespoons curd, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 3 tablespoons besan, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 2 tablespoons oil
instructions: 1. Rinse the fish well and pat dry. 2. To a mixing bowl, add gram flour, salt, red chilli powder, garam masala, turmeric, amchur, kasuri methi, ajwain, ginger garlic paste & lemon juice. 3. Mix all of them well. Taste test & add more salt and red chilli powder if needed. 4. Transfer the fish pieces and add 2 tablespoons curd or water. Coat the fish well with all of these. 5. If it is too dry, you may sprinkle some water. All of the fish pieces should be coated well with the masala. 6. Pour ¼ to ½ cup oil and heat it. When the oil turns hot, test by dropping a small portion of the masala to the oil. 7. It has to rise with out browning a lot. This is the right temperature. Gently slide the fish pieces into the oil. 8. Deep fry them until crisp and golden. You will begin to smell it good when the fish is done. 9. Serve Amritsari fish hot as a side or appetizer. 10. Dry roast besan on a medium flame for few mins till you get a nice roasted aroma. This is done to remove the raw smell from the besan. Do not darken the flour. 11. Set this aside to cool. 12. Rinse fish and cut to desired sizes. Drain the water completely. 13. To a mixing bowl, add ginger garlic, red chilli powder, garam masala, turmeric, salt, amchur, kasuri methi & lemon juice. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 14. Add the fish to this and marinate well. If the fish looks too dry then sprinkle some water. I don't use curd for pan frying. Set aside for 10 mins. 15. Heat a wide pan with 2 tablespoons oil. Add ajwain. 16. When they begin to splutter, place the fish pieces one by one and fry them on a medium flame for 4 mins. 17. Flip the pieces and let them fry for another 5 mins. 18. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons besan evenly all over on one side and continue to fry without burning. To avoid lumps I sieve the flour directly on the fish. 19. Flip the pieces and fry again. The flour that we sprinkle on one side will be enough for both the sides. If needed can increase the flame slightly and fry on both sides till cooked. 20. When it is done you will begin to smell the whole thing good. Serve tawa fish hot as a starter or as a side dish.

title: amti recipe | maharashtrian amti dal recipe | toor dal amti
ingredients: 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tsp mustard rai, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin, pinch of hing asafoetida, few curry leaves, 1 green chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 2 cup toor dal, 1 tbsp goda masala, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tbsp jaggery gud, 5 kokum punarpuli, 2 tbsp coconut, 0.75 tsp salt, water, 2 tbsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp ghee and saute 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp jeera, pinch of hing and few curry leaves. 2. also add 1 green chilli, ¼ tsp turmeric and saute on low flame. 3. further add 2 cup cooked toor dal (1 cup dal + 3 cup water pressure cooked for 5 whistles) 4. get the dal to boil and add 1 tbsp goda masala, ¼ tsp chilli powder, ½ tbsp jaggery, 5 kokum, 2 tbsp coconut, ¾ tsp salt. mix well. 5. add ½ cup water and adjust consistency as required. 6. mix well and boil for 5 minutes or till spices gets cooked completely. 7. finally, add coriander leaves and serve amti dal with hot steamed rice.

title: Andhra Chicken Biryani
ingredients: 2 cups basmati rice, 3.5 cups water, 4 tablespoons oil, 1 onion, 2.5 green chili, 1 medium tomato, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup mint, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 4 green cardamoms, 0.375 teaspoon Pepper corn, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon stick, 0.75 teaspoon fennel seeds, 1 bay leaf, 3 green cardamom, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon stick, 0.25 pepper corn, 0.125 nutmeg, 1 strand mace, 1.5 star anise, 0.5 kg bone-in chicken, 0.5 cup yogurt, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Marinade: Marinate the chicken with yogurt, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, salt and red chilli powder. Keep aside. You can also marinate the previous day and refrigerate up to 48 hours. 2. Biryani masala: Powder the ingredients mentioned under to powder or use a readymade biryani masala. 3. Prepare Rice: Wash rice several times and soak for 20 mins. Drain and set aside. 4. Meanwhile, slice onions and green chilies. Pluck the mint and coriander leaves. Clean, rinse and fine chop them. Keep all the whole spices ready for tempering. 5. Heat a deep pan or a casserole or a pressure pan or cooker with oil. 6. Add all the whole spices - bay leaf, green cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon stick, black pepper corn (optional), nutmeg (optional), mace and star anise. 7. When they begin to sizzle, add onions & green chili. On a medium heat, fry till the onions turn golden color and lose the raw flavor. 8. Add ginger garlic paste and fry till the raw smell disappears. 9. Add the mint & coriander leaves. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes and then stir in the tomatoes and saute until they turn pulpy & soft. 10. Add marinated chicken and fry on high flame till the chicken turns white in color. This usually takes 3 to 4 mins. 11. Add powder masala or biryani masala powder and mix well. Saute till the mix begins to leave the sides of the pan. At this stage you will see oil begins to release. 12. Pour hot water, cover and bring the water to a rolling boil on a medium heat. Chicken will be almost cooked by this time else cook further by covering until chicken turns tender. 13. Drain the water completely from the rice and and add it here. Stir in the salt. 14. On a medium flame, Cook without the lid till almost all the water evaporates. Later turn down the heat to low. Mix well from the bottom. 15. To Cook in a Pot: Cover the pot with a lid and cook till the rice is fully done. If it looks too dry, pour some boiling hot water as this depends on the kind of rice used. 16. Optional Dum Cooking: To prevent the rice being burnt at the bottom, place the biryani pot on a hot tawa/ griddle and dum cook till the rice is cooked fully. When you see the rice is cooked and no excess moisture is left, turn off the heat. 17. If using a pressure cooker, close the lid and cook for about 5 minutes on a low flame. Turn off and Do not let it whistle. When the pressure drops open the lid. 18. Let rest the cooked biryani for at least 10 mins. Fluff up Andhra chicken biryani with a fork and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve with Onion Raita.

title: Andhra Chicken Curry Recipe (Kodi Kura)
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 tablespoon red chili powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 3.5 tablespoons Oil, 1.5 cups onions, 1.5 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tablespoon red chili powder, 1 tomato deseeded and chopped finely, 1 handful mint leaves, 0.625 cup water, 3 tablespoons white poppy seeds, 2 tablespoons raw coconut, 1 bay leaf, 2.5 cloves 2 to 3 cloves, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 0.375 teaspoon shahi jeera, 2.5 tablespoons Coriander seeds, 2 inch Cinnamon piece, 5 cloves 4 to 6 Cloves, 3 green cardamom, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 6 pepper corn
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with ginger garlic, salt, turmeric, red chili powder and lemon juice. Cover and rest in the refrigerator till you prepare the other ingredients. 2. If using poppy seeds - Sun dry or dry roast poppy seeds until crunchy. First powder them thoroughly to a fine powder and add very little water and coconut (optional). Grind to a smooth paste or thick milk like consistency.If using cashews - Soak them in little hot water for 10 minutes and grind them to a smooth puree with 3 to 4 tablespoons water. 3. Heat oil in a pan and add the whole spices. When they begin to sizzle, add green chilies, onions, curry leaves (optional) and ⅓ teaspoon salt. 4. Saute on a medium to low heat until deep golden but not burnt. This takes about 10 minutes, keep stirring to brown them evenly. 5. Add ginger garlic paste and sauté for a minute, till it becomes fragrant. Do not burn. (If using tomato, add now and fry till the mixture is roasted very well and oil begins to separate.) 6. Add chicken and saute on a high flame for 3 to 4 mins. Stir in the chopped mint leaves, followed by chili powder and garam masala. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. 7. Pour hot water just enough to immerse the chicken. Avoid adding lots of water as the chicken lets out some moisture as it cooks and the curry can become runny. 8. Bring to a boil, cover and cook on a low flame till the chicken is fully cooked and tender. 9. Add the poppy seeds or cashew puree/paste. Stir well and cook till you get the required consistency for about 3 to 5 minutes. Stir often to prevent the poppy seeds paste from sticking at the bottom. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala to taste. 10. Serve andhra chicken curry with garelu (Medu vada) pulao, rice, chapati or phulka.

title: Andhra chilli chicken
ingredients: 3 grams chicken, 1 tsp solid ghee, 2 onions, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala powder, turmeric powder, Salt, 0.5 cup coriander leaves, 2 green chilies, 1 tbsp oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 tsp cumin, 2.5 green chilies
instructions: 1. Soak chicken in butter milk overnight or at least for 2 hours. 2. Blend green chilies, coriander leaves in a jar. 3. Marinate chicken with marination ingredients. Cook until the meat is done fully. 4. Heat a pan with cumin, curry leaves and slit chilies. Add chicken and then the blended paste. Fry for 3 to 5 minutes. Serve andhra chilli chicken hot. 5. Chicken 65 Recipe Chicken manchuria... Chicken Fry Recip... Chicken kebab rec... Chilli Chicken Re... Chicken Curry Rec... 6. Wash and soak chicken in buttermilk overnight and refrigerate. Drain off the buttermilk fully, the next morning.This is done only to tenderize the chicken. If you have meat tenderizer you can use it too. You can skip this step totally and just marinate with the marination ingredients and set aside for 45 minutes to 1 hour 7. Make a fine paste of the coriander leaves and green chili, set aside till we use it for seasoning. Coarsely crush or pulse onion in a mixer. 8. Mix all the marination ingredients along with crushed onions and chicken. If your chicken is not soaked in buttermilk for atleast few hours, then i suggest you set aside after marination for 45 minutes to 1 hour 9. Cook chicken on a medium flame till tender and soft. If needed you can sprinkle little water. If the chicken oozes out more moisture, evaporate by cooking on high. But do not overcook at high temperature for long. It can make the chicken hard. 10. Heat a pan with oil, add cumin, when they begin to splutter add curry leaves and green chilies. Fry till the leaves turn crisp 11. Transfer the cooked chicken and add ground coriander chilli paste. 12. Mix well and fry for about 3 to 5 minutes on a medium flame, till you begin to get a nice aroma of the coriander leaves. 13. Serve with onion and lemon wedges

title: Andhra Fish Fry
ingredients: 1 tablespoon Oil, 25 grams fish fillet, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon Lemon juice, 0.75 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. I have used a single steak weighing 250 grams. You can increase the quantity of ingredients proportionately if you are using more fish. Do not use too much masala powder, red chili powder and ginger garlic paste for marination. It will taste bitter. For added spice, sprinkle more spice powders at step 5. 2. Make a paste of salt, red chili powder, garam masala, ginger, garlic, turmeric and lemon juice. Taste test and adjust as required. Marinate fish with this and keep aside for just 10 minutes. 3. Heat a heavy bottom pan with 1 tbsp. oil or ghee. If deep frying use oil. If pan frying, to get an Andhra taste, use ghee. To get a Kerala taste use coconut oil. But any oil will do as well. 4. You can shallow fry, deep fry or pan fry, use oil accordingly. I suggest using a small pan, so less oil can be used to fry. 5. When the oil becomes just hot enough, place the fish in the oil and fry on a medium flame. After 3 to 4 minutes turn it to the other side and add curry leaves. Fry until the fish is cooked through. If you are pan frying and want to make a spicy fish fry, sprinkle more red chili powder, black pepper or garam masala now (not for deep fry or shallow fry). 6. Tip: You know it is fully cooked, if the fish flakes easily at the prick of a fork. Use a food thermometer to check if you have one. The internal temperature should reach 145 F or 63 C in the thickest part of the fish. 7. Switch off the stove. Garnish Andhra fish fry with onion wedges and serve.

title: Anjeer Barfi
ingredients: 0.75 cup figs, 0.75 cup dates, 2 tablespoons raisins, 3.5 tablespoons water, 0.75 cup nuts, 0.125 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 pinch cinnamon, 1 teaspoon oil
instructions: 1. Dry roast chopped nuts till crunchy. keep this aside in a plate. If you want you can also toast the whole nuts until crunchy. Cool and then pulse in a grinder. 2. To the same pan, add chopped figs with 3 table spoons water to soften them. By the time i could take the picture, the water got evaporated. But make sure figs turn soft. 3. Tip: If your figs are hard, soak them in little boiling hot water. As soon as they turn soft, remove from water and chop them. 4. Add chopped dates, raisins, cinnamon or nutmeg powder and cardamom powder. cook on a low flame for few minutes. If your dates are hard, soak them in hot water and then chop. 5. After 3 to 4 minutes, it turns mushy and sticky. But this depends on the type of your dates too, but cook till they turn soft and mushy. I added 1 tsp. organic virgin coconut oil, this is optional. Since I used a stainless pan, it began to stick , so the oil helped me to cook it further without burning. If you are not using oil or ghee, use a non stick. 6. Anjeer and dates mixture should become quite hot and thick, not soggy, else the burfi turns soggy and turns sticky while you cut. 7. Mix well. the mixture should looks firm and fairly dry like you see in the picture below. After adding nuts if the mixture doesn’t look firm, then cook for another two minutes. Transfer it to a butter paper. Cover it with another parchment paper. Spread the mixture with a rolling pin immediately and cool it. Cut to pieces once it cools completely.

title: Apollo Fish Recipe
ingredients: 5 grams fish fillet, Oil, 2.5 tablespoons corn flour, 1.5 tablespoons all-purpose flour, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 egg white, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 tablespoon ginger, 1.5 tablespoon garlic, 2 green chilies, 2 onions, 1 tablespoon soya sauce
instructions: 1. Cut the fish to desired size pieces (about ¾ to 1 inch) or to strips and rinse and drain completely. 2. Pour the lemon juice and salt over the fish and set aside for 5 minutes. 3. Marinate with red chillii powder, turmeric, salt,, ginger garlic paste & egg white. Set aside for about 30 minutes. 4. Heat oil for deep frying on a medium heat. 5. Sprinkle corn flour and all-purpose flour. Mix well to coat them. 6. When the oil is hot enough, reduce the heat to medium, deep fry the pieces until crisp and golden. Remove to a cooling rack or a colander. 7. Heat 1 tbsp. oil in another pan. Fry curry leaves till crisp, add ginger garlic and green chilies and fry till you get an aroma. 8. Add chopped onions and fry till lightly brown. If you want you may add soya sauce and mix well. Add the fried fish and saute on high for 3 mins. 9. Apollo fish fry is ready.

title: appalu recipe | andhra rava appalu | sweet rava appam recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cup water, 1.5 tsp ghee, 1 cup rava semolina suji, 1 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 1 cup water and ½ tsp ghee. 2. get the water to a boil. 3. now add 1 cup rava stirring continuously to avoid any lump formation. 4. keep stirring until the water is absorbed completely. 5. cover, and simmer for 2 minutes making sure the rava is cooked well. 6. now add 1 cup sugar and mix well. 7. sugar starts to melt, mash the rava if there are any lumps. 8. cover and simmer for 2 minutes until the water is absorbed completely. 9. the mixture turns soft and fluffy. 10. transfer to a plate and cool slightly. 11. now add 1 tsp ghee and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. mix well. 12. knead for a minute making sure the dough is soft and non-sticky. 13. grease hands with oil and pinch a ball sized dough. 14. smoothen out and flatten slightly. 15. now deep fry in hot oil or ghee. frying in ghee will enhance the flavour. 16. do not touch for a minute, as there are chances for appalu to break. 17. flip over and fry on both sides on medium flame. 18. fry until the appalu turns golden brown and crisp. 19. press slightly, and remove oil. drain off over kitchen towel to remove excess oil. 20. finally, rava appalu is ready for prasadam and can be consumed for 3 days.

title: Appe Recipe (Instant Rava Appe)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoons cashews, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 cup rava, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 cup water, 0.5 cup carrot, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.5 cup yogurt, 1.5 teaspoon lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon baking soda
instructions: 1. Add the ghee or oil to a large pan and heat it. 2. When the oil is hot, turn the flame to low and add the mustard seeds along with cumin seeds. 3. Add the chopped cashews and fry till golden. 4. Add the curry leaves, chilies and ginger garlic paste for about a minute, without burning. 5. Add the rava/semolina and increase the flame to medium. Roast till crunchy, stirring often so it does not burn. Turn off. Add the salt and cool completely. 6. Pour water and mix well, let it rest for a minimum of 10 mins. 7. Meanwhile grate the carrot and chop the coriander leaves finely. 8. Add these to the mixture and add the yogurt with the lemon/lime juice. Mix well and taste test. Adjust salt to taste. 9. The consistency should be thick yet a little loose than the rava idli batter. (not runny). If it is too thick sprinkle 1 to 2 tablespoons water. 10. Heat the appe pan on a medium flame and pour ⅛ teaspoon oil to each cavity. If using cast iron pan, spread the oil all over the cavities. 11. While the oil heats, mix the baking soda or eno with 1 teaspoon water. Pour it to the appe batter and mix well. 12. Pour the batter to fill the cavities just until the rim. 13. Cover and cook until the base is firm, golden and slightly crisp. Using a skewer or a wooden appe spatula turn them to the other side. Press down gently into the cavities. 14. Cook well on this side as well. Test if they are cooked by poking with a skewer or a fork in the center of the appe. They should be clean without any wet batter. Remove them to a plate. 15. Before proceeding to fry the next batch, make sure the pan is not too hot. I usually turn off the stove for a while to bring down the temperature. If the pan is too hot, the base of the appe with brown too quickly without cooking them from inside. 16. Serve instant rava appe with Coconut chutney, Tomato Chutney or onion chutney. They taste good on their own or even with a cup of chai or coffee.

title: Apple Chutney Recipe
ingredients: 5 grams apples, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 gram cinnamon, 2 parts star anise, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon fennel, 1 teaspoon cumin, 3 tablespoons brown sugar, 3 tablespoons sugar, 0.25 teaspoon pepper, 0.5 cup apple cider vinegar
instructions: 1. Pour 1 tablespoon ghee or oil to a wide pan or pot. When the oil turns hot, add 2 inches piece of cinnamon (1 gram), 4 cloves and 2 segments of star anise. When they sizzle, add 1½ tablespoon ginger. Fry it for about a minute. Then add ¼ teaspoon red chili powder or flakes. 2. Give a quick stir. 3. Quickly add 1 lb. (450to 500 grams) chopped apples (3 medium) and pour the ½ cup apple cider vinegar. 4. Add 3 tablespoons brown and 3 tablespoons white sugar along with 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon fennel powder & ¼ teaspoon ground pepper. Mix all of them together. 5. Cover and let it simmer until the apples turn tender. Covering the pot at this stage helps to retain the flavors of spices. 6. Sugar dissolves and the entire mixture will have a lot of moisture. Open the lid when the apples are tender and you begin to smell the mixture good. Continue to cover in the open pot stirring until all of the excess moisture evaporates and the apple chutney turns thick. 7. At this stage, mash a small portion of the chutney with a spatula to give a texture to the chutney. Next turn off the stove and cool this completely. 8. This chutney keeps good for several months when stored in a clean, dry & air tight glass jar. Place the bottle in a dry cabinet. Avoid keeping it in the kitchen if it is very humid. If making in large quantity then store them in smaller jars. Use moist free spoons to serve it.

title: Apple Jam Recipe
ingredients: 45 grams apples, 0.75 cup sugar, 3 tablespoons apple vinegar, 3 tablespoons water, 1 tablespoon vanilla extract, 2.5 inch cinnamon, 0.25 teaspoon cinnamon, 1 star anise
instructions: 1. Rinse, peel, core and quarter the apples. Grate them in a food processor. If you don't have a food processor simply chop them as fine as possible. 2. Add the apples to a heavy bottom saucepan or pot. Add vinegar, water, cinnamon stick and star anise. 3. Cover and cook on a low heat until the apples are slightly softened. 4. Add sugar and continue to cook stirring often until thick. Add ground cinnamon and vanilla. 5. When it thickens test by swiping the bottom of the pot with a spatula. It should leave a trail behind the spatula. (Check step by step photos in the post) 6. Do a final test by dropping a spoonful of apple jam on a cold plate. It should not spread and must retain its shape. The other way to check is to cool it slightly and run your finger in the center of the jam. It should leave a space showing the line. 7. Cool apple jam completely and store in a clean air tight dry glass jar. It lasts for few months in the refrigerator. Spread it on toasts, parathas or fresh bread.

title: Apple Juice Recipe
ingredients: 2 apples, 2.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 6 ice cubes, 0.5 cup water, 0.375 inch ginger, 1.5 teaspoons sweetener, 4 apples, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 lemon, 1 lb apples, 1.25 cup water, 2 inch cinnamon piece
instructions: 1. Wash apples under running water. To make 2 servings, you will need 2 apples for blender juice or 4 to 6 apples for cold pressed juice. If you prefer to use the skin, then make sure they are at room temperature and spray vinegar. Set these aside for 5 mins. Rub them gently with sea salt and rinse off under running water. 2. It is up to you if you want you may simply wash them well and peel off the skin. Apple skin has a natural substance known as pectin which is highly beneficial for health, so many people prefer to use the skin for juicing. Keep your juicer or blender jar ready before you begin to peel and cut them as the cut apples turn brown quickly. 3. There may be many ways of cutting apples, but I do it as shown in the image below. You can also use a apple slicer or a corer. The whole apple can be directly added to many slow juicer chutes, but we prefer to discard the seeds and the core since there may be infestations inside the core. 4. Cut the apples to a size that fits your juicer chute. If making in a blender cut to small pieces. To make in a slow juicer, cut each apple to 4 pieces & discard the core. 5. For this version I basically use 1¼ cup water to 1 pound (450 grams apples). If using organic apples, I don’t peel them but wash thoroughly and then seed. 6. Deseed and chop or slice 1 pound (450 grams) apples. Add them to a large bowl along with cinnamon and 1¼ cups water. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, Cover and simmer until the apples are soft and mushy. If you do not cover you will end up with only pulp and no juice. 7. Place a colander/strainer over a bowl and line with a muslin cloth. Cool down and pour the apples along with the liquid to the lined strainer. 8. Transfer the juice to serving glasses or a pitcher. If you want you may stir in some honey to taste. Use the pulp just like your apple sauce. It also goes well in your dairy free shakes, smoothies, soups and porridge.

title: apple kheer recipe | apple ki kheer | seb ki kheer | apple payasam
ingredients: 1 apple, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 3 cup milk, 0.25 tsp saffron kesar, 0.25 cup condensed milk milkmaid, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp dry fruits
instructions: 1. firstly, peel the skin of the apple and grate it. 2. transfer the grated apple to a pan. make sure not to rest long as it oxidised and turns brown. 3. saute with 1 tsp ghee on medium flame. 4. cook until the water is all evaporated and apple is cooked well. cool completely. 5. in a large kadai heat 3 cup milk and add ¼ tsp saffron. get to a boil. 6. add ¼ cup condensed milk and mix well. 7. boil for 10 minutes or until the milk thickens. 8. add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. allow the milk to cool completely. 9. now add cooked apple and mix well. make sure both apple and milk is to room temperature, else there are chances of milk to curdle. 10. cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes or until its chilled. 11. finally, serve apple kheer garnished with few chopped nuts.

title: Apple Milkshake (Apple Shake)
ingredients: 1 apple, 1 cup milk, 1.5 teaspoon sugar, 1 cashews, 2 pinches cinnamon powder, 2 ice cubes, 0.25 teaspoon vanilla extract
instructions: 1. To make one serving of apple shake, you will need 1 apple, 1 cup milk (dairy or vegan) & 1 tsp to 1 tbsp sugar or maple syrup or use 2 soft dates. Optionally if you want to flavor you may use ⅛ to ¼ teaspoon ground cinnamon (powder) and a few drops of vanilla extract. To thicken and add protein, use up to 10 cashews. 2. I use Homemade Almond Milk . For this add 3 tablespoons almond flour to a bowl and pour 4 tablespoons hot water. Let it sit for 20 mins. If using almonds, soak them (12) for 5 to 6 hours and peel them. Later grind in a small jar with 4 tablespoons water to a super smooth milk. Add ½ cup water and grind again. You will get a little over 1 cup almond milk. 3. Wash your apples very well under running water and peel them. Slice & then chop them as needed. 4. Don’t let your apples sit for longer, they will begin to oxidize and turn brown. Add them to a blender. 5. Add all the other ingredients you are using – milk & sweetener. Optional – If using add cinnamon, vanilla and cashews too. 6. Pour the milk. If you want cold shake, add 1 to 2 ice cubes or milk cubes. 7. Blend until smooth and frothy. 8. Pour apple milkshake to a serving glass. If you want drizzle some cinnamon or brown sugar for garnishing. Serve apple milkshake immediately.

title: Apple Orange Smoothie Recipe
ingredients: 2 apples sweet tasting, 2.5 oranges, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 11 almonds
instructions: 1. Wash apples under running water. Spray vinegar and leave them aside for 2 to 3 mins. 2. Rub them well with sea salt and wash under running water. 3. Peel the skin if apples are not organic. Keep the skin on for organic apples. Wash and peal the oranges. 4. Remove the pith of the oranges and halve the oranges, lengthwise . Then make a deep cut in the halved oranges in the center, so you cut all of the orange segments. Remove the seeds. 5. Optional - If using nuts, add them to the blender jar with ½ to ¾ cup water or any liquid. Blend until smooth. 6. Add the oranges and the chopped apples. Add the optional ingredients. Blend until smooth. If it is too thick for your liking simply pour more liquid like water or juice & blend. 7. Method 2 - Juice the oranges in a orange juicer or a blender. Strain the juice and then add the rest of the ingredients to the blender. We need one cup of orange juice. 8. Blend well to make a smoothie. Adjust the consistency by adding more orange juice or water. 9. Fix your smoothie attachments to the juicer, run your machine and add all the ingredients one after the other into the juicer chute. Add more oranges if you like a thin smoothie. 10. Serve apple orange smoothie immediately.

title: Apple Tea Recipe (Apple Cinnamon Tea)
ingredients: 3 cups water, 1 apple, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 tablespoon Lemon juice, 4 black pepper corn, 0.5 teaspoon tea powder, 1 tablespoon Honey
instructions: 1. Pour water to a pot and add tea powder, ginger, cinnamon and black pepper. Bring this to a boil on a medium heat. 2. Reduce the heat and simmer this for 5 to 7 minutes until you begin to smell the cinnamon. 3. While the tea simmers, rinse and peel the apple. Chop to small bite sized pieces or grate it. 4. Add the apple to the boiling tea and simmer for 2 to 3 minutes. 5. Turn off the heat and cover the pot. Do not disturb for 5 mins. 6. After 5 mins, strain the apple tea and add sugar if using. 7. If adding lemon juice or honey, let the apple tea cool down to warm temperature. Then stir honey and lemon juice as required. 8. Serve apple cinnamon tea warm or hot. You can also eat up the warm mushy apple.

title: ashoka halwa recipe | asoka halwa recipe | pasi paruppu halwa
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 cup moong dal, 2 cup water, 0.25 cup ghee clarified butter, 2 tbsp wheat flour atta, 0.75 cup sugar, pinch saffron food colour, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 2 tbsp raisins kishmish
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker heat 1 tsp ghee and roast ½ cup moong dal. 2. add 2 cup water and mix well. 3. cover and pressure cook for 3 whistles or until dal is cooked well. 4. cool the dal completely and blend to smooth paste. keep aside. 5. in a large kadai heat ¼ cup ghee and roast 2 tbsp wheat flour. 6. roast until the flour turns aromatic and lump-free. 7. further, add in prepared moong dal paste. 8. stir and cook making sure the dal paste is well combined. 9. cook for 5 minutes or until the mixture starts to thicken slightly. 10. add in ¾ cup sugar and mix well. 11. dissolve the sugar keeping the flame on medium. 12. keep cooking for 15 minutes or until the mixture turns glossy. 13. now add a pinch saffron food colour and mix well. 14. keep cooking on low flame until the mixture separates the pan. takes approx 25 minutes. 15. further in another pan heat 1 tbsp ghee and roast 2 tbsp cashew and 2 tbsp raisins until the nuts turn golden brown. 16. add in roasted nuts over the halwa and add ¼ tsp cardamom powder. mix well. 17. finally, ashoka halwa is ready to serve.

title: Atta Ladoo Recipe
ingredients: 1 cups atta, 0.5 cup 2 tbsps sugar, 0.25 cup ghee, 0.3333333333333333 cup almonds cashews, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Heat ghee in a deep pan and fry the nuts until golden. You can chop before frying or fry the whole nuts & pulse them in mixer. 2. Remove them to a plate and set aside. 3. Add atta and begin to roast on a medium heat stirring continuously. 4. After frying it for sometime the color of the atta begins to deepen and it turns aromatic. 5. Reduce the flame to lowest and keep frying until it turns to a deep golden color with an unique aroma in your kitchen. 6. Set this aside to cool completely. 7. Meanwhile grate jaggery or powder the sugar. 8. When the atta cools down completely then add cardamom powder and powdered sugar. 9. You can also blend the nuts and add them here. 10. Now mix every thing well until uniform. 11. The atta ladoo mixture at this stage will be quite greasy and can be easily rolled to balls. 12. If not just knead the mixture well with your hands to release the oils. 13. You can also heat 1 to 2 tbsps more ghee and add it. OR Just blend the mixture in a blender. 14. Take small portions of this mixture to your palm and press down gently to give the shape of ladoo. 15. You can make about 9 to 10 atta ladoos. 16. Store them in a air tight jar. They keep good for several weeks at room temperature if handled with dry hands.

title: Atta Ladoo Recipe | Atta Besan Ladoo - Wheat Laddu
ingredients: 0.5 cup ghee, 2 tbsp gond edible gum, 1 cup wheat flour, 1 cup besan, 2 tbsp rava suji, 2 tbsp melon seeds, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 1 cup sugar, silver vark
instructions: 1. Firstly, heat ½ cup ghee and fry 2 tbsp gond on low flame. 2. Fry till the gond puffs up and turns crystal. 3. Drain off the gond and keep aside. Make sure to fry the gond on low flame, or else the gond will not get cooked from inside and will remain sticky while eating. 4. Add 1 cup wheat flour, 1 cup besan, and 2 tbsp rava in the same ghee. 5. Keep roasting on low flame till the flour is well combined. 6. Now add 1 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp melon seeds and roast well. 7. Roast until the flour turns golden brown and aromatic. 8. Also, thee has been released from the flour, indicating the flour has roasted well. 9. Transfer to the large plate and cool slightly. 10. Add fried gond and crush slightly. 11. Also, add ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. Mix making sure all the ingredients are well combined. 12. In a mixer jar take 1 cup sugar and grind to a fine powder. 13. Once the besan atta mixture has cooled add in powdered sugar and mix well. Make sure to add the sugar powder when the mixture is warm, or the sugar will melt. 14. Mix well till the mixture is well combined. 15. Prepare the ladoo by shaping them gently. 16. Finally, decorate the Atta Besan Ladoo Recipe with vark and chopped cashews.

title: Authentic Chana Masala (Chickpea Masala)
ingredients: 1 cup chana, 1.5 cups water, 2 tablespoon oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional)), 2 green cardamoms, 1.5 cups onions, 1 green chili, 0.875 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 cups tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 2 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 teaspoon amchur, 2 tbsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse dried chickpeas at least thrice. Soak them in 3 ½ to 4 cups of water overnight or for at least 8 hours. 2. Drain the water and rinse them well. Pour water and pressure cook for 5 to 6 whistles on a stovetop cooker. You may also cook in an instant pot for 18 minutes on a high pressure. Let the pressure drop naturally. 3. Make sure your chickpeas are soft and tender. Squeeze a chickpea to test, it should get mashed fully. If not cook them for a little longer. 4. Heat a large pot and pour oil. When the oil turns slightly hot, add the whole spices – cinnamon, cloves, cardamoms and bay leaf. 5. When they begin to sizzle add the onions and green chili. Saute until they turn light golden. 6. Add ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute without burning. 7. Add tomatoes and salt. Let cook until mushy, pulpy and thick. 8. Stir in red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder and cumin powder. Saute until the masala begins to smell good, for about 3 to 4 mins. 9. Optional – If you want a smooth curry, cool this, discard the bay leaf, green chili and cinnamon. Blend in a blender to a smooth or coarse paste to suit your liking. I prefer to blend in 2 tbsps cooked chana along with the onion tomato masala. Add that puree back to the pan. 10. Add the chana along with the chana stock or water (1¼ cups). Pour another ¾ to 1 cup water. If you don't puree the onion tomato masala, you will require lesser water. If using canned chickpeas, drain and rinse them before adding with 1¾ cups water. 11. Mix well. Taste test and add more salt. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes. 12. When the consistency is thick (check pics), add amchur powder (optional) and kasuri methi. 13. Garnish Chana masala with coriander leaves. Sprinkle some lemon juice if required. Serve with Basmati Rice, Jeera Rice, Naan or Chapati. 14. To make the recipe in Instant pot, make the onion tomato masala on saute mode. Optionally If you want you may cool and blend this. Add the soaked chickpeas with 2 cups water. Deglaze and Pressure cook on high for 35 minutes. When the pressure drops, open the lid. Cook on a saute mode for a few minutes until thick. Add kasuri methi and amchur.

title: Authentic Garam Masala Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup coriander seeds, 0.25 cup cumin seeds, 2 tablespoons green cardamoms, 2 tablespoons cloves, 1 tablespoon black pepper corn, 8 grams cinnamon, 4 medium dried bay leaves, 3 star anise, 1 tablespoon fennel seeds, 0.5 nutmeg, 3.5 black cardamoms, 3 strands mace
instructions: 1. Before you make the garam masala, it is essential to clean and toast the spices. You may do the toasting in your oven or on the stovetop in a pan. I have the instructions below for both. Break open the nutmeg and check for worms. Sometimes, nutmeg turns hallow inside after being eaten up by the worms 2. For hygiene purpose cinnamon has to be cleaned. It could have a kind of larvae, fungi or infestations in the inner parts. While growing they form into curls (swirls) with several layers. Break open the quills and wipe off with a dry or damp cloth/ kitchen paper. 3. To a heavy bottom pan, add the following spices. Roast them on a low heat, stirring constantly, till crispy, crunchy and aromatic. Transfer to a large tray/ plate. 4. medium dried bay leaves (1 gram, tej patta, torn) 2 tablespoons green cardamoms (17 grams) (elaichi) 2 tablespoons cloves (12 grams) (laung) 1 tablespoon pepper corns (9 to 10 grams) kali mirch 8 grams cinnamon approx. 3 inch – 8 pieces (dalchini)(prefer ceylon cinnamon, cut down to half if using cassia) ½ small nutmeg (2 grams) ( jai phal) 3 star anise (4 grams, chakri phool) 3 to 4 black cardamoms (3 to 4 grams) badi elaichi 3 strands mace (javitri) 5. Add ¼ cup (30 grams) cumin seeds and roast on a low flame, stirring constantly until slightly darker but not burnt. You will smell them aromatic & they turn crunchy when they are ready. Transfer to the same large tray/ plate for cooling. 6. Add ½ cup (35 grams) coriander seeds and stir until slightly dark/ browned but not burnt. They are done when crunchy and aromatic. Transfer to the same tray for cooling. 7. Add 1 tablespoon (8 grams) fennel seeds to the pan and roast just for 2 mins until aromatic. We don’t deep roast fennel seeds. Transfer these as well to the same tray and cool down. 8. In Oven: Spread the spices on a clean baking tray and place it in the oven. Place the tray in the middle rack and turn on the oven with the temperature set to lowest. Roast until the spices become aromatic. In my oven it is 40 C (100 to 105 F). 9. Your spices are ready to grind when they cool down.

title: Avakaya Pachadi (Avakai Pickle)
ingredients: 5 cups cut mango pieces, 2 cups oil, 1 cup mustard powder, 1 cup red chili powder, 0.75 cup crystal salt, 1.5 teaspoon Methi powder, 0.5 cup garlic
instructions: 1. Wash mangoes thoroughly and soak in a large wide pail or bucket for 1 hour. This is done to remove any dirt deposited on them. 2. Remove them from water and allow to dry. 3. Wipe them with a cloth and spread them on a large cotton cloth. They must be completely moisture free and dry. 4. The place where you intend to make the pickle has to be absolutely clean, dry and free from moisture. 5. Move the mangoes to this work area and all the preparation has to be done here to prevent spoilage. Get all the other things we prepared the previous day to this place . (Read the preparation part from the blog post) 6. Remove any portions of stems still on the mangoes. Cut each mango to half, with the shell and kernel intact. Mango can also be wrapped in a cotton cloth and then halved to prevent slipping or injury. If the shell or the covering of the kernel gets separated from the pieces, they are not used for pickle since they turn soggy and the taste of the entire pickle changes. 7. With the help of a spoon, remove the kernel and discard. You can also see a paper thin layer in between the kernel and the inner portion of the shell. Remove that as well. Use a spoon to remove that completely. Wipe off with a clean dry cloth. 8. Cut each halved mango to 2 (quarters) and then further to 3 pieces each. This way a medium sized mango gets about 12 pieces. Make sure each piece also has a shell part on it. 9. Measure & place these cut pieces on a cloth or utensil. 10. Peel the garlic cloves. Make sure they don't have any green shoots at the base. Set them aside. We also crush lightly 10 garlic cloves. This is optional. If the garlic cloves turn green, do not use them. 11. Powder the mustard seeds. Measure 1 cup and add it to a large dry utensil. Set aside the left over. 12. Powder the methi seeds if using. Add this to the same utensil along with mustard powder. 13. Powder the crystal salt. Measure ¾ cup plus 2 tbsp powdered salt and add it to the utensil. Also measure and add red chili powder, garlic. Mix all these. 14. Pour 1½ cups oil to another dry utensil. Dunk the mango pieces in the oil, a handful at one time. Take them off from the oil with a deep frying spatula or slotted spoon. Add them to the spice mix. Coat them well with spice powder and add it to the bharani/ glass jar. 15. Repeat this process of dunking a handful of them in oil and then in the spice mix. Then to the bharani/ jaadi. Once you are done, there will no spice mix left. You may be short of oil for the last few batches. Then use up the rest of the half cup. 16. When you are done with this, if you are left with oil or any spice powder, just add the spice powder and then pour the oil to the jar. 17. Cover with a lid. Place a cotton cloth and then tie up with a cloth ribbon or a thin long piece of cloth, across the neck of the jar. 18. Keep this in a dry cabinet/shelf for 3 full days. We wait for 72 hours. 19. On the fourth day, open the lid and then mix up using a dry ladle. We use strong wooden ladles when made in large quantity. 20. Taste the pickle and add little more salt if needed. Wait till the next day to check if there is a layer of oil floating on the pickle, if not add the rest of the oil. 21. Do not skimp on the salt and oil, they act as natural preservatives to preserve pickle for a year. If there is not enough oil or salt, pickle may get spoilt. 22. Do not serve pickle directly from the jar especially if you have made in large quantity. Opening the jars frequently will diminish the quality, color and flavour of pickle. Always take a portions of this to another glass bottle for everyday use. 23. Always use dry hands, ladles and spoons while handling the pickles. We usually heat them up a bit near the gas flame.

title: Aval Laddu (Poha Ladoo)
ingredients: 1 cup poha, 0.75 cup jaggery, 2 green cardamoms, 0.5 cup desiccated coconut, 0.25 cup ghee, 9 cashews, 9 raisins
