instructions: 1. Dry roast aval/ poha till crunchy. Set this aside. Add coconut and fry till you begin to smell good. Cool these completely. 2. Transfer this to a blender along with cardamoms. Make a fine powder, add jaggery and blend well again. If using sugar then powder the sugar first and then powder the poha coconut. Transfer this to the plate. 3. Heat a pan with ghee, add cashews, fry till they turn golden, and add raisins. Switch off the stove. 4. Pour this to the poha mixture. Mix everything well and take portions of this to your hands. Press down with your fingers to form balls. 5. If the mixture is dry and does not bind,, heat more ghee and add. If using milk, replace ghee with milk for binding. Dry roast nuts and use. Use milk only if using sugar and not jaggery. 6. Store aval laddu in air tight jar.

title: Aval Payasam Recipe for Krishna Jayanthi
ingredients: 2 tablespoons ghee, 8 cashew nuts, 9 sweet raisins, 0.5 cup poha, 2 cups milk, 0.5 cup jaggery, 3.5 tablespoons Water, 3.5 green cardamoms, 2 tablespoons aval, 8 cashews
instructions: 1. Add jaggery to a small pot along with 3 to 4 tbsp water. Melt the jaggery on a low flame. 2. When the jaggery melts and begins to bubble up set this aside to cool completely. The syrup must turn sticky and don't need a string consistency. 3. Powder cashews and poha in a blender to a fine powder. Omit this step if using rice flour. 4. Heat ghee in a pan and fry the cashews until lightly golden. Add raisins and fry until they turn plump. Set these aside. 5. Add poha to the pan and keep frying on a low flame until they begin to smell good. Stir in the powered poha and saute for a minute or 2. 6. Pour milk and begin to cook on a medium heat until they turn soft. Stir often to prevent the milk scorching at the bottom. 7. In the meantime, payasam thickens as well and the jaggery syrup has to cool down completely. 8. Add cardamom powder. If using rice flour, stir it in ¼ cup milk. Make sure there are no lumps. Add it to the pot. Stir and cook on a medium heat until the payasam turns thick. 9. Filter the cooled jaggery syrup to the payasam. Turn off the stove and mix everything well. Do not cook further, take it off from the stove. 10. Add cashews and raisins. If desired some ghee as well. Serve aval payasam warm or cold.

title: Aval Upma Recipe (Poha Upma)
ingredients: 2 cups aval, 1 medium onion, 2 green chilies, 1 medium tomato, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3 tablespoons peanuts, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon turmeric, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 tablespoons raw coconut
instructions: 1. Add 2 cups poha to a bowl and rinse them well twice. Drain the water completely, cover and set aside to soften for 10 mins. Meanwhile prepare the following: 2. 1 medium onion (slice or chop) 3. 1 medium tomato (deseed and chop or omit and use lemon juice while serving) 4. 2 green chilies (chop or slit) 5. After 10 mins, check the aval if they are soft. Press down a few flakes with your fingers. They should get mashed easily. Else sprinkle some water, mix and rest until the tempering is ready. 6. Pour 2 tablespoon oil to a hot pan. Add 3 tablespoons peanuts or 12 to 14 cashews and fry them until golden. Remove to a plate and reserve to garnish later. I did not reserve them in the pictures but I recommend doing that. Add 7. ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. When they splutter, add 1 pinch of hing. 8. Add green chilis, ginger, onions and curry leaves. 9. When the onions turn transparent, add chopped tomatoes, half teaspoon salt and ¼ teaspoon turmeric. You can also skip tomatoes and use lemon juice towards the end of making it. Fry until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. If the pan is too dry, add a splash of water and cook down. 10. Add soaked poha and mix well. If your poha is too dry, sprinkle little water and mix well (do not pour). If using frozen cold coconut add 2 tablespoons at this stage. 11. Cover with a lid and steam cook on the lowest heat till aval upma turns hot. Turn off the stove. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Add lemon juice and garnish with 2 tablespoons fresh coconut (optional), fried peanuts and chopped coriander leaves. Serve aval upma hot with curd or tea/coffee.

title: Avocado Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 1 avocado, 1 tablespoon Lemon juice, 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil, 1 tablespoon cilantro, 1 garlic clove, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.5 teaspoons pumpkin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 4 slices bread, 1.5 tablespoon butter, 0.25 cup sweet corn kernels, 2 tablespoons feta, 1 tomato, cucumber slices, 1 carrot, pickled onion jalapenos, salt and pepper
instructions: 1. Butter your bread on both the sides and toast in a pan or in the airfryer/oven. 2. Meanwhile, prepare the other ingredients to assemble the sandwich. You will need these: 3. ¼ cup sweet corn kernels (roasted or boiled, sub with chickpeas or any beans mashed slightly) 4. For the avocado mash, you will need the following: 5. 1 garlic clove fine chopped or ⅓ teaspoon garlic powder 6. Cut the avocado and discard the stone. Scoop out the flesh with a spoon. 7. Using a fork, mash it. 8. Add the rest of the ingredients and mix well. Taste test and adjust salt and lemon. 9. Spread 1 tablespoon of avocado mash on each of the 2 bread slices. Do not use a lot of spread. Keep them aside. Divide the rest of the avocado mash to 2 other bread slices. Spread the corn or slightly mashed chickpeas and press down into the mash. Layer with feta if using. 10. Place the tomato slices, cucumbers and carrots. 11. Place the pickled onions and jalapenos. Sprinkle little salt and pepper. If you want to use arugula or any micro greens, place them. 12. Cover the avocado sandwiches with the bread slices we prepared first. Press down gently and serve immediately.

title: Awadhi chicken biryani
ingredients: 42 grams basmati rice, 1 onion, 0.25 cup ghee, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 bay leaf, 4 green cardamoms, 1.5 cinnamon sticks, 0.5 shahi jeera, 0.5 tsp kewra water, 2 drops meeta attar, 3.5 green chilies, 1 Pinch saffron, 4 tbsp cream, 1.5 cup mint, 1 kg chicken, 1.5 gram cinnamon, 6 green cardamoms, 0.5 tsp cloves, 2 bay leaves, 0.75 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 15 grams curd, 1.5 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp Yellow chili powder, 1 pinch mace powder, 1 tsp rose water, 0.5 tsp kewra water, 0.5 tsp shahi jeera, 1.5 bay leaf, 3 green cardamoms, Cinnamon stick, 1 tsp oil, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp salt
instructions: 1. Please follow the instructions from the step by step photo instructions.

title: Baby Corn Fry Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 cup onions, 1 teaspoon ginger, 2 grams baby corn, 0.5 cup bell peppers, Salt, 1 pinch turmeric, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper corn, 0.25 teaspoon fennel seeds
instructions: 1. Heat a pan with oil and add cumin seed. When they begin to splutter, add grated ginger or ginger garlic paste. Saute for a minute until aromatic. Do not burn. 2. Add onions and saute until transparent. 3. Add baby corn, bell peppers, turmeric and salt. Stir fry on a high heat for 3 mins. I use red and green bell peppers. You may use what you like. If you don’t have bell peppers, you may use carrots. 4. Cover and cook on a low flame for another 4 to 5 mins, until slightly tender yet crunchy but not too soft. Keep stirring in between to prevent burning. The cook time changes with the freshness of baby corn. Mine are very fresh and just take less than 4 to 5 mins. If yours’ isn’t fresh, splash a tbsp of water and cook. 5. While the baby corn cooks, crush pepper corn and fennel seeds/ saunf to a coarse powder or use garam masala. When the baby corn is tender, add the ground spices and curry leaves. On occasions I also use a handful of methi or spinach that I need to use up. 6. Stir fry for 2 to 3 minutes till the leaves wilt and ground spices begin to smell aromatic. Taste test and add more salt if needed. A splash of lemon juice is also nice on this. Serve stir fried baby corn with rice, roti or any meal. 7. Baby Corn Manchur... Mushroom Pepper F... Crispy Corn Recip... Vegetable Jalfrez... Cauliflower Stir ... Cabbage Stir Fry ...

title: Baby Corn Manchurian Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams baby corn, 2 tbsp corn flour, 2 tbsp all-purpose flour, salt, 0.25 tsp black pepper, 2.5 tbsp oil, 0.5 tbsp ginger, 1 tbsp garlic, 2.5 tbsp onions, 1 green chili, 2.5 tbsp spring onions, 3 tbsp bell pepper, 1 tbsp soya sauce, 0.375 tbsp vinegar, 1.5 tbsp tomato sauce, 1.5 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp spring onion greens
instructions: 1. Wash baby corn and cut them diagonally. If you like to make a restaurant style baby corn manchurian, make a batter first. To make the batter, add corn flour, plain flour, salt and black pepper to a bowl. Add water and make a thick batter. Dip the baby corn in the batter and fry in oil. 2. I like to minimize the corn and plain flour so omitted plain flour and used 2 tbsp corn flour. I added the baby corn to a bowl and then corn flour, salt and pepper powder. 3. Tossed everything well, sprinkled little water and tossed again. 4. Deep fry or shallow fry. You can also grill these. I shallow fried in 2 batches. 5. Fry them until golden, remove to a paper towel or a plate. 6. Transfer the oil to a bowl and keep 1 to 2 tbsps in the pan. Put on the flame to high this lends a smoky flavor. Fry ginger garlic until they smell good. Add onions and spring onion whites. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes. 7. Add capsicums, saute for 2 to 3 minutes. 8. Add 1 tbsp. soya sauce. 9. Add ¼ to ½ tbsp. vinegar. 10. Add 1½ tbsp tomato sauce and ¾ to 1½ tbsp. chilli sauce (optional). 11. Add ½ tsp sugar and 2 to 3 tbsp. water to make a sauce. Taste test and adjust the sauce to your liking. Add more tomato or chilli sauce if needed. If you like to make a gravy, stir in 1 tsp corn starch (corn flour) in 3 tbsps water. For more gravy, add more sauces and more corn flour. Cook until the sauce thickens. 12. Add baby corn, spring onions greens and saute for 2 to 3 minutes. Serve baby corn manchurian as a starter or snack. You can serve with fried rice or noodles. Garnish with spring onions. 13. Baby Corn Fry Rec... Paneer Manchurian Mushroom Manchuri... Veg Manchurian Re... Gobi Manchurian R... Soya Chunks Manch...

title: Baby Corn Manchurian Recipe | Baby Corn Manchuria Dry Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 baby corn, water, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 4 cloves garlic, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp sugar, 3 tbsp corn flour slurry
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take ½ cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp salt and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 2. Add water as required and prepare a smooth thick batter. 3. Now add 10 baby corn and coat with batter. 4. Deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 5. Stir occasionally, until the baby corn turns golden and crispy. 6. Drain off the baby corn and keep it aside. 7. In a large wok heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 4 cloves garlic, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion and stir fry on high flame. 8. Add ½ onion, ½ capsicum and stir fry on high flame. 9. Also add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, ¼ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp pepper powder, ¼ tsp salt and ¼ tsp sugar. 10. Stir fry until all the sauces are combined well. 11. Now add 3 tbsp corn flour slurry and mix until the sauce thickens. To prepare the slurry, mix 1 tsp corn flour in 3 tbsp water until there are no lumps. 12. Once the sauce turns glossy, add in fried baby corn, 2 tbsp spring onion and toss. 13. Mix until the sauce is coated well. 14. Finally, enjoy Crispy Baby Corn Manchurian with tomato sauce.

title: Baby Corn Manchurian Recipe | Baby Corn Manchuria Dry Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 baby corn, water, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 4 cloves garlic, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp sugar, 3 tbsp corn flour slurry
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take ½ cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp salt and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 2. Add water as required and prepare a smooth thick batter. 3. Now add 10 baby corn and coat with batter. 4. Deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 5. Stir occasionally, until the baby corn turns golden and crispy. 6. Drain off the baby corn and keep it aside. 7. In a large wok heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 4 cloves garlic, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion and stir fry on high flame. 8. Add ½ onion, ½ capsicum and stir fry on high flame. 9. Also add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, ¼ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp pepper powder, ¼ tsp salt and ¼ tsp sugar. 10. Stir fry until all the sauces are combined well. 11. Now add 3 tbsp corn flour slurry and mix until the sauce thickens. To prepare the slurry, mix 1 tsp corn flour in 3 tbsp water until there are no lumps. 12. Once the sauce turns glossy, add in fried baby corn, 2 tbsp spring onion and toss. 13. Mix until the sauce is coated well. 14. Finally, enjoy Crispy Baby Corn Manchurian with tomato sauce.

title: Baby Dal Khichdi (Lentils for Baby)
ingredients: 0.25 cup moong dal, 1.25 cup water, 0.25 tablespoon moong dal, 1 tablespoon uncooked rice, 0.75 cup water, 2 inch carrot, 1 drop ghee, 1 tablespoon moong dal, 2 tablespoon uncooked rice, 3 inch carrot, 1 cup water, 2 drops ghee, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon moong dal, 2.25 tablespoon uncooked rice, 1.25 to 1 cup water, 3 tablespoon carrots, 0.125 teaspoon ghee, 1 pinch turmeric, 1 pinch black pepper, 0.125 teaspoon ajwain powder
instructions: 1. Soak half cup lentils for at least 1 hour up to overnight, as this helps in cooking them faster. You are free to skip this but your baby will be able to digest the soaked and cooked lentils easily. 2. Rinse the lentils well after soaking and pour 2 cups of fresh water. Bring it to a rolling boil and simmer. Cook uncovered until tender. You may see a lot of froth, remove that with a slotted spoon and discard. 3. When the lentils turn tender, Strain the stock and cool it. This gives you stage 0 dahl for your baby. 4. To make the baby food for other stages, you simply add the other ingredients as mentioned in the recipe card and cook. 5. Wash rice and dal together at least twice in lot of water. Optionally soak them for at least 30 to 60 minutes with 1 cup water in a small pressure cooker or pot. I use a 3lts pressure cooker. 6. Just before you cook, chop or grate veggies freshly and add to the soaked rice and dal. 7. On a medium heat pressure cook for 2 whistles. If you do not have a cooker, bring the water to a rolling boil and turn down the heat to low. Simmer until the lentils and rice are tender. If you find any froth, discard that with a slotted spoon. 8. When the pressure reduces , open the lid and check. The rice, lentils and veggies must be soft and mushy. While the rice is still hot, mash it to smooth with a spoon or a masher. 9. Add ghee, a pinch of crushed pepper and turmeric if you want. Babies need healthy fat, we use ghee. You can add salt after 1 year. 10. Mix everything well. If the consistency is too thick, pour some boiling hot water. Avoid adding cold water. Serve lentil rice warm. Warm foods digest better. 11. Soak rice and dal for at least 30 minutes. Heat ghee and saute cumin. Add grated ginger and saute. It will begin to smell good. 12. Add veggies and saute again for 2 minutes. 13. Add rice and dal. Repeat sautéing for 2 mins. 14. Add salt and water as needed. 15. Close the pressure cooker and allow to whistle once or twice depending on the brand.

title: Badam Burfi | Badam Katli | Almond Burfi
ingredients: 1 cup blanched fine almond flour, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.375 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.25 cup Water, 2 teaspoons ghee, 1 tablespoons pistachios
instructions: 1. Grease 2 parchment papers lightly with ghee or any neutral oil. Keep them aside. You can make this in a heavy bottom steel pan or non-stick. If using a non-stick pan, add half to one teaspoon ghee first. This is just to protect your non-stick pan. 2. Pour ¼ cup (60 ml) water and add half cup organic sugar. Give a gentle mix. 3. Dissolve it completely on a medium heat. Mix well to check if all the sugar is dissolved. 4. Turn the heat to low and add 1 cup blanched fine almond flour (100 grams). 5. Mix well to incorporate the flour into the sugar syrup, making sure to break up any lumps. Increase the heat to medium and cook stirring constantly until the mixture turns thick, for about 7 minutes. 6. Add 1 teaspoon ghee across the pan and cook for a little longer, for 2 minutes until the almond mixture turns thicker and comes together like a mass (check next picture). 7. It also leaves the sides of the pan. The whole cooking process takes you around 9 to 10 mins (for 1x & 2x). Note that if you are using a kadai and not a wide pan, the cook time can change. Turn off the heat & stir in the ¼ to ½ cardamom powder. My cardamom powder is with the shells so I use half tsp. 8. It should look smooth and moist at this stage. 9. Test if the mixture is ready to set: With a small spoon, take a small portion of the mixture from the center of the pan (not from the edges) and cool down. Grease your fingers and roll to a ball. It should hold shape and be dry but not sticky. If it is sticky cook for a little longer, another 1 to 2 mins. 10. Scrape off all the mixture from the spatula using a spoon. Transfer the hot mixture to a greased parchment paper. 11. Hold the edges of the parchment paper to cover the hot mixture and knead it several times for 2 minutes, until the mixture becomes really smooth. (See the pictures and video). If you feel the mixture is turning dry at the edges, you need to be faster. 12. You will have a smooth badam burfi mixture. Shape it to a square or rectangle using the parchment paper. 13. Cover with another parchment paper and roll with a rolling pin to a thin sheet of 1/8 to ¼ inch thickness. 14. If you want you may sprinkle some thin sliced pistachios and roll once more. Or pat the edible silver paper (vark) gently. 15. Let it cool down a bit and cut with a greased knife to squares or diamonds. I made 2x recipe, so got about 18 burfis. If you make 1x – cut them to 9 squares. Knead the edges again and shape to burfi if you want. If it dries out you won’t be able to do that. Cool down badam burfi completely before you store it in an air tight container. It keeps good for 7 to 10 days at room temperature and in the refrigerator for a month. 16. To increase the shelf life at room temperature, wrap individual pieces of badam burfi in a parchment paper and store them in an air tight container for 2 weeks.

title: Badam Burfi Recipe | Almond Burfi with Tips & Tricks
ingredients: 0.75 cup badam almonds, 1 cup water, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp saffron milk, 4 cardamom elachi, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak almonds in hot water for 1 hour. 2. now slowly peel the skin from badam and keep aside. 3. also pat dry the badam making sure there is no moisture present. 4. transfer the badam into a small blender and blend to coarse powder. keep aside. 5. meanwhile, prepare the sugar syrup by adding ¾ cup sugar and ½ cup water. 6. keeping the flame on low, stir continuously. 7. boil the sugar syrup till it forms one string consistency. 8. now add the prepared almond powder, saffron milk and cardamom powder. 9. mix continuously keeping flame on low to medium. 10. continue to mix continuously till the mixture starts to leave the pan. 11. transfer the badam dough onto the butter paper. 12. and allow to cool for 5 minutes. 13. grease your hand with little ghee and knead for a minute. 14. once you get smooth dough, roll the dough with a rolling-pin,. 15. furthermore, cut the dough into square shape or the shape you wish for. 16. you can also decorate with silver leaf / silver vark. 17. finally, serve badam burfi / badam katli on diwali or any festival.

title: Badam Burfi Recipe | Almond Burfi with Tips & Tricks
ingredients: 0.75 cup badam almonds, 1 cup water, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp saffron milk, 4 cardamom elachi, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak almonds in hot water for 1 hour. 2. now slowly peel the skin from badam and keep aside. 3. also pat dry the badam making sure there is no moisture present. 4. transfer the badam into a small blender and blend to coarse powder. keep aside. 5. meanwhile, prepare the sugar syrup by adding ¾ cup sugar and ½ cup water. 6. keeping the flame on low, stir continuously. 7. boil the sugar syrup till it forms one string consistency. 8. now add the prepared almond powder, saffron milk and cardamom powder. 9. mix continuously keeping flame on low to medium. 10. continue to mix continuously till the mixture starts to leave the pan. 11. transfer the badam dough onto the butter paper. 12. and allow to cool for 5 minutes. 13. grease your hand with little ghee and knead for a minute. 14. once you get smooth dough, roll the dough with a rolling-pin,. 15. furthermore, cut the dough into square shape or the shape you wish for. 16. you can also decorate with silver leaf / silver vark. 17. finally, serve badam burfi / badam katli on diwali or any festival.

title: Badam Halwa (Almond Flour Halwa)
ingredients: 0.75 cup badam almonds, 0.5 cup sugar, 1 pinch saffron, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.75 cup water, 0.375 cup ghee, 0.25 teaspoon rose water
instructions: 1. You can skip this section if using almond flour. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a pot. Add badam and leave them for 2 to 3 minutes. Then drain the water and soak them in fresh water for 30 to 40 mins. Peel off the skin. 2. Add badam to a mixer jar. Blend badam, cardamoms, sugar, saffron with milk or water to a slightly coarse paste. Usually badam halwa is slightly of fine rava texture. 3. Heat a nonstick pan or heavy bottom pan with 3 tbsp of the ghee. 4. Add the ground badam paste & cook stirring consistently till the halwa thickens. 5. Next add the rest of the ghee & keep stirring nonstop. The mixture will begin to leave the sides of the pan. 6. Switch off when it is of pouring consistency yet thick. It will thicken further upon cooling. Lastly add a spoon of ghee. 7. Press SAUTE button on the Instant pot and pour ghee to the steel insert. 8. Then add the almond flour & saute for 2 mins. Press CANCEL button & add sugar, water/ milk & saffron. 9. Whisk it well to break any lumps & to dissolve the sugar. 10. Secure the Instant pot with the lid & position the steam release vent to sealing. 11. Press PRESSURE COOK button & set the timer to 0 mins (ZERO). 12. The IP beeps when it is done. Gently do a quick release by moving the steam release vent to venting from sealing. 13. Add the cardamom powder & Press SAUTE button. Saute until the badam halwa thickens slightly. Do not leave it unattended. If needed switch to low saute by pressing the saute button again. 14. Next add the rest of the ghee & saute. The halwa will begin to release the sides of the pot. 15. Press CANCEL button when it reaches a thick yet of pouring consistency. (refer video)

title: Badam Kheer (Almond Payasam)
ingredients: 2.5 cups full fat milk, 0.25 cup almonds, 0.5 cup full fat milk, 3.5 tablespoons sugar, 3 green cardamom, 1 pinch saffron, 2 tablespoons silvered almonds
instructions: 1. Soak ¼ cup almonds (30 almonds – 35 grams) in 1 cup hot water, for 30 minutes. Alternately you may also soak them for 3 to 4 hours in cold water. You want to soak them really well else it is difficult to grind them to a smooth paste. Any grits in the kheer is going to take too long to soften. 2. When the almonds are soaked, peel the skin. 3. On a medium flame, bring 2½ cups milk to a boil in a heavy bottom pot. Reserve another half cup cold milk aside. 4. Add the peeled almonds to a blender along with 2 to 3 green cardamoms, 1 large pinch of saffron and 3 to 4 tablespoons sugar. 5. Pour ¼ cup milk from the reserved milk and blend them to a very smooth puree, adding the rest as you grind. Using lot of milk at one time won’t blend the nuts well, so add them in 2 batches, ¼ cup each time. 6. When the milk comes to a boil, add the ground almonds. If you like to add vermicelli, then roast them lightly in ghee and add it to the boiling milk. When the vermicelli is cooked then add almond mixture. 7. Stir and continue to cook on a low to medium flame. 8. When the milk reaches thick consistency as seen below, turn off the stove. If you want to serve it chilled, cool down and refrigerate. Whisk well with a fork or a whisk to a smooth texture. 9. Transfer to serving bowls and garnish with blanched/ silvered almonds or chopped pistachios. Serve badam kheer hot or chilled as desired.

title: Badam Ladoo (Almond Flour Laddu)
ingredients: 1 cup almonds, 1 cup coconut, 1 cup jaggery, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 0.25 teaspoon ginger, 1 cup almonds, 1 cup coconut, 0.5 cup sugar, 1 teaspoon vanilla extract, 2 tablespoons raisins, 2 tablespoons pistachios, 2 tablespoons ghee, 38 grams Medjool dates, 1.5 cups almond flour, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon cinnamon, 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
instructions: 1. Roast almonds: Dry roast almonds on a medium flame until they begin to smell roasted. Remove to cool down and roast coconut until aromatic. Cool down and grind both to a powder. 2. Recipe 1 - With jaggery: Add the jaggery, ginger and cardamom. Grind until the mixture comes together and turns slightly greasy. Do not overgrind. Taste test and add more jaggery if you want. Take portions to your hand and roll to almond ladoo. 3. Recipe 2 - With sugar: Powder sugar finely and mix together with the almond mixture and vanilla/cardamom. Heat a pan with ghee and fry the chopped nuts and raisins until the raisins turn plump. Pour it to the mixture and mix with a spoon, later with hand. Take portions of this and roll to balls. If the mixture is dry, add some hot ghee or milk. 4. Recipe 3 - With dates: Dry roast almond flour for 4 to 5 mins and cool down. Heat the dates in a pan or in the oven (7 to 10 mins at 350 F /175 C, no preheat needed) until really hot. 5. Cool down and add them to a food processor with cinnamon and vanilla. Process until the dates break down and combine with almond flour. Take portions and roll to balls (makes about 20). 6. Store badam laddu in an airtight jar and handle with moist-free hands.

title: Badam Milk Recipe | Badam Doodh
ingredients: 22 almonds, 5 ml Milk, 2 teaspoons Sugar, 1 pinch Saffron strands, 0.125 teaspoon Green cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Omit this to use almond flour. Add almonds to a bowl and rinse them well. Soak them in ¾ cup hot water for 30 mins. Later peel them. 2. To a blender jar, add almonds (or flour), cardamom, sugar and saffron (if using). Pour half cup milk and blend to a very smooth paste. 3. On a medium flame, bring the rest of the milk to a boil and stir in the almond paste. Mix well and simmer for 2 to 3 mins until bubbly and frothy. 4. Taste test and add more sugar or flavorings if you want. Cool down or cover and chill in the refrigerator for a few hours. It keeps good in the refrigerator for 2 days. 5. Whisk well until uniform and pour to serving glasses. Garnish with thin sliced almonds or pistachios or rose petals.

title: badam powder | badam milk powder | almond milk | almond milk powder
ingredients: 0.5 cup badam almonds, 2 pods cardamom, 0.5 tsp kesar saffron, 0.5 cup milk powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan dry roast ½ cup badam on low flame. 2. roast on low flame until the badam turns golden and crunchy. 3. cool completely and transfer to the blender along with 2 pods cardamom and ¼ tsp kesar. 4. blend to a fine powder without adding water. 5. transfer the almond powder to a large bowl. 6. also add ½ cup milk powder, ¼ tsp turmeric and ¼ tsp kesar. 7. mix well making sure everything is combined well. if you prefer, you can also add ¼ cup powdered sugar at this stage. 8. transfer the badam milk powder mix to an airtight container. 9. to prepare badam milk, take 3 cup milk in a saucepan. 10. add 2 tbsp of homemade badam mix to milk and mix well. 11. get the milk to a boil. you can add sugar if you prefer at this stage. 12. stir and boil for a minute or until milk thickens slightly. 13. finally, pour the badam milk to a cup and enjoy.

title: Bagara Baingan Recipe for Biryani
ingredients: 2 tablespoons peanuts, 1 tablespoon sesame seeds, 3 tablespoon coconut, 2 tablespoons tamarind, 0.25 cup water, 3 grams eggplants, 3.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.875 cup onions, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. On a medium heat, dry roast peanuts until they begin to smell good and turn light brown. 2. Reduce the heat and add sesame seeds. When they begin to splutter, turn off the heat and add coconut. 3. Saute it in the hot pan till the coconut smells good. (If using fresh coconut, saute it separately until aromatic) 4. Cool these and blend with little water to a coarse or smooth paste as desired. Set aside. 5. To a large bowl pour 2 to 3 cups water and add half teaspoon salt. Rinse the eggplants under running water. Slit them from the base to 4 portions, keeping the stem intact. (Check the photos in the post). Add the eggplants to the water. 6. Heat oil in a pan. When the oil is medium hot, take off the eggplants one at a time and wipe dry with a kitchen tissue or cloth and add to the hot oil. 7. Stir and fry them until the skin looks blistered completely, meaning the brinjal is almost cooked but not completely. Remove these from oil and keep them aside. 8. Transfer the hot oil to a bowl and just retain 2 tablespoons in the pan 9. Add cumin and mustard to the pan. When they begin to sizzle, add onions, curry leaves and sprinkle salt. 10. Fry until the onions turn transparent. Next fry ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes away. 11. Pour half a cup of water to the pan. 12. Add the blended peanut mixture, red chili powder, garam masala and turmeric. Stir and begin to cook on a medium flame. 13. Filter the tamarind juice or paste directly to the pan and stir. 14. When the gravy begins to bubble well, add the fried brinjals and stir. Add more water if needed to bring the gravy to a consistency. 15. Cover and cook till the brinjal looks completely wilted or cooked through. 16. Add coriander leaves. Stir and serve bagara baingan with biryani, pulao or roti.

title: Bagara Rice (Chawal)
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 3 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 1 onion, 1.5 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 3 tablespoons mint leaves, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.75 cups water for pressure cooker, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 3 green cardamoms
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse well atleast thrice. Pour 3 to 4 cups water and soak for atleast 30 mins. 2. Meanwhile slice the onion and green chilies. Make a fine paste of ginger and garlic. Fine chop mint and coriander leaves. 3. Crush ½ inch cinnamon, 2 cloves and 3 green cardamoms to a fine powder. I did it in a mortar pestle. 4. After 30 mins drain water from the rice completely. 5. Heat a pot or pressure cooker with ghee. Add shahi jeera, cloves, green cardamoms, black cardamom, bay leaf and cinnamon. 6. When they begin to sizzle add the onions and green chilies. Saute until they turn slightly golden. 7. Stir in the ginger garlic paste and fry until a nice aroma comes out. 8. Add drained rice, mint leaves and coriander leaves. Fry on a moderate high heat for 2 mins. 9. Next add salt and ground spice powder. Pour water and give a good stir at the bottom. 10. Taste the water and add more salt if needed. 11. If cooking in Pot: Cover and simmer on a low flame until all the water is absorbed and the rice is fully cooked. If the rice is underdone and there is not enough moisture, you may add few more tbsps of hot water. Give a good stir and cook. 12. If cooking in pressure cooker: Cover and cook on a medium high flame for 1 whistle. When the pressure releases naturally, open the lid and fluff up the bagar rice with a fork. 13. Press the SAUTE button on the Instant pot and pour ghee to the inner pot. Add all the whole spices - shahi jeera, cloves, green cardamoms, black cardamom, cinnamon & bay leaf. 14. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and green chilies. Saute until golden. 15. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for 30 seconds. Then add the drained rice, mint and coriander leaves. 16. Press CANCEL button and saute the rice for 1 minute. Then add salt, ground spice powder and water. 17. Deglaze by scrubbing the bottom of the pot with a spatula. Taste and add more salt if needed. 18. Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position the steam release handle to sealing. 19. Press PRESSURE COOK button (or manual button) and set the timer to 5 mins. 20. The instant pot will beep when it is done. Wait for the pressure to release naturally for 5 mins. Release the rest of the pressure manually by moving the steam vent from sealing to venting. 21. Gently give a good stir and fluff up bagara rice with a fork. Serve it with any curry or raita.

title: Baingan Bharta Recipe (Eggplant Bharta)
ingredients: 5 grams eggplants, 4 medium garlic cloves, 1.5 green chilies, 1 teaspoon oil, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomato, 3 garlic cloves, 0.625 inch ginger, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse eggplants well under running water and wipe dry with a kitchen tissue. Make 4 slits on the brinjal. If you want to use the garlic & chili - push the garlic cloves deep inside 2 slits. Also push the slit green chilies in the other 2 slits. Brush them with oil. 2. On Direct Fire: With the help of a tong, place it on direct fire. You can do it either on the red hot coal or stovetop. Keep turning it to all the other sides and grill until it is done completely roasted & cooked through. It takes about 12 to 14 mins. 3. To grill in oven: Line a metal tray with foil. Cut each eggplant to 2 parts and place them on the tray with the skin side on top. You can also place the tomatoes in the same tray. Broil/Grill them at 460 F or 240 C for 16 to 20 mins. 4. In air fryer: Place baingan, chilies, garlic and tomatoes in the air fryer basket. Air fry at 400 F or 200 C. It takes roughly about 22 mins for the whole eggplant, 16 mins if you halve the eggplant & air fry. 5. Once done, check by inserting a fork into the eggplant. It should go in smoothly & easily meaning it is soft and cooked well. (Check step-by-step photos above) 6. Cool the eggplant and peel the skin. Optionally - Transfer the eggplant to a bowl and cover it for 5 mins. Once done remove the skin. 7. Mash or chop the eggplant, grilled garlic and green chilies. Keep these aside. Peel and chop the tomatoes, onions and raw garlic as well. 8. Pour oil to a hot pan. When the oil turns hot, add chopped ginger and garlic. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds until aromatic. 9. Stir in the chopped onions and saute until they turn light golden, for 6 to 7 mins. 10. Add tomatoes and salt. Cook until they become soft for 5 to 6 mins. Add red chili powder and cook for a few minutes until the raw smell from the tomatoes is gone, for 1 to 2 mins. 11. Stir in the mashed eggplants, garlic and green chilies. (You may want to save half of the green chilies for later after taste testing) 12. Mix and mash it a bit to blend well. Saute for 7 to 8 minutes on a medium heat & stir in the garam masala if using. Turn off the stove. 13. Taste test and add more salt and green chili if required. Garnish with coriander leaves. If you like squeeze some lemon juice. 14. Serve baingan bharta with roti, rice or naan.

title: baingan masala recipe | brinjal masala recipe | eggplant masala curry
ingredients: 6 sized purple brinjal baingan eggplant, 3 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds rai, 0.25 tsp methi fenugreek seeds, few curry leaves, 1 onion, 1 cup tamarind water, 0.5 tsp jaggery gud, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 2 tsp oil, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste adrak lasun paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric powder haldi, 1 to mato, 1 tsp coriander powder daniya powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 1 tsp kashmiri red chili powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala powder, salt to taste, 2 tbsp sesame seeds til, 0.5 cup coconut, 0.25 cup peanuts groundnuts, 1 tsp kashmiri red chili powder lal mirch powder, salt to taste
instructions: 1. firstly, take the cut brinjal and stuff the prepared stuffing. 2. and fry on medium flame with 3 tbsp of oil. 3. cover and simmer for 2-4 minutes or till they are half cooked. 4. further, take off and keep aside. 5. now in the same kadai add mustard seeds, methi and curry leaves. 6. allow to splutter. 7. further add onions and saute well. 8. also add prepared onion-tomato paste and continue to saute. 9. furthermore, place the stuffed and fried brinjals. 10. and pour tamarind wate add a piece of jaggery. 11. mix gently, cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 12. stir occasionally and cook till the brinjal gets cooked completely. 13. finally, serve baingan masala with chapathi or roti.

title: Bajji Recipe (Aratikaya Bajji)
ingredients: 1 plantain, 1 onion, 1.5 green chilies, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon Red chili powder, 1 pinch turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon salt, 0.1875 teaspoon ajwain, 1 Pinch baking soda, 1 cup besan, 0.25 cup rice flour, Water, Oil
instructions: 1. Make a paste of onion, green chili and ginger garlic paste with very little water. You will make a coarse paste with minimum water. keep this aside. 2. Add flour, turmeric, ajwain, salt, red chili powder and soda to a large mixing bowl. 3. Peel raw banana and slice them thin. Add them to a bowl of water. 4. On a medium flame, heat a deep pan with enough oil for frying. 5. Add the paste made at step 1 to the mixing bowl and mix well along with extra enough water to make the batter of a thick but pouring consistency. I suggest you refer the pic. Add more salt if needed. 6. Drain the sliced banana and add them to a colander. There must be no water left dripping. 7. When the oil gets hot, put on the flame to medium heat. 8. Dip sliced banana one at a time in batter and gently drop in the hot oil. Avoid disturbing for a few minutes. 9. Deep fry till golden & crisp on a medium flame. 10. Remove them to a steel colander or cooling rack. 11. Mix chopped onions, green chili, coriander leaves, salt and lemon juice. slit the fritters in the center and stuff them with the onion mix and serve immediately.

title: bajra roti recipe | bajre ki roti | pearl millet roti recipe | sajje rotti
ingredients: 2 cup bajra atta pearl millet flour sajje hittu, 0.5 tsp salt, water, wheat flour
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cup bajra atta, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 2. add ½ of hot water and start to knead. 3. knead to the soft dough for at least 10 minutes. 4. add water as required and knead well as there is no gluten in the dough. 5. pinch a small ball sized dough and knead again. 6. dust with wheat flour and pat gently. you can alternatively use a rolling pin to roll as done for paratha. 7. pat with both the hands until the roti turns as thin as possible. if the roti breaks, it means it needs more kneading. 8. dust off excess flour and put over hot tawa. 9. now spread water over the roti with help of hand or wet cloth removing excess dough. 10. wait until the water evaporates then flip it to the other side. 11. press gently and cook all the sides. 12. finally, serve bajra roti / sajje rotti with jaggery or curry.

title: Baked Fish (Oven Grilled Fish)
ingredients: 3 grams fish fillet, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.3333333333333333 cup shallots, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 1.25 tbsp lemon juice, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon roasted semolina
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan and saute shallots until golden. Add ginger garlic and saute for 1 minute. Stir in all the ground spices and salt. Saute for a minute. Cool down and grind or crush to a coarse thick paste with lemon juice. If it is too thick, add a splash of water. 2. Grease a prepared tray with little oil. Pat dry the fish and score in 3 to 4 places, keeping the base intact. Apply half of this over the fish, on the skin side. (If you want remove the skin and apply.) Turn it to the other side and apply the rest. 3. Grill in oven: Preheat your oven at 450 F - 230 C, set to grill function for at least 15 mins. Place your tray in the center rack and grill for 18 to 20 mins depending on the thickness of your fish. Optionally if you want - during the last 5 mins bring the tray out of the oven and sprinkle the semolina and continue to bake. 4. When done your fish flakes easily at the prick of the fork. The internal temperature of the fish in the thickest part should reach 145 F. 5. Serve Baked fish hot with rice or as a side in any meal.

title: Balushahi Recipe | Badusha Sweet - Halwai Style
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 cup ghee, water, oil, 2 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, few saffron, 4 pods cardamom, pinch saffron food colour
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cup maida, 1 tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp salt, and ¼ cup ghee. 2. Crumble and mix well making sure the flour is moist. 3. Now add cup water and start to make dough without kneading. 4. Cover and rest for 30 minutes. 5. Meanwhile, to prepare sugar syrup, take in 2 cup sugar and ½ cup water. 6. Also add few thread saffron, 4 pods cardamom and mix well. 7. Further, boil for 5 minutes or till sugar syrup turns slightly sticky. If you are looking for more sweet badushahi then get to 1 string consistency sugar syrup. 8. Add a pinch of saffron food color and mix well. Sugar syrup is ready, cover and keep aside. 9. After the dough has rested for 30 minutes, slightly knead the dough. 10. Pinch a small ball-sized dough and make a ball. 11. Make a hole at the center with the help of a finger. 12. deep fry in warm hot oil on low flame till the balls turn golden brown and crisp. 13. Drop them into warm sugar syrup immediately. 14. Coat the badhusha with sugar syrup on both sides and soak for 5 minutes. 15. Finally, serve Balushahi Recipe or Badusha Recipe garnished with silver vark or few chopped cashews. 16. Pinch a small ball-sized dough and make a ball. Make a hole at the center with the help of a finger.

title: Balushahi Recipe | Badusha Sweet - Halwai Style
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 cup ghee, water, oil, 2 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, few saffron, 4 pods cardamom, pinch saffron food colour
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cup maida, 1 tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp salt, and ¼ cup ghee. 2. Crumble and mix well making sure the flour is moist. 3. Now add cup water and start to make dough without kneading. 4. Cover and rest for 30 minutes. 5. Meanwhile, to prepare sugar syrup, take in 2 cup sugar and ½ cup water. 6. Also add few thread saffron, 4 pods cardamom and mix well. 7. Further, boil for 5 minutes or till sugar syrup turns slightly sticky. If you are looking for more sweet badushahi then get to 1 string consistency sugar syrup. 8. Add a pinch of saffron food color and mix well. Sugar syrup is ready, cover and keep aside. 9. After the dough has rested for 30 minutes, slightly knead the dough. 10. Pinch a small ball-sized dough and make a ball. 11. Make a hole at the center with the help of a finger. 12. deep fry in warm hot oil on low flame till the balls turn golden brown and crisp. 13. Drop them into warm sugar syrup immediately. 14. Coat the badhusha with sugar syrup on both sides and soak for 5 minutes. 15. Finally, serve Balushahi Recipe or Badusha Recipe garnished with silver vark or few chopped cashews. 16. Pinch a small ball-sized dough and make a ball. Make a hole at the center with the help of a finger.

title: Banana Bread Recipe - Egg Free | Homemade Banana Bread
ingredients: 3 banana, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.5 cup oil, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 0.75 cup maida plain flour all-purpose flour, 0.75 cup wheat flour atta, 1 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp baking soda sodium bicarbonate, 0.25 tsp cinnamon powder dalchini powder, pinch of salt, 0.5 cup walnut akharot
instructions: 1. firstly, take ripped bananas and cut them into pieces. 2. also add sugar and mash the banana. 3. furthermore, add oil and vanilla extract. 4. also sieve maida, wheat flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon powder and salt. 5. blend and combine all the dry ingredients with the banana puree. 6. furthermore, add walnut and fold gently. 7. transfer the cake batter into the cake mould or bread mould. 8. pat the tray twice to remove the air incorporated into the batter. 9. place the tray into preheated oven. bake the cake at 180 degree celsius or 356 degree fahrenheit for 40 minutes. 10. further, allow the cake to cool completely. 11. and later cut to slices and serve. 12. finally, serve the eggless & vegan banana bread or store in airtight container.

title: Banana Buns Recipe | Mangalore Buns - Banana Puri
ingredients: 2 ripe banana, 3 tbsp sugar, 0.25 cup curd, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp baking soda, 0.5 tsp salt, 2.5 cup maida, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly in a large mixing bowl take 2 ripened banana. If you are having overripened banana (black) it works great. 2. Add in 3 tbsp sugar depending on the sweetness of the banana. 3. Mash smooth with the help of a fork. But do not blend banana in mixi. 4. Now add ¼ cup curd, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp baking soda, and ½ tsp salt. Combine well with the help of a spoon. 5. Further, add 2½ cup maida and prepare the dough. 6. Knead for 5 minutes or till dough turns smooth. 7. Grease the dough with a tsp of oil. Cover and rest in warm place for 8 hours. 8. Next day, knead the dough slightly and pinch a ball-sized dough. 9. Dust with some maida and roll slightly thicker than poori. 10. Deep fry the buns in hot oil. Further fry the buns on both sides keeping flame on medium. 11. Fry till banana poori turns dark golden brown on both sides. 12. Finally, serve the Banana Buns / Mangalore Buns / Banana Poori with chutney or saagu. You can also have them plain.

title: Banana Cake Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour, 2.5 teaspoons baking powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 cup sugar, 1 grams unsalted butter, 2 eggs, 2 teaspoons vanilla extract, 16 ml milk, 1 cup ripe banana
instructions: 1. Keep the eggs, butter and all other ingredients out of the fridge a little before you begin to work on the recipe. Use all of them at room temperature except butter that needs to be cold, yet soft and should hold its structure. Here are the quantities: 2. cups all-purpose flour or whole wheat flour. Fluff up the flour with a fork in the pack. Spoon it to the measuring cup and level it with a straight edged spoon or knife. 100 grams unsalted butter 2 eggs ½ cup milk + 2 .5 tablespoons (160 ML) 1 cup mashed bananas (use only ripe bananas) 3. Preheat the oven to 170 C or 340 F for at least 15 minutes. If you have a fan forced oven, then preheat to 160 C or 320 F. 4. Grease a 8 by 8 inch square cake pan and line with a parchment paper. If you do not have a parchment paper then simply dust some flour all over the cake pan including the sides. Shake well and invert it and dust off the excess in your kitchen sink. 5. Before you begin to make the batter, ensure the butter is soft but still cold. To check press the block of cold butter with your finger, it should dent slightly yet hold its solid structure and should not be too soft. More importantly it has to be still cold. This is the kind of butter we want here. Add 1 cup sugar and 100 grams butter to a mixing bowl. You can also use your stand mixer. 6. Sieve together 1 cup wheat flour (120 grams), ¼ teaspoon sea salt (or pink salt) and 1¼ teaspoon baking powder. Sieve them thrice so the banana cake comes out light. 7. Ensure all the ingredients are at room temperature. Melt ¼ cup butter to a liquid state. Crack open the 1 egg to a bowl and beat with a fork so you have a uniform mixture. Mash bananas to make ½ cup. Measure ¼ cup plus 2 tablespoons milk. 8. To a mixing bowl, add mashed bananas, beaten egg, melted butter, milk, 6 to 8 tablespoons ( ½ cup) sugar and 1 teaspoon vanilla extract. Mix all of them. 9. Then add flour in 2 batches mixing just until combined. 10. You should have a uniform batter with no streaks of flour in it. Use a spatula to clean out the sides of the bowl while you mix. Do not over mix as the cake can end up being gluey. 11. Transfer this to the prepared pan. Tap the pan to the kitchen counter. 12. Bake at 170 C for 30 mins if using a 6 inch pan, if using a loaf pan (8x4x2.5 inches) then bake for about 35 to 36 minutes. When done, a skewer inserted in the center comes out clean. If the skewer comes out with batter or wet mixture, bake it a little longer until it comes out clean. You need to adjust the baking temperature depending on the bakeware and oven. 13. Cool the cake in the pan for 10 minutes and then remove it to a wired rack. Cool completely before slicing. Avoid slicing hot banana cake.

title: Banana Curry Recipe (Plantain Curry)
ingredients: 2 green banana, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1 green chili, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 1 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat a pan with 2 tablespoons oil. Add ½ teaspoon mustard seeds and ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. When they begin to splutter, add curry leaves (rinsed & pat dry) & a pinch of hing. 2. Add 1 cup onions and 1 green chili chopped. 3. On a medium heat, stir fry or saute until light golden. Add 1½ teaspoon ginger garlic paste. Meanwhile fill a small bowl or pot with 2 cups water. Peel 2 green bananas, Remove the greens completely and chop them. Add them to the water and keep aside. 4. Saute until it turns aromatic for a minute or so. 5. Add ¾ cup chopped tomatoes, ¼ to ⅓ teaspoon salt, ⅛ teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, ½ teaspoon coriander powder (optional) and 1 teaspoon garam masala. 6. Saute well until the onion tomato masala smells good. This takes only a minute. 7. Option 1 : If using hybrid or young green bananas, add peeled and chopped green bananas pieces (from 2 green bananas) to the pan. 8. Mix well. 9. Pour half cup hot water and mix well. 10. Cover and cook until tender. Use a spoon or a fork to check it is fork tender and fully cooked but not mushy. If it is undercooked and there is no moisture, you may add some hot water and cook further. 11. We like a dry curry so I prefer to dry out the excess moisture. Taste test and add more salt or spices. 12. Option 2: If using Non-GMO green bananas or mature bananas that are harder, boil the cubed pieces in water in a separate pot for a few minutes until slightly tender but not mushy. Pour half cup water of this water to the onion tomato masala and cook it until thick like a gravy. Then add the boiled banana pieces to the gravy and simmer for a while until the pieces are cooked through completely. As the curry cools down, it will dry out so it is best served hot or warm. Serve Banana Curry with rice or chapati. 13. Raw Banana Fry (A... Green Banana Reci... Bajji Recipe (Ara... Vazhakkai Fry (Po... Cucumber curry | ... Sweet Potato Curr...

title: Banana Dosa Recipe | Instant South Indian Pancake
ingredients: 1 banana, 1 cup rava, 0.75 cup poha avalakki, 1 cup curd, 2 tbsp jaggery, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp baking soda
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar take 1 banana. 2. Add 1 cup rava, ¾ cup poha, 1 cup curd, 2 tbsp jaggery, ½ tsp cardamom powder and ½ tsp salt. 3. Blend to smooth batter adding water as required. 4. Transfer the batter into the large bowl and add ¼ tsp turmeric. 5. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 6. Also, add ¼ tsp baking soda and mix gently forming a frothy batter. 7. Pour the batter over the hot tawa. 8. Cook until the top of the dosa is cooked completely. 9. Finally, enjoy Instant Banana Dosa with chutney or honey.

title: Banana Milkshake Recipe
ingredients: 2 Bananas, 3 dates, 0.5 teaspoon cinnamon, 1 teaspoon vanilla extract, 1.5 cups pasteurized chilled milk, 24 cashews
instructions: 1. Add 2 ripe bananas. Here I have also added 20 to 24 cashews for a protein boost. You may skip them if you don’t prefer or add 2 tablespoons of nut butter or 6 scoops of vanilla ice cream instead. Cashews give a more rich and creamy consistency to your banana shake. Also you can optionally soak them for 3 to 4 hours for health benefits. 2. You may skip this step if using ice cream. Add sugar or honey as per your taste. 2 tbsps is a good amount. I used 4 pitted dates instead of sugar. Just note that adding too many dates can alter the color of your shake. Also add ½ teaspoon ground cinnamon or ¼ tsp cardamom powder. 3. I also add 1 tsp vanilla extract for extra flavor. 4. Pour 1½ cups chilled milk. If using ice cream you will need only half a cup of milk. I used this homemade almond milk here. You can also use coconut milk. 5. Blend everything to smooth shake. You may add more milk if you prefer to thin down or add more bananas to thicken. 6. Pour Banana Shake to serving glasses immediately and serve. 7. If using regular milk, then boil, cool and chill in the fridge. According to ayurveda boiled milk is easier to digest. 8. I make almond milk instead of using regular milk. So I wash and soak the almonds for 4 to 6 hours. However you can skip and just add them directly to the blender. 9. Peel off the skin. If well soaked the peels just come off easily. 10. Give the nuts a good rinse. 11. Add them to the blender. 12. Add the sweetener. I added 2 dates. Do add as needed. 13. Pour chilled water or milk. You can also use coconut milk. 14. I added vanilla extract. Cinnamon powder goes well during winters and cardamom powder during summers. 15. Blend until the nuts are smoothly blended and you have frothy milk. 16. Add bananas. Break them to 2 to 3 parts. 17. Blend until smooth and frothy. Serve Banana Milkshake immediately.

title: banana rasayana | balehannu rasayana recipe
ingredients: 10.5 bananas, 1 cup coconut, 4.5 tbsp of sugar, 3.5 cardamom, pinch of salt
instructions: 1. finely chop the bananas into small pieces and keep it aside. 2. take 1 cup of grated coconut and grind it to smooth paste with 1/4th cup of water. add 3-4 cardamom extract before grinding. 3. squeeze and extract the coconut milk from the grated coconut. 4. add 4- 5 tbsp of sugar and a pinch of salt to the chopped banana and mix it well. 5. add the extracted coconut milk to the chopped bananas and mix well. 6. banana rasayana is ready. serve it with neer dosa, plain dosa, tuppa dosa and pooris.

title: Bandar Laddu (Thokkudu Ladoo)
ingredients: 3 grams besan, 3 grams jaggery, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 cup ghee, 1 handful cashews, Rock sugar
instructions: 1. Mix besan with just enough water to make it a stiff dough. The dough must be stiff when you touch and not sticky or soggy else it will absorb all the ghee when you deep fry the karapusa. 2. Heat a deep pan with 1 cup ghee. 3. Grease your muruku mold or chakkali press.Take a portion of the dough and fill the mold or press. 4. When the ghee is hot, reduce the flame to medium and press the dough into the ghee and deep fry till crusty. The color of the karapusa should not turn dark. To get the right color, the heat must be reduced to low when they are half fried and fry till they turn crusty. So the resulting ones must be of light color and not brown and yet crusty. Check the pics 5. Transfer these to a ventilated sieve or a tray. Finish frying till the dough finishes and crumble them to pieces. Save the left over ghee. 6. Meanwhile pound this crumble karapusa in a stone pestle or mixer till it turns to a fine powder. There should be no large chunks left, if needed can use a sieve with large holes to remove the pieces. Divide the whole kara to 2 batches and pound them. Do not let your mix heat up else it becomes like a nut meal, will begin to ooze out ghee. So take a break for few minutes and repeat. Keep the pound mix ready.

title: Barley Water Recipe
ingredients: 0.25 cup Barley, 3 cups water, 1 teaspoon rock sugar, 2 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Add barley to a large bowl. Pick and discard any debris, hulls or stones. 2. Pour water and rinse them well at least 4 times in plenty of water. Rub and rinse them well. Drain the water completely. 3. Optional but recommended: Soak them for at least 7 hours. Soaking the grains not only reduces the cook time but also avoids flatulence or abdominal gas, breaks down hard to digest gluten and reduces the phytic acid in the grains. After soaking drain the water and rinse them. 4. Cooking in pot: Add them to a large pot and pour 4 cups water. Bring to a boil on a medium high heat and then reduce to medium to low flame. Let simmer for 40 to 50 minutes until the barley is soft and slightly mushy. If required add more hot water as you cook. 5. Pressure cooker: Add the barley to a pressure cooker and pressure cook on a medium heat for at least 7 to 8 whistles, or until soft. When the pressure releases open the lid. 6. Instant pot : Add the soaked barley to the inner pot/ steel insert along with 3 cups water for soaked barley or 4 cups water for unsoaked . Secure the lid and position the vent to sealing. Press the porridge mode & set the timer to 20 mins. When the pressure drops, open the lid. 7. If you live in a colder country you can serve it warm or hot. But barley water in Asian countries is consumed at room temperature. 8. Strain barley water to serving glasses and consume it plain or sweetened with rock sugar, honey or any other sweetener you wish. You can also squeeze some lemon juice and relish. 9. Some of the cooked barley can be eaten along with the water or add it to salads. It can also be added to yogurt and mixed. Then topped with pomegranate arils.

title: Basil Seed Drink (Sabja Seeds Drink)
ingredients: 2 teaspoons Sweet basil seeds, 0.5 cup luke warm water, 0.75 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 teaspoon Rock sugar
instructions: 1. To start, you’ll want to pick over the basil seeds to remove any possible stones or debris. This is a post I published almost 9 years ago. Those days it was hard to find these seeds outside India and only a few places would sell them in Singapore. But we would see a lots of stones and debris in the seeds. So I still keep the steps of cleaning & rinsing in the post. Feel free to skip a few steps if you don’t want to clean. 2. This step is optional. Next, you’ll want to rinse the basil seeds if they’re not already cleaned for consumption. For this either add them to a bowl or a fine strainer. Pour clean drinking water and drain completely in a fine strainer. I use my tea/coffee strainer for this. 3. Add them to the serving glass or a bowl. The general ratio for soaking basil seeds is half cup of luke warm water for every 2 teaspoons of basil seeds. Combine the water and basil seeds and let them sit for 30 minutes to overnight. You can use more water if you want, but the basil seeds won’t absorb more water than they need. 4. As the basil seeds soak up the water, they’ll expand and develop a gray outer gel layer with a crunchy black interior. Leave them overnight if you have digestive issues.

title: Basmati Rice Recipe, How to Cook Basmati Rice
ingredients: 1 cup aged basmati rice, 2 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon oil, 0.375 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Add 1 cup basmati rice to a bowl and rinse well thrice. During the first rinse, you will see the water is cloudy and white due to the excess starch. 2. Drain the water and pour fresh water. The water looks clearer at this stage. Soak the rice for a minimum of 15 minutes. If you are using newly harvested rice, do not soak. 3. Pour water to a heavy bottom pot. If you want to add salt, whole spices, oil or ghee, you may add at this stage. 4. After 15 mins, drain to a colander/strainer and shake off gently to get rid of the excess water. 5. Bring the water to a rolling boil on a high heat. 6. Add the drained rice and spread it with a little spoon. 7. Increase the heat to high, to keep the rice boiling rapidly. 8. Reduce the heat to low and cook covered until the rice is al dente, slightly undercooked. This step should take you no longer than 10 to 11 minutes. Do not stir or mix at all during this stage of cooking. If you see the rice bubbling and frothing up, you may open the lid one to 2 times & cover back. 9. When the rice is almost cooked al dente, you will see little holes and the water is fully absorbed. If you find your rice, is undercooked and there is not enough water, add a few tbsps of hot water. 10. At this stage, check by inserting a rice paddle straight into the pot, from a side towards the bottom & look down. Ideally there should be no excess water at the bottom. (read troubleshooting tips below). Optionally, using the paddle, turn the rice from the bottom to the top only once to get the top layer rice down and bottom wet rice to the top. Level the rice on top. Do not over do this as the grains can break. 11. Cover the pot & turn off the stove. Let rest on the hot burner for 10 to 15 mins. Basmati rice will still be steaming in the residual heat. This helps the rice to turn fluffy and will cook completely. 12. After resting, each grain should look like biryani rice grains – long, fluffy, non-sticky and not broken. Use a rice paddle to serve the basmati rice with Dal tadka , Chickpea Curry , Butter Chicken or Tikka masala . 13. Troubleshooting Tip: If you use the correct amount of water you won’t be left with any excess water when the rice is al dente. But if you find little excess water, simply increase the heat to medium high and cook without lid for a few minutes to evaporate the water. 14. The other way is to drain the rice to a colander placed inside the sink or over a large bowl. Add the rice back to the hot pot & cover it. 15. Always choose premium quality basmati rice that is aged and has been stored in good condition. Wash it well a few times in ample amount of water. Soak for at least 30 to 40 minutes again in apmple amount of water depending on the kind and brand. Some kinds of rice need about 60 minutes plus for the grain to absorb water. This helps the grain to expand well while cooking. Drain the rice. 16. Bring water to a boil in a large pot. Usually for 1 cup rice, any where from 4 cups to 5 cups can be used. If you are not using premium quality rice, you should be using up to 5 cups water to get rid of starch completely. Add salt as needed. Taste the water, it has to be slightly salty. 17. Add in the spices along with 1 tsp to 1 tbsp oil again depending on the quality of rice. Spice like bay leaf, star anise, shahi jeera, cinnamon, cloves and mace can be used. All the spices are optional, but most commonly a bay leaf and some shahi jeera does the job of infusing flavor to the rice. If you do not like to get the whole spices in your biryani then you can also tie them in a muslin cloth and add it to the water. Discard it when the rice is drained. 18. When the water comes to a boil, allow to boil rapidly. Add the drained rice. Do not stir too much since the rice is soaked for long time it tends to break.

title: Basundi Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups milk, 0.5 tin condensed milk, 1 pinch nutmeg powder, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 pinch saffron strands, 0.25 cup mixed nuts, 4 cups full fat milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 saffron strands, 0.125 teaspoon nutmeg powder, 4 tablespoons nuts
instructions: 1. Rinse a heavy bottom pot with little water. This prevents the milk from getting scorched. Then pour 2 cups full fat milk to the pot. 2. When the milk turns hot, take 2 tbsps of it to a small bowl and soak the saffron. Keep stirring the milk to prevent the milk solids sticking at the bottom. 3. Bring the milk to a boil on a medium heat & then continue to boil for 5 to 7 mins, stirring often. 4. Then add ½ cup + 2 tbsps condensed milk and stir well. For less sweeter basundi you can use only half cup. 5. Scrape off the cream that sticks on the sides and add back to the pot. Boil until the milk reaches a thick consistency. I boiled for another 8 minutes. 6. You may boil it for longer if you want it to be slightly more thick. The consistency of basundi is not as thick as rabri but must be runny yet slightly on the thicker side of pouring consistency. 7. Add ¼ teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 pinch nutmeg powder and saffron infused milk. Stir well and cook for a minute. Taste test and add more condensed milk for sweeter basundi. 8. Transfer Basundi to a serving bowl and cool completely. Then chill it for at least 2 hours if you prefer to serve as a chilled dessert. You can also serve it warm with poori . 9. Pour 4 cups full fat milk to a heavy bottom pot and begin to heat it on a medium heat. 10. When it comes to a boil, reduce the heat. You will see layer of cream at this stage. 11. I use a whisk to break up the cream and continue to simmer until the quantity reduces to 2 cups approximately. 12. Crush 1 pinch of saffron in your fingers and add it here. Also add ¼ teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 pinch nutmeg and 3 to 4 tablespoons sugar. 13. Mix well and let it simmer for 5 mins, until you begin to smell it fragrant. It turns slightly thick and creamy. 14. Cool this and transfer to a serving bowl. Chill in the fridge. Add chopped nuts and serve basundi with poori or as a dessert.

title: Basundi Recipe | How to Make Easy Milk Basundi Sweet
ingredients: 1 grams khova mava, 1.5 litre milk, 2 tbsp kesar milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp almonds badam
instructions: 1. Firstly, crumble 100 grams khova and keep it aside. If you are not having access to mawa then you can use ½ cup milk powder. 2. In a large kadai, take 1½ litre milk and get to a boil by stirring in between. 3. Add 2 tbsp kesar milk and mix well. 4. Boil for 5 minutes, or until the milk thickens slightly. 5. Once the milk reduces, add crumbled khova and mix well. 6. Continue to boil for 5 minutes or until the milk thickens. 7. Further, add ¼ cup sugar and get to a boil. Make sure the sugar is dissolved and cooked well. 8. Now add ½ tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp almonds & badam. Mix well. 9. Cool the milk slightly before serving. The basundi will thicken once cooled. 10. Finally, enjoy Basundi Recipe chilled or warm up for a week when refrigerated.

title: Batata Vada Recipe (Aloo Vada)
ingredients: 3 medium potatoes, 2.5 green chilies, 0.75 inch ginger, 2.5 cloves garlic, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 pinch hing, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.125 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 cup besan, 2.5 tablespoons rice flour, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 1 Pinch Hing, 1 pinch soda-bi-carbonate, 1 pinch Turmeric, 0.125 teaspoon red chili powder, 2.5 cups oil
instructions: 1. Boil potatoes whole in a pressure cooker or diced in a pot of water, until fork tender. Do not make them mushy or waterlogged, they should be fully cooked yet hold their shape. Later remove from water, peel and crumble them. 2. Mince or crush together ginger, garlic and green chilies to a paste. 3. On a medium flame, heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds. When they begin to pop, add curry and fry until crisp. Add ginger garlic and green chili paste. 4. Saute until fragrant and stir in hing, salt, turmeric and red chili powder. 5. Add the crumbled potatoes and coriander leaves. Turn off the stove and mix everything well. Taste test and adjust salt to your preference. Cool this and divide to 7 to 8 equal portions. Roll them to balls. 6. On a medium flame, heat oil for deep frying in a kadai. 7. Meanwhile stir in besan ,rice flour, salt, hing, turmeric and chili powder in a mixing bowl. Pour water little by little (start with ¼ cup and add more) and mix to make a batter of thick flowing consistency. (check video or photos). Taste test and adjust salt as need. 8. When the oil is hot enough, add a tiny pinch of soda to the batter and mix it well. 9. Test - Drop a small amount of batter to the hot oil to test if it is hot enough. The batter should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning brown. If it sinks it means the oil is not hot enough. 10. Regulate the flame to medium. Dip each ball in the batter with a spoon or fingers, to coat it well and gently slide to the hot oil. You can fry about 3 to 5 vadas in each batch depending on the amount of oil and the size of cookware. 11. Do not disturb them for 2 to 3 mins, until the batter becomes firm. Gently stir and fry until golden and crisp. Remove to a cooling rack. To fry the next batch make sure the oil is hot but not smoking hot. 12. Serve batata vada hot with green chutney or sandwich them in pav along with green chutney, dry garlic chutney & fried green chilies.

title: Beans Curry
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.75 cup onions, 2.5 garlic, 1 green chili, 0.75 cup tomato, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 25 grams green beans, 1 medium carrot, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.375 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Pour 2 tablespoons oil to a pan and heat it. When the oil turns hot, add ¼ teaspoon mustard (optional) and ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. 2. As soon as the spices splutter, add ¾ cup chopped onions (1 medium onion), 2 to 3 cloves chopped garlic and 1 slit green chili (optional, omit for kids). 3. Saute the onions on a medium heat until transparent and lose the raw smell. Add ¾ cup tomatoes chopped (1 large tomato), ½ teaspoon salt and a pinch of turmeric. 4. Cook until mushy and soft. If required you may cover the pan, so the tomatoes cook faster. Tomatoes should lose the raw flavor. Meanwhile chop 250 grams (½ pound) green beans and 1 medium carrot (optional). 5. Add them to the pan and saute for 3 to 4 mins. 6. Sprinkle ¼ to ½ teaspoon red chili powder and ¼ to ½ teaspoon garam masala. Stir fry on a medium heat for 2 mins. 7. Cover and cook until the beans are tender. I do not add water to cook this curry. But it depends on the kind of beans you use. If required you may splash some water as they cook. Once the beans are tender, taste test and add more salt if required. If you want you may spinkle a tsp of crushed kasuri methi. Garnish with 1 tablespoon chopped coriander leaves. Serve Beans Curry with Rice, roti or have it on its own.

title: beans ki sabji recipe | beans curry | beans ki sabzi | french beans curry
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 4 grams beans, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 tomato, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute ½ tsp cumin and 1 tsp kasuri methi until it turns aromatic. 2. add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute well. 3. further, add 400 grams beans and stir fry for 2 minutes. 4. also add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp aamchur and ¾ tsp salt. 5. saute for a minute or until spices are well combined. 6. cover and cook for 5 minutes or until beans are half cooked. 7. now add 1 tomato and mix well. 8. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until tomatoes soften. 9. finally, enjoy beans ki sabji with chapati or rice.

title: Beans Poriyal (Indian Green Beans Fry)
ingredients: 25 grams green beans, 3.5 tablespoons coconut, 0.5 teaspoon chilli powder, 1.25 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 1 red chilli, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Cut off both the edges of beans. Rinse them well in lot of water or under running water. Drain them. 2. Chop to ¼ inch pieces. 3. Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard, urad dal, chana dal and red chili. 4. When the dal turn golden, add hing and curry leaves. 5. Fry till the curry leaves turn crisp. 6. Add chopped beans,turmeric along with salt and saute for 2 mins. 7. Cook covered for 2 to 3 mins on a low flame. This helps to release the moisture. 8. If needed sprinkle 1 to 2 tbsps water. Cover and cook until soft and tender. 9. Add sambar powder or chili powder and coconut. 10. Mix well and saute for a minute. 11. Switch off and serve beans poriyal with rice. 12. It is important to chop the beans to at least ½ inch pieces. If you chop them too small, they will become mushy. 13. Press saute button on your instant pot. Pour oil to the steel insert. 14. When the oil turns hot, add mustard, red chili, urad dal and chana dal. Saute for a minute or so until the dal turns golden. 15. Then stir in curry leaves and hing. Add chopped beans, turmeric and salt. 16. Press cancel and pour 2 tablespoons water. Mix and secure the Instant pot with the lid. Press pressure cook and set the timer to "zero" minutes. 17. Once done, quick release the instant pot carefully. Open the lid and add red chilli powder or sambar powder. Mix well and saute just for a minute or so until the sambar powder smells good. If you see any moisture at the bottom, simply evaporate it. Add coconut and mix well. 18. Stir beans poriyal and serve it.

title: Beerakaya fry
ingredients: 25 grams ridge gourd, 1 tbsp Oil, 0.25 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 tsp chana dal, 1.5 tsp urad dal, 1 Pinch hing, 1 garlic clove, 1 red chili, salt, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 1.5 tbsps coconut, 1 tbsp peanuts, 0.75 tbsp chana dal, 0.75 tbsp urad dal, 0.75 tbsp coconut, 0.75 tbsp sesame seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 garlic clove, 2 red chilies
instructions: 1. Wash the beerakaya under running water, brush it well since we are using the skin too. Wash well. Devein and chop to bite sized pieces. 2. Heat a pan with oil. Add mustard, cumin, dals, and fry till the dals turn golden 3. Add curry leaves, red chili and garlic. Fry till the curry leaves turn crisp. Sprinkle hing. 4. Then add beerakaya, sprinkle turmeric and fry for about 2 to 3 minutes stirring. 5. Reduce the flame to low to medium and fry till beerakaya is cooked completely. If your gourd is not tender and juicy. You can sprinkle water, cover and cook. 6. On another burner, dry roast chana dal, urad dal & red chilies until dal turns golden. Then add sesame seeds, coconut, cumin & garlic. Roast on a low heat until the sesame turns aromatic. Do not over fry. Cool and powder this. 7. Add salt, 1½ tbsp vepudu karam and coconut. Fry till you get a nice aroma for about 3 minutes. Then add peanuts and fry for another 2 mins. 8. Serve beerakaya fry with rice, sambar or rasam

title: Beerakaya Pachadi Recipe (Ridge Gourd Chutney)
ingredients: 3 cups ridge gourd, 3.5 red chilies, 0.5 cup tomato, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 medium garlic clove, 1 pinch turmeric, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon tamarind paste, 0.75 tablespoon oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch mustard seeds, 1 pinch cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 teaspoon channa dal, 0.25 tsp urad dal, 1 garlic clove, 1 red chili
instructions: 1. Optional - Spray vinegar all over the ridge gourd and sprinkle some salt or baking soda. Let rest for 5 to 7 mins and then scrub them with a vegetable brush. 2. Rinse under running water and peel the ridges. Cut off both the ends. Cube them to 1 inch pieces. 3. Dry roast chana dal, urad dal and dried red chilies on a medium flame till you get a nice aroma. Add the cumin seeds and toast for a minute. Transfer this to a plate to cool. 4. Heat a wide pan with half of the oil. If you want to use green chili as a substitute to red chili, fry them in oil until blistered. 5. Add ridge gourd and saute till they are almost cooked. You can fry on high for the first 3 mins and reduce the flame. Cook covered until they soften. Transfer these to a plate to cool. 6. Add a teaspoon of oil to the same pan and add garlic, tomatoes, salt and turmeric. Saute till the tomatoes turn mushy and lose the raw flavor. 7. Transfer the roasted lentils, red chilies and cumin to the grinder and powder the, Add the rest of the ingredients - gourd, tomatoes and chilies. If you want you can also add little tamarind paste. 8. Grind to a coarse or smooth pachadi to your liking. Taste test and adjust salt. 9. Add oil to the pan for tempering and heat. Add all the ingredients except curry leaves and hing. Fry till golden. 10. Add curry leaves and fry till crisp. Add hing. Pour this tempering over the beerakaya pachadi.

title: Beerakaya Pappu
ingredients: 0.25 cup Toor dal, 0.25 cup Moong dal, 1.5 green chilies, 0.25 cup onion, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 1 tsp Tamarind, 1.5 cups ridge gourd, 0.75 tsp ginger garlic paste, Salt, 1.5 tbsp Oil, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 2.5 garlic cloves, 0.25 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 red chilies, 0.5 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder
instructions: 1. Wash the ridge gourd well and peel the skin. Cube it to 1 inch pieces. 2. Add dal to a pot or pressure cooker. Rinse it well a few times and drain the water. Pour 1½ cups water. 3. Then add ridge gourd, onions, green chilies, tamarind, tomatoes & ginger garlic paste. Cover with a lid. 4. Pressure cook for 3 whistles if cooking in a pot in pot method. I usually cook directly in the cooker for 2 whistles. 5. Once the pressure reduces. open the lid & mash the dal to smooth. Add turmeric and salt. 6. Adjust the consistency by adding water if desired. Bring it to a boil if you add more water. 7. Heat oil in a pan for tadka. Add red chilies, mustard, and cumin when they begin to splutter, add garlic and curry leaves. Fry till the curry leaves turn crisp. Turn off the stove. Add hing, turmeric & red chilli powder. 8. Quickly mix & pour this over the dal. Off the heat and mix well. Serve beerakaya pappu with rice ghee and pickle.

title: Beerakaya Pappu Recipe | Turai Dal | Ridge Gourd Dal
ingredients: 0.5 cup toor dal, 0.5 cup moong dal, 1 ridge gourd turai beerakaya, 1 tomato, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp ghee, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 chilli, 6 cloves garlic, 0.5 onion, 2 red chilli, few curry leaves, pinch hing, 1 tsp red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
