instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker, take ½ cup toor dal and ½ cup moong dal. Make sure to soak the toor dal so that it cooks faster. 2. Also, add 1 ridge gourd, 1 tomato, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp ghee and 3 cup water. 3. Mix well, pressure cook for 4 whistles or until the dal is cooked well. 4. Mash the dal slightly, adjusting the consistency as required. 5. In a pan heat 2 tbsp oil and 1 tbsp ghee. Add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 tsp urad dal, ½ tsp cumin. Splutter the tempering. 6. Also add 1 chilli, 6 cloves garlic, ½ onion and saute till the onions soften slightly. 7. Now add 2 dried red chilli, few curry leaves, pinch thing and splutter the tempering. 8. Keeping the flame on low, add 1 tsp red chilli powder and ½ tsp salt. Mix well. 9. Transfer the tempering over the dal and mix gently. Cook for 3 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 10. Finally, add 2 tbsp coriander to Beerakaya Pappu Recipe and enjoy with hot steamed rice.

title: Beetroot chutney (Beetroot pachadi)
ingredients: 1.25 cups beetroot, 1.5 green chilies, 2.5 red chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 1 pod garlic, 1.5 tsp chana dal, 1.5 tsp urad dal, salt as needed, 1 tbsp oil, lemon juice
instructions: 1. Heat a pan with oil. When the oil, turns hot add the urad dal and chana da or peanuts. Fry them until golden and aromatic. Then add curry leaves, garlic, red chilies and green chilies. 2. Fry until the leaves turn crispy. You can set aside few curry leaves, dal and spices from the final garnishing. Set aside to cool. 3. Fry the beetroot in the pan for 2 to 5 minutes until slightly tender. You can also cover and cook on a low heat until tender. 4. Cool roasted ingredients. Blend everything well without adding water. The moisture in the beets is enough to blend it to a chutney. 5. Scrape the sides and add lemon juice or tamarind. Blend once more until the chutney is slightly smooth or coarse to suit your liking. 6. Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with the tempering that was set aside. Serve beetroot chutney with rice, paratha or any breakfast.

title: Beetroot Curry (Beetroot Sabzi)
ingredients: 2 cups beetroot, 0.25 cup raw coconut, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoon ghee, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida, 2 green chili, 1 red chili, 2 garlic, 1 teaspoon skinned black gram, 1 teaspoon bengal gram
instructions: 1. Wash 2 medium sized beetroots well under running water and peel them. Cut them to quarters. Grate using a large hole grater or chop finely which ever is comfortable. You can also use a food processor to grate or a onion chopper to chop. 2. On a medium flame, heat 1½ tablespoons oil or ghee in a pan. Add 3. ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds ½ teaspoon cumin seeds (jeera) 1 sprig curry leaves (pat dry first) 1 dried red chili broken – optional 1 teaspoon skinned black gram (urad dal, optional) 1 teaspoon bengal gram (chana dal, optional). Let the lentils turn golden & aromatic, then add 4. 2 green chilies chopped or slit (or 1 tsp chili flakes) 5. 2 garlic cloves or ¾ inch ginger (peeled, crushed or fine chopped). Saute for a minute and add 1 pinch of hing (asafoetida). 6. Add grated beets and ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. Saute until they are lightly cooked. If your beets are not juicy and are dry, you can cover and cook on a low flame. Usually chopped beetroot takes longer to cook. Cover and cook them on a low heat until tender. If they dry out, simply sprinkle water as needed and continue to cook. 7. Lastly sprinkle salt as required and ¼ cup coconut (raw/fresh frozen or coconut flakes). Sprinkle lemon juice or 1 to 2 teaspoons spice powder like idli podi or flaxseed podi if you want. Taste test and add more salt, coconut or lemon juice to taste. Serve beetroot curry or sabzi with rice or chapathi. It goes well with rice and dal.

title: Beetroot Halwa Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups beetroot, 1.5 cup milk, 0.29166666666666663 cup sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 tablespoon ghee, 12.5 cashews
instructions: 1. Wash beetroots under running water. Peel the skin and grate them. A food processor eases the job of grating. You can also grate with a hand held grater. 2. Heat a pan with ghee. Fry cashew nuts until golden and crunchy. Remove to a plate and keep aside. 3. To the same pan, add beetroot and fry until it turns aromatic for about 3 to 4 minutes. 4. Reduce the flame. Pour 1½ cups milk or condensed milk + ¼ cup milk as per your choice. 5. Give a good stir and cook covered for a 3 to 4 minutes on the lowest heat. This helps the beets to release all of the moisture. 6. Open the lid and simmer until most of the liquid evaporates and the halwa turns thick. Beets should be softened by now. 7. Add sugar and cardamom powder. Make sure the beetroot is soft cooked before adding sugar. Sugar melts and moisture is released making the halwa gooey again. 8. Cook till the moisture evaporates. If you prefer a dry halwa, continue to cook further. 9. Transfer beetroot halwa to serving bowls and garnish with nuts. Best served chilled during summers and warm during winters.

title: Beetroot paratha
ingredients: 0.75 cup beetroot, 1.5 cups atta, salt, 1 clove garlic, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 green chili, 0.75 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp garam masala, water, ghee
instructions: 1. Wash and chop beets. Add them to a small pan and sprinkle 1 tbsp water. You can also skip these steps and use grated beetroot directly to make the dough. 2. Cover and cook just for 2 to 3 mins. They have to become slightly tender and soft. 3. Do not overcook. Cool this. 4. Add garlic, ginger, salt and chilli or pepper to a blender jar. You can also add little garam masala if you like. Then transfer the cooled beets. 5. Blend everything until smooth. I did not add water. If the mixture is dry then you may need to sprinkle 1 to 2 tbsp of water. 6. Measure atta to a mixing bowl. Transfer the beetroot puree. 7. Mix the flour to make a dough by adding water as needed. Drizzle little oil and knead it. 8. If you do not have time to rest, then knead it well until pliable and soft. I do not rest instead knead it well. 9. Divide the dough to 6 to 8 parts. 10. Sprinkle little flour over the rolling area. Flatten a ball and roll it to paratha. Make them slightly thicker than chapati. If you roll them very thin they turn hard. 11. Heat a tawa until hot. Dust off the excess flour from the paratha. Then transfer to the tawa. When you see bubbles over it, flip it. 12. Press with a spatula to puff the parathas. 13. Toast the beetroot paratha until golden to brown spots appear. Apply some ghee. If making for lunch box, smear some oil. 14. Place a kitchen tissue in a plate. Then stack the parathas to keep them soft. Serve beetroot paratha with curry, dal or chutney. 15. To remove the pungent flavor cook beetroot with 1 tbsp water just for 2 to 3 mins until tender or soft. 16. Add the flour to a mixing bowl along with the beetroot puree or gratings. 17. Mix to make a dough using water as needed. 18. Next pour oil and knead the dough until smooth and pliable. If you time you can rest the dough for 30 mins. 19. Sprinkle little flour on the rolling board. Flatten a ball and sprinkle little more flour. 20. Heat a griddle or tawa. 21. Smear oil or ghee over it and cook until golden to light brown spots appear on them. 22. Spread a kitchen tissue on a plate and remove the paratha to it. Similarly toast all the parathas and stack them. 23. Serve beetroot paratha with chutney, dal or curry.

title: Beetroot Rice Pulao
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 1.75 cups water for cooker, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1 onion, 1 green chili, 0.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.29166666666666663 cup green peas, 1 beetroot, 0.3333333333333333 cup potato, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon mint, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 star anise, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves 3 cloves ((laung)), 2 cardamoms
instructions: 1. ½ tablespoon ginger garlic paste (or ½ tablespoon each fine chopped) 2. ¼ to ⅓ cup green peas (or ½ cup boiled/canned chana/ chickpeas) 3. ⅓ cup potato (1 small, chopped, optional) 4. 1 tablespoon coriander leaves finely chopped 5. 1 tablespoon mint leaves /pudina finely chopped (optional) 6. Heat 1½ tablespoons oil in a pressure cooker or pot. Add 1 bay leaf, ½ teaspoon jeera or shahi jeera, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves and 2 cardamoms. I also like to add star anise & a strand of mace. If you like you can use it. 7. When they begin to sizzle, add the onions and chilies. Saute them until lightly golden. For kids you can deseed chili and use or substitute with red chili powder. If using red chili, use along with veggies later. 8. Add ½ tablespoon garlic ginger paste and fry until the raw smell goes off, for about 30 to 60 seconds. 9. Add chopped potatoes, beetroot and peas. I also like to add few coriander and mint leaves. If using chili powder, you can use now. Add turmeric as well, i added to the ginger garlic, so did not use here. 10. Fry everything well for 2 minutes. 11. Pour water and add salt. Stir and check the salt. Water should be slightly salty. 12. Add soaked and drained rice. 13. Cover the lid and pressure cook for 1 whistle on a medium flame. If cooking in a pot, simply bring the water to a boil and turn down the heat. Cook covered on a low heat until done. 14. When the pressure drops on its own, open the lid. Fluff up the rice. Beets get settled at the bottom of the pot. Pulao was yellow due to the addition of turmeric. When fluffed up and mixed, the color of beets spreads to the rice. Beetroot rice can be served with a simple raita. 15. For the kids’ lunch box, I usually pack a vegetable side like a salad or a dry curry with this pulao. This time it was the corn carrot salad. To make the salad, I cooked corn lightly and set aside to cool. Added the grated carrots, coriander leaves, pepper powder and olive oil. Tossed everything well. My kid sprinkles salt just before eating.

title: Beetroot salad
ingredients: 1 cup beetroot, 0.5 cup moong dal, 0.375 cup coconut, 1.5 green chilies, 1.5 tablespoons Lemon juice, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 carrot, 0.5 cup radish, 0.25 cup cucumber, 2 tablespoons onions
instructions: 1. Wash moong dal thoroughly 3 to 4 times and soak for at least 1 hour in enough water. Moong dal has to be soaked well without any hard center. Check by eating a few lentils. If they are hard then soak them for a little longer. 2. Wash thoroughly and drain off the water completely to a colander. Spread it in the colander so most of the moisture dries up. 3. Rinse beetroot under running water and pat dry with a kitchen tissue/ paper. 4. Peel it and cut to quarters. 5. Grate it using a grater or a food processor with medium large holes. If you fine grate, beets will release lots of moisture. (check my picture) 6. Add it to a mixing bowl along with lentils. Ensure there is no excess moisture in the lentils. 7. Add salt, coconut, lemon juice and coriander leaves. Toss well and serve right away without resting. If the salad sits on the counter, it will release juices. So add salt and lemon juice only when you are ready to serve. 8. Serve beetroot salad as a side in a meal.

title: Beetroot Thoran
ingredients: 25 grams beetroot, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons coconut oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 cup onions, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon Salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 cup coconut, 1.5 green chilies, 2 garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds
instructions: 1. Wash beetroot thoroughly and peel it. Grate it using a medium grater or chop very finely. If you have a food processor you can use it. 2. Add coconut, garlic, green chili and cumin to a blender jar and blend it coarsely. 3. Heat a pan with oil and add mustard. When they begin to crackle, add curry leaves. 4. Saute for few seconds and add chopped onions. Fry until they turn pink or transparent. 5. Add grated beetroot and turmeric. Sprinkle salt and begin to stir fry for 1 to 2 mins. 6. Cover and cook until tender on a low heat. If the beetroot is not tender, you may sprinkle little water and cook. 7. Add the coconut, garlic mixture and mix well. Saute for 2 mins on a medium to low flame. Taste test and adjust salt. 8. Serve beetroot thoran with steamed rice or chapati.

title: Bellam Undrallu | Bellam Kudumulu Recipe | Vinayaka Chavithi Recipes
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour, 0.75 cup water, 2 green cardamoms, 1 cup jaggery, 1 tsp ghee, 0.25 cup coconut, 1 tbsp chana dal
instructions: 1. To make rice flour, firstly wash rice and drain in a colander. Soaking for about 2 hours helps to make a fine powder quickly. So i like to soak for 2 hours. Spread the drained rice on a clean cloth. When it dries up completely, process either to rava or flour in a mixer jar. Set this aside. This can be done the previous day. If storing in a air tight jar, make sure the flour is not moist. 2. Add grated or powdered jaggery to ¾ cup water, melt it completely. 3. Filter to a pan or pot to remove stones or debris. 4. Bring the jaggery water to a boil, Let it boil for about 2 minutes. Switch off the stove. 5. Add the rice flour and cardamom powder slowly stirring constantly to prevent lumps. 6. Add coconut and stir well. 7. Break up lumps if any quickly and form a soft dough. If needed add a bit more of hot water and mix. 8. Add ghee and mix it once more. You must get a soft dough now. 9. Shape these to undrallu (small balls), kudumulu (large round balls) or like discs to suit your liking. Place them in a wide utensil or idli plates for steaming. 10. Add water to a steamer and bring it to a boil on a high flame. You can use a pressure cooker, idli cooker or a rice cooker or just a pot with a small vent for the steam to escape. 11. Place the balls in a wide bowl or a idli stand. When the water comes to a rolling boil, Place the bowl in the steamer and steam for 7 minutes on a high flame. Over steaming can make them hard.

title: Bendakaya Pulusu
ingredients: 25 grams bendakayalu, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 cup onion, 1 green chili, 1 medium tomato, 1 medium potato, 2 tablespoons Tamarind, 1.5 tablespoons jaggery, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves with tender stalks, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.125 teaspoon methi seeds powder, 0.75 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak tamarind in ⅓ cup hot water. Once cool, squeeze up and strain to remove the pulp. 2. Chop onions, garlic and tomatoes. Keep them aside. Rinse ladies finger under running water. Wipe them dry with a clean kitchen cloth. Chop them to 1 inch sized pieces. You can use a kitchen tissue in between to wipe off the sticky part from the knife. 3. Heat a pan with oil. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and methi seeds if using. When they begin to crackle, add curry leaves, green chilies, garlic and onions. 4. Saute until the onions turn soft and transparent for 5 to 6 mins. Add the tomatoes, turmeric and salt. Cook till the tomatoes break down and turn mushy. 5. Transfer ladies finger and potatoes. Stir fry on medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes. 6. Add red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder and methi powder. Mix and stir fry for 2 mins. 7. Stir in the tamarind water and cook covered for 3 to 4 mins. 8. Pour 1½ water and bring to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat and cook on a low flame till the ladies finger and potatoes are soft and tender. It takes me about 15 mins. If your pulusu is too thick to your liking, pour half cup hot water and cook down. 9. Stir in jaggery and coriander leaves. Taste test to adjust salt, tamarind and jaggery. It should typically taste tangy and slightly hot with mild sweetness. 10. Serve bendakaya pulusu with hot short grain rice and plain pappu / mudda pappu.

title: bendekai gojju recipe | bendekai kayirasa | bendekayi gojju - karnataka style
ingredients: 1 tbsp oil, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 0.25 tsp methi fenugreek, 1 tbsp sesame til, 5 red chilli, few curry leaves, 0.5 cup coconut, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 red chilli, pinch hing asafoetida, few curry leaves, 15 okra bendekayi, 1.5 cup tamarind extract, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp jaggery, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tbsp oil and add 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, ¼ tsp methi, 1 tbsp sesame, 5 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 2. saute on low flame until the spices turn golden brown. 3. cool completely and transfer to a blender. 4. also, add ½ cup coconut and ½ cup water. 5. blend to smooth paste adding more water if required. keep aside. 6. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and splutter 1 tsp mustard, 1 dried red chilli, pinch hing, few curry leaves. 7. add 15 okra and stir fry for 2 minutes. 8. now add 1½ cup tamarind extract, ½ tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp jaggery and 1 tsp salt. 9. mix well, cover and boil for 10 minutes or until bhindi is cooked well. 10. further, add in the prepared masala paste and mix well. 11. add ½ cup water adjusting consistency as required. 12. boil on a medium flame for 5 minutes or until the masala paste is cooked well. 13. finally, enjoy bendekayi gojju with hot steamed rice or chapathi.

title: besan bhindi recipe | besan wali bhindi | rajasthani bhindi recipe
ingredients: 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 1 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 bhindi okra, 3 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp fennel saunf, pinch of hing asafoetida, 2 tbsp onion, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 tbsp besan gram flour, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small mixing bowl take ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp aamchur and ½ tsp salt. 2. mix well making sure the spices are combined well. 3. now add sliced bhindi and mix well. 4. make sure to coat the spices well and rest for 15 minutes. 5. in a kadai heat 3 tbsp oil and splutter 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp fennel and pinch of hing. 6. further saute 2 tbsp onion and ½ tsp ginger garlic paste. 7. also saute 2 tbsp besan on low flame for 5 minutes or till the besan turns aromatic. 8. add in prepared bhindi masala mix and saute for a minute. 9. cover and simmer for 10 minutes or till bhindi gets cooked completely. 10. make sure the bhindi turns dark and cooke completely. 11. further add ¼ tsp garam masala and 1 tbsp coriander leaves. mix well. 12. finally, serve besan bhindi masala recipe with rice / roti.

title: Besan bread toast recipe on tawa under 15 minutes
ingredients: 2 slices bread, 1 tbsp ghee, 5 tbsps gram flour, 0.5 tsp carom seeds, 2 pinch turmeric, 0.1875 tsp red chili powder, Pinch hing, Salt, 0.25 tsp ginger, 1 green chili, 2 tbsp onions, 2 tbsp tomatoes, 1 tbsp coriander leaves, 3 tbsp water
instructions: 1. Add flour, ajwain, salt, turmeric, chilli powder to a mixing bowl. You can also add a tbsp rice flour or semolina for a slight crispy texture. 2. Add onions, tomatoes, green chilies and coriander leaves. 3. Add water just enough to make a thick batter. Mix well. 4. This is the batter consistency I usually follow. Make sure it is not thin or dripping other wise the bread turns soggy and even it takes very long to cook. 5. Heat the pan and spread oil evenly. A smaller pan works better since the oil doesn’t go off to the sides. When the pan is hot enough, turn on the flame to medium. Dip only one side of the bread in the batter. Place it on the tawa and cook until firm. 6. When the base of the bread begins to leave the pan, meaning it is done. Spoon the batter on the top side as well and flip it. 7. Cook on both the sides on a low to medium heat until cooked and golden. 8. The color difference is due to the harsh sun light. Cut the bread to halves before serving. Serve besan bread toast while hot. 9. Make a batter by mixing flour, onions, tomatoes, carom seeds, chili, ginger, coriander leaves, red chili powder and salt with enough water to make a thick batter. 10. Heat a pan with oil. 11. On a low to medium heat, toast until firm and bread comes off from the pan. Spoon the batter on the top side or the other side, just before flipping it. Do it carefully. Toast on the other side as well. 12. Repeat frying on both the sides on a low flame until the batter is completely cooked. The toast must turn golden. 13. Serve besan bread toast right away.

title: Besan Burfi Recipe
ingredients: 21 grams besan, 2 tablespoon semolina, 12 grams ghee, 0.75 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 grams sugar, 0.5 cup water, 12 almonds
instructions: 1. Grease a 7 inch pan and line it with a parchment paper. You can also use a steel plate. Slice handful of almonds or pistachios and keep aside. 2. Heat ghee in a large heavy bottom pan. Add semolina and let it fry for about 50 to 60 seconds. 3. Then add besan and mix well with the ghee. Begin to roast stirring continuously on a medium heat for 3 to 4 mins. At this stage the mixture will be thick. 4. Next after 3 to 4 mins, reduce the flame/heat to lowest (as low as possible) & continue to stir. As it gets fried, the mixture will turn thinner and the ghee begins to release from the besan. 5. Continue to fry stirring constantly until besan turns deep golden color and begins to smell aromatic. After frying for 20 mins, the color of the besan changes to deep golden, meaning it is done roasting. It will also turn fluffy, light, airy and very aromatic. 6. Note: Depending on the kind of besan and ghee used, your mixture may turn runny but it is not essential. If your besan is not runny at this stage, don't panic as some times it will never turn runny. It may remain in a solid or semi solid state. 7. Remove it from the stove and place it on the counter. Continue to stir for another 3 mins as we don't want the mixture to burn. Cool little besan and taste test it, it should not taste raw and should be nutty. Set this aside to cool down slightly. 8. Add sugar and pour water to a pot. Stir well and dissolve it. Begin to cook on a medium heat. 9. Let it bubble and boil until the syrup reaches a 1 string consistency. 10. Lower the flame completely and check the consistency. Take ¼ teaspoon sugar syrup from the center of the pot and cool it. 11. Take a few drops of this in between your thumb and forefinger. When you separate the fingers, you should see a single long thread. Turn off the stove immediately. 12. Check this 2 to 3 times to ensure it is right. Check the step-by-step photos above. Cool the sugar syrup a bit to slightly hot temperature. 13. Ensure besan has cooled down to slightly hot or warm temperature but the ghee is not solidified. The syrup is slightly hot & not boiling hot. If you put your finger in the syrup, you should feel it is hot and not warm. Check the picture in the step by step photo instructions so you don't go wrong at this stage. The syrup has to be all liquid and still hot. 14. Add cardamom powder to the besan and then pour half of the sugar syrup. 15. Quickly mix both of them and then pour the rest of the syrup. Mix well. You have to be quick at this stage as the mixture will begin to thicken fast. 16. Immediately pour to the greased tray. Level it with the spatula and sprinkle chopped nuts. Press down with the spatula so the nuts stick to the besan burfi. 17. It will begin to set immediately as it cools down depending on the temperature in your home. Mine took about 10 mins to cool down and set. Cut the burfi when it is slightly warm. 18. Transfer the besan burfi to a air tight jar. Store at room temperature and use up within 2 weeks.

title: Besan Chilla Recipe (Cheela)
ingredients: 1 cup besan, 0.5 teaspoon ajwain, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 3 tablespoons onions, 3 tbsps tomatoes, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 handful coriander leaves, 0.8333333333333333 cup water, 2 teaspoons oil
instructions: 1. Add besan, ajwain, turmeric and salt to a mixing bowl. Mix everything well. 2. Add ginger, tomatoes, onions, coriander leaves and chilies. Pour enough water and make a batter of thick but pourable consistency. If needed add more water as needed. 3. Heat a pan and grease it lightly with few drops of oil. 4. Allow it to turn hot and reduce the flame to medium. 5. Mix the batter well from the bottom. Take a ladle of batter and pour it in the center. Quickly spread it to a round cheela. 6. Drizzle some oil around the edges and the center. 7. Allow to cook until the edges leave the pan. Flip it to the other side. 8. Press down the edges with a spatula. Fry until it is cooked completely and gets golden spots. 9. Remove to a serving plate. To make the next one ensure the pan is hot enough and not very hot otherwise they will brown too quickly. 10. Serve besan cheela with some tea or chutney.

title: Besan Chilla Recipe | Paneer Stuffed Besan Ka Cheela
ingredients: 1.5 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rava suji, 0.25 cup curd, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 cup water, oil, 1 cup paneer, 1 carrot, 0.5 capsicum, 1 tomato, 3 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixer jar take 1½ cup besan, ¼ cup rava, ¼ cup curd, ¼ tsp turmeric and ¾ tsp salt. 2. add 1 cup water and blend to smooth batter. 3. pour the batter into a large bowl and rest for 10 minutes. 4. meanwhile, to prepare the stuffing, in a bowl take 1 cup paneer, 1 carrot, ½ capsicum, 1 tomato, 3 tbsp sweet corn, and 2 tbsp coriander. 5. add ½ tsp salt and mix well. 6. paneer stuffing is ready. 7. to prepare the chilla, heat the dosa pan and pour the chilla batter. 8. spread as thin as possible. 9. once the chilla is cooked from the top, spread 1 tsp oil. 10. cover and roast on low flame until the chilla is cooked well. 11. now spread prepared paneer stuffing uniformly and cook for some time. 12. roll the chilla tight keeping the stuffing intact. 13. finally, enjoy Besan Chilla Recipe with green chutney and tomato sauce.

title: Besan Ladoo Recipe (Besan Ke Laddu)
ingredients: 2 cups besan, 0.5 cup ghee, 1 cup sugar, 0.6666666666666666 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 3.5 tablespoons cashews
instructions: 1. Powder Sugar: Add sugar and cardamom powder to a grinder jar and grind to a very fine powder. Keep aside. 2. Fry Nuts: Heat a pan with 1½ tablespoons ghee and fry the cashews until lightly golden. Remove to a small bowl. 3. Roast Besan: Add besan and roast on a medium flame for 5 to 6 mins, until it begins to change the color & smell slightly aromatic. Reduce the heat to lowest and add rest of the ghee. 4. Deep Roast: Mix well and keep roasting on a low flame. stirring continuously to prevent burning. Besan absorbs the ghee and turns to a mass at this stage. Just continue to roast, stirring constantly, until it begins to turn deep golden in color and aromatic. 5. Finishing Stage: Turn off the stove when your kitchen is filled with a aroma indicating the flour is well roasted. It also turns airy and fluffy. It took me 25 mins to reach this stage. Timing may vary depending on the kind of pan and intensity of heat used. 6. Cool Down: Remove the pan quickly from the stove and stir for another 2 to 3 mins to cool down. Taste test little to make sure it is nutty and not raw. 7. Prepare the Mixture: Cool down the besan completely and mix in the powdered sugar (start with lesser for low sweet) until well combined. (see pictures in the post) 8. Shape Besan Ladoo: Stir in the fried cashews. Pinch off portions of this to your palm and press down with your fingers to shape them round like balls. (Check video) 9. Storage: Store besan ladoo in a air tight jar at room temperature and use within 3 weeks. Or Refrigerate up to 2 to 3 months. Reheat them in microwave for 15 to 20 seconds or in the oven at 80 C or 160 F for a few minutes until they become warm.

title: Besan Ladoo Recipe | Besan Ke Laddu - Halwai Style
ingredients: 0.75 cup ghee, 2 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rava semolina, 2 water, 1 cup powdered sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp pumpkin seeds
instructions: 1. In a kadai pan heat ¾ cup ghee and add 2 cup besan. 2. Cook until the besan absorbs all the ghee. 3. Add 2 tbsp rava and continue to roast till the besan turns aromatic. 4. Keep roasting on low flame till the ghee releases from the sides and turns golden brown. 5. Sprinkle 2 tbsp water and mix well. This helps the besan and rava to turn ga rainy texture. 6. Cook till the mixture turns aromatic. 7. Transfer to the plate and cool completely. 8. Once the besan mixture is cooled, add 1 cup powdered sugar, ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 9. Also, add 2 tbsp of roasted pumpkin seeds and mix well. 10. Shape the ladoo and garnish it with pistachios. 11. Finally, enjoy Besan Ladoo Recipe tastes great for a week.

title: Besan Toast | Besan Bread Toast in Tawa - Just 10 Mins
ingredients: 1 cup besan, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, Pinch of hing, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 1 tomato, 3 beans, 0.5 capsicum, 2 green chilli, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 6 bread slices, Butter for roasting
instructions: 1. In a bowl take besan, turmeric, cumin powder, chaat masala, chilli flakes, hing and salt. 2. Add water gradually and prepare a smooth, flowing batter without lumps. 3. Whisk the batter well for a smooth consistency. 4. Add onion, carrot, tomato, beans, capsicum, green chilli and coriander. 5. Mix well until everything combines evenly. 6. Dip the bread slice into the batter and coat well on both sides. 7. Top extra vegetables over the bread for better texture and flavour. 8. Heat a tawa and spread a little butter. 9. Place the coated bread on a hot tawa and roast on a medium flame. 10. Apply a little butter on top and flip gently. 11. Roast both sides until golden, crisp and crunchy. 12. Finally, serve Besan Bread Toast Recipe hot with green chutney, ketchup or masala chai.

title: Bhapa Doi Recipe | Bengali Steamed Yogurt Cheese Cake
ingredients: 3 cup curd, 1 cup mango pulp, 1 cup condensed milk, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, pinch saffron food colour, pistachios
instructions: 1. Take 3 cup curd and squeeze the water from it. 2. Rest for 2 hours or until the water drips out completely. 3. Take the hung curd in a bowl and whisk it smooth and creamy. 4. Add 1 cup mango pulp, 1 cup condensed milk and mix well. 5. Also add ½ tsp cardamom powder and a pinch of saffron food colour. Mix well. 6. Transfer the mixture to the bowl lined with baking paper. 7. Steam for 20 minutes, or until the curd thickens completely. 8. Refrigerate for 4 hours or until it sets completely. 9. Finally, cut the Mango Bhappa Doi Recipe and garnish with pistachios.

title: Bharwa Baingan Recipe
ingredients: 275 grams eggplants, 3 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch mustard, 1 pinch hing, 1 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 13 cashew nuts, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon saunf powder, 1.125 teaspoon red chilli powder, 2 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon amchur powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric
instructions: 1. Heat a deep pan with 2 tablespoon oil. Add the cumin seeds. When they sizzle, add the onions. Saute on a medium heat until light golden, for about 8 to 9 minutes. 2. Stir in the ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute, until the raw smell goes away. Add the tomatoes and add salt. Saute until the tomatoes break down and lose the raw flavor. 3. Add all the ground spices and saute for 2 to 3 mins until aromatic. Stir in the cashew powder or roasted besan and cook for a minute. Taste test and adjust salt or spice to preference. 4. Turn off and cool down, while you prep up the baingan. Pour 3 cups water a bowl and stir in half teaspoon salt. 5. Rinse the eggplants well and slit them lengthwise to make 4 parts keeping the stem intact. Add them to the salted water, while you prepare all the brinjals. 6. Remove each eggplant from the water and spoon the prepared masala into it and keep aside. 7. Heat the same pan with 1 tablespoon of oil. Add mustard seeds (optional) and let them splutter. Add the hing and stuffed eggplants. 8. Fry them in oil until the skin discolors stirring often for 3 mins. Add the remaining masala and pour ¾ cup water. Mix well to make a gravy. 9. Cover and simmer on a low flame until the eggplants are fully cooked from inside and gravy turns thick. Keep stirring in between and add more water if needed. 10. Taste test and add more salt or chaat masala if you want. Garnish with fined chopped coriander leaves. Serve bharwa baingan with rice or roti.

title: bhatura recipe | punjabi bhature recipe | bhatura for chole bhatura
ingredients: 2 cups maida all-purpose flour plain flour, 1 tbsp rava semolina, 1 tsp sugar, salt to taste, 0.5 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp baking soda cooking soda, 2 tsp oil, 1 cup curd yogurt, 0.5 cup maida all-purpose flour plain flour, oil for deep frying, full recipe of chole
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take maida. 2. to that add rava, sugar and salt. 3. also add baking powder and baking soda and mix well. 4. furthermore, add oil and rub with the flour. 5. also add curd and start to knead dough. 6. knead and punch the dough well for atleast 5 minutes. 7. further, grease the dough with oil. 8. cover with moist cloth and rest for atleast 2 hours. 9. further, roll and get to a long shape. pinch the dough into medium sized balls. 10. then make balls between your palms. 11. finally, roll the dough evenly into circles using rolling pin. roll neither too thin nor thick. 12. firstly, heat oil in a deep frying pan or kadai. when the oil is sufficiently hot, add one bhatura. 13. and, press with the spoon to puff up. 14. also once they begin to puff, splash oil over the bhatura till they puff completely. 15. flip over and fry the bhatura till golden brown all over. 16. then drain the poori into tissue paper to remove excess oil. fry all batura same way. 17. finally, serve bhatura with chole curry. 18. full recipe for chole masala

title: Bhatura Recipe, How to Make Bhature
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons fine semolina, 1 tablespoon oil, 4 tablespoons curd, 1 teaspoon Eno, 0.5 cup warm water, 1.5 cups oil
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, mix together all-purpose flour, semolina and salt. 2. Next add curd and oil. 3. Mix all of these until the oil and yogurt incorporates well into the flour. 4. Then sprinkle eno (or baking powder & baking soda) and give a good stir again. 5. Pour ¼ cup warm water and begin to mix to form a dough. 6. Add more warm water as needed and make a soft dough. 7. Knead the dough very well until soft. If using a stand mixer, then knead it on a medium speed for 5 minutes. 8. When you press down the dough, it should dent meaning it is soft. If you pull the dough, it should be elastic and stretchy. This is what gives the bhatura it's chewy texture. If the dough hasn't got this texture, knead it a little longer. 9. Smear few drops of oil. If you have used eno then do not rest the dough. You have to make the bhaturas immediately. If you have used baking soda and baking powder, you can cover and rest the dough for 2 to 4 hours in a warm place. 10. Divide the dough to 8 parts to make medium sized or 5 parts to make large bhaturas, 11. Smoothen the balls to make them crack free. If you find cracks just join them and smoothen the dough balls. 12. Smear oil over the balls and keep them covered. 13. Grease your rolling surface well and place a dough ball. 14. Pour few drops of oil and flatten it slightly. 15. Begin to roll the bhatura to a oval or round shape as you desire. 16. Do not remove the bhatura from the rolling surface. Instead turn your rolling board and roll the bhaturas. (check the video) 17. You will see the bhatura keeps bouncing back. So you need to keep rolling them slightly bigger. 18. Roll them big or small depending on the size of your kadai. 19. You can roll about 5 large bhaturas before you proceed to the next step. 20. Heat oil in a deep and wide kadai that suits the size of your bhatura. 21. When the oil is hot enough, drop a small piece of the dough in the oil. 22. The dough has to rise well. This is the right temperature for frying. 23. Gently slide in the bhatura from the sides of the kadai. 24. When it comes half way to the surface, press down gently with a spatula to help it puff up completely. 25. Then check if the bottom part is cooked, crisp and golden then flip it. 26. Flip it again and fry until crisp and golden. 27. Remove the bhatura to a plate and then immediately serve with chole, sliced onions & lemon. 28. Continue to fry the rest while you make the rest of the bhaturas. 29. Do note that the oil has to be hot enough for the subsequent bhaturas as well.

title: Bhel Puri Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups puffed rice, 2 tablespoons onions, 3.5 tablespoons tomatoes, 3.5 tablespoons boiled potato, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon chaat masala, 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts, 1 papdis, 0.25 cup nylon sev, 1.5 pinches salt, 1 tablespoons green chutney, 2.5 tablespoons tamarind paste, 3.5 tablespoons jaggery, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon roasted jeera powder, 0.125 teaspoon ginger powder, 0.125 teaspoon fennel powder, 4 tablespoons water, 1.5 tablespoons red chilli powder, 4.5 garlic cloves, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon cumin powder, 2.5 tablespoons water, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.75 cup coriander leaves, 0.25 cup mint leaves, 1.5 spicy green chilli, 9 green chilies, 0.25 inch ginger, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 1 tablespoon roasted gram, 2 teaspoons lemon juice, 2 tablespoons water, 0.25 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. To a small sauce pan, add tamarind paste, jaggery, salt, red chilli powder, ginger powder, fennel powder, cumin powder and salt. Pour water and make a smooth mixture. 2. Bring this to a rolling boil and reduce the heat. Let simmer until thick. (check the step by step pics for consistency) 3. Cool the tamarind chutney completely. 4. Add ¾ cup coriander leaves, ¼ cup mint or pudina leaves,1 to 2 green chilies , 8 to 10 less hot chilies (optional), ¼ inch ginger piece, ¼ teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon jeera powder, 1 tablespoon fried gram, 2 teaspoons lemon juice & 2 tablespoons water to a blender jar. 5. Blend everything until smooth. Scrape the sides and pour more water only as required and blend to a thick chutney. 6. Taste and add more salt and chili if needed. green chutney is ready to use for bhel puri. 7. To a grinder jar, add red chilli powder, garlic cloves, sugar, salt and 2 to 4 tablespoons water. If you want to use red chilies, boil half cup water and soak 10 to 15 deseeded red chilies. When they soften remove them from the water and add to a blender jar. 8. Blend all the ingredients until smooth & thick, adding more water as required. Add little lemon juice. 9. Taste it and add more salt or sugar if needed. Red chutney is ready for bhel puri. 10. Optional - Boil potato just until done without making it mushy. I usually cube them and cook in a small pot until fork tender. Drain the potatoes. Cool them & air dry completely. You can also air fry the potatoes and use. 11. If your puffed rice is not crisp then dry roast them in a pan until crisp. Cool completely. 12. Add them to a mixing bowl along with boiled potatoes, onions, tomatoes, chilies & coriander leaves. Mix them up gently. 13. Then add in tamarind chutney, green chutney, chaat masala & red chutney (or red chilli powder). 14. Do not add a lot of chutney at one time, add only little by little as needed to prevent the bhel puri from turning mushy. 15. Gently mix well to coat the puffed rice with chutney. 16. Taste this and add in more chutney & salt as needed. 17. Crush the papdis and add them along with peanuts and sev. Give a good stir. 18. Garnish bhel puri with more sev and peanuts. Serve immediately.

title: Bhel Puri Recipe | Bhel Poori | Bhel Puri Chaat - 2 Ways
ingredients: 0.5 cup peanut, 0.5 cup roasted gram dal, 0.75 cup thick poha, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 4 cup murmura, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp kala namak, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp powdered sugar, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 0.5 cup spicy boondi, 0.5 cup sev, 1 papdi, 2 cup bhel mix, 0.5 onion, 0.5 tomato, 0.5 boiled potato, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 2 tbsp green chutney, 2 tbsp tamarind chutney, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 lemon
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a hot oil, place a sieve and fry ½ cup peanut on low flame. 2. Once the peanut turns crunchy, drain off and keep aside. 3. Now fry ½ cup roasted gram dal on low flame till it turns crunchy. 4. Drain off and keep aside. 5. Also, fry ¾ cup thick poha in hot oil till the poha turns crispy. 6. Drain off and keep aside. 7. In a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 8. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 9. Add 4 cup murmura and fry til the murmura turns crispy and the spices are well combined. 10. Turn off the flame and add ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp kala namak, ½ tsp salt, 2 tbsp powdered sugar and ½ tsp aamchur. 11. Add ½ cup spicy boondi, ½ cup sev, crush 10 papdi and mix well. 12. Mix well making sure all are well combined. 13. Finally, Behl Puri Mix is ready to make chaat. 14. Firstly, in a bowl take 2 cup bhel mix. 15. Add ½ onion, ½ tomato, ½ boiled potato,½ tsp chaat masala, 2 tbsp green chutney, 2 tbsp tamarind chutney. 16. Mix well making sure all the chutney are well combined. 17. Further add 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, ½ lemon and mix well. 18. Finally, enjoy Bhel Puri Recipe topped with more puri.

title: Bhindi Do Pyaza
ingredients: 3 grams bhindi, 1 medium onion, 2 tablespoon oil, 2 red chilies, 0.5 inch ginger, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon carom seeds, 2 medium onions, 1 teaspoon garlic, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 2 medium tomatoes pureed, 0.875 teaspoon kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.625 cup water, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.5 teaspoon amchur
instructions: 1. Rinse okra very well under running water and drain them completely. Wipe them with a clean kitchen towel and chop to 1 inch pieces. 2. Fine chop 2 onions. Puree 2 tomatoes in a blender. Fine chop ginger and garlic. 3. Heat a non-stick pan with 1 tablespoon oil and fry okra on a medium high flame for 5 minutes stirring often. 4. They would be partially cooked & most of the slime goes away. Remove them to a plate. 5. Pour 2 tablespoon oil to the same pan. When the oil becomes hot, add cumin and carom seeds. Take care not to burn the spices as the pan is too hot at this stage. 6. Then add onions and saute until they turn golden. 7. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for 30 to 50 seconds. Then add tomato puree. 8. Cook until most of the moisture in the tomato puree dries up. Then add red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala and salt. 9. Mix all of these and cook the masala on a low heat until a nice aroma comes out. This takes about 3 minutes. 10. Then pour ½ to ¾ cup water and cook the masala until it turns thick. 11. Transfer the fried okra, amchur and kasuri methi. I poured another 4 to 6 tablespoons water to bring it to a curry consistency. 12. Stir and cook covered until okra turns tender. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 13. In a small pan, heat 1 tbsp oil. Add the onions (layers separated) and fry them on a medium high heat until a nice sweet aroma comes out. This takes about 2 to 3 mins. Do not overcook the onions, they should remain crunchy. 14. Then add dried red chilies and stir. The chilies will begin to emit a strong pungent aroma. Turn off quickly and pour this to the bhindi do pyaza. If you fry the chilies longer, they will burn and will impart a bitter flavor. 15. Also add ginger juliennes. Stir and cover the pan. 16. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve bhindi do pyaza with basmati rice or roti.

title: Bhindi Fry | Okra Stir Fry
ingredients: 5 grams okra, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.5 tablespoons peanuts, 1.5 tablespoons desiccated coconut, 1 tablespoons white sesame seeds, 1.5 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds
instructions: 1. Choose okra or bhindi that are young and tender. Wash them well under running water. 2. Drain them to a colander. Wipe off with a clean kitchen cloth or tissues. 3. Cut off both the ends and discard. Chop them to half inch pieces. Set aside. 4. Pour oil in a pan. When the oil turns hot, add half teaspoon cumin and allow them to splutter. 5. Then add chopped bhindi and 1 slit green chili. 6. Begin to saute them in oil on a medium heat. Stirring as often as needed. Over stirring can make them more slimy. 7. You can skip this step and just use garam masala. While the bhindi gets fried, in a different pan dry roast peanuts and garlic on a medium flame until deep golden and aromatic. Then add coconut and sesame (both optional). Lastly add in jeera. Saute just for a minute. When the sesame seeds begin to splutter turn off the stove. Cool completely. Make a coarse powder. 8. When the bhindi shrinks and is no more slimy or sticky, then add salt and turmeric. 9. Fry until bhindi is done to your liking. I usually take off the bhindi when it is no more slimy. 10. Pour 1 tsp oil to the center of the pan, add chili powder to the oil. 11. Mix well and saute the bhindi for another 2 mins until the raw smell of the chili goes away. 12. Now add the powdered peanuts and mix well. 13. Serve bhindi fry with rice or roti as a side.

title: Bhindi Ki Sabji (Ladies Finger Recipe)
ingredients: 3 grams okra, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 onion, 1 green chili, salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tomato, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse bhindi under running water, Drain them completely. 2. Wipe them dry with a clean cloth or kitchen tissues. Trim off the ends. Cut them to half inch pieces. 3. Then slice onions. Chop garlic, tomatoes and green chili. 4. Pour oil to a pan and heat it. Add in cumin. When the seeds begin to sizzle add garlic. 5. Saute garlic just for 30 seconds. Do not brown them. 6. Next add in onions and green chilli. Fry until the onions turn slightly golden. Do not brown them. 7. Add chopped bhindi. Mix and fry on a medium flame until it is no more slimy. 8. Keep stirring and fry. By the end of this step bhindi is half cooked. 9. Next sprinkle salt, turmeric, red chili powder and garam masala as well. Mix and fry just for a minute or two. 10. Add in tomatoes. Mix well and fry for 2 mins. Cover and cook bhindi ki sabji on a low flame. 11. When the tomatoes turn soft and mushy, check the salt and spice. 12. If needed add more of them. Add kasuri methi or curry leaves. 13. Fry for a few minutes till the raw smell of tomatoes goes away. You can cook the bhindi to your liking partially cooked or fully cooked. 14. Serve bhindi ki sabji with plain rice or chapati.

title: Bhindi Masala Recipe - Dhaba Style
ingredients: 1 bhindi, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tsp oil, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 8 clove garlic, 1 tsp ginger paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1.5 cup tomato puree, 0.25 cup curd, 1 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 onion, 1 to mato, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, take 10 bhindi and chop them into pieces. make sure to slit in the centre so that masala can be absorbed. 2. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala. 3. mix well coating all the spices to bhindi. 4. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, add 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp cumin and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. now add 1 onion, 8 clove garlic and 1 tsp ginger paste. 6. saute well making sure the onions turn golden brown. 7. further add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and ½ tsp cumin powder. 8. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 9. add 1½ cup tomato puree and saute well. 10. saute until the oil separates from the puree. 11. now add ¼ cup curd and cook until the oil separates. 12. add 1 cup water and 1 tsp salt. mix well adjusting the consistency of the curry. 13. in another pan heat 2 tsp oil and fry the bhindi. 14. fry until the bhindi changes colour and is almost cooked. 15. transfer the fried bhindi to the curry, add ½ onion and 1 tomato. 16. mix well, cover and simmer for 10 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 17. add ¼ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 18. finally, enjoy bhindi masala recipe with roti.

title: Bhindi Masala Recipe (Okra Masala)
ingredients: 25 grams bhindi, 3 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 cup onions, 1 green chilli slit, 0.75 tablespoons ginger garlic, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, water as needed, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse bhindi very well under running water & drain them. 2. Wipe off with a clean kitchen cloth or tissues to remove all the moisture. You can also leave on the counter top for a while to air dry. Any moisture on the bhindi will make them slimy while cooking so wipe dry them completely. 3. Chop off both the ends and discard them. Chop the bhindi to ¾ inch pieces. 4. Heat 1½ tablespoons oil in a pan. 5. Saute bhindi on a medium flame for about 5 to 7 mins stirring often until they are partially cooked. At this stage they should be no more sticky & slimy after sauteing. 6. Transfer them to a plate and set aside. 7. Add 1½ to 2 tablespoons more oil to the same pan. Then add cumin seeds. 8. When they begin to splutter, saute onions and green chili until they turn golden. 9. Next saute ginger garlic for 2 mins till the raw smell of the garlic goes away. 10. Then saute tomatoes with salt until they turn soft and mushy. 11. Stir in red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala & coriander powder. Saute until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan. 12. Pour 6 tablespoons (little less than half cup) water & mix well. Cook the masala until it turns thick. This brings out the flavors of spices. (check video for consistency) 13. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Stir in the kasuri methi & bhindi. 14. Cover and cook on a low heat until bhindi turns slightly tender and the aroma comes out. 15. Open the lid and evaporate any excess moisture. 16. Serve bhindi masala with roti or rice.

title: bhindi pakora recipe | okra fritters | bende gatti baje recipe
ingredients: 1 bhindi okra lady finger, 2 green chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, pinch of hing asafoetida, 0.75 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.5 tsp salt, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, clean and wipe the bhindi well making sure there is no moisture before chopping finely. 2. further add 2 green chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder and pinch of hing. 3. also add ¾ cup besan, ¼ cup rice flour and ½ tsp salt. 4. combine all the ingredients well without adding any water. 5. also squeeze the bhindi well to release moisture and make dough moist. 6. now take a small ball sized dough and drop into hot oil. 7. make sure to spread the dough and not pile up one over another. 8. fry on medium flame stirring occasionally. 9. also fry till the pakodas turn crisp and golden brown for approximately 15 minutes. 10. finally, serve crispy bhindi pakoda with green chutney along with masala tea.

title: bhindi rava fry recipe | crispy okra rava fry | ladies finger fry recipe
ingredients: 1 bhindi okra lady finger, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, pinch of hing asafoetida, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup rava sooji semolina, 2 tbsp oil
instructions: 1. firstly, wash the bhindi and wipe off clean, cut the head and tail. 2. further slice them half. 3. add in 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp aamchur, pinch of hing, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, ½ tsp salt. 4. also add ½ cup rava and mix well. 5. combine and squeeze all together well and soak for 30 minutes. 6. heat the kadai with 2 tbsp oil. 7. place in bhindi coated with rava. 8. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or till the bhindi gets cooked well. 9. flip over and contine to roast on low flame. 10. cook till the bhindi turns crisp and golden. 11. finally, serve rava bhindi fry as a side dish or enjoy with tea.

title: biryani gravy recipe | biryani shorba | gravy for biryani | sherva recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp peanut, 2 tbsp sesame til, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tomato, 2 tbsp mint pudina, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom, 1 bay leaf, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp kashmiri chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, to prepare masala roast 2 tbsp peanut until the skin separates. 2. also roast 2 tbsp sesame, 2 tbsp dry coconut on low flame until it turns golden. 3. cool completely and transfer to the blender. 4. in a pan heat 2 tsp oil and saute ½ onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 5. also saute 1 tomato and 2 tbsp mint. 6. saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 7. cool completely and transfer to the same blender. 8. add ½ cup water and blend to smooth paste. keep aside. 9. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 1 tsp cumin, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom and 1 bay leaf. 10. add in prepared masala paste and saute for 3-4 minutes. 11. now add ¼ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ¾ tsp salt. 12. saute until the oil is separated from masala paste. 13. further add 2 cup water and mix well. 14. cover and boil for 10 minutes or until oil starts to float. 15. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy biryany gravy as a side dish with biryani or pulao.

title: Biryani Masala Recipe (Biryani Masala Powder)
ingredients: 2 bay leaf, 15 green cardamom, 2.5 black cardamom, 2.5 star anise, 1.5 tablespoons fennel seeds, 1 teaspoon black pepper corn, 5.5 pieces cinnamon, 1 tablespoon cloves, 0.25 cup coriander seeds, 2 tablespoons caraway seeds, 1 mace, 0.5 teaspoon nutmeg, 3 stone flowers
instructions: 1. Clean up the spices one after the other to pick and discard stones and debris, chaffs etc. Wipe clean bay leaf and cinnamon pieces with a tissue. Break the nutmeg to 2 portions with a nutcracker or a mortar pestle. 2. You can skip roasting and just sun dry the spices for several hours until crisp. Cool them completely. 3. On a medium flame dry roast coriander seeds until aromatic, crunchy and light brown. Transfer to a wide tray placed over a cooling rack. This way your roasted spices & the tray won't sweat. 4. Add half of the nutmeg (about 1.5 to 2 grams) to the pan along with cinnamon, mace, star anise, black cardamoms and bay leaf. 5. Dry roast them on a medium flame until the spices begin to smell aromatic. Keep stirring so the spices roast evenly. Transfer to the cooling tray. 6. Add cloves, black pepper corn and green cardamoms (with the shell). Dry roast until they begin to smell good, for 2 to 3 mins. Transfer them to the same tray. 7. Dry roast caraway seeds on a low to medium heat until fragrant. This spice can burn faster so be attentive. Transfer to the tray. 8. Dry roast the fennel seeds until you begin to get a floral smell for 3 mins. Transfer to a tray. 9. When all the spices cool down, add them to a grinder jar or spice jar. Grind to a fine powder. The spices can also be milled to a fine powder in a mill. 10. Sieve and store biryani masala powder in an airtight glass jar. You can use the coarse particles to make masala tea. Biryani masala can also be freezed for an year without losing the flavor. 11. Use biryani masala powder as needed while making any kind of biryani. To make chicken masala curry, use as needed based on the recipe.

title: biryani masala recipe | how to make homemade biryani masala powder
ingredients: 3 kashmiri red chilli, 7 bay leaf tej patta, 2 tbsp coriander seeds dhaniya seeds, 1 tbsp cumin jeera, 1 tbsp caraway seeds kala jeera shah jeera, 3 mace javitri, 2 inch cinnamon stick dalchini, 1 nutmeg jaayaphal, 1 tsp cloves lavang, 3 black cardamom badi elachi, 3 star anise chakkar phool, 1 cardamom elachi, 1 tbsp pepper kali mirch, 1 tsp fennel saunf, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi
instructions: 1. firstly, in a thick bottomed pan dry roast 3 red chilli and 7 bay leaf till they turn crisp. keep aside. 2. further dry roast 2 tbsp coriander seeds, 1 tbsp cumin and 1 tbsp caraway seeds till they turn aromatic. keep aside. 3. also dry roast 3 mace, 2 inch cinnamon stick, 1 nutmeg, 1 tsp cloves, 3 black cardamom, 3 star anise, 10 cardamom, 1 tbsp pepper and 1 tsp fennel / saunf. 4. transfer to a blender and allow to cool completely. 5. also add in ½ tsp turmeric. 6. blend to fine powder and transfer to the airtight container. 7. finally, store biryani masala powder in airtight container and use to prepare biryani.

title: Biryani Rice Recipe (Kuska Rice)
ingredients: 1 cup aged basmati rice, 1.75 cups water, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.29166666666666663 cup onion, 0.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup tomatoes, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 3 tablespoons mint leaves, 2 tablespoons curd, 0.125 teaspoon Turmeric, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon biryani masala powder, 1 bay leaf, 2.5 cloves 2 to 3 cloves ((laung)), 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 star anise, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 strand mace, 1 inch cinnamon piece
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak rice for at least 20 minutes. Drain water completely. Set aside. 2. On a medium flame, heat a pressure cooker or a heavy bottom pan with oil & add the whole spices. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and sauté until evenly golden. 3. Add ginger garlic paste and saute till it smells fragrant. Do not burn 4. Transfer tomatoes, curd, red chili powder, biryani masala (or garam masala), pudina, coriander, salt and turmeric. Saute till tomatoes turn mushy, soft and pulpy. 5. Pour water or stock and mix well. Taste test to adjust salt. Bring water to a rolling boil on a high heat. (you could vary the amount of water based on your rice) Add drained rice and mix well. 6. Stovetop Cooker - Cover the pressure cooker with the lid, cook on a medium flame until you hear 1 whistle. When the pressure drops, open the lid. 7. No cooker - Pot Method - if cooking in a pan, bring to a rolling boil. Reduce to medium and cover with a lid. Cook until the water is absorbed and the rice is cooked through. 8. Fluff up the biryani rice with a fork. 9. Pour oil to the steel insert of the Instant pot and press the saute button. Add all the whole spices, followed by onions. 10. Saute until the onions turn golden. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for 30 to 50 seconds. 11. Add tomatoes, yogurt, red chilli powder, biryani masala, mint, coriander, salt and turmeric. Saute until the yogurt and tomatoes cook down to a mushy paste. 12. Drain the water from the rice and add it here. Pour 1¼ cups water and deglaze the pot. Taste test and add more salt if required. Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position to the steam release value to sealing. 13. Pressure cook for 5 minutes on high pressure. Let the pressure drop manually for 5 minutes, after that release manually. Gently fluff up the rice with a fork and serve biryani rice with any curry or a simple raita.

title: bisi bele bath masala powder recipe | karnataka style bisibelebath powder recipe
ingredients: 4 tsp coriander seeds, 4 tsp chana dal, 1 tsp sesame seeds, 2 tsp urad dal, 11 byadagi, 1 tsp cumin seeds jeera, 0.5 tsp black pepper, 3 green cardamom, 1.5 inch cinnamon dalchini, 3.5 cloves lavang, 2 tsp poppy seeds khus khus, 0.25 tsp fenugreek seeds methi, 13 curry leaves kadi patta, 1 tsp asafoetida hing, 3 tsp of dried coconut desiccated coconut
instructions: 1. keep all the spices ready 2. firstly, dry roast the coriander, cumin and fenugreek, channa dal, urad dal until they start turning fragrant. 3. to the same dry roast sesame seeds, poppy seeds along with curry leaves till they become crisp. remove and keep aside. 4. in the same pan, dry roast the cinnamon, cloves, black pepper, cardamom till they become aromatic. keep aside. 5. now dry roast the dry red chilies till they become crisp. keep aside. 6. lastly, roast the desiccated coconut / dry coconut till they become golden. 7. allow the roasted spices to cool. 8. grind to a fine powder adding little asafoetida. 9. store the bisi bele bath powder jar and prepare bisi bele bath when wished.

title: Bisi Bele Bath Recipe
ingredients: 4 red chilli, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 2 pinches fenugreek seeds, 1.5 tablespoon coriander seeds, 1 marati moggu, 1 tablespoon coconut, 0.75 cup rice, 0.5 cup toor dal, 3 tablespoons tamarind, 1.5 teaspoon jaggery, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon ghee, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup green peas, 5 french beans, 8 shallots, 0.25 cup capsicum, 1.5 tablespoon ghee, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 cashews, 1 Pinch asafoetida, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 red chili
instructions: 1. You can skip this section and just use ready made bisi bele bath. On a low flame dry roast red chilies, chana dal & urad dal until golden. 2. Then fry coriander seeds, cloves, cinnamon & moggu. When they turn aromatic remove to a plate. 3. Add methi seeds & roast. When the seeds turn aromatic, add in cumin and coconut. 4. Next when these turn aromatic turn off and cool all the ingredients. Make a fine powder & set aside. 5. Add rice to a bowl and rinse it well a few times. In a separate bowl wash dal a few times. 6. Pour 1¾ cups water to the rice bowl & 1 cup water to the dal bowl. 7. Pressure cook both of them in a cooker in pot-in-pot method for 3 to 4 whistles. The rice and dal must be cooked soft. 8. While the rice & dal cooks, soak tamarind in half cup hot water. Squeeze it and filter to a bowl. Keep 2 to 3 tablespoons of this aside for later use. 9. To the remaining tamarind water, add the bisi bele bath powder, jaggery & salt. Mix well to remove lumps. Set aside. 10. Mash the dal to smooth and add to rice. If desired can mash rice as well. 11. Heat a pan with 1 teaspoon ghee & add the vegetables. 12. On a medium high flame saute for 3 to 4 mins to bring out the flavors. 13. Pour 1 cup of water and cook the veggies till they are ¾ done. This way they retain the crunchiness. 14. Pour the tamarind mix and stir well. Allow it to boil for few minutes. 15. Add the mashed rice dal and mix well. Next add ¾ to 1 ¼ cups water and stir well. 16. Let the rice come to a boil on a medium flame. This takes around 4 to 6 mins. 17. Cook till the rice reaches a desired thick consistency. Taste test this. If needed, add more tamarind water, salt and jaggery. 18. Bisi bele bath usually turns thick upon cooling so remove it a bit early. 19. Heat 1 to 2 tablespoons ghee or oil in a small pan. 20. Add cashews and fry them until light golden. Then add mustard and red chilli. 21. When the mustard splutters, add curry leaves. When the leaves turn crisp, add hing. 22. Pour the seasoning over the bisi bele bath. Top bisi bele bath with a generous amount of ghee before serving. It is served hot with papad. 23. Soak tamarind in half cup hot water. 24. Press saute button on the instant pot and pour ghee or oil to the inner pot of the Ip. 25. Then fry cashews until golden and set aside. 26. Add mustard, red chilies, curry leaves and hing. 27. When the mustard pops, add onions and fry for 2 mins. 28. Then add all the veggies and stir fry for a minute. Add bisi bele bath powder, salt, half of the tamarind water & jaggery. (I usually mix the jaggery with tamarind water and filter both together) 29. Mix well and pour 7 cups water. Add rice and dal. Stir well and scrub the bottom to deglaze. 30. Press cancel button. Secure the instant pot with the lid. Position the steam release handle to sealing. 31. Then press pressure cook button and set the timer to 9 minutes. Once the IP beeps, press cancel and turn off. 32. Wait for natural pressure release for 10 minutes. Then release the rest of the pressure manually. Open the lid and stir well. Taste test and add more tamarind water, salt and jaggery is needed. 33. Add additional ghee and sprinkle coriander leaves. If desired you can also make a fresh tadka and pour on the bisi bele bath.

title: Bitter Gourd Curry | Karela Sabzi Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams karela, 1 onion, 1 green chilli, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch asafoetida, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tablespoons coriander powder, 1 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons Lemon juice, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash and slice bitter gourd thin. Or you may chop them to bite size pieces. You can also discard seeds if you prefer. 2. Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seed. when they begin to sizzle, add hing. 3. Add chopped onions and curry leaves. Saute until the onions turn pink/ transparent. 4. Add bitter gourd slices and sprinkle salt. Saute for 2 to 3 mins on a medium heat. 5. Cover and cook on a low heat until slightly tender, half cooked. Keep stirring in between. If the pan is too dry, you may sprinkle some water and mix. 6. Then the bitter gourd is slightly tender, add turmeric, fennel powder, coriander powder, red chili powder. Mix every thing well. 7. Cover and cook again on a very low heat until soft cooked. If the curry looks very dry sprinkle very some water. Taste test and add more salt or spices to your liking. 8. Turn off the stove. Squeeze in some lemon juice & stir. Garnish bitter gourd curry with coriander leaves. Serve with rice or roti.

title: black chana chaat recipe | kala chana chaat | black chickpeas chaat
ingredients: 1 cup kala chana black chickpea, 0.75 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 2 tsp oil, 1 chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tbsp green chutney, 1 tbsp tamarind chutney, 2 tbsp curd yogurt, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp tomato, 1 potato aloo, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp mint pudina, 1 tbsp curd yogurt, 1 tsp tamarind chutney, 1 tsp green chutney, 2 tsp onion, 2 tsp tomato, 3 tbsp sev, 1 tsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup soaked kala chana. make sure to soak 1 cup black chickpea overnight. 2. add ¾ tsp salt and 3 cup water. 3. pressure cook for 8 whistels or until chana is cooked well. 4. drain off the water and keep aside. 5. in a kadai heat 2 tsp oil and saute 1 chilli till it turns aromatic. 6. add in cooked chana and continue to saute for 2 minutes. 7. keeping the flame on low and add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp garam masala, ¼ tsp aamchur, ½ tsp chaat masala, ¼ tsp cumin powder and ½ tsp salt. 8. saute on low flame until spices turn aromatic. 9. further mash off couple of chana to have good texture. 10. transfer the spiced kala chana to a bowl. 11. add 1 tbsp green chutney, 1 tbsp tamarind chutney and 2 tbsp curd. mix well. 12. further add ½ onion, 2 tbsp tomato, 1 potato, 2 tbsp coriander and 2 tbsp mint. 13. mix well making sure everything is combined well. 14. plate the black chickpea over the plate. 15. top with 1 tbsp curd, 1 tsp tamarind chutney, 1 tsp green chutney. 16. also add 2 tsp onion, 2 tsp tomato, 3 tbsp sev and 1 tsp coriander. 17. finally, kala chana chat or black chickpea chaat is ready to serve.

title: Black Eyed Peas Curry Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup dry black eyed peas, 1 cup water, 1 cup onions, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 1 potato, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 1.125 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon salt, 1.5 cups water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse the dry black eyed peas well thrice and soak them for at least 4 to 8 hours. Cover the bowl with a mesh basket/ lid or cloth if you want. Avoid covering it completely. 2. Later rinse them well with fresh water twice and add them to a cooker or bowl along with water. 3. Place the bowl in your pressure cooker and secure the lid. Pressure cook until you hear 2 to 3 whistles on the stovetop pressure cooker. If using Instant pot, pressure cook for 8 to 10 mins. (read notes) If you do not have a cooker, please read pro tips section in the post to cook them in a pot. 4. Once the pressure drops naturally, open the lid and check, black eyed peas should be soft yet hold shape. They should get mashed easily when you squeeze. 5. While the beans cooks, chop onions, grate ginger garlic and puree tomatoes. Peel and dice the potatoes if using. 6. Pour oil to a pan and heat it. Add bay leaf and cinnamon if using. 7. When they begin to sizzle, add the onions and stir fry on a medium high heat for 6 to 7 minutes, until golden. 8. Add the ginger garlic and saute for a minute. Reduce the heat and stir in salt along with all the ground spices - red chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin and turmeric. 9. Immediately pour the tomato puree (read notes). Cook for a few minutes until the raw flavor of tomatoes is gone & the onion tomato masala turns thick. This step takes 3 to 4 minutes. 10. Optional - Add the potatoes along with 1 cup hot water. Stir well & bring to a boil on a high heat. Cover and reduce the heat to medium. Cook until the potatoes are fork tender yet firm and not mushy. 11. Transfer the black eyed peas (precooked or canned) along with the stock (beans cooked water). (If using canned beans, drain the liquid off and rinse them well under running water before adding to the curry). 12. Pour 1 cup or more hot water (as required) and bring this to a rolling boil on a high heat. Reduce and cook covered on a medium heat for 7 to 10 mins, until the curry is thick and the beans are flavorful. 13. Take a few tablespoons of black eyed peas & mash them well to smooth with another spoon. Stir that back to the curry to thicken it further. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala for more flavor. 14. Turn off and garnish black eyed peas curry with chopped coriander leaves. Serve it over steamed rice, quinoa, steel cut oats or millets. It also goes well with naan, roti, chapati or artisan breads.

title: Blueberry Juice Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup blueberries, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 2.5 tablespoons sweetener, 3 cups ice
instructions: 1. Place blueberries in a colander and spray some vinegar all over. Drizzle some baking soda and gently smear it all over the berries. Leave them for 5 minutes. 2. Rinse them well under running water. Repeat the rinse several times until the water runs clear. 3. Add them to a blender jar along with lemon, sweetener and 3 cups ice (or 2 cups chilled water and 1 cup ice) 4. Blend until smooth, add more ice or water if needed for a thinner consistency. 5. Pour to serving glasses and serve blueberry juice immediately. If you let it sit, it will set like a jelly due to high pectin.

title: Boiled Egg Fry
ingredients: 2 Boiled eggs, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 1 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons Oil, 1 garlic clove, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon Salt, 0.25 teaspoon Red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon pepper powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 capsicum, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. Add oil to a pan and heat it. Add cumin, ginger and garlic, allow to sizzle. 2. Add onions, fry until they turn golden, add eggs, tomatoes, turmeric , salt and chili. 3. Fry everything together till the tomatoes turn soft and mushy, if needed sprinkle little water. 4. Add garam masala, chili powder and pepper. fry everything together until the mixture is nicely roasted for about 2 to 3 minutes. 5. Serve egg fry with rice, chapathi or even with bread or buns 6. Make some gashes on your boiled eggs. Heat ghee in a pan. Add 2 to 3 crushed garlic cloves and boiled eggs. 7. Fry the eggs until blistered stirring with a long spatula. Add curry leaves and green chilies (optional). 8. Reduce the heat to low or turn off the heat if your pan is too hot. And add ½ teaspoon red chilli powder,¼ teaspoon salt, ¼ teaspoon cumin powder, ¼ teaspoon coriander powder and a pinch of turmeric. If you want you may add ⅛ teaspoon garam masala and ⅛ teaspoon fennel powder. too. 9. Stir the eggs with the spices. If you continue to cook the spices will burn and taste bitter. Taste test and add more salt or spices if you want. Your boiled egg fry is ready.

title: Boiled Egg Salad
ingredients: 3 hard boiled eggs, 2 tablespoons onions, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 garlic clove, 3 tablespoons mayonnaise, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper, 0.75 teaspoon red chili flakes, 0.125 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 teaspoon herbs
instructions: 1. Place the eggs (at room temperature) in a pot and fill with water, about ½ inch above the eggs. Pour 2 tablespoons vinegar. Bring to a boil on a medium heat and cover the pot. 2. Let boil on a medium heat for 8 to 9 mins if using old eggs and for 12 to 14 mins if using farm fresh eggs. Turn off the heat and let rest for 15 mins. 3. Remove the eggs, drain the hot water. Fill the pot with cold water and place the eggs. The eggs will peel easily without the egg whites begin stuck to the shell. 4. Get your perfectly hard boiled eggs ready. 5. Chop them to tiny cubes and add to a mixing bowl. 6. Add chopped onions, garlic, herbs, mustard and chilies. 7. Add mayo or Greek yogurt (strained yogurt or hung curd). 8. Mix gently. If not using mayo but using Greek yogurt, add salt. Sprinkle chili flakes. Serve the creamy egg salad over toasted bread or wrap it in tortilla/ roti.

title: Bombay Chutney Recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup onions, 0.29166666666666663 cup tomatoes, 3 tablespoons besan, 3 cups water, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon chana dal, 1 red chilli, 1.5 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.25 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan. Add mustard, cumin, urad dal, chana dal and red chilies. 2. Saute until the dal turns golden. Add curry leaves and green chilies. Saute for a minute. 3. Add ginger and fry until aromatic for about 30 seconds. 4. Add onions and stir fry until transparent. Add tomatoes, turmeric and salt. 5. Saute for a minute. Cover and cook until tomatoes turn slightly soft. 6. Meanwhile in a bowl mix together half cup water with 3 tbsps of besan. Whisk well until no lumps remain. Then pour the rest of the water and mix. 7. When the tomatoes turn soft, pour the besan mixture to the pan. 8. Bring it to a rolling boil on a high heat and turn down the heat. Cover and cook until Bombay chutney becomes thick and is of pouring consistency. This takes about 9 to 10 mins depending on the kind of your pan & flame. 9. Open the lid and taste test. Add more salt if required. Turn off the heat, cover and rest for few minutes. 10. Before serving squeeze in some lemon juice and add coriander leaves. Mix and serve Bombay chutney with dosa, poori, roti, chapati, rava idli and paratha.

title: Bombay Karachi Halwa Recipe | Corn flour halwa
ingredients: 1 cup corn flour, 3 cup water, 2.5 cup sugar, 0.5 cup ghee, 2 tbsp cashew
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup corn flour and 3 cup water. 2. Using a whisk mix well making sure there are no lumps. Keep aside. 3. In a large kadai take 2½ cup sugar and ½ cup water. 4. Stir and cook until the sugar dissolves completely, 5. Transfer the corn flour mixture to the kadai and mix well. 6. Keep stirring until the mixture is well combined with the sugar syrup. 7. Continue to stir until the mixture starts to thicken. 8. Further, add 2 tbsp of ghee and continue to mix. 9. Mix until the ghee is well absorbed. 10. Keep adding ghee in batches until it is completely absorbed. 11. Once the mixture turns glossy (after 30 minutes), add ½ tsp red food color and mix well. 12. Now add 2 tbsp cashew and mix well. 13. After 40 minutes, the mixture will turn super glossy. 14. Now add 1 tsp of more ghee and continue to stir until the mixture releases ghee. 15. Transfer the halwa mixture to the tray. 16. Sprinkle few chopped nuts and level it up. 17. Rest for 30 minutes or until the halwa is set completely. 18. finally, cut Karachi Halwa or Bombay Halwa Recipe into pieces and enjoy for 2 weeks when refrigerated. 19. Sprinkle few chopped nuts and level it up. Rest for 30 minutes or until the halwa is set completely.

title: Bombay Potatoes Recipe (Bombay Aloo)
ingredients: 5 grams potatoes, 1 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1 green chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.1875 teaspoon Turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon Garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon Cumin Powder, 0.625 teaspoon Coriander powder, 0.375 Fennel powder, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 0.25 teaspoon Mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon Cumin seeds, 2.5 tablespoons oil
instructions: 1. Wash the potatoes well under running water. If you want to keep the skin on, brush the potatoes well and rinse else peel the potatoes. If you are using large potatoes, dice them to 1½ inch sizes. If using whole baby potatoes, poke them with a fork or pick. 2. Add to a pot with 3 cups water, 1 teaspoon salt and turmeric (optional). Optionally you may add garam masala and little red chilli powder too to flavor up your aloo. 3. Bring the water to a rolling boil and reduce the heat to medium. Boil until the potatoes are fork tender but not mushy. Turn off and remove the potatoes from water. Cool completely. While the potatoes boil, chop the onions, tomatoes, ginger, garlic and chili. 4. Pour 2 tablespoons oil to a hot pan. When the oil is hot enough, add the potatoes and fry them until golden and crisp. Remove them to a plate. 5. Pour another tablespoon oil to the pan and add mustard and cumin seeds. When the seeds begin to splutter, stir in the curry leaves. 6. Add onions, salt and green chilies. Saute on a medium high heat until they turn golden. 7. Lower the heat. Add ginger garlic and saute for a minute. Stir in all the ground spices - red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, cumin powder, coriander powder & fennel powder. 8. Add the tomatoes and saute until they turn mushy and soft. Reduce the heat and saute the onion tomato masala for a few more minutes until aromatic. 9. Transfer the fried potatoes and pour half cup hot water. Mix well and bring the potatoes to the center of the pan. Taste test and adjust the salt. Cover them with the onion tomato masala. (Check the pictures) 10.
