Cover and cook on a low heat for 5 to 6 mins so the potatoes absorb the flavors. Make sure you don't overcook the potatoes as they can easily turn mushy. 11. Turn off the heat and garnish coriander leaves. Serve Bombay potatoes with rice, roti or chapati. You can also mash them slightly and fill your sandwiches and wraps.

title: bombay sandwich masala recipe | sandwich masala recipe
ingredients: 1 tsp cumin seeds jeera, 1 tsp fennel seeds, 2 tsp black pepper, 0.5 inch cinnamon stick, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a thick bottomed pan dry roast cumin seeds, fennel seeds, black pepper and cinnamon stick. 2. roast on medium flame for a minute or till turn aromatic. allow them to cool completely. 3. furthermore, transfer to small mixer grinder. also add chaat masala and little salt. 4. blend to fine powder. 5. finally, bombay sandwich masala is ready. use it to prepare bombay sandwich and store remaining in airtight container.

title: Bombay Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 1 medium potato, 0.5 beetroot, 1 medium tomato, 1 medium onion, 1 medium Cucumber, 0.5 teaspoon chat masala, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon kala namak, 6 slices Bread, 3 tablespoons Butter, 1 cup coriander leaves, 0.5 bread slice, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 green chilies, 1 garlic clove, Lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Boil 1 medium potato and 1 beetroot (optional) in a pressure cooker or in a pot, until fork tender and not mushy. You have to boil them separately so your potato does not get discolored with beets. Later cool them, peel and slice them to 1/4 inch thickness. Also rinse and slice 1 onion, 1 cucumber, 1 tomato and if you like capsicum too. I haven’t used beetroot & capsicum here. 2. We need soft butter to make the sandwiches. So keep the butter out of the fridge. 3. While the potatoes boil, make the sandwich chutney. Rinse coriander leaves under running water. Drain completely and remove the roots and stalks. You may keep the tender stalks. 4. Add 1 cup coriander leaves to a grinder jar along with 1 tablespoon roasted peanuts or roasted chana dal or half bread slice, little salt, 2 pinches kala namak (optional), 1 to 2 green chilies and 1 garlic clove. I also love to add ½ teaspoon chaat masala & ½ teaspoon roasted cumin powder. Blend to a smooth chutney adding some lemon juice towards the end if you like. If you are unable to blend add a splash of water. The chutney must be thick and of spreading consistency. I use about 2 tablespoons water. 5. Do not make it runny as we don’t want the bread to get soggy. We don’t want a coarse chutney too. Taste test and adjust salt and spice. You can store this in a clean dry glass jar. Refrigerate and use up with in 3 to 4 days.

title: bombay sandwich recipe | mumbai sandwich | bombay grilled sandwich recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp cumin, 2 tbsp fennel saunf, 2 tsp pepper, 2 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom, 1 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp aamchur, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 slice bread, butter, green chutney, 1 potato, 1 tomato, 1 cucumber, 1 beetroot, onion, tomato sauce, 3 slice bread, butter, green chutney, 1 potato, 1 tomato, 1 cucumber, 2 slice cheese, 1 beetroot, onion
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan take 2 tbsp cumin, 2 tbsp fennel, 2 tsp pepper, 2 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom. 2. roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 3. cool completely and transfer to the mixi. 4. add 1 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp aamchur and ½ tsp salt. 5. blend to a fine powder and sandwich masala is ready. keep aside. 6. firstly, take 3 slice bread, and trim the sides. 7. spread butter on 2 bread slice and green chutney on another. 8. on the green chutney slice, place boiled potato slices and sprinkle sandwich masala. 9. also, place tomato and cucumber slices. 10. further, sprinkle a generous amount of sandwich masala. 11. cover with buttered bread slice and spread green chutney on top. 12. now top with beetroot slice and chopped onion. also, sprinkle a generous amount of sandwich masala. 13. insert 4 toothpick leaving equal spaces and cut into pieces. toothpick helps to hold layers together. 14. finally, garnish with tomato sauce and enjoy bombay sandwich. 15. firstly, take 3 slice bread, and spread butter on 2 bread slice and green chutney on another. 16. on the green chutney slice, place boiled potato slices and sprinkle sandwich masala. 17. also, place tomato and cucumber slices. 18. further, sprinkle a generous amount of sandwich masala. 19. place a cheese slice and cover with buttered bread slice and spread green chutney on top. 20. now top with beetroot slice, chopped onion and a cheese slice. also, sprinkle a generous amount of sandwich masala. 21. cover with bread slice and spread a generous amount of butter. 22. grill to golden brown sandwich and cut into 4 pieces. 23. finally, enjoy grilled bombay sandwich with tomato sauce

title: bonda recipe | urad dal bonda | ulundu bonda | urad dal vada
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp pepper, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 0.75 tsp salt, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup urad dal in water for 2 hours. 2. drain off the water and transfer to a mixi or grinder. 3. blend to smooth thick and fluffy batter adding 1-2 tbsp of water. 4. transfer the urad dal batter to a bowl. 5. beat in one direction for 2 minutes or until the batter turns smooth and fluffy. 6. now add 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, ½ tsp pepper, 2 tbsp dry coconut and ¾ tsp salt. 7. combine and beat for a minute. 8. now dip your hand in the water, and start dropping them one by one to hot oil. 9. keep the flame in lower to medium stir occasionally. 10. also, fry the vada till they turn golden brown and crisp. 11. drain off the vada on to kitchen towel to absorb oil. 12. finally, serve bonda with coconut chutney.

title: Boondi Ladoo Recipe (Tirupati Ladoo)
ingredients: 1.25 cup besan, 0.625 cup water, Ghee, 6 green cardamoms, 12 cashews, 15 raisins, 5 grams rock sugar pieces, 1 pinch edible camphor, 1.25 cup sugar, 0.75 cup water
instructions: 1. Measure all the ingredients and keep them ready. 2. Add besan to a large mixing bowl and pour ⅓ cup water. Begin to mix to make a smooth thick batter, adding only as required. 3. Once the batter is lump free and smooth, then pour more water only as needed to make a free flowing batter. The consistency should not be not too runny/ thin or too thick. Please check the step by step photos to understand the consistency. 4. Heat up the ghee or oil in a kadai until hot enough, not smoking hot. If using oil, add 2 tablespoons ghee to the oil and heat, this will enhance the flavor of your boondi ladoos. 5. To test if the oil is hot enough, drop a few drops of batter to the hot oil. The batter should form to boondi, sizzle and come up without browning the boondi. This is the right temperature. 6. Pour little batter over the boondi ladle to check if the consistency is right. You must get them right in round shape. 7. Troubleshooting: If you get long tailed boondi, it means the batter is thick, add a teaspoon water and mix well. If you get flat boondi, it means the batter is runny or the oil is not hot enough. I this case sprinkle some flour and mix well to thicken the batter slightly. 8. Pour one large ladle of batter over the boondi jhara and spread the batter continuously to drop the batter in the shape of boondi. 9. You will see lots of bubbles. Stir and fry just until the bubbles begin to reduce. 10. Do not fry the boondi till crunchy but fry them just until the raw smell goes away. 11. Remove them to a large steel colander. 12. Continue to make more boondi in batches until all the batter finishes. 13. In the same ghee or oil, fry cashews until light golden, then add raisins. 14. When they plump up remove them to the boondi bowl. if using rock sugar add it to the bowl now. 15. Add sugar and water to a pot. Boil until 1 string consistency. Turn off the stove. To check the consistency, take little syrup in a spoon and cool it. Take it in between your thumb and index finger, gently move the fingers apart you should see a single string consistency. 16. Remove from stove. Next add 2 slightly crushed cloves, cardamom powder and edible camphor. Mix well. 17. Immediately add boondi, cashews and raisins. Ensure you add all of these when the syrup is hot. 18. Mix well and set aside until the sugar syrup is absorbed completely. 19. This may take about 5 to 10 mins. But keep checking. 20. Take small portions of this mixture and make ladoos. You don't need to grease your hands. Do not let the boondi mixture cool down completely. 21. Cool boondi ladoo completely and store in a air tight jar.

title: Boondi Ladoo Recipe | Boondi Laddu - No Boondi Mould
ingredients: 2 cup besan, pinch kesar food colour, water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, oil, 1.5 cup sugar, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp kesar food colour, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. Firstly, to prepare the boondi, in a large bowl take 2 cup besan, pinch kesar food colour and water. 2. Whisk and mix well making sure there are no lumps in the batter. 3. Further, add water slowly, and make smooth flowing consistency batter. If the batter is watery, then the boondi will turn flat. And if it is too thick, then the boondi will be formed with a tail. 4. Also, add ¼ tsp baking soda and mix well. 5. Boondi can be prepared using an oil strainer or small holes perforated spoon. 6. Pour the prepared besan batter slowly, making sure the drops of besan fall into the oil. 7. Do not overcrowd the boondis as they stick to each other. Stir gently. 8. Once they are almost crisp, take off and drain over the kitchen paper towel. 9. Prepare small boondi in batches and keep them aside. 10. Now to prepare the sugar syrup, in a large kadai take 1.5 cup of sugar. 11. Add 1 cup water and boil for 2 minutes or until the sugar syrup turns sticky. Do not get string consistency sugar syrup. 12. Further add ¼ tsp kesar food colour, ½ tsp cardamom powder and 1 tsp lemon juice. mix well. 13. Now add in prepared boondi, 2 tbsp cashew and 2 tbsp pistachios. Mix until the sugar syrup is well coated. 14. After some time, you can see the sugar syrup is absorbed by boondi. This makes boondi juicy. 15. When the mixture is still lightly warm, start shaping the ladoo. 16. Finally, Boondi Ladoo Recipe is ready to enjoy for up to 7 days when refrigerated.

title: Boondi Raita
ingredients: 1 cup curd, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 2 pinches Black pepper, 0.375 teaspoon chaat masala, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.3333333333333333 cup boondi
instructions: 1. Add 1 cup chilled curd to a large mixing bowl. 2. Add salt to taste. Start with a small quantity and adjust later. 3. Add ½ tsp roasted cumin powder . To make this powder, you can roast cumin on a low flame till it turns crunchy. Cool and powder. 4. Add ¼ tsp red chilli powder. You can also use chopped green chilli if you don’t like the flavor of chilli powder. 5. Add black pepper powder to taste. You can skip this. 6. Next add in ¼ to ½ tsp chaat masala. You can skip this and add few drops of lemon juice. But make sure your curd is not sour. 7. Chop 2 stalks of coriander leaves finely and add it. You can also add mint leaves if you like. 8. Whisk everything well till smooth. If you are using store bought yogurt that’s too thick, then add few tbsps of water. Boondi will absorb some moisture and the raita turns thick. 9. If you want to add softened boondi, add ¾ cup boondi in 1 ½ to 2 cups of warm water. Rest the boondi until soft for 5 mins. Drain to a colander and squeeze gently to remove excess water. Do not overdo as it turns mushy. Then add to the curd. I used 1/3 cup of boondi directly. 10. Gently mix. Taste test and add more salt if required. You can add more boondi or more yogurt to taste. Garnish boondi raita with more spice powders and boondi. Serve with your meal or as a snack.

title: Boondi Recipe | Kara Boondi - Bakery Style
ingredients: 2 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, pinch hing, water, oil, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp peanuts, few curry leaves, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp kala namak
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl, place a sieve and add 2 cups of besan and ¼ cup of rice flour. 2. Sieve the flour, making sure there are no lumps. 3. Add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, a pinch of hing and water. 4. Add water slowly and mix using the whisk, forming a smooth lump-free batter. 5. Whisk well, making sure its smooth sliky batter. 6. To prepare boondi, pour the batter slowly over a skimmer or grater, keeping the oil hot. 7. Stir occasionally, until the boondi turns golden and crisp. 8. Collect all the boondi and make sure to drain off the oil completely. 9. Also fry, cashew, peanuts and curry leaves. 10. Now add 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp kala namak and ½ tsp salt. 11. Mix well, making sure all the spices are well combined. 12. Finally, enjoy the Khara Boondi Recipe, which tastes great on chaats.

title: Bottle Gourd Curry Recipe
ingredients: 2.125 cups bottle gourd, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon Oil, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons Coconut, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon white sesame seeds, 1 tablespoon coconut, 3.5 red chilies, 1.5 garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds
instructions: 1. Rinse bottle gourd under running water. Peel the skin and taste test the center a bit to make sure it is not bitter. Then chop to bite sized pieces. 2. Pour oil to a wide heavy bottomed pan. When the oil is hot, add cumin, mustard, urad dal and chana dal. 3. Fry till the dal turns golden. Add curry leaves and chili. Fry till the leaves turn crisp. 4. Add the bottle gourd, salt and turmeric. Fry on a medium to high flame for 3 mins, keep stirring to avoid burning. 5. Lower the heat. Cover the pan and cook till the gourd is cooked fully, but not mushy. 6. Meanwhile, dry roast chana dal, urad dal and red chilies until the lentils are aromatic and golden. Lower the heat and add sesame seeds, coconut and cumin. Roast until the seeds begin to splutter. 7. Cool and add to a grinder along with garlic cloves. Make a fine powder. Keep checking the gourd in between so you don't over cook. 8. When the bottle gourd is just cooked to your liking, sprinkle coconut and the spice powder as much as you want. Start with 1 tablespoon spice powder. 9. Stir fry the bottle gourd for 1 to 2 minutes. Taste test and add salt if required. Save the left over spice powder in a glass bottle for the next use. 10. Serve bottle gourd curry with roti or with rice and rasam or sambar.

title: bounty chocolate recipe | bounty bar recipe | chocolate coconut bars
ingredients: 1.5 cup milk, 0.75 cup sugar, 2 cup coconut, 0.25 cup cream, 3 grams milk chocolate
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 1½ cup milk and ¾ cup sugar. 2. stir well and dissolve sugar completely. 3. now add 2 cup coconut and mix well. 4. further, add ¼ cup cream and mix well. adding cream makes the coconut layer creamy and tasty. 5. continue to cook on medium flame until the mixture starts to hold the shape. 6. now transfer the mixture to a plate and cool slightly. 7. take a ball sized mixture and shape it into bar shapes. 8. keep refrigerated for 1 hour or until it sets completely. 9. now prepare the melted chocolate by melting 300 grams of milk chocolate using the double boiler method. 10. keep stirring until the chocolate melts completely. 11. cool the melted chocolate slightly. if the chocolate is too hot then coconut bars will melt when dipped. 12. now dip the coconut bars into the melted chocolate and coat uniformly. 13. place into butter paper and mark 3 lines using a butter knife. 3 lines are marked to just mimic the store brought bounty bars. 14. refrigerate for at least 1 hour, so that the chocolate will set completely. 15. finally, enjoy homemade chocolate bounty bars for a week when stored in an airtight container.

title: bread balls recipe | bread potato balls | bread bites recipe
ingredients: 3 potato aloo, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 2 tbsp pudina mint, 0.75 tsp salt, 8 slices bread, breadcrumbs, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 potato. 2. add 1 chilli, ½ tsp ginger paste, ½ tsp turmeric powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp coriander powder. 3. also add ¼ tsp ajwain, 2 tbsp pudina and ¾ tsp salt. 4. mix well making sure everything is combined well forming a dough. 5. furthermore, take bread slices and cut the sides. 6. dip the bread slice into the water just for a second and squeeze off the excess water. 7. make sure to squeeze the bread slice completely else it will absorb oil while deep frying. 8. place the ball shaped prepared stuffing. 9. and wrap the bread making sure to pinch the edges and secure tight. if unable to hold the shape dip your finger in water and make bread flexible to hold and roll. 10. further, with the help of both hands roll to a round shape. 11. roll into panko bread crumbs to get more crispy layer. 12. deep fry the prepared bread roll into the hot oil. make sure to fry in hot oil, else bread will absorb oil and turn soggy. 13. stir and fry occasionally on medium flame till it turns golden brown. alternatively, bake the bread to golden brown if you are diet conscious. 14. finally, serve bread balls hot along with ketchup or green chutney.

title: bread bhatura recipe | how to make no yeast easy bread bhatura
ingredients: 5 slice bread, 1 cup maida plain flour, 0.25 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp sugar, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 cup curd yogurt, 1 tsp oil, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, cut 5 slice bread and transfer to a blender. 2. blend to a fine powder (breadcrumbs) without adding any water. 3. transfer the prepared bread crumbs into a large mixing bowl. 4. also add 1 cup maida, ¼ cup rava, ½ tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp baking soda, 1 tsp sugar and ¼ tsp salt. 5. mix well making sure all the flour are combined well. 6. further, add 1 cup curd and knead dough for at least 5 minutes. 7. add water if required and knead to smooth dough. 8. knead the dough again making sure it's stiff as rava absorbs water. 9. pinch a ball sized dough and flatten. 10. roll the dough evenly into an oval shape using a rolling pin. roll neither too thin nor thick. 11. drop the rolled bhatura into the hot oil and fry. 12. finally, serve bread bhatura with chana masala.

title: bread cake recipe | instant bread ka cake | no bake cake from left over bread
ingredients: 2 cup whipping cream, 0.25 cup icing sugar, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 1 cherry, 1 tbsp sugar, 0.5 cup water, 5 slices bread, 0.5 cup dark chocolate, 1 cherry
instructions: 1. firstly, trim off the sides of 5 slices of bread. 2. place a slice of bread over turning table. 3. brush with a tsp of cherry syrup. 4. now spread a tbsp of frosting over it. 5. place another bread slice and repeat cherry syrup and frosting alternatively for 5 slices of bread. 6. over the last bread slice cover with frosting uniformly on sides. 7. stick the dark chocolate shavings over sides of cake. 8. squeeze out piping pagand create design over cake. 9. top with cherry making the cake look beautiful. 10. also, around the sides of cake, decorate with frosting. 11. finally, refrigerate for 30 minutes and enjoy bread cake recipe.

title: bread cheese balls recipe | cheese bread balls | how to make bread cheese balls
ingredients: 2 potato aloo, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp pepper, 1 tsp mixed herbs, 0.25 cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 slice bread, 1 cubes cheese, 1 cup corn flakes, 2 tbsp corn flour, 2 tbsp maida plain flour, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 potato. 2. also add 1 chilli, ½ tsp ginger paste, 2 tbsp coriander, ¼ tsp pepper, 1 tsp mixed herbs, ¼ cumin powder and ½ tsp salt. 3. additionally, tear 2 bread slices. 4. make a soft non-sticky dough. keep aside. 5. now pinch a ball sized aloo mixture and flatten slightly. 6. place a cubed sized cheese in the centre. 7. get the edges together and stuff the cheese well. 8. form a smooth ball, making sure there are no cracks. 9. further, roll in crushed cornflakes or breadcrumbs covering uniformly. 10. fry in hot oil or bake in preheated oven at 180 degree celcius for 15-18 minutes. 11. stir occasionally without breaking cheese balls. 12. fry until bread cheese balls turns golden brown and crisp. 13. drain off the balls over kitchen paper absorbing excess oil. 14. finally, enjoy bread cheese balls with tomato sauce.

title: bread cutlet recipe | crunchy vegetable bread cutlets recipe
ingredients: 4 bread slice, 2 potatoes, 0.5 medium onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp corn, 1 green chilli, 1 tsp ginger paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, salt to taste, 0.5 tsp garam masala powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 1 tbsp corn flour, 0.25 tsp pepper, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, cut the sides of bread. use any bread of your choice. 2. further, crumble the bread slices as small as possible. 3. furthermore, add all the vegetables and spices. 4. additionally add 1 tbsp corn flour, 1 tbsp lemon juice and 2 tbsp coriander leaves. 5. combine well to form a dough. add in more bread or bread crumbs if there is to much moisture. 6. make sure to have a non sticky dough. 7. further, make round shaped cutlets greasing hands with oil. as they help to prevent from dough sticking to hands. 8. deep fry them into hot oil. alternatively, bake them or pan fry if you are diet conscious. 9. stir occasionally without breaking the cutlets. 10. further fry till the cutlets turn golden brown on medium flame. 11. finally, serve bread cutlet with tomato sauce and few slices of onion.

title: bread dhokla recipe | instant bread dhokla | quick & easy bread dhoklas
ingredients: 4 slice bread, 0.5 cup rava bombay rava sooji semolina, 0.75 cup curd yogurt, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 0.5 tsp green chilli paste, salt to taste, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 cup water, 1 tsp eno fruit salt, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard rai, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp sesame seeds til, pinch of hing asafoetida, 1 green chilli, 0.25 cup water, 0.5 tsp sugar, pinch of salt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tsp coconut, 2 tsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, add in bread crumbs, ½ cup rava, ¾ cup curd. 2. also add ½ tsp ginger paste, ½ tsp green chilli paste, ¼ tsp turmeric, 2 tsp oil and ½ tsp salt. 3. combine adding ½ cup water and mix well. 4. allow to rest for 15-30 minutes. 5. additionally add eno fruit salt. 6. mix gently till the batter turns frothy. 7. transfer and steam for 15 minutes. 8. prepare the tempering by heating oil. 9. add in ½ tsp mustard, ½ tsp jeera, ¼ tsp sesame seeds and pinch of hing. 10. also add 1 green chilli, few curry leaves and allow to splutter. 11. further add ¼ cup water, ½ tsp sugar and pinch of salt. 12. add in 1 tbsp lemon juice and mix well. 13. pour the tempering over dhokla and cut. 14. finally, garnish with coriander and grated coconut and serve instant bread dhokla.

title: bread gulab jamun recipe | instant gulab jamun with bread
ingredients: 1 cup sugar, 1 cup water, 3 cardamom, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 6 slices bread, 2 tbsp full cream milk powder, 1 tbsp cream, tbsp milk, ghee oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a wide pan take 1 cup of sugar. 2. further, to that add 1 cup of water and get to a boil. 3. then simmer for 4 minutes till the sugar syrup turns slightly sticky. 4. now add cardamom powder. 5. also add lemon juice to stop crystallization process. 6. cover and keep aside. 7. firstly, cut the sides of 6 bread slices. 8. furthermore, blend to fine powder like breadcrumbs. 9. also add 2-3 tbsp of milk powder and a tbsp of cream. 10. crumble and mix well. 11. slowly add warm milk little by little and knead to a smooth and soft dough. 12. furthermore, make small balls greasing ghee to hands. 13. make sure there are no cracks on balls. 14. heat the oil or ghee on low flame to medium flame and when the it is moderately hot, fry the jamuns. 15. fry the balls on low flame stirring in between. 16. fry till the balls turns golden brown. 17. immediately, drop the hot jamuns into hot sugar syrup. 18. boil the sugar syrup for a minute after you add fried jamuns. 19. cover the lid and rest for 2 hours. 20. finally, bread gulab jamuns have doubled in size. serve warm with ice cream or cold garnished with few chopped nuts.

title: Bread Halwa
ingredients: 5 slices Bread, 6 tbsps ghee, 0.25 cup sugar, 2 cups milk, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 7 almonds, 7 cashews, 1 tbsp chironji
instructions: 1. Heat a pan with ghee and dry roast the nuts on a medium flame until golden. Set these aside in a plate. 2. Tear off the bread slices to small pieces, about 1 inch cubes and add to the same pan. If you prefer you can also discard the browns of the bread, however they give a texture so I kept it. 3. Begin to roast the bread until evenly golden. 4. Add milk and sugar. 5. Mix everything well and begin to cook until the bread turns soft and mushy. 6. The mixture turns very gooey initially, allow the mixture to thicken. 7. Then add cardamom powder and 2 tbsp ghee. 8. Begin to continuously saute it until the mixture begins to leaves the sides of the pan. 9. Add nuts and continue to cook for 2 to 3 mins. At this stage you can see bread halwa begins to release ghee. 10. Switch off and garnish with nuts. 11. Serve bread halwa chilled or warm.

title: bread halwa recipe | bread ka halwa recipe | bread sheera recipe
ingredients: 5 slice bread, 3 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tbsp raisins kishmish, 1 tbsp cashew kaju, 0.25 cup sugar, 1 cup milk, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder elachi powder
instructions: 1. firstly, trim the sides of 5 slices of bread and cut into pieces. 2. now in a large kadai heat 3 tbsp ghee and roast 1 tbsp raisins and 1 tbsp cashew. keep aside. 3. add in bread pieces and roast for 2 minutes. 4. roast till the bread slices turn golden and crisp. 5. further add 1 cup milk and soak all bread pieces. 6. boil for 2 minutes or until the bread turns soft. 7. mash the bread pieces well stirring continuously. 8. cook till the ghee starts to separate from sides. 9. add in ¼ cup sugar and stir well. 10. sugar dissloves and the starts to separate from pan. 11. cook till a lump is formed. 12. now add in roasted cashes, raisins and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. mix well. 13. finally, serve bread halwa hot or can be stored for 2 days.

title: bread idli recipe | instant bread idli recipe | instant idli recipe
ingredients: 4 bread slices, 1 cup idli rava rice rava, salt to taste, 1 cup thick curd, 1.5 cup water, pinch of baking soda, oil to grease idli plate
instructions: 1. firstly, take bread slices and cut the sides. this helps to prepare white idlis. 2. furthermore, grind to smooth powder like bread crumbs. 3. then transfer the bread crumbs to a large mixing bowl. 4. additionally, add a cup of idli rava. 5. also add salt to taste and a cup of thick curd. 6. furthermore, give a good mix. 7. add water as required to prepare idli batter consistency. 8. also add ¼ cup of water above the batter so that it doesn't dry up. do not worry if your battery is watery, as bread and idli rava will absorb water. 9. rest the batter for 20 -30 minutes. 10. then, give a good mix, add water if required. 11. adjust the consistency to of idli batter. 12. just before steaming add a pinch of baking soda and mix well till it turns frothy. 13. brush the plates with oil and pour the batter immediately into the idli plate. do not rest the batter. 14. as other idlis you have to steam it for 8-10 mins on medium flame. allow it to rest for 5 mins then unmould. 15. serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.

title: bread kachori recipe | bread khasta kachori | bread moong dal kachori
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 green chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp chilli powder lal mirch powder, 2 tbsp water, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 8 slices bread, 0.5 tsp salt, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, dry roast ½ cup moong dal on low to medium flame till it turns aromatic. 2. allow it to cool completely, then coarsely powder. keep aside. 3. now in a kadai heat 2 tsp oil and saute ½ tsp jeera, 1 tsp crushed coriander seeds, ½ tsp fennel and pinch hing. 4. also saute 1 green chilli and 1 inch ginger. 5. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ¼ tsp aamchur and ½ tsp salt. 6. saute on low flame till the spices gets cooked well. 7. now add in coarsely powdered moong dal and combine well. 8. add water and mix well. 9. now take bread slices and trim off the edges. 10. dip in water just for a second and squeeze off the water. 11. place 1 tbsp of prepared kachori stuffing in centre. 12. bring the edges together, press and seal kachori. 13. wet your hands with little water if unable to form kachori. 14. deep fry the kachoris in hot oil. 15. stir till the kachoris turn golden brown from all sides. 16. finally, serve bread kachori with green chutney.

title: bread kulfi recipe | easy bread & milk ice cream | bread ki kulfi
ingredients: 3 slice bread, 1 litre milk, 0.25 tsp saffron kesar, 0.5 cup sugar, 4 anjeer, 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp pistachios, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, trim the sides of the bread and cut into pieces. 2. in mixi, using pulse mode powder into breadcrumbs. keep aside. 3. in a large kadai take 1 litre of milk and ¼ tsp saffron. 4. stir and get the milk to a boil. 5. once the milk comes to a boil add ½ cup sugar and 4 anjeer. 6. stir and boil making sure the sugar dissolves completely. 7. further, add prepared breadcrumbs and mix well. 8. continue to boil until the milk thickens. bread helps to thicken the milk and give a grainy texture like in malai kulfi. 9. now add 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp pistachios and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. 10. mix well, pour the kulfi mixture into kulfi moulds. if you do not have moulds, you can pour until matka or glass cups. 11. cover and freeze for 8 hours or until sets completely. 12. after 8 hours, the kulfi has set completely and is ready to serve. 13. finally, garnish the bread kulfi with a few chopped almonds and enjoy.

title: bread malai roll recipe | malai bread roll | instant rabri malai roll
ingredients: 1 tsp butter, 0.25 cup milk, 2 tbsp cream malai, 0.5 cup milk powder, 1 cup milk, 0.5 cup cream malai, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 4 slice bread, 5 cashew, 5 almonds, 2 tbsp saffron milk, 4 cherry
instructions: 1. firstly, in a tawa heat 1 tsp butter with ¼ cup milk and 2 tbsp cream. 2. stir and combine well. 3. now add ½ cup milk powder and stir well. 4. break lumps making sure the mixture is a smooth consistency. 5. mawa paste thickens and turns creamy. keep aside. 6. firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 cup milk and ½ cup cream. 7. stir well and boil for 2-3 minutes or until milk thickens. 8. now add ¼ cup sugar and stir well until sugar dissolves. 9. add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. malai milk is ready. 10. firstly, take a bread slice and roll thin trimming off the edges. 11. spread a tbsp of mawa paste uniformly. 12. top with 1 tbsp of nuts (cashew & almonds). 13. cut into half and roll tight. 14. place the rolled malai bread in a deep serving plate. 15. pour in prepared malai milk slowly. 16. decorate with saffron milk and top with a cherry. 17. finally, refrigerate for 30 minutes and bread malai roll is ready to be served.

title: bread medu vada recipe | instant medu vada with leftover bread slices
ingredients: 6 slice bread, 0.25 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 cup rice flour, 0.75 cup curd yogurt, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp salt, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl break 6 slices of potato into small pieces. 2. add ¼ cup rava, ½ cup rice flour, ¾ cup curd. 3. also add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp cumin and ½ tsp salt. 4. squeeze and mix well. 5. mix until a soft dough is formed. if the mixture is dry, you can add a tsp of more curd. and if it is sticky, break another bread slice. 6. now grease hand with oil and pinch a ball sized mixture. 7. flatten slightly, and make a hole in the centre. 8. deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 9. stir occasionally, until the vada turns golden brown and crisp. 10. finally, drain off and enjoy bread medu vada with tomato sauce or chutney.

title: Bread Omelette Sandwich
ingredients: 2.5 slices bread, 2 eggs, 1 tablespoon oil, salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.125 teaspoon crushed black pepper, teaspoon sandwich masala, 2 tablespoons onions, 1 tablespoon capsicum, 1 green chili, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 tsp butter, 1.5 slices cheese, 1.5 tsps Tomato ketchup, 0.25 teaspoon Italiann herbs
instructions: 1. Chop onion, chilies, coriander leaves and capsicum. Rinse a bowl and break the egg to it. Discard the shell. Beat the egg until frothy. 2. Add the chopped onions, chilies, coriander and capsicum. Add salt and turmeric. Beat the mixture once more. 3. If you like you can butter the bread and then with chutney or ketchup. 4. Add oil to a pan and heat it. Pour the beaten eggs and allow to cook until the base firms up. 5. While it cooks, you can also shape it to a square with the help of spatula. Flip it and fry until the egg is completely cooked. 6. Flip it back and sprinkle crushed pepper and herbs if using. 7. Place your cheese slice on the omelette. Lift up the omelette with a spatula. 8. Place a bread slice on the tawa and place the omelette along with cheese. Place another slice of bread and toast until the cheese melts. Cheese will melt very quickly under 2 minutes since the omelette is very hot. 9. Halve the bread omelette sandwich and serve hot or warm with a cup of tea.

title: Bread Pakora Recipe (Bread Pakoda)
ingredients: 6 slices bread, oil, 1.5 cups potatoes, 0.25 cup green peas, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida, 2 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger paste, 3 tablespoon coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.75 cup gram flour, 2.5 tablespoons rice flour, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon carom seeds, Water as needed
instructions: 1. Steam or boil potatoes just until done without making them mushy. 2. You can also boil the potatoes in a cooker for 2 whistles. I prefer to steam them as it is easy to keep an eye. 3. You can skip green peas. If using then steam the green peas too along with the potatoes. 4. When they are done, peel and mash them well and set aside. 5. Heat 1 teaspoon oil in a pan 6. Then saute ginger, green chilies and curry leaves (optional) until it begins to smell good. Turn off the stove. 7. Add red chili powder, garam masala and turmeric. Mix well. 8. Transfer mashed potatoes, peas, half teaspoon salt and coriander leaves. Squeeze some lemon juice if you like. Mix everything well. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 9. Arrange 3 bread slices on a chopping board or plate. 10. Optional - If you have green chutney or sauce, you can also smear that to one or both the slices. 11. Place the potato masala over 3 bread slices. Cover each slice with another one. 12. Press gently and cut each sandwich to 2 triangles. Set these aside. 13. Heat oil in a pan for shallow or deep frying. You can use as less as possible if using a nonstick pan. 14. While the oil heats prepare the batter. In another bowl, mix together besan, ajwain, red chili powder, salt, rice flour and turmeric. 15. Pour water just enough to make the batter that is neither too thick nor too thin. It must be similar to the mirchi bajji batter. 16. Check if the oil is hot by dropping a little amount of batter in the oil. The batter has to rise without browning. This is the right temperature. 17. Do this only when the oil is ready to fry. Place each sandwiched bread in the batter and gently coat it with the batter on both the sides. You may need to use both your hands. 18. Swipe off the excess batter and Gently slide this to the hot oil and do not disturb it for a minute. 19. You can add 2 or more pakoras in one batch depending on the size of your pan. 20. Fry bread pakora on a medium heat until golden on one side. Then turn them to the other side and fry till both the sides turn crisp and golden. Remove to a colander or kitchen tissue. 21. Ensure the oil is moderately hot and not extremely hot before you fry the next batch of bread pakora. If needed reduce the heat/flame before adding the next one. 22. Serve bread pakora hot or warm with green chutney or sauce. 23. For the air fryer method make the batter slightly thick and dip your potato filled sandwiches in the batter. Coat them with the batter on both the sides. Gently shake and place on the parchment paper. 24. Air fry at 200 C or 400 F for 8 mins. Gently loosen and remove with a wooden spatula and turn it to the other side. Air fry for another 6 to 7 mins. Adjust the timings slightly. The bread pakora covering has to turn golden and crisp.

title: bread pakora recipe | aloo stuffed bread pakora | bread bajji recipe
ingredients: 3 potato aloo, 1 green chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp salt, pinch of soda, 0.5 cup water, 4 slices bread, 4 tsp green chutney, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 3 boiled potato. 2. also add 1 green chilli, 1-inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander,¼ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp aamchur and ½ tsp chaat masala. 3. combine all the ingredients well and aloo stuffing is ready. 4. further, spread 1 tsp of green chutney over bread slice and cut half. 5. spread a tbsp of prepared aloo stuffing over half slice of bread. 6. cover with another half slice and dip into besan batter covering both sides. 7. deep fry immediately into hot oil. 8. splash oil over bread pakora and fry on medium flame flipping occasionally. 9. fry till the pakora turns golden and crisp. 10. finally, serve bread pakora with green chutney or tomato sauce.

title: bread paneer pakora recipe | aloo paneer bread pakora | paneer bread bajji recipe
ingredients: 3 aloo, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 cup besan, 3 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 tsp salt, 1.25 cup water, 4 slice bread, 4 tsp green chutney, 2 slice paneer cottage cheese, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 aloo, ½ tsp ginger paste, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder. 2. also add ¼ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp chaat masala, ¼ tsp salt and 2 tbsp coriander. 3. mix well, aloo stuffing is ready. keep aside. 4. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup besan, 3 tbsp rice flour. 5. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp baking soda and ½ tsp salt. 6. further, add 1¼ cup water and prepare a thick lump free batter. 7. finally, besan batter is ready to prepare pakoda. keep aside. 8. firstly, spread 1 tsp of green chutney on 2 slices of bread. 9. also spread 2 tbsp of prepared aloo stuffing uniformly. 10. now place 1 thick slice of paneer. 11. cover with other bread slice and cut half. 12. further, dip the stuffed bread in besan batter coating uniformly. 13. deep fry in oil, keeping the flame on medium. 14. flip over and fry both sides until the pakora turns golden brown and crisp. 15. finally, aloo paneer bread pakora recipe is ready to enjoy with tomato sauce.

title: Bread Pizza Recipe
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 1 teaspoon oil, 2 tablespoon pizza sauce as needed, 0.625 cup cheese, 0.5 teaspoon Italian herbs, 0.375 teaspoon red chili flakes, 1 medium onion, 0.5 cup bell pepper cubed, 1 tomato, 1 Olives
instructions: 1. Cube the onions and separate the layers. 2. Deseed the tomatoes and bell pepper. Cube them as well. 3. Grate the cheese and keep it ready. 4. Grease a pan/ griddle with 1 tsp oil. Toast the bread lightly on one side. Switch off the heat. 5. Remove the bread to a board or tray, so the crisp side is on top. This step is optional but helps to keep the bread firm. 6. Smear pizza sauce thinly as desired. 7. Layer onions, bell pepper and tomatoes. Next grate and layer cheese and then the herbs and chili flakes. If you have olives, place them as well. 8. Place the bread on the griddle. Cover the pan & begin to toast on a low to medium heat. 9. Toast until the cheese melts. Keep checking to avoid burning the bread. 10. Arrange the bread slices on a baking tray & preheat the oven at 430 F or 220 C. 11. Meanwhile , arrange the bread slices on a baking tray. 12. Spread pizza sauce over the bread. Layer onions, capsicum and tomatoes. Next grate and layer cheese. Sprinkle herbs and chili flakes. Place olives as well. 13. Keep the tray in a preheated oven in the middle rack. Bake/ grill for 8 mins at 430 F or 220 C. Adjust the grill time as needed. The veggies get grilled partially and the cheese melts and turns golden. Serve bread pizza hot as a snack or breakfast. 14. Smear some sauce over the bread and place it in the air fryer basket. Spread all your topping along with cheese. Air fry at 180 C or 350 F for 5 to 6 mins or until golden and crisp. Without preheating it takes up to 7 to 8 mins.

title: bread rasmalai recipe | bread ki rasmalai with milkmaid | instant rasmalai
ingredients: 4 cups full cream cows buffalo milk, 1 cup condensed milk milkmaid, 2 tbsp saffron milk, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 5 tbsp dry fruits, 5 slices bread
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large nonstick pan heat thick cows or buffaloes milk. 2. stir occasionally making sure the milk doesn't stick to bottom of vessel and get to a boil. 3. furthermore add 1 cup of condensed milk. 4. stir well and get the milk to boil. 5. also reduce the flame to medium. and once a layer of cream is formed over the milk, stick it to sides of vessel. 6. make sure to stir from bottom of vessel so that milk doesn't burn. 7. further, get the milk to boil again. 8. and repeat the gathering of cream to sides of kadai for atleast 3 -5 times or till milk reduces to one-third. 9. add cardamom powder and saffron milk. 10. give a good mix and get the milk again to boil. 11. also scrape off the collected cream from sides. and give a good stir. 12. furthermore, simmer for 2 minutes. 13. allow it cool to room temperature and further chill for 2 hours. 14. now cut the bread into round pieces with the help of cookie cutter or glass. 15. dip both sides of bread into prepared rabdi. 16. and place into plate. make sure to pour 2-3 tbsp of rabdi in plate. 17. further, serve garnished with chopped dry fruits. 18. finally serve bread rasmalai chilled or hot.

title: Bread Roll Recipe (Potato Stuffed Rolls)
ingredients: 1.5 cups potatoes, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.625 tsp red chili powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp amchur, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, Salt, 1 slices bread, Oil, 0.5 cup milk, 2 tbsp flour
instructions: 1. Wash and peel the potatoes. Cut them to 2 and add to a pressure cooker or pot. Pour water just enough to cover the potatoes partially. 2. Pressure cook for 1 to 2 whistles on a medium flame. Potatoes must be just cooked and not mushy. If using carrots and peas, you can just cook them in the same pot after removing potatoes. 3. You can also steam the potatoes in a rice cooker until they are cooked al dente. Move the potatoes to a side, add carrots and peas to the steamer. Steam until the carrots are tender. 4. Mash potatoes or crumble them. Add carrots and peas, slightly mash them. 5. Add chili powder, garam masala, turmeric, salt, coriander leaves and amchur or lemon juice. Mix everything well and taste test to adjust salt and spice levels. 6. Divide the mixture to 10 equal balls and make an oblong or oval shaped balls. Set these aside. 7. Trim the edges of the bread. Begin to roll them with a rolling stick to flatten them. Flatten to a thin sheet. This prevents the bread from absorbing oil. 8. Stir 2 tbps flour with half cup water. Set this aside. Smear the milk or water all over the bread slices with a brush to make it moist and wet. (not with excess dripping water) 9. Smear the flour mix on the edges. Place one ball towards one end and begin to roll. 10. Stick the edges together and seal the inner filling. If needed apply more flour mixture on the edges. 11. Transfer them to a plate and keep them covered to prevent drying. If the rolls dry up the edges will open up. 12. To Bake - preheat the oven to 390 F - 200 C. Brush the rolls with some melted butter. Bake for 10 to 12 mins or until golden on both the sides. 13. Pan frying - Brush the bread rolls with a generous amount of melted butter, ghee or oil. Heat the pan until hot and toast these until golden. These rolls do not get even golden color but will turn crisp. 14. Deep Frying: Heat only a few tbsp oil, just enough to partially cover the rolls while frying. When the oil turns hot, drop a small piece of bread. It must rise without browning quickly. 15. Make sure the oil is hot enough. Slide 2 to 4 rolls at one time depending on the size of the pan used. Fry until golden. Turn them and fry evenly. Remove to kitchen papers. 16. Serve bread roll with sauce or green chutney.

title: bread roll recipe | stuffed bread roll | bread potato rolls
ingredients: 3 tsp oil, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp chilli, 3 tbsp peas matar, 3 tbsp sweet corn, 3 potato aloo, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam amasala, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tbsp panner cottage cheese, 2 tbsp coriander, 6 slice bread, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 3 tsp oil and saute 1 onion until it changes colour. 2. add 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 1 tsp chilli. saute well. 3. now add 3 tbsp peas and 3 tbsp sweet corn. saute and cook until they are almost cooked yet hold their shape. 4. further, add 3 boiled and mashed potato. i have pressure cooked potato for 4 whistles. 5. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp aamchur and ½ tsp salt. 6. mix well until the spices are well combined. 7. furthermore, add 3 tbsp panner and mix well. 8. additionally, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. allow to cool the stuffing completely. 9. furthermore, take bread slices and cut the sides. 10. dip the bread slice into the water just for a second and squeeze off the excess water. 11. make sure to squeeze the bread slice completely else it will absorb oil while deep frying. 12. place the cylindrical shaped prepared stuffing. 13. and wrap the bread making sure to pinch the edges and secure tight. if unable to hold the shape dip your finger in water and make bread flexible to hold and roll. 14. further, with the help of both hands roll and make a cylindrical shape. 15. deep fry the prepared bread roll into the hot oil. make sure to fry in hot oil, else bread will absorb oil and turn soggy. 16. stir and fry occasionally on medium flame till it turns golden brown. alternatively, bake the bread to golden brown if you are diet conscious. 17. finally, serve stuffed bread roll hot along with ketchup.

title: Bread Tikki Recipe | Potato Bread Tikki
ingredients: 2 slice bread, 1 potato, 0.5 cup paneer, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 chilli, breadcrumbs, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, take 2 bread slices and pour water over them. 2. squeeze off the water and transfer the bread slices to a large bowl. 3. add 1 potato, ½ cup paneer, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur and ½ tsp salt. 4. squeeze and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 5. add 2 tbsp corn flour, ½ onion, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 chilli and mix well. 6. grease hand with oil and shape the tikki. 7. roll in breadcrumbs to get crunchy outer surface. 8. deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 9. stir occasionally, until the tikki turns golden brown and crispy. 10. drain off to remove excess oil. 11. finally, enjoy bread tikki recipe with chutney or sauce.

title: Bread Upma for Breakfast
ingredients: 2.5 slices bread, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.75 teaspoon ginger paste, 1 green chili, 9 cashew nuts, 0.25 cup onions, 0.5 cup ripe tomatoes, salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 3.5 tablespoon water, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, Lemon juice
instructions: 1. Heat a pan and toast the bread until crisp on both the sides. Cool completely. Cut them to small cubes and set aside. 2. Pour oil to the same pan and on a medium heat fry cashews or peanuts until golden, crunchy and aromatic. Set these aside with bread. 3. Add mustard seeds and urad dal (optional). Saute till urad dal turns lightly golden. 4. Next add green chilies and curry leaves. Saute until the leaves turn crisp. 5. Sprinkle hing. Add onions and saute until they turn golden. Saute the ginger paste as well for 30 seconds. 6. Next add tomatoes, salt and turmeric. Note that your bread is salted so add only as required, start with ¼ teaspoon. Saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 7. Then add chili powder and sugar. Saute for 1 to 2 mins until the raw smell goes off. 8. Pour 3 to 4 tablespoons water and mix. 9. Cook until the mixture turns thick. Taste test and adjust salt if required. 10. Add bread cubes and cashews. Mix gently to coat the bread with the onion tomato masala. 11. Sprinkle coriander leaves. 12. Serve bread upma hot. Sprinkle some lemon juice.

title: bread upma recipe | south indian bread upma recipe
ingredients: 6 slices bread, 3 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds rai, few curry leaves kadi patta, 0.5 medium sized onion, 1 green chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste adrak paste, 2 medium sized tomatoes, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp kashmiri red chili pwoder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp sugar, salt to taste, 0.5 sized capsicum, 2 tbsp water, 3 tbsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a toaster or tawa toast the bread to golden brown and crisp. 2. further, cut the bread slices into cubes and keep aside. 3. in a large kadai heat oil. 4. further add mustard seeds and curry leaves. 5. saute till the mustard seeds to splutter. 6. furthermore, add onions, ginger paste and green chilli. 7. saute till onions sweat and turn translucent. 8. now add tomatoes and saute well. 9. saute till the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 10. add turmeric, chili powder, sugar and salt. 11. saute for a minute on low flame till the spices are cooked well. 12. now add capsicum and continue to saute. 13. do not over cook and make sure to retain the crunchiness of capsicum. 14. add 2 tbsp of water and mix the mixture well. 15. further add toasted bread pieces. 16. and mix gently without breaking the bread pieces. 17. also cover and simmer for 2-3 minutes, making sure the bread absorbs masala. 18. finally, serve bread upma garnished with coriander leaves and lemon wedges.

title: Bread Uttapam Recipe | Instant Bread Dosa Recipe | Instant Uttapam
ingredients: 3 slices bread, 0.5 cup rava suji, 0.5 cup curd, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 2 green chilli, pinch of salt
instructions: 1. Take white or brown bread and cut into small pieces. 2. Add the bread pieces to a mixer jar. 3. Add rava, curd and water. 4. Grind to a smooth batter. 5. Transfer the batter to a large bowl. 6. Add salt and baking soda. Mix well. 7. Keep the batter aside for a few minutes. 8. In another bowl, add onion, carrot, coriander, curry leaves, chilli and salt. 9. Mix well. Vegetable topping is ready. 10. Heat a tawa and pour one ladleful of batter. 11. Do not spread the batter. Keep it thick like uttapa. 12. Add vegetable topping evenly on top. 13. Drizzle oil around the uttapa. 14. Roast on low flame until the bottom turns golden. 15. Flip and cook the other side. Roast until both sides are cooked well. 16. Finally, bread uttapa is ready to serve hot with coconut chutney, podi or sauce.

title: Bread Vada Recipe
ingredients: 3 slices bread, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1 red chilli, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, salt, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoons rice flour, 2 tablespoons water, oil
instructions: 1. Tear the bread slices to pieces. Add them to a mixing bowl. 2. Add ginger, carrots, onions, pudina, coriander leaves, salt, green chili, red chili, cumin, garam masala. Mix them gently and check if water is needed. If the veggies used has lot of moisture then you may not need any water. 3. Add 1 to 2 tbsp water if needed and begin to mix. Add rice flour and make a non-sticky dough. 4. Heat oil for frying. Divide the mixture to 7 to 8 parts and make balls. 5. Flatten them gently and make a patty. Do not make thick patties, they should be thin otherwise the vada will have the taste of dough. 6. Gently slide these in hot oil and fry on a medium heat. These tend to turn brown quickly, so keep watching. 7. Fry them stirring often and turn to the other side. When bread vada turns golden, remove them to a kitchen tissue. 8. Serve bread vada hot with any chutney or tea. 9. Preheat your air fryer at 390 F (200 C) for at least 15 mins. Brush the vadas with oil or ghee and place then in the basket and air fry for 10 to 12 mins, turning them to the other side after 5 to 6 mins. 10. Adjust the timings as needed depending on your air fryer and the size of your vadas. I prefer to brush them with ghee after they are done. 11. Preheat the oven to 400 F (210 C) for at least 15 mins, Brush the bread vada with oil or ghee and place them on a parchment paper. 12. Bake them for 14 to 16 mins until crisp and golden, depending on the size of your vadas.

title: bread vada recipe | bread palak vada recipe | palak bread vada
ingredients: 4 slice bread, 0.5 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup rava semolina suji, 1 green chilli, 0.75 cup curd yogurt, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 onion, 1 bunch spinach palak, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, oil for deep frying, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl break 4 slice bread into small pieces. you can alternatively crumble them in food processor. 2. add in ½ cup rice flour, ¼ cup rava and ¾ cup curd. 3. also add 1 green chilli, ½ tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 onion, 1 bunch spinach, ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp chaat masala and ½ tsp salt. 4. combine well making sure to squeeze and mix. 5. prepare a soft dough, adding water if required. 6. now grease hands with oil and pinch a small ball sized dough and flatten it. 7. deep fry in hot oil or bake them in preheated oven at 180 degree celsius for 25 minutes or till it turns golden and crisp. 8. stir occasionally, and fry on medium flame till it turns golden and crisp. 9. finally, serve bread palak vada with coconut chutney or tomato sauce.

title: bread vada recipe | instant bread carrot vada | crispy instant vada
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 0.25 cup rice flour, 3 tbsp rava semolina suji, 1 cup curd yoghurt, 0.5 cup carrot, 0.5 onion, 2 green chilli, 0.25 tsp ginger paste, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp salt, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl break 4 slice bread into small pieces. you can alternatively crumble them in a food processor. 2. also add ¼ cup rice flour, 3 tbsp rava and mix well. 3. further add 1 cup curd, ½ cup carrot, ½ onion, 2 green chilli, ¼ tsp ginger paste, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander leaves 4. additionally add ¼ tsp pepper, ½ tsp cumin and ½ tsp salt. 5. combine well making sure to squeeze and mix. 6. prepare a soft dough, adding water or bread crumbs if required. 7. now grease hands with oil and pinch a small ball sized dough and flatten it. 8. deep fry in hot oil or bake them in a preheated oven at 180-degree celsius for 15 minutes or till it turns golden and crisp. 9. stir occasionally, and fry on medium flame till it turns golden and crisp. 10. finally, serve bread vada with coconut chutney or tomato sauce.

title: Brinjal Curry Recipe (Eggplant Curry)
ingredients: 0.5 kg eggplants, 3.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 0.75 tablespoon garlic fine chopped, 0.5 tablespoon ginger fine chopped, 0.5 cup green peas, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.5 tablespoon sambar powder, 0.875 cup water stock, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Dice ½ kg (1 lb.) eggplants to 1 inch pieces and add to a large bowl. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon salt & enough pour water to cover them. Rest for 15 mins, while you prep up the rest. (If you prefer to oven roast them, add only half tsp salt, omit adding water, instead let them sit for 10 mins in a colander & later transfer to a prepared baking tray. Drizzle with oil and broil for 8 to 10 mins.) 2. Prep up the following ingredients: 3. 1 large onion fine chopped to make 1½ cup onions (175 grams) 4. Chop or slit 1 green chili (deseed if you want, optional) 5. Fine chop 4 large garlic cloves to make ¾ tablespoon (or use 1½ teaspoon ginger garlic paste) 6. Fine chop half inch ginger to make ½ tablespoon 7. Fine chop or puree 2 medium tomatoes to make 1½ cup (230 grams or use lesser amount of canned) 8. ½ cup green peas (60 grams, optional or use 1 cup canned/cooked chickpeas or black eyed peas) 9. Rinse and pat dry 1 sprig curry leaves (omit if you don’t have) 10. To garnish, fine chop 2 sprigs of coriander leaves – optional 11. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan and add mustard and cumin seeds. When the mustard begins to pop, add the curry leaves. 12. Stir in the onions and green chilies. Saute on a medium heat, for 5 to 6 mins until light golden. Add the ginger garlic and saute for 1 to 2 mins. 13. Add tomatoes and half teaspoon salt. Cook until the tomatoes break down. 14. Stir in the red chili powder and sambar powder or curry powder. 15. Saute for 1 to 2 mins, until fragrant. 16. Pour ¾ to 1 cup water or coconut milk and bring to a boil.

title: Brinjal Fry (Vankaya Fry)
ingredients: 25 grams brinjal, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons Oil, 3 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.5 teaspoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 3 tablespoons peanuts
instructions: 1. Pour 2 cups water to a bowl and stir in ¼ teaspoon salt. Keep this aside. 2. Rinse brinjals & slice them or cut to desired sizes. I chop them to ¾ to 1 inch pieces. Add them to salted water. 3. Heat oil in a wok or a pan. Add cumin & mustard. Let them splutter. 4. Add ginger garlic paste & green chilies. Saute quickly until aromatic for a minute. 5. Now add chopped brinjal, salt & turmeric. Stir fry on a medium high flame for 3 mins. 6. Then cover and cook till soft on a low flame stirring in between. While these cook, proceed to the next step. 7. Optional: This brinjal fry recipe can be made without peanuts too but they impart a nutty taste. So this step is optional. Dry roast or air fry them until golden and aromatic. Cool & crush them coarsely either in a mortar pestle or grinder. (check pics in the post) 8. When the brinjals are completely cooked and soft, add curry leaves, crushed peanuts & garam masala (or idli podi). 9. Stir fry on a medium heat until aromatic for 2 to 3 mins. Taste test and add red chilli powder & salt if you want. 10. Turn off and when the brinjal fry cools down a bit, pour lemon juice if you want. 11. Serve brinjal fry with rice, chapati or Rasam-rice or Sambar-rice.

title: brinji rice recipe | vegetable brinji | veg brinji | brinji sadam
ingredients: handful coriander, handful mint pudina, 2 pods cardamom, 4 clove 4 clove, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp fennel saunf, 2 tbsp water, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tbsp oil, 4 bay leaf tej patta brinji ilai, 1 star anise, 2 pods cardamom, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 3 chilli, 1 tomato, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp peas matar, 0.5 potato aloo, 7 florets cauliflower gobi, 5 beans, 1 tsp salt, few curry leaves, 1 cup water, 1 cup coconut milk, 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tbsp coriander chopped, 2 tbsp mint pudina
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare the masala paste by taking handful coriander, handful mint, 2 pods cardamom, 4 clove, ½ inch cinnamon, 1 tsp fennel in a blender. 2. blend to smooth paste adding 2 tbsp water. 3. now in a cooker heat 2 tbsp ghee and 1 tbsp oil. 4. add 4 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 2 pods cardamom and saute until it turns aromatic. 5. further add 1 onion followed by 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 3 chilli. saute well. 6. also add 1 tomato and saute until tomato turn soft and mushy. 7. now add ½ carrot, 2 tbsp peas, ½ potato, 7 florets cauliflower, 5 beans and 1 tsp salt. 8. saute for a minute until vegies turn aromatic. 9. additionally, add in prepared masala paste and few curry leaves. 10. continue to saute for a minute until raw flavour disappears. 11. pour 1 cup water and 1 cup coconut milk. mix well and get to a rolling boil. 12. further add 1 cup basmati rice (soaked 20 minuteand give a good stir. 13. spreadingly add 2 tbsp coriander and 2 tbsp mint. 14. cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles on medium flame. 15. finally, cool slightly and enjoy brinji rice with raita.

title: Broccoli Corn Carrot Stir Fry
ingredients: 1 cup broccoli, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.5 cup sweet corn, 2 garlic cloves, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Heat 3 to 4 cups of water in a large pot. Stir in some salt and add broccoli florets. Leave it for 2 minutes, Rinse them under running water. If you want you may add them to boiling water and rest for 2 mins. Drain off to a colander. 2. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. When the oil turns hot, add cumin seeds. As soon as they begin to sizzle, add 2 chopped garlic cloves or 1 teaspoon chopped ginger. If you want you may add 1 chopped green chili for heat. Let them fry until aromatic, for about a minute. 3. Add 1 cup broccoli florets, ½ cup corn and ½ cup chopped carrots. Begin to saute on a medium high heat, until they are almost cooked & tender. 4. Add ¼ teaspoon red chili powder (or flakes), ¼ teaspoon garam masala and ¼ teaspoon salt and stir fry for a while. A good garam masala adds a nice flavor to the dish. You can also you kitchen king masala and curry leaves if you have. When the veggies cool down a bit, I add 1 tsp lemon juice and mix well. 5. If you want to make it Chinese style, leave out garam masala and red chilli powder. Instead add 2 tsps soya sauce and black pepper or any hot chilli sauce. 6. Serve broccoli corn carrot stir fry as a side in a meal or with roti, paratha. Or spread it over a bread slice, place a cheese slice and cover with another bread slice. Butter the bread and toast until crisp. 7. Broccoli Stir Fry... Zucchini Stir Fry... Cabbage Stir Fry ... Mushroom Pepper F... Cauliflower Stir ... Baby Corn Fry Rec...

title: Broccoli Stir Fry Curry (Indian Style)
ingredients: 0.75 cup potatoes, 2.5 cups broccoli florets, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chili, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
instructions: 1. Preparation: Add broccoli florets to warm water and rest for 2 mins. Drain, rinse and drain to a colander or a basket. Shake off to get rid of excess water. Add peeled & chopped potatoes to a bowl of water until used. 2. Make the broccoli stir fry curry: Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add chopped garlic and chilies. Saute for 30 to 40 seconds, remove the potatoes from water and add them. 3. Stir fry for a minute and cook covered on a medium heat till al dente, yet fork tender. Stir in the turmeric, chili, garam masala, coriander powder and cumin powder. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of water to deglaze the pan and add the broccoli florets and salt. 4. Stir fry for 3 to 4 minutes, until tender and cooked through yet crunchy. Taste test and adjust salt. Sprinkle lemon juice as needed. Remove broccoli to a serving bowl and garnish with sesame or roasted peanuts. 5. Heat 3 to 4 cups of water in a pot until slightly hot. Add little salt and add 2 heaped cups of broccoli florets. Rest for 5 minutes, drain and rinse well. Drain. 6. On a medium flame, heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan. Optionally, add 1 pinch of mustard and half teaspoon cumin seeds. When they begin to crackle, add 1 teaspoon grated ginger or ginger garlic paste. Saute for 40 to 50 seconds, until aromatic. 7. Stir in 1 small sliced onion and saute for 4 to 5 mins, until transparent. Add 1 medium finely chopped tomato, ¼ teaspoon salt, 1 pinch of turmeric and saute until the tomatoes break down. 8. Stir in ½ to ¾ teaspoon garam masala, ¼ to ½ red chili powder and saute for one to 2 minutes. 9. Add broccoli, ½ to 1 cup capsicum or peas. Saute until broccoli & bell peppers are tender, yet slightly crunchy. 10. Garnish with 2 tablespoons roasted crushed peanuts if you want. Serve broccoli sabzi with roti or rice.

title: Broken wheat upma
ingredients: 1 cups broken wheat, 3 cups water, 1 onion, 0.25 cup green peas, 1 medium carrot, 1 potato, 2 green chillies, 1 sprig curry leaves, Pinch asafoetida, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp jeera, Salt to taste, 2 tbsp oil
instructions: 1. Dry roast rava on a medium heat till you get a nice aroma. Set aside. 2. Heat oil in a pan or kadai. Add mustard, jeera, and curry leaves. 3. Allow them to splutter. Then add hing , onions, potatoes, carrots, peas and fry for 3 to 4 mins. 4. Pour water. Then add turmeric & salt. Taste the water and add more salt if needed. 5. Bring the water to a rapid boil. Regulate the flame to medium. 6. Add broken wheat in a slow stream stirring with one of your hands. Stir well and close the lid. 7. Cook till the veggies are cooked fully or pressure cook for 1 to 2 whistles on a low flame. If you are not making it in a pressure cooker, then just cook it till it is fully done and the water is absorbed. 8. Once the pressure is down, mix well broken wheat upma and serve with your favorite side. A chutney or pickle or sambar or pappu podi goes well. 9. Dry roast dalia till you begin to get a good aroma. Set this aside to cool. Add oil to a pan or pressure cooker and heat up. Add mustard, jeera and curry leaves. 10. When they begin to crackle add hing, and vegetables of your choice like onions, potatoes, carrots, peas and fry for about 3 to 4 minutes. 11. Pour water, add turmeric salt, bring the water to boil. 12. Add dalia or broken wheat slowly stirring with one of your hands. Stir well to break any lumps. Cover and cook, if using pressure cooker close the lid. 13. Let it cook till dalia is soft cooked, you can add more hot water as needed if you want a porridge like consistency. Keep the pan covered for about 5 to 10 minutes. If using a pressure cooker, let it whistle once or twice on a low flame. When the pressure goes down, mix well and serve broken wheat upma. title: Brown Lentils Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup brown lentils, 0.5 cup red lentils, 4 cups water, 3 tablespoons oil ghee, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3 red chilies, 1 cup onion, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1.5 cup tomato, 4 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 1 tablespoon fenugreek leaves, 1 tablespoon amchur, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder
instructions: 1. Rinse lentils in a large pot and pour water. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat and reduce to medium. Cook until tender, 20 to 25 mins. We are looking for the red lentils to break down completely and the brown lentils to remain in shape. 2. While the lentils cook, chop and begin to make the onion tomato masala. 3. On a medium flame, heat ghee in a large pan. Add cumin and fennel seeds, followed by dried red chilies. 4. Once the seeds begin to sizzle and chilies turn crisp (not burnt), add onions and saute until light golden. Stir in ginger garlic and coriander stems. Saute till aromatic for 1 to 2 mins. 5. Stir in turmeric, salt, chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder (optional) and garam masala, followed by tomatoes. Add ¼ cup water and cook until the tomatoes break down completely. 6. Add the lentils along with the liquid in the pot and mix well. Pour hot water as needed (about ½ cup) and bring to a boil. Simmer for 5 minutes until thick and traces of fats visible on top. (Check photo in the post) 7. Optionally mash the lentils a bit, for a thicker & creamy consistency. Stir in amchur & fenugreek . Taste test and more salt, chili flakes or garam masala as needed. 8. Serve brown lentils, with a garnish of coriander leaves. Squeeze some lemon juice if you want. Serve it with a side of cucumber salad, quinoa or rice. 9. Follow all the above steps on a saute mode. Pressure cook for 10 mins and let the pressure drop naturally. Open the lid and mix. If it is too thick, pour little hot water and saute for 1 to 2 mins. Stir in amchur and fenugreek.

title: Brown Rice Dosa
ingredients: 0.5 cup skinned urad dal, 0.25 cup chana dal, 2 cups raw short thin brown rice, 0.25 cup poha, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons oil, 0.875 cup water, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse well at least thrice. Pour fresh water and soak for no longer than 6 hours. 2. To another bowl, add chana dal and urad dal. Rinse well at least thrice and soak for no longer than 6 hours. Rinse and soak poha in enough water to cover, for no longer than 30 mins before grinding. 3. When you are ready to grind the batter, drain the water from rice and dal. Rinse them well again. 4. Add the chana dal, urad dal and poha along with the poha soaked water to a grinder, Pour water and grind to a smooth and bubbly batter. Transfer to a large mixing bowl. 5. Grind brown rice with water to a smooth or slightly coarse batter. Transfer it to the mixing bowl. 6. Mix both the batter well and cover with a lid. Ferment overnight or as required until the batter rises and turns slightly bubbly. Check pics in the post & read pro tips section. Do not over ferment to the extent the batter turns sour and rises a lot. 7. Take the required amount of batter to a bowl. You can stir the remaining batter with a ladle and refrigerate for up to a week. 8. Add salt as required and mix well. If needed add some water to bring it to a consistency. It must be thick yet of pouring and spreading consistency. 9. Heat a cast iron tawa and add few tablespoons of oil. Carefully spread it with a kitchen tissue or a cut onion. If you are using a non-stick you don't need to grease it. 10. When the pan turns hot, regulate the flame to medium and pour a ladle full of batter to the center of the tawa. 11. Quickly begin to spread evenly starting from the center in a circular motion in one direction (clockwise or anit-clockwise) to make a thin dosa/crepe. 12. Drizzle a teaspoon of oil all over the dosa. Let the brown rice dosa cook on a medium heat until lightly brown and crisp. 13. Turn it to the other side and cook just a minute. Turn it again and cook for 30 to 40 seconds to make it extra crisp. Remove to a serving plate. 14. To make the next one make sure the tawa is hot enough and not smoking hot. If it is smoking hot, your dosas will brown a lot without cooking well. 15. Serve brown rice dosas with peanut chutney, coconut chutney, carrot chutney or beetroot chunty.

title: Brownie Recipe | Eggless Chocolate Brownies
ingredients: 15 grams dark chocolate, 6 grams butter, 1 cup condensed milk milkmaid, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 1 gram maida, 2 grams cocoa powder, 0.25 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. First, melt the chocolate using a double-boiler method. Heat some water in a saucepan and place a bowl on top, ensuring the bottom of the bowl does not touch the water. Add 150 grams dark chocolate and 60 grams butter to the bowl. 2. Stir gently until the chocolate is fully melted. This method helps to melt the chocolate evenly without burning. 3. Once the chocolate is melted, let it cool slightly. 4. Now add 1 cup condensed milk and 1 tsp vanilla extract. 5. Mix well until everything is fully combined. 6. Place a sieve over the bowl and add 100 grams maida, 20 grams cocoa powder, ¼ tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp baking soda, and ¼ tsp salt. 7. Sift the ingredients to ensure there are no lumps. 8. Gently mix the batter using the cut-and-fold method. 9. Mix slowly and do not overmix, as this can make the brownie hard. The batter should have a thick ribbon consistency. 10. Pour the batter into a cake pan. Tap the pan lightly to level the surface and remove any air bubbles. 11. Preheat the oven and bake at 160°C for 25 minutes. 12. Once baked, allow the brownie to cool completely in the tray. This step is important to set the perfect crackly layer texture. 13. Finally, cut into pieces and enjoy the Chocolate Brownie Recipe.

title: Brussels Sprouts Curry (Indian Sabzi)
ingredients: 9 oz Small Brussel sprouts, 0.625 cup onions, 0.625 cup tomato, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 garlic cloves, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 cup green peas, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Make sure you use fresh and smaller brussels sprouts in this recipe. Remove yellow leaves if any over the brussels sprouts. Prepare the following: 2. 250 grams (9 oz) Small Brussel sprouts – Rinsed well, pat dry, remove the core and quarter them 3. Fine chop 1 medium onion to make ½ to ¾ cup fine chopped onions 4. Deseed and chop 1 large tomato to make ½ to ¾ cup chopped tomatoes 5. Slit or chop 1 green chili (optional) 6. 2 garlic cloves chopped 7. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan. Add ½ to ¾ teaspoon cumin seeds and garlic. 8. Let the garlic fry in the oil until it begins to smell good for a minute. Add onions and saute until light golden. 9. Next add tomatoes, ¼ to ½ teaspoon salt and ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. Saute until the tomatoes turn mushy. If needed cook covered till they become soft. 10. Add the green chili, ¼ to ½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, ½ teaspoon fennel seeds powder (saunf) and 1 teaspoon coriander powder & ¼ ½ teaspoon garam masala (optional). 11. Next saute until the masala smells good, for 1 to 2 mins. 12. Add Brussel sprouts and ½ to ¾ cup frozen green peas. Saute for 3 to 4 mins on a medium heat. 13. Sprinkle 2 to 3 tablespoons water. Regulate the flame to low. Cover and cook until fork tender. Keep stirring in between to prevent burning. It can take about 10 minutes to longer to cook them well. Avoid adding too much water as excess water alters the flavors. 14. Lastly open the lid and saute for 2 to 3 mins. Taste test and adjust salt as required. Sprinkle coriander leaves. Switch off. Add lemon juice and mix. Serve Brussel sprouts curry with rice or roti. Mushroom Pepper F... Cabbage Sabzi ( C... Cabbage Curry Cabbage Stir Fry ... Broccoli Stir Fry... Zucchini Stir Fry...

title: bun dosa recipe | soft spongy thick dosa | how to make soft bun dosa
ingredients: 2 cup raw rice, 0.5 tsp methi, 1 cup poha aval beaten rice, 1 cup coconut, water, 1 tsp salt, 3 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 3 chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup raw rice, ½ tsp methi. 2. rinse well and soak in water for 4 hours. 3. after 4 hours, drain off the water and transfer to the mixi. 4. blend to a smooth paste adding water as required. 5. transfer the rice batter to a large bowl. 6. now in a mixi take 1 cup poha, 1 cup coconut and ¾ cup water. 7. blend to smooth batter. 8. transfer the paste into the same bowl. 9. adding water as required mix well. 10. cover and allow to ferment in a warm place for 8 hours. 11. after 8 hours, the batter has well fermented. 12. now to prepare the tempering, heat 3 tsp oil. 13. splutter 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 3 chilli and few curry leaves. 14. pour the tempering over the batter and mix well. 15. also, add 1 tsp salt and give a good mix. 16. now heat the appe pan and grease the tawa with oil. pour a ladleful of batter over the pan. 17. cover and simmer for 3 minutes. make sure not to spread the batter. 18. once the base turns golden brown, flip over the dosa. 19. cook on both sides making sure the dosa is cooked uniformly. 20. finally, enjoy bun dosa with coconut chutney.

title: Butter Cake Recipe (Moist Basic Cake)
ingredients: 2 grams all-purpose flour, 25 grams butter, 2 teaspoons baking powder, 2 grams castor sugar, 4 eggs, 6 ml milk, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.125 tablespoons pure vanilla extract
instructions: 1. Grease a 7 inch round or 8 inch square pan and line it with parchment paper. Preheat the oven to 360 F or 180 C for a minimum of 15 minutes. 2. Make sure your ingredients are room temperature. Crack your eggs into a small bowl. 3. Mix flour, salt and baking powder. Sift/ Sieve and set aside. I did mine directly over the mixing bowl later at step 13. 4. Add butter to a mixing bowl. It has to be soft but cold. 5. Add fine sugar. I used organic fine sugar. If you have granulated sugar then pulse it in a blender to get fine sugar. Make sure you don’t make a fine powder. 6. Mix sugar and butter on a low speed till well combined. Next set to medium speed and beat until pale, light and fluffy. Scrape the sides and bottom in between. The creamed butter and sugar turns fluffy with soft peaks. The color too changes and turns light. Sugar does not dissolve at this stage. 7. Add one egg at a time. 8. Beat after each addition until creamy. 9. Scrape down the sides & bottom before adding the next egg. 10. Continue to add one egg at one time and beat until the mixture is creamy. 11. Add in vanilla extract. Mix until well combined. 12. We are going to add flour with salt & baking powder in 3 parts. Divide the flour into 3 parts, add one part at a time. Mix on lowest or medium speed, and scrape down the sides. 13.
