I directly placed the sieve and added the dry ingredients – flour, salt and baking powder. If you are doing this, mix gently all the 3 ingredients. 14. Sieve only 1/3 rd of the total flour. 15. Mix on lowest or medium speed, and scrape down the sides and bottom as well. 16. Continue adding the flour in parts of 1/3. Mix on lowest or medium speed until well combined. 17. Add milk as needed in parts. Mix on a medium speed. 18. The cake batter is smooth, creamy, lump free and more importantly without any streaks of egg etc. 19. Transfer the batter to the greased tin. Even out the top to prevent uneven cake. and knock it to the kitchen counter few times to distribute the batter evenly. This is very important else the cake will raise unevenly. 20. Bake the cake at 360 F or 180 C for 45 to 60 mins depending on the size of the pan. Details mentioned below. After 25 to 30 mins, cover the cake pan with a foil. Do not bring the cake out of the oven. Just place a foil over the tin in the oven directly. Take care, wear oven mitts and do it. 21. I baked for 55 mins. When the cake is done, a skewer inserted comes out clean. 22. Cool for about 5 to 10 mins and then invert it over a rack. 23. Cool it completely before frosting. I went ahead dusting powdered sugar and cocoa. 24. This cake stays good at room temperature for 24 to 48 hours depending on the temperature or climatic conditions. If you intend to frost it, you can leave it at room temperature and frost after few hours. This also freezes well. You can also refrigerate and heat up for 30 to 40 seconds in microwave.

title: Butter Chicken Recipe (Indian Chicken Makhani)
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.875 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.3333333333333333 cup Greek yogurt, 0.75 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.875 tablespoon oil, 2.5 tablespoons butter, 2 inch cinnamon, 3 green cardamoms, 3 cloves 2 to 4 cloves ((optional)), 1.5 cups onions, 6 grams tomatoes, 0.75 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chilies, 28 whole raw cashews, 0.5 cup water, 1.5 teaspoons Kashmiri chili powder, 1.25 teaspoons garam masala, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 tablespoon Kasuri methi, 1.5 cups water, 0.3333333333333333 cup heavy cream, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with lemon juice, chili powder and salt. Cover and rest for 20 minutes. Next add yogurt, kasuri methi, oil, turmeric, ginger garlic paste, coriander powder, cumin powder and garam masala powder. 2. Marinate and rest for a minimum of 30 minutes, or overnight for best results. Cover and refrigerate. 3. Optional – If using onion, sauté in 1 tablespoon oil until lightly golden, for 7 minutes. Cool down. 4. To a blender, add tomatoes, sautéed onions, cashews and water. Blend to a smooth puree. If you want you can also blend the cashews separately with water to a smooth cream. 5. Remove the chicken from the refrigerator, to bring it close to room temperature. 6. Melt butter in a pan & add all the whole spices – cinnamon, cloves and cardamoms. 7. When they begin to sizzle, stir in the ginger garlic paste & green chilies. Fry on a low heat, for a minute or 2, till it turns fragrant but not burnt. 8. Turn off the heat and stir in red chili powder, garam masala, cumin and coriander powder. 9. Stir in the tomato cashew puree. If it is not smooth, strain it to the pan. 10. Mix well and cover partially. Bring it to a boil on a medium high heat, reduce the heat to low or medium. Cook until the puree turns thick, keep stirring occasionally. 11. Pour hot water and simmer for 10 mins, until the sauce thickens & traces of fats are visible on top of the sauce. Remove the whole spices and discard at this stage. 12. Spread one tbsp. butter or ghee in a griddle/pan and heat it well. Place the marinated chicken pieces, spacing them a little apart. 13. Fry on a medium heat for 2 mins and turn them. Don’t burn and do not overcook. Cook till the chicken is just cooked and all the marinade dries up. It is not necessary to fully cook the chicken at this stage. 14. This way roast the chicken pieces in 2 to 3 batches, depending on the size of your skillet. 15. Add chicken to the butter chicken gravy. Pour more hot water (about ½ cup, not in the recipe) if the sauce is too thick. Cover and simmer for about 5 to 7 minutes till it becomes tender. 16. Stir in salt, sugar and kasuri methi. Taste test and optionally add more garam masala & another tablespoon butter for additional flavour (both are optional). 17. Turn off and stir in the heavy cream. Garnish Butter Chicken with chopped coriander leaves and some cream. Serve with Turmeric rice, steamed Basmati Rice, Butter naan or Cumin Rice.

title: Butter Dal Fry Recipe | How to Make Butter Dal & Jeera Rice - Dhaba Style
ingredients: 0.25 cup toor dal, 0.25 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup moong dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp oil, 2.25 cup water, 2 tbsp butter, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 3 clove garlic, 0.5 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tomato, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker take ¼ cup toor dal, ¼ cup chana dal ¼ cup moong dal. make sure to soak the dal atleast for 20 minutes. 2. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt and 1 tsp oil. 3. add 2¼ cup water and pressure cook for 5 whistles. 4. cook until the dal is cooked well. keep aside. 5. in a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp butter. add 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 chilli, slit, 1 inch ginger and 3 clove garlic. 6. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 7. now add ½ onion and saute slightly. 8. further, add ½ tsp turmeric and 1 tsp chilli powder. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 9. add 1 tomato and saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 10. pour in cooked dal and ½ tsp salt. 11. mix well combining everything. 12. furthermore, add 1 cup water or as required adjusting the consistency. 13. simmer for 3 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 14. now add 2 tbsp coriander and ¼ tsp garam masala. mix well. 15. finally, enjoy butter dal fry with jeera rice. 16. firstly, soak 1 cup basmati rice for 20 minutes. 17. now in a large vessel take enough water. 18. add 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp oil and get to a boil. 19. add in soaked rice and boil for 5 minutes. 20. once the rice is cooked well, drain off and keep aside. 21. in a pan heat 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp pepper and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 22. further add cooked rice, ½ tsp salt and 2 tbsp coriander. 23. mix gently, without breaking the rice grains. 24. finally, enjoy jeera rice with dal.

title: Butter Murukku Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups rice flour, 2 tablespoons fried gram flour, 3 tablespoons besan, 1.5 tablespoons soft butter, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, Water, 1 pinch hing, 0.75 teaspoon cumin, oil
instructions: 1. Measure and keep all the ingredients ready. Powder 2 to 3 tbsp fried gram in a mixer/ grinder jar. 2. Measure 2 tbsp flour and set aside. 3. Grease the murukku maker and put on the star plate. 4. Optional - You can sieve together rice flour, gram flour and besan if the flour is not fine. 5. Mix together rice flour, fried gram flour, besan, salt, hing , cumin and soft butter. Make sure the butter incorporates well and evenly with the flour. Set this aside. 6. Heat oil on a medium flame for deep frying. While the oil heats up knead the dough. 7. Pour water little by little as needed and mix together to form a smooth crack free dough. 8. Divide the dough to 3 parts. 9. Fill the mould with one of the part. Keep the rest of the dough covered with a moist cloth. 10. Check if the oil is hot by testing. To test add a small flat piece of dough to the hot oil. If the dough raises the oil is hot enough to fry. 11. Press murukku maker over the hot oil gently to release the dough. You can also make them to spirals on a moist cloth. Gently lift them and drop in the hot oil. 12. Do not crowd the pan as the murukku needs enough space to fry crisp. Also do not disturb them for a minute otherwise they will break as the dough is light. 13. After a minute stir them often to fry evenly. When they turn golden, drain them on a kitchen tissue. Repeat to make more until the entire dough finishes. 14. Cool butter murukku and store in a airtight jar.

title: Butter Naan Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour, 0.5 cup yogurt, 7 tablespoons luke warm milk, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 teaspoons sugar, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.25 teaspoon garlic, 0.5 teaspoon baking powder, 0.25 teaspoon baking soda, 5.5 garlic cloves, 3 tablespoons melted butter, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1 tsp kalonji seeds
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl add flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, sugar and grated garlic . (If using instant yeast in place of baking powder and baking soda add it now. To use dry active yeast, refer notes.) 2. Pour yogurt, oil and 4 tbsps luke warm milk. Begin to mix to form a smooth dough adding more warm milk as needed. You may need up to half cup milk. 3. Knead well until the dough turns soft, elastic & pliable. When you press down the dough with your finger, it should dent easily. 4. Optionally you can cover and rest the dough for 1 to 2 hours in a warm place. You can also make them instantly like I made here. (For yeast dough, rest in a warm place for 2 hours or until the dough rises & doubles, Then punch the dough one to 2 times to deflate slightly). 5. Grease your fingers and divide the dough to 6 to 8 parts depending on the size you prefer. 6. Make smooth balls and keep them covered until you finish rolling the naans. 7. Sprinkle little flour on the rolling area and place a dough ball. 8. Roll the ball to 8 to 9 inches long and 5 to 6 inches wide depending on the size of the dough ball. 9. Sprinkle kalonji seeds, sliced garlic and coriander leaves. Press down or roll them gently. 10. When you are done with 5 to 6 naans, begin to heat a griddle or pan. 11. Ensure the pan is hot enough, then transfer the naan to it. Within a minute or so bubbles will be visible on top. 12. With the help of a tong, flip it and cook either on the pan or directly over the gas fire or stove. Or place a wired rack on the stove and toast it. (check video or step by step photos). 13. When you see brown spots on the naan, remove them to a plate and brush with some butter. Ensure it is cooked through on both the sides well. 14. Smear some butter on top and serve naan right away with paneer butter masala, dal makhani, butter chicken or any other curry.

title: buttermilk vada recipe | instant mor vadai | crispy chaas vada recipe
ingredients: 1 cup poha aval flattened rice, 0.5 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup buttermilk, 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger paste, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 1 tbsp sesame, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp coconut, 1 tsp cumin, 0.25 cup rice flour, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl place a sieve and rinse 1 cup poha. 2. transfer the rinsed poha to a large bowl and add ½ cup rava. 3. also, add ½ tsp salt, 1 cup buttermilk and mix well. 4. rest for 20 minutes or until the poha and rava absorbs buttermilk. 5. after 20 minutes, the poha and rava have softened well. 6. now add 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger paste, ½ tsp chilli flakes, 1 tbsp sesame, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp coconut and 1 tsp cumin. 7. squeeze and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 8. further, add ¼ cup rice flour and mix forming a soft dough. 9. grease hands with oil and pinch a ball sized dough. 10. flatten slightly and make a hole in the centre. 11. deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 12. stir occasionally, and fry on medium flame until the vada turns golden and crisp. 13. drain off the vada on kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 14. finally, enjoy buttermilk vada with chutney or tomato sauce.

title: Butternut Squash Curry Recipe
ingredients: 7 grams butternut squash, 1 cup onion, 1 cup tomatoes, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 4 cups baby spinach, 1 cup black eyed peas, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 1.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.75 cup coconut milk, 0.5 cup water, 1 tablespoon butter, 2 medium garlic
instructions: 1. On a medium flame, heat oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven. Add cinnamon and curry leaves, followed by onions. 2. Saute until light golden for 5 mins and add ginger garlic. Saute for a minute and stir in all the ground spices - turmeric, salt, red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder and cumin. 3. Stir in the tomatoes and cook until they lose the raw flavor and turn thick/mushy. Mix the butternut squash and pour water, followed by coconut or nut milk. 4. Cover and cook for 3 to 4 minutes and later add the black eyed peas. (Your black eyed peas should be soft/tender and get mashed easily when you press down. If they are undercooked they will remain the same and won't soften in the curry) 5. Cover and cook until fork tender but not mushy. Mine takes about 12 mins to cook but it may be different for you. If required you may add more hot water as it cooks. 6. Optional - Meanwhile, if using spinach, melt butter in a skillet on a medium flame and fry sliced garlic for a minute. Add the spinach and saute for 3 to 4 mins until wilted. 7. When the butternut squash is fork tender, add the sauteed garlic butter spinach and mix well. 8. Taste test to adjust salt and more garam masala if required. If the curry is runny, mash some of the butternut squash pieces with a spoon and mix well. 9. Serve butternut squash curry over rice/quinoa or with flatbreads chapati, roti, tandoori roti, naan or with bhakri.

title: Butterscotch Ice Cream Recipe
ingredients: 1 can sweetened condensed milk adjust to taste, 3 cups heavy cream, 1 tablespoon vanilla extract, 0.25 cup Praline, 1 cup dark brown sugar, 1 cup heavy cream, 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, 1 tablespoon vanilla extract, 1 pinch salt
instructions: 1. Add butter to a saucepan and begin to heat on a low heat. 2. As the butter begin to melt, add brown sugar and 1 cup heavy cream. Stir together and bring it to a boil on a low to medium heat. 3. Boil for 5 minutes on a medium heat, scraping down the sides every 2 mins. The sauce turns slightly thicker but still looks way runny but it turns thicker after cooling. 4. Pour vanilla extract. Turn off the heat and cool completely. After cooling down it should be thick and pourable. 5. Store butterscotch sauce in a cool dry jar and refrigerate. This keeps good for about 3 to 4 weeks. 6. Chill a large bowl and the whisk or attachments of the stand/ hand mixer for at least 24 hours in the freezer. 7. Pour the chilled cream to the bowl. Begin to whip on a low speed and then on a medium speed until stiff peaks form. 8. Pour condensed milk and butter scotch sauce (saving ¼ cup for later). Gently fold the whipped cream, taking care not to deflate it. 9. Taste test the ice cream. Add more butterscotch sauce for more sweetness. 10. Transfer to a metal loaf pan. Drizzle some butter scotch sauce & sprinkle praline. Place a cling wrap & press it down over to the mixture to avoid ice crystals. 11. Freeze overnight or more until the ice cream is set. 12. Scoop the ice cream to serving bowls, Drizzle butterscotch sauce and praline before serving.

title: Buttery Garlic Green Beans Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoons oil, 45 grams green beans, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 1 tablespoon unsalted butter, 1.25 tablespoon garlic minced, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon red chili flakes, 3 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Prep up the following ingredients 2. 450 grams green beans (1 pound or 4 cups chopped) (I prefer to chop mine to 2 inch portions, but that’s optional) 3. Heat a wok and pour oil. Stir in the green beans and salt. 4. Stir fry on a high heat for 2 to 3 mins and add 2 tablespoons stock/water in 2 batches, stir frying after each addition, until the beans are tender to your liking. It takes me 6 mins. 5. Make a well in the center and add the butter, garlic and black pepper. Let fry for 40 to 50 seconds & mix well. 6. Continue to stir fry for a minute or 2, to coat the green beans with butter and garlic. 7. Taste test to adjust salt and black pepper. If your pan is too dry, garlic can get stuck to the bottom of the pan. If so, splash a tablespoon stock/water and turn off. (The water will evaporate soon but will release the stuck garlic) 8. We usually serve this with rice and Lentil curry . Any simple Indian dal like this Masoor dal or Toor dal also goes well.

title: Cabbage Curry
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.625 cup onions, 0.625 cup tomatoes, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.375 cup green peas, 4.5 cups cabbage, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Remove the outer leaves of cabbage and cut to quarters. 2. Heat 4 to 6 cups water in a large pot. The water should be slightly hot but not very hot. Turn off the stove. Keep the cabbage in this water and leave for 2 to 3 mins. 3. Discard the water and rinse them a few times in fresh water. Drain the cabbage completely. Shake off to drain the excess water. 4. Chop the cabbage and set aside. Ensure the water is completely drained from the cabbage. Excess moisture makes mushy cabbage curry. 5. Pour oil to a hot pan or wok. When the oil turns hot, add mustard and cumin. 6. As soon as they begin to sizzle, add ginger garlic and fry till it turns aromatic. 7. Add sliced onions and fry till they turn transparent. Transfer tomatoes, salt and turmeric. Fry until the tomatoes turn mushy and soft. 8. Add spice powders and fry for a minute or 2 until the raw smell has gone. Taste test and adjust salt at this stage. (Adding later often release more moisture from cabbage) 9. Next add green peas and then saute for a minute. 10. Next add chopped and drained cabbage. Stir for the masala to coat the cabbage well and fry for 2 to 3 minutes on a medium high heat. 11. Fry until the cabbage is tender yet slightly crunchy. If you see the cabbage oozing out water, rise the heat to highest and stir fry. If your cabbage is hard, then cover and cook just for 2 mins. 12. Sprinkle Coriander leaves. Serve cabbage curry with rice or roti.

title: Cabbage Kootu Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup dal, 2 cups cabbage, 2.5 tablespoons dry tamarind, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 3 tablespoons coconut, 3 red chilies, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.25 tablespoons chana dal, 2 teaspoons ghee, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. If using dry tamarind, soak it in ⅓ cup hot water and set aside. When it cools down, mash it with your fingers and keep ready. 2. Add dal to a cooker or pot. Rinse it well a few times. Pour 1½ cups water and pressure cook for 3 whistles. If using a instant pot, set the timer to 10 minutes with pressure cook setting. If using a regular pot, cook on a medium heat until dal is soft and mushy. 3. While the dal cooks, chop cabbage. Rinse it well and drain completely. Keep this aside. 4. Meanwhile in a small tadka pan, dry roast chana dal and red chilies until deep golden and aromatic. 5. Add cumin seeds and fry for 30 seconds. Turn off and cool. Later transfer to a grinder along with coconut. Pour little water and make a fine paste. 6. When the pressure releases, open the cooker and check if the lentils are cooked. They should be really soft and mushy. 7. Add chopped cabbage and turmeric. Stir and cook until the cabbage is soft and tender but not overcooked. (if using Instant pot, press the saute button) 8. Add the ground kootu paste and cook just for 2 mins. If the kootu turns thick, pour little hot water and bring to consistency. It should be ideally thick but of pouring consistency. 9. When the cabbage is cooked, add tamarind water or paste. I prefer to pass soaked tamarind through the strainer. Taste test and add more salt or tamarind if needed. 10. Heat ghee or oil in the same tadka pan for tempering. 11. Add mustard seeds, red chilli and curry leaves. When they begin to splutter, add hing. 12. Switch off and pour this to the cabbage kootu. Give a good mix and serve with rice or roti.

title: cabbage kootu recipe | cabbage dal recipe | muttaikose kootu
ingredients: 0.5 cup coconut, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tbsp rice, 2 chilli, 0.5 cup water, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, few curry leaves, pinch of hing asafoetida, 1 red chilli, 0.5 cup moong dal, 2.5 cup water, 2 cup cabbage, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a kadai take ½ cup moong dal soaked for 30 minutes. 2. add 2 cup water, cover and cook for 10 minutes. 3. cook till the dal is half cooked. 4. further, add 2 cup finely chopped cabbage, few curry leaves and ¼ tsp turmeric. 5. mix well and cover and boil for 10 minutes. 6. now in a blender take ½ cup coconut, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp soaked rice and 2 chilli. 7. add ½ cup water and blend to smooth paste. 8. add in prepared masala paste to the mixture, ¾ tsp salt, ½ cup water and mix well. 9. cover and cook for 10 minutes or till coconut gets cooked completely. 10. now prepare the tempering by heating 2 tsp oil. 11. add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, few curry leaves, a pinch of hing and 1 dried red chilli. 12. all the tempering to splutter and pour over kootu. mix well. 13. finally, serve cabbage kootu / cabbage dal with hot steamed rice.

title: Cabbage Pakoda (Cabbage Pakora)
ingredients: 3 cups cabbage, 0.75 cup onions, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 cup besan, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.875 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.875 teaspoon carom seeds, 1.5 Green chilies chopped, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1 sprig curry leaves, Oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. Cut cabbage to quarters and add them to warm water. Leave it for 10 mins, later remove and rinse with fresh water. Shred and drain completely to a colander. 2. Before making the cabbage pakora make sure there is no moisture in it. Add cleaned and shredded cabbage to a mixing bowl and add salt. 3. Add ginger garlic paste, coriander, curry leaves, chilies and ajwain. 4. Mix everything very well and squeeze well for 30 seconds. Keep this aside for 10 mins. 5. Add rice flour and besan. 6. Mix everything very well and taste test. The mixture has to be dry and not soggy. If it turns too dry, squeeze the mixture , cabbage will release moisture. You can also sprinkle 1 tbsp water and mix. If the mixture is soggy, add flour in the same ratios. 1 tbsp besan : 1/2 tbsp rice flour. 7. Heat oil in a kadai on a medium heat. When the oil turns just hot enough, regulate the flame to medium high, then slide small flat portions of the pakoda dough to the oil. 8. Fry until lightly golden. Then reduce the flame to low and continue to fry stirring often. 9. Take them off a bit early since they continue to cook even after removing from oil. Remove to a cooling rack or a kitchen paper. Serve cabbage pakoda with tea or coffee and chutney.

title: cabbage pakoda recipe | lachhedar patta gobhi ke pakode | cabbage bhajiya
ingredients: 0.5 cabbage, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup besan gram flour, 0.5 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup corn flour, oil, 1 cup hung curd, 2 tbsp green chutney, 2 clove garlic, 0.25 tsp chilli flakes, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take ½ cabbage. make sure to shred the cabbage slightly thin. 2. add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp salt. 3. squeeze and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. now add ½ cup besan, ½ cup rice flour and ¼ cup cornflour. 5. mix well making sure the flour is coated well to the flour. 6. further, pour 2 tbsp hot oil over the mixture and mix well. pour oil makes the pakoda crispy. 7. now taking a small ball sized mixture and drop in hot oil. 8. deep fry keeping the flame on medium. 9. stir occasionally, until the pakoda turns golden brown and crisp. 10. drain off over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 11. finally, enjoy lachedar cabbage pakoda with dahi lahsun chutney. 12. firstly, in a small bowl take 1 cup hung curd. 13. add 2 tbsp green chutney, 2 clove garlic, ¼ tsp chilli flakes, ¼ tsp pepper and ¼ tsp salt. 14. mix well until the chutney turns to silky smooth consistency. 15. finally, enjoy dahi garlic chutney with pakoda or kebab.

title: Cabbage Poriyal Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups cabbage, 3 tablespoons moong dal, 0.75 inch ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 tbsp oil, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, salt, 2 tbsp coconut
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak dal in water for about an hour. Drain it and set aside. 2. Shred cabbage and add to a bowl of salted warm water. Drain the water and rinse well again twice. Drain to a colander and set aside. Make sure there is no water left in the cabbage before cooking. 3. Heat oil in a heavy bottom pan. Add mustard and cumin. When they splutter, add curry leaves, green chili and ginger. Fry them till a nice aroma comes out. 4. Drain water from the dal and add it here. Cover and cook on a low heat until dal becomes tender & soft but not mushy. This takes around 3 to 4 mins. Do not pour lots of water. If needed sprinkle 1 to 2 tbsps water while cooking. 5. Increase the flame to medium high, add cabbage and turmeric. Toss and fry for 3 to 4 mins until cabbage is slightly tender yet crunchy. Troubleshooting: If the cabbage is not young it won't cook well. In that case, cover and cook until it turns tender. But be cautious & don't cover if using young cabbage as it may let out water, making the whole thing mushy. 6. Add salt, toss and fry for a min on high flame. If you cook longer after adding salt, it will let out water. So add salt only when cabbage is cooked to your liking. 7. If you have grated coconut you can sprinkle it and switch off the stove. Serve cabbage poriyal with rice or chapati.

title: Cabbage Rice (Cabbage Fried Rice)
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice, 2 cups cabbage, 0.25 cup Green peas, 1.5 green chilies, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 2.5 cloves garlic, 0.625 teaspoon rice vinegar, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1.5 teaspoon soya sauce
instructions: 1. Rinse rice well until water runs clear. Drain completely. Pour 1 cup cup water and bring it to a rolling boil. 2. Reduce the heat. Cover and cook on a low heat until the rice is fully cooked but not mushy. Turn off the heat. Fluffy up with a fork and cover. Rest aside for 5 mins. Later fluff up with a fork and cool completely. 3. Quarter cabbage and add them to a large pot of warm water. Add some salt and leave for 5 mins. Later rinse them under running water. Shake off to drain all of the excess water. 4. Cut quartered cabbage to slices and then chop. Add them a colander and shake/ toss to drain excess moisture. Set aside. 5. Heat a wok with oil on a high heat. Add garlic and chilies and fry until fragrant for about 30 to 60 seconds. I also add a star anise and a strand of mace for flavor. This is optional. 6. Add cabbage and peas. Fry for 2 to 3 mins on the highest flame. Add vinegar. 7. Saute for few more mins until the cabbage turns slightly golden yet crunchy. 8. Pour soya sauce (optional). Add rice, pepper and salt. Mix and fry for another 1 to 2 mins. Do not over cook. 9. Serve cabbage rice hot or warm.

title: Cabbage Sabzi ( Cabbage Potato Recipe)
ingredients: 25 grams cabbage, 2 potatoes, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 pinch hing, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. If you are new to cooking, I know your first question would be about “how to clean and chop cabbage?” I have you covered with that part here. Cabbage especially organic cabbage can be prone to tiny bugs, hidden inside. So in India, the cabbage is soaked in hot water to bring out these creatures, if any. I haven’t come out of this practice even after living outside India for many years. If you don’t care, just leave out the hot water step mentioned below. 2. Cut the whole cabbage to quarters (4 portions) and remove the core to discard. Heat water in a pot until slightly hot, not very hot. When you dip your finger into the water, you should feel it is hot, not burning hot. If you add to very hot water, it will soften, so don’t do that. 3. Stir in a tablespoon of salt and immerse the cabbage quarters into it. Let rest for 5 mins. Any bugs hidden in the cabbage would swim out to the water. Drain the water, remove the quarters and immerse in fresh water. Remove from water and shake off gently to get rid of the excess water. 4. Chop them the way you want. I slice each lengthwise and then chop to smaller portions. More similar recipes you may like Cabbage Curry Easy Cabbage Stir Fry Cabbage Fried Rice Cabbage Poriyal 5. Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add garlic or ginger garlic paste. Saute until the raw smell has gone. Add hing (optional). 6. Add chopped aloo and saute for 2 to 3 mins. 7. Cover and cook until fork tender. If required, sprinkle 1 to 2 tbsps water as you cook. Potatoes must be just cooked yet firm and not mushy. 8. Add chili powder, half of the salt, turmeric and green chilies. I used deseeded chilies just to reduce the heat. 9. Add coriander powder or garam masala. Saute for a minute until aromatic. 10. Add cabbage. Make sure the potatoes are cooked before adding cabbage. 11. Begin to saute on a high flame if your cabbage is still moist. Else medium heat will do. 12. Stir fry until cabbage is done to your liking. I prefer to cook it partially so it won’t loose the crunch & become soggy. Taste test and stir fry as much as you like. Sprinkle salt and coriander leaves. Stir fry on a high flame until the salt dissolves. Serve cabbage sabzi with rice or roti. Saag Aloo Recipe Cabbage Curry Cabbage Stir Fry ... Cabbage Rice (Cab... Steamed Cabbage Veg Manchurian Re...

title: Cabbage Stir Fry Recipe
ingredients: 3 cups cabbage, 1 cup carrots, 1 cup bell peppers, 1.5 green chilies, 2 garlic cloves, 2 tablespoons cooking oil, 1 teaspoon black pepper, Salt, 0.5 tablespoon soya sauce, 0.5 tablespoon Hoisin sauce, 0.5 teaspoon vinegar, 1 teaspoon roasted sesame oil
instructions: 1. Cut the cabbage to 4 quarters and keep them in warm water (not hot) for 2 mins. Remove them and rinse a few times in a pot filled with cold water. Drain them completely. Even little moisture in the cabbage is going to make it mushy. 2. Wash carrots & bell peppers. Drain well. Pat dry and Julienne or shred all of the veggies. 3. To a small bowl, add soya sauce, Hoisin sauce, vinegar and sesame oil. Mix and set aside. 4. Heat a large wok with oil on a high heat. Add garlic and slit green chili. Fry for 30 seconds. 5. Add all the veggies and stir fry for 2 to 3 mins until they are half cooked yet crunchy. Do not over cook to retain the crunch. Make sure the heat is set to highest possible setting and keep stirring to avoid burning. 6. Add the black pepper and mix. Pour the sauces all around the edges of the pan, making sure that most of the sauce hits the pan. 7. Toss the veggies or stir fry for a minute to coat the sauce. Remove the pan from the stove when the cabbage is partially cooked yet crunchy. 8. Cool it over a rack so the veggies don't sweat. Transfer to serving bowl when it cools down. You may add more salt and pepper while serving the cabbage stir fry.

title: Cabbage Thoran (Indian Cabbage)
ingredients: 4 cups cabbage, 0.75 cup raw coconut, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 green chili, 0.5 tablespoon garlic, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 onion, 1 red chili, 2 tablespoons coconut oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt
instructions: 1. Remove the outer leaves of the cabbage head and rinse well under running water. I started with 450 grams (1 pound) of whole cabbage and got about 4 cups (fine chopped) that weighed 400 grams. If you are using more or less cabbage than mentioned in my recipe, adjust all the ingredients accordingly else the flavors won’t be the same. 2. Cut the cabbage to 2 portions and remove the core to discard. Place each portion on a chopping board and make thin slices. Remove to discard any hard or fibrous parts. 3. Chop as fine as possible. If you want you may use a chopper or a food processor for this purpose. 4. Gather the following ingredients 5. Rinse 1 sprig curry leaves and pat dry (about 12 leaves) 6. Chop or slit 1 green chili (deseed if you prefer, use more for extra heat) 7. Fine chop 2 medium garlic cloves to make ½ tablespoon (8 grams) 8. Optionally peel and fine chop ½ inch ginger to make 1 teaspoon (6 grams) 9. Fine chop or slice 1 small onion or 3 shallots to make ⅓ cup (50 grams)

title: Cabbage Vada | Cabbage Patties
ingredients: 0.75 cup chana dal, 2.5 cups cabbage, 1 teaspoon ginger paste, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoons red chili powder, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 2 green chilies chopped, 2 tablespoons rice flour, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add chana dal to a medium sized bowl and rinse well a few times. Soak for 1 to 1½ hour. Drain completely. 2. Cut cabbage to quarters and remove the core. Dip them in slightly hot water and leave it for 5 mins. Drain and rinse well in fresh water in a pot. Drain completely and shred the cabbage and chop to very small size bits. 3. Add the cabbage to a mixing bowl along with coriander leaves, salt, green chili, ginger, curry leaves and ¼ cup soaked chana dal. Keep this aside. 4. Pulse the remaining chana dal in a grinder or a food processor to a coarse mixture without adding any water. Check the picture in the post for texture. 5. Add this to the cabbage & other other ingredients. Also add garam masala and red chili powder. Mix this well and set aside for 10 minutes optionally. This draws out the moisture from the cabbage. 6. Add 2 tbsp rice flour and mix. Take small portion (about 2 to 3 tbsps) of this mixture and check if it binds well. 7. If the mixture looks too loose to bind, add more flour and mix. Taste test and adjust salt and spices as required. 8. Heat oil in a deep pan for deep frying. Test if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of the dough. If the oil is hot enough the dough will sizzle and come up. It should be medium hot and not brown the dough instantly. This is the right temperature. 9. Divide the dough to 10 to 12 portions and make round balls. Grease your palm and flatten a dough ball and shape it to a patty as shown in the picture in the post. 10. You can make the cabbage vada on a greased foil or cling film. You can also make it directly in your palm and shape them. 11. Regulate the flame to medium. Gently slide 3 to 4 to the hot oil, depending on the size of your kadai. Do not disturb them for 2 mins or so until they firm up. 12. Later stir and fry until golden and crisp. Drain them to a cooling rack or a steel colander. Fry all the cabbage patties in batches of 2 to 3. 13. Serve cabbage vada with a cup of Indian tea or any chutney. This coconut chutney & mint chutney go well. 14. Do not make thick patties, they should be thin enough so the cabbage gets cooked well. Place them in the air fryer basket and spray oil. 15. Air fry for 18 mins at 180 C or 350 F. Turn them to the other side after 10 mins. For the extra crisp texture I air fry at 200 C or 390F. 16. We prefer to brush the cabbage patties with a bit of ghee and serve them hot. 17. Place them on a greased baking sheet. Place the tray in the center of the preheat oven. Bake them for 12 mins and then turn them to the other side and fry for another 8 mins. Adjust the time as needed depending on the oven.

title: cabbage vada recipe | cabbage vadai | cabbage dal vada
ingredients: 0.75 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup toor dal, water, 2 cup cabbage, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp dill leaves, few curry leaves, 1 tsp cumin jeera, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 tsp salt, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take ¾ cup chana dal, ¼ cup urad dal and ¼ cup toor dal. 2. soak in enough water for 2 hours. 3. drain off the water completely and rest for 10 minutes. 4. blend to a coarse paste without adding any water. 5. transfer the dal mixture to a large bowl. 6. now add 2 cup cabbage, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp dill leaves, few curry leaves, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing and 1 tsp salt. 7. squeeze and mix well combining the mixture well. 8. make a soft dough adding 2 tbsp of rice flour if the mixture is watery. 9. grease hands with oil and prepare small balls, flatten the vada. 10. and deep fry in hot oil. or preheat and bake at 180 degree celcius for 25 minutes or until it turns golden and crisp. 11. also, stir occasionally till until the dal vada turns golden and crisp. 12. finally, serve cabbage vada hot along with masala chai.

title: Capsicum Chutney (Bell Pepper Chutney)
ingredients: 0.5 cup capsicum, 0.5 cup onions, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 medium garlic, 2.5 red chilies, 0.75 tablespoon chana dal, 0.75 tablespoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, salt, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 1 red chili, 1 pinch mustard seeds, 0.5 cup raw coconut, 1 tablespoon roasted gram, 0.875 cup capsicum, 3.5 green chilies, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, Salt
instructions: 1. Dry roast urad dal, chana dal and red chili until golden and aromatic. Add cumin and toss. Remove to a plate to cool down. 2. Add oil to the pan. Saute onions, garlic and capsicum until soft and the raw smell of capsicums is gone completely. Stir in the tomatoes (if using) and cook until they break down and lose the raw flavor. 3. Cool down completely. Powder the dal in a grinder and add sauteed capsicum, onions, tomatoes or lemon juice. 4. Blend to a smooth or coarse chutney as per your preference. If the chutney is too thick, you may splash little water. Taste test and adjust salt or lemon juice. 5. Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, dried red chili and curry leaves. When they turn crisp, add hing. Pour this over the capsicum chutney. 6. Saute green chilies, ginger/garlic and bell peppers in 1 teaspoon oil until the peppers are blistered, cooked fully and the raw flavor is gone. Stir in the cumin seeds and saute for a minute. Cool down and add to a grinder. 7. Also add coconut, roasted gram, salt and lemon juice. Pour water only as required and make a smooth chutney. Taste test and adjust salt or lemon juice. 8. If you want you may temper the chutney. Serve capsicum chutney with breakfasts and snacks. This chutney won't go well with rice.

title: capsicum chutney recipe | capsicum tomato chutney recipe
ingredients: 1 tbsp oil, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp channa dal, 4 kashmiri red chilli, 1 garlic clove, 0.5 onion, 1 to mato, 1 capsicum, salt to taste, 1 tbsp water, 1 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp urad dal, pinch of hing asafetida, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. in a wide pan, heat a tbsp of oil. add urad dal, channa dal and red chillis. fry till they turn golden brown. 2. then add a chopped garlic. fry for a minute till the raw smell of garlic goes away. 3. now add finely chopped onions and fry till they sweat. 4. add chopped tomatoes and fry till they become soft and gets cooked well. 5. finally, add capsicum and fry till they shrink. capsicum doesn't take more time to cook. 6. allow the mixture to cool completely before you blend. 7. once they are cooled completely, take them in a blender and add some salt and a tbsp of water. 8. blend to smooth chutney. 9. prepare the tempering. add a tsp of oil in a small kadai. along with mustard seeds, urad dal and hing. once they splutter add curry leaves and pour the tempering over chutney. 10. enjoy capsicum chutney with your favourite dosa, idli or with plain rice.

title: Capsicum Curry Recipe | Capsicum Masala Gravy
ingredients: 1.5 cup capsicum, 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.75 cup onions, 1 medium onion, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 1 green chili, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoon peanuts, 1.5 tablespoon desiccated coconut, 0.75 tablespoon white sesame seeds, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Finely chop one onion and cube the other one and separate the layers. 2. Cube capsicum and set aside. 3. Dry roast peanuts on a medium heat until deep golden and aromatic. Reduce the heat and then add coconut and sesame seeds. Stir and lightly roast until the seeds begin to splutter. Transfer to a plate and set aside to cool. 4. Pour 1 tablespoon oil to the same pan. Add one finely chopped onion, green chili and fry till golden. 5. Then add ginger garlic paste and fry till the raw smell disappears. 6. Add tomatoes and fry till they turn mushy. 7. Add salt, turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder and garam masala. 8. Fry till the mixture gets cooked well and begins to leave the sides of the pan. Cool completely. 9. Grind the peanuts, coconut and onions tomatoes mixture, together with little water to a smooth paste. I used about ¾ cup water in 2 batches as this makes blending easy and smooth. 10. Heat the same pan with 1 tablespoon oil. Then add cumin, cardamom and bay leaf or curry leaves. 11. When the cumin begins to sizzle, add capsicum and onions (cubed and layers separated). Next fry on a high flame for 2 mins. 12. Pour the ground mixture and any spice powders to adjust to your taste. Also pour water as needed to adjust the consistency. 13. Bring it to a boil on a medium flame. Cover and simmer until bell peppers are tender yet crunchy. 14. Sprinkle kasuri methi, stir well. Over cooking will make the capsicums soggy, so switch off in time. 15. Serve capsicum curry with biryani, pulao , chapathi or any of your favorite dish.

title: Capsicum Fry Recipe (Bell Pepper Stir Fry)
ingredients: 1.5 cups bell pepper, 0.625 cup onions, 1.25 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.1875 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon sesame seeds, 1 teaspoon dry coconut, 1 red chili, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 garlic
instructions: 1. This step is optional, if you do not want to make the spice powder then you can use idli podi or sambar powder or garam masala. To make the spice powder, dry roast 1 dried red chili, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 teaspoon chana dal until golden & aromatic. Then add 1 teaspoon sesame seeds, 1 teaspoon coconut, 1 garlic clove & ¼ tsp cumin seeds. 2. Fry until sesame seeds begin to splutter. Cool the roasted ingredients and make a fine powder. While these cool you can begin to make the stir fry curry. 3. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. Add ½ teaspoon cumin and ½ teaspoon mustard. I have used yellow mustard as well. 4. When the mustard begin to splutter, add ½ to ¾ cup thinly sliced onions (1 medium). 5. Stir fry until golden. 6. Add 1½ cups capsicum, ⅛ to ¼ teaspoon red chili powder, ⅛ teaspoon turmeric and ¼ to ⅓ teaspoon salt. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes. Skip chili powder if making for kids. 7. Cover and cook on a low flame until the bell peppers are cooked to your liking. You can keep them half cooked or soft cooked. We prefer them slightly crunchy. 8. Add the spice powder we made in step 1 or idli podi. If using garam masala or sambar use it here. 9. Stir until you begin to smell good, just for a minute or two. Capsicum stir fry is ready to serve with rice rasam, sambar or chapati.

title: Capsicum Rice | Bell Pepper Rice
ingredients: 1 cup uncooked rice, 1.25 tablespoons oil, 1 onion, 1 green chili, 0.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup green peas, 1.125 cup capsicums, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons mint leaves chopped, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, whole spices
instructions: 1. Optional - Wash rice and soak for 20 mins. Cook just until done without making it mushy. Fluff it up and cool completely. 2. Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and all the whole spices if using. Also add bay leaf or curry leaves. 3. Once the spices begin to splutter add onions and green chili. Fry until the onions turn slightly pink. 4. Then add ginger garlic paste and saute until it begins to smell aromatic. Add capsicum and green peas. Fry for 3 to 4 minutes until tender yet crunchy and not mushy. 5. Add mint, coriander, chilli powder, garam masala and salt. Fry for about 2 minutes till the mixture turns aromatic. 6. Optional - If the mixture is too dry, sprinkle 2 tbsp water and cook until the water evaporates. 7. Then add cooled rice to the capsicum mixture. Mix well and taste test. Add more salt if required and pour the lemon juice. Mix well. Serve capsicum rice with a raita. 8. Press SAUTE button on the Instant pot and pour oil to the inner steel insert. 9. Add cumin. When the seeds splutter, add curry leaves, green chilli and onions. Saute until the onions turn golden. 10. Next add ginger garlic and saute for 30 seconds. Then add in the capsicum and peas. 11. Saute for 2 to 3 mins until the capsicum is done to your liking. Then add salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala and mint leaves. 12. Give a good stir and pour 2 tbsps of water. Press CANCEL button. 13. Deglaze the pot and mix everything well. The heat in the pot will cook the masala and evaporate the water completely. Remove this to a plate and set aside. 14. Add soaked rice and pour water. For 1 cup basmati rice, you will need 1¼ cups water. For 1 cup sona masuri rice, you will need 2 cups water. 15. Give a good stir to ensure there are no food particles stuck at the bottom. Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position the steam release handle to sealing. 16. Press PRESSURE COOK button and set the timer to 5 to 6 mins for basmati rice or 10 mins for sona masuri rice. 17. For basmati rice, release the pressure manually after 5 mins. Remove the pot and cool the rice a bit. 18. Then add the coriander leaves, capsicum masala and lemon juice to the pot. Give a good mix. Adjust salt if needed.Serve capsicum rice with yogurt or curd.

title: Capsicum Rice Recipe | Capsicum Rice Bath - Canteen Style
ingredients: 2 tbsp peanut, 2 tbsp sesame, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 2 red chilli, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 1 onion, 1 capsicum, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli pwoder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 4 cup cooked rice, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 lemon juice
instructions: 1. Firstly, dry roast 2 tbsp peanut till they turn crunchy. 2. Add 2 tbsp sesame, 1 tsp cumin ½ tsp coriander seeds, ½ tsp urad dal, and ½ tsp chana dal. 3. Dry roast on low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 4. Cool completely, and grind to a fine powder. Keep aside. 5. In a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. Splutter 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, 2 dried red chilli, pinch hing, and few curry leaves. 6. Add 1 onion and saute slightly till the onion softens. 7. Now add 1 capsicum and saute till the capsicum turns crunchy. 8. Further, keeping the flame on low add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, and 1 tsp salt. 9. Saute on low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 10. Add in 4 cup of cooked rice and prepare peanut masala. 11. Mix gently, making sure everything is well combined. 12. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the spices are absorbed well. 13. Further add 2 tbsp coriander, ½ lemon juice and mix well. 14. Finally, enjoy Capsicum Rice Recipe with raita.

title: caramel bread pudding recipe | steamed caramel custard bread pudding
ingredients: 0.25 cup sugar, 3 slice bread, 2 cup milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.25 cup custard powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan take ¼ cup sugar and heat on low to medium flame. 2. stir in between until sugar melts completely. 3. continue to heat on low to medium flame until the sugar turns golden brown. this stage of sugar is called caramelized sugar. 4. pour the caramelized sugar into a small bowl and swirl slightly to coat the wall of the bowl. keep aside. 5. now take 3 bread slices, and trim off the sides. 6. pulse into crumbs using a mixi. make sure to pulse and blend and not make a paste. keep aside. 7. in a large bowl, take 2 cup milk, ¼ cup sugar and ¼ cup custard powder. 8. whisk and mix well until everything is well combined. make sure there are no lumps. 9. transfer the milk mixture into a large kadai and cook on medium flame until the milk thickens. 10. now add in crumbled bread and mix well. 11. continue to cook until the mixture turns super creamy and thick. 12. pour the mixture into the bowl of caramelised sugar. 13. place in the steamer for 30 minutes. 14. cool completely and refrigerate for 1 hour or until it sets completely. 15. finally, unmould and enjoy caramel bread pudding.

title: caramel custard recipe | caramel pudding recipe | caramel custard pudding
ingredients: 0.25 cup sugar, 0.5 cup milk, 0.5 cup curd yogurt, 0.5 cup cream, 0.5 cup condensed milk milkmaid, 1 tbsp custard powder, 1 tsp vanilla extract
instructions: 1. firstly, transfer the caramelised sugar into a bowl. 2. allow to cool for 5 minutes or until the caramelised sugar sets completely. 3. further in a large bowl take ½ cup milk, ½ cup curd, ½ cup cream and ½ cup condensed milk. 4. also, add 1 tbsp custard powder and 1 tsp vanilla extract. 5. whisk and mix until everything is well combined. 6. pour in caramelised set bowl. 7. cover the bowl and steam for 30 minutes or until its set completely. 8. cool down to room temperature and refrigerate for 2 hours. 9. finally, unmould the caramel custard or custard pudding and enjoy.

title: caramel kheer recipe | caramel vermicelli payasam | caramel semiya kheer
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 1 cup vermicelli semiya, 4 cup milk, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 2 tbsp raisins, 2 tbsp cashew
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 tsp ghee and roast 1 cup vermicelli on low flame until it turns golden brown. 2. now add 4 cup milk and give a good stir. 3. boil for 7 minutes or until the semiya is cooked completely. 4. now to prepared caramelised sugar, in a pan take ½ cup sugar. 5. keep stirring and cook on low flame until the sugar melts. 6. continue to cook until the sugar syrup turns golden brown. 7. pour the caramelised sugar over the kheer and mix well. make sure the kheer is super hot, else the caramelised sugar will harden if the kheer is cold. 8. continuously stir until everything is well combined. 9. simmer for 2 minutes or until everything is fully cooked. keep aside. 10. in a pan heat 1 tsp ghee and roast 2 tbsp raisins and 2 tbsp cashew. 11. roast until the cashew turn golden brown. 12. pour the dry fruit over the kheer and add ¼ tsp cardamom powder. mix well. 13. finally. enjoy caramel semiya kheer piping hot or chilled.

title: caramel toffee recipe | caramel candy | how to make chewy caramels
ingredients: 2 cup sugar, 1 cup cream, 3 tbsp salted butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a thick bottomed pan take 2 cup sugar. 2. keep stirring on a low flame until the sugar melts. 3. make sure to keep the flame on low and keep stirring to prevent it from burning. 4. sugar melts and turns golden brown. do not burn the caramelised sugar. 5. further, add 1 cup cream and stir continuously. 6. keep stirring until the caramelised sugar and cream is mixed well. 7. now add 3 tbsp salted butter and mix well. 8. mix until the mixture turns glossy. 9. immediately transfer the mixture to the tray lined with butter paper. 10. tap twice to remove air bubbles. 11. cool completely and rest for at least 5 hours to set completely. 12. now cut into pieces using a greased sharp knife. 13. wrap it in butter paper to prevent it from sticking. 14. finally enjoy caramel toffee for a week when stored in an airtight container.

title: Carrot Chutney
ingredients: 1.5 cups carrots, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 inch ginger, 2.5 garlic, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 tablespoon urad dal, 3 red chilies, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoons lemon juice, 1.5 teaspoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 red chili broken, 1.5 garlic, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Pour 1 teaspoon oil to a hot pan. Add chana dal, urad dal and red chilies to the hot oil and fry until the dal turns light golden. 2. Add garlic, ginger, green chilies and curry leaves. Fry until the dal turns deep golden to light brown. Add cumin seeds, stir and remove all of the fried ingredients to a plate. 3. To the same pan, pour 2 teaspoons oil. Add carrots, salt and turmeric. Stir fry on a medium high heat for 3 to 4 mins until the raw smell has vanished. Covered and cook for 2 to 3 mins until tender. Turn off the heat and cool. 4. Add the cooled lentils, ginger, garlic, chilies, cumin and curry leaves to a grinder jar and make a slightly corse powder. (To make it kids’ friendly, grind the chutney without chilies, set aside their portion and then add the chilies at the last stage & grind.) 5. To this add the carrots and lemon juice. Pour ¼ cup water. Grind to a smooth or coarse chutney to suit your liking. Scrape the sides grind. 6. Taste test and add more salt if you want. Carrot chutney is thick at this stage, good to serve with rice, paratha, roti or dosa. If you want it to be runny, pour more water and grind it to the consistency you like. 7. Heat oil in a pan. Add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, crushed garlic and dried red chilies. Fry until the leaves turn crisp. Add hing and turn off. Pour this over the carrot chutney. 8. Add chopped ½ cup chopped carrot, ½ cup chopped or grated coconut, 1 tablespoon roasted chana dal, (fried gram), 2 green chilies, ½ inch ginger or 1 garlic clove, 1/3 teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon cumin seeds and to a blender jar. 9. Add tamarind or 1 to 2 tsp lemon juice. Blend everything well to a smooth chutney. 10. A thick pachadi goes well with rice. If making carrot chutney for rice, use little or no water. If making for breakfast, pour little water and blend. Use water as required to make a thin or thick chutney to suit your taste.

title: Carrot Curry
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 0.75 teaspoon garlic, 1 green chilies, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1 onion, 3 medium tomatoes, 2 cups carrots, 0.75 cup green peas, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon Kasuri methi, 0.875 cup water, 0.75 cup coconut milk
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan/pot. Add ½ teaspoon mustard seeds and ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. 2. When the spices splutter, add 1 sprig curry leaves and 1¼ cup fine chopped onions (1 medium). 3. Stir and saute the onions until golden. Add ¾ teaspoon minced ginger, ¾ teaspoon minced garlic and 1 chopped green chili. Saute for a minute or until a nice aroma comes out. 4. Add ¾ cup chopped or pureed tomatoes and saute until the tomatoes break down and lose the raw flavor. 5. Stir in 6. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes till the masala smells aromatic. 7. Add 2 cups (325 grams) chopped carrots and ¾ cup green peas. Saute for 2 mins. 8. Pour water just as needed to partially cover the carrots. I use 1 cup water. 9. Cover and cook on a low heat until carrots turn tender but not mushy. I cooked for 8 mins. 10. Carrot curry is ready at this stage. But I prefer to add 1 teaspoon kasuri methi. You may omit if you don’t like. 11. Mix well and pour ¾ cup coconut milk or nut milk. To make nut milk, soak 15 cashews in little hot water for 15 mins and blend with half cup water in a small grinder. Ensure there are no bits of nuts in the milk. 12. Give a good stir and bring it to a boil. Cover and cook for 2 mins. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Serve this delicious carrot curry with rice, roti or with any Indian breads. 13. To make the spice powder, dry roast chana dal and urad dal till slightly golden, off the stove and add coconut, sesame seeds, cumin, garlic and red chili to the hot pan. When the coconut turns aromatic. Transfer to a plate and cool. 14. Next to the same pan, add oil and heat up. Add mustard, cumin, and dal. When the dal turns slightly golden, add ginger and curry leaves. Sprinkle hing. Saute for a min. Do not burn ginger, it gives a bitter taste. 15. Lower the flame. Add carrots, peas, turmeric and salt. Fry for two minutes. 16. Cover and cook till the carrots become tender. Keep stirring in between and put back the lid. If your carrots are not juicy, sprinkle some water as needed. 17. While the carrots cook, powder the roasted ingredients in a blender jar. Sprinkle the spice powder. Adjust salt and stir well. Fry for two minutes till the aroma of the spice powder comes out. Transfer to a serving bowl. Serve with rice and rasam or sambar. 18. Notes: to use moong dal as a substitute to peas, rinse the dal well and soak it for 1 hour or 30 mins in hot water. Later drain the water and add it along with carrots.

title: Carrot Halwa Recipe | Gajar ka Halwa
ingredients: 0.5 kg carrot, 2 cups milk, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 tablespoons ghee, 2 mixed nuts, 2 tablespoons raisins
instructions: 1. Wash carrots under running water & peel them. 2. Grate them in a food processor or with a hand grater. 3. Dry roast nuts on a low heat until crunchy, cool & chop them. OR Alternately chop the nuts first & fry them in 1 tbsp ghee until slightly golden. Then add raisins & fry until they plump up. Remove to a plate. 4. Next bring milk to a boil in a heavy bottom pot or deep pan. 5. Add grated carrots and cook on a medium heat stirring often. Do not leave the pot unattended as the milk will get scorched. 6. When the milk has completely evaporated add sugar and stir well. Sugar will release lot of moisture & will make the entire carrot halwa gooey. 7. Cook until half of the moisture evaporates, then stir in the ghee. 8. Saute the carrot halwa well in ghee until the moisture evaporates completely. 9. When it is done gajar halwa turns aromatic & thickens. It will still be moist. If you overcook your halwa turns dry. Sprinkle cardamom powder. 10. Garnish carrot halwa with nuts. Serve warm or chilled with Ice cream. 11. Press the saute button and add 1 tbsp ghee to the steel insert of the IP. 12. When it is hot fry the nuts until light golden. Then add in raisins if using. When they plump up remove to a plate. 13. Then add in the carrots & saute for 2 to 3 mins stirring well. 14. Pour 1 cup milk and mix well. Deglaze by scrubbing the bottom of the pot with a spatula. 15. Secure the instant pot & position the steam release handle to sealing. Press the pressure cook (manual) button & set the timing to 3 mins. 16. When the instant pot beeps and finishes, do a manual pressure release carefully. 17. Stir in the sugar, ghee & cardamom powder. Press the saute button & saute until the milk evaporates completely. 18. Garnish gajar halwa with chopped nuts & raisins.

title: Carrot Juice Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams carrots, 6 ice cubes, 0.5 inch ginger, 3 oranges, 5 grams carrots, 1 inch ginger, 3 oranges
instructions: 1. Wash carrots under running water. Use a vegetable scrub or brush to clean them. Rinse well. If you want you may peel them. 2. Cut off both the ends and optionally soak the carrots in clean water for 30 mins. This helps in better extraction of juice. Later chop them to 1 to 1½ inch pieces. 3. Rinse and Peel the ginger if using. Wash and peel oranges. Halve them and remove the pith & seeds from the center to avoid bitter taste. 4. Add the chopped carrots, half inch peeled ginger, ice cubes & deseeded oranges. [You can also extract orange juice separately and pour it here] 5. Blend until smooth adding more ice if the juice looks too thick. If it is too thick you won't be able to strain it so add more ice or liquid. 6. Place a fine strainer over a bowl and pour this juice in small batches to the strainer. (Check step by step photos in the post) 7. Use a strong spoon and then a cup, keep pressing it down to extract most of the juice. Make sure you use a fine strainer. 8. Serve carrot juice fresh and immediately. 9. Run your juicer. Slowly add the carrots, ginger and oranges to the chute. 10. Collect the juice from the juice spout on your juicer. Serve it immediately.

title: Carrot Pickle Recipe (Gajar Ka Achar)
ingredients: 0.5 cup carrot, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons oil, 2 tablespoons ginger, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 2 teaspoon mustard seeds, 2 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.125 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Dry roast methi seeds on a low heat until aromatic. As soon as they begin to change to slightly dark color switch off the stove and add mustard. 2. Cool them and grind to a fine powder. 3. Rinse carrots & ginger thoroughly and peel them. Air dry or wipe dry with a cloth to remove moisture. 4. Chop them to tiny cubes and set aside. Next chop ginger or garlic as well. 5. Pour oil to a pan and heat it. When the oil turns sizzling hot, add chopped carrots, ginger and mix. Turn off the heat. If you want soft carrots in the pickle, you may cook them in the oil for 3 minutes. 6. Add the rest of the ingredients except lemon juice and mix well. 7. Cool it and then add lemon juice or vinegar. Taste test and add more salt or spices to taste. 8. Store carrot pickle in a clean, dry airtight glass jar. Refrigerate and use after 2 to 3 days.

title: Carrot Poriyal (Carrot Fry)
ingredients: 25 grams carrot, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.625 tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 red chili, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 cup coconut
instructions: 1. Rinse 250 grams carrots well under running water. Peel them and chop them to bite sized pieces. You can also use a onion or vegetable chopper to dice the carrots. 2. Pour 1½ tablespoon oil to a pan and heat it. Add ¼ to ½ teaspoon mustard seeds to the hot oil. Followed by 1 tablespoon chana dal, ½ to ¾ tablespoon urad dal and 1 dried red chili (broken). Fry them on a medium heat. 3. When they turn golden, add 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon fine chopped ginger or 1 garlic clove (optional) and 1 to 2 chopped green chilies. Leave out green chilies and ginger for kids. 4. Soon the curry leaves will turn crisp then add a pinch of hing (optional), chopped carrots, ¼ teaspoon salt and ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. Adding salt at this stage is important as it lets the carrot sweat and cook in their own moisture. 5. Stir fry for 2 minutes. 6. Cover and cook on a low heat until carrots become tender. In between keep stirring so they don’t burn. If the carrots are too dry then you may sprinkle some water. Continue to cook covered. If using grated carrots, do not cover and cook. Instead stir fry on a low to medium heat as required until tender. 7. When the carrots are fork tender and not mushy add ¼ cup grated coconut and mix well. If you do not have coconut, use the spice powder I mentioned in the intro or sprinkle idli podi or peanut podi. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 8. Stir fry for a minute and turn off. If using frozen coconut, stir fry until it becomes hot. Serve Carrot Poriyal with rice, chapati or Rasam -rice or Sambar -rice.

title: Carrot Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 1 onion, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 2 medium carrots, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 12 cashews, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Wash and soak rice for about 20 minutes. While the rice soaks, prepare the veggies mentioned in the ingredients list. After 20 mins drain the rice and set aside. 2. Bring 2 cups water to a rolling boil, add drained rice and cook covered on a low heat until fully done. Fluff with a fork and cool completely. To cook in a pressure cooker, cook for 1 whistle with 1¾ cups water. You can also cook in an instant pot for 6 mins with 1¼ cups water. 3. Heat a pan with oil and add cumin, cardamoms, cinnamon and bay leaf. 4. When they begin to splutter, add onions and saute until transparent or golden. 5. Saute ginger garlic paste till the raw smell goes away. Add carrots and saute for one to two minutes. Sprinkle salt and cook till carrot is slightly tender. 6. Add spice powders - chili powder, garam masala and mint or coriander leaves. Stir and saute for a minute for the raw smell of chili powder to go away. 7. Cover and cook on a low heat for 1 to 2 mins. 8. Add cooked and cooled rice and sprinkle very little salt. 9. Mix everything well and squeeze in some lemon if you like. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 10. Garnish carrot rice with toasted cashews. Serve with a Raita.

title: carrot rice recipe | carrot pulao recipe | carrot pulav recipe
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 1 carrot, 2 tsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin seeds, few curry leaves, 1 cashews, 0.25 onion, 2 green chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric powder, salt to taste
instructions: 1. firstly, in wide pan, take a tsp of ghee. optionally, you can use oil. 2. furthermore, add mustard seeds, jeera and curry leaves. allow to splutter. 3. additionally, add cashews and fry till they turn golden brown. 4. once the cashews turn almost golden brown, add chopped onions and green chilli. fry till they turn translucent. 5. time to add grated carrot and fry for a minute till they shrink and change its colour. 6. once the carrot are cooked, add turmeric powder and salt to taste. give a good stir. 7. also add cooked rice. 8. furthermore, mix well without breaking rice grains. cook for a minute. 9. finally. serve carrot rice with raita or as it is.

title: Cauliflower Curry Recipe
ingredients: 1 medium head of cauliflower, 2 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.25 cup onions, 1 green chili, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.5 tablespoon garlic, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.875 cup water
instructions: 1. On a low to medium flame, heat oil in a pan. Add mustard, cumin and curry leaves. 2. When the seeds begin to sizzle & pop, add onions and green chilli. Saute until golden, for about 5 to 6 mins. Stir in ginger garlic and saute for about 30 to 60 seconds. 3. Add tomatoes and salt. Saute until the tomatoes break down. Stir in turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala and coriander powder. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds until the masala smells aromatic. 4. Add cauliflower florets and saute for 1 to 2 mins on a medium high heat. 5. Pour ½ to 1 cup water or coconut milk and give a good mix. I used 1 cup water at this step. Young cauliflower releases lots of moisture, so be careful not to add a lot of water at one time. 6. Cook covered until the cauliflower is tender but not mushy. If required add more water or coconut milk to bring the curry to a desired consistency. I added half cup coconut milk at this stage. 7. Taste the curry and adjust salt and garam masala to your preference. Garnish cauliflower curry with coriander leaves.

title: Cauliflower Kurma Recipe (Cauliflower Korma)
ingredients: 1.5 tbsp oil, 2 cup cauliflower, 0.75 cup green peas, 1 cup onions, 1 green chili, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.25 to 1 cups water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 12 cashew nuts, 0.25 cup coconut, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 star anise, 3 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves 3 cloves ((laung)), 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Cleaning - Cut the cauliflower florets to desired sizes and spray vinegar and drizzle some salt. Let them site for 5 minutes. This step helps to get rid of some of the pesticide residue in the cauliflower. 2. Meanwhile heat 4 cups water in a pot until slightly hot and not boiling hot. Add the cauliflower florets and turn off the heat. Let the florets rest in water for 2 to 3 mins. 3. Drain the water and rinse the florets in fresh running water. Set aside to drain in a colander. Blanching is done here to get rid of the worms or insects in the cauliflower and can be skipped. 4. Add cashews, fennel seeds and coconut to a grinder jar. Make a fine paste adding 4 to 6 tablespoons of water. Ensure the paste is smooth. [If using yogurt or coconut milk in place of fresh coconut, you will need lesser water. ] 5. Pour oil to a heavy bottom pan and heat it. When the oil is slightly hot, add star anise, cumin seeds, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, mace (optional) and bay leaf. Let them sizzle for 30 seconds or so the aroma comes out. 6. Add onions and green chili. Saute until the onions turn light golden. 7. Next add ginger garlic paste and fry this as well till the raw smell disappears. 8. Add tomatoes and salt. Fry till the tomatoes turn mushy and soft. If the tomatoes are not juicy, sprinkle little water. 9. Throw in all the spice powders - red chilli powder, garam masala, turmeric and coriander powder. Saute for 2 minutes. 10. Add the ground paste. Mix well and saute until the mixture smells aromatic. 11. Add peas and cauliflower. Mix and saute for 4 to 5 more minutes. Add water just enough to bring the cauliflower kurma to a consistency. 12. Next cook covered till the cauliflower is done to your liking. Stir every few minutes to prevent the kurma sticking to the pan. 13. Add chopped coriander or mint leaves when the gravy reaches the desired consistency. 14. Keep cauliflower kurma covered until served.

title: Cauliflower pakoda
ingredients: 325 grams cauliflower, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 2 green chilies, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 teaspoon ajwain, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 cup besan, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, water, oil
instructions: 1. Separate the florets from cauliflower. Do not chop them to small florets. (check the pictures in the step-by-step guide) 2. Heat 4 cups of water in a pot until hot (not boiling). Add the cauliflower florets and sprinkle ½ teaspoon salt. You may also add turmeric to the water. This step is done to bring out the worms if any in the florets. 3. Leave them for a minute or 2 and then drain the water. Rinse them thoroughly. Drain to a colander. 4. Transfer them to a mixing bowl. Add ginger garlic paste, chopped curry leaves & green chilies. Sprinkle chili powder, carom seeds and garam masala all over. Mix them well. 5. Sprinkle gram flour, rice flour and salt all over evenly. Sprinkle 2 to 3 tbsps water and begin to coat the gobi well with all the dry ingredients. 6. Keep splashing more water as needed and mix until all of the flour coats the cauliflower well. It should resemble a wet marinade. (check pictures above) 7. Taste test the batter and add more salt if needed. 8. Heat oil in a deep pan for frying until hot enough. 9. To check if the oil is ready to fry, Drop a small portion of batter to the hot oil. The batter should rise if the oil is hot enough. 10. Regulate the heat to medium. Gently drop each floret separately in the hot oil. Do not crowd a lot of them as they will get stuck and won't fry well. 11. Do not stir for 1 to 2 minutes. Then use a perforated ladle and stir them gently. Fry until golden & crisp on a medium heat. 12. The bubbles in the oil will reduce when the florets are fried well. 13. Remove them to a steel colander. Serve cauliflower pakoda hot with a cup of hot tea or coffee.

title: Cauliflower Stir Fry Recipe
ingredients: 5 cups cauliflower, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 tablespoon garlic, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon Garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon garlic powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon maple syrup, 0.75 tablespoon apple cider vinegar, 0.625 tablespoon soya sauce
instructions: 1. For this recipe you will need 5 cups of cleaned cauliflower florets (450g,1 lb). Make sure most of the florets are of the same size as you want them to be cooked evenly. If you are new to cleaning cauliflower, read my pro tips below. 2. Add the following spices to a small bowl and mix them. 3. ½ to 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder or chili flakes (adjust to heat tolerance) 4. Also measure ¼ teaspoon salt and keep it aside (do not mix with the spices) 5. You may leave out this stir fry sauce but it adds another layer of flavors. Optionally make the stir fry sauce by mixing 6. Chop 2 tablespoons of coriander leaves or any other fresh herbs. Slice ¾ to 1 tablespoon garlic cloves. Keep them thick. If you are using small cloves, just crush them and use. 7. Pour 2 tablespoons oil to a heavy bottom pan or wok and heat it. Add garlic cloves. 8. Stir in the cauliflower and fry for 3 to 4 minutes on a medium high heat. 9. Sprinkle the salt. 10. Reduce the heat to low. Mix all the spices and sprinkle them all over. Mix well. 11. Splash 1 to 2 tbsps water & mix. Cover and lower the heat. Let cook until the florets are tender/cooked but not too soft. If the pan is too dry, you may splash a few tbsps of water in between and continue to cook. It takes me 4 to 5 mins for this step. 12. Optionally if you want to use the sauce, increase the heat to medium high or high and pour the sauce all over. 13. Stir fry on the highest heat till all of the sauce is absorbed by the cauliflower. Taste test and add more salt, spices or sauce to your liking. Adding too much of sauce or spices is going to make your cauliflower too salty, spicy and is going to overpower the flavors. Sprinkle coriander leaves. 14. Remove Cauliflower Stir Fry to serving plate and serve hot as a side with just anything. We serve with basmati rice & dal, rice-curry or quinoa and raita. 15. Basmati Rice Reci... Tofu Curry Recipe... Vegetable Curry R... Turmeric Rice Rec... Dal Fry Recipe (R... Mushroom Pepper F...

title: Chaas Recipe (Indian Buttermilk)
ingredients: 0.75 cup curd, 0.25 cup mint leaves, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 green chilli, 0.625 inch ginger, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon black salt, 0.25 teaspoon regular salt, 0.75 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 1.75 cups chilled water, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala
instructions: 1. Add yogurt, mint leaves, coriander leaves, green chili, ginger, cumin powder and salt to a blender. 2. Blend until smooth. Then pour water and lemon juice. Blend until frothy. Taste test and add more salt or spices to taste. You can add more water if you prefer a thinner consistency. 3. Pour to serving glasses. Garnish masala chaas with chaat masala, roasted cumin powder and coriander leaves. 4. To a mixing bowl, add ¾ cup chilled yogurt, ¾ teaspoon roasted cumin powder, ½ teaspoon chaat masala or ¼ teaspoon dry ginger powder, salt, ¼ teaspoon black salt, ½ teaspoon crushed black pepper, ¾ tablespoon fine chopped mint leaves, ¾ tablespoon coriander leaves and 1 to 2 tbsps lemon juice (optional). 5. Whisk all of them until smooth using a whisk. Pour 1¾ cups water and whisk until frothy. Taste test and add more salt or spice. 6. Pour chaas to serving cups and garnish with more chaat masala and coriander powder.

title: Chai (Tea) Spice Recipe
ingredients: 3 tablespoons green cardamoms, 1 tablespoon black pepper, 1 stick cinnamon, 1 tablespoon cloves, 2 tablespoons ginger, 1.25 tablespoons fennel seeds, 1 star anise, 1 medium nutmeg, 3 Black cardamoms
instructions: 1. Break open the nutmeg with a nut cracker or in a mortar pestle and peel the black cardamoms. 2. To roast them in a oven, spread all the spices in a tray and roast them in the oven at 50 C / 120 F in the middle rack, until the spices turn aromatic. When you begin to smell the wonderful roasted spices, turn off and leave them in the oven with the door open. 3. I did it in a pan on the stovetop. Add the larger spices to the pan – cinnamon, green cardamoms, black cardamom peels and star anise. Roast them on a low flame for 3 to 4 mins. 4. Add black pepper, black cardamom seeds, cloves and fennel seeds. Roast for 2 to 3 mins, stirring often. 5. Add the crushed/broken nutmeg and roast just for a minute. 6. Turn off the heat and add the ground ginger. 7. Roast mixing (stove turned off) until ginger turns hot. 8. Cool down completely and add then to a grinder jar. Avoid using a plastic jar. 9. Grind to a powder. If you are going to use it only in chai/ tea, coarse powder is fine. I prefer to make a fine powder so it blends well in anything. 10. Cool down the chai spice mix and store in a air tight glass or steel jar. 11. To make 1 cup of black chai, add half teaspoon loose tea or 1 tea bag to 1 cup hot water along with a sweetener to taste (1 tsp sugar or jaggery). 12. Bring it to a boil. Add ¼ teaspoon chai spice. Turn off and cover. Let rest for 3 to 4 mins on the stove 13. Strain it. Serve hot.

title: chai | masala tea | indian masala chai
ingredients: 2 tsp tea powder, 2 cardamoms, 4 black pepper, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 inch ginger, 1 half cup milk, half cup water, 2 tsp sugar
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small pan, take half cup of water and add cardamom, cloves, ginger, black pepper and boil for 2-3 min 2. furthermore, bring it to boil and lower the flame. simmer it for 1-2 minutes until you get the nice aroma. 3. also and tea leaves or tea powder and simmer for 3-4 minutes. 4. now add milk, sugar. 5. bring it to boil and brew for 2 minutes. 6. furthermore, switch of the flame and filter out the tea powder and spices with a fine mesh sieve. 7. finally, serve the masala chai with biscuits or with any breakfast recipes.

title: Chai Latte Recipe
ingredients: 2.5 tea bags, 0.6666666666666666 cup water, 1 tablespoon sugar, 3.5 green cardamoms, 1.25 inch cinnamon piece, 4 black pepper, 2.5 cloves 2 to 3 cloves, 0.5 teaspoon vanilla extract, 1 cup whole milk
instructions: 1. You first need to prepare the whole spices like cloves, cinnamon sticks, black peppercorns, and cardamom pods are a great combination for this recipe. 2. Crush them in a mortar and pestle/ grinder or under a heavy object like a rolling pin or frying pan. I use my grinder. 3. They don’t need to be pulverized to powder, as you’ll remove them before serving. But if you want stronger spice flavors, make a fine powder.
