Toasting the spices before you crush them brings out extra fragrance. 4. You can use either black tea bags or loose leaf tea according to your preference. 5. To sweeten the chai latte, you may use sugar, maple syrup or any other sweetener of choice. You will also need a little Vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste. 6. Add the crushed spices to a saucepan with water, sugar and bagged or loose-leaf tea or tea powder. 7. Bring it to a boil and simmer for 3 to 4 mins. Boiling loose tea for too long imparts bitter tones. Add Vanilla extract. Turn off and Let it steep for about 10 mins. 8. Strain to get rid of the whole spices. 9. You can either refrigerate the concentrate to use later or keep it warm to serve immediately. 10. Now you must froth the milk. Over medium-high heat, heat the milk in a sauce-pan until you see steam coming. Do not boil. Turn off the heat as soon as you see the milk begins to steam. 11. Pour the milk to a heat proof mason jar or a tall jar (16 oz or 500 ml). Make sure milk is only filled up to half the jar. Milk should be hot but not boiling hot. 12. Using a frothing wand/ milk frother, froth the milk for 25 to 35 seconds, until the milk is frothy. If you do not have a frother, simply cover the mason jar tightly and begin to shake shake and shake, until frothy. 13. Bring the Chai Latte together by adding the warm concentrate to a cup or mug. I prefer stronger chai flavors so use up the entire concentrate. You may start with ½ to ¾ of the prepared concentrate. 14. Then adding the warmed milk and topping everything with the delicious milk froth.

title: Chakli Recipe (Chakralu Recipe )
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup besan, 0.25 cup fried gram powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon sesame seeds, 1 tablespoon butter, 1 tablespoon ajwain, 0.625 tablespoon red chilli powder, 1 pinch hing, water, 2 cups oil
instructions: 1. The recipe needs ¼ cup fried gram powder. You can replace it with besan. If using fried gram, add them to a chutney jar and make a fine powder. Use only ¼ cup powder in this recipe. 2. Add rice flour, besan, fried gram powder, salt, sesame seeds, ajwain, hing & chilli powder to a large mixing bowl. Mix up well. 3. Heat 1 tbsp butter or oil until very hot (don't smoke). Pour it quickly to the mixture and mix with a spoon (not fingers) first. Later use your fingers to incorporate the oil evenly. This makes the chakli crunchy. 4. Pour water little by little and make a dough. Start with 3 tablespoons and keep adding 1 tsp at a time until the dough forms. Do not knead it just mix to get a non sticky dough and shape it to a log. 5. Grease the chakli maker and fill it with a portion of the dough. 6. You can shape the dough either on a moist cloth, dry cloth or parchment paper. Slowly press the chakri maker to release the dough & shape to spirals. Use your finger to end the spiral. Join the end of the chakli to the spiral. (check video or pics) 7. Heat oil on a medium high flame. Continue to make the rest while the oil heats up. 8. Test if the oil is hot by dropping a small portion of the dough, it should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning brown. If it sinks, the oil is not hot enough. 9. Remove the chakli to your fingers and gently slide to the hot oil. You can fry about 3 to 4 in each batch depending on the size of your pan. 10. Do not disturb them (touch) for 1 to 2 minutes, until they become firm. Later turn them to the other side. Fry on a medium flame on both the sides until crunchy & golden. 11. Remove chakli to a steel colander or a cooling rack and cool them completely. Transfer chakli to a air tight steel or glass jar.

title: Chalimidi Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 cup rice flour, 0.875 cup jaggery, 1.5 tbsp Water
instructions: 1. For homemade flour, you need no water. Just knead damp flour and jaggery for 2 mins and set aside. It will begin to release moisture after 5 mins or so. If not just moisten your palm and knead the flour to get a smooth ball. Chalimidi is ready. 2. For store bought flour, knead flour& jaggery with 1 to 2 tbsps water. Knead till you get smooth dough. 3. Grease your palms if needed, make 9 undrallu and set aside 4. Make a round ball with the left over chalimidi and set it aside with vada pappu. 5. Though it is made of the same dough, It is counted as 2 naivedhyam dishes when you offer to Lord Ganesha. This recipe is a good stand by, if you are trying to offer a particular number of dishes like 5, 7 or 9 for naivedhyam.

title: challa punugulu recipe | bullet bonda recipe | dahi bonda | bullet bajji
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.75 cup curd, water, 1 tsp cumin, 3 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup maida, ½ tsp salt and ¼ tsp baking soda. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 2. now add ¾ cup curd. you can alternatively use buttermilk. 3. mix well with your hand in one direction. add water if required. 4. beat the batter making sure to incorporate air well. 5. now add 1 tsp cumin, 3 chilli and 2 tbsp coriander. 6. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 7. cover and rest for 3 hours for best results. 8. after 3 hours, mix the batter again. 9. now dip your hand in cold water and pinch a small ball sized batter. 10. drop it in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 11. stir and fry uniformly on medium flame. 12. drain off the bajji once it turns golden brown and crisp. 13. finally, challa punugulu is ready to enjoy with coconut chutney.

title: Cham cham sweet recipe
ingredients: 4 cups milk, 1.5 tbsp lemon juice, 1.25 cup sugar, 3.25 cups water, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 4 tbsp desiccated coconut, 4 tbsp Milk solids, 1 tbsp sugar, 4 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. Boil milk on a medium flame. Add diluted lemon juice to the pot and stir. Use as needed until the milk curdles completely. 2. Add ice cubes or ice cold water to the curdled milk. Drain it off to a muslin cloth. 3. Rinse it under running water. Squeeze excess water from the chenna. 4. Hang it for about 45 minutes. 5. Knead it well for 3 to 4 minutes until smooth. Make 8 to 10 oval shaped balls. 6. Make sugar syrup by adding sugar and water to a pot. Bring it to a boil. When it begins to boil, then add cardamom powder and cham cham. The syrup must be boiling consistently. 7. Cover and cook on a moderately medium heat for 8 to 9 minutes. The heat must be such that the syrup is boiling consistently. 8. After a while, transfer the cham cham to a plate and cool completely. 9. Slit them length wise . 10. Make mawa stuffing by mixing mawa with powdered sugar. 11. Stuff the cham cham with this mawa. Sprinkle chopped pistas on the mawa. 12. Roll them in desiccated coconut. 13. Serve cham cham sweet chilled.

title: chammanthi podi recipe | coconut chutney powder | kobbari chutney pudi
ingredients: 2 cup coconut, 0.25 cup urad dal, 7 red chilli, handful curry leaves, ball sized tamarind, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai add 2 cup coconut and roast on medium flame for 2-3 minutes. 2. now add ¼ cup urad dal and roast on medium flame. 3. roast until the colour changes slightly. 4. further add 7 dried red chilli and handful curry leaves. 5. continue to roast until the coconut turns dark golden brown. 6. cool completely and transfer to the blender. 7. add ball sized tamarind, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 8. pulse and blend to coarse powder. 9. finally, enjoy chammanthi podi with idli and dosa.

title: Chana Chaat Recipe (Chickpea Chaat)
ingredients: 0.5 cup chickpeas, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 0.25 cup cucumber, 2 sprigs coriander leaves, 1.5 green chili, 1.5 tablespoon tamarind chutney, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons sev, 2 tablespoons curd, 1 teaspoon green chutney, 1 potato
instructions: 1. Rinse chickpeas well under running water at least thrice. Soak them in lot of water for at least 6 to 8 hrs or overnight. 2. Later drain the water and add them to the cooker. 3. Pour water just enough to cover them or about 1 cup. Cover the pressure cooker. 4. Pressure cook for 3 to 4 whistles on a medium flame or in the instant pot for 16 minutes. You can even cook in a pot with enough water to cover them. Chana should be soft and cooked well but not mushy. 5. When the pressure drops, open the cooker and drain off the water. Add it soups, curries, rasam, sambar or make dough. 6. Cool the chana completely. Add salt, chaat masala and cumin powder 7. Add tamarind chutney if using. If you do not have tamarind chutney simply squeeze in some lemon juice or make your own following my notes below. 8. Add chopped onions, chilies, tomatoes and cucumber. 9. Toss all of these and taste test. Add more spices or chaat masala if required. Optionally you may add green chutney, potatoes (boiled or fried) or even whisked thick curd. 10. Transfer to serving bowls. Garnish with some sev or mixture or crushed cornflakes. Sprinkle coriander leaves. 11. Serve chana chaat immediately.

title: chana chilli recipe | chilli chana recipe | chilli kabuli channa recipe
ingredients: 1 cup chikpea chana, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, oil for deep frying, 0.25 cup corn flour, 2 tsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 green chilli, 4 tbsp spring onions, 0.25 onion, 8 cubes capsicum, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 0.25 tsp pepper, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp sugar, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, take 1 cup chikpea, ¼ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp salt and ¼ cup corn flour. 2. mix well making sure to coat the chana well. 3. deep fry the coated chana in hot oil or bake at 180 degree celsius for 15 minutes. 4. stir occasionally, and fry till the chana turns golden and crisp. 5. now prepare the sauce by heating 2 tsp oil and saute 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger and 2 green chilli. 6. further saute 2 tbsp spring onions slightly. 7. now add ¼ onion and 8 cubes capsicum. 8. saute for a minute or more, till the capsicum shrinks yet crunchy. 9. furthermore add 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, ¼ tsp pepper, ¼ tsp sugar and ¼ tsp salt. 10. saute on high flame till the sauce thickens and turns translucent. 11. now add in fried chana and 2 tbsp spring onions. mix well. 12. finally, serve, chilli chana as a starter or enjoy with fried rice.

title: chana dal payasam recipe | kadalai paruppu payasam | senaga pappu payasam
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 cup chana dal, 2 cup water, 0.25 cup sabudana sago, 1 cup water, 0.5 cup jaggery, 0.75 cup coconut milk, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 cashew kaju, 2 tbsp raisins kishmish
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 tsp ghee and ½ cup chana dal. make sure to rinse the dal well. 2. roast for 2 minutes or until it turns aromatic. 3. now add 2 cup water and mix well. 4. pressure cook for 5 whistles or until dal is softened. 5. in a large kadai take ¼ cup sabudana (soaked 30 minutes) and 1 cup water. 6. boil for 10 minutes or until it softens completely. 7. add in pressure cooked dal mix well. 8. further, add ½ cup jaggery and mix well. 9. boil for 5 minutes or until jaggery is cooked well. 10. further keeping the flame on low add ¾ cup coconut milk and mix well until everything is well combined. 11. in another pan heat 1 tbsp ghee and roast 10 cashew and 2 tbsp raisins. 12. roast until the nuts turn golden brown. 13. transfer the nuts to payasam and mix well. 14. finally, enjoy chana dal payasam with more dry fruits.

title: Chana Dal Recipe (Bengal Gram Dal)
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 3 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.75 cup onions, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 cup tomatoes, 1 Indian bay leaf, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 1 black cardamom, 3 green cardamoms, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon fenugreek leaves, 0.5 teaspoon amchur, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green chilies, 2 garlic cloves, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.25 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder
instructions: 1. Add chana dal to a colander or a bowl and rinse well thrice, rubbing with your fingers. Drain and transfer to a pressure cooker or a large bowl. Pour water and add turmeric. 2. Pressure cook on a medium heat, until you hear 4 to 5 whistles. Or in the instant pot for 10 mins. Wait until the pressure drops. (For sauce pan method, check post intro) 3. While the chana dal cooks, heat oil in a pan and add the bay leaf, cinnamon, black and green cardamoms. 4. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and saute on a medium heat, until golden, for 6 to 7 mins. 5. Add ginger and garlic and saute for 2 mins, until aromatic. Stir in the tomatoes and cook down for 4 to 5 mins, till they break down to a mush. 6. Stir in the ground spices – chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder and salt. Saute for 2 mins and add the cooked chana dal with the remaining stock/water. Optionally, mash gently to break down some of the lentils. 7. If your dal is too thick you may add little hot water. Mix well and simmer for 5 mins, until thick. Lastly stir in kasuri methi and amchur if using. Taste test to adjust salt. (If you want you may pick and discard the black cardamom, bay leaf and cinnamon stick) 8. On a medium flame, heat ghee in a small pan. When it is medium hot, add cumin seeds. When they sizzle, add the garlic, ginger, red and green chilies. 9. Fry for 1 to 2 mins, until aromatic but not burnt. Turn off and add red chili powder (optional) and hing. 10. Pour this over the Chana Dal and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve it hot with plain basmati rice, saffron rice, Jeera rice, plain parathas or roti. 11. Press saute button and pour oil to the steel insert. Add bay leaf, green & black cardamoms, cinnamon and onions. 12. Saute for 5 mins and stir in the ginger and garlic. Cook for a minute and add tomatoes. Saute until they break down slightly for 2 to 3 mins. 13. Stir in red chili powder, turmeric, salt, coriander powder, cumin powder and garam masala. 14. Add the dal together with water. Mix well and pressure cook for 13 mins. Wait until the pressure drops & mash some of the lentils, for a thicker consistency. Stir in amchur and kasuri methi. 15. Taste test to adjust salt. Make the tadka following the above section.

title: Chana Dal Recipe | Dhaba Style Chana Dal Fry Tips & Tricks
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 2 tbsp masoor dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tbsp ghee, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 bay leaf, 2 pods cardamom, 1 pod black cardamom, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 5 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tomato, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 2 red chilli, pinch chilli powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, soak 1 cup chana dal and 2 tbsp masoor dal for 30 minutes. You can also use moong dal in place of masoor dal. 2. Transfer the soaked dal to the pressure cooker. 3. Add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt, 1 tbsp ghee and 3 cup water. 4. Cover and pressure cook for 5 whistles or until the dal is cooked well. 5. Once the pressure is released, check whether the dal is well-cooked or not. Keep aside. 6. In a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee. add 1 bay leaf, 2 pods cardamom, 1 pod black cardamom, 1 tsp cumin and pinch hing. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 7. Add 5 cloves garlic and saute slightly. 8. Further add 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger paste and saute until the onions turn golden brown. 9. Further keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and ¾ tsp salt 10. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 11. Also, add 2 tomato and saute until the oil releases. 12. Now add in cooked dal and mix gently. adjust the consistency as required. 13. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the dal absorbs the flavour. 14. Furthermore, in a pan heat 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 2 dried red chilli and pinch chilli powder. 15. Pour the tempering over the dal. 16. Also add 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp kasuri methi and mix well. 17. Finally, enjoy Chana Dal Tadka Recipe with jeera rice. 18. Further keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and ¾ tsp salt.

title: chana dal vada recipe | dal vada recipe | paruppu vadai
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 2 tbsp coriander, 3 tbsp dill leaves, 2 tbsp curry leaves, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp salt, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup chana dal for 2 hours. 2. drain off the water and rest for 20 minutes or until the dal drains of water completely. 3. transfer the soaked & drained chana dal into mixi. 4. also add 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli and 1 tsp cumin. 5. blend to a coarse paste without adding any water. 6. now transfer the chana dal paste to a large mixing bowl. 7. further add 2 tbsp coriander, 3 tbsp dill leaves, 2 tbsp curry leaves, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 8. squeeze and combine them well. 9. furthermore, grease your hand with oil and prepare small balls, flatten the vada. 10. and deep fry in hot oil. 11. also stir occasionally till until the dal vada turns golden and crisp. 12. finally, serve dal vada hot along with masala chai.

title: Chana Dosa (Chickpea Crepes)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 1 cup chickpeas, 1 inch ginger, 2 Green chilies, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tbsp oil, Salt, 1 carrot, 0.5 cup onions, 2 green chilies, Salt
instructions: 1. Soak rice for at least 3 hours or overnight. 2. Soak chana for at least 8 hours. 3. Grind all the ingredients with enough water except the vegetables and oil. 4. Make a smooth batter of a medium consistency, do not make it runny as you cannot make them crisp. Too thick batter with result in very thick chana dosa and will be unable to spread the batter when you make them. 5. Heat a pan with 1 tsp oil, add the vegetables and salt. Fry them on a high flame for 2 to 3 mins, till they are partially cooked to retain the crunchiness of veggies. Off the flame and set aside. 6. Grease a non-stick flat pan / dosa tawa and heat it till smoky hot. 7. Drizzle few drops of water, set the flame to medium, pour a ladle full or 1/3 cup of batter and spread the batter evenly with the base of the ladle to get a 7 “ dosa. 8. Flip the dosa to the other side and toast it. 9. Flip it back, let it toast till crisp. 10. Add the vegetables, fold as desired. 11. Proceed to make more chana dosa. 12. Serve chana dosa hot with chutney or as it is.

title: chana masala powder recipe | chole masala powder | homemade chana powder
ingredients: 0.25 cup coriander seeds, 0.25 cup cumin jeera, 2 tsp pepper, 1 tsp clove, 2 pod black cardamom, 2 inch cinnamon, 1 mace javitri, 1 tsp fennel saunf, 1 tsp shah jeera caraway seed, 1 red chilli, 2 tbsp aamchur, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp turmeric
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan take ¼ cup coriander seeds, ¼ cup cumin, 2 tsp pepper, 1 tsp clove, 2 pod black cardamom, 2 inch cinnamon, 1 mace, 1 tsp fennel, 1 tsp shah jeera. 2. roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 3. further, add 10 dried red chilli and roast until the chilli puffs up and turns crunchy. 4. cool completely and transfer to a mixi. 5. add 2 tbsp aamchur, 2 tbsp kasuri methi and 1 tsp turmeric. 6. blend to a fine powder without adding any water. 7. finally, chole masala powder is ready. store in an airtight container for longer shelf life and prepare chana masala when required.

title: chana masala recipe | chickpea masala recipe | chana curry | chana masala curry
ingredients: 1 cup chickpea chana, water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.75 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 0.25 cup coriander seeds, 0.25 cup cumin jeera, 2 tsp pepper, 1 tsp clove, 2 pod black cardamom, 2 inch cinnamon, 1 mace javitri, 1 tsp fennel saunf, 1 tsp shah jeera caraway seed, 1 red chilli, 2 tbsp aamchur, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 3 pod cardamom, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 onion, 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 cup tomato puree, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup chickpea and soak in enough water for 8 hours. 2. transfer the soaked chickpea to a pressure cooker. 3. add ¼ tsp baking soda, ¾ tsp salt and 3 cup water. 4. pressure cook for 5 to 6 whistles or until the chana is cooked well. 5. firstly, in a pan take ¼ cup coriander seeds, ¼ cup cumin, 2 tsp pepper, 1 tsp clove, 2 pod black cardamom, 2 inch cinnamon, 1 mace, 1 tsp fennel, 1 tsp shah jeera. 6. roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 7. further add 10 dried red chilli and roast until the chilli puffs up and turns crunchy. 8. cool completely and transfer to a mixi. 9. add 2 tbsp aamchur, 2 tbsp kasuri methi and 1 tsp turmeric. 10. blend to fine powder without adding any water. 11. chole masala powder is ready. store in an airtight container for longer shelf life. 12. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee and saute 3 pod cardamom, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp cumin until spices turn aromatic. 13. add 1 onion, 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until the onions turn golden brown. 14. further add in 1 tbsp of prepared chole masala and ½ tsp salt. saute on low flame until the masala turn aromatic. 15. now add 2 cup tomato puree and mix well. to prepare tomato puree, blend two ripened tomatoes to a smooth paste without adding any water. 16. saute until the oil separates from the masala paste. 17. now add in pressure cooked chana and mix well adjusting consistency as required. 18. cover and simmer for 10 minutes or until the flavours are well absorbed. 19. finally, enjoy 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy chana masala with roti, puri or hot steamed rice.

title: Chana Paneer
ingredients: 0.5 cup chickpeas, 1 cup water, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 cup paneer, 0.75 cup yellow onions, 1.25 cup tomatoes, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash chana few times and soak them overnight. Cook them turmeric & water. Pressure cook for 3 whistles on a medium heat or in the instant pot for 16 mins. 2. While the chana cooks, chop or make a fine paste of onions and tomatoes separately. Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. 3. When they begin to sizzle, add the ginger garlic and saute for a minute, until a nice aroma comes out. Do not burn it. 4. Add chopped onions or paste. 5. Saute until golden, stirring constantly. 6. Add chopped tomatoes or puree. Saute for 4 to 5 mins until the raw smell is gone. 7. Add garam masala powder, red chili powder, coriander powder (optional) and salt. 8. Saute until the mixture begins to smell really good and aromatic. 9. Add cooked chana along with the stock (chana cooked water). If using canned chickpeas drain them and rinse well, use fresh water here. 10. If required add more water to make a slightly runny gravy. 11. Simmer with the pan covered on a low flame for 8 to 10 mins. 12. When the gravy turns thick, add paneer and kasuri methi. If you prefer to make a semi dry curry, evaporate the water by cooking further on a slightly high flame. 13. Simmer just for a minute and turn off. Taste test and adjust salt or add more garam masala if you want. Add chopped coriander leaves. Serve Chana Paneer with rice, roti or bread.

title: chana pulao recipe | channa rice recipe | kabuli chana pulao
ingredients: 3 tsp oil, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 1 star anise, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon dalchini, 1 tsp jeera cumin, 0.75 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 to mato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 1 cup chickpea chana, 1 cup basmati rice, coriander to garnish
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker heat 3 tsp oil and saute 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 5 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon and 1 tsp jeera till it turns aromatic. 2. saute onion, ginger garlic paste and tomatoes. 3. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ¾ tsp aamchur, 1 tsp kasuri methi and 1 tsp salt. saute well. 4. now add 1 cup soaked and boiled chickpea and saute for a minute. 5. furthermore add 1 cup basmati rice (soaked for 20 minutes) and saute gently. 6. now add 2 cup water and stir well. 7. cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles. 8. finally, garnish with coriander and serve chana pulao along with onion tomato raita.

title: Chana Saag Recipe (Chana Palak)
ingredients: 1 cup chana, 4.5 cups spinach, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1 cup onions, 0.75 inch ginger, 2 garlic cloves, 1.5 green chilies, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon salt, 1.5 cups water, 1 tablespoon ghee, 2 red chilies, 1.5 garlic cloves, 0.125 teaspoon hing
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak chana overnight. Later drain them and rinse well. Pressure cook them with 1 cup water for 3 to 4 whistles until soft and tender. To cook in instant pot, pressure cook for 16 to 18 minutes and let the pressure release naturally. 2. Pour 1½ tablespoon oil to a pan and heat it. Saute ginger, garlic and green chilies for a minute. 3. Add onions and saute until golden and fully cooked. Then stir in spinach and cook for 2 to 3 minutes, just until wilted. Transfer to a plate and cool this completely. 4. Later transfer the cooled ingredients to a blender and add coriander leaves. You may want to set aside one of the green chilies, taste test and add it later. Make a fine or coarse paste as per your liking. We prefer smooth paste. 5. While the greens cool, heat another tbsp oil in the same pan. Add tomatoes and salt. Saute and cook until soft and mushy. 6. Add garam masala, cooked chana and 1½ cups liquid (chana cooked water plus more water). 7. Cover and simmer chana for 5 minutes. Then reduce the heat and transfer the saag paste to the pan. 8. Stir well and cook just for a minute or 2 until chana saag begins to bubble. 9. Taste test and adjust salt and more garam masala if required. Turn off the heat and transfer to a serving bowl. 10. On a low heat, melt 1 tbsp butter or ghee in a small pan. Add dried red chilies and chopped garlic. Fry on a low heat until garlic turns slightly golden but not brown. Add hing and turn off. 11. Quickly pour the tempering over the chana saag.

title: Channa Kurma Recipe - South Indian Style | Kondakadalai Kurma
ingredients: 1.5 cup chole chickpea, 4 cup water, 1 tsp salt, pinch baking soda, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp fennel, 1 black cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp pepper, 1 tsp cloves, 1 onion, 1 inch ginger, 3 clove garlic, 1 tomato, 1 cup coconut, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp chana masala, 1 tomato, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 lemon
instructions: 1. Firstly, put 1½ cups chole, 4 cups water, 1 tsp salt, and a pinch of baking soda in a pressure cooker. 2. Pressure cook for 5 whistles or until the chole is cooked well. Keep aside. 3. To prepare the masala paste, heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan. Add 1 tsp fennel, 1 black cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp pepper, and 1 tsp cloves. 4. Saute on a low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 5. Add 1 onion, 1 inch ginger, 3 clove garlic and saute well. 6. Also, add 1 tomato and cook till the tomato softens. 7. Further add 1 cup of coconut and cook till the coconut turns golden brown. 8. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. Add 1 ladleful of boiled chole and water. 9. Blend to a smooth paste. Keep aside. 10. In a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 1 tsp cumin. 11. Saute 1 onion till it turns golden brown. 12. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp chana masala and saute till the masala turns aromatic. 13. Further add 1 tomato and saute well. 14. Now add prepared masala paste and cook well till the oil separates. 15. Also add boiled chole, 1 tsp salt and mix well, adjusting the consistency. 16. Simmer for 10 minutes, or until the chole absorbs all the flavour. 17. Add in 2 tbsp coriander, ½ lemon and mix well. 18. Finally, enjoy Chana Kurma Recipe with poori and roti.

title: chapati noodles recipe | chilli chapathi recipe | roti noodles recipe
ingredients: 5 chapati, 3 tsp oil, 3 clove garlic, 1 green chilli, 0.25 onion, 4 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 cup cabbage, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, take 5 chapati and roll tight. 2. cut into thin strip forming noodles. 3. separate the chapati strips and keep aside. 4. now in a large kadai heat 3 tsp oil and saute 3 clove garlic and 1 green chilli. 5. also, saute ¼ onion and 2 tbsp spring onion. 6. further add ½ carrot, ½ cup cabbage and ½ capsicum. 7. saute slightly till the vegetables shrink. 8. now add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce and ¼ tsp salt. 9. mix well making sure sauces are combined well. 10. further, add prepared chapati strips, 2 tbsp spring onions and mix gently. 11. finally, serve chapati noodles hot or pack to a lunch box.

title: Chapati Recipe (Indian Flatbread)
ingredients: 2 cups chakki atta, 0.75 cup warm water, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.25 tablespoons oil, 2.25 tablespoons ghee
instructions: 1. Add atta to a wide mixing bowl & make a well in the center. Add oil and salt if using. Pour warm or hot water and begin to mix. If using hot water use a spoon first and then your hand. 2. Mix well to form a soft dough, splashing more water only as required. Knead the dough for a minute or 2. It has to be soft and not sticky or crumbly. 3. Wet your hands and smear water all over the dough ball. Cover and rest for a minimum of 30 mins. You can leave up to 2 hours at room temperature. 4. When you are ready to make the chapati, knead the dough until soft and pliable. If the dough looks dry, moisten your palm and knead. It should not be sticky. 5. Divide the dough to 6 portions to make layered chapati or to 8 portions to make single layer chapati. Gently roll the balls in between your palms to smoothen them. Cover until you use up. 6. Slightly flatten a ball and dip in flour to coat it with flour all over. Shake it gently to dust off the excess flour. 7. Begin to roll the dough evenly without putting pressure. After 2 to 3 rolls, lift it and move it in clock-wise direction a little. Continue to roll it and then move it in clock-wise direction. If the dough begins to stick to the rolling pin or to the kitchen counter, sprinkle little flour. (not too much) 8. Single Layer Chapati: Repeat these steps until you make a 7 to 8 inch chapati. 9. Layered Chapati: After rolling the dough to 4 to 5 inches disc, smear melted ghee or a few drops of oil and then sprinkle 2 pinches of flour. Fold it to half, to make a half circle. Smear oil and flour and fold again to make a triangle. (Check the pictures). Sprinkle a bit of flour and roll to a 7 inch chapati. 10. After rolling the chapati, take it to one of your hands & dust the excess flour with the other hand. Transfer to a dry place, away from wind and heat. After rolling 3 to 4 begin to cook. 11. Heat a tawa/ pan on a medium high heat. It should be hot enough but not smoking. 12. Gently transfer the chapati to the hot tawa and wait until you see a few bubbles. Using a spatula, turn it to the other side. Spread little oil or ghee all over the chapati. Let cook for a minute or 2. (If required regulate the flame to medium. This depends on the kind of stove & pan.) 13. Turn back to the other side. It will ideally begin to puff. Press down with a spatula gently in place where it doesn't puff up. This helps the chapati to cook and puff. Keep rotating it and cook on the edges too. 14. Spread some ghee or oil all over and turn it to the other side. Spread ghee here as well. You will begin to see some smoke from the pan. It is normal. Remove to a plate. 15. Before cooking the next chapati, reduce the heat and wipe your tawa with a thick clean cloth or tissues. Be careful while you do this. To avoid this step, make sure you dust off the excess flour from the chapati after rolling. 16. Stack the hot chapatis on a plate. Once done cooking all of them, fold each to a triangle, while still hot. Transfer to a container & place over a cooling rack. Cover partially so they don't sweat. This keep them soft. 17. Serve chapati with curries, stew, dal or use them to make wraps, rolls etc.

title: chat masala recipe | chaat masala powder recipe | homemade chaat masala ingredients
ingredients: 0.25 cup cumin, 2 tbsp coriander seeds, 2 inch dry ginger, 1 red chilli, 2 tbsp black pepper, 0.5 tsp clove, 0.5 nutmeg, 3 tbsp mint, 0.25 cup dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp hing, 1 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a heavy bottomed pan take ¼ cup cumin and 2 tbsp coriander seeds. 2. dry roast on low flame until the spices turn aroamtic. 3. transfer to a large plate to cool completley. 4. in the same pan take 2 inch dry ginger, 1 dried red chilli, 2 tbsp black pepper, ½ tsp clove and ½ nutmeg. 5. dry roast until the spices turn aromatic. 6. transfer to the same plate allowing to cool compeltley. 7. now take 3 tbsp mint and roast on very low flame. 8. roast until the mint leaves turns crisp. you can alternatively sun dry until it turns crisp. 9. transfer to the same plate, allowing to cool compeltley. 10. once all the spices are cooled completley, transfer to a mixi jar. 11. add ¼ cup dry mango powder, ½ tsp hing and 1 tsp salt. 12. blend to fine powder making sure all the spices are powdered. 13. finally, chaat masala is ready to prepare aloo chaat or chaat of your choice.

title: Chayote Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 kg medium sized chayote, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 inch ginger, 2.5 garlic cloves, 1 green, 0.5 teaspoon chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon cumin, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tomato, 2 tablespoons shredded coconut, 1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves cilantro
instructions: 1. Choose chayote that’s light green in color with minimal bruises and wrinkles. Wash them well under running water. Beneath the skin, chayote has a slippery substance due to which it may jump out of your hand while peeling. So it is ideal to peel it either under running water in your clean kitchen sink or over a bowl of water. 2. The slippery substance and the seed inside the veggie can cause itch and dry skin in some people, so it is ideal to wear gloves. Begin to peel it over a bowl of water and keep rinsing it often in the bowl, to rinse off the substance. 3. Place it on the chopping board and trim off both the ends. Cut in the center, lengthwise. Remove the seed and pith to discard. 4. Place the cut side down on the chopping board and slice. Later chop them. I prefer to keep mine small as they soak the flavors well from spices and cook faster. 5. This recipe makes 4 servings. If you want to make 2, halve the ingredients. Gather the following ingredients: 6. Chop 1 green or red chili (or slit, omit for low heat) 7. 1 small tomato deseeded and chopped (1/3 cup) 8. 2 tablespoons coriander leaves/cilantro (chopped or any other herb of choice, to garnish)

title: Cheese Balls Recipe, How to Make Cheese Balls
ingredients: 25 grams potatoes, 1 teaspoon garlic, salt, 0.625 teaspoon chilli flakes, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon herbs, 6 tablespoons bread crumbs, 2 tablespoons cilantro, oil, 1 grams cheese, 0.5 teaspoon herbs, 0.375 teaspoon chilli flakes, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper, 0.125 teaspoon allspice, 2 tablespoons corn flour, 2 tablespoons corn flour, 0.5 cup bread crumbs
instructions: 1. Boil potatoes with the skin on till fork tender and not mushy. 2. Remove the skin and mash them well until smooth with a masher. There should be no lumps. 3. Add garlic, herbs, coriander leaves, salt (very little), chili flakes, pepper and bread crumbs. 4. Mix them well and form a ball. The mixture must bind together and should not be sticky or too dry. Taste test and add more spice or salt as you prefer. 5. Divide the mixture to 8 to 10 equal sized balls. Cover and keep aside. 6. Cut the cheese to 10 portions about ½ to ¾ inch cubes. Mix together herbs, chili flakes, pepper and spice powder. Toss the cheese in this mix. Set aside. 7. Take a potato ball and spread it evenly to a small patty in your palm. Place a cheese cube in the center. (Check step-by-step pictures) 8. Gently bring the edges together to make a ball. Ensure the ball is sealed well without any cracks. 9. Add 2 tbsps corn flour to another wide plate. Spread it. 10. Roll the finished ball in corn flour. The same way make all the cheese balls. 11. If you do not prefer to coat them in bread crumbs, you can pan fry these straight away. 12. Transfer ½ cup bread crumbs to a separate plate. Set this aside. 13. Make slurry: Add 2 tbsps corn flour to another bowl and mix with 3 to 4 tbsps water. Make a thin mixture. Alternately you can also use a beaten egg. If you are baking use egg. 14. Gently drop each ball in the corn flour mixture or egg and make it moist. 15. Immediately roll it in bread crumbs. Set this aside in a plate. Finish making all the balls. 16. Double bread the cheese balls - Repeat the same steps of dipping in flour mixture and then coating the bread crumbs again. 17. Set aside in a plate. Rest them for 10 mins. 18. To a wide deep pan pour oil just enough to half immerse the cheese balls. Heat the oil on a medium flame. 19. Drop a small piece of the potato mixture to check if the oil is hot enough. It has to come up immediately without browning. This is the right temperature. 20. Regulate the flame to medium. Gently slide the balls one by one & do not disturb them for 1 to 2 mins. 21. Gently stir them and fry until golden and crisp. 22. Serve potato cheese balls hot with any dip or sauce. 23. Add bread crumbs to a pan and toast them stirring often until light golden on a medium to low heat. Cool them and keep aside. 24. To bake - Preheat the oven at 200 C ( or 390 F). Grease a wired rack and place it on a tray. 25. Place the cheese balls over the rack and bake them for 15 mins. Adjust the timings as needed. or You can pan fry them in 2 batches using 2 to 3 tbsps oil per batch. 26. Some amount of bread crumbs will come off the balls and it is just okay. They will still be good.

title: cheese dabeli recipe | kacchi cheese dabeli with dabeli masala
ingredients: 2 kashmiri red chilli, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin, 0.25 tsp saunf fennel, 1 whole black pepper, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 3 cloves lavang, 1 black cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon stick, 2 tbsp dry coconut kopra desiccated coconut, pinch of hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 2 tbsp tamarind chutney, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 potato, 2 tbsp coconut, handful of pomegranate seeds anardhana, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, handful of sev, 2 tbsp masala peanuts, 6 pav, 0.25 cup tamarind chutney, 0.25 cup garlic chutney, 1 cup cheese, 0.5 onion, 3 tsp butter, 0.25 cup sev
instructions: 1. firstly, slit 2 sides of the pav and create pocket. 2. spread one side of pav with a tsp of tamarind chutney and other side with garlic chutney. 3. also stuff a tsp of prepared dabeli stuffing. 4. top with a tbsp of grated cheddar cheese and a tbsp of chopped onions. 5. further pour ½ tsp of tamarind chutney and garlic chutney. 6. again stuff the prepared dabeli stuffing and level it out. 7. heat the pan with some butter and place stuffed pav. 8. roast on medium flame flipping both sides till the dabeli turns crisp. 9. finally, roll the sides of dabeli in fine sev and serve cheesy kacchi dabeli.

title: Cheese Dosa Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup dosa batter, 1 medium onion, 2 green chilies, 3 tablespoons red chutney, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 3 teaspoons oil, 0.5 cup mozzarella cheese
instructions: 1. We will need dosa batter that can make crisp dosas. I make cheese dosa using the 1st recipe from this dosa recipe post. You can also use this restaurant style masala dosa batter . If you love soft spongy dosas, then you can also use the batter of set dosa recipe . 2. Make sure the batter is slightly thick but of good spreading consistency to yield crisp cheese dosa. 3. Grease or season your pan very well. I season my pan with oil after every wash and wipe off with tissue to get rid of excess oil. If you are new to using cast iron griddle, please refer my dosa post for details. Heat the pan until hot enough. Regulate the flame to medium and pour one ladle full of batter. 4. Quickly spread it evenly to a round dosa. 5. Allow the batter to slightly dry up on top. Sprinkle onions as much as you wish. You can also add thick grated carrots. Add 1 tbsp of any sauce or chutney. You can use whatever chutney or thick gravy you have at home. The gravy must be thick and of dropping but not pouring consistency. I used sweet and hot red chilli sauce here. 6. Spread everything well. Avoid adding too much sauce or curry. Add 1 tsp oil towards the edges. 7. Cover and cook for 2 minutes so the onions cook well. 8. Open the lid. Roast the dosa further on a slightly higher flame until the edges begin to leave the pan. Sprinkle coriander leaves. 9. Lift it slightly and check if the dosa has become crisp. Add grated cheese as per your liking. Also add green chilies or red chili flakes (optional). 10. When the cheese begins to melt. Switch off. Fold as per your liking. I prefer to fold mine like a triangle. 11. Serve cheese dosa hot. I usually cool it slightly on a cooling rack and then transfer to plate. This way it remains crisp for longer. The dosa begins to turn soft as it cools down. To make the next dosa, make sure the pan is hot enough but not smoking hot. Stir the batter well before to use it.

title: cheese dosa recipe | cheese masala dosa recipe | how to make cheese dosa
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 2 tbsp chana dal, 4 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 onion, 3 whole kashmiri red chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, salt to taste, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin, pinch of hing asafoetida, 0.75 tsp urad dal, 0.75 tsp chana dal, few curry leaves, 0.5 onion, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, salt to taste, 2 potato, 0.25 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 2 cups dosa batter, 4 tsp butter, 0.5 cup cheese
instructions: 1. firstly, heat the griddle and pour a ladleful of the dosa batter on it and spread it in a circular motion thin circle. 2. once the dosa dries out from top, add a dollop of butter. 3. also add prepared red chutney, adjust the chutney according to the spice level you are looking for. 4. and mix red chutney and butter together. 5. furthermore, mix and spread all over the dosa. 6. also spread 1 tbsp of prepared aloo bhaji over dosa. 7. additionally, grate cheese generously. 8. cook till the bottom of dosa turns golden brown. 9. then roll the dosa along with cheese - bhaji and cut half. 10. finally, cut half cheese dosa and serve immediately with chutney.

title: Cheese Omelette Recipe
ingredients: 2 eggs, 0.25 cup onions bell peppers, Salt, coriander leaves, 1.25 tsp oil, Cheese, 2 pinches red chilli powder, 1 pinch turmeric, 2 pinches garam masala
instructions: 1. Whisk eggs well in a bowl. 2. Heat a pan with oil, when it is just hot enough add the onions, other veggies & salt. Saute on a high flame for 1 minute. 3. Add garam masala, turmeric and red chili powder. Mix well. Spread the mixture. 4. Reduce the flame to medium and pour the eggs. 5. Allow to cook until the base is set. Flip it and cook. 6. Fry it to and fro on both the sides until fully cooked. Then add the grated cheese as desired. 7. Fold it half way and cook on a low heat until the cheese melts. 8. Serve cheese omelette warm.

title: cheese recipe in 30 minutes | how to make mozzarella cheese at home
ingredients: 3 litre full cream milk, 0.5 cup vinegar, 1 tbsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, take 3 litre full cream milk. make sure the milk is unhomogenised or use any raw milk. 2. stir continuously and heat the milk until it turns lukewarm. the temperature of the milk should be approximately 45 degree celcius. 3. now turn off the flame and add ½ cup vinegar. 4. stir for exactly 25 seconds. do not over stir as the curd formed may break. 5. cover and rest for 20 minutes or until the curd is formed and whey separates completely. 6. drain of the curds over the colander and squeeze gently. 7. take the whey water and add 1 tbsp salt. stir well. 8. heat the water well, but do not boil. the temperature of the water should be approximately 80 degree celcius. 9. turn off the flame and drop in squeezed curds. 10. stir and dip in hot water for 5 times. squeeze gently removing excess water. 11. again dip in hot water for 5 times. 12. continue this step until the curds turn cheesy and smooth. 13. do not over stretch as the cheese turns rubbery. 14. dip in ice-cold water and rest for 2 minutes. this helps to set the cheese completely. 15. wrap the cheese in cling wrap and refrigerate for 2 hours. 16. finally, mozzarella cheese is ready to use for 1 week when refrigerated.

title: Cheese Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 4 slices mozzarella cheese, 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, 0.5 teaspoon oregano, 0.75 teaspoon chili flakes, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: 1. Butter each bread slice on both the sides. Heat a pan and place 2 buttered bread slices on it. 2. Grill until golden. Flip the slice and place /spread the cheese on the hot bread. Sprinkle chili flakes & herbs, if you want. 3. Cover the bread with another slice with the hotter side over the cheese. 4. On a low to medium heat , grill it until golden and the cheese begin to melt. Halve the sandwiches and serve. 5. Start with your oven set to 450 F or 220 C. Or If using a sandwich toaster, preheat it. 6. Generously butter your bread and assemble your sandwiches, by placing the buttered side down on a baking sheet or a tray. 7. Place the cheese slices and add your spices or herbs of choice, if you want to. Cover with another bread slice, with the buttered side on top. 8. Place tray on the middle rack of your oven and toast until the bread begins to turn golden brown, about 3 to 5 mins. If you are using a OTG, it just takes about 3 to 4 mins. 9. Flip once with a wooden spatula and continue to grill until the cheese is visibly melted and your sandwich has developed an even, light brown color. This takes another 3 to 5 mins depending on your bread and oven. 10. Remove & let cool down on a wired rack before you cut, and enjoy hot! 11. Place the assembled sandwiches in the air fryer and air fry at 350F or 180 C for 5 mins. Do not air fry for too long, the edges turn hard and too crisp.

title: Cheese Sandwich Recipe | Veg Cream Cheese Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup cream cheese, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp dill leaves, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp mixed herbs, 1 inch ginger, 4 slice bread, 1 cucumber
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take ½ cup cream cheese, ½ carrot, 2 tbsp dill leaves, ¼ tsp pepper powder, ¼ tsp salt, ¼ tsp mixed herbs, and 1 inch ginger. 2. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 3. Spread cream cheese spreading on the bread. 4. place cucumber slices on the bread. 5. Also, spread cream cheese stuffing and cover. 6. Cut half and the sandwich is ready to serve. 7. Finally, enjoy Cream Cheese Sandwich Recipe tastes great when packed in lunch box.

title: Cheesy Garlic Bread Recipe | Homemade Dominos Style Cheese Garlic Bread in Pressure Cooker
ingredients: 1 cup water, 2 tsp sugar, 1 tsp instant dry yeast, 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp milk powder, 1 tsp garlic, 1 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 4 tbsp butter, 1 tsp garlic, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, cheese, sweet corn, chilli flakes
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup of warm water. 2. add 2 tsp sugar and 1 tsp instant dry yeast. mix well until the yeast activates. 3. now add 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp milk powder, 1 tsp garlic, 1 tsp mixed herbs, ½ tsp salt and 2 tbsp oil. 4. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 5. start to knead the dough adding water as required. 6. knead to smooth and soft dough. 7. cover and rest for 2 hours or until the dough doubles. 8. to prepare the garlic butter, in a bowl take 4 tbsp butter. 9. add 1 tsp garlic, ½ tsp mixed herbs and ½ tsp chilli flakes. 10. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 11. after the dough has rested for 2 hours, the dough would have doubled in size. 12. punch the dough and knead slightly. 13. pinch a ball-sized dough and flatten. 14. place it over a plate making sure it fits into the cooker. 15. now spread prepared garlic butter generously. 16. top with cheese, sweet corn and chilli flakes. 17. fold into half and seal the sides. 18. using the knife, make mark thick strips. spread garlic butter on top of it. 19. now place the bread into the preheated cooker. 20. cover and cook on a medium flame for 20-25 minutes. 21. brush with garlic butter and is ready to serve. 22. finally, the cheesy garlic bread recipe tastes when served hot.

title: cheesy sandwich dosa recipe | sandwich uttapam recipe
ingredients: 1 bowl set dosa batter any dosa batter, 1 to mato, 1 onion, 1 carrot, 1 tsp pepper powder, 3 tsp green chutney mint chutney sandwich chutney, 2 tsp oil, 0.25 cup cheese, salt to taste
instructions: 1. mix the set dosa batter gently and now add salt to the batter and mix well. 2. heat gridle, sprinkle a little oil on the tava (griddle) and wipe it off gently using a tissue. 3. pour a ladleful of the batter on tava. 4. and spread it in a circular motion thick circle. 5. on one half of the dosa sprinkle some chopped onions, sliced tomato and carrot. 6. now sprinkle some pepper powder. 7. grate generous amount of cheese on half of the dosa. 8. now on other half of dosa, apply generous amount of green chutney. and spread it out. 9. lastly fold the dosa and slightly press uniformly so that it gets sticked. 10. flip the other side and cook for a minute so that cheese melts. 11. finally serve cheesy dosa sandwich by cutting into desired shape or serve as it is.serve immediately with chutney. 12. spread some chopped onions over the dosa. 13. followed by grating generous amount of cheese over the dosa. 14. now place sliced carrot placing uniformly over the dosa. 15. sprinkle some pepper powder and spread some green chutney over it. 16. finaly place sliced tomato. 17. and pour dosa batter as thinly as possible and spread slightly. 18. cover the dosa for a minute. surface of the dosa should look completely cooked in the presence of steam. 19. then fry the dosa on other side until it turns golden brown. 20. finally serve cheesy dosa sandwich by cutting into desired shape or serve as it is. serve immediately with chutney.

title: Chekkalu Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour, 1 tbsp butter, 1 tbsp chana dal, 0.41666666666666663 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 green chilli paste, 0.75 tsp jeera
instructions: 1. Wash and soak chana dal for 30 mins. I washed and soaked in hot water for 10 mins. 2. Add rice flour to a mixing bowl. I used store bought fine rice flour. If using store bought please use a Indian brand rice flour as the texture of chekkalu will be different. 3. Add chopped curry leaves, ginger paste, salt, red chili powder and cumin. I used ginger garlic paste. 4. Mix up everything well. 5. Drain the water from chana dal. Bring about 6 tbsps water to a boil and add butter to it. I used the same pan I used to soak chana dal. 6. The butter must melt completely in the hot water. 7. Pour this to the flour and add the chana dal too. 8. The mixture will be hot just be careful. Mix everything well with a spoon first and then with your hand. 9. Pour more warm to hot water as needed and make a non sticky dough without kneading. Don’t worry if it is crumbly. Taste the dough and add more salt if required. 10. Keep it covered until the entire dough is used up. 11. Pour oil to a kadai and begin to heat up on a medium flame. While the oil heats up, make chekkalu on a clean dry white cloth or parchment paper. Divide the dough to 14 to 15 parts and roll them to balls. 12. Begin to spread the balls. As you flatten the ball, the edges will crack a lot. Just join them with fingers. You can also dip your fingers in a bowl of water and spread. 13. Chekkalu must be as thin as possible else they will not turn crunchy. If you do not want them to puff, prick randomly each one with a fork. If there is less chana dal in the dough then they are most likely to puff. Puffed chekkalu must be fried well else they may turn soft. 14. Test if the oil is hot enough – Drop a small flat piece of dough. It should sizzle and come up immediately without turning brown. This is the right temperature. 15. Regulate the flame to medium high and make sure the oil is hot (not smoking). Remove the chekkalu gently from the cloth and slide in to the oil. You can add as many as your pan fits but do not overcrowd. Avoid touching for a minute or 2. 16. Fry them until crisp and golden. Remove them using a spider. 17. Transfer to a cooling rack or a wide colander. This helps the air to circulate. They will keep crisp and cool them quickly.

title: Chettinad Biryani
ingredients: 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 inch cinnamon stick, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 3 green cardamoms, 2.25 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, paste from above, 0.5 kg chicken, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons curd, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon red chilli powder, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon stick, 2 cups seeraga samba rice, 3.5 cups thin coconut milk, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 onion, 2.5 green chillies, 1 tomato, 0.25 cup mint leaves, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 3.5 boiled eggs
instructions: 1. Grind fennel, cinnamon, cloves and cardamoms to a fine powder in a grinder. Add ginger garlic. Make a fine paste of all. If your blender doesn't do it fine, add curd mentioned for marination and blend. You can also use a hand pestle to do this. 2. Add the paste, salt, curd, turmeric, chili powder and chicken to a mixing bowl. Marinate and set aside. 3. Wash and soak rice for at least 20 to 30 mins. Drain the water. Keep your coconut milk and water ready. 4. Add oil to a hot pot or pressure cooker and add bay leaf, cloves and cinnamon.. 5. Stir in the onions and green chilies. Saute until they turn golden. Add chicken and saute on a medium heat until the it turns pale and whitish. 6. Add tomatoes, mint and coriander leaves. Cook covered on a low heat untl the tomatoes turn mushy. Chicken is almost half cooked at this stage. Do not cook on high heat as it will make the chicken chewy. Cook without lid until the moisture from tomatoes and curd evaporates completely. 7. Heat water and coconut milk in a separate pot, until really hot. Pour to the pan and add rice. 8. If making in pressure cooker. cover and cook on a medium heat for 1 whistle. 9. If making in pot, Cook in open pot until almost the water is absorbed. Then cover and cook on a low heat until soft cooked. If the rice remains undercooked, you may need to sprinkle some water and cook until done. 10. Allow the cooked chicken biryani to rest for atleast 15 mins. Open the lid and fluff up gently. 11. Garnish Chettinad biryani with eggs or coriander leaves.

title: Chettinad Egg Curry
ingredients: 5 hard boiled eggs, 1 onion, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, Salt, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.625 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 8 cashews, 0.25 cup raw coconut, 2 teaspoon coriander seeds, 0.75 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 4 red chilies, 2 green cardamoms, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon stick, 1 portion stone flower
instructions: 1. Boil eggs in a pot of water and a tablespoon of vinegar. Discard the hot water and rest them in cold water for 5 to 10 mins. Peel them and prick with a fork. 2. The key to making this chettinad spice powder is roasting them right without burning else they can impart a bitter taste. Heat a pan and dry roast red chilies and coriander seeds on a low to medium heat. If your red chilies turn crisp at any time during this process set them aside. 3. Add green cardamom, saunf, cumin, pepper corn, cloves, stone flower and cinnamon stick. Roast lightly on a low flame, make sure the spices do not get over roasted else they will taste bitter. 4. Add coconut and roast until it smells good, for 2 mins. Transfer to a plate and cool these completely. Meanwhile toast poppy seeds in the same pan until crunchy. I used cashews so did not have to toast them. Add the poppy seeds or cashews to a grinder. 5. Add the cooled spices also to the grinder and make a fine powder. 6. Pour little water and make a thick paste. 7. This step is optional. Most people like to use roasted eggs in gravy, so just showing it. I do not prefer to reheat cooked eggs. If you like to then proceed with heating a pan with 1 tsp ghee. Saute on a medium flame until golden and sprinkle some salt and chili powder. Set these aside. 8. To the same pan, add more oil and heat up. Add mustard seeds, when they begin to splutter, add the curry leaves. 9. Add finely chopped onions. Saute until golden. 10. Add ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw smell goes off, for about a minute. 11. Add finely chopped or pureed tomatoes. 12. Saute until the moisture from tomatoes evaporate and they lose the raw smell. 13. Add the ground masala and then enough water to make a gravy. 14. Simmer until the gravy becomes thicken & traces of oil appears on top. Add salt and eggs. Simmer for 3 to 4 minutes. Turn off and add coriander leaves. Serve with rice, chapati, ghee rice, biryani or jeera rice.

title: Chicken 65 Recipe
ingredients: 6 grams chicken bone-in chicken, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 4 tablespoons curd, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 4 tablespoons cornstarch, 2 tablespoons rice flour, 1 egg white, 1.5 cups Oil, 2 sprigs Curry leaves, 5 green chilies, 1 tablespoon garlic, 0.75 tablespoon garlic paste, 2 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 2 tablespoons water, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoons black pepper
instructions: 1. Cut the chicken to approximately 1 by 1 inch pieces. Try to keep the sizes as uniform as possible, this helps in getting all the pieces fried evenly. 2. To a large mixing bowl, add chicken, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala, ginger garlic paste, lemon juice, yogurt and finely chopped curry leaves. 3. Mix well and cover. Marinate for a minimum of 1 hour. You can refrigerate for upto to 36 hours. 4. Prepare all the ingredients to temper before frying the chicken. Pat dry the curry leaves and deseeded green chilies. Keep them aside. 5. To a small bowl add the crushed garlic paste, red chili powder, sugar & lemon/ vinegar/ yogurt (optional). If not using yogurt pour water. Mix together to get a thick paste. Keep this aside. ½ to 1 tablespoon garlic paste , 2 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder , ½ teaspoon sugar, 1 teaspoon lemon juice or vinegar (optional, or 4 tablespoons thick yogurt), 2 tablespoons water (leave out if using yogurt to temper) 6. To a small bowl add the crushed garlic paste, red chili powder, sugar & lemon/ vinegar/ yogurt (optional). If not using yogurt pour water. Mix together to get a thick paste. Keep this aside. 7. On a medium flame, heat oi in a heavy bottom deep pan for frying. While the oil heats up, add cornstarch, rice flour and egg white to the marinated chicken. 8. Mix well to coat the chicken well with flour and eggs or yogurt. The coating should be moist and not runny or too dry. If it is too dry, you may splash some water and mix well. 9. Test if the oil is hot enough by dropping a pinch of batter in the hot oil. It must come up immediately without becoming brown. If it sinks and remains there, it means the oil is not hot enough yet. 10. When the oil is hot enough, gently drop the chicken pieces into the oil. Do not crowd the pan. 11. Do not disturb the chicken pieces for 2 minutes. Stir and let them cook on a medium heat until crisp and golden. Reduce the heat slightly and let them fry the last 1 minute until deep golden & really crisp. 12. Remove them to a steel colander and continue to fry them in batches. 13. Once you are done with the chicken, reduce the flame to lowest and place 2 sprigs of curry leaves on a skimmer or strainer ladle and gently lower to the hot oil. Fry the curry leaves until crisp. Remove to the colander. 14. Place your slit and deseeded green chilies on the strainer ladle and lower it to the hot oil. Fry them until blistered. Turn off the heat. Toss all of these and serve this Chennai style Chicken 65 immediately or go ahead with the tempering below. 15. To Bake in the oven, preheat the oven to 200 C or 390 F for at least 15 mins. Place the chicken pieces on a tray lined with parchment paper or foil. Bake for 35 to 40 mins, flipping after 20 mins. When the chicken is almost done, Place the curry leaves and slit green chilies. Broil for 2 mins. Adjust the baking time as every oven is different. 16. Place the chicken on an air fryer parchment paper (perforated parchment paper). Air fry at 375 F or 180 C for 8 minutes, flip them and air fry for another 3 to 4 mins depending on how dry or crispy you want it. Place the sprig of curry leaves and slit green chilies and air fry for 2 more mins. Adjust the timings a bit as every air fryer is different. 17. Pour 2 tablespoons oil to wok. You can use fresh oil or use the same fried chicken oil. When the oil turns hot, add chopped garlic, 5 to 6 curry leaves and 2 slit green chilies. Stir fry until the curry leaves turn crisp, do not brown the garlic. 18. Slide the curry leaves and the chilies to a side in the wok. Lower the heat completely and pour the chilli garlic paste, 2 tablespoons water and salt. Mix well and let it cook on a medium heat until all of the water evaporates. 19. Taste test the fried chicken and the sauce too. Adjust your sauce to your liking, by adding more salt or red chilli to your taste. 20. Once it is done, you should have a thick oily chilli sauce in the wok. Add the fried chicken (set aside the previously deep fried curry leaves and green chilies for garnish). 21. Turn off and Toss well to coat the chicken with the tempering. Garnish chicken 65 with crunchy curry leaves and green chilies. Squeeze some lemon juice and serve with fresh cut raw onions.

title: Chicken Bhuna Masala Recipe
ingredients: 1 kg chicken, 0.25 cup oil, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 bay leaf, 1.5 black cardamoms, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon, 2 red chilies, 2 cups onions, 1.5 green chilies, 1 cup tomatoes, 1.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 tablespoon Lemon juice, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.625 teaspoon black pepper, 1.5 to 3 teaspoon Kashmiri Red chilli powder, 2.5 teaspoon garam masala, 2 teaspoons coriander powder, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.5 cup yogurt, 1.5 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup Coriander leaves
instructions: 1. In a bowl mix together all the ground spices – red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder, black pepper & fennel powder. Keep aside. 2. Add chicken and ½ tablespoon ginger garlic to a mixing bowl. Sprinkle ½ to ¾ teaspoon salt and half of the ground spices we just combined. Pour lemon juice. 3. Mix together to marinate the chicken. Cover and set aside until used later in the recipe or overnight in the refrigerator. 4. Meanwhile slit the green chilli. Chop onions & begin to cook from the next step. While you sauté the onions deseed & chop tomatoes. Grate ginger & garlic if you do not have the paste. This saves some time. 5. Heat oil in a wok or a skillet/ pan on a medium flame. 6. Add onions and green chilies. Saute on a medium high heat for 6 to 7 minutes and then on a medium low until golden brown but not burnt. 7. Add ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute until the raw smell has gone. 8. Stir in the rest of the ground spices and saute for a minute. 9. Stir in the tomatoes and salt. Cook until they dry out, blend well with onions & begin to release oil. 10. Take 2 tbsps of the hot onion tomato masala and add it to the whisked yogurt. Mix well until well combined. 11. Reduce the heat and slowly add this to the pan and saute until the yogurt cooks down and the bhuna masala turns thick. 12. Meanwhile (while bhuna masala cook) in another pan, heat 1 tablespoon ghee. 13. Add the red chilies and let them fry for 30 to 40 seconds. 14. Add all the whole spices – bay leaf, black cardamoms, green cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon & dried red chilies. (Be careful as the spices splutter) 15. Transfer the chicken. Fry on a medium high heat for 2 to 3 mins and then on a medium to low heat for another 2 to 3 mins. Turn off. 16. Add the bhuna chicken & 2 tablespoons of chopped coriander leaves (optional) to this masala and continue to saute for 2 to 3 minutes. 17. Cover and cook on a low heat, until the chicken is fully cooked and tender. If you feel the masala is sticking to the pan or about to burn you may splash little hot water. I do about ¼ cup, pour on the sides and not over the chicken. 18. Taste test and adjust salt. Sprinkle crushed kasuri methi. Garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Chicken Biryani Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 3 tablespoons yogurt, 1.25 tablespoons ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.75 tablespoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon Lemon juice, 1 bay leaf, 4 green cardamoms, 6 cloves 6 cloves ((lavang)), 1 inch cinnamon, 1 star anise, 0.75 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 strand mace, 2 cups basmati rice, 2 tablespoon ghee, 1 onion, 0.375 cup mint leaves, 1 green chili, 0.25 cup yogurt, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 3 cups water, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoon fried onions, 1 pinch saffron
instructions: 1. To a bowl, add yogurt, ginger garlic paste, salt, garam masala, turmeric, lemon juice & red chili powder. 2. Mix up well and taste the marinade. Add more salt and spice if needed. 3. Make slits on the chicken pieces. Add it to the marinade & mix well. Cover and set aside for 1 hour to overnight. 4. Rinse basmati rice thrice and soak for 30 mins. Drain to a colander after 30 mins. 5. Heat ghee or oil in a pot or pressure cooker. 6. Add whole spices – bay leaf, cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, star anise, shahi jeera & mace. Skip them if you do not have. 7. When the spices begin to sizzle, add onions & fry them evenly stirring often until uniformly light brown but not burnt. 8. Add chicken & saute until pale for 5 mins on a medium heat. 9. Cover & cook on a low flame until the chicken is soft cooked or tender. Saute to evaporate any excess moisture in the pot. 10. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Add yogurt (curd), half of the chopped mint leaves, slit green chili, red chili powder & garam masala. ½ to 1 teaspoon red chilli powder , 1 green chili, ¼ cup plain yogurt, 1 teaspoon garam masala 11. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Add yogurt (curd), half of the chopped mint leaves, slit green chili, red chili powder & garam masala. 12. Mix everything well and layer chicken evenly at the bottom. (check video) 13. Spread drained rice in a layer over the chicken. 14. To a separate pot, pour water. If cooking in a pot use more water. 15. Add salt and mix. Taste the water. It must be slightly salty. Bring this to a rolling boil. 16. Pour 2 cups of this hot water across the sides of the cooker or pot. Gently pour the rest on top of the rice. (check video) 17. Level the rice if needed. Sprinkle the rest of the mint leaves. Optionally add fried onions & saffron soaked milk if using. 18. Pot method – If cooking in a pot, cover and cook on a medium flame until the rice in the chicken biryani is cooked completely but not mushy or undercooked. (Takes me 15 mins, from the time the water boils in the pot, may be different for you). If the rice is still under cooked or hard & there is no water at the bottom, pour ¼ to ½ cup more boiling water depending on the kind of rice used and cook further. When done it has to be fully cooked, yet remain grainy but not mushy. 19. Cooker method – Set the flame to medium high. Cover & place the whistle. Cook until you hear 1 whistle. Move the cooker away from the burner to stop cooking further. 20. Rest until the pressure drops naturally. Fluff up the chicken biryani with a fork. 21. Serve from top to the bottom layer. Each serving gets biryani rice from top and chicken from the bottom layer. 22. Enjoy chicken biryani with raita and sliced veggies, papad and shorba (biryani gravy) . 23. In a large mixing bowl, stir together yogurt, lemon juice, ginger garlic, green chili, oil, salt, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, biryani masala (or garam masala), coriander powder, roasted cumin powder, mint leaves and coriander leaves. Mix all the ingredients well and taste test to adjust salt and spice/heat. 24. Make gashes over the chicken and add to the marinade. Marinate for a minimum of 30 minutes (at room temperature) or overnight in the refrigerator for best results. 25. On a medium flame, heat oil or ghee in a heavy bottom pan/Dutch oven. Add onions and sauté until golden to light brown, for 14 mins. (7 mins on medium, 4 mins on low heat and 2 to 3 mins with the stove turned off). Remove them to a plate, retaining the excess ghee in the pan. Spread the onions. 26. Add the marinated chicken and saute for 4 to 5 minutes on a medium heat. Reserve 2 tablespoons brown onions for later and add the rest here. 27. Pour ¼ cup hot water and cook covered on a medium heat for 10 to 15 mins (depending on the size/kind of chicken used, organic and bone-in chicken takes longer). Chicken should be almost cooked through (95% cooked) and the gravy thick. Turn off the heat. 28. While your chicken gravy cooks (requires multitasking), rinse rice well thrice and soak for a minimum of 15 mins. Meanwhile, pour water to a large pot and add whatever whole spices you have – cardamoms, cloves, bay leaf, cinnamon, star anise, shahi jeera and mace. 29. Bring the water to a rolling boil, add oil, salt and drained rice. (If you don’t like whole spices in your biryani, boil the spices in water for 5 mins and fish out before adding the rice). 30. Bring the water to a rolling boil again and cook on a high heat for 5 mins, until the rice is 90% cooked. Drain to a colander immediately. 31. Meanwhile, soak saffron strands in hot milk + hot water (or rice cooked water). 32. Once the chicken is cooked through, layer 1 tablespoon brown onions and then spread the rice all over the chicken gravy. Sprinkle 1 to 2 pinches biryani masala, mint coriander leaves, ghee and saffron milk water & the rest of the fried onions. 33. Cover and dum cook on a medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes, lower the heat to low and dum cook for another 18 to 20 minutes. Turn off and rest for 10 minutes. (If you are not using a Dutch oven, seal the pot with a foil or a moist clean cloth so most of the steam is trapped). 34. Garnish with more coriander/mint leaves if you want. Serve Chicken biryani with a large rice paddle, such that each portion gets the chicken from the bottom and the rice from top in layers.

title: Chicken Burger Recipe
ingredients: 3 Chicken breasts, 0.25 cup yogurt, 0.25 tsp all spice powder, 1 cup water, Salt, 1 cup all-purpose flour, 1 tsp chili flakes, 1.25 cup bread crumbs, 1 egg, Oil, 6 burger buns, 0.5 cup Mayonnaise, 2.5 tbsp extra virgin olive oil, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1.5 tsp dry mustard, 0.5 tsp pepper, 5 lettuce leaves, Tomatoes
instructions: 1. Whisk together yogurt, salt, all spice or ground pepper and water. Slice the breasts if they are too thick. Place them in the buttermilk and rest for a minimum of 4 hours to overnight in the refrigerator. 2. Mix together little salt, red chili powder and flour. Keep aside. Beat egg with 2 tbsp water. Keep aside. 3. Remove the chicken and place them in the flour and coat them well. Dip in the egg mixture and then roll in the bread crumbs. Rest for 10 mins. 4. Deep fry in hot oil on a medium high heat until golden and crisp. Your meat thermometer should read 165F in the thickest part. Remove to a cooling rack. Fry the chicken in batches. 5. Slice and toast the dinner rolls on a griddle. Place the fried chicken, then lettuce and tomato. 6. Prepare a sauce by stirring mayo, mustard, olive oil, pepper, salt and lemon juice. 7. Add this generously over the tomatoes. You can also add tomato ketchup or any other preferred sauce. Place the other half of the dinner roll. 8. Serve chicken burger immediately.

title: Chicken Butter Masala
ingredients: 0.5 cup Greek yogurt, 6 grams chicken thighs, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon oil, 1.5 cup sliced onions, 15 grams ginger, 15 grams garlic, 1 teaspoon salt, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 5 cups tomatoes chopped, 25 whole cashews, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 2 teaspoon coriander powder, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1.5 cups water, 2.5 tablespoons butter, 1 green chilli, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 cup water, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 2 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.3333333333333333 cup chilled cream
instructions: 1. Cut chicken to ¾ inch pieces and add them to a bowl. Pat dry with kitchen tissues. 2. Add all the ingredients for marinade - Greek yogurt / hung curd, red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, salt, ginger garlic paste, kasuri methi, oil and lemon juice. 3. Marinate and rest covered for a minimum of 60 mins, overnight is best (refrigerated). 4. Meanwhile slice the onions and peeled ginger. Heat a pan with oil and begin to saute the onions until transparent (pink), till they lose the raw flavor. While the onions fry, chop the tomatoes. 5. Add ginger and garlic. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. Lower the heat and stir in red chilli powder. 6. Add the chopped tomatoes, cashews and salt. Cook on a medium high heat until the tomatoes break down to a mush. 7. Stir in garam masala, cumin powder (optional), coriander powder, sugar and Cardamom powder. 8. Turn off and cool down (read notes). Pour 1 cup water to the blender and make a super smooth puree. If required pour ½ cup more. (Some blenders do the best job with no extra liquid added, while some need only little, so adjust depending on your blender) 9. Wipe down the pan with some tissues and heat it with butter on a low to medium heat. 10. As the butter begins to melt, stir in green chilli (optional) and red chilli powder (optional). Taste test the onion tomato puree and add the chilli powder accordingly. 11. Strain the puree directly to the pan. Make sure your pan is not too hot else your spices will burn. 12. Pour ½ cup hot water and begin to cook the butter masala until it reaches a thick and creamy consistency. 13. While the butter masala simmers, heat a pan/ skillet with 1 tablespoon oil. When the oil becomes hot, layer the chicken pieces on the pan and cook on a high heat. 14. When the chicken turns brown at the bottom, turn them to the other side and cook until golden and slightly charred. Depending on the size of your pan, you may need to grill the chicken in batches. Chicken may not be cooked fully at this stage. 15. When the butter masala turns thick and creamy, pour ½ cup hot water (to rehydrate the chicken) and 1 tablespoon ghee. 16. Stir in the grilled chicken and let simmer for a 3 to 4 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through and the butter masala reaches a thick and creamy consistency. 17. Taste test and adjust salt. Add garam masala and kasuri methi. 18. Stir in the chilled cream. Turn off the heat. Mix and let rest for at least 5 mins. Garnish chicken butter masala with a tablespoon cream and fine chopped coriander leaves. Serve with Butter naan, Jeera rice or plain basmati rice.

title: Chicken Chettinad
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 cup onions, 1.5 cups tomatoes, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 0.75 cup water, 4.5 red chilies, 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon pepper corn, 5 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon stick, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 star anise, 1 piece stone flower, 0.25 cup coconut, 8 cashews
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with chili powder, turmeric, salt, ginger garlic and lemon juice or yogurt. Cover and rest aside until needed. 2. To a pan, add red chilies, cinnamon, cloves, star anise, dagad phool, black pepper corn, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, coriander seeds and cardamoms. 3. Heat on a medium flame for 2 mins. Reduce to low and roast until crunchy and golden, for 7 to 8 mins. Do not burn and keep stirring constantly. Remove to cool down. 4. Add coconut, half of the curry leaves and half tablespoon oil to the same pan and roast until light golden, for 7 to 8 mins. Turn off. 5. Transfer all the roasted spices and make a fine powder. Your chettinad masala is ready. To make the paste, add coconut, curry leaves and cashews if using to the same grinder jar. Pour half cup cold water and make a super smooth paste. This is Chettinad masala paste. 6. Heat oil in a pan, add bay leaf (optional) and onions. Saute until the onions turn light golden, for 7 to 8 mins. 7. Add the chicken and fry on a high heat for at least 4 to 5 mins. 8. Stir in the tomatoes. (and red chili powder if using). Saute until the raw smell of tomatoes is gone and the oil begins to ooze out of the tomatoes. 9. Stir in the rest of the curry leaves and transfer the ground Chettinad masala paste and hot water. 10. Mix well and simmer covered until the chicken is cooked through. If the gravy is runny, evaporate some of the liquid by cooking on a high heat. If it's too thick add some hot water. (A meat thermometer inserted in the thickest part of the chicken should read 165 F or 75 C.) 11. Taste test and adjust salt to your liking. 12. Serve chicken chettinad gravy with rice, chapatti, idli, dosa and appam.

title: Chicken Curry Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onions, 1.5 green chilies, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.25 cup yogurt, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.75 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 bay leaf, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 3 green cardamom
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in heavy bottom pan. When the oil turns hot, add 2. small bay leaf (or 1 sprig curry leaves) 2 inch cinnamon piece 4 cloves 3 green cardamoms. (All of these are optional but recommended) 3. Add 1 cup very finely chopped onions and 1 chopped green chili pepper. Saute the onions till they turn golden.
