instructions: 1. In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat. Add 1 ½ cups of chopped onions. Cook the onions until they turn golden, stirring every 2 minutes to prevent burning. 2. Add 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger and 1 tablespoon of finely chopped garlic. Sauté with the onions until you can smell the garlic and ginger, about 1 to 2 minutes. Remember to stir to prevent burning. 3. Turn the heat to low and add 4. ½ teaspoons salt 1 teaspoon red chili powder or mild smoked paprika ¼ teaspoon ground turmeric 1 teaspoon ground cumin 1 tablespoon ground coriander 1 tablespoon curry powder (or 1 teaspoon garam masala). 5. Toast the spices for 30 to 40 seconds and stir to keep them from burning. 6. Once the spices become aromatic, stir in the 1½ cups fresh or 1 cup canned/bottled tomato purée. 7. Cook it on medium heat for 4 to 5 minutes to remove the raw flavor of the tomatoes. 8. Add the 3 cups of canned or pre-cooked chickpeas, ¾ cup water or chickpea stock, and 1¼ cups coconut milk. If using canned chickpeas, make sure they’re tender and you’ve drained and rinsed the chickpeas before adding them. 9. Cover the pan and simmer the curry on low heat for 15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes so the bottom of the curry doesn’t burn. 10. You’ll know it’s done when the chickpeas are melt-in-your-mouth soft and have absorbed the flavors of the curry. If they taste bland or too firm, cook at least another 5 to 10 minutes or until ready, adding more water if required. 11. Stir in ½ teaspoon garam masala and 1 teaspoon sugar (optional), then taste test and add either more garam masala (up to another ½ teaspoon if using store bought) or salt to your preference. If you want you may add more coconut milk to your taste. Turn off the heat and keep the curry covered until serving time; this helps retain the flavors of chickpea curry. 12. Once the curry cools slightly, garnish with ¼ cup cilantro leaves. Top the curry with some freshly squeezed lemon juice and serve.

title: Chilli Bread Recipe | Spicy & Tasty Bread Snack - Party Starter
ingredients: 4 slice bread, 2 tbsp butter, 2 tsp oil, 2 chilli, 3 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 onion, 2 tomato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 capsicum, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, cut the bread slices into pieces and toast with 1 tbsp butter. 2. in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp butter, 2 tsp oil. saute 2 chilli, 3 clove garlic and 1 inch ginger. 3. also, saute 1 onion until it softens. 4. further, add 2 tomato and saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 5. now add ½ tsp turmeric and 1 tsp chilli powder. saute slightly. 6. add ½ capsicum and stir fry keeping the capsicum crunchy. 7. furthermore add 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce and ½ tsp salt. 8. cook until the sauces are well combined. 9. add in toasted bread, 2 tbsp coriander and mix gently. 10. finally, enjoy chilli bread with tomato sauce when it is still hot or pack in the lunch box.

title: Chilli Cheese Toast Recipe | Chilli Cheese Sandwich | Chilli Garlic Toast
ingredients: 0.25 cup mayonnaise, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 0.5 cup paneer, 2 chilli, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, bread, green chutney, butter, cheese slice
instructions: 1. firstly, to prepare the stuffing, in a large bowl take ¼ cup mayonnaise and 2 tbsp tomato sauce. 2. add ½ tsp chilli flakes, ½ tsp mixed herbs and mix well. 3. now add 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato, 2 tbsp sweet corn, ½ cup paneer, 2 chilli and ½ tsp salt. 4. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 5. to make the stuffing extra spicy, you can add ½ tsp chilli powder. 6. take a bread slice, and spread butter. spreading butter prevents the bread from turning soggy. 7. also spread green chutney to make the sandwich spicy. 8. further place a cheese slice. you can alternatively use grated cheese. 9. now spread 2 tbsp of prepared chilli paneer stuffing. 10. top with little grated cheese. 11. toast on tawa by covering. toast until the bread turns crisp and the cheese melts completely. 12. finally, cut it in half and enjoy chilli cheese paneer toast. 13. to make a sandwich, you cover it with a cheese and bread slice. 14. spread butter and toast using the gas toaster. 15. finally, cut the chilli cheese paneer sandwich in half and enjoy.

title: Chilli Chicken Recipe (Crispy Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 25 grams chicken, 1.5 teaspoon red chilli sauce, 1.5 teaspoon soya sauce, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon Salt, 2.5 tablespoons cornstarch, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour, 1 egg white, 0.25 cup Oil, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 2 tablespoons red chilli sauce, 2 tablespoons tomato ketchup, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.875 teaspoon vinegar, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, 2.5 tablespoons water, 1.5 tbsp oil, 1 medium onion, 1 tablespoon garlic, 0.375 cup bell pepper - cubed, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig spring onion, 2 tablespoon Spring onion
instructions: 1. Slice chicken to bite sized pieces, about 1 by ½ inch slices or cubes and add to a mixing bowl. 2. Add soya sauce, red chilli sauce, black pepper, red chili powder (optional), salt, corn starch and all-purpose flour. (Quantities as mentioned under the section "To Fry the Chicken"). 3. Add the egg white or water only as needed to bind the flour with the chicken. If the mixture is dry add more egg white or sprinkle some water. 4. Keep this aside until you prepare the ingredients & the sauce. You can also refrigerate overnight. 5. Cube the onions & bell pepper. Separate the layers and slit green chilies. 6. Prepare the sauce in a bowl. Add soya sauce, chili sauce, tomato ketchup, red chili powder, vinegar, sugar and water to a bowl. (Quantities as mentioned under the section "for the chilli sauce"). Mix well. Keep this aside. 7. On a medium flame, heat oil (about 1 inch) in a pan to deep or shallow fry. The oil is hot enough to fry, when a small portion of batter dropped, sizzles and rises to the surface steadily without browning. 8. Add the chicken pieces one by one without crowding the pan. 9. Fry on a medium high heat stirring often until crisp, golden and evenly cooked from inside. Do not overcook as it can dry out the chicken. 10. When the chicken is cooked completely, bubbles in the oil will begin to reduce. Remove to a kitchen tissue or a wired rack. 11. For extra crisp chicken, refry them in hot oil again on a medium heat. 12. Heat oil in a wok or pan on a high flame. Add garlic & fry for about a min. 13. Add onions, bell peppers, spring onion whites and green chilies. 14. Fry till they turn slightly soft for about a minute or so. The veggies must still be crunchy. 15. Pour the sauces. Let the sauces bubble up well & slightly thicken. Taste the sauce and chicken as well. Add more salt, spice or sauce as needed. 16. Add the fried chicken & toss well until it is coated with the sauce. Sauté chili chicken on a high flame for 1 minute and sprinkle black pepper. 17. Garnish with spring onion greens. Serve chilli chicken immediately hot as appetizer or with fried rice or noodles.

title: Chilli fish
ingredients: 0.25 cup Oil, 25 gms fish fillet, 1.5 tbsps corn starch, 1.5 tbsps all-purpose flour, 0.125 tsp salt, 0.75 tsp soya sauce, 0.25 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 tsp garlic paste, 3 garlic cloves, 2 stalks spring onions, 0.25 cup green bell pepper, 0.25 cup red bell pepper, 1 tbsp soya sauce, 1 tsp vinegar, 1.5 tbsp red chili sauce, 2 tbsp water, 1 tsp sugar, 0.75 tsp corn starch
instructions: 1. Wash and cut the fish to cube sized pieces. 2. Add ginger garlic, soya sauce and pepper powder. Marinate and Set aside for at least 10 mins. 3. Stir to a bowl vinegar, sugar, soya sauce and red chili sauce. Set this aside. Add more or less sauce as needed. To get a nice red color you can also add little red chili paste. I mix little red chili powder with very little water and then use it. 4. Heat oil in a pan for deep or shallow frying or can even bake these. 5. Add corn flour, all-purpose, little salt and mix well. Sprinkle very little water and mix well to coat the fish with flour. Do not make it runny. Refer the pic. Fish must be coated with a thin layer of batter. 6. When the oil is hot, reduce the flame slightly and then fry these pieces to golden. Drain these on kitchen tissue. 7. Once you finish frying, drain off the oil from the pan retaining 2 tbsp. oil in the same pan 8. Add garlic and fry till you get an aroma. Add capsicum, layered onions (white parts of spring onions) and fry on high for 2 mins. Onions and capsicums must be crunchy (partially cooked) and not soft. 9. Stir the prepared sauce mix from step 3 with a spoon for uniform mixing. Pour this to the pan along with 2 tbsps water. Allow the sauces to bubble. 10. Add fried fish & stir fry on the highest flame for 2 mins. Garnish with spring onions More fish recipe s Apollo fish baked salmon recipe 11. Prepare the sauce for seasoning – Add the soya sauce, chilli sauce, sugar and vinegar to a bowl and set aside. 12. Rinse the fish and cut to cubes. 13. Add ¾ tsp soya sauce, 1 tsp ginger 1 tsp garlic paste, and ¼ tsp black pepper. Mix and set aside for 10 mins. 14. Heat a pan with oil for deep or shallow frying or preheat the oven and bake. 15. Add 1 ½ tbsps each of corn starch, all-purpose flour, salt to the fish. Sprinkle some water and mix to coat the fish with flour. The batter should not be runny. Fish has to be coated with a thin layer of batter. 16. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small amount of batter. It has to rise without turning brown. Gently slide the fish pieces to the hot oil. 17. Fry them to golden on a medium flame. Drain these on a absorbent tissue. 18. After you finish frying fish, transfer the oil from the pan to a bowl retaining 2 tbsps in the same pan. 19. Add garlic and fry till it gets fragrant. Add capsicum, onions and fry on high flame for about 2 mins. Onions and capsicums should be crunchy (not over cooked) and not soft. 20. Stir in the sauces we made at step 1 and pour 2 tbsps water. Give a good stir and taste it. Add more sauces or salt if needed. 21. When the sauces begin to bubble, add the fried fish & give a stir. Stir fry on the highest flame for 2 mins. To make a gravy version, double the sauces and stir ¾ tsp corn flour with half cup water and pour it to the pan. Boil until thick then add the fried fish. 22. Garnish with chopped spring onions

title: chilli garlic breadsticks recipe | chilli garlic toast sticks | garlic breadsticks
ingredients: 0.25 cup butter, 6 clove garlic, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp mixed herbs, 1 tsp chilli flakes, 0.25 tsp white pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 4 slice bread
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small bowl take ¼ cup butter and 6 clove garlic. 2. add 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp mixed herbs, 1 tsp chilli flakes ¼ tsp white pepper powder and ¼ tsp salt. 3. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. now cut the sides of bread. i have cut only 2 sides of the bread. 5. then cut into thick strips. 6. spread the garlic butter on the bread stirps. 7. toast the breadsticks on pan. you can alternatively bake in oven or grill. 8. turn over and toast until the bread turns golden brown. 9. finally, enjoy chilli garlic breadsticks with more chilli flakes.

title: Chilli Garlic Fried Rice Recipe | Spicy Garlic Fried Rice
ingredients: 1 red chilli, 4 cloves garlic, 2 tbsp oil, 5 cloves garlic, 2 red chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 onion, 1 beans, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 0.5 capsicum, 1 carrot, 2 tbsp spring onion, 4 cup cooked rice, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp spring onion
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar take 10 soaked dried red chilli, 4 cloves garlic and grind them to a smooth paste. Make sure to soak the dried red chilli till it softens. Keep aside. 2. In a wok heat, 2 tbsp oil. Add 5 cloves garlic, 2 dried red chilli and stir fry till the garlic turns golden brown. 3. Add 1 inch ginger and saute well. 4. Also, add ½ onion and saute until the onion softens. 5. Further, add prepared chilli garlic paste and cook till the oil separates. 6. Add 10 beans, 2 tbsp sweet corn, ½ capsicum, 1 carrot, 2 tbsp spring onion, and stir until the vegetables turn crunchy. 7. Add in 4 cup cooked rice, ½ tsp pepper powder, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce and 2 tbsp spring onion. 8. Stir fry and mix until the rice is well combined with the sauce. 9. Finally, enjoy Chilli Garlic Fried Rice Recipe with Manchurian.

title: chilli gobi recipe | gobi chilli recipe | chilly gobi recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup maida plain flour refined flour, 0.25 cup corn flour, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp chilli powder lal mirch powder, salt to taste, water as required, 1 cup gobi cauliflower, oil for deep frying, 2 tsp oil, 1 clove garlic, 2 spring onion, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, 0.25 red capsicum, 0.25 green capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 0.5 onion, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, 1 tbsp soy sauce, salt to taste, 0.25 tsp sugar, 1 tsp corn flour, 2 tbsp water
instructions: 1. firstly, in prepare smooth flowing consistency batter. 2. add in boiled gobi florets. 3. dip the florets into batter and deep fry or shallow fry in hot oil. 4. stir occasionally and fry on medium flame. 5. drain off over kitchen towel and keep aside. 6. now in a kadai heat oil. 7. saute in garlic, ginger, chilli and spring onion. 8. also add in capsicum and onion. saute till they shrink slightly. 9. now add in tomato sauce, chilli sauce, soy sauce, salt to taste and sugar. mix well. 10. now add corn flour paste and give a quick stir. 11. stir till the corn flour paste turn translucent. 12. add fried gobi and spring onions green. stir gently. 13. finally, serve chilli gobi hot as a starter or as a dish for roti, noodles or fried rice.

title: chilli paneer gravy recipe | easy paneer chilli with gravy recipe
ingredients: 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.25 cup maida plain flour all purpose flour refined flour, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 1 tsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 0.25 cup water, 9 cubes paneer cottage cheese, oil for deep frying, 4 tsp oil, 2 cloves garlic, 4 tbsp spring onion, 2 tbsp onion, 2 green chilli, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tbsp corn flour, 1 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare corn flour batter adding spices. 2. further dip cubes of paneer / cottage cheese and deep fry in hot oil. 3. fry the paneer till they turn golden and crisp. 4. drain off the fried paneer onto a kitchen paper and keep aside. 5. prepare the the gravy by heating 4 tsp oil and saute 2 cloves garlic. 6. also add 2 tbsp spring onion, 2 tbsp onion and 2 green chilli. saute well. 7. furthermore add ½ capsicum (green & red) and saute slightly. 8. additionally, add 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, ¼ tsp pepper and ¼ tsp salt. 9. saute on high flame till the sauce thickens. 10. pour the corn flour water and stir continuously. 11. add in fried paneer. 12. finally, serve chilli paneer gravy recipe garnished with few chopped spring onions along with fried rice.

title: Chilli Paneer Recipe (Dry & Gravy)
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 3 tablespoons cornstarch, 3 tablespoons all-purpose flour, 0.125 teaspoon black pepper, 0.125 teaspoon salt, water, 3.5 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 2 tablespoons red chilli sauce, 2 tablespoons tomato ketchup, 0.5 teaspoon vinegar, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon sugar, 1 tablespoon garlic, 2 tablespoon spring onions, 2 tablespoon spring onion, 0.5 cup capsicum cubed, 1 medium onion, 1 green chilies, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon cornstarch, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. Add cornstarch, all-purpose flour, red chilli powder, crushed black pepper and salt to a mixing bowl. 2. Pour water, about 6 tablespoons and begin to mix to make a batter. It should be neither too thick nor too runny. You may add 1 to 2 tablespoons more water if required. The batter has to be smooth without any lumps. 3. On a medium flame, heat 3 to 4 tablespoons oil in a deep pan for shallow frying. 4. Add 200 to 250 grams of cubed paneer to the batter. Gently coat paneer with the batter. 5. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of batter. It must sizzle and come up to the surface without turning brown. This is the correct temperature. 6. Pick up each batter coated paneer with a spoon and gently slide them in the hot oil. Space them apart from each other, do not crowd. Do not disturb them for a minute or until the batter firms up. 7. Later stir and fry them on a medium heat on all the sides until crispy. Drain to a kitchen tissue or a steel colander. 8. Option 1 - For dry chilli paneer : Stir in 1 tsp cornstarch to a bowl and mix with half cup water. Stir well & set aside. To another small cup, add 2 tbsps chilli sauce, 1 tbsp soya sauce, 2 tbsps ketchup, 1 tsp sugar, half tsp vinegar and half tsp red chilli powder (optional). Set this aside. 9. Option 2 - For chilli paneer gravy : Add 2 tsp corn flour & 1 cup water to a bowl. Stir well and make a lump free slurry. Keep aside. Add in 2 tablespoon soya sauce, 4 tbsp chilli sauce, 1 teaspoon of vinegar, 3 tablespoons tomato ketchup and little more salt & sugar. For color add ¾ tsp red chilli powder. Stir well and set this aside. 10. Remove excess oil from the fry pan retaining 1 tbsp. Fry one tablespoon finely chopped garlic until it smells good. 11. Then add in 2 tablespoons of spring onion whites and 1 medium onion cubed & layers separated. 12. Also add in 1 slit green chili and half cup diced capsicum. Saute on a high flame for 2 mins. 13. Stir the prepared sauce in the bowl and pour it to the pan. 14. Next stir the prepared corn slurry and pour it to the pan. Mix every thing & cook on a medium heat till the sauce thickens. 15. Check the taste of the sauce. You can add more sauces or salt if you wish to adjust to your taste. 16. If you want to thin down the sauce, just add some hot water to get the desired consistency. Turn off the heat and cool down slightly. 17. Add crushed black pepper & fried paneer cubes to the chilli sauce and mix. Add the spring onion greens. 18. Serve paneer chilli hot as a appetizer. You can also serve this with hakka noodles or fried rice.

title: chilli paneer recipe | paneer chilli dry | how to make cheese chilli
ingredients: 0.25 cup corn flour, 2 tbsp maida plain flour, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup water, 12 cubes paneer cottage cheese, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli, 4 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp tomato saue, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tsp corn flour, 2 tbsp water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small bowl take ¼ cup corn flour and 2 tbsp maida. 2. also add 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ½ tsp salt. 3. form a smooth lump free batter adding ¼ cup water. 4. now dip 12 cubes paneer and coat uniformly. 5. deep fry in hot oil making sure the flame is on medium. 6. stir occasionally until the paneer cubes turn golden brown and crisp. 7. drain off the fried paneer and keep aside. 8. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp spring onion. 9. also, saute ½ onion and ½ capsicum on high flame for a minute. 10. further add 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar and 2 tbsp soy sauce. 11. now add ¼ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ¼ tsp salt. 12. stir fry until the sauce is combined well. 13. prepare the corn flour slurry by mixing 1 tsp corn flour in 2 tbsp water. 14. pour the cornflour slurry into the wok and mix continuously. 15. stir fry until the sauce turns glossy and thickens. 16. add in fried paneer and 2 tbsp spring onion. mix well. 17. finally, enjoy chilly paneer with fried rice.

title: Chilli Parotta
ingredients: 2 parathas, 0.5 cup onions, 0.5 cup bell pepper, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 green chilli, 0.875 tablespoon soya sauce, 1.25 tablespoon red chilli sauce, 0.1875 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 cup water, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Cut 2 parathas or 3 rotis to ¾ inch cubes and set aside. Avoid using dry and hard flatbreads. If they are hard, place them in a bowl, cover and steam them first in a steamer or cooker. 2. Heat 1½ tablespoons of oil to a thick bottomed pan on medium. Add 1 teaspoon of chopped ginger, 1 teaspoon of chopped garlic and 1 sliced green chilli. Sauté until aromatic but be careful not to brown it. 3. Now add ½ cup of cubed onions and ½ cup of cubed bell pepper. 4. Stir and sauté on a medium heat, until the veggies are slightly tender and yet crunchy. 5. Add ¾ to 1 tablespoon of soya sauce or tamari according to your preference. Mix well. 6. Then add 1¼ tablespoon of red chilli sauce or any hot sauce that you prefer. I used sweet red chilli sauce. 7. Add ¼ cup of water to the masala and bring it to a boil on a medium heat. 8. Finally add the following ingredients: ⅛ to ¼ teaspoon salt (skip if your parotta is salted) ¼ teaspoon of red chilli powder ¼ teaspoon of garam masala Taste and then add extra salt if required, as the sauces contain salt. 9. Mix well and cook for a few minutes. Taste test and adjust spices and sauce as required. When the liquid starts bubbling and the masala becomes slightly thick, add the chopped parotta or roti pieces. 10. Mix well quickly and fry until heated up. 11. Make sure there is some liquid left or your chilli parotta will be dry and chewy. Remove from heat and add 1 tablespoon of finely chopped coriander leaves. Mix well to combine. Serve chilli parotta hot or warm with chutney, raita , salna or any other gravy or stew.

title: Chilli Potato Recipe (Chilli Aloo)
ingredients: 3 medium potatoes, 2 tablespoons corn starch, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.3333333333333333 cup oil, 1 tablespoon oil, 1.5 tablespoon garlic, 1 medium onion, 2.5 spring onions, 0.75 cup capsicum, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.75 tablespoon soya sauce, 2 tablespoons red chilli sauce, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon vinegar, salt
instructions: 1. Peel potatoes and cut each to half inch slices, Slice each to few half inch sticks. So you will have each stick of about half by half inch thickness. Immerse them in a bowl of water for 30 minutes & drain the water completely. 2. Boil them in fresh water for 3 to 4 mins until they are slightly cooked but firm. Drain them to a colander. 3. Sprinkle flour, red chili powder and salt over the drained potatoes. Toss well to coat the flour evenly. If it seems too dry, sprinkle some water. 4. Heat oil in a deep pan. When hot enough slide the potatoes one by one to hot oil. Deep fry on a medium heat undisturbed for 2 to 3 mins, later stir and fry until crisp & golden. 5. For extra crispness, you may remove them from oil and refry on a low flame. Later remove them to a wired rack or steel colander. 6. With a clean cloth wipe dry the soaked potatoes. Spray some oil all over the them. Sprinkle salt, red chilli powder and flour. Mix gently to coat. If it looks too dry, drizzle some oil and mix. 7. Air Fryer: Spread them in the basket in a single layer and air fry at 400 F or 200 C for 8 to 9 mins. Shake them & repeat for another 8 to 9 mins until crisp and golden. 8. In the Oven: Preheat the oven to 200 C or 400 F. Grease or line a baking tray & arrange the potatoes. Bake them for 23 to 30 minutes until crisp and golden. Adjust the baking time as it depends on the size of your potatoes and oven. 9. Optional for extra color and heat: Mix together red chilli powder, red chilli sauce and 2 tsps water to a smooth paste. Keep this red chilli sauce aside. 10. Heat one tbsp oil on a high heat in a wok or pan. Fry garlic and chilli until they smell good. 11. Add onions and stir fry for a minute followed by capsicums. Fry until they turn slightly tender yet crunchy. 12. Add the sugar, red chilli sauce, soya sauce, vinegar & 2 to 3 tablespoons water. Let the sauces begin to bubble. 13. When the sauce thickens taste the sauce and adjust more salt and sauce if needed. 14. Turn off and cool down slightly. Add fried potatoes and toss. Add spring onion greens. 15. Serve chilli potato hot or warm immediately as they lose the crispness if rested. Serve as an appetizer or as a side with noodles and fried rice.

title: Chilli Prawns (Shrimp Manchurian)
ingredients: 25 grams deveined prawns, 1.5 tablespoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 cup water, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 2 tablespoon corn flour, 2 tablespoon all purpose flour, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon olive, 0.5 cup capsicum, 0.25 cup spring onions, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1.5 green chili, 1.5 tablespoon Asian Chili sauce, 1.25 teaspoon soya sauce, 1.25 teaspoon rice vinegar, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon sugar
instructions: 1. Add ½ teaspoon salt, 1½ tablespoons sugar and 1 cup of water to a bowl. Dissolve the sugar completely and add the deveined shrimp. Use about 9 ounces (250 grams) shrimp. I used about 11 tiger prawns here. Let rest for 20 mins while you prepare the following ingredients: 2. 1 tablespoon (4 medium) garlic coves chopped 3. Drain the water completely from the prawns after 20 mins. Mix together 2 tablespoons corn flour, 2 tablespoons all purpose flour and ¼ teaspoon pepper in a small bowl and sprinkle all over the prawns. Coat them well and let rest for 10 mins so the dry flour absorbs the excess moisture. 4. Heat 1½ tablespoon oil in a pan until hot enough. Place the prawns one by one in the hot oil. 5. Let cook until they begin to swirl. Using a tong, turn them to the other side. Cook until they swirl to C shape, turn crisp and cooked through. It only takes about 3 to 4 mins as they are brined. Remove to a plate. 6. Clean up the pan with a paper towel and add 1 tablespoon oil. Saute garlic until fragrant. Add chilies, capsicum, onion cubes or spring onions whites and a pinch of salt. Saute on a high flame just for one to 2 mins until the bell peppers are half cooked yet retain their crunch. 7. Add 1½ tablespoon red chili sauce, 1 teaspoon red chili powder mixed with 2 tbsps water, 1 to 1½ teaspoon soya sauce, 1 to 1½ teaspoon rice vinegar, ½ teaspoon sugar, ½ teaspoon pepper and 2 tablespoons of water. 8. Bring to a boil and let cook until bubbling. Taste test to adjust sauces as per your preference. 9. Switch off the stove and add the fried prawns. Toss well and garnish with green onions. Serve chili prawns immediately to enjoy the crust. Tandoori Shrimp Prawn Curry (Praw... Prawns Fry Prawn biryani | S... Shrimp Pakora (Sh... Shrimp Tikka Masa...

title: Chinese Chilli Mushroom Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup button mushrooms, 0.5 cup bell pepper, 0.3333333333333333 cup spring onions, 1 green chili, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1.25 tablespoon olive oil, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons chili sauce, 0.875 tablespoon tomato ketchup, 0.375 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 2.5 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Stir in soya sauce, tomato ketchup and red chili sauce in a small bowl. Taste test and add salt or sugar if you want. 2. Rinse mushrooms well under running water. Slice or halve or quarter them. 3. Heat oil in a wok/skillet, on a medium high heat. 4. Add garlic and sauté for 30 to 60 seconds until aromatic. Do not brown it. 5. Next add mushrooms and slit green chilli. Saute for 2 mins. 6. Add the capsicum and half of the spring onions, toss and fry till the capsicums are almost cooked yet crunchy. 7. Make sure mushrooms are cooked yet not mushy. 8. Add the sauces to the skillet all over and pour 2 to 3 tablespoons of water. Quickly stir well. The water immediately blends with the sauce and evaporates. 9. Next sprinkle black pepper. Toss and fry for another min or until the sauce thickens. 10. Check salt and add more sauces to suit your taste. Chili sauce & ketchup already have salt so I have not used. Off the heat. 11. Serve chilli mushroom with hakka noodles or fried rice.

title: chinese pakoda recipe | chinese pakora recipe | chinese bhaji
ingredients: 1.5 cup cabbage, 1 carrot, 0.25 capsicum, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp vinegar, 1 tsp soy sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup maida plain flour, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp red food colour, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 1½ cup cabbage, 1 carrot, ¼ capsicum, 2 tbsp spring onion and ½ onion. 2. also add 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp vinegar, 1 tsp soy sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce and ½ tsp salt. 3. mix well making sure the sauces are well combined. 4. now add ½ cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour and ½ tsp red food colour. 5. mix well forming a loose dough. add more maida or corn flour if required. 6. grease your hand with oil and pinch a small ball sized pakoda mixture. 7. deep fry in hot oil or preheat and bake at 180 degree celcius for 20 minutes. 8. stir occasionally until the pakoda turns golden and crisp. 9. drain off the pakoda over kitchen towel to remove excess oil. 10. finally, enjoy chinese pakoda recipe with tomato sauce and green chutney.

title: chiroti recipe | padhir peni | chiroti sweet | chiroti with badam milk
ingredients: 2 cup chiroti rava semolina suji, 2 tbsp butter, 0.75 cup water, 0.25 cup maida plain flour, 0.25 cup butter, 2 tbsp rice flour
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cup chiroti rava and add 2 tbsp butter. 2. crumble and mix well making sure the rava is moist. 3. now add ¾ cup water and start to knead the dough. 4. knead for 5-8 minutes or until the rava turns soft. 5. knead really hard adding water (only if required) until the dough turns soft and smooth. 6. rest the dough for 15 minutes, or until the rava absorbs moisture. 7. pinch a large ball sized dough and dust with maida. 8. roll as thin as possible dusting maida if required. 9. now spread the prepared 1 tsp of saati (butter rice flour paste) uniformly. repeat 5 times. 10. now roll into log tightly. 11. cut into 1 inch pieces and press gently. 12. deep fry in medium hot oil. 13. drain off the chiorti and keep aside for 10 minutes to drain off the oil completely. 14. finally, enjoy chiroti by crushing into plate and top with powdered sugar and badam milk.

title: Chocolate Burfi (Barfi Recipe)
ingredients: 2.5 cups mawa, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon vanilla extract, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon cocoa powder, 1 teaspoon ghee
instructions: 1. Bring the khoya to room temperature and grate. Heating very cold mawa can sometimes impart bitter taste to your burfi. If frozen, defrost it either in the refrigerator overnight or keep it at room temperature for few hours or defrost in microwave. 2. Add this to a nonstick pan, (remember to use a nonstick) begin to heat it on a very low flame. Keep stirring constantly till the last step else the mixture will get burnt. 3. Once it is warm, add sugar, cardamom powder, and/or rose water (optional) over the sugar. Keep stirring. 4. Sugar begins to melt and the mixture comes to a halwa like consistency. Continue to cook. Do not forget, continue stirring. 5. After sometime it comes together like a mass and leaves the sides of the pan. It took me around 7 mins, but it depends on the kind of your pan or stove. So make sure it becomes like a mass and thick and not runny like halwa. To test, grease your fingers and roll a small portion to a ball. It should be dry and non-sticky, yet hold the shape. 6. Grease a butter paper or line a square tray. Divide the mix to 2 parts, mix one portion to smooth dough with a spatula. Shape to a square on the butter paper or transfer to the tray and even out the mixture. Quickly add cocoa powder & vanilla to the other part & incorporate with a spatula. Mix thoroughly for 3 mins, until really smooth. Transfer this over the first plain layer and add nuts. Smoothen with a spatula. The layers stick together without much effort only if the base layer is still hot. 7. Freeze it for 10 mins or refrigerate for 20 to 30 minutes and cut to desired shapes. Do not leave it in the freezer for longer. Store them in a air tight container and serve within 8 to 10 days.

title: chocolate custard recipe | eggless chocolate pudding custard recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup custard powder, 0.25 cup cocoa powder, 0.75 cup sugar, 5 cup milk, 1 cup cream malai
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take ½ cup custard powder, ¼ cup cocoa powder and ¾ cup sugar. 2. add 1 cup milk and mix using a whisk. 3. form a smooth lump free mixture. 4. pour the prepared chocolate custard mixture into the kadai. 5. add 1 cup cream and 4 cup milk. adding cream makes pudding rich and creamy. 6. keeping the flame on low and stir continuously. 7. continue to stir until the mixture turns smooth and silky. 8. keep cooking on low flame until the mixture thickens and turns glossy. takes approx 15 to 20 minutes. 9. transfer the chocolate custard pudding into small cups. 10. rest for 2 hours in the refrigerator to set completely. 11. now sprinkle with cocoa powder and garnish with white chocolate. 12. finally, enjoy eggless chocolate custard recipe with strawberry.

title: Chocolate Ice Cream Recipe
ingredients: 1 tin sweetened condensed milk, 2 cups cream, 2 teaspoons vanilla extract, 0.5 cup cocoa powder
instructions: 1. Add condensed milk and vanilla extract to a bowl. (1 tin sweetened condensed milk (397 grams, 14 oz can) & 2 teaspoons vanilla extract) 2. Add ½ cup cocoa powder. 3. Give a good mix to incorporate the cocoa into the condensed milk. 4. The mixture has to be without any lumps of cocoa. 5. Chill the ice cream bowl & beaters for at least 24 hours. Chill the cream as well in the fridge. 6. Pour 2 cups chilled heavy whipping cream to the chilled bowl. 7. Begin to whip on a low speed initially. Then on a medium speed. 8. The cream turns frothy first. 9. As it thickens be alert and reduce the speed. Do not over whip. 10. Next it reaches stiff peaks. 11. Transfer the chocolate condensed milk. 12. Gently give a good stir without deflating the cream. 13. Level the mixture. 14. Next place a cling wrap so that it touches the cream. This helps prevent ice crystals. 15. Cover with a lid. Freeze until set. It takes about 15 hours. Scoop and serve chocolate ice cream.

title: Chocolate Mousse Recipe - Without Eggs
ingredients: 1 cup cream, 3 grams dark chocolate chips, 2 cup thickened cream, 1 cup icing sugar, 0.5 cup condensed milk
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a saucepan, heat 1 cup of cream till it turns warm. Make sure not to boil the cream, as there is a chance the cream will curdle. 2. Pour the warm cream over 300 grams of dark chocolate chips. 3. Rest for 5 minutes, allowing the chocolate to melt. 4. Mix well, ensuring the cream is thoroughly combined. Keep aside. 5. In another bowl, take 2 cup thickened cream and beat on low speed. 6. Also, add 1 cup icing sugar and continue to beat on low speed. 7. Beat till the cream turns soft peaks. 8. Now add in the melted chocolate cream mixture and mix gently. 9. Check for sweetness, and add ½ cup condensed milk. 10. Mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined. 11. Using a piping bag, transfer the mixture to the glass. 12. Refrigerate for 4 hours, or until the mousse has thickened. 13. Finally, enjoy Chocolate Mousse Recipe garnished with white chocolate.

title: chocolate mug cake | 2 minute eggless chocolate mug cake recipe
ingredients: 3 tbsp flour maida all-purpose flour, 1.5 tbsp coco powder, 0.125 tsp baking powder, a pinch of baking soda, 3 tbsp condensed milk, 2 tbsp melted butter, 4 tbsp milk, fistful chocolate chunks, fistful raisins, sugar powder for toppings, chocolate syrup for toppings
instructions: 1. prepare all your ingredients before you start to bake cake. 2. add 3 tbsp of plain flour / all-purpose flour / maida flour to microwave safe coffee mug 3. add one and half tbsp of cocoa powder. 4. add 1/8th tsp of baking powder to mixture 5. add a pinch of baking soda 6. mix it properly and make sure no lumps are formed. 7. now add 2 tbsp of melted butter 8. add 3 tbsp of condensed milk 9. add 4 tbsp of thick milk 10. give a proper mix, so that no lumps are formed. 11. the chocolate mug cake is almost ready 12. top with sugar powder and chocolate syrup. 13. the chocolate mug is ready to serve.

title: chocolate poke cake
ingredients: 1.5 cups wheat flour, 4 tablespoons cocoa powder, 1 teaspoon Baking soda, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup milk, 0.3333333333333333 cup melted butter, 1 tablespoon vinegar, 1 tablespoon vanilla extract, 6 tablespoons sugar, 3 tablespoons cocoa powder, 0.75 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon vanilla extract, 1 cup whipping cream, 1 tablespoon powdered sugar, 0.5 teaspoon vanilla extract
instructions: 1. Grease a 6 inch square cake pan. Preheat the oven to 170 C for at least 15 mins. 2. Place a sieve over a mixing bowl and add flour, baking soda, salt and cocoa powder. Sieve it at least thrice. Add sugar and mix it well. 3. Make a well in the center and pour water or milk, oil or melted butter, vanilla and vinegar.Mix the wet ingredients gently and then slowly incorporate the flour with the liquid ingredients. Do not over mix but ensure there are no lumps in the batter. 4. Pour this to the grease tray and bake for 23 to 25 mins. When done a tester inserted comes out clean. Cool down the cake in the pan itself over a wire rack until it cools down a bit but still warm. 5. Mix together cocoa powder, sugar and water in a pot. Mix well to break the lumps and boil the syrup until it thickens. It took me about 7 to 9 minutes. The mixture must turn thick & coat the back of a spoon. If using palm jaggery or jaggery, just filter the chocolate syrup and use. Cool this a bit. 6. Chill the bowls and the beaters for few hours. Pour the whipping cream to the chilled bowl. Begin to whip on a low speed initially and then increase the speed to medium. When the mixture turns bubbly add the sugar and beat until you see stiff peaks. Take care not to over whip as it can turn to butter. Keep this chilled until needed. 7. When the temperature of the cake comes down, poke the cake several times with a chop stick about ¾ inch apart. Avoid poking the cake towards the edges. Make several holes all over the cake. Ensure the holes are deep reaching the base. 8. The temperature of the chocolate syrup should have come down slightly but still warm. Mix well and pour the syrup all over the cake. The syrup must go through the holes. Set this aside to cool the cake completely. You can also chill it in the fridge. 9. Spread the whipped cream generously all over the cake. Decorate with some sprinkles or chocolate shavings. Chill the chocolate poke cake until served. Slice the cake when needed and serve.

title: chocolate pudding recipe | eggless choco pudding | choc pudding
ingredients: 1.5 cup milk, 2 tbsp cocoa powder, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.5 cup cream, 0.5 cup chocolate chip, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup milk and add 2 tbsp cocoa powder and 2 tbsp corn flour. 2. whisk well until the mixture is well combined without any lumps. 3. transfer the mixture to a kadai and add ½ cup milk. 4. cook on low flame stirring continuously. 5. once the mixture is well combined add ¼ cup sugar. if you are looking for slightly sweeter pudding then add ½ cup sugar. 6. stir until the sugar is dissolved completely. 7. further, add ½ cup cream and continue to stir until the mixture turns smooth and silky. 8. now add ½ cup chocolate chip. i have used a milk chocolate chip, you can use dark chocolate for intense chocolate flavour. 9. stir until the chocolate chip melts completely. 10. keep cooking on low flame until the mixture thickens and turns glossy. 11. turn off the flame and add 1 tsp vanilla extract and ¼ tsp salt. mix well. 12. transfer the chocolate pudding into small cups and cover with a lid. you can alternatively use cling wrap to cover. rest for 2 hours in the refrigerator to set completely. 13. finally, enjoy eggless chocolate pudding recipe garnished with chocolate chips.

title: Chocolate Sandwich
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 2 tablespoons Butter, 0.25 cup chocolate chips, 0.25 teaspoon cinnamon, 3 tablespoons walnuts, 1 ripe banana
instructions: 1. Place 2 bread slices on a board and spread mini chocolate chips or chocolate of choice on one slice. Layer the chopped nuts all over the chocolate or spread peanut butter over the other slice. 2. If you prefer sprinkle some ground cinnamon and place the sliced banana (or maple syrup if required). 3. Cover the chocolate filled bread with the other slice of bread. Spread ½ tablespoon butter over the chocolate sandwich 4. Melt ½ tablespoon butter in the pan. 5. Place the sandwich gently in the hot pan, with the buttered side up. Let toast on a low heat until the bread turns crisp. Gently turn it to the other side with a spatula. 6. Immediately sprinkle chocolate chips on top of the hot toasted chocolate sandwich. The chocolate begins to melt, spread it with a butter knife or a spoon. 7. Remove to a rack once the sandwich is crisp at the bottom. Garnish with chopped nuts or chopped white chocolate. 8. Add ½ tablespoon butter to your preheated sandwich toaster and place the sandwiches with the buttered side up. Cover and toast them until golden and crisp. 9. You can also place the prepared sandwich (buttered on both the sides) in the air fryer. Air fry for 5 mins at 350 F or 180 C. Do not air fry for too long the chocolate can burn on the sides and the bread turns too hard.

title: Chocolate Sponge Cake
ingredients: 0.5 cup all-purpose flour, 2.5 tbsp cocoa, 0.125 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp baking powder, 5 tbsp sugar, 2 eggs, 1 egg yolk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 4 tbsp oil, 0.5 cup 2 tbsp semi sweet chocolate chips, 1 chocolate bar
instructions: 1. Make sure all the ingredients are at room temperature. 2. Grease & line a 6 inch pan. Alternately grease & dust some cocoa evenly. Invert the pan to remove excess. To make layers for black forest cake you can also use two 6 inch pans. Preheat the oven at 340 F - 170 C for at least 15 mins 3. Sieve together cocoa, flour, baking powder & salt to a mixing bowl. Mix well and keep aside. 4. Break 2 whole eggs to a large mixing bowl. Separate 1 egg yolk and add it as well along with sugar. 5. Begin to beat on a low speed to avoid splashes. Then increase the speed to high and beat until frothy. 6. Pour vanilla & continue to beat until a ribbon consistency is achieved. The eggs turn frothy & foam up. The volume will more than double and will reach a ribbon stage. 7. Lift the whisk & check to find the ribbon consistency. The egg mixture will fall like a ribbon from the whisk when you lift it. If this is not achieved then continue to beat. 8. Next keep beating and pour oil in a slow stream to the egg mixture. Beat the mixture on a high speed just until combined. Do not over beat. 9. The flour needs to be added in 3 small batches. Now sprinkle 1/3 of the dry mixture/ ingredients to the egg mixture. 10. Using a whisk gently mix just until partially incorporated. Do not over mix or beat the batter at this stage as it deflates the mixture & can make a flat dense cake. 11. Repeat adding another 1/3 portion of the flour and mix. Finish off by adding the last part. Mix until just combined well. 12. Transfer to the prepared pans. Tap the pan to the counter twice. 13. Bake at 170 C for 15 mins if using two 6 inch pans. Bake for 20 to 24 mins if using a single 6 inch pan. 14. When the cake is done a tester inserted comes out clean. Transfer the cake pans to a cooling rack and cool down for 15 mins. Invert on a cooling rack and cool completely. 15. Dust some powdered sugar or make a frosting. This chocolate sponge cake can be used to make black forest cake or just melt some chocolate chips and pour it over the cake. 16. I melted little over half a cup of choco chips and frosted the cake. Decorated with some chocolate shavings.

title: chole bhature recipe | chhole bhature | chana bhatura | chola batura
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp rava semolina, 1 tsp sugar, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 0.25 cup curd, water, oil, 1 cup chana chickpea, 2 tea bags, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 2 pod cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur, 0.25 tsp salt, 1.5 cup tomato puree, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 2 chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp rava, 1 tsp sugar, ¼ tsp baking soda, 1 tsp sugar, ½ tsp salt and 2 tbsp oil. mix well. 2. now add ¼ cup curd and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 3. further, add water as required and knead the dough. 4. knead to the smooth and soft dough without putting much pressure. 5. grease the dough with oil, cover and rest for 2 hours 6. after 2 hours, knead the dough slightly. 7. pinch a ball sized dough and make a ball without andy cracks. 8. roll slightly thick, applying oil to prevent from sticking. 9. drop the rolled dough into the hot oil. 10. press until the bhature puffs up and splash oil to puff up fully. 11. flip over and fry until it turns golden brown. 12. finally, drain off the bhatura and is ready to enjoy with chole masala. 13. firstly, in a pressure cooker take soaked chana. i have soaked 1 cup chana in enough water for 8 hours. 14. add 2 tea bags, ¼ tsp baking soda, 1 tsp salt and 3 cup water. 15. cover and pressure cook for 5 whistles. if you do not have tea bags, then you can prepare tea decoction and add to cooker. 16. once the pressure releases, open the cooker and discard the tea bags. keep aside. 17. in a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 2 pod cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp kasuri methi. saute on low flame until the spices turn aroamtic. 18. now add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until onions turn golden brown. 19. add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur and ¼ tsp salt. 20. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 21. further add 1½ cup tomato puree and saute until the oil separates. to prepare tomato puree, i have blended 2½ ripened tomatoes in blender without adding water. 22. now add boiled chole and mix well. adjust consistency by adding water if required. 23. cover and simmer for 10 minutes, or until chole absorbs all the flavour. 24. to prepare the tempering, heat 1 tbsp ghee in a pan. 25. add 2 chilli, ¼ tsp turmeri, ¼ tsp chilli powder and ¼ tsp garam masala. 26. saute on low flame without burning the spices. 27. pour the tempering over the chole masala, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 28. finally, chole bhature is ready to enjoy with some onions. 29. finally, drain off the bhatura and is ready to enjoy with chole masala .

title: Chole Recipe (Punjabi Chole Masala)
ingredients: 1 cup chickpeas, 2 cups water, 1 black tea bag, 1 black cardamom, 4 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 0.5 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 2 green cardamon, 0.5 bay leaf, 2 tablespoons oil, 1.5 cup onions, 1 green chili, 0.875 tablespoon ginger garlic, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tablespoon chole masala, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon amchur, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 inch ginger, 3 green chilli, 0.25 teaspoon hing, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add 1 cup dried chickpeas (chole) to a pot and rinse them well at least thrice. Pour 3 to 4 cups water and soak them overnight or at least for 8 hours. Longer soaking time helps in better digestion and chole turns out softer. Later drain and rinse them once with fresh water. 2. Pour 2 cups water. Also add one tea bag or 2 pieces of amla. I prefer loose leaf tea so I make a decoction. Bring 1 cup water to a rolling boil and add 1 tablespoon loose leaf tea (like wagh bakri) or 1 teaspoon tea dust. Boil for 2 to 3 minutes. Turn off and let rest for 3 to 4 mins. Later filter the decoction to the chickpeas and pour 1 more cup water (total 2 cups). Optionally add the following spices for extra flavor: 3. black cardamom 4 green cardamoms 1 inch cinnamon 2 cloves optional – half of a small bay leaf/tej patta (optional, make sure it is fresh else it can make your dish bitter) If you want you may also add a pinch of baking soda so the chickpeas cook faster. 4. Cook the chole till soft but not mushy. On a medium flame, I pressure cook for 5 to 6 whistles (without soda) or for 2 whistles with soda. If cooking in an Instant pot (no soda), you will cook for 16 minutes in 6qt IP or for 13 to 14 mins in 3qt IP. When the pressure drops, the spices will float on top, I prefer to discard the bay leaf and cinnamon. If you use tea bag, remove that as well. 5. Chole must be soft cooked and not remain al dente. If they are al dente or even slightly hard, cook them again for 1 to 2 whistles. When you mash a chickpea, it must get mushy else it is undercooked. 6. Rinse and soak chana for 8 hours and drain the water. Give them a good rinse. Press saute button on your Instant pot. Pour 2 tablespoons oil to the steel insert of your instant pot. Then add 2 cloves, 2 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom,1 inch piece of cinnamon and optionally half of a small bay leaf. 7. As soon as they begin to sizzle add 1½ cup fine chopped onions & 1 green chili (optional). Begin to saute them. 8. Spread them evenly in the oil so they fry faster and uniformly. 9. Saute until the onions turn golden. If the pot becomes too hot, press cancel sometime in between or switch to saute – less. Then add ¾ to 1 tablespoon ginger garlic (paste or chopped) & saute for 30 to 40 seconds. 10. Add 1 cup tomatoes (Fine chopped or pureed, 3 No) and ¾ teaspoon salt. 11. Saute until the tomatoes break down and become mushy. 12. Then add ½ to ¾ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (adjust to taste) and ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. If you are using homemade chole masala from this post, add the entire ground spices. However if you want to use store bought powder use the following spices: 13. tablespoon chole masala (ready made) ¾ teaspoon garam masala (not required if using homemade ground spices) 1 teaspoon coriander powder (not required if using homemade ground spices) If you do not have chole masala, replace it with another ¾ teaspoon garam masala. This is just a substitute but won’t give you the same flavors. 14. Saute for a few minutes until onion tomato masala begins to smell good. This is a very important step so do not skip it. 15. Next pour 2 cups water (or 1 cup water + 1 cup tea decoction). Deglaze by mixing, at the bottom of the pot with a spatula so any bits of masala stuck there will release. This also avoids burning of any food particles at the bottom. 16. Add chickpeas. Mix well and press cancel. Secure the instant pot with the lid. Position the steam release vent to sealing. 17. Press pressure cook button (high pressure) and set the timer to 35 minutes. If using canned chickpeas set the timer to 10 minutes instead. The IP beeps when it is done. Wait for the pressure to release naturally for 18 to 20 mins. Then open the lid. 18. Taste test and adjust salt, garam masala or more amchur for tang if required. Press the saute button and simmer for 1 to 2 minutes. If you prefer a thicker curry, take 3 to 4 tablespoons chole to another bowl and mash them very well. Transfer this back to the pot and simmer for 3 minutes. 19. Pour 1 tablespoon ghee, ½ inch ginger juliennes and 1 teaspoon kasuri methi. Or make a tadka, by heating 1 tablespoon ghee in a tadka pan. Slightly slit 2 to 4 green chilies with the ends and seeds intact. Fry them in the ghee until blistered, using a splatter screen. Add the ginger juliennes and turn off the heat. Add ¼ teaspoon hing. Pour this over the Chole. 20. Garnish chole with 2 tablespoons fine chopped coriander leaves and serve with bhatura, poori or naan.

title: Chum Chum Recipe | Cham Cham Bengali Dessert
ingredients: 2.5 litre milk, 2 tbsp vinegar, 1.5 cup sugar, 6 cup water, 1 cup milk, pinch kesar food colour, 1 cup milk powder, 2 tbsp sugar, cherry
instructions: 1. Firstly, bring 2½ litres of milk to a boil, stirring occasionallyo prevent it from burning. 2. Add 2 tbsp vinegar and give it a stir. You will notice the milk starts to curdle. 3. Add 1 more tbsp of vinegar and stir until the milk curdles fully, separating the water. 4. Drain off the water over the cheesecloth. You can use any clean cloth here. Rinse with cold water to remove sourness and stop cooking. 5. Squeeze the paneer gently and let it rest for 10 minutes to allow excess water to drain off. 6. Now take the moist paneer and crumble gently. 7. Using the palm, start to knead gently. 8. Knead until the paneer mixture turns smooth without any grains. Do not over-knead here, as the chum chum will turn hard. 9. Pinch a small ball-sized chenna and prepare smooth, crack-free cylindrical balls, flatten slightly. 10. Keep the flattened paneer ball aside and cover it with a moist cloth. 11. Meanwhile, in a large vessel, take 1½ cup sugar, 6 cup water. Stir and dissolve sugar. 12. Now boil the water for 5 minutes or until the syrup turns slightly sticky. 13. Drop in a prepared paneer ball, keeping the flame on high. 14. Cover and boil for 5 minutes, or until the ball's size has doubled. 15. Flip over and continue to boil for 15 minutes, or until the chenna is cooked through. Keep aside. 16. To prepare the stuffing, combine 1 cup of milk, a pinch of kesar food colour, and 1 cup of milk powder in a pan. 17. Keep stirring and cook till the mixture thickens. 18. Now add 2 tbsp sugar and mix well. 19. Cook until the khova is well-cooked and no longer sticky. The stuffing will thicken once cooled. 20. Now take the chenna and slit it in the centre. 21. Stuff the mixture in the centre, making sure the stuffing is uniformly distributed. 22. Decorate with a cherry and keep it in the refrigerator. 23. Finally, enjoy the Chum Chum Recipe chilled for a week when stored in the refrigerator.

title: Chum Chum Recipe | Cham Cham Bengali Dessert
ingredients: 2.5 litre milk, 2 tbsp vinegar, 1.5 cup sugar, 6 cup water, 1 cup milk, pinch kesar food colour, 1 cup milk powder, 2 tbsp sugar, cherry

title: Churumuri Recipe (Puffed Rice Snacks)
ingredients: 3 cups Puffed Rice, 1 onion, 1 medium Tomato, 1 Green chili, 0.375 teaspoon red chilli powder, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 2.5 tablespoon Peanuts, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon cumin
instructions: 1. First roast peanuts on a medium flame until aromatic and golden. Set aside to cool. Once they cool down you can remove the skin if you prefer. For this recipe you will need 2 to 3 tablespoons peanuts. 2. Prepare the following: 3. 1 small onion (fine chop) 4. 1 medium Tomato (fine chop) 5. 1 green chili (chopped, deseeded if required, leave out for kids) 6. 2 tablespoon coriander leaves (fine chop) 7. 2 tablespoons grated carrots or chopped cucumber (optional) 8. You need 3 cups muri/ puffed rice for this recipe. To make churumuri, you don’t need to dry roast the puffed rice unless they are soft. You can also pop them in the oven for a few mins at 50 to 60 C. I usually break open a new pack to make this so I don’t dry roast. But clean them. I sieve them to remove the powdery part. Add the puffed rice to a wide bowl.

title: churumuri recipe | masala mandakki recipe | spiced puffed rice recipe
ingredients: 2 cup churumuri puffed rice murmura, 2 tbsp coconut oil, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp fried peanuts, 0.5 onion, 0.5 tomato, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp sev
instructions: 1. firstly, dry roast 2 cup churumuri on low flame until it turns crisp. you can alternatively keep under hot sunlight. 2. now take crispy murmura in a large mixing bowl. 3. add 2 tbsp coconut oil, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp salt and 2 tbsp fried peanuts. 4. mix well making sure the spices are coated well to churumuri. 5. now add ½ onion, ½ tomato, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp coriander and 2 tbsp lemon juice. 6. mix roughly without allowing churumuri from turning soggy. 7. further, add 2 tbsp sev and mix roughly. 8. finally, enjoy churumuri or masala mandakki topped with more sev and coriander.

title: Chutney Powder (Pappula Podi)
ingredients: 1 cup peanuts, 0.3333333333333333 cup fried gram, 9 kashmiri dried red chilies, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 tablespoon cumin seeds, 2.5 garlic, 1 teaspoon oil
instructions: 1. Dry roast peanuts on a medium flame until golden and crunchy. When they turn aromatic, add fried gram or desiccated coconut. 2. Roast for another minute and remove to a plate and cool. 3. Pour 1 teaspoon oil and heat it. Add curry leaves and dried red chilies. Fry them on a medium heat until crisp. Then turn down the flame to low and add cumin and garlic. Stir just for 30 seconds. 4. Turn off the heat and let them cool down. 5. Once cool, add the curry leaves, garlic, red chilies and cumin to a grinder or spice jar and make a smooth powder. 6. Then peanuts, fried gram and salt. Run the grinder in short intervals of 30 to 50 seconds scraping down the sides. Make sure everything is mixed well. Avoid overgrinding else peanuts will release oil. 7. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 8. Store in an air tight jar. keeps fresh for about 2 to 3 months.

title: chutney sandwich recipe 2 ways | chutney cheese sandwich & chutney club sandwich
ingredients: 1 cup coriander, 0.5 cup mint, 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, 4 chilli, 2 inch ginger, 4 cloves garlic, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tbsp water, 3 slice bread, butter, 4 slice potato, 4 slice tomato, 2 slice bread, butter, 4 slice potato, 6 slice cucumber, 4 slice tomato, 5 slice onion, 2 tbsp cheese
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 1 cup coriander and ½ cup mint. 2. add 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, 4 chilli, 2 inch ginger, 4 cloves garlic, and 2 tbsp lemon juice. 3. also add ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp aamchur, 1 tsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. 4. blend to smooth paste adding 3 tbsp water. 5. finally, green chutney recipe can be stored in the refrigerator for a week. 6. firstly, take 3 slices of bread and spread butter and green chutney on one slice. 7. place 4 slices of potato and place another slice of bread spread with butter and green chutney. 8. now on top of bread, spread butter, and green chutney. 9. place 4 slices tomato and cover with bread slice. make sure to spread bread with butter and green chutney. 10. finally, cut chutney sandwich into 4 triangles and is ready to serve or pack into your lunch box. 11. firstly, take a slice of bread and spread butter and green chutney. 12. place 4 slice potato, 6 slice cucumber, 4 slice tomato, 5 slice onion. 13. also, top with 2 tbsp cheese. 14. now cover with bread slice. make sure to spread bread with butter and green chutney. 15. grill the sandwich spreading butter as required. 16. grill to golden brown and cut in half. 17. finally, enjoy cheese sandwich topped with more cheese if required.

title: Cilantro Chutney (Coriander Chutney)
ingredients: 3 cups cilantro, 1.5 green chilies, 1.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 2.5 medium garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 3 tablespoons cold water, 3 tablespoons roasted peanuts
instructions: 1. Rinse cilantro under running water and shake off very well to get rid of the excess moisture. Cut down the stems and roots, discard them. Remove & discard mature stalks and keep the tender/ young ones. Roughly chop leaves & tender stalks. 2. Peel the garlic and make sure they have not sprouted or begun to sprout. 3. Remove the stalks of green chilies. If you prefer low heat, deseed the chilies. 4. Add the peanuts, green chilies, salt, cumin and garlic to the base of the blender jar. If you can't eat spicy and pungent foods, you may start with a little piece of chili and 2 garlic cloves, adjust towards the end. 5. Transfer coriander leaves, lemon juice and water. Start with only a few tablespoons of water (2 tbsps). 6. Blend the ingredients to a smooth cilantro chutney, scraping the sides every 30 to 40 seconds. Add more water only as required while blending. 7. Chutney has to thick, of spreading consistency. If it is too thick add a tbsps more water. 8. Taste test and add more salt, green chilies or lemon juice as required to taste. 9. Transfer cilantro chutney to serving bowl. Serve with any snacks like pakora, samosa and sandwiches. 10. Cover and refrigerate the leftovers for 4 days. You may optionally refresh the chutney the next day with few tbsps of thick strained yogurt. Give a good stir.

title: Classic Chocolate Cake
ingredients: 0.75 cup 2 tbsp flour, 0.25 cup 2 tbsp cocoa powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.75 tsp baking powder, 0.75 tsp baking soda, 1 cup sugar, 1 egg, 0.5 cup milk, 0.25 cup oil, 1.5 tsp vanilla extract, 0.5 cup boiling hot water, 0.5 cup butter, 1.75 cup powdered sugar, 4 tbsp cocoa powder, 1.5 tbsp milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract
instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 175 C or 350 F for at least 15 mins. Grease and line 2 – 6 inch cake pans. You can also make a single layer cake in a 7 or 8 inch pan. You can also just grease and dust with some flour or cocoa. 2. Place a sieve over a mixing bowl. Add flour. 3. Add the rest of the dry ingredients – cocoa, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Sieve them all. 4. Next add sugar. 5. Mix everything very well until uniform. Make a well in the center. 6. Heat half cup water in a pot. (Don’t add the water here). Meanwhile pour milk, egg, oil and vanilla to the dry ingredients. 7. Whisk the wet ingredients first. Then whisk together wet and dry ingredients just until combined. Do not over mix. 8. You will have a slightly thick batter at this stage. 9. Quickly pour the hot water to the batter. The water must be very hot. 10. Immediately whisk the batter just until the water is absorbed and the batter looks uniform. Batter is very runny at this stage. 11. Pour this to the pans. Tap the pan to the counter 2 to 3 times. 12. Bake for 28 to 30 mins or until the tester comes out clean. Please adjust the timing depending on the oven and pan sizes. If baking in a 7 inch pan you may need to bake up to 35 to 38 mins. Cool the pans on a wire rack for 10 mins. 13. Then invert to a cooling rack. Cool completely before frosting.

title: Clear Soup Recipe (Clear Vegetable Soup)
ingredients: 1 yellow onion, 1 cup celery stalks chopped, 2 carrots, 1 french beans, 0.5 cabbage, 1.5 stalks celery leaves, 1.5 cup mushrooms, 8 florets cauliflower, 1 tsp garlic, 1 tsp ginger, 1 tbsp oil, 1 stalk spring onion, 0.5 tsp crushed pepper
instructions: 1. Add all the veggies except mushrooms & spring onions to a large pot. 2. Pour water just enough to immerse them. I used 2½ cups of water. 3. Cover and simmer on a low flame until the veggies wilt off completely and turn flavorless. 4. Place a strainer over a large pot and strain the veggies. 5. If you intend to eat the veggies then skip this step. Mash the veggies well & leave in the strainer for 20 mins. You will get about half cup soup. 6. Heat the same pan with oil. 7. Saute ginger and garlic for a minute. 8. Then add the mushrooms and saute well until they begin to smell good. 9. Pour the strained clear soup to this and simmer until the mushrooms are done to your liking. 10. If desired add some salt to taste. You can also serve the veggies on the side if you desire. 11. Add 250 grams bone-in chicken along with veggies & 3 cups water to the pot. 12. Cook until the chicken falls off the bone. 13. Remove the chicken aside and then strain the clear soup. 14. Shred the chicken and set aside. 15. Heat oil and saute ginger garlic until aromatic. 16. Saute the shredded chicken and pour the clear soup. You can also saute mushrooms first and then add chicken. 17. Let the soup come to a boil to bring out the flavors of ginger and garlic. 18. Add spring onion greens and season with salt.

title: Cluster Beans Recipe (Goruchikkudu Recipe)
ingredients: 25 grams cluster beans, 0.875 cup water, 2 onions, 1 green chilli, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Rinse the cluster beans in lot of water and drain them 2. Gently break/ cut one end of the bean. If needed string them by pulling the string lengthwise. 3. Repeat the same with both the ends. Cut them to ½ to ¾ inch sized pieces. I usually hold a bunch of them and chop at one time. 4. Add them to a bowl and pour water. Cover it. 5. Pressure cook for 3 whistles by placing the bowl in the cooker. I do it for 10 mins along with my rice in Instant pot. 6. Meanwhile heat oil in a pan. Add mustard and cumin. 7. When they splutter, add curry leaves, onions and chilies. 8. Saute and fry the onions until deep golden to light brown. 9. Then add ginger garlic paste and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. 10. Stir in red chilli powder, garam masala, salt and turmeric. 11. When the pressure releases from the cooker, remove the cluster beans and check. 12. Option 1 - If the beans are firm yet cooked correctly then transfer the cooked cluster beans along with the stock to the pan. Stir and cook covered on a medium flame until all the water evaporates. 13. Option 2 - If the cluster beans overcooked, then just pour the stock to the pan. Cook until the water is almost absorbed by onions. Then just stir in the cooked cluster beans and stir fry for a while until the masala blends well. 14. Cluster beans should be soft cooked yet should hold shape and not turn mushy. Check salt and add more if needed. 15. Serve goruchikkudu curry/fry with rice, ghee & rasam.

title: Coconut Burfi (Nariyal Barfi)
ingredients: 4.25 cups coconut, 1.5 cups sugar, 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 tablespoons cashews, 2 cups milk, 1.75 cup desiccated coconut, 0.5 tin condensed milk, 0.5 cup milk, 0.125 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 cups desiccated coconut, 1.5 cups mawa, 0.5 cup milk, 1 cup sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon ghee, 2 tablespoons pistachios
instructions: 1. Grease a pan and optionally line it with a parchment paper. 2. Heat a pan with 1 tbsp ghee. Fry cashews until golden and remove to a plate. 3. Stir together coconut and milk. Bring it to a boil on a medium high flame. 4. Reduce the heat and cook until the milk evaporates. Stir in the sugar and cook until all of the moisture has completely evaporated. 5. Add ghee and continue to cook stirring constantly until the mixture turns dry & leaves the pan. 6. Press down the mixture with a spatula to check if there is any excess moisture. If it still has moisture then cook further to dry it. (check video) 7. Keep stirring continuously at this stage and cook until the mixture turns dense, thick and sticky. It also turns very aromatic and leaves the pan. If you prefer a very soft set burfi then take it off a bit early. 8. Stir in cardamom powder and Transfer the mixture to the greased pan. Press down with a spatula. 9. Sprinkle nuts and keep aside at room temperature for 3 to 4 hours to set. Slice the coconut burfi. Leave it at room temperature for 8 to 10 hours to set completely. Cool and store coconut barfi in a air tight jar. 10. To a pan, add coconut, desiccated coconut and milk. 11. Mix and cook on a medium flame until the entire mixture turns thick and to a mass. Add cardamom powder and mix. Transfer this to a tray. 12. Press down with a spatula. Sprinkle some nuts. Refrigerate for an hour. Cut coconut barfi to squares and enjoy. 13. Add mawa, sugar and milk to a heavy bottom pan. Heat until the mawa and sugar dissolves completely. 14. Stir in the coconut and cook until the coconut burfi mixture turns thick to a mass and begins to leave the sides of the pan. 15. Stir in cardamom powder and transfer to a greased pan. Level with a spatula and press down the nuts with spatula. 16. When the mixture cools down to warm, cut the coconut burfi to desired shapes. Do not wait to cool completely as it turns to powder. 17. Remove from the tray only after cooling completely.

title: Coconut Chutney Recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup coconut, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons fried gram roasted split chana dal, 1 garlic clove, 1.5 green chilies, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.29166666666666663 cup water, 1.5 teaspoons lemon juice, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 1 pinch split urad dal
instructions: 1. Wash and chop capsicum to cubes, slit green chilies. Pour 1 teaspoon oil to a pan. Add ginger and green chilies. Saute them for a minute, then add the bell peppers. Saute all of them until the bell peppers turn soft. Then add cumin seeds and stir. Turn off the stove. If using frozen coconut you may saute it in the same pan until slightly hot. 2. Cool all the ingredients and transfer them to a grinder jar along with roasted gram, salt & lemon juice. You may want to set a few green chilies aside. You can add them later if needed. 3. Blend to a smooth chutney adding water as needed. The amount of water to add depends on the capsicum. Taste test and add more salt or fried green chilies. 4. I usually don’t temper this. But you can do it by heating 1 teaspoon oil in the same pan. Add a pinch of mustard, 1 pinch of urad dal, 1 broken dried red chilli and crushed garlic clove. When the urad dal turns golden, add curry leaves and hing. Turn off and pour this to the capsicum coconut chutney. 5. Substitutes: Roasted gram can be substituted with chana dal or Bengal. But it has to roasted first on a low to medium heat until lightly brown or deep golden. Cool and add directly to the grinder jar along with other ingredients.

title: Coconut Chutney Recipe | Nariyal Ki Chatni 4 Ways
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tomato, 2 clove garlic, 3 red chilli, 0.75 cup coconut, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 2 tsp oil, 0.25 cup peanut, 3 red chilli, 0.5 cup coconut, piece tamarind, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 cup coriander, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 cup coconut, 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, 1 chilli, 0.5 lemon, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup coconut, 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, piece tamarind, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tomato, 2 clove garlic, 3 dried red chilli and saute well. 2. saute until the tomato softens well. 3. cool completely, and tranfer to a mixer jar. 4. add ¾ cup coconut, ½ tsp salt and ½ cup water. 5. grind to smooth paste. 6. add the tempering and enjoy tomato coconut chutney with idli or dosa. 7. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil and fry ¼ cup peanut until it turns crunchy. 8. add 3 dried red chilli and roast until the chilli puffs up. 9. cool completely and transfer to the mixer jar. 10. add ½ cup coconut, small piece of tamarind and ½ tsp salt. 11. grind to fine powder without adding water. 12. now add ½ cup water and grind to a smooth paste. 13. add the tempering and enjoy peanut coconut chutney with idli or dosa. 14. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil. add ½ cup coriander, 2 chilli and 1 inch ginger. 15. saute until the coriander shrinks slightly. keep aside. 16. in a mixer jar take 1 cup coconut, 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, 1 chilli, ½ lemon, and ½ tsp salt. 17. grind to smooth paste adding ½ cup water. 18. now add fried coriander, pulse, and grind to coarse texture. 19. add the tempering and enjoy coriander coconut chutney with idli or dosa. 20. firstly, in a mixer jar take 1 cup coconut, 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, small piece tamarind, and ½ tsp salt. 21. grind to smooth paste adding ½ cup water. 22. add the tempering and enjoy ginger coconut chutney with idli or dosa.

title: Coconut Ladoo | No Sugar Nariyal Ke Laddu
ingredients: 4 tbsp ghee, 0.5 cup almond, 0.5 cup cashew, 0.25 cup walnut, 2 tbsp sunflower seeds, 2 tbsp pumpkin seeds, 2 tbsp raisins, 4 cup coconut, 2 cup jaggery, 1 tbsp poppy seeds, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in pan heat 2 tbsp ghee. add ½ cup almond, ½ cup cashew, ¼ cup walnut, 2 tbsp sunflower seeds, 2 tbsp pumpkin seeds and 2 tbsp raisins. 2. roast on low flame until the nuts turn crunchy. keep aside. 3. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee and add 4 cup coconut. 4. Roast on low flame until the coconut turns aromatic. If you are using desiccated coconut, then make sure to soak it in warm water for at least 10 minutes. 5. now add 2 cup jaggery and mix well. 6. Continue to cook until the jaggery melts. 7. Keep cooking till the mixture starts to hold shape. 8. Add in roasted nuts, 1 tbsp poppy seeds, and ½ tsp cardamom powder. 9. Mix well combining everything well. 10. Now cool the mixture slightly. 11. When the mixture is still warm start making ladoo. 12. Finally, the coconut dry fruit ladoo is ready to enjoy for 2 weeks when refrigerated.

title: Coconut Ladoo Recipe (Nariyal Laddu)
ingredients: 2 cups coconut, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 8 cashews, 1 teaspoon ghee, 0.75 cup sugar, 1 cup milk, 1.5 cups desiccated coconut, 0.5 cup 2 tbsps condensed milk, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 cups coconut, 1 cup jaggery, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 cashews
instructions: 1. Heat 1 teaspoon ghee in a pan, add 8 broken cashew nuts. 2. Fry them stirring often until they turn golden. Remove these to a plate and set aside. 3. To the same pan, add grated coconut, milk and sugar. 4. If using desiccated coconut set the pan aside for 15 minutes for the coconut to absorb the milk and soften. 5. Mix all of the ingredients well. 6. Begin to cook till the coconut ladoo mixture thickens and all the moisture evaporates completely. 7. The mixture must still be moist and not dry. 8. Add cardamom powder and nuts. Mix well. Cool the mixture slightly. 9. When it comes down to warm temperature, take small portions of this to your palm and press down with your fingers to bind the mixture. This gives the round shape coconut ladoos. (Check video) 10. When they are still warm and moist you can also roll the ladoos in desiccated coconut. 11. Store coconut laddu in an airtight jar and refrigerate. Use within 5 to 7 days. 12. To a pan, add 1½ cups desiccated coconut, ¼ tsp cardamom powder and ½ cup + 2 tablespoons condensed milk. 13. Mix up all of these and cook on a low flame stirring often. Taste test and If needed add more condensed milk for more sweetness. 14. Cook stirring continuously, till the mixture thickens and turns to a mass. (check step-by-step photos above for consistency) 15. Turn off the stove. At this stage the coconut ladoo mixture must be still moist. If you overcook then the ladoos will turn hard. 16. Cool and add chopped pistachios. When it is still warm, take small portions of this mixture to your palms and press down with your fingers to give a round shape to the coconut ladoos. 17. Cool these completely. Store in a air tight box and refrigerate. Use up within a week. 18. Heat 1 tsp ghee and fry cashews. Remove and keep them aside. In the same pan, mix together fresh coconut and grated jaggery in a pan. 19. Once the jaggery melts, taste test and add more if needed. Cook until the mixture smells good , turns thick & binds together. 20. Add cardamoms and fried nuts. Cool down and roll to balls. 21. Store coconut laddus in a air tight jar and store at room temperature. Use up within 7 days.

title: Coconut Ladoo Recipe with Jaggery | Kobbari Louz Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups coconut, 0.75 cup jaggery, 0.25 tsp Green cardamom, Ghee, 0.25 cup Water
instructions: 1. Add ¼ cup water to grated jaggery and melt it on a low flame. You can do it in a microwave or stove top. Set this aside. If your jaggery is clean you can directly add it to the coconut. 2. Heat a heavy bottom pan, add 1 tsp. oil or ghee. Add the coconut and fry for about 2 to 3 mins on a medium to low flame, stirring constantly to prevent burning. This step is optional. You can skip to the next step if you wish not to fry the coconut. I feel this step brings out the aroma in coconut. 3. Filter the jaggery syrup to the coconut and mix well 4. Add cardamom powder and give a good stir. 5. Continue to cook till all the moisture evaporates from the mixture and it begins to look dry. You can even see jaggery strings in the mix. This took around 3 mins. But this depends on the kind of pan you use. Off the stove. Set aside to cool down slightly. 6. Grease your palms and roll to lemon sized balls. Badam Laddu (Almo... title: Coconut Ladoo with Condensed Milk
ingredients: 0.5 cup 2 tbsps condensed milk, 0.25 cup milk, 1.5 to 1 cups desiccated coconut, 1 pinch cardamom powder, 2 tablespoons nuts, 2 tablespoons desiccated coconut
instructions: 1. Add half tin (½ cup plus 2 tbsp or about 200 grams) condensed milk to a heavy bottom or non stick pan. 2. Pour ¼ cup milk and add desiccated coconut to suit your taste: If you have a sweet tooth you can use 1 ½ cups or around 130 grams. If you like moderately sweet then 1¾ cups works good. For mildly sweet ladoos, you can use 2 cups equal to 175 grams. It works with all the above mentioned quantities. This time I added about 2 cups to make them mildly sweet. With 2 cups they have a dominating taste of coconut and less of the condensed milk. So judge and use accordingly. 3. Mix it well. Begin to cook on a low flame stirring frequently. 4. If you are using 2 cups, the texture remains same. Cook until the mix turns warm. If you are using lesser than 2 cups, the mixture turns slightly gooey. Continue to cook until it becomes a thick mass, for 2 to 3 mins. Switch off the stove. The timings vary if you are using only 1½ cups coconut. 5. Add a large pinch of cardamom powder and 1½ to 2 tablespoon roasted sliced pistachios. 6. Cool down slightly. While the mixture is still hot (not burning hot), take small portions and roll to balls. 7. Add 2 tablespoons desiccated coconut to a plate and roll them. 8. You can also garnish them with more nuts. 9. Diwali Sweets & D... Gond Ke Laddu, Ho... Badam Laddu (Almo... Ragi laddu | Nac... Rava Laddu Recipe... Dry Fruit Laddu (...

title: Coconut Milk Pulao
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 2 cups coconut milk, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 onion, 1 green chilli, 0.25 cup carrot, 0.25 cup potato, 0.25 cup green beans, 0.25 cup green peas, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup mint leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1 cashewnuts, 1 bay leaf, 3 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 star anise, 0.75 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 strand mace, 1 black cardamom, 0.5 teaspoon crushed fennel seeds, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper
