instructions: 1. Rinse rice well thrice and soak for 15 mins. Drain to a colander and set aside. Do not over soak. 2. While the rice soaks, prepare the veggies and the spices. 3. Heat ghee in a pot or a pressure cooker. 4. Add all the whole spices. When they begin to sizzle add onions and saute them until deep golden but not burnt. 5. Stir in cashews, fennel seeds and black pepper if using. 6. Add ginger garlic and green chilies. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds until aromatic. 7. Add all the chopped veggies and mint. Fry for 1 to 2 mins until aromatic. 8. Pour coconut milk (mixed with water) and add salt. Stir and taste test to check the salt levels. Add more salt if required. The coconut milk should be slightly salty. 9. Bring the coconut milk to a rolling boil on a high heat. 10. Stir in the drained rice. 11. If cooking in a regular pot, reduce the heat to low or medium and cover. Cook until all of the coconut milk is absorbed & the rice is cooked fully yet not mushy. 12. To cook in a pressure cooker, cover and cook on a medium heat until you hear 1 whistle. Let the pressure drop and open the lid. 13. Fluff up the coconut milk pulao with a fork. Cover and rest for 5 mins before serving with a curry or raita. 14. If using an Instant pot, perform all the above steps on a saute mode. Secure the IP, position the steam release valve to sealing and press the pressure cooker. Set the timer to 5 mins. Let the pressure release for 3 to 4 mins. Release the rest manually. 15. Fluff up with a fork. If the top layer is too dry and looks under cooked, remove the steel insert from the IP. Mix gently with a fork and cover with a plate. Let rest for 5 mins.

title: Coconut Milk Pulao Recipe | Coconut Rice Pulao - Healthy & Tasty
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp pepper, 3 pods cardamom, 0.5 tsp cloves, 2 bay leaf, 1 tsp fennel, 1 inch cinnamon, 6 chilli, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 1 onion, 1 carrot, 2 tbsp peas, 2 tbsp mint, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup coconut milk, 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute spices till it turns aromatic. I have added, 1 tsp pepper, 3 pods cardamom, ½ tsp cloves, 2 bay leaf, tsp fennel, and 1 inch cinnamon. 2. Add 6 chilli, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste and saute well. 3. Now add 1 onion and saute till the onion softens slightly. 4. Also, add 2 tbsp mint, 1 tsp salt and saute till the mint shrinks slightly. 5. Further, pour in 2 cup of coconut milk and mix well. 6. Once the milk comes to a rolling boil add 1 cup basmati rice and mix gently. 7. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked well. 8. Fluff gently, and add 2 tbsp coriander. Mix gently. 9. Finally, enjoy Coconut Milk Pulao Recipe, which tastes great with raita and salan.

title: Coconut Milk Rice Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups Basmati rice, 1.5 cup coconut milk refer notes, 2 cups water 1 cups for IP, 2 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chili, 1 medium carrots, 1 cup green peas, 6 green beans, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1 bay leaf, 4 green cardamoms, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 cashew nuts, 4 tablespoons coconut flakes
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse it well thrice. Drain the water. 2. Pour fresh water and let it soak for 20 mins while you prepare the other ingredients. Later after 20 mins, drain the water completely. 3. Add oil or butter to a pot or pressure cooker. Fry cashews on a medium heat until golden. Remove to a small plate and set aside for garnish. 4. Reduce the heat. To the same pot, add whole spices and fry for 30 seconds, until they turn fragrant. 5. Add ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute without burning, until it smells fragrant. 6. Add slit green chili, chopped vegetables and saute for 2 to 3 mins. 7. Pour the coconut milk along with the water and add salt. Taste and adjust the salt. 8. Drain the water completely from the rice and add it to the pot. Mix well and cover with the lid. 9. Cooking in pot: Let the rice cook on a low heat until all of the water is absorbed and is fully tender but not mushy. If the rice is undercooked, sprinkle some warm water and cook until soft. Turn off the heat and let the coconut milk rice rest for 10 mins. Later fluff up with a fork. 10. Pressure Cooker: Cook on a medium heat until you hear 1 whistle. When the pressure drops, open the lid and gently fluff with a fork. 11. Optional: Toast the coconut until golden. Garnish coconut milk rice with toasted coconut and cashews. 12. Press Saute button on the instant pot and pour ghee to the steel insert. Add the whole spices. 13. When the spices begin to sizzle, add ginger garlic and stir well. Add green chilies, cashews and vegetables. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. 14. Add 2 cups drained rice, 1½ cups water, 1 cup coconut milk and salt. Give a good stir. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 15. Secure the instant pot with the lid and position the steam release handle to sealing. Press the pressure cook button (high) and Set the timer to 6 mins. 16. When the IP finishes, let the pressure release manually for 7 to 8 mins. Later do a manual pressure release. Gently fluff up the coconut milk rice with a fork. 17. If you want you may toast the coconut separately and garnish.

title: Coconut Oats Dinner
ingredients: 1.5 cup rolled oats, 1 cup coconut, 1.5 tbsp oil, 0.25 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 2 tbsp peanuts, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.75 tsp ginger, 2 green chilies, salt, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 3 cup coriander leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. Rinse rolled oats and drain them in a colander. 2. Gently fluff up immediately and set aside. 3. Heat a pan with oil. 4. Add mustard, cumin , urad dal, chana dal and peanuts. 5. Fry them until the peanuts are golden. 6. Then add in hing, ginger, green chilies and curry leaves. 7. Saute them quickly until the curry leaves turn crisp. 8. Then pour 2 tbsp water. This will soften the dals slightly. 9. Add turmeric and salt. 10. Then add in oats. Gently give a good stir. 11. On a very low heat cook covered until the oats turn slightly hot. This only takes about 1 to 2 mins. 12. Turn off the heat. Add coconut and coriander leaves. 13. Mix well and check salt. If needed add more and sprinkle lemon juice over the salt. 14. Mix well and serve coconut oats with pickle or yogurt.

title: coconut peda recipe | coconut malai milk peda | nariyal ka peda
ingredients: 1.5 cup coconut, 1 cup milk, 0.5 cup cream, 1 cup sugar, 1.5 cup milk powder, 0.25 cup strawberry jam
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1½ cup coconut and soak in 1 cup milk. 2. mix well making sure the coconut is well combined and absorbs all the milk. you can alternatively use fresh coconut and mix with just ½ cup milk. 3. transfer the coconut into large kadai. 4. add ½ cup cream and cook for a minute. 5. also, add 1 cup sugar and cook until the sugar melts. 6. further, add 1½ cup milk powder and continue to cook on medium flame. 7. add 1 tsp ghee and continue to cook. adding ghee gives a nice flavour to peda and also shine. 8. cook until the mixture starts to hold shape and separates from the pan. 9. cool the mixture slightly. 10. and start to prepare small ball sized peda. 11. roll the peda in desiccated coconut to get the beautiful coating. 12. make a small hole using the back of the spatula. 13. and garnish with a tsp of strawberry jam. you can alternatively use nuts to garnish. 14. finally, enjoy coconut peda recipe or store in the refrigerator for a week.

title: Coconut Rice Recipe (Thengai Sadam)
ingredients: 1 cup uncooked aged rice, 2 cups water, 1.25 cup coconut, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.5 green chili, 1 red chili, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 1 teaspoon ginger, 13.5 cashewnuts
instructions: 1. Wash rice at least thrice and drain the water. If using basmati rice, soak it for 20 to 30 mins. 2. To cook rice in pot, bring water to a rolling boil and add the rice. Cook covered on a low heat until fully done but not mushy. 3. To pressure cook basmati rice, pour water & pressure cook for 1 whistle. To pressure cook normal rice, pour more water & pressure cook for 2 whistles. The rice must be grainy and not mushy. When the pressure releases, open the lid. 4. Fluff up the cooked rice with a fork and cool completely. 5. Slit the green chilies, fine chop ginger, and grate the coconut. If using frozen coconut, break up the lumps and loosen the coconut. 6. Pour ghee or oil to a pan and heat it. 7. Fry cashews or peanuts until golden. Remove to a plate and set aside. 8. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, red chilli, chana dal and urad dal. Saute them and fry until the dals turn golden and aromatic. 9. Then add ginger, slit green chilli & curry leaves. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds until the curry leaves turn crisp. Add hing. 10. Optional - Quickly pour 3 tablespoons water to the pan. This will soften the dals and bring out the flavors of the ginger and curry leaves. If you want your dal to be very soft, then you can add little more water. Cook until all of the water evaporates. 11. Then add fresh or frozen coconut and sprinkle salt on it. Then add rice. Off the heat. If using frozen coconut, then saute the coconut a bit until it becomes hot. Then add the rice. 12. Mix well and taste test. Add more salt if needed. Serve coconut rice with a simple curry or plain yogurt.

title: coconut rice recipe | nariyal chawal | south indian coconut rice
ingredients: 3 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard rai, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, few curry leaves, 1 red chilli, 2 green chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 cashew kaju, 0.75 cup coconut, 2 cup cooked rice, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 3 tsp oil and saute 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, few curry leaves, 1 dried red chilli. 2. also add 2 green chilli, 1-inch ginger. saute slightly. 3. now add 10 cashews and roast till the cashews turn golden brown. 4. add in ¾ cup grated coconut and saute for a minute or till raw smell goes away. 5. further, add 2 cups cooked rice and ½ tsp salt. mix gently without breaking the rice. 6. now cover and simmer for 2 minutes, or till rice absorbs coconut flavour. 7. finally, serve south <g class="gr_ gr_64 gr-alert gr_spell gr_inline_cards gr_run_anim ContextualSpelling ins-del multiReplace" id="64" data-gr-id="64">indian</g> style coconut rice with kuzhambu or any curry.

title: Cold Coffee Recipe (Cafe Style)
ingredients: 1.5 to 2 tablespoons instant coffee, 4 tablespoons sugar, 1.5 cup full fat milk, 5 ice cubes, 4 tablespoons luke warm water, 3 scoops vanilla ice cream, 2 tablespoons chocolate syrup, Blender with a jar capacity of over 500 ml
instructions: 1. If you want to garnish your serving glasses with chocolate, melt chocolate chips or use chocolate syrup. Pour 1 tablespoon chocolate across the inner side of the serving glass. Keep them in the fridge for 5 mins. 2. Add 1½ to 2 tablespoon instant coffee granules or powder. You can even use ¼ cup strong cold brew or filter coffee decoction. 3. Add 4 tablespoons fine sugar or powdered sugar. Adjust to taste. 4. Add 4 tablespoons warm water to the blender jar. Skip water if using cold brew or filter coffee. 5. Run the blender until the coffee becomes frothy meaning sugar and coffee has dissolved completely. 6. It turns frothy as shown in the picture. 7. Add 1½ cup chilled milk. 8. Add 4 to 6 ice cubes & 2 scoops vanilla ice cream (optional). I do not like diluted cold coffee so I prefer to use milk cubes instead of ice cubes. This will give you a thicker drink and richer like the one with ice cream. 9. Blend until frothy. Do not over do else you will end up with butter on top. Then pour it to the garnished glass. If you want you can top with ice cream and drizzle some chocolate syrup. Serve cold coffee immediately.

title: cooked rice dosa recipe | leftover rice dosa | dosa with leftover rice
ingredients: 3 cup dosa rice, 0.25 cup urad dal, 0.5 tsp methi, water, 1.5 cup cooked rice, 1 tsp salt, 3 tsp oil, 1 tbsp chana dal, 4 red chilli, 3 shallot, 1 clove garlic, 1 tomato, piece tamarind, 0.5 tsp salt, water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 cup dosa rice, ¼ cup urad dal and ½ tsp methi. 2. rinse with water and soak for 4 hours. 3. drain off the water and grind to a smooth paste. 4. transfer the batter to a large vessel. 5. now take 1½ cup cooked rice and blend to smooth paste adding water as required. 6. transfer the rice paste to the same vessel. 7. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 8. allow fermenting for 8 hours or until the batter doubles. 9. mix the batter slightly, and then add 1 tsp salt. 10. mix well making sure the batter is well combined. 11. now pour the batter on hot dosa tawa and spread slightly. 12. cook on medium flame until the dosa is cooked well. you can also cover and cook to hasten the cooking process. 13. finally, enjoy cooked rice dosa with spicy tomato chutney. 14. firstly, in a pan heat 3 tsp oil, add 1 tbsp chana dal and 4 dried red chilli. 15. roast on low flame until the dal turns crunchy. 16. add 3 shallot, 1 clove garlic and saute until the onions shrink slightly. 17. further, add 1 tomato and saute until the tomato softens slightly. 18. cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 19. add a small piece of tamarind and ½ tsp salt. 20. grind to smooth paste adding water as required. 21. now pour the tempering over the chutney and mix well. 22. finally, enjoy spicy tomato onion chutney with idli and dosa.

title: Cooker Cake, How to Make Cake in Pressure Cooker
ingredients: 1.5 cups all-purpose flour, 1.25 teaspoons baking powder, 0.5 teaspoon baking soda, 1 pinch salt, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.5 cup oil, 1 cup milk, 1.25 tablespoon vinegar, 2 teaspoons vanilla extract, 1 grams chocolate
instructions: 1. Grease a 7 inch cake tray. Dust with flour evenly, shake it and invert it in the kitchen sink to remove excess flour. Or simply line a parchment paper. Set this aside. 2. Add salt to a pressure cooker or any heavy bottom large pot/ kadai and spread it evenly. 3. Cover the salt with a plate. Place a ring/trivet or hot pot stand over the plate. 4. Remove the gasket and weight, if using a cooker. Cover the cooker or pot with a lid and begin to heat it on a medium heat. 5. Pour milk, sugar, vinegar, melted butter (or oil) and vanilla to a mixing bowl. Stir well until sugar dissolves. 6. Place a sieve over the bowl. Add flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt to the sieve. Lightly mix them up with a spoon. Sieve it. (check video for help) 7. Gently mix the wet and dry with a spatula or a whisk, until the flour is just combined well. Do not over mix the batter. 8. Pour this to the cake pan. Knock it a few times over the counter. Brush milk on top to get a golden top. This is optional. 9. Hold the cake tray with a tong and place inside the cooker/ pot, on top of the trivet/ring or stand. The cooker has to be hot inside at this stage. 10. Cover the cooker with the lid. Pls note the gasket & weight have to be removed. 11. Bake for 50 to 60 mins on a low flame. When the cake is completely baked, a tester inserted comes out clean. 12. The cake top will be fairly dry when you touch it. If it is still moist, bake for few more mins. 13. Cool the cake tray for 10 to 15 mins over a wire rack, so it won't sweat at the bottom. Later invert it on to a wire rack. 14. Cool completely before frosting or slicing. 15. Preheat the oven to 180 C or 350 F for at least 15 mins. 16. Bake it for 30 to 35 mins. Tester inserted comes out clean. Adjust the time to suit the size of your pan and oven. 17. Pour water to a pot and heat it. The water level has to be less than half. 18. Place another bowl or pan over the pot. The bottom of the bowl or pan must touch the hot water but not the pan inside. 19. Also make sure water doesn’t seep into the top bowl or pan. 20. Add 100 grams of chocolate or chips. Keep stirring until the chocolate melts and turns smooth. 21. Make sure the cake and the chocolate both are cool and not hot. 22. Pour the chocolate over the cooled cake and spread it evenly. Top with some sprinkles if you have. Slice the cake and enjoy.

title: cooker pav bhaji recipe | pav bhaji in cooker | pressure cooker pav bhaji
ingredients: 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp pav bhaji masala, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tomato, 1 carrot, 1 florets cauliflower, 3 tbsp peas, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 beetroot, 2 potato, 1.5 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 1 tbsp butter, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp pav bhaji masala, 0.5 tsp butter, 0.25 tsp pav bhaji masala, 1 tbsp coriander, pav
instructions: 1. firstly, in a cooker heat 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp oil and saute 1 tsp cumin. 2. add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute well. 3. keeping the flame on low add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp pav bhaji masala and 2 tbsp coriander. 4. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 5. now add 2 tomato and saute making sure to turn tomato soft and mushy. 6. mash the tomato really well and cook until the oil separates. 7. further add 1 carrot, 10 florets cauliflower, 3 tbsp peas, ½ capsicum, ½ beetroot and 2 potato. 8. saute for a minute until it turns aromatic. 9. pour in 1½ cup water and 1 tsp salt. mix well. 10. cover and pressure cook for 7 whistles. you can also prepare in an instant pot and pressure cook for at least 15 minutes. 11. once the pressure releases the vegetables are cooked well. do not worry if there is water. 12. mash the vegetables really well. mash for at least 5 minutes to get a smooth texture. 13. get the bhaji to a rolling boil and adjust the consistency. 14. to prepare the special tadka, in a small pan heat 2 tsp oil, 1 tbsp butter, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander and ¼ tsp pav bhaji masala. 15. once the spices turn aromatic, pour it over bhaji. 16. mix well and bhaji is ready to be served. 17. to prepare the pav, in a pan heat ½ tsp butter, ¼ tsp pav bhaji masala and 1 tbsp coriander. 18. saute for a couple of seconds and spread uniformly over the pan. 19. now take 2 pav and slit in the centre, toast on both sides. 20. finally, enjoy mumbai street style pav bhaji with a dollop of butter.

title: Coriander Rice Recipe | Cilantro Rice | Coriander Pulao
ingredients: 0.5 bunch coriander, few mint, 5 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 7 cloves garlic, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 black cardamom, 5 pods cardamom, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 0.5 tsp pepper, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 1 potato, 1 carrot, 5 beans, 4 tbsp peas, 3 cup water, 2 tsp salt, 1.5 cup basmari rice, 2 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Firstly, to prepare the masala paste. In a mixer jar take ½ bunch coriander, few mint, 5 chilli, 1 inch ginger and 7 cloves garlic. 2. Also add 1 inch cinnamon, 1 black cardamom, 5 pods cardamom, 6 cloves and ½ tsp pepper. 3. Add water as required and grind to a smooth paste. Keep aside. 4. In a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Add 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion and saute well. 5. Also add ½ capsicum, 1 potato, 1 carrot, 5 beans, and 4 tbsp peas. 6. Stir fry till the vegetables turn crunchy. 7. Further, add prepared masala paste and cook well. 8. Now add 3 cup water, 2 tsp salt and mix well. 9. Once the water comes to a boil, add 1½ cup basmati rice, 2 tsp lemon juice and mix well. 10. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes or until the rice is cooked well. 11. Fluff the rice gently without breaking the rice grains. 12. Finally, enjoy Coriander Rice Recipe / Cilantro Rice with raita.

title: Corn Ambode (Corn Vada)
ingredients: 0.5 cup chana dal, 1.5 cups corn kernels, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 2 tbsp pudina, 2 tbsp coriander leaves chopped, 1 green chilli, 1 sprig curry leaves, salt, 1 onion, 2.5 tbsps besan, oil, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 red chili, 0.75 tsp cumin, 0.75 tsp saunf, 13.5 red chilies, 3.5 garlic cloves, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp lemon juice, salt as needed, water as needed, 0.5 tsp roasted cumin
instructions: 1. Wash and soak chana dal for just 1½ to 2 hours. Drain the water and set aside. 2. Add red chilies, jeera, cinnamon stick and saunf. The flavor of saunf will not be strong. If you don’t like it just skip. If you have kids home, then deseed the chilies and use. I used byadgi chilli. 3. Make a slightly coarse powder. 4. Set aside half of the chana dal. Add corn and the rest of the dal to the blender. I used frozen corn so I rinsed them well and used. Don’t forget to drain them completely if rinsing. 5. Blend to a slightly coarse mixture. 6. Add the ground mixture to the chana dal. 7. Add salt, onions, chilies, ginger paste, curry leaves, coriander leaves and pudina. If you have kids home, skip chilies or set aside a portion of these. Mix everything well. 8. If the mixture is too moist or soggy, then add 2 to 3 tbsps of besan or rice flour. I didn’t drain the corn properly after rinsing so the mixture was very moist. You may not need any flour depending on how your mixture turns out. 9. The mixture has to be come together and bind well when you make a ball. 10. You can use a clean dry cloth or foil or grease proof paper for making vadas. Divide the dough to 10 balls. 11. Flatten them gently. 12. Heat the oil on a medium flame. Check if the oil is hot by dropping a small piece of the mixture. The dough has to rise up to the surface and not brown too quickly. 13. Remove the vada to your fingers. 14. Gently shake off your fingers over the hot oil. Regulate the flame to medium. 15. Do not disturb for 1 to 2 minutes. Slowly they will firm up. Flip them and fry on the other side. 16. Fry them until golden. 17. Remove the corn ambode /vada to a kitchen tissue. Serve corn vada with a spicy chutney. To make the red chilli garlic chutney, soak deseeded 12 to 15 red chilies in water for 30 mins. Blend together soaked chilies, 3 to 4 garlic cloves, salt, ½ tsp sugar and ½ tsp lemon juice. Add little water to make a smooth chutney. You can replace sugar with jaggery.

title: Corn Chaat Recipe (Indian Corn Snack)
ingredients: 1.5 to 2 cup sweet corn kernels, 2 tablespoons onions, 3.5 tablespoons tomato, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.625 teaspoon chaat masala powder, 0.25 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.375 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon oil, 4 tablespoons sev
instructions: 1. If using corn on the cob, pick the corn kernals and add them to a bowl. Rinse and drain the water. 2. Boil them with little water until tender & drain completely. or Saute them in a tsp of butter or oil until tender. 3. Cool down. Add red chilli powder (optional), cumin powder, chaat masala and salt. 4. Stir in the onions, tomatoes, green chilies, lemon juice and coriander leaves. 5. Transfer corn chaat to serving bowls and top with peanuts, sev or farsan. I did not have any so I fried paneer in little ghee until crisp and added.

title: corn cheese balls recipe | how to make sweet corn & veg cheese balls
ingredients: 1 cup sweet corn, 3 potato aloo, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.25 cup mozzarella cheese, 2 tbsp bread crumbs, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp corn flour, 2 tbsp maida plain flour, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup panko breadcrumbs, oil, 7 mozzarella cheese
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup sweet corn and 3 potato. 2. also add ½ onion, ½ capsicum, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp coriander. 3. now add ½ tsp chilli flakes, ½ tsp pepper powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala. 4. further add ¼ cup mozzarella cheese, 2 tbsp bread crumbs and ½ tsp salt. 5. mix well, make sure to mash and combine well forming a dough. 6. now prepare corn flour slurry by taking 2 tbsp corn flour, 2 tbsp maida, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp salt. 7. add ½ cup water and mix well making sure there are no lumps. 8. pinch a ball sized mixture and flatten slightly. 9. place a small mozzarella cheese ball in centre and secure well. 10. roll to form a smooth ball. 11. dip in corn flour slurry and roll in panko bread crumbs. 12. freeze for 20 minutes to prevent from cheese melting while deep frying. 13. deep fry in hot oil stirring occasionally. 14. fry on medium flame, until the cheese balls turn golden brown and crisp. 15. finally, enjoy corn cheese balls with chilli sauce or tomato sauce.

title: Corn cutlet - Corn patties
ingredients: 2 medium potatoes, 0.75 cup sweet corn kernels, 1 green chilli, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon ginger paste, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 cup bread crumbs, 2 tablespoons oil
instructions: 1. To make corn patties firstly boil whole potatoes just until fork tender without making them mushy. You can boil them in a pot covering them with enough water or in the instant pot for 6 mins. Alternately you may also peel and cube them and boil in a pan. 2. If using corn on the cob, boil them until tender and remove the kernels. If using frozen corn, prepare them by defrosting and cooking in a pan until tender. 3. Mash the boiled potatoes well in a bowl. Add sweet corn, red chilli powder, garam masala, chaat masala (optional), green chilies, ginger, salt, bread crumbs or coriander leaves. 4. Mix all of the ingredients and taste test. If required add more salt and spices. 5. Mix well to bind the mixture to a dough. If the dough becomes dry, knead with moist fingers. Troubleshooting tip: You can also blend half of the mixture in a blender and mix with the rest. 6. Divide the dough to 8 balls and flatten to corn patties. 7. Heat a pan with 1 tbsp oil. When the oil becomes hot, place the corn cutlets and toast them. When one side is golden and crisp, turn them to the other side with a spatula and toast until golden and crisp. 8. Pan fry all of the corn patties in batches using 1 tbsp oil per batch. 9. Serve corn cutlet hot or warm with chutney or sauce.

title: Corn Fried Rice Recipe (Corn Rice)
ingredients: 3 cups cooked rice, 1 cup corn kernels, 2 tablespoon cooking oil, 2 garlic cloves, 1 medium onion, 1.5 green chili, 0.75 cup bell peppers, 2 tablespoons celery, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.5 teaspoons soya sauce, 1.5 teaspoons rice vinegar
instructions: 1. Rinse bell peppers, green chilli and onions under running water. Chop the bell peppers, slice the green chilli and onions. You will need about 2. 2 garlic cloves fine chopped 3. ¾ cup chopped bell peppers/ capsicum 4. If using corn on the cob, boil the corncob until the kernels are tender. Then with a knife remove the kernels. I prefer to use frozen corn for convenience. You also need cooled grainy cooked rice. 5. Heat a wok or pan. Add 2 tablespoons oil or butter. Once hot, fry garlic until it begins to smell good. 6. Add sliced onions and chili. You can also use spring onions whites. If making for kids skip chilli or deseed and use . 7. Stir fry until the onions turn lightly golden but not too soft and soggy. 8. Increase the heat/ flame on your stove to the highest. Add corn and capsicum. Stir fry until the bell peppers become slightly tender, yet crunchy and not too soft. Your corn kernels should also be tender by then. 9. Add fresh herbs of your choice. 10. Add cooled rice, ¼ teaspoon Crushed black pepper and ½ teaspoon salt. You can also drizzle some toasted sesame oil or olive oil like the way I did. This is optional and not included in the recipe below. Optionally pour 1 to 2 teaspoons of rice vinegar or apple cider vinegar and 1 to 2 teaspoons of soya sauce or tamari, across the pan. 11. Mix and Stir fry the rice for 2 minutes. Taste test and add more salt, black pepper, soya sauce or vinegar as per your taste. Serve sweet corn fried rice hot.

title: corn fried rice recipe | sweet corn fried rice | chinese corn fried rice
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 3 clove garlic, 0.25 onion, 4 tbsp spring onions, 2 tbsp green capsicum, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp red capsicum, 0.25 cup sweet corn, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 cups basmati rice, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp pepper
instructions: 1. firstly, heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 3 clove garlic. 2. further saute ¼ onion and 2 tbsp spring onions. 3. also saute 2 tbsp green capsicum, ½ carrot, 2 tbsp red capsicum and ¼ cup sweet corn on high flame. 4. now add 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tsp chilli sauce. saute on high flame for 30 seconds. 5. add in 2 cups of cooked basmati rice along with ½ tsp salt and ½ tsp crushed pepper. 6. mix gently, without breaking rice grains. 7. finally, serve corn fried rice with gobi manchurian gravy or enjoy as it is.

title: Corn Pakoda (Indian Corn Pakoras)
ingredients: 1.5 cups Sweet corn, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 tablespoons besan, 2.5 tablespoons rice flour, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 onion, 2 green chilies, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, Oil
instructions: 1. If using corn on the cob, boil them until tender. Using a knife scrape them off from the cob. I use frozen corn, So I rinse them and drain well. You will need 1½ cups kernels. 2. Add them to a blender jar and pulse coarsely. 2 to 3 times is good enough. Do not over do this. Make sure all the corn are crushed else they may burst while frying. 3. Next add the mixture to a bowl along with 1 small sliced onion (½ to ¾ cup), 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste (or chopped), 1 to 2 green chilies chopped, ½ teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 to 2 sprigs chopped curry leaves or mint leaves or coriander leaves. 4. Add 2 to 3 tablespoons besan, ½ teaspoon salt and ½ teaspoon garam masala. You can use this garam masala powder . 5. Add 2 to 3 rice flour (or corn starch), ⅛ teaspoon turmeric and ½ teaspoon red chili powder. 6. Mix well. The mixture must be like a dough and not batter. If it is soggy, then you can add more besan and rice flour in equal amounts. 7. Heat oil in a kadai. When it is hot enough, test by dropping a small portion. It should sizzle and rise up without turning brown. This is the right temperature. Take a handful of this and drop small portions (about 1 to 1½ tbsp) to the hot oil. Do not disturb for a few minutes. Fry until golden on a medium heat, stirring often. Lastly remove them to a cooling rack or to a steel colander. Serve sweet Corn Pakoda hot with tea or chutney.

title: corn pakoda recipe | sweet corn pakora | corn bhajiya recipe
ingredients: 2 cup sweet corn kernels, 0.5 onion, 0.5 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp chaat masala, pinch of hing asafoetida, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp salt, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cup boiled sweet corn kernels and ½ thinly sliced onion. 2. squeeze and mix well making sure corn is mashed well. alternatively, pulse in a mixi. 3. now add ½ cup besan, 2 tbsp rice flour, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, pinch of hing, few curry leaves and ¼ tsp salt. 4. combine and mix well without adding any water. 5. add more besan if required and form a dough. 6. now drop the corn pakoda mixture, making rough balls into hot oil. 7. stir occasionally, and fry on medium flame. 8. fry till the pakora turns crispy and golden. takes approximately 10-15 minutes. 9. finally, serve crispy corn pakoda with chutney or tomato sauce.

title: Corn Pulao Recipe | Sweet Corn Pulao Rice
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 2 bay leaf, 1 tsp cumin, 1 black cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cardamom, 1 javetri, 7 cloves 7 cloves, 5 cloves garlic, 4 chilli, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger paste, 0.5 capscium, 1.5 cup sweet corn, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 2 bay leaf, 1 tsp cumin, 1 black cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cardamom, 1 javetri, 7 cloves, and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 2. Add 5 cloves garlic, 4 chilli and saute slightly. 3. Also add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger paste and saute until the onion shrinks slightly. 4. Further add ½ capsicum, 1½ cup sweet corn, ½ tsp garam masala, and 1 tsp salt. 5. Saute for a minute or until the corn turns aromatic. 6. Pour in 2 cup water and get to a boil. 7. Once the water comes to a boil, add 1 cup basmati rice, and 2 tsp lemon juice and mix well. 8. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked well. 9. Finally, garnish with 2 tbsp coriander, and Corn Pulao Recipe is ready to enjoy.

title: Corn Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 6 slices bread, 2.5 tablespoons Butter, 0.5 cup cheese, 0.5 cup sweet corn kernels, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 0.625 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 cup green chutney, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 carrot, 2 tablespoons onions, 0.25 cup tomatoes, 1.5 green chilies
instructions: 1. Prepare the corn kernels. If using corn on the cob, boil, grill or bake until tender. I use the frozen corn so I cook for 1 to 2 mins in a pan until cooked completely. Turn off and cool. 2. To the cooled corn, add grated carrots, onions, tomatoes & green chilies. All these are optional and you may skip. 3. Add black pepper, chaat masala and cumin powder. Mix well. Taste test and adjust the spices if you want more. Avoid adding salt. 4. Butter your bread on the inner side and spread chutney or mayo or any spread of your choice. 5. Place the cheese slice over the bread. [If using grated cheese, simply mix that with the veggie corn kernels we prepared earlier.] 6. Spread the corn filling on the prepared bread slice. Smear some chutney or spread on the other bread slice too. Cover the sandwich with the chutney side in. Spread little butter on the outside too. 7. Heat a toaster or a pan and drizzle little softened butter. Place the corn sandwich and toast it till golden. 8. If making on a pan, press down the sandwich with a spatula, toast on a low heat until the bread becomes crisp on one side. Carefully turn it to the other side and toast until the cheese begins to melt. 9. Transfer the corn sandwich to a wooden board and cut before serving.

title: Corn Vada Recipe | Sweet Corn Garelu - Street Style
ingredients: 1.5 cup sweet corn, 0.5 cup besan, 0.5 cup rice flour, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar, add 1½ cups of sweet corn and pulse until it forms a coarse paste. 2. Transfer the corn paste to the large plate. 3. Add ½ cup besan, ½ cup rice flour, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp cumin powder and 1 tsp salt. 4. Mix well, making sure all the spices are well combined. 5. Further add ½ onion, ½ carrot, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli. Mix well. 6. Now pinch a small ball-sized mixture and flatten slightly with your palm, making sure to keep the edges thin compared to the centre. 7. Drop the vada into hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 8. Do not touch the vada for 2 minutes, or else there is a chance that the vada will break. 9. Stir occasionally, and fry on medium flame, until the vada turns golden and crunchy. 10. Drain off and keep aside. Make sure to fry on medium flame, or else the vada will turn crispy on the outside and remain soft on the inside. 11. Finally, Corn Vada Recipe tastes great when served with green chutney.

title: Cream of Mushroom Soup Recipe - Easy Homemade
ingredients: 15 mushroom, 2 tbsp butter, 3 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 onion, 1.5 tbsp maida, 1 tsp salt, 4 cup water, 0.5 tsp thyme, 1 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 cup cream, 2 tsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a wok, heat 2 tbsp butter. Saute 3 cloves garlic and 1 inch ginger. 2. Also, sauté ½ onion until it softens. 3. Now add 15 sliced mushroom and saute well. 4. Cook till the mushroom softens and water is released. 5. Now add 1½ tbsp maida, 1 tsp salt and cook till it turns aromatic. Maida helps to thicken the soup. 6. Pour in 4 cup water and mix well. 7. Collect few mushroom and grind to a smooth paste. Mushroom paste helps to make mushroom soup creamy and flavourful. 8. Add in mushroom paste and cook well. 9. Simmer for atleast 5 minutes, or until the flavours are absorbed well. 10. Now add ½ tsp thyme, 1 tsp pepper powder and ¼ cup cream. Mix and get to a boil. 11. Also add 2 tsp coriander and mix well. 12. Finally, enjoy the Cream of Mushroom Soup Recipe with bread.

title: Crispy Corn Recipe (Crunchy Corn Snack)
ingredients: 3 cups corn kernels, 6 tbsp corn flour, 5 tbsp rice flour, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.375 tsp salt, 4 cups water, 0.75 tsp jeera powder, 0.875 tsp chaat masala, 0.625 tsp red chili powder, 2.5 tbsp onions, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Choose corn that have firm and plump kernels. Avoid too young, tender or juicy ones. Also do not use hard kernels. 2. Cut the corn cob to 2 parts. Begin to pick the whole kernels from the cob without cutting them with a knife. Add them to a bowl. Do not cut them with a knife as the cut kernels tend to splutter while frying. 3. Next add them to salted hot water and boil for 2 minutes. You can skip this step if the kernels are moderately milky and not hard. 4. Drain them and rinse under running water. When excess water drains up, add them to a wide bowl. 5. While you prepare the corn also prepare to deep fry them or preheat your oven to 400 F or 200 C for at least 15 mins. 6. Corn have to be moist at this stage. Then sprinkle salt, pepper, 3 tbsps corn flour and 3 tbsps rice flour. Toss them to coat with the flour. If using egg, pour 1 egg white and mix well. Then sprinkle flour. Toss well. 7. Next repeat adding the rest of the flour as needed. Toss them again. If needed sprinkle 1 tbsp water. Toss. Avoid water if using egg. 8. If you like more crispy flour coated on the corn then repeat coating them once more, sprinkling 1 tbsp more water if needed. The corn kernels must look floury at the same time they should not be dry. I did not do it the third time. 9. Spread the prepared corn on a parchment paper or greased sheet. Grill them for 24 to 30 mins until they turn golden and crisp. 10. Pour oil to a hot deep pan for frying. Heat it on a medium flame. 11. When the oil turns hot, drop one corn kernel and check. The oil must be quite hot at this stage otherwise the flour may disperse in the oil. 12. I added about 2 to 3 ladles of corn for each batch and quickly covered with the splatter screen. 13. Regulate the flame to medium and do not disturb until you see them floating on top. If they turn lumpy gently separate them. 14. When they turn crunchy remove to a colander. A plate or kitchen tissue tends to make them soft quickly. 15. Before adding the next batch of corn to oil, ensure the oil is hot enough again. 16. Add all the fried crispy corn to a bowl. Sprinkle red chili powder, cumin powder and chaat masala. 17. Immediately remove to serving plates. Sprinkle some onions and chopped coriander leaves.. 18. Serve crispy corn immediately. If desired just squeeze in few drops of lemon juice.

title: crispy veg starter recipe | veg crispy restaurant style | veg crispy starter
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 2 chilli, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 onion, 1 carrot, 5 beans, 3 tbsp sweet corn, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp aamchur, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 potato, 2 tbsp paneer, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 cup bread crumbs, 1 cup maida, 0.5 cup corn flour, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 cup bread crumbs, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil, 2 chilli and ½ tsp chilli flakes. saute slightly. 2. now add ½ onion and saute until the onions sweat. 3. further add 1 carrot, 5 beans, 3 tbsp sweet corn and ½ capsicum. 4. saute for a minute without overcooking the vegetables. 5. keeping the flame on low add ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp aamchur, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp salt. 6. saute for a minute until the spices turn aromatic. 7. cool the vegetables and transfer them to a large bowl. 8. add 3 potato, 2 tbsp paneer, 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 9. now add ¼ cup bread crumbs and mix forming a soft dough. bread crumbs help to absorb the excess moisture present in vegetables. 10. grease hands with oil and pinch a ball sized mixture. 11. press and shape to an ice cream stick like of kebab. you can shape to your choice. 12. to prepare the slurry, in a large bowl take 1 cup maida, ½ cup corn flour, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ¼ tsp salt. 13. mix well making sure there are no lumps. 14. now dip the stick into the slurry and coat over bread crumbs. 15. deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 16. flip over and fry uniformly, until the sticks turn golden brown and crispy. 17. drain off over kitchen towel to remove excess oil. 18. finally, enjoy crispy veg starter with tomato sauce.

title: crushed peanut chikki recipe | crush chikki | crushed shengdana chikki
ingredients: 2 cup peanuts, 2 cup jaggery, 1 tsp ghee, 2 tbsp water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a thick bottomed pan dry roast 2 cups peanut on low flame. alternatively, keep in the microwave till the peanuts are roasted well. 2. keep roasting on low flame until the peanuts turn crunchy. 3. once the peanuts start to separate its skin, turn off the flame and cool completely. 4. peel off the skin and transfer to the mixi. 5. pulse and blend to form a coarse peanut powder. make sure not to powder it too finely. keep aside. 6. in a large kadai, take 2 cup of jaggery. use dark coloured jaggery as the colour of chikki depends on jaggery. alternatively, use sugar, caramelise the sugar without adding any water. 7. add in 1 tsp ghee, 2 tbsp water and stir till the jaggery melts keeping flame on low. 8. stir well till the jaggery melts. 9. boil the jaggery syrup on low flame for 5 minutes. 10. boil till the syrup turns glossy and thickens. 11. check the consistency, by dropping syrup into a bowl of water, it should form a hard ball and cut with a snap sound. else boil for another minute and check. 12. turn the flame to low and add crushed peanuts. 13. stir well making sure jaggery syrup is well combined. 14. transfer the mixture over butter paper. you can alternatively use a chopping board greased with ghee. 15. cover with another butter paper and roll to slightly thick thickness. 16. allow to cool for a minute, and when it is still warm cut it into pieces. 17. finally, serve crushed peanut chikki once cooled completely, or store in an airtight container and enjoy up to a month.

title: Cucumber curry | Andhra style dosakaya curry
ingredients: 2.5 cups cucumber, 0.75 cup onions, 0.3333333333333333 cup tomatoes, 1 green chili, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.125 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon chana dal, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric
instructions: 1. Rinse the cucumber, onions, tomatoes and chilies. 2. Peel the cucumber and remove the seeds. Chop them to ½ inch pieces and set aside. 3. Fine chop onions, tomatoes and slit the green chilli. 4. Heat oil a pan or pot. 5. When the oil turns hot add mustard, cumin, urad dal and chana dal. 6. Fry until the dals turn golden, then add curry leaves and fry for a minute. 7. Next add in onions & green chili. Fry stirring often until transparent and they lose the raw flavor. 8. Add in tomatoes, salt and turmeric. 9. Fry until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 10. Add red chilli powder and fry just until the raw smell goes away. 11. Next stir in the cucumber pieces and fry for 2 mins. 12. Cover and cook on a very low flame until cucumbers turn tender. 13. Stir in between. I do not add water as the cucumber pieces will let out lot of moisture. 14. But if they do not let out moisture pour very little water. 15. Garnish with coriander leaves. 16. Serve cucumber curry with plain rice and ghee or roti.

title: cucumber idli recipe | taushe idli | southe kaayi sihi kadubu
ingredients: 1 cucumber, 1 cup rice, 0.5 cup jaggery, 0.5 cup coconut, 0.25 tsp salt, ghee clarified butter
instructions: 1. firstly, take a large cucumber and grate full. 2. squeeze off the juice. keep cucumber and juice aside. 3. in a large bowl soak 1 cup rice for 3 hours or overnight. 4. drain off the water and transfer to a mixi. 5. add ½ cup jaggery and ½ cup coconut. 6. also, add cucumber juice and blend to a coarse paste. 7. make sure to add cucumber juice in batches, else the batter may turn watery. 8. add grated cucumber and ¼ tsp salt to the batter. 9. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 10. add cucumber juice as required to adjust the consistency of the batter. 11. now take a large piece of banana leaf and grease with ghee. 12. pour 2 ladleful of batter and fold the banana leaf. 13. make sure the batter is well sealed without overflowing. 14. now steam the idli for 25 minutes or until it is cooked completely. 15. finally, enjoy cucumber idli with ghee or with honey.

title: Cucumber Juice Recipe
ingredients: 1 cucumber, 5 ice cubes, 1 mint leaves, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 pinch salt, 0.25 inch ginger, 0.125 teaspoon black pepper, 0.375 teaspoon chaat masala, 3.5 pieces celery stalks
instructions: 1. To prepare cucumbers before cooking or juicing them, you’ll want to rinse them under cold water and give them a gentle scrub with a vegetable or fruit brush. 2. While cucumbers are not current members of the Dirty Dozen 2022 list, they have been on the pesticide watch list in the past. Cleaning and scrubbing them will help get rid of leftover bacteria, pesticides, and dirt. 3. You don’t even have to peel them. English or Japanese cucumber skins are thin, tender, and contain many nutrients! Just chop off the ends of the cucumber before you work with it. 4. Rub the ends with the other part of cucumber for a minute or 2. You will be able to bring out some foam out of the cucumbers. This step gets rid of the bitter taste from the cucumbers. After this give a quick rinse under the running water. 5. For the cucumber juice, chop up the whole cucumber (skins included) and add it to a blender, food processor, or juicer. 6. Add the mint, lemon juice, and Himalayan salt or black salt. If you want to add fresh ginger or black pepper, include them in this step. Add ice cubes or coconut water cubes. 7. Blend or process everything together until smooth. 8. Then strain the mixture into a bowl through a fine mesh sieve (optionally lined with a cheesecloth.) Alternatively, you can use a jelly bag. Gently press on the pulp with the back of a spoon to squeeze out any leftover juice. 9. Test the cucumber juice and season to taste with more lemon juice or salt if desired. If you want you may add some chaat masala. 10. Pour over ice and enjoy! (Don’t be afraid to garnish with some extra mint or a lemon wedge.) Too much ice is going to dilute the flavors so follow the recipe card. 11. If you use a slow juicer, add the whole cucumbers with skin , ginger and mint into the chute. Stir in lemon juice or calamansi and serve.

title: Cucumber Raita Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup cucumber, 1.5 cup yogurt, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon roasted ground cumin, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 4.5 mint leaves, 1 green chili pepper
instructions: 1. Add thick yogurt to a mixing bowl. Very runny yogurt with lot of whey is not suitable to make this cucumber raita. Chopped cucumbers will let out moisture and make the raita more runny. If you do not have thick yogurt then you may strain the whey following this hung curd recipe . 2. Whisk it well with a fork or a hand whisk until smooth. We want a smooth yogurt here. Add salt and black pepper. You may also add cumin powder and chaat masala at this stage. But I prefer to keep it for the garnish. 3. Mix well and taste test at this stage. If your yogurt is too sour, you can balance it with very little sugar at this step but otherwise it is not required. We don’t want to sweeten the cucumber raita. We just want to balance the taste. 4. Rinse cucumbers well and peel them if you don’t want to keep the skin. 5. You can chop to bite sized pieces or grate them to your liking. Grated cucumber is going to make the raita runny, so grate only if you are using greek yogurt. 6. Also deseed and chop the green chilies. You may skip them if you don’t prefer heat in your raita. The other option is to just add the whole slit green chili so the raita won’t become hot but will add some flavor. Also fine chop coriander leaves and mint if using. 7. Add the cucumbers, herbs and chilli. Mix well. I also add a teaspoon of ginger juice at this stage. Ginger aids digestion so we prefer it. 8. Mix all of them and then sprinkle roasted cumin powder and coriander leaves. If you want you can also add chaat masala and black pepper. Serve cucumber raita with Chicken biryani Mutton biryani Veg biryani

title: cucumber sasmi (chutney) / southekai sasmirecipe
ingredients: 1 medium size cucumber, kashmiri red chillies, grape sized tamarind, 1 tsp of mustard seeds sasive, 1 cup of coconut, salt to taste, 2 tsp coconut oil, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp mustard seeds, few curry leaves, 1 red chilli
instructions: 1. firstly, chop the cucumber finely and keep it aside. 2. further in a small blender add coconut, mustard seeds, red chillies, salt and tamarind together. 3. blend to a fine paste adding water as required. 4. add finely chopped cucumber to grounded coconut paste. 5. mix the coconut paste and cucumber well. 6. furthermore, heat a kadai add coconut oil, mustard seeds, urad dal, broken red chilly and curry leaves. 7. when the mustard seeds splutter pour the seasoning to the saasmi. 8. finally, serve cucumber saasmi along with hot rice or with dosas and idliy's.

title: Cumin Powder (Roasted Jeera Powder)
ingredients: 0.5 cup cumin
instructions: 1. Clean cumin seeds and add them to a heavy bottom pan. Turn on the heat to medium and keep stirring until the seeds become hot to touch. 2. Reduce the heat to the lowest and roast them. Keep stirring continuously so the seeds roast evenly. 3. When done, they will emit a strong earthy, pungent and smoky aroma. Remove from heat. Do not over roast they will burn and taste bitter. 4. Cool them completely and add to a grinder. Grind in short intervals of 30 to 50 seconds until ground to a fine powder. 5. Cool completely and transfer to a air tight glass jar. If you plan to store for longer, store in smaller jars and use them one after the other so most of the aroma is retained. 6. Use cumin powder as needed in curries, stir fry dishes, soups, salads and chaats.

title: Curd Dosa Recipe | Instant Soft & Sponge Dosa
ingredients: 1 cup poha, 1 cup rava semolina, 1 cup curd, 1 tsp salt, water, 0.25 tsp baking soda
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl rinse 1 cup poha with water. 2. Squeeze off the water and transfer the rinsed poha into a large bowl. 3. Add 1 cup rava, 1 cup curd, and 1 tsp salt. mix well. 4. Now add ½ cup water, cover, and rest for 15 minutes. 5. Make sure the rava is soaked well. 6. Transfer the soaked rava mixture into the mixer jar. 7. Add ½ cup water and grind to smooth batter. 8. Transfer the batter to the large bowl. 9. Add ¼ tsp baking soda and mix well. 10. Pour the dosa batter into the hot pan and spread gently. 11. Cook on medium flame until the dosa is cooked well. 12. Finally, enjoy a super soft Curd Dosa Recipe with chutney and sambar.

title: Curd Rice Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice, 1.5 cup water, 1.25 cup curd, salt, 2 tablespoon carrot, 2 tablespoon cucumber, 1 teaspoon coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.875 teaspoon chana dal, 0.875 teaspoon urad dal, 1 red chilli, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chili, 7 roasted cashews
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse it well a few times. Then pour 1½ cups water and ½ teaspoon salt. Keep it in a pressure cooker. Pressure cook rice for 3 whistles until soft. You can also cook the rice directly in the cooker for 2 whistles. 2. When the pressure releases, remove the rice & mash it lightly. You can also fluff up & use if you do not like mushy rice. If you prefer to use milk, refer my notes below. Cool it completely. 3. Add curd to cooled rice. Mix well until combined. If you have cooked the rice without salt, then add it now. 4. Stir in coriander leaves, grated carrots and cucumbers (not gourd) to the curd rice. Taste the curd rice & add more salt if needed. 5. Heat oil in a pan. Then add cumin & mustard. When they begin to splutter, add red chilli, chana dal, urad dal & cashews if using. Fry until the dal turns golden. 6. Add ginger, green chili and curry leaves. When the curry leaves turn crisp, add hing. 7. Optional to soften the lentils - pour 2 to 3 tablespoons water to cook down the lentils, until slightly soft. If the lentils are hard, add a bit more water & cook until they soften to your liking. 8. Pour the tempering over the curd rice and mix. South Indian style curd rice is ready. 9. Garnish with pomegranate seeds.

title: Curry Base Recipe | Master Curry Sauce
ingredients: 1 kg tomato, 0.5 cup oil, 4 bay leaf, 2 cinnamon, 1 tsp cloves, 2 pods black cardamom, 0.5 javetri, 1 tsp cumin, 2 tbsp ginger garlic, 5 onion, 2 tbsp salt, 1 cup cashew paste, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp tumeric, 1.5 tbsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tbsp garam masala, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. Firstly, grind 1 kg of tomatoes to a smooth paste and keep aside. 2. In a small bowl, take 1 tsp tumeric, 1½ tbsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tbsp garam masala. 3. Prepare a smooth paste by adding ½ cup of water. Keep aside. 4. In a large kadai, heat ½ cup oil. Add 4 bay leaf, 2 cinnamon, 1 tsp cloves, 2 pods black cardamom, ½ javetri and 1 tsp cumin. Sauté on a low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 5. Add 2 tbsp of ginger-garlic and sauté well. 6. Now add 5 onions and sauté until golden brown. 7. Further, keep the flame on low and add the prepared masala paste, stirring until well combined. 8. Cook till the oil separates from the sides. 9. Add the tomato puree and cook until well combined. 10. Also, add 2 tbsp of salt and cook until well combined. Be careful as the base starts to splutter. 11. Cover and cook till the oil separates. 12. Now add 1 cup of cashew paste and cook until well combined. Continue cooking until the oil separates. 13. Add 2 tbsp kasuri methi and mix well. 14. Finally, store Gravy Base in an airtight container and refrigerate for up to 3 months.

title: Curry Leaves Chutney
ingredients: 2 cups curry leaves, 3.5 green chilies, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 3 garlic cloves, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoon chana dal, 0.75 tablespoon urad dal, 0.5 cup coconut
instructions: 1. Rinse curry leaves in plenty of water at least thrice. If you are using store bought curry leaves that are not organically grown, simple spray some vinegar and sprinkle baking soda. Leave them for 5 mins. Later Rinse them in water. 2. Drain them to a colander and shake off the excess water. Spread them on a cloth or kitchen tissues and wipe dry. You can also leave them to air dry for a few minutes. 3. Pour ½ tablespoon oil to a pan and add the chana dal, urad dal and cumin seeds. Let the dal turn light golden. Then add the chilies and garlic 4. Fry them until blistered or crisp. Remove to a plate to cool down. 5. Add another ½ tablespoon oil and add the curry leaves. Fry them on a low to medium heat until the leaves are crisp but not burnt. 6. Stir in the coconut and saute for a minute or 2 until the coconut turns hot. 7. Cool and add to a grinder. Squeeze some lemon juice (or tamarind paste) and add salt. 8. Blend all these starting with ¼ cup water, adding more up to ¾ cup in total. Taste test and adjust salt and lemon juice. 9. Curry leaves chutney tastes good on its own without a tempering. But if you want you may temper it optionally & it is not a part of the recipe. 10. For this heat ½ tablespoon oil in a small tadka pan. Add ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 pinch of urad dal, 1 broken dried red chili, 1 to 2 small garlic cloves and 5 to 6 curry leaves. When the leaves turn crisp, add a pinch of hing and pour this over the curry leaves chutney.

title: Curry Leaves Powder (Karivepaku Podi)
ingredients: 1 cup curry leaves, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon white sesame seeds, 1 tablespoon coconut, 3.5 red chilies, 0.875 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 garlic cloves, 1 teaspoon Oil, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 1.5 cups rice, 2.25 tablespoon curry leaves podi, salt, 0.25 cup roasted peanuts, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Always use mature fresh curry leaves that do not have infestations. Remove and discard any leaves that don’t look good. Add a handful of rock salt to a large pot filled with water. Pour little vinegar and mix well to dissolve the salt. Add the whole curry leaf sprigs or measure 1 cup tightly packed leaves (35 grams). Leave them to rest for 10 minutes. Later remove them and discard the water. This way rinse them a few times until the water runs clear. Shake the sprigs well to remove excess water. Separate the leaves from the sprigs and leave them in a colander. 2. When the water drains off completely, spread them on a thick cotton cloth or a parchment paper until the moisture dries up completely. I usually dry these leaves in the morning around 10 am and leave it on the dinning table until evening 4. You can also leave them overnight. (Measure 1 cup leaves (35 grams, tightly packed, dry leaves) to use in this recipe. You can go up to 45 grams.) 3. On a low to medium flame dry roast 1 tablespoon (14 grams) urad dal and 1 tablespoon (14 grams) chana dal until light golden, then add 3 to 4 dried red chilies. Adjust the amount of red chilies to suit your taste. I used Kashmiri or byadgi red chilies. Roast until the dal turns golden and chilies turn crisp. Keep stirring in between to prevent burning. It takes me about 7 mins on a medium flame. 4. When the dal is golden, reduce the heat to low. Add ¾ to 1 teaspoon cumin/ jeera seeds, and the optional ingredients – 1 tablespoon sesame seeds and 1 tablespoon (8 grams) dried coconut/unsweetened shredded coconut or desiccated. If you have dried coconut (copra) use that since it is higher in nutrition than desiccated coconut. Dry roast until the seeds begin to sizzle. Turn off and set these aside to cool. 5. To the same pan, add 1 teaspoon (5 ml) oil and heat it a bit. If you want you can also dehydrate the curry leaves in the oven. Check the recipe card below to get the instructions. 6. Add the curry leaves and begin to fry. Make sure the leaves are completely moisture-free else they won’t fry well. 7. On a low to medium flame, fry them until the leaves turn crisp. This takes sometime so be patient. (takes me 7 to 8 mins but may take longer depending on the leaves). Add 1 to 3 garlic pods to this and fry for a minute. Switch off the stove. 8. Cool all the ingredients completely. If you like to add tamarind, you can add a bit of it. I prefer fresh lemon juice for the rice while serving rather than tamarind. Powder them all in a blender jar along with half teaspoon sea salt. 9. Add curry leaves powder, salt, peanuts and cold pressed oil of choice. 10. Mix them all together. I prefer to use extra virgin olive oil and little fresh lemon juice. Since the oil is not heated in this method, all the nutritional and health qualities of extra virgin olive oil are retained. Add more or less podi to suit your taste. Serve karivepaku podi rice with salad, kachumber, kosambari or plain yogurt.

title: curry leaves rice recipe | karuveppilai sadam | karibevu chitranna
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 tbsp urad dal, 1 tbsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin, 3 red chilli, 2 tbsp dry coconut, ball sized tamarind, 0.5 cup curry leaves, 3 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 red chilli, pinch hing asafoetida, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp peanuts, 2 cup cooked rice, 1 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare the masala powder by heating 2 tsp oil and add 1 tbsp urad dal, 1 tbsp chana dal. 2. also add ½ tsp coriander seeds and ½ tsp jeera / cumin. roast until the dal turns golden brown. 3. further add 3 dried red chilli, 2 tbsp dry coconut, small ball sized tamarind and continue to roast on low flame. 4. now add ½ cup curry leaves and roast on low flame. 5. roast until the curry leaves turn crisp. 6. cool the spices completely and transfer to the small blender. 7. blend to smooth powder without adding any water. curry leaves masala powder ready. 8. now in a large kadai heat 3 tsp oil and add 1 tsp mustard, 1 dried red chilli, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 9. also add 2 tbsp peanuts and splutter the tempering. 10. further add 2 cup cooked rice. make sure to use leftover rice or cool the rice completely. 11. also add prepared masala powder and 1 tsp salt. 12. mix well making sure the spice powder is combined well. 13. further add 2 tbsp water and mix gently. 14. cover and simmer for 3 minutes, or until the flavours are absorbed completely. 15. finally, enjoy curry leaves rice recipe with raita.

title: Curry Powder Recipe
ingredients: 2 tablespoons coriander seeds, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper corn, 2.5 red chilies, 0.5 tablespoon cumin seeds, 1.5 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.5 teaspoon fenugreek seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 tablespoon Bengal gram, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1 star anise, 1 two inch piece Ceylon cinnamon, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 5 green cardamoms
instructions: 1. Heat a pan and add 2 tablespoons (10 grams) coriander seeds, ½ teaspoon (1 gram) black pepper, 3 to 4 dried red chilies (deseeded for low heat), 1 star anise, 1 (1 gram) two inch piece cinnamon, 4 cloves and 5 (2 grams) cardamoms. Toast them (do not brown) on a low to medium heat until coriander seeds turn crunchy. Add the ½ tablespoon (5 grams) cumin seeds and 1½ teaspoon (4.5 grams) fennel seeds. Toast until they begin to smell earthy. Transfer to a cooling tray. 2. To the same pan add ½ teaspoon (2 grams) fenugreek seeds and roast on a low heat until they change their color to deep golden & begin to smell nutty/aromatic. Be careful as these can lend a bitter taste to your curry if they are overtoasted or undertoasted. Add the ½ teaspoon (2 grams) mustard and keep stirring just for a minute. Remove them to the tray. 3. Roast 1 tablespoon (14 grams) Bengal gram/ urad dal – lentils/raw rice on a low to medium heat until aromatic, deep golden or light brown. If using rice, you won’t brown them. Transfer to the cooling tray. Adding more lentils will take you close to sambar powder. 4. Add 10 to 20 curry leaves and toast them until crisp. Turn off and transfer to the tray. The picture shows more curry leaves but don’t use more than 20. 5. Add ¼ teaspoon organic turmeric and ½ teaspoon dried ginger (optional) to grinder jar along with all the cooled ingredients. Powder them fine. Cool down if the curry powder is warm. If you use a lot of turmeric it can stain your grinder jar. Also you may keep aside some of the toasted chilies and use after taste testing the powder. I use only 2 deseeded chilies. 6. Transfer to an air tight glass jar and use as needed. If making a larger batch, store in smaller jars so it remains fresh for longer. This recipe makes about 6 tablespoons. 7. Lentils: Also known as dal are another key ingredient in the curry powder. It not only adds flavor and a creamy texture to the curries but also works as a healthy thickener. Here we use chana dal – Bengal gram & it can be substituted with split pigeon peas (toor dal), skinned urad dal or red lentils. But the flavor changes. 8. Dried Red Chilies: I use low heat Mathania chilies. You can use Kashmiri for low heat or Byadgi for medium heat or any other of choice. To make a hot curry powder, use hot chilies instead of increasing the quantity. We found this way the flavors are more balanced. Using a lot of chilies will impart more chili flavor to the powder & will go closer to the traditional thool/ karam. 9. Ginger: A lot of times I do not include dry ginger in my curry powder because I love using fresh ginger over dried. Also note that both add different kind of flavors. So you can use dried here and still use fresh while cooking. Using dried ginger too often can dehydrate and cause constipation in a lot of people. So use with caution.

title: Curry Sauce Recipe (Instant Pot and Stovetop)
ingredients: 2 cups onions, 3.5 cups tomatoes, 0.875 cup carrots, 0.875 cup red bell pepper, 1 inch ginger, 6 medium garlic, 2 cup water, 0.25 cup oil, 0.41666666666666663 tablespoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tablespoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.5 tablespoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 cup cream, 1 medium bay leaf, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 2.5 inch cinnamon piece, 1 star anise
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a large pot. Add the whole spices, onions, ginger & garlic. 2. Saute/fry them on a medium heat until transparent. For deeper flavors saute them until light golden brown on a low flame, 3. Stir in red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala, cumin powder & fennel powder. 4. Stir in the tomatoes, carrots and bell peppers. Stir fry for 2 to 3 mins on a medium heat. 5. Pour 1½ cups water or broth. Bring it to a rolling boil. 6. Cover and cook on a medium heat until all the veggies are mushy. Add more water/broth at this stage, as you cook. 7. Add salt and sugar when they are soft. 8. Cool this and remove the bay leaf, black cardamom, star anise and cinnamon. Blend to a smooth thick sauce, adding more liquid as required. To use the sauce as a dip or on pizza, in sandwiches and marinades, keep the consistency thick. 9. Taste test and more garam masala if you want. Transfer to a glass freezer safe containers. 10. Vegetable Curries: Grill, roast or stir fry your favorite veggies until tender. Season with salt and spice. Add the required amount of sauce and simmer for 2 to 3 mins. For a loose consistency, add some broth, coconut milk or cashew cream or heavy cream and bring it to a gentle boil. 11. Meat Curries: Marinate prawns, chicken or lamb with spices, yogurt/buttermilk & herbs or with this sauce. Grill, roast or stir fry them until tender and add to the hot sauce. If the sauce is too thick, add some broth/coconut milk or cashew milk. Simmer for a few minutes until the flavors are absorbed. 12. Perform the steps till you saute the onions on a SAUTE mode. Turn off the IP. Stir in all the ground spices, followed by the veggies and 1½ cups water. 13. Pressure cook on high pressure for 16 minutes. Let pressure drop naturally, discard bay leaf, cinnamon, star anise and black cardamom. Blend the ingredients & pour back to the IP. 14. Add sugar (optional) and salt. Taste test and add more garam masala if required. If the sauce is too thick you may add little water. Cook on a saute mode until you see traces of oil/fats on the sauce and the curry sauce turns thick. This takes about 5 minutes.

title: curry without onion and garlic recipe | all purpose curry base
ingredients: 0.25 cup oil, 1 tsp cumin, 2 bay leaf, 3 inch cinnamon, 1 pods cardamom, 1 cloves 10 cloves, 0.5 tsp pepper, 1 kg tomato, 3 gram ginger, 3 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp sesame, 2 tbsp melon seeds, 0.5 cup oil, 1 tsp turmeric, 3 tbsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 2 tsp kasuri methi, 0.25 tsp hing
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat ¼ cup oil. add 1 tsp cumin, 2 bay leaf, 3 inch cinnamon, 10 pods cardamom, 10 cloves and ½ tsp pepper. 2. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 3. add 1 kg tomato, 30 gram ginger and saute well until tomato softens. 4. also, add 3 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp sesame and 2 tbsp melon seeds. 5. saute for a minute or until everything is well combined. 6. cover and cook for 10 minutes or until everything is cooked well. 7. cool completely, and transfer to the mixi jar, grind to smooth paste. keep aside. 8. in a large kadai heat ½ cup oil, keeping flame on low add 1 tsp turmeric, 3 tbsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 2 tsp kasuri methi and ¼ tsp hing. 9. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic without burning. 10. add in a prepared tomato base and mix well. 11. cook until the oil separates from the base. 12. cover and cook for 5 minutes or until the oil separates completely. 13. finally, no onion no garlic gravy base is ready to prepare the curry of your choice.

title: Custard Ice Cream Recipe (Eggless)
ingredients: 1 cup heavy whipping cream, 1.5 cups milk, 1.5 tablespoons custard powder, 1 tablespoons sugar, 1 teaspoon vanilla extract, 3 tablespoons milk
instructions: 1. Add 1½ tablespoons custard powder to a mixing bowl. 2. Measure 1½ cups milk to a cup. Take 3 tbsp milk from this cup into the mixing bowl. Make a smooth lump-free slurry. 3. Next add 8 to 10 tablespoons (½ to ¾ cup) sugar to milk. Please adjust the quantity to your taste. I use 10 tablespoons because the sugar I use is low in sweetness. Begin to heat. 4. Dissolve the sugar completely. Then begin to add 1 tbsp custard mixture each time, in parts. 5. Mix well. 6. Again add another 1 to 2 tbsps. Then mix well again after every addition to make sure there are no lumps. 7. Cook the mixture on a medium to low flame stirring consistently to prevent lumps. 8. Turn off the stove when it turns thick. The mixture has to coat the back of the spoon well when you dip it in the mixture. 9. Cover as soon as it is done and cool this completely. The mixture has to be smooth without lumps. 10. While it cools, pour the cream to the chilled bowl. Begin to whip it on a low speed first and then on a medium speed. If using a mixer jar, pour the cream to it. Run the mixer until the cream turns thick. Do not over whip else you will end up with butter. Check after 1 minute and then again after 30 seconds. 11. Soon the mixture turns frothy first and then to stiff peaks. Do not over whip the cream. 12. Whisk the custard in the pan to smooth. If it is too thick, you can add little milk and whisk again. Then transfer it to the whipped cream. I prefer to pass it through a strainer just to make sure there are no lumps. I add it in 2 batches and mix. 13. Finally the mixture has to be thick but of flowing consistency. Very runny mixture may later form ice crystals. 14. I forgot to add in 1 teaspoon vanilla extract, so added later. But you can add it along with custard. 15. Mix well and cover the bowl with a cling wrap. Make sure the wrap touches the cream to prevent crystals. Freeze it overnight or until set.

title: custard powder halwa recipe | how to prepare custard halwa
ingredients: 1 cup sugar, 0.5 cup custard powder, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tbsp saffron water kesar water, 5 cashew
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup sugar and ½ cup custard powder. 2. add in 1 cup water and whisk well. 3. make sure there are no lumps and sugar dissolved completely. keep aside. 4. now heat 2 tbsp ghee in a large kadai and roast 5 chopped cashews. 5. now pour prepared sugar-custard mixture stirring continuously, keeping flame on low else lumps might form. 6. also pour 1 tbsp of kesar water / saffron water. 7. stir continuously till the mixture thickens and starts to separate from pan. 8. further, stir till the mixture turns glossy and translucent. 9. transfer the custard halwa into small bowl or into greased tray like prepared in corn flour halwa. 10. rest for 30 minutes or till it cools slightly. 11. finally, unmould onto a plate and serve custard powder halwa recipe garnished with cashew.

title: Custard Recipe | Homemade Creamy Fruit Custard
ingredients: 0.25 cup custard powder, 5 cup milk, 0.5 cup sugar, pomegranate, apple, banana, strawberry, grapes, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl, take ¼ cup custard powder and dissolve with ½ cup milk. 2. Make sure there are no lumps, and keep aside. 3. In a kadai, heat 4 cups of milk and bring to a boil. 4. Once the milk comes to a boil, mix the prepared custard milk mixture continuously. 5. Keep stirring till the milk thickens and turns creamy. 6. Add in ½ cup sugar and mix well. Continue to cook till the custard turns creamy. 7. Transfer to a bowl and refrigerate for 2 hours. 8. Once the custard milk is chilled, it becomes more creamy. Whisk smooth to achieve a uniform thickness. 9. Add in fruits of your choice; I have added pomegranate, apple, banana, strawberry and grapes. 10. Also, add ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 11. Finally, enjoy the chilled Fruit Custard Recipe.

title: custard sarbath recipe | doodh ka sharbath | custard milk & sago sarbath
ingredients: 1 litre milk, 0.25 cup custard powder, 0.5 cup sugar, 4 cup water, 0.25 cup sabudana, 2 tsp sabja sweet basil seeds falooda seeds tukmaria, 1 cup water, 4 ml water, 1 pack strawberry jelly mix, 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp raisins, 2 tbsp dates, few ice cubes, 1 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 litre milk, ¼ cup custard powder and ½ cup sugar. 2. whisk and mix well until everything is well combined. 3. transfer the mixture to a large kadai and stir well. 4. keep stirring and get the milk to boil. 5. boil for 2-3 minutes or until the milk thickens slightly. 6. cool completely, and custard milk is ready. 7. firstly, make sure to read the package instruction. in a saucepan take 400 ml water. 8. once the water comes to a boil add 1 pack of the strawberry jelly mix. 9. stir continuously until everything is well combined. 10. pour the mixture into a glass mould, and rest for 15 minutes. 11. refrigerate for 1 hour or until the kelly sets completely. 12. cut into the desired shape and strawberry jelly is ready to serve. 13. firstly, in a large saucepan take 4 cup water and ¼ cup sabudana. make sure to rinse the sabudana well remove excess starch. 14. boil the sabudana for 10 minutes or until it turns soft. 15. drain off the water and rinse with cold water. 16. finally, sabudana bubbles are ready. keep aside. 17. firstly, in a bowl take 2 tsp sabja. 18. soak in 1 cup water for 15 minutes or until the sabja swells up completely. 19. after 15 minutes, the sabja would have turned jelly texture. 20. firstly, in a large bowl take chilled custard milk. 21. add in soaked sabja, jelly and sabudana. 22. also add 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp raisins and 2 tbsp dates. 23. further add few ice cubes and mix well. 24. finally, serve the custard sarbath in a tall glass garnished with chopped pistachios.

title: cut kulfi ice cream recipe | sliced roll malai kulfi no mawa no milk powder
ingredients: 1 litre milk, 1 cup sugar, 0.5 cup cream, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 1 litre milk. 2. stir and get the milk to boil. 3. simmer for 10 minutes or until the milk thickens slightly. keep aside. 4. in a thick bottomed pan take 1 cup sugar. 5. keep stirring on a low flame until the sugar melts. 6. make sure to keep the flame on low and keep stirring to prevent it from burning. 7. sugar melts and turns golden brown. do not burn the caramelised sugar. 8. further, add ½ cup cream and stir continuously. 9. keep stirring until the caramelised sugar and cream is mixed well. 10. pour the caramelized syrup into the thickened milk. 11. stir and cook on medium flame. 12. now add 2 tbsp cornflour in a small bowl and add half a cup milk. 13. stir and mix making sure there are no lumps. 14. pour the cornflour slurry into the milk mixture. 15. stir continuously, keeping the flame on medium. 16. continue to cook until the mixture thickens and turns creamy. 17. also, add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 18. cool slightly and pour the mixture into a glass or jar. 19. freeze for 8 hours or until the ice cream is set completely. 20. unmould the kulfi and cut into pieces before serving. 21. finally, enjoy cut roll malai kulfi topped with nuts. 22. keep stirring on a low flame until the sugar melts. make sure to keep the flame on low and keep stirring to prevent it from burning.

title: Cutting Chai Recipe | Mumbai Cutting Tea Recipe | How to Make Cutting Chai
ingredients: 1 inch ginger, 3 pods cardamom, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 3 cup water, 3 tsp tea powder, 3 tsp sugar, 1 cup milk
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mortar pestle take 1 inch ginger, 3 pods cardamom, 4 cloves, ½ tsp pepper, and ½ inch cinnamon. 2. Gently pound the pestle into the mortar to help masala release its flavors. Keep aside. 3. In a saucepan take 3 cup water and warm it on medium flame. 4. Once the water is hot enough, add prepared chai masala and boil for 3 minutes or until the flavors are infused in water. 5. Now add 3 tsp tea powder and boil for 2 minutes. 6. Once the tea flavors are infused in water, add 3 tsp sugar and boil until the sugar is melted. 7. Further, add 1 cup milk and get to a boil. 8. Just mix it and boil for 2 minutes for the aroma to be absorbed by the chai. 9. Finally, filter out the chai and serve Cutting Chai with biscuits.

title: Cutting Chai Recipe | Mumbai Cutting Tea Recipe | How to Make Cutting Chai
ingredients: 1 inch ginger, 3 pods cardamom, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 3 cup water, 3 tsp tea powder, 3 tsp sugar, 1 cup milk
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mortar pestle take 1 inch ginger, 3 pods cardamom, 4 cloves, ½ tsp pepper, and ½ inch cinnamon. 2. Gently pound the pestle into the mortar to help masala release its flavors. Keep aside. 3. In a saucepan take 3 cup water and warm it on medium flame. 4. Once the water is hot enough, add prepared chai masala and boil for 3 minutes or until the flavors are infused in water. 5. Now add 3 tsp tea powder and boil for 2 minutes. 6. Once the tea flavors are infused in water, add 3 tsp sugar and boil until the sugar is melted. 7. Further, add 1 cup milk and get to a boil. 8. Just mix it and boil for 2 minutes for the aroma to be absorbed by the chai. 9. Finally, filter out the chai and serve Cutting Chai with biscuits.

title: Dabeli Recipe
ingredients: 1 pound potatoes, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.875 teaspoon sea salt, 2.5 tablespoons Dabeli masala, 1 cup roasted skinned split peanuts, 0.75 cup tamarind chutney, 1 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 6 garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 1.25 teaspoon jaggery powder, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts, 1.5 tablespoon unsalted butter, 8 pav, 0.5 cup onions, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.75 cup pomegranate arils, 0.75 cup sev, roasted spiced peanuts from above
instructions: 1. Peel and dice potatoes to 2 inch cubes and add them to a pot. Cover with water and bring to a rolling boil. Reduce to medium and boil until fork tender. Remove from water and mash them well with a masher. 2. Soak red chilies in 1 cup boiling hot water until soft, for about 10 mins. Remove from water and add to a grinder along with garlic, salt, jaggery, peanuts, cumin and ¼ cup water. Grind to a thick smooth chutney adding more water as required. 3. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan and add the roasted peanuts, salt and 1 tablespoon dabeli masala. (If using store bought spice blend or salted peanuts, cut down or avoid salt.)
