Toast for 2 mins and remove to a plate. 4. To the same pan, add the rest of the oil and the dabeli masala. Stir well and add 2 tablespoons sweet tamarind chutney. Cook for a minute until aromatic and pour half cup water. Bring to a boil. 5. Stir in the mashed potatoes and add little salt. Mix well to combine. Taste test to adjust salt, tang and spices. If you want add more tamarind chutney or dabeli masala for more flavor. Cook for 2 to 3 mins and turn off. Let cool down a bit. 6. Transfer to a serving bowl if you want and even out the surface. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons each of roasted peanuts, sev, onions, pomegranate arils and coriander leaves. 7. Slit the pav (horizontally) on three sides, keeping one side intact. Smear 1 tablespoon tamarind chutney on one side (inside) of the pav and ½ to 1 tablespoon garlic chutney on the other side (see pics in the post) 8. Stuff 1½ to 2 tablespoons of potato stuffing,1 teaspoon chopped onions, 1 tablespoon peanuts and a few pomegranate arils. 9. Stuff more potato masala and even out the surface on three sides using a spoon. This way assemble all the 8. 10. Spread remaining sev and half of the remaining peanuts in one plate. Add remaining chopped onions, 2 tablespoons pomegranate arils and coriander leaves to another plate. Press down the dabeli over the ingredients, one after the other into both the plates. Rotate the bun on all the 3 sides to coat. 11. Add 1 to 2 teaspoons butter or ghee to a pan and place the dabeli. Smear soft butter or ghee on top of the dabeli and toast them on both sides until hot & crisp. If you want you may press down the dabeli while toasting. 12. Serve them immediately. Lassi and kachumber go well in this meal.

title: dabeli recipe | dhabeli recipe | how to make kacchi dabeli
ingredients: 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 1 pod black cardamom, 6 clove 6 clove, 1 star anise, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp sesame seed, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 3 red chilli, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp tamarind chutney, 0.25 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 3 potato aloo, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tbsp coconut, 1 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp sev, 2 tbsp pomegranate, 2 tbsp spiced peanut, 5 pav, 5 tsp green chutney, 5 tsp tamarind chutney, 5 tsp onion, butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan take 1 tsp coriander seeds, ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp fennel, ½ tsp pepper, ½ inch cinnamon, 1 pod black cardamom and 6 clove. 2. also, add 1 star anise, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp sesame seed, 2 tbsp dry coconut and 3 dried red chilli. 3. dry roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 4. cool completely, and transfer to a small mixi jar. 5. add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. 6. blend to a fine powder and dabeli masala is ready. keep aside. 7. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. 8. now take 3 tsp of prepared dabeli masala into a small cup, along with 2 tbsp tamarind chutney and ¼ cup water. 9. mix well making sure there are no lumps. pour in the masala mixture into the hot oil. 10. cook for 2 minutes or until it turns aromatic. 11. further, add 3 potato, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 12. mash and mix making sure everything are well combined. 13. transfer the mixture into a plate. 14. top with 1 tbsp coconut, 1 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp sev, 2 tbsp pomegranate and 2 tbsp spiced peanut. 15. firstly, slit the pav in the centre and spread 1 tsp green chutney on one side of pav and 1 tsp of tamarind chutney on another side. 16. stuff in prepared aloo dabeli mixture into the pav. 17. also stuff in 1 tsp of onion and aloo dabeli mixture. 18. now toast the pav in butter making sure both sides turn slightly golden brown. 19. finally, roll the dabeli into sev and serve immediately.

title: Daddojanam
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice, 1.5 cups water, 1.5 cups curd, 0.5 cup full fat milk, 1.5 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 Pinch hing, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.75 tbsp chana dal, 0.5 tbsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp pepper
instructions: 1. These are the ingredients to use. If you are not making this for offering to God, then you can also use slit green chilies and half tsp of grated ginger. 2. Wash rice a few times and soak it for about 20 minutes. You can skip soaking and cook the rice for longer. Pressure cook it until soft and mushy. I pressure cook for 2 whistles on a medium heat. 3. Once done, open the lid and mash the hot rice very well. 4. When the rice is still very hot, add boiled milk. Using boiled milk instead of unboiled will keep the rice good for longer. The milk can be very hot to warm. 5. Mix up everything very well. The rice has to look very moist at this stage. If needed you can add in more milk. If the rice is aged, then it usually needs little more milk. Set this aside to cool completely. 6. When the rice is cooled, you will see the milk has been absorbed completely and it looks dry. Add salt at this stage. 7. Then the curd. 8. Mix up everything well. Rice must not be dry, it has to be gooey and creamy. Add in more curd if needed. 9. Heat oil in a kadai. Add mustard and cumin. 10. When they begin to splutter, add chana dal and urad dal. Fry until the dals turn golden. 11. Add curry leaves, pepper and hing. Switch off the stove. 12. Cool down & Pour this over the curd rice. Mix very well. 13. Temple style daddojanam is ready. After offering to the Goddess, I garnished with some pomegranate seeds. You can also use grated carrots and cucumber.

title: daddojanam recipe | andhra style curd rice | bagalabath
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice, 1.5 cup water, 0.5 cup milk, 1 cup curd yogurt, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp pepper, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker take ½ cup rice and add 1½ cup water. 2. pressure cook for 5 whistles or until rice is cooked well. 3. once the pressure is released open the cooker and mash the rice smooth. 4. transfer to the bowl and add ½ cup milk. 5. mix well making sure rice is well combined. adding milk helps to soften rice and also prevents curd rice from turning sour when kept for long time. 6. allow it to rest for 5 to 10 minutes or until its cooled completely. 7. further, add 1 cup curd and ½ tsp salt. mix well. 8. now prepare tempering by heating 2 tbsp oil. 9. add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, pinch hing, ½ tsp pepper and few curry leaves. 10. splutter the tempering and pour over the curd rice. 11. mix well making sure everything is well combined. for prasadam do not add chilli, ginger and coriander. 12. finally, daddojanam or temple style curd rice is ready for naivedyam.

title: dahi bhindi recipe | dahi wali bhindi recipe | okra yogurt gravy
ingredients: 0.5 onion, 1 inch ginger, 2 cloves garlic, tsp oil, 2 tbsp water, 1 bhindi, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp oil, 0.75 tsp mustard seeds, 0.25 tsp methi seeds fenugreek seeds, 0.75 tsp jeera cumin seeds, pinch asafoetida hing, few curry leaves, 1.5 tsp kashmiri chilli powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric powder, 1 cup thick curd yogurt, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 cup water, few coriander leaves, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. in a kadai add 2 tsp of oil and fry onions, ginger and garlic. 2. fry till they turn slightly golden in colour. allow to cool completely. 3. add required water and blend to smooth paste. keep aside. 4. in the same kadai, add a tsp of oil and fry chopped bhindis. 5. fry till bhindi cook from all sides and changes colour to dark green. 6. remove and keep aside. 7. in the same kadai, add a tsp of oil along with mustard seeds, jeera, methi and hing. 8. once they splutter add curry leaves. 9. now add prepared onion puree and saute for a minute. 10. add spices like chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder and salt to taste. fry the masala on low flame. 11. add some water. 12. then add 1 cup curd / yogurt and stir on low flame. 13. immediately add fried bhindi / okra and give a good stir. 14. remember to switch off the flame. close the lid and allow to cook for 5 minute. 15. garnish with few coriander leaves. 16. then add a pinch of garam masala. and give a good mix. 17. serve dahi bhindi with hot rice / chapathi.

title: dahi idli recipe | curd idli chaat recipe 2 ways | suji dahi idli
ingredients: 1 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 cup curd, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.75 cup water, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt, 1 cup curd, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup water, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp raisins, 2 chilli, 2 inch ginger, pinch hing, 2 tbsp coriander, boondi, chilli powder, green chutney, tamarind chutney, chilli powder, cumin powder, chaat masala, onion, sev, coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan dry roast 1 cup rava on low flame. roasting rava prevents idli from turning sticky. 2. cool completely, and transfer to the bowl. 3. add ½ cup curd, ½ tsp salt. 4. mix well until everything is well combined. 5. further, add ¾ cup water and mix well until there are no lumps in the batter. 6. cover and rest for 20 minutes, or until the suji is soaked well. 7. now add ½ tsp eno fruit salt and 2 tbsp water. 8. mix gently forming a frothy idli consistency batter. 9. pour the batter into greased idli plate. 10. steam the idli on a medium flame for 10 minutes. 11. finally, rava idli is ready to prepare chaat. 12. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup curd, 1 tsp sugar, ½ tsp salt and ¼ cup water. 13. whisk and mix forming a smooth consistency curd. 14. sweetened curd is ready. make sure the consistency is slightly watery, else the idli will not absorb the curd. 15. firstly, prepare the tempering to mix with sweetened curd. for that heat 2 tsp oil. 16. add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp chana dal, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp raisins, 2 chilli, 2 inch ginger and pinch hing. 17. splutter the tempering and roast the cashew until it turns golden brown. 18. pour the tempering over the sweetened curd. 19. also, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 20. to serve the thayir idli, in the plate place 3 idli. 21. pour prepared tempered curd over it generously. 22. top with boondi and sprinkle a little chilli powder. 23. finally, enjoy south indian style thayir idli. 24. firstly, to serve the curd idli chaat, on the plate place 3 idli and pour prepared sweetened curd over it generously. 25. top with green chutney, tamarind chutney. 26. sprinkle a little chilli powder, cumin powder and chaat masala. 27. also, top with onion, sev and coriander. 28. finally, enjoy street style dahi idli chaat.

title: Dahi Kabab | Dahi Ke Kabab
ingredients: 2 cups curd, 1.25 cup paneer, 1 cashewnuts, 0.25 cup onions very finely chopped, 1.5 teaspoons ginger paste, 2 green chilies, 1.125 teaspoons garam masala, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, Salt as needed, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, 2.5 tablespoons besan, Oil as needed for shallow frying
instructions: 1. Place a strainer over a large deep bowl. Spread a thin muslin cloth or cheese cloth over the strainer. Pour the curd and bring together the edges of the cloth. Then wring the cloth to drain up excess whey from curd. (check pics in the post) 2. Place 2 to 3 heavy ceramic bowl or a heavy object over the pack of yogurt. Refrigerate the whole thing for 12 to 14 hours. The whey drips and gets collected to the bowl. Later hung curd is ready. 3. Crumble paneer and add it to a mixing bowl along with hung curd. 4. Add all the other ingredients - ginger, garlic, chilli, garam masala, onions, cashews and coriander leaves. 5. Mix well just to make a non sticky dough. If the dough is still sticky add more bread crumbs. 6. Divide the dough to equal sized balls. Flatten them. 7. Then roll them in besan or any flour you prefer. Shake them off gently to remove excess flour. Set aside for 5 to 7 mins. 8. Heat up oil for shallow frying. Test if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of the mixture. It should sizzle, rise up and not turn brown. 9. Gently drop the dahi kabab one after the other. Regulate the flame to medium. 10. Do not disturb them for a few minutes. When one side turns golden, flip them and fry on the other side as well. 11. Fry all the dahi kababs in batches until golden and crisp. 12. Serve dahi kabab hot with coriander chutney, green chutney or mint chutney.

title: Dahi Ke Kabab Recipe | Hung Curd Dahi Paneer Cutlet - Tea Time Snack
ingredients: 3 cup curd, 1 cup paneer, 3 tbsp onion, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp cashew, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp besan gram flour, 1 cup breadcrumbs, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, take 3 cup curd and tie in a clean white cloth. 2. place a heavy object on it to strain off excess water, and keep aside for 4 hours. 3. transfer the hung curd into a large bowl. 4. add 1 cup paneer, 3 tbsp onion, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, and 2 tbsp cashew. 5. also add ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ¼ tsp pepper powder, and ½ tsp salt. 6. further, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix using a whisk to break any lumps. 7. also, add 2 tbsp roasted besan and continue to mix combining everything well. 8. take a small ball-sized mixture and shape to the cutlet. 9. roll over breadcrumbs for uniform coating. 10. freeze for 1 hour or until it turns firm slightly. 11. now deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 12. immediately, the kebab turns golden brown. 13. so gently fry on both sides until it turns golden brown and crisp. 14. drain off kebab to remove excess oil. 15. finally, enjoy dahi ke kebab with green chutney.

title: dahi ke sholay recipe | dahi bread roll | bread curd fire roll | dahi ke sholey
ingredients: 0.5 cup hung curd thick yogurt, 1 cup paneer cottage cheese, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala b, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp capsicum, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp salt, 6 slices bread
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take ½ cup hung curd and 1 cup grated paneer. 2. also add ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp coriander, ½ tsp aamchur and ¼ tsp salt. 3. combine well making sure all the spices gets mixed with paneer and hung curd. keep aside. 4. now take a bread slice and trim off the edges. you can use sides of bread to prepare breadcrumbs by blending dry in mixi. 5. roll the bread slice as thin as possible, this prevent bread from absorbing oil. 6. place a tbsp of prepared dahi stuffing in one side of bread slice leaving space at the ends. 7. brush some water around the edges. 8. roll and press the edges to seal them tight. else the stuffing will be in oil while deep frying. 9. furthermore, heat the oil and deep fry the dahi bread rolls on medium flame. or preheat oven and bake at 180 degree celsius for 15-20 minutes. 10. fry them till they turn golden brown. drain and place on an absorbent paper. 11. finally, serve dahi ke sholay recipe or dahi bread roll hot with tomato ketchup or green chutney.

title: dahi paneer recipe | dahi ka paneer | dahi wala paneer ki sabji
ingredients: 12 cubes paneer cottage cheese, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp oil, 2 bay leaf, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 3 pods cardamom, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 2 tbsp besan gram flour, 2 cup curd, 1 cup water, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take 12 cubes of paneer. make sure to use fresh and soft paneer else the curry does not taste good. 2. add ¼ tsp turmeric and ¼ tsp chilli powder. mix gently making sure all the spices are well coated. 3. cover and allow to marinate for 30 minutes. 4. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 2 bay leaf, 5 cloves, 3 pods cardamom, 1 tsp kasuri methi and 1 tsp cumin. 5. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 6. now add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until the onions turn golden brown. 7. further keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and 2 tbsp besan. 8. roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic and besan is cooked well. make sure to roast really well, else there will be a raw flavour of besan in the curry. 9. now add 2 cup curd and stir continuously. whisk the curd really well before adding, else there are chances curd to split. 10. keep stirring continuously, until the oil releases from the mixture. 11. now add 1 cup water and ¾ tsp salt. mix well adjusting the consistency as required. 12. after marinating the paneer for 30 minutes, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil. 13. roast ½ onion and ½ capsicum until it turns crunchy. 14. further, add marinated paneer and roast for a minute. 15. transfer the roasted paneer into the curry and mix well. 16. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 17. also, add 2 tbsp coriander and ¼ tsp garam masala. mix well. 18. finally, enjoy dahi paneer ki sabji with roti or phulka.

title: dahi papdi chaat recipe | dahi papri chaat | papdi chaat recipe
ingredients: 13 papdi, 3 tbsp potato aloo, 3 tbsp chickpea, 3 tbsp onion, 5 tbsp curd, 3 tsp green chutney, 3 tsp tamarind chutney, pinch chilli powder, pinch cumin powder, pinch chaat masala, pinch salt, 1 tbsp tomato, 3 tbsp sev, 1 tsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a serving plate take 13 papdi. to prepare homemade papdi using wheat flour check out my papdi recipe. 2. top with 3 tbsp potato and 3 tbsp chickpea. 3. also, sprinkle 2 tbsp of chopped onions. 4. now take 3 tbsp of whisked curd and drizzle over it. 5. further, add 2 tsp of green chutney and 2 tsp of tamarind chutney. 6. sprinkle chilli powder, cumin powder, chaat masala and salt. 7. additionally, add 1 tbsp onion and 1 tbsp tomato. 8. now add 1 tbsp of more curd, 1 tsp green chutney and 1 tsp tamarind chutney. 9. top with 3 tbsp sev and garnish with 1 tsp coriander. 10. finally, enjoy dahi papdi chaat with a hot cup of tea.

title: dahi puri recipe | how to make dahi batata puri recipe
ingredients: 6 puris papdis, 0.5 potato, 1 cup curd yogurt, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 onion, 0.5 to mato, 0.5 cup sev, 5 tsp tamarind chutney, 3 tsp green chutney, pinch of kashmiri chili powder, pinch of chaat masala, salt to taste, 3 tsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, make a hole at the centre of puri with your thumb. 2. further, stuff half tsp of boiled potatoes into each puris. 3. furthermore, take a cup of curd and mix sugar in it. 4. also add generous amount of sweet curd into each puris. 5. sprinkle generous amount of chopped onions. 6. also sprinkle and spread chopped tomatoes. 7. furthermore, top with generous amount of thin sev. 8. then pour and spread tamarind chutney. 9. further add green chutney onto each puri. 10. then also top with a tsp of curd. 11. sprinkle chili powder, chaat masala and black salt or cooking salt. 12. finally, garnish with coriander leaves and serve sev puri immediately.

title: Dahi Vada Recipe (Dahi Bhalla)
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 3 tablespoons chilled water, 6 cashew nuts, Oil, 1 cup coriander leaves, 0.25 cup mint leaves, 1 green chili, 0.25 inch ginger, 1 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon Lemon juice as needed, 1 tablespoon roasted gram, 0.25 teaspoon salt, water very little if needed, 0.25 cup tamarind, 0.5 cup jaggery, 1 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon dry ginger powder, 0.25 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.375 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 2.5 to 3 cups curd, 1 tablespoon powdered sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash urad dal thrice and soak for at least 4 to 6 hours. Chill 1 cup water in the refrigerator to blend the lentils later. 2. Add 1 cup water, tamarind, jaggery and dates to a pot. Simmer for 4 to 5 mins until the tamarind turns completely mushy. 3. Stir in red chili powder, salt, cumin powder, fennel powder, dry ginger powder and coriander powder or garam masala. Mix and boil for 2 minutes until the aroma of spices comes out. If the mixture becomes too thick, pour ¼ cup hot water. 4. Taste test to adjust salt and jaggery to your preference. Cool down, blend and pass through a sieve to a wide bowl. 5. Optionally temper - Heat a teaspoon of oil or ghee in a pan. Add hing and then pour the tamarind chutney. Simmer for 2 to 3 mins until bubbling hot & thick. Cool this completely. 6. Transfer this to a dry glass or ceramic jar. Store this in fridge and use up within 4 to 6 weeks. 7. Add coriander leaves, mint leaves, green chili, ginger, garlic, cumin seeds, lemon juice, roasted gram & salt to a blender jar. Grind to a smooth chutney, adding water (2 to 3 tbps) only as needed. Taste test to adjust salt and spice. 8. Drain the water from soaked urad dal and add to a blender jar. Also add cumin, salt, green chili, ginger, hing and pour 2 tbsps chilled water. 9. Grind for a minute and scrape the sides, repeat blending adding 1 tbsp water each time only as needed until the batter turns thick, fluffy and light. (Avoid adding too much water as it makes the batter runny. We want a thick & fluffy batter here.) 10. When the batter is ground perfectly, it looks lighter in color. Remove to a bowl. Using your hand or a whisk, whisk/ aerate it well for 1 min in one direction. 11. Test the consistency – drop ¼ tsp batter to a bowl of water. If it floats it means the batter is aerated well else blend it a little longer or whisk a little longer. 12. Stir in the cashews if using. 13. To a large bowl, pour 2 to 3 cups water and ½ teaspoon salt. Keep this aside on the kitchen counter. 14. On a medium flame, heat oil in a kadai. Test if the oil is hot enough by sliding ½ tsp batter. It should sizzle and come to the surface steadily without turning brown. 15. Grease your fingers or a tablespoon or a fruit scooper. Take small portions of the batter and gently slide into the oil. 16. Fry on a medium heat, untouched for 3 minutes and then stir them. Continue to fry until golden and crisp. Remove to a steel colander or lined rack. 17. Transfer the hot fried vadas to the bowl of water. Rest them for 15 to 20 mins, rotating them with a spoon in between. Timing may vary depending on the size of the vadas. 18. While these rest fry the next batch of vadas. Repeat the same process of soaking them in water. 19. Add 2½ cups yogurt, a tbsp of powdered sugar and ½ teaspoon salt to a large wide bowl (big enough to hold all your vadas). Beat well with a whisk until smooth. It should not be too thick like Greek yogurt and not very runny. Taste test and adjust salt. 20. Gently press down the vadas in between spatulas or your palms one after the other to remove most of the water. Drop these to the bowl of yogurt & ensure all of the vadas are dunked in the yogurt well. 21. Cover this and chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours. This is when the dahi vadas soak up a bit of yogurt and flavor. 22. They will be softer by 2 hours. If you like more yogurt, whisk another half cup yogurt with sugar and salt. Pour that as well. Transfer the dahi vadas to serving tray or plates. 23. Top with a generous amounts of green chutney and sweet tamarind chutney. 24. Sprinkle cumin powder, chaat masala, black salt and red chili powder. Garnish with coriander leaves. Adjust the quantity of chutneys, chaat masala, cumin powder and red chili powder to suit your taste. 25. Serve dahi vada immediately after you sprinkle the spice powders.

title: dahi vada recipe | dahi bhalla recipe | soft dahi balle | dahi bade
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup moong dal, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp salt, oil, 5 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp hing asafoetida, 2 cup curd, 2 tbsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, green chutney, tamarind chutney, chilli powder, cumin powder, chaat masala, boondi, coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, soak 1 cup urad dal for 5 hours. 2. drain off the water and transfer to the mixi or grinder. also, add 1 chilli and 1 inch ginger. 3. blend to smooth paste adding water as required. try to add a minimal amount of water to prevent the batter from turning watery. 4. transfer the urad dal batter to a large bowl. 5. take ¼ cup soaked moong dal into the blender. 6. blend to smooth paste and transfer to the same bowl of urad dal batter. 7. now add 1 tsp salt and mix well using the whisk. 8. prepare a thick batter. if the batter is runny then add a tbsp of rice flour. 9. now wit wet spoon or using hand, drop a spoonful of batter into the hot oil. 10. keeping the flame on medium, stir occasionally. 11. fry until the vada turns golden brown and crisp. 12. drain off the vada over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 13. now in a large bowl take 5 cup hot water. 14. add ½ tsp salt and ¼ tsp hing. mix well making sure it is well combined. 15. now drop the hot fried vada into the water and dip completely. 16. soak for 30 minutes, or until the vada absorbs water. 17. after 30 minutes, squeeze off the water and transfer to plate. 18. firstly, take 2 cup curd, 2 tbsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. 19. whisk and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 20. firstly, pour the sweetened curd over the vada. 21. also pour a generous amount of green chutney and tamarind chutney. 22. sprinkle chilli powder, cumin powder, chaat masala. 23. top with boondi and coriander. 24. finally, enjoy dahi vada or dahi bhalla chilled.

title: Dal Fry Recipe - Dhaba Style with Secret tadka
ingredients: 0.75 cup toor dal, 3.5 cup water, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tsp mustard rai, 1 tsp jeera cumin, 1 red chilli, few curry leaves, pinch of hing asafoetida, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 green chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tomato, 1 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves
instructions: 1. firstly in a pressure cook ¾ cup toor dal and 3 cup water for 5 whistles. 2. now in another kadai heat 1 tbsp ghee and splutter 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp jeera, 1 dried red chilli, few curry leaves and pinch of hing. 3. add in 1 onion followed by 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 1 green chilli. saute well. 4. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder and ½ tsp coriander powder. saute on low flame. 5. additionally add 1 tomato and saute till it turns soft and mushy. 6. add the cooked dal along with 1 tsp salt. mix well. 7. simmer for 5 minutes or till dal turns smooth and absorbs flavour. 8. now add ¼ tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp coriander and 1 tsp crushed kasuri methi. 9. finally, serve dal fry with hot jeera rice or ghee rice.

title: Dal Fry Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 2.5 cups water, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 1 red chilli, 1.5 green chilies, 1 medium onion, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 tomato, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup water, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Add dal to a pot or cooker. Rinse it well a few times until water runs clear. (optional - To cut down the cook time, soak them for 30 minutes and drain the water.) 2. To cook in pressure cooker: Pour 2½ cups water. Pressure cook on a medium heat for 2 to 3 whistles. When the pressure releases, open the lid and mash the dal slightly. 3. To cook in pot: Add dal to a heavy bottom pot and pour 3 cups water & bring it to a boil. Simmer uncovered until soft & mushy, adding more hot water as needed. To cut down the cooking time, soak it warm water for 30 mins and cook. 4. Heat a pan with ghee, oil or butter. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and dried red chili. When the spices begin to splutter, add hing. 5. Next add onions and green chilies. Saute until the onions turn golden. 6. Add ginger garlic and saute until the raw smell of ginger garlic goes off. This takes about a minute. 7. Then add tomatoes and salt. Saute on a medium flame until tomatoes turn soft. 8. Add red chili powder, garam masala and turmeric. Saute for 2 mins till the masala begins to smell good & leaves the pan. 9. Add the mashed dal & mix well. Pour more water as needed to bring it to a desired consistency. (watch video) 10. Cook until the dal begins to bubble well. When dal fry reaches the desired consistency, crush kasuri methi in your palms and add it. 11. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Garnish with coriander leaves. 12. Serve dal fry with rice, roti, butter naan or paratha. When the temperature comes down slightly squeeze in some lemon juice. 13. Press the SAUTE button instant pot and pour ghee to the steel insert. 14. When the ghee melts, add the cumin, red chilli & mustard (optional). 15. As soon as the spices pop, add hing, green chilli & onions. 16. Saute for 2 mins and then fry ginger garlic for 30 seconds. 17. Add in tomatoes and salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 18. Stir in red chilli powder, turmeric and garam masala. Add rinsed dal and pour 3 cups water. 19. Press CANCEL button. Mix well and scrape the bottom with the spatula to release any bits of food stuck. 20. Optional - You can also place a long legged trivet and then place your rice bowl and cover. 21. Secure the Instant pot with the lid. Set the steam vent to sealing position. 22. Press the PRESSURE COOK button, high pressure and set the timer to 8 minutes. 23. When the IP beeps wait for the natural release for 10 mins. 24. Mash the dal to suit your liking. Then add kasuri methi and coriander leaves. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 25. If you like thinner consistency you can add little hot water and mix. 26. Sprinkle some lemon juice over the dal fry & serve with rice.

title: Dal Maharani Recipe - Restaurant Style
ingredients: 0.75 cup black urad dal, 0.25 cup rajma, 2 tbsp chana dal, 1 inch ginger, 5 cloves garlic, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp butter, 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 tsp cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 javetri, 1 onion, 2 tbsp ginger garlic, 2 chilli, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, pinch hing, 2 tomato, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 2 tbsp cream, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Firstly, wash ¾ cup black urad dal and ¼ cup rajma. 2. Soak in water for at least 5 hours or overnight. 3. Take the soaked dal to the pressure cooker. Add 2 tbsp chana dal, 1 inch of ginger, and 5 cloves of garlic. 4. Also add ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt and 2 tsp oil. 5. Pressure cook for 6 whistles, adding 3 cups of water. 6. Make sure to cook only 90% of the dal, as the remaining cooking will happen in the kadai. 7. In the kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil and 1 tsp butter. Add 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, ½ tsp cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, ½ javetri. Sauté till the spices turn aromatic. 8. Now add 1 onion, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic, and 2 chillies, and sauté till the onions turn golden brown. 9. Keeping the flame low, add 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and pinch hing. Sauté on low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 10. Add 2 more tomatoes and cook until the tomatoes soften and the oil separates from the sides. 11. Add in cooked dal and mix gently. 12. Adjust the salt and consistency. 13. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the dal is cooked completely. 14. Mash the dal slightly to get the desired consistency. 15. Now add 2 tbsp of cream, 2 tbsp of coriander, and 2 tbsp of kasuri methi. Mix well. 16. Finally, top with cream, butter and enjoy Dal Maharani Recipe with rice and roti.

title: Dal Makhani Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup rajma, 4 cups water to pressure cook the dal, 2 tablespoons ghee, 3 tablespoons unsalted butter, 0.5 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste each grated, 1 cup tomato puree, 1.125 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 2 cups water, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.29166666666666663 cup heavy cream, 1 bay leaf 2 green cardamoms 1 black cardamom
instructions: 1. Add urad dal and rajma to a large bowl and rinse them rubbing very well. Drain and repeat the rinse thrice. Soak in 4 cups of water, overnight. Later drain, rinse well and drain again. 2. Pour 4 cups fresh water to the dal and pressure cook for 10 whistles on a medium heat or in the instant pot for 20 mins (high pressure). If you don’t have a cooker, cook in a heavy bottom pot until soft and tender, adding more hot water as needed. 3. Both urad dal and rajma must cook down very soft. When mashed with a spoon the dal and rajma must be soft and get mashed well easily. If not then pour another half cup hot water and continue to cook for another 3 to 4 whistles on a medium heat. 4. Heat a heavy bottom pot with ghee. Add the whole spices if using and let them sizzle for a minute. 5. Stir in the onions and saute until golden, for 7 mins. Stir in ginger garlic paste and saute for 1 minute until aromatic. 6. Pour the tomato puree and cook for 3 to 4 minutes. Stir in salt, chili powder & garam masala. Saute well until the masala smells good and turns thick, for 4 to 5 mins. 7. Mash the cooked urad dal slightly and pour it along with the stock (lentil cooked liquid). Pour 1 cup hot water & mix. 8. Cook on a low flame until the dal becomes thick, stir often in between to prevent burning the bottom. After 60 mins, the dal thickens. 9. Add another ½ to 1 cup hot boiling water and continue to cook until really thick. (This takes another 30 mins. Totally cook for about 90 mins from the time you added the cooked dal to the pot. The cook time may vary depending on your pot and stove) 10. Crush kasuri methi in your palm and add it. Also pour the cream. Stir and cook again for 6 to 8 mins until it becomes thick and creamy. Taste and add more salt or garam masala if needed. Add butter and mix well. 11. Place a small piece of foil or an aluminum foil cup or a tall steel cup on the dal makhani. Hold 1 to 2 inch piece of natural wood charcoal with a tong and burn it on direct fire until red hot, for 3 to 4 mins. Then place it on the foil/cup kept over dal. 12. Quickly pour ¼ tsp ghee on the hot coal. Immediately cover the pot and allow to smoke for 3 to 4 mins. For stronger flavor, add another ¼ tsp ghee on the coal and repeat the smoking. 13. Garnish dal makhani with more butter or cream & serve with fluffy Basmati rice or naan. 14. Heat ghee in the instant pot and add onions. Saute until they turn golden, for about 5 mins. 15. Stir in the ginger garlic paste and saute for 1 minute. 16. Add the tomato puree, red chili powder, garam masala and salt. Saute for 3 to 4 mins and add the soaked & drained lentils. 17. Pour 3 cups water and pressure cook for 30 mins. Wait until the pressure drops, for about 15 mins and open the lid. 18. Mash some of the urad dal, not rajma and press saute mode. Pour 1 cup hot water, mix and continue to cook on saute mode for 12 to 15 mins (switch bet low saute to med saute & vice versa as required) 19. (Optionally if you want you may add 1 more cup hot water & cook for 12 to 15 mins more – I found this way it tastes exactly as the stovetop version) 20. Taste test and adjust salt. Stir in the crushed kasuri methi, cream and more garam masala if you want. Add the butter and turn off.

title: dal pakwan recipe | sindhi dal pakwan | sindhi breakfast recipes
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 4 cup water, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 2 green chilli, few curry leaves, 0.5 TOmato, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, pinch asafoetida hing, 0.75 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp jaggery gud, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 cup wheat flour atta, 1 cup maida plain flour, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom, 0.25 tsp cumin jeera, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp rava semolina suji, 2 tbsp oil, water to knead, oil for deep frying, 2 tbsp green chutney, 2 tbsp tamarind chutney, 0.5 onion, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 2 tsp coriander
instructions: 1. to serve, take chana dal in a bowl and top with 1 tsp of green chutney and tamarind chutney. 2. also top with 2 tsp of chopped onion, a pinch of chaat masala and coriander. 3. finally, enjoy pakwan with chana dal as dal pakwan.

title: Dal Palak
ingredients: 0.5 cup toor dal, 1.25 cup water, 1 tomato, 2 cups palak, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger chopped, 1.5 green chilies, 1 pinch hing, 0.1875 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon Lemon juice
instructions: 1. Add dal to a bowl and rinse it well thrice. 2. Option 1 - Cook dal in a pressure cooker: Transfer to a pressure cooker or a bowl. If using tomato, add it as well and pour water. Pressure cook for 2 whistles on a medium heat or in the instant pot for 10 minutes. When the pressure releases naturally, open the lid and mash or whisk dal well until slightly smooth. 3. Option 2 - Cook in a pot: Add the rinsed dal & tomato (optional) to a sauce pan and pour water. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat and reduce to medium. Cook until mushy, adding more hot water if required. 4. On a medium flame, heat a pan with ghee or oil and add cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add ginger, garlic, red and green chilies. Let them fry for a minute and add hing. 5. Add spinach and saute for 3 to 4 mins on a medium heat, until the leaves wilt & soften. Transfer the cooked dal, salt and turmeric. Mix well and stir in more hot water if you want a slightly loose consistency (to serve with rice). 6. Stir in kasuri methi, taste test and adjust salt. Sprinkle lemon juice and serve with rice or roti. If you want to make a special tadka, check the special tadka section in the post. 7. Add 1 to 1½ tablespoon ghee/butter to a small tadka pan and heat on a medium flame. Stir in a large pinch of mustard seed, ¼ teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 broken dried red chili and 1 sliced garlic. 8. When the mustard seeds begins to splutter, turn off the stove and add 8 to 10 curry leaves (pat dry first). Stir in a pinch of hing and ¼ teaspoon red chili powder. Mix well and pour over the spinach dal. 9. Press SAUTE button on the Instant pot and pour ghee to the steel insert. Add cumin, dried red chilies, garlic/ginger and green chilies. When the cumin seeds splutter, add tomatoes (optional) and saute for 1 minute. 10. Add dal, turmeric and salt. If you prefer to use chilli powder as a substitute to green chilies, add it now. 11. Press CANCEL button. Pour 1½ cups water and deglaze with a spatula to release any bits of food stuck at the bottom. Optionally you can also place a trivet and keep your rice bowl over it & cover. 12. Secure the Instant pot with the lid. Press PRESSURE COOK button (high pressure) and set the timer to 10 mins. Let the pressure release naturally for at least, 15 mins. Release the rest manually. 13. Add the chopped spinach to the dal and give a good mix. Press SAUTE button and cook for 2 mins until the spinach wilts and softens. 14. Press CANCEL and sprinkle kasuri methi (optional). Cover the IP until you serve. This helps spinach to cook completely with the residual heat in the IP. 15. Taste test and add more salt if needed. When the spinach dal cools a bit, add lemon juice. Serve with rice or roti.

title: Dal Palak Recipe | Hotel Style Spinach Dal - Tips & Tricks
ingredients: 0.5 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup moong dal, 0.25 cup toor dal, 0.25 cup masoor dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 4 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 5 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp coriander-cumin powder, 2 tomato, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 bunch palak, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 red chilli, 1 tsp chilli powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker take ½ cup chana dal, ¼ cup moong dal, ¼ cup toor dal and ¼ cup masoor dal. 2. Add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt and 4 cup water. 3. Cover and pressure cook for 5 whistles. 4. Once the pressure is released, check the doneness of the dal. 5. In a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 5 cloves garlic and 1 onion. 6. Saute until the onion shrinks slightly and changes colour. 7. Keeping the flame on low, add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp coriander-cumin powder. 8. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 9. Further add 2 tomato, ½ tsp salt and cook until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 10. Now add 1 bunch palak and saute until the palak shrinks slightly. 11. Further, add in cooked dal, 1 cup water and mix well adjusting the consistency. 12. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 13. Now to prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp ghee. add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 dried red chilli and 1 tsp chilli powder. 14. Do not burn the tempering, immediately pour over the dal and mix well. 15. Finally, enjoy Palak Dal Recipe with roti or rice.

title: dal papdi recipe - crispy & crunchy tea time snack | moong dal ki papdi
ingredients: 0.25 cup moong dal, 0.25 cup urad dal, 2 cup rice flour, 0.75 tbsp cumin, 0.5 tsp pepper, 2 tbsp sesame, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 3 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 2 tsp kasuri methi, water, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, soak ¼ cup moong dal and ¼ cup urad dal in hot water for 10 minutes. 2. drain off the water and transfer to the mixer jar. 3. grind to a coarse paste. keep aside. 4. in a large bowl, take 2 cup rice flour. add ¾ tbsp cumin, ½ tsp pepper, 2 tbsp sesame and 1 tsp salt. mix well. 5. add 2 tbsp hot oil. crumble and mix well making sure the flour is moist. 6. now add prepared dal paste, 3 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves and 2 tsp kasuri methi. 7. start to mix well without adding any water. 8. add water as required and start to knead the dough. 9. form a smooth and soft dough adding water as required. 10. further, take a zip lock bag and grease it with oil. 11. place a small ball sized dough, press and flatten using a cup. 12. flatten as thin as possible. 13. drop the papdi into hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 14. fry both the sides on medium flame until the papdi turns golden and crisp. 15. drain off the papdi onto kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 16. finally, store in an airtight container and enjoy dal papdi up to 3 weeks. 17. finally, store in an airtight container and enjoy dal papdi for up to 3 weeks.

title: dal paratha recipe | dal ka paratha | lentil stuffed paratha recipe
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 ball sized wheat dough, oil for roasting
instructions: 1. firstly, in large vessel take 1 cup chana dal, soaked for 3 hours. 2. boil for 15 minutes or till chana dal turns soft yet retain their shape. 3. drain off the water and keep aside for 30 minutes or till water is drained off completely. 4. transfer the cooked chana dal into a blender and grind to a fine powder. 5. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp aamchur, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp ajwain, ½ tsp salt and 2 tbsp coriander leaves. 6. mix well making sure all the spices are combined well. 7. pinch a ball sized wheat dough and dust with some wheat flour. check pizza paratha recipe to check how to prepare the wheat dough. 8. further, roll it in a circle of about 5 to 5.5 inches in diameter. 9. place a ball sized chana dal stuffing in the centre. 10. take the edge and start pleating bringing to centre. 11. sprinkle some wheat flour and roll slightly thick. 12. on a hot tawa place the rolled paratha and cook both sides brushing oil. 13. finally, serve hot chana dal paratha with sauce, raita or pickle.

title: Dal Soup Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup dal, 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 1 teaspoon ginger, 3 cups water, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrots, 0.6666666666666666 cup pumpkin, 0.3333333333333333 cup tomatoes, 0.3333333333333333 cup zucchini, 1 teaspoon garlic, 0.75 teaspoon red chilli flakes, 0.875 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse the lentils well at least thrice. Drain the water and set aside. 2. Add 1 tsp to tbsp ghee or butter to the pot and melt it on a low heat. Saute ginger for 30 seconds without burning. Next add all the chopped veggies and saute for 3 mins. 3. Transfer the rinsed lentils. Add salt and turmeric. Pour water/stock and deglaze the pot. 4. Cooking in pot: If using a regular sauce pot, let it cook on a medium heat until mushy. Add more hot water accordingly as the lentils cook. It should take 30 to 40 mins. 5. Pressure cooker: Pressure cook for 12 mins on high pressure or for 3 to 4 whistles on a medium heat. 6. Mash with a masher or cool down to blend the cooked ingredients to a smooth soup. (you may use a immersion blender if you want) 7. This step is optional but it sure adds aroma to the dal soup. Add 1 tbsp ghee or butter to a small pan and melt it. Add garlic and saute until a nice aroma comes out for 30 seconds. 8. Add red chilli flakes, cumin and black pepper. Mix with the blended soup. You may adjust the consistency by adding half cup water/stock if you want. 9. Bring the dal soup to a gentle simmer and turn off. 10. When the dal soup cools down a bit add lemon juice and mix well. Taste test and adjust the seasoning as required. 11. Garnish with more red chilli flakes, coriander leaves and black pepper. Serve Indian dal soup hot with crusty bread or quinoa, millets or paratha.

title: Dal Tadka Recipe - Punjabi Dhaba Style
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 0.5 cup moong dal, 2 tomato, 1 onion, 1 cloves garlic, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 2 tsp ghee, 4 cup water, 2 tbsp ghee, 4 cloves garlic, 1 tsp cumin, 2 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp chilli powder, pinch hing, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup toor dal and ½ cup moong dal. make sure to rinse the dal well. 2. add 2 tomato, 1 onion, 10 cloves garlic, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, ½ tsp turmeric, and 1 tsp salt. 3. also, add 2 tsp ghee and 4 cup water. 4. cover and pressure cook for 5 whistles or until the dal is cooked well. 5. mash the dal slightly and adjust consistency as required. 6. to prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp ghee. 7. add 4 cloves garlic and fry for a minute. 8. also add 1 tsp cumin, 2 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp chilli powder, pinch hing. 9. splutter the tempering and pour over the dal. 10. add in 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 11. finally, enjoy cooker dal tadka with jeera rice.

title: Dal Tadka Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 3 cups water, 0.75 cup water, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 medium onion, 1 green chilli, 2 medium tomatoes, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1.5 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 medium lemon, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 red chilies, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. Add dal to a pot or pressure cooker and rinse them very well with lots of water. Rub the lentils well with your hand while you rinse. Drain completely. 2. Pour water and pressure cook the dal on a medium heat for 3 to 4 whistles. To cook in instant pot, pressure cook on high for 10 minutes. To cook in pot check my notes below. 3. When the pressure drops naturally, open the lid. Dal should be soft cooked. 4. Lightly mash keeping some of the dal intact. Add more water only if required to bring to consistency. 5. While the lentils cook, to a medium pan pour oil and heat it. Add cumin seeds to the hot oil. 6. They will begin to splutter, then add ginger garlic and saute on a low heat until fragrant. 7. Add onions and green chilies. Fry them until slightly golden. 8. Reduce the heat. Add red chilli powder, turmeric and garam masala. Give a quick stir. 9. Stir in the tomatoes along with salt. Saute until the tomatoes become soft. The mixture begins to smell good at this stage. 10. Transfer this to the cooked dal and simmer for about 6 to 7 minutes. Taste test and add more salt at this stage. Then add kasuri methi and give the boiling dal a good stir. Add coriander leaves and turn off the heat. 11. Place a tall steel cup in the center of the pot. With a tong hold a wood coal over direct fire like a candle or lighter until a portion of it becomes red and hot. This will take about 2 minutes. 12. Place this gently into the cup and pour ½ teaspoon ghee directly over the hot coal. It will begin to release smoke. Quickly cover the pot and let the dal smoke for 3 to 4 minutes. 13. Transfer the hot cooked lentils to a serving bowl. Pour 2 tbsps ghee to a small pan & heat it on a medium heat. 14. Add cumin seeds and mustard seeds (optional). Let them fry & splutter on a low heat until aromatic. 15. Next add red chilies and give a good stir. Add garlic and curry leaves (optional). Fry until light and aromatic. Do not brown. 16. Stir in asafoetida and red chili powder. Quickly remove from heat and pour the tadka over the dal. 17. Garnish dal tadka with coriander leaves. Squeeze some lemon juice over the dal while serving. Serve with Basmati rice, roti, paratha, naan, jeera rice or ghee rice.

title: dal tikki recipe | channa dal stuffed aloo tikki | tikki dal
ingredients: 0.5 cup chana dal, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 tsp ginger, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp peas, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp aamchur, 0.25 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp salt, 0.75 cup paneer, 2 tbsp coriander, 4 potato, 0.25 tsp corn flour, 2 tbsp besan, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker take ½ cup chana dal. make sure to soak the dal for 1 hour. 2. add 1 cup water, ¼ tsp turmeric and 1 tsp oil. 3. pressure cook for 3 whistles or until the dal is cooked well yet retain their shape. 4. drain off the dal to remove excess water. 5. in a pan heat 2 tsp oil, add ½ tsp cumin, 1 tsp ginger, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp cashew and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 6. also, add 2 tbsp peas, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp aamchur, ¼ tsp chaat masala and 1 tsp salt. 7. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 8. now add ¾ cup paneer and boiled dal. 9. mix well until everything is well combined. 10. further, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 11. finally, transfer to the plate and cool completely. dal stuffing is ready. 12. firstly, in a bowl take 4 boiled potatoes. make sure to boil the potato and cool it completely before using it. 13. add ¼ tsp cornflour, 2 tbsp besan. adding cornflour helps to remove moisture. 14. also add ½ tsp pepper powder, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 15. squeeze and mix until the dough is formed. 16. if the mixture is sticky, add 2 tsp oil and mix gently. 17. aloo mixture is ready to make tikki. 18. firstly, pinch a ball sized aloo mixture and flatten slightly. 19. place a small ball sized dal stuffing. 20. bring together making sure the stuffing is well stuffed. 21. flatten slightly, giving the tikki shape. 22. now fry the tikki on medium flame. you can pan fry or deep fry as per your preference. 23. fry on both sides until it turns golden brown and crispy. 24. drain off to remove excess oil. 25. finally, enjoy stuffed dal tikki with green chutney.

title: Dal Vada Recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup moong dal, 0.25 cup bengal gram, 2 tablespoons urad dal, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 onion, 2.5 green chilies, 2 red chilies, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.625 teaspoon salt, Oil
instructions: 1. Wash and soak all lentils for 2 hours. Drain and rinse them well. 2. Keep aside 2 to 3 tbsp of the soaked dal separately. 3. Pulse red chilies (if using) in the grinder. Then add the rest of the lentils to a grinder. Pulse to a coarse paste without adding any water. 4. Transfer this to a mixing bowl. Add the lentils that we kept aside, garam masala powder, green chilies, ginger garlic paste, curry leaves, coriander, onions and cumin. Mix well. 5. The mixture has to bind well. Check by making a ball and then flatten to see if it holds shape. Otherwise, take about ¼ cup of this mixture to the blender and blend again. Mix everything well. 6. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 7. Heat oil in a deep pan for frying. 8. Pick up small portions, shape them to small balls, and flatten on a greased sheet. You will be able to make 10 vadas out of this. 9. Check if the oil is hot by dropping a small portion of the mixture. It must rise slowly without browning. 10. Transfer the moong dal vada on to your greased fingers and drop in the hot oil gently. You can drop 2 to 3 vadas in one batch, depending on the size of the pan. 11. Fry them on a medium flame. Do not disturb for 2 minutes otherwise they will break. 12. After 2 mins, flip the dal vada around to the other side. Fry till golden and crisp. Remove and drain them on a kitchen tissue. 13. Repeat making vadas and frying till you finish the ground mixture. 14. Serve moong dal vada hot with your favorite chutney or with tea. 15. Preheat air fryer for 10 minutes at 180 c or 360 F for 10 minutes. Place the dal vadas on the rack and spray with oil. I do not spray at this stage. 16. Air fry for 15 to 17 minutes until golden and crisp, if needed turn them to the other side after 10 minutes. Do not handle them when they are still soft. 17. Once done, brush them with ghee and serve hot.

title: dal vada recipe | mix dal vada recipe | dalwada recipe | parippu vada
ingredients: 0.75 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup toor dal, 0.25 cup moong dal, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 tsp salt, oil for frying
instructions: 1. blend the soaked dal, 2 chilli and 1 inch ginger to a coarse paste without adding water. 2. add reserved dal, 2 tbsp curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, pinch hing and 1 tsp salt. squeeze and combine them well. 3. furthermore, grease your hand with oil and prepare small balls, flatten the vada. 4. and deep fry in hot oil. or preheat and bake at 180 degree celcius for 25 minutes or until it turns golden and crisp. 5. also, stir occasionally till until the dal vada turns golden and crisp. 6. finally, serve mix dal vada hot along with masala chai.

title: Dalia Recipe (Daliya)
ingredients: 0.25 cup broken wheat, 0.25 cup moong dal, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chilli, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.75 cup mix veggies chopped optional, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 cups water
instructions: 1. Rinse ¼ cup dalia and ¼ cup moong dal thrice. Soak them for at least 30 mins. If you are using the large size broken wheat, I suggest soaking for at least an hour. You can also dry roast dal and daliya first separately, then cool down and rinse. 2. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee or oil in a cooker on a medium to low heat. Add ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. When they begin to splutter, add a pinch of hing and 1 teaspoon grated ginger. You can also add bay leaf if you like the flavor. 3. Add ½ cup chopped tomatoes, 1 slit green chili (optional), ¼ teaspoon turmeric and ½ teaspoon salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn soft. If using onions, saute onions first until slightly golden, then add tomatoes. You can also skip tomatoes and just use onions. 4. Add ½ teaspoon red chili powder and saute just for a min. You can omit chili powder if using green chili or cut down the powder if making for kids. You can also add ¼ teaspoon garam masala if you like. 5. Add ½ to 1 cup mix veggies like carrots, green beans or peas. I had only capsicum so used that. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. 6. Drain off the water completely from the broken wheat and dal. Add it to the cooker. 7. Saute on a medium high heat for about 2 to 3 mins. 8. Pour 2 cups water, mix well and taste test to adjust salt. 9. Cover and pressure cook on a medium to low flame for 2 whistles. Off the stove and leave it on the stove for a while. When the pressure drops, open the lid and stir. If you want stir in lemon juice and add black pepper. 10. To cook in the instant pot, set to porridge mode and set the timer to 14 mins. Alternately set to pressure cook mode and timer to 12 mins. Serve Daliya hot with pickle or papad. 11. This recipe is healthy if you use jaggery. There are 2 ways you can use them here. One is to add it directly to the slightly cooled dalia. The other way is to dissolve it first and boil for a few minutes. Then cool it completely, filter and stir it with your cooked sweet dalia. 12. If your jaggery is not clean then, you have to melt it first with 3 tbsps of water, bring it to a good boil and cool completely. Set aside and use it later at the last step. Adding hot jiggery to hot dalia will split the milk or curdle it. So ensure it is cooled completely before adding. 13. Add ½ cup dalia and 2 tablespoons moong dal (optional) to a bowl and rinse them very well a few times. Drain the water completely and keep aside. 14. Pour 2 tbsps ghee to a pot and heat it. Add 8 split cashews and fry until golden. Stir in 15 raisins too. When they turn plumpy, remove all of these to a plate. Set aside. 15. To the same pot, add drained daliya and moong dal. 16. Saute and fry until dalia turns golden and aromatic. Keep stirring to avoid burning or browning the dalia. 17. When you begin to get a nice smell, pour 2 cups water and bring it to a boil. To quicken this process, you can also pour hot water to the dalia. 18. Give a good mix. Cover and cook on a low to medium heat until all the water is absorbed and dalia is soft cooked. 19. It will absorb all the water. 20. Pour 1 ½ cups of milk and give a good stir. Mix well and cook until the dalia turns slightly thicker yet is of pouring consistency. 21. When it reaches a desired consistency, add cardamom powder. If using sugar, add it as well and give a good mix until it dissolves. Turn off the stove. To know the consistency, you may check the video above in the recipe card. If using jaggery, remove the pot from the stove and cool down a bit, then stir in the jaggery. I used jaggery here. 22. Add cashews and raisins. Serve sweet dalia warm.

title: Date Balls (Date Nut Seed Balls)
ingredients: 19 medjool dates, 1 cup nuts seeds, 0.625 teaspoon cardamom, 0.25 cup desiccated coconut
instructions: 1. Add 18 to 20 pitted dates to a pan and heat them on a medium flame until hot and soft. Alternately you may heat them in the oven. Pit the dates and spread them on a baking tray lined with parchment paper. Place it in the center of your oven and heat for 7 to 10 mins at 350 F /175 C, until hot. You don’t need to preheat the oven. 2. Toast ¾ cup nuts in a pan until nutty and crunchy. Transfer to a cooling tray. Toast ¼ cup seeds on a medium heat until crunchy and nutty. Cool them completely. Here I used pistachios, 2 tablespoons flax seeds and 2 tablespoons pumpkin seeds. You can use whatever you like. 3. Add the nuts and seeds to a food processor and pulse them until broken. I powder the flaxseeds separately to a fine powder, using a small grinder. We prefer a meal like texture in our date balls so I process them longer. 4. Add the dates, ½ to ¾ teaspoon vanilla extract or ground cardamom and process until the mixture comes together. Taste test and add more dates to make it sweeter or more nuts to make it less sweet. 5. Scoop out 1 heaped tablespoon of the mixture and roll to balls. Roll them in coconut if you want. 6. Store date balls in an air tight jar up to 2 weeks at room temperature or in the refrigerator for a couple of months.

title: Date Syrup Recipe, How to make Date Syrup
ingredients: 1 cup dates, 1 cup water
instructions: 1. Soak dates in boiling hot water for about 2 hours. If using a softer variety like medjol you can soak only for a while. 2. Mash the dates using a masher. Strain through a nut bag or a muslin cloth, placed over a strainer. Squeeze the pulp well to extract the juices. 3. Place the pulp back to the soaking bowl and add more water. Mash up and strain again. 4. Add this water to a heavy bottom pot. Bring it to a boil and reduce the heat. 5. Boil this until the mixture thickens and reaches a thick consistency. It will thicken further upon cooling. 6. When done it becomes glossy and thick. Cool and pour dates syrup to a clean air tight jar. 7. Refrigerate and use date syrup as required on your toasts, cakes, smoothies, ice creams and pancakes.

title: dates ladoo recipe | khajur laduu recipe | dates nuts laddu recipe
ingredients: 1 cup dates khajoor, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 cup almonds badam, 0.5 cup cashew kaju, 2 tbsp dates kishmish, 0.25 cup dry coconut copra, 1 tsp poppy seeds khus khus
instructions: 1. firstly, take 1 cup seedless dates in a blender and blend to coarse paste without adding any water. keep aside. 2. in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp ghee and add ½ cup almonds, ½ cup cashew, 2 tbsp dates and ¼ cup dry coconut. 3. roast on low flame for 5 minutes or till nuts turn crunchy. 4. now add 1 tsp poppy seeds and roast for 2 minutes. 5. add in crushed dates and mix well. 6. continue to fry on medium flame smashing dates with a spatula. this helps dates to separate out and mix uniformly with other dry fruits. 7. furthermore, saute till the dates start releasing oil. 8. turn off the flame and allow to cool for a minute or 5. 9. then immediately start making laddus. do not cool completely, then you wont be able to make laddus. 10. finally, serve immediately or store dates ladoo in airtight container for a month or more.

title: deep fried icecream recipe | icecream pakora | ice cream bhajiya
ingredients: 1 slice bread, 0.5 cup maida, 0.25 cup corn flour, 1 cup water, 1 cup breadcrumbs, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, scoop large-sized ice cream. you can take any flavour of ice cream, however, i have used vanilla ice cream. 2. using a cling wrap try to shape to a round shape. 3. freeze for at least 5 hours or until it turns firm. 4. after 5 hours, you can wrap it with bread. for that trim the sides of bread. 5. flatten slightly using a rolling pin. 6. now brush the sides of the bread with water. this helps to seal tight. 7. now place the ice cream ball in the centre. 8. and cover with another bread slice and seal the sides. you can brush more water if it is difficult to seal. 9. now shape to a round shape and wrap in a cling wrap. 10. freeze for at least 5 hours so that the ice cream and bread are frozen. 11. now to prepare the slurry, in a small bowl take ½ cup maida, ¼ cup cornflour and 1 cup water. 12. prepare a smooth lump-free batter by mixing well. 13. now take the bread-rolled ice cream and dip it in the slurry. 14. immediately roll into the breadcrumbs making sure to coat uniformly. you can double coat the breadcrumbs to get extra crispiness for pakoda. 15. freeze for 5 hours again to set completely. this is the final freezing stage. 16. after 5 hours of freezing, deep fry in hot oil. make sure to use a deep vessel as the whole ice cream ball must be dipped in completely. 17. fry for just 30 seconds and make sure it turns golden brown. over frying will melt the icecream from inside. 18. finally, enjoy fried ice cream with chocolate sauce.

title: desi chinese noodles recipe | desi hakka noodles | desi masala style noodles
ingredients: water, 1 tsp salt, 1 pack hakka noodle, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 3 clove garlic, 2 inch ginger, 2 tbsp spring onion, 1 chilli, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 capsicum, 5 beans, 0.25 cup cabbage, 2 tbsp spring onion
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large vessel take water, 1 tsp salt and get to a boil. 2. now add 1 pack hakka noodle and boil for 2 minutes. refer to the package instructions to know the cooking time. 3. once the noodles cook to al dente, then drain off. 4. rinse with cold water to stop from further cooking. 5. add 1 tsp oil and mix well. this prevents from noodles sticking to each other. 6. firstly, in a small bowl take 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar. 7. also add ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ½ tsp salt. 8. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 9. firstly, in a large wok heat 2 tbsp oil add 3 clove garlic, 2 inch ginger, 2 tbsp spring onion and 1 chilli. 10. stir fry on high flame. 11. now add ½ onion and cook slightly without browning the onions. 12. further add ½ carrot, ½ capsicum, 5 beans and stir fry making sure the vegetables are crunchy. 13. add in boiled noodles, prepared sauce mix and ¼ cup cabbage. 14. mix well combining everything well. 15. also, add 2 tbsp spring onion and give a final mix. 16. finally, enjoy desi noodles when it is still hot.

title: Dibba Rotti
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 1 cup rice rava, 0.5 tsp Cumin, Salt as needed, 3.5 tbsp Oil
instructions: 1. Wash urad dal at least thrice and soak in enough water for 4 hours. 2. If using rice, then rinse it well and drain it to a colander. Allow it to dry completely until the dal is soaked. You can also spread it on a cloth. Then pulse the rice in a blender to get a coarse rice rava texture. If using idli rava, rinse it a few times and drain the water completely. Squeeze excess water with both your palms. Set this aside until the dal is soaked. 3. Drain the water from urad dal and add it to a blender jar. Pour little water and add salt. 4. Grind the dal to thick smooth & frothy consistency. Transfer this to a bowl. 5. Fluff up the rice rava well. Add it to the batter along with cumin and mix well. The consistency of the batter should be thick to get the right crust. 6. Set this aside for about 15 to 45 minutes if you desire. Resting the batter makes the dibba rotti soft from inside. 7. Heat a heavy bottom Kadai or a cast iron pan with oil. Spread the oil all over the kadai to coat it well in oil. 8. When the oil becomes hot pour the batter to the center in the oil and level it with a ladle. Pour the batter till 1½ to 2 inches thickness or height. There should be enough oil in the kadai which spreads around when the batter is poured to the center. 9. Cover and cook on a medium flame till the batter firms up. For even crust, the flame should reach till the edges of the batter under the kadai. 10. Remove the lid and cook till you get a crusty thick golden base. 11. Flip it to the other side and cook till the other side also cooks well and gets a crusty base. 12. Off the flame and leave it in the kadai for about 5 minutes. I move it to a wire rack to retain the crust. Then slice the dibba rotti. 13. If you are left with more batter, repeat adding more oil to the kadai and heat it. When the oil turns hot, pour the batter and make more dibba rotti following the same method. 14. Serve dibba rotti with any chutney or pickle.

title: Dill leaves recipe (shepu bhaji)
ingredients: 25 grams dill leaves, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 garlic, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 0.375 cup moong dal, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. If using moong dal, add it to a bowl and rinse well. Soak it well for a minimum of 30 mins in warm water. Later drain the water before using. 2. Add dill leaves along with the stems to a large pot filled with water. Let them rest for about 5 mins. Repeat the rinse well. Drain to a colander. 3. Pour oil to a pan and heat it. 4. Add mustard, cumin, chana dal and urad dal. Fry them until the dals turn golden. 5. Then add garlic, chilli and curry leaves. Stir fry until garlic turns fragrant. 6. Drain the water from moong dal and add it to the pan. Cover and cook on a low heat until tender. If needed sprinkle water in between. 7. The moong dal must be cooked well but not mushy. This takes only a few minutes as the lentils are presoaked. 8. While the lentils cook, chop the dill as desired. If you do not like to use the stems you may remove them. 9. When the lentils are tender and done, add salt and turmeric. Evaporate any excess moisture in the pan. 10. Add the dill to the pan and stir fry until the leaves wilt. The stems also will turn slightly tender. 11. Serve dill stir fry with rice or roti.

title: Dondakaya Curry | Tindora Curry
ingredients: 25 grams ivy gourd, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 Pinch turmeric, 1 Pinch hing, 2 onions, 1 tomato, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2.5 tablespoons coconut
instructions: 1. Add ivy gourds to a large pot filled with water. Add 2 teaspoons of rock salt and a few tablespoons of vinegar. Let them rest for 5 to 10 mins. Rinse them well under running water. 2. Nip off both the ends and chop them to desired size. An easier way is to slice them in a food processer (with a slicing attachment) or dice them in a onion chopper (with a dicing attachment). 3. Heat oil in a pan, Add mustard and cumin. 4. When the seeds crackle, add curry leaves, green chilies and hing. 5. When the curry leaves turn crisp, add onions and fry until they turn slightly golden. 6. Add tomato, salt and turmeric. Saute until they become mushy and blend well with onions. 7. Add ivy gourd and fry for 2 to 3 mins on a medium high heat. Add red chili powder and saute for another 2 to 3 minutes. 8. Cover and cook on a very low flame stirring occasionally to prevent burning. 9. When the gourd is half cooked, add garam masala and saute for 2 mins. Taste test and adjust salt. 10. Cover and simmer until cooked & tender. Sprinkle coriander leaves and coconut. Stir well. Serve dondakaya curry with rice or chapathi.

title: Dondakaya Fry (Tindora Fry)
ingredients: 25 grams tindora, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon channa dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 2 tablespoons Oil, 1.5 garlic cloves, 1 pinch hing, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 tablespoons peanuts, 0.75 tablespoon chana dal, 0.75 tablespoon urad dal, 0.75 tablespoon coconut, 0.75 tablespoon sesame seeds, 1 red chili, 1 garlic, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds
instructions: 1. Wash 250 grams ivy gourd very well in your preferred way. I rinse them under running water and add them to a bowl filled with water, little vinegar and salt. Let soak for 5 to 10 mins and rinse them well again. Wipe dry with a cloth, chop both the ends and discard. You can slice to thin round slices or lengthwise as shown above. But I simply add them to a processor/chopper and use the pulse button (do not process). 2. Heat 1 to 2 tablespoons oil in a kadai. Add the following ingredients one after the other 3. 1 teaspoon chana dal (soak in hot water for 15 mins & pat dry, if you want) 4. 1 teaspoon urad dal (soak in hot water for 15 mins, if you want) 5. Let fry until the lentils turn golden and aromatic. Add a pinch of hing (optional) and the chopped dondakaya or slices. Also add in ⅛ teaspoon turmeric and ⅓ teaspoon salt. Saute on a medium high flame for 3 mins and cook covered on a low heat until tender and fully cooked through. Keep stirring in between to prevent burning. 6. You will need 2 to 4 tablespoons grated raw coconut here. Or use idli podi , peanut podi or vepudu karam. If you want to make my aromatic vepudu karam make it from the recipe card below. Here I am using raw coconut and 1 tablespoon raw sesame seeds ground coarsely. 7. When the gourd is tender add in the coconut. Mix and fry until it smells good, for 2 mins. 8. Sprinkle ¼ to ½ teaspoon red chilli powder. You can also add some 1 to 2 tbsps roasted skinned peanuts at this stage (optional). 9. Stir fry until the raw smell is gone, for another 2 to 3 mins. Taste test to adjust salt or chili powder. Serve dondakaya fry with hot rice and ghee or with chapati. Dondakaya Curry |... Dondakaya Pachadi... Ladies Finger Fry... Raw Banana Fry (A... Yam fry recipe | ... Beerakaya fry

title: Dondakaya Pachadi
ingredients: 0.25 kg dondakaya, 2 tablespoons peanuts, 5 green chilies, 2 garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon tamarind paste, 1.5 tablespoons Oil, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoob Salt, 1 tablespoons Oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon jeera seeds, 1 Pinch hing, 1 red chili, 0.25 cup mint leaves
instructions: 1. Soak dondakaya in lot of water for 10 mins. Rinse them. Dry them on a cloth, trim the edges. Chop to thin strips or circles. 2. Heat pan with oil. Fry green chilies and garlic for 1 to 2 mins. Set them aside. 3. Fry dals till golden. Set aside. 4. Add salt to dondakaya and fry till they wilt off. 5. Allow to cool. 6. Grind green chilies, and jeera together without water. 7. Add dals and powder, then add dondakaya and tamarind paste or lemon juice. Grind to smooth or coarse, do not add water. 8. Adjust salt if needed. 9. Add little oil to a pan and add mustard, cumin, curry leaves and red chili. When they splutter sprinkle hing. Switch off the stove and add it to the chutney. 10. Season dondakaya pachadi and serve with dosa / rice/ roti. 11. We like to use pudina for dondakaya pachadi, if you like to use it fry ¼ cup of pudina leaves after frying the dals.

title: donut recipe | chocolate donut recipe | chocolate doughnut recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup milk, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp dry yeast, 2 cups maida plain flour all purpose flour refined flour, 0.25 tsp baking powder, 2 tbsp butter, pinch of salt, 0.5 cup water, oil to grease deep fry, 1 cup powdered sugar, 0.25 cup cocoa powder, 1 tsp vanilla extract essence, 3 tbsp milk
instructions: 1. firstly, activate yeast by adding milk and sugar. 2. now add maida, baking powder, butter and salt. 3. combine and knead to smooth dough adding water as required. 4. grease, cover and rest for 1-2 hours. 5. punch the dough and roll slightly thick using rolling pin. 6. now with the help of donut cutter, cut round. 7. allow to rest in warm place for 2 hours. 8. deep fry in medium hot oil to golden brown. 9. dip the doughnuts and serve chocolate donuts sprinkled with sugar crystals.

title: dosa kurma recipe | kurma for dosa | instant kurma for idli & dosa
ingredients: 1 tbsp oil, 1 inch ginger, 2 clove garlic, 0.5 onion, 1 tomato, 0.5 coconut, 3 red chilli, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp poppy seeds khus khus, 1 tsp fennel saunf, 1 tbsp putani roasted gram dal, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 3 pod pod cardamom, few curry leaves, 0.5 onion, 1 chilli, 0.5 tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tbsp oil and saute 1 inch ginger and 2 clove garlic. 2. add ½ onion and saute until it softens. 3. further add 1 tomato and saute until it turns soft and mushy. 4. now add ½ coconut, 3 dried red chilli, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp poppy seeds, 1 tsp fennel and 1 tbsp putani. 5. saute for a minute until it turns aromatic. 6. cool completely and transfer to the blender. 7. blend to smooth paste adding ½ cup water. keep aside. 8. now in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute ½ inch cinnamon, 3 pod cardamom, few curry leaves. 9. add ½ onion, 1 chilli and saute until it softens slightly. 10. further add ½ tomato and saute until it turns soft and mushy. 11. add in prepared masala paste, ¼ tsp turmeric and ¾ tsp salt. 12. saute for 2 minutes or until masala turns aromatic. 13. now add 3 cup water or as required adjusting the consistency. 14. simmer and boil for 5 minutes. 15. also add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 16. finally, enjoy dosa kurma recipe with dosa, idli or chapathi.

title: Dosa Recipe | 7 Pro Tips South Indian Dosa Batter Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup chana dal, 1 tsp methi fenugreek seeds, 3 cup sona masuri rice, 1 cup poha, water, 2 tsp salt, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup urad dal, ¼ cup chana dal and 1 tsp methi. 2. Rinse with water and soak for 3 hours. 3. In another bowl rinse and soak 3 cup sona masuri rice for 5 hours. 4. Drain off the urad dal and transfer to the grinder. you can use mixi if you are comfortable. 5. Grind to smooth paste adding water as required. 6. After grinding for 30 minutes, the batter will turn soft and fluffy. 7. Scoop out urad dal batter and keep aside. 8. In the same grinder add soaked rice and 1 cup rinsed poha. 9. Also, add water as required and grind the batter. 10. Grind to a slightly coarse batter. if you grind to a very smooth paste, then the dosa will be very soft and not crisp. 11. Transfer the batter to the same vessel of urad dal batter. 12. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 13. Cover and allow to ferment for 8 hours in a warm place. 14. After 8 hours, the batter would have doubled indicating it is well fermented. 15. Now mix the batter gently and use the batter to prepare dosa adding salt as required. 16. Finally, the dosa batter can be stored in the refrigerator for up to a week and used to make weekly breakfast.

title: Dosa Recipe with homemade Dosa Batter
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 2 tablespoons chana dal, 1.5 cups raw rice, 2 tablespoons poha, 0.5 teaspoon methi seeds, 1.25 cups cold filtered water, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt
instructions: 1. Rinse together urad dal, chana dal and methi seeds thrice, draining out the water after each rinse. Soak in 3 cups water for 5 to 6 hours. 2. Rinse rice thrice and soak in 3 cups water for 5 to 6 hours. (If you have a high speed blender you may rinse and soak them together, read faqs) 3. Rinse and soak poha in ¼ cup water for 30 mins, before blending the batter. 4. Drain water from dal & transfer to a blender jar. Also add soaked poha and ¾ to 1 cup water (start with lesser). Grind to a smooth & bubbly batter. Transfer this to a large bowl or an instant pot steel insert. 5. Drain water from rice & add it to the jar. Pour ½ cup water and blend to a smooth or slightly coarse batter. 6. Transfer to the dal batter bowl and mix well. (Check video to see the final consistency). During winters you may add sea salt & mix at this stage to help with fermentation. 7. Cover the bowl loosely and ferment the batter in a warm place overnight, up to 12 hours until it rises, turns light & bubbly. 8. During colder season, ferment the batter in oven at 100 F/ 40 C for 3 hours & leave it overnight there with the oven light ON. Alternately, place the bowl in the IP or the instant pot steel insert, press yogurt settings with a timer set to 8 hours (12 to 14 hours during colder days). Use an external lid and not the IP lid. 9. Optional Fermentation test: Well fermented dosa batter rises & increases in volume. It looks airy, with plenty of tiny bubbles.
