To test, drop half a spoon of this into a bowl of water. Well fermented batter floats. If it has not reached this stage, ferment longer. 10. Stir the dosa batter once. Transfer the required portion to a small bowl and add salt as required. Refrigerate the rest for up to 2 weeks. 11. The fermented batter usually becomes thick, pour little water to thin down only needed & bring it to a spreadable consistency. (Check video or photos in the post) 12. Heat a dosa tawa on a medium heat. When it is slightly hot, add a few drops of oil and spread it with a kitchen paper or a half cut onion (pierced with a fork/ butter knife on the rounded side to hold).This prevents the dosas from sticking to the pan. 13. To check if the pan is hot enough, sprinkle a few drops of water over the pan. If it is ready, it should sizzle. 14. Reduce the heat to medium, stir the batter with a ladle and take a ladle (¼ cup) of dosa batter & pour in the center of the hot tawa. 15. Immediately begin to spread it evenly with the base of the ladle – starting from the center, in an outward circular motion in a clockwise direction. (Check photos or video in the post) 16. Increase the heat to medium high or high and drizzle oil/ghee/ butter across the edges. 17. Cook until the base turns golden & crisp. Run a thin wooden turner/spatula across the base of the dosa, starting from the edges to the center. 18. Optional – If you want you may cook on the other side – turn it to the other side and cook for a minute. Turn again and cook the base for 30 seconds to crisp it. 19. Fold the dosa and remove from pan. Before making the next dosa, reduce the heat to low. You can also rub the cut onion and then pour the batter. 20. Serve dosa with coconut chutney, potato masala & tiffin sambar.

title: Dosakaya Pachadi | Cucumber Chutney
ingredients: 1 cucumber, 4 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.5 garlic, 1.5 tablespoons Lemon juice, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 red chili, 1 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon channa dal, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 tablespoon chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal
instructions: 1. Wash cucumber and peel off the skin. 2. Cut it to 2 large pieces. Remove the seeds and discard. Check if the cucumber is bitter. 3. Fry green chilies in 1 tsp oil for 1 to 2 min. Cool them. If using chana dal and urad dal, fry them too until golden & aromatic in the same pan. Cool completely. 4. Add green chilies, dal, cumin, salt and garlic to a grinder jar and make a fine powder. 5. Then add 2 tbsp of the chopped cucumber pieces and tamarind paste to the same grinder jar. Grind them to a fine chutney. 6. Add the blended mix with the rest of the cucumber pieces. Then add salt and chopped coriander leaves. Mix well. 7. Heat the same pan with the remaining oil. 8. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and red chili. 9. When the seeds pop, add chana dal and urad dal. Fry till the dals turn golden. 10. Next add the curry leaves and hing. 11. Pour this to the dosakaya chutney, check salt and spice. If needed you can add lemon juice or more tamarind paste. 12. Serve cucumber chutney as a side with rice, roti or dosa.

title: Dosakaya Pappu
ingredients: 0.75 cup dal, 2.25 cups water, 1.5 to 2 cups dosakaya, 1 onion, 1 medium tomato, 2 green chilies, salt as needed, 2 garlic, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1.5 tbsp ghee, 1 red chili, 0.5 tsp red chili powder, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 1 sprig curry leaves, pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. Wash dal few times till the water runs clear. 2. Wash and peel Cucumber / dosakaya. Cut to quarters, remove the seeds and discard. Chop and add them to the cooker. 3. Add dal, cucumber, sliced onion, chopped tomato and green chili to the cooker. Pressure cook for 1 to 3 whistles depending on the kind of cooker you use. 4. If using tamarind, soak it in 4 tbsps water and extract thick juice. 5. When the pressure releases, Add salt and mash the dal. 6. Heat ghee or oil in a pan, add red chili, mustard and cumin. 7. When they crackle, add garlic and curry leaves. Allow the curry leaves to turn crisp. 8. Reduce the flame to low. Then add hing, chili powder, turmeric and salt to the hot ghee. 9. If using tamarind, you can pour the tamarind juice to the pan and quickly pour everything to the dal. 10. Stir and cook for a while until the raw smell of chili powder goes off. Dosakaya pappu is ready to serve with rice or chapathi.

title: Dragon Chicken (Indo-Chinese Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 15 Kashmiri dried red chilies, 0.25 cup water, 25 grams chicken, 0.5 tablespoon garlic, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 1 teaspoon red chili paste, 1 teaspoon vinegar, 1 teaspoon soya sauce, 0.125 teaspoon sea salt, 1 egg white, 4 tablespoons cornstarch, 2 tablespoon all-purpose flour, 0.75 cup high smoke point oil, 2.5 tablespoons red chili paste, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 2.5 tablespoons Maggi tomato sauce, 1.5 teaspoons vinegar, 1.5 teaspoons sugar, 0.25 cup water, 2 teaspoons cornstarch, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.375 cup roasted unsalted cashews, 2 red chilies, 0.5 cup onion, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1 teaspoon tender coriander stems, 0.5 cup bell pepper, 1 spring onion
instructions: 1. Deseed dried chilies and soak in boiling hot water, for about 15 to 20 mins. Drain the water and add them to a grinder. Blend with fresh water to a smooth paste. This makes 4 tablespoons chili paste. 2. Cut chicken to thin stirps of ½ inch by 2 to 3 inch and add to a mixing bowl. 3. Add salt, ginger garlic paste, soya sauce, black pepper or chili paste, vinegar (omit to use sambal oelek). Mix well and cover. Rest for 20 mins while you prepare the other ingredients. 4. In a small bowl, stir soya sauce, maggi tomato sauce, sugar, red chili paste, vinegar (omit to use sambal oelek). Taste test to adjust salt and vinegar to your preference. Optionally for a slightly saucy version - In another small bowl, stir together cornstarch and water. 5. On a medium high flame, heat oil in a wok to temper. Add red chills, white scallions, ginger, garlic, onions, coriander stems and bell peppers. Stir fry on a high heat for 2 to 3 mins. 6. Stir in the prepared dragon sauce and hot water. Let cook on a medium high heat until thick and glossy. 7. Optional - Pour the cornstarch slurry (make sure you mix it first with a spoon). Bring to a boil and let simmer for a minute, until thick and bubbling. 8. Taste test and adjust sauce to taste. It should be prominently hot, with a slight hint of soya and faintly sour (from tomato sauce). Turn off and keep it warm. 9. When you are ready to fry, on a medium flame heat oil in a deep pan. 10. Stir in the egg white and coat the chicken well. Mix together both the flour and cornstarch. Sprinkle evenly over the chicken and coat it well. If it is too dry, add a splash of water or egg. But avoid making the batter runny. 11. Test if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of batter to the hot oil. It should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning too brown. 12. Gently slide the chicken pieces one by one to the hot oil. Do not overcrowd the pan. Fry without disturbing them for 2 mins. Stir and fry further for another minute, until they turn evenly golden and crisp. 13. Remove to a wired rack or a steel colander and fry the remaining marinated chicken in batches. Optionally, flash fry (refry) the chicken on a medium high heat just for a minute or 2. Overdoing can make the chicken chewy. 14. Cool down slightly for 1 to 2 mins. 15. Add the fried chicken and roasted cashews. Mix to coat with the dragon sauce. Garnish with spring onion greens. 16. Serve immediately as an appetizer/ snack or side with fried rice or noodles or soup.

title: Drumstick Curry (Drumstick Vegetable Recipe)
ingredients: 2.5 drumsticks, 2.5 tbsps oil, 2 onions, 2 tomatoes, 1 green chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 garlic cloves, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5416666666666666 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 cup water
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a deep pan. When the oil turns hot add mustard & cumin. They will begin to splutter, then add curry leaves, garlic and green chili. Saute until the leaves turn crisp. 2. Add the onions, sprinkle salt and fry till they turn transparent. You don’t need to brown the onions but the raw smell should be gone. 3. Add drumsticks & fry for 2 to 3 mins to remove the raw smell. 4. Add tomatoes, some more salt &turmeric. Fry till the tomatoes become completely soft and pulpy. Do not cover and cook but fry in a open pan as it tastes better. 5. Add red chili powder & coriander powder(optional). Saute well until the raw smell of the chilli powder goes away. 6. Pour water just enough to cover the drumsticks. Stir well. 7. Cook on a medium flame till they are thoroughly cooked. If you have enough time, try cooking on the lowest heat for a long time. I sometimes do that, it yields a very tasty curry. Serve drumstick curry with rice or chapathi. 8. You can also make a variation to this by adding boiled eggs. When the drumsticks are almost cooked but not fully, prick boiled eggs with a fork and add them here. Cook till the drumsticks are completely done. You can also fry the eggs in 1 tsp oil and then add them here If you are new to cooking, then the following tip may help you: 9. Choosing drumsticks: Always choose young and fresh looking drumsticks. The veggies that are harvested long time ago will usually dry up and it is harder to cook them. They will also lack the flavor.

title: Drumstick Leaves Stir Fry | Moringa Leaves Recipe
ingredients: 3 potatoes, 2 cups drumstick leaves, 3 garlic cloves, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 3.5 green chilies, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 1 red chili, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon Oil, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon cumin, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Separate the leaves from the stalks and wash thoroughly in enough water, drain the leaves in a colander to remove water thoroughly. 2. Option 1 - Boil potatoes al dente. I usually boil them in the instant pot for 7 to 9 minutes depending on the size. The potatoes must be just cooked and not mushy, Once done, peel the skin and cube them. 3. Option 2 - You can also simply stir fry the potatoes first instead of boiling. For this cube the potatoes to ½ to ¾ inch pieces and keep them immersed in a bowl of water until used. When you are ready to add them to the pan, drain the water and rinse them once. 4. Heat a pan with oil. Add cumin, mustard, dal, garlic and red chili. Fry till the dal turn golden. Add curry leaves and green chilies fry for 1 to 2 mins. 5. Add hing, boiled potatoes, salt, turmeric and fry for 3 to 4 mins. If using raw potatoes, stir fry them on a medium heat for 2 to 3 mins. Cover and cook on a low heat until fork tender. If required you may splash little water to help them cook faster. 6. Increase the flame to medium high. Add the moringa leaves and fry till they wilt off. Keep stirring and fry. If the leaves are not tender, you may cover and cook. 7. Serve drumstick leaves stir fry with rice and sambar or rasam.

title: Drumstick Sambar
ingredients: 0.5 cup Toor dal, 2 tablespoons moong dal, 1.5 cups water, 3 vegetable drumsticks, 1 shallots, 1 tomato, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 tablespoons ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 to 2 tablespoons sambar powder, tamarind, 2.5 tablespoons coconut, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1 Handful Coriander leaves, 2.5 cups water, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 1 clove garlic, 2 tablespoons ghee, 2 Pinches asafoetida
instructions: 1. Add dal to a pressure cooker or bowl. Wash it well a few times and pressure cook with 1½ to 2 cups water. If cooking directly cook for 2 whistles. If following the pot-in-pot method cook for 3 whistles on a medium flame. 2. When the pressure releases naturally, remove the cooked dal and mash it well. It has to be smooth without any grainy texture. 3. Pour 2 to 3 cups of water in a pot and add all the chopped drumsticks, onion & tomatoes. Cook them until the drumsticks are half done. You can add more or less water to adjust the consistency. 4. While the veggies cook, take half a cup of hot water to a bowl. Soak tamarind in it. When it cools down squeeze well , and set aside. 5. When the drumsticks are half cooked, add salt, turmeric, red chili powder, sambar powder and ginger garlic paste. Continue to cook till the drumsticks are fully done & tender. 6. Next add coconut and tamarind water. Mash the dal to smooth and pour to the sambar. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt and tamarind. Bring to a gentle boil and turn off. Add coriander leaves. 7. Add ghee to a small pan and heat it. Add mustard, cumin and methi. When they splutter, add red chilli curry leaves and garlic. Add hing. 8. Pour this tempering over the drumstick sambar. Turn off the stove. If you plan to refrigerate, do it immediately once it cools down. Serve drumstick sambar with rice, papad or idli dosa.

title: Drumstick Soup Recipe | Healthy Zero Oil Moringa Soup
ingredients: 3 drumstick, 3 tbsp masoor dal, 1 tomato, 0.5 onion, 2 cloves garlic, 2 chilli, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp cloves, 1 tsp pepper, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 0.5 tsp kala namak, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp mint
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a cooker, add 3 drumsticks and 3 tablespoons of masoor dal. 2. Also, add 1 tomato, ½ onion, 2 cloves of garlic, and 2 chillies. 3. Add 1 inch cinnamon, ½ tsp cloves, 1 tsp pepper, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin and 1 tsp salt. 4. Pour 3 cups of water, and pressure cook for 5 whistles. 5. Drain off and transfer the drumstick to the mixer jar. 6. Add water and grind to extract the pulp. 7. Filter out, extracting the pulp easily. 8. Now, add the drumstick pulp water to the kadai. 9. Add water and adjust the consistency. Also, add ½ tsp kala namak and bring to a boil. 10. Now, in a mixer jar, add 2 tbsp of coriander and 2 tbsp of mint. Grind to a smooth paste. 11. Transfer the coriander mint paste to the soup and mix well. 12. Do not over-boil as it will lose its flavour. 13. Finally, enjoy the Drumstick Soup Recipe piping hot.

title: Dry chana recipe
ingredients: 2 cups chickpeas, 0.5 tsp red chili powder, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 green chili, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. Wash and soak chana over night in lot of water. 2. Drain off the water and add them to a cooker or pot. Add water just enough to immerse them. Add 1 pinch soda-bi-carbonate (optional), turmeric (optional) and cook until soft or pressure cook for 1 whistle. If you skip soda, chickpeas will take longer. So pressure cook for 6 to 7 whistles. 3. Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and mustard seeds (optional). When they splutter add green chili, hing and curry leaves. Add ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell goes off just for a minute or two. 4. Add cooked chana, red chili powder, turmeric, salt and garam masala. I also added a bit of kitchen king masala. Saute everything well for a minute. 5. Pour little water or the stock left after cooking channa. Cook for a while for the masala to coat the chana well. Add coriander leaves. 6. Dry chana or sukhe chole is ready to serve with rice. You can add a tsp of olive oil or ghee for health benefits just before serving. 7. 2 cups chickpeas (cooked, or 1 cup dry chana) 8. 1 green chili slit or chopped 9. Soak chana overnight or 8 hours. Drain and rinse them well. 10. Add them to a pressure cooker or pot along with turmeric. To cook them faster, you can also add a pinch of soda-bi-carbonate. This is optional. 11. If using pressure cooker , pour 1 cup water. If not using soda, pressure cook for 6 to 8 whistles on a medium heat. With soda, pressure cook just for 1 to 2 whistles. If cooking in a pot, start with 2 cups water. Add more if needed as you cook them. 12. They are ready when they are completely cooked, soft and tender. Chana should not be al dente but should be soft enough yet hold their shape. 13. Heat the pan with oil, add mustard, jeera, chili, curry leaves & hing. Let them splutter. 14. Add ginger garlic paste and saute quickly to remove the raw smell. Add the cooked channa. 15. Add garam masala, salt, turmeric and red chili powder. Mix well. Saute for a minute. Any left over water from the cooked chickpeas can be poured and evaporated. 16. Taste test and add more spices and salt to your taste. Transfer dry chana to a serving bowl. Squeeze some lemon juice and serve as a side. 17. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > title: Dry Chicken Ghee Roast
ingredients: 2 tablespoons ghee, 3 sprigs curry leaves, 2 cloves garlic, 0.5 tablespoon black pepper, 0.5 kg chicken, 1.5 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon mint leaves, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoon Garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. To a pot, add chicken along with the ingredients mentioned under to marinate. 2. Mix everything well and cook on a low flame till the chickn is tender and cooked completely. 3. If you find moisture in the pot, Cook on a slightly high flame and evaporate it. Set this aside for about 30 to 45 minutes. 4. Heat a pan with ghee. Add chopped curry leaves and garlic. Fry until the curry leaves turn crisp. You can set aside few leaves for garnish if you want. 5. To the pan, add the cooked chicken and begin to roast until all of the moisture dries up. 6. When the masala gets roasted well but not burnt, sprinkle black pepper and salt if needed. 7. Fry till you get a nice an aroma of pepper chicken ghee roast, for about 2 mins. The dish is done when the masala becomes aromatic. Switch off the flame. 8. Garnish with curry leaves. Serve dry chicken ghee roast with rice and rasam or with roti. Squeeze some lemon juice if you want.

title: Dry Fruit Barfi Recipe | Sugar Free Khajoor Dates Barfi
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee, 0.5 cup almonds, 0.5 cup cashew, 0.25 cup walnuts, 3 tbsp pumkin seeds, 3 tbsp pistachios, 2 tbsp sunflower seeds, 1.5 cup dates khajoor, 1 tsp ghee, 2 tbsp anjeer, 2 tbsp raisins, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tsp ghee. add ½ cup almonds, ½ cup cashew, ¼ cup walnuts, 3 tbsp pumkin seeds, 3 tbsp pistachios, and 2 tbsp sunflower seeds. 2. roast on low flame until the nuts turn crunchy. keep aside. 3. in a mixer grinder take 1.5 cup dates. 4. pulse and grind to a smooth paste. 5. in a large kadai heat 1 tsp ghee, add 2 tbsp anjeer, 2 tbsp raisins. 6. roast on low flame until they turn aromatic. 7. add in ground dates paste and cook on medium flame. 8. mash and cook until the dates paste turn glossy. 9. further add roasted nuts, ½ tsp cardamom powder, and mix well. 10. combine until everything is well combined and starts to hold shape. 11. cool slightly, and shape into a small ladoo. 12. or you can roll over poppy seeds and shape them into a cylindrical log. 13. wrap in aluminum foil and refrigerate for 2 hours. 14. finally, cut into thick slices and enjoy dry fruit burfi for a month.

title: dry fruit kheer recipe | khajoor ki kheer | mewa ki kheer
ingredients: 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tsp ghee clarified butter, 0.75 cup milk, 1 cup milk powder, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 5 cashew, 5 almonds, 2 tbsp raisins, 1 pistachios, 5 dates, 2 tbsp charoli chironji, 1.5 litre milk, 0.25 tsp saffron, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in pan dry roast 2 tbsp cashew and 2 tbsp almonds. 2. roast until the nuts turn crunchy. 3. cool completely, and blend to a coarse powder. keep aside. 4. firstly, in a pan take 2 tsp ghee, ¾ cup milk and 1 cup milk powder. 5. stir and mix well making sure there are no lumps. 6. continue to cook on low flame without burning. 7. keep stirring until the mixture thickens and separates from the pan. 8. instant mawa is ready, you can use immediately or refrigerate and use up to a week. 9. firstly, in a large kadai take 1½ litre milk and ¼ tsp saffron. 10. keep stirring and get the milk to a boil. 11. once the milk comes to a boil, add powdered nuts powder. 12. stir well and boil for 5 minutes. 13. further, add prepared mawa and mix well. you can alternatively use 1 cup store brought khova too. 14. mix well and simmer for 15 minutes. 15. now add ½ cup sugar and boil until the sugar dissolves. 16. get the kheer to a rolling boil or until the kheer turns creamy texture. 17. to roast the dry fruits, heat 2 tbsp ghee. 18. add 5 cashew, 5 almonds, 2 tbsp raisins, 10 pistachios, 5 dates and 2 tbsp charoli. 19. saute until the nuts turn crunchy. 20. pour in fried nuts and add ½ tsp cardamom powder. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 21. finally, enjoy dry fruits kheer recipe topped with more nuts.

title: Dry Fruit Laddu
ingredients: 0.25 cup cashews, 0.25 cup almonds, 0.25 cup pista, 8.5 dates, 7 apricots, 4 figs, 2 tbp dried coconut, 1 tsp poppy seeds, 1.5 tbsps sesame seeds
instructions: 1. On a medium heat dry roast almonds and cashews until slightly aromatic. Add pistas and fry for another 2 mins. Turn off the stove. You can also do this in oven. Dry roast nuts in microwave for 1 minute on high. 2. Remove to a plate to cool. Add dates, apricots and figs to the hot pan and just stir them until slightly hot. This is done for shelf life. You can also skip this to prevent slight loss of nutrition. 3. If using seeds, toast them until they turn aromatic. Set these aside. 4. When all of them cool down. Add dates, apricots and figs to a blender along with pistachios. Blend them to a slightly coarse mixture. Pieces of pista in the mixture is just fine. Please use steel or glass blender jars. You can also use a food processor. 5. Remove to a plate and add the cashews and almonds. Pulse them a few times to get coarse mixture. If your blender doesn't have sharp blades then it is good to chop and add them. 6. Remove the entire blended mixture to a plate. If using seeds add them now and just knead or mix well. 7. Divide the mixture to 12 portions and roll to balls. Do not roll them too tight as the ladoo will turn hard. Gently shape them. 8. For garnishing - roll them in fine chopped pistachios or desiccated coconut or toasted poppy seeds or sesame seeds. 9. Store dry fruit laddu in airtight jar.

title: Dry Fruit Laddu Recipe | No Sugar Healthy Dry Fruits Ladoo
ingredients: 0.25 cup ghee, 1 cup badam, 0.5 cup cashew, 0.25 cup pistachios, 0.5 cup raisins, 6 anjeer, 3 tbsp pumpkin seeds, 3 tbsp sunflower seeds, 2 tbsp sesame, 1 tbsp poppy seeds, 1 cup dry coconut, 35 grams dates
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 1 tsp ghee. roast 1 cup badam, ½ cup cashew and ¼ cup pistachios. 2. Roast until the nuts turn crunchy. Keep aside. 3. In the same pan heat 1 tsp ghee, add ½ cup raisins and 6 anjeer. 4. Dry roast 3 tbsp pumpkin seeds, 3 tbsp sunflower seeds, 2 tbsp sesame, 1 tbsp poppy seeds, and 1 cup dry coconut. 5. Roast until the seeds turn crunchy. Keep aside. 6. Mix all the roasted nuts and seeds well. Keep aside. 7. In a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee. add 350 grams dates and cook on low flame. 8. After 5 minutes, the dates turn soft and mushy. Mash the dates until it turns paste texture. 9. Now add in roasted nuts and mix well. 10. Mix until everything is well combined. 11. Cool slightly, and start shaping into ladoo. 12. Finally, Dry Fruits Ladoo is ready to enjoy for up to a month.

title: Dry fruits milkshake recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsps pistas, 1 almonds, 1 cashews, 2 figs, 3 dates, 0.375 tsp cardamom powder, 2.25 cups milk
instructions: 1. Add cashews, almonds and pistas to a large bowl. You can substitute any of these nuts with the other. I used about 14 almonds, 10 cashews and 3 tbsps pistas. It gives a thick shake and we love it that way thick and creamy. If you like it slightly thin, then reduce the nuts. 2. Add dried figs to the bowl. Rinse them well before using if they are not preservative free. 3. Pour water just enough to soak them. I used about 1 cup. 4. Soak them at least for 45 to 60 mins. If making for toddlers, soak them for 3 to 4 hrs. If you prefer cold shake, then do chill the milk for few hours. 5. Drain the water. Peel and discard the almond skin. I also partially peel the pistas. 6. Add all of them to the blender. Add chopped dates. I used 3 large medjool dates which came up to 1/4 cup after chopping. 7. Add cardamom powder. You can skip but it helps in digestion and is cooling for the body. Also it flavors the milkshake. 8. Pour milk just enough to cover the nuts and fruits. I used about 3/4 cup. Adding all the milk at once leave lot of nut chunks, so I add only a little at this stage. 9. Blend it to a smooth puree. If needed you can also add more milk. 10. Pour rest of the milk. Blend until frothy and thick. 11. You can add more milk to make it thin. Serve dry fruits milkshake immediately. 12. Soak almonds, pistas, cashews and figs in enough water for at least 45 mins to 1 hour. Soaking gives a creamy taste to the shake. If you prefer cold shake, then chill the milk in the fridge. 13. Peel off the almond skin. Drain the water and add all of these to a blender jar. 14. Add cardamom powder and chopped dates. 15. Pour 1 cup milk and blend them very well to a smooth mixture. 16. Add more milk and blend again.

title: dry garlic chutney recipe | dry garlic powder | dry garlic chutney powder
ingredients: 1 tbsp oil, 0.25 cup garlic, 1 tbsp peanut, 1 tbsp sesame til, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.25 tsp methi fenugreek, 0.25 cup dry coconut, 1 tbsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tbsp oil and add ¼ cup garlic. 2. saute on low flame until it turns golden brown. 3. now add 1 tbsp peanut and saute till peanuts turn crunchy. 4. further, add 1 tbsp sesame, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp coriander seeds and ¼ tsp methi. 5. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 6. now add ¼ cup dry coconut and saute well. 7. cool completely and transfer to the blender. 8. add 1 tbsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp aamchur, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 9. blend to coarse powder without adding water. 10. finally, dry garlic chutney is ready to enjoy with vada pav or batata vada.

title: Dry Gulab Jamun Recipe | Dry Kala jamun with Milk Powder
ingredients: 2 cup milk powder, 0.25 cup maida, 0.5 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp ghee, 0.5 cup milk, 2.5 cup sugar, 1 cup water, saffron, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl, take 2 cups of milk powder, ¼ cup of all-purpose flour (maida), ½ tsp of baking powder, and 1 tsp of ghee. 2. Crumble and mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined. 3. Now add ½ cup milk and mix using a spatula. 4. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes, or until the milk is well absorbed. 5. To prepare a sugar syrup, take 2½ cups of sugar and 1 cup of water. 6. Also, add a few saffron and boil for 5 minutes. 7. Do not attain string consistency; make a sticky sugar syrup. 8. Add ½ tsp cardamom powder and 2 tsp lemon juice. Mix well and keep aside. Lemon juice prevents sugar syrup from crystallising. 9. After 10 minutes of resting the dough, start preparing small, ball-sized jamuns. 10. Make sure there are no cracks in the jamun. If there are cracks then there are high chances for jamuns to break while frying. 11. Deep fry in medium-hot oil or ghee. Frying in ghee gives a good flavour to jamuns. 12. Stir continuously and fry on a low flame. Fry until the jamuns turn black. 13. Drain off the excess oil and transfer the jamun into a hot sugar syrup. 14. Cover and rest for 2 hours, or until the jamuns have absorbed the sugar syrup and doubled in size. 15. Drain off the Kala Jamun and roll into dessicated coconut. 16. Finally, enjoy the Dry Jamun Recipe as it is. 17. Make sure there are no cracks in the jamun. If there are cracks then there are high chances for jamuns to break while frying. Deep fry in medium-hot oil or ghee. Frying in ghee gives a good flavour to jamuns.

title: Dum Aloo Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 0.5 kg potatoes, 1.25 cup onions, 1 cup tomatoes, 12 cashew nuts, 2.5 tablespoon oil, 1 green chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 cup curd, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 bay leaf, 2 green cardamom, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.875 teaspoon kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.25 teaspoon fennel seeds powder, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi
instructions: 1. I have used 450 to 500 grams baby potatoes, but you can also use large potatoes. Wash, peel & cube them to 1½ inch. If using uneven sized baby potatoes, cut the large size ones to make all of them the same size. This way they cook evenly. 2. Bring 3 to 4 cups water to a boil in a pot. Then add the potatoes and boil them till half cooked. To check, prick the potato with a fork. You should feel it hard but not very hard. Remove them from water and cool completely. Also ensure there is no water over them. 3. This step is optional but the texture of potatoes fried in oil is very nice. Pour 3 tablespoons oil to a wide non-stick and heat it. Add the potatoes and fry them on a medium high heat until golden. 4. I drained off the oil after 2 minutes and pan roasted them until golden. I do this just to avoid heating oil for a long time. You may do the same. Set these aside. Prick each of them with a fork in 2 to 3 spots, so the potatoes absorb some flavors. 5. Boil 1¼ cup onions (230 grams) in 2 cups water until transparent for about 6 to 8 mins. This step helps to remove the pungent smell and bitter taste from the onions. Drain them & rinse well. Add the cooled onions, 1 cup tomatoes (180 grams) and 12 cashews (24 split) to a blender jar. 6. Make a smooth paste. If your blender is not strong enough then filter the puree otherwise the gravy will not be smooth enough. 7. Boil 250 grams potatoes until slightly tender but undercooked. 8. Drain them to a colander and prick each potato a few times (2 to 3) with a fork. Let them cool and air dry for a while. Meanwhile blend the yogurt with cashews to a smooth puree. If using almonds, soak them in hot water for 4 to 5 hours, peel and use. 9. Pour 3 tbsps oil to a small pan and add the potatoes. Fry them until blistered or light golden. Remove and set them aside. 10. Pour 2 tbsps oil to a hot pot or pan. If you want you can use the same oil in which you fried the potatoes. Regulate the flame to low and add bay leaf and half tsp shahi jeera or cumin seeds. As soon as the seeds begin to splutter, add 2 pinches of hing and half tsp fennel seeds powder. 11. Stir and add 1 teaspoon ginger paste. Saute until aromatic. 12. Add 200 grams tomatoes, half teaspoon salt and half cup green peas (optional). Saute until the tomatoes turn mushy and soft. 13. Next add red chilli powder, garam masala and coriander powder. Saute until the masala smells good and begins to separate the oils. 14. Then add the potatoes. 15. Add the ground yogurt cashew paste. Stir and cook for a few minutes until bubbling. Pour ¾ cup water. Bring it to a gentle boil. 16. Cover with a tight lid or foil. Simmer completely and dum cook for 5 to 7 minutes until the potatoes are completely tender and the gravy becomes thick. 17. Taste test and adjust the spices and salt if required. Add coriander leaves and serve dum aloo with roti, poori or with basmati rice. 18. Wash and peel potatoes. If using large potatoes, then cube them to equal size about 1½ inch each. If using baby potatoes make sure all are of the same size. 19. Bring 3 to 4 cups of water to a rolling boil in a pot. Then add the potatoes. Boil them until they are half cooked. To check, prick them with a fork, you will feel them hard but not very hard. Remove them from water with a slotted spoon. Set aside to cool & dry. 20. This step is optional but gives a good texture to the potatoes . Heat 3 tablespoons of oil in a non-stick pan. Add the potatoes and shallow fry them on a medium high flame until golden. Remove them to a plate. 21. Bring 2 cups of water to a boil and add onions to it. Boil for 6 to 8 minutes until they turn pale and transparent. Drain them completely & rinse with cold water. Drain again. 22. Add them to a blender jar along with cashews and tomatoes. Make a smooth puree. If you do not have a powerful blender, then filter the puree. Keep this aside. 23. Heat a pan with 2 tablespoons oil. Add bay leaf and cardamom. Then add ginger garlic paste & fry until the raw smell goes away. 24. Add the onion tomato paste and saute until the raw smell goes away and the paste thickens slightly. 25. Next add chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, salt, fennel seeds powder, sugar and turmeric. 26. Saute until the masala becomes thick, smells good and aromatic. (check step-by-step photos above) 27. Meanwhile, whisk yogurt in a small bowl until smooth. Then add 2 tablespoons of the onion tomato masala to the whisked yogurt. Mix it well and add back to the pan. 28. Saute until the masala turns thicker. Add potatoes and pour ½ cup water. Stir well to make a gravy. If needed add more water as required. Bring the gravy to a boil. 29. Cover with a tight lid or foil. Dum cook on a low heat for 5 to 7 minutes. At this stage the gravy must be thick and potatoes fully cooked. 30. Sprinkle crushed kasuri methi. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Turn off. 31. Lastly sprinkle coriander leaves and transfer dum aloo to a serving bowl. Keep covered until dum aloo is served. 32. Serve it with basmati rice, roti, butter naan or even with flavored rice like jeera rice, ghee rice or pulao. 33. Bring 1 ½ cups water to a rolling boil in a small pot. Add the cubed onions and boil them for 6 to 8 mins, until transparent. Drain them completely and rinse with cold water. Drain again. 34. Add these to a blender jar along with tomatoes & cashews. Blend to a smooth puree. 35. Press the SAUTE button on the Instant pot and pour oil to the steel insert. 36. Add bay leaf and green cardamoms to the hot oil. Followed by ginger garlic paste. 37. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds until the raw smell goes away. 38. Then add the onion tomato paste, red chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder, salt, sugar and fennel powder. 39. Saute for 3 to 4 until the mixture turns slightly thicker. 40. While this is done, whisk yogurt in a small bowl until smooth. Take 2 tablespoons of the onion tomato masala to the yogurt and mix it well. Pour it back to the pot. 41. Mix everything well and saute for another 3 to 4 minutes. The mixture turns very aromatic and you will get the caramelized smell. The raw smell of tomatoes has to go away by then. Do not burn. Press CANCEL button. 42. Pour 1 ¾ cups water and deglaze the pot with a wooden spatula to release the bits of masala stuck at the bottom. Do not skip this step. 43. Drain the water from diced potatoes and add them here. Taste test the gravy and add more salt if needed. 44. Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position the steam release value to sealing. Press PRESSURE COOK button and set the timer to 4 minutes. 45. The IP beeps when it is done. Wait for 6 to 7 minutes and then release the pressure manually by gently moving the steam release handle to venting. 46. You see the dum aloo gravy is almost thick yet slightly runny. The potatoes also will be perfectly done. Crush kasuri methi and add it. 47. Press SAUTE button and let it cook for 30 to 60 seconds. The gravy will thicken further after cooling. 48. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve with naan or plain basmati rice.

title: Dum ka Chicken
ingredients: 1 kg bone-in chicken, 3 cups red onions, 0.3333333333333333 cup ghee, 2.5 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1.5 teaspoon sea salt, 1 cup yogurt, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 0.75 tablespoon garlic paste, 0.75 tablespoon ginger paste, 3 green chilies slit, 0.25 cup pudina mint leaves, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.625 teaspoon shahi jeera, 8 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 1 star anise, 0.25 cup almonds, 2 tablespoons shredded coconut, 0.25 cup milk
instructions: 1. Heat ghee in a wide pot. Fry the onions on a medium heat, stirring constantly for 12 to 13 mins, until golden & caramelized but not burnt. You may also fry them in 2 batches, each for 9 to 10 mins. 2. Turn off and reserve 2 tablespoons each - ghee and fried onions aside, retaining the rest in the pot. Add all the whole spices - shahi jeera, cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, star anise to the hot pan. Cool down the pot. 3. Meanwhile, prep up the other ingredients and blend drained soaked nuts, coconut and half of the cardamoms with milk/water to a smooth or slightly coarse paste. 4. Add chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder, salt, ginger garlic paste, green chili, yogurt, lemon juice, mint, coriander leaves and the nut paste. 5. Mix well & taste test the marinade to adjust salt & spice levels. Add the chicken and marinate for 30 mins. Turn on the stove and saute marinated chicken on a medium high heat for 7 to 8 mins. Pour ½ cup hot water and mix well. 6. Turn down the heat to low. Cover with a foil and a heavy lid. Cook on a very low heat for 25 mins. Mix well and cook for another 20 mins. 7. Layer the reserved fried onions and ghee. Cover back and raise the heat to medium and cook for 5 mins. 8. Let rest for another 30 mins, before opening. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve dum ka chicken with tandoori roti, kulcha, plain paratha, naan or with bagara rice, saffron rice or turmeric rice.

title: easy gulab jamun recipe | instant gulab jamun with ready mix recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cup sugar, 1.5 cup water, few strands of saffron kesar, 3 cardamom, 1 tsp lemon juice, 1 cup instant gulab jamun mix, 3 tbsp milk, oil ghee for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large pan take 1½ cup sugar and 1½ cup water. 2. also add few strands of saffron / kesar and get to a boil. 3. further simmer for 4 minutes or till sugar syrup turns slightly sticky. 4. switch off the flame and add cardamom powder and lemon juice. 5. sugar syrup is ready, cover and keep aside. 6. now in a mixing bowl take 1 cup instant gulab jamun mix. 7. add 2 tbsp of warm milk and combine dough. 8. make a smooth and soft dough. 9. furthermore, make small balls greasing ghee to hands. 10. make sure there are no cracks on balls. 11. heat the ghee on low flame and when the ghee is moderately hot, fry the jamuns. 12. fry the balls on low flame stirring in between. 13. fry till the balls turns golden brown. drain and keep aside. 14. immediately, drop the hot jamuns into hot sugar syrup. 15. cover the lid and rest for 30 minutes to 2 hours. 16. finally, the gulab jamun have doubled in size. serve warm with ice cream or warm.

title: easy gulab jamun recipe | instant gulab jamun with ready mix recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cup sugar, 1.5 cup water, few strands of saffron kesar, 3 cardamom, 1 tsp lemon juice, 1 cup instant gulab jamun mix, 3 tbsp milk, oil ghee for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large pan take 1½ cup sugar and 1½ cup water. 2. also add few strands of saffron / kesar and get to a boil. 3. further simmer for 4 minutes or till sugar syrup turns slightly sticky. 4. switch off the flame and add cardamom powder and lemon juice. 5. sugar syrup is ready, cover and keep aside. 6. now in a mixing bowl take 1 cup instant gulab jamun mix. 7. add 2 tbsp of warm milk and combine dough. 8. make a smooth and soft dough. 9. furthermore, make small balls greasing ghee to hands. 10. make sure there are no cracks on balls. 11. heat the ghee on low flame and when the ghee is moderately hot, fry the jamuns. 12. fry the balls on low flame stirring in between. 13. fry till the balls turns golden brown. drain and keep aside. 14. immediately, drop the hot jamuns into hot sugar syrup. 15. cover the lid and rest for 30 minutes to 2 hours. 16. finally, the gulab jamun have doubled in size. serve warm with ice cream or warm.

title: Egg Bhurji Recipe (Indian Anda Bhurji)
ingredients: 4 eggs, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.625 cup onion, 1 green chili, 0.875 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 tomato, 0.25 cup bell pepper
instructions: 1. Break 4 regular sized eggs (or 2 to 3 jumbo eggs) to a bowl. Add ⅛ teaspoon turmeric and ¼ teaspoon salt. (you can adjust the number of eggs to your preference) 2. Beat the eggs till frothy. Set this aside. This step is optional and you can add eggs without beating to the pan as well. But the fluffy texture would be missing. Some people also add few tbsps of milk to yield a fluffy scrambled egg. 3. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan. Add ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. When they begin to splutter, add curry leaves (optional). Saute till the leaves turn crisp. 4. Add ½ to ¾ cup finely chopped onion and 1 green chili. 5. Saute until the onions turn golden. Add ¾ to 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste. Saute well until the raw smell of the ginger and garlic goes away. 6. This step is optional, If you don’t like to use tomatoes, just skip them or use capsicum. Add 1 finely chopped tomato and saute well. 7. The tomatoes should break down well and blend well with onions. You can also add ½ to ¾ teaspoon red chilli powder and ¾ to 1 teaspoon garam masla at this stage. Fry till the masala begins to smell good. But I have added the spice powders later. 8. Pour the beaten eggs. You can also straight away add the eggs without beating. 9. Scramble the eggs & cook until almost done. Add garam masala and chili powder. Stir and fry until the raw smell of the chilli powder has gone completely. Also do not overcook as the eggs loose the softness. 10. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Cook until just done. Garnish with coriander leaves. You can also squeeze some lemon juice. Serve egg bhurji with pav, bread, roti or rice.

title: Egg biryani
ingredients: 3 tablespoon ghee, 2 cups basmati rice, 1 tablespoon Ginger garlic paste, 1 cup onion, 1.5 green chilies, 4.5 boiled eggs, 1 medium tomato, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 tablespoon garam masala, 0.25 cup mint, 1 teaspoon Salt, 6 tablespoons curd, 3.75 to 4 cups water, 2.5 cups water, 3.5 cups water, 1 star anise, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves (laung), 1 inch cinnamon, 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Soak rice for at least 20 to 30 mins. Drain water and set the rice aside in a colander. 2. Boil eggs and remove the shells and rinse them. Make few gashes using a knife or a fork. Set aside. 3. Heat a pressure cooker or a pot with oil. 4. Add star anise, bay leaf, shahi jeera, cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon and mace. Allow them to sizzle. 5. Next add onions and green chilies. Fry/saute till golden or light brown. 6. Add ginger garlic paste. Fry till the raw smell goes off. 7. Add tomatoes, mint, red chili powder, turmeric, salt and yogurt. Cook until the tomatoes turn mushy. 8. Add eggs, potatoes (if using) and biryani masala powder. Fry till the mixture thickens. 9. Pour water and add salt as needed. Taste to check for salt and spice levels. 10. Bring it to a boil & add drained rice. 11. If cooking in pot lower the flame completely, cover and cook till the rice is done fully. If the rice seems to be uncooked, then pour few tablespoons of hot water. If using pressure cooker, close the lid and cook for 1 whistle. Set this aside, don’t leave on the hot burner. 12. Fluff up the egg biryani and serve with raita. 13. Press SAUTE button on the Instant pot. Pour ghee to the inner pot of the IP. 14. When the ghee melts add the onions. Spread them in the oil & saute occasionally until deep golden. 15. Remove half of the onions and set aside for garnish. 16. Add the whole spices to the pot and stir. 17. Next add ginger garlic & chilli. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds. 18. Then add tomatoes and sprinkle salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn mushy. 19. Stir in the spice powders – chilli powder, turmeric and biryani masala. 20. Then add the yogurt and deglaze the pot by scrubbing the bottom with a spatula. This releases any bits of food stuck there and avoids burn notice. 21. Press CANCEL button when the mixture turns thick in a minute or so. 22. Then make incisions on the eggs and stir them with the spicy masala. 23. Layer the rice evenly in the pot. 24. Pour 2 cups water around the edges and half cup on top. 25. Ensure the rice is under the water. (Check the video below.) 26. Add some mint leaves and saffron soaked in 1 tbsp milk (optional). Taste the water and add more salt if needed. 27. Secure the Instant pot with the lid & position the steam release handle to sealing. 28. Press PRESSURE COOK button (high pressure) & set the timer to 5 mins. 29. When the Instant pot beeps wait for 6 to 7 mins. Then release rest of the pressure manually. 30. Do not stir the egg biryani a lot. Serve it gently with a spatula. 31. Whisk 1 cup yogurt with salt. Add 1 sliced onion, green chilli & 2 tbsps coriander leaves. Mix well and serve.

title: Egg Bonda (Egg Pakora)
ingredients: 4.5 hard boiled eggs, 0.5 cup besan, 2 tablespoons rice flour, 0.25 teaspoon carom seeds, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 green chillies, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.41666666666666663 cup water, oil, 0.25 cup onions, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, lemon juice
instructions: 1. Prick each hard boiled egg with a fork randomly on all the sides. This prevents the eggs from bursting. Slice each egg to 3 to 4 thick portions and drizzle a pinch of salt. Avoid slicing too thin to prevent messing the yolks. 2. To a wide shallow bowl, add besan, corn starch, salt, turmeric, coriander leaves, garam masala, ginger, chili powder, green chilies and ajwain. 3. Pour water little by little in batches, using a whisk make a thick lump free batter. Bring it to a thick yet flowing consistency by pouring more water as needed. (Check the pictures for details). Taste test and add more salt or spices if you want. 4. Heat oil (about 2 inches) in a deep pan. When it is medium hot, place each slice of egg in the pakora batter. Using a spoon, coat it with batter on all the sides. 5. Pick up with the spoon, letting the excess batter drip down in the bowl. Slowly slide into the hot oil. This way add as many slices as you can without over crowding the pan. Fry them on a medium heat. 6. Avoid disturbing for 2 to 3 mins, later stir gently and fry until crisp and golden. Fry the remaining in batches. Remove to a cooling rack. 7. Mix together fine chopped onions, lemon juice and coriander leaves in small bowl. When you are ready to serve, place the chopped onions on the egg pakora, sprinkle a bit of chaat masala and serve. You can also serve them with a drizzle of mint chutney.

title: Egg Butter Masala
ingredients: 3.5 hard boiled eggs, 2 tbsp butter, 0.75 cup onions, 1 cup tomatoes, 3 medium garlic cloves, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 cashew nuts, salt, 0.75 tsp red chili powder, turmeric, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 2 cardamoms, 1 stick cinnamon, 0.625 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Heat 1 tablespoon butter in a pan. I suggest not to substitute butter with oil. Eggs roasted in butter taste and smell very good. 2. Prick boiled eggs with fork few times. Roast them until golden. Set these aside 3. To the same pan, add onions, tomatoes, ginger, garlic, cashews and salt. Saute on a high to medium flame until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. If needed cover and cook. Do not saute on a low flame. 4. Add chili powder and turmeric. 5. Saute until the raw smell of the chili powder has gone. 6. Cool and add to a blender jar with ¾ cup water. 7. Blend to a smooth puree. 8. Add another 1 tbsp butter. Optionally add a small cinnamon piece, cloves and cardamom. 9. Pour the onion tomato puree. Add more water as needed to bring it to a consistency. I used ¼ cup water. 10. Cover and cook. 11. When the gravy turns thick, add garam masala and coriander powder. Cook further for a while. 12. It is done when traces of fat are visible on the gravy. Sprinkle kasuri methi. 13. Add eggs, juliennes of ginger and lower the flame. Taste test and adjust salt. Cook covered for about 2 to 3 mins. If you like you can also add cream. Garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Egg Curry Recipe
ingredients: 4.5 boiled eggs, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 teaspoon ghee, 1.25 cups onions, 1 cup tomatoes, 1 green chilli chopped, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.125 cup water, 1 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon Kasuri methi, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 4 tablespoons cream, 1 bay leaf, 1 cinnamon piece, 2 green cardamoms, 0.75 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric
instructions: 1. Fine chop or puree the onions. If you prefer to puree the onions, first boil diced onions in 2 cups of water for 4 mins until transparent. Drain the water and puree them in a blender. 2. Refer notes below to boil eggs perfectly. Wipe dry the boiled eggs or air dry until moist free. Prick each egg with a fork 2 to 3 times. We do this so the eggs don't splatter oil and burst in the pan while frying. 3. Heat 1 teaspoon ghee or oil in a nonstick pan & Fry the eggs until golden & slightly blistered. Remove the eggs to a plate & optionally sprinkle 2 to 3 pinches each of chilli powder & garam masala. 4. Heat 2 tbsp oil and add bay leaf, cardamoms and cinnamon. 5. When they begin to sizzle, add onions & green chilli. Saute onions until golden and the raw smell has almost gone. 6. Add ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw smell goes off, for about a minute. 7. Then add tomatoes and saute well until the mixture turns mushy. 8. Next add red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, turmeric and salt. Saute everything well until a nice aroma comes out & the mixture leaves the sides of the pan. 9. Pour water 1 to 1¼ cups water and mix well. Cover and cook until the curry thickens and you see traces of oil over the curry. 10. Taste test and adjust salt. If required add sugar. Add the fried eggs and kasuri methi. Mix and simmer for a while. Sprinkle chopped coriander leaves. Serve punjabi egg curry with roti or naan or plain rice. 11. Optional - Pour 3 to 4 tbsps cream or Blend together 10 cashews with half cup water to smooth milk. Pour it to the curry. Cook covered for 2 to 3 mins until the curry turns thick. 12. Press saute button and add oil to the inner pot of the Instant pot. Add spices & bay leaf. 13. Add ginger garlic and fry for 30 seconds. Then add the tomatoes and salt. 14. Fry until the tomatoes breakdown. Add chilli powder and turmeric. Saute for a min. 15. When the cumin begins to sizzle add onions and green chilli. Saute until the onions turn light golden. 16. Then pour 1 cup water and mix well to release any bits of food stuck at the bottom of the pot. 17. Place a long leg trivet. Place your basmati rice bowl. Cover it. Then place the eggs in another bowl. Cover it. 18. Secure the IP with the lid. Position the steam release handle to sealing. 19. Press the pressure cook button (high pressure). Set the timer to 7 mins. 20. When the IP is done, press the cancel button. 21. When the pin drops, open the lid. Do a quick manual release by moving the steam release handle from sealing to venting. You can also wait for 5 mins for a natural release. 22. Pour cold water to the eggs and let them cool down. Peel the eggs and prick them with a fork. 23. Press the saute button and simmer the eggs in the curry for a minute. Then pour the cream or cashew milk or coconut milk. 24. Stir well and cook until the curry thickens. Taste and add more salt if needed. Turn off and garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Egg Dosa
ingredients: 2 eggs, 1 cup dosa batter, 1 onion, 2 green chilies, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoons carrot, salt, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1.5 tablespoons chutney
instructions: 1. Get the ingredients ready. You need batter close to room temperature (cold is alright), 1 egg per dosa and few chopped onions, grated carrots (optional), green chili and coriander leaves. If you like to make the street style egg dosa, you will also need some chunky curry/ chutney/ sauce /gravy. 2. Grease a tawa and heat it on a medium flame until hot enough. Pour one full ladle of batter in the center and spread it. Do not make a very thin dosa. I prefer to keep the edges thick to prevent the egg flowing out of the dosa. Cook until the batter gets cooked for about 2 mins. Add a tbsp of thick curry, chutney or sauce as per your preference to the center of the dosa. I do not use. 3. Pour the egg in the center . I prefer to break the egg to a bowl first to make sure no shells fall into it. 4. Break the yolk with a spoon and spread it evenly around the dosa. 5. Add veggies. 6. Pour 1 teaspoon oil around the edges and another spoon all over the toppings. Use generous amount of oil to cook the egg else it won’t smell good. 7. When the base is firm, turn it to the other side and press down with a wooden spatula. Cook on a medium heat until the egg is fully cooked through. Avoid cooking on low heat, egg turns harder. Cooking on very high heat only burns the eggs without cooking well. 8. Turn it to the other side and cook it until the dosa turns crispy. Serve Egg Dosa hot with or without a chutney.

title: Egg Fried Noodles Recipe
ingredients: 2 cakes noodles, 3.5 eggs, 2 tablespoons peanut oil, 1.25 tablespoon garlic cloves, 2.5 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon rice vinegar, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 1 tablespoon red chilli sauce, 1 onion, 0.5 cup bell peppers, 1 carrot, 5 French beans
instructions: 1. Bring 8 to 10 cups of water to a rolling boil in a large pot. Add the noodles and let cook al dente, meaning fully cooked but the noodles should have a bite to them. Follow the cook time from your pack of noodles. Mine takes about 3 mins. 2. Drain the noodles to a colander. I rinse mine under cold running water following the manufacturers instructions. 3. While the noodles cook, rinse and chop the veggies. Separate the greens and the whites from the green onions while you chop. 4. Break the eggs in a rinsed bowl & scramble them with a fork until frothy. Set aside. 5. Heat a wok with oil. When the oil is hot enough, add the chopped garlic and the spring onion whites. Let fry for 30 seconds. 6. Add carrots, bell peppers and green beans. Fry them on a high heat until slightly tender yet crunchy. 7. Slide the veggies aside in the wok and reduce the heat. Pour the eggs. Scramble them and cook till done but not overcooked. 8. Sprinkle salt and pepper. Pour the sauces and vinegar. Add the cooked noodles. 9. Stir fry the noodles just for a minute or so on the highest heat. Sprinkle pepper. 10. Serve egg fried noodles while still hot.

title: Egg Fried Rice Recipe
ingredients: 3 cups cooked rice, 1.25 tablespoon soya sauce, 1.5 teaspoon rice vinegar, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chili, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 3.5 stalks spring onions, 3 eggs, 1.25 tablespoons schezwan sauce, 0.5 cup carrot, 0.5 cup bell peppers, 0.5 cup cabbage
instructions: 1. Wash rice and soak for 30 mins. Meanwhile bring 5 to 6 cups water to a rolling boil in a large pot. Drain the water from the soaked rice and add it to the boiling water. If you want you can add half teaspoon salt and cook. 2. When the rice is cooked al dente (meaning the rice has a bite to it and not fully cooked), drain it to a colander. Do not over cook the rice. Cool completely. 3. Chop the veggies and garlic. Break the eggs to a bowl and beat until fluffy. 4. To be cooked on high flame. Heat a pan with oil, add chopped garlic & green chili. Fry for 30 seconds. Add spring onion whites and fry for a minute. 5. Add chopped veggies and fry for about 2 minutes. Do not make them mushy, they should be crunchy and half cooked. Slide the veggies to one side of the pan. 6. Reduce the flame to medium. Pour eggs and scramble them. Cook till they are soft, fluffy almost cooked but not overcooked. Sprinkle little salt. 7. Increase the heat and pour the soya sauce, vinegar and any hot sauce if you prefer. The soya hits the hot pan and emits some steam. This imparts a nice Chinese flavor. 8. Add cooled rice, ground black pepper and spring onion greens. If you want you may add some toasted sesame oil and it's optional. 9. Fry on the highest flame for about 2 minutes. Adjust salt and spice as needed. 10. Garnish egg fried rice with some spring onions and serve.

title: Egg Keema
ingredients: 4 boiled eggs, 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 2 green cardamoms, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 cinnamon stick, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.75 cup onions, 1 green chili, 0.5 cup tomatoes, salt, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp red chili powder, 0.875 tsp meat masala, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 cup green peas, 1 handful mint
instructions: 1. Boil 4 eggs in a pot. Remove the shell and cut them to small cubes without messing up the yolk much. Set aside. Heat oil and add cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, bay leaf and cumin. Saute until they begin to crackle. 2. Fry finely chopped onions until slightly golden. *note: finely chopped onions blend well with the keema. 3. Fry ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes off. 4. Add chopped tomatoes and saute until they turn completely mushy. 5. Add turmeric, chili powder and garam masala or meat masala. I suggest adding coriander powder too if the masala has a very sharp taste. Saute until the raw smell of the red chili powder and tomatoes goes off. I used this homemade garam masala . 6. This step is optional. Green peas or soaked cashews can be used. Saute until the raw smell goes away. 7. Add very little water, salt, handful of pudina and coriander leaves. You can add kasuri methi instead of pudina. Cook until the water almost evaporates and a thick curry base is left. Check the salt if needed add more.

title: Egg Kurma (Egg Korma)
ingredients: 5 eggs, 2 tbsp oil, 0.75 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1.25 cup tomatoes, salt, 1 pinch turmeric, 0.75 tsp red chili powder, 1.25 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp coriander powder, 2 tbsps mint leaves, 1 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 bay leaf, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 2 green cardamom, 1 strand mace, 1 star anise, 0.5 tsp shahi jeera, 3 tbsp raw coconut, 8 cashews, 0.75 tsp fennel seeds
instructions: 1. Boil the eggs, remove the shells and make few incisions randomly and set aside. Heat oil in a pan, add cinnamon, star anise, cloves, jeera or shahi jeera and bay leaf. I used it along with the other spice powders later. Do not use too many spices if you are making this to go with a biryani, stick on to the quantity mentioned in the recipe. 2. When the spices begin to sizzle, add finely chopped onions and green chili. 3. Saute until the onions turn golden and add ginger garlic paste. Saute till the raw smell disappears. 4. Add chopped tomatoes, turmeric and salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn mushy. While the tomatoes cook, make a fine paste of raw coconut/yogurt, cashews & fennel seeds. If using coconut, add a splash of water to grind. Set the paste aside till we need it. 5. Add garam masala and chili powder. 6. Mix and saute until the masala smells aromatic. 7. Add the cashew paste. 8. Saute until it begins to smell good. This step takes about 3 to 5 minutes depending on the thickness of your paste. 9. Add mint and saute well for 2 to 3 mins. Pour water and mix well to make a gravy. Bring it to a rolling boil and simmer until thick. Add the boiled eggs. You can also fry the eggs in ghee at the beginning and add them to the pan now. Fried eggs do lend a nice flavor to the korma. 10. Simmer until you see traces of oil on top. Taste test and adjust salt or garam masala. If you want add little black pepper. Add coriander leaves and switch off. Serve Egg kurma with rice, roti, biryani, parotta and pulav.

title: Egg Masala Gravy Recipe
ingredients: 3 boiled eggs, 2 tbsp oil, 1.25 cup onions, 1.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1.5 green chili, 0.625 tsp red chili powder, 0.875 tsp garam masala, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp Salt, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp mint, 3 tbsp curd, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 tsp cumin, 3 cloves 3 cloves (or laung (optional)), 2 cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon
instructions: 1. Pour 3 cups water to a pot. Gently place the rinsed eggs and boil on a medium heat. Cover the pot & boil until you see cracks on the eggs. Turn off the stove and rest the eggs for 10 mins. Discard the water & keep the eggs immersed in cold water to cool down. 2. Then remove the shell. Pierce with a fork randomly to make some gashes. This helps the eggs to absorb the flavors. 3. Heat a pan with oil. Add cumin, bay leaf, cloves, cardamoms and cinnamon. Allow them to splutter. 4. Add onions, sprinkle salt and fry till onions turn slightly brown or caramelize to enhance the aroma. 5. Optional step - Skip this step if using yogurt. While the onions fry, make a fine paste of the cashews or coconut with some water. You can also blend together tomatoes and cashews. 6. Add ginger garlic paste to the onions and saute till it becomes fragrant. 7. Next add tomatoes, fry till they turn mushy. 8. Add chili powder, turmeric and garam masala powder. Fry till it begins to smell good. 9. Pour the yogurt or cashew paste or coconut paste, fry till the mix leaves the sides of the pan. 10. Add eggs, mint leaves, coriander leaves. Fry for 2 mins , preferably till you get a mint aroma. 11. Pour water just enough to partially cover the eggs. Cook covered till the egg masala thickens. 12. Sprinkle some kasuri methi if you have. 13. Serve egg masala curry with rice, roti or paratha.

title: Egg Mayo Sandwich
ingredients: 4 slices Bread, 1 tablespoon butter, 3 Boiled eggs, 3.5 tablespoon mayonnaise, 0.875 tablespoon mustard, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper, Salt, 2 tablespoons onion, 1 teaspoon Lemon juice, 0.625 teaspoon Red chill flakes, 1 garlic clove, 1 teaspoon herbs, 2.5 tablespoons parsley
instructions: 1. Boil eggs in pot or pressure cooker. Cool them and peel. I boil them in my instant pot for 7 minutes. Then let the pressure release naturally. I keep them in cold water for a while. Then peel them. 2. Chop the eggs to small bites without messing up the yolks much. 3. Add finely chopped onion, garlic, pepper, mustard powder or sauce, coriander or parsley and dried herbs if using. If you want to make your own mustard powder, just run a spoon full of seeds in a blender and make a fine powder. 4. Use about 1/8 tsp i.e 2 generous pinches. You can skip onions and garlic. You can also soak them in lemon juice for 20 mins and use. That helps to remove the pungent taste and flavor. 5. Add mayo as desired. You can use as much as you like. If you do not eat mayo then add cream cheese or Greek yogurt. 6. Mix everything well. Add red chilli flakes. You can add very little salt if needed since mayo is already salted. Taste this and add lemon juice if desired. 7. I prefer to toast the bread in preheated oven for 3 to 4 mins at 180 C. You can also toast on a griddle/ tawa or in a toaster. Spread the prepared egg mayo mixture on the bread. Serve egg mayo sandwich within 2 hours or chill in refrigerator until used. 8. Sandwich Recip... Vegetarian Club S... Veg Grilled Sandw... Yogurt Sandwich (... Tomato Cucumber S... Bombay Sandwich R...

title: Egg Paratha Recipe (Anda Paratha)
ingredients: 0.5 cup wheat flour, warm water as needed, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 eggs, 1 onion, 1 green chili, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 pinch turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon sea salt, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoon carrots
instructions: 1. Make the Dough: Add flour, a pinch of salt and 3 tbsps warm water to a mixing bowl. Mix and begin to form a soft non-sticky dough, adding more water as you mix. Add ½ teaspoon oil and knead well until pliable. Cover and prep up the veggies. 2. Divide the dough: Divide the dough to 2 portions and roll them to smooth balls. Sprinkle a teaspoon of flour on the rolling area and coat one dough ball with the flour on both the sides. 3. Roll the Paratha: Flatten it and begin to roll to a 6 inch roti. Spread ¼ teaspoon oil and fold the paratha to a semi circle. (check the pictures) Spread oil again and fold to a triangle. Roll it to a triangle shaped paratha. This way make both the parathas. 4. Cook the Paratha: Heat a griddle until hot enough on a medium flame. Dust off the excess flour and place it on the griddle. Cook until you see bubbles, turn it to the other side and press down with a spatula. 5. Return to the other side and cook, pressing down until it begins to puff. It is done when you see brown spots all over. Cook both the parathas and cool down the pan slightly. 6. Prepare the Egg: Meanwhile, In a mixing bowl stir together half of the onions, chilies, coriander, salt, turmeric, black pepper and carrots (optional). Reserve the other half for the next paratha. Break an egg to the same bowl. Whisk well until frothy. 7. Make the Egg Paratha: Pour a teaspoon oil on the pan. When it is medium hot, pour the egg mixture and let cook until the base is firm and top still gooey. Place the paratha over it and press down with a spatula so it sticks to the egg. 8. Turn it to the other side and cook until hot, making sure the eggs are cooked through and the egg paratha is hot. This way cook the other egg paratha too.

title: Egg Pasta (Scrambled Egg Spaghetti)
ingredients: 2 grams spaghetti, 3 tablespoons olive oil, 2 tablespoons garlic, 1.25 tbsp red chilli flakes, 0.25 teaspoon sea salt, 3.5 eggs, 0.25 cup parsley
instructions: 1. Pour 10 cups of water to a large pot and bring it to a rolling boil. Add the pasta. 2. Add some salt. Cook until al dente. Check the package for cook time. Reserve ¼ cup pasta cooked water/stock aside. 3. Drain the cooked pasta to a colander. 4. While the pasta cooks, pour oil to a pan and heat up. Add chopped garlic. 5. Saute until the garlic begins to shrink. Do not wait until the garlic turns browns. When it turns aromatic, add red chili flakes. 6. Saute for about 30 to 50 seconds. Pour the pasta cooked stock, we reserved earlier and add salt. Note that we added salt while cooking pasta, so your reserved pasta stock has some salt. Add only as needed. Cook until the water evaporates and a slightly thick sauce like mixture is left. 7. Pour the eggs. 8. Scramble them and cook until the eggs are fully cooked and set, yet soft. Taste test and add salt to your preference. 9. Add the drained pasta. 10. Toss well with the eggs and add chopped parsley or your favorite herbs. Serve egg pasta hot.

title: Egg Puff Recipe (Bakery Style)
ingredients: 1 puff pastry sheet, 2 hard-boiled eggs, 1 raw egg, 1.5 tablespoons coconut oil, 4.5 curry leaves, 2 cups red onions, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon or crushed fennel seeds, 0.25 teaspoon crushed pepper, 2 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a wide pan and add onions, green chilies, curry leaves chilies and salt. Saute on a medium heat for until golden and evenly caramelized. 2. Stir in the ginger garlic and cook for a minute until aromatic. 3. Reduce the heat and stir in chili powder, garam masala, fennel & black pepper. Deglaze with water (or tomato ketchup) & cook for a minute. Turn off and cool down completely. 4. Meanwhile thaw your puff pastry sheets at room temperature, until easy enough to handle. Mine takes only 6 to 7 mins. (May be different depending on the temp) 5. Cut each sheet to 4 portions. 6. Place 1½ to 2 tbsps. of onion masala in the center of each cut puff pastry. Place a halved egg, (yolk facing the onions) on each pastry. If your pastry is too dry, you can brush the corners with little water. 7. Bring one of the corners over the egg & place it there. Bring the diagonally opposite corner over it & stick. Repeat with the other 2 corners, leaving a little gap for the steam to escape. 8. Brush with beaten egg and place in the refrigerator for 15 to 20 mins. Meanwhile preheat the oven to 390 F/ 200 C for 15 to 20 mins. 9. Place in the center and bake for 18 to 20 mins or as instructed on the package, until golden, crisp & flaky. (Smaller ovens and toasters will take much lesser time, around 15 mins) 10. Cool the tray on a wired rack for at least 5 mins. Serve Egg Puffs with ketchup & chai.

title: Egg Pulao Recipe
ingredients: 3 tablespoon ghee, 2 cups aged basmati rice, 1.5 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 0.5 cup onion thinly sliced, 1.5 green chilies slit, 4.5 boiled eggs, 1 medium tomato sliced, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 teaspoons garam masala, 0.25 cup mint leaves finely chopped, 0.25 cup yogurt, 3.5 cups water for pressure cooker 3 to 4 cups for regular pot, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 star anise, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 3.5 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse it well thrice. Soak it for 20 to 30 mins, then drain the water and set the rice aside. 2. While the rice soaks, boil the eggs and have the shells removed. Make a few gashes with a fork and set the eggs aside. Slice onions and tomatoes. 3. Add oil to the pressure cooker or pot and heat. 4. Add star anise, bay leaf, shahi jeera, cardamoms, cloves and cinnamon and let them sizzle. 5. Add the onions next and fry till golden. 6. Throw in some ginger garlic paste and fry till the raw smell is gone. 7. Put the eggs, tomatoes, mint, red chili powder, turmeric and salt. Fry until the oil separates. 8. Add the mint and saute for a minute. Whisk the yogurt well and lower the flame. Incorporate the yogurt slowly into the pan. Stir and cook until really thick. 9. Add rice, water and salt as needed. Mix the water with the ingredients and taste test to suit likings. 10. Bring the water to a rolling boil. 11. Lower the flame entirely, cover and cook when using a pot. If using a pressure cooker, close the lid and cook till you hear 1 whistle. 12. When the pressure drops open the lid and fluff up with a fork. If using pot, let the egg pulao cook until the rice is fully cooked, yet not mushy. If the rice is undercooked, splash some hot water and cook. Let the hot egg pulao rest in the cooker or pulao for a while.

title: Egg Pulusu
ingredients: 5 hard boiled eggs, 1 cup onions, 2 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp mustard, 1 Pinch fenugreek seeds, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp Oil, 1 tsp jaggery, Salt, 0.625 tsp red chili powder, tamarind, 0.625 tsp Coriander powder, 0.125 tsp cumin powder
instructions: 1. Soak tamarind in hot water and filter the pulp to remove impurities 2. Heat a deep pan with oil. 3. Add mustard,methi and cumin, when they splutter add curry leaves and sauté until crisp. 4. Add onions and green chili, sprinkle salt and fry till they turn pink 5. Add eggs, coriander and cumin powder,red chili powder, turmeric, salt. Fry for 2 mins on medium. Pour the tamarind pulp. Mix well. Cover and cook on low heat for 3 to 4 mins. If you wish you can add a tbsp. jaggery or sugar now. 6. Pour enough water to partially cover the eggs . 7. Mix and simmer for atleast 5 to 8 mins. Adjust the salt and tamarind. 8. Off the heat and add coriander leaves. Serve egg pulusu with rice or chapathi.

title: Egg Roast (Spicy Roasted Eggs)
ingredients: 4 Boiled Eggs, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon Mustard Seeds, 0.5 teaspoon Fennel Seeds, 2 cups onions, 1 teaspoon Ginger, 1 teaspoon Garlic, 1 sprig Curry Leaves, 0.25 teaspoon Turmeric Powder, 0.875 teaspoon Red Chili Powder, 0.375 teaspoon Black Pepper Powder, 1 teaspoon Garam Masala, 2 teaspoons Coriander Powder, 1 medium Tomato, 0.25 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Once it starts boiling, lower the heat and allow it to simmer for another 10 minutes. When the time is up, remove pan from the heat and set it aside for 5 to 10 minutes, if time permits. Now, drain the water and deshell the eggs. Make slits or poke holes on it randomly. 2. Next, heat 2 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Tip: If you have a cast-iron skillet, that would be perfect for making this egg roast as it lends a darker color to the gravy. 3. Once the oil is hot, stir in the ½ tsp. mustard and ½ tsp crushed fennel seeds. When they start to crackle, add the 4 finely chopped onions. Lower the heat to medium. 4. Sauté them until golden. Spoon in 1 tsp. of both crushed ginger and garlic to it along with a bit more of oil if required and salt. Tip: Adding salt helps to sweat the onions. You may add about ½ tsp. of salt at this point. Continue cooking on a low to medium heat, until the mixture has browned lightly but not burnt. 5. After that, reduce the heat to low. Add ¼ tsp. of turmeric powder, ¾ to 1 tsp. red chili powder, ¼ tsp. black pepper powder, 1 tsp. Garam Masala and 2 tsp. of coriander powder to it. 6. Give the spicy onion mix a good stir.. Once the spices have no lingering smell, add the 1 finely chopped tomato and stir well. At this point, the oil would be well absorbed by the spices, and the mix will be dry. 7. Cook for another 5 to 10 minutes or until the tomatoes have broken down and the gravy has absorbed the masala’s flavors. 8. Add ¼ cup of hot water and bring the whole mixture to a boil. Cook until the water evaporates. 9. Finally, add the 4 boiled eggs and a few sprigs of curry leaves. Keep stirring and roast the eggs until the whole mixture has caramelized and turns darker. The longer you roast the better the flavors are. It takes about 7 to 10 mins for the egg roast to reach that stage. Taste test and add salt if required at this stage. 10. Tip: If you prefer, you can mash one egg yolk to it for a thicker consistency. Set it aside for ten minutes for the flavors to meld. Roast until the oil starts to separate from it. Garnish with coriander leaves, if preferred. Serve it hot.

title: Egg Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 4 eggs, 7 slices bread, 2.5 tablespoons unsalted butter, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 1 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.5 cup coriander leaves, 13.5 mint leaves, 1 garlic clove, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoons coconut, 1 green chili, 1 onion, 1 medium carrots, 2 sprigs coriander leaves, lettuce
instructions: 1. To make the sandwich chutney, add all the ingredients to make the chutney to a grinder. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons water and grind to a smooth and thick chutney. 2. Scrape the sides and splash more water if required to help grind. Do not add lots of water, we don't want a runny chutney. 3. Taste test and add more salt, lemon or chilli as required to suit your taste. 4. Butter the bread slices generously and toast them either on a griddle or in a toaster. You can also air fry for 3 mins at 180 C or 360 F or toast in the oven. 5. Spread the chutney/ pesto/ spread on the bread with the buttered side out. Slice each egg to 4 slices (roundels) and place them on the chutney smeared bread. 6. Sprinkle Some chaat masala and roasted cumin powder over the eggs if you have. Layer the veggies over the eggs. 7. Place another chutney smeared slice of bread over the sandwich, with the chutney side in and buttered side out. 8. Press down gently and slice the egg sandwich to triangles. Serve immediately. 9. Boil the eggs, remove the shells and slice each egg to 3 to 4 slices. 10. Trim the edges of the bread. smear butter or oil on one side of the slices. 11. heat a pan with oil, add cumin and garlic, when the garlic is fried lightly, add onions and sprinkle salt. fry till the onions turn lightly golden. 12. Add red chili powder, kasuri methi, turmeric and the spice powders. 13. Fry everything well till you smell good for about a minute. 14. Move the onions to one side of the pan or transfer to a plate. 15. Toast 2 slices of bread to lightly golden or crusty. set them aside and add 2 other slices. 16. Spread a thin layer of fried onions, place the sliced eggs and spread another layer of onions. 17. Spread a layer of veggies and then the coriander leaves. off the stove. 18. Place the other slice of bread on top and press it lightly with the wooden spoon. Boiled egg sandwich is ready.

title: Egg Toast Recipe
ingredients: 3 slices bread, 2 eggs, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.25 salt, 3 tablespoons onion, 3 tablespoons tomato, 1 green chili, 1.5 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 pinch turmeric, 1 pinch chili flakes
instructions: 1. Break the eggs to a mixing bowl and whisk them well with a fork. 2. Add chopped onions, chilies, tomatoes & coriander leaves. Sprinkle, turmeric, salt and chili flakes over the dry ingredients. Whisk well until frothy. 3. Heat a pan with oil or butter on a medium heat and spread it well. 4. Dip a bread slice in the egg mixture to coat on both the sides. Place it over the hot pan. You can cook 2 to 3 slices at one time depending on the size of your bread and pan. (check video) 5. Pour 2 tablespoons of egg mixture on top of each bread slice and let cook until the base is firm. 6. Turn the bread slices to the other side and cook, pressing down with a spatula. If you are left with a little more egg mixture & don't want to use more bread, you may pour 2 tablespoons of the mixture on each (already cooked toast) and cook further. 7. Serve Egg Toast hot or warm. 8. Preheat the oven to 380 F or 160 C for at least 15 mins. Spread butter on one side of your bread and place them over a baking tray with the buttered side up. 9. Gently pour a few tablespoons of the egg mixture over the bread slices. 10. Place the tray in the middle rack and bake for 14 mins or until the eggs are thoroughly cooked through.

title: Eggless Apple Cake
ingredients: 1 cup all-purpose flour, 2 medium apples, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.125 tsp cinnamon powder, 0.3333333333333333 cup butter, 0.5 cup milk, 0.75 tbsp vinegar
instructions: 1. Wash the apples. I used 2 medium sized apples. Do not use very sour apples as it makes the cake sour. 2. Peel and cube them. Add them to a blender. 3. Make a smooth puree. 4. Pour milk to a mixing bowl. Do not use chilled milk. Add sugar. 5. Measure and use only half cup of the apple puree. I was left with just another 2 to 3 tbsp of the puree which I stirred in my black tea and strained. 6. Melt butter and use only 1/3 cup. 7. Pour ¾ tbsp vinegar or lemon juice to activate the soda. I used apple cider vinegar. I also added 1 tsp vanilla extract which you can omit, as the cinnamon powder we are using is good enough to flavor the cake. 8. Give all these a good mix. It is just fine even if the sugar doesn’t dissolve. 9. Place a sieve over the mixing bowl. Add flour, cinnamon, baking powder, baking soda and a pinch of salt if desired. Please be sure that you measure baking powder & soda correctly without interchanging one for the other. 10. Give a good stir. 11. Sieve and begin to incorporate the dry ingredients with the wet.
