title: fried milk recipe | custard milk fried dessert | crispy deep fried milk
ingredients: 0.75 cup custard powder, 0.5 cup sugar, 3 cup milk, 0.5 cup maida, 0.25 cup cornflour, water, 1 cup panko breadcrumbs, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take ¾ cup custard powder, ½ cup sugar, and 3 cup milk. 2. whisk and mix well until sugar melts and cornflour is well combined without any lumps. 3. transfer the custard mixture into large kadai. 4. stir continuously, and cook on medium flame. 5. keep cooking until the mixture thickens and starts to turn glossy. 6. once the mixture turns to paste consistency, transfer the mixture to a tray. 7. make sure to line the tray with baking paper to prevent it from sticking. 8. level up uniformly, and refrigerate for 2 hours or until it sets completely. 9. after 2 hours, the custard has set well and is ready to cut into pieces. keep aside. 10. in a bowl take ½ cup maida, ¼ cup cornflour, and prepare a smooth slurry. 11. dip the custard cube into a slurry and then roll in breadcrumbs. 12. double coat the custard piece to prevent it from melting while frying. 13. now deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 14. stir gently without damaging the pieces. 15. fry until the custard turns golden brown and crisp. 16. drain off the pieces to absorb excess oil. 17. finally, enjoy fried milk custard with chocolate sauce.

title: Fried Modak Recipe
ingredients: 1 cups flour, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 pinch salt, water, Oil, 1 cup coconut, 0.5 cup jaggery, 7 cashews, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Stir together flour and salt in a mixing bowl. Add ghee and rub very well for 2 to 4 minutes to incorporate the ghee. 2. Pour warm water little by little and make a stiff dough. Cover and set aside for 10 mins. 3. Meanwhile add coconut, water, cardamom and jaggery/ sugar to a pan. Cook until the mixture turns sticky, switch off and add the cashews. Cool this mixture and divide to 11 equal sized portions. Roll to balls. 4. Divide the dough to 4 equal balls. Grease the rolling area and roll the ball to a roti that is neither too thick nor too thin. 5. Cut them to roundels of 4 inch using the lid of a jar or any round cookie cutter. 6. Place a ball in the center and begin to make pleats from one side. Bring together all the pleats and stick them up. Remove excess dough on top. Finish making all the modak. 7. Knead all the left over dough and make a roti. Repeat the round cuts and finish to make totally 11 modak. Keep them covered with a cloth. 8. Heat oil in a deep pan. When the oil is medium hot, put on the flame to medium. Gently slide the modaks. Do not disturb them for a while until they become firm. Then keep stirring gently and consistently to fry them evenly until golden and crisp. 9. Remove them to a cooling rack. Fried modak are ready to serve. Cool them completely and store in a air tight steel or glass jar. To reheat, preheat the oven to 440 F or 220 C and bake for 5 to 6 mins.

title: Fried Onions Recipe (Crispy Onions/Birista)
ingredients: 1 onion, 1 teaspoon corn starch, 0.625 cup oil
instructions: 1. Wash and peel onion. 2. Cut onions into evenly thin slices and ensure they are almost of same thickness. 3. Sprinkle corn flour on the slices and toss them. 4. Heat oil in a deep pan till medium hot but not smoking hot. 5. Reduce the flame to medium. Add few onion slices at one time and deep fry until crisp. 6. Transfer them to an absorbent paper. Fry all the onions in batches. Cool them completely and store in a airtight jar.

title: Fried Paneer Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams Paneer, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper ground, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.25 teaspoon ginger powder, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.75 tablespoon poha powder, 2 teaspoons oil
instructions: 1. Add all the spices & poha powder to a plate and mix them well. Taste test and adjust spices and salt. (To make poha powder, grind ¼ cup poha in a grinder to make a slightly coarse powder) 2. Rinse paneer block with 1 cup water and place it on a chopping board. Dice to 4 parts and then slice each portion to 2. You will have 8 slices of paneer. 3. If using lemon juice in place of chaat masala, dip each slice of paneer in the lemon juice before you go ahead with the next step. 4. Place the moist paneer in the spice mixture to coat evenly. Coat one after the other and place them on the plate. 5. Spread half of the oil on a non-stick pan or a well-seasoned cast iron pan and heat it well. 6. Once hot, place the paneer pieces over the hot pan and let fry on a medium heat for 3 to 4 mins, until the bottom is crisp. 7. Reduce the heat and using 2 spatulas (1 small and 1 medium), turn the pieces gently to the other side. 8. Add the rest of the oil and let fry until crisp on a medium heat. 9. Hold with the spatulas and position one of the sides on the pan, fry for a minute. Turn them to the other side (Check pictures in the post). 10. Turn off the pan and leave them on the sides. Transfer pan fried paneer to a serving plate and serve with mint chutney & onion salad. Or Serve with dal-rice, curry-rice, pilaf or biryani.

title: Fruit custard recipe
ingredients: 2.5 cups milk, 3 tablespoons custard powder, 0.25 cup warm milk, 4.5 tablespoons sugar, 1 medium apple, 0.5 mango, 1 medium banana, 0.5 medium pomegranate
instructions: 1. Rinse the fruits thoroughly and chill them in the fridge. Refrigerating after chopping will let out juices from the cut fruits making the custard runny. 2. Heat 2½ cups milk in a wide heavy bottom pan or pot. Add sugar and stir until it dissolves. 3. Meanwhile add custard powder to a small cup and stir it with ¼ cup milk (warm or at room temperature). The mixture has to be smooth without any lumps. 4. When the milk turns hot (not boiling), pour 2 tbsp of the custard mix to the hot milk. Whisk well to incorporate it with the milk. 5. Repeat adding another 2 tablespoons custard mixture to the milk and whisk well. This way incorporate all of the custard mixture to the milk in 3 batches, whisking after each addition. 6. You may taste test it at this stage and add more sugar if needed. 7. Keep stirring and cook on a low to medium heat until the custard turns thick. The custard must coat the back of the spoon well but must be yet of runny consistency. (check video) 8. Take it off the heat and whisk it well to make it super smooth. Cover immediately. Cool this completely and chill it in the fridge. 9. Whisk the custard well before serving. This ensures even and smooth fruit custard. 10. Peel and chop the fruits to bite size. Add them to serving bowl or individual serving cups. 11. Pour the custard over the fruits and gently mix. Serve fruit custard immediately.

title: garam masala recipe | how to make homemade garam masala spice mix powder
ingredients: 0.75 cup coriander seed dhaniya, 0.5 cup cumin jeera, 1 tsp caraway shah jeera, 2 tbsp pepper kali mirch, 3 red chilli, 5 star anise chakra phool, 3 inch cinnamon dalchini, 2 mace javitri, 5 black cardamom, 2 nutmeg jaiphal, 3 tsp cardamom elachi, 1 tbsp clove lavang, 2 tsp fennel saunf, 5 bay leaf tej patta, 1 tsp ginger powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a heavy-bottomed pan take ¾ cup coriander seed and roast on low flame. 2. roast until the spices turn aromatic. keep aside. 3. in the same pan add ½ cup cumin, 1 tsp caraway and roast on low flame. 4. roast until it turns aromatic. keep aside. 5. further, take 2 tbsp pepper and 3 dried red chilli. 6. roast until the chilli turns crisp. keep aside. 7. now add 5 star anise, 3 inch cinnamon, 2 mace, 5 black cardamom, 2 nutmeg, 3 tsp cardamom, 1 tbsp clove, 2 tsp fennel and 5 bay leaf. 8. roast until all the spices turn aromatic without burning. keep aside. 9. cool all the spices and transfer to the mixi. 10. also, add 1 tsp ginger powder and blend to a coarse powder. 11. finally, garam masala is ready. store in an airtight container and use as required for curries.

title: Garlic Broccoli Recipe
ingredients: 1 head broccoli, 1.5 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 teaspoon garlic, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 cup liquid, 1 tablespoon unsalted butter, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon black pepper, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon red chili flakes
instructions: 1. Rinse the broccoli head under running water and shake off to get rid of the water. Cut to florets. 2. Heat oil in a wok and add sliced garlic. Saute for 30 seconds and add the broccoli. Sprinkle salt and saute on a medium high heat for 2 to 3 mins. 3. Add the liquid in batches and stir fry until the broccoli is tender, yet crunchy (or to your liking). It takes about 6 to 8 mins. 4. Move the broccoli to the sides and add black pepper, garlic and butter to the center of the pan. Let the garlic sizzle for about 30 to 40 seconds, until aromatic. Taste test and adjust salt. 5. Coat the broccoli with the melted garlic butter and turn off. Avoid overcooking. Squeeze lemon juice and serve hot.

title: Garlic Cheese Toast
ingredients: 6 slices bread, 3.5 tablespoons butter, 2 tablespoons garlic, 1.25 cup cheese, Salt, 1 tablespoon parsley, 0.75 teaspoon oregano, 1 teaspoon red chili flakes
instructions: 1. Add softened butter and chopped garlic to a bowl. Preheat the oven to 370 F – 190 C. 2. Whisk it up well with a fork to make a smooth garlic butter. 3. Smear the butter generously. Press the garlic chunks a bit into the bread to prevent them from falling off. Put this in the oven and bake for 7 to 8 mins. 4. While the bread toasts, grate your cheese. I avoid buying ready grated cheese since most of these have preservatives in E series added to it. 5. The garlic bread is done to golden. Bring the tray out of the oven. 6. Sprinkle cheese generously on the hot bread along with your favorite seasoning like chili flakes, herbs etc. Put back in the oven for 2 to 3 mins or until the cheese melts and begins to turn golden. Serve cheese garlic bread hot.

title: Garlic Cheese Toast Recipe | Garlic Bread Toast on Tawa
ingredients: 1 pod garlic, 0.25 cup butter, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 5 slices bread, 0.5 cup cheese
instructions: 1. Firstly, roast 1 pod garlic on direct flame. 2. Roast on medium flame till the garlic is cooked well. 3. Cool completely, and mash the garlic really smooth. 4. Add ¼ cup butter, ½ tsp mixed herbs, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp salt. Mix well. 5. Also add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well, making sure everything is well combined. 6. Spread the garlic butter on a bread slice and top with cheese. 7. Toast ina pan on low to medium flame by covering. 8. Cook till the cheese melts. 9. Finally, cut it in half and enjoy the Garlic Cheese Toast Recipe.

title: Garlic Cheese Toast Recipe | Garlic Bread Toast on Tawa
ingredients: 1 pod garlic, 0.25 cup butter, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 5 slices bread, 0.5 cup cheese
instructions: 1. Firstly, roast 1 pod garlic on direct flame. 2. Roast on medium flame till the garlic is cooked well. 3. Cool completely, and mash the garlic really smooth. 4. Add ¼ cup butter, ½ tsp mixed herbs, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp salt. Mix well. 5. Also add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well, making sure everything is well combined. 6. Spread the garlic butter on a bread slice and top with cheese. 7. Toast ina pan on low to medium flame by covering. 8. Cook till the cheese melts. 9. Finally, cut it in half and enjoy the Garlic Cheese Toast Recipe.

title: Garlic Chicken
ingredients: 55 grams chicken breast, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 2 teaspoons lemon juice, 1 teaspoon Italian herbs, 0.5 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.5 teaspoon onion powder, 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour, 2 tablespoons oil to fry chicken, 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, 1.5 tablespoon garlic, 2 teaspoon all-purpose flour, 1 cup chicken broth, Salt, 0.75 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.75 teaspoon chili flakes, 1 tablespoon parsley
instructions: 1. Gather the ingredients you need for the recipe. 2. Chop 550 grams (1.2 lbs.) chicken breasts (2 medium sized) to bite sized pieces and add to a mixing bowl. Pat dry with kitchen papers if required. Squeeze 2 tsps lemon juice (optional) and sprinkle the following: 3. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons of organic all-purpose flour or ¼ cup corn starch. Use corn starch for a crispy chicken. 4. Coat the chicken and keep aside. 5. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan and fry the chicken for 3 mins on each side, until cooked through. A non-stick pan is best, else the flour gets stuck to the pan. 6. Add 2 tablespoons butter to the same pan and add 1½ tablespoon minced garlic. Let fry on a low heat for 40 to 50 seconds. 7. Add 2 teaspoon all-purpose flour. If you want a spicy version of this you can either add a chopped green chili (serrano pepper) or half tsp red chili powder/ cayenne and 1 teaspoon garam masala. 8. Mix well and cook for a minute. Reduce the heat and pour the chicken broth/stock. 9. Cook until the sauce is thickened and coats the back of a spoon, for about 7 minutes. Add lemon juice, taste test and adjust salt to taste. Add the chicken. Make sure your sauce is thick before adding it. 10. Cook on a high heat until some of the sauce is absorbed, for 2 mins. If you want very thick sauce, cook a little longer. 11. Add black pepper or chili flakes or both. 12. Garnish Garlic Chicken with chopped parsley or rosemary. Serve over hot rice or flatbreads like naan, roti or artisan breads. You can also stir in cooked spaghetti or pasta.

title: Garlic chutney (red chutney)
ingredients: 25 red chilies, 1 medium garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons sugar, 1.25 tablespoon lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 pinch mustard seeds, 1 pinch hing, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. You can skip this step and use Kashmiri red chilli powder. Please use gloves if you cannot tolerate the heat of the chilies. Remove the stalks of the red chilies and break them to 2 parts. Deseed as much as you can. Add them to a bowl. Next pour hot water and let them soften for a while. Once they soak, remove the chilies from the water leaving the seeds that settle down. 2. To a grinder jar, add sugar, salt, cumin seeds and garlic cloves. Do not use garlic cloves that have begun to sprout as they taste bitter. To check, you can trim off one end of the garlic cloves and take a look. 3. Add lemon juice or vinegar and the red chilies. I drained off the water as my red chilies were too hot and pungent. Add little fresh water & blend. 4. Scrape off the sides. Keep adding more water as needed & blend to a smooth yet thick chutney. The taste of this garlic chutney has to be well balanced – slightly hot, pungent, slightly sweet & tangy. Taste test at this stage and add more salt, sugar or vinegar. 5. Transfer it to a clean glass container and refrigerate. This can be used for chaats and snacks. 6. I made this for a chaat so I did not temper. If you want to temper this, heat 1½ tablespoons oil in a pan. Add a pinch of mustard seeds & 3 to 4 curry leaves. Next when the leaves turn crisp, add little hing. Pour the chutney and cook for just 1 minute on a low heat. 7. Cooking longer can make the chutney bitter. To serve the tempered chutney, I usually add a few tablespoons of hot water to bring it to consistency. 8. Optional – Skip this step if you prefer to use red chilli powder. Break red chilies to 2 parts each and deseed them. Remove the stalks. Add them to a bowl and pour hot water. Once they soften, remove them from water and add to a grinder/ blender jar. 9. Add garlic, salt, sugar, vinegar, cumin seeds and 3 to 4 tablespoons water to the same blender jar. 10. Blend all of them until smooth and thick adding more water as needed. It should have a sauce like consistency. 11. Taste test and add more sugar, salt or vinegar to suit your taste. It should be well balanced in flavors and taste slightly hot, spicy, tangy and mildly sweet. 12. Garlic chutney is ready to serve with snacks, breakfasts and chaats. 13. If you prefer to temper, heat oil in a pan. Add mustard and curry leaves. 14. When the leaves turn crisp, add hing and lower the heat completely. Pour the garlic chutney. 15. Stir and cook just for a minute. Avoid over cooking to prevent bitter tones. Set aside to cool and transfer to a clean dry glass jar. Refrigerate and use within 3 to 4 weeks. 16. Use low heat red chilies such as Kashmiri or Byadgi. 17. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > title: garlic chutney recipe | red chilli garlic chutney for chaat | lasun chutney
ingredients: 0.75 tbsp chilli powder, 0.75 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.5 lemon, 12 garlic, sal to taste, water as required to blend smooth
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small blender take garlic, chilli powder, coriander powder, salt, sugar and lemon juice. 2. furthermore, add water as required and blend to smooth paste. 3. finally, transfer to a box and use to prepare dabeli or vada pava. store in refrigerator.

title: Garlic Mushrooms Recipe | Spicy Dry Mushrooms
ingredients: 1.5 cup mushrooms, 0.5 cup onions, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 5 garlic, 5 red chilies, 0.75 tablespoon Vinegar, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, salt, 1.5 tablespoon water, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 Onion rings, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add red chilies, garlic, salt, sugar, vinegar or lemon juice to a blender jar. You may deseed the red chilies, but we love the half crushed seeds in this, so I did not deseed them. For a cut short method, you can also skip blending and make a paste using chili powder, garlic mince and the rest of the ingredients with water as needed. 2. Blend them together with just 1 to 2 tbsps water. You must get a slightly coarse thick paste. You can also add little chilli powder for color, i used about ¼ tsp. 3. Heat a pan with oil. Add a single strand of mace or half tsp cumin. Saute for 30 seconds. Add finely chopped onions. Saute until they turn lightly golden. While the onions fry, you can wash and chop or slice the mushrooms. 4. Then add the garlic paste. 5. Saute very well on a low to medium flame for 2 to 3 minutes. Make sure the raw smell of garlic has gone completely. 6. Add chopped mushrooms. 7. Mix well to coat the mushrooms with garlic sauce. Fry for about 3 mins, till you begin to smell the mushrooms good. 8. Cover and cook until the mushrooms are done to your liking. Do not over cook as the texture of mushrooms change & will turn soggy. 9. You can add few coriander leaves. You can also add in some cooked noodles or al dente cooked rice and saute for few minutes. Serve as a starter or with bread, roti or plain rice.

title: garlic naan recipe | homemade garlic naan recipe without yeast
ingredients: 2 cup maida plain flour, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 1 tsp garlic, water, 3 tbsp butter, 1 tsp garlic, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cup maida, ¼ tsp baking soda and 1 tsp baking powder and ¾ tsp salt. mix well. 2. add 2 tbsp oil, ¼ cup curd and 1 tsp garlic. 3. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. further, add water as required and knead to a smooth and soft dough. 5. grease the dough with oil, cover and rest for 2 hours. 6. meanwhile prepare garlic butter by mixing 3 tbsp butter, 1 tsp garlic and 2 tbsp coriander. 7. after 2 hours, knead the dough slightly. to remove if any air present in the dough. pinch a ball sized dough. 8. gently roll to oval shape using a rolling pin. make sure you do not make too thin nor thick. and also keep the size in mind. it should not be larger than your tawa size. 9. grease with water over naan. make sure you coat uniformly. this helps naan to stick on tawa. 10. furthermore, slowly flip over and put it on hot tawa. remember, be gentle and put the water coated side down to tawa. also do not use nonstick tawa. 11. slightly press. this helps the naan to stick on to the tawa and when you flip the tawa upside down it will be intact. 12. now after a minute, flip the tawa upside down and cook naan directly on flame till they turn golden brown. 13. also brush with some prepared garlic butter mixed with coriander leaves. 14. gently scrape the naan from the bottom and remove. 15. finally, serve garlic naan hot with your favourite curry like mattar paneer.

title: Garlic Paneer Recipe (Quick Paneer Starter)
ingredients: 1.25 cup paneer, 6 garlic, 1 teaspoon vinegar, 5.5 red chilies, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.5 cup onions, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon soya sauce
instructions: 1. Blend together garlic, chilies, sugar, salt and vinegar with 2 to 3 tbsp water to a smooth or coarse paste. 2. Heat a pan with oil and add cumin seeds. 3. When the cumin seeds sizzle, add onions & saute until golden. Regulate the heat to medium. Add the sauce and cook until it thickens and the oil separates. 4. Reduce the flame completely and taste test the sauce. Adjust salt and if you want you may add soya sauce. 5. Add paneer and gently mix well to coat the sauce. If it is too dry you may splash little water, about a tablespoon. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes. Garnish with coriander or celery. 6. Serve garlic paneer hot as an appetizer, side or with fried rice, noodles. You can also stuff it in your wraps, kathi rolls, sandwiches etc.

title: Gathiya Recipe | Bhavnagari Tikha Gathia Sev Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup oil, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup besan gram flour, 0.5 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 0.5 tsp crushed pepper, pinch hing, 2 tbsp water, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar take ½ cup oil, ½ cup water, ½ tsp baking soda and 1 tsp salt. 2. Blend till the mixture turns creamy consistency. Keep aside. 3. In a large plate, sieve 2 cup besan, ½ tsp ajwain, ½ tsp crushed pepper and pinch hing. Mix well. 4. Now add the prepared oil-water mixture and knead the dough. 5. Knead well making sure the dough is well combined and soft. 6. Further, add 2 tbsp water and start to knead again. 7. Knead until the dough changes colour slightly and turns soft. 8. Take the flower mould and fix it to chakli maker. 9. Stuff the dough into the chakli maker and press into hot oil. 10. Stir occasionally, until the sev turns crispy. 11. Drain off to remove excess oil. 12. Finally, enjoy Crispy Gathiya for a month when stored in an airtight container.

title: Ghee Biscuits Recipe | Ghee Cookies - Ghee Naankhati
ingredients: 1.5 cup maida plain flour, 1 cup powdered sugar, 2 tbsp milk powder, pinch salt, 0.25 tsp baking powder, 0.5 cup ghee
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl, place a sieve and add 1½ cup maida, 1 cup powdered sugar, 2 tbsp milk powder, pinch salt, and ¼ tsp baking powder. 2. Sieve and mix well making sure all the dry ingredients are well combined. 3. Now add ½ cup ghee, crumble and mix well. Gently mix the flour and ghee mixture. 4. Lightly crumble with fingers and get the mixture into dough. 5. Do not knead the dough, just press and make a soft dough. If you feel the dough is dry, add some ghee or butter. but do not add milk or water as it makes cookies dense. 6. Now make ball-sized cookies and place them over a greased plate. 7. Now bake the cookies in a kadai, by adding salt at the bottom of the kadai. 8. Cook on a medium flame for 15 minutes or until the bottom of the cookies gets golden brown. 9. You can alternatively bake in the oven at 170 degrees celsius for 15 minutes. Cool the cookies completely before taking out the plate. 10. Finally, enjoy the crunchy Ghee Cookies recipe with a cup of coffee.

title: ghee rice kurma combo meal recipe | kurma for ghee rice or nei choru
ingredients: 0.75 cup coconut, 5 cashew, 1 tsp poppy seeds, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp fennel, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 3 pod cardamom, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tomato, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 cup peas, 5 beans, 1 potato, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp mint, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 lemon, 2 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp raisins, 1 bay leaf, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp pepper, 0.5 onion, 2 chilli, 2 cup basmati rice, 4 cup water, 1 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, to prepare masala paste, in a mixer jar take ¾ cup coconut, 5 cashew and 1 tsp poppy seeds. 2. add 1 tsp coriander seeds, ½ tsp cumin, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp fennel. 3. grind to smooth paste adding ½ cup water. keep aside. 4. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 bay leaf, ½ inch cinnamon, 4 cloves, 3 pods of cardamom and 1 tsp cumin. 5. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 6. add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until onions shrink slightly. 7. now add 1 tomato, ½ carrot, ½ cup peas, 5 beans, 1 potato, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp mint and 1 tsp salt. 8. saute for 2 minutes or until the vegetables shrink a little. 9. now add the prepared masala paste and cook until the raw flavour is gone. 10. add 2 cup water and mix well adjusting consistency as required. 11. cover and boil for 10 minutes or until the vegetables are cooked well. 12. now add 2 tbsp coriander and ½ lemon. mix well. 13. finally, veg kurma recipe is ready to enjoy with ghee rice. 14. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee. fry 2 tbsp cashew and 2 tbsp raisins until it turns golden brown. 15. keep the fried cashew and raisins aside. 16. now add 1 bay leaf, 5 cloves, 1 tsp cumin and 1 tsp pepper. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 17. add ½ onion, 2 chilli and saute until the onions shrink slightly. 18. further, add 2 cup of basmati rice (soaked 20 minutes) and saute for a minute without breaking the rice. 19. pour in 4 cup water and 1 tsp salt. mix and get to a boil. 20. cover and simmer for 20 minutes or until the rice is cooked fully. 21. mix gently, and add fried cashew and raisins. 22. finally, enjoy ghee rice with veg kurma.

title: Ghee Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1 cups basmati rice, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 medium onion, 1.75 cups water for Indian cooker, 1 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 14 cashewnuts, 14 raisins, 2 inch cinnamon, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 3.5 green cardamoms, 1 strand mace, 1 star anise, 1 bay leaf
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse well at least thrice. Soak it for at least 20 to 30 minutes. Drain and set aside. While the rice soaks, slice onion thinly & slit green chilies. 2. Heat a pressure cooker or a pot with ghee. Fry cashews till lightly golden, add raisins and stir up. When they turn plump set them aside in a plate. 3. To the same pan, add onions. You can also skip onions from the recipe. Fry them till golden, about to caramelize but not burnt. Remove them to a plate and keep aside to garnish. (These are the fried onions / barista. ) 4. Add all the whole spices to the pan & fry for a min until aromatic. 5. Add green chilies, drained rice & fry on a medium flame for about 3 to 4 minutes. Stir to prevent burning . 6. Pour water and salt as needed. You may need to add more or less water depending on the rice (read my notes below). 7. Taste the water and check the salt. If needed add more. Ideally the water has to be slightly salty. 8. If making in a pressure cooker, cook on a medium heat until you hear 1 whistle. If making in pot, cover and cook on a very low flame until the rice is cooked fully but not mushy. 9. When the pressure releases, Open the lid and gently fluff up the rice with a fork. 10. Transfer ghee rice to a serving bowl or plate. Garnish with raisins, cashews, fried onions and coriander leaves. 11. Serve ghee rice with any gravy like kurma, curry or even with egg roast. You can also eat it plain. 12. Press the SAUTE button & add ghee to the inner steel pot of the Instant pot. 13. When the ghee melts, add the cashews & saute until they turn light golden. 14. Then add in the raisins and stir until they plum up. Remove the cashews and raisins to a small plate. 15. Stir in the onion slices to the ghee and spread them in a single layer. 16. Next stir them every now and then until they turn golden to light brown & aromatic. If the pot is too hot, you can switch the saute to low by pressing (saute button again). Drain the ghee well and remove the fried onions to the same plate. 17. Now add in all the whole spices, followed by the green chilli and drained rice. 18. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds & press CANCEL button. 19. Pour water and add salt. Deglaze by scrubbing the bottom of the steel pot with a spatula. Taste the water and add more salt if needed. 20. Secure the instant pot with the lid. Position the steam release handle/ vent to sealing. 21. Press the PRESSURE COOK button (or manual on older IPs) and set the timer to 5 mins. 22. When the IP beeps, wait for natural pressure release for 2 to 3 mins. For softer rice, you can wait upto 5 mins. Then release the rest manually by moving the steam release handle from sealing to venting. 23. Cool down a bit and then garnish ghee rice with fried onions, raisins and cashews.

title: Ghee Rice Recipe | Neychoru Recipe | Nei Choru | Ghee Bhat
ingredients: 2 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp raisins, 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 pods cardamom, 1 tsp pepper, 1 tsp cloves, 0.5 onion, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 2 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup basmati rice
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee and fry 2 tbsp cashew and 2 tbsp raisins. 2. Roast them till the cashews turn golden brown. Keep aside. 3. In the same ghee, add spices and saute till they turn aromatic. 4. Further add ½ onion, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli till the onion softens. 5. Pour in 2 cup water and 1 tsp salt. Mix and get to a boil. 6. Add 1 cup basmati rice and mix gently. 7. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes or until the rice are cooked well. 8. Fluff the rice gently, and add fried cashew, raisins, and 2 tbsp coriander. Mix gently. 9. Finally, enjoy Ghee Rice Recipe with curry or dal.

title: Ghughra Sandwich Recipe | Double Cheese Sandwich - Gujarati Street Style
ingredients: 1 capsicum, 1 onion, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.25 tsp salt, 3 tbsp coriander, cheese, green chutney, butter, bread
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take 1 capsicum, 1 onion, 2 chilli and 1 inch ginger. 2. add ½ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp pepper powder, ½ tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ¼ tsp salt. 3. also, add 3 tbsp coriander and mix well. the stuffing is ready. 4. to prepare the sandwich, take 2 bread slices and spread butter on them. 5. also spread green chutney uniformly on both bread slices. 6. now place 2 tbsp of prepared stuffing on each slice. 7. top with a generous amount of cheese. 8. layer one above the other stacking uniformly. 9. cover with a bread slice. make sure to spread butter and green chutney on it. 10. now place the sandwich in the gas sandwich maker and close. 11. trim off the sides which have come out. also, make sure to butter the top and bottom of the bread before placing it into the sandwich maker. 12. grill both sides until the sandwich turns golden brown. 13. finally, ghughra street-style sandwich is ready to enjoy.

title: Ginger Chutney | Allam Chutney
ingredients: 2 tbsp chana dal, 1 tbsp urad dal, 1.5 inch ginger, 0.3333333333333333 tsp Salt, 3 red chilies, 1 tbsp Tamarind, 1 tbsp jaggery, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 tbsp Oil, 1 tsp oil, 1 pinch mustard, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 red chilli, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Wash ginger well, peel the skin and cut them to thick slices. 2. Heat oil in a pan. Fry chana dal & urad dal until light golden. Add red chilies and saute until they turn crisp. Dal should become deep golden. Transfer these to a plate. 3. Add onions and ginger. Saute until they turn pink or light golden. Do not brown the ginger. Add cumin and turn off the stove. You can also skip frying ginger to retain the medicinal benefits. 4. Cool all the roasted ingredients. Soak tamarind in water for a while. You can also use tamarind directly if you prefer. Set this aside. 5. Add all the cooled ingredients to a blender jar along with jaggery, little tamarind, salt and little water. Blend all these till smooth or coarse chutney to suit your taste. 6. Add more water if needed to bring to consistency. Adjust salt, jaggery & tamarind as needed. Allam chutney should taste spicy, hot, sweet & tangy. 7. Heat a tbsp oil in a small pan. Add mustard, urad dal & red chilli. When the mustard splutters, add curry leaves. When they turn crisp, sprinkle hing. Turn off the stove and pour this over the allam chutney. 8. Serve ginger chutney with pesarttu, dosa or idli.

title: ginger chutney recipe | allam pachadi | adrak chatni | allam chutney
ingredients: 6 tsp oil, 75 grams ginger, 3 clove garlic, 1 tbsp chana dal, 1 tbsp urad dal, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, 0.25 tsp methi fenugreek, 1 tsp cumin, 3 red chilli, 5 grams tamarind, 75 grams jaggery, 1 tbsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp urad dal, pinch hing, 2 red chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large pan heat 3 tsp oil, add 75 grams ginger and saute for 2 minutes. 2. saute on medium flame until ginger changes colour slightly. 3. also, add 3 clove garlic and saute until it turns slightly golden brown. keep aside. 4. in the pan heat 3 tsp oil, add 1 tbsp chana dal, 1 tbsp urad dal, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, ¼ tsp methi and 1 tsp cumin. 5. roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 6. further, add 30 dried red chilli and saute until the chills puff up. 7. cool completely, and transfer to the mixi. 8. blend to coarse powder without adding water. 9. now add roasted ginger and garlic. 10. blend to a thick paste by scraping sides. 11. now add 50 grams tamarind soaked in ¼ cup hot water for 30 minutes. 12. also, add 75 grams of jaggery and 1 tbsp salt. 13. blend to smooth paste. 14. to adjust the consistency, add ½ cup of water and blend well. 15. blend to a slightly thick paste, making to a silky texture. 16. now to prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp oil. 17. add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp chana dal, ½ tsp urad dal, pinch hing, 2 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 18. splutter the tempering and pour over the pachadi. 19. finally, enjoy ginger chutney or allam pachadi with idli or dosa.

title: Ginger Garlic Paste
ingredients: 125 grams ginger, 125 grams garlic, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon Turmeric
instructions: 1. If you do not have a small blender consider doubling the recipe. Some blenders can't really do a good job when the ingredients are too little. 2. Rinse ginger under running water. Pat dry and peel it. Chop to half to 1 inch pieces. Cut off both the ends of garlic. Peel them. 3. You can also add them to a box or tight lid container. Cover the jar and shake it vigorously for a minute. You will see the skin of the garlic loosens or comes off. This tip works only with certain kinds of garlic. 4. You can also pour some oil over the garlic and rub off to loosen the skin. (This is how we peel garlic when we make pickles.) 5. Add ginger, garlic and oil to a grinder/ blender jar. Blend well until smooth and light in color. 6. Scrape off the sides and repeat blending until you see a smooth mixture. You may add more oil to get the ingredients over the blade. 7. Transfer the ginger garlic paste to a clean dry glass jar. Refrigerate ginger garlic paste up to a month or 6 months in a freezer. 8. To Freeze, spoon into ice tray moulds and knock the tray to the counter a few times. Cover with a cling wrap or a lid. Freeze overnight or until set. Remove them to a freezer safe container or a ziplock bag. Use as desired whenever needed by defrosting one cube each time.

title: Ginger Milk Tea (Adrak Chai)
ingredients: 1.25 cup water, 0.75 cup whole milk, 1 tablespoon loose leaf tea, 0.875 tablespoon ginger root, 1 tablespoon sugar, 0.375 teaspoon cardamom, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon ginger
instructions: 1. Pour 1¼ cup water to a pot and begin to heat it. Add ¾ to 1 tablespoon grated ginger and 1 tablespoon tea. 2. Bring it to a rolling boil and then simmer on a medium heat for 3 mins. 3. Pour milk. 4. Let it come to a boil on a medium high heat. Reduce the heat immediately and bring back to a boil on a medium heat again. 5. Reduce the heat completely and add 1 tablespoon sugar. For less sweeter adrak chai, reduce the sugar or omit it completely to use another sweetener later. 6. Add the other spices – 7. Give a quick stir with a soup ladle and begin to aerate the adrak chai 5 to 6 times. Turn off the heat and cover the pot. Do not simmer the tea any longer. Let it rest for 3 to 4 mins. 8. Strain the ginger milk tea and serve it hot. Limit adrak chai to 1 to 2 cups a day. Too much of this can easily make you dehydrated and cause acidity. 9. Store the leftover strained tea in the refrigerator for a max of 2 to 3 days. Reheat gently on a low heat and serve. Reheating on a high heat can leave a more bitter flavor.

title: Ginger Shots Recipe
ingredients: 1 inch ginger, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon honey, 0.1875 teaspoon crushed black pepper
instructions: 1. Begin by rinsing the ginger well under running water. Scrub with a vegetable brush and rinse to remove any mud stuck there. Pat dry with kitchen papers or air dry. 2. Peel the ginger root with a spoon or table knife to remove the skin. Since I was making 3 servings, I peeled the full root. If making a single serving shot, peel only an inch of ginger (about 5 grams) keeping the rest intact so it can be used the next day. 3. Rinse and cut the lemon. Make sure you use fresh cut lemon every day. 4. Begin to grate the ginger. 5. I grate only as needed. 6. Place strainer over a cup and transfer the ginger to a fine strainer. Press down with a spoon to extract juice. Lastly squeeze the pulp with your fingers to extract as much as possible. 7. You will have clear ginger juice. Measure this as per your requirement. Here is the amount that we follow at home. 15 ml per adult, 5 ml for kids between 3 to 6 years and 10 ml for kids between 7 to 12 years. I separate the portions to small individual cups. 8. Squeeze the lemon. 9. Mix together ginger juice and lemon juice. Ginger shot is ready at this stage. 10. Adding honey and black pepper is optional. But we always add honey as we don’t have any diet restrictions. You may avoid it if you don’t prefer to begin your day with sweetener. I use 1 teaspoon honey per shot. 11. Honey takes a while to dissolve with the other ingredients. Stir it well. The color of your ginger shot depends on the kind of honey used. 12. Tip: Avoid using more than 15 ml ginger in each shot. You can add more lemon juice but not more ginger juice as it can cause heartburn & acidity. Turmeric Latte Re... Turmeric Tea Reci... Ginger Tea Recipe Turmeric Shots Re... Turmeric Milk Rec... Wheatgrass Shot

title: Ginger Tea Recipe (Fresh Ginger Root Tea)
ingredients: 5 grams ginger, 1 cup water, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 1 teaspoon honey, 7 Holy Basil
instructions: 1. Method 1: Rinse ginger under running water. If required soak it in a bowl of water to loosen the mud deposits and scrub it well with a vegetable brush. 2. If you want you may peel the ginger with a table knife or a spoon. Do not peel the whole root, peel only an inch keeping the rest intact. 3. Bring water to a rolling boil. Meanwhile grate or chop the ginger. 4. Add to the boiling water. If you want to use spices and herbs add them too at this step. 5. Simmer covered for 3 to 4 mins. If using chopped or sliced ginger boil for 5 to 6 mins. 6. When the ginger tea is ready you will see it turns to a light yellow color. Turn off the heat and strain to a serving cup. Press down with a spoon to extract most of the ginger flavor. Stir in lemon juice. 7. If you want to sweeten it add a tsp of honey when it cools down slightly. Do not stir in honey to very hot tea. If the tea is too spicy for your taste, simply dilute it with hot water. 8. Method 2: You may also add ¼ teaspoon tea powder or tea leaves, ⅛ teaspoon ground cinnamon and a pinch of black pepper along with ginger. Follow the rest from above method 1. 9. Method 3: To make Indian ginger tea, Boil 1 tsp ginger, 1 to 1.5 teaspoons tea leaves or tea powder in ¾ cup water for 3 to 4 mins. If you want you can also add tulsi or mint leaves. When the tea is boiling rapidly, pour ¼ to ⅓ cup milk and sugar to taste. Simmer for a while. Strain and drink. Avoid lemon in this adrak chai.

title: Goat Curry Recipe (Chettinad Goat Kulambu)
ingredients: 6 grams bone-in goat, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 green cardamoms, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2.5 garlic, 1 cup onions, 0.25 cup shallots, 1.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 cup tomatoes, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 2.75 cups water, 6 red chilies, 2 tablespoon coriander seeds, 0.5 teaspoon pepper corn, 1 teaspoon cumin, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 5 green cardamoms, 4.5 cloves 4 to 5 cloves, 2 two inch cinnamon, 1 star anise, 3 sprigs curry leaves, 1.5 portions kalpasi, 0.3333333333333333 cup raw coconut, 1.5 teaspoon fennel, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, curry leaves
instructions: 1. Heat half a tablespoon oil in a pan on a low heat. Add red chilies, coriander seeds, star anise, cinnamon, cloves, black pepper and cardamoms. 2. Roast for a minute and then add the fennel seeds, cumin and curry leaves. Roast until crunchy and aromatic but not burnt. 3. Cool completely and transfer them to a grinder jar. Save half of the curry leaves for the last step. Make a coarse powder and grind with half cup water to make a thick paste. Add more water as required. 4. On a low to medium flame, heat oil in a heavy bottom pan or Dutch oven. Add the whole spices and garlic. 5. When they begin to sizzle, add the shallots and sliced onions. Regulate the heat to medium and sauté until transparent, for 5 to 6 mins. 6. Turn down the heat to low and add the ginger garlic paste. Saute for a minute until aromatic. 7. Add the tomatoes, turmeric and salt. If the pan is too dry, add a splash of water. Saute until the tomatoes break down and turn soft. 8. Add the goat and saute for 3 to 4 minutes until the color of the meat changes slightly. 9. Pour hot water (start with lesser if using boneless) and add the ground masala. Mix well and bring to a gentle boil. 10. Cover and simmer until the meat is tender and almost cooked, for 1 hour 30 minutes. During this time, stir every 15 to 20 min and add more hot water in batches as required. 11. Leave out this step if using canned coconut milk or prefer not to use coconut. To make the coconut paste, grind together raw coconut, fennel, crisp fried curry leaves & black pepper with ¼ to ⅓ water to a very smooth paste. Keep this aside. 12. When the goat meat is fully cooked & tender, stir in the coconut paste. If using coconut milk add it here along with the curry leaves, black pepper and fennel powder. Avoid cold coconut at this stage. 13. If your curry is too thick, pour ¼ to ⅓ cup hot water and simmer for at least 10 minutes. Turn off once done and rest for at least 15 to 20 mins before serving. The gravy will thicken a bit after cooling so turn off accordingly. Taste test and add more salt as needed. 14. Serve goat curry with rice, Chapati, Tandoori Roti, Naan or Ragi Mudde. You can also serve with Dosa, idli, Idiyappam or appam.

title: Gobi 65 Recipe (Cauliflower 65 Fry)
ingredients: 2.5 cups cauliflower florets, 0.25 cup all-purpose flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.25 cup water, 1.5 cups oil to deep frying, 2 green chilies, 2.5 garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 2.5 tablespoons thick yogurt Greek yogurt, salt
instructions: 1. Clean and cut a medium sized cauliflower. You can use about 2.5 cups florets in this recipe. Heat 4 cups water in a pot until medium hot (not boiling) & turn off. Add the florets and let sit for 5 mins. Drain to a colander and rinse under running water. Shake the colander to drain off all the water. If you want spread them on a kitchen towel to drain out the excess moisture. To a mixing bowl, add 2. ¼ cup all-purpose flour or wheat flour 3. Also add 1 teaspoon red chili powder and 2 sprigs of chopped curry leaves. (To get a good bright red color I mix Byadgi red chili powder with few drops of oil and little water, before adding in here. If you want you may do that). 4. Pour water little by little. Start with ¼ cup water and add more only as required. 5. Mix everything well to make a medium thick batter. Taste test and adjust salt & spice to your preference. It has to be slightly salty & spicy. 6. Add cauliflower to the prepared batter. (Make sure there is no water drippings in your cauliflower florets else the excess water will thin down your batter) 7. Mix to coat the florets well with batter. 8. Add cauliflower florets to salted warm water and set aside for 15 minutes. Drain off. 9. Wash and pat dry the cauliflower florets. 10. Grease or prepare a baking tray and preheat the oven to 240 C/ 460 F. 11. Mix together cornstarch and all-purpose flour. Sprinkle all over the gobi. 12. Add olive oil and a splash of water, mix well to coat the florets with the flour. Sprinkle as much water as required so the flour is no longer dry. 13. Transfer these to a baking tray and bake for 20 to 30 mins until crisp, time depends on the size of your florets. Halfway through shake the try. 14. Heat oil in a pan, add curry leaves and green leaves. Fry till they turn crisp, remove and set aside. 15. Add chopped ginger garlic and fry until aromatic for a minute. 16. Add onions, capsicum and salt. Fry on a high flame for 2 to 3 mins or till the capsicum is cooked slightly. If using yogurt add it, mix and fry for 2 to 3 minutes until most of the liquid evaporates. 17. Add in baked gobi, curry leaves, chili and mix well and fry for 2 mins on high flame. 18. Oven baked Gobi 65 is ready. 19. Veg Puff (Bakery ... Diwali Snacks Rec... Gobi Masala Recip... Cauliflower pakod... Cauliflower Kurma... Cauliflower Curry...

title: gobi 65 recipe | cauliflower 65 | how to make crispy gobi fry 65
ingredients: 15 florets gobi cauliflower, 0.5 tsp salt, 4 cup water, 2 tbsp curd yogurt, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp lemon juice, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, oil, 3 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 1 inch ginger, 2 clove garlic, 2 red chilli, few curry leaves, 1 tbsp curd yogurt, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 15 florets gobi and ½ tsp salt. 2. pour 4 cup hot water and allow to blanch for 5 minutes. 3. drain off the water and take blanched gobi in a large bowl. 4. add 2 tbsp curd, 1 tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 1 tsp lemon juice. 5. also add few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp oil and ½ tsp salt. 6. mix well and allow to marinate for 30 minutes. 7. now add ¼ cup cornflour and ¼ cup rice flour. 8. mix well coating flour uniformly. 9. deep fry in hot oil stirring occasionally. 10. drain off over kitchen paper once gobi turns crispy and golden. 11. to prepare tempering, heat 3 tsp oil and splutter ½ tsp cumin, 1 inch ginger, 2 clove garlic, 2 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 12. keep the flame on low and add 1 tbsp curd, ½ tsp chilli powder and ¼ tsp salt. 13. saute well until it is well combined well. 14. add in fried gobi and mix well coating sauce uniformly. 15. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy gobi 65 for an evening snack.

title: gobi manchurian gravy recipe | cauliflower manchurian gravy recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup maida plain flour refined flour, 2 tbsp corn flour, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste adrak-lasun ki paste, 1 tsp kashmiri red chili powder lal mirch powder, salt to taste, 1 tsp soy sauce, water as required, 2 cups gobi cauliflower, oil for deep frying, 2 tsp oil, 2 cloves garlic lasun, 0.5 cup spring onions, 1 tbsp chili sauce, 1 tbsp tomato sauce, 0.5 tsp pepper, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, salt to taste, 2 tsp cornflour, 1 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take maida and corn flour. 2. further add ginger garlic paste, chili powder, salt and soy sauce. 3. add water as required and make a smooth batter. 4. now add in boiled gobi florets and deep fry in hot oil till they turn golden brown. 5. drain off the gobi over a kitchen towel to remove excess oil. keep aside. 6. in a large kadai heat oil and saute garlic on high flame. 7. further saute spring onion and capsicum. 8. additionally add chili sauce, tomato sauce, soy sauce, vinegar, crushed pepper and salt. 9. mix well and saute for a minute. 10. add corn flour water and stir well till they turn translucent. 11. additionally, add fried gobi. 12. mix gently making sure the sauce has coated well uniformly. 13. finally, transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with chopped spring onions green. serve with fried rice.

title: Gobi Manchurian Recipe | Cauliflower Manchurian
ingredients: 1 head cauliflower, Oil, 0.5 cup all-purpose flour, 0.25 cup cornstarch, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1 tablespoon garlic, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 1 green chili, 0.3333333333333333 cup onions, 0.25 cup bell pepper, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 2.5 tablespoon Red Chilli sauce, 1.5 tablespoon tomato ketchup, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 tablespoon rice vinegar, 1 teaspoon sugar, salt, 3.5 tablespoons water, 0.625 teaspoon black pepper, 0.125 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons spring onion greens
instructions: 1. Cut gobi to medium size separate florets and add them to 3 cups of slightly hot water (not boiling hot). Rest for 5 mins, discard the water and rinse with cold water. Drain them to a colander. 2. Spread on a clean cloth or pat dry with paper tissues, to remove excess moisture. You can do this ahead and set aside at room temperature. Any moisture in the gobi will cook it to soft and not crisp. 3. Heat up the oil for deep frying on a medium heat. 4. Meanwhile make the batter by adding cornstarch, all-purpose flour, red chilli powder, black pepper and salt to a mixing bowl. 5. Mix and add water little by little as needed and make a free flowing lump-free batter. The consistency has to be medium and not too thick or very runny. Adjust salt as needed by tasting the batter. (check video) 6. Add the gobi florets to the batter (in batches), coat them well. 7. When the oil turns hot enough, check by dropping a small amount of batter to the hot oil. The batter has to sizzle and rise to the surface without browning. This is the correct temperature. 8. Pick up each cauliflower floret with a spoon or fingers & gently drop them one by one to the hot oil. The flame has to be medium initially and should be increased slightly as more gobi is added. Avoid disturbing/ stirring them for 1 to 2 minutes, as the coating can easily turn messy. 9. Reduce the flame to medium and fry until the florets turn golden and crisp. Keep stirring in between for even frying. Remove them with a spider, shaking gently to get rid of the excess oil. 10. Transfer to a kitchen tissue/paper towel or a steel colander. Repeat the same steps of adding the rest of the cauliflower florets to the batter, coating them well and then frying them in hot oil until crisp. Oil should be medium hot and not smoking hot, before you fry the next batch. 11. For extra crisp gobi manchurian, you can also refry the once fried gobi again in oil. I usually do not do it as frying them once is good enough to get very good crisp manchurian with this recipe. 12. Optional: Mix together red chili powder with little water (1 tbsp) to a paste and keep aside. This step is optional and is used mainly for the red color. 13. Prepare the sauce: Add soya sauce, red chili sauce, tomato ketchup, red chili paste, sugar and vinegar to a small bowl and keep this aside. (for gravy version refer the notes section below). 14. Heat oil in a wide pan or wok. Add garlic, ginger and green chilies. Saute for a minute or two. 15. Then add spring onions whites & bell pepper. Saute them on a high flame for 2 mins. 16. Pour the sauce and water. Mix well and cook stirring until the sauce turns thick. Add black pepper and turn off the heat. 17. Taste test and adjust the heat and salt levels to your taste. The sauce has to be slightly tangy, sweet & hot. If you want you may add more sauce to preference. 18. Allow the sauce to cool for 1 to 2 mins. Add the fried cauliflower and stir gently to coat well with the manchurian sauce. 19. Garnish gobi manchurian with spring onion greens. Serve immediately as a appetizer or as a side with noodles or fried rice.

title: Gobi Manchurian Recipe | Gobi Manchurian Dry Street Style 5 Secret Tips
ingredients: 4 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 florets gobi cauliflower, 1 cup cold water, 0.75 cup maida plain flour, 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, oil for deep frying, 4 tsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, 0.25 onion, 0.25 cup spring onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tsp vinegar, 2 tsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tsp corn flour, 0.25 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 4 tsp oil and saute 2 clove garlic,1 inch ginger and 1 green chilli on high flame. 2. also, saute ¼ onion and 2 tbsp spring onion on high flame. 3. further add ½ capsicum and continue to saute till they slightly change colour. 4. additionally add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tsp vinegar, 2 tsp soy sauce, ¼ tsp pepper and ¼ tsp salt. 5. mix well and saute for a minute. 6. add cornflour slurry. 7. give a good mix until the gravy slightly thickens and turns translucent. 8. additionally, add fried gobi. 9. mix gently making sure the sauce has coated well uniformly. 10. finally, transfer gobi manchurian to a serving bowl and garnish with chopped spring onions green. serve with fried rice.

title: Gobi Masala Recipe (Cauliflower Masala)
ingredients: 25 grams cauliflower, 0.5 cup onions, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 2 garlic, 1 green chili, 3.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 cup water, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.875 cup ripe tomatoes, 1 cashew nuts, 1 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom
instructions: 1. Add tomatoes, soaked cashews, coriander leaves with stalks and cardamom powder to a blender. Make a smooth puree. 2. Heat 1½ to 2 tablespoon oil in a pan and add the cauliflower florets. Saute on a medium high heat until the gobi is slightly golden and begins to smell aromatic. This takes about 6 to 7 mins. Remove to a plate. 3. Pour the rest of the oil to the pan and add onions. Saute until golden, for about 6 to 7 mins. Saute ginger garlic and green chili for a minute. Pour 2 tablespoons water and deglaze. 4. Stir in salt and all ground spices – turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala, coriander and cumin. 5. Pour the tomato puree and stir well. Cover partially and cook down until the raw smell of tomatoes is gone or until the gravy turns thick. 6. Pour water and add the cauliflower. Taste test and add more salt if you want at this stage else the gobi will taste bland. 7. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat and reduce to medium. Cover and cook until the gobi is fully cooked, yet crunchy. If required add more water as you cook. 8. Add kasuri methi and adjust salt or garam masala if needed. Turn off and garnish gobi masala with coriander leaves & optionally with heavy cream.

title: Gobi Paratha Recipe (Cauliflower Paratha)
ingredients: 2 cups wheat flour, 0.625 water, 1 teaspoon oil, 3 tablespoons ghee, 45 grams cauliflower, 1.5 green chilies, 3.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon ginger powder, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.25 teaspoon ajwain
instructions: 1. Add cauliflower florets to a food processor chute, with a medium grater attachment. Grate the cauliflower florets to rice. Alternately use a hand held grater (medium grater). If it is too moist, spread it on a kitchen towel or tissues and pat dry. 2. Preheat the oven to 430 F / 220 C for 15 mins. Spread the cauliflower rice/ gratings in 2 trays and bake for 10 mins. Give a stir and bake for another 10 mins or as required. It takes me 25 mins. It should be still crunchy and not too soft. Remove the bottom tray early if required. Cool down completely. 3. Meanwhile, add flour to a mixing bowl /food processor and pour water along with oil. Knead to a soft and non-sticky dough, adding more water as required. Cover and rest aside until the stuffing is ready. 4. Once the gobi is cool, stir in the rest of the ingredients for the stuffing. Taste and adjust salt. 5. Divide the dough to 8 equal portions. Roll them to smooth balls and keep them covered to prevent drying out. 6. Sprinkle little flour on the work area. Dip a dough ball in the flour, dust off the excess. Press down on the rolling board and flatten, roll to a 4 inch disc or shape to a cup (check pics/ video). Fill 3 to 4 tbsp cauliflower mixture in the center. 7. Push down the filling inside to bring the edges higher and closer. Seal it. Gently flatten with your fingers to spread the filling evenly. Roll to a 6 to 7 inch gobi paratha. This way roll 6 to 7 parathas. 8. Heat a tawa/ pan until medium hot. Dust any excess flour on the surface of the paratha and transfer it to the hot tawa. 9. Cook until bubbles begin to appear. Turn them to the other side and let cook. Spread ghee all over, turn them to the other side & press down with a spatula to help them puff. 10. Cook them on both the sides until golden spots appear on both the sides including the edges. Remove to a serving plate. If required wipe clean the skillet with several tissues/ thick cloth before frying the next paratha. 11. Serve gobi paratha with pickle, chutney or yogurt.

title: Gobi Paratha Recipe | Cauliflower Paratha - Tips & Tricks
ingredients: 0.5 gobi cauliflower gobi, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, for dough, 2 cup wheat flour, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp ajwain, water, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, grate ½ gobi coarsely. 2. In a bowl take 2 cup grated gobi. 3. Add ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp aamchur and ½ tsp salt. 4. Also add 1 inch ginger, grated, 2 chilli and 2 tbsp coriander. 5. Mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 6. Further to prepare the dough, in a large bowl take 2 cup wheat flour, ½ tsp salt, ½ tsp ajwain and mix well. 7. Add water as required and knead to smooth and soft dough. 8. Now pinch a ball-sized dough and roll about 4 inches in diameter. 9. Place a ball-sized prepared gobi stuffing in the centre. 10. Take the edge and start pleating bringing it to the centre. 11. Also, join the pleats together and secure tight pinching off excess dough. 12. Sprinkle some wheat flour and roll slightly thick. 13. On a hot tawa place the rolled paratha and cook for a minute. 14. Furthermore, flip the paratha when the base is partly cooked (after a minute). 15. Also, brush oil/ghee and press slightly. Flip again once or twice till both sides are cooked properly. 16. Finally, serve hot Gobi Paratha with sauce, raita or pickle.

title: gobi pepper fry recipe | cauliflower pepper fry | gobi pepper dry
ingredients: 2 cup gobi cauliflower, 1 tsp salt, water, 1 cup maida, 0.75 cup corn flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup water, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, few curry leaves, 1 onion, 5 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 capsicum, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup gobi. make sure to take the florets of the same size. 2. now add 1 tsp salt and pour hot water. 3. soak for 5 minutes, or until the gobi is blanched slightly. 4. drain off and keep aside to cool completely. 5. in a bowl take 1 cup maida and ¾ cup corn flour. 6. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp pepper powder and ½ tsp salt. 7. adding 1 cup water, mix well using a whisk making sure there are no lumps. 8. now add blanched gobi and coat well with batter. 9. deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 10. stir occasionally, making sure to fry uniformly. 11. fry until the gobi turns golden brown and crisp. you can double fry for a crunchier bite. 12. drain off making sure to get rid of excess oil. keep aside. 13. in a large wok, heat 2 tbsp oil. add ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp fennel and few curry leaves. 14. splutter until the spices turn aromatic. 15. now add 1 onion, 5 clove garlic and 1 inch ginger. stir fry on high flame. 16. further, add ½ capsicum and stir fry until it turns crunchy. 17. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp pepper powder, ½ tsp salt and 2 tbsp tomato sauce. 18. stir fry on high flame until the spices are well combined. 19. add in fried gobi and mix gently. do not overcook as gobi will lose its crunchiness. 20. finally, and 2 tbsp coriander leaves and enjoy crispy gobi pepper fry.

title: goli baje recipe | mangalore bajji | how to make goli baje recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cup maida plain flour, 0.25 tsp baking soda, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 cup buttermilk, 2 tbsp curry leaves, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coconut, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1½ cup maida, ¼ tsp baking soda, pinch hing and ¾ tsp salt. 2. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 3. now add 1 cup buttermilk. you can alternatively use curd. 4. mix well with your hand in one direction. 5. beat the batter making sure to incorporate air well. 6. now add 2 tbsp curry leaves, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger and 2 tbsp coconut. 7. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 8. cover and rest for 3 hours for best results. 9. after 3 hours, mix the batter again. 10. now dip your hand in cold water and pinch a small ball sized batter. 11. drop it in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 12. stir and fry uniformly on medium flame. 13. drain off the goli baje once it turns golden brown and crisp. 14. finally, goli baje is ready to enjoy with coconut chutney.

title: Goli Idli Recipe | Masala Rice Balls
ingredients: 1.5 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp ghee clarified butter, 1.5 cup rice flour, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 tsp urad dal, 2 tsp sesame, 1 red chilli, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 1½ cup water, ½ tsp salt and ½ tsp ghee. 2. get the water to a rolling boil. 3. now add 1½ cup rice flour and mix well. 4. continue to mix until the water is absorbed well. 5. cover and simmer for 2 minutes or until the flour turn moist. 6. now transfer the flour into a large mixing bowl. 7. with a wet hand start to knead the dough. 8. sprinkle hot water if required and knead to a smooth and soft dough. 9. now take a very small sized ball and roll. 10. place the ball into steamer and steam for 10 minutes. 11. after 10 minutes, take the steamed goli idli and keep aside. 12. to prepare the tempering, heat 2 tsp oil. splutter 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 tsp urad dal, 2 tsp sesame, 1 dried red chilli, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 13. splutter the tempering making sure all the spices turn aromatic. 14. further, add 1 chilli and 1 inch ginger. saute slightly. 15. now add steamed idli and mix gently without breaking the idli. 16. cover and simmer for 2 minutes, or until the flavours are absorbed. 17. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy goli idli with spicy chutney.

title: Gond Ke Laddu Recipe | Gond Ladoo - Dinkache Ladoo
ingredients: 6 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 cup gond dinka edible gum anutu, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 2 tbsp almonds badam, 2 tbsp raisins kishmish, 1.5 cup dry coconut kopra grated, 2 tbsp poppy seeds khus khus gasgase, 0.75 cup dry khajur chuara dry dates, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder elachi powder, 0.25 tsp nutmeg powder jaiphal powder, 1 cup jaggery gud, 2 tbsp water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a tawa heat ¼ cup ghee and roast ½ cup gond in batches. 2. crush the gond using your hand or with a help of rolling pin. 3. also roast dry fruits, 1½ cup dry coconut and 2 tbsp poppy seeds. 4. roast khajur powder with 2 tbsp ghee on low flame. 5. transfer the roasted khajur powder into the same bowl. 6. additionally add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and ¼ tsp nutmeg powder. 7. mix well making sure all the dry fruits are well combined. keep aside. 8. pour 1 string jaggery syrup over dry fruit mixture and mix well. 9. now start making ladoo (greased hand) when the mixture is still hot / warm. 10. finally, enjoy gond ladoo / dinkache ladoo for a month when stored in airtight container.

title: Gond Ke Laddu, How to Make Gond Laddu
ingredients: 1.25 cup wheat flour, 0.875 cup jaggery, 0.5 cup ghee more if needed, 0.3333333333333333 cup gond, 2 tablespoons coconut, 0.25 cup almonds, 1 tablespoons chironji, 0.375 teaspoon cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Clean gond well removing pieces of bark or stones if any. It is good to buy cleaned gond. 2. Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a heavy bottom pan. Fry the almonds, cashews and chironji until slightly golden. Then add coconut and fry just for a minute. Remove and set aside. 3. Pour ¼ cup more ghee to the pan and heat it. The ghee has to be medium hot not very hot otherwise gond will turn bitter. Check if the ghee is hot by dropping 1 small piece of gond, it has to be rise and puff well. 4. Fry gond on a medium heat stirring well. Soon the gond will puff and turn light. Remove this as well to a plate and coarsely crush this with a rolling pin. If you like it fine, blend it with few fried nuts. Keep this aside. 5. Next pour 3 tbsps more ghee and heat. Saute wheat flour in this stirring often until it turns aromatic and deep golden. Remove from the stove. 6. Add the fried cashews, almonds and gond. Also add in cardamom powder. Mix all of these and let the temperature slightly come down but still hot. 7. Then add in jaggery and mix well to incorporate the ghee in gond with the laddu mixture. If using sugar, powder it and use. 8. When the mixture is still hot, take small portions of this and roll to balls. If the mixture is too dry heat 1 to 2 tbsps ghee and pour it. 9. Store gond ke laddu in air tight jar and use upto a month.

title: Gongura Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 1.5 cups gongura leaves, 3.5 green chilies, 1 cup onions, 0.25 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 2 tablespoon poppy seeds paste, 2 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 bay leaf, 2 green cardamoms, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 pinch cumin, 0.75 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Dry roast or sundry poppy seeds until crunchy. 2. Make a fine powder of the poppy seeds first. Add very little water and grind to smooth. Very smooth else you will get the seeds in your mouth while you eat the curry. 3. If your blender is unable to make a fine powder, I suggest you to use smooth cashew paste instead of poppy seeds. Set this aside. 4. Marinate the chicken with ginger garlic paste, turmeric, red chili powder and salt. Set aside. 5. Pluck the leaves (discard even tender stems), wash thoroughly. 6. Heat a pan with 1 tsp oil, add these leaves along with slit green chilies and fry till they wilt off. Add little sugar as soon as you add leaves if you desire to retain the green color, but it has never helped me. 7. Set aside to cool. Once cool, grind the leaves & chilies coarsely. It is difficult to grind it to smooth. Grind it according to your desire smooth or coarse. Set aside. 8. Heat a pan with oil, add the whole spices and sauté till you get an aroma. 9. Add onions, green chilies, sprinkle some salt and fry till the onions turn brown. 10. Add ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell goes off. 11. Add chicken and fry on medium high for 2 to 3 mins, till the chicken turns white. 12. Pour enough water to partially cover the chicken. Add garam masala. 13. Cover and cook till the chicken is tender and fully cooked. 14. Add the poppy seeds paste or cashew paste and mix well. Let it begin to cook and bubble. Add gongura paste and mix well. You must be stirring to prevent sticking at the bottom. 15. Cook on a low flame for 3 to 4 mins or till the desired consistency is reached. To adjust the sourness, you can add more poppy seeds paste or cashew paste. Serve gongura chicken curry hot with rice, raw onions and ghee.

title: Gongura Pachadi (Gongura Pickle)
ingredients: 2 cups red sorrel leaves, 1 onion, 3.5 Green Chilies, Salt to taste, 2 tablespoons Oil, 2 garlic, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 0.25 teaspoon cumin, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 red chili, 6 Majjiga Mirapakayalu, Vadiyalu
instructions: 1. To make this gongura pachadi, I cleaned and plucked the leaves first. Then measured them tightly to fill a 3 liters pot. This is just for you to know the amount of leaves I use for this recipe. Rinse them thoroughly few times in clean water. Drain to a colander, shake off to get rid of excess moisture and spread them on a clean cotton cloth. The leaves must wilt slightly on their own and no moisture should be left. It just took me 5 to 6 hours on a hot day. 2. Heat ¼ cup oil in a pan. 3. Add the leaves to the hot pan. 4. Fry until the leaves cook and wilt completely. Keep them aside to cool completely. 5. To the same pan, add more oil and fry 8 to 10 dried red chilies as needed until crisp. Switch off and add 1 tablespoon cumin/ jeera. Increase or decrease the amount of chilies to your taste and preference. 6. When they cool down add them to a grinder along with 4 cloves of garlic and salt. Make sure your garlic is not moist. If you want, saute it for a minute in the same pan. Blend as smooth as possible. 7. Add the leaves and pulse to get a coarse texture.

title: Gongura Pappu (Gongura Dal)
ingredients: 0.75 cup toor dal, 2 cups water, 1 cup gongura leaves, 1 onion, 2 green chili, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon Salt, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 red chilli, 4 Curd chilies, 1 Pinch hing, 1.5 garlic pods, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder
instructions: 1. Add dal to a bowl or cooker and rinse it well a few times. Pour 2 cups water and pressure cook on a medium heat for 1 to 3 whistles depending on your cooker. 2. Mean while soak gongura leaves in vinegar and salt water for some time. Rinse them well a few times and drain aside. 3. Chop gongura finely, slice onions, slit green chillies. 4. When the pressure drops, open the lid and mash the dal slightly. Add gongura, chilies, onions and slit green chilli. 5. Mix well and check the consistency. If it is too thick pour some hot water and mix. Bring to a rolling boil and reduce the heat. Cook until the leaves are fully tender and soft. Taste test and adjust salt. 6. Heat a small tadka pan with ghee or oil. Add cumin, mustard, garlic and red chili, curd chili. when the spices begin to splutter, add curry leaves and hing. If you want you can also set aside the curd chilies and turn off. Then add the red chilli powder. 7. Pour this to gongura pappu. 8. Pour oil or ghee to the steel insert. Press the saute button, add mustard, cumin, garlic, red chilies, curry leaves and hing. 9. Add onions and green chilies. Saute for 1 to 2 mins. Add red chilli powder, turmeric, dal and gongura. 10. Press cancel and pour 2¼ cups water. Deglaze the pot. Secure the IP with the lid and position the steam release vent to sealing. If you want you may put a trivet and your rice (short grain rice only) bowl over that. 11. Press pressure cook and set the timer to 10 to 12 mins, depending on how mature your gongura is. 12. When the pressure drops, open the lid and mash the dal. Add salt and mix well. Serve gongura pappu with rice and ghee.

title: Grape Juice Recipe
ingredients: 1 kg grapes, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.25 tsp pink salt, 0.25 tsp pepper corn, 2 tbsps Sea salt, Vinegar, Water
instructions: 1. Here are the simple steps I follow to clean up the grapes whether organic or not. 2. Make sure you begin to clean them just before you make the juice otherwise ripe fruits may begin to rot once they come in contact with moisture. I usually clean them about 30 mins before I intend to juice the grapes. I also ensure they are at room temperature. If the grapes are cold, getting rid of the unwanted particles from the surface of fruits is hard. 3. Wash the grapes very well under running water and place them in a wide bowl. For 1 kg of grapes, sprinkle 2 tbsps of sea salt (powder not crystal) then spray vinegar generously. 4. Begin to destem & remove over ripe or rotten grapes. This would take about 10 to 15 mins. By then the salt completely melts. 5. Add water just enough to cover them. Rub them gently and discard the water. Repeat the rinse under running water with ample amount 3 to 4 times. 6. Drain them to a colander. You may still see whitish coating like on the grapes, just ignore it as they are the naturally occurring substance on grapes. 7. Add them to your juicer or blender jar and begin juicing. Other ingredients that go well with grapes are ginger, lemon juice, handful of holy basil or tulsi, mint leaves etc. If you are using a blender, then you will have to strain the juice in a strainer. Use a spoon to press down the juice. 8. If you have used salt for washing, the grapes turn salty so don’t need to add salt while making the juice. Skip adding lemon juice if the grapes are sour. Serve fresh grape juice immediately before it begins to oxidize.

title: Green Banana Recipe (Aratikaya Kura)
ingredients: 2 green bananas, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.25 teaspoon ginger, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 0.75 tablespoon chana dal, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 1 medium onions, 2 green chilies, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Wash green bananas under running water. Cut off both the ends and add them to a pot of water. Bring water to a rolling boil on a high heat and reduce the flame to medium. Boil until the skin discolors and the inside becomes tender. To check poke it with a knife or a fork, if done it goes in without any resistance. If it is slightly under cooked it’s still okay. Alternately they can be grilled on charcoal or in the oven on broil mode. 2. Remove the banana from hot water and cool down. You will be able to easily peel them. Cube them or mash them gently as per your preference. 3. Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds, cumin, urad dal and chana dal. When the lentils turn golden and aromatic, add curry leaves and ginger. Saute until the ginger begins to smell good, for about a minute. Add hing/asafetida if you want. 4. Add onions and slit green chilies. Saute until the onions turn transparent. Stir in turmeric and salt. I was making this for the kids, so had to cut down the green chilies. But note that the heat comes from green chilies alone so use as many as you prefer. If you want to use red chili powder (half teaspoon) and coriander powder (1 tsp), add them now. 5. Add banana. 6. Stir well. Cover and cook on a low flame for 5 to 6 minutes. If the banana is under cooked, sprinkle little water and let cook until fully done. 7. If you want you may use black pepper at this stage. Taste test and add salt before turning off. Sprinkle lemon juice and coriander leaves. Serve Aratikaya kura with rice, ghee, rasam and sambar. 8. Banana Curry (Pla... Bajji Recipe (Ara... Raw Banana Fry (A... Rasam Recipe (Sou... Sambar Recipe, Ho... Kids Lunch Box Id...

title: Green Beans and Potatoes (Aloo Beans)
ingredients: 25 grams green beans, 25 grams potatoes, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 4 garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chili, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan and add green chili, ginger and garlic. Saute for 1 to 2 mins and add the potatoes. Saute for 2 to 3 mins and pour water. Mix well and cook covered until fork tender, not mushy. If you want remove the green chilies and discard. 2. Add the green beans and stir fry for 2 to 3 minutes. Pour 2 to 3 tablespoons water and deglaze the pan. Cover and cook until tender yet crunchy, for 5 to 6 minutes. Sprinkle more water if required as you cook. 3. Stir in turmeric, salt, chili powder, garam masala and chaat masala to coat the green beans and potatoes well. 4. Stir fry for 2 and take off the heat. Taste test and adjust spices or salt to your preference. Sprinkle lemon juice if you prefer. Serve aloo beans with rice or roti alongside dal or any curry.

title: green chilli chutney
ingredients: 25 grams green chilies, 9 cloves garlic, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 1 pinch turmeric, Salt, 1 tsp Vinegar, Oil, 2 tbsp coconut
instructions: 1. Wash the green chilies and allow them to dry for a while. Slit them with a knife. Please do wear a mask & ventilate your kitchen well. 2. Heat a pan with oil and fry the chilies till they wilt off fully and begin to get discolored. Add garlic cumin, turmeric to the hot pan. Cool completely. 3. Optionally you can also fry the coconut and peanuts until golden and aromatic. Then cool completely & add them to the blender. 4. Then add the chilies, salt, vinegar and left over oil in the pan to the blender jar. Blend everything well to a coarse green chilli chutney. 5. Store it in an airtight glass jar and refrigerate. Keeps good for about a month. Serve it with rice, roti or bread as a side.

title: Green Chutney Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups coriander leaves, 1 cup mint, 1 teaspoon roasted cumin seeds, 3 green chilies, 2 garlic, 0.375 inch ginger, 1.25 tablespoons Lemon juice, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon Salt, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts
instructions: 1. Cut down the roots and the mature stalks from the fresh coriander. You can keep the tender stalks. (Mature stalks impart a soapy taste so discard them.) Rinse the cleaned coriander well in lot of water a few times. 2. Pluck mint leaves and add them to a bowl of water. Avoid using the stalks as they taste bitter. Rest for a while and repeat rinsing at least twice. (I usually spray vinegar over the greens first and sprinkle some baking soda, leave for 5 mins and then soak them in water for another 5 mins.
