Later rinse them a few times) 3. Drain them well to a colander. Shake off to get rid of excess water from the herbs. 4. Add all the ingredients to a grinder jar - coriander leaves, mint, roasted peanuts, cumin, garlic, ginger, green chilies, salt and lemon juice. 5. Make a thick paste by adding 1 tbsp water at one time and blending until smooth. 6. Taste test and add more salt, lemon juice and green chili if required to suit your taste. 7. Store this green chutney in a clean dry airtight glass jar. Refrigerate and use with in 2 to 3 days. Use dry spoons to serve it. 8. This green chutney keeps good for about a week in refrigerator but tastes best for 2 to 3 days and 1 month in freezer. 9. Serve green chutney with snacks, sandwiches, chaats and tikkis.

title: green chutney recipe | hari chutney | green chutney for chaat
ingredients: 1 cup coriander leaves, 0.5 cup mint leaves pudina, 3 clove garlic, 3 inch ginger, 2 tbsp roasted gram dal putani, 3 green chilli, 0.5 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, pinch of hing asafoetida, 0.5 cup water, 1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 1 cup coriander leaves and ½ cup mint leaves. 2. also add 3 clove garlic, 3 inch ginger and 3 green chilli. 3. additionally add 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp sugar, ½ tsp salt and pinch of hing. 4. blend smooth adding ½ cup water. 5. transfer to a cup and mix 1 tbsp lemon juice. 6. finally, green chutney for chaat is ready to use for sandwich or chaats.

title: Green Moong Dal (Mung Bean Curry)
ingredients: 0.5 cup green gram, 0.5 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1 green chili, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 2.5 garlic, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1.5 cups water, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak green gram for 3 to 4 hours. Avoid over soaking as the skin begins to separate on its own while cooking. Once soaked, discard the water and rinse them well. 2. Heat a pan or pot or pressure cooker with oil. Add mustard seeds (optional) & cumin seeds. 3. When they begins to splutter, fry curry leaves & green chilies for 1 minute. 4. Add onions & saute until golden. Add the ginger garlic & fry for a minute. 5. Next add tomatoes & saute until they break down and turn mushy 6. Stir in salt, turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala & coriander powder. Saute for about 1 to 2 minutes. 7. Add the green gram and pour water. 8. If cooking in a pot: Cook till the green gram is tender & soft. Add more hot water if required, as you cook. 9. If using a pressure cooker, cook on a medium heat for 3 whistles. When the pressure releases, open the lid. 10. If the curry is runny, mash the green moong dal a bit and cook further for a while until it becomes thick. 11. Pour a teaspoon of ghee before serving. Squeeze in some lemon juice. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve with rice or chapati. 12. Rinse the green moong dal well and keep immersed in fresh water until you prepare the ingredients. Press saute button on your Instant pot and wait for the display to show “HOT”. 13. Pour oil or ghee. Add cumin seeds, curry leaves, onions and green chili. 14. Saute until the onions turn light golden. Stir in the ginger garlic & saute for 30 to 60 seconds. 15. Add tomatoes and sprinkle salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 16. Add chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder & turmeric. Saute for 30 seconds & press cancel button. 17. Pour water and mix well at the bottom of the steel insert to deglaze any bits of food stuck. At this stage if you desire you can also blend the onion tomato masala using a hand blender. 18. Drain water from the green gram and add them. (Optional for cooking rice – Place a trivet and then the rice bowl. Cover the rice bowl.) 19. Secure the IP with the lid and position the steam release handle to sealing. 20. Press pressure cook button and set the timer to 12 mins. If using dried mung beans (unsoaked) then cook them for 15 mins. 21. Let the pressure release naturally for 10 mins, then release the rest manually. 22. Garnish green gram dal with coriander leaves. You can also stir in some coconut milk or lemon juice if you want.

title: Green Tomato Chutney Recipe
ingredients: 2.5 cups green tomatoes, 2.5 green chilies, 2 red chilies, 2 garlic cloves, 0.5 tablespoon cumin seeds, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon oil, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.625 teaspoon tamarind, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 pinch mustard seeds, 1 pinch urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon chana dal, 1 garlic cloves, 1 Pinch hing, 0.5 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse the tomatoes well and slice them. Slit or chop green chilies. Crush and peel the garlic cloves. 2. Pour oil to a pan and heat if on a medium heat. Add chana dal and urad dal or peanuts. Fry them until light golden. Add cumin seeds and dried red chilies. 3. Fry until the chilies turn crisp for about 30 to 40 seconds. Add the green chilies, garlic and curry leaves. Fry them until the curry leaves turn crisp and the green chilies are blistered. (If you use peanuts, make sure they are roasted well until aromatic and remove all the roasted ingredients to a plate and set aside) 4. Add tomatoes, salt and turmeric. Stir fry for 2 mins and cook covered on a medium to low heat until soft and mushy. If the pan is too dry you may sprinkle some water. 5. Optional - If your tomatoes are tangy you may leave out using tamarind. Soak tamarind in 3 to 4 tablespoons of water and keep aside. When it softens, squeeze with your fingers to extract the juice. 6. Once the tomatoes are soft, mushy and completely cooked, taste test this and add tamarind only if required (about 1 tsp extract). Turn off the heat and cool this mixture. 7. Keep aside some of the dried/green chilies. Add the rest of the cooled mixture/ingredients along with coriander leaves to a grinder or blender. Grind on a pulse mode to make a slightly coarse chutney. 8. Taste test and add more salt, tamarind or chilies (that we set aside) to suit your taste. Transfer to a serving bowl. 9. Pour ghee or oil to a small pan and heat it on a medium flame. Add mustard, chana dal, urad dal and garlic. When the lentils turn golden, add red chili, curry leaves and hing. 10. Pour this over the green tomato chutney.

title: Grilled Chicken Sandwich
ingredients: 25 grams chicken, 2 tablespoons Greek yogurt, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon herbs, 4 bread slices, 1.5 tablespoons butter, 0.25 cup Mint chutney, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 2 sprigs coriander leaves
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl, add Greek yogurt, black pepper, red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, ginger garlic, turmeric, dried herbs and salt. 2. Mix well and taste test. The marinade has to be thick. Add more salt or ground spices to your taste. Wipe dry the Chicken with few kitchen tissues. 3. Marinate the chicken and rest aside for 30 minutes. You can also refrigerate and use up to 48 hours. The longer it rests the tender and flavorful chicken tastes. 4. Butter the bread slices and toast them until crisp and golden, on a hot grill pan or on a griddle. Remove them and keep aside. 5. Spread a teaspoon of oil or butter over the grill pan or griddle. Once hot, Shake off the excess marinade from the chicken and place it over the pan. 6. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes on a medium to high heat, depending on the thickness of the chicken. At this stage, using a tong turn the chicken to the other side. 7. Grill on both the sides until fully cooked and tender. The internal temperature of the chicken reaches 165F. If the chicken oozes out plenty of moisture, simply grill on the highest heat until it evaporates. 8. Remove the chicken to a wooden board or plate. 9. Optionally - Spread soft butter on the inner side of the toasted bread. This way your bread doesn't get soggy with all the sandwich spread or chutney. 10. Spread your mint chutney or sandwich spread all over the bread. Layer the chicken, followed by slices of onions, tomatoes & coriander leaves or parsley. 11. Cover with another buttered and spread smeared bread slice. Press down well so the sandwich binds together. 12. Serve grilled chicken sandwich immediately.

title: Grilled Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 1 potato, 1 Beetroot, 1 medium onion, 1 tomato, 1 medium cucumber, 0.25 Bell pepper, 4 slices bread, 0.625 teaspoon sandwich masala, 2 tablespoons butter, 2 cheese slices, 1.5 to 2 cups coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon roasted gram, 1.5 green chilies, 1 garlic clove, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.25 tbsp Lemon juice
instructions: 1. Making chutney (optional) - To a blender jar, add coriander leaves, fried gram, cumin, green chilies, garlic cloves, lemon juice, chaat masala and salt. Blend well adding water minimally. I add about 2 tablespoons. 2. Do not add too much water as we do not want a runny chutney. It has to be thick and smooth. Taste the chutney and add more salt & chili if required. 3. Prepare veggies - Pour 1 ½ cups water in a pressure cooker or pot. Place a rack and keep the potatoes over the rack. Pressure cook for 2 whistles or in the instant pot for 10 mins. When the pressure drops, open the lid. Cool, peel and slice the potatoes. 4. If you want to use beetroot, pressure cook the same way or in a pot. 5. Slice the remaining veggies - cucumber, onions, tomatoes & bell peppers. 6. Butter each slice of bread on both the sides. Spread the green chutney as needed. 7. Place the veggies - potatoes, onions, beets, tomatoes, cucumber and then capsicum. Sprinkle a generous amount of chaat masala and cumin powder. 8. Optionally if you want you may add grated cheese or cheese slice. Cover with another slice of buttered & chutney smeared bread. 9. Place them in a hand held or electric griller and grill the sandwiches until golden. You can also grill them on a grill pan, pressing down with a spatula until golden & crisp. 10. Slice the veg grilled sandwich to halves and serve.

title: Guacamole Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 4 bread slices, 1.25 tablespoon unsalted butter, 1 ripe avocado, 3 tablespoons onions, 0.25 cup tomatoes, 2 lettuce leaves, 1.5 garlic, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 green chili, 0.125 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons cilantro fine chopped
instructions: 1. Butter your bread slices on both the sides and toast them on a large pan on a medium heat. You can also do it in the air fryer at 400 F / 200 C for 3 to 4 mins. 2. When one side is done, turn them to the other side and toast it. Do not over toast unless you like very crusty sandwich. Also if you prefer to store it for an hour at room temperature, you can toast one side a little longer so it turns really hard. 3. Meanwhile, fine chop onions, tomatoes, jalapenos, garlic and cilantro. Rinse your lettuce and shake off to drain excess water. 4. Cut open the avocado length-wise. 5. Remove the stone. 6. Scoop the flesh to a mixing bowl. 7. Mash with a fork or a masher to a slightly coarse texture. 8. Add all the other ingredients – onions, tomatoes, cilantro, jalapenos, garlic, salt and black pepper. 9. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt and lemon juice. Place a lettuce over the toasted bread slice and scoop half of the guacamole. Spread it. 10. Cover with another slice of toasted bread. 11. Serve guacamole sandwich immediately. You can also keep this for about an hour an room temperature, if you toast your bread really well.

title: Gujiya Recipe for Diwali
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour, 0.125 teaspoon ⅛ teaspoon (sea salt or table salt), 0.25 cup ghee, 0.3333333333333333 cup water, 2 grams mawa khoya, 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 cup nuts, 1.5 tablespoons sooji fine semolina, 0.25 cup shredded coconut, 0.3333333333333333 cup sugar, 0.75 teaspoon green cardamom, 0.25 teaspoon nutmeg jaiphal
instructions: 1. Heat a medium pan with ½ tbsp ghee and fry mawa for 8 to 10 mins. Transfer to a plate to cool down. 2. Add the remaining ghee and fry the nuts until light golden, for 1 to 2 mins. Stir in the rava and coconut. Fry on a low to medium heat until rava is light golden and crisp. (Takes about 4 mins). Turn off and cool down completely. 3. Meanwhile add sugar, cardamom seeds, mace and nutmeg to a small grinder and grind to a super fine powder. 4. Mix together cooled mawa, rava, coconut, and fried nuts. Stir in the powdered sugar, starting with lower amount and adjust to taste. 5. Mix together flour, salt and ghee with your fingers, until well incorporated & looks like bread crumbs. 6. Pour water little by little to form a non-sticky and slightly stiff dough. It should not be too dry as well. Cover and rest for 15 to 20 mins. 7. When you are ready to shape the gujiya, knead the dough for 3 mins until elastic and pliable. 8. Divide into 15 portions. Knead for a min & roll to smooth balls in between your palms. Keep them covered in the bowl with a cloth or plate. 9. Place a dough ball on a rolling board and roll to a little larger than 4 inch even roti. Using a 4 inch cookie cutter or a steel lid cut to a puri. Remove the excess dough around and keep it covered to reuse later, at the end. 10. Transfer a puri to a parchment placed over a plate/tray. Make all the puris and stack them. 11. Spoon 2 to 3 teaspoons gujiya filling to the center of the puri. Dip your finger in water and smear across the edges. 12. Shape/fold it to a half moon/ semi-circle. Press down the edges to secure the filling. Place on a board and make design with a fork or a pastry cutter wheel or make small folds from one end to the other. Alternately you may use a greased gujiya mold to shape. 13. Transfer to a deep tray and keep the gujiyas covered with a cloth or a tray. This way shape all the gujiyas. Use up the remaing cut out dough and roll to a gujiya. 14. Heat oil in a deep pan/ kadai until slightly hot (medium hot). A small piece of dough added to the oil should sink down and create only a few small bubbles in the oil. (It should not be sizzling hot) 15. Gently slide as many gujiyas (one by one) as your pan fits (I fry 4 to 5) and let them fry for 12 mins on a low heat. Do not disturb/ touch them. When they are firm (at 10 to 12 mins mark), regulate the heat to medium and turn them to the other side with a spider. 16. Fry until golden and crisp. It takes me another 8 mins (total 16 to 20 mins, timing may vary). Remove to a cooling rack. Let the oil temperature come down before you fry the next batch of gujiya. 17. Preheat the air fryer to 360 F - 180 C for 5 mins. Place the gujiyas in the air fryer basket and air fry for 7 mins. 18. Turn them to the other side with a tong and air fry for 5 more mins. 19. Brush them with ghee or oil. Let cool down and brush sugar syrup if you want. Garnish with sliced nuts, crushed dried rose petals or varq.

title: Gulab Jamun Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup milk powder, 5 tbsp all-purpose flour, 1 tsp ghee, 1 tbsp ghee, 3 tbsp milk, 1 tbsp curd, 1 pinch Baking soda, Ghee, 1 tsp pistachios, 1.25 to 1 cups Sugar, 1.5 cup water, 4 pods green cardamom, 1 tsp rose water
instructions: 1. Add 1.5 cups sugar and lightly crushed 4 green cardamoms to a pot. You can also use organic or turbinado sugar, the color of the syrup will be darker. 2. Pour 1.5 cups water. 3. Boil this until the syrup turns slightly sticky. To check this, cool little syrup in a small plate. Dip your fore finger in it and touch with your thumb. You should feel it is slightly sticky. It should not reach one string consistency. 4. If you fail to make the syrup correctly, then the jamuns will not soak the syrup and turn soft. If you go past the sticky syrup stage, you will end up in a one string consistency. In that case, add little water and stir. Check again. Switch off. Add rose water. 5. To a wide pot, add sugar and water. Heat on a medium flame until the sugar dissolves. The let the syrup boil until it reaches a sticky consistency. 6. Add rose water, cardamom powder and lemon juice. Mix well and set it aside to keep the syrup slightly hot. 7. In a mixing bowl, stir together grated mawa, add flour, cardamom powder and then baking powder. 8. Pour milk only as needed little by little to make a smooth crack-free dough. You can gently knead it but don’t over knead as it affects the texture and forms gluten. You must get a dough ball that is smooth with not many cracks. It should not be dry or crumbly. 9. Heat oil in a deep pot or kadai. 10. Check if the oil is hot by gently sliding a small piece of dough. It must go in and rise slowly with bubbles & should not brown. This is the right time to fry the balls. 11. Regulate the flame to medium. Begin to drop one ball at a time. Slowly add as many balls as possible. 12. After frying for few minutes they will firm up. Keep stirring and fry them uniformly until golden brown. If needed switch the stove flame to low and medium. 13. Remove the gulab jamuns to a colander. Continue frying and removing the fried ones to a colander. 14. Allow the balls to cool a bit for 3 to 5 mins. Add them to slightly hot syrup. The syrup should not be very hot. When you dip your finger you must feel the syrup is hot but not very hot. 15. Gently rotate the gulab jamuns in the syrup. Let them soak for 45 mintues before serving. 16. Serve gulab jamun with sugar syrup or vanilla ice cream. Garnish with thinly sliced pistachios.

title: Gulab Jamun Recipe - Milk Powder: Delicious Indian Dessert
ingredients: 0.75 cup milk powder unsweetened, 0.5 cup maida plain flour, 0.5 tsp baking powder, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, milk, ghee, 2 cup sugar, 2 cup water, 2 cardamom, 0.25 tsp saffron kesar, 1 tsp lemon juice, 1 tsp rose water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take ¾ cup milk powder, ½ cup maida and ½ tsp baking powder. 2. mix well, homemade gulab jamun mix is ready. 3. now add 2 tbsp ghee and mix well making the flour moist. 4. further, add milk as required start to combine. 5. combine well forming a soft dough. do not knead the dough. 6. cover and rest for 10 minutes. 7. meanwhile, prepare the sugar syrup by taking 2 cup sugar, 2 cup water, 2 cardamom and ¼ tsp saffron. 8. mix well and boil for 5 minutes or until the sugar syrup turns sticky. do not attain any string consistency. 9. turn off the flame and add 1 tsp lemon juice and 1 tsp rose water. lemon juice is added to prevent sugar syrup from crystallizing. 10. cover and keep the sugar syrup aside. 11. after 10 minutes or resting the dough, start to prepare small ball sized jamuns. 12. make sure there are no cracks in the jamun. if there are cracks then there are high chances for jamuns to break while frying. 13. deep fry in medium hot oil or ghee. frying in ghee gives good flavour to jamuns. 14. stir continuously and fry on low flame. 15. fry until the jamuns turn golden brown. 16. drain off and transfer the jamun into a hot sugar syrup. 17. cover and rest for 2 hours or until jamuns absorb the sugar syrup and doubles in size. 18. finally, enjoy gulab jamun with ice cream or as it is.

title: Gummadikaya Pulusu (Pumpkin Pulusu)
ingredients: 2.25 cups pumpkin, 1 green chili, 0.875 cup onions, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch fenugreek seeds, 1.5 tablespoons tamarind, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons coriander powder, 2 tablespoon jaggery, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 2 tablespoon Oil, 0.5 cup toor dal, 1 cup water, 1 tablespoon ghee
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak tamarind in ½ cup warm water until required later. Squeeze and filter the thick juice. Keep this aside. 2. Heat oil in a deep pot. Add cumin, mustard and methi. When they begin to splutter, add curry leaves and green chili. Sauté for a min. 3. Add onions and sprinkle salt. Fry till they turn slightly pink, needs no browning of onions. 4. Next add the pumpkin pieces, fry on high flame for 2 to 3 mins. 5. Add red chili powder, coriander powder, turmeric and fry for a min. 6. Next add jaggery and pour the tamarind juice. Mix, cover and cook on a low flame for 3 to 4 mins. This helps the pumpkin pieces to absorb the flavors. 7. Add enough water to cover the pumpkin pieces. 8. Cover and cook till pumpkin is form tender. Cooking on a very low flame makes the pulusu tasty. Taste test and add more salt, jaggery and tamarind water if required. 9. Off the heat and add coriander. Keep gummadikaya pulusu covered until served so it absorbs the flavors. 10. Rinse dal well at least thrice and pour water. Cover the bowl. Place this in your cooker over the rice bowl. Pressure cook in the instant pot for 10 minutes or in a stovetop cooker for 3 whistles. 11. When the pressure drops, mash it well. Add salt to taste. It should be thick and smooth. 12. Serve hot Gummadikaya Pulusu with mudda pappu and rice, topped with ghee.

title: Gun Powder Recipe (Paruppu Podi, Kandi Podi)
ingredients: 0.5 cup toor dal, 0.25 cup chana dal, 1 tablespoon cumin seeds, 1 red chilies, 4 medium garlic, 1.5 sprig curry leaves, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt
instructions: 1. On a medium heat, dry roast red chilies in a heavy pan, until they begin to smell pungent and slightly crisp. Do not burn. Remove to a plate. 2. Add toor dal and begin to roast on a medium heat until aromatic and evenly roasted to a golden brown color. It takes about 6 to 7 mins. Transfer to the plate, separate from the chilies. Repeat roasting chana dal and transfer to the plate. 3. Dry roast curry leaves and garlic for 3 to 4 mins until the leaves turn crisp. Add cumin seeds and toast until aromatic. This should take a minute. Stir in the salt and let cool down completely. 4. Grind the chilies, garlic, curry leaves, salt and cumin seeds to a fine powder. Add the lentils and grind to a fine powder, in intervals, scraping the sides as required. You won't get a super fine powder due to the lentils, a very little grainy texture is what is preferred here. 5. Cool down completely and store the gunpowder in a air tight steel or glass jar. Serve it as a side in your South Indian meal. It can be mixed with hot rice and ghee and eaten at the start of the meal.

title: Gutti Vankaya Curry
ingredients: 5 brinjals, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 cup onions, 0.5 cup tomato, 1.5 green chilies, 1.25 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.75 cup water, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 5 red chilies, 0.25 cup peanuts, 2 tablespoons raw coconut, 1.5 tablespoons white sesame seeds, 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 2 green cardamom, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Roast the ingredients: Dry roast peanuts and red chilies until the peanuts turn aromatic and crunchy. Add the coriander seeds, cumin seeds, cloves, cardamoms and cinnamon. Toast until the cumin seeds turn smell aromatic. 2. Make the peanut masala: Add the coconut and toast for 2 mins, followed by sesame. When they begin to splutter, turn off. Cool them and add to a grinder along with salt and lemon juice or tamarind. Grind as fine as possible. 3. Prepare the eggplants: Wash and cut brinjals with one end of the brinjal and the stalk leaving intact. Keep them immersed in salted water till you use. 4. Temper: Heat oil in a pan and add mustard, cumin, curry leaves and hing if using. 5. Make the curry: Once they begin to splutter, add onions and green chilies. Saute till they turn golden. Add ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute. Add chopped tomato, turmeric and salt. Cook until the tomato turns mushy. 6. Stuff the eggplants: Meanwhile stuff the brinjals with as much masala as possible and add them to the pan. Saute just for 2 to 3 minutes. Pour water and the remaining stuffing if any. Cover and cook on a low to medium flame until eggplants are tender, adding more water if required. 7. Taste test and adjust salt or lemon juice. Garnish Gutti Vankaya Curry with finely chopped coriander leaves. Serve with hot rice, ghee or chapati.

title: Hakka Noodles Recipe
ingredients: 1 pack hakka noodles, 2.25 tablespoons oil, 1 tablespoon garlic, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 2.5 spring onion green onions, 1 onion, 1 green chilli, 1 carrot, 0.625 cup capsicum, 6 French beans, 0.5 cup cup cabbage, 1.25 tablespoon soya sauce, 1 teaspoon rice vinegar, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.375 teaspoon sea salt, 1 tablespoon sauce, 0.75 tablespoon chilli oil
instructions: 1. Pour 8 cups of water to a large pot and bring to a rapid boil. Add noodles and cook till al dente. Follow the instructions on the pack for cook time. 2. Drain them to a colander and rinse under running water to remove excess starch. Add ¼ tablespoon oil to the noodles and smear it well to prevent them from turning sticky. 3. In a small bowl, mix soya sauce, hot sauce, vinegar and chili oil if using. Keep it aside. 4. Heat a wok on a high heat and pour oil. Gently coat the oil all over the wok. 5. Add garlic and ginger if using. Fry for 30 seconds and add spring onion whites, onions and chili. Fry until the onions turn transparent for a minute. 6. Next add carrots, bell pepper and cabbage. Stir fry for 1 to 2 minutes until the veggies are partially cooked yet remain crunchy. 7. Add noodles, salt & pour the prepared sauce all over. Toss well and stir fry for about 1 to 2 minutes. Taste test and add more salt or sauces to your taste. Add pepper and spring onion greens. 8. Serve hakka noodles hot with gobi manchurian or chilli paneer.

title: hakka noodles recipe | veg hakka noodles recipe | vegetable noodles
ingredients: 8 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp salt, 2 pack noodles, cold water, 2 tbsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli, 2 inch ginger, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 cabbage, 1 carrot, 2 beans, 2 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 2 tbsp vinegar, few bean sprouts
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large vessel take 8 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp salt. 2. once the water comes to a boil, add 2 pack noodles. 3. stir and boil for 3 minutes or until the noodles turn al dente. refer package instructions to know the cooking time. 4. drain off the noodles and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process. keep aside. 5. in a large wok heat 2 tbsp oil and add 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli, 2 inch ginger and 2 tbsp spring onion. 6. stir fry on high flame. 7. now add ½ onion, ½ capsicum, ½ cabbage, 1 carrot, 2 beans. stir fry until the vegetables shrink slightly. 8. further add 2 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, ½ tsp salt and ½ tsp pepper powder. 9. stir fry until the sauces well combine. 10. now add boiled noodles, 2 tbsp vinegar and stir fry. 11. also add few bean sprouts and 2 tbsp spring onions. stir fry. 12. finally, enjoy veg hakka noodles garnished with more spring onions.

title: Halwa Recipe (Sooji Halwa)
ingredients: 1 cup semolina, 0.5 cup ghee, 0.875 cup sugar, 2.5 cups water, 0.3333333333333333 cup cashews, 9 almonds, 2 tablespoons sweet raisins, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Heat ghee in a pan or pot. Fry cashews until light golden and then stir in the chopped almonds and raisins. 2. Fry until the raisins turn plump. Set aside half of the nuts & raisins if you want for garnish. 3. Reduce the heat and add the semolina to the ghee. Begin to fry on a low to medium heat, stirring continuously. 4. Meanwhile pour water to a pot or kettle and heat it. You don’t need to boil it, just very hot water is enough. 5. When the semolina turns light golden and aromatic, pour all of the hot water at once carefully. 6. Give a good stir to ensure there are no lumps. Cover and cook until all of the water is absorbed, for a few minutes. 7. Stir in the sugar & cardamom powder. You will see the halwa turns runny. At this stage you can also taste test and add more sugar if you want. 8. Cover and cook until all of the sugar has dissolved and the halwa begins to leave the pan. At this stage you can see ghee begins to release from the halwa. If you prefer more ghee, add it at this stage. 9. Add rose water if using and turn off. Add the fried nuts and raisins if you had set aside. 10. Serve Halwa hot or warm as a dessert or with Poori.

title: Handvo Recipe (Baked & Stovetop)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 0.25 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup tuar dal, 2 tablespoons urad dal, 2 tablespoons yellow moong dal, 0.5 cup curd, 0.875 cup bottle gourd, 0.625 cup carrots, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon ginger paste, 1.5 green chilies, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon baking soda, 0.5 teaspoon lemon juice, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons oil, 0.875 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seed, 1.25 teaspoons sesame seeds
instructions: 1. Add rice and lentils to a large pot and rinse well a few times. Drain the water. Soak it in enough water for 3 to 4 hours. 2. Drain the water and add the rice, lentils, plain yogurt/curd and ½ cup to a grinder jar. 3. Blend to a smooth yet slightly coarse and thick batter (idli batter consistency). Add more water carefully if required, we want a thick batter. 4. Cover and ferment overnight or skip the fermentation to use baking soda or eno. 5. When you are ready to make the handvo, grate the vegetables and chop the greens. If you want to bake, preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F) for at least 15 mins. 6. Add the vegetables, salt, turmeric, ginger paste, hing, green chili, red chili powder and sugar to the batter. Stir well. Taste test and adjust the seasonings. 7. Pour oil to a 6 or 7 inch skillet and heat it on a low heat. Add mustard and jeera seeds. When they splutter, add curry leaves. Fry until they turn crisp. 8. Add the sesame seeds and spread them well all over the pan. (If you intend to bake then set aside half of the tempering.) 9. While you perform the above step, add baking soda to the batter and pour lemon juice over it. The mixture will get frothy. Mix it well with the batter. 10. Pour the batter to the hot skillet as much as you want. Gently spread the top evenly. (for baking, sprinkle the tempering we set aside all over on the top) 11. To bake in oven: Turn off the stove and transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 35 to 45 minutes until a tester inserted comes out clean. The baking time depends on the pan size. For a 6 to 7 inch skillet it takes 35 to 40 mins. For the top golden crust you may broil for 2 mins. 12. Stovetop: Cover and cook on a low heat, until the top layer becomes almost dry and the bottom layer turns golden brown. This takes about 5 to 7 mins, flip it gently. The cook time depends on how much batter you pour and the size of your pan. 13. Cook the other side until crisp and golden. Flip again and cook on a medium heat until crusty. 14. Repeat with the remaining batter to make handvo. 15. Remove the handvo to a wired rack and cool before cutting it. Serve with chutney or any sauce.

title: handvo recipe | how to make gujarati handvo recipe | mixed dal handvo
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 0.5 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup toor dal, 2 tbsp urad dal, 0.5 cup curd yogurt, 1 cup bottle gourd lauki, 0.5 cup cabbage, 0.25 cup carrot, 3 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 1 green chilli, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 2 tsp oil, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 tsp eno fruit salt, 3 tsp oil, 0.75 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp sesame til, few curry leaves, pinch hing asafoetida
instructions: 1. firstly, soak 1 cup rice, ½ cup chana dal, ¼ cup toor dal, 2 tbsp urad dal for 3-4 hours. 2. add ½ cup curd and blend to a smooth but slightly coarse paste. 3. further add 1 cup grated bottle gourd, ½ cup grated cabbage, ¼ cup grated carrot and 3 tbsp coriander. 4. also add ½ tsp ginger paste, 1 green chilli, ½ tsp sugar, ¼ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, 2 tsp oil and ¾ tsp salt. 5. mix well making sure the batter is slightly thick like idli batter consistency. 6. now add 1 tsp eno / fruit salt and mix gently. 7. bake the handvo in over or cook on tawa

title: Hara Bhara Kabab Recipe (Veg Kabab)
ingredients: 3 grams potatoes, 0.5 cup coriander leaves, 1 cup green peas, 2 green chilies, 12 grams spinach, 0.75 inch ginger, 1 teaspoon oil, 2 tablespoon roasted gram flour, 0.25 cup bread crumbs, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 1.5 tablespoons oil
instructions: 1. Pluck and rinse the spinach well in lots of water thrice. Transfer them to a colander to ensure all the water has drained. Keep these aside. 2. Rinse and boil potatoes in a pot or cooker until fork tender (just cooked without turning mushy). To boil in pressure cooker, cook for 3 to 4 whistles. To cook in Instant pot, I cut each potato to 2 pieces and pressure cooked them 6 minutes. 3. Cool them completely and peel the potatoes. 4. Pour 1 teaspoon oil to a pan and heat it. Saute ginger & green chilies for 30 seconds. 5. Then add green peas and saute them for 4 to 5 mins, until the raw smell goes away. 6. Add palak & saute on a medium high flame until it wilts off completely. (Or If you are on a low oxalate diet, then you can also blanch the palak in 3 cups hot water, drain completely and use it to blend with green peas.) But the sauteing method tastes best. 7. Turn off the stove and cool all of these. Add them to a small grinder/ blender along with coriander leaves. 8. Blend all of them to a smooth paste without adding water. 9. Crumble the potatoes in a mixing bowl & add the ground green peas and spinach paste. 10. Add salt, garam masala, chaat masala, roasted gram flour and bread crumbs. 11. Mix all of them to a non-sticky dough. If the dough turns sticky, then add more bread crumbs to absorb excess moisture. This is optional. 12. Divide the dough to 10 portions. Roll them to balls. Flatten them to shape like patties or kababs. Keep them aside. 13. To pan fry: Heat a griddle with 1 tablespoon oil. When the pan turns hot, place the hara bhara kababs on the pan and pan fry them until golden & crisp. 14. To grill them in oven: Preheat the oven to 200 C for 15 minutes and place the patties on a greased tray. Brush the kababs with oil and grill them for 10 mins on each side. Adjust the timings as needed. 15. To air fry: Brush them with oil and place them in the basket. Air fry at 180 C for 10 mins on each side. After 10 mins check and flip them to the other side and air fry for another 10 mins. Adjust the timings as needed. If your air fryer needs preheating, preheat it before air frying them. 16. Serve hara bhara kabab with yogurt cilantro chutney, ketchup or green chutney.

title: Hariyali chicken (green chicken)
ingredients: 5 gms chicken, 2 tbsp oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup yogurt, 0.75 cup water, 0.5 tsp Salt, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 cup coriander leaves with tender stalks, 0.5 cup mint leaves, 1 medium onion, 3 green chilies, 3 green cardamoms
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin. When they begin to splutter then add cumin. The seeds will sizzle then add the ginger garlic and fry for a minute until the raw smell goes off. 2. Add 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala and the chicken. Saute for 2 to 3 mins until pale. 3. Then add the prepared hara masala. 4. Saute for 3 to 4 mins until the raw smell of onion goes off. 5. Optional – Reduce the flame to low. Then add in whisked yogurt if using. I used about 4 tbsps and sauteed until the yogurt blended well. Cook on a low heat until yogurt is absorbed. 6. Then pour water or coconut milk. Give a good stir and add salt. 7. Simmer until the chicken is tender and cooked through well. When done the chicken will become fork tender. 8. Optional – I prefer to saute some bell peppers & curry leaves in oil and add them towards the end. This enhances the flavor. Serve green chicken with rice or roti. 9. Add coriander leaves, mint leaves, green chilies, green cardamoms, onion, green chilies to a blender jar. If you do not have ginger garlic paste, then add half inch ginger and 3 medium garlic cloves to the same jar. 10. Make a fine paste without adding water. Set this aside. 11. Heat a pan with oil. Add cumin and allow them to crackle. 12. Then saute the curry leaves until crisp. Then add ginger garlic paste and fry for a minute. 13. Add garam masala, coriander powder and chicken. Fry till it turns white in color. 14. Cover and cook for 2 to 3 mins. 15. Add green paste, fry for 3 to 4 mins until a nice aroma comes out. 16. Cover and cook for another 2 mins. This way the aroma of green masala and spice is absorbed by the chicken. 17. Optional – Turn the flame to low. If using yogurt whisk it very well until smooth and then add it to the pan. Stir and cook for 3 to 4 mins till the yogurt is absorbed. 18. Then pour water & add salt. 19. Stir well and cook covered until the chicken is tender. 20. Open the lid and cook until the gravy reaches a desired consistency. 21. Serve hariyali chicken with rice or roti. title: hariyali paneer tikka recipe | paneer hariyali tikka | dry paneer hariyali
ingredients: 0.5 cup curd yogurt, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp coriander powder daniya powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp ginger - garlic paste, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 2 tsp besan gram flour, 1 tbsp lemon juice, salt to taste, 3 tsp oil, 0.5 cup coriander leaves, 0.25 cup mint leaves pudina leaves, 2 green chilli, 0.5 onions, 0.5 capsicum, 5 cubes paneer cottage cheese
instructions: 1. firstly, take ½ cup thick curd / yogurt. 2. further add in all spices along with green chutney and salt. 3. mix till all the spices are combined well with curd. 4. now add ½ onion petals, ½ cubed capsicum (red & green) and 5 cubes paneer. 5. mix gently to coat vegetables well. 6. furthermore, to marinate, cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes. 7. after marination, insert the marinated paneer, capsicum and onions into wooden skewers. 8. further, roast it on a hot tawa or grill in oven or tandoor. 9. also spread a tsp of oil over the tikka. 10. finally, sprinkle some chaat masala and serve hariyali paneer tikka immediately.

title: hesaru bele tove recipe | moong dal recipe
ingredients: 1 cup hesaru bele, 1 medium size onion, 1 medium size tomato, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, 0.3333333333333333 tsp turmeric powder, 3 cups water, salt as required, 1 tsp saasuve mustard seeds, 1 tsp cumin seeds jeera, 0.25 or tsp garam masala powder, 1 or 2 dried red chilli, a pinch of asafoetida, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 finely chopped garlic, tbsp oil
instructions: 1. finely chop onion, tomato and ginger 2. wash the hesaru bele or dal and add onion, tomato, ginger, turmeric and chilli powder and pressure cook for 1 whistle. 3. remove the lid of pressure cooker and check the consistency. if you feel it is thick add some water. 4. switch on the flame, and add salt and garam masala powder and cook for 2-3 minutes. 5. in a small pan, heat oil or ghee. add mustard seeds and cumin seeds and fry for 1-2 minutes till it starts splutter. 6. add the garlic and red chili and fry for some seconds. switch of the flame. 7. add hing or asafoetida. 8. add the seasoning mixture to moong dal. 9. stir the moong dal and serve hot moong dal with steamed rice, chapatis or fried rice. garnish with coriander leaves to it.

title: Home Remedies For Back Pain - Natural Remedies
ingredients: 4 tbsp ghee, 0.25 cup gond edible gum, 0.5 cup badam, 0.5 cup walnut, 0.5 cup pistachios, 5 dry khajoor, 0.5 cup sun flower seeds, 0.5 cup pumpkin seeds, 0.5 cup flaxseeds, 2 tbsp chia seeds, 2 tbsp sesame seeds, 2 tbsp poppy seeds, 2 tbsp fennel, 1 cup makhana, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 cup dry coconut, 0.5 cup raisins, 1 cup jaggery, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 cup roasted chana dal, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp ghee and fry ¼ cup gond. 2. fry on low flame until the gond puffs up. keep aside. 3. in the same ghee, add ½ cup badam, ½ cup walnut, ½ cup pistachios, and 5 dry khajoor. 4. roast on low flame until the nuts turn crunchy. keep aside. 5. further in the pan take ½ cup sunflower seeds, ½ cup pumpkin seeds, ½ cup flaxseeds, 2 tbsp chia seeds, 2 tbsp sesame seeds, 2 tbsp poppy seeds, and 2 tbsp fennel. 6. roast until the nuts turn crunchy. 7. transfer to the plate and keep aside. 8. also, roast 1 cup makhana until it turns crunchy. 9. cool completely, making sure all the nuts and seeds are cooled. 10. transfer to the mixer jar and pulse to a coarse powder. keep aside. 11. in a pan heat 2 tbsp ghee and add 1 cup rava. 12. roast the rava on low flame until the rava turns golden brown and aromatic. 13. further, add ½ cup dry coconut and ½ cup raisins. 14. roast until the coconut turns golden brown. keep aside. 15. in a large kadai, take 1 cup jaggery and ½ cup water. 16. stir and dissolve the jaggery completely. continue to boil until the 1 string jaggery syrup is attained. 17. further add roasted rava, powdered nuts, ½ cup roasted chana dal, and ½ tsp cardamom powder. 18. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 19. finally, enjoy 1 tbsp of nuts mixture every day to cure back pain problems.

title: Homemade Cappuccino without Machine in Mixer Blender - 3 Steps
ingredients: 0.5 cup instant coffee powder, 0.5 cup sugar, 4 ice cubes, milk, chocolate sauce
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar take ½ cup instant coffee powder and ½ cup sugar. 2. Pulse and grind to a fine powder. You can also use hand beater to make coffee. 3. Now add 4 ice cubes, pulse, and blend. 4. Scrape in between to prevent heating and uniform grinding. 5. Continue to pulse and grind until the mixture turns thick and frothy. Also, you can notice the mixture has turned light brown. 6. You can store the cappuccino mixture in an airtight box for a week in the refrigerator. 7. To make coffee, take 1 tbsp of prepared cappuccino mixture. 8. Pour in hot milk and mix well. 9. You can decorate and design Homemade Cappuccino with chocolate sauce.

title: homemade garlic bread recipe | simple & easy garlic bread recipe 1
ingredients: 0.25 cup warm milk, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp dry yeast any yeast, 1.5 cups maida plain flour all-purpose flour, salt to taste, 1 tsp oil, 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 3 cloves garlic
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare the bread and cut bread into thick slices without cutting all the way through. 2. furthermore, combine butter, coriander and garlic in a bowl. 3. spread mixture over cut sides of bread slices. 4. wrap the prepared bread loaf in foil and bake for 10 minutes or until butter is melted and bread turns crisp. 5. finally, serve garlic bread hot along with veg pizza.

title: Homemade Horlicks Recipe | How To Make Horlicks At Home
ingredients: 1 cup wheat, water, 3 tbsp badam almonds, 2 tbsp peanut, 0.25 cup mishri rock candy, 4 pods cardamom, 0.75 cup milk powder, 1 tbsp cocoa powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup wheat in water for 12 hours. make sure to rinse the wheat a couple of times before soaking. 2. after soaking the wheat, drain off the water and tie in a cloth. 3. rest for 2 days or until the sprouts are formed. 4. now take the sprouted wheat and roast on low flame. 5. roast until the wheat turns crisp, removing the moisture from the wheat. 6. now add 3 tbsp badam, 2 tbsp peanut, and continue to roast. 7. roast until the nuts turn crisp too. 8. cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 9. add ¼ cup mishri, 4 pods cardamom, and grind to a fine powder. 10. transfer the mixture to the sieve. 11. add ¾ cup milk powder and sieve using a fine sieve. 12. mix well combining everything well. 13. finally, homemade Horlicks are ready to enjoy by mixing them with warm milk. 14. to prepare chocolate-flavoured horlicks, for 4 tbsp of prepared horlicks, add 1 tbsp cocoa powder. 15. mix well combining everything well. 16. finally, Homemade Chocolate Horlicks are ready to enjoy by mixing them with warm milk.

title: Homemade Paneer - 2 Ways | Making Paneer Cheese at Home
ingredients: 3 litre milk, half lemon, 2 tbsp water
instructions: 1. Firstly, rinse a thick-bottomed vessel to prevent milk from burning. 2. Add 3 litres of milk and bring to a boil by stirring occasionally. 3. Once the milk is about to boil, add in lemon juice. Make sure to mix lemon juice with water to reduce the concentration. 4. Stir well till the milk curdles. 5. Add lemon water until the milk curdles completely, and the water separates. 6. Now, drain off the curdled milk over cheesecloth. 7. Rinse with cold water and squeeze out the water. 8. Shape the paneer into a block, and keep the heavy object over it. 9. Rest for 20 minutes or until the paneer sets completely. 10. After 20 minutes, the cottage cheese is set well and ready to cut. 11. Finally, store the Homemade Paneer in an airtight container and use it for a week when refrigerated. 12. Rest for 20 minutes or until the paneer sets completely. After 20 minutes, the cottage cheese is set well and ready to cut.

title: Homemade Ubtan Face Mask Recipe | Mamaearth Ubtan Face Mask
ingredients: 0.25 cup masoor dal, 0.25 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup oats, 0.25 cup rice, 2 tbsp sesame, 1 almonds, 2 tbsp sandalwood powder, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp ubtan powder, 2 tbsp curd, 1 tsp honey
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan take ¼ cup masoor dal, ¼ cup chana dal, ¼ cup oats, ¼ cup rice, 2 tbsp sesame, and 10 almonds. 2. dry roast on low flame until the dal turns aromatic. and the moisture in lentils is lost. 3. cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 4. add 2 tbsp sandalwood powder and 1 tsp turmeric. 5. grind to fine powder making sure the pulses are ground smooth. 6. you can sieve using a fine sieve and grind the coarse powder again. 7. transfer the powder into the glass jar and ubtan powder is ready. 8. to make mask, in a bowl take 1 tbsp ubtan powder and 2 tbsp curd. 9. mix well forming a smooth paste. 10. if your skin is dry, then make sure to add 1 tsp honey. 11. apply the ubtan mask on the face and body. 12. rinse off with cold water after 30 minutes. 13. finally, Homemade Ubtan Face Mask Recipe can be stored for 3 months in an airtight container.

title: Horse Gram Dal Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup Horse gram, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon chana dal, 1 red chilli, 2 garlic cloves, 1 green chilli, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon red chlli powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 3.5 tablespoon coconut
instructions: 1. Add horse gram to a pot. Pick and discard any stone and debris. Rinse very well 4 to 5 times. Pour 3 cups water and soak them overnight. 2. In the morning discard the water if you want. A lot of people want to use the same water since all of the flavor is in the soaked water. 3. On a medium heat, pressure cook the lentils with 1 cup water for 1 whistle. Lower the heat and cook for another 30 minutes, until soft and tender. To cook in instant pot, press the pressure cook button and set the timer to 10 mins. 4. When the pressure drops, open the lid and check horse gram has to be soft yet hold its shape. If the lentils are still undercooked pressure with a little more water for little longer. 5. Pour oil or ghee to a pan and heat it. Turn the flame to medium and add mustard, cumin, dal and red chilli. 6. Saute until the dal turns golden. Then add crushed garlic, green chili and curry leaves. 7. Fry till the garlic and chilli begins to smell good. Reduce the heat and add salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, cumin powder and coriander powder. 8. Stir and add the cooked horse gram along with the stock (water in which they were cooked). Increase the flame slightly and cook covered so they absorb the flavors of the spices. 9. After 3 mins, open cook until all of the water evaporates. Taste test and add more salt if required. Add the coconut and mix well. Turn off the heat. 10. Squeeze some lemon juice before serving the tempered horse gram dal with plain rice, roti or a side with rasam rice.

title: hot and sour soup recipe | hot n sour soup | hot sour soup recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion, 1 carrot, 3 tbsp cabbage, 0.5 capsicum, 5 beans, 4 cup water, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp spring onion, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.25 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and stir fry 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger and 1 chilli. 2. add 2 tbsp spring onion and stir fry until it softens. 3. further, add 1 carrot, 3 tbsp cabbage, ½ capsicum and 5 beans. 4. stir fry for 2 minutes or until the veggies are half cooked. 5. add 4 cup water, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar and 1 tsp chilli sauce. 6. also, add ½ tsp pepper powder, ½ tsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. 7. boil for 3 minutes or until flavours is absorbed well. 8. now prepare cornflour slurry by mixing 2 tbsp cornflour in ¼ cup water. 9. pour the slurry into the soup and mix well. 10. continue to boil for 2 more minutes or until soup thickens slightly. 11. finally, add 2 tbsp spring onion and enjoy hot and sour soup.

title: How to cook Millet (Lemon Millet)
ingredients: 0.5 cup millet, 1.5 cups water, Salt as needed, 1.5 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 1.25 tbsp bengal gram, 1.25 tbsp skinned black gram, 0.25 cup peanuts, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1 green chili, 1 red chilli broken, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. Add millets to a large pot and rinse them well a few times rubbing with your fingers. Here I have used kodo millet. I prefer to soak them for 30 mins as they cook faster and lighter to digest. You can cook proso millet, little millet and barnyard millet the same way. It is optional and you can skip it too. 2. Open pan/ pot method: 1:3 ratio works well for me. Add water along with salt to the pot and cook them on a medium flame until the water is absorbed. When there is little moisture, cover and cook on the lowest flame until soft and tender. Fluff up gently with a fork. Cool this completely. 3. First heat oil in a pan. Add mustard and allow to splutter. Add chana dal, urad dal, red chili and green chili. If you do not prefer heat from the green chili add it later with ginger. 4. Add cashews and fry all until the dals turn golden. 5. Add curry leaves, ginger and green chili if you have not added earlier. Saute for a minute until the leaves turn crisp. 6. Add hing and turmeric. Pour 3 to 4 tablespoons water and cook until all the water evaporates. This will bring out the aroma of the spices and soften the dal slightly. If you want more softer dals, then you can 1 to 2 tbsps more water. 7. Turn off the heat. Add the cooled millets and squeeze in lemon juice. 8. Lastly mix everything well. Serve lemon millet with curd, pickle, papad or vegetable salad.

title: How to Cook Oatmeal (Oatmeal with Milk)
ingredients: 0.5 cup rolled oats, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup milk, 0.5 cup instant, 1 cup milk, 0.25 cup steel cut oats, 0.75 cup water, 1 cups milk, 7 dates, 1 nuts
instructions: 1. Rolled Oats: Pour water to a pot, bring it to a boil and stir in oats. Let cook for 1 to 2 mins, until most of the water is absorbed. Pour milk and bring to a boil. 2. Reduce the heat and let simmer for another 3 to 4 mins, until it reaches a slightly thick & creamy consistency. Oatmeal will thicken upon cooling, so turn off when it is slightly runny yet thick. 3. Instant Oats: Stir in milk and instant oats and bring it to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat and simmer until thick and creamy, yet slightly runny. 4. Steel Cut Oats:: Rinse the steel cut oats and soak them overnight. Next morning drain the soaked water and pour fresh water. Bring to a rolling boil and turn the heat down. Cook until tender, adding more water if required. Add milk and simmer until thick and creamy. 5. Serving: If you want to sweeten with sugar you may add it now. Cool down slightly and transfer to serving bowl. Add sliced nuts and dates or peanut butter & bananas. 6. With Instant Oats: Pour milk and instant oats to a bowl. Place the bowl one the trivet, with 1 cup water in the steel insert. Do not cover the bowl. Secure the IP with the lid, with vent in sealing mode. Pressure cook for 0 mins. Let the pressure release naturally. Stir and add your favorite toppings. 7. With Rolled Oats: Pour the milk and oats to the inner pot of the Instant pot. Mix and secure the lid of the IP. Press the pressure cook button and set the timer to 1 minute high pressure for rolled oats. 8. With Steel Cut oats: Pour water, milk and add rinsed steel cut oats. Mix well and secure the Instant pot. Position the vent to sealing. Press porridge button and set the timer for 10 mins. 9. Allow the pressure to release naturally for 10 mins. Stir the oatmeal and remove to serving bowls. Top with fruits, nuts, dates, maple syrup or chocolate.

title: How to Make Almond Flour
ingredients: 1.5 cups almonds, 8 cups water
instructions: 1. In a large and wide pan, bring water to a rapid boil. Switch off the heat. 2. Immediately add almonds to the pan, Spread evenly. Do not crowd them. 3. Let them rest for a min or two. Drain them in a colander. Rinse under running water. 4. Drain them to a colander and transfer to a clean kitchen cloth. 5. Spread them. The skin will loosen on its own. You can just remove the skin easily. Peel them or (You can hold each almond in between your thumb and forefinger and squeeze it off inside the napkin to prevent it popping elsewhere in the kitchen.) 6. Transfer these to a wide pan, and lightly roast them on a low heat until they look moist free. Keep tossing them to prevent discoloring. Cool them. 7. Add them to a grinder and pulse them in intervals of 30 o 40 seconds. Do not run the grinder too long else the almonds may begin to release oil. If you want you may sieve this. 8. Transfer almond flour to a air tight glass jar and store it in a dry cabinet or in the refrigerator. You can also freeze this for 3 months.

title: How to Make Aloe Vera Juice
ingredients: 1 aloe vera leaf, 2 inch aloe vera gel, 0.875 cup water, 1 teaspoon sweetener, 1 tablespoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Spray some vinegar and scrub the leaf well with a brush. Rinse this very well under running water & pat dry. Be sure to wash your hands to make sure you don’t contaminate it. Some people are sensitive to aloe latex. It can irritate the skin and cause rashes & itch. So please wear gloves. 2. Lay the leaf flat on a cutting board and trim off the bottom thick white part as shown in the picture below. 3. Either hold the cut side down over a bowl or place the cut side down in a tall container, so the aloe leaf is in an upright position. This allows the aloin, which appears as a yellowish sap, to drain. Removing the aloin gets rid of possible bitter flavors and prevents stomach upset. 4. Leave it for 10 mins in the upright position. Later wipe off with a clean kitchen tissue. 5. You will see some of the latex like this. 6. This is not edible directly. It is processed and medically used as a laxative. Discard this. 7. You can work on the whole leaf at one time or just cut out 1 small portion of the leaf & use every time. If you want to remove the gel and store it, cut the leaf to several pieces like shown below. This is a easier way to extract maximum gel. So I prefer to cut the leaf into smaller pieces (2 inches) and discard the top end (about 2 inches as it is best to discard). 8. Add them to a large bowl of water & rest for 1 to 2 minutes. If you are working with the whole leaf, you may rinse it under running water. Once the aloin is drained to the water, remove them. Discard and refill the bowl with fresh water and rinse them again and remove from water. 9. Wipe dry a piece of leaf with a kitchen tissue. 10. Lay it flat on a cutting board. Remove the spiny / thorny edges of the leaf with a sharp knife. 11. If you have really large leaf like mine, you may again cut each to 2 pieces so it is easy to work. 12. I prefer to juice fresh every time to minimize oxidization & it only takes 5 mins. So I add 2 inches portion of aloe gel along with ¼ cup chilled water or juice to a blender to make 2 servings. If you prefer to make the gel at one time and store it, you may blend all of the gel at one time and store it. More in the Pro tips section below. 13. Blend only for a 30 to 60 seconds until the aloe vera gel is fully crushed. Do not over do as we don’t want to rise the temperature of the gel. 14. Strain this and run a spoon all around to let much of the gel pass through the strainer. You will need some more water or juice at this stage so most of the gel passes through. Sometimes you may see some very thin green particles in the strainer simply discard it. 15. Divide the aloe juice to 2 serving cups and top with more juice or with water. If you use water, add some lemon juice. To sweeten it add some honey or any other sweetener. 16. You can make this in many flavors. I poured some aloe gel to serving cups. Then poured orange juice to one serving and added some frozen mango puree cubes to another and topped with some cold water. Add what you like to make it delicious.

title: how to make biriyani rice recipe | how to prepare non sticky rice for fried rice & pulao
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, water, 1 tsp pepper, 1 star anise, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 1 pod black cardamom, 4 pod cardamom, 0.5 tsp clove, 3 bay leaf tej patta, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp lemon juice, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 2 bay leaf tej patta, 1 tsp pepper, 1 star anise, 5 pod cardamom, 0.5 tsp clove, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 1 pod black cardamom, 1 cup basmati rice, water, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup basmati rice and rinse well. 2. soak in enough water for 20 minutes. 3. in a large vessel take enough water and add 1 tsp pepper, 1 star anise and ½ inch cinnamon. 4. also add 1 pod black cardamom, 4 pod cardamom, ½ tsp clove and 3 bay leaf. 5. now add 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp lemon juice and 1 tsp ghee. 6. boil for 2 minutes or until the water is infused with flavours. 7. add in soaked basmati rice and give a good mix. 8. boil for 2 minutes or until it is 90% cooked. 9. drain off the basmati rice and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process. 10. finally, basmati rice is ready. 11. in a large kadai take 1 tsp ghee and add 2 bay leaf, 1 tsp pepper, 1 star anise and 5 pods cardamom. 12. also add ½ tsp clove, ½ inch cinnamon and 1 pod black cardamom. 13. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 14. add in rinsed basmati rice and saute on low flame for 2 minutes. 15. further add water, 1 tsp salt and 1 tsp lemon juice. mix well. 16. boil for 2 minutes or until it is 90% cooked. 17. drain off the basmati rice and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process. 18. finally, basmati rice is ready to prepare biryani, pulao or fried rice.

title: how to make butter recipe, ghee recipe, buttermilk & whipped cream from cream
ingredients: 1.2 liter pure cream
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1.2 liter cream. you can take any heavy cream, whipping cream, or homemade cream of at least 35% milk for best results. 2. using a hand beater start to beat on low speed. you can use a stand mixer or food processor. 3. the cream thickens, continue to beat until stiff peaks appear. whipped cream will be ready. 4. continue to beat further on low speed for another 10 minutes. 5. the whipped cream will collapse and decrease in volume. 6. keep beating and the cream turns yellowish. 7. after 20 minutes, the water separates resulting into butter and buttermilk. 8. scrape the edges and collect the butter together. 9. butter and buttermilk is ready. you can store the buttermilk in a glass jar and use it for 3-4 days for preparing curry, cake. 10. now collect the butter and form a ball squeezing off the water. 11. squeeze and rinse in ice-cold water to remove any buttermilk. 12. shape the butter and wrap in butter paper. 13. refrigerate for 4 hours to harden. you can use butter for 2 weeks and use it as a spread or in any cooking. 14. to prepare ghee, in a large kadai take butter. 15. keeping the flame on medium, melt the butter completely. 16. continue to stir until the butter changes color and turns frothy. 17. now, boil for a minute or until it turns golden brown and aromatic. the bubbles turn transparent. 18. cool slightly, and filter out the ghee to remove any residue. 19. finally, ghee is ready to use and can be consumed for a month.

title: How to make Celery Juice
ingredients: 1 bunch organic celery, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. Cut down the root of the celery to separate the stalks. You may reserve the leaves for another recipe. Rinse the stalks very well under running water. 2. Trim off the tops and discard. Chop them to 2 inch pieces. 3. Pour ¼ cup cold water to your blender and add the celery and squeeze the lemon juice if you want. 4. Blend until smooth. Place a strainer & a nut bag over a bowl and pour the blended celery juice. Lift the bag and squeeze to extract all of the juices. 5. Serve celery juice immediately or pour to a jar and refrigerate. Consume with in 2 days. 6. Start your juicer. Add the celery stalks to the juicer chute. You may add any other fruits like apple, watermelon or Asian pear to make the juice more palatable. 7. Collect the juice from the spout of your juicer and squeeze lemon juice. 8. Serve celery juice immediately or transfer to a mason jar & refrigerate for up to 2 days.

title: How to Make Curd (Dahi - Indian Yogurt)
ingredients: 1 liter milk, 1 teaspoon curd dahi
instructions: 1. Rinse a pot well. This reduces the chances of milk solids getting stuck to the bottom. 2. Pour milk and bring it to boil on a medium to low flame. 3. To get thick curd, once it comes to a boil simmer the milk for 15 mins on a very low flame. 4. Keep stirring in between else the milk will get burnt and smell bad. 5. Simmering step is to get a very thick curd. You can skip this if you are ok to have a moderately thick yogurt. 6. Allow the milk to cool down. 7. If you are intending to use a curd culture that has come down to room temperature, then allow the boiled milk to come down to luke warm temp. 8. But if you are using the culture directly from the fridge, then the temperature of the milk must be mildly warmer than the luke warm. 9. Immerse your clean finger in the milk and check. or Your food thermometer should ideally read 110 to 115 F (or 43 to 46 C). Immerse your thermometer into the milk and check. 10. Add a tsp of curd to the warm milk and stir well. You will have to experiment with the amount of starter to use. 11. In India, my mother uses less than half tsp for 1 liter milk as it is not homogenized milk. But in Singapore I use about 1 tbsp or even more for 1 liter. It depends on the milk, climate and starter. 12. Cover & move the bowl to a warm place. 13. Allow it to set for 6 to 10 hrs depending on the climate. 14. If you wish to set the curd well, you can break a red chili and drop it in the milk. it is optional and does not make the dahi or curd spicy or hot. 15. If you live in a cold place, then keep it in a casserole or thermocol box or oven with the light on. 16. Next if you intend to make curd in small bowls, check the step by step instructions after the recipe card. 17. When the curd is set, move it to refrigerator. 18. Ensure your sealing ring and instant pot are clean and are free from any kind of strong flavors from the previous cooking. Pour 1 cup water to the steel insert of your instant pot. 19. Pour 4 cups of milk to a stainless steel bowl. Place a trivet and then place the bowl of milk over it. Do not cover the bowl. 20. Secure the instant pot with the lid and position the steam release value to sealing. Press pressure cook button and set the timer to “zero”. 21. The Instant pot beeps when it is done. Let the pressure release naturally. Carefully remove the bowl and let the milk cool down to warm temperature. 22. When the milk is warm, place your clean finger to ensure it is not too hot. It must be warm. Stir in half teaspoon yogurt and place the bowl back on the trivet in the instant pot. The pot should not be too hot. 23. If you live in a cold region or during winters, you may turn on instant pot & press the yogurt button. But during other times, you don’t need to turn on the instant pot at all. I usually place the bowl in the instant pot without turning it on. The residual heat from the previous cooking will be enough to set the curd. 24. It will take 6 to 8 hours for the curd to set well. Remove the bowl and place it in refrigerator.

title: How to make Ghee (Ghee Recipe)
ingredients: 227 grams unsalted butter, 5 sprigs curry leaves
instructions: 1. Add unsalted butter to a heavy bottom deep pot and begin to heat it on a medium flame. Using a deep & heavy bottom pot is essential to avoid overflowing. 2. I always leave a ladle in the pot as it prevents overflowing. 3. As the butter begin to turn hotter, it will froth up a lot. Give a good stir and reduce the flame. 4. Soon the froth evaporates and butter begins to bubble rapidly. 5. Within few minutes, the color of the butter begins to change from yellow to light golden. 6. At this stage, the milk solids begin to settle at the bottom & the bubbles will reduce significantly. You can see them in the picture. I have shown little milk solids in the ladle. When they just begin to turn light brown, remove the pot from the stove. 7. Ghee will continue to cook in the hot pot and brown the milk solids. Let the temperature cool down a bit. 8. Place a clean and dry strainer/filter over a clean jar. Pour the ghee through the strainer/ filter. Press down the milk solids. I usually strain the ghee to several small jars. This helps the ghee to remain fresh for longer. 9. Culture the cream : Follow either method (A) or (B) or (C) based on your convenience. Methods B and C are traditionally followed. 10. Method A – Heat up 2 cups of heavy cream until warm enough. Stir in 2 tablespoons of yogurt (at room temperature). Cover and keep it in a warm place for 8 to 16 hours as you would keep to make yogurt. When your cultured cream is ready, it smells like yogurt. Chill it for few hours and then proceed to step 2 to make your own cultured butter. 11. Method B – Boil non-homogenized full fat milk. When the temperature comes down you will see a layer of cream over it. Make yogurt using both the cream and milk, following this post on homemade yogurt or curd . 12. When the yogurt is set remove the top portion of the cultured cream and store in a separate jar. Refrigerate and keep adding the cream to it every time you make yogurt. You will need at least a cup of this cultured cream to go ahead and make your butter, followed by ghee. 13. Method C – Boil milk and let it cool down for a few hours. Remove the milk cream that forms on top & store in a separate jar. Refrigerate this and keep adding more cream every time you boil the milk. 14. When you have a cup of this cream, heat it to warm temperature and then add a tbsp of yogurt/curd culture to this and mix well. Allow this to set in a warm place like regular yogurt for 5 to 8 hours depending on the temperature.

title: How to Make Ice Cream (Ice Cream Recipe)
ingredients: 1 can sweetened condensed milk, 2 cups Heavy whipping cream, 2 teaspoons vanilla extract, 1.5 cups Heavy cream, 0.75 cup sugar, 2 teaspoons cornstarch, 0.75 cup full fat milk, 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
instructions: 1. Chill all the ingredients, mixing bowl and beaters in the refrigerator for 12 to 24 hours. Also freeze a metal tray or container overnight to store your ice cream. 2. Pour the chilled cream to the chilled bowl. Begin to whip on a low speed first. Then increase the speed to medium and whip to stiff peaks. 3. It takes about 7 to 8 mins to get to the stiff peak stage, on medium speed. Be careful not to over whip the cream as it can turn grainy. 4. Pour the condensed milk and vanilla to the whipped cream. Gently fold to incorporate the condensed milk into the whipped cream. Do not mix vigorously so you don't deflate the whipped cream. 5. Transfer this to a freezer safe container and place a parchment paper or cling wrap. Press down on to the ice cream to remove any trapped air in between the ice cream and the parchment paper. 6. Freeze until set, for 8 to 15 hours. If the ice cream is too hard, place it on the counter for 5 to 6 mins. Scoop the ice cream and serve. 7. To a sauce pan, add corn starch, vanilla extract (or pod), sugar and milk. Whisk well until the sugar dissolves. 8. Cook this on a medium heat stirring constantly until the custard thickens and coats the back of a spoon well. Do not over cook. Taste test this to make sure it is cooked thoroughly as we don't want a starch flavor in the ice cream. 9. Strain this and cool, before chilling in the fridge. 10. When the custard becomes really cold, beat with the electric beater until smooth. 11. Pour cream to another chilled bowl and whip it until stiff peaks. Add 2 scoops of this whipped cream to the custard and mix gently. 12. Pour this back to the whipped cream. Mix together gently to incorporate the cream and the custard. Pour this to a freezer safe container. Place a cling wrap over the cream. Freeze for 8 to 16 hours. 13. Scoop the ice cream and serve with your favorite toppings.

title: How to make Paneer
ingredients: 2 liters full fat milk, 2.5 tablespoons vinegar, 1 colander, 1 cheese cloth, 1 bowl, 1.5 heavy objects
instructions: 1. Heat Milk: Pour milk to a heavy bottom pot & bring it to a gentle boil on a medium heat. Stir occasionally to ensure the milk doesn't get scorched at the bottom. 2. Curdle Milk: When the milk comes to a boil, turn off the stove and stir in 2 tablespoons of vinegar. You should see the milk begins to split, separating the solids and the whey. If done correctly, whey should be clear or yellowish but not milky. 3. Troubleshooting: If the milk did not curdle fully or still looks milky, stir 1 tbsp more vinegar. Boil on a medium heat till it curdles fully. Turn off immediately and do not overcook else paneer can become hard. 4. Strain & Rinse: Place a colander over a large bowl to collect the whey. Spread a cheese cloth over it. Transfer the paneer and the whey in 2 batches. Pour cold water all over the paneer to rinse off the vinegar really well. 5. Drain the Liquid: Gather the edges of the cloth in your hand and bundle the paneer to form a round shape. Squeeze off any excess liquid. Make a knot and hang the paneer on to your faucet, for 30 mins to drain completely. (see post for pictures) 6. Set the Paneer: Remove the knot and twist the edges of the cloth. Place bundle on a flat colander or a wooden board. Press down the cloth & place a heavy object (like a cast iron pot & rice bags) on it. (check the video below) Let the paneer set at room temperature or in the refrigerator (if hot) for 3 to 4 hours, 7. Dice the Paneer: Unpack the cheese from the cloth. You will see a dent and it is normal. Cut the paneer to 1 inch cubes. Refrigerate in a air tight container and use within 2 to 3 weeks or freeze up to 3 months. 8. Recipes to try: Use the diced cheese in curries, kebabs or rice pilafs. You may check out these 60 amazing Paneer Recipes for ideas.

title: How to make Rice Flour at Home
ingredients: 1 cup rice, water
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and pour water. Rub the rice well and discard the water. 2. Repeat the rinse a few time, 3 times. Drain to a colander. Shake off gently to remove the excess water and leave it in the colander for 5 mins. 3. Transfer to a absorbent cloth and pat dry. 4. Spread the rice and let it air dry on the cloth until crunchy. Or alternately add it to a pan and roast the rice on a medium heat for 2 to 3 mins. Later reduce the heat and roast until crunchy. It takes about 6 to 7 mins. 5. Cool and add to a grinder jar. Grind in intervals of 1 minute until the rice grains are ground to a fine powder. If you want coarse flour you can instead pulse the rice in the grinder. 6. Place a fine flour sieve over a large mixing bowl and transfer the flour to the sieve. 7. Sieve the flour and add the coarse grains if any to the grinder. Grind again. 8. If your grinder is not powerful enough, grind the sieved flour again. This gives you very very fine flour. 9. Store rice flour in a air tight dry glass jar at room temperature for 2 months. You can refrigerate for an year or more.

title: how to make soft idli recipe | idli batter recipe | soft idli with wet grinder
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 2 cup idli rice, water, 2 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup urad dal for 2 hours. 2. also, soak 2 cup idli rice for hours. 3. after, 1 hour of soaking of urad dal, drain off the water and transfer to the grinder. 4. grind for 45 minutes adding water as required. use urad dal soaked water for grinding, as it helps in fermentation. 5. scrape the sides and grind until the batter turns soft and fluffy. 6. transfer the batter to a large vessel and keep it aside. 7. in the same grinder add soaked rice and grind. 8. grind to a coarse paste adding water as required. 9. transfer the rice batter to the same vessel. 10. mix well making sure everything is well combined. mixing the batter with hand helps to aid fermentation. 11. cover and ferment in a warm place for 8 hours. 12. the batter is well fermented when it has doubled in volume. 13. mix the batter gently, without disturbing the air pockets. 14. add 2 tsp salt and mix gently. 15. grease the idli plate with oil and pour the batter. 16. steam the idli for 10 minutes or until the toothpick inserted comes out clean. 17. finally, enjoy soft idli with chutney and sambar.

title: How to Make Tahini (Sesame Paste)
ingredients: 0.75 cup sesame seeds, 2.5 tablespoons olive oil, 1 pinch salt
instructions: 1. Dry roast cleaned sesame seeds on a medium flame until they begin to pop. If you prefer raw tahini, you can skip roasting. 2. Cool completely and add them to a grinder or a food processor jar. Add a pinch of salt if you like. 3. Blend it on a medium speed until all the seeds are crushed well. You can scrape off the sides and repeat again. Make sure the jar or the seeds do not turn hot or warm while blending as this can easily make your tahini bitter. 4. Add olive oil. Make sure the seeds are well crushed before adding oil. 5. Blend well again until you get a smooth consistency. Tahini is ready. 6. Store it in a clean airtight glass jar. This keeps good for few months, but best thing is to make it in small quantities and use up with in a month. Singapore has a tropical climate, some foods do not keep good without refrigeration so I refrigerate it and use up within 2 weeks. 7. Wraps & sandwich: Spread it on a chapati/tortilla/roti / bread. Toss some steamed or fresh vegetable juliennes in little salt, black pepper, lemon juice and any spice blend like garam masala or all spice. Add these to the roti. Wrap it and serve. If using bread, just cover with another slice of toasted bread. 8. Pasta & noodles: Add a spoonful of this to your pasta or noodles towards the end and toss. Falafel Recipe Chicken Shawarma ... Chicken kebab rec... Chicken Burger Re... Veg Wraps Recipe ... Til Ke Laddu Reci...

title: hummus recipe | hummus dip recipe | 2 ways easy hummus recipe
ingredients: 1 cup chickpea chana, 2 tbsp sesame seeds til, 3 clove garlic, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 lemon, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup olive oil, pinch of kashmiri red chilli powder paprika, 1 cup chickpea chana, handful coriander cilantro, 2 tbsp jalapeno, 2 tbsp sesame seeds til, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 3 clove garlic, 0.5 lemon, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup olive oil
instructions: 1. firstly, dry roast 2 tbsp sesame seeds on low flame till it turns golden brown. 2. blend to fine powder and sesame seeds powder ready. 3. now in a blender take 1 cup chickpea / chana. 4. add in 3 clove garlic, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ lemon, ½ tsp salt, 2 tbsp roasted sesame seeds powder. 5. blend to smooth paste adding ¼ cup olive oil. 6. transfer the hummus into bowl and garnish with a ½ tsp olive oil and sprinkle chilli powder. 7. finally, serve basic hummus recipe with falafel or as a dip to vegetables. 8. firstly, in a blender take 1 cup chickpea / chana. 9. also add handful coriander, 2 tbsp jalapeno, 2 tbsp sesame seeds, ¼ tsp cumin powder, 3 clove garlic, ½ lemon and ½ tsp salt. 10. blend to smooth paste adding ¼ cup olive oil. 11. finally, serve basic hummus recipe with falafel or as a dip to vegetables.

title: Hung Curd Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups curd, 1 pinch salt
instructions: 1. Place a strainer over a deep bowl and spread a thin muslin or cheese cloth. Pour yogurt in the center of the cloth. 2. Bring all the 4 edges together to the center and make a knot with one edge to secure the yogurt. Squeeze gently to drain excess whey. Check pics in the post. 3. Hang it just above your kitchen sink for 30 to 45 mins, so that the whey drips to the sink. 4. Place back the yogurt pack in the strainer. Place an heavy object such as a ceramic bowl or mortar pestle over it. 5. Move the entire setup (bowl, strainer, yogurt and the heavy object) to the refrigerator. Rest for 4 to 8 hours, until most of the why is strained. 6. Open the know and check. Hung curd must be thick and creamy without any whey. 7. Transfer to a air tight container, preferable glass container and refrigerate until used. You can sprinkle some salt and also eat it right away.

title: Hung Curd Sandwich Recipe | Dahi Sandwich Recipe - 3 Ways
ingredients: 2 cup curd, 2 tbsp mayonnaise, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp cabbage, 2 tbsp coriander, bread
instructions: 1. firstly, in a cloth take 2 cup of curd. make sure to take thick curd. 2. tie tight and place a heavy weight over it. 3. refrigerate for at least 8 hours or until the water separates from the curd. 4. thick and creamy curd is ready. 5. break and mash into a smooth consistency. you can alternatively use hung curd if you have one. 6. add 2 tbsp mayonnaise, ¼ tsp pepper powder, ¼ tsp chilli flakes, ½ tsp mixed herbs and ½ tsp salt. 7. mix well combining everything well. adding mayo gives a nice creaminess to the sandwich. 8. further add 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp cabbage, and 2 tbsp coriander. 9. mix well and the curd stuffing is ready. 10. you can make a curd sandwich by just stuffing in between 2 slices of bread. 11. trim off the sides and cut into 4 pieces. 12. or you can grill the sandwich to golden brown by spreading butter over it. 13. or you can roll the bread slice too thin. 14. trim off the sides and join two bread slices. 15. then spread the curd stuffing generously. 16. roll tight and cut into pieces. 17. finally, mix veg curd sandwich or dahi sandwich is ready to eat.

title: Huruli Saaru Recipe | Kudu Saaru - Horsegram Rasam
