ingredients: 1 cup horse gram huruli kulthi bean gahat, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 5 cup water, 1 tsp coconut oil, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tbsp cumin seeds, 0.25 tsp methi, 6 red chilli, 0.5 cup coconut, 0.5 cup cooked horse gram, 1 clove garlic, 1 cup tamarind extract, 2 tsp jaggery, 3 chilli, 0.5 tsp turmeric, few curry leaves, 1 tsp salt, 1 tbsp coconut oil, 8 cloves garlic, 1 tsp mustard, 1 red chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker, add 1 cup of horse gram, ½ tsp of turmeric, 1 tsp of salt, and 5 cups of water. 2. Pressure cook for 8 whistles or until the horse gram has cooked well. 3. Strain the horse gram extract and keep aside. 4. To prepare the masala paste, in a pan, heat 1 tsp coconut oil. Add 1 tbsp coriander seeds, ½ tbsp cumin seeds, ¼ tsp methi, 6 dried red chilli. 5. Saute on low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 6. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 7. Add ½ cup coconut, ½ cup cooked horse gram, 1 clove garlic. 8. Grind to a smooth paste, adding water as required. Keep aside. 9. Take the horse gram extract into the large vessel. Add 1 cup tamarind extract, 2 tsp jaggery, 3 chilli, ½ tsp turmeric, few curry leaves and 1 tsp salt. 10. Mix well and boil for 5 minutes or until the raw flavour of tamarind is gone. 11. Add in prepared masala paste and mix well, adjusting the consistency. 12. Boil for 5 minutes or until the raw flavour of the masala is gone. 13. To prepare the tempering, heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil. 14. Add 8 cloves of garlic and sauté until they are well roasted. 15. Add 1 tsp mustard, 1 dried red chilli, and few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering. 16. Pour the tempering over the rasam and mix well. 17. Finally, enjoy the Horse Gram Rasam Recipe, which pairs perfectly with hot steamed rice.

title: Hyderabadi Biryani
ingredients: 1.5 cups basmati rice, 0.5 kg chicken, 0.375 teaspoon biryani masala, 1 onions, 4 tablespoons coriander leaves, 4 tablespoons mint leaves, 4 tablespoons oil, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 teaspoon saffron strands, 1.25 tablespoons ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 green chilies, 0.6666666666666666 cup yogurt, 1 tablespoons lemon juice, 1.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.25 teaspoon biryani masala powder, 0.375 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 7 cups water, 1.125 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon oil, 1 bay leaf, 3 inch cinnamon, 6 cloves 6 cloves ((laung)), 4 green cardamom, 1 star anise, 1 black cardamom, 2 strands mace, 0.5 tsp caraway seeds
instructions: 1. Slice onion thinly, separate the layers and set aside. 2. Heat 4 tbsps oil or ghee in a deep pan. 3. Fry the onions stirring often until deep golden to brown. 4. Do not burn else they turn bitter. Remove them from oil & set aside. 5. Cool & pour this oil to the marinade later. 6. Add yogurt, salt, turmeric, chili powder, biryani or garam masala powder, cardamom powder, green chilli, ginger garlic paste & lemon juice to a mixing bowl. 7. Mix well & taste this. If needed add more salt or spice. 8. Next add in chicken & marinate for 2 hours to overnight for best results. 9. Overnight marination in fridge yields best soft succulent chicken. 10. Wash rice till the water runs clear. Soak for 30 minutes. Drain & set aside. 11. Heat 6 to 7 cups water in a pot. Add 1 tsp oil, whole spices & salt. 12. Taste this. The water has to be slightly salty. 13. Boil water rapidly for 5 mins to release the aroma of spices. 14. Add rice & cook till it is ¾ or 75% done. 15. The center of the rice must be still slightly uncooked. 16. Do not make the rice mushy. Drain off to a colander. 17. While the rice cooks, transfer marinated chicken to a heavy bottom casserole or non-stick pan. 18. Add oil, ½ of the fried onions, mint & coriander leaves. Mix & spread chicken in a single even layer. 19. Layer the cooked rice evenly, sprinkle fried onions, mint & coriander leaves over the chicken. 20. Sprinkle ¼ tsp masala powder. Pour the saffron milk all over & then ghee. 21. Cover the pot with a foil to trap the steam or dum. Then cover with a tight & heavy lid. 22. Bake for 30 to 40 mins at 390F or 200C in a preheated oven. The timings will slightly vary depending on the depth of the casserole/pot used. Once done, if the chicken is too moist then bake for another 10 mins. 23. Place this over a thick hot griddle or tawa. Set the flame to medium high such that the flame reaches all over the diameter of the casserole. Cook this way for exactly 20 mins. 24. Reduce the flame to lowest. Cook for exactly 15 mins. Off the flame. Rest for 20 to 30 mins. 25. Garnish hyderabadi biryani with fresh chopped coriander leaves & serve with raita.

title: Hyderabadi Dum Chai Recipe | Irani Dum Chai | Dum Tea Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup water, 2 tbsp tea powder, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 inch ginger, 6 pods cardamom, 0.5 tsp pepper, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp cloves, 2 cup milk
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small vessel take 1 cup water. 2. tie a cloth on top using a rubber band or thread. 3. add 2 tbsp tea powder, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 inch ginger, 6 pods cardamom, ½ tsp pepper, 1 inch cinnamon and ½ tsp cloves. 4. place the vessel in the cooker. 5. add some water on the bottom of the cooker. 6. cover and pressure cook for 1 whistle or until all the flavours are absorbed by the water. 7. after the pressure settles down, sqeeze off the decoction from the cloth. 8. a strong tea decoction is ready. 9. in a saucepan take 2 cup milk and get to a boil. 10. add prepared tea decoction and mix well. 11. get the tea to a boil. 12. finally, enjoy Dum Ki Chai Recipe with some biscuits.

title: Hyderabadi Egg Dum Biryani
ingredients: 3 green cardamoms, 0.375 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1.5 cups aged basmati rice, 6 hard boiled eggs, 0.25 cup ghee, 1 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 green chili, 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 0.25 teaspoon shahi jeera, 2 inch cinnamon stick, 5 cloves 4 to 6 cloves, 4 green cardamom, 1 black cardamom, 1 strand mace, 1 teaspoon biryani masala powder, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.75 cup yogurt, 0.25 cup mint leaves, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 3 tablespoons milk, 1 pinch saffron strands, 1 teaspoon rose water, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 pinch biryani masala, 1 pinch salt
instructions: 1. Hard boil eggs, remove the shells. Pierce with fork randomly or make incisions on 3 eggs. Set them aside. 2. Wash and soak rice for about 20 to 25 minutes. Drain the water. Bring 5 cups of water to a boil, add few green cardamoms, half tsp. shahi jeera and rice to the boiling water. Cook till al dente. Fully cooked but grainy and firm. Under cooked rice doesn’t work well for this recipe, since eggs do not release any moisture like meat or veggies, rice doesn’t get cooked further during the dum process. Drain the rice to a colander. Set this aside. 3. You can skip this step if using store bought fried onions. Heat oil/ghee in a pan, add thinly sliced onions and fry until deep golden (avoid burning them). Set these aside to a plate. Also set aside half of the oil to a small bowl. Turn off the stove as the pan will be very hot now. 4. Soak a pinch of saffron in 3 tbsp. hot milk. Also set aside kewra water. 5. Turn on the stove. Add whole spices to the pan and let them sizzle. 6. Add ginger garlic paste, green chili and saute until the raw smell is gone, for about a minute. 7. Add red chili powder and biryani masala. Give a quick stir. 8. Add yogurt, salt and mint leaves. Stir and cook. 9. Add the eggs and 3/4 portion of the fried onions. 10. Stir and cook on a medium flame till the gravy turns thick. The gravy must look thick and uniform, of a flowing consistency. Switch off the stove and set the pan away from the burner else the gravy tends to get cooked further and reduce the quantity. 11. Grease a small pot generously. Spread some of the rice to a thin layer. We are making a small quantity here, so avoid a wide pan. I used a 3.5 liters pressure cooker here. 12. Pour the saffron milk over the rice. Evenly layer the egg mixture over the rice. The quantity is less, but try to spread it well evenly. 13. Layer with the remaining rice. 14. Put back the same pan on the stove and heat it with the oil/ghee we had set aside earlier. Fry the eggs lightly. Add red chili powder, a pinch of salt and biryani masala or garam masala. 15. Flip the eggs. Switch off the stove. Do not over fry. 16. Add the left over fried onions, few mint and coriander leaves. Transfer the eggs gently to the biryani, also pour the spiced oil/ghee from the pan. Pour the oil over the rice and not to the eggs. 17. Cover the pot. You can use a aluminum foil to seal the pot or a moist cloth too works well. Place a lid over it. I just went ahead using a tight and heavy lid. Heat a tawa or a pan, When it turns hot, place this dum pot over the hot tawa and set the flame to very low. 18. Cook it this way for 10 minutes. Switch off and let rest for another 10 minutes. If you do not own a tawa, you can use any heavy bottom pan or pot. Any stainless steel or cast iron pans too work well. I have used stainless pressure pan. Do not use nonstick tawa as the coating gets damaged. Serve with raita or shorba or hyderabadi mirchi ka salan .

title: Hyderabadi Marag (Mutton Soup)
ingredients: 0.5 kg lbs Bone-in mutton, 0.25 cup ghee, 1 cup onions, 3 green chilies, 1 cup mint leaves, 1 cup coriander leaves, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup yogurt, 2 cashews, 2 blanched soaked almonds, 3.25 cups water, 4 cups water, 0.625 cup full fat milk, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 2 cinnamon pieces, 1 black cardamom, 8 green cardamoms, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 0.5 teaspoon all-spice, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 teaspoon black pepper corn
instructions: 1. Heat ghee on a medium flame and saute onions and green chilies, until light golden. (not brown, Takes about 6 to 7 mins) 2. Lower the heat and stir in all the spices. Saute for a minute and add the mutton. Increase the heat to medium and saute for 5 to 6 mins. 3. Add salt, ginger garlic, mint and coriander (reserving 1 tablespoon each for later). Saute for 2 mins. Pour half of the hot water and cook for 1 hour. 4. Reduce the flame to low to bring down the temperature slightly. Whisk the yogurt well with a fork to a really smooth texture. Pour a few tablespoons of the stock, in batches. Mix well and pour back to the marag. 5. Cover and cook until the meat is completely tender (takes me 30 mins). Meanwhile grind cashews and almonds in a grinder with ½ cup milk, to a smooth puree. Stir in the rest of the milk and mix. 6. Stir in this nut puree and more hot water during the last 10 minutes along with the rest of the mint and coriander leaves. 7. Taste test and add more salt as needed along with garam masala (optional) if you want. 8. Garnish Marag with fried onions and serve hot. It is usually eaten with Hyderabadi Square naan but artisan bread, pav, brioche or naan also make great sides.

title: idli batter recipe | idli dosa batter | multipurpose batter for idli & dosa
ingredients: 1.5 cup urad dal, 3 cup idli rava, water
instructions: 1. firstly, soak 1½ cup urad dal for 2 hours. make sure to rinse urad dal well. 2. drain off the water and transfer to the grinder. 3. blend for 40 minutes to smooth and fluffy batter adding water as required. 4. transfer the urad dal batter to a large bowl and keep aside. 5. in a large bowl take 3 cup idli rava and rinse 3 times. 6. drain off the water making sure the water is running clean. 7. now squeeze off the idli rava and transfer to the urad dal batter. 8. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 9. cover and ferment for 8 hours or until it is well fermented. 10. now add 1 tsp salt and mix gently. 11. finally, idli batter is ready to prepare idli, uttapam, appe or punugulu.

title: Idli Chutney (Dosa Chutney Recipe)
ingredients: 1 grams raw coconut, 1.5 tablespoons chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 3 green chilies, 0.875 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 3.5 garlic, 0.3333333333333333 inch ginger, 1 tablespoon oil
instructions: 1. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a medium pan and add 2 tablespoons chana dal (or 1½ tablespoons chana dal and ½ tablespoon urad dal). Alternately, you can use 3 tbsps peanuts. 2. Fry them until lightly golden. 3. Add 2 to 4 slit green chilies, 3 to 4 small crushed garlic and 1 to 2 sprigs curry leaves. Do not forget to slit or chop the chilies else they will burst in the pan. Also do the same with garlic and pat dry the curry leaves to avoid splatters. 4. Fry until the chilies blister and curry leaves turn crisp. Turn down to low and stir in the cumin seeds. Fry for 40 seconds. Be careful as they can burn very fast. 5. As soon as you smell the roasted cumin, add the raw coconut -100 grams (1 cup loosely measured). 6. Stir and saute for 2 to 3 mins until really hot. Turn off and cool down. 7. Transfer to a grinder with salt and pour water in batches. Start with half cup and add more as you grind. I usually reserve one to 2 chilies aside and add them if required while blending. This way it won’t become too hot/spicy. 8. Grind to a thick chutney. Taste test to adjust salt. 9. If you want you may temper – heat 1 tsp oil and add a pinch of mustard seeds, 1 pinch of split urad dal, 1 broken chili and 2 to 3 torn curry leaves. When the urad dal turns golden, turn off and add hing. Pour this over the idli dosa chutney. Serve with idli dosa or any snacks.

title: idli dhokla recipe | instant dhokla in idli stand | idli khaman
ingredients: 1.5 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rava semolina suji, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp sugar, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.25 cup curd, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp sesame, pinch hing asafoetida, 3 chilli, few curry leaves, 0.25 cup water, 1 tbsp sugar, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coconut, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1½ cup besan, 2 tbsp rava, ½ tsp ginger paste and 1 chilli. 2. also add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp sugar, pinch hing and ¼ cup curd. 3. now add 2 tbsp oil, ½ tsp salt and 1 cup water. 4. whisk and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 5. now add ½ tsp eno fruit salt and mix gently. 6. mix until the mixture turns frothy. 7. now immediately pour the batter into greased idli plate. 8. place the idli plate in steamer and steam for 12 minutes. 9. now prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp oil. 10. add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, 1 tbsp sesame, pinch hing, 3 chilli and few curry leaves. 11. stir and splutter the tempering. 12. now pour ¼ cup water, 1 tbsp sugar, ¼ tsp salt and 1 tsp lemon juice. 13. mix and boil making sure everything is well combined. 14. pour the tempering over the idli, make sure the water is absorbed by idli. 15. garnish with 2 tbsp coconut and 2 tbsp coriander. 16. finally, enjoy idli dhokla with green chutney.

title: idli manchurian recipe | idli manchuria recipe | leftover idli recipes
ingredients: 5 idlis, 0.5 cup maida all-purpose flour, 0.25 cup corn flour corn starch, salt to taste, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp soy sauce, water as required, oil for deep frying, 2 tsp oil, 0.5 onion, 1 inch ginger, 3 garlic cloves, 0.5 capsicum, 0.25 cup tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, salt to taste, 2 tsp vinegar, 2 tsp soy sauce, 1 spring onion
instructions: 1. chop all the idlis into 4 quarters and keep aside. 2. in a large bowl mix together take maida, corn flour, salt, kashmiri red chilli powder, black pepper and soy sauce. give a good mix. 3. now add little water and mix well. make a smooth batter. the batter should be of dropping consistency. 4. dip each idli pieces into the batter. coat them well and deep fry. 5. flip over and fry till they turn light golden brown and crispy. 6. drain the fried idlis on a kitchen paper towel to remove excess oil. 7. in a large kadai add 2 tsp oil add chopped onion. saute for a minute. 8. now add chopped ginger and garlic. saute for a minute. you can also add green chilli if you prefer spicy. 9. next fry chopped capsicum. 10. add tomato sauce, chilli sauce add salt to taste. 11. add 2 tsp of soya sauce and vinegar. give a good stir and cook for a minute. 12. now add the deep fried idlis and give a good mix. coat the idli pieces well with sauce. 13. lastly add some chopped spring onions greens and mix. 14. serve with veg fried rice or mushroom fried rice.

title: idli pakora recipe | idli pakoda recipe | crispy idli pakora
ingredients: 5 idlis, 0.5 cup maida all-purpose flour, 0.25 cup corn flour corn starch, salt to taste, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp soy sauce, water as required, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. chop all the idlis into 4 quarters and keep aside. 2. in a large bowl mix together take maida, corn flour, salt, kashmiri red chilli powder, black pepper and soy sauce. give a good mix. 3. now add little water and mix well. make a smooth batter. the batter should be of dropping consistency. 4. dip each idli pieces into the batter. coat them well and deep fry. 5. flip over and fry till they turn light golden brown and crispy. 6. drain the fried idlis on a kitchen paper towel to remove excess oil. 7. your idli pakora is ready, serve immediately.

title: Idli Podi Recipe (Milagai Podi)
ingredients: 0.25 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup sesame seeds, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 12 red chilies, 1 tablespoon cumin seeds, 2.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.25 cup coconut, 1.5 garlic cloves, 0.25 teaspoon hing
instructions: 1. To make idli podi prepare the following ingredients: 2. Here are the optional ingredients which are our favorite. These ingredients are going to take your podi to the next level. 3. 2 to 3 sprigs curry leaves (optional, rinse and pat dry) 4. Since the roasting time of each ingredient is different, you need to dry roast them individually / separately for best results. To a heavy bottom pan, add chana dal. On a medium heat, dry roast them stiring often until they change their color to light brown. 5. Roasting them on a high heat is only going to change their color fast without roasting them from inside. So it is important to roast on a low to medium heat. Transfer them to a plate when they change to light brown color. 6. To the same pan, add urad dal and stir fry on a medium heat. 7. These lentils tend to burn fast if you don’t stir often. So be careful. Remove them to the same plate once they turn to light brown color. 8. Add sesame seeds and roast them the same way. They may sizzle or steam when they are about to be done. Remove to the same plate. 9. Add cumin seeds and roast until deep golden to light brown. They begin to smell earthy and pungent when done. Remove to the same plate. 10. Next add red chilies and roast until crisp. Keep regulating the flame from low to medium and vice versa as needed. 11. They turn slightly crisp when done but will turn fully crisp upon cooling. Transfer to a plate. 12. To the same pan, add curry leaves. Please make sure you pat dry them before using. 13. Roast the leaves until crisp. This takes only a few minutes. Remove them to the plate. 14. Add garlic and dry roast for 2 to 3 mins. 15. Stir in the coconut and roast until hot and aromatic. Keep this in the pan itself 16. Cool all the ingredients. 17. If you try to grind all the roasted ingredients together, you may end up with a too coarse powder. First grind the red chilies, salt and curry leaves in a grinder to make a fine powder. A lot of people also add hing. If you want you may add ¼ teaspoon at this stage. 18. Try to grind as fine as possible because this also gives a nice color to your podi. You may set aside some of this chilli podi so you can adjust the heat level later. 19. Scrape off the sides of the blender jar, then add dal, cumin and sesame. 20. Grind to a fine or coarse powder as per your liking. Add coconut and garlic. 21. Grind again. Taste test and add more salt. If the idli podi is not spicy, you may add little red chilli powder and grind again. You can also heat the chili powder, cool and add it here for better shelf life. Store idli podi in an airtight glass or steel jar at room temperature. It stores well for several months.

title: Idli Podi Recipe | Idli Milagai Podi - Magic Gun Powder
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 cup urad dal, 1 cup chana dal, 2 tbsp toor dal, 3 tbsp sesame, 75 grams red chilli, few curry leaves, 3 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp hing
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a thick-bottomed pan heat 1 tsp oil and roast 1 cup urad dal on low flame until it turns aromatic. 2. Add 1 cup chana dal, 2 tbsp toor dal, and 3 tbsp sesame. 3. Continue to roast until the dal turns golden brown and aromatic. 4. Transfer to the plate and allow it to cool completely. 5. In the same pan heat 1 tsp oil, add 75 grams dried red chilli and a few curry leaves. 6. Roast until the chilli puffs up. 7. Also, add ball-sized tamarind and roast for a minute. 8. Now cool completely, transfer to the mixer jar, and grind to a fine powder. 9. Further add in roasted dal, 3 tsp salt, and ½ tsp hing. 10. Grind to a fine powder. make sure to mix in between to avoid any blockage. 11. Finally, store idli podi in air tight container and enjoy it with idli, dosa, and rice.

title: Idli Recipe (Idli Batter Recipe with Pro Tips)
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 1 cup idli rice or, 2 tablespoon thick poha, 0.5 teaspoon Non-iodized salt, 0.5 cup chilled water, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 cup urad dal, 1.5 cups idli rice, 0.5 teaspoon fenugreek seeds, 2 tablespoons thick poha, 0.5 cup chilled water, 0.75 cup water
instructions: 1. Add urad dal & methi seeds to a bowl. Add rice or idli rava to another bowl. 2. Wash them very well separately until water runs clear. Soak them separately in lot of water for at least 6 hrs. 3. Rinse and soak poha with ¼ cup water for about 30 mins before blending. 4. After 6 hours, drain the water from both the bowls. 5. Add dal, methi, soaked poha, salt & water to a blender jar or a wet grinder. 6. Blend to a thick, smooth, bubbly & frothy batter. The idli batter should not turn hot or even warm as it makes dense idli. If the batter is too thick you may add 2 to 4 tbsps more water while blending. 7. If you live in a hot climate then skip adding salt now and add it just before making idli else the batter turns sour. You will have to experiment with salt to know what works well for you. 8. Transfer urad dal batter to a large bowl. 9. A. If using rice : Add rice to the blender with water (mentioned in the ingredients). Grind to a little coarse batter (semolina texture). Pour this to the dal batter. Mix both of them well with your clean hands. The batter must be thick yet of pouring consistency. B. If using idli rava : Drain off the water thoroughly from the rava. Squeeze excess water from the rava with the help of both your palms. Rava must not look soggy. It must absolutely have no water in it, else the batter will become runny. 10. Transfer this to the urad dal batter and mix well. 11. Now mix everything well. Use your hand to mix as it helps to ferment faster and better. If needed can add little water, if the batter is too thick. 12. Set the batter aside in a warm place for at least 8 to 14 hrs. The batter ferments and rises well to double the quantity. Sometimes it may take up to 18 hours depending on the weather and temperature. 13. If you live in a cold country, keep it in the oven with the light bulb ON. Or preheat the oven to 120 F or 50 C for 10 to 12 mins. Keep the loosely covered batter bowl inside & turn off. If you have a instant pot or microwave (with yogurt function), then you can also turn it ON with the yogurt setting (low) for 6 to 12 hours or until the batter doubles. 14. A well fermented batter will yield good soft idli. The batter must rise and look fluffy but not turn sour. You will need to experiment to know the fermentation time. 15. On a high flame, bring water to a boil in an idly steamer or a pressure cooker. If using instant pot, pour 1½ cups water to your inner pot. Press the saute button and bring the water to a rolling boil. 16. Meanwhile grease your idly plates lightly. Mix the idli batter gently 1 to 2 times only. Do not over do as the aerated batter will turn flat. Fill the molds with batter. 17. When the water begins to bubble and steam up, place the stand in the steamer. Cover and steam for exactly 10 mins on a high flame. If using Instant pot, when the water begins to boil, press CANCEL button. Keep the idli stand in the IP. Cover with the lid & position the steam release handle to venting . Then press STEAM button. Use an external timer to monitor & steam for 10 to 12 mins. 18. Once done turn off and wait for 2 mins. Remove the idly stand. Set aside to cool down for 2 to 3 mins. When cooled, the idli must not be wet on top. 19. With the help of a spoon remove the idlis to a plate. Serve idli with a chutney or sambar.

title: Idli Sambar Recipe (Tiffin Sambar)
ingredients: 0.75 cup dal, 2 cups water, 2 tablespoon tamarind, Salt as needed, 13.5 shallots, 7 french beans, 1 medium carrot, 0.5 cup pumpkin, 1 tomato, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon red chilli powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 4.5 cups water, 1 tablespoon oil, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 red chili, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 pinch methi seeds, 1 pinch hing, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 3 red chilies, 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 2 tablespoon coconut
instructions: 1. Rinse and pressure cook dal with 2 cups of water for 2 whistles or until soft. 2. Or alternately cook in a pot until soft. If using a Instant pot, pressure cook on high pressure for 10 mins & wait for natural pressure release. 3. Mash it and set aside. If you like the hotel style tiffin sambar, you can also add pumpkin to the dal for cooking. 4. Mash everything together and set aside. 5. Soak tamarind in little hot water, squeeze and extract the pulp. Keep this aside. 6. In a pan, dry roast red chilies, chana dal and urad dal on a medium flame until lightly golden. 7. Next add coriander seeds and roast all of them until a nice smell comes. 8. When the dals turn deep golden remove to a plate. 9. Next in the same pan saute methi seeds until slightly dark then add cumin. 10. When the cumin turns aromatic remove to a plate. Cool these completely and make a fine powder. 11. Add oil to a pot and heat up. Throw in the cumin, mustard and methi. 12. When they pop, add red chilies, curry leaves and hing. 13. When the leaves turn crisp, set aside half the tempering if you wish to add at the end. 14. Add veggies and saute for 2 to 3 minutes. Add tomatoes and saute until soft. 15. Sprinkle sambhar powder and red chilli powder if using. Saute for a minute without burning. 16. Pour water and cook until the vegetables are soft cooked. 17. Next pour filtered tamarind and then the mashed dal. Stir and bring the idli sambar to a boil. 18. Taste test and add more tamarind or salt as needed. Add the rest of the tempering and coriander leaves. 19. When it begins to bubble. Switch off. You can also make a fresh tempering but I don't do. 20. Serve idli sambar hot with soft idli, dosa, uthappam, pongal & vada.

title: Idli Sambar Recipe | Hotel Style Tiffin Sambar
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 1 carrot, 1 potato, 6 beans, 1 pieces drumsticks, 2 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 2 chilli, few curry leaves, 4 cup water, 0.5 cup tamarind extract, 1 tsp jaggery, 2 cup cooked toor dal, 2 cup water, 2 tbsp sambar powder, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tbsp coconut oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 1 red chilli, pinch hing, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large vessel, take 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 onion and saute well. 2. Add 1 tomato, 1 carrot, 1 potato, 6 beans, 10 pieces of drumsticks, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp turmeric, 2 chillies, and a few curry leaves. Stir fry for 2 minutes. 3. Add 4 cups of water and boil for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are half-cooked. 4. Now, add ½ cup tamarind extract and 1 tsp jaggery, and continue to boil until the vegetables are cooked well. 5. Add 2 cups of cooked toor dal and 2 cups of water, and mix well, adjusting the consistency as needed. 6. Add 2 tbsp sambar powder and 1 tsp salt. Mix and boil for 5 minutes. 7. Now, prepare the tempering by heating 1 tbsp of coconut oil. Add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, 1 dried red chilli, a pinch of hing, and a few curry leaves. 8. Splutter the tempering and pour over the sambar. 9. Also, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 10. Finally, enjoy Idli Sambar with hot steamed idlis or dosas.

title: Idli Upma Recipe (Leftover Idli Recipes)
ingredients: 6 Idli, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 teaspoon chana dal, 1.5 teaspoon urad dal, 2 tablespoons peanuts, 1 pinch hing, 1 onion, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1.5 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoons coriander leaves, 3 tablespoons coconut
instructions: 1. Crumble the idlis. For this recipe, we need cooled and preferably refrigerated or frozen idlis work well. Idli made using rice and not idli rava taste best for this. I have used about 6 large idli. I use this Idli recipe . 2. Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard and cumin, when the mustard begins to crackle, add chana dal & urad dal. Dals lend a good aroma to the upma. You can also skip them. 3. Fry peanuts until golden. I prefer them in any of my upma recipes, if you do not like you may skip. But without dals and peanuts upma will turn out bland. 4. Add chili, ginger, curry leaves and onions. You can skip ginger if you do not like. Saute for 2 to 3 mins until the onions turn slightly transparent. 5. Sprinkle 1 tbsp water to the pan. This step brings out the flavor of onions, curry leaves and green chili like in the regular rava upma. Water also helps to soften the chana dal and urad dal. 6. Sprinkle hing and turmeric. You can skip turmeric, we use turmeric (atleast a bit) for all savory foods, so used it here. 7. Add crumbled idli, coconut and coriander leaves. Coconut adds a different taste to the idli upma. 8. Mix everything well and sprinkle salt if needed. If your idlis do not have a slight sour hint, you can also add a tsp of lemon juice. Cover and cook on a low heat until the mixture turns hot. Serve Idli upma as breakfast or snack.

title: Indian Butter Chickpeas
ingredients: 8.5 oz cans chickpeas, 0.125 teaspoon baking soda, 0.3333333333333333 cup raw cashews, 3.5 tablespoons unsalted butter, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.75 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 4.5 oz tomato paste, 1 cup water chickpea cooked liquid, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.29166666666666663 cup heavy cream, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, lemon juice
instructions: 1. You will need the following ingredients: 2. 2 – 15 oz cans chickpeas (drained or 3 cups boiled chickpeas from 1 cup dried) 3. 1½ to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder (adjust to heat tolerance, I use 2 tsps home ground) 4. Omit this step if you are using nut butter or heavy cream as a cashew substitute. Soak cashews in ¼ cup hot water for 15 mins. Grind with the same water, in a small grinder to a smooth thick puree. Stir in another half cup water and reserve aside for later. (While they soak, proceed with the next step.) 5. Begin to melt 2 tablespoons butter on a low flame. Before it melts completely, add ginger and garlic. Sauté for 1 to 2 mins without burning. 6. Stir in the ground spices – red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder and salt. 7. Add the tomato paste, baking soda (optional), 1 cup water (or chickpea cooked liquid) & chickpeas. (Or Add 1¼ cup passata/tomato puree and omit water) 8. Mix well and bring to a gentle boil on a medium heat. 9. Cover and cook for 10 mins, until your sauce is really thick and the chickpeas are tender. (While this cooks, grind the soaked cashews.) 10. Stir in the cashew puree/nut butter and heavy cream (start with lower amount of cream and adjust as per taste & consistency). If you want, you may temper the cream by mixing 3 to 4 tbsps of cooked sauce with the heavy cream. This way you will make sure the cream is not split after adding to the sauce. 11. Continue to simmer for another 4 to 5 mins, or a little longer until slightly thick, yet runny. 12. If you have used cashew cream, the sauce will thicken after cooling down, so make sure you turn off when the sauce is slightly runny/ flowy. The sauce won’t taste good if it is too thick. 13. Crush kasuri methi and add it along with the reserved butter and more salt or garam masala if required. Taste test first before making the adjustments. (I erred in adding these ingredients early before adding the cream and nut puree, but add them when you are done with the cooking) 14. Let the butter chickpeas cool down a bit. Squeeze lemon juice. Garnish with cream and coriander leaves. Serve it hot over rice, quinoa, millets or flatbreads like Naan or kulcha. (If you have used baking soda, I highly recommend adding lemon juice before serving)

title: Indian Cucumber Salad
ingredients: 2 cucumber, 3 tablespoon roasted skinned peanuts, 2 tablespoons raw coconut, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon sugar, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves cilantro, 0.5 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch mustard seeds, 7 curry leaves, 1 green chili, 1 pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. Here are the ingredients you need: 2. 2 to 4 tablespoon roasted skinned peanuts (crushed/chopped/ coarsely ground or seeds) 3. 2 tablespoon coriander leaves/ cilantro (fine chopped) 4. 1 small green chili (chopped/ slit sub with red chili flakes) 5. Rinse, brush, rinse and pat dry the cucumbers. Leave the skin-on if using organic else peel them. Chop finely and add to a bowl. 6. On a medium flame, heat ghee in a small/tadka pan. Add the cumin and mustard seeds if using. When they begin to sizzle and pop, add green chili and curry leaves, followed by hing. Cool down slightly. If you want you may remove the chilies & curry leaves with a spoon. We eat the curry leaves so I keep them. 7. Pour the tadka all over the chopped cucumbers & mix well. 8. When you are ready to serve, stir in salt, sugar, lemon juice and coriander leaves. Lastly sprinkle crushed peanuts. 9. Serve Indian Cucumber Salad alongside Roti/rice and Red Lentil Curry , Saag Paneer , Butternut Squash Curry or any dal or curry.

title: Indian Drumstick Vegetable Recipe
ingredients: 3 drumsticks, 1.5 cups onions, 2 medium potatoes, 2 tomatoes, 2 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.5 tablespoon Oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash drumsticks under running water. If not tender scrape off the skin lightly. Wash and chop to finger length pieces. Cube potatoes to medium sized pieces, and add them to a bowl of water until used. 2. Add oil to a pan and heat. Add mustard and cumin. When they begin to splutter, add curry leaves, green chilies and finely chopped onions. Fry until transparent or pink stirring often. 3. Add drumsticks and potatoes. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes. Add chopped tomatoes, salt and turmeric. I usually deseed the tomatoes and use for this recipe. Fry until the tomatoes turn mushy. 4. Then add red chili powder and fry for 2 minutes. If you wish to add garam masala or coriander powder add it now. 5. Pour water just enough to cover the drumsticks and potatoes. Cover and cook till the veggies are soft cooked. Garnish drumstick gravy with coriander leaves. Serve with plain rice & ghee.

title: Indian Lamb Curry Recipe
ingredients: 2.2 lbs bone-in lamb, 0.25 cup yogurt, 2.5 teaspoons Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 2.5 teaspoons garam masala, 0.875 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 2 teaspoon salt, 6 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 6 green cardamoms, 3 inch cinnamon piece, 2 black cardamom, 1.5 star anise, 2 cups onions, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 1.5 green chilli slit, 2 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 4 tablespoons mint leaves, 4.5 cup water, 0.5 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add all the ground spices to a small bowl and mix them. 2. Marinate lamb with half of this spice mix, half of the ginger garlic, salt, kasuri methi and yogurt (or lemon juice/ vinegar). Cover and set aside for about 2 hours to overnight in the refrigerator. 3. Heat 1 to 2 tablespoon oil/ghee in a heavy bottom pan and add star anise, bay leaf, black cardamom, green cardamom & cinnamon. 4. Once the spices begin to sizzle, add the mint leaves and stir in the marinated lamb and braise on a medium heat for about 8 to 10 minutes. 5. Pour half of the hot water and mix well. Cover and let cook on a low to medium heat for 1 hour. Keep stirring every 15 mins and add more hot water as you cook. 6. Meanwhile in another pan, heat the rest of the oil/ghee. Add the onions & green chilies. Saute on a medium heat for 6 to 7 mins. 7. Reduce the heat and stir fry for a few more minutes until deep golden but not burnt. 8. Add rest of the ginger garlic & saute until the raw smell of ginger garlic goes away. This takes 1 to 2 minutes. 9. Turn off the heat & stir in the rest of the ground spices, immediately pour the tomato puree. Cook on a medium heat until the raw flavor of tomatoes is gone. 10. Transfer this to the lamb after 1 hour of cooking lamb. Cook covered on a low heat until the meat is fully cooked and fork tender, for another 50 mins or more. Pour more hot water if required as you cook & keep stirring often, every 5 to 7 mins. 11. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala & kasuri methi if needed. If you want more heat in your lamb curry, add some freshly crushed black pepper. 12. Garnish lamb curry with coriander leaves and serve with roti/ naan/ rice or with any artisan bread. 13. Lamb curry tastes best after resting for a few hours or even the next day. Reheat & adjust the salt when you are ready to serve.

title: Indian Rice Pudding (Better than restaurant)
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice, 5 cups whole milk, 0.29166666666666663 cup sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom, 0.25 cup unsalted pistachios, 1 tablespoon rice flour, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.25 cup cashews cup sweet raisins
instructions: 1. Pour 5 cups milk to a heavy bottom pot. Bring to a gentle boil on a medium heat. Meanwhile rinse half cup rice, drain the water and add it to the hot milk. 2. Cook on a medium heat, stirring often at the bottom and sides. until the rice is soft, and slightly mushy. It takes me 20 mins to reach this stage. 3. This step is optional but helps thicken the pudding faster. Add 1 tablespoon rice flour (not glutinous flour) to a small bowl and mix with half cup milk/ water. 4. Mix well to make a lump-free slurry. 5. When the rice is slightly mushy, mash it slightly with the back of the ladle/ spatula to thicken. 6. Pour the rice slurry slowly to the rice pudding, stirring well with your other hand to incorporate it. The temperature falls here, bring it back to a boil and simmer for 3 minutes or until it turns thick and creamy. 7. Add ¼ to ⅓ cup sugar (or 3 to 4 tbsps condensed milk) & cook until the pudding is thick, yet of flowing consistency. It will become thicker after cooling. Taste test and add more sugar if you want. 8. Stir in ½ teaspoon ground cardamom. If you want you may also add 1 pinch of saffron (crushed) or/ and 1 tsp rose water. 9. Turn off the heat. Add ¼ cup chopped unsalted pistachios. (If you have only salted, blanch them in hot water for 1 to 2 mins. Drain, pat dry in a cloth and chop.) 10. This is the consistency we prefer. It will become slightly thicker after cooling down.

title: Indian Tofu Kadai Recipe
ingredients: 225 grams extra firm tofu, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 0.75 cup onion, 1 cup bell peppers, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 tablespoon oil, 1.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons garam masala, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 1 teaspoon sugar
instructions: 1. Thaw the frozen tofu in the refrigerator & press down gently in between your palms to remove excess liquid/water. 2. Wrap in a kitchen towel/ papers to get rid of excess moisture. Chop to 1 inch portions of ½ to ¾ inch thickness. 3. On a low flame, heat half of the oil in a pan. Add curry leaves and ginger. Stir fry until aromatic without burning, for 30 to 60 seconds. 4. Stir in red chili powder and half of the garam masala. Add the tomatoes and let cook, partially covered until thick and oil begins to separate. 5. Meanwhile, heat 1 tsp oil in a non-stick or cast iron pan. Place the tofu and fry them until golden and crisp. Turn them to the other side with a spatula & fry until crisp. Transfer this aside to a plate. 6. Add the rest of the oil and add onions and bell peppers. Stir fry on a high heat until aromatic and slightly soft, for 3 mins. 7. Stir in tofu, onions, bell peppers, soya sauce (optional), sugar, salt & kasuri methi (optional). If it is too thick & dry, pour ¼ cup hot water. Stir well and let cook covered for 3 mins. 8. Taste test to add more salt & garam masala as needed. If you want squeeze some lemon juice while serving. 9. Serve tofu stir fry with rice or chapatti/roti & a vegetable salad. Alternately stir in some cooked noodles or pasta for a complete meal.

title: Indori Poha Recipe | Street Style Steamed Poha From Indore
ingredients: 2 tsp coriander seeds, 2 tsp cumin seeds, 2 tsp fennel seeds, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 nutmeg, 2 bay leaf, 2 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 2 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp kala namak, pinch hing, 2 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 cup poha, water, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 0.25 cup peanut, 1 tbsp mustard, 1 tbsp cumin, 0.5 tbsp fennel, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 3 chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 cup milk, few coriander, boondi, onion, ratlami sev, peanut, coriander, lemon
instructions: 1. Firstly, to prepare Indori poha masala, in a pan take 2 tsp coriander seeds, 2 tsp cumin seeds, 2 tsp fennel seeds, ½ tsp pepper, ½ tsp cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, ½ nutmeg and 2 bay leaf. 2. Saute low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 3. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixi jar. 4. Add 2 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 2 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp kala namak, pinch hing, 2 tsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. 5. Grind to fine powder. You can store the Indori Poha Masala in an airtight container. 6. To prepare the poha, in a large bowl take 2 cup poha. 7. Rinse with water making sure not to soak the poha. 8. Drain off the poha, add ¼ tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp sugar and 1 tsp salt. 9. Mix gently making sure everthing is well combined. Keep aside. 10. In a pan heat 2 tbsp oil and fry ¼ cup peanut until they turn crunchy. Keep aside. 11. In the same oil add 1 tbsp mustard, 1 tbsp cumin, ½ tbsp fennel, pinch hing and few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering. 12. Now add 3 chilli, and ¼ tsp turmeric and saute slightly. 13. Pour the tempering over the soaked poha. 14. Mix the tempering well with the poha. 15. Now transfer the poha into the large plate placed over the water to steam. 16. Also sprinkle ¼ cup milk, few coriander, fried peanut and Indori Poha Masala. 17. Steam for 10 minutes, or until the poha is softened and flavours are absorbed. 18. To serve, take the poha in a plate. 19. Top with Indor poha masala, boondi, onion, ratlami sev, peanut, coriander and lemon. 20. Finally, enjoy Indori Poha with Jalebi, Sev and Usal.

title: instant bread masala dosa recipe | instant bread dosa recipe
ingredients: 8 bread slices, 0.5 cup sooji bombay rava semolina, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 0.25 cup water, salt to taste, oil for roasting, aloo bhaji potato masala potato sabzi- for stuffing
instructions: 1. take 8 bread slices and cut the edges. 2. crumble the bread slices well with you hand. make sure there are no big pieces of bread. 3. now add half cup sooji (rava / semolina), 2 tbsp rice flour, ¼ cup curd, ¼ cup water and some salt to taste. 4. mix them well and allow to soak for 20-30 minutes. 5. now take the soaked ingredients in a blender and blend to smooth dosa batter consistency. add water if required. 6. heat the griddle. smear some water over the tawa and wipe off with a tissue paper. 7. sprinkle some oil and wipe off with a tissue. (do not add oil if using non-stick pan) 8. once the tawa is hot; pour a ladleful of batter and spread out in a circular motion. 9. add some oil on top of the dosa 10. cook them covered for a minute. 11. add in potato sabzi filling center of the dosa. 12. cook for 15-30 seconds or till it turns golden brown and crisp. 13. fold the dosa into half or roll into circular. 14. serve masala dosa hot with coconut chutney and sambar

title: Instant Chakli Recipe | Instant Chakkuli | Instant Murukku
ingredients: 2 cup rice flour, 1 cup maida plain flour, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp butter, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp red chilli powder, 2 tbsp sesame til, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 tsp salt, water, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cup rice flour, 1 cup maida and 1 tsp salt. 2. also add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp red chilli powder, 2 tbsp sesame, pinch hing and 1 tsp salt. 3. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. now add 2 tbsp butter, crumble and mix until the flour is well combined with butter. 5. add water slowly and start to knead the dough. 6. knead to smooth and soft dough adding water as required. 7. now take star mould and fix to the chakli maker. 8. grease the chakli maker with some oil. this prevents the dough from sticking to mould. 9. furthermore, make a cylindrical shape out of dough and place the dough inside the maker. 10. tighten the lid and start preparing chaklis. on the wet cloth or butter paper make small spiral shape chaklis by pressing 11. seal the ends so that it doesn’t fall apart while deep frying. 12. take one murukku at a time and slide it into the hot oil. 13. flip the murukku and fry on medium flame till they turn crispy from both sides. 14. furthermore, drain over a paper towel to remove excess oil. 15. finally, once cooled enjoy instant chakli or store in an airtight container for 2 weeks.

title: instant chole bhature recipe | chole bhatura recipe with soda water
ingredients: 2.5 cups maida plain flour all-purpose flour refined flour, 0.5 cup rava semolina sooji, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tbsp oil, 1 cup soda water, oil for deep frying, 1 cup chana masala chole masala
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2½ cups maida. 2. add in ½ cup rava. rava helps to make bhatura soft from inside and crisp from outside. 3. also add 1 tsp sugar, ½ tsp salt and 1 tbsp oil. 4. combine well making sure everything is combined well. 5. now add ½ cup of soda water. 6. knead the dough well adding more soda water as required. 7. prepare smooth and soft dough. do not make too soft dough as it absorbs oil. 8. cover the dough with moist cloth and rest for 30 minutes or more. 9. after 30 minutes, knead dough slightly and pinch a small ball sized dough and flatten. 10. roll the dough evenly into circles using rolling pin dusting maida as required. 11. further, roll and get to a circular shape. roll neither too thin nor too thick. 12. when the oil is very hot, drop one bhatura and, press with the spoon to puff up. 13. also once they begin to puff, splash oil over the bhatura till they puff completely. 14. flip over and fry the bhatura till golden brown all over. 15. then drain the bhatura onto tissue paper to remove excess oil. 16. finally, serve instant bhatura with chana masala / chole masala.

title: instant chole bhature recipe | chole bhatura recipe with soda water
ingredients: 2.5 cups maida plain flour all-purpose flour refined flour, 0.5 cup rava semolina sooji, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tbsp oil, 1 cup soda water, oil for deep frying, 1 cup chana masala chole masala
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2½ cups maida. 2. add in ½ cup rava. rava helps to make bhatura soft from inside and crisp from outside. 3. also add 1 tsp sugar, ½ tsp salt and 1 tbsp oil. 4. combine well making sure everything is combined well. 5. now add ½ cup of soda water. 6. knead the dough well adding more soda water as required. 7. prepare smooth and soft dough. do not make too soft dough as it absorbs oil. 8. cover the dough with moist cloth and rest for 30 minutes or more. 9. after 30 minutes, knead dough slightly and pinch a small ball sized dough and flatten. 10. roll the dough evenly into circles using rolling pin dusting maida as required. 11. further, roll and get to a circular shape. roll neither too thin nor too thick. 12. when the oil is very hot, drop one bhatura and, press with the spoon to puff up. 13. also once they begin to puff, splash oil over the bhatura till they puff completely. 14. flip over and fry the bhatura till golden brown all over. 15. then drain the bhatura onto tissue paper to remove excess oil. 16. finally, serve instant bhatura with chana masala / chole masala.

title: instant dal premix recipe | homemade ready to cook dal mix
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 1 cup moong dal, 0.75 cup masoor dal, 2 tbsp oil, 2 tsp mustard, 2 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp hing, 4 red chilli, few curry leaves, 3 bay leaf, 3 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp chilli powder, 1 tbsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp aamchur, 4 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan take 1 cup toor dal, 1 cup moong dal and ¾ cup masoor dal. make sure to wipe off the dal clean to remove dirt. you can alternatively, rinse the dal and dry it in sun. 2. dry roast on low flame until the dal turns aromatic. 3. cool completely, transfer to the mixi jar and grind to a fine powder. keep aside. 4. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, add 2 tsp mustard, 2 tsp cumin, ¼ tsp hing, 4 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 5. splutter the tempering. 6. add 3 bay leaf, 3 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander and 2 tsp kasuri methi. 7. saute on low flame until it turns crisp. 8. add in powdered dal, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp chilli powder, 1 tbsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp aamchur and 4 tsp salt. 9. saute until the spices are well combined with dal. 10. cool completely, and transfer to the zip lock bag. 11. dal premix is ready and can be stored for 3 months. 12. to prepare the dal, in a bowl take ½ cup dal premix and 2 cup water. 13. mix well and transfer to the large kadai. 14. boil for 5 minutes, or until the dal thickens. 15. adjust the consistency adding water as required and get to a boil. 16. finally, homemade dal prepared from dal premix is ready.

title: Instant Dosa Recipe | Quick & Easy Crisp Dose
ingredients: 1 cup rava, 2 tbsp wheat flour, 0.75 cup curd, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp baking soda, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar take 1 cup rava, 2 tbsp wheat flour, ¾ cup curd and 1 cup water. 2. Grind to a smooth batter. 3. Transfer the batter to a large bowl, add ½ tsp salt and ¼ tsp baking soda. 4. Mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined and adjusting the consistency as needed. 5. Pour the dosa over the hot tawa and spread gently. 6. Pour a tsp of oil and roast on a low to medium flame until it turns golden brown. 7. Scrape the dosa from the sides and fold in half. 8. Finally, Instant Dosa Recipe with sooji is ready to enjoy with chutney.

title: instant jalebi recipe | homemade crispy jalebi recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup all purpose flour maida, 1 tsp corn flour, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 tsp vinegar, 1 tsp yogurt curd, 5 tbsp water, 0.125 tsp turmeric powder haldi yellow food colour, 0.25 cup water, 1 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp saffron strands kesar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder elachi powder, 1 tbsp ghee, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. in a mixing bowl, mix maida, corn flour, curd. 2. then add half tsp vinegar and water. 3. mix well in round circular directions for 4 minutes. 4. now add baking soda and give a gentle mix. 5. now pour this batter in the tomato ketchup bottles carefully. 6. heat oil in a pan and add 1 tbsp of ghee. 7. squeeze the bottle and make round spirals with the batter. 8. when one side is partly cooked, turn over and fry the other side. 9. fry till the jalebis are a light golden. 10. then immediately drop the fried jalebis in the warm sugar syrup for 30 seconds (1 string consistency). 11. finally, serve jalebis hot, warm or at room temperature.

title: instant jalebi recipe | homemade crispy jalebi recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup all purpose flour maida, 1 tsp corn flour, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 tsp vinegar, 1 tsp yogurt curd, 5 tbsp water, 0.125 tsp turmeric powder haldi yellow food colour, 0.25 cup water, 1 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp saffron strands kesar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder elachi powder, 1 tbsp ghee, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. in a mixing bowl, mix maida, corn flour, curd. 2. then add half tsp vinegar and water. 3. mix well in round circular directions for 4 minutes. 4. now add baking soda and give a gentle mix. 5. now pour this batter in the tomato ketchup bottles carefully. 6. heat oil in a pan and add 1 tbsp of ghee. 7. squeeze the bottle and make round spirals with the batter. 8. when one side is partly cooked, turn over and fry the other side. 9. fry till the jalebis are a light golden. 10. then immediately drop the fried jalebis in the warm sugar syrup for 30 seconds (1 string consistency). 11. finally, serve jalebis hot, warm or at room temperature.

title: instant neer dosa recipe | neer dose recipe with rice flour
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour akki hittu, salt to taste, 1.5 cup water, oil for roasting
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 1 cup rice flour and add salt to taste. 2. add in 1½ cup water, or as required. 3. mix with the help of whisk to avoid forming lumps. 4. add more water if required, and get to flowing consistency. 5. now heat dosa tawa very well. 6. add a tsp of oil and wipe off with the help of kitchen towel. 7. pour the prepared dosa batter over tawa. you should be able to see some holes forming while pouring batter over tava. thats the right consistency of batter. if not you need to add some more water. 8. cover the dosa with a lid. and allow to cook on medium flame. 9. scrape from sides once the dosa is cooked completely. 10. finally, serve instant neer dosa with chutney or coconut-jaggery mixture.

title: instant oats dosa recipe | oats masala dosa | oats dosa with aloo bhaji
ingredients: 0.75 cup oats, 0.5 cup rice flour, 1 tsp jeera cumin, 0.25 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 cup curd yogurt, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, few curry leaves, 0.5 tsp pepper, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp salt, 3 cups water, oil for roasting, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard rai, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp urad dal, pinch of hing asafoetida, 1 red chilli, few curry leaves, 0.5 onion, 1 green chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 potato, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 lemon juice
instructions: 1. firstly, take ¾ cup powdered oats, ½ cup rice flour, ¼ cup rava and ½ cup curd. 2. now add 1 tsp jeera, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, few curry leaves, ½ tsp pepper, 2 tbsp coriander, ½ onion and 1 tsp salt. 3. add 3 cups of water and prepare a flowing consistency batter. 4. rest the batter for 20 minutes. 5. now carefully pour the dosa batter over hot tawa. 6. sprinkle ½ or 1 tsp of oil from the top. 7. once the dosa roasts to golden brown, add in a tbsp of aloo bhaji. 8. finally, fold the dosa to half and serve instant oats masala dosa immediately with coconut chutney.

title: instant pizza recipe | quick & easy pizza base
ingredients: 4 slice bread, 0.5 cup rava semolina, 0.25 cup curd, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt, 3 tbsp pizza sauce, onion, capsicum, tomato, sweet corn, olives, jalapeno, cheese, chilli flakes, mixed herbs
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixer jar take 4 slices of bread, ½ cup rava. 2. pulse and grind to coarse texture. 3. transfer the mixture to a large bowl. 4. add ¼ cup curd, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ¼ tsp salt. 5. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 6. further, add 1 cup of water and mix well. 7. cover and rest for 10 minutes. 8. after 10 minutes, the rava has soaked well. 9. now add ½ tsp eno fruit salt and mix gently until the batter turns frothy. 10. heat the pan with 2 tsp oil. 11. transfer the batter and spread uniformly. 12. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the base is cooked well. 13. flip over making sure not to damage the base. 14. spread 3 tbsp pizza sauce. 15. also, top onion, capsicum, tomato, sweet corn, olives, jalapeno, cheese, chilli flakes and mixed herbs. 16. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the cheese melt completely. 17. finally, cut the instant pizza into slices before serving.

title: Instant Pot Brown Rice Recipe
ingredients: 2 cup brown rice, 2.5 cup water, 0.75 tablespoon oil, 0.125 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Rinse 1 cup brown rice thrice & drain the water completely. You can also soak it up to 30 mins for softer rice. 2. Add it to a pot along with 1¼ cups water and 1 tsp oil (optional). 3. Pour 1 cup water in the inner pot of instant pot. 4. Secure the lid and set the steam release handle to sealing position. 5. Pressure cook for 20 mins & allow the pressure to release naturally. 6. Following this method I cook up to 2 cups of brown rice along with any one of these – ¾ cup dal, 1 cup soaked chana, 1 cup soaked rajma or up to 4 chilled eggs at one time in my 6 Qt IP. Some times I also cook whole potatoes, yam, beetroot or sweet potatoes along with the brown rice. 7. This method is great as you don’t need to clean the large pot of the IP. You will be left with just the inner pots for a clean up. 8. Cooking in the inner pot : I cook brown rice directly in the inner pot when I have to make over 4 servings or over 2 cups. Usually rice tends to get mushier when made in large quantity following the pot-in-pot method. 9. Cooking it directly in a larger pot prevents the mushy texture. Each grain gets enough space to expand and cook well in a larger pot. 10. Adding a small amount of oil or ghee to the pot prevents the grains from turning sticky & mushy. So absolutely no mushy brown rice even when you cook a lot of it! 11. Sometimes I saute the brown rice in little oil or ghee for 2 mins before pressure cooking. I always feel this sauteing also brings out the nutty flavor of brown rice. 12. Most times I prefer not to flavor my brown rice as we mostly serve it with curry. However you can also flavor it with some garlic, cumin, whole spices, lemon zest or herbs. All you need to do is just saute your flavoring ingredient before adding the rice to the pot. 13. Heat the instant pot by pressing saute button. When it displays hot, pour oil or ghee in the inner pot. 14. Add the rinsed & drained brown rice. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. If you like saute some spices or garlic prior to adding the rice. Cumin, star anise, mace, cinnamon, cloves & cardamoms go well. 15. Pour water and add salt if you like. I do not add salt. You can also use broth or stock instead of water. Give a good mix. 16. Secure the lid of the Instant pot. Set the steam release handle to sealing position. 17. Pressure cook on high pressure and set the timer to 23 mins. 18. When the pressure releases naturally open the lid. If you see a layer of rice being hard, then quickly fluff up with a fork and cover the lid back. Rest for 2 to 3 mins. 19. You will get fluffy, flavorful & perfect instant pot brown rice. Serve brown rice with any curry or dal. You can also use this for fried rice or any tempered rice.

title: Instant Pot Dal and Rice Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup toor dal, 1.5 cups water, 0.625 cup tomatoes, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 green chili, 1 red chili, 1 garlic clove, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoons lemon juice, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 cup rice, 1.25 cup water
instructions: 1. Pressure cook for 10 mins and wait for the pressure to drop naturally. Then make a tempering with spices & ghee in a separate small pan and pour it over the dal. I prefer this method when I cook less dal & rice as the large steel insert doesn’t need cleaning. 2. Second method is to cook dal at the bottom in the steel insert directly and then place a trivet and then the rice bowl over it. I have shown this method below. 3. Press the saute button on the Instant pot. By default it is set to 30 mins. Just ignore this. The display will show you as “ ON ” when the inner pot turns hot. 4. Pour oil to the inner steel pot. Add mustard, cumin, red chili and green chilli. When the mustards pop, add curry leaves, ginger and garlic. Saute for a minute. 5. Add chopped tomatoes, salt, turmeric and red chilli powder. Saute for a minute. 6. Then add the rinsed dal and pour water. Deglaze the pot by scraping the bottom with the spatula. This releases any food bits stuck at the bottom. 7. Place a long legged trivet. 8. Place the rice bowl and pour water. Cover the rice bowl with a plate. 9. Press cancel. Secure the Instant pot with the lid. Position the steam release handle to sealing. Press pressure cook or manual (on old IPs) button. Set the timer to 9 mins. Use the + or – buttons to increase and decrease the timings. The pot will take time to come to pressure and then cook. 10. When the IP finishes, press cancel. Let the pressure release naturally. The pin drops when the IP is depressurized. Open the lid. Remove the rice bowl carefully. 11. Stir well. Check the consistency and salt. If needed add more salt and lemon juice. If the consistency is too thick then add little water. I prefer to add 1/4 cup hot water at this stage. Sprinkle coriander leaves. 12. Serve hot rice & dal with some ghee topped over it. We usually eat with some pickle, pappads or salad. I had set aside some curry leaves and red chilli for garnishing.

title: Instant Pot Tikka Masala Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon ghee, 1 pound chicken, 0.25 cup Greek yogurt, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1.5 cups yellow, 1 inch ginger, 4.5 garlic, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 pound ripe tomatoes tomato puree, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 cup water, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.5 cup heavy cream, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with the ingredients mentioned under “to marinate”. Cover and rest in the refrigerator for at least 30 mins to overnight. (If you refrigerate overnight, leave it on the counter for 30 mins before you begin to cook it.) 2. Add ghee/oil to the steel insert of the instant pot and press saute button. Spread it all over the pot. When the ghee is hot, transfer the chicken and spread it to a single layer. 3. Let cook for 2 to 3 mins and stir to turn the pieces to the other side. Cook for another 2 to 3 mins, until the spices begin to brown slightly. Press cancel and remove the chicken to a bowl. It’s alright if the chicken is slightly undercooked. 4. Pour 2 to 3 tablespoons of water and deglaze the pot well. Pour the liquid over the chicken. 5. Add the rest of the ghee or oil and add onions, salt, ginger and garlic. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. If you want you may saute until golden. 6. Stir in the tomatoes and all the ground spices – chili powder, 1 tsp garam masala, coriander powder and cumin. Pour water. 7. Secure the lid and pressure cook for 10 mins. Release the pressure slowly and blend the sauce with a hand/stick blender to a smooth or coarse sauce. Pour the deglazed liquid to the IP and simmer the sauce for 5 mins, until thick. 8. Add the chicken & mix well. Cook for 3 mins until the chicken is fully cooked through and tender. 9. Crush kasuri methi in your palms and add it along with sugar, more salt & garam masala if required (taste test first). Press cancel and stir in the cream. Garnish Instant Pot Tikka Masala with chopped coriander leaves.

title: instant pulao recipe | leftover pulao rice | rice pulao with cucumber raita
ingredients: 1 cup coriander, 0.5 cup mint pudina, 3 chilli, 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 3 tsp oil, 1 tbsp ghee, 1 cashew, 1 bay leaf, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 4 pods cardamom, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 carrot, 1 capsicum, 0.5 cup peas, 1 beans, 4 cup leftover rice, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 lemon juice, 1 cup curd, 1 cup water, 0.5 onion, 0.5 cucumber, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, to prepare the masala paste, in a mixi take 1 cup coriander, ½ cup mint, 3 chilli, 2 clove garlic and 1 inch ginger. 2. blend to a coarse paste without adding water. keep aside. 3. in a large kadai heat 3 tsp oil and 1 tbsp ghee. 4. add 10 cashews and fry until it turns golden brown. keep aside. 5. in the same oil, add 1 bay leaf, 6 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 4 pods cardamom and 1 tsp cumin. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 6. further, add 1 onion and saute until the onions change colour slightly. 7. now add 1 carrot, 1 capsicum, ½ cup peas and 10 beans. 8. add ½ tsp salt and saute for 2 minutes. cook until it turns crunchy. do not overcook the vegetables. 9. add in the prepared masala paste and cook for 2 minutes or until the raw flavour disappears. 10. now add 4 cup leftover rice, ½ tsp salt and ½ tsp garam masala. 11. mix well combing all the masala with rice. 12. cover and simmer for 5 minutes, absorbing all the flavours. 13. add in fried cashew and ½ lemon juice. mix well. 14. finally, enjoy instant pulao with cucumber raita. 15. firstly, in a bowl take 1 cup curd and 1 cup water. 16. whisk until the curd turns silky smooth texture. 17. add ½ onion, ½ cucumber, ½ carrot, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 chilli, ½ tsp cumin powder and ¼ tsp salt. 18. mix well combining everything well. 19. finally, cucumber raita is ready to serve with pulao, biryani or paratha.

title: instant rava masala dosa recipe | sooji masala dosa | suji masala dosa
ingredients: 1 cup rava semolina suji, 2 tbsp wheat flour, 0.75 tsp sugar, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup curd, water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 3 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 4 potato, 0.25 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 lemon
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixer jar take 1 cup rava, 2 tbsp wheat flour, ¾ tsp sugar and 1 tsp salt. 2. grind to a fine powder and transfer to a large bowl. 3. add 1 cup curd and mix well. 4. also, add water as required and whisk to form a smooth batter. 5. cover and rest for 15 minutes or until the rava is absorbed well. 6. firstly, in a large pan heat 2 tbsp oil, add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, ½ tsp chana dal, ½ tsp cumin, pinch hing and few curry leaves. splutter the tempering. 7. add 3 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 onion and saute until the onions soften slightly. 8. further, add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 9. further, add 4 potato and mix well. 10. mash and mix add ¼ cup water forming a smooth texture. 11. also, add 2 tbsp coriander and ½ lemon. mix well. 12. finally, aloo bhaji is ready to stuff into masala dosa. 13. firstly, pour a ladleful of batter on hot tawa. 14. spread slightly thin to make crispy dosa. 15. spread some oil and butter uniformly. 16. also, place 2 tbsp of prepared aloo masala in the centre. 17. roast until the dosa turns golden brown and crisp. 18. scrape the sides of the dosa and fold half. 19. finally, rava masala dosa recipe is ready to serve with coconut chutney.

title: Instant Wheat Dosa Recipe - 10 Mins | Goduma Dosa or Atta Dosa
ingredients: 0.5 cup wheat flour, 1 cup rice flour, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp cumin, 4 cup water, 1 onion, 1 carrot, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take ½ cup wheat flour, 1 cup rice flour, 1 tsp salt and 1 tsp cumin. 2. Add 3 cup water and mix well. Mix well making sure there are no lumps. 3. Rest the batter for 5 to 10 minutes. after resting, the batter will thicken slightly as the flour is soaked well. 4. Add in 1 onion, 1 carrot, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, and few curry leaves. 5. You can adjust the consistency by adding water if required. 6. When the pan is super hot, pour the batter on the pan. 7. Spread 1 tsp oil and allow to roast for 2 minutes or until the dosa turns crisp. 8. Finally, enjoy the crispy Wheat Flour Dosa Recipe with spicy tomato garlic chutney.

title: Irani Chai Recipe (Hyderabadi Irani Tea)
ingredients: 1.5 cups water, 2 tablespoons black tea, 6 green cardamoms, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons refined sugar, 1.5 cup full fat milk
instructions: 1. Pour 1½ cups milk to a pot and add 1 to 1½ tablespoons sugar. Bring it to a boil & reduce the heat. Let simmer until it reduces to ½ the quantity. 2. You can leave a spoon in the pot to avoid overflowing. 3. Meanwhile crush 4 green cardamoms and keep aside. 4. Add the cardamoms, 2 tablespoons black tea (loose tea leaves or dust) and 1½ cups water to a pot. 5. Cover the pot and bring it to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for 7 to 8 minutes, until the decoction reduces to 1 cup. This decoction is actually made in a special closed pot which traps the steam and retains the flavors of chai. Since we don’t have that kind of pots at home, it can be made in sauce pan. You can place an heavy object so the lid sits tightly on the pot and does not let the steam escape. This is a optional step. 6. This is how it looks when it is done. 7. Strain the chai to a kettle. It should be around a cup. 8. Once the milk has reduced to half the quantity, you will see layers of cream on top. If you don’t like the cream, take a ladle full of milk and pour it back to the pot. Repeat this a few times until the cream breaks down & the milk turns frothy & is aerated. Keep doing this a few times. 9. Divide the milk among 2 to 3 serving cups. 10. Top with the decoction. You can play with the quantity of decoction and milk to suit your taste. Serve Irani Chai hot or warm with your favorite tea-biscuits.

title: Jackfruit Seeds Kurma Curry
ingredients: 1 cup jackfruit seeds, 1 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1.5 teaspoon Ginger garlic paste, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 2 tablespoons Oil, 1 teaspoon Garam masala powder, 0.25 tsp Red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon Salt, 1 tbsp tamarind paste, 0.25 cup coconut, 2 tbsps poppy seed
instructions: 1. Chop each jack fruit seed to half or quarters. Peel off the white skin. 2. Scrapping off the brown skin is optional. It can be scraped off with a knife if you prefer. 3. Rinse the seeds and boil them till tender in enough water. Drain the water and set them aside. 4. Dry roast the poppy seeds on a low heat until crunchy. Cool and make a fine powder in a blender. Add coconut to the same blender and make a smooth paste with little water 5. Pour oil to a hot pan and add cumin & curry leaves (or bay leaf). 6. Once the seeds crackle, add ginger garlic paste. Saute till it turns aromatic. 7. Add chopped onions , slit green chilies and fry till the onions turn golden. 8. Saute jack fruit seeds, turmeric, garam masala and red chili powder for about 2 mins. 9. Add the coconut paste and fry for another 3 minutes. 10. If using add the tamarind paste, salt and water just enough to cover the seeds. 11. Cover and cook jackfruit seeds curry on a low flame for 5 to 8 minutes or till you see traces of oil on top. 12. Serve the curry with rice or roti.

title: Jal Jeera Recipe | Jal Jeera Drink with Homemade Powder
ingredients: 2 tbsp cumin, 2 tsp pepper, 2 tbsp black salt, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp ginger powder, 2 tbsp amchur powder, 2 tbsp sugar, 3 tbsp mint, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.75 tsp jal jeera powder, ice cubes, lemon slices
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan take 2 tbsp cumin and roast on low flame. 2. Roast until the cumin turns aromatic and golden brown. 3. Also, add 2 tsp pepper and continue to roast on low flame until it turns aromatic. 4. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 5. Add 2 tbsp black salt, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp ginger powder, 2 tbsp amchur powder and 2 tbsp sugar. 6. Grind to fine powder and Homemade Jal Jeera Powder is ready. 7. To prepare pudina jal jeera drink, in mixer jar take 3 tbsp mint, 2 tbsp coriander, ¾ tsp jal jeera powder and grind to fine paste. 8. In a tall glass take a few ice cubes and pour in the prepared pudina jaleera drink mixture. 9. Add in ice-cold water or soda and lemon slices. 10. Finally, enjoy Jal Jeera Recipe garnished with mint.

title: Jalebi recipe
ingredients: 1 cup all-purpose flour 125 grams, 2 tablespoons corn starch white corn flour, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 cup curd, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon baking soda, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, oil, 1 cup sugar, 0.625 cup water, 1 pinch saffron, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 sauce bottle
instructions: 1. Add sugar and water to a pot. 2. Boil on a medium heat until it reaches a 1 string consistency. 3. Take a small portion of the syrup in a spoon. Cool it slightly. 4. Take it in between your thumb and fore finger. 5. Gently move the fingers away from each other, you must see a single string. 6. Pour lemon juice, cardamom powder and saffron. Remove from heat. Stir and set aside. 7. If your syrup goes beyond this stage, then sprinkle some water and mix. Recheck for the 1 string consistency. 8. A. Method 1: For Instant jalebi : Add maida, cornflour and turmeric to a mixing bowl. Mix everything well until uniform. Next add curd. Pour water & make a thick lump free batter. The batter has to be thick but of flowing consistency. If needed add more water. Beat the batter well with a whisk in one direction in a circular motion for 4 mins. The batter will turn smooth. 9. B. Method 2: For fermentation : Mix together maida, besan & turmeric in a bowl. Pour water & make a thick batter of pouring consistency. Cover and ferment for 12 to 24 hours until the batter gets a very mild sour flavor. The batter will not rise, but will have tiny bubbles on top. 10. Heat ghee or oil on a medium heat to fry jalebis. 11. If using oil, then add 1 to 2 tbsp ghee to the oil. This enhances the flavor. 12. Pour 1 tsp lemon juice to the batter & mix. Skip the lemon juice if you have fermented the batter. 13. Add soda and mix gently just until combined. 14. Check batter consistency: The prepared batter must be smooth free flowing and thick. Spoon just 2 to 3 tbsp of batter to the sauce bottle to check if the consistency is right. 15. Next check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of the batter. It has to come up immediately without browning. 16. Now squeeze the bottle gently and move in circular motion to get spiral. If you are getting very thick jalebi, then the batter is thick. Next if you are getting very thin flat jalebis the batter is thin. 17. To fix thick batter, add a tbsp or more water. Next to fix thin batter, add a tbsp of maida (all purpose flour). Take a look at the pic in the FAQ section to fix the batter. 18. Mix the batter well. Spoon it to the bottle. 19. Ensure oil is hot and the flame set to medium high heat. 20. Squeeze in the batter gently in circular motions starting from the center moving outside. 21. You can also do it the other way. You will get properly shaped ones after making a few. 22. While the jalebi is getting fried, check the syrup. It must be slightly hot to warm when the jalebi is dipped into it. If not heat up a bit. 23. The last 1 minute, turn the flame to low and fry the jalebi. This helps to make them extra crisp. 24. When the jalebi is done it turns crisp. Remove it with a skewer and add to the warm sugar syrup directly. 25. Allow to rest for 2 mins. Remove to a plate. Continue to make more jalebis. 26. Serve jalebi hot.

title: Javvarisi Payasam Recipe (Sabudana Payasam)
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 15 cashewnuts, 1 raisins, 0.5 cup Javvarisi, 2 cups water, 1 cup full fat milk, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.3333333333333333 cup jaggery
instructions: 1. Heat ghee in a heavy bottom pot. When the ghee becomes hot, fry cashews until light golden. 2. Then add raisins and stir until they turn plump. Remove them to a bowl and set aside for garnishing later. 3. Add javvarisi and fry them in the ghee stirring continuously. Under 1 to 2 minutes they will begin to pop and become light. 4. Pour water & give a good stir. Let the water come to a rolling boil. Simmer & cook until sabudana is transparent meaning it is cooked. Keep stirring in between so they do not stick to the bottom of the pot & burn. 5. When they are done, they will turn transparent and the whole mixture will become starchy. If javvarisi is still undercooked then you may add more hot water and cook further. 6. Pour milk and give a good stir. If you prefer more milk you can add more milk. Cook on a low to medium heat until it turns thick. Stir in the ground cardamom. 7. If using Sugar : – Stir in the sugar and cook until it dissolves for 2 to 3 minutes. 8. If using Jaggery: – To use jaggery let the javvarisi payasam cool down a bit. Take off the pot from the heat and set aside to cool. 9. Meanwhile make the jaggery syrup. Or you can add the jaggery powder directly after the payasam cools down a bit. 10. To make the jaggery syrup dissolve ⅓ cup (80 grams) jaggery with 3 tbsps water. Bring it to a rolling boil. Simmer until it dissolves and thickens slightly. Turn off and cool this as well. 11. Filter the jaggery syrup to the cooked sabudana and give a good stir. But remember not to add hot syrup to the hot payasam. I cool down both and mix when they are warm. 12. Add ghee fried cashewnuts and raisins. Serve javvarisi payasam warm or cold as a dessert. 13. Press saute button on the Instant pot and add ghee to the steel insert. When the ghee melts, fry the nuts until light golden. Then fry the raisins until they become plump. Remove them to a plate. 14. Add javvarisi and fry them for 1 to 2 mins. They will pop in sometime and become light. 15. Pour water and deglaze the pot by scrubbing the bottom with the spatula. Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position the steam release valve to sealing. Press the pressure cook button and set the timer to 3 mins. I used the regular sized normal sago. If you use smaller sago then you may cut down the time. 16. When the instant pot is done, it beeps. Wait for the pressure to drop naturally. Then open the lid and give a good stir. 17. Pour milk and cook on saute mode until the payasam turns thick and creamy. 18. Add cardamom powder and sugar. Let it simmer for 2 to 3 mins until the sugar dissolves. To use jaggery, remove the steel insert from the Instant pot and cool down slightly. 19. Stir in powdered jaggery until all of it dissolves. You can also make jaggery syrup by dissolving it with 3 tbsps water. Bring it to a rolling boil and then simmer until thick. Cool down and filter this to the payasam. Ensure both these are not very hot. 20. Garnish javvarisi payasam with fried nuts and cashews. 21. Add jaggery and ½ cup water to a pan, melt it on a low heat without burning. Filter the syrup and boil it for 2 to 3 minutes or till it becomes thick. Cool this completely. 22. Soak sabudana in water until needed. 23.
