Wash chana dal and cook with 1 & ½ cup water until half done. 24. Add milk, soaked sabudana and cook till the sabudana and dal are fully cooked. 25. Turn off the stove. Add cardamom powder, cooled jaggery syrup and coconut. Mix well and do not cook any longer as it can curdle the payasam. 26. Fry nuts in ghee and add to the channa dal sabudana kheer.

title: Jeera aloo recipe
ingredients: 4 medium potatoes, 2 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1 green chilli, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.25 teaspoon mango powder, 0.375 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon fenugreek leaves, 3 tablespoon water, 2.5 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.75 teaspoon cumin powder, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder
instructions: 1. Rinse potatoes under running water and boil them just until fork tender, without making them mushy. 2. To boil on stovetop - Add them to a pot & pour enough water to cover them. Bring the water to a rolling boil and reduce the flame to medium. Cover the pot and boil them until fork tender yet firm. 3. To cook them in pressure cooker, pour 1 cup water to the pressure cooker. Place the potatoes in it. Pressure cook for 3 to 4 whistles depending on the size of the potatoes. When the pressure releases naturally, open the lid and remove the potatoes. 4. Cooking in Instant pot - Pour 1 cup water to the IP. Place a trivet and then the potatoes over them. Press pressure cook button and set the timer for 6 to 8 mins, depending on the size. The largest russet potatoes will take about 10 minutes. When the pressure drops, remove the potatoes. 5. Cool them and peel off the skin. Cube them to desired sizes. I cut mine to about 1 inch pieces. Set them aside. 6. Pour oil or ghee to a hot pan. When the oil turns hot, add cumin seeds. 7. When they begin to splutter, add ginger and green chilies. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds. 8. Reduce the flame to low. Then add in hing, turmeric, red chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, salt and mango powder (optional). Make sure the pan is not too hot at this stage as we don't want the spices to burn. 9. Take kasuri methi in your palm and crush them in between your palms. Add it here. Saute till fragrant. This takes just a minute. 10. Pour 3 tablespoons water and combine well. Very soon the mixture thickens. 11. Add in the boiled aloo. Toss them gently and cover the pan. Cook for 2 to 3 mins on a low heat. 12. Sprinkle coriander leaves. Taste test and add more salt if needed. If you have not added mango powder, sprinkle lemon juice. 13. Serve jeera aloo with rice-dal, poori, roti or chapati.

title: jeera aloo recipe | aloo jeera recipe | aloo jeera fry
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 tbsp cumin seeds jeera, 1 inch ginger, 2 green chilli, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.75 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, pinch of hing asafoetida, salt to taste, 2 boiled potatoes, tbsp water, 2 tbsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, heat 2 tsp oil and saute 1 tbsp cumin seeds till it turns aromatic. 2. add in 1 inch ginger and 2 green chilli. saute for a minute. 3. further keeping the flame low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¾ tsp aamchur, pinch of hing and salt to taste. 4. saute on low flame without burning the spices. 5. additionally add 2 boiled and cubed potatoes. (i have pressure cooked potatoes for 2 whistles) 6. mix gently without breaking potatoes. 7. add 2 tbsp of water, cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 8. after 5 minutes, potatoes have absorbed all spices. 9. finally, add coriander leaves and serve jeera aloo with rice or chapati.

title: Jeera Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups aged basmati rice, 2 tablespoon ghee, 1 green chili, 2.5 cups water to pressure cook or, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2 teaspoons cumin seeds, 1 bay leaf, 4 green cardamoms, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Rinse 1½ cups aged basmati rice thrice and soak in 3 cups water for 20 mins. You can also follow the instructions on the pack. Drain to a colander and keep aside. If you are not using aged premium quality basmati, please do not soak. Just rinse, drain and start to cook. 2. On a low to medium flame, heat 2 tablespoons ghee or oil in a pot or pressure cooker. (Alternately use a instant pot on saute mode). Add the following whole spices: 3. bay leaf (Indian tej patta) 2 inch cinnamon piece 4 cloves 4 green cardamoms 2 teaspoons jeera (cumin seeds) 1 strand of mace (javitri) (optional). You can also omit all the whole spices mentioned above and just use jeera but you will miss those other flavors. 4. Let them sizzle for 2 mins, until jeera gets roasted well and begins to smell aromatic. This is the key step to bring out the flavor from jeera. Optionally add ¼ cup sliced onions and saute for 5 to 6 mins, until golden. 5. Add green chili and drained basmati rice. 6. Fry for 2 to 3 mins on a high flame, stirring only 3 to 4 times. Do not stir vigorously the rice grains can break. 7. Wash rice very well a few times. Drain and soak it for 20 mins. Drain the rice to a colander. Bring 4 cups of water to a boil. Then add some salt. When the water comes to a rolling boil, add the drained rice and cook on a medium high heat until the rice is just done. The rice has to be al dente meaning just cooked and not mushy. Then drain off the rice to a colander and keep it covered. 8. After 2 to 3 mins, fluff up the rice with a fork. You will have al dente cooked soft yet fluffy rice. Cool this completely. 9. Add 1 tbsp butter to a hot pan. Next add star anise, mace, cloves, cinnamon, cardamom, bay leaf and green chili. Saute for 1 to 2 mins. 10. Lower the heat, add jeera. 11. Saute until you begin to smell them good. It takes around 2 to 3 mins. Switch off the stove. If using butter it will froth up, you can just ignore it. 12. Add 1 tbsp more of butter along with finely chopped coriander leaves. If your rice isn’t got enough salt you can add a bit more to the pan. 13. Then add the tempering to the rice. Mix gently all the spices & herbs. Make sure the rice is cooled completely before mixing it otherwise it will clump up and turn mushy. Serve jeera rice with any gravy, curry or dal.

title: jeera rice recipe | how to make jeera rice | jeera pulao
ingredients: 0.5 cup basmati rice, 1 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 2 tbsp coriander, water, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 cup basmati rice, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. now in a large heat 1 tsp ghee and splutter 1 tsp cumin seeds. 2. now add in cooked basmati rice, ¼ tsp salt and mix gently. 3. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander leaves and enjoy jeera rice with dal. 4. firstly, in a cooker heat 1 tsp ghee and splutter 1 tsp cumin seeds. 5. now add ½ cup basmati rice (soaked 20 minutes) and saute for a minute. 6. add 1 cup of water, ¼ tsp salt and mix well. 7. cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles. 8. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander leaves and enjoy jeera rice with dal.

title: Jeera Water (Cumin Water)
ingredients: 0.5 teaspoon Jeera, 1.25 cups water
instructions: 1. Pour water to a non-reactive pot or pan and then add jeera. You can also crush the seeds before adding to the water, so they release their flavor and volatile oils. You can do this easily by using a rolling pin and a board or using a mortar pestle. 2. On a medium heat, bring the water to a gentle boil. 3. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 4. Increase the heat slightly and let it come to a boil again. You will see the seeds being swollen up and begin to release the aroma. 5. Turn off the stove and cover it. Remove pan from heat and let cumin water cool somewhat. 6. It is best to have this warm. You may leave it on the stovetop so it remains warm longer. 7. Strain out the seeds from the water with a fine strainer or muslin. Strain it to serving cups. If the flavor is too strong for your taste you can also dilute it with more warm water. 8. Traditionally jeera water is consumed plain & more often at room temperature. This is how I always have it. But if you are trying to incorporate this for weight loss then you may squeeze in some lemon juice. Remember not to add lemon juice to hot jeera water. 9. Add honey if you prefer, if possible Manuka honey. Both lemon juice and honey are optional. Serve cumin water warm and fresh, the same day for maximum health benefits. 10. When mint or basil is added to the water, it enhances its digestive benefits. 11. Lemon juice added to jeera water helps with weight loss. 12. Honey and cinnamon powder added to cumin water fights respiratory infections. 13. Adding cinnamon powder to jeera water balances blood sugar levels. 14. While you can have jeera water at any time of the day, you get best results when you have it in the morning, on an empty stomach. Lastly if you are having it fresh, drink it at room temperature or when it is still warm.

title: Jhal Muri Recipe (Jhalmuri)
ingredients: 2.5 cups puffed rice, 1 potato, 1 onion, 1 medium tomato, 0.25 cup cucumber, 2 green chilies, 3 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp red chilli powder, 1 tsp roasted cumin powder, 0.75 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 cup roasted peanuts, 0.25 cup Chanachur, lemon juice, 1 tbsp mustard oil
instructions: 1. To make jhal muri, we need crisp puffed rice. If they are not crisp then you can dry roast them in a pan for few minutes. Set them aside. 2. Boil potato until just cooked. Cube them and set aside. 3. Dry roast peanuts and remove the skin. Set these aside. 4. Chop tomatoes, onions, green chilies and coriander leaves 5. To a mixing bowl add tomatoes, cucumber, onions, potatoes, coriander leaves and green chilies. 6. Add salt,red chili powder, cumin powder and chaat masala. 7. Squeeze the lemon. Give them a good stir. 8. Add peanuts and puffed rice. 9. Toss them all. You can add some more puffed rice if the mixture is moist. Then add chanachur or sev. 10. Serve jhal muri immediately.

title: jhal muri recipe | jhal muri masala recipe | jhalmuri or jhaal muri
ingredients: 3 cup puffed rice murmura churmuri, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp cucumber, 1 cubes potato, 3 tbsp peanuts, 2 tbsp tomato, 1 green chillli, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 tbsp tamarind pulp, 2 tbsp mustard oil, 3 tbsp sev, 1 tsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 cup puffed rice. dry roast puffed rice if they are not crispy. 2. also add ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp chaat masala, ¼ tsp aamchur and ¼ tsp salt. 3. additionally add 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp cucumber, 10 cubes potato, 3 tbsp peanuts, 2 tbsp tomato, 1 green chillli and ½ inch ginger. 4. further add 1 tbsp tamarind pulp, 2 tbsp mustard oil and 3 tbsp sev. 5. mix well without making puffed rice soggy. 6. finally, serve jhal muri topped with coriander leaves.

title: Jowar Dosa Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 0.5 cup jowar, 0.5 cup raw rice, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 1 cup urad dal, 3 cups cups jowar flour, 0.25 cup thick poha, 1.5 cups water, 1 cup jowar flour, 2 tablespoons onions, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 5.5 curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 cups water, 2 tablespoons ghee
instructions: 1. Wash jowar several times until the water runs clear. Soak it for about 6 hours. Wash rice, dal and methi. Soak them separately for 4 hours. Soaking the lentils and rice for longer can make the batter sour quickly with in few hours. (you may experiment to see what works) 2. Drain the water and blend dal, rice and methi with water just as needed until batter is smooth, fluffy and bubbly. 3. Drain water from jowar. Blend with water as needed, try to use less water. Since the grains are soaked well, the batter gets done well to smooth. 4. Transfer this to the urad dal batter. Mix well. The consistency should be moderately thick like the regular dosa batter. 5. Ferment overnight in a warm place or for 4 to 6 hours depending on the weather conditions. When you are ready to make the dosa, pour water as required and bring to a pouring consistency. (jump to make dosa section below) 6. Wash and soak urad dal for 6 hours. Rinse and soak poha in 1/3 cup water for at least 15 mins. Later rinse the urad dal and blend with poha and water to a bubbly, smooth and thick consistency. 7. To a large mixing bowl, add the jowar flour and pour water. Mix well with a whisk or hand to a smooth batter. Transfer the urad dal batter and mix both, very well. 8. Cover and ferment in a warm place overnight to 12 hours, until slightly bubbly and raised. Avoid over fermenting. (Read tips in the post to ferment well) 9. When you are ready to make your dosa, add salt as required and water (only if necessary) to bring to a spreading consistency. I add about 3 to 4 tbsps only. Mix very well to collapse some of the bubbles. 10. Grease if using a cast iron pan, a non-stick pan does not require greasing. Heat on a medium flame until hot enough, a sprinkle of little water should sizzle and evaporate. 11. Pour a ladle of batter in the center of the pan and begin to spread in circular motion, from the center moving to the edges. (see pictures) 12. Drizzle some butter/ghee/oil and let cook on a medium high flame until golden. The sides of the dosa will begin to leave the pan when it is ready. Gently remove to a plate or turn it to the other side and cook for a minute. 13. Turn it to the other side again and cook for 30 to 60 seconds until crisp. Remove to a serving plate. Make sure the pan is hot before making the next one and do not forget to stir the batter in the bowl. 14. In a mixing bowl, stir together flour, onions, chilies, curry leaves, coriander leaves, cumin, salt and ginger. Pour half of the water and begin to mix to a lump free batter, adding more water as needed. 15. The consistency of the batter should be runny for a thin dosa, slightly thicker for a thicker dosa. Rest aside for 15. 16. Grease your pan and heat on a medium high flame until really hot. A few drops of water sprinkled on the pan should sizzle and evaporate instantly. 17. Stir the batter very well with a ladle, as the solids settle at the bottom. Regulate the flame to medium high and begin to pour the batter, moving forward – starting from the edges in a circular pattern to cover the entire pan. 18. You will see some unfilled pockets that you need to fill up with the batter. (Check the pictures) 19. Drizzle 1 teaspoon ghee or oil across the edges & over the dry spots. Let cook for 3 mins on a medium high until the edges begin to leave the pan. 20. Gently turn with a spatula to the other side and cook for another 1 min. Lastly turn it again and cook until the base is crisp, for another minute. This is just a guideline for timing, it may vary depending on the kind of stove and cookware used. 21. Remove to a serving plate. Before you make the next dosa, make sure the pan is really hot (not smoking) and give the batter a good stir else the solids settle at the bottom.

title: Jowar Pancake Recipe | Jowar Bun Dosa - Healthy Dosa
ingredients: 0.75 cup jowar flour, 0.5 cup rava semolina, 0.75 cup curd, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp cabbage, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp turmeric, pinch hing, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take ¾ cup jowar flour, ½ cup rava, ¾ cup curd and 1 tsp salt. 2. Add ½ cup of water and mix well, making sure to have a smooth consistency. 3. Add ½ carrot, ½ onion, ½ capsicum, 2 tbsp cabbage, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp turmeric and a pinch of hing. 4. Mix well, making sure everything is well combined. 5. Further add ½ tsp eno fruit salt and mix till the batter turns frothy. 6. Now, heat the small pan and grease the tawa with oil. Pour a ladleful of batter over the pan. 7. Cover and simmer for 2 minutes. 8. Once the base turns golden brown, flip over the pancake. 9. Cook on both sides, making sure the dosa is cooked uniformly. 10. Finally, enjoy the Jowar Pancake with sauce or chutney. 11. Cover and simmer for 2 minutes. Once the base turns golden brown, flip over the pancake.

title: Jowar pops recipe | Spiced jowar puffs recipe | Jonna pelalu
ingredients: 25 grams Jowar, 1 tbsp oil, 0.375 tsp red chili powder, salt, 0.25 tsp turmeric, Roasted Peanuts, Fried gram
instructions: 1. Wash jowar in lot of water and drain them quickly. Do not allow them to soak up in water. Drain to a colander. Spread them on a cotton cloth and allow to air dry until all the water dries up. I do not allow to dry sometimes I use another cloth and wipe off the water. 2. I use a 4.5 liters steel cooker that is wide enough to make these. Add some grains good enough to spread them in a single layer. Make sure you do not add too many grains at one time. It is good to make these in a wide steel pot. 3. Cover with a loose fitted lid leaving a vent for the steam to escape. Put on the flame to medium high. Very high flame will burn them and very low flame will not pop them. So keep the flame right. With in minutes, you will hear them popping. Once the popping reduces, transfer to a wide dry utensil. Continue to make the next batch. 4. I made a lot in just 15 mins. Allow them to cool completely. 5. Heat a pan with oil. When the oil turns slightly hot, switch off the stove. Add salt, turmeric and chili powder. You can also fry peanuts, garlic & curry leaves before adding the spice powders. 6. Mix everything well and check the salt. Allow this to cool down a bit. 7. Add pops little by little and mix. 8. Cool these and store in a air tight jar. Serve jowar puffs as a snack. 9. Wash and drain jowar. Air dry them by spreading on a cloth for about 30 mins or until they dry completely (no dripping water). 10. To a dry heavy bottom pot, add some jowar and spread them across to a thin layer. Do not crowd them. You can add as much as possible but there must be enough space for them to pop well. 11. Cover with a loose fitting lid leaving a small vent allowing the steam to escape. 12. Add oil to a wide mixing pan, heat the oil slightly and switch off. If you like to have some roasted peanuts in this, fry them in the same pan until golden. You can also add some chopped garlic and curry leaves if you like. 13. Add salt, chili powder and turmeric. Cool the ingredients in the pan, add the jowar puffs and mix.

title: jowar roti recipe | jowar bhakri recipe | jowar ki roti | jolada rotti recipe
ingredients: 1 cup jowar flour sorghum flour jolada hittu, 0.75 cup water, 0.5 cup jowar flour sorghum flour jolada hittu
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl or kadai take jowar flour and make a well in the centre. 2. furthermore, add ¾ cup hot water and knead to smooth dough. 3. take a small ball sized dough and make a ball out of it. 4. further, pat and flatten with the help of palm and finger. 5. also dust the work station with little jowar flour to prevent roti from sticking while patting. 6. now pat gently with one hand and the other over side to get round shape. 7. make sure to dust with jowar flour if required. 8. further, with both the hands pat as thin as possible. 9. also dust off excess flour from the roti. 10. furthermore, put the flattened dough onto the hot tawa. 11. now spread water over the roti with help of hand or wet cloth removing excess dough. 12. wait until the water evaporates then flip it to the other side. 13. press gently and cook all the sides. 14. finally serve jolada rotti / jowar roti recipe / jowar bhakri with ennegai or dry garlic chutney.

title: Jowar upma recipe | Jonna upma | Jowar recipes
ingredients: 0.75 cup jowar, 1 cup water, oil, 0.625 tsp cumin, 0.625 tsp mustard, 1.25 tsp urad dal, 1 pinch hing, 1 onion, 2.5 green chilies, 1 tsp ginger, 0.75 cup mixed veggies, salt, turmeric, 0.25 cup coconut
instructions: 1. Wash jowar several times until water runs clear. You may need to rub them in between your palms. Add fresh water and soak for 8 to 12 hours. If soaking for over 8 hours, change the water after 6 to 8 hours. Soaking breaks the hard to digest fibers and increases the bio availability of nutrients. 2. Drain up the water completely. Add fresh water and turmeric. Pressure cook for 3 whistles. 2 whistles on a medium flame and 1 whistle on a low flame. 3. When the pressure goes off, fluff them up. With the amount of water i mentioned in the recipe card, there was no water left after pressure cooking. If it is left, then continue to cook and evaporate the left over water. Set this aside. Even after thoroughly cooking, they will be chewy but easy for digestion. 4. Heat oil in a pan. Saute cumin and mustard. When they splutter, add urad dal and fry until golden. 5. Add onions, chili and ginger.Saute until the onions turn soft. 6. Add chopped veggies. I used potatoes, beans, peas and capsicum. You can add whatever you like. 7. Cover and cook on a low heat until the veggies are soft cooked. If needed sprinkle little water and cook. 8. Add salt and turmeric. Mix. 9. Add the cooked jowar and coconut. You can skip coconut. But it adds taste and flavor. 10. Saute for 1 to 2 mins. Add lemon juice and coriander leaves. Serve hot. It can be served alone or with papad or yogurt. 11. Wash and soak jowar for at least 8 hours. Drain and add 1 cup water and pressure cook for 2 whistles on medium flame with little turmeric. 1 more whistle on a low flame. 12. Heat a pan with oil, allow cumin and mustard to crackle. Add urad dal, saute until golden. 13. Fry chilies, onions and ginger. 14. When the onions turn soft, add mixed veggies and saute for 2 mins. 15. Cover and cook until soft done. If needed sprinkle some water and cook. 16. Add salt and turmeric. Stir. 17. Add cooked jowar, coconut and mix. 18. Stir well and saute for 2 mins. 19. Add lemon juice and coriander leaves.

title: Junnu Or kharvas recipe
ingredients: 1 cup cow s colostrum milk, 6 cups Full fat whole milk, 0.375 tsp Green cardamom powder, 2 pinches Pepper powder, 4 grams Jaggery
instructions: 1. Mix jaggery, whole milk and colostrum milk in a large bowl. 2. Stir well till the jiggery melts off completely. 3. Filter this to a wide and deep bowl. 4. Sprinkle pepper powder and cardamom powder. 5. If you use a bowl that is not deep enough the milk might spill over from the bowl while steaming. 6. Add water to a steamer or pressure cooker or idli cooker as you would do to steam idlis. 7. Place a plate that comes with the steamer. When the water begins to bubble, put on the flame to medium. 8. Steaming the milk on a very high flame will seep the water into the milk bowl and the milk can spill to the steamer. So control the flame well at this stage. 9. Place the milk bowl in the steamer and steam for about 25 to 40 minutes. 10. After 25 minutes, you can open the lid of the steamer and check if the pudding is done. 11. If it is cooked well, you can find some jaggery water over the pudding and the milk all set well. 12. This is the best indication to know if the pudding is correctly cooked. 13. If you find no jaggery water over the junnu or kharva, close the lid and steam for some more time also check if more water is needed in your steamer. Repeat checking. 14. Cool junnu completely or place it in the refrigerator for a while to cool quickly. Cut to desired size pieces.

title: Kachori Recipe (Khasta Kachori with dal)
ingredients: 1.25 cups all-purpose flour, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon carom seeds, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 cup water, 0.25 cup moong dal, 3 tablespoons besan, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon pepper corn, 0.875 tablespoon fennel seeds, 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 3 red chilies, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon dry ginger powder, 0.75 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 2 cups oil to fry, 6 green chilies to serve
instructions: 1. Rinse moong dal in a small bowl and soak for 3 to 4 hours. Later drain them to a strainer. 2. Add cumin seeds, coriander seeds, black pepper, fennel seeds and dried chilies to a small grinder. Pulse them to a coarse powder. 3. Heat oil in a pan and the crushed spices. Saute on a medium flame, for 2 mins until aromatic. Add gram flour/besan and saute for 6 to 7 mins, until very aromatic. You will see it gets clumpy and begins to release oil. 4. Stir in the red chili powder, turmeric, hing and salt. Add the moong dal and continue to saute for 7 to 8 minutes until most of the moisture from the dal is dried out. 5. Stir in dry ginger, sugar/ jaggery, amchur + black salt or chaat masala, garam masala and kasuri methi. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt and spices. Saute for 2 mins more and turn off. 6. Cool down slightly. Divide into 9 portions (1 tightly packed tbsp) and shape to small balls. You will have 1 extra ball that you may use to taste test and adjust salt or spice. 7. Stir in flour, salt and oil in a bowl. Mix well for 2 to 3 mins, until the ghee is incorporated well into the flour & forms crumbs. 8. Pour water little by little to form a non-sticky and slightly stiff dough. (It shouldn't be dry or crumbly). If the dough is dry, dip your fingers in water as you work. Cover and rest for 30 mins. 9. When you are ready to shape the kachori, knead the dough for 2 to 3 mins, until elastic and smooth. Divide the dough to 8 portions and roll to smooth balls. Keep them covered. 10. With the help of your fingers, shape a ball to a small cup or a disc of 3 inches. Place the kachori stuffing in the center. 11. Bring the sides up by pushing the stuffing inside. Bring all the edges on top and smear water. Join and press down to seal. 12. Roll to round ball in between your palms. If the joint looks too dry, dip your finger in water and press down the joint well. (Don’t make it too wet.) 13. Finish stuffing all the balls and keep them aside covered with a cloth or lid to prevent drying out. 14. Heat oil in a deep fry pan on a medium heat. You want the oil to be slightly hot, not very hot. While the oil is heating, shape the kachoris. 15. Without a rolling pin: Take a stuffed dough ball and using your fingers, flatten gently to spread the filling across evenly and shape it to a small puri/ disc (3 inches). 16. With a rolling pin: Place a dough ball on a rolling board and roll with light hands to a small puri (3 inches). Avoid rolling a lot on the edges. 17. Repeat shaping half of the balls. Check if the oil is medium hot by dropping a small portion of dough to the oil. It should not sizzle and rise to the surface & the oil shouldn’t bubble much. You should see few very tiny bubbles at the bottom. This is the right temperature to fry. 18. Gently slide the kachoris, one by one to the pan and avoid touching them for 10 to 12 mins. (They will begin to rise to the surface at 4 to 5 mins mark but will completely float & begin to puff by 10 mins.) 19. When they are firm by 10 to 12 mins, turn them to the other side with a spider and increase the flame slightly to medium heat. 20. Keep stirring every few minutes until they are evenly golden & crisp. (It takes me another 8 mins, but this may vary slightly depending on how many kachoris you are frying, kind of stove, cookware and intensity of heat.) 21. Remove to a cooling rack or a steel colander. 22. To fry the next batch of kachoris, turn off the stove and shape the rest. Turn on again and fry them in medium hot oil. 23. To fry chilies, pat dry the rinsed chilies and poke deep holes with knife or deseed them. Add them to a spider and dip in hot oil & fry in short intervals of 15 to 20 seconds, until blistered. 24. Enjoy khasta kachori hot with fried chilies, tamarind and green chutney.

title: Kachumber Salad | Indian Salad
ingredients: 25 grams cucumbers, 15 grams tomatoes, 5 grams onions, 1 green chili, 0.25 teaspoon Himalayan salt, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 0.125 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 lemon, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse cucumbers, onions and tomatoes under running water. I prefer to sprinkle some sea salt, and spray vinegar over the salad veggies, peeled onion & rest them for 5 minutes. Rub and then rinse them off under running water. 2. I do the same with the rest – lemon, green chilies and coriander leaves. Fill a bowl with clean water and rinse the coriander leaves well. Change the water in the bowl and repeat a few times. 3. Chop or julienne all the veggies. You may peel the cucumbers if you want. Since mine had thin skin, so I preferred to keep them. Add all of them to a bowl. 4. Then add some lemon juice, salt & ground black pepper. Taste test and add more as needed. Add lemon and salt only when you are ready to serve. Do not rest the salad after adding salt and lemon as the veggies will let out moisture. 5. Add little red chilli powder (optional). My Mom always made a spice mix with roasted red chilies, roasted cumin and garlic to sprinkle on this. I use the same. It is super flavorful with hints of heat and pungent flavors. If you don’t like plain kachumber, you can do the same. Serve kachumber right away with an Indian meal. Raita Recipe Cucumber Raita Re... Onion Salad Recip... Vegetable Salad R... Sprouts Salad Boiled Egg Salad

title: Kadai Chicken Recipe (Chicken Karahi)
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken bone-in chicken curry-cut, 0.75 cup onions, 1.25 cup tomato, 0.75 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 Kashmiri dried red chili, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 3 tablespoons heavy cream yogurt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon butter, 0.5 cup onions, 0.5 cup capsicums, 1.5 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1 Kashmiri red chili, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 3 green cardamoms, 2.5 black pepper corn
instructions: 1. On a medium heat, dry roast red chili and coriander seeds for 2 minutes. Add cloves, cardamoms, fennel seeds and cinnamon. Roast until they smell aromatic, for 1 to 2 mins. 2. Cool this and make a fine powder. Keep this aside. 3. On a medium flame, heat ghee/oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and red chili. 4. When the seeds begin to sizzle, add the onions and saute until golden for 4 to 5 mins. 5. Stir in ginger garlic paste and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. 6. Add chicken and stir fry for 3 to 4 minutes, until the chicken turns pale. Lower the heat and stir in turmeric, red chili powder, salt and kadai masala. 7. Mix well and pour the pureed tomatoes. Cook on a medium heat until the tomato puree turns slightly thick & loses its raw flavor, for 3 to 4 mins. 8. Partially cover the pan and cook further until the chicken is tender and fully cooked through. The gravy should be thick at this stage. 9. Optional: Meanwhile melt butter in a wok. Add the bell peppers and onions, stir fry on a high heat until the capsicum is slightly blistered, for 2 to 3 mins, Avoid overcooking, keep them crunchy. Add this to the kadai chicken. 10. Taste test and add more salt if needed along with garam masala and kasuri methi. 11. Stir in the cashew puree. If using heavy cream or yogurt, pour it to a small bowl. Take a portion of the gravy and mix with it. Add back the tempered cream/yogurt to the kadai chicken. 12. Simmer for 2 mins & avoid overcooking to retain the crunch of bell peppers. Garnish with coriander leaves or ginger juliennes and slit green chilies. 13. Serve kadai chicken with flatbreads or basmati rice.

title: Kadai Mushroom Recipe (Dry & Gravy)
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon coriander seeds, 2.5 red chilies, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 6 cashew nuts, 1 cup onions, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.75 inch ginger, 2.5 cloves garlic, 0.625 teaspoon kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2 grams mushrooms, 1 medium onion, 0.375 cup capsicum, 0.75 teaspoon ginger julienne, 1 green chili
instructions: 1. To a grinder jar, add 2. 2 to 3 Kashmiri dried red chilies (or any heat chilies) 3. Make a coarse powder. Set this kadai masala aside. 4. Heat a pan with 1 tablespoon oil. Next saute ¾ inch chopped ginger and 2 to 3 garlic cloves for a min. 5. Add 1 cup cubed onions (2 large) and saute them until they turn pink. Then add 1 cup chopped tomatoes (2 large) and ½ teaspoon salt as well. 6. Mix and fry or cook covered until the tomatoes turn completely soft. Add 6 cashews to the pan. 7. Cool the onion tomatoes completely. Then add them to a blender jar. After making the kadai masala powder, I transferred it to a small bowl. And then used the same jar here. 8. Make a smooth paste of the onions and tomatoes without adding water. Set this aside. 9. Add another tbsp of oil to the pan. Wash and slice mushrooms just before cooking them. Fry them well for 2 to 3 mins. 10. Add ¼ to ½ cubed bell peppers (capsicum) and 1 medium onion (diced & layers separated). Saute for another 2 to 3 mins on a high flame until a nice aroma comes out. Set these aside in a plate. 11. Powder red chilies, coriander seeds and fennel seeds finely in a mixer jar. 12. Add oil to a hot pan. Add 1 fine chopped onion and saute until golden. I preferred to skip using onions here so I save some time. 13. Then add the ground kadai masala powder and saute on a low heat until it begins to smell good. The raw smell of the spices has to go away completely. Next add fine chopped ginger & saute for 30 to 40 seconds until it turns aromatic. 14. Then add fine chopped tomatoes, salt and turmeric. 15. Saute for a few minutes and cook covered until the tomatoes turn mushy. At this stage taste test the onion tomato masala and add more red chilli powder if you prefer the dish to be spicy. Pour ¼ cup water & cook until the masala turns thick. 16. Add sliced mushrooms, diced onions, bell pepper, garam masala & kasuri methi. Mix and fry stirring often for 3 to 4 mins until the raw smell of the onions reduce. 17. After that I prefer to cover and cook on a low heat until mushrooms turn tender so they absorb the flavors & release some moisture. If the veggies become too dry in the pan, sprinkle some water. I did not add any. Taste test and adjust the salt. Garnish kadai mushroom with chopped coriander and grated ginger. 18. Notes: For a slightly creamy consistency of the gravy, blend tomatoes with 10 cashews until smooth and use. After sauteing the mushroom, pour little water and cook until the gravy turns thick and the mushrooms are done.

title: Kadai Paneer Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1.5 Kashmiri dried red chilies, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 0.25 inch cinnamon, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 cup onions, 1.5 to 2 cups tomatoes, 6 cashew nuts, 1.25 tablespoon oil, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 1 onion, 0.5 cup capsicum, 225 grams paneer, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. To a small grinder jar, add red chilies, cardamoms, fennel seeds, coriander seeds, cloves & cinnamon. If you add more cinnamon it can ruin the dish. Make a fine powder and keep aside. 1½ tablespoons coriander seeds , 1 to 2 Kashmiri dried red chilies, 2 to 3 green cardamoms, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 cloves, ¼ inch cinnamon 2. To a small grinder jar, add red chilies, cardamoms, fennel seeds, coriander seeds, cloves & cinnamon. If you add more cinnamon it can ruin the dish. Make a fine powder and keep aside. 3. Heat oil in a pan. Add onions & cashew nuts. Saute them on a medium high flame until the onions turn transparent or pink. 1 tablespoon oil, 1 cup (125 grams) onions , 6 cashew nuts 4. Heat oil in a pan. Add onions & cashew nuts. Saute them on a medium high flame until the onions turn transparent or pink. 5. Add tomatoes and saute until completely mushy. 1½ to 2 cups (300 grams) tomatoes 6. Add tomatoes and saute until completely mushy. 7. Cool this completely and add to a blender jar. Blend to a smooth paste or puree. Set this aside. 8. To the same pan, add oil and heat it. 1 to 1½ tablespoon oil 9. To the same pan, add oil and heat it. 10. Saute onion and capsicum on the highest flame until half done. They must be slightly tender yet crunchy. 1 onion, ½ cup capsicum 11. Saute onion and capsicum on the highest flame until half done. They must be slightly tender yet crunchy. 12. Add paneer and toss for 1 to 2 mins. Set all these aside. Do not over fry these. 200 to 250 grams paneer 13. Add paneer and toss for 1 to 2 mins. Set all these aside. Do not over fry these. 14. Heat the rest of the oil and saute ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes off. 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste 15. Heat the rest of the oil and saute ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes off. 16. Next transfer the ground kadai masala and saute on a low to medium heat for 2 to 3 mins until it turns fragrant. Do not burn. 17. Add the ground onion tomato paste and saute for 2 mins. 18. Next add garam masala and red chili powder. Saute for another 2 mins until the masala turns fragrant. ½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, ½ teaspoon garam masala 19. Next add garam masala and red chili powder. Saute for another 2 mins until the masala turns fragrant. 20. Pour 1 cup water and salt. If you feel the gravy is too thick, pour ¼ to ½ cup more water. Allow it to boil until the gravy thickens and traces of oil appear on top. ½ to ¾ teaspoon salt 21. Pour 1 cup water and salt. If you feel the gravy is too thick, pour ¼ to ½ cup more water. Allow it to boil until the gravy thickens and traces of oil appear on top. 22. Then crush and add kasuri methi. Stir well again. Taste the gravy and add more salt or garam masala as needed. 1 teaspoon kasuri methi 23. Then crush and add kasuri methi. Stir well again. Taste the gravy and add more salt or garam masala as needed. 24. Add the sauteed paneer, capsicum and onion. 25. Stir well & finally add ginger juliennes and coriander leaves. ½ to 1 teaspoon ginger , 2 tablespoons coriander leaves 26. Stir well & finally add ginger juliennes and coriander leaves. 27. Cover and rest for 2 mins. Transfer to a serving bowl and rest for at least 15 mins for the paneer to absorb the flavors. 28. Enjoy kadai paneer gravy with roti, butter naan , paratha or jeera rice.

title: Kadai Paneer Recipe | How to make Karahi Paneer Gravy Restaurant Style
ingredients: 2 tbsp coriander seeds, 2 tsp cumin, 2 tsp fennel, 3 red chilli, 3 red chilli, 2 tsp pepper, 2 tbsp oil, 2 tsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tbsp kadai masala, 1 tsp salt, 3 tomato, 2 tbsp cream, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 inch ginger, 2 tsp oil, onion, capsicum, tomato, paneer, 1 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. Firstly, to prepare kadai masala, in a pan take 2 tbsp coriander seeds, 2 tsp cumin, 2 tsp fennel, 3 dried red chilli, 3 dried red chilli and 2 tsp pepper. 2. Dry roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 3. Cool completely, and grind to a coarse powder. kadai masala is ready. 4. To prepare the sabzi, in a kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, 2 tsp ghee and saute 1 tsp cumin until it turns aromatic. 5. Now add 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 onion and saute until the onions turn golden brown. 6. Further keeping the flame low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tbsp kadai masala, 1 tsp salt. 7. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 8. Now add 3 tomato and saute until the tomato turn soft and mushy. 9. Continue to cook until the oil separates from the sides. 10. Further, in a pan take 2 tsp oil. add onion, capsicum, tomato, paneer and 1 tsp garam masala. 11. Stir fry on high flame until the vegetables turn crunchy. 12. Transfer the stir-fried vegetables, and add half a cup of water. 13. Mix well and cook for 2 minutes. 14. Further add 2 tbsp cream, 1 tsp kasuri methi, crushed, 2 tbsp coriander and 1-inch ginger. mix gently. 15. Finally, Kadai Paneer Recipe is ready to enjoy with roti or naan.

title: Kadala Curry Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup kadala, 2 cups water, 1 medium onion, 1 medium tomato, 1 green chilli, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.875 teaspoon kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 cup raw coconut, 3 cardamoms, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 2 inch Ceylon cinnamon piece, 0.1875 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.5 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons coconut oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1.5 red chilli, 3 shallots, 1 spring curry leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak kadala/chickpeas overnight. Later drain the water and rinse them well before cooking. 2. Add 1 tablespoon oil to a pressure cooker or pot. Heat it. 3. Saute onions and green chilies slightly until transparent. Add ginger garlic and saute for 1 minute. 4. Add the tomatoes, salt, turmeric, coriander powder and red chilli powder. Mix and saute for a few minutes. 5. Add soaked kadala and pour 2 cups water. Pressure cook for 10 whistles on a medium heat, until kadala is soft cooked. 6. While the kadala cooks, on a low heat, dry roast cardamoms, fennel seeds, pepper, cloves, cinnamon and nutmeg, until they begin to smell good. 7. Add the curry leaves and coconut. Increase the flame to medium and roast all of this until for 5 mins, stirring continuously. If you want you can also roast this until golden without burning. 8. Cool and grind to as smooth as possible. Pour half cup water and blend to a very smooth paste. If required you may add 1 to 2 tbsps more water. 9. When the pressure drops, check if the kadala is cooked well. They must be soft cooked. 10. Add the coconut paste. Mix well. Stir and continue to cook for 4 to 5 minutes or until the curry reaches a desired consistency. Taste test and adjust salt. 11. Pour 1 ½ tbsps oil to a small pan. When it is hot add mustard seeds and 1 broken red chilli. As soon as they splutter add curry leaves and sliced shallots. 12. Stir fry until the onions turn golden and aromatic. Pour this directly over the hot kadala curry and cover it.

title: Kaddu Ki Kheer Recipe | Hyderabadi Style Pumpkin Milk Pudding
ingredients: 1.5 cup lauki kaddu bottle gourd, 1 tsp ghee, 5 cup milk, 0.25 cup sabudana, 0.25 cup cream, 0.5 cup mawa khoya, 3 tbsp nuts, 3 drops green food color, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, silver vark
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 tsp ghee and add 1½ cup lauki. 2. saute for 2 minutes or until the lauki turns aromatic. 3. now add 5 cup of milk and get to a boil stirring in between. 4. also, add ¼ cup sabudana and mix well. 5. boil for 5 minutes or until the sabudana and sago gets cooked well. 6. further add ¼ cup cream, ½ cup mawa, and 3 tbsp nuts. stir well. 7. add 3 drops of green food color and mix well. adding food color is optional. 8. simmer for 15 minutes or until the kheer thickens. 9. now add ¾ cup sugar and mix well. 10. boil for 5 minutes or until the kheer thickens and turns creamy. 11. further, add ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 12. cool completely, and transfer to a large bowl. 13. refrigerate for 2 hours or until the kheer thickens. 14. now add nuts and decorate with silver vark. 15. finally, enjoy kaddu ki kheer chilled or you can warm it up.

title: Kaddu ki Sabzi (Pumpkin Sabzi)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1.25 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 2.5 cups pumpkin, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.625 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat a heavy bottom pan with 2 tablespoons oil. When the oil turns hot, add ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. The seeds will soon splutter, reduce the heat to low and add 1 teaspoon grated or crushed ginger (or ginger garlic grated). Saute for 1 minute until it begins to smell good. 2. Add 1¼ cup chopped onions (1 medium onion) and ¼ teaspoon salt. If you don’t want to use onions, you can omit them. 3. Saute until the onions turn golden. Add ¾ cup chopped tomatoes if you want. I have not used here. Saute them well until soft and mushy. 4. Add 2 to 3 cups kaddu (chopped to ¾ inch pieces). Saute for about two to three minutes. 5. Add the following spices: 6. Mix everything well and fry for another two to three minutes. 7. Cover and cook on a low flame till the pumpkin becomes tender. If the pumpkin dries out, sprinkle a few tablespoons water or as needed. I do not add any water. 8. Taste test and add more salt if needed. If you want you can add 1 teaspoon kasuri methi and 2 to 3 pinches of amchur (dried mango powder). I prefer to squeeze some lemon juice while serving, so leave out amchur. Garnish with 1 tablespoon chopped coriander leaves. Serve pumpkin sabzi with roti, quinoa, rice or even with bread.

title: Kadhi Pakora Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup curd, 0.25 cup besan, 3.125 cups water, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili, 2.5 tablespoons onions, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.5 tablespoon garlic, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 teaspoons salt, 1.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 cup besan, 0.75 tablespoon corn starch, 0.75 cup onions, 0.5 teaspoon ajwain, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon baking soda, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 3.5 tablespoons water, oil for deep frying, 1.5 tablespoon ghee, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 red chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon Hing
instructions: 1. To a large mixing bowl, add gram flour and half of the yogurt. Mix well with a whisk until smooth and lump-free. Add rest of the yogurt & mix. Pour water and mix well. 2. Pour ghee/oil to a large deep pot and heat it. Add cumin, methi seeds & dried red chilli. 3. When they begin to sizzle add onions and green chilies. Saute on a medium heat until the onions turns transparent & lose the raw flavor. 4. Reduce the heat to low and add ginger garlic. Saute until aromatic for a minute. Stir in red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric and garam masala. 5. Mix the gram flour and yogurt mixture with a spoon and pour that into the pot. 6. Mix well and bring to a gentle boil on a low heat. As the mixture begins to heat, keep stirring so the besan does not settle at the bottom. 7. Once the mixture is hot and turns slightly thicker, increase the heat slightly and bring to a boil. 8. Regulate the heat to medium and simmer until the kadhi becomes thick and you see spiced oil floating over it. It takes about 30 mins. Keep stirring the kadhi in intervals of 5 to 8 mins throughout the process. 9. When the kadhi is done, stir in the salt and taste test. At this stage kadhi should be thick but not too thick. It becomes more thick as it cools down and also note that pakoras need some moisture to soak up. If it is too thick you may add some boiling hot water. (Check Video for consistency) 10. While the kadhi cooks, you can begin to make the pakoras. Heat oil for frying in a deep pan/pot. 11. To a mixing bowl, add onions, green chilies, salt, besan, corn starch (optional), crushed ajwain, red chili powder, garam masala, ginger, baking soda & coriander leaves. 12. Mix well to a thick pakora batter, adding water as required. Start with 3 tablespoons water and add more as required. 13. Taste test and adjust salt and spice to taste. 14. To test if the oil is hot enough, drop a small portion of pakora batter to the oil. If should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning too brown. 15. Regulate the heat to medium, Drop portions of pakora batter to the hot oil and do not disturb them for a minute or 2. 16. Later stir and fry them on a medium heat until crisp and golden. When they are done, the bubbles will reduce. At this stage, reduce the heat and fry them for a minute or 2 to make them crisp. 17. Remove the pakoras to a cooling rack or a steel colander. Meanwhile make the pakoras with the rest of the batter. 18. Optional - To keep the pakoras extra crisp for longer, after frying all the pakoras, fry them again for a minute on a medium heat. Be careful not to burn them. 19. It is advised to add the pakoras and temper the dish only before serving. Add the coriander leaves to the kadhi. 20. Add the pakoras to the hot kadhi if you want softer pakoras. If you prefer them crisp, add them while serving. 21. Pour ghee/oil in a small tadka pan. When it becomes hot, add cumin seeds and dried red chilies. When the seeds begin to sizzle, stir in the fine chopped curry leaves until crisp. 22. Turn off the heat and add red chili powder and hing. 23. Pour this over the kadhi pakora and serve with rice/chawal or with naan.

title: Kadhi Pakora Recipe | Punjabi Kadhi Pakoda
ingredients: 0.5 cup besan gram flour, 1 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 2 cup curd, 4 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp methi, 1 tsp coriander seeds, pinch hing, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, few curry leaves, 1 tbsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 2 cup besan gram flour, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, pinch hing, 0.5 tsp salt, water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, oil, 1 tbsp ghee, 0.5 tsp cumin, 2 red chilli, pinch hing, pinch chilli powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take ½ cup besan, 1 tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder and 2 cup curd. 2. Whisk and mix well making sure there are no lumps. 3. Add 4 cup water and prepare a smooth watery consistency mixture. Keep aside. 4. In a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, ¼ tsp methi, 1 tsp coriander seeds, pinch hing. 5. Also add 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering. 6. Pour in the besan curd mixture and mix well. 7. Keep stirirng till the besan and curd mixture are well combined, and starts to thicken. 8. when the mixture comes to a boil, add 1 tbsp salt and mix well. 9. Stir occasionally, and boil for 15 to 20 minutes or until the raw flavour of besan is gone. 10. The mixture will turn glossy, adjust the consistency as required. Keep aside. 11. To prepare the pakoda, in a bowl take 2 cup besan. 12. Add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 13. Mix well, ensuring all the spices are well combined. 14. Now add water slowly, and prepare a smooth lump free batter. 15. Also add ¼ tsp baking soda and mix well till the batter is light and fluffy. 16. Wet your hands with water, and pinch off a ball-sized piece of dough. 17. Drop in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 18. Stir occasionally, until the pakoda turns golden brown and crisp. 19. Drain off to remove excess oil. 20. Drop the pakoda into the kadi. 21. To prepare the tempering, heat 1 tbsp ghee. Add ½ tsp cumin, 2 dried red chilli, pinch hing, pinch chilli powder. 22. Pour the tempering over the kadhi. 23. Also add 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp kasuri methi and mix well. 24. Finally, enjoy Kadhi Pakora Recipe with jeera rice.

title: kadle-uddina bele chutney recipe | channa dal chutney recipe
ingredients: 1 cup coconut, 1 tsp kadle bele channa dal, 1 tsp of urad dal, 3 red chilli, 5 curry leaves, ball of tamarind, 0.5 tsp turmeric powder, salt to taste, 0.5 tsp jaggery, 2 tsp oil, 0.75 tsp mustard seeds, 0.75 tsp urad dal, 2 red chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. fry channa dal and urad dal with 2 tsp of oil. 2. add 3 red chillies and continue to fry. 3. also add curry leaves and fry. 4. switch off and allow them to cool. 5. grind the roasted ingredients along with coconut, tamarind, salt and ½ cup of hot water to a coarse paste. 6. take a small pan, add in oil, mustard seeds, urad dal, curry leaves, red chilies. allow to splutter. 7. immediately pour the tempering on the chutney and serve.

title: Kaju Katli Recipe (Kaju Barfi)
ingredients: 1.5 cups cashew nuts, 9 ml water, 0.75 cup sugar for low sweet refer notes, 1 teaspoon rose water, 2 teaspoons ghee, 1 teaspoon ghee
instructions: 1. If you store your cashews in the refrigerator, bring them to room temperature. (Spread on a tray and leave on the counter for about 2 to 3 hours.) Add them to a grinder or food processor jar and pulse a few times until it turns to a powder. 2. Do not run the grinder for long time as the cashews begin to release oil and turn lumpy. 3. To ensure the powder is not very grainy, sieve it if you want and blend the coarse particles again. The cashew powder will be slightly grainy. 4. Add ¾ cup sugar and 90 ml water to a non stick or a heavy bottom steel pan. Heat & dissolve it well. 5. When the sugar melts completely and begins to boil rapidly, add the cashew powder. Lower the heat. 6. Mix well to blend the sugar syrup and cashew powder. The mixture must be smooth without any lumps. 7. Keep stirring and cook on a medium heat until slightly thick, for about 5 mins. Pour 1 teaspoon rosewater or ⅛ teaspoon cardamom powder and one teaspoon ghee. 8. Cook till the kaju katli mixture turns thick, for another 4 mins. It should begin to leave the sides of the pan. If it doesn't then drizzle few drops of ghee again and mix. (It takes me 9 mins in total) 9. At this stage the mixture should not look dry/crumbly but should be moist and sticky. 10. To check if the mixture is ready. Turn off the stove and set the pan aside. Take ¼ teaspoon of this kaju katli mixture and cool down a bit. 11. Roll it to a ball with greased fingers. You must get a ball that isn't sticky. Upon cooling completely this has to get the texture of the katli or fudge. 12. Transfer the cooked kaju katli mixture immediately to a greased parchment paper. Let it cool down to a warm or slightly hot temperature that you can handle. 13. Grease your fingers and knead the mixture until it looks really smooth. Takes me under 2 mins and avoid over-kneading. (Alternately, hold the edges of the parchment paper to cover the mixture partially and knead it) 14. Troubleshooting - If your kaju katli mixture tends to stick a lot, it means it is still undercooked. Add back to the pan and cook the mixture for a little longer. If your mixture looks dry & crumbly, sprinkle some boiled and cooled water and knead. 15. Shape the mixture to a ball and place on parchment paper. Cover it and using a rolling pin, roll to a large sheet of ¼ inch thickness. If you want place the edible silver/ vark directly on it and press down. 16. Cool completely and cut to desired shapes - squares or diamonds. Store kaju katli in an airtight box.

title: kaju paneer masala recipe | kaju paneer curry | paneer cashew curry
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 12 cube paneer cottage cheese, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 1 tsp butter, 1 bay leaf, 3 cloves lavang, 2 pod cardamom elachi, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 1.5 cup tomato puree, 2 tbsp cashew paste, 2 tbsp cream malai, 0.25 cup water, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 12 cubes paneer till it changes colour slightly. 2. keep the roasted paneer aside. 3. in the same oil fry 2 tbsp cashew until it turns golden brown. 4. keep aside once the cashew turns golden brown. 5. now add 1 tsp butter and saute 1 bay leaf, 3 cloves, 2 pods cardamom and 1 tsp cumin. 6. add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until it turns golden brown. 7. further keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp garam masala and 1 tsp salt. 8. saute on low flame until it turns aromatic. 9. add in 1½ cup tomato puree and mix well. to prepare tomato puree, blend 3 ripened tomatoes to smooth paste. 10. cover and boil for 10 minutes or until the oil separate from sides. 11. further, add 2 tbsp cashew paste and saute until oil separates. 12. also, add 2 tbsp cream and cook on low flame. 13. now add in ¼ water and mix well adjusting consistency as required. 14. furthermore, add fried paneer, cashew and mix well. 15. cover and simmer for 3 minutes or until the flavours are well absorbed. 16. also, add 1 tsp kasuri methi and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 17. finally, enjoy kaju paneer masala with roti or naan.

title: kaju pista roll recipe | kaju roll recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup pistachios pista, 0.25 cup sugar, 3 drops of green food colour, 1 tbsp milk powder, 3 tbsp water, 1 cup cashew, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 cup water, 0.125 th tsp cardamom powder elaichi powder, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, silver vark saffron - optional
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take pistachios powder and powdered sugar. 2. also add green food colour and mix well. 3. now add water little by little and knead. 4. further, add milk powder and knead to a non sticky dough. 5. firstly, take sugar and water. 6. furthermore, stir the sugar syrup till it forms 1 string consistency. 7. firstly, add cashew powder to sugar syrup and give a good mix. 8. further, add cardamom powder add a tsp of ghee. 9. continue till the dough is formed leaving the pan. 10. transfer the cashew dough on to the butter paper. 11. furthermore, grease your hand with little ghee and knead 30 seconds. 12. finally, roll the dough lightly thick. 13. firstly, cut the rolled cashew dough to half. 14. further, take prepared pistachios dough and roll into a long cylinder. 15. then slowly start to wrap the cashew sheet and roll enclosing the pistachio roll. 16. then slowly roll to make the kaju pista roll smooth and uniform cylinder. 17. finally cut the roll 2 inches long and serve.

title: Kaju Pulao Recipe | Cashew Peas Pulav - Lunch Box Special
ingredients: 1 cup coriander, 2 inch ginger, 4 clove garlic, 3 chilli, 2 tbsp ghee, 2 tsp oil, 2 bay leaf, 2 inch cinnamon, 5 pods cardamom, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 3 tbsp cashew, 1 carrot, 2 potato, 8 beans, 3 tbsp peas, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 cup curd, 2 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup mint, 3 cup water, 1.5 cup basmati rice
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mortar pestle take 1 cup coriander, 2 inch ginger, 4 clove garlic and 3 chilli. 2. crush to a coarse paste. keep aside. 3. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee, 2 tsp oil. add 2 bay leaf, 2 inch cinnamon, 5 pods cardamom, 5 cloves and 1 tsp cumin. 4. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. now add the prepared masala paste and saute for 1 minute. 6. further, add 1 onion and saute until the onions change golden brown. 7. now add 3 tbsp cashew and fry till they turn golden brown. 8. add 1 carrot, 2 potato, 8 beans, 3 tbsp peas and ½ capsicum. 9. saute until the vegetables turn crunchy. 10. further add ½ cup curd, 2 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt and 1 cup mint. 11. cook on low flame until everything is well combined. 12. add 3 cup water and bring to a rolling boil. 13. once the water comes to a boil, add 1½ cup basmati rice and mix gently. make sure to rinse the rice well and soak for 20 minutes. 14. cover and simmer for 20 minutes or until the rice is cooked well. 15. rest for 15 minutes before opening the lid. 16. finally, enjoy kaju pulao with raita.

title: Kakarakaya fry
ingredients: 25 grams karela, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoons garlic, 5 grams jaggery, 3.5 tablespoons copra, 1 tbsp Tamarind, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 Pinch hing, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.125 teaspoon Turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder
instructions: 1. Skip this step if using curd. Soak tamarind in ¼ cup hot water. 2. Wash bitter gourd thoroughly. Cut off both the ends. Cut to thin round slices. 3. If desired remove the seeds & discard. 4. Squeeze up the tamarind very well and make a thin liquid by adding another 2 to 4 tbsp water. 5. Make sure tamarind water is thin otherwise it may prevent the gourd from getting cooked. 6. Heat a pan with oil and add cumin. 7. When they begin to splutter, add garlic and fry for 2 mins. 8. Add the curry leaves, hing and turmeric. Saute for a minute. 9. Next add the bitter gourd and begin to stir fry for 2 to 3 minutes. 10. Add salt and filter the tamarind across the pan. Use only as needed. 11. Mix well. Cover and cook on a medium heat until the bitter gourd is cooked well. 12. Keep stirring in between to prevent burning 13. Add coriander powder, red chili powder and mix. Saute for 2 mins. 14. Add coconut and jaggery. On a low to medium heat stir fry until the jaggery completely dissolves. Takes about 3 minutes. 15. Check the taste. We prefer to make it slightly hot, sweet and tangy. You can add in more chili powder, jaggery and tamarind water at this stage. 16. Cook until all moisture dries up well. 17. Kakarakaya fry is ready to serve with rice and ghee.

title: Kala Chana Recipe (Black Chickpeas Curry)
ingredients: 1 cup kala chana, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 cup onions fine chopped, 1 green chilli, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 3 cups water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 lemon for serving, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.5 inch ginger juliennnes
instructions: 1. Add kala chana to a large bowl and rinse them at least thrice. Soak them for 6 to 8 hours or overnight in 4 to 6 cups of water. 2. Once soaked drain the water and rinse them well. 3. Heat oil in a pressure cooker. Then add cumin seeds. 4. When they sizzle, add onions and saute until they turn light golden. 5. Next add ginger garlic and saute for a minute or until a nice aroma comes out. 6. Add tomato puree and cook for a while until it turns thick. 7. Next stir in the spice powders - turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder and salt. Saute well until the mixture smells good and begins to leave the sides of the pan. 8. Pour 3 cups water and add the drained kala chana. Stir well and cover the pressure cooker with the lid. 9. Pressure cook on a medium flame for 10 whistles. Let the pressure release naturally. 10. Open the lid and check if the kala chana is tender & cooked. If not pressure cook for 2 more whistles. 11. Then add coriander leaves and all the other optional ingredients - ginger juliennes, ghee and crushed kasuri methi. Simmer for 2 to 3 minutes. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Serve kala chana with rice or roti. 12. Soak them overnight. Drain and cook with 3 cups of water. Keep adding more water as they cook. They will take about 1 hour 30 mins to cook tender. Then add them to the onion tomato masala & simmer for 15 to 20 mins so they absorb the flavors. 13. Press saute button. When the IP displays hot, pour oil to the inner pot of the IP. 14. Add cumin and let them sizzle. Stir in the onions and salt. Saute until they turn light golden. 15. Saute ginger garlic for 30 seconds. Add tomatoes, salt and all the spice powders. Saute until the mix turns slightly thick and aromatic for 2 mins. 16. Pour 2 cups water and deglaze the pot by scrubbing the bottom with a wooden spatula. This releases any bits of food stuck & prevents a burn sign. 17. Then add the drained kala chana and give a good stir. Press CANCEL button. 18. Secure the IP with lid and position the steam release handle to sealing. 19. Press PRESSURE COOK button (high) and set the timer to 35 to 40 mins. Or you can also use the bean chilli mode and set the timer to 40 mins. 20. When the IP is done cooking, it will beep. Allow the pressure to release naturally for atleast 15 mins. 21. This step is optional - Add some ghee, kasuri methi and ginger juliennes. Mix well and cook on saute mode for 2 minutes. Garnish kala chana with coriander leaves. Sprinkle some lemon juice and serve with rice or roti.

title: kala jamun recipe | black jamun recipe with instant khoya or mawa
ingredients: 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 cup milk, 0.25 cup thickened cream, 1 cup milk powder, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup water, 3 cardamom, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 0.5 cup paneer, 1 cup khoya kova mawa, 0.25 cup maida plain flour all-purpose flour, pinch baking soda, tbsp water milk, oil for deep frying, 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp cashews, 1 tbsp saffron water, pinch red food colour
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take crumbled paneer. 2. mash the paneer till it forms a smooth dough. 3. mash till the paneer forms a smooth dough without any paneer particles. 4. now add prepared khoya and mash well. 5. furthermore, add maida and combine with paneer khoya mixture. 6. add a pinch of baking soda and water or milk if required to get moisture. 7. mix gently to form a smooth dough. 8. also prepare stuffing by mixing dry fruits and saffron water, colour with prepared dough. make balls. 9. firstly, take a small ball sized dough and make ball. 10. and place the prepared dry fruit ball in centre. 11. cover and seal the ball. 12. heat the ghee on low flame and when the ghee is moderately hot, fry the jamuns. 13. fry till the balls turns black. 14. immediately, drop the hot kala jamuns into hot sugar syrup. 15. cover the lid and rest for 2 hours. 16. finally, serve kala jamuns when doubled in size.

title: kala jamun recipe | black jamun recipe with instant khoya or mawa
ingredients: 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 cup milk, 0.25 cup thickened cream, 1 cup milk powder, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup water, 3 cardamom, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 0.5 cup paneer, 1 cup khoya kova mawa, 0.25 cup maida plain flour all-purpose flour, pinch baking soda, tbsp water milk, oil for deep frying, 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp cashews, 1 tbsp saffron water, pinch red food colour
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take crumbled paneer. 2. mash the paneer till it forms a smooth dough. 3. mash till the paneer forms a smooth dough without any paneer particles. 4. now add prepared khoya and mash well. 5. furthermore, add maida and combine with paneer khoya mixture. 6. add a pinch of baking soda and water or milk if required to get moisture. 7. mix gently to form a smooth dough. 8. also prepare stuffing by mixing dry fruits and saffron water, colour with prepared dough. make balls. 9. firstly, take a small ball sized dough and make ball. 10. and place the prepared dry fruit ball in centre. 11. cover and seal the ball. 12. heat the ghee on low flame and when the ghee is moderately hot, fry the jamuns. 13. fry till the balls turns black. 14. immediately, drop the hot kala jamuns into hot sugar syrup. 15. cover the lid and rest for 2 hours. 16. finally, serve kala jamuns when doubled in size.

title: Kalakand Recipe (Kalakand Sweet)
ingredients: 1 can tin condensed milk, 4 grams paneer, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 tablespoons nuts, 1 teaspoon edible rose water
instructions: 1. Skip this section if you have paneer or ricotta. Your homemade paneer/chenna has to be moist and unset with some amount of whey in it. 2. Pour full fat milk to a pot and bring it to a boil. Add 2 tablespoon lemon juice or half cup yogurt to the hot milk. 3. Stir and continue to boil till the milk begins to curdle. Turn off when you see the whole pot of milk curdled well & whey has separated completely. 4. Pour 2 cups cold water immediately. Drain this to a cheese cloth lined over a colander. Hold the cheese cloth carefully under running water and rinse it well to remove the lemon flavor. 5. Bring the edges together and squeeze off to drain all the excess whey from the cheese. Hang it to the faucet for about 10 to 20 mins. It should weigh about 350 to 400 grams. 6. Grease a small tray and line with parchment paper. Keep this aside. 7. Pour condensed milk to a heavy bottom non-stick pan. Add the paneer or ricotta cheese. Mix them well and begin to cook on a medium flame stirring continuously. 8. The mixture thins down initially and then slowly begins to thicken. Continue to stir and cook until the mixture begins to thicken. 9. Then reduce the flame to low and continue to cook stirring until it becomes thick. It took about 8 to 9 mins for me. If you are using store bought ricotta it will take longer. (Check the video or step by step photos) 10. Do not overcook. The mixture has to be moist and juicy but not dry. 11. When you see the mixture begins to leave the sides yet remains juicy (not runny) transfer the kalakand immediately to the greased tray. 12. Spread it to ½ to ¾ inch thickness. Sprinkle nuts and gently level with a spatula. 13. Refrigerate kalakand for at least 2 hours. It will set to a soft set crumbly cake. Then cut to desired sizes. I cut mine to 12 (¾ inch thickness) or to 16 (½ inch thickness) 14. Remove it from the fridge 30 to 60 mins before serving. Kalakand is best served warm or chilled but the texture won't be good if it is too cold.

title: Kalakand Sweet Recipe | Kalakand Milk Cake Barfi
ingredients: 3 litre milk, 2 tbsp vinegar, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp nuts
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large vessel take 2-litre milk and get to a boil. 2. once the milk comes to a boil add 2 tbsp vinegar and mix well. 3. add vinegar until the milk curdles. 4. drain off the milk and rinse with water. rinsing with water helps to remove sourness. 5. now squeeze off the water and transfer to a large plate. 6. mash the paneer to smooth texture. keep aside. 7. in a large kadai, heat 1-litre milk. 8. stir and get to a boil. 9. continue to boil until the milk reduces to half. 10. add ¾ cup sugar and mix until the sugar dissolves. 11. further, add prepared paneer and mix well. 12. continue to cook until the mixture starts to separate the pan. 13. further, add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 14. now transfer the mixture to the tray and level up. 15. top with chopped nuts and press gently. 16. rest for 8 hours or until it sets completely. 17. cut into pieces and is ready to serve. 18. finally, enjoy kalakand recipe with chopped nuts for a week when refrigerated.

title: kanda bhaji pav recipe | onion bhaji pav | mumbai style kanda bajji pav
ingredients: 2 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup besan gram flour, oil, green chutney hari chutney, tamarind chutney imli chutney, dry garlic chutney, 4 pav
instructions: 1. firstly, take sliced onion in a large bowl. 2. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp ajwain, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp coriander. 3. also add ½ tsp salt and mix well. 4. squeeze the onions well making sure to release moisture. 5. further add ½ cup besan and mix well. 6. add more besan as required and form a thick batter. 7. grease hand with oil and take a small portion of bhajiya mixture. 8. drop in hot oil and deep fry on medium flame. 9. deep fry until the onion pakoda turns golden brown and crisp. 10. drain off over kitchen paper to remove excess moisture. 11. take pav and slice in centre without spliting apart. 12. on one side apply green chutney and other side tamarind chutney. 13. also sprinkle generous amount of dry garlic chutney. 14. further place a kanda bhaji in centre and press slightly. 15. finally, enjoy kanda bhaji pav with hot cup of chai.

title: Kandi Pachadi
ingredients: 0.25 cup toor dal kandi pappu, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 3 red chilies, 2 garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon tamarind, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 clove garlic, 1 red chili, 0.25 teaspoon cumin, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 1 teaspoon Oil
instructions: 1. Rinse toor dal very well a few times & drain the water completely. 2. Heat 1 tsp of oil & fry the dal on a medium flame till you get a nice aroma. Dal has to turn deep golden. Remove to a plate. 3. Fry red chilies on a low flame till they turn crispy. Add garlic & cumin to the same pan & fry just for 30 seconds. 4. Cool all the ingredients and add to a blender along with salt & tamarind. 5. We usually soak tamarind in little warm water and filter it to the grind the chutney. 6. Grind the ingredients with little water to a smooth or coarse kandi pachadi. Adjust the consistency as desired. Smooth chutney goes well with snacks and breakfasts. Coarse kandi pachadi tastes good with rice. 7. Taste the toor dal chutney and add more salt and tamarind if needed. 8. Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan, add cumin and mustard. 9. When they begin to crackle, add curry leaves and garlic. 10. Fry til curry leaves turn crisp. Pour this to the kandi pachadi and mix well.

title: Karanji Recipe (Kajjikayalu)
ingredients: 1 teaspoon ghee, 0.5 cup coconut, 0.25 cup cashews, 0.5 cup sugar, 1 tablespoon poppy seeds, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 cup all-purpose flour, 2 tablespoons semolina, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 Pinch salt, 0.3333333333333333 cup warm water, oil
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl, add flour, semolina, salt and hot ghee. (Heat ghee in a kadai and pour it here.) Mix everything well with a spoon first and then with fingers. 2. Pour warm water little by little to make a stiff and non-sticky dough. Start with ¼ cup water and add more as required. 3. Cover and set aside till the filling is ready. 4. Heat a pan with 1 teaspoon ghee and fry cashews until light golden. 5. Add coconut and saute until a nice aroma comes out. This takes just 1 to 2 mins. Add cardamom powder and nutmeg (optional). If using poppy seeds you can add them now and saute for just 1 minute until they turn crunchy. 6. Turn off and transfer this to a plate/ bowl to cool. Meanwhile add sugar to a grinder and make a fine powder. 7. When the coconut cools down completely, add the sugar and mix well. For a less sweeter karanji, you may start with lesser sugar, taste test and add more as needed. 8. Divide the dough to 8 equal parts and keep them covered. Begin to roll the balls gently to small puris of about 4 to 5 inches. It should be neither too thick not too thin. 9. If you do not get round shapes, use a round cookie cutter, pastry cutter or a lid of a steel jar and cut to round shaped puris. 10. Add 1½ to 2 tbsps. mixture to these center of the puri. Dip your finger in a bowl of water and smear the water around the edge of the puri. 11. Join the edges to make a semi circle shaped karanji. Make sure you press down well and the edges stick up well. 12. With the help of a fork make light incisions to get a design. 13. Heat oil in a kadai for deep frying on a medium flame. 14. To test if it is hot enough, drop a small portion of the dough to the oil. If the oil is ready for frying, the dough will sizzle and rise up to the surface. If it is not ready it will sink. Also the dough should not brown which means it is too hot. 15. When the oil is just right - medium hot, add the karanji one after the other. Do not disturb them for 2 minutes. 16. When they firm up, turn them to the other side and fry until crisp and golden. Remember that karanji should be fried on a medium heat else they won't turn crispy. 17. Cool karanji completely and store in a airtight jar.

title: karanji recipe | gujiya recipe | karjikai recipe | kajjikayalu recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup dry coconut kopra desiccated coconut, 0.5 cup powdered sugar, 1 tbsp poppy seeds khus khus, 5 cashews, 1 rasins, 5 almonds, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 cup maida all-purpose flour plain flour, salt to taste, 2 tbsp ghee, 0.5 cup warm milk
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take dry coconut. 2. further add powdered sugar and poppy seeds. 3. further add chopped dry fruits. 4. then add cardamom powder and mix well. 5. finally, the stuffing is ready to prepare karanji. keep aside. 6. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take maida and salt to taste. 7. further pour very hot ghee over maida. 8. now rub and crumble the dough well between your hands. 9. furthermore, add milk and knead the dough. 10. further, cover with a moist cloth and rest for 30 minutes. 11. now pinch a small ball sized dough and roll it to a ball. 12. further, roll to a slightly thick puris and cut to a round shape. 13. also grease with warm milk to the edges of the puri. 14. now place a tsp of the prepared coconut stuffing in the middle of the puri. 15. now stick the edges together and press slightly. 16. furthemore, press the edge gently and fold it inside. 17. now take the tip that comes out press it again and fold it. 18. furthermore, deep fry the karanjis till they turn golden brown. 19. finally, drain over the kitchen towel and serve.

title: Karanji Recipe 2 Ways - Authentic & Layered | How to make Maharashtrian Karanji
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp rava, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp ghee, water, oil, 1 tsp ghee, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp raisins, 1.5 cup dry coconut, 2 tbsp poppy seeds, 0.5 cup powdered sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp rava, and ¼ tsp salt. mix well. 2. add 2 tbsp ghee, crumble and mix well making sure the flour is moist. 3. now add warm water and start to knead the dough. 4. knead the dough well making sure the dough is smooth and tight. 5. cover and rest for 15 minutes. 6. to prepare the stuffing, in pan heat 1 tsp ghee. add 2 tbsp cashew, and 2 tbsp raisins, and roast until it turns golden brown. 7. also add 1½ cup dry coconut, and 2 tbsp poppy seeds, and roast on low flame. 8. roast until it turns aromatic. 9. cool completely, and transfer to the large bowl. 10. add ½ cup powdered sugar, and ½ tsp cardamom powder, and mix well. 11. coconut stuffing is ready. 12. after the dough has rested well, knead the dough slightly and pinch a small ball-sized dough. 13. take a small ball-sized dough and flatten it slightly. roll slightly thin making sure it is of uniform thickness. 14. place a tbsp of stuffing in the center and spread water around the corners. 15. fold half, and seal by pressing gently. 16. now you can decorate by folding with hand, with a pastry cutter, or with a knife. 17. deep fry in medium hot oil. make sure to drop them gently. 18. stir occasionally keeping the flame on low. 19. fry until the karanji turns golden brown and crisp. 20. drain off the karanji over a kitchen towel to drain excess oil. 21. finally, enjoy karanji for up to a week when stored in an airtight container.

title: Karasev
ingredients: 1 cup Besan, 3 tablespoons rice flour, 2 tablespoons fried gram flour, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1 pinch soda-bi-carbonate, 0.75 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 teaspoon garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon hing, 1.25 teaspoon ajwain, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, water, Oil
instructions: 1. Pour oil in a kadai heat it on a medium flame. 2. Sieve together besan, rice flour, fried gram powder and soda to a large mixing bowl. 3. Make sure you add only a pinch of soda. 4. Add chili powder or crushed pepper, garlic paste, hing, salt and ajwain. 5. Add 1 ½ tbsps hot oil. Make sure you don’t add more. 6. Mix everything together. You can check the taste now. The mixture has to be slightly salty and spicy. If needed you can add more of salt and chilli. 7. Pour water little by little only as needed. 8. Make a stiff dough. The dough should not be sticky.
