Too much moisture in the dough will make the karasev oily. 9. I choose to go with the plate that has medium holes. You can even choose one with larger holes but it takes a little longer to fry them. 10. Check if the oil is hot. Drop a small piece of dough in the oil. It has to rise and not brown quickly. 11. Hold the murukku mould with both your hands and gently press down to release the sev. When they are about 3 inches long, take a spoon and break them. 12. Karasev dough is very light so they will get broken easily and fall off in the oil. 13. After few seconds gently stir with a ladle to separate any joined sev. Fry them on a medium heat until golden and crisp. 14. Drain them to a kitchen tissue. Continue to make the next batch. 15. Cool karasev completely. Store them in a airtight steel or glass jar. The garlic flavor in the karasev gets enhanced the next day and these turn very aromatic. 16. Powder 2 tbsps of roasted gram in a mixer or spice jar. The powder must be fine. 17. On a medium flame heat oil in a kadai for deep frying. While the oil heats, Place a sieve over a mixing bowl. Add besan, flour, soda and fried gram flour and sieve them together. 18. Add red chili powder, ajwain, hing,salt, garlic paste and hot oil. Mix everything well. Pour water little by little and make a dough. It should not be sticky or too stiff. 19. Sticky dough will make very oily karasev. Too stiff and crumbly dough will make hard karasev. If the dough turns sticky, just drizzle some dry flour and mix. If it turns too dry then just sprinkle little water and make mix it. 20. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small piece of dough in the oil. If it raises and doesn’t brown quickly the oil is hot enough & is right for deep frying. 21. Do not add too much of dough at one time. Overcrowding the pan will reduce the temperature of oil making the karasev absorb oil. Do not disturb for a minute or so. 22. Fry them on a medium heat until golden and crisp stirring often. Drain them on to a steel colander or kitchen tissues. Cool karasev completely before transferring to a air tight steel or glass jar.

title: karasev recipe | kara sev recipe | besan sev recipe
ingredients: 1 cup besan gram flour, 0.5 cup rice flour, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp black pepper, pinch of hing asafoetida, 1 tbsp butter, 0.5 tsp salt, water as required, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 1 cup besan and ½ cup rice flour. 2. add in ½ tsp cumin, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp crushed pepper, pinch of hing and 1 tbsp butter and ½ tsp salt. combine well. 3. now add ¼ cup water (or as required) and prepare smooth dough. the dough will be slightly sticky, thats normal. 4. grease the large holed mould with some oil and stuff dough into it. 5. further, press and spread the sev in hot oil forming a circle in the oil and make sure you do not overlap. 6. after a minute, flip over and fry the other side. 7. finally, drain over kitchen paper to absorb oil and repeat making more khara sev with the remaining dough.

title: kashaya recipe | kashayam powder recipe | shunti jirige kashaya
ingredients: 0.5 cup coriander seeds, 0.25 cup cumin seeds, 2 tsp pepper, 1 tsp fennel saunf, 6 pods cardamom, 1 clove 10 clove, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp ginger powder, 2.5 cup water, 2 tbsp jaggery, 0.25 cup milk
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan take ½ cup coriander seeds, ¼ cup cumin seeds, 2 tsp pepper, 1 tsp fennel. 2. also add 6 pods cardamom, 10 clove and dry roast on low flame. 3. dry roast until spices turn aromatic. 4. cool completely, and transfer to a blender. 5. also, add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp ginger powder. 6. blend to fine powder without adding any water. kashaya powder is ready, store in an airtight container. 7. to prepare kashaya, in a saucepan take 2½ cup water and add 3 tsp of prepared kashaya powder. 8. also, add 2 tbsp jaggery. you can alternatively add sugar for sweetness. 9. stir well and boil for 5 minutes or until flavours are absorbed well. 10. turn off the flame and add ¼ cup milk. mix well. 11. finally, enjoy kashaya to cure cough, fever or boost immunity.

title: kashmiri dum aloo recipe | how to make authentic kashmiri dum aloo
ingredients: 1 baby potato aloo, 1 cup water, oil for frying, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 black cardamom badi elachi, 2 cardamom, 5 cloves 5 cloves, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 cup water, 0.75 cup curd yogurt, 1 tsp ginger powder, 2 tsp fennel powder saunf powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. firstly, in a kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute ½ tsp cumin, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 black cardamom, 2 cardamom, 5 cloves and pinch hing. 2. turn off the flame and add 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder. 3. further, add ½ cup water and ¾ cup curd. 4. mix well till the curd combines well without getting curdled. 5. now add 1 tsp ginger powder, 2 tsp fennel powder and ½ tsp salt. 6. mix well making sure the spices are mixed well. 7. add in fried potato and mix well. 8. add ½ to 1 cup water adjusting consistency. 9. cover and simmer for 30 minutes stirring occasionally. 10. after 30 minutes, the curry will separate oil and also thicken. 11. now add ¼ tsp garam masala and mix well. 12. finally, serve kashmiri dum aloo with roti / rice.

title: Kashmiri Pulao Recipe
ingredients: 2.25 tablespoon ghee, 1 cup basmati rice, 1 onion, 2 pinch saffron, 3 tablespoon milk, 9 cashew nuts, 1 tablespoon raisins, 1.5 cup water for pressure cooker, 1.5 tablespoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon caraway seeds, 1 black cardamom, 1 cinnamon, 3 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 0.5 teaspoon dry ginger powder, 0.25 cup pomegranate arils, 0.5 cup apple and pineapple
instructions: 1. Rinse rice well a few times to remove the surface starch. Pour fresh water and soak for at least 30 mins. After 30 mins, drain completely. Slice an onion and squeeze them gently to separate the layers. 2. Optional : You may skip this step if you want. Heat up milk in a small bowl and add saffron. Stir and let soak. You can microwave milk for 20 to 30 second to heat up the milk or heat on stovetop. Crush fennel seeds coarsely. Set aside. 3. Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a pot or pressure cooker. Add nuts and fry till light golden. Then add raisins and fry until they become plump. Remove them to a plate for later use. 4. To the same pot, add 1 tbsp ghee and heat up. Add sliced onions (layers separated) and fry till light golden. Then turn down the heat to lowest and continue to fry until deep golden but not burnt. Set these aside as well. 5. Transfer any ghee left in the nuts and fried onions to the pot. Add all the spices and fry till they begin to smell good. Do not burn. 6. Add the drained rice. Sprinkle salt, sugar, ½ tbsp ghee and fry for about 2 to 3 minutes. 7. Pour the saffron milk and water. If you prefer to skip the milk, you may add the saffron strands directly. Stir and check salt. The water has to be slightly salty. 8. Cover the pressure cooker and cook on a medium heat until you hear 1 whistle. If cooking in pot bring the water to a rolling boil and then reduce the heat. Cover and cook until the rice is cooked. 9. When the pressure drops, open the lid and fluff up the Kashmiri pulao with a fork. 10. Transfer Kashmiri pulao to a serving bowl. 11. Garnish with fried onions, nuts, raisins, and pomegranate, apple and pineapple chunks. 12. Follow all the saute steps in saute mode. While frying the onions switch to saute low so the onions get fried evenly. Pressure cook for 5 mins and wait for natural pressure release for 5 to 6 minutes and then open the lid. 13. Fluff up the kashmiri pulao with a fork and let cool down for sometime before moving to serving dish. Later garnish with all the fried garnishes and fresh fruits.

title: kat vada recipe | kat wada recipe | kolhapuri kat vada | vada usal
ingredients: 3 tsp oil, 2 pods cardamom, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tsp khus khus poppy seeds, 1 onion, 2 clove garlic, 1 tomato, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 2 tbsp oil, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 3 cup water, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 chilli, 3 clove garlic, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 3 potato aloo, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.75 cup water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, oil for frying, 1 onion, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 cup sev
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 3 tsp oil and saute 2 pods cardamom, ½ inch cinnamon, 2 cloves, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin seeds and 1 tsp khus khus. 2. now add 1 onion and 2 clove garlic. saute slightly until onions turn golden brown. 3. also, add 1 tomato and saute until it turns soft and mushy. 4. further, add 2 tbsp dry coconut and saute slightly. 5. cool completely, and transfer to the blender. 6. blend to smooth paste adding water as required. 7. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and add pinch hing, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder and ¼ tsp garam masala. 8. saute on low flame until spices turn aromatic. 9. further, add the prepared masala paste and saute well. 10. saute until oil separates from sides. 11. now add 3 cup water, ¾ tsp salt and mix adjusting consistency as required. 12. cover and boil for 10 minutes or until oil separates. 13. kat is ready to serve. 14. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tsp oil and splutter 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 chilli, 3 clove garlic and few curry leaves. 15. also, add ¼ tsp turmeric and saute slightly. 16. now add 3 potato and ½ tsp salt. 17. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 18. further, add 1 tsp lemon juice and 2 tbsp coriander. 19. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 20. now prepare besan batter by sieving 2 cup besan and 2 tbsp rice flour. 21. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 22. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 23. further, add ¾ cup water or as required and whisk to a smooth lump-free batter. 24. add ¼ tsp baking soda and mix well forming a smooth batter. 25. now take a small ball sized aloo mixture and flatten slightly. 26. dip in besan batter coating uniformly. 27. deep fry in hot oil carefully. 28. stir occasionally, until the batata vada turns golden and crisp. 29. drain off over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 30. firstly, pour a generous amount of kat into the plate. 31. place 2 batata vada in it. 32. top with few chopped onion, coriander and sev. 33. finally, enjoy kat vada recipe as an evening snack.

title: kathal ki sabji recipe | raw jackfruit curry recipe | kathal sabji
ingredients: 15 pieces raw jackfruit, 0.5 cup curd, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp oil, 2 tbsp oil, 2 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 4 pods cardamom, 7 cloves 7 cloves, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 to mato, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 15 pieces of raw jackfruit. make sure to soak the jackfruit in saltwater soon after chopping to prevent discolouring. you can also use frozen or tin jackfruits. 2. add ½ cup curd, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, ¼ tsp salt and ½ tsp garam masala. 3. mix well and marinate for 30 minutes. 4. now in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil and add marinated jackfruit. 5. fry on medium flame until the kathal is cooked well. keep aside. 6. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 2 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 4 pods cardamom, 7 cloves and 1 tsp cumin. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 7. further, add 1 onion and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 8. saute until the onions turn golden brown. 9. keeping the flame on low add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and ½ tsp salt. 10. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 11. furthermore, add 3 tomatoes and cook well. 12. cook until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 13. add in fried raw jackfruit (kathal) and mix well. 14. add 1 cup water and mix adjusting consistency as required. 15. cover and simmer for 15 minutes or until the jackfruits are cooked well and flavours are absorbed. 16. now add ¼ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 17. finally, enjoy kathal ki sabji with roti or naan.

title: Kathi Roll Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 cup whole wheat flour, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.3333333333333333 cup water, 1 teaspoon oil, 1 potato, 0.375 cup peas, 0.75 cup bell peppers, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 Pinch asafoetida, 0.625 cup onion, 0.75 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoons Green chutney
instructions: 1. Steam or boil potato until just done without making mushy. 2. Add atta to a mixing bowl along with salt and oil. Pour water just as needed and make a soft dough. Knead well until it becomes soft and pliable. Cover and set aside for 20 mins. 3. Divide the dough to 4 to 5 equal parts. Lightly sprinkle the rolling area and the dough ball with flour. Using a rolling pin roll the ball evenly to a round layer. 4. Heat a pan / griddle or tawa on a medium high heat. Gently transfer the roti to the tawa. When you see tiny bubbles, flip it and press down gently with a spatula. 5. Cook on both the sides flipping until both the sides are cooked with golden to brown spots. Add ghee or oil now. 6. Spread a kitchen cloth or tissue over a plate. Transfer the roti to it. Make all the rotis and stack them in the plate. 7. Heat a pan with oil. Allow mustard and cumin to crackle. Add hing, onions and a pinch of salt. Saute until onions turn golden. 8. Fry ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes off. Add all the veggies. Next add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, garam masala & the rest of the salt 9. Saute for 3 mins. Cover and cook on a low flame until the veggies are cooked to your liking. Taste test and add more salt if you want. Add the coriander leaves if using and set this aside to cool. 10. Smear chutney or sauce over the roti. If you are making these ahead to serve later, do not smear the chutney as the rolls can easily turn soggy. Instead drizzle a little over the filling and serve the chutney/ sauce at the time of serving. 11. Add a sufficient amount of cooked veggies to one side of the roti. Begin to roll it tightly and prick a sandwich pick to hold in shape. Or wrap them with a parchment paper to cover half of the kathi roll and tuck the bottom. 12. They are ready to serve with more chutney or sauce of choice.

title: katori chaat recipe | chaat katori recipe | how to make tokri chaat
ingredients: 2 cup maida plain flour all purpose flour, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, water to knead, oil for frying, 1 cup chickpea chana, 1 potato aloo, 0.25 cup green chutney, 1 cup moong sprouts, 0.5 cup tamarind chutney, 1 cup curd yogurt, sprinkle kashmiri red chilli powder, sprinkle cumin powder jeera powder, 1 onion, 1 to mato, 0.5 cup sev, few coriander leaves, sprinkle chaat masala, sprinkle salt
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare maida dough. 2. now pinch a ball sized dough, and flatten it. 3. dust flour as required and roll slightly thick. 4. prick the flattened dough with fork. 5. place a small katori or cup and wrap the dough. 6. deep fry in hot oil or bake in preheated oven at 180 degree celsius for 30 minutes. 7. splash oil till the katori separates from the dough. 8. now fry the katori, till it turns golden and crisp. 9. drain off over kitchen paper and keep aside. 10. prepare chaat by assembling katori in a plate. 11. add in 1 tsp boiled chana, 1 tsp boiled and cubed potato, 2 tbsp moong sprouts, ½ tsp green chutney, 1 tsp tamarind chutney and 1 tbsp curd. 12. also sprinkle chilli powder, cumin powder and salt. 13. add in a tbsp of onion and tomato. 14. garish with 2 tbsp sev and few coriander leaves. 15. finally, serve katori chaat immediately, sprinkling chaat masala over it.

title: kayi holige recipe | naariyal puran poli | kayi obbattu | coconut poli
ingredients: 3 cup maida plain flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, water, 4 tbsp sesame oil, 2 cup jaggery gud, 4 cup coconut, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 cup maida and ¼ tsp turmeric. mix well. 2. now add water and start to knead the dough. 3. add water as required forming a sticky dough. 4. continue to kenad for 10 minutes or until the dough turns soft. 5. also add 2 tbsp sesame oil and knead for a minute. 6. now pour 2 tbsp of sesame oil and soak the dough for 40 minutes. keep aside. 7. firstly, in a large kadai take 2 cup jagger and ½ cup water. 8. stir until the jaggery melts completely. 9. boil for 5 minutes or until a softball jaggery syrup consistency is attained. 10. now add 4 cup of coconut. make sure to grind the grated coconut without adding any water. 11. mix well and continue to cook for another 5 minutes. 12. cook until the mixture holds the shape. 13. transfer to a bowl and cool the mixture completely. keep aside. 14. grease the banana leaf with oil and pinch a ball sized dough. 15. press and flatten slightly. 16. now take a ball sized coconut mixture and place in the centre. 17. pull the sides and stuff in securely. 18. now place it on the banana leaf, and start to press gently. 19. spread it outwards making sure the hoorna (puran) is distributed uniformly. 20. grease the tawa and place the holige. 21. peel gently, making sure not to damage. 22. cook on medium flame until the base is cooked well. 23. flip over and spread ½ tsp oil. 24. cook on both sides until slight caramel spots appear. 25. finally, fold half and kayi obbattu is ready to serve. 26. firstly, grease the baking paper with oil and place a ball sized dough. 27. press and flatten slightly. 28. now take a ball sized coconut mixture and place in the centre. 29. pull the sides and stuff in securely. 30. now place it on the baking paper, and start to press gently. 31. using a rolling pin, spread it outwards making sure the hoorna (puran) is distributed uniformly. 32. grease the tawa and place the holige. 33. peel gently, making sure not to damage. 34. cook on medium flame until the base is cooked well. 35. flip over and spread ½ tsp oil. 36. cook on both sides until slight caramel spots appear. 37. finally, fold half and kayi holige is ready to serve.

title: Keema Naan
ingredients: 2.5 cups all-purpose flour, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 2 teaspoons sugar, 0.5 teaspoon instant yeast, 0.5 cup yogurt, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.3333333333333333 cup luke warm milk, 1 teaspoon kalonji onion seeds, 3 tablespoons unsalted butter, 25 grams keema, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon tomato paste, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Stir in flour, salt, sugar and yeast in a mixing bowl or food processor or stand mixer. Pour yogurt, oil and half of the milk/water,. 2. Mix to form a soft dough, adding small quantities of the remaining milk or water only as required. Knead the dough for 8 to 9 minutes on a low speed or with your hands, until soft and pliable. 3. Oil the bowl and the dough ball with a tablespoon oil. Cover and let rise until doubled or for 2 to 3 hours in a warm place. 4. Heat oil in a pan and add onions and green chilies. Saute until light golden for 6 to 7 mins. Stir in the ginger & garlic. Saute for a minute and add the ground spices – turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder and cumin powder. 5. Deglaze with 2 tbsps water. Add the keema & saute for 4 to 5 mins on a medium high heat. 6. Pour ¼ cup hot water and cook covered until the meat is tender and fully cooked to your liking. (adjust hot water as needed) It is cooked when it reaches 161℉ or 72℃. (read notes) 7. If using tomato paste, dilute it with 2 tbsps water and add it along with salt and kasuri methi. Cook until all the moisture evaporates. Taste test to adjust salt and heat. 8. Turn off and cool down completely. Mix in the coriander leaves and divide to 6 portions. 9. Your dough should have doubled. Divide to 6 portions and roll to smooth balls. Keep them covered until used up. 10. Lightly grease your rolling area. Spread/roll a dough ball to a 4 to 5 inch disc/roti. 11. Place a portion of keema in the center and bring the edges together to seal them. Shape to a round ball. Stuff all the dough balls with keema and place them in a bowl, untouched to each other. 12. Preheat your oven for atleast 10 mins, on a broil mode, with top bottom heating elements ON. Place a dough ball on the rolling area and sprinkle with 2 pinches of seeds and few coriander leaves. 13. Roll it to a 6 inch keema naan. You may keep them thicker or thinner to make larger naans. 14. Place 2 to 3 naans on a tray, lined with foil. Bake in the center rack, for 5 to 6 mins or until golden and crisp. Adjust the baking time as it varies by oven and temp of your dough. 15. Remove and brush with melted butter. Serve keema naan hot or warm with mint chutney, raita and salad. Bake the rest of the naans this way adjusting the baking time.

title: Keema Recipe (Mutton Keema Curry)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 1.5 inch cinnamon, 2.5 cloves 2 to 3 cloves ((laung)), 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 green chili, 1 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 3 grams keema, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 0.375 cup tomato puree, 0.25 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat a pan with oil and add the whole spices - bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, cardamoms. 2. When they begin to sizzle, add green chilies and onions. Saute them well on a medium flame until evenly golden. 3. Add ginger garlic paste & saute for a minute or until the raw smell goes away. 4. Add keema and break down any lumps. Saute for 3 to 4 mins on a low to medium heat. 5. Sprinkle salt & all the ground spices - turmeric, red chili powder and garam masala. Also add mint now. 6. Saute for 3 to 4 mins on a low heat. 7. Cover and cook for about 8 to 10 mins on the lowest heat. You will see some moisture being released and the keema is slightly tender. 8. Add tomato and saute until the tomato blends well with keema completely. This takes about 5 to 6 mins. The raw smell of tomatoes should go away by now. 9. Pour hot water, stir and cook covered until the keema is completely tender meaning it's cooked. 10. Taste test and add more salt and garam masala if you want. Open the lid, evaporate any excess liquid left to suit your taste. We personally love the semi-dry keema so I evaporate the excess liquid by sautéing a little longer. 11. Keep mutton keema covered until served. Garnish with coriander leaves. 12. Sprinkle some lemon juice before serving with plain rice, saffron rice, paratha and fresh cut onions, cucumbers and carrot.

title: Keema Sandwich Recipe | Soya Keema Toast Recipe | Keema Cheese Toastie
ingredients: water, 1 cup soya chunks, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 3 clove garlic, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp peas, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp mayonnaise, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, bread, butter, green chutney, cheese
instructions: 1. firstly, in pan take enough water, 1 tsp salt and get to a boil. 2. add 1 cup soya chunks and boil for 5 minutes. 3. drain off the water and squeeze off soya. keep aside. 4. pulse grind the soya chunks to a coarse texture. keep aside. 5. in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 3 clove of garlic. 6. add ½ onion and saute until onions shrink slightly. 7. now add 2 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp peas, 2 tbsp carrot and 2 tbsp capsicum. 8. saute until the veggies shrink slightly. 9. further add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce and ½ tsp salt. 10. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 11. add in the prepared soya mixture and mix well. 12. now add 2 tbsp mayonnaise, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp mixed herbs. 13. mix well and do not over-cook, as there are chances for mayo to split. 14. keema stuffing is ready. keep aside. 15. to prepare the toast, spread butter and green chutney on the bread slice. 16. top with prepared soya keema stuffing. 17. and again top with cheese. 18. now cover and toast until the cheese melts. 19. finally, cut the toast into half and enjoy soya keema cheese toast.

title: Keerai Kootu (Spinach Kootu)
ingredients: 0.3333333333333333 cup moong dal, 0.875 cup water, 4.5 cups spinach keerai, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 1 green chili, 3.5 medium garlic cloves, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 1 red chili, 0.375 cup water, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 cup raw grated coconut, 0.25 water
instructions: 1. Add ⅓ cup moong dal to a bowl or use ¼ cup moong dal and 2 tablespoons toor dal. If you like more dal in your kootu, you may use ¼ cup moong dal & ¼ cup toor dal. 2. Rinse it well a few times and drain the water. Pour ¾ cup water. If using more dal use 1 cup. Cover, place in the cooker and cook until soft and mushy for 3 whistles or in the instant pot for 11 minutes. You can also cook your short grain white rice (like sona masuri or ponni) in another bowl, along with the dal. 3. While the dal cooks, 4. Wash and chop 4 to 5 cups of spinach (about 120 grams leaves). 5. Also chop 1 small onion or 7 to 8 shallots to make half cup. (may omit if you don’t eat onions) 6. Deseed and chop 1 medium tomato to make half cup. 7. You will also chop 3 to 4 medium garlic cloves (or sub with half tbsp chopped ginger) 8. 1 green chili (you may omit for no heat) 9. Heat 2 tablespoons ghee or oil on a medium heat. Add ¼ teaspoon mustard, ½ teaspoon cumin seeds and 1 dried broken red chili. mustard seeds 10. When the seeds begin to splutter, add the garlic, 1 sprig curry leaves and green chilies and a pinch of hing/ asafetida (optional). If you want you may add the whole sprig of curry leaves as it is , this way you can easily discard it before serving. 11. Saute for a minute and add the onions and a pinch of salt. Saute for 4 to 5 mins until the onions turn light golden or transparent. 12. Add tomatoes and continue to cook until the tomatoes break down, for 4 to 5 mins. 13. Add the ground spices. You can either add sambar powder or curry powder or use the below spices 14. Saute for a minute. 15. Add the chopped keerai/leafy greens. 16. Saute until wilted for 4 to 5 mins. If you are using more mature greens, cook them down until soft for a little longer. They won’t actually cook much after adding the dal. 17. Meanwhile, add ¼ cup raw grated coconut and ¼ cup water to a grinder. (If using unsweetened shredded coconut, soak ¼ cup in ¼ cup boiling hot water until soft, about 15 to 20 mins. Add to the grinder and pour ¼ cup full fat milk.) 18. Make a smooth paste. If you are using shredded coconut, make sure it’s been ground to a very smooth consistency else your dal will be gritty with coarse coconut. 19. When the dal is cooked, add it to the keerai along with the coconut. 20. Mix well and add more hot water as required. You will need about ¼ to ½ cup water depending on the amount of dal you used. Simmer for 3 to 4 mins, until bubbling hot. Taste test to adjust salt. If it is low on heat, add little ground black pepper. If you want remove the curry leaves sprig. 21. I do not make an additional tempering to this keerai kootu because I cook in ghee and it already has a lot of flavor from the coconut and dal. However if you want, you may heat a tablespoon of ghee in a small pan. Add a pinch of each, mustard and cumin seeds, a red chili and curry leaves. When the leaves turn crisp, add a pinch of hing and turn off. Pour it over the keerai kootu.

title: keerai kootu recipe | spinach moong dal | keerai molagootal | spinach kootu
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, 1.5 cup water, 0.5 cup coconut, 1 tbsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp pepper, 3 red chilli, 0.25 cup water, 3 cup palak spinach, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 3 clove garlic, 3 cup water, 2 tsp coconut oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 red chilli
instructions: 1. firstly in a pressure cook take ½ cup moong dal, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil and 1½ cup water. 2. pressure cook for 2 whistles or until dal is cooked completely. 3. now prepare the kootu masala paste by taking ½ cup coconut, 1 tbsp cumin, ½ tsp pepper, 3 dried red chilli. 4. blend to smooth paste adding ¼ cup water. keep aside. 5. in a large kadai, take 3 cup palak, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt and 3 clove garlic. 6. cover and boil for 5 minutes adding 3 cup water. 7. now add the prepared coconut masala paste and mix well. 8. boil for 3 minutes or until the raw flavour of coconut is disappeared. 9. now add in cooked moong dal and mix well. 10. adjust the consistency adding water if required. 11. simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 12. prepare the tempering by heating 2 tsp coconut oil and splutter 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, pinch hing and 1 dried red chilli. 13. pour the tempering over kootu and mix well. 14. finally, enjoy keerai kootu with hot steamed rice.

title: Kerala Chicken Curry (Nadan Kozhi Curry)
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.375 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 2 tablespoons coconut oil, 0.75 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.75 cup coconut milk, 1.5 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((or ⅛ tsp ground cloves)), 2 pods green cardamom, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper corn, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3 red chilies, 1 tablespoon coconut oil, 1 red chili, 3.5 pearl onions, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with garam masala, salt, turmeric, red chili powder, lemon juice and black pepper. Cover and keep aside until you prepare the rest. If you want you may use store bought garam masala and leave out making the spice blend here. Dry roast the whole spices on a low to medium heat until aromatic and crisp. Cool them and make a fine powder. Pour little water and make a paste of pouring consistency. Keep this aside. 2. Add coconut oil to a hot pan and saute sliced onions till lightly golden. Stir in the ginger and garlic. Saute for a minute until the raw smell is gone. If you want add tomatoes at this stage and cook down until they lose the raw smell. I don’t use them in this recipe. 3. Add the marinated chicken and saute for 3 to 4 minutes on a high flame. Pour the ground garam masala paste and mix well. 4. Cook covered on a medium to low flame till the chicken is tender and cooked completely. I didn’t pour water to cook the chicken, the moisture in the spice paste was good enough. But if your pan is too dry, you may add a splash of hot water. When the chicken is tender/ cooked, pour coconut milk. If you are using homemade fresh extracted, use thin coconut milk to prevent splitting. 5. Simmer uncovered for 5 minutes. You should have a thick consistency like seen in the picture below. Taste test and adjust salt and black pepper if you want. Heat a small pan with coconut oil for tempering. Add sliced onions/shallots and fry till they turn pink. Add curry leaves and broken dried red chili. When they turn crisp. Switch off the stove. 6. Pour this hot tempering over the Kerala Chicken Curry.

title: kesar burfi recipe | kesar barfi with milk powder | kesar khoya barfi
ingredients: 0.75 cup milk, 0.25 tsp saffron kesar, 0.25 cup ghee clarified butter, 2.25 cup milk powder, 0.25 cup cashew powder, 0.5 cup sugar, pinch saffron food colour
instructions: 1. firstly, soak ¼ tsp saffron in ¾ cup of warm milk for 20 minutes. 2. take saffron milk into large kadai and add ¼ cup ghee. 3. further add 2¼ cup milk powder, ¼ cup cashew powder, ½ cup sugar and pinch saffron food colour. 4. stir well until the mixture is well combined and separates the pan holding shape. 5. transfer the prepared mixture into a greased plate lined with baking paper. 6. set well forming a block. top it with chopped nuts and press gently. 7. allow to set for 30 minutes, or till it sets completely yet warm. 8. now unmould and cut into pieces. 9. finally, serve kesar burfi or store in an airtight container for a week in the refrigerator.

title: KFC fried chicken
ingredients: 5 grams chicken, 1.5 cup buttermilk, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, 1 egg white, 1 cup bread crumbs, 2.5 cups Oil, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon allspice, 0.75 tablespoon garlic powder, 1 teaspoon onion powder, 1 teaspoon mixed herbs
instructions: 1. Choosing chicken : For starters, you can choose what kind of chicken you prefer. Be it boneless thighs, or a whole bird that is cut into pieces, you can be selective. Most people love fried drumsticks. But you can also use chicken breasts & thighs. 2. Brining : Next is the decision to brine or not to brine. The benefit of using a buttermilk brine is that it tenderizes the chicken and adds moisture while contributing a pleasing kiss of acidity. 3. While skipping this step is an option, a soak in buttermilk for at least 4 hours will take your fried chicken to new heights which is certainly worth the extra planning. 4. To make the butter milk, add ½ cup plain yogurt to a bowl and whisk it with 1 cup water. Add about ¼ teaspoon salt & give a good mix. You can also add some crushed black pepper if you like. 5. Rinse chicken and add it here. Cover the bowl with a cling wrap and refrigerate 4 hours to overnight. Overnight gives best results. I do not suggest skipping this as the chicken won’t cook through properly without brining. 6. When you are ready to make the fried chicken prepare the crumbs for coating. To a bowl add, 1 cup bread crumbs or panko crumbs. I used ¼ teaspoon allspice & ½ teaspoon red chilli powder or paprika. These are optional and you may add any other seasoning if you prefer here. 7. Break an egg to a bowl and separate the white. 8. Remove the chicken pieces one after the other from the buttermilk and add them a large mixing bowl. Or drain the buttermilk from the chicken. We don’t want the buttermilk here. 9. Pour the egg white to the chicken and 3 to 4 tablespoons buttermilk. Mix well. Chicken must be moist enough to hold the bread crumbs at the next stage. Pick up each piece of chicken & roll them in the bread crumbs. 10. Press down each piece of chicken so the bread crumbs stick to the chicken well. Set aside for at least 10 minutes. A better way is to place these on a wired rack.

title: Khaman Dhokla Recipe (Soft & Fluffy)
ingredients: 1 cup besan, 1 tablespoon semolina, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 cup water, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 tablespoon ginger paste, 1 green chili paste, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon natural citric acid, 0.25 cup water, 0.75 teaspoon Eno, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 slit green chilies, 1.5 tablespoons sugar, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. Pour water to a bowl. Add salt, sugar, oil, ginger paste, green chili paste and lemon juice or citric acid. Mix all the ingredients and stir well until the sugar dissolves. Keep this aside. ½ cup water, 1 teaspoon sugar, ⅓ teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon oil, ½ tablespoon ginger paste, 1 green chili paste, ⅓ teaspoon natural citric acid 2. Pour water to a bowl. Add salt, sugar, oil, ginger paste, green chili paste and lemon juice or citric acid. Mix all the ingredients and stir well until the sugar dissolves. Keep this aside. 3. Place a sieve over a large mixing bowl. Add besan and turmeric to it. Sieve them well. To the same bowl, add semolina & give a good mix. 1 cup (125 grams) besan, ¼ teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon (10 grams) fine semolina 4. Place a sieve over a large mixing bowl. Add besan and turmeric to it. Sieve them well. To the same bowl, add semolina & give a good mix. 5. Pour the spiced water we made at step 1. Mix well with a whisk to a lump-free batter. 6. Batter will be thick at this stage. Pour more water as needed to make a free flowing yet slightly thick batter. The quantity of water to use depends on the texture of flour as well. ¼ cup water 7. Batter will be thick at this stage. Pour more water as needed to make a free flowing yet slightly thick batter. The quantity of water to use depends on the texture of flour as well. 8. Beat the batter very well in one direction (clock-wise or anti-clock wise) with your whisk for exactly 50 to 60 seconds. 9. Batter has to be neither too thick nor too runny. To check the consistency of the dhokla batter, dip a spoon. It has to be thick enough to coat the back of the spoon yet should not be of a ribbon consistency. (Check the step-by-step pictures). Keep this aside and prepare the steamer. 10. Pour 2½ to 3½ cups water to a pot or a steamer. Place a steel rack or a ring or a long legged trivet. Cover and bring the water to a rolling boil. (I pour about 4 cups as I use a large pot) 11. Meanwhile grease a 5 inch pan or container with ½ to 1 teaspoon oil. Grease the sides as well. 12. When you see the water in the steamer is about to come to a boil, then add eno (fruit salt) and pour 1 tablespoon water to the dhokla batter. You will see the eno activates and froths up. ¾ teaspoon Eno 13. When you see the water in the steamer is about to come to a boil, then add eno (fruit salt) and pour 1 tablespoon water to the dhokla batter. You will see the eno activates and froths up. 14. Quickly mix everything well to incorporate it. As you mix, the khaman dhokla batter begins to thicken and turns light. Run the whisk around the bowl including the sides to mix it evenly. 15. Immediately pour this to the greased pan. Even out the top with the whisk. Quickly place it in the steamer and cover it. 16. Steam it for 18 to 20 mins on a moderately high flame. It has to be steamed constantly through out the time. Once done, a knife inserted in the center should come out clean. Turn off the stove and leave it covered in the pot for another 5 mins. 17. Pour oil to a tadka pan and heat it. Add mustard seeds. When they begin to crackle, add curry leaves and sliced green chilies. Saute for a while until the curry leaves turn crisp. 1 tablespoon oil, ¾ teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 slit green chilies 18. Pour oil to a tadka pan and heat it. Add mustard seeds. When they begin to crackle, add curry leaves and sliced green chilies. Saute for a while until the curry leaves turn crisp. 19. Add hing, sugar and salt. Pour water. Bring it to a rolling boil and turn off. Stir a few times to dissolve the sugar. Cover this and leave it to cool down a bit. ⅛ teaspoon hing, 1 to 2 tablespoons sugar, ¼ teaspoon salt, ½ cup water 20. Add hing, sugar and salt. Pour water. Bring it to a rolling boil and turn off. Stir a few times to dissolve the sugar. Cover this and leave it to cool down a bit. 21. Remove the dhokla pan from the steamer and cool it completely. After it cools down, using a knife to loosen the sides if needed. 22. Keep a plate over the pan and invert the dhokla to the plate. Ensure the dhokla has cooled completely before removing it. Cut to squares. 23. Spread the spices all over the khaman dhokla. First pour half cup of the tempering water and wait for a while. Then pour the rest. All of the water will be absorbed by the dhokla. 24. Cool completely. Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds along with fresh coconut & fine chopped coriander leaves. Serve dhokla with Coriander chutney or Red Garlic Chutney .

title: khara avalakki recipe | karnataka style poha recipe
ingredients: 2 cups thick avalakki poha beaten rice, fistful of groundnuts peanuts, 0.5 onion, 1 green chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.5 tsp sugar, salt to taste, 1 tbsp lemon juice, few coriander leaves, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin seeds, 0.5 tsp urad dal, pinch of hing asafetida, curry leaves
instructions: 1. soak 2 cups of avalakki / poha in water for about 5 minutes or till they turn soft yet retain their shape. drain them and make sure the pohas are easily smashed when you press it. 2. heat oil and add mustard seeds, urad dal, jeera and hing. allow them to crackle. also add curry leaves at this stage. ( i dint have curry leaves so i skipped it) 3. add peanuts in the pan and fry till they become crunchy. 4. now add chopped onions, chilli and ginger. stir fry till the onions sweat. 5. add turmeric powder, salt, sugar and saute for a few seconds. 6. now add soaked-drained avalakki (poha) and stir on a low flame. cover and cook for 1-2 minutes on very low flame. 7. sprinkle some lemon juice and chopped coriander leaves. 8. serve khara poha / khara avalakki hot with curd and mixture along with masala tea.

title: Khara Bath Recipe (Masala Rava Bath)
ingredients: 0.75 cup rava, 2 tbsp ghee, 0.25 tsp mustard, 0.75 tsp urad dal, 11 cashews, 1 green chilli, 1 onion, 3 tbsp capsicum, 0.5 carrot, 3 tbsp green peas, 1 tomato, 2.25 cups water, 0.875 tsp MTR vangi bath powder, 0.125 tsp turmeric powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp ginger, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Roast rava: Dry roast rava on a low to medium flame stirring often until crunchy and light. Do not brown. Keep aside to cool down. 2. Tempering: Heat 1½ tbsp ghee in a pan and add mustard seeds, dal and cashews. Let fry until golden and add the curry leaves, ginger and hing. Add onions and chilies. Saute until transparent and add all the veggies. Stir fry for 2 mins and cook covered until tender. 3. Make khara bath: Add vangi bath powder, turmeric and salt. Pour water and mix. Taste test to adjust salt. Bring the water to a rolling boil, reduce the heat to low. Pour the rava in a slow stream with your left hand while you stir the water with your other hand to incorporate it into the water 4. Quickly stir to break down any lumps formed. Cover and simmer until the water is absorbed completely. Turn off and let rest for 5 mins. Sprinkle coriander leaves and the rest of the ghee. Optionally you may also add some lemon juice and grated coconut. 5. Serve khara bath hot with coconut chutney.

title: Khatti Dal Recipe | Hyderabadi Special Khatti Dhal
ingredients: ball sized tamarind, 1 cup toor dal, 2 tomato, 5 chilli, few curry leaves, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp ghee, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp ghee, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 cloves garlic, 3 red chilli, pinch hing, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl soak ball sized tamarind for 15 minutes. Keep aside. 2. In a pressure cooker, take 1 cup toor dal, 2 tomato, 5 chilli, few curry leaves, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, ½ tsp salt, ½ tsp turmeric and 1 tsp ghee. 3. Pour in 3 cup water and pressure cook for 9 whistles. 4. Once the pressure settles down, mash the dal slightly. 5. Add 2 tbsp coriander and squeeze the tamarind extract. 6. Mix well and boil for 5 minutes or until the raw flavour of tamarind is gone. 7. Also add 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp salt and 1 cup water. 8. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 9. Continue to boil for 5 minutes or until the dal turns aromatic. 10. To prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Add ½ tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, 10 cloves garlic, 3 dried red chilli, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 11. Splutter the tempering. 12. Pour the tempering over the dal and mix well. 13. Finally, enjoy Khatti Dal Recipe with hot steamed rice.

title: Kheer Recipe (Rice Kheer)
ingredients: 4 cups whole milk full fat, 0.25 cup water, 0.25 cup rice, 5 tablespoons sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.25 cup unsalted nuts, 2 tablespoons sweet raisins, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 pinch saffron
instructions: 1. Wash rice a few times & drain completely. 2. Pour water (if using) and milk to a heavy bottom pot or Dutch oven. Bring it to a boil on a medium heat and stir in the rice. 3. Continue to cook on a medium to low heat, until the rice is soft, completely cooked and lightly mushy. It takes about 25 to 30 mins. During this step, keep stirring every 3 minutes to prevent the milk getting scorched at the bottom. 4. Stir in the sugar and continue to cook until it turns thicker but is yet of flowy consistency. It takes about 10 mins. (Note: becomes thicker upon cooling down) 5. Taste test, if you want add more sugar & simmer for another 5 mins (if you add more). Stir in cardamom powder, saffron (optional) rose water and chopped nuts. 6. Optional: If you prefer ghee in your rice kheer, heat ghee in a small pan and fry the nuts until light golden. Stir in the raisins & fry until plump. Pour that over the kheer. 7. Press the saute button. Optional – add ghee to the inner pot of the Instant pot. Fry the cashews until light golden. Add raisins and fry for 30 seconds. Remove to a plate. 8. Pour water (to avoid burn) and milk. Stir in rice and sugar. Secure the lid of the instant pot. Position the steam release handle to sealing. Press the porridge button. 9. Set the timer to 18 mins. When it is done press cancel & switch off. Wait for the pressure to release naturally. 10. When the float valve drops, open the lid and stir in cardamom powder & rose water. 11. Rice kheer will be runny at this stage. For a thicker consistency cook on saute mode for a few mins until thick. It will thicken further after cooling. 12. Transfer kheer to serving bowls & garnish with nuts & raisins. You can also chill the rice kheer and serve.

title: Khichdi Recipe | Moong Dal Khichdi
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.875 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 bay leaf, 0.875 teaspoon ginger, 0.25 cup onion, 1 green chili, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup rice, 0.5 cup moong dal, 4 cups water, 1 pinch asafoetida, 0.5 tablespoon ghee, 1 medium carrot, 8 french beans, 1 potato
instructions: 1. Add rice and moong dal to a pot. Rinse them at least thrice & drain water completely. If you do not have a cooker, then soak them for 30 to 40 mins. This will help them cook faster. 2. Heat ghee in a pressure cooker or pot on a medium heat. Once the ghee is hot, add bay leaf (optional) and cumin seeds. 3. Soon the seeds begin to splutter, then add ginger & saute until fragrant, for about 30 to 60 seconds & sprinkle hing. 4. Add onions and green chilies. Saute them until the raw smell of onions goes away & they begin to turn golden. 5. Next add tomatoes, carrots, beans, salt and turmeric. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. 6. Then add drained daal and rice. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. 7. Pour 4 cups water for porridge like consistency or 3 cups for mushy consistency. 8. Stir well & taste the water. Add more salt if needed. 9. Cover the pressure cook and let it cook for 2 whistles on a medium flame for a mushy khichdi. Cook for 4 to 5 whistles on a medium flame for porridge consistency. 10. Let the pressure release on its own, then open the lid and mix well. If the consistency is too thick for your liking, pour about ½ to ¾ cup hot boiling water and simmer for 1 to 2 mins until dal khichdi begins to bubble. Taste test and adjust salt. 11. If cooking in a pot, bring water to a boil on a high flame. Then reduce the flame to medium to low and continue to cook. 12. Leave the pot uncovered or partially covered so the froth won't overflow. Keep checking and add more hot boiling water (about ½ to ¾ cups) as needed until the lentils are fully soft cooked. 13. When both are done, mash the khichdi slightly so you get a thicker consistency. Adjust salt as needed. 14. Transfer to serving plates and pour 1 tsp ghee on the hot khichdi. Serve with papad and pickle. 15. Press saute button (medium) on your Instant pot. Pour ghee to the steel insert. 16. As soon as the ghee melts, add bay leaf and cumin seeds. 17. When cumin begins to sizzle add onions & chopped chili. 18. Saute until onions turn transparent. Next add in ginger, hing, tomatoes, all veggies & turmeric. 19. Saute for 2 to 3 mins until tomatoes turn mushy. 20. Add drained rice & lentils. Saute them for another 2 to 3 mins. 21. Next add salt & pour water. Deglaze the pot by scraping the bottom with a spatula to remove bits of food stuck in there if any. Mix well & taste the water. 22. Add more salt if needed. Secure the lid & move the steam release handle to sealing. 23. Press the pressure cook or manual button (high pressure). Set the timer to 8 mins. Or you can also press porridge button & set timer to 9 mins. 24. Once it is done, the instant pot beeps, then press cancel and switch off. 25. Wait for a natural pressure release for 10 minutes & release the rest manually. 26. When the float valve drops, open the lid & stir gently. 27. If you like soupy khichdi, add some hot boiling water to bring to consistency you like. Saute for 1 minute if you prefer. 28. Top dal khichdi with little ghee & serve with pickle or papad or chutney.

title: Khichdi Recipe 2 Ways | Moong Dal Khichdi - Tips & Tricks
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice, 0.5 cup moong dal, 1 tsp ghee, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 3.25 cup water, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, pinch hing, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl rinse and soak ½ cup rice, ½ cup moong dal for 10 minutes. 2. in a cooker heat 1 tsp ghee and add soaked dal and rice. 3. saute for 2 minutes, or until the dal turns aromatic. 4. now add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt and 3¼ cup water. mix well. 5. cover and pressure cook for 5 minutes. 6. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee. add 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf and pinch hing. 7. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 8. now add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute well. 9. further, add 1 tomato and saute until the tomato turns soft and mushy. 10. keep the flame on low add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp salt. 11. saute for 2 minutes or until the spices turn aromatic. 12. now add cooked rice and dal. 13. also, add 1 cup water and mix well adjusting consistency as required. 14. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 15. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy dal khichdi with pickle and curd.

title: Khoya gulab jamun
ingredients: 125 grams khoya, 2.5 tbsps maida, 0.25 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 3 tbsps milk, 2 tbsp paneer, Oil, 1.25 to 1 cups sugar, 0.75 cup water, tsp cardamom powder, 1 tsp rose water, 1.5 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan. Check if the oil is hot by dropping a very small piece of the dough in the hot oil. The ball has to go down and slowly rise up with bubbles. If the oil is not hot enough, a crusty layer will form over the jamuns. If the oil is too hot, mawa may get dispersed in the oil or may get browned without cooking inside. 2. Regulate the flame to medium. Drop the balls one after the other slowly while stirring the oil in between. 3. Continue adding and keep stirring. If needed regulate the flame between low to medium. 4. As you see the balls that were first added gets fried to golden brown. Remove them to a kitchen tissue. Keep stirring for frying the rest evenly. 5. Remove them to a kitchen tissue and cool for 3 to 5 mins. 6. Add them to slightly hot syrup. The syrup must be slightly hot and not very hot. When you dip your finger in the syrup it must be hot but not very hot. 7. Gently stir them in the syrup. Soak for 30 to 45 mins. Serve gulab jamun with syrup. 8. Add sugar to a medium wide pot. Pour water and heat it. Boil the syrup till it turns sticky. The syrup should not reach 1 string consistency otherwise it may not be absorbed by the jamuns. 9. Turn off the stove. Add rose water, cardamom powder and lemon juice.Stir and set aside to keep the syrup slightly hot. 10. Add grated mawa to a mixing bowl. Add maida, cardamom powder and then baking powder to the bowl. 11. Mix up the flour with baking powder and cardamom powder. 12. Add milk as needed little by little and make a smooth dough. You can gently knead it but don’t over knead as it may affect the texture. You must get a dough ball that is smooth with not many cracks. It should not be dry. 13. Heat oil in a deep pan or kadai. 14. Check if the oil is hot by dropping a small piece of ball to the hot oil. The ball must go in and rise slowly with bubbles. This is the right time to fry the balls. 15. Regulate the flame to medium. Begin to drop one ball at a time. Slowly add as many balls as possible. Keep stirring the oil to fry them evenly. 16. After frying for few minutes they will firm up. Keep stirring and fry them uniformly until golden brown. If needed regulate the flame to low and medium often. 17. Remove the balls that are done to a kitchen tissue. Continue frying and removing when ever a ball is done frying. 18. Allow the balls to cool a bit for 3 to 5 mins. Add them to slightly hot syrup, The syrup should not be very hot. When you dip your finger you must feel the syrup is hot but not very hot. 19. Gently rotate the gulab jamuns in the syrup and allow them to soak for 30 to 45 mins. 20. They soak up the syrup quickly and turn soft and juicy. 21. Serve gulab jamun with syrup.

title: khoya paneer recipe | how to make khoya paneer curry
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 cup milk, 0.75 cup milk powder, 1 tbsp butter, 11 cubes paneer cottage cheese, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 inch cinnamon dalchini, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 3 pods cardamom elachi, 3 cloves lavang, 1 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 1 onion, 2 cup tomato pulp, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 green chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.75 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, roast spices with butter. 2. now add 1 onion followed by 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 1 green chilli. saute well. 3. further, add 2 cup tomato pulp. to prepare tomato pulp, blend 3 raw tomatoes to smooth puree without adding any water. 4. cover and cook for 10 minutes or till oil separates from the tomato paste. 5. now add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ¾ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp garam masala and 1 tsp salt. saute well. 6. now add ¾ cup of prepared khoya and cook well. 7. further, add 1 cup of water and adjust consistency. 8. add in roasted paneer and give a good mix. 9. cover and simmer for 10 minutes or till paneer absorb flavours. 10. finally, serve khoya paneer with roti or naan.

title: Khoya Recipe | Instant Mawa
ingredients: 1 cup milk powder, 0.25 cup full fat milk, 1 tablespoon ghee
instructions: 1. To make instant mawa, firstly pour ½ tablespoon ghee and milk to a wide pan. Begin to heat it on a low flame. 2. Add milk powder and begin to mix well continuously breaking lumps. 3. As the milk turns hot, you will see the lumps dissolve easily. 4. Keep cooking on a medium heat stirring continuously for approximately 6 mins. You will see the milk begins to thicken slowly & begin to leave the sides of the pan. (check the pictures in the step by step photos) 5. Reduce the heat to low and add ½ tablespoon ghee. Mix well and cook. 6. As it cooks, take a small wooden spoon and scrape the sides of pan and the spatula you used to stir the mixture. The mixture here usually turns chewy or hard. So do not miss this step. 7. Option 1 - Take it off the stove when it is sticky and loose yet thick if you want to use it in rabri, kheer, kulfi, falooda, sheer khurma or shahi tukda. 8. Option 2 - Continue cooking stirring non-stop until all of the mixture turns to a mass. Turn off the stove at this stage if you want to use it in gulab jamun or any of the recipes mentioned earlier. 9. Test - When the temperature cools down, grease your fingers and take a tiny portion of this mawa. You should be able to roll it to a ball. It should not be too sticky, a little sticky is just okay. This is soft khoya. 10. Option 3 - Cook for 2 to 3 mins more if you want to use the khoya in peda, gujiya, ladoos, burfi or koftas. 11. Cool the khoya - mawa completely and store it in a air tight glass jar in the fridge for 1 week.

title: Kitchen King Masala Recipe | Homemade All Purpose Spice Mix Powder
ingredients: 3 tbsp coriander seeds, 1.5 tbsp cumin, 1 tsp shahi jeera, 2 tsp fennel, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 tsp urad dal, 2 tbsp yellow mustard, 0.25 tsp methi, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 bay leaf, 1 inch dry ginger, 2 pods black cardamom, 1 tsp cardamom, 1 javitri, 1 star anise, 1 tsp cloves, 0.5 nutmeg, 2 tbsp poppy seeds, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 0.5 tbsp pepper, 8 red chilli, 1 tsp black salt, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp aamchur, 1 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a thick bottomed pan take 3 tbsp coriander seeds, 1½ tbsp cumin, 1 tsp shahi jeera, 2 tsp fennel, 1 tsp chana dal, and 1 tsp urad dal, 2 tbsp yellow mustard and ¼ tsp methi. 2. dry roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 3. now add 1 inch cinnamon, 2 bay leaf, and 1 inch dry ginger. 4. also add 2 pods black cardamom, 1 tsp cardamom, 1 javitri, 1 star anise, 1 tsp cloves, and ½ nutmeg. 5. further add 2 tbsp poppy seeds, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, ½ tbsp pepper, and 8 dried red chilli. 6. dry roast on low flame until the spices turn crunchy and aromatic. 7. cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 8. add 1 tsp black salt, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp aamchur, and 1 tsp salt. 9. pulse and grind to a fine powder. 10. finally, kitchen king masala is ready to use in making sabzi or pulao.

title: Kitchenaid Ice Cream Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups heavy whipping cream, 1 cup full fat milk, 1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract, 0.75 cup sugar
instructions: 1. To make ice cream with a KitchenAid ice cream attachment, you first need to freeze the ice cream maker bowl for at least 24 hours or according to your instructions manual. The longer the better so 24 hours is ideal. 2. For the second preparation step, gather all your ingredients – milk, sugar, cream and vanilla extract. 3. Whisk together the milk, sugar, and vanilla extract in a large mixing bowl until the sugar dissolves. 4. Mix in the whipping cream and chill the mixture for at least 24 hours. If you live in a hot region a longer chill works better. 5. Once your tools and base are properly chilled, you can make the ice cream! Fit your ice cream attachment bowl & the hook to the kitchen aid stand. (Please follow the instruction manual for hook attachment as it is different for every model.) 6. Turn your kitchen aid on to the lowest speed and pour your ice cream base slowly. 7. Churn the ice cream for about 15 to 20 minutes, until you hear a click noise. Depending on the indoor temperature in your house, it may take up to 30 minutes. But also remember not to over churn. If you live in a hot region, it is ideal to turn on your air conditioner so your ice cream churns faster. 8. A click noise is a right indicator of the ice cream being ready. At this stage you will also see your bowl is almost full due to the incorporation of air. If your ice cream does not turn thick by 20 to 30 mins mark, it means something has gone wrong. May be your bowl wasn’t chilled enough or the cream you used wasn’t right with the required amount of fats. 9. It should look like shown in the picture. 10. At this stage you can actually serve it too. But it’s soft set. You can also use this right away to make some Milkshake, either in a mason jar or in a blender. 11. Transfer it to a chilled freezer-safe container and place a cling wrap such that it touches the ice cream all over. This prevents any ice crystals. Cover and Freeze it for anywhere from 2 hours to overnight, until set. When the vanilla ice cream is set, scoop it and serve it however you like. 12. If you plan on making the vanilla ice cream with a KitchenAid bowl and whisk, chill your heavy whipping cream and mixing bowl in the refrigerator for a minimum of 24 hours, before making it. Using cold heavy whipping cream and cold tools helps the cream whip up much faster. 13. The heavy whipping cream in the no-attachment recipe adds a creamy mouthfeel and traps air that mimics the overrun (air content) and fluffiness of regular ice cream. The condensed milk not only adds sweetness, but the sugar in it also prevents the formation of ice crystals and gives the ice cream that scoopable texture. 14. No matter what kind of ice cream you make, chill your freezer-safe containers in the freezer before adding your churned ice cream to them. This not only helps your ice cream cool down and set up faster, it also prevents the formation of ice crystals that would degrade the texture and flavor of your ice cream.

title: kodubale recipe | ring murukku recipe | spicy kodbale recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice flour akki hittu, 0.25 cup semolina sooji bombay rava, salt to taste, 0.5 cup maida plain flour all-purpose flour, 0.5 tsp ajwain carom seeds oma, pinch of hing asafoetida, 2 tbsp oil, oil for deep frying, 0.25 cup coconut, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin seeds, 5 whole dried kashmiri red chilli, 0.25 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixing bowl take rice flour, rava and maida. 2. also add ajwain and salt. combine well. 3. furthermore pour 2 tbsp of hot oil and mix well. 4. meanwhile prepare the masala by taking coconut, jeera and red chilli. 5. blend to smooth paste adding water as required. 6. now transfer the prepared masala paste over the flour. 7. knead to smooth and soft dough. 8. also roll and prepare small circles. 9. now deep fry the kodubale in hot oil in batches. 10. fry the kodubale till they turn golden brown and crisp on medium flame. 11. and drain off on to a kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 12. finally, enjoy kodubale, or store in a airtight container for 10-15 days once its cooled completely.

title: Kofta Curry (Meatball Curry)
ingredients: 1.5 cups onions, 0.5 cup tomato, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.3333333333333333 cup yogurt, 0.25 cup oil, 0.25 teaspoon shahi jeera, 4 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((omit if you don't like)), 1.5 to 3 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 2.25 cups water, 15 almonds cashews, 25 grams minced ground meat, 2 tablespoons onion, 0.5 inch inch ginger, 2 garlic cloves, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 teaspoons oil, 2 tablespoons egg, 0.25 cup bread crumbs, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 1 teaspoon red chili flakes, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder
instructions: 1. Omit this step to use heavy cream or coconut cream. If you are using nuts or seeds, soak them in half cup boiling hot water. While the curry simmers, drain the water and grind with ¼ cup water (adjust as required) to a smooth thick cream. 2. Heat oil/ghee in a kadai or deep pot and add the whole spices - shahi jeera, cloves, green and black cardamoms. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and saute for 10 minutes on a medium heat. Do not burn. 3. Turn down the flame to low and continue to saute until deep golden/ brown but not burnt. It takes me another 4 minutes. 4. Pour 3 tablespoons of water and deglaze. Saute until the onions look jammy, for another 3 mins. 5. Stir in the ginger garlic and green chilies. Saute on a medium heat for another minute or 2, until aromatic. 6. Stir in all the ground spices, followed by tomato puree. Mix and cook down until thick, for about 3 to 4 mins. 7. Lower the flame and stir in a tablespoon of the cooked masala in the whisked yogurt and mix well. Pour it into the pan and cook until it blends well with the rest of the ingredients. 8. Saute this masala for another 3 mins and pour water. Bring to a rolling boil and simmer for 5 mins. 9. Pulse or mince, onion, ginger, garlic, green chili (optional), mint and coriander leaves until slightly coarse. Don’t overdo to the extent they release too much moisture. I use a food processor or a onion chopper, you may do it on a board and transfer to a bowl. 10. Mix or pulse it together with meat, ground spices, egg and salt, until well mixed and comes together. (if using ground meat (not keema/mince), use your hands to mix all the ingredients, you don't need a processor) 11. Stir in the flour/bread crumbs and oil. Mix just until combined. Do not process or overwork here. Grease your hands generously and divide to 12 equal portions. Roll to kofta/ balls. 12. Option 1 - Heat ¼ to ½ cup oil in a deep pan, until hot enough to fry. Slide the koftas and fry them for 6 to 7 mins, in 2 batches. Remove to a plate. 13. Option 2 - Preheat your air fryer for 2 mins at the highest heat. Spray the basket with oil and place the kofta in the basket. Brush them with oil. Air fry at 380 F/190 C for 10 to 12 mins, as per your preference. Shake the basket, halfway through. 14. You should have your curry cooked down for at least 5 to 7 mins before you proceed. Reduce the flame to low and add the hot koftas to the simmering curry. Mix gently, cover and cook for 25 mins on a low heat, stirring at 5 to 7 mins interval. 15. Stir in 2 to 3 tablespoons almond cream/ khus khus paste and let simmer for another 3 to 4 mins, or until thick enough. (You may use the entire amount or lesser cream as per your preference) 16. Taste test and adjust salt. For heat you may add more black pepper. Garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Korma Masala Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 cup coriander seeds, 16 Green cardamom, 6 inch cinnamon stick, 1.5 tablespoons Cloves, 1.5 tablespoon Shahi jeera, 1 bay leaf, 1 Star anise, 2 tablespoons saunf, 1 grams white poppy seeds, 0.5 cup coconut, 1 tablespoon pepper corn, 2 Red chilies, 1 black cardamom, 2.5 strands mace, 0.3333333333333333 nutmeg
instructions: 1. Clean the spices, pick and discard stones and debris. Dry roast lightly one after the other till they turn crisp. 2. Dry roast poppy seeds, coconut and red chilies too. Poppy seeds must turn very crunchy & this helps to make a fine powder. 3. Add poppy seeds to a grinder and make a fine powder, as fine as possible. Remove to a plate. 4. Make a fine powder of the spices, red chilies and coconut. Add poppy seeds to this and grind well again. 5. Store korma masala in an airtight glass jar and refrigerate up to 6 months.

title: Kothu Parotta Recipe
ingredients: 4.5 parotta roti paratha, 0.75 cup onions, 1 green chilli, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 9 mint leaves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 cup water, 0.875 cup kurma
instructions: 1. Tear the parottas/ parathas to bite sized pieces, about 1 inch. If you do not have a sharp metal spatula, simply shred the parottas to fine pieces with a knife on your chopping board. 2. Pour oil to a hot pan and add mustard seeds. When they begin to splutter, add curry leaves, ginger, garlic and green chilies. 3. Saute them for 1 to 2 minutes and add chopped onions. 4. Stir fry until golden and add tomatoes along with salt. 5. Cook till the onions become soft and break down. Add red chilli powder, garam masala, fennel powder & turmeric. 6. Saute for 1 to 2 mins. Add coriander and mint leaves, saute for another 1 to 2 minutes until aromatic. 7. Pour ¼ cup water. Or 2 to 3 eggs for an egg version. 8. Stir well and continue to cook till most of the water is evaporated. The spicy mixture has to be moist and not dried out completely. This is very important, especially if you are not using salna. If you have used eggs in the earlier step, ensure the eggs are soft cooked and not over cooked. 9. Add the paratha/ parotta/ roti pieces. Optional – Add ¾ cup salna or 1 cup vegetable kurma (not very runny). 10. Reduce the heat to low and mix to coat the parotta with the spicy masala. 11. Break the pieces finer using a sharp metal spatula or a scraper. If you find the paratha pieces too dry, sprinkle little water all over. 12. Cook this on a medium heat just until hot. 13. Garnish kothu parotta with coriander leaves and serve hot with a onion raita.

title: Kova ladoo recipe | Kobbari kova kajjikayalu | Kova coconut ladoo recipe
ingredients: 1 tbsp ghee, 2 cups coconut, 1 cup jaggery, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 25 grams kova, 5 tbsps sugar
instructions: 1. Add ghee to a pan and heat it. Add coconut and jaggery. Mix well and cook on a medium heat. 2. Jaggery melts within minutes, Continue to cook until all the moisture dries up. Do not over cook else the mixture will not bind to make ladoo. The mixture should be moist and not completely dry. otherwise ladoo may also turn hard. 3. Allow this to cool down a bit. When the mixture is warm, make 10 equal sized balls and set them aside to dry completely. 4. Meanwhile add kova or khoya to a nonstick pan and heat it on a medium flame. 5. When it is warm, add powdered sugar and stir. 6. Continue to cook on a medium heat stirring often until the mixture comes together. The mixture turns gooey and then turns thick. At this stage it begins to leave the pan. Do not overcook otherwise the mixture discolors and turns to brown. 7. Immediately transfer it to a plate and cool down. 8. When the mixture is warm then knead it just for a minute to make it smooth. You can skip this step if the mixture is already smooth. 9. Divide the mixture to 10 balls. 10. Flatten the kova ball and place the coconut ladoo in the center. At this stage make sure the coconut ladoo is completely dry. 11. Cover the coconut lado with the kova / khoya. 12. Gently smoothen the ladoo. Do not press or put pressure on the ball. 13. Follow the same procedure to complete all the ladoos. 14. Allow them to dry for 2 to 4 hours. 15. Store kova ladoo in a air tight jar and serve with in 3 days or refrigerate and use up to 2 weeks.

title: Kozhukattai Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup sesame seeds, 1 cup jaggery, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 cup rice flour, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp ghee, 1 pinch salt, ghee
instructions: 1. Dry roast sesame seeds until aromatic. Cool and powder them finely. Blend it a jar to powder. Add jaggery, cardamom and blend well. Make balls . Set aside. 2. Bring water to a boil. Switch off the stove and add flour. 3. Stir well and cover. Set aside for a while. 4. Knead the dough well to make crack free dough. 5. Make 11 equal sized balls. Grease your fingers and begin to flatten the ball. 6. Spread the dough to a small puri. If the edges crack, dampen your fingers and join the cracks. 7. Place a ball and bring all the edges together. Give a modak shape to it. 8. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a steamer or pressure cooker. Place the kozhukattai on a basket lined with cloth. 9. Place the basket in the steamer when the water comes to a boil. If using cooker, then do not use weight. 10. Steam ellu kozhukattai in a steamer for 8 to 10 minutes.

title: Kozhukattai Recipe | Thengai Poorna Kolukattai
ingredients: 1 cup jaggery, 0.25 cup water, 2 cup coconut, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tsp ghee, 2 cup water, 0.5 tsp ghee, 2 cup rice flour, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 1 cup jaggery and ¼ cup water. stir until the jaggery melts completely. 2. now add 2 cup coconut and mix well. 3. continue to cook until the mixture starts to separate the pan and holds the shape slightly. 4. further, add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and 1 tsp ghee. mix well. 5. finally, thengai poorna is ready for stuffing. keep aside. 6. firstly, in a saucepan take 2 cup water and ½ tsp ghee. 7. mix well and get to a rolling boil. keep aside. 8. in a large bowl take 2 cup rice flour and ¼ tsp salt. mix well. 9. now add hot water in batches and mix well. 10. add the water until the flour turns moist. 11. now start to knead the dough sprinkling water if requried. 12. knead for 5 minutes or until the dough turns soft. 13. kozhukattai dough is ready. wet your hand and knead if the dough seems dry. 14. firstly, to prepare modak shape, pinch a ball sized dough and with the help of both thumbs start pressing the edges and create a dent in the centre. 15. now place a ball sized coconut stuffing in the centre. 16. bring the edges together and seal by pressing. 17. now prepare the modak shaped kolukattai. 18. to prepare half-moon shaped, place a ball sized dough on the greased banana leaf. 19. press as this as possible. 20. now place the coconut stuffing and fold half. 21. seal the sides and half-moon shaped kolukattai is ready to steam. 22. to prepare round ball-shaped, take ball sized rice flour dough and flatten on the palm. 23. place the coconut stuffing and roll into a round ball. ready to steam. 24. to prepare star and flower-shaped modak, cut using a cookie maker. 25. place the modak in steamer leaving gaps in between. 26. cover and steam the modak for 10 minutes or till the shiny texture appears on them. 27. finally, offer kozukkattai to lord ganesha and celebrate ganesh chathurthi. 28. Place the modak in a steamer, leaving gaps in between. 29. Finally, offer kozukkattai to lord ganesha and celebrate Ganesh Chaturthi.

title: Kudumulu | Undrallu recipe | Vinayaka Chavithi Recipes
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 2.25 cups water, 2 tbsp channa dal, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 0.29166666666666663 tsp salt, 1 tsp ghee, 0.25 cup coconut
instructions: 1. Rinse 1 cup rice thrice and drain to a colander. You can also soak the rice for 2 hours and use. Soaking helps to blend the rice easily. 2. Sun dry or fan dry rice on a cloth till completely dry. 3. Blend/grind in a dry mixer jar to a slightly coarse texture. Make sure rice rava is moisture free before you proceed. 1 cup rice will yield you more than 1 cup rava. For this recipe we need only 1 cup rava. So measure it and set aside. Store the excess in air tight jar in the fridge and use up when you make punugulu. 4. Wash and soak a handful of chana dal / senaga pappu for 15 to 20 minutes. To speed up, you can also soak in hot water for 10 minutes and use. 5. Add ghee to a hot heavy bottom pot. When the ghee melts, add the cumin seeds if using. I did not use here. 6. Drain off the water from dal completely and add to the ghee. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes until it turns fragrant. 7. I used 2 cups of water and salt. Please refer notes for more details on water. 8. Bring it to a boil. 9. Reduce the flame to medium. Measure 1 cup rava. Pour it slowly with one hand and keep stirring with the other to prevent lumps. 10. Simmer the flame. Keep stirring and cook. 11. Cook covered as it begins to splash a lot. 12. When the entire water evaporates. Open the lid. 13. Mix well to prevent burning. Turn off. Keep covered and leave it on the stove top covered for at least 5 to 15 mins depending on the age of your rice. For aged rice, you might need to leave for 15 minutes. 14. Transfer this to a wide plate and cool. When it reaches to a temperature you can handle, knead the dough once. 15. Meanwhile place a steamer on stove with enough water (about 1 to 1.5 cups) to steam the balls, you can use a pressure pan or a cooker (without whistle) or a idli steamer or any kind of steamer. 16. Dip your fingers in water and take small portions of the mixture. Make small balls and place them in a wide steam basket or a bowl. 17. When the water starts to boil, place this steam basket/ bowl. Cover with a lid that has a steam vent. Steam them exactly for 8 minutes on a high flame. If using a large pressure cooker, you can steam for 10 minutes. Do not over steam else they will become hard.

title: kulcha naan recipe | plain kulcha recipe | butter kulcha on tawa
ingredients: 2 cup maida plain flour all purpose flour, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 0.75 cup water, 4 tsp black sesame seeds til, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 2 tbsp butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cup maida, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp baking soda, ½ tsp salt and 2 tsp oil. 2. add in ¼ cup curd and mix well. 3. further add ¾ cup water or as required and knead dough. 4. knead to smooth and soft dough adding more water if required. 5. cover and rest for 2 hours or more. 6. pinch a ball sized dough and flatten. 7. sprinkle some black sesame seeds and coriander leaves. 8. gently roll to oval shape using rolling pin. 9. brush with water over kulcha. 10. furthermore, slowly flip the kulcha and put it on hot tawa. 11. now after a minute, flip the tawa upside down and cook kulcha directly on flame till they turn golden brown and puffs up. 12. gently scrape the kulcha from bottom and remove. 13. also brush some butter. 14. finally, serve plain kulcha hot with your favourite curry like paneer butter masala.

title: Kulcha Recipe
ingredients: 2.25 cups all-purpose flour, 0.5 cup yogurt, 0.25 cup warm milk, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 teaspoon sugar, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon baking soda, 1 teaspoon baking powder, 1 tablespoon ghee, 2 grams potato, 2 grams paneer, 0.25 cup onion, 0.25 cup carrot, 2 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon white sesame seeds, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon amchur, 4 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoons butter
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, stir together flour, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, salt and instant yeast if using. Pour warm milk, yogurt & oil/ melted butter. 2. Mix well to make a soft non-sticky dough, adding more water or milk if required. Knead until smooth & elastic for 6 to 7 mins. Shape to a ball. 3. Grease the bowl and dough ball with a tsp of oil. Place in the bowl. Cover and rest in a warm place for 20 mins or for 1 to 2 hours if using yeast. 4. Heat ghee in a pan. Saute green chilies and onions until golden and soft. 5. Add ginger and saute for a minute. Stir in the sesame seeds if using and toast for a minute. Stir in chili powder, cumin, coriander, garam masala, amchur and salt. 6. Add carrot and saute for 1 to 2 mins. Stir in the paneer and cook for 2 to 3 mins, until heated through. 7. Add half of the coriander leaves and potato. Mix well & taste test to adjust the seasoning. Cool down completely. 8. Punch down the dough with your hand once and divide to 6 portions. Shape and smoothen them to balls, tucking the ends in the center. Cover until used up. 9. Take a portion of the dough and spread to a 4 inch disc, with your fingers. Place 2 tablespoons of the stuffing in the center, keep pushing it down into the dough and bring the sides over the stuffing. Seal the edges well and shape to balls. 10.
