Grease a rolling board, place the dough and roll it to a 6 inch kulcha. Sprinkle some coriander leaves and sesame or kalonji seeds. Roll lightly. Transfer to the kitchen counter. 11. Heat a pan on a medium high heat. Transfer a kulcha to the pan and cook until you see bubbles, for 3 to 4 mins. Turn it to the other side. Cook until brown spots are visible and the edges are cooked through. 12. Optional - transfer the cooked kulcha (upper side facing down) over a wired rack, placed on the fire/ stove. Cook gently on a low heat, until you see some charred spots. 13. Smear butter all over the kulcha. Serve them hot on their own or with a curry and raita on the side. 14. Store the leftover filling in the refrigerator for 2 days or in the freezer for a month. Use it to make another batch of kulchas or fill your sandwiches & dosas.

title: Kulcha Recipe | Amritsari Aloo Kulcha - Street Style on Tawa
ingredients: 3 cup maida, 0.5 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 1 tbsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup curd, water, 2 tbsp ghee, 3 potato, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur, 0.25 tsp ajwain, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp kala namak, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, kalonji, sesame, coriander, chilli powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take 3 cup maida, ½ tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp baking soda, 1 tbsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. Mix well. 2. Add ½ cup curd, and water. Start to knead the dough adding water as required. 3. At first, the dough will be sticky. Keep kneading to make the dough soft and smooth. 4. Also, add 2 tbsp ghee and continue to knead the dough. 5. Knead to smooth and soft dough, tuck the dough and rest for 30 minutes. 6. Firstly, in a large bowl take 3 potato. Make sure to grate the boiled potato, so it will be easy while rolling the kulcha. 7. Add 1 onion, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur, ¼ tsp ajwain, ½ tsp salt, ½ tsp kala namak and ½ tsp ginger paste. 8. Also, add 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 9. Aloo stuffing is ready for preparing kulcha. Keep aside. 10. Firstly, take the dough and dust with madia. 11. Stuff a ball sized stuffing into the dough and seal. 12. Now roll gently keeping it uniform thickness. 13. Spread some water on top, sprinkle kalonji, sesame, coriander and chilli powder. 14. Flip over and spread water over kulcha. This helps kulcha to stick on the tawa. 15. Furthermore, slowly flip the kulcha and put it on hot tawa. Sprinkle salt water, as it helps to stick the kulcha well. 16. After a minute, flip the tawa upside down and cook the kulcha directly on flame till they turn golden brown and puff up. 17. Gently scrape the kulcha from bottom and remove. 18. Also brush some butter. 19. Finally, serve Aloo Kulcha Recipe hot with your favourite curry like chana masala. 20. After a minute, flip the tawa upside down and cook the kulcha directly on flame till they turn golden brown and puff up.Gently scrape the kulcha from bottom and remove.

title: Kulfi Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup milk, 2 teaspoons corn starch, 2 cups full fat milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 Pinch saffron, 12.5 pistachios, 0.25 cup cream, 4 pistas
instructions: 1. Bring milk to a boil in a heavy bottom pot. Allow it to boil further for 10 mins on a medium flame. Keep stirring often to prevent the milk solids getting burnt at the bottom. 2 cups full fat milk 2. Bring milk to a boil in a heavy bottom pot. Allow it to boil further for 10 mins on a medium flame. Keep stirring often to prevent the milk solids getting burnt at the bottom. 3. Meanwhile add pistachios along with saffron to a blender jar and make a slightly coarse powder. Set this aside. 10 to 15 pistachios, 1 Pinch saffron 4. Meanwhile add pistachios along with saffron to a blender jar and make a slightly coarse powder. Set this aside. 5. After 10 minutes of boiling the milk, add sugar and continue to boil for another 10 to 12 mins. Keep stirring often to prevent the milk getting scorched at the bottom. ¼ cup organic sugar 6. After 10 minutes of boiling the milk, add sugar and continue to boil for another 10 to 12 mins. Keep stirring often to prevent the milk getting scorched at the bottom. 7. Add corn flour to the reserved milk and stir well. Make sure there are no lumps. 1 cup milk, 2 teaspoons corn starch 8. Add corn flour to the reserved milk and stir well. Make sure there are no lumps. 9. Lower the flame completely and pour this to the pot. Keep stirring and boil. 10. Add the nuts powder and cardamom powder. Mix well and make sure there are no lumps. ¼ teaspoon cardamom powder 11. Add the nuts powder and cardamom powder. Mix well and make sure there are no lumps. 12. Pour cream if using and Cook for 6 to 8 minutess more. Keep stirring. Under 8 mins it turns thick. It should be of pouring consistency. ¼ cup cream 13. Pour cream if using and Cook for 6 to 8 minutess more. Keep stirring. Under 8 mins it turns thick. It should be of pouring consistency. 14. Switch off the stove & Cool this completely. The mixture thickens further upon cooling. 15. Pour this to kulfi molds, steel cups or Popsicle molds. Cover with a foil and gently insert a ice cream stick. I use 120 ml steel cups and it fills about 3 cups. 16. Freeze overnight. Immerse these in a bowl filled with water for 1 min or rub the moulds in between your palms for 30 seconds. 17. Gently hold the stick and demould it. Sprinkle chopped pistas and serve kesar pista kulfi immediately.

title: kulfi recipe | kesar pista kulfi recipe | kulfi ice cream recipe
ingredients: 1.5 litre milk, 0.25 cup cream, 0.25 cup sugar, 2 pistachios, 2 tbsp saffron milk, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a thick bottomed pan take 1.5 - 2 litres of full cream milk and get to a boil. 2. once the milk comes to a boil, add cream and give a good stir. 3. furthermore, simmer for 30 minutes and keep stirring in between. 4. next, add sugar and give a good mix. 5. simmer for 5 minutes or till the milk thickens completely. 6. additionally, add chopped pistachios, saffron milk and cardamom powder. 7. then give a mix and simmer further more for 5 minutes. 8. allow the thickened milk to cool completely. this may take atleast an hour. 9. after the milk is cooled completely, transfer them to kulfi moulds or to a plastic glass. 10. cover the glass with cling wrap or you can use aluminium foil. 11. furthermore, freeze the cup for atleast 5 hours or overnight. 12. after 5 hours or more when the kufi is set completely. insert a stick in between the cup. 13. then slowly warm up by rubbing in between your hands. you can also dip in warm water. 14. gently, remove the kulfi from the glass / mould and garnish with few chopped pistachios. 15. finally, serve kulfi immediately.

title: Kurkuri Aloo Tikki Chaat Recipe | Aloo Tikki Chaat
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 4 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp cashew, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander cumin powder, 1 tsp amchur, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup chana dal, 2 tbsp coriander, 3 potato, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp corn flour, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 4 chilli, 1 inch ginger and saute until it turns aromatic. 2. Add 2 tbsp cashew and roast till the cashew turns crunchy. 3. Keeping the flame on low add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander-cumin powder, 1 tsp amchur and ½ tsp salt. 4. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 5. Now add pressure-cooked chana dal and mix well. 6. Mash slightly, making sure everything is well combined. 7. Add in 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. Chana dal stuffing is ready. Keep aside. 8. Now in a large bowl take 3 boiled and mashed potato, ½ tsp salt and ½ tsp pepper powder. 9. Also, add 2 tbsp coriander and 2 tbsp corn flour. 10. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 11. To prepare the tikki, grease your hands with oil and pinch a ball-sized dough. 12. Flatten slightly, and stuff into a portion of prepared aloo stuffing. 13. Stuff in and secure making sure the stuffing doesn't come out. 14. Flatten slightly, to shape into tikki. 15. Now fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 16. Fry both the sides until they turn golden brown and crispy from both the sides. 17. Now to prepare the chaat, place the tikki onto the plate. 18. Top with curd, green chutney, tamarind chutney, chaat masala and coriander. 19. Finally, Kurkuri Aloo Chaat Recipe can be topped with sev before serving.

title: Ladies Finger Fry (Bendakaya Fry)
ingredients: 25 grams ladies finger, 1 green chili, 1 medium onion, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 cloves garlic, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 0.25 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 0.375 tsp red chili powder, 0.625 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 cup coconut, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 pinch turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 tsp Salt, 2 tbsp Oil, 0.25 cup peanuts
instructions: 1. Rinse 250 grams ladies finger under running water. Set them aside to dry. Then wipe off with a clean cloth or absorbent papers. I usually spray some vinegar, let them sit for about 10 minutes and then rinse off well. Then dry them and wipe with a fresh kitchen towel. 2. Chop the ladies finger to thin circles. Before you chop make sure they are not wet otherwise they will become slimy while chopping and while cooking too. 3. Heat a pan with 2 tablespoons oil. Next add ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds, ½ teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon chana dal and 1 teaspoon urad dal. Fry till the dal turns golden. Add 2 crushed garlic cloves, 1 sprig curry leaves and 1 dried broken red chili or 1 slit green chilli as well. Fry until the leaves turn crisp. 4. Add 1 medium sliced onion and fry till golden. They should not be raw anymore. While the onions fry, roast ¼ cup peanuts in a heavy bottom pan until golden, aromatic and crunchy. Cool them completely. Add them to a cloth and rub off to roughly remove the skin. Set these aside. 5. Transfer chopped ladies finger and squeeze the lemon juice. I add about a tablespoon. It helps to get rid of the slime faster. 6. On a medium heat, fry till the sticky substance goes away from the ladies finger. It takes about for about 5 to 7 minutes. 7. Next cover and cook till the ladies finger becomes tender for 2 to 3 mins. Covering helps to keep them moist without drying out. Cover only after the bhindi is fried well till it loses its stickiness. Otherwise they will become more slimy and mushy. 8. Sprinkle ⅓ to ½ teaspoon salt, ¼ to ½ teaspoon red chili powder, 1 pinch of turmeric, ½ teaspoon coriander powder or garam masala (optional) , ¼ cup fresh coconut and ¼ cup roasted peanuts. You can also crush the peanuts slightly if you like. If you want them more crunchy, add them while serving. 9. Lastly fry on medium flame till the coconut becomes aromatic takes around 3 minutes. Serve bendakaya fry with rice, phulka or roti. 10. Aloo Bhindi Recip... Bhindi Masala Rec... Bhindi Ki Sabji |... Bhindi Do Pyaza Bhindi Fry Recipe... Brinjal Fry (Andh...

title: Lamb Karahi (Mutton Karahi)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3.5 Kashmiri red chilies, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 3 inch cinnamon, 4 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 0.625 teaspoon black pepper corn, 6 grams lamb goat leg shoulder, 3 tablespoons ghee, 1 black cardamom, 0.75 cup onions, 1.25 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 4 grams tomatoes, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 cups water, 0.25 cup yogurt, 0.75 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1 green chili, 1 inch ginger slice, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. On a medium heat, dry roast red chilies and coriander seeds for 2 minutes. Add cloves, black cardamoms, green cardamoms, cumin seeds, fennel seeds and cinnamon. Roast until they smell aromatic but not burnt, for 2 to 3 mins. Cool this and make a fine powder. Keep this aside. 2. On a medium flame, heat ghee/oil in a pan. Add the black cardamom and onions. Saute until transparent for 4 to 5 mins. 3. Add lamb and saute for 5 to 6 minutes, until the color of the mutton changes. Stir in ginger garlic paste and saute for 1 to 2 mins without burning. 4. Lower the heat and stir in turmeric, half of the salt and half of the prepared kadai masala. Pour hot water (start with less) and cook covered for an hour. If the pan dries out add more water and keep stirring in between. 5. After an hour of cooking, pour the pureed tomatoes. Cook covered on a medium heat until the lamb is tender and fully cooked. This step takes about 35 to 40 mins. The sauce should have reduced and turned thick. 6. Add yogurt to a small bowl & stir in 2 tbsps of the kadai gravy. Add back the tempered yogurt to the lamb karahi along with the rest of the kadai masala. 7. Mix well. If it is too thick you may add a splash of hot water. Simmer covered until you see traces of fats on top. This takes about 3 to 4 mins. 8. Taste test. Stir in more salt if needed along with crushed kasuri methi. For more heat you may add some freshly cracked black pepper or chili flakes. 9. Garnish with slit/ sliced green chilies, ginger juliennes and coriander leaves. Serve lamb karahi with tandoori roti, naan or kulcha.

title: Lamb Madras
ingredients: 1.1 lbs lamb, 1.5 tablespoon ginger garlic, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoon lemon juice, 1.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 1.5 tablespoon Madras curry powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 3 tablespoons ghee, 1 star anise, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 1.5 cup onions, 1 cup tomato puree, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrots, 0.3333333333333333 cup red bell pepper, 0.5 cup water, 1 tablespoon tamarind paste, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl, add lamb, lemon juice, salt, ginger garlic, curry powder, chili powder, garam masala and coriander leaves. Mix well and cover. Keep aside until needed. 2. Puree tomatoes, carrots, bell peppers & half cup water or broth in a blender, to get a smooth puree. 3. Pour oil/ghee to a pot and add star anise, cinnamon and onions. Saute for 8 to 9 mins until transparent. Deglaze with 2 tablespoons water. Add the lamb and sear on a medium high heat for 6 to 7 mins. 4. Add the pureed tomatoes and cook covered on a medium heat for about 25 mins. 5. Pour half cup hot water or broth if needed (in batches) and cook covered until the lamb is tender (takes me about 1 hour 10 mins). Keep stirring every 10 to 15 mins to prevent burning. 6. Stir in tamarind and sugar. Taste test to adjust salt, curry powder & garam masala to your preference. Lamb madras should be hot, spicy and tangy. 7. Sprinkle coriander leaves and serve hot with Naan, paratha, chapati, roti, plain rice, saffron rice or turmeric rice.

title: Lamb Vindaloo Recipe (Goat Vindaloo)
ingredients: 1.25 lbs lamb, 2 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 black pepper, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 2.5 inch cinnamon piece, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 2 Kashmiri dried red chilies, 2 teaspoons Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.25 cup palm vinegar, 1 teaspoon jaggery, 0.25 cup water, 25 grams garlic, 25 grams ginger, 4 tablespoons oil, 1 onion, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 teaspoons jaggery, 1.5 to 2 cups hot water
instructions: 1. First deseed the dried red chilies and soak them in vinegar and ¼ cup hot water. If using red chilli powder in place of red chilies you may skip this soaking step. Keep this aside. 2. Meanwhile add all the spices – coriander, cumin, black pepper, cinnamon, cloves, mustard and salt to a small grinder jar. Make a fine powder. 3. Add jaggery, ginger, garlic, soaked red chilies (or chilli powder), & vinegar. Grind to a smooth paste adding 1 to 2 tablespoons more water if required. 4. Taste test this and add more salt or vinegar or red chilli powder to taste. 5. Marinate the lamb with turmeric and this vindaloo paste. Cover and rest overnight or at least for 6 hours. 6. When you are ready to cook the vindaloo, take it out of the refrigerator and keep aside. 7. Chop the onion very finely if using. 8. Heat oil in a pan or a dutch oven or a braiser. 9. Add the onions and saute them on a medium heat until golden. 10. Add the marinated lamb and begin to saute on a medium high heat for 5 minutes. You will see the vindaloo paste begins to smell good & leave the sides of the pan. At this stage cover and cook for 3 mins on a medium to medium-low heat. 11. Add the curry leaves and saute for 2 mins. Pour 1 cup boiling hot water and mix well. 12. Let lamb vindaloo cook for 1 ½ hours or until tender. Keep stirring every 15 mins. You will need to add more hot water in batches after 40 mins. 13. You know the lamb vindaoo is done when the meat is soft and fork tender. Taste test and add salt and jaggery (or brown sugar) at this stage and cook further without covering to evaporate the excess water. 14. Vindaloo is a semi-dry dish with thick sauce. Check pictures in the post to see the consistency of the sauce. 15. Lamb vindaloo tastes best the next day. Serve it over Basmati Rice, bread or with naans. If you are lucky enough to make or buy the Goan Poee bread, go for it.

title: langar dal recipe | amritsari dal | punjabi langar wali dal ki recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup black urad dal, 2 tbsp chana dal, water, 3 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 bay leaf, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 3 chilli, 1 tomato, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak ½ cup black urad dal and 2 tbsp chana dal in enough water for 4 hours. 2. drain off the water and transfer the soaked dal into the cooker. 3. add 3 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil and ½ tsp salt. 4. further, add 3 cup water and pressure cook for 6 whistles. 5. mash the dal slightly making sure it turns slightly creamy texture. 6. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee and splutter 1 tsp cumin and 1 bay leaf until it turns aromatic. 7. add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 3 chilli. 8. saute until onions turn golden brown. 9. further add 1 tomato, ½ tsp salt and saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 10. add in pressure cooked dal and mix well. 11. also, add 1 cup water and mix adjusting consistency as required. 12. cover and simmer for 10 minutes making sure all the flavours are well absorbed. 13. now add ¼ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 14. finally, enjoy langar wali dal with hot steamed rice or naan.

title: lapsi recipe | fada lapsi recipe | gujurati fada ni lapsi recipe
ingredients: 3 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom, 2 cloves lavang, 0.5 cup daliya broken wheat, 2 cup water, 0.25 cup jaggery, 0.5 cup water, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 2 tbsp almonds badam, 2 tbsp raisins kishmish, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, few threads saffron kesar
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker heat 3 tbsp ghee and saute ½ inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom and 2 cloves until it turns aromatic. 2. now add ½ cup daliya and roast on low flame. 3. roast for 5 minutes or until the daliya turns aromatic. 4. further, add 2 cup hot water and mix well. 5. cover and pressure cook for 5 whistles or until daliya are cooked completely. 6. open the cooker and add ¼ cup jaggery and ½ cup water. 7. mix well making sure the jaggery melts completely. 8. cook for 5 minutes or more until the jaggery thickens slightly. 9. now in a small kadai heat 1 tbsp ghee and add 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp almonds and 2 tbsp raisins. 10. roast until the nuts turn golden brown. 11. add in roasted nuts and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. mix well. 12. finally, enjoy fada lapsi garnished with chopped nuts and kesar.

title: Lassi Recipe (Sweet and Salt Lassi)
ingredients: 2 cups curd, 0.75 cup water, 3.5 tablespoon sugar, 0.375 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon rose water, 1 pinch saffron strands optional, 1 tablespoon nuts, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.375 teaspoon salt, 2 pinches chaat masala, 0.625 cup mint, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.125 teaspoon chaat masala, 2 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon sugar, 0.375 teaspoon salt, 0.25 green chili deseeded
instructions: 1. Add 2 cups chilled curd to a deep bowl or a blender. Do note that using sour curd may need addition of milk and more sugar. I do not prefer the combination of curd and milk together. So I make sure to use fresh curd. 2. Whisk it well until smooth. You should not see any grainy yogurt. I used a whisk here. You can also use a immersion blender or mixing rod. 3. Next add 3 to 4 tablespoons sugar, 1 pinch of saffron strands or 1 teaspoon rosewater. If you do not have saffron strands or rose water, simply skip both. 4. Add ¼ to ½ teaspoon cardamom powder. I removed the husks of the 10 whole green cardamoms & powdered them with little sugar in a grinder. Use only a bit for this. 5. Whisk everything well until sugar dissolves. 6. Add half to 1 cup chilled water or whey from yogurt or milk. Adjust water as required to suit your taste. If you want very thick consistency then use half cup. Start with ¾ cup water if you are using Greek yogurt. If you are not serving it right away then you can also use boiled and chilled milk in place of water. Whisk very well until frothy. Pour this to the serving glasses. Taste test and add more sugar or cardamom powder to suit your taste. Or simply blend in a blender until frothy. You can garnish with any of these – saffron, chopped nuts or cardamom powder. 7. On a low heat, dry roast jeera/ cumin seeds until they begin to smell good and aromatic. Cool them completely and powder finely in a spice jar. 8. Whisk 2 cups chilled curd in a bowl or jug. Add half teaspoon black salt or Himalayan salt and ½ to 1 teaspoon roasted cumin powder. Whisk everything well until smooth. 9. Pour half to 1 cup chilled water and whisk again until you see froth on top. Taste test and add more salt or cumin as required.

title: lassi recipe | punjabi lassi 4 ways | sweet lassi - dry fruits, chocolate & rose
ingredients: 1 cup curd, 2 tbsp sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 cup curd, 2 tbsp pistachios, 2 tbsp almonds, 1 anjeer, 2 tbsp sugar, 0.25 tsp saffron food colour, 1 cup curd, 2 tbsp sugar, 2 tbsp rooh afza, 1 cup curd, 3 tbsp chocolate sauce, 2 tbsp sugar
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 1 cup curd. make sure the curd is thick and chilled. 2. add 2 tbsp sugar and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. 3. blend to make sure everything is well combined. 4. finally, pour punjabi lassi in a tall glass and top with malai or cardamom powder. 5. firstly, in a blender take 1 cup curd. make sure the curd is thick and chilled. 6. add 2 tbsp pistachios, 2 tbsp almonds, 1 anjeer, 2 tbsp sugar and ¼ tsp saffron food colour. 7. blend to make sure everything is well combined. 8. finally, pour dry fruit lassi in a tall glass and top with chopped pistachios. 9. firstly, in a blender take 1 cup curd. make sure the curd is thick and chilled. 10. add 2 tbsp sugar and 2 tbsp rooh afza. adjust the sugar to your choice as rooh afza also has sugar. 11. blend to make sure everything is well combined. 12. finally, pour rose lassi in a tall glass and top with rose syrup. 13. firstly, in a blender take 1 cup curd. make sure the curd is thick and chilled. 14. add 3 tbsp chocolate sauce and 2 tbsp sugar. 15. blend to make sure everything is well combined. 16. finally, pour chocolate lassi in a tall glass and top with chocolate syrup.

title: Lauki Halwa | Dudhi Halwa
ingredients: 3 cups bottle gourd, 5 tbsp sugar, 3 tbsp ghee, 1.5 cups milk, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tbsp almonds, 1 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. Bring 1½ cups full fat milk to a boil in a heavy bottom pan or pot. 2. While the milk boils, rinse 500 grams of bottle gourd under running water. Peel the skin slightly deep and taste the bottle gourd to make sure you are not using bitter or very sour one. Then grate it either using a hand grater or a food processor. I prefer using the grater with the smallest holes. I discarded the center portion of the lauki as it was not young. After grating I had 3 cups of gratings. 3. Keep checking the milk, stir occasionally to make sure it doesn’t get scorched at the bottom. The milk must reduce to half or at least to 1 cup. 4. Heat 2½ tbsps ghee in a heavy bottom pan. 5. Add the grated lauki and saute it well in ghee. Fry well until the raw smell goes away and the moisture evaporates. 6. This may take about 7 to 10 mins. At the end of this step, there should be no excess moisture and the ghee begins to separate from the lauki. 7. Now time to check the milk. Make sure it is very hot, whisk it well with a fork or whisker. Whisking helps to break up the cream. Make sure both milk and lauki are hot at this step. 8. Pour the hot milk to the sauteed lauki. 9. Cook stirring consistently on a medium to low flame. 10. You will see the milk is absorbed fully in some time, then add 4 to 6 tablespoons sugar depending on the level of sweetness you want. 11. Lauki halwa will turn gooey as the sugar melts. Again continue to cook stirring continuously until the halwa turns thick. Reduce the flame completely. 12. Sugar, milk and ghee in the lauki halwa will begin to smell great as you cook. Add ¼ to ½ teaspoon cardamom powder. If you don’t have the powder, crush 4 green cardamom seeds in a mortar pestle and add it. 13. Pour 1 tsp ghee. If you want you can add more. 14. Cook further just for 1 to 2 mins. 15. Garnish lauki ka halwa with sliced almonds and pistas. Serve warm or chilled. It keeps good in the refrigerator for 2 days.

title: lauki ka halwa recipe | dudhi halwa recipe | loki ka halwa recipe
ingredients: 6 grams lauki bottle gourd doodhi ghiya, 0.25 cup ghee clarified butter, 5 cashew kaju, 5 badam almond, 2 tbsp raisins kishmish, 0.5 cup milk, 0.75 cup sugar, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 0.25 cup milk, 0.5 cup milk powder, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, heat ¼ cup ghee and roast 5 cashew, 5 badam and 2 tbsp raisins. 2. roast the nuts to golden brown and keep aside. 3. now add in grated lauki and saute for 5 minutes. 4. saute till the lauki shrinks slightly and changes colour. 5. add in ½ cup milk and mix well. 6. cook for 10 minutes or till milk thickens and lauki gets cooked completely. 7. further add in ¾ cup sugar and mix well. 8. sugar melts and boil for 5 minutes or till it thickens. 9. add in khoya into lauki mixture and mix well. 10. keep stirring till the mixture thickens and forms a mass. 11. add in roasted nuts and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. mix well. 12. finally, transfer dudhi halwa / lauki halwa to a bowl and serve hot or chilled.

title: lauki ki sabji recipe | lauki sabzi | ghiya ki sabji | bottle gourd curry
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 1.5 tomato, 45 grams lauki bottle gourd, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp cumin until it turns aromatic. 2. add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until it turns golden brown. 3. further, add ½ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder and ¾ tsp salt. 4. saute on low flame until it turns aromatic. 5. additionally, add 1½ tomato and saute until it turns soft and mushy. 6. now add 450 grams lauki and saute for 2 minutes. 7. also, add 1 cup water and mix well. 8. cover and boil for 20 minutes on medium flame or until lauki are cooked well. 9. adjust the consistency as required adding more water if required. 10. now add ¼ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 11. finally, enjoy lauki ki sabji with chapathi or rice.

title: Lauki Paratha (Bottle Gourd Paratha)
ingredients: 1.25 cups bottle gourd, 2.25 cups whole wheat flour, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 cup methi leaves fine chopped, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 tablespoon oil, 2 tablespoons Ghee
instructions: 1. Add all the ingredients to a bowl except ghee and oil. Begin to knead well without adding water. The moisture begins to release from gourd and helps to bind the flour. If required add a splash of water. 2. Pour oil and knead to a soft non-sticky pliable dough. If you have time rest for 30 mins. Take a few tablespoons of flour in a small bowl and keep aside. 3. Divide the dough to 8 portions and roll to smooth balls. Keep them covered till they are used up. 4. Sprinkle a teaspoon of flour on the rolling area and flatten a ball. Dip it in the bowl with flour & coat on both the sides, shake gently to get rid of the excess. 5. Begin to roll to a slightly thin or thick paratha as per preference. Making them too thick won't cook the gourd. So I prefer to keep them thin and roll to a 7 to 8 inch paratha. 6. This way roll 4 to 5 in each batch and place them on the kitchen counter. Heat a pan until hot enough. (medium hot, not smoking hot) 7. Dust off the excess flour from the paratha and transfer to the pan. When you see bubbles over it, turn it to the other side with a turner and cook for 2 mins. 8. Return to the other side and press down with a spatula until the paratha has some golden brown spots. Drizzle a generous amount of ghee all over and remove to the plate. (your paratha may or may not puff) 9. This way toast the first batch and stack them on a cooling rack or in a insulated box lined with a clean muslin cloth. Roll the rest and toast them in another batch. 10. Serve lauki paratha with raita, chutney or curry.

title: Lauki Sabzi (Dudhi Recipe)
ingredients: 1 lb lauki, 0.5 cup onion, 1 cup tomato, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon fennel powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5833333333333333 cup water
instructions: 1. Often lauki with darker skin (dark green skin) indicates tougher skin which requires peeling deeper. This kind may also have larger seeds, which you may need to discard while chopping. 2. Prepare the following ingredients: If you have a chopper or dicer, use that to save time. 3. 1 lb (450 to 500 grams, 3 cups) lauki (chopped to ½ to ¾ inch pieces) 4. ½ cup (1 medium) onion (fine chopped) 5. 1 cup (1 large) tomato (chopped, deseeded if preferred) 6. ½ inch (1 teaspoon) ginger or garlic (peeled and fine chopped) 7. 1 sprig curry leaves (optional, pat dry rinsed leaves) 8. If you want you may chop the lauki to larger pieces but that increases the cook time slightly. 9. Pour 2 tablespoons oil to a hot pan. Add half teaspoon cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add the curry leaves (pat dry first). 10. Add the onions, ginger/garlic and green chili. 11. Saute until transparent on a medium heat. 12. Add tomatoes and ¼ teaspoon salt. Mix and cook on a medium heat until soft and mushy. 13. Stir in the following ground spices: 14. ¾ teaspoon coriander powder (optional, I don’t add) 15. Mix and saute for a minute. 16. Add the chopped lauki and ¼ teaspoon salt. 17. Stir fry for 3 to 4 minutes on a high heat. 18. Pour ½ cup water and mix well. When the water comes to a boil, reduce to medium and cover. Let cook until tender, adding more water only as required. I used ⅔ cup water in total but that change depending on your gourd. 19. When the lauki is soft and tender, taste test and add salt as required. If there is a lot of water in the pan, evaporate by cooking on a high heat for 2 to 3 mins. Add ½ teaspoon garam masala and ½ teaspoon teaspoon fennel powder (saunf powder). Turn off and keep covered for 5 mins before serving. Garnish Lauki sabzi with 1 tablespoon chopped coriander leaves. Serve hot with rice, roti or bhakri.

title: lauki thepla recipe | dudhi na thepla | bottle bourd paratha
ingredients: 2 cup lauki bottle gourd, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger pasate, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup wheat flour atta, 0.5 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 2 tbsp oil
instructions: 1. firstly, peel the skin of lauki and grate finely discarding the seed. 2. take 2 cup of grated lauki into a large bowl. 3. also, add ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and ¼ tsp ajwain. 4. further add 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander and 1 tsp salt. 5. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 6. now add 2 cup wheat flour, ½ cup besan and ¼ cup curd. 7. knead the dough adding more wheat flour if required. 8. knead for 5 minutes or until a soft dough is formed. 9. further, add 2 tbsp oil and continue to knead well. 10. pinch a ball sized dough and dust with wheat flour. 11. dust with wheat flour and roll to small disc. 12. further, roll it in thinly like chapati or paratha. 13. now on a hot tawa place, the rolled thepla and cook for a minute. 14. furthermore, when the base is partly cooked, flip the dudhi thepla. 15. also, spread ½ tsp olive oil / ghee and press slightly both the sides. 16. flip again once or twice till both the sides are cooked properly. 17. finally, serve lauki thepla with raita and pickle.

title: leftover rice idli recipe | cooked rice idli | instant idli with leftover rice
ingredients: 1.5 cup cooked rice, 1 cup rava semolina suji, 1 cup water, 1 cup curd, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 1½ cup cooked rice and add 1 cup water. 2. blend to smooth paste. 3. transfer the rice batter to a large bowl. keep aside. 4. in a pan dry roast 1 cup rava. roast on low flame until it turns aromatic. 5. cool completely, and transfer to the same bowl. 6. also, add 1 cup curd, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 7. beat for 3 minutes until the batter turns light. 8. cover and rest for 20 minutes or until the rava absorb well. 9. now add water as required to adjust the consistency of the batter. 10. further, just before steaming, add ½ tsp eno fruit salt and mix gently. 11. mix until the batter turns frothy. 12. now pour the idli batter into the greased plate. make sure to grease the plate before in hand. 13. steam the idli on a medium flame for 13 minutes. 14. finally, enjoy instant leftover rice idli with onion chutney.

title: leftover roti kofta curry recipe | basi chapati ke kofte | bachi roti ke kofta
ingredients: 4 roti chapati, 4 potato, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 2 tbsp cashew raisins, 2 tbsp coriander, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 2 pods cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup tomato puree, 0.5 cup curd, 2 cup water, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp kasuri methi
instructions: 1. firstly, take leftover roti and toast until it turns crisp. 2. break 4 toasted roti into mixi jar. 3. grind to a fine powder without adding water. 4. transfer the roti powder to a large bowl and add 4 potatoes. 5. also add ½ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp salt, 2 chilli and ½ tsp ginger paste. 6. further add 2 tbsp cashew & raisins, 2 tbsp coriander. 7. mix well forming a soft dough. roti powder helps to absorb moisture from the aloo. 8. now prepare ball-sized kofta and deep fry in hot oil. 9. stir occasionally, and fry on medium flame until the kofta turns golden brown and crisp. 10. drain off and keep fried kofta aside. 11. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp cumin, 2 pods cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon and 4 cloves. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 12. add 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste and saute until the onions turn golden brown. 13. further add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and 1 tsp salt. 14. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 15. now add 2 cup tomato puree and cook until the oil separates from the sides. 16. also, add ½ cup curd and cook on low flame until the oil separates. 17. further, add 2 cup of water and mix adjusting consistency as required. 18. once the curry comes to a boil, drop in kofta curry and mix gently. 19. cover and simmer for 2 minutes or until the kofta absorb the flavour. 20. further add ¼ tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander and 1 tsp kasuri methi. 21. finally, garnish roti kofta curry with fresh cream and then enjoy with roti or rice.

title: Lemon Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 3 grams chicken, 1.5 tbsp oil, 1 tsp ginger, 1.5 to 2 tbsp lemon juice, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp lemon zest, 0.75 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp ginger, 0.5 tbsp garlic, 1 green chili, 1 medium yello onion, 0.5 tsp sugar, 1 tsp cajun seasoning, 0.5 tbsp soya sauce, 9 mint leaves
instructions: 1. Add diced chicken, ginger paste, red chili powder, salt, turmeric, lemon juice, lemon zest and oil. Do not omit oil, it is the key to tenderize chicken along with lemon juice. I used half of a large lemon. I suggest using fresh lemon and freshly ground ginger or grated ginger. 2. Marinate the chicken and set aside for at least an hour up to overnight in the refrigerator. 3. Heat oil in a pan and saute ginger and garlic for 1 minute. 4. Add onions, slit green chili, sugar and salt. 5. Saute until the onions turn transparent. 6. Add marinated chicken along with the lemon juice. Turn down the flame to low, Stir and saute till the chicken is half cooked, for 2 mins. 7. Sprinkle garam masala or cajun spice mix or all spice. Stir Fry for another 2 to 3 mins. 8. Pour soya sauce and cook on a slightly high heat till the chicken is cooked through for another 1 to 2 mins. Evaporate the soya completely. Taste test and adjust salt, sauce or spices to your taste. 9. Add finely chopped mint leaves or cilantro/coriander leaves. 10. Stir through and saute until the herbs wilt, for another 1 minute. Garnish lemon chicken with mint or cilantro/coriander leaves. Serve with onion wedges.

title: Lemon Pepper Fish Recipe
ingredients: 1 lb fish, 1 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chilies, 2 tablespoon oil, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Add the fish pieces to a mixing bowl. Pour lemon juice all over and sprinkle salt, turmeric and red chilli powder. Set this aside for minimum 15 mins. 2. Powder together fennel seeds and pepper corn in a small grinder. 3. Heat a wide pan with ghee or oil. 4. Once it becomes hot, reduce the heat and add ginger garlic paste, green chilies. Fry until it turns aromatic, for about 60 seconds. Do not burn this. 5. Spread the oil well in the pan. Place the fish pieces. Fry them for 2 to 3 mins. 6. Turn them to the other side and fry again for another 2 to 3 mins. 7. Sprinkle half of the pepper and saunf powder evenly. Add curry leaves. 8. Turn them and sprinkle the rest of the powder. Fry until the fish is cooked through. To test, prick it with a fork at the thickest part, it should flake easily. The internal temperature should reach 145F or 65C. 9. Taste test and add more salt if required. If using garam masala powder you can sprinkle now. If you want more lemony flavor, add more lemon juice while serving. 10. Lemon pepper fish is ready to serve with roasted veggies or rice, rasam or as appetizer with soup.

title: Lemon Pickle Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams lemons, 0.5 cup lemon juice, 1.25 tablespoons salt, 2.5 tablespoons red chilli powder, 0.75 teaspoon roasted methi seeds powder, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 4 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 2 red chilies, 2.5 garlic cloves, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida
instructions: 1. Optional - If using fenugreek seeds, roast 1 tsp seeds on a low heat until they turn fragrant and deep golden in color. Cool this and powder finely. Measure ½ to 1 tsp powder for this pickle and set aside. 2. Rinse the lemons well until running water and wipe dry with a clean cloth. Air dry them preferably overnight to ensure they are moisture free. 3. Ensure the chopping board, knife, bowls, spoons and pickle jar are clean and moist free. Do not use moist surfaces or cutlery while you make the pickle to avoid spoilage. 4. Chop 200 grams lemons to desired sizes and deseed them gently with a small fork or a knife. 5. Extract the lemon juice from the rest of the lemons (another 200 grams) and discard the seeds. 6. Spread the chopped lemon pieces on a clean tray and sun dry them for at least 5 hours. If you do not have sunlight, then put the tray in the oven and turn on the oven at 40 C (104 F), at the lowest possible settings in your oven for 1 hour, shake the tray after 30 mins. Keep the oven door ajar (little open) through out to avoid moisture building up inside. 7. At the same time, spread red chilli powder, salt and turmeric in a tray. Keep it in the sun for 1 hour or in the oven at 40 C or 104F (without salt) for 20 mins. Keep the oven door ajar (little open) through out to avoid moisture building up inside. Remove the tray and place on a wired rack so the ingredients don't sweat. 8. Also keep the lemon juice in the sun or in the oven. If keeping in the sun, spread a thin muslin cloth over the bowl of lemon juice. 9. Before you make the pickle, cool down all of the ingredients including salt. This step is very important. They will become warm or slightly hot after keeping in the sun/ oven. 10. Add the lemon pieces to a clean glass or ceramic bowl or jar. 11. Pour the lemon juice, add 1 tbsp salt and turmeric. 12. Cover and rest it for 1 to 2 days at room temperature in a dry clean place. Avoid keeping this in a humid atmosphere like in kitchen. Keep it in the living. 13. Stir the lemon pieces together with chilli powder and ½ to 1 tsp methi seeds powder. Cover and set aside. 14. Heat oil in a pan until hot enough. Then add mustard, red chilies and crushed garlic. 15. Turn off when the garlic is fried to crisp and golden. Add hing and cool down the oil completely. 16. Pour half of the oil to the lemon pickle and give a good stir. Taste it and add more salt and chilli powder if needed. 17. Transfer this to a glass bottle. Pour the remaining oil over the pickle. 18. Press down the pickle so the oil floats on top. Ensure there is a layer of oil over the lemon pickle as this helps in a good shelf life. 19. Cover with a lid and refrigerate the lemon pickle for better shelf life. 20. It takes 10 to 20 days for the lemon pieces to be ready. When the pickle is ready, the lemon pieces will soften. 21. Always use dry spoons to serve the pickle. Wipe clean the sides of the jar with a clean tissue after every use. Ensure most of the lemon pickle is under the oil at all times. This helps in preserving the pickle for longer.

title: Lemon Rasam (Nimmakaya Pappu Charu)
ingredients: 0.5 cup toor dal, 1.5 cups water, 3.5 cups water, 1 tomato, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, 1.25 teaspoon salt, 1 pinch turmeric, 2.5 teaspoons rasam powder, 1 handful coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch methi seeds, 1 red chilli, 1 pinch hing, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. Pressure cooker: Add dal to a bowl and rinse it well at least thrice. Drain the water and pour 1½ cups water. Place it in the cooker and pressure cook for 3 to 4 whistles on a medium flame. When the pressure releases remove the dal and mash it well to smooth. 2. Pot: To cook in a sauce pot without a pressure cooker, simply cook on a medium heat until tender and soft, skim off any foam that comes on the surface. You may require more water, if so pour more boiling water and cook. 3. To cook in Instant Pot, place the bowl in the IP, over a steam rack and pressure cook for 10 mins. Let the pressure drop naturally. 4. Dal must be soft cooked and mushy. Mash it well with the back of a ladle to smooth texture. 5. While the dal cooks, pour 4 cups water to a pot. Add tomatoes, turmeric, rasam powder, green chilli (optional) and crushed ginger. Cook until the tomatoes are soft cooked and mushy. 6. Add the mashed dal and salt. Bring it to a boil & add chopped coriander leaves. 7. Heat a small tadka pan and add oil or ghee. When it is hot, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, methi seeds and dried red chilies. 8. When the seeds begin to crackle, add curry leaves and hing. Pour it to the lemon rasam. Simmer the rasam for 2 mins on a very low flame. 9. Allow the lemon rasam to cool down a bit then squeeze off lemon as needed. Adjust salt as well. 10. Serve lemon rasam with soft cooked rice or enjoy as a soup.

title: Lemon Rasam Recipe | Nimbu Rasam Recipe | South Indian Lemon Rasam
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 1 tomato, 5 chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp oil, 3 cup water, 3 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp pepper, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 lemon, for tadka, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 red chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup toor dal, 1 tomato, 5 chilli, 1 inch ginger and few curry leaves. 2. Also add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp oil and 3 cup water. 3. Pressure cook for 5 whistles or until the dal is cooked well. 4. Whisk the dal to make it a silky smooth texture. 5. Pour in 3 cup water, ½ tsp salt and ½ tsp pepper. Mix well. 6. Boil for 3 minutes or until flavours are well absorbed. 7. Now to prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Add, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 8. Splutter the tempering and pour over rasam. 9. Also add 2 tbsp coriander, ½ lemon and mix well. 10. Finally, enjoy Lemon Rasam Recipe with hot steamed rice.

title: Lemon Rice | South Indian Chitrannam & Gojju Premix in 1 min
ingredients: 0.25 cup oil, 0.5 cup peanut, 1 tbsp mustard, 2 tbsp urad dal, 2 tbsp chana dal, 1 tbsp cumin, 3 chilli, 2 inch ginger, 3 tbsp curry leaves, 2 red chilli, 0.75 tsp hing, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp salt, 3 cup leftover rice, 0.5 lemon, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat ¼ cup oil and roast ½ cup peanut until it turns crunchy. 2. add 1 tbsp mustard, 2 tbsp urad dal, 2 tbsp chana dal and 1 tbsp cumin. 3. splutter tempering keeping the flame on low. 4. now add 3 chilli, 2 inch ginger and roast well. roast in batches for uniform frying. 5. further add 3 tbsp curry leaves, 2 dried red chilli and fry until it tunrs crunchy. 6. turn off the flame and add ¾ tsp hing, 1 tsp turmeric and 2 tbsp salt. 7. mix well making sure everything is well combined. heat of the pan is enough for turmeric to cook. 8. Transfer to an airtight container and lemon rice gojju is ready to store upto a month. 9. firstly, take 3 cup of leftover rice and add 2 tbsp prepared lemon rice gojju. 10. Also add ½ lemon and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 11. finally, enjoy lemon rice with coconut chutney.

title: Lemon Rice Recipe (Chitranna)
ingredients: 1.5 cups raw rice - uncooked, 2 tablespoon oil, 0.25 cup peanuts, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1.5 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 2 green chilies, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 pinch hing, 2.5 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Omit this if you have precooked rice - Add raw rice to a strainer and rinse under running water. Transfer to a pot and pour water (read notes). Bring to a rolling boil and reduce the heat to medium. Cook covered until the rice is cooked through. (takes me 15 mins) 2. Cool the rice completely & fluff with a fork. 3. Pour ½ tablespoon oil to a pan and add the nuts. Fry them on a medium heat, until golden and aromatic. Remove to a plate and reserve aside to garnish. wipe down with a kitchen paper. 4. Pour the remaining oil and add mustard, urad dal & chana dal. Fry on a medium flame until the dal turns golden & aromatic. (for 2 to 3 mins) 5. Add ginger, green chilis, red chilies(optional) and curry leaves. Fry them until the leaves turn crisp for about 30 seconds. 6. Stir in hing, turmeric and salt. Immediately pour 3 tbsps water & mix. Cook until the water evaporates and the lentils soften. (If you want the dal very soft, add 3 tbsps more and cook down). Turn off the stove. 7. Add the cooled rice and squeeze the lemon juice. Give a good mix. 8. Taste test to adjust salt and lemon. Garnish with roasted nuts. Serve lemon rice with plain yogurt, papad or pickle. 9. Add 1½ cups basmati rice to a strainer & rinse under running water. Press the saute button and pour 2 tablespoons oil to the steel insert. Add ¼ cup peanuts or cashews and fry until golden. Remove to a plate. 10. Add 1½ tsp chana dal, 1 tsp urad dal, ¾ tsp mustard seeds, 1 to 2 slit green chilies & 1 sprig curry leaves. Fry until lightly golden. Stir in ½ tbsp ginger, pinch of hing and ½ tsp turmeric. 11. Pour 1¾ cups water and add ½ tsp salt. Deglaze the pot and add rice. Mix well. 12. Secure the lid & position the steam release vent to sealing position. Press the PRESSURE COOK button and set the timer to 5 mins. 13. When the IP finishes (it beeps), wait for 5 mins and release the pressure manually with a long spoon (move the steam release handle from sealing to venting) 14. Remove the steel insert to the counter and let cool down for 2 to 3 mins. Taste test and sprinkle more salt if required. Pour the lemon juice all over and mix gently. 15. Garnish lemon rice with roasted nuts and serve with yogurt.

title: luchi recipe | puffed bengali luchai bread | luchai puri recipe
ingredients: 3 cup maida plain flour, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, water for kneading, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 3 cup maida, ½ tsp salt and 1 tbsp ghee. 2. crumble and mix well making sure the flour holds the shape when pressed with the fist. 3. now slowly add water as required and prepare a smooth dough. 4. grease the dough with oil and rest for 15 minutes. 5. pinch a small lemon sized balls and grease with oil. 6. roll the dough evenly into circles using a rolling pin. roll neither too thin nor thick. 7. heat oil in a deep frying pan or kadai. when the oil is sufficiently hot, add one poori. 8. and, press with the spoon to puff up. 9. once they puff, it will automatically turn over. if not, once the bottom side is golden brown, turn over the poori. 10. do not allow the poori to turn golden brown. 11. then drain the poori into tissue paper to remove excess oil. 12. finally, serve luchi with curries of your choice. 13. dd

title: Lunch Thali Recipe | Mess Wali Thali Recipe | Thali meals under 30 mins
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp ghee, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 cup basmati rice, 1 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp ghee, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 onion, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 3 tomato, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup matar peas, 1 pieces paneer, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander, 3 tsp oil, 2 potato, 1 cup cauliflower gobi, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take ½ cup basmati rice, 1 tsp cumin, ¼ tsp salt, ½ tsp ghee and 1 cup water. 2. along with jeera rice, your can also cook dal. to cook dal, in a cooker take ½ cup moong dal. 3. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp ghee, 1 cup water and ½ tsp salt. 4. place a stand and place the bowl of jeera rice. 5. cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles on medium flame. 6. finally, jeera rice is ready to serve. 7. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 8. now add 2 onion, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste and saute until the onions turn golden brown. 9. keep the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder. 10. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 11. further, add 3 tomato and saute until the tomato turns soft and mush. 12. divide the onion tomato base into 3 parts (in ratio of 2:1:1) used to make matar paneer, dal and aloo jeera. 13. to the pressure cooked dal, add 1 portion of onion tomato base. 14. also, add ½ tsp salt and 1 cup water. mix well adjusting consistency as required. 15. boil for a minute, or until the flavours are absorbed well. 16. now add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 17. finally, enjoy moong dal fry with roti or jeera rice. 18. firstly, in a kadai heat 3 tsp oil. add 2 potato and saute on medium flame. 19. saute until the potato turns slightly golden brown. 20. add 1 cup cauliflower, ¼ tsp turmeric and ½ tsp salt. 21. saute for a minute until the vegetables are half cooked. 22. add 1 portion of prepared onion tomato base and mix well. 23. further add 2 tbsp water, cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 24. or cook until the aloo and gobi are cooked well. 25. finally, enjoy aloo gobi sabji garnished with add 2 tbsp coriander. 26. firstly, to the prepared onion tomato base add 1 cup water and ½ tsp salt. 27. mix well adjusting the consistency as required. 28. further, add ½ cup matar and 10 pieces paneer. mix gently. 29. cover and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the flavours are absorbed well. 30. also, add ¼ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 31. finally, enjoy matar paneer recipe with roti or jeera rice. 32. firstly, in a large thali plate, add in prepared aloo gobi, matar paneer and moong dal fry. 33. also, keep jeera rice, roti, salad, pickle and papad. 34. finally, enjoy north indian mess thali with boondi raita.

title: maa ki dal recipe | kaali dal | maa ki daal | black gram dal
ingredients: 0.75 cup black urad dal, water, 3 cup water, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 2 tomato, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take ¾ cup black urad dal and soak in enough water for 3 hours. 2. drain off the water and transfer to a pressure cooker. 3. add 3 cup water and 1 tsp ghee. 4. cover and pressure cook for 5 whistles or until dal is cooked well. keep aside. 5. in a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp ghee and saute 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp cumin and 1 tsp kasuri methi. 6. add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 1 chilli. 7. saute well making sure onions turn golden brown, 8. keeping the flame on low, add ¼ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp garam masala and 1 tsp salt. 9. saute on low flame, until spices turn aromatic. 10. further, add 2 tomato and saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 11. add in pressure cooked dal and mix well. 12. also, add 1 cup water or as required adjusting consistency as desired. 13. cover and simmer for 10 minutes or until flavours are absorbed. 14. now add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 15. finally, enjoy ma ki dal recipe with roti or rice.

title: macaroni kurkure recipe | crispy macaroni snack | pasta kurkure
ingredients: water, 1 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 2 cup macaroni pasta, 0.25 cup maida, 0.5 cup corn flour, oil, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large vessel take enough water. 2. add 1 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil and get to a rolling boil. 3. once the water boils, add 2 cup macaroni pasta. you can use any shape of your choice. 4. boil for 7 minutes, or check package instructions to know the cooking time. 5. once the pasta turns al dente, drain off the water. 6. rinse with cold water to prevent from overcooking the pasta. 7. transfer the boiled pasta to a large bowl. and add ¼ cup maida and ¼ cup cornflour and mix well. 8. add more cornflour if required and coat the pasta with flour. 9. deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on low to medium. 10. keep stirring in between and fry until the pasta turns golden and crispy. take at least 15 minutes to get crispy kurkure. 11. drain off over kitchen towel to get rid of excess oil. 12. take fried pasta in a large bowl. add 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi and ¼ tsp salt. 13. mix well making sure all the spices are well coated. 14. finally, enjoy tandoori flavoured kurkure pasta with evening chai. 15. take fried pasta in a large bowl. add 1 tsp chaat masala, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ¼ tsp salt. 16. mix well making sure all the spices are well coated. 17. finally, enjoy chatpata chaat flavoured kurkure pasta with evening chai.

title: Maddur Vada Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour, 0.5 cup semolina, 0.5 cup all-purpose flour, 1 cup onions, 1.5 tablespoon ginger, 2.5 green chilies, 0.25 cup curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 teaspoon salt, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1.5 tablespoons sesame seeds, 135 ml water, 1.5 to 2 cups oil
instructions: 1. Slice onions thin and evenly so they brown evenly when fried. After slicing, halve the slices so they are not too long. (check step-by-step pictures in the post) 2. To a mixing bowl, add rice flour, all-purpose flour, semolina, onions, ginger, curry leaves, green chilies and salt. 3. Mix all of them together squeezing the onions so they release the moisture. 4. Heat 2½ tablespoons oil in a kadai. It has to be very hot so it sizzles when poured into the mixture. When it becomes hot, pour it to the mixing bowl. 5. Add sesame seeds and red chilli powder. Give a quick stir with a spoon as the oil is still hot. 6. Meanwhile heat oil in a kadai on a medium flame for deep frying. 7. Then pour ¼ cup water and begin to form a non-sticky soft dough adding more water as required. I used half cup plus 2 to 3 tbsps more. 8. Dough has to be soft & smooth without any cracks. If it becomes sticky sprinkle little flour, if it is dry sprinkle little water. Divide the dough to 16 equal portions & roll them to balls. 9. Grease a parchment paper or a banana leaf. Place a ball on the sheet and begin to spread the dough to make a patty that is neither too thin nor too thick. Keep the edges thinner than the center. (check the pictures and video for thickness) 10. You will see the edges crack and it is just fine to leave them or you can even join them. You can make 3 to 4 maddur vade at one time. 11. Test if the oil is hot enough by gently dropping a small portion of dough to the hot oil. It has to rise without browning quickly. 12. Gently lift the parchment paper and transfer the maddur vada to your fingers. Carefully transfer it to the hot oil & fry them on a medium heat. 13. This way you can fry 2 to 3 vadas at one time depending on the size of your kadai. Do not disturb them for 1 to 2 minutes until they become firm otherwise they break. 14. When you see the edges light golden, turn them to the other side and fry until golden and crisp. When you see the onions are deep golden, reduce the flame to low and fry for 1 to 2 mins. 15. Remove them to a steel colander. This way shape and fry all of them. 16. Maddur vada are best served they cool down as they firm up & get the perfect texture. Serve them with masala tea or coconut chutney. 17. Preheat air fryer at 180 C or 360 F. Place the vadas in the basket and air fry them for 12 mins. Adjust the timing as required until they become crisp and golden. Mine take about 14 mins. 18. Remove and brush them with ghee or oil. Let them cool down completely.

title: Maddur Vada Recipe | Crispy Maddur Vade - Tea Time Snack
ingredients: 1 onion, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 cup rice flour, 1 cup maida, 0.5 cup rava, 2 tbsp sesame, 2 tbsp roasted peanuts, pinch hing, 1 tsp salt, water, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 1 onion, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger and 2 tbsp coriander. 2. Add 1 cup rice flour, 1 cup maida and ½ cup rava. 3. Also add 2 tbsp sesame, 2 tbsp roasted peanuts, pinch hing and 1 tsp salt. 4. Crumble and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 5. Further, add 2 tbsp hot oil and mix. This helps the vada to turn crunchy. 6. Add water slowly and knead the dough. 7. Do not add more water as the dough needs to be tight. 8. Pinch a ball-sized dough, press and flatten it slightly thick. 9. Deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 10. Do not touch for 2 minutes as there are chances for the vada to break. 11. Stir occasionally and fry both sides until the vada turns golden brown. 12. Drain off to remove excess oil. 13. Finally, enjoy Maddur Vada Recipe with coconut chutney.

title: maggi manchurian recipe | noodles manchurian | manchurian maggi gravy
ingredients: 1 tsp butter, 1.5 cup water, 2 pack maggi tastemaker, 2 pack maggi noodles, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp cabbage, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp spring onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup corn flour, 1 pack maggi noodles, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tsp corn flour, 0.5 cup water, 1.5 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan take 1½ cup water and 2 pack maggi tastemaker. 2. mix well and get to a boil. 3. crush 2 pack of maggi noodles into boiling water. you can use noodles of your choice. 4. mix well and boil for 2 minutes or until noodles are cooked well. 5. do not overcook noodles, as it turns mushy. 6. cool completely, and transfer to a large bowl. 7. add 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp cabbage, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp spring onion, and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 8. also add 1 tsp chilli sauce, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 9. now add ½ cup cornflour and mix gently. 10. mix well forming a soft dough. make sure not to mash the noodles. 11. grease hands with oil and prepare ball-sized manchurian balls. 12. coat with crushed maggi to get an extra crispy bite. 13. deep fry in medium hot oil, keeping flame on medium. 14. stir occasionally, and fry until the balls turn golden brown. 15. drain off and keep aside. 16. in a large wok, heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp spring onion. 17. add ½ onion, ½ capsicum and stir fry on high flame. 18. now add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ¼ tsp salt. 19. mix well until the sauces are well combined. 20. further, add cornflour slurry. to prepare cornflour slurry, mix 2 tsp of cornflour in ½ cup water. 21. mix continuously, making sure the sauce thicks and turns glossy. 22. pour in 1½ cup water and mix well. 23. stir and boil until the sauce thickens slightly. 24. drop in prepared noodle balls and mix well. 25. boil for a minute until the flavour is well absorbed. 26. finally, enjoy maggi manchurian gravy recipe with fried rice.

title: maggi noodles recipe | maggi masala 4 ways | cheese & vegetable maggi
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, 0.5 tomato, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 1 pack maggi noodles, 1 pack tastemaker, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp butter, 2 clove garlic, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 tomato, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp capsicum, 1 pack maggi noodles, 1 pack tastemaker, 2 slice cheese, 2 tbsp spring onion, 1 tsp butter, 2 clove garlic, 2 chilli, 0.5 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 cup tomato puree, 2 tbsp beans, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 1 cup water, 1 pack maggi noodles, 1 pack tastemaker, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tsp oil, 2 chilli, 2 clove garlic, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp spring onion, 2 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 1 cup water, 1 pack maggi noodles, few capsicums, 1 pack tastemaker, 2 tbsp spring onion
instructions: 1. firstly, in pan heat 2 tsp oil and saute 2 clove garlic and ½ onion. 2. add ½ carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, ½ tomato and 2 tbsp sweet corn. 3. saute until the vegetables turn crunchy. 4. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp salt and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. add 1 cup water and get to a boil. 6. now bread 1 pack maggi noodles roughly and add 1 pack tastemaker. 7. mix well and boil for 2 minutes or until the noodles are cooked well. 8. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy masaledar maggi. 9. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tsp butter, add 2 clove garlic, ½ tsp chilli flakes, ½ tsp mixed herbs and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 10. add ½ onion, 2 tbsp spring onion and saute slightly. 11. further add ½ tomato, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp capsicum and saute until the vegetables turn crunchy. 12. add 1 cup water and get to a boil. 13. now bread 1 pack maggi noodles roughly and add 1 pack tastemaker. 14. mix well and boil for 2 minutes or until the noodles are cooked well. 15. now add 2 slice of cheese and mix until the cheese melts completely. if the noodles are thick, then add water if required. 16. finally, add 2 tbsp spring onion and enjoy cheese maggi. 17. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tsp butter, add 2 clove garlic, 2 chilli and saute slightly. 18. add ½ onion and saute until the onion shrink slightly. 19. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi and ¼ tsp salt. 20. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 21. further, add ½ cup tomato puree and satue until the tomato puree thickens. 22. now add 2 tbsp beans, ½ carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum and 2 tbsp sweet corn. saute for a minute, or until the vegetables turn crunchy. 23. add 1 cup water and get to a boil. 24. now bread 1 pack maggi noodles roughly and add 1 pack tastemaker. 25. mix well and boil for 2 minutes or until the noodles are cooked well. 26. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy spicy curry maggi. 27. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tsp butter, add 2 clove garlic, 2 chilli and saute slightly. 28. add ½ onion, 2 tbsp spring onion and saute until the onion shrink slightly. 29. now add 2 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tsp soy sauce and ¼ tsp pepper powder. saute until the sauces turn aromatic. 30. add 1 cup water and get to a boil. 31. now bread 1 pack maggi noodles, few capsicums and add 1 pack tastemaker. 32. mix well and boil for 2 minutes or until the noodles are cooked well. 33. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy spicy curry maggi.

title: maggi pakoda recipe | maggi ke pakode | maggi pakora recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup water, 1 pack maggi tastemaker, 1 pack maggi, 1 cup cabbage, 1 carrot, 1 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.75 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup corn flour, 2 tbsp rice flour, oil for frying
instructions: 1. in a large mixing bowl take, 1 cup cabbage, 1 carrot, 1 onion, ½ capsicum and 2 tbsp spring onion. 2. also add ½ tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp chaat masala, ¼ tsp ajwain and ½ tsp salt. 3. further add ¾ cup besan and ¼ cup corn flour. 4. squeeze and mix until a thick mixture is formed. 5. now add in prepared maggi. 6. mix gently without breaking them. 7. pinch a small ball sized mixture and drop in hot oil. 8. stir occasionally, frying on medium flame until the pakoda turns golden and crisp. 9. finally, enjoy maggi pakoda recipe with tomato sauce or green chutney.

title: maggi pizza recipe | maggi noodles pizza recipe | maggi noodles as pizza base
ingredients: 1.5 cup water, 2 pack maggi tastemaker, 2 pack maggi noodles, 1 tbsp corn flour, 1 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp pizza sauce, 6 tbsp mozzarella cheese, few capsicum, 3 slice tomato, few jalapeno, few olives, 0.25 tsp mixed herbs, 0.25 tsp chilli flakes, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 1 tsp olive oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large wok pour 1½ cup water and add 2 pack maggi tastemaker. 2. mix well and get the water to a boil. 3. now break 2 pack maggi noodles and mix well. 4. boil for 2 minutes or until the noodles softens. 5. now add 1 tbsp cornflour and mix well. corn flour helps to give a crunchy base. 6. continue to cook for a minute or until there is no more water. 7. in a pan spread 1 tbsp butter and then transfer the maggi. 8. spread the maggi uniformly forming the pizza base. 9. cover and simmer for 5 minutes. make sure to keep the flame on low else the base will burn. 10. cook until the base turns crisp. flip over gently. 11. now spread 2 tbsp pizza sauce and top 4 tbsp mozzarella cheese. 12. also, top with few capsicum, 3 slice tomato, few jalapeno and few olives. 13. further sprinkle ¼ tsp mixed herbs, ¼ tsp chilli flakes and add 2 tbsp sweet corn. 14. if you want to make it cheesy, add 2 tbsp of more mozzarella cheese. 15. spread 1 tsp olive oil on sides, cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the cheese melt completely. 16. finally, slice the maggi pizza and enjoy it when it is hot.

title: Majjiga Charu (Majjiga Pulusu)
ingredients: 1 medium onion, 1 cup curd, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch fenugreek seeds, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.1875 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon chana dal, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, chana dal and urad dal. 2. When the dal turns golden, add ginger and saute until it turns fragrant. 3. Add curry leaves and green chilies. Stir fry for a while. When the curry leaves turn crisp, add onions and fry lightly until transparent yet crunchy. 4. Add hing, turmeric and salt. If using fenugreek powder add it. Cool this completely. 5. Meanwhile add curd or yogurt to a serving bowl. Whisk it well with a fork. Add the cooled mixture to the whisked curd. Add finely chopped coriander leaves. Taste and add more salt if needed. 6. Serve with cooled rice. If you mix it with hot rice, the curd coagulates. We usually have a stir fry, dal or any curry on the side along with this. Finish up the dish with in 2 hours and don’t store the leftovers.

title: majjige huli recipe | kovakkai mor kuzhambu | tindora yoghurt curry
ingredients: 0.75 cup coconut, 3 green chilli, few curry leaves, 1 tbsp rice, 0.5 cup water, 2 cup tindora dondakaya ivy gourd kovakkai tendli tondekai, 1 cup water, few curry leaves, 0.75 tsp salt, 0.5 cup curd yoghurt, 2 tsp oil, 0.75 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 red chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare masala paste by taking ¾ cup coconut, 3 green chilli, few curry leaves and 1 tbsp soaked rice in a small blender. 2. add ½ cup water and blend to smooth paste. keep aside. 3. now in a large kadai take 2 cups sliced tindora. 4. further add 1 cup water, few curry leaves, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 5. cover and boil for 10 minutes or till tindora cook well. 6. now add in prepared coconut masala paste along with ¼ tsp salt. 7. mix well adjusting consistency as required. 8. boil for 5 minutes or till the raw smell of coconut disappears. 9. turn off the flame and add ½ cup sour curd. make sure to whisk the curd before adding to prevent from curdling. 10. mix well till the kodekane / majjige huli turns silky texture. 11. pour the tempering over majjige huli and mix well. 12. finally, serve majjige huli / kovakkai mor kuzhambu with hot steamed rice.

title: Makhana Recipe (Phool Makhana Snack)
ingredients: 2.25 cups makhana, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 tablespoon water, 1 teaspoon ghee, 2 cups makhana, 5.5 tablespoons jaggery powder, 0.5 tablespoon water, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.625 tablespoon ghee, 1 tablespoon crushed roasted peanuts
instructions: 1. To a small bowl add chili powder, salt, garlic powder, garam masala (or chaat masala). Taste test and adjust salt and spice to taste. 2. Carefully, pour water in 2 batches to make a thick paste (not dry, not runny) of the spices. Check pictures in the post. 3. Add your makhana to a pan. Drizzle half of this paste all over the makhana. It will fall in lumps, using both your hands rub the spice paste all over the makhana. 4. Add more paste as required to suit your taste. Repeat rubbing the makhana until all of them are seasoned well. 5. Toast them on a low to medium-low heat until they turn crisp and crunchy. If you want add ghee at this stage and roast for another 1 minute. Turn off and let cool in the pan itself. 6. Dry roast makhana on a low to medium-low heat until crispy and crunchy. Turn off the stove. Transfer to a tray to cool down. 7. Add jaggery, cardamom and water to the same wide pan and mix well until the jaggery dissolves. 8. Cook on a low heat, until you see the jaggery bubbling up. There is very little water used, so you won't see a lot of bubbles. 9. Add the crushed peanuts (sesame or coconut flakes) and the makhana. Quickly begin to coat the makhana until most of the jaggery stuck at the bottom coats them, 10. Make a space in the center and add the ghee. Stir well and roast just for a minute or 2 until the makhana are crunchy. 11. Leave the sweet makhana in the pan to cool down a bit, before serving.

title: Maladu recipe | Fried gram or roasted gram ladoo recipe
ingredients: 1 cup roasted bengal gram, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 cup ghee, 3 green cardamoms, 7 cashews
instructions: 1. Add sugar and cardamoms to a blender jar. It is good to use seeds than the entire cardamom pods. 2. Make a fine powder. 3. Transfer this to a bowl. If you are using a bigger jar, you can just keep it in the jar. 4. Add fried gram. 5. Make a fine powder. You may need to scrape the sides and blend again to make very fine powder. Make sure sugar and fried gram turn to a very fine powder. 6. Set this aside together with the sugar. 7. Heat 1/4 cup ghee and fry cashews lightly if using. The ghee turns very hot. 8. Add the flour and powdered sugar. Off the stove. 9. Begin to mix well. The mixture turn really hot and begins to emit a nice aroma. At this stage the flour must get coated with the ghee well. If you see dry powder, just go on mixing. Sugar begins to melt and helps to bind the flour well. If you see any dry flour, then immediately add 1 tbsp more ghee and mix. Then add another tbsp if needed. I used 2 tbsp extra ghee apart from 1/4 cup. 10. Allow the mixture to cool down a bit. When it is still warm take small portions of this and make balls. You don’t need to grease your palms. Do not allow to cool down completely.

title: Malai Chicken Recipe (Creamy White Chicken Curry)
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons Greek yogurt, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon oil, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1.5 cups onions, 0.75 inch ginger, 2 garlic, 15 cashew nuts, 0.5 cup full fat milk, 2.25 tablespoon ghee, 1.5 green chili, 1 black cardamom, 2 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional)), 2 strands mace, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon Garam Masala, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.1875 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 cup heavy cream
instructions: 1. Soak cashews in half cup hot water until needed later. 2. To a mixing bowl add Greek yogurt (hung curd), salt, black pepper, ginger garlic paste, kasuri methi, coriander powder and garam masala. 3. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt and spice. Add the chicken and marinate. Cover and set aside at least for 30 minutes to overnight in the refrigerator. 4. Heat oil in a pan. Add cardamoms, sliced onions, ginger and garlic. Saute until transparent for about 4 to 5 mins. Do not brown them. 5. Cool down and add them to a grinder jar along with cashews and milk/cream (added in 2 batches if needed). Grind to a smooth puree. 6. Pour half of the ghee to a pan & heat it on a low flame. Add all the optional ingredients – chopped green chili, cloves, black cardamom, cinnamon and mace. Be careful, the spices can splutter. 7. Pour the blended mixture along with water. Bring it to a gentle boil on a medium heat. Cook covered until thick & you see specks of oil/fats on top. This takes about 12 to 13 minutes. Keep stirring every 5 mins. 8. While the gravy simmers, heat a pan with the rest of the ghee and place the chicken pieces. Fry them until almost cooked, for 3 mins on each side. 9. It’s okay if the chicken is undercooked, if you want you may brown the chicken. 10. When the sauce is thick, stir in the garam masala, nutmeg, salt, kasuri methi, black pepper and the pan-fried chicken. 11. Cover and cook for 2 to 3 mins. Taste test and adjust salt and more black pepper or garam masala. If you want you may add heavy cream. 12. Garnish with black pepper. Serve Malai chicken with Tandoori roti, Naan, Yellow rice or Saffron rice. If you want you may squeeze some lemon juice before serving. 13. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan. Add all the whole spices and let them sizzle for 30 seconds. Be cautious as they may splutter. 14. Add ginger garlic paste and sauté until a nice aroma comes out. Do not brown and burn this. 15. Add chicken and fry on medium heat till it turns white or pale in color. This takes around 3 to 4 mins. Transfer this to a plate. 16. Pour 1 teaspoon oil to the same pan, add pureed tomatoes and sprinkle salt. Cook this until all of the moisture evaporates and turns thick like a sauce. This step takes around 4 to 5 mins. 17. Turn off the stove and transfer the chicken to the tomato mixture. Cool all of these completely. Cooling them is very important. 18. Once cool, combine the chicken, tomato mixture, yogurt, fried onions, mint, coriander leaves, turmeric, green chili, red chili powder, salt & half of the chicken masala powder. 19. Marinate the chicken, set aside for at least 1 hour. For best flavor, taste & tender meat marinate overnight in the fridge. 20. Heat 1 tablespoon butter or oil in a pan. Then add marinated chicken. 21. Turn down the flame to lowest, Cover and cook till the chicken turns tender. Stir occasionally to prevent burning. 22. I do not add any water as the chicken releases some juices. However this depends on the kind of meat. 23. Once the chicken is tender, add the rest of the chicken masala powder & kasuri methi. Cover and cook for 2 to 3 mins. If the curry looks runny, evaporate the excess moisture by cooking further (uncovered) on a higher heat. At the end of this step the curry must be quite thick since it will thin down after adding the cream. 24. Add the cream and mix well. Taste test and add more salt or cream to taste. Garnish with more cream and coriander leaves. 25. Serve malai chicken hot with roti, naan, chapati or basmati rice.

title: Malai Kofta Recipe (Restaurant Style)
