ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon oil, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 inch cinnamon piece, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional)), 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 cups onions, 1 inch ginger ginger garlic paste, 5.5 garlic cloves, 2.25 cups red ripe tomatoes, 18 raw cashew nuts, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 cup water, 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, 1 tablespoon jaggery powder, 1.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup heavy fresh cream, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 grams boiled potato, 2 grams paneer, 1 tablespoon cashews, 1 tablespoon raisins, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1.5 green chilies, 2.5 tablespoons corn starch, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 2 cups oil to deep fry
instructions: 1. Heat ghee in a pan and fry cardamoms & all the optional spices - cumin seeds, bay leaf, cinnamon & cloves for a minute. Add onions & saute for 6 to 8 mins until light golden. 2. Stir in ginger garlic and saute for 1 to 2 mins. Add tomatoes, cashews and salt. Saute or Cover and cook until mushy, for 8 mins. 3. Stir in chili powder, coriander powder and garam masala. Saute on a medium high heat for 3 mins, until aromatic. 4. Turn off and cool down slightly. Pour 1 cup cold water to a blender. Discard the bay leaf and transfer the cooked onion tomatoes to the blender. Blend to a smooth puree. 5. Melt butter in a pan and stir in the optional red chili powder for a brighter color. 6. Strain the onion tomato puree directly to the pan and mix well. Pour another ½ cup water (or omit if not using cashews). 7. Bring to a gentle boil and simmer covered for 10 mins, until thick and creamy. (Keep stirring every 3 to 4 mins). 8. Taste test to adjust salt & garam masala. Crush kasuri methi in your palms and add it. Also stir in the jaggery and cream. Cover and set aside to keep it warm. 9. While you make the gravy, boil potato just until fork tender or pressure cook for 8 to 10 mins, placed on a trivet. Cool down completely. Pat dry paneer with kitchen papers. 10. Finely grate boiled potato and paneer (separately) to a mixing bowl. (Weigh or measure). 11. Stir in chopped cashews, raisins, ginger paste, green chilies, corn starch, salt, garam masala and chopped coriander leaves. 12. Mix well for 2 to 3 mins to form a non-sticky smooth dough. Divide to 12 to 13 portions. Roll to smooth balls in between your palms. If you want roll the koftas in 1 teaspoon flour or corn starch, roll again and dust off the excess. 13. Deep frying: Heat oil in a deep pan/kadai for deep frying. Test the oil by dropping a large pinch of the kofta mixture. It should sizzle and rise to the surface, shouldn’t disintegrate or too brown quickly. 14. Testing: Deep fry one kofta to test the texture and if it does not disintegrate, go ahead to fry all of them. If it disintegrates, add more flour or starch and mix. 15. Slide the koftas one by one gently to the hot oil. Do not disturb for 2 mins. Deep fry on a medium flame until crisp and golden. Remove them to a steel colander or a cooling rack. 16. Cool down the gravy to a warm temperature and transfer it to a serving dish. Cool the koftas slightly and place in the malai gravy. Garnish with heavy cream and coriander leaves. 17. Serve Malai kofta with Tandoori roti, Butter Naan, Plain Kulcha, Jeera rice or plain basmati rice.

title: malai kofta recipe | malai kofta curry | creamy kofta balls curry
ingredients: 3 potato aloo, 0.75 cup paneer cottage cheese, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp raisins, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 2 tbsp maida plain flour, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 tomato, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 1 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 2 pod cardamom, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 clove 2 clove, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp salt, 0.25 cup cream malai, 0.5 cup water, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 3 potato and ¾ cup paneer. 2. also add 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, ¼ tsp cumin powder and ½ tsp salt. 3. add 2 tbsp raisins and 2 tbsp cashew to have crunchy bite in kofta. 4. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined 5. now add 2 tbsp maida and mix well forming a soft dough. maida helps to absorb moisture and bind the mixture well. 6. prepare a small ball sized kofta by greasing hand with oil. 7. deep fry on medium hot oil. 8. stir occasionally, making sure the koftas are cooked uniformly. 9. fry until the kofta turn golden brown and crisp. 10. drain off the koftas and keep aside. 11. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 12. saute until onions changes colour slightly. 13. further add 2 tomato and saute slightly. 14. now add 2 tbsp cashew and continue to saute until tomatoes soften completely. 15. cool completely and transfer to a blender. 16. blend to smooth paste adding water if required. 17. now filter the mixture to get rid of skin and seeds. 18. filter until silky smooth onion-tomato puree is attained. keep aside. 19. in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp butter and 2 tbsp oil. 20. saute 1 tsp cumin, 2 pod cardamom, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 clove until it turns aromatic. 21. further keeping the flame on low, add 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp coriander powder and ¼ tsp cumin powder. 22. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 23. further add in the prepared onion tomato puree, 1 tsp salt and mix well. 24. cover and cook until the mixture starts to thicken and oil separates from sides. 25. now add ¼ cup cream and mix on low flame until it's well combined. 26. further, add ½ cup water and mix well adjusting consistency as required. 27. get the curry to a boil, add 1 tsp kasuri methi and ¼ tsp garam masala. mix well. 28. finally, pour the curry over kofta and malai kofta is ready to enjoy.

title: Malai Paneer (White Curry)
ingredients: 25 grams paneer, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 cup Greek yogurt, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon oil, 2.5 green cardamoms, 2 cups yellow onions, 0.75 inch ginger, 3.5 garlic, 0.25 cup cashews, 0.5 cup cold water, 1 black cardamom, 2 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional)), 2 strands mace, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon ghee, 1.5 green chili, 1.5 cups water, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.875 teaspoon Garam Masala, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.1875 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 cup heavy cream
instructions: 1. Soak cashews in ¼ cup hot water and keep aside until needed. 2. In a bowl, mix together Greek yogurt, salt, black pepper, ginger garlic paste, kasuri methi and garam masala, Coat the paneer well with the marinade and rest aside. (or up to 24 hours in the refrigerator) 3. Heat oil in a pan and add the green cardamoms, sliced onions, ginger and garlic. Sprinkle salt and saute for about 7 to 8 mins. Pour ½ cup water and cook covered for 5 to 6 mins. 4. Cool down and add to a blender. Also add the soaked cashews and cold water or milk. Blend to a super smooth puree. (If your blender can't make a fine puree, strain it.) 5. On a low flame, heat half of the ghee in the same pan. Add black cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, mace and green chili, wait until they begin to sizzle. 6. Transfer the onion cashew puree along with water. Bring it to a gentle boil on a medium heat. Cover partially and cook on a low heat, for 12 to 13 minutes, until the raw flavor of onions is completely gone. (Keep stirring every 5 mins.) 7. Meanwhile, heat a non-stick pan with rest of the ghee. Place the paneer pieces and fry them until golden. Turn them to the other sides halfway. Fry until most of the yogurt is cooked through and paneer is golden. (takes about 5 to 6 mins) 8. When the sauce is medium thick, stir in the garam masala, nutmeg, salt, kasuri methi and black pepper. Cook for 2 to 3 mins, taste test to adjust salt, garam masala and pepper. Turn off the heat. 9. Mix in the heavy cream and add the fried paneer. (If you use milk to blend, start with only 2 tbsps and adjust) 10. Garnish with black pepper if you want. Serve Malai paneer with Tandoori roti, Naan, Yellow rice or Saffron rice. If you want you may squeeze some lemon juice before serving.

title: Malpua Recipe (Crisp & Fluffy)
ingredients: 1.25 cup all-purpose flour, 0.5 teaspoon elaichi, 1 teaspoon saunf, 1.5 cup milk, 3.5 tablespoons Ghee, 3 tablespoons milk powder, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 cup water, 2 teaspoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Pour milk to a mixing bowl. If using mawa or milk powder, add it and mix well. 2. Add flour, fennel seeds and elaichi powder. 3. Make a batter mixing everything well. 4. Use more milk as needed to make a batter that is not too thick or too runny. 5. But should be of flowing consistency. By adjusting the consistency of batter, you can make thick or thin malpuas as per your liking. 6. If the batter is too thin, the batter spreads a lot and makes thin ones. Very thick batter makes thick & dense malpuas. 7. If you prefer fluffy malpuas, beat the batter for a few minutes with a fork or whisk. 8. Keep the batter aside until the sugar syrup is ready. 9. If you have time, you can set this aside for 4 hours. 10. Add sugar and water to a pot. 11. Dissolve the sugar on a medium flame. 12. Boil the sugar syrup until it turns sticky or reaches a 1 string consistency. 13. To prevent crystallization in sugar syrup: Add 2 tsps lemon juice to the syrup or In another large pot, bring 3 cups of water to a boil. Turn off the stove. Place the sugar syrup pot in the hot water. 14. Heat ghee for frying malpuas. 15. When the ghee is hot, pour 2 to 3 tbsps of batter. 16. The batter spreads depending on the consistency. 17. You can add more milk at this stage to thin down the batter to make thin malpuas. 18. Allow them to fry until golden. Flip and fry on the other side as well. 19. Remove them from the pan and add directly to the syrup. 20. Give a quick stir to the sugar syrup before adding the malpuas. 21. If the sugar crystallizes then add 2 tbsp water and heat it. 22. You can allow them to soak in the syrup for 10 mins if you have made thick malpuas or remove to a plate just after dipping them. 23. Garnish malpua with chopped pistas. 24. You can also top these with rabdi and then garnish with pistas.

title: Mamidikaya Pappu | Mango Dal
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 1 cup raw unripe green mango, 2.5 cups water, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 3 green chilies, 2 tablespoon ghee, 1 clove garlic, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 pinch asafoetida, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 broken dried red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.3333333333333333 cup mango
instructions: 1. Add dal to a pressure cooker and wash it few times. Drain off the water. 2. Pour 2 ½ cups of fresh water to the pressure cooker. 3. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon salt over the green mango. Scrub it very well and rinse off. Chop it to pieces, discard the seed/kernel/stone. You don't need to peel the mango as the actual flavor comes from the mango skin. 4. Add the mango pieces to the cooker along with turmeric & 2 green chilies (reserve the rest for later). 5. Pressure cook for 2 to 4 whistles if cooking directly in the cooker. or 3 whistles if cooking in a bowl placed inside the cooker. 6. When the pressure drops, open the lid. Mango dal must be soft cooked. 7. Add salt and mash lightly. If the consistency is too thick you may pour half cup boiling water and mix to bring to consistency you like. Taste test and adjust the salt. 8. Heat ghee or oil in a small tadka pan. Reduce the heat and add mustard, cumin, dried chilies, garlic, ginger and green chilies (optional, use for more heat). 9. When the ginger turns aromatic, add curry leaves, hing and more turmeric (if required for color). [If you like chunky pieces of mango in your dal, add ⅓ cup bite sized mango pieces to the tempering and pour few tablespoons of water. Cook until soft.] 10. Pour this tempering to the mamidikaya pappu and stir well. Keep it covered for a while so the flavors are infused. 11. Serve mango dal with rice or chapathi. 12. Press saute button and pour oil to the steel insert. Once hot, add mustard, cumin, 2 green chilies, chopped ginger, garlic and curry leaves. 13. When the ginger becomes aromatic, press cancel. Add hing, turmeric, dal, mangoes and 3 cups water. Mix well. 14. You can also place your rice bowl over a trivet. Secure the IP with the lid and position the steam release vent to sealing. 15. Pressure cook for 11 minutes. When the pressure drops open the lid and add salt. Mix well and pour ¼ cup boiling hot water if required to adjust the consistency. 16. If your dal is not spicy enough, you may fry fine chopped green chilies in 1 teaspoon ghee and add it to the mamidikaya pappu.

title: Manchow Soup Recipe (Chinese Soup)
ingredients: 1.25 tablespoons oil, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chilli, 0.25 cup onions, 2.5 stalks spring onions, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrot, 0.875 cup cabbage, 2.5 tablespoons French beans, 2.5 tablespoons bell peppers, 3.5 mushrooms, 1.5 tablespoons coriander stems, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons soya sauce, 0.5 teaspoon pepper, 1.5 tablespoon cornstarch, 2 teaspoons rice vinegar, 15 grams noodles, 1 cup oil, 2.25 tablespoons corn starch
instructions: 1. Pour oil to a hot pan. When the oil turns hot, regulate the flame to high and add fine chopped garlic, ginger, chilli and coriander stems. 2. Saute for 30 seconds and add spring onion whites and onions. Fry until onions turn golden. 3. Add shredded cabbage, chopped beans, bell peppers, mushrooms and carrots. Fry stirring constantly for 2 minutes until you smell the veggies aromatic. 4. Pour 4 cups of water or broth and bring it to a rolling boil. 5. Meanwhile stir together 1½ tablespoon cornstarch with 3 to 4 tbsps of water to make a smooth slurry. Reduce the heat slightly and keep stirring with a ladle and slowly incorporate the corn slurry into the boiling manchow soup. 6. Cook until the soup turns slightly thick. Add coriander leaves and spring onions. 7. Pour soya sauce and add ½ teaspoon salt and crushed pepper. Stir and taste test. Adjust the quantity of soya sauce, salt and pepper to your taste. 8. Turn off when the manchow soup thickens, do not overcook we want the veggies crunchy. Keep this covered until served. Stir in the vinegar when you are ready to serve. 9. Boil the noodles following the instructions on your pack of noodles. Make sure you cook them al dente and not mushy. 10. Once cooked, drain to a colander and rinse them under running water to get rid of the starch. 11. You can also skip all of the following steps and serve the soup with boiled noodles. To fry them proceed further. Shake off the colander to remove all the excess water and spread the noodles on a tray. Let them air dry for 30 minutes until completely dry. 12. Sprinkle 2 to 2½ tablespoons cornstarch all over the noodles and mix gently to coat the noodles with the flour. 13. Heat 1 cup oil in a frying pan until hot enough. When the oil is hot, drop some noodles to to the oil and fry until they turn crisp on the base. 14. Turn them to the other side and fry again until crisp and light golden. Remove them from oil when they are crisp. Continue to fry all of the noodles in 4 to 5 batches ensuring the oil is hot enough before every batch. 15. Ladle the soup to serving bowls. Top with crispy noodles and green onions. If you prefer you may add some chilli vinegar or any hot sauce.

title: manchurian fried rice | veg manchurian rice recipe | fried rice with manchurian balls
ingredients: 2 cup cabbage, 1 carrot, 3 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tbsp chilli sauce, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup maida, 0.25 cup corn flour, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 1 inch ginger, 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 1 carrot, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 0.5 tbsp chilli sauce, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup corn flour slurry, 0.25 cup cabbage, 3 cup cooked rice, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 2 tbsp spring onion
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup cabbage, 1 carrot, 3 tbsp spring onion, ½ onion, ½ tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tbsp chilli sauce and ½ tsp salt. 2. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 3. now add ¼ cup maida and ¼ cup corn flour. 4. combine well forming a soft dough. if the dough is loose, then add a tbsp of maida and form a dough. 5. grease hands with oil and prepare a small ball sized balls. 6. deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 7. fry until the ball turns golden brown and crisp. drain off the manchurian balls over kitchen paper and keep aside. 8. in a large wok take 2 tbsp oil, 1 inch ginger, 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp spring onion. 9. stir fry on high flame without burning. 10. now add ½ onion, ½ capsicum and 1 carrot. 11. stir fry on high flame without overcooking the vegetables. 12. now add 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, ½ tbsp chilli sauce and ½ tsp salt. 13. stir fry until everything is well combined. 14. add ¼ cup corn flour slurry. to prepare corn flour slurry, mix 1 tsp corn flour in ¼ cup water. 15. cook until the sauce turns glossy. 16. now add prepared manchurian balls and ¼ cup cabbage. 17. stir fry until the balls are well coated with sauce. 18. further, add 3 cup cooked rice and ¼ tsp pepper powder. 19. stir fry without breaking the rice grains. 20. finally, add 2 tbsp spring onion and enjoy veg manchurian fried rice.

title: manchurian gravy recipe | veg manchurian gravy | vegetable manchurian gravy
ingredients: 1 carrot, 5 tbsp cabbage, 5 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp maida plain flour, 2 tbsp cornflour, oil, 2 tbsp oil, 1 inch ginger, 3 clove garlic, 1 chilli, 4 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 1.25 cup water, 2 tsp cornflour, 0.25 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 carrot, 5 tbsp cabbage, 2 tbsp spring onion, ½ onion and ½ tsp ginger garlic paste. 2. also add 1 tsp chilli sauce, ½ tsp salt. 3. mix well making sure everything is combined well, 4. further, add 2 tbsp maida, 2 tbsp cornflour and mix well. 5. form a soft dough adding maida if required. 6. now prepare a small ball sized balls. 7. deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 8. stir occasionally, until the balls turn golden brown. 9. drain off the manchurian balls and keep aside. 10. now in a large wok, heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 1 inch ginger, 3 clove garlic, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp spring onion. 11. add ½ onion and stir fry on high flame. 12. further, add ½ capsicum and stir fry until it shrinks slightly. 13. add 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, ½ tsp pepper powder and ¼ tsp salt. 14. stir fry making sure all the sauces are well combined. 15. add in 1¼ cup water and boil on medium flame. 16. in a small bowl take 2 tsp cornflour and ¼ cup water. 17. mix well forming a lump-free slurry. 18. pour in cornflour slurry and mix well. 19. continue to boil until the gravy thickens and turns glossy. 20. add in prepared veg manchurian balls, 2 tbsp spring onion and mix well. 21. finally, serve veg manchurian gravy with fried rice.

title: Mangalore Bajji Recipe (Goli Baje)
ingredients: 0.75 cup all-purpose flour, 1 tablespoon rice flour, 6 tablespoons curd, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1.5 green chilies, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves chopped finely, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 tablespoons coconut, 0.25 teaspoon soda, oil, 3.5 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Mix together flour, rice flour, soda & salt in a mixing bowl. Add ginger, chilies, curry leaves, coconut, coriander leaves, curd and mix well. Pour water in small quantities only as needed and make a thick batter. 2. It should be thick, sticky & of dropping consistency. Cover and rest for at least 2 hours. 3. Beat the batter very well with your hand in a circular motion for 30 seconds to aerate it. Do not over beat. 4. It should be of dropping consistency and not pouring or flowing consistency. If it is not, then add some flour and mix. I did not add any. 5. On a medium flame, heat oil in a pot or kadai until hot. Test by dropping a small portion of batter to the oil. It should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning brown. If it sinks, it means the oil is not hot enough. 6. Take small portions of batter in your hand, shape them with your fingers and drop to the oil. They will double or triple in size so do not make them too big. 7. You can add as many as possible without overcrowding. Do not touch them for a minute or 2. Later stir them with a spoon/spider and fry on a medium heat until golden and crisp. 8. Remove them to a cooling rack or a steel colander. Repeat and fry in batches till you finish the batter. 9. Serve goli baje hot with coconut chutney or any other chutney.

title: Mango Chutney Recipe
ingredients: 7 grams mangoes, 1 tablespoon oil, 1.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.75 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon ground cumin, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon ground fennel, 1 cup sugar, 0.1875 salt, 0.375 teaspoon pepper, 0.375 cup vinegar, 1 gram cinnamon piece, 4 green cardamoms, 1 star anise
instructions: 1. Rinse mangoes well under running water and peel them. Grate them using a grater or a food processer or dice them to ¼ inch cubes. 2. Heat oil or ghee in a pan and add the optional whole spices - cinnamon, cardamoms & star anise. 3. When they begin to sizzle add the ginger & fry for a minute. Lower the flame and quickly stir the red chilli, cumin powder & fennel powder. 4. Add mangoes, sugar/ jaggery and vinegar. Increase the heat and bring this to a boil. 5. Stir and cook covered on a medium heat until the mangoes turn tender & mushy. Open the lid and cook further until it turns slightly thick yet runny. (check picture in the post). The chutney turns thick upon cooling. 6. Add salt and mix well. Taste test and add more sugar, chili, salt and pepper if you want. If you add any more sugar simply cook further until the sugar dissolves and cooks for a few more mins. 7. If you want you may mash the chutney with a masher. Overcooking at this stage caramelizes the sugar and makes the mango chunks chewy. So avoid that. 8. Cool the mango chutney completely and store it in a clean dry glass jar. 9. Serve as a side with grilled appetizers, sandwiches, bread or with chips.

title: mango chutney recipe | green mango chutney | mango chutney sauce
ingredients: 1 tsp oil, whole kashmiri red chilli, 1 tbsp channa dal, 0.5 tbsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds jeera, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.25 tsp methi fenugreek seeds, 1 cup raw mango, 3 cloves garlic, salt to taste, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard seeds rai, 1 kashmiri red chilli, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi
instructions: 1. firstly, in a heavy bottomed kadai heat oil. 2. add in 5-6 red chilli, 1 tbsp channa dal, ½ tbsp urad dal, ½ tsp cumin seeds, 1 tsp coriander seeds and ¼ tsp methi. 3. roast in low flame for 2 minutes or till the spices turn aromatic. 4. transfer to the blender. 5. also add in 1 cup raw mango, 3 cloves garlic and salt to taste. 6. blend to coarse paste without adding any water. keep aside. 7. now heat oil in the same kadai. 8. once the oil is hot, add in 1 tsp mustard seeds, 1 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. allow to splutter. 9. further add prepared mango chutney. 10. also add turmeric and saute well. 11. saute on low flame for 2 minutes. 12. finally, serve raw mango chutney along with rice, idli or dosa.

title: Mango Curry Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon coconut oil, 2 green chilies, 1 tablespoon ginger, 0.5 tablespoon garlic, 0.1875 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 0.75 tablespoons jaggery, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 2 ripe mangoes, 0.5 cup water, 27 ml coconut milk, 0.5 cup Greek yogurt, 1.5 tablespoon coconut oil, 0.5 teaspoon black mustard seeds, 3 red chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 cup shallots, 0.125 teaspoon methi powder
instructions: 1. Blend ½ cup diced mangoes (use the sides of cut mango) and coconut milk to a smooth puree. (If using grated raw coconut, blend with ¾ cup water & mango) 2. Heat coconut oil and add green chilies, ginger and garlic. Saute for a minute until the ingredients turn aromatic. 3. Stir in the cumin, coriander powder, red chili powder and turmeric. Add the remaining mangoes and pour water. Cook covered on a medium high heat for 2 to 3 mins. 4. Pour the mango coconut milk & bring to a gentle simmer. Cook for 2 to 3 mins and jaggery and salt. Mix and turn off the heat. 5. Add yogurt to a bowl and whisk well until really smooth. Stir in 2 to 3 tablespoons of the mango curry. Add the tempered yogurt back to the pot in 2 to 3 batches. Taste test to adjust salt and sweetness. It should ideally be slightly sweet, tangy and spicy. 6. Heat oil in a small pan and add mustard seeds, red chilies, curry leaves and sliced shallots. Saute until aromatic and the onions are golden to light brown. Turn off and add ground fenugreek if using 7. Pour over the mango curry and cover with a lid to infuse the flavors. Serve with brown or white rice.

title: Mango Dal Recipe | Mamidikaya Pappu | Raw Mango Pappu
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, 3 cup water, 1 raw mango, 6 chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 red chilli, pinch hing, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. Firstly, take 1 cup toor dal, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, and 3 cups water in a pressure cooker. 2. Pressure cook for 5 whistles or until the dal is cooked well. 3. Mash the dal slightly to get a silky smooth texture. Keep aside. 4. In a large kadai take 1 raw mango, 6 chilli, ¼ tsp turmeric, and 1 cup water. 5. Cover and boil for 10 minutes or until the mangoes are cooked well. 6. Add in pressure-cooked dal, 1 tsp salt, and mix well. 7. Simmer for 10 minutes or until the flavors are well absorbed. Adjust the consistency as required. 8. To prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 dried red chilli, pinch hing, and few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering. 9. Pour the tempering over the dal and mix gently. 10. Finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy Mango Dal Recipe with hot steamed rice.

title: Mango Ice Cream Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups heavy cream, 2 cups mango puree, 0.875 can sweetened condensed milk, 0.5 cup milk powder
instructions: 1. Chill the bowl, beater, condensed milk and heavy cream overnight. Freeze a metal tray for atleast a few hours. 2. Peel and chop 2 to 4 mangoes. Puree them in a blender until smooth. Measure 2 cups of puree and pour to a large mixing bowl. (refer notes for tips) 3. Add milk powder and mix well. Pour chilled condensed milk and mix until combined. [Start with a lower amount of condensed milk, about ¾] Refrigerate this. 4. Pour whipping cream to the chilled mixing bowl and begin to whip on a medium speed until you see soft peaks. You can use a electric hand mixer or a stand mixer. 5. It takes anywhere from 6 to 8 mins depending on the temperature. Mine was done in 9 mins. Make sure you don't overwhip. 6. Add a ½ to ¾ cup of whipped cream to the mango condensed milk mixture. Gently fold just until combined. Keep adding more whipped cream to the mango mixture & combine. Repeat this mixing in batches until both are combined and you see no streaks of cream. 7. Pour this to the freezed metal tray and press down a parchment paper. Cover & refrigerate the mixing bowl for later use. Freeze it for 4 hours. 8. After 4 hours, add it back to the cold mixing bowl (from the refrigerator) and whisk the mango ice cream until really smooth for 2 mins or so. (do not overdo). Once it is smooth, pour it back to the tray, press down the parchment paper and freeze just until set, for 8 hours. 9. When you are ready to serve, rest the ice cream on the counter for 5 mins. Scoop mango ice cream and enjoy it! It is good to finish the tray of ice cream or level the leftovers with a spoon and press down the parchment paper. 10. Freeze a metal tray overnight to freeze the ice cream. Add 1½ cup chopped mangoes and ½ can of condensed milk to a blender and blend until smooth. Make sure there are no chunks. Sprinkle ¼ cup milk powder and gently incorporate it into the mango puree until smooth. Chill this in the fridge for 1 to 2 hours until really cold. 11. Transfer the mango puree to a mixing bowl. Pour chilled whipping cream to the blender jar. Using a pulse option whip the cream until soft peaks (thick and light). Do not overdo. 12. Mix together cream and the chilled mango puree. Transfer to a freezed metal tray. Press down a parchement paper or cling wrap over the ice cream. 13. Freeze it for 4 hours. After 4 hours, add it to the blender and pulse it until smooth. Pour back to the tray and place the parchment paper or wrap. Freeze until set, usually overnight. 14. Scoop the mango ice cream. To soften, simply leave it at room temperature for 5 mins, then scoop it.

title: mango jelly recipe | mango halwa recipe | mango coconut jelly
ingredients: 35 grams mango, 0.5 cup corn flour, 1 cup water, 0.75 cup sugar, 1 cup water, 6 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 0.25 cup desiccated coconut
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 350 grams mango and blend to smooth puree. you can also use store brought mango pulp. 2. transfer the mango pulp to a large bowl. 3. add ½ cup cornflour and 1 cup water. 4. whisk and mix well making sure everything is well combined without any lumps. keep aside. 5. in a large kadai take ¾ cup sugar and 1 cup water. 6. stir and dissolve the sugar completely. 7. now add in the prepared mango cornflour mixture and stir continuously. 8. stir on low to medium flame until the mixture starts to thicken slightly. 9. add a tbsp of ghee and continue to stir until the mixture absorbs all the ghee. 10. further, add a tbsp more ghee and starts to mix continuously in batches. 11. after 30 minutes the mixture will start to turn glossy absorbing almost 4 tbsp of ghee. 12. now add 2 tbsp more ghee and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. 13. mix well until the ghee starts to release from sides. it takes almost 40 minutes to cook completely. 14. now transfer the halwa mixture to a greased pan and level it up. 15. rest for 2 hours or until it cools completely. 16. unmould the jelly and cut into desired shapes. 17. also roll into desiccated coconut for extra flavour. 18. finally, enjoy mango jelly or mango halwa for a week.

title: Mango Juice Recipe
ingredients: 3 medium mango, 3 tablespoons sugar, 1.5 to 2 cups chilled water, 5.5 mint leaves, 1 inch ginger, 1 lemon, 2 teaspoons chia seeds
instructions: 1. Choose fully ripe sweet mangoes. I have used alphonso mangoes here. You can also use semi-ripe mangoes but they will be tart so you will need to add more sweetener. Rinse them well under running water. 2. Peel the mangoes and chop them. The other way is to just halve them. 3. Cut the inner flesh into cubes with a knife, without cutting through the skin of the mango. 4. Then use a slightly large spoon to detach the flesh from the skin and scoop out the cubes. 5. Add them to a blender jar along with 2 to 3 tbsps sugar or honey. If you want you can also add ginger and mint leaves. I have not added here. 6. Add some ice cubes or cold water or orange juice or coconut water. I used about 1 cup orange juice here. 7. Blend until smooth. For a thinner juice like consistency you may need to add more liquid. I added another half cup water at this stage. 8. I had some soaked sabja seeds, added them here to the serving glasses. If you want to know more about these seeds you may check this sabja seeds post . 9. Pour mango juice to serving glasses. This is the consistency we want, pouring consistency yet thick. If you want you can add more ice cubes but the flavors will be diluted.

title: Mango Kesari (Mango Halwa, Mango Sheera)
ingredients: 1.5 cups mango, 0.75 cup sugar, 1 pinch kesar best quality, 4 green cardamoms, 1 cup rava, 1.5 cups water, 4 tablespoons Ghee
instructions: 1. Add mango cubes, cardamom powder, saffron and sugar to a blender and blend to a smooth puree. Set this aside for 15 minutes to lend a good color naturally to the kesari. 2. Dry roast semolina on a medium to low flame till you get an aroma of the rava. Do not brown the rava but it has to turn crunchy and aromatic. Transfer this to a bowl. 3. To the same pan add 3 tablespoons ghee and heat it. Fry cashews until light golden. Then add raisins and fry till they swell. Transfer the cashews and raisins to a small bowl. set aside. 4. To the same pan, add 1½ cups of water. Bring it to a rapid boil. 5. Reduce the flame to low. Add rava in a stream slowly and keep stirring with another hand. Be quick in stirring and slow in adding rawa to prevent the formation of lumps. 6. Break any lumps and stir it gently. Cook covered on a lowest heat till all the water evaporates. 7. Add 1 tbsp. Ghee and mix well . 8. Add the blended mango sugar puree. Mix gently. Cook for about 7 to 10 minutes stirring occasionally to prevent burning. 9. Garnish mango kesari with nuts and serve.

title: Mango Kulfi Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups ripe mango, 0.5 tin sweetened condensed milk, 1 cup whipping cream, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 Pinch saffron, 2 tablespoons pistachios
instructions: 1. If using saffron soak in 2 tablespoons hot water and keep aside. Wash and peel mango. Chop to cubes and add to a blender jar. 2. Add chilled cream, saffron, condensed milk and cardamom powder to the jar. 3. Blend on a medium speed until smooth. Stir in the chopped pistachios, reserving some for garnish. 4. Transfer to kulfi moulds or steel cups or Popsicle moulds. Cover, insert sticks in the center and freeze them overnight or until set. 5. Rub the moulds in between your palms or dip them in a bowl of water. You can also keep the base under running water. 6. Gently loosen and remove mango kulfi. Garnish with crushed pista. Serve immediately.

title: Mango Lassi Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 1.5 cup Mangoes chopped, 0.75 curd cold plain yogurt, 0.75 cup cold milk, 1.5 to 3 tablespoons sugar, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon nuts, 1 pinch saffron strands, 4 tablespoons whipped cream
instructions: 1. You need all the ingredients chilled including mango or you have to use ice while blending to keep the lassi cold. I generally rinse, peel, chop and chill or freeze the mangoes until really cold. Chill the milk as well. If you don’t consume milk with fruits, simply use nut milk or yogurt whey. You will need 2. 1½ cups (280 grams) chopped mangoes or 1 cup (240 grams) canned sweetened mango pulp 3. 1 pinch of saffron & 1 tbsp chopped nuts like pistachios or almonds to garnish – optional 4. Add mangoes, sugar, cardamom powder and milk (or water or ice cubes) to a blender jar. If you are not using a high speed blender, add the yogurt later. 5. Blend until very smooth. Make sure there are no mango fibers left. 6. Add the yogurt. 7. Blend just until smooth, avoid over blending as it can sometimes become slimy. 8. Taste test & adjust the ingredients. If you like it sweeter or prefer more mango flavor, simply add more mangoes. To cut down the sour taste either add milk or cream. If you want it to be slightly runny, pour more milk or ice cubes and blend. It should be thick, yet of pouring and drinking consistency. 9. Pour mango lassi to serving glasses and garnish with chopped pistas or almonds. If you want it colder, chill it for an hour before serving.

title: Mango Lassi Recipe | Aam Ki Lassi Drink
ingredients: 2 cup curd, 1.5 cup mango pulp, 2 tbsp sugar, 2 tbsp kesar water, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 0.5 cup ice cubes
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a blend jar take 2 cup curd, 1½ cup mango pulp and 2 tbsp sugar. 2. Also add 2 tbsp kesar water, ½ tsp cardamom powder and ½ cup ice cubes. 3. Blend together to have a smooth consistency. 4. Finally, pour the Mango Lassi into a glass and top it with pistachios.

title: Mango Mastani Recipe | Mastani Drink Recipe | Mastani Cold Drink
ingredients: 2 mango, 2 scoops vanilla ice cream, 1 cup milk, mango, nuts, tutti frutti, vanilla ice cream
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 2 mango. make sure to use fresh and sweet mangoes, else you may have to add sugar for sweetness. 2. add 2 scoops vanilla ice cream and 1 cup milk. 3. blend into a thick milkshake. keep aside. 4. for assembling, in a tall glass take 2 tbsp mango cubes. 5. add 2 tbsp chopped nuts and 2 tbsp tutti frutti. 6. also, add 1 scoop vanilla ice cream. you can alternatively use mango ice cream. 7. pour in the prepared mango milkshake. 8. top with 1 scoop vanilla ice cream, nuts, and tutti frutti. 9. finally, enjoy mango mastani immediately.

title: Mango Milkshake Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 to 2 cups mangoes, 1.25 cup chilled milk, 2 tablespoons sugar, 1.5 teaspoon vanilla extract, 2 tablespoons sliced nuts, 25 cashews
instructions: 1. Rinse and chop the mangoes. To make chilled mango shake without ice cream I always chill the chopped mangoes overnight. 2. Optional – You can skip adding nuts but they add creaminess to the shake. I suggest adding them if you are not using ice cream. I soaked cashews for 30 minutes in water, drained & added them here. If you want to use almonds soak them for at least 6 hours and peel the skin. For every serving of the shake you can add about 10 almonds or 12 whole cashew nuts. I made 4 servings so you will see more. If you want to skip nuts and use ice cream, add 2 scoops ice cream for each serving. 3. Add sugar or honey or maple syrup or any other you prefer. You can start with 1 tablespoon per serving. Skip sugar if using ice cream. 4. Add the chilled mangoes. Every half cup mango pulp or ¾ cup chopped mangoes will make 1 serving. This again varies depending on the kind of mangoes & the thickness of the shake you want . 5. Pour chilled milk (vegan or dairy) and vanilla extract or cardamom powder. You can also skip the flavoring. Start with half cup milk per serving. You can adjust later after blending. I used coconut milk here. You can also use soy milk and both these won’t alter the taste of flavor of your shake. 6. Blend until super smooth. If you have added nuts, make sure they are blended to super fine texture. 7. Taste test and adjust the ingredients according to your taste. I had to add more mangoes and milk as I made 4 servings. 8. Blend until smooth. 9. Pour mango milkshake to serving glasses and garnish with sliced nuts or any others as mentioned above.

title: mango mousse recipe | how to mango mousse dessert
ingredients: 1.5 cup mango, 1.5 cup whipping cream, 0.25 cup powdered sugar, 1 mango, 0.25 cup mango pulp, 2 tbsp tutti frutti, 6 cherry
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 1½ cup mango and blend to smooth paste. keep aside. 2. in a large bowl take 1½ cup whipping cream and ¼ cup powdered sugar. 3. using a beater, beat on low speed. you can alternatively use whisk. 4. beat until the cream turns stiff peaks. 5. now add in prepared mango puree and mix gently. 6. mix until everything is well combined. keep aside. 7. in a small cups, layer with 2 tbsp chopped mango. 8. next layer it with prepared mango cream. 9. further add a tbsp of mango pulp in centre. 10. refrigerate for 30 minutes or until it sets well. 11. finally, garnish with tutti frutti, cherry and enjoy mango mousse.

title: mango phirni recipe | aam ki phirni | how to make mango firni
ingredients: 2 tbsp basmati rice, water for soaking, 1 litre milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder elachi powder, 2 tbsp saffron milk, 0.5 cup mango pulp, few dry fruits
instructions: 1. firstly, soak 2 tbsp of basmati rice for 30 minutes. 2. discard the water and blend rice to coarse paste. keep aside. 3. now in a large kadai boil 1 litre milk. 4. stir occasionally, and simmer for 10 minutes. 5. keep the flame on low or till milk thickens. 6. now add in prepared coarse rice paste. 7. stir continuously for 5 minutes. else, lumps might form. 8. simmer for another 5 minutes stirring in between. 9. boil the milk till the rice gets cooked completely. 10. further add ¼ cup of sugar and 2 tbsp saffron milk. 11. mix well till the milk thickens and turn creamy. 12. add in cardamom powder and serve chilled garnished with chopped nuts. 13. if looking to prepare mango phirni, allow the phirni to cool completely. 14. now add in ½ cup mango pulp and mix well. add more pulp depending on the sweetness. 15. finally, serve chilled mango phirni garnished with few chopped nuts.

title: Mango Pickle Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 cup mango pieces, 1 tablespoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon methi seeds, 3 tablespoon red chili powder, 1.5 teaspoon salt, 4 garlic cloves, 3 tablespoon oil
instructions: 1. Choose raw, fresh and unripe green mango that is firm and has a clear skin. Wash and dry mango thoroughly. Wipe clean with a cloth. Do this the previous night. 2. If possible sun dry the jar and other utensils used for making pickle, Make sure all the bowls, spoons, chopping board and knife are dry. These precautions will increase the shelf life of the mango pickle. 3. Dry roast methi seeds on a low flame until deep golden and aromatic. Transfer to a plate and cool them completely. 4. To the same pan, pour oil and heat it up until slightly warm. Set aside to cool completely. You can skip warming the oil if you intend to finish the mango pickle within 10 to 12 days. Skip heating if using cold pressed oil. 5. Blend methi seeds along with mustard seeds to a fine powder. Crush garlic. 6. Chop mangoes to slightly large cubes of 1 inch, using a large knife. 7. Optional for shelf-life: Sun dry each separately - spice powders, salt, garlic and chopped mangoes. Alternately you may heat them in oven at 60 to 70 C (140 to 160F) for 1 hour (avoid keeping salt in oven) 8. Add them to a dry bowl, along with red chili powder, mustard powder, salt and lightly crushed garlic. 9. Pour the oil and mix well. The pickle will be dry at this stage. Cover and set aside for 24 to 28 hours in a dry place. 10. After 24 to 28 hours, mango pieces will release juices and the consistency of the pickle will change. Check the step-by-step photos above. Stir mango pickle well and taste test it. If needed add more salt. 11. Store this mango pickle in a clean dry glass jar. Store it in the refrigerator after 24 hours. If storing at room temperature avoid storing in the kitchen or places with high moisture. 12. It will be ready to serve after 3 days but it tastes best as it ages. 13. Use dry clean spoons every time you use. After every use, wipe off the sides of the jar with a clean tissue or dry cloth to avoid mold.

title: mango pudding recipe | mango pudding dessert | mango panna cotta
ingredients: 1 tsp agar-agar gelatin china grass, 2 tbsp water, 2 cups mango puree, 1 cup milk, 1 tsp agar-agar gelatin, 0.5 cup sugar, 1 cup cream, 1 tsp vanilla extract vanilla essence, pinch of salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 1 tsp of agar-agar / gelatin and add 2 tbsp hot water. 2. mix well and dissolve agar-agar completely. 3. further add 2 cups of mango puree. 4. mix well making sure the pulp gets mixed up with agar-agar uniformly. 5. furthermore, place the serving glass in a bowl so that it gets tilted slightly. 6. pour in prepared mango mixture till it reaches the rim. 7. now refrigerate it for 2 hours or overnight, till it sets completely. 8. after 2 hours, make sure to check the mango has set completely. 9. now prepare cream layer by heating up milk. 10. add in 1 tsp agar-agar / gelatin and whisk well. 11. cook the milk on medium-low heat till it steams (do not boil). 12. further add 1 cup cream and whisk well. 13. additionally add ½ cup powdered sugar, 1 tsp vanilla extract and pinch of salt. 14. whisk and dissolve the sugar completely. 15. let the milk cool for 10 minutes before pouring to the glass. 16. pour the milk into glass. 17. refrigerate it for 2 hours or overnight, till it sets completely. 18. finally, garnish with few chopped mangoes and enjoy mango panna cotta or mango pudding.

title: mango rasayana | mavina hannu rasayana | aam ras recipe
ingredients: 2 mangoes mavina hannu, tbsp cup grated jaggary bella, 1.5 cup coconut milk, 2 cardamom elakki elachi, pinch of salt
instructions: 1. peel the mangoes and chop them into small pieces as shown in the picture below. 2. put the chopped mangoes in a bowl and squeeze and mix well with your hands 3. squeeze and extract the mango juice from mangoes. 4. grate the coconut, approximately 1 cup. grind the coconut with 1/4th cup of water. 5. filter out the coconut paste to remove and extract the coconut milk from the grounded coconut. 6. add coconut milk, jaggary, pinch of salt and crushed cardamom and mix them well 7. serve mango rasayana or mavina hannu rasayana or aam ras with ice cubes or serve it with neer dosa, plain dosa, tuppa dosa and pooris.

title: Mango Rice Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups rice, 1 raw unripe green mango, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 3.5 green chilies, 1.5 red chilies, 0.875 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 cup peanuts, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 tablespoon urad dal, 1 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 inch ginger, 3 tablespoons oil, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Cook rice to grainy texture: Add rice to a bowl & rinse it a few times. Pour 4 cups water & place the bowl in a pressure cooker. Cover the bowl & pressure cook for 3 whistles. 2. When the pressure drops, remove the rice & cool completely. 3. Wash, peel and grate or chop the mango. You can also grate in a processor. Update: Lately I have started to make this with cooked mango. I just peel, cut and add them to a bowl. Cover and place it in the pressure cooker over the rice bowl (PIP). Once cooked I mash it and use as mentioned below. 4. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan and fry the peanuts on a medium heat until aromatic and golden. Remove them to a plate for later. 5. Pour 2 tablespoons more oil and heat it. Add chana dal, urad dal, mustard seeds and dried red chilli. 6. When the lentils turn light golden, add ginger, green chilies & curry leaves. Fry till the curry leaves become crisp, then add hing. 7. Add mango, salt and turmeric. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes. Cook covered until the mango turns mushy, completely soft & pulpy. (skip this with cooked mango.) 8. Add this to the cooked rice little by little and begin to mix. Taste test and add more mango mixture as required. Adjust salt and oil at this stage. 9. Transfer mango rice to serving plates and garnish with roasted peanuts.

title: mango rice recipe | mavinakayi chitranna | mamidikaya pulihora
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, pinch of hing asafoetida, 1 tsp chana dal, few curry leaves, 3 tbsp peanuts groundnut, 1 cup raw mango, 2 green chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 2 cup cooked rice, salt to taste, 2 tbsp coconut, 2 tbsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat oil. 2. add in 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal and 1 tsp chana dal, pinch of hing and few curry leaves. 3. allow to splutter. 4. furthermore add in 3 tbsp roasted peanuts and saute for a minute. 5. also add in 1 cup raw mango, 2 green chilli and ¼ tsp turmeric. 6. saute for a minute or till mango shrinks. 7. now add in 2 cup cooked rice and salt to taste. 8. mix gently without breaking rice grains. 9. cover and simmer for 2 minutes or till rice absorbs flavours. 10. furthermore add in 2 tbsp coconut and 2 tbsp coriander leaves. mix well. 11. finally, serve mango rice or mavinakayi chitranna hot or to lunch box.

title: Mango Smoothie Recipe, How to make Mango Smoothie
ingredients: 2.25 cups mangoes, 2 sweet oranges, 1 medium banana, 0.5 cup yogurt, 0.5 cup ice
instructions: 1. To cut around the seed, place the mango flat on a clean cutting board and make sure the stem is at the top, facing away from you. With a sharp knife, carefully cut the mango lengthwise about ¼ inch away from the midline to remove one big slice. Repeat the step for the other side. 2. If desired, use a paring knife to slice off the skin and flesh around the seed and prepare as desired. Now that you have two big mango halves, there’s two ways to remove the flesh. 3. Alternately you may simply rinse and peel mangoes. Chop or slice them. Peel oranges with a peeler and remove most of the pith and deseed them. Alternately you can use orange juice. If you prefer a chilled smoothie, chill your mangoes and oranges in the refrigerator or freeze for an hour. 4. Add the mangoes to a blender. 5. 3, Peel and add the banana along with oranges. You may check my orange juice post to know how I deseed the oranges easily. 6. Pour water/ milk or juice. You can use plant based milk like almond milk or cashew milk. 7. Add chilled yogurt and honey. You may add more yogurt or juice to bring to consistency. Also adjust the sweetener. 8. Blend until smooth. 9. Pour mango smoothie to serving glasses and enjoy!

title: Masala Chai Recipe (Indian Masala Tea)
ingredients: 4 green cardamoms, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((3 for stronger flavor or ⅛ teaspoon ground)), 0.625 inch cinnamon piece, 2 pepper corn, 1.5 cups water, 1 cup full fat milk, 2.5 to 3 teaspoons black tea leaves, 2.5 teaspoons sugar, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon green cardamoms, 0.5 teaspoon cloves, 2.5 to 3 grams cinnamon, 0.25 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper corn, 0.5 teaspoon nutmeg, 2 petals star flower
instructions: 1. Add cloves, cardamom, cinnamon stick & black pepper to a mortar pestle or a spice grinder. Crush or grind them to a powder. 2. Heat water in a pot. Add the ground spices, crushed/chopped ginger and tea. (Start with lower amount of tea and if required you may add more after adding milk) 3. Bring this to a rolling boil and reduce the heat to medium. Boil for 3 mins until the decoction turns dark. 4. Pour milk and add sugar. Bring it to a boil on a medium high heat. (If using tea bags remove them at this stage and keep aside.) 5. Reduce the heat and simmer until the chai (tea) becomes dark, for about 2 mins. 6. When the masala tea is ready, you will see a layer of cream on top and also the milk flavor is gone. 7. Optionally aerate or pull your masala chai for the same Chaiwallah taste. Take a soup ladle and aerate the masala chai or simply pour some chai to a measuring cup. Pour the hot tea in a slow stream, back to the chai pot from a height of 1 to 1½ feet. 8. Repeat this step of pulling hot masala chai from the pot to the cup, a few times (4 to 5 times). This breaks down the layer of cream and incorporates it back to the masala chai making it more tasty and thick. 9. Strain the masala chai to serving cups & serve with any breakfast, snack or biscuits. 10. Clean all the spices and add to a blender jar. Powder finely. Store it in an airtight glass jar. 11. Use about ½ to ¾ tsp or more to make 2 servings of tea. For stronger tea you can use about ¾ tsp.

title: Masala Dosa Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 3 tablespoon chana dal, 1.5 cup raw sona masuri rice, 0.5 cup idli rice, 3 tablespoon poha, 0.5 teaspoon methi seeds, 0.75 teaspoon pink salt, 1.25 cups water to blend dal, 0.625 cups water to blend rice, 2 cups potatoes cubed, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.375 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.875 cup onions, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 green chilies, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, Butter as needed
instructions: 1. Add urad dal, chana dal and methi seeds to a large bowl. Rinse them well a few times and pour lots of fresh water. To another large bowl, add rice and rinse it well few times. Pour lots of fresh water. 2. Soak for 4 to 5 hours. 30 mins before you begin to make the batter, rinse poha twice. Pour water enough to immerse it fully. Soak it for 30 mins. 3. Later drain the water from the dal completely and add to a blender jar along with poha. 4. Pour 1¼ cup water and salt. If you live in a hot weather then skip salt now and add after fermentation. 5. Blend the batter well until thick, frothy and smooth. While you blend ensure your blender doesn't heat up and get overloaded. So give a break in between and blend. 6. Transfer the batter to the bowl in which you soaked the dal. 7. Drain the water from rice completely and add it along with ½ cup water to the blender jar. 8. Blend slightly coarse or smooth to suit your liking. I make a slightly coarse batter of fine semolina texture. If needed pour the rest of the ¼ cup water while blending. I use up full 2 cups water mentioned in the recipe to blend. 9. Pour this rice batter to the dal batter & mix well. You can check the video or step by step photos to know the consistency of batter. 10. I divide the batter to 2 large bowls. You can do the same if you prefer to use it on 2 different days. Cover the bowl. 11. Allow the masala dosa batter to ferment in a warm place for about 6 to 16 hours depending on your weather. The batter has to rise and turn slightly fluffy. Do not over ferment the batter. 12. If you live in a cold place, then preheat the oven to 60 C or 140 F for 10 mins. Keep the batter inside with the oven light ON. Or You can also use your instant pot in the yogurt settings. 13. Boil or steam potatoes until just done. Do not cook them until very mushy. 14. Instant pot: You can pressure cook the potatoes in your IP following these timings - 5 mins if using small potatoes, 6 to 7 mins if using medium ones and 10 mins if using large potatoes. 15. Pressure cooking: If using a pressure cooker, then add peeled and diced potatoes to a bowl and pour water to partially cover them. Place it in the pressure cooker and let it whistle thrice. 16. While the potatoes boil, soak 1 teaspoon chana dal and 1 teaspoon urad dal in little hot water so they soften. After 30 mins drain the water from the dal and keep aside. When the pressure releases, remove the potatoes. 17. Heat a pan with oil. Then add cumin, mustard, chana dal and urad dal. Saute until the dals turns golden. 18. Then add onions, curry leaves and chilies. Fry until the onions turn lightly golden. 19. Add hing and ginger. Fry until the aroma of ginger comes out. 20. Crumble the potatoes and add them to the pan. Add turmeric and salt along with 2 to 3 tbsps of water. Skip pouring water if your potatoes are too moist or lightly mushy. 21. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. Add coriander leaves. Taste and add more salt if needed. Set this aside. 22. Bring the batter to consistency by adding little water. It must be of thick pouring and spreading consistency. 23. Heat your dosa tawa on a medium heat. If using cast iron pan, when it turns slightly hot, drizzle few drops of oil on it. Insert a fork or a butter knife into a cut onion and rub the oil all over the pan. If using cast iron griddle, you can also season it the previous night. 24. Check if your pan is hot enough by sprinkling little water on it. It is hot enough it will sizzle and the water evaporates. This tip is only for cast iron pan. Do not try this on a non-stick. 25. When the pan is hot enough, stir your batter once and take a ladle full of it. 26. Pour it in the center of the hot tawa. Immediately begin to spread it in a circular motion starting from the center, keep moving your hand in clockwise direction, making circles until you reach the edges of the pan. Refer video or check images below. 27. You can make thick or thin dosas as per your desire. If you are unable to spread the batter, it means the batter is too thick or the pan is too hot. Check the troubleshooting tips above. 28. Pour about half tsp oil or spread ¼ tsp soft butter around the edges & a little here and there. 29. Cook the masla dosa on a medium heat. Within few minutes, you can see the edges leave the pan. Flip and cook just for 1 minute. 30. Flip it back and cook until the base turns golden and crisp. If making mysore masala dosa, smear the red chutney (recipe below) as desired. Place the curry in one half of the dosa. Fold it or roll it. 31. To make the next dosa, if using cast iron pan repeat the process of rubbing with cut onion. Make sure the tawa is hot enough and not over hot otherwise it will burn the dosa. Do not cook the dosa on low heat as it makes the dosa hard. 32. Serve crispy masala dosa hot with coconut chutney and tiffin sambar. 33. Roast 2 tbsps chana dal until golden along with 6 to 8 dried red chilies. Chilies have to turn crisp. 34. Saute 1 large onions lightly in 1 tsp oil until the raw smell goes off. Add 2 pinches of cumin. 35. Cool everything. Powder the dal and chilies first. 36. Add half tsp tamarind (optional), 2 garlic cloves and salt. Blend till smooth. You will have a thick chutney. If the chutney turns very thick, add 1 to 2 tbsps of water and blend again.

title: Masala Khichdi Recipe | Vegetable Khichdi | Moong Dal Masala Khichdi
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 0.5 cup toor dal, 0.5 cup moong dal, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeirc, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tomato, 3 tbsp peas, 1 carrot, 0.5 potato, 5 beans, 2 tsp salt, 4 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 red chilli, pinch chilli powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl soak 1 cup rice, ½ cup toor dal, and ½ cup moong dal for 10 minutes. 2. In a cooker, take 2 tbsp ghee. Add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, and 1 tsp kasuri methi. Saute until the spices turn aromatic. 3. Further add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute till they are cooked well. 4. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, and ½ tsp coriander powder. Saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. Also, add 1 tomato and cook well. 6. Add 3 tbsp peas, 1 carrot, ½ potatoes, 5 beans, and 1 tsp. Saute for 1 minute until the vegetables turn crunchy. 7. Add in soaked rice and dal. Saute for 1 minute until the rice turns aromatic. 8. Pour in 4 cup water, 1 tsp salt, and mix well. 9. Cover and pressure cook for 3 whistles or until the rice and dal are cooked well. 10. Add 1 cup water, and mix well adjusting the consistency as required. 11. To prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 dried red chilli, broken and pinch chilli powder. 12. Splutter the temperign and pour over the khichidi. 13. Also, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 14. Finally, enjoy Masala Khichdi Recipe with raita.

title: masala kulcha recipe | aloo paneer kulcha | stuffed kulcha recipe
ingredients: 2 cup maida plain flour, 1 tsp sugar, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tsp oil, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 0.75 cup water, 2 cup potato aloo, 0.75 cup paneer cottage cheese, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tsp black sesame seeds til, 3 tbsp coriander leaves finely chopped, 2 tbsp butter
instructions: 1. pinch a ball sized dough and roll to 5-inch 2. place a ball sized prepared aloo paneer stuffing in the centre. 3. bring the edge to centre and start pleating. 4. also join the pleats together and secure tight. 5. reverse the ball and sprinkle some black sesame seeds and coriander leaves. 6. flip it over, making sure the coriander leaves and sesame seeds facing down. 7. gently roll to oval shape using a rolling pin. 8. brush with water over kulcha. 9. furthermore, slowly flip the kulcha and put it on hot tawa. 10. gently press the kulcha. 11. now after a minute, flip the tawa upside down and cook kulcha directly on flame till they turn golden brown and puffs up. 12. gently scrape the kulcha from the bottom and remove. 13. also brush some butter 14. finally, serve masala kulcha or aloo paneer kulcha hot with your favourite curry like paneer butter masala.

title: Masala Milk Recipe | Doodh Masala | Masala Milk Powder
ingredients: 0.25 cup almonds, 0.25 cup cashew, 2 tbsp pistachios, 3 pods cardamom, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp ginger powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp saffron
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large heavy-bottomed pan take ¼ cup almonds, ¼ cup cashew and 2 tbsp pistachios. 2. Roast on low flame until the nuts turn golden and crunchy. 3. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 4. Also add 3 pods cardamom, ¼ tsp pepper powder, ¼ tsp ginger powder, ¼ tsp turmeric. 5. Pulse and grind for 10 times or until the nuts turn into coarse powder. 6. Now add ½ tsp saffron and give a good mix. 7. Transfer the mixture into airtight container, and you can store the Masala Milk mixture for upto 2 months in refrigerator. 8. To prepare the masala doodh, in saucepan take 3 cup milk and warm on medium flame. 9. Add 3 tbsp of prepared masala milk mixture and mix well. 10. Continue to mix and boil on medium flame preventing from milk from sticking to the bottom. 11. Simmer for 5 minutes or until the milk thickens slightly. 12. Now add 2 tbsp sugar and boil for 1 minute. 13. Pour the Masala Milk into a glass and enjoy piping hot, or you can chill it and serve chilled with ice cubes.

title: masala mirchi bajji recipe | masala mirapakaya bajji | onion masala bajji
ingredients: 6 chilli, 2 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 0.75 cup water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, oil, 1 onion, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp sev
instructions: 1. firstly, wipe the chilli clean and slit in centre. 2. remove seeds to create some space to fill onion masala at the end. 3. now in a large bowl take 2 cup besan, ¼ cup rice flour. 4. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp ajwain and ½ tsp salt. mix well. 5. now add ½ cup water and mix well. 6. add water as required and prepare a smooth thick batter. 7. further add ¼ tsp baking soda and mix well. 8. now dip the chilli in besan batter and drop in hot oil. 9. deep fry on medium flame stirring occainnally. 10. fry until the bajji turns golden brown and crisp. 11. drain off over the kitchen paper to remove excess oil from mirapakaya bajji. 12. in a small bowl take 1 onion, 2 tbsp coriander, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp salt and 1 tsp lemon juice. 13. mix well making sure everyhting is well combined. 14. now open the mirchi bajji and stuff the onion masala. 15. finally, garnish with 2 tbsp sev and enjoy masala mirchi bajji with chai.

title: masala noodles recipe | mumbai street style vegetable masala noodles
ingredients: 2 litre water, 1 tsp oil, 1 tsp salt, 2 pack noodles, 2 tbsp oil, 2 inch ginger, 3 clove garlic, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 1 carrot, 1 cup cabbage, 5 beans, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp black pepper powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large vessel take 2 litre water, 1 tsp oil and 1 tsp salt. 2. once the water comes to a boil, add 2 pack noodles. 3. boil for 3 minutes or until the noodles turn al dente. 4. drain off the water and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process. keep aside. 5. firstly, in a large wok, heat 2 tbsp oil, saute 2 inch ginger, 3 clove garlic, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp spring onion. 6. now add ½ onion and stir fry on high flame. 7. add 1 carrot, 1 cup cabbage, 5 beans and ½ capsicum. 8. stir fry on high flame until the vegetables are cooked yet remain crunchy. 9. now ad ½ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp salt. 10. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 11. further add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce and ¼ tsp black pepper powder. 12. stir fry until the sauces are well combined. 13. add in boiled noodles and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 14. finally, add 2 tbsp spring onions and enjoy masala noodles.

title: Masala Oats Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 3.5 cups water, 0.25 cup onions, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup green beans, 0.25 cup green peas, 0.25 cup sweet corn, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 0.75 teaspoon garlic, 1 green chili, 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 medium lemon, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Prepare the following vegetables 2. ¼ cup onions chopped (optional) 3. ½ cup tomatoes (deseeded & chopped or pureed) 4. 1 green chili (slit or chopped, Optional, leave out for kids) 5. ½ cup carrots (peeled and fine chopped) 6. ¼ cup green beans or French beans (fine chopped) 7. ¾ teaspoon ginger (peeled, crushed or chopped) 8. ¾ teaspoon garlic (peeled, crushed or chopped) 9. Pour 1 to 2 tablespoons ghee or oil to a pot & heat on a medium flame. When the oil becomes hot, add ½ to ¾ teaspoon cumin seeds. Soon the seeds will begin to sizzle, add the onions and green chili. Stir fry until lightly golden. 10. Add the ginger garlic and saute for a minute. 11. Stir in the tomatoes and saute until they turn soft. You can also cover and cook at this stage. I feel using pureed tomatoes cook faster so I often do that. 12. Stir in the following ground spices 13. ½ to ¾ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (use ¼ for low heat) 14. Add the veggies and stir fry for 2 mins. Pour 3½ cups water and bring to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat to medium and cook until the veggies are slightly tender, for 3 mins. If using instant oats use 2½ cups. 15. Add the 1 rolled oats. 16. Stir and leave the spoon in the pot to prevent overflowing. Let cook on a medium heat until thick. Stir occasionally. 17. Stir in the salt. Taste test and add more garam masala if you want. For more heat you can also add crushed black pepper. If it is too thick for your liking you may add little more boiling hot water and simmer for a minute or 2. 18. Oats Recipes f... Savory Steel Cut ... Oats Upma Recipe Oats Khichdi Reci... How to Cook Oatme...

title: Masala Oats Recipe | Healthy Weight Loss Masala Oats Upma
ingredients: 2 tbsp ghee, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp cumin, 4 pod cardamom, 0.5 tsp cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 onion, 2 chilli, 0.5 carrot, 5 beans, 2 tbsp peas, 0.5 tomato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp pav bhaji masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup palak, 3 cup water, 1 cup rolled oats, 2 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee. Add 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp cumin, 4 pods cardamom, ½ tsp cloves and 1 inch cinnamon. Saute until the spices turn aromatic. 2. Add ½ onion, 2 chilli and saute until the onion softens. 3. Further add ½ carrot, 5 beans, 2 tbsp peas, ½ tomato saute for a minute or until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 4. Now add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp pav bhaji masala and ½ tsp salt. Saute until the spices turns aromatic. 5. Also, add 1 cup palak and saute for 2 minutes. 6. Add 3 cup water and get to a rolling boil. 7. Once the water comes to a boil, add 1 cup rolled oats and mix well. 8. Cover and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes or until the oats are cooked well. Add water adjusting the consistency as required. 9. Further add 2 tsp lemon juice and 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 10. Finally, enjoy Masala Oats Recipe with raita.

title: Masala Paniyaram Recipe | Instant Masala Kuzhi Appe
ingredients: 1 cup rava, 0.75 cup curd, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp urad dal, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coconut, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 1 cup rava, ¾ cup curd, 1 tsp salt and 1 cup water. 2. Mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined. Rest the batter for 15 minutes. 3. In a pan, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp chana dal, ½ tsp urad dal, a pinch of hing, and a few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering. 4. Add ½ onion and sauté until the onion softens. 5. Transfer the tempering to the rava mixture. 6. Also, add ½ a carrot, ½ a capsicum, 2 tablespoons of sweet corn, 2 tablespoons of coriander, 1 inch of ginger, and 2 tablespoons of coconut. 7. Mix well, adjusting the consistency. 8. Furthermore, add ½ tsp of Eno fruit salt and 2 tbsp of water. Alternatively, you can use baking soda. 9. Mix gently to form a frothy batter. 10. Heat the appe pan and add oil to each mould. 11. Once the pan is hot enough, add in the prepared rava batter. 12. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 13. Roast on low to medium flame until the appe turns golden brown. 14. Flip over and cook both sides. 15. Finally, enjoy Masala Paniyaram with chutney.

title: Masala Pasta Recipe (Indian Style Pasta)
ingredients: 1.5 cups Penne Pasta, 2.25 tablespoon olive oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 cloves garlic, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1.25 tablespoon tomato ketchup, 2.5 tablespoon cream, 1.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 cup onions, 1.25 cup tomatoes do not use sour tomatoes, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup green peas, 0.25 cup capsicum, 3 tablespoons sweet corn kernels
instructions: 1. Fine chop 1 medium onion (½ cup), 2 medium tomatoes (1¼ cup deseeded & chopped or 1 cup pureed). I use 1 cup pureed tomatoes. Grate or mince 2 to 3 garlic cloves & ½ inch ginger to make 1½ to 2 teaspoons ginger garlic. 2. Peel & chop 1 medium carrot (½ cup) & ¼ cup bell peppers. I also use ¼ cup green peas & 2 tablespoons tender/young sweet corn. If you don’t prefer veggies in your masala pasta you may simply skip all of these. 3. You will also need 4. I also use an additional ½ teaspoon roasted cumin powder & fennel powder. If you want you may use, they are optional. 1 to 1½ tablespoons tomato ketchup or 2 tablespoons passata or 1 tsp tomato paste (optional). This adds umami flavor to your masala pasta. 5. In a large pot, bring 6 cups of water to a boil. 6. When the water begins to boil rapidly, add half teaspoon salt and pasta. 7. Cook till al dente on a medium to high flame. 8. While the pasta cooks, rinse and chop the veggies. 9. Drain off the pasta to a colander when done and rinse in cold water. 10. Set aside. Reserve 3 to 4 tablespoons of pasta cooked water. 11. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. When the oil turns hot, add cumin. 12. When they begin to splutter, then add finely chopped garlic. 13. Fry till it begins to smell good for a minute or two. 14. Add onions and fry until they turn pink. 15. Then add chopped veggies and fry for about 2 minutes. 16. Next mash the tomatoes well and add it to the pan along with salt. 17. Cook until the tomatoes turn soft and blend well with veggies. The raw smell of tomatoes should disappear. 18. Add kasuri methi, red chili powder and masala powder. 19. Stir well and fry for a minute. Switch off the stove. If using cream add now and stir. 20. Add cooked pasta and 1 tablespoon oil. Toss well. Adjust salt as needed. 21. Add coriander leaves. If the pasta looks dry, then pour 1 to 2 tablespoons pasta cooked water. 22. Serve masala pasta hot.

title: Masala Pav Recipe
ingredients: 2 tablespoons butter, 4 pavs, 0.75 cup onions, 1 cup tomatoes, 1 green chilli, 0.375 cup capsicum, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon pav bhaji masala powder, Salt, 1 lemon, 1 Handful coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat a tawa or a griddle with 1 tbsp butter on a high flame. 2. Saute onions & green chilli in the butter until lightly golden. 3. Add ginger garlic paste and saute tiill the raw smell goes away. 4. Stir in the tomatoes and sprinkle salt. Cook until they turn soft. 5. Add capsicums, chili powder and pav bhaji masala. Saute very well for 2 to 3 mins. 6. Add very little water and allow to cook the masala until soft. Mash up very well with the spatula. 7. When the masala begins to smell good, add some coriander leaves and mix. 8. Move all the masala to one side of the tawa. 9. Add little butter and slit the pav. Toast them on both the sides. 10. Transfer the masala over the pav. Squeeze in some lemon juice and coriander leaves. 11. Smear some masala on the top as well. Add some onions and coriander leaves on top. 12. Serve masala pav immediately.

title: masala poori recipe | tikhat poori | tikhi puri | masaledar poori
ingredients: 2 cup wheat flour atta, 2 tbsp rava semolina suji, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 0.75 cup water, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cup wheat flour and 2 tbsp rava. adding rava makes poori crisp. 2. add ½ tsp red chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp aamchur, ¼ tsp ajwain, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 3. mix well adding 1 tsp oil. 4. now add ¾ cup water and knead the dough. 5. knead for 5 minutes or until a tight dough is formed. 6. add a tsp of oil and knead the dough again. 7. pinch a small lemon sized balls and grease with oil. 8. roll the dough evenly into circles using a rolling pin. roll neither too thin nor thick. 9. heat oil in a deep frying pan or kadai. when the oil is sufficiently hot, add one poori. 10. and, press with the spoon to puff up. 11. once they puff, it will automatically turn over. if not, once the bottom side is golden brown, turn over the poori. 12. then drain the poori into tissue paper to remove excess oil. 13. finally, serve tikhat puri with curries of your choice.

title: masala pulao recipe | spicy masala veg pulav | masala veg pulao
ingredients: 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 pod cardamom, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 tsp garlic paste, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 to mato, 1 carrot, 0.5 potato, 3 tbsp peas matar, 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 cup water, 1 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee and saute 1 inch cinnamon, 3 pod cardamom, 4 cloves, 1 bay leaf, ½ tsp fennel and 1 tsp cumin. saute until spices turn aromatic. 2. now add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 tsp garlic paste and 1 chilli. 3. saute well until onions change colour slightly. 4. keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi. 5. saute on low flame until spices turn aromatic. 6. further, add 1 tomato and saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 7. now add 1 carrot, ½ potato and 3 tbsp peas. 8. saute for a minute or until vegetables are coated well with the masala. 9. add in 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tbsp coriander and saute gently for a minute. make sure to soak basmati rice for 20 minutes. 10. pour 2 cup water and 1 tsp salt. mix well. 11. cover and simmer for 20 minutes or until rice gets cooked completely. you can alternatively, pressure cook for 2 minutes on medium flame. 12. finally, enjoy masala pulao with boondi raita.

title: Masala Puri Recipe (Bangalore Style Masala Poori)
ingredients: 1 cup green peas, 1 medium potato, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 3 cups water, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.75 teaspoon fennel seeds, 1 inch ginger, 2.5 garlic cloves, 1 green chilli, 1 medium onion, 1 medium tomato, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 cup coriander leaves, 17.5 mint leaves, 1 cup water, 1 teaspoon jaggery, 35 puris, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 cup tamarind chutney, 0.25 cup nylon sev
instructions: 1. Add green peas to a pot and rinse them well under running water a few times. Cover them with fresh water and soak for 6 to 8 hours. 2. Later drain the water and rinse them well with fresh water. Wash and peel a potato. Add that as well to the bowl and pour 3 cups water. 3. Pressure cook these for 4 to 5 whistles or 20 minutes on a medium heat. 4. While the peas and potatoes pressure cook, heat oil in a pot or pan. 5. Add fennel seeds and cumin seeds. They will begin to splutter, add ginger garlic and green chilli. 6. Saute for 1 to 2 minutes to bring out the aroma of ginger and garlic. Then add onions sand saute until transparent. 7. Add tomatoes and salt. Saute until the tomatoes soften and the raw smell disappears. 8. Slide the onions and tomatoes to a side and add red chilli powder and garam masala. Mix and saute until the masala begins to smell, for a minute or so. 9. Cool this completely and add to a grinder jar. 10. Meanwhile check the peas. When the pressure releases naturally open and check. They should be soft cooked, almost mushy & partially holding shape. 11. Take 2 tbsps of the cooked peas, cool them and add to the grinder. I usually take the top portion of the cooked peas, which mostly has the loosened skin from the peas. Also add the coriander and mint leaves. 12. Grind all of these to a smooth paste without adding water. 13. Add this back to the hot pan and saute for a few minutes until aromatic. This takes about 4 minutes. 14. Next transfer the boiled peas, potato and stock (the water in which the peas and potatoes cooked). 15. Mash the potato with a spatula and mix everything. I don't mash the peas as they are already mushy. 16. Pour 1 cup more water and cook on a medium heat until thick. Add jaggery and tamarind if desired. 17. Once it came to a boil I cooked for about 7 to 8 minutes. The raw flavor of the mint and coriander leaves goes away by then. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Turn off the stove. 18. Before assembling ensure you keep everything ready. Chop onions, tomatoes, lemon and coriander leaves. You can use make without tamarind chutney too. But if you want you can check my easiest tamarind chutney recipe here. 19. Crush 7 crisp puris or papdis and add them to a serving plate or bowl. Pour masala puri as much as you want. 20. Add tamarind chutney, onions, tomatoes and coriander leaves. Add sev and more crushed puris if you prefer. 21. Serve masala puri immediately as the crunchies soften after a while.

title: Masala Puri Recipe | Bangalore Street Style Masala Poori Chaat
ingredients: 1 cup white peas, water, 1 potato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp clove, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp fennel saunf, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tomato, 0.5 cup mint pudina, 0.75 cup coriander, 2 tbsp oil, 0.75 tsp cumin jeera, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 4 puri, 1 tsp tamarind chutney, 1 tsp green chutney, 2 tbsp spicy sev, 2 tbsp tomato, 2 tbsp onion, 1 tsp coriander, pinch chaat masala
