instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup white peas and soak in enough water for 8 hours. 2. take soaked peas in the cooker and add 1 potato, ½ tsp turmeric and ½ tsp clove. 3. add 3 cup water and pressure cook for 6 whistles. 4. mash the potato slightly, adjusting the consistency as required. keep aside. 5. now prepare masala paste by heating, 2 tbsp oil and saute 1 tsp fennel. 6. also add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute slightly. 7. further, add 1 tomato and saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 8. cool completely and transfer to a blender. 9. add ½ cup mint and ¾ cup coriander. 10. blend to smooth paste adding water if required. keep aside. 11. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and ¾ tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf and 1 inch cinnamon. saute until spices turn aromatic. 12. now add ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder and ½ tsp garam masala. 13. saute on low flame until spices turn aromatic. 14. further, add in boiled white peas and mix well. 15. add 1 cup water and 1 tsp salt. 16. mix well adjusting consistency as required. 17. cover and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the flavours are well absorbed. 18. masala puri curry is ready to enjoy. 19. firstly, in a plate crush 4 puri used to prepare pani puri. 20. pour in a ladleful of prepared masala puri gravy. 21. now top with 1 tsp tamarind chutney, 1 tsp green chutney and 2 tbsp spicy sev. 22. also, top with 2 tbsp tomato, 2 tbsp onion and 1 tsp coriander. 23. finally, enjoy masala puri recipe sprinkling chaat masala if required.

title: Masala Rice Pulao Recipe - Lunch Box Special
ingredients: 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp saunf fennel, pinch asafoetida hing, few curry leaves, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 green chilli, 0.5 onion, 1 tomato, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp peas matar, 3 beans, 0.25 capsicum, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 cup cooked rice, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp ghee and splutter 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp saunf, pinch asafoetida and few curry leaves. 2. now add ½ tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 green chilli and ½ onion. saute well. 3. additionally, add 1 tomato and saute till tomatoes turn soft & mushy. 4. further add chopped vegetables and cook for 5 minutes. 5. now add ¼ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp red chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and ½ tsp garam masala. 6. saute until the spices start to release oil. 7. furthermore, add 2 cups cooked rice and 1 tsp salt. 8. mix gently without breaking rice grains. 9. cover and simmer for 4 minutes. 10. finally, add coriander leaves and serve masala rice with raita or pack to a lunch box.

title: masala sandwich recipe | mumbai masala toast sandwich
ingredients: 1 cup coriander, 0.5 cup mint, 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, 4 chilli, 2 inch ginger, 4 cloves garlic, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tbsp water, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 3 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 4 potato, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander, bread, butter, tomato slice, chaat masala, beetroot slice, onion slice, capsicum slice, cucumber slice, cheese
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 1 cup coriander and ½ cup mint. 2. add 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, 4 chilli, 2 inch ginger, 4 cloves garlic, and 2 tbsp lemon juice. 3. also add ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp aamchur, 1 tsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. 4. blend to smooth paste adding 3 tbsp water. 5. finally, green chutney recipe can be stored in the refrigerator for a week. 6. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add ½ tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin and pinch hing. 7. also add 2 chilli, 3 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves and splutter the tempering. 8. now add ¼ tsp turmeric and saute until the raw aroma disappears. 9. further add 4 boield and mashed potato, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 10. mix and mash until everything is well combined. 11. additionally, add ½ lemon juice and 2 tbsp coriander. 12. finally, aloo masala is ready to prepare for a sandwich. 13. firstly, spread butter on 2 slice of bread. 14. also spread green chutney on both the slice of bread. 15. further spread prepared aloo masala on both the slice. 16. now place tomato slice and sprinkle chaat masala. 17. also place beetroot slice, onion slice, capsicum slice, cucumber slice. 18. sprinkle chaat masala and then cover with the other bread slice. 19. to prepare a cheese masala toast sandwich, top with grated cheese and then cover with bread slice. 20. spread butter as required and grill golden. 21. now cut the sandwich into 4 pieces and spread butter and green chutney. 22. also, top with sev before serving. 23. finally, enjoy mumbai masala toast sandwich with cutting chai.

title: Masala Vada | Chana Dal Vada
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1 inch cinnamon, 1.5 red chili, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.75 teaspoon saunf, 1 onion, handful pudina, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chilies, oil
instructions: 1. Add chana dal to a bowl and wash it with lot of water a few times. 2. Soak it in good amount of water for 2 hours. Drain completely. Chana dal must be fully tender and soaked without any hard center. 3. Add cinnamon, red chili, jeera and saunf to a blender jar. Make a coarse powder. Keep this aside with 2 tbsp soaked chana dal. 4. Next add the drained chana dal & salt to the same blender jar. 5. Pulse it a few times until it turns to a coarse mixture. The dough must have some uncrushed chana dal. Transfer to a bowl. 6. Next add finely chopped mint, curry leaves, onion, masala powder we just made above. 7. Also add green chilies and ginger garlic paste. Mix everything well. Taste and add more salt if needed. 8. Take a handful of the dough & check if it binds well. To check make a ball & then flatten it to a masala vada. The mixture must have a good binding to shape them to vadas. 9. Remove this to your fingers & check if the masala vada is binding well. If the mixture doesn't bind well, then take a handful of this mixture and blend to a smooth paste. 10. Mix that with the rest of the dough. 11. Heat oil in a deep pan on a medium flame. 12. While the oil heats, divide the dough to 10 parts. 13. Make small balls and flatten them on your palm. You can also make these on a greased plastic sheet or dry cloth. 14. Check if the oil is hot enough by gently dropping a small portion of the dough to the oil. The dough must rise without browning quickly. This is the right temperature. 15. Slide masala vada one by one gently to the hot oil. You can add about 3 to 4 vadas in each batch. 16. Do not disturb them immediately otherwise they will break. Wait until they firm up for 2 to 3 mins before turning them over to fry the other side. 17. On a medium flame deep fry the masala vadas until golden & crisp on both the sides. 18. Transfer the masala vadai to a kitchen tissue. Fry the rest of them in batches following the same procedure. 19. Serve masala vada hot with coconut chutney or ketchup. 20. If your air fryer needs preheating, then preheat it for 15 mins at 180 C or 350 F. Place the masala vadas on the rack. I don't brush mine with oil or ghee in the beginning. You may do if it is okay with your air fryer. 21. Air fry for 10 mins at 180 C or 350 F. Turn them to the other side and air fry for another 6 to 7 mins. Please adjust the time as needed depending on your air fryer. They will turn hard if you over fry them. Once done brush the air fried masala vadas lightly with oil or ghee.

title: Masala Vada Recipe | Masala Vadai | Paruppu Vadai | Chattambade Recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup chana dal, 0.75 cup toor dal, water, 2 red chilli, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp fennel, 2 tbsp onion, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take ¾ cup chana dal and ¾ cup toor dal. Adding the mixture of toor and chana dal gives nice crunchiness and taste. 2. Add water and soak for 3 to 4 hours. Make sure not to oversoak, else the vada will not turn crispy. 3. Drain off the water, and reserve ¼ cup dal aside. 4. Transfer the soaked dal into the mixer jar. 5. Add 2 dried red chilli, 1 tsp cumin andd 1 tsp fennel. 6. Pulse and grind to a coarse paste. You can also use a chopper to prevent it from turning into a smooth paste. 7. Transfer the dal paste to a large bowl. 8. Add 2 tbsp onion, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, ¾ tsp salt and mix well. 9. Squeeze and mix making sure everything is well combined. If you find the mixture is watery, then add 2 tbsp rice flour. 10. Also add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 11. Now wet your hand or grease your hand and pinch a ball-sized mixture. 12. Flatten slightly with your palm, make sure to keep the edges thin compared to the centre. 13. Drop the vada into hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 14. Do not touch the vada for 2 minutes, else there are chances for the vada to break. 15. Stir occasionally, and fry on low to medium flame. 16. Fry until the vada turns golden and crunchy. 17. Drain off and keep aside. Make sure to fry on low to medium flame, or else the vada will turn crispy from out and remain soft from the inside. 18. Finally, Masala Vada Recipe tastes great for up to 2 days when stored in an airtight container.

title: Masale Bhat Recipe | Authentic Maharashtrian Masala Baath
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp cashew, 0.5 onion, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp goda masala, 1 tindora, 0.5 potato, 3 tbsp peas, 1 brinjal, 1 cup rice, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp jaggery, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp coconut, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom, 5 cloves, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing and a few curry leaves. splutter the tempering. 2. Add 2 tbsp cashew and fry until the cashew turns crunchy. 3. Further add ½ onion, 2 chilli and 1 inch ginger. saute until the onion softens slightly. 4. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp goda masala. 5. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 6. Now add 10 tindora, ½ potato, 3 tbsp peas, 1 brinjal and fry for 2 minutes or until the vegetables shrink slightly. 7. Add 1 cup of rice. i have used sona masuri rice, soaked for 20 minutes. traditionally, it is made using Maharashtrian short-grain Ambemohar rice. However, you can also make using any short-grain rice like sonamasuri, surti kolam, kalijeera. 8. Add 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp jaggery and saute for 2 minutes without breaking the rice. 9. Further, add 3 cup water and mix well. 10. Add in 2 tbsp ghee, cover and simmer for 20 minutes. 11. Once the rice is cooked well, mix gently making sure everything is well combined. 12. Finally, Masale Bhat Recipe can be served topped with coconut and coriander.

title: Masoor Dal Recipe (Red Lentil Dal)
ingredients: 0.5 cup masoor dal, 2 tablespoons moong dal, 2 medium tomatoes, 1 green chili, 1.25 to 1 cup water, 1.5 tablespoon ghee, 1 red chili, 2 garlic, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch asafoetida, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.125 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Add masoor dal & moong dal (optional) to a pot or pressure cooker. Wash it a few times until the water runs clear. 2. Next add chopped tomatoes, water and green chili. 3. Pressure cook for 1 to 2 whistles on a medium heat. If cooking in a pot, bring the water to a rolling boil, reduce the heat and cook until soft, adding more hot water if needed. Skim off any froth that rises to the top. 4. Once the pressure drops, open the cooker. 5. Mash the dal to suit your liking. You can make it smooth or keep it coarse for a texture. If needed you can pour 2 to 3 tbsp hot water to bring it to a consistency. 6. Heat ghee (or oil or butter) in a small pan, on a medium heat. 7. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, dried red chilies and chopped garlic. 8. Saute until the garlic turns slightly golden. Turn off the heat and add hing, chili powder, turmeric & garam masala. 9. Quickly pour this off to the dal. (If you want you may set aside half of the tempering along with red chili to garnish later.) 10. Time to add salt and crushed kasuri methi if using. Mix and simmer for 2 mins. Taste test and add more salt if required. 11. Squeeze in some lemon & garnish with chopped coriander leaves before serving. Masoor dal is ready to serve with roti or rice. 12. Press saute button on the Instant pot and pour oil to the inner pot. 13. Add mustard, cumin, broken red chilies and garlic. 14. When they splutter add tomatoes along with salt, chilli powder, hing, garam masala (optional) and turmeric. 15. Saute for 1 min. Next add dal, green chilli and pour water. 16. Stir well and deglaze to release any bits of food stuck. Secure the Instant pot with lid. Set the steam release handle to sealing. 17. Optional :You can cook basmati rice together by placing the rice bowl on a long legged trivet. 18. Press pressure cook button and set the timer for 7 mins. 19. Wait for the pressure to release naturally. When the pin drops, open the IP. 20. Add kasuri methi and stir well. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala if required. If the dal is runny, cook on saute mode for a few minutes. If it is too thick pour little hot water and saute for a minute. 21. Squeeze lemon juice and garnish with chopped coriander leaves. Serve masoor dal with rice.

title: Masoor Dal Tadka Recipe - Dhaba Style | Akkha Masoor
ingredients: 1 cup whole masoor dal, 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 tsp cloves, 1 pod black cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp cumin, 4 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, pinch hing, 1 tsp salt, 2 tomato, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 2 chilli, pinch hing, pinch chilli powder, pinch garam masala
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl soak 1 cup whole masoor dal for 4 hours. If you are soaking in hot water then soak for 30 minutes. 2. In a pressure cooker take 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 bay leaf, ½ tsp cloves, 1 pod black cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon and 1 tsp cumin. Saute until the spice turn aroamtic. 3. Also saute 4 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger till it turns aromatic. 4. Further, add 1 onion and saute well. 5. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, pinch thing and 1 tsp salt. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 6. Add 2 tomato and saute until the tomato softens. 7. Now add soaked dal, 3 cup water and mix well. 8. Pressure cook for 5 whistles or until the dal is cooked well. 9. Mash the dal slightly, to get desired consistency. 10. To prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Add 1 tsp cumin, 2 chilli, pinch thing, pinch chilli powder and pinch garam masala. 11. Mix till the spices turn aromatic. 12. Pour the tempering over the dal and add 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 13. Finally, enjoy Akhha Masoor Dal Recipe with roti or rice.

title: Matar Mushroom | Mutter Mushroom
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.875 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.625 cup green peas, 2 grams mushrooms, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash and chop onions, green chilies and set aside. Deseed and chop tomatoes or puree them. 2. Rinse the mushrooms and set aside. Slice them when you are just about to add them to the pan. 3. Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin and allow to splutter. 4. Fry onions and chili until golden. 5. Add ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw flavors go away. 6. Next put in the tomatoes with salt and fry until mushy. 7. Add garam masala, coriander powder, turmeric and chili powder. Saute until aromatic and thick. (To make a gravy dish with more sauce, read my notes below from the recipe notes) 8. Then add peas and mushrooms. Fry for 4 to 5 mins on a medium heat. 9. Cover and cook on a low flame until the mushrooms are tender but not mushy and soggy. Keep stirring in between. 10. As the mushrooms cook, they release moisture. When the mushrooms are tender, open the lid and mix well. Saute on a slightly high heat for a minute. 11. Taste and add more salt if needed. If you prefer you may add crushed kasuri methi (optional). 12. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve matar mushroom with rice or chapathi or with toasted bread.

title: matar mushroom recipe | mutter mushroom | mushroom mutter masala
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic, 2 to mato, 2 tsp oil, 1 mushroom, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.75 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 cup cashew paste, 1 cup water, 0.5 cup peas matar, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. in the same kadai heat 2 tsp oil and saute 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp cumin. saute till the spices turn aromatic. 2. keeping the flame on low saute, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder and ¾ tsp coriander powder. 3. add in prepared onion tomato paste and cook well. 4. keep stirring till the onion tomato paste thickens and releases oil. 5. now add in ¼ cup cashew paste and mix well. 6. add in 1 cup water and stir well adjusting consistency as required. 7. further add ½ cup peas and sauted mushrooms. mix well. 8. cover and boil for 5 minutes or till mushrooms and peas gets cooked completely. 9. now add 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander leaves and ¼ tsp garam masala. mix well. 10. finally, serve matar mushroom recipe with roti, naan or kulcha.

title: matar paneer pulao recipe | Kaju Paneer Peas Pulao - Lunch Box Recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp ghee, 2 grams paneer, 3 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 0.5 javitri mace, 0.5 tsp cloves, 4 pods cardamom, 1 onion, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1.5 cup basmati rice, 1 cup peas matar, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 0.5 lemon, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee. add 200 grams paneer, 3 tbsp cashew, and roast on low flame. 2. roast until the paneer and cashew turn golden brown. keep aside. 3. in the same kadai, add 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp cumin, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, ½ javitri, ½ tsp cloves, and 4 pods of cardamom. 4. roast until the spices turn aromatic. 5. now add 1 onion, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, and saute until the onions change color slightly. 6. further, add 1½ cup basmati rice and saute gently without breaking rice grains. 7. also add 1 cup peas, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, and 1 tsp salt. 8. mix well making sure the spices turn aromatic. 9. now add 3 cup water and ½ lemon. mix well. 10. cover and simmer for 10 minutes. 11. after 10 minutes, add 2 tbsp coriander and fried cashew, paneer. 12. mix gently. cover and simmer for another 10 minutes. 13. finally, enjoy matar paneer pulao with raita.

title: Matar Paneer Recipe - Dhaba Style
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 2 onion, 2 tomato, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 2 tbsp ginger garlic, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup peas, 1 cup water, 2 cup paneer, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp cream, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. First, heat 2 tbsp of oil in a pan. Add 2 onions and saute slightly. 2. Also, add 2 tomatoes and sauté until the tomatoes soften. 3. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 4. Add 2 tbsp of cashew and grind to a smooth paste. Keep aside. 5. In a kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 1 tsp cumin till it turns aromatic. åNow add 2 tbsp ginger garlic, 1 onion and saute till the onions turn golden brown. 6. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp kasuri methi, and 1 tsp salt. Saute till the spices turn aromatic. 7. Add the tomato-onion paste and cook until the oil separates. 8. Also add 1 cup peas and mix well. 9. Now add 1 cup of hot water and mix, adjusting the consistency as required. 10. Cover and cook for 5 minutes or until the curry is cooked well. 11. Add 2 cup paneer, ½ tsp sugar and mix well. 12. Simmer for 3 minutes, or until the curry is cooked well. 13. Now add ½ tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp cream and 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 14. Finally, enjoy Matter Paneer Recipe with roti or rice.

title: Matar Paneer Recipe, How to Make Mutter Paneer
ingredients: 1 tablespoon oil, 1.5 green cardamoms, 3 garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 cup onions, 1.5 to 1 cups tomatoes, 12 cashewnuts, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.29166666666666663 cup yogurt curd, 1.5 tablespoon ghee, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 1 bay leaf, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 cup green peas, 1.125 cup water, 1 green chili, 25 grams paneer, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 teaspoon sugar, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 0.25 cup heavy cream
instructions: 1. Heat a pan and add 1 tbsp oil. When the oil turns hot, add 2. 1 inch (8 grams) ginger (peeled & sliced/ roughly chopped) 3. 3 medium garlic cloves (8 grams, sliced). Saute just for a minute. 4. Add 1 cup (110 grams) cubed onions from 1 large onion or 2 medium onions. Saute them until they turn pink to light golden, for 5 to 6 mins. 5. Then add 1½ to 1¾ cups (340 grams) chopped tomatoes, ½ teaspoon salt and 12 whole cashew nuts. To make this nut-free, simply omit the cashews and double the heavy cream at the last step. 6. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. Cover the pan. Cook on a medium flame until the onions and tomatoes turn mushy. If your pan is too dry, you may add a splash of water. Turn off the stove. 7. Cool this mixture completely. 8. Transfer to a blender jar and optionally add ¼ to ⅓ cup plain yogurt (curd) or 1 tablespoon tomato paste if you want. Plain yogurt or tomato paste are optional, use for a rich and tangy flavor. This matar paneer tastes great even without yogurt/ tomato paste. 9. Grind to a smooth puree without adding water. We don’t want any grits in the curry, so make sure this is smooth. 10. Though I have skipped this step in the step by step pictures below, I don’t suggest skipping it because a lot of times raw onions taste bitter after blending and ruins the entire dish. Bring 2 cups of water to a boil & add the 1 cup cubed onions (110 grams). Boil for 2 to 3 mins. Drain the blanched onions. 11. Add blanched onions, 1½ to 1¾ cups tomatoes (340 grams), 1 to 2 small cardamoms, 1 green chili (optional) and 12 to 14 cashew nuts to a grinder jar. If you want you can also add ¼ cup yogurt at this stage and then blend. 12. Make a fine smooth puree. We don’t want any chunks of cashews in the puree. If you do not have a powerful blender, you may pass through a sieve and blend the coarse mixture again. 13. Optional step. Saute paneer in little oil and immerse them in warm water until used. 14. Heat 2 tablespoons oil or ghee in a pan. Add ½ teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 green cardamom, 2 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon & bay leaf. When the spices sizzle, reduce the heat, add 1½ teaspoon ginger garlic paste. 15. Fry ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes off. Do this on a low flame to avoid splatters. 16. Add the ground onion tomato puree & saute on a medium heat stirring often. You can partially cover the pan if it splatters a lot. 17. Saute until the masala cooks well and the raw smell disappears completely. 18. Add ¾ to 1 teaspoon chili powder, ¾ to 1 teaspoon garam masala, ½ to ¾ teaspoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon sugar and ½ to ¾ salt. Sauté for 2 to 3 minutes. 19. Add1 cup peas and pour 1.5 cups water. If you are using frozen peas, you may add them later. 20. Mix well and cook until the gravy thickens and the peas soft cook. If using frozen tender peas, you can add them after the gravy cooks for a while as the frozen ones cook faster. 21. When the gravy turns thick, you will also see some oil on top of the gravy. There is not much oil used in the recipe so you can see very little oil on top. To the thick gravy, add paneer and 1 tsp crushed kasuri methi. Crush the kasuri methi in between your palms and then add. 22. Cook for just 1 to 2 minutes. If you fry the paneer at the earlier step, skip cooking again here. Using cream is optional. Pour 4 tbsps cream to a small bowl, add little gravy and mix well. Stir this into the pan. Switch off the stove and add finely coriander leaves. Serve matar paneer with roti or rice.

title: Matar Pulao Recipe | Green Peas Pulao Recipe | Matar Rice | Green Peas Pulav
ingredients: 2 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp oil, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 grams paneer, 1 tsp cumin, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp cloves, 3 pods cardamom, 1 black cardamom, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 1 onion, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 carrot, 0.75 cup peas, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 1 cup basmati rice, 3 chilli, 2 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker heat 2 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp oil, and roast 2 tbsp cashew on low to medium flame. 2. Roast until the cashew turns golden brown, and keep aside. 3. In the same oil fry 200 grams paneer on medium flame. 4. Roast until the paneer turns slightly golden brown. keep aside. 5. Now add 1 tsp cumin, 1 inch cinnamon, ½ tsp cloves, 3 pods cardamom, 1 black cardamom and 2 tbsp kasuri methi. 6. Roast until the spices turn aromatic. 7. Further add 1 onion, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute well. 8. Also add ½ carrot, ¾ cup peas, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala and 1 tsp salt. 9. Saute for 2 minutes, or until spices turn aromatic. 10. Pour in 2 cup water and get to a boil. 11. Once the water comes to a boil, add 1 cup of basmati rice, fried cashew, and paneer. 12. Also add 3 chilli, 2 tbsp lemon juice and mix well. 13. Now cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles on medium flame. 14. Once the pressure settles down open the cooker and fluff gently. 15. Transfer the pulao to the large vessel and cool for some time. This prevents from rice getting overcooked from the bottom of the cooker. 16. Finally, enjoy Matar Pulao Recipe with raita.

title: Mathri Recipe (Mathiya)
ingredients: 2 cups wheat flour, 0.25 cup besan, 6 tablespoons semolina, 1.125 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon carom seeds, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1 teaspoon onion seeds, 1 teaspoon pepper, 6 tablespoons oil, 0.625 cup water, 1.5 to 2 cups oil
instructions: 1. Add these ingredients to a large mixing bowl: 2 cups of whole wheat flour, ¼ cup of besan (gram flour), 6 tablespoons semolina (I used whole wheat fine rava) 1 to 1¼ teaspoon salt 2. Next add the below mentioned ingredients to the flours in the bowl: 3. Mix well to combine. Now add 6 tablespoons (90 ml) of hot ghee or oil to the dry ingredients. You may heat the oil or ghee in a kadai which you will use for frying the mathri later. 4. Avoid using your fingers at this stage and mix the oil into the flour first, since it is hot. Later rub well using both your hands. This helps the oil to mix in with the flour. 5. Take a handful of flour in your palm and press down. 6. Flour should hold shape, meaning it has the right amount of oil required for the crust. 7. Make a well in the center of the flour mixture and pour ½ cup of warm water. Mix to form a tight dough. 8. This is how the mixture looks. Sprinkle 2 to 4 tablespoons of water, only as required and make a dough. The dough has to be stiff and slightly hard, but not dry and crumbly. 9. You don’t want a soft and smooth dough here. 10. If you divide the dough into 2, the inside dough should be crumbly but not dry. Cover and rest it for 10 mins. 11. Divide dough into equal portions. Smoothen dough pieces and roll in small balls (size of a lemon) on your palms. Don’t worry about cracks in the dough as this is normal. 12. Heat 1½ to 2 cups of oil on medium-low in a large, thick bottomed pan or kadhi. The oil should be medium hot and not sizzling. To test, drop a miniscule piece dough in to the hot oil. If it sinsk and rises up slowly with few baubles, the oil is heated right enough (moderately hot). 13. If the oil is very hot, the insides of the mathri will remain uncooked and won’t be crunchy. Gently slide each mathri into the oil, as many as your kadai can handle. Reduce the flame to low and let the mathri fry slowly. 14. These take time to cook. Don’t be tempted to cook faster by increasing the heat. Fry until they turn golden brown and crispy. Remove to a colander and fry the remaining batches. Cool the mathri completely before you store in an airtight container. 15. You can fry these in an airfryer at 190C for 10-15 minutes. Flip the mathri halfway through cooking. Check after 8 minutes as they start burning quickly.

title: Mathri Recipe | Methi Mathri - Bakery Style Snack
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 0.25 cup rava semolina, 0.25 tsp ajwain, 1 tsp fennel, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp pepper, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp salt, pinch baking soda, 3 tbsp ghee, water, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, take 2 cup maida, ¼ cup rava, ¼ tsp ajwain, 1 tsp fennel, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp pepper, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp salt and pinch baking soda. 2. Add 3 tablespoons of ghee, crumble, and mix well, ensuring the flour is moist. 3. Add water as needed and knead the dough. 4. Bring the dough together until it is tight, adding more water if required. 5. Cover and rest for 20 minutes; this helps the rava absorb the water. 6. Pinch a ball-sized dough and flatten it with your hand. 7. Fry the mathri in medium-hot oil. Alternatively, bake in a preheated oven at 180 degrees Celsius for 25-30 minutes. 8. Fry on a low flame till the mathri floats. This takes approximately 3 minutes. 9. Now, continue to fry on low to medium flame till they turn crispy and golden in colour. 10. Drain off the Mathiya over kitchen paper to absorb excess oil. 11. Finally, serve Mathri Recipe with evening chai or store it in an airtight container for a month.

title: Mawa barfi
ingredients: 2.5 cups mawa, 0.75 cup sugar, 1 tsp rose essence, 0.125 tsp cardamom powder, ghee, 2 tbsp nuts, 1.5 tbsp milk powder
instructions: 1. Grease a aluminum foil or use a butter paper/ parchment paper. Keep it ready and set aside. 2. Bring mawa or khoya to room temperature before you crumble it. Then measure it and add to a pan along with sugar, cardamom powder and rose water. 3. Begin to heat on a low flame, stirring constantly. 4. Mawa and sugar will begin to melt and turn gooey.Continue to cook on a low flame stirring constantly. 5. All you need to do is just keep stirring gently till the mixture turns thick and becomes a mass. Do not whisk it vigorously, the end results will be sticky.The mixture should not get discolored, so cook it on a low flame. It took around 8 to 10 minutes for me to get to this stage. but i insist not to go by the timing as this can differ based on your flame or heat and also i take sometime to take the pictures in between. 6. Transfer the mixture to the greased foil. Spread it evenly with the wooden spatula used for stirring. When the temperature comes down slightly, make a square or rectangle. 7. Leave it to cool completely.I left it for around an hour. Cut to desired shapes. I prefer squares since there will be lesser uneven pieces left. When the mixture cools down, it should not be sticky otherwise you will not get good shapes. If it is sticky it means it is slightly under cooked. Then add the milk powder and knead the mixture. Make a square and cut to pieces. Adding milk powder is optional and is only a quick fix to the sticky barfi. 8. Cool mawa burfi completely. The color variations in the pictures are due to the different lighting sources. 9. Grease a aluminum foil and set aside.Make sure khoya is at room temperature. Crumble it and measure.Add it to a pan along with sugar, rose water, cardamom powder. 10. begin to cook on a low flame stirring, sugar melts and makes the mixture gooey.Continue to cook stirring constantly but gently. 11. With in few minutes, the mixture turns to a single mass. Transfer this to the foil and spread it with the spatula. Allow it to cool completely. Cut to desired shapes with a knife or pizza cutter. cool completely and store in a airtight container. Refrigerate and use with in 3 to 4 days. 12. This step is optional.If your burfi doesn’t set or turns sticky and you are unable to cut it well. Then add milk powder and knead the mixture. Make a square and then cut it.I did not use.

title: mawa modak recipe | khoya modak | mawa ke modak | yellow modak
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 cup milk, 3 tbsp saffron milk, 1.5 cup milk powder, 0.5 cup powdered sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, oil, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 2 tbsp almond badam
instructions: 1. firstly, in a kadai heat 1 tsp ghee and add ½ cup milk. 2. also, add 3 tbsp saffron milk. to prepare saffron milk, soak few threads of saffron in 3 tbsp of warm milk. 3. stir well making sure everything well combined. 4. now add 1½ cup milk powder 5. keeping the flame on low, stir continuously. 6. the mixture starts to thicken. 7. after 13 minutes, the mixture starts to separate from the pan. 8. keep mixing until it forms a lump. finally, instant mawa is ready. 9. transfer to a bowl and allow it to cool slightly. 10. now add ½ cup powdered sugar, ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 11. form a non-sticky dough by combining well. 12. grease the modak mould with ghee to prevent from sticking. 13. stuff the mawa mixture into the mould. 14. also, place a tsp of dry fruit mixture in the centre. 15. close tightly and scrape off excess mixture. 16. unmould the modak gently without breaking. 17. finally, garnish mawa modak with silver vark and is ready to be served.

title: Mayonnaise Pasta Recipe | Pasta Salad with Mayo
ingredients: water, 1 tbsp salt, 1 cup macaroni pasta, 2 tsp oil, 1 tbsp butter, 5 cloves garlic, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup eggless mayonnaise, 2 tbsp cheese
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large pan, take enough water and bring it to a boil. Once the water comes to a boil, add 1 tbsp salt. 2. Add 1 cup of macaroni pasta and boil for 6 to 8 minutes. Refer to the package information to know the cooking time of pasta. 3. Once the pasta is cooked al dente, drain off. Also reserve half a cup of pasta-boiling water. 4. In a wok, heat 2 tsp oil and 1 tbsp butter, then sauté 5 cloves of garlic. 5. Also add ½ onion, ½ capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn and saute till the vegetables turn crunchy. 6. Now add ½ tsp mixed herbs, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp salt. Stir fry till it turns aromatic. 7. Add ½ cup of the reserved pasta water and mix well. 8. Turn off the flame and add ½ cup eggless mayonnaise. Mix well till the sauce turns creamy. 9. Also, add 2 tbsp cheese and mix till the sauce turns creamy and cheesy. 10. Now add boiled pasta and mix well. Simmer for 2 minutes or till the pasta absorbs the sauce. 11. Finally, the Mayonnaise Pasta Recipe can be served hot or cold.

title: Mayonnaise Sandwich Recipe (Veg Mayo Sandwich)
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 1.5 tablespoons butter, 3 tablespoons Mayonnaise, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon red chili flakes, 0.25 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.25 teaspoon dry ground mustard, 1 pinch salt, 1 teaspoon Lemon juice, 2 tablespoons Onions, 0.25 cup bell pepper, 3 tablespoons Cabbage, 2 tablespoons carrots, 2 tablespoons sweet corn, 1 Green chili
instructions: 1. Butter the bread slices and toast on a large griddle until golden and crisp. You can also skip toasting. You can also just butter the bread, then fill the sandwich and toast in sandwich maker. For this recipe you will need 4 bread slices. 2. Add the following ingredients to a mixing bowl: 3. 2 tablespoons Onions or shallots or green onions chopped finely 4. 1 Green chili (deseeded and chopped optional) 5. Here are the veggies you will need – about ½ cup: 6. ¼ cup red bell pepper (capsicum, or firm ripe tomatoes, chopped) 7. ¼ cup lettuce or cabbage fine chopped 8. 2 tablespoons sweet corn (Boiled, optional) 9. Add ¼ teaspoon crushed black pepper, ¼ to ½ teaspoon red chili flakes, 3 tablespoons mayo and ¼ teaspoon dry mustard powder (or 1 tsp Dijon mustard). Mix all the ingredients. The filling is ready. Taste test and add more of chilli, pepper or mayo etc if you want. You may add any other sauce like red chilli sauce, mustard sauce or schezwan sauce.

title: Mayonnaise Sandwich recipe | Veg Mayo Sandwich
ingredients: 1 carrot, 3 tbsp peas, 3 tbsp sweet corn, 1 capsicum, 0.5 onion, 1 inch ginger, 1 cup veg mayonnaise, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp salt, 5 slices bread
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 1 carrot, 3 tbsp peas, 3 tbsp sweet corn, 1 capsicum, ½ onion and 1 inch ginger. 2. Also, add 1 cup of veg mayonnaise, 2 tbsp of coriander, ½ tsp of pepper powder, ½ tsp of mixed herbs, and ½ tsp of salt, and mix well. 3. Spread butter on both slices of bread. 4. Now top with prepared mayo stuffing. 5. Cover up with another slice of bread. 6. Trim the sides and cut into half. 7. Finally, enjoy Veg Mayonnaise Sandwich for your lunch box.

title: medhu pakoda recipe | pattanam pakoda recipe | hotel style methu bonda
ingredients: 3 tbsp ghee, 0.5 tsp baking soda, 0.5 cup roasted gram dal, 0.5 cup rice flour, 0.5 cup besan gram flour, 1 onion, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp fennel, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp mint, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp water, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 tbsp ghee and ½ tsp baking soda. 2. whisk and mix well. 3. make sure to beat with your hand until the ghee turns smooth creamy texture. 4. in a mixer jar take ½ cup roasted gram dal and grind to a fine powder. 5. transfer the roasted gram dal powder to the same bowl. 6. also, add ½ cup rice flour and ½ cup besan. 7. mix well making sure the flour is moist. 8. further add 1 onion, 2 chilli and 1 inch ginger. 9. also add 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp fennel, ½ tsp salt, 2 tbsp mint, 2 tbsp coriander and few curry leaves. 10. squeeze and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 11. now add 2 tbsp water and start to mix well. onion releases moisture so add water as required. 12. mix forming a soft dough. 13. pinch a ball sized dough and roll uniform. 14. deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 15. fry until the pakoda turns golden brown and crisp. 16. finally, enjoy medhu pakoda with coconut chutney.

title: Medu Vada Recipe (Garelu)
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, Ice cold water, Oil, 0.375 teaspoon pepper corn, 2.5 green chilies, 4 tablespoon onions, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon ginger
instructions: 1. Wash dal several times and soak in lot of water for at least 4 to 5 hrs. 2. Drain the water completely and transfer the dal to a mixer grinder jar. If you have a wet grinder, you can use it. 3. Sprinkle 3 tablespoons water. Next add salt and start grinding. 4. Next scrape off the sides and then grind again. Whenever needed sprinkle only as much as 1 ½ tsps. Ice cold water evenly. 5. Again continue to grind to a coarse thick batter. Ensure your grinder does not become hot. 6. Sprinkle water. Grind again till the batter looks fluffy and white. 7. Transfer this to a bowl. Aerate the batter by beating it with your hand in a clockwise direction for 30 to 60 seconds. 8. Test the batter if it is aerated well. Drop a small batter ball in bowl full of water. The ball has to float meaning it is light. This is needed to get fluffy medu vada. 9. If the batter doesn't float well, means either the batter is runny or is not aerated well. Read the notes below to know what to do with runny batter. 10. If the batter is in right consistency still it is not floating well. Then beat the batter once more. 11. Add crushed pepper, cumin, ginger,scurry leaves, green chilies and onions. Mix everything well. 12. Heat oil in a deep heavy bottom kadai or pan on a medium heat till hot enough. 13. Drop a small amount of batter to check if the oil is hot. The batter raises if the oil is hot enough. 14. Dip your fingers in water or oil to moisten your fingers. Water should not be dripping. 15. Take small portions of batter. Shape to a ball, slightly flatten with thumb. 16. Dip the thumb in water, again no dripping water. Make a hole in the center. 17. Gently slide the vada in the hot oil by gently shaking off the fingers. 18. Or place the ball on a greased sheet. Flatten it slightly and make a hole in the centre. 19. If the oil becomes too hot, regulate the flame to medium. 20. Moisten your fingers again. Lift the corner part of the sheet and transfer the vada on to your fingers. Then drop it just by shaking your fingers. 21. Fry medu vada till they turn golden on both sides. 22. Make them and fry them in batches till you use up all the batter. In between every batch, wait till the oil heats up, if not hot. 23. Serve medu vada with coconut chutney or tiffin sambar.

title: medu vada recipe | uddina vada recipe | urad dal vada recipe
ingredients: 1 cups of urad dal, 0.25 cup of channa dal, 2 tsp of rice flour, 2 green chillies, 3 tbsp of dry chopped coconut, few curry leaves, 1 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 inch ginger, pinch of hing, salt to taste, oil to fry
instructions: 1. wash and soak dal in about 3 cups of water for about 2-3 hours. 2. drain all the water from soaked dal. grind urad dal and channa dal together to smooth paste using very little water. i have used authentic grinding stone, but wet grinder or even mixer grinder can be used. 3. the batter should be thick and use very little water. the right consistency is very important or otherwise, you will not be able to get the vada shape. 4. add coriander, green chilli, ginger and chopped dry coconut to the batter. mix it very well. 5. add 2-3 tsp of rice flour. rice flour is added to make it crispy. 6. add pinch of hing to make it more digestible. 7. heat the oil in a frying pan in medium flame. wet your palms and take a lemon size batter. 8. make hole in the center and slide it into the hot oil. 9. the vada should float on top of oil. 10. make sure your oil is not too hot. otherwise the vadas would not cook evenly. 11. fry on both sides till it becomes golden brown in colour. 12. serve medu vada immediately with chutney collections or sambar collections.

title: Medu Vada Recipe in Mixer Grinder | Medhu Vadai
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 2 tbsp rice, pinch hing, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 1 tsp cumin, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 1 cup urad dal, 2 tbsp rice and rinse well. 2. Soak for 6 to 8 hours making sure the dal is soaked well. 3. Drain off the water and transfer to the mixer jar. You can also grind in grinder for better texture. 4. Grind to smooth paste adding water as required. Make sure to add very little water as it helps to make the batter thick. 5. Transfer the fluffy batter to a large plate. 6. Add a pinch hing ¾ tsp salt and whisk well. 7. This helps to incorporate air in the batter and make the batter fluffy. 8. Also add 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, and 1 tsp cumin. 9. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 10. Now you can shape the vada using a tea strainer. Make sure to wet your hands and tea strainer. Place a ball-sized batter and make a hole in center. 11. Drop down gently in medium hot oil. 12. Flip over and fry on low to medium flame. 13. Fry till the vada turns golden brown and crispy. Drain off. 14. Finally, enjoy Medu Vada Recipe with chutney and sambar.

title: Methi Chaman Recipe
ingredients: 112.5 grams paneer, 2 cups fenugreek leaves, 0.75 cup onion, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 green chili, 1 cup tomatoes, 12 whole raw cashews, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.25 teaspoon ginger powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.75 cup water, 0.125 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.5 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup cream, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Soak cashews in half cup boiling hot water for 10 mins, cool down and blend with ¼ cup soaked water to a smooth puree. 2. Heat ghee in a pan and add cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add the onions and saute until deep golden, for 7 to 8 mins. Stir in the green chili, ginger & garlic. Saute for a minute. 3. Add the tomatoes and salt. Saute until they break down and turn mushy. If you want, you may splash little water and cook covered. 4. Stir in turmeric, red chili, coriander powder, ginger powder, garam masala and fennel powder. Move the sauteed masala to a side in the pan and add the fenugreek leaves. Saute on a medium heat for 4 to 5 mins, until aromatic. 5. Pour the cashew puree and hot water. Mix well. Cook covered on a medium heat until thick & the leaves become tender. 6. Add the paneer, cardamom powder and kasuri methi. Let the paneer heat through and turn off. Stir in the cream and taste test to adjust salt and garam masala. Add more cream if you want, to adjust the consistency. The curry turns very thick after cooling down. 7. Garnish methi chaman with coriander leaves. Serve it hot with flatbreads or with plain basmati rice.

title: methi chaman recipe | methi chaman curry | paneer methi chaman
ingredients: 3 cup water, 1 bunch methi fenugreek leaves, 0.5 bunch palak spinach leaves, 4 green chilli, 2 tbsp mustard oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 back cardamom, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 2 pods cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 bay leaf tej patta, pinch hing asafoetida, few seeds methi fenugreek, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp fennel powder saunf powder, 0.5 tsp ginger powder, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tbsp cream, 3 cube paneer cottage cheese, 0.5 cup paneer cottage cheese, 1 tsp kasuri methi
instructions: 1. firstly in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp mustard oil and saute spices. 2. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp fennel powder, ½ tsp ginger powder, ½ tsp sugar and ¾ tsp salt. 3. saute on low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 4. now add prepare methi palak puree. 5. cook for 3 minutes stirring continuously. 6. furthermore, add 1 cup of water (leftover water from methi palak) 7. adjust consistency by adding water as required. 8. now add 2 tbsp cream and mix well. 9. also, add 30 small cubes paneer and ½ cup grated paneer. 10. mix well and simmer for 5 minutes. 11. now add 1 tsp kasuri methi and mix well. 12. finally, serve methi chaman with garlic naan / roti.

title: Methi Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onions, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 medium tomato, 0.5 cup curd, 0.625 teaspoon chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 to 2 cups methi leaves, 0.25 teaspoon pepper powder, 1 green chili, 0.25 teaspoon fennel seeds powder saunf powder, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.5 bay leaf, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((laung)), 4 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon
instructions: 1. Pluck the methi leaves only and not the stems or stalks. Rinse the leaves well a few times in ample amount of water. Drain them completely and set aside. 2. Rinse onions, tomatoes and green chili. Chop them finely and set aside. 3. Deseed tomatoes and fine chop or puree them. Once the methi leaves are drained measure 1½ to 2 cups and chop them roughly. 4. Heat oil in a pot. Add bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves and cardamoms. 5. When they begin to sizzle, add chopped onions and green chili. Saute until the onions turn golden. 6. Add ginger garlic paste & saute until a nice aroma comes out, for about 50 to 60 seconds. 7. Lower the flame and stir in the chilli powder and turmeric. 8. Quickly add chicken and stir fry for 3 to 4 mins. Cover and cook the chicken on a medium flame for about 3 to 4 mins or until tender. 9. Meanwhile whisk the chilled curd with a fork until smooth. Keep it ready. 10. Then add tomatoes, salt and curd. Cover and simmer the chicken on a low heat until the tomatoes dissolve completely. 11. Sprinkle garam masala and fennel powder if using. Then add the chopped methi leaves & mix well. 12. Cover and simmer until the methi leaves wilt completely. It just takes a few minutes for the leaves to get cooked. 13. Sprinkle kasuri methi and pepper powder. Transfer methi chicken to a serving bowl. Garnish with coriander leaves or cream (optional).

title: Methi Dal Recipe (Menthi Kura Pappu)
ingredients: 0.5 cup dal, 2 tablespoons moong dal, 1.5 cups water, 2 cups methi leaves roughly chopped, 0.75 cup tomato, 1.5 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 lemon, 1.5 tablespoon Ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 2 garlic cloves, 1 red chili, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. Add dal to a pot or pressure cooker. Rinse it very well. 2. Pressure Cooker Method: Add water, turmeric, tomatoes and chili. If using dried or fresh unripe mango, add few pieces. 3. Pressure cook for 2 whistles. If cooking in a separate bowl, place it in the cooker and pressure cook for 4 whistles. If cooking in Instant pot, Press the pressure cook button & set the timer for 10 minutes. 4. Without Pressure Cooker: Pour water and bring it to a rolling boil. Reduce heat and cook on a medium heat until the lentils are almost tender, adding more hot water if required. Add tomatoes, turmeric and chilli. Cook until the tomatoes break down & the lentils are soft. 5. While the dal cooks, pluck methi leaves and add them to a large pot filled with water. Rinse them and discard the water. Repeat rinsing and drain. 6. When the dal is cooked, mash it with the back of a ladle, if you like smooth dal. 7. Pour ghee or oil to a small hot pan. 8. Add cumin, mustard, red chili. When they begin to splutter add hing, curry leaves and garlic. 9. When the curry leaves turn crisp, add the methi leaves & fry until they wilt off completely. Turn off and add the red chili powder and garam masala (optional). 10. Add salt & the tempered methi leaves to the dal. If the methi dal is too thick pour some hot water and simmer for 2 to 3 mins. Taste test and adjust salt and garam masala. If you want it more hot, you can mash the green chilies in the dal. 11. Let the dal cool down slightly and squeeze the lemon juice. Serve methi dal with rice, phulka or mudde. 12. Press saute button on the Instant pot and pour ghee or oil to the inner pot / steel insert. 13. Then add mustard, cumin, red chilli & garlic. When the spices pop, add curry leaves and hing. 14. Add methi leaves and green chillies. Saute for a minute. 15. Press cancel button & then add tomatoes, dal, turmeric, red chili powder and salt. If you have small kids, leave out the red chili powder or reduce the quantity. 16. Pour 2 cups water and deglaze the pot by scrubbing the bottom of the pot with a spatula. 17. You can also keep a trivet in the pot and place your rice bowl over it. 18. Secure the Instant pot with the lid & position the steam release handle to sealing. 19. Press the pressure cook button (high pressure) and set the timer to 10 minutes. 20. When the IP is done wait for the natural pressure release. If you want you may add garam masala. 21. If the dal is too thick stir in some hot water. Taste test and add more salt if required. Squeeze some lemon juice. 22. Serve methi dal with rice or roti.

title: Methi Dal Recipe | Methi Dal Fry | How to Make Dal Methi Fry
ingredients: 1 bunch methi fenugreek, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 1 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 2 tomato, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup toor dal, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 cloves garlic, pinch chilli powder, 1 red chilli
instructions: 1. Firstly, finely chop 1 bunch methi and keep it aside. 2. In a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing and a few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering. 3. Add 1 onion and saute until the onion softens slightly. 4. Now add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. Furthermore, add 2 tomato and saute until the tomato softens. 6. You can add ½ cup water, cover and cook until the tomato turns soft and mushy. 7. Now add in finely chopped methi and saute for a minute. 8. Add 1 cup toor dal, 1 tsp salt and water as required to adjust the consistency as required. 9. Get to a rolling boil making sure to adjust the consistency. 10. To prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp ghee, add 10 cloves garlic and fry well. 11. Fry until the garlic turns golden brown. 12. Keeping the flame on low add a pinch chilli powder and 1 dried red chilli. Mix well. 13. Pour the tempering over the dal and mix well. 14. Finally, enjoy Methi Dal Recipe with hot steamed rice topped with ghee.

title: Methi Dal Recipe | Methi Dal Fry | How to Make Dal Methi Fry
ingredients: 1 bunch methi fenugreek, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 1 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 2 tomato, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup toor dal, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 cloves garlic, pinch chilli powder, 1 red chilli
instructions: 1. Firstly, finely chop 1 bunch methi and keep it aside. 2. In a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing and a few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering. 3. Add 1 onion and saute until the onion softens slightly. 4. Now add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. Furthermore, add 2 tomato and saute until the tomato softens. 6. You can add ½ cup water, cover and cook until the tomato turns soft and mushy. 7. Now add in finely chopped methi and saute for a minute. 8. Add 1 cup toor dal, 1 tsp salt and water as required to adjust the consistency as required. 9. Get to a rolling boil making sure to adjust the consistency. 10. To prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp ghee, add 10 cloves garlic and fry well. 11. Fry until the garlic turns golden brown. 12. Keeping the flame on low add a pinch chilli powder and 1 dried red chilli. Mix well. 13. Pour the tempering over the dal and mix well. 14. Finally, enjoy Methi Dal Recipe with hot steamed rice topped with ghee.

title: Methi Matar Malai Recipe
ingredients: 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper corn, 0.75 inch cinnamon piece, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional)), 0.3333333333333333 inch ginger, 2 garlic cloves, 1 green chili, 1 cup yellow onion, 15 cashews, 0.5 cup water, 1 tablespoon butter, 2.25 cups methi leaves, 0.5 cup green peas, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon fennel seed powder, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon Garam masala, 0.41666666666666663 cup water, 2.5 tablespoons heavy cream
instructions: 1. Pluck 2 to 2½ cups leaves (50 grams) from the fenugreek stalks (125 grams). Add them to a large pot of water and rinse them 2 to 3 times. Drain the water completely and sprinkle 1 teaspoon salt. Gently spread the salt over the leaves and rest for 5 mins. Later rinse them once more & drain to a colander. This salt step is optional but will cut down the bitter taste from the leaves. 2. Meanwhile prepare the following: 3. 1 cup yellow onion sliced (125 grams, or red onion but the gravy turns out deeper in color) 4. 1 green chili (chopped to 2 portions, omit for kids) 5. ⅛ to ¼ teaspoon black pepper (cut down for low heat) 6. On a medium flame, heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. Add cumin, cinnamon, cardamom, black pepper and cloves. (be careful, they can splutter if the oil is too hot). When they begin to sizzle, add ginger & garlic. 7. Followed by onions and green chilies. Saute them for 6 mins until transparent. Add cashews and pour half cup water. 8. Cook covered until very soft, for 5 to 6 mins. 9. Cool down and remove the chilies for kids-friendly/ low heat option. 10. Transfer the onions (along with the leftover liquid in the pan), to a blender jar and blend to a super smooth puree. 11. Run a spoon and make sure the spices are ground well. 12. Optional – If you want to omit cream, you may add ⅓ to ½ cup milk and run the blender once more. Use pasteurized or raw milk that is boiled and cooled previously. Do not add raw milk here. I have not used milk here. If you want a step by step help for this part, take a look at this Malai chicken post.

title: Methi Paneer Recipe
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 1 teaspoon ginger chopped, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 0.625 tablespoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1 cup fenugreek leaves, 225 grams paneer, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 13 Cashews
instructions: 1. Pluck methi leaves and soak them in a large utensil filled with water. Repeat rinsing them in fresh water and drain off and set aside. 2. Add 1 tbsp oil to a hot pan. Fry ginger garlic till they smell good. Add onions and fry till lightly golden. 3. Add tomatoes and sprinkle salt. Fry till the tomatoes turn mushy. 4. Add red chili powder and garam masala. 5. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes till the masala smells good. 6. Switch off the stove. If using cashews add them now. Cool this and blend with ½ cup water to a smooth puree. 7. In the same pan, saute methi leaves for 3 to 4 minutes. 8. Add paneer and saute for another 2 minutes. Set this aside. 9. Add 1 tbsp oil to the same pan, add cumin and cardamom. When they sizzle, add the onion tomato puree. Add more water as needed to bring it to a gravy consistency. 10. Bring it a boil and allow to cook well until the gravy thickens. you will see traces of oil on the gravy. 11. Add the methi paneer that was set aside. 12. Do not over cook to avoid rubbery or hard paneer. If you want you can stir in 3 to 4 tbsps heavy cream. 13. Transfer methi paneer to a serving bowl and serve with rice, roti or paratha.

title: Methi Paratha Recipe
ingredients: 2 cup whole wheat flour, 1 cup methi leaves, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon garlic, 0.25 teaspoon chilli powder, 1 teaspoon oil, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.3333333333333333 cup water, 2.5 tablespoons yogurt
instructions: 1. Pluck methi leaves from the stems. Spray some vinegar and sprinkle salt all over and leave them for 5 minutes. Later rinse them well in a pot of water. Repeat rinsing a few times. Drain them to a colander and shake off so the excess water drains off. 2. Chop the methi leaves and grate the garlic. If using green chilies fine chop them. 3. In a bowl mix together, atta, salt, finely chopped methi leaves, curd (optional), garam masala, chili powder or green chilies and garlic. 4. Begin to knead, adding water little by little to make a soft pliable non-sticky dough. 5. Pour 1 tsp oil & knead again for a min or 2 until soft. You may rest the dough for 30 minutes if you have time. Resting also helps in getting softer parathas. 6. Divide the dough to 8 portions. Roll them to smooth balls. Set aside. 7. Flour the rolling area, place a ball of dough on the flour. Dust it with flour evenly on both the sides. 8. Roll the ball to round shape parathas that are not very thin or thick. 9. To make layered methi paratha, smear half teaspoon ghee or oil all over the paratha. Fold it to half. Then repeat smearing ghee. Fold to a triangle. Roll this to a paratha of triangle shape. Refer stepwise pictures for understanding. 10. This way make 3 to 4 parathas and set aside. 11. Heat a tawa till hot on a medium high flame. Dust off the excess flour from the paratha. Transfer the methi paratha on the tawa. 12. Soon you will see tiny bubbles on the paratha, then flip it. Press down with a wooden spatula to help in puffing. 13. Drizzle some oil or ghee all over the methi paratha. 14. Flip it to the other side, repeat to press and cook till they are slightly browned and done thoroughly. Stack them one over the other to keep them soft. 15. Clean up the flour on the griddle with a kitchen towel before you proceed to toast the next one. 16. Serve methi paratha with yogurt, chutney, dal or pickle.

title: Methi Pulao Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 1 cup methi leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 0.875 cup green peas, 1 onion, 1 green chili, 2 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 star anise, 1 bay leaf, 3.5 cloves 3 to 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse it well in lots of water at least thrice. Drain the water and soak it in fresh water for at least 30 minutes. 2. Pluck the methi leaves from the stems and add them to a large pot of water. Remove them to a colander and then repeat the rinse. I sprinkle salt and vinegar and leave them for a while before rinsing them to get rid of the pesticides. 3. Drain them completely to a colander. Shake the colander to remove any excess water in the leaves as it can make your methi pulao mushy. 4. Heat a cooker or pot with oil or ghee. When the oil turns hot, add bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, cardamoms, cumin seeds and mace. 5. As soon as the spices begin to sizzle, add sliced onions and green chilies (optional). 6. Saute until the onions golden. Add ginger garlic paste and fry till you get an aroma or till the raw smell disappears. 7. Pour water and add salt. Stir and taste test the water. It has to be slightly salty. Bring the water to a boil. 8. Transfer the drained rice, green peas and methi leaves. Give a good stir. 9. Cooking in pot: Cover and cook on a low heat till the rice is fully done. Turn off and let the methi pulao rest in the pot for at least 10 to 15 minutes. Tip - if the rice is undercooked, then add little hot water and cook further. 10. Pressure cooker: close the lid and cook on the lowest flame for about 5 minutes. Alternately cook on a medium heat until it whistles once. Let the pressure release on its own, then remove and fluff up the methi pulao. 11. Press saute button on the Instant pot and pour oil to the steel insert. 12. Add all the wholes spices. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and green chilies. 13. Saute until they turn light golden or transparent for about 2 minutes. 14. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for 30 seconds. Then add green peas and methi leaves. 15. Saute until the methi leaves wilt off for about 1 minute. Then press cancel and add rice. 16. Pour water and add salt. Stir and deglaze. Taste test. Water must be slightly salty. 17. Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position the steam release valve to sealing. 18. Press pressure cook button and set the timer to 5 minutes. The IP beeps when it is done. Wait for the pressure to release naturally for 2 minutes. 19. Release the rest manually. Give a gentle mix with a fork and keep the methi pulao covered until you serve.

title: methi pulao recipe | methi rice recipe | fenugreek rice recipe
ingredients: few mint pudina, few coriander, 2 tbsp coconut, 3 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp oil, 2 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 0.5 tsp cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp fennel, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 tsp garlic paste, 1 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 2 tbsp peas, 1 tomato, 1 bunch methi fenugreek, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tsp lemon juice, 1 tsp ghee
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a blender jar take a few mint, a few coriander, 2 tbsp of coconut, 3 chili, and 1 inch of ginger. 2. Add water and grind to smooth paste. Keep aside. 3. In a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 2 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, ½ tsp cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp fennel, and 1 tsp cumin. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 4. Further add 1 onion, 1 tsp garlic paste and saute till the onion turns golden brown. 5. Keeping the flame on low, add 1 tsp garam masala, ½ tsp coriander powder and saute til the spices turn aromatic. 6. Add in prepared masala paste and cook well. 7. Add 2 tbsp peas, 1 tomato, 1 bunch methi, and 1 tsp salt. 8. Saute on low flame until the masala turns aromatic. 9. Pour in 2 cups water and mix well. 10. Also, add 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tsp lemon juice, and 1 tsp ghee. Give a good mix. 11. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes or until the rice is cooked well. 12. Finally, fluff the rice gently and enjoy Methi Pulao Recipe with raita.

title: methi puri recipe | how to make methi puri | methi poori | methi ki puri
ingredients: 2 cup wheat flour atta, 0.25 cup rava semolina suji, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, pinch hing asafoetida, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.75 cup methi fenugreek leaves, 0.25 cup water, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly take 2 cup wheat flour. 2. also add ¼ cup rava, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp pepper, ¼ tsp ajwain, pinch hing, 2 tbsp ghee and ½ tsp salt 3. crumble and mix well making sure all the spices are mixed well. 4. further, add ¾ cup methi and ¼ cup water. 5. knead a tight dough well-adding water as required. 6. dust wheat flour and roll slightly thick as of poori. 7. using a cookie cutter / glass cut small round puri. 8. fry the poori in medium hot oil. alternatively, bake in preheated oven at 180-degree celsius for 15 minutes. 9. fry on medium flame till they turn crispy and golden in colour. 10. drain off the methi puri over kitchen paper to absorb excess oil. 11. finally, serve methi puri with evening chai or store in airtight container for a month.

title: Methi Rava Chips Recipe | Methi Ka Nasta | Crispy Methi Shankarpali
ingredients: 1 bunch methi fenugreek leaves, 2 cup maida, 0.25 cup rava semolina, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp pepper, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tbsp ghee, water, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, using a food processor finely chop the methi. keep aside. 2. in a large bowl, take 2 cup maida and ¼ cup rava. adding rava makes chips crispy. 3. also add 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp pepper, 1 tsp chilli powder, and ¾ tsp salt. 4. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 5. pour in 2 tbsp hot ghee and mix well. 6. crumble and mix making sure the flour holds the shape and is moist. 7. also add finely chopped methi and mix well. 8. add water, as required, and start to knead the dough. 9. knead to a smooth and tight dough. 10. now pinch a ball-sized dough and dust with maida. 11. roll to slightly thick thickness. 12. now using a cutter or sharp knife, cut into diamond shapes. 13. drop into hot oil, keeping the flame on low to medium. 14. fry on medium flame until the chips turns crispy and golden brown. 15. drain off to remove excess oil. 16. finally, enjoy crispy methi chips for a month when stored in an airtight container.

title: Methi Rice Recipe (Methi Leaves Rice)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 2 tablespoon oil, 1.5 garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 green chili, 0.5 cup green peas, 1.25 cup methi leaves, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Wash and soak rice for about 20 minutes. Bring 2 cups water to a boil and add rice. Cook covered until the rice is done to a grainy texture and not mushy. Cool completely. 2. Pour oil to a hot pan and add cumin, green cardamoms, green chilli and garlic. 3. Saute garlic until a nice aroma comes out, then add green peas and methi leaves. Saute for 3 to 4 minutes until the leaves wilt completely. 4. Add garam masala powder, turmeric and mix well. Turn off the stove. 5. Transfer cooled rice and sprinkle salt. Mix everything well. Add some lemon juice. Taste test and add more salt and lemon juice if needed. 6. Serve methi rice with raita or a veggie salad. 7. Rinse 1 cup basmati rice and soak it. Set aside for 20 mins. Meanwhile pluck the methi leaves. 8. Press SAUTE button on the instant pot. Pour oil to the steel insert. 9. Add cumin, green cardamoms, garlic and green chilies. When they begin to splutter, add green peas and methi leaves. 10. Saute for a minute or 2 until the leaves wilt. Add garam masala, turmeric, salt and drained rice. Pour 1¼ cup water. 11. Press CANCEL button and scrape the bottom with a spatula to release any bits of food stuck. 12. Press PRESSURE COOK button and set the timer to 5 minutes if using basmati rice. If using regular rice, set the timer to 10 minutes. 13. Allow natural pressure release for 5 minutes. Release the rest manually. Let the methi rice cool down a bit and then taste test it. Then add more salt if needed and squeeze in lemon juice.

title: Milk Cake Sweet Recipe | Milk Cake Kalakand Mithai
ingredients: 3 litre milk, 0.5 lemon, 1 cup sugar, 4 tbsp ghee, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a kadai, take 3 litres of milk and boil it till it reduces. 2. Add ½ lemon by dropping the juice slowly. 3. Stir and mix slowly. 4. Milk starts to curdle little by little. Continue to cook on a low flame till the milk reduces further. 5. Once the milk has reduced completely, add 1 cup of sugar and mix until well combined. 6. The sugar dissolves, continue to cook, stirring continuously. 7. Be careful as the milk starts to splutter. 8. Continue to cook until the water is completely evaporated. 9. Add 4 tbsp ghee, ½ tsp cardamom powder and cook well. 10. Cook till the mixture starts to hold its shape and no water is in the mixture. 11. Transfer the mixture to the tray and level it. 12. Cover and rest for 4 hours till the mixture cools slightly. 13. Finally, cut into pieces and serve the Milk Cake Recipe.

title: Milk Cake Sweet Recipe | Milk Cake Kalakand Mithai
ingredients: 3 litre milk, 0.5 lemon, 1 cup sugar, 4 tbsp ghee, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a kadai, take 3 litres of milk and boil it till it reduces. 2. Add ½ lemon by dropping the juice slowly. 3. Stir and mix slowly. 4. Milk starts to curdle little by little. Continue to cook on a low flame till the milk reduces further. 5. Once the milk has reduced completely, add 1 cup of sugar and mix until well combined. 6. The sugar dissolves, continue to cook, stirring continuously. 7. Be careful as the milk starts to splutter. 8. Continue to cook until the water is completely evaporated. 9. Add 4 tbsp ghee, ½ tsp cardamom powder and cook well. 10. Cook till the mixture starts to hold its shape and no water is in the mixture. 11. Transfer the mixture to the tray and level it. 12. Cover and rest for 4 hours till the mixture cools slightly. 13. Finally, cut into pieces and serve the Milk Cake Recipe.

title: Milk Peda Recipe | Doodh Ka Peda - Halwai Style
ingredients: 2 tsp ghee, 2 cup milk powder, 1 cup milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large wok, heat 1 tsp ghee and add 2 cups milk powder. 2. Add 1 cup of milk and mix until well combined. 3. Mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined. Make sure there are no lumps. 4. Continue stirring until the milk becomes creamy. 5. Now add ¼ cup sugar and mix well. Keep mixing till the mixture is well combined. 6. Continue to cook till the mixture thickens and holds its shape. 7. Add ½ tsp of cardamom powder and 1 tsp of ghee, and mix well. 8. Mix until everything is well combined and separates from the pan. 9. Transfer the mixture to a large plate and cool it slightly. 10. Now, smooth the peda dough slightly, and pinch off a ball-sized piece of dough. 11. Design with peda mould to the shape of your choice. 12. Finally, garnish the Milk Peda with pistachios and enjoy.

title: Milk Powder Burfi (Barfi Recipe)
ingredients: 2 cups milk powder, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 cup full fat milk, 7 tablespoons powdered sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder
instructions: 1. To a heavy bottom non-stick pan, add 1 tablespoon ghee and milk. 2. Begin to heat on a low flame and add milk powder. 3. Mix together continuously to incorporate the powder into the milk. 4. Regulate the flame to medium and continue to cook, stirring non-stop until the milk begins to thicken. This step takes about 10 minutes. 5. Regulate the flame to low and add ghee. Mix and keep stirring until the whole thing becomes a mass. This step takes about 5 minutes, but this is just an approximation and may vary depending on your pan and flame (check the step by step pictures in the post) 6. Take the pan down from the stove and let it cool down. 7. Test if it is done correctly. For this grease your fingers and take a small portion of the mixture cool it completely and roll to a ball. It should be non-sticky and almost dry, but should be a soft ball. 8. Troubleshooting: If the mixture is still too sticky it means it is under cooked, put the pan back and cook until it passes the test. Do not proceed to the next step until the mixture passes this stage. 9. Cool the milk powder mixture completely. Meanwhile, powder sugar in a grinder until very smooth and fine. 10. Once the milk powder mawa is cool, add the cardamom powder and half of the sugar and mix. Taste test and add more to suit your taste. 11. Mix the entire milk powder burfi mixture well until it is uniform and smooth. The mixture at this stage will be dry to little sticky. 12. Let rest in the bowl for about 3 to 4 mins. The mixture will turn dry by then & won't be sticky after resting. 13. Grease a parchment paper with few drops of ghee. Place the burfi mixture on the paper. Cover with the parchment paper and begin to roll evenly with a rolling pin. 14. Sprinkle thinly sliced almonds and pistachios all over. Cover and again roll lightly so the nuts go into the burfi. 15. Let the burfi set at room temperature for a minimum of 1 hour. Slice the burfi to squares. I sliced mine to 16 pieces. 16. Store milk powder burfi in a air tight jar at room temperature for 3 to 4 days or in the refrigerator for 2 weeks.

title: Milk Powder Burfi Recipe | Instant Burfi in 10 Mins
ingredients: 2 cup milk powder, 1 cup ghee, 1.5 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, few saffron, 2 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 2 cup milk powder and 1 cup ghee. 2. Crumble and mix well making sure the milk powder is moist. Keep aside. 3. In a large kadai take 1½ cup sugar and ½ cup water. 4. Stir and dissolve the sugar. 5. Continue to boil on medium flame until 1-string sugar syrup consistency is attained. 6. Further, add the prepared milk powder mixture and mix well. 7. Keeping the flame on low, continuously stir until the mixture turns silky smooth consistency. 8. Add few saffron for enhanced kesar flavor. 9. Transfer the burfi mixture to the tray lined with baking paper. 10. Top with saffron and 2 tbsp pistachios and press gently. 11. Rest the milk burfi for 1 hour to set well. 12. Now cut into pieces and enjoy. 13. Finally, Milk Powder Burfi Recipe is ready to enjoy for a month.

title: Milk Powder Burfi Recipe | Instant Burfi in 10 Mins
ingredients: 2 cup milk powder, 1 cup ghee, 1.5 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, few saffron, 2 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 2 cup milk powder and 1 cup ghee. 2. Crumble and mix well making sure the milk powder is moist. Keep aside. 3. In a large kadai take 1½ cup sugar and ½ cup water. 4. Stir and dissolve the sugar. 5. Continue to boil on medium flame until 1-string sugar syrup consistency is attained. 6. Further, add the prepared milk powder mixture and mix well. 7. Keeping the flame on low, continuously stir until the mixture turns silky smooth consistency. 8. Add few saffron for enhanced kesar flavor. 9. Transfer the burfi mixture to the tray lined with baking paper. 10. Top with saffron and 2 tbsp pistachios and press gently. 11. Rest the milk burfi for 1 hour to set well. 12. Now cut into pieces and enjoy. 13. Finally, Milk Powder Burfi Recipe is ready to enjoy for a month.

title: milkmaid recipe | condensed milk recipe | homemade milkmaid in 3 minutes
ingredients: 2 cup milk powder, 1 tsp corn flour, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup milk
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take 2 cup full cream powder, 1 tsp corn flour, 1 cup sugar, ¼ tsp baking soda and 1 cup milk. 2. whisk and mix well until everything is well combined. 3. now pour the milk mixture into a large kadai and keep stirring. 4. keep the flame on medium and keep stirring. 5. the mixture will start to thicken slightly. 6. after 3 minutes, the mixture will turn frothy and silky smooth texture. 7. now turn off the flame and cool completely. make sure to turn off the flame when the mixture is slightly thin consistency. as it thickens once cooled completely. 8. finally, condensed milk or milkmaid is ready to enjoy.

title: Millet Dosa (Kambu Dosa)
ingredients: 0.5 cup pearl millets, 0.5 cup urad dal, 0.5 cup rice, 0.5 teaspoon salt, Water, 3.5 tablespoons Oil
instructions: 1. Wash urad dal until water runs clear. Soak it in enough water for 4 hours. Wash rice and kambu/ bajra together in a large pot. Soak them in enough water for 4 hours. You can also soak bajra for longer if you prefer. 2. Drain the water from both the pots. Add urad dal to a blender jar along with some water. Do not add too much water as it makes the batter too thin. 3. Blend it until smooth and bubbly. 4. Add bajra and rice to the same jar and pour some more water. If your blender jar is small, first transfer the urad dal batter to a pot, then add the rice and bajra. Do not add too much water. 5. Blend until the batter is smooth. The batter must be of the dosa batter consistency. Very thin or runny batter will not ferment well. 6. Transfer this to a large bowl for fermentation. Cover and set aside in a warm place for 4 to 8 hours. Depending on the indoor temperature & climate the timing may vary. 7. It usually takes about 16 hours for the batter to rise in my home. Do not let the batter over ferment as it turns sour. These dosas turn slightly hard without fermentation but even little fermentation yields very good dosa. So allow the batter to rise at least a bit. 8. Add salt and water as needed. Bring it to a pouring consistency. If you prefer to set some batter in the fridge, then you can put it in without adding salt and water. 9. Heat a tawa or griddle until hot. If using cast iron pan, sprinkle some oil and grease it well with a kitchen tissue or piece of onion. Wipe off the excess oil. When the pan is hot, pour a ladle full of batter in the center. 10. Spread it to a dosa. You can make thick or thin dosas to your liking. 11. Sprinkle some oil and allow to cook until the edges begin to leave the pan. 12. Flip and cook on the other side for 1 minute. Flip back and cook until crisp. Serve millet dosas hot with your favorite chutney. Set Dosa Recipe (... Pesarattu Recipe Wheat Dosa | Godh... Ginger Chutney | ... Adai Recipe | Ada... Neer Dosa Recipe

title: Millet Khichdi (Foxtail Millet Khichdi)
