ingredients: 0.5 cup millets, 0.5 cup moong dal, 2.5 cups water, 1 cup mixed veggies, 0.25 cup methi leaves, 1 tomato, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 teaspoon ginger grated, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. Wash millets and dal thoroughly until water runs clear. Soak for about 30 mins to 1 hour. The longer they are soaked the quicker and softer they turn. Drain them to a colander. 2. Heat a pressure cooker or pot with ghee. Add cumin and allow them to splutter. Saute ginger until a nice flavor comes out. 3. Add all the veggies including tomato. Saute on a medium flame for 2 to 3 mins. 4. Add red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala and salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn mushy. 5. Then add drained millets and dal. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. 6. Add water and check the salt. 7. Pressure cook on a medium flame for 2 to 3 whistles. I cook for 2 whistles. 8. When the pressure drops, open the lid. Fluff up and add a tsp of more ghee if you want 9. Serve millet khichdi with papad and pickle. 10. Perform all the steps on saute mode. Press cancel and secure the Instant pot with the lid. Position the steam release valve to sealing. 11. Press pressure cook or porridge button and set the timer to 10 mins. When the pressure drops open the lid. I prefer to add some hot boiling water to adjust the consistency and add more salt .

title: mini rasgulla recipe | bengali chenna rasbari | angoor rasgulla
ingredients: 2 litre milk, 3 tbsp lemon juice, 1.5 cup sugar, 2 pods cardamom, 7.5 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, get 2 litre to a boil stirring in between to prevent from burning. 2. add 2 tbsp lemon juice and give a stir. you will notice the milk starts to curdle. 3. add 1 more tbsp of lemon juice and stir until the milk curdles fully separating water. 4. drain off the water over cheesecloth. you can use any clean cloth here. 5. rinse with cold water to remove sourness and stop cooking. 6. squeeze the paneer gently and hang for 1 hour. 7. now take the moist paneer and crumble gently. 8. using the palm, start to knead gently. 9. knead until the paneer mixture turns smooth without any grains. do not over-knead here as the rasgulla will turn hard. 10. pinch a small ball sized chenna and prepare smooth crack free balls. 11. keep the paneer ball aside and cover with a moist cloth. 12. firstly, in a large vessel, take 1½ cup sugar, 2 pods cardamom and 7½ cup water. 13. stir and dissolve sugar. 14. now boil the water for 5 minutes or until the syrup turns slightly sticky. 15. drop in prepared paneer ball keeping the flame on high. 16. cover and boil for 10 minutes or until the size of ball doubles. 17. drain off the rasgulla immediately into ice-cold water to prevent from losing its shape. 18. finally, enjoy rasgulla with sugar syrup.

title: Mint Raita Recipe (Raita Chutney)
ingredients: 0.75 cup mint leaves fresh, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.375 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.25 cup curd, 1.5 green chili, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoon cucumber, 1.5 tablespoon onions
instructions: 1. This recipe requires dry roasted cumin powder. You can make a batch of the powder following this Cumin powder recipe or make a quick powder from my instructions below. On a medium heat, dry roast half teaspoon cumin seeds in a pan until aromatic & deep golden but not burnt. Cool and powder it in a small grinder. 2. Rinse ¾ cup mint leaves/pudina and ¼ cup coriander leaves (optional) in surplus water at least thrice. Drain off the water. Add the leaves to the grinder along with 1 to 2 green chili (or serrano peppers), ¼ to ½ teaspoon sugar, ¼ teaspoon salt and one tbsp. of yogurt. We add yogurt to help in grinding. If you want you can also add a small garlic clove (peeled). 3. Grind to a super smooth consistency. Pour 1¼ cup yogurt to a bowl and whisk well until it is smooth. Add the mint puree to yogurt. Whisk everything together. Taste test and add more salt if required. If you want you may add 1 teaspoon lemon juice or half teaspoon chaat masala. It is optional to use them. We prefer to stir in a few tablespoons of peeled & chopped cucumbers or onions. This goes well with biryani. Serve with any flavored rice like Veg biryani , Mushroom biryani , Chicken biryani Hyderabadi chicken biryani 4. Wash pudina and coriander in enough water. Drain them to a colander completely. 2. Dry roast jeera and cool it. Powder it in a blender jar. You can also use roasted powder and skip this step. 3. Add 1 to 2 tbsp of curd, coriander, mint, salt, sugar, ginger, garlic and green chili to the blender jar. 4. Blend them all well until smooth. Add it to curd. Stir well. Serve with kababs or Indian snacks .

title: mirchi bada recipe | mirchi vada | how to make rajasthani mirchi bada
ingredients: 2 potato aloo, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp fennel seeds saunf, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 0.75 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, pinch hing asafoetida, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 8 chilli, oil for frying
instructions: 1. in a large mixing bowl take 2 boiled and mashed potato. 2. also add 1 tsp coriander seeds, ½ tsp fennel seeds, 1 chilli, ½ tsp ginger paste, ¾ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp aamchur, ¼ tsp ajwain, pinch hing, 2 tbsp coriander and ½ tsp salt. 3. combine well making sure all the spices are combined well. 4. further stuff the prepared aloo masala into the chilli. 5. now dip the stuffed mirchi into besan batter and coat completely. 6. deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 7. stir occasionally, until the bajji turns golden and crisp. 8. drain off the mirchi bajji over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 9. finally, enjoy mirchi bada recipe with green chutney and tamarind chutney.

title: Mirchi Bajji (Mirapakaya Bajji)
ingredients: 13 bhajji mirchi, 2 cups besan, 5 tablespoons corn flour, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 1.125 teaspoon crushed ajwain, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon soda-bi-carbonate, 2 tablespoons oil, Oil for deep frying, 0.75 cup water, 1 teaspoon crushed ajwain, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon tamarind paste, 1 medium onion, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 lemon, 1 salt to taste, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.375 teaspoon Chaat Masala
instructions: 1. Rinse the green chilies under running water and wipe dry with a clean cloth or kitchen tissues. 2. Take a butter knife and slit the chilies lengthwise (on one side), keeping intact the stem/stalk. Deseed them using a knife. 3. Optional - Mix together crushed ajwain, salt and tamarind paste (optional). Stuff your chilies with this. You can put in as much as you want. Usually 2 to 3 pinches is enough. 4. On a medium flame, heat oil in a deep pan for frying. 5. Mix together flour, salt, soda, turmeric, red chili powder & ajwain thoroughly in a bowl. 6. Pour ½ cup water. Begin to mix to make a thick lump free batter, adding more water as required. Avoid pouring all the water at one time. 7. Once you have a lump-free thick batter, pour more water little by little to get the right consistency, not thin and not too thick. The batter should just coat the chili when it is dunked in the batter. Too thin batter will begin to drip off from the chilies .Too thick will not cling on to the chilies. 8. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a separate small pot until really hot (not smoking hot). Pour that to the batter. It should sizzle. Mix with the whisk or spoon. 9. Once you finish mixing, just whisk 4 to 5 times with a whisk on a medium speed to aerate the batter. 10. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of the batter to the hot oil. If it is ready, the batter will sizzle & rise immediately without browning. Reduce the flame to medium. 11. Hold a green chili with the stem/stalk, dip it in the batter and gently rotate it to coat the batter all over. (optionally you may gently swipe off one side of the chilli to the batter bowl before sliding to the oil. This helps to cut the chilies easily later for stuffing). 12. Gently slide it in hot oil & fry on a medium heat. You can fry 3 to 4 in each batch depending on the size of your kadai. Do not disturb them for 2 mins. When they puff and firm up, stir them to cook evenly. 13. Turn them to the other sides and fry until crisp and golden. Remove to a cooling rack or a steel colander. 14. Once done frying all of the mirchi bajji, refry them in batches on a medium heat. Ensure the oil is medium hot. This step makes them extra crisp and they remain crisp for quite long. 15. Let them cool down a little. Meanwhile mix the onions, coriander leaves, lemon juice and salt in a bowl. 16. Take a small spoon or knife and open up the slit gently. Stuff all of them as desired with the onion & chilies. Sprinkle red chilli powder and chaat masala if you want. 17. Serve Mirchi bajji immediately with a cup of tea or as a side with your dinner.

title: Mirchi Ka Salan Recipe (Biryani Salan)
ingredients: 4 tablespoons peanuts, 3 tablespoons sesame seeds, 3 tablespoons coconut, 1 cup onions, 1 inch ginger, 4 medium garlic, 0.375 cup oil, 3 tablespoons tamarind, 1 cup water, 7 green chilies, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 2 cups water
instructions: 1. Rinse the green chilies and make a slit in the center, lengthwise, keeping the stem and the other end intact. For low heat you may deseed. 2. Dry roast peanuts on a medium heat until golden and aromatic, for 6 to 7 mins. 3. Stir in the sesame seeds and coconut. Roast for 2 mins, until aromatic. Do not burn. Remove from the pan to a plate and cool aside. 4. To the same pan, add half of the oil. Saute onions, ginger and garlic until golden, for 9 mins. Remove to the same plate, retaining any excess oil in the pan. Cool down. 5. Add the sautéed onions, ginger, garlic, roasted peanuts, coconut & sesame to a blender jar. Pour water and blend to a smooth puree. 6. Add the green chilies to the same pan and fry them on a medium heat until blistered. If your pan is dry, add a tsp of oil. Mine takes about 5 to 6 mins. Remove to a plate. 7. Pour remaining oil to the pan and heat it. Add mustard, kalonji, cumin seeds and curry leaves. 8. When they begin to pop, stir in the red chili powder, turmeric, salt, garam masala, followed by the salan paste/puree. 9. Mix well and add tamarind, followed by water. Bring to a rolling boil. Cook covered on a medium heat for a minimum of 15 to 20 mins, until thick, smooth, and glossy and you see traces of oil on top. Keep stirring in between. 10. Add the fried green chilies and jaggery. 11. Cover and cook until the chilies wilt but not mushy, for another 5 to 10 mins. Mine cook down by 10 mins and this can vary for you, depending on the kind of chilies. If your salan is too thick and your chilies are undercooked, add some hot water. 12. Taste test and adjust salt and tamarind. Mirchi ka salan should typically taste tangy, spicy, hot and slightly sweet. Turn off when you see oil on top of the gravy and the consistency is slightly runny. 13. Serve Mirchi Ka Salan with biryani, plain rice, pulao, chapati, roti or naan.

title: Mirchi Ka Salan Recipe | Hyderabadi Biriyani Salan
ingredients: 0.25 cup peanut, 2 tbsp sesame, 2 tsp poppy seeds, 2 tbsp dry coconut, piece tamarind, water, 3 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp methi, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 4 chilli, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp jaggery, 3 cup water
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan, dry-roast ¼ cup of peanuts until they turn crunchy. 2. Add 2 tbsp sesame, 2 tsp poppy seeds and 2 tbsp dry coconut. 3. Dry roast on a low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 4. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 5. Add a small piece of tamarind, add water and grind to a smooth paste. Keep aside. 6. In a kadai, heat 3 tablespoons of oil. Add ½ tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, ¼ tsp methi, a pinch of hing and a few curry leaves. 7. Splutter the tempering. Also add ½ tsp of turmeric, 5 cloves of garlic, and 1 inch of ginger, and sauté well. 8. Now add 4 chilli and saute for a minute. 9. Keeping the flame on low, add 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala. and 1 tsp salt. Sauté till the spices turn aromatic. 10. Further add prepared masala paste and cook till the oil separates. 11. Add 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp jaggery, 3 cups of hot water. 12. Mix well and boil for 10 minutes or until the oil separates. 13. Finally, enjoy Mirchi Ka Salan Recipe with Biryani.

title: Misal Pav Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups matki sprouts, 3.5 tablespoon oil, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.1875 teaspoon hing, 1 cup onions, 3.5 garlic cloves, 1 inch ginger, 2 tablespoons desiccated coconut, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.625 tablespoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 3 tablespoons goda masala, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 1 teaspoon tamarind paste, 4.5 cup water, 8 pav buns, 2 tablespoons butter, 0.25 cup onions, 1 lemon, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 cup farsan thick sev
instructions: 1. Rinse half cup matki well and soak them in enough water for 8 hours. Later, drain the water completely and wrap them in a clean cloth or add to a box. Cover well and keep it in a dark warm place for 12 to 24 hours to sprout. 2. Depending on the temperature they will sprout. If the weather is too hot, sprinkle some water on them when the cloth dries up. 3. Grate or grind together ginger garlic and coconut in a small grinder. Keep this aside. 4. Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard and cumin seeds. When they splutter add curry leaves. 5. Saute till the leaves turn crisp. Then stir in hing. Add onions and saute until translucent. Then add ginger garlic coconut paste & saute till it turns aromatic for 2 to 3 minutes. 6. Add tomatoes and salt. Cook until the tomatoes turn completely mushy and soft. 7. Add chili powder, misal masala or goda masala & turmeric. Mix and cook until oil begins to separate, just for 2 minutes. 8. Next add the sprouts, tamarind and pour water as needed to make the kat or tarry. If you prefer more kat you can pour upto 5 cups like I did. For a less kat, add 3 cups. 9. Mix everything and bring it to a rolling boil. Reduce to medium heat and cook for 5 minutes till the oil floats on top and the sprouts are cooked, yet firm. 10. Taste test and add more salt or misal masala or goda masala if needed. 11. Slice the each pav to 2, keeping one of the sides intact. Spread soft butter on one side and toast them on a pan. Alternately you may place all the buttered pavs on a prepared baking tray and bake for 3 minutes in the top rack. Adjust the time as required, no preheating required. 12. Transfer misal to 4 serving bowls. Sprinkle lemon juice, onions, coriander leaves and then farsan. 13. Serve misal pav immediately.

title: misal pav recipe | how to make maharashtrian misal pav recipe
ingredients: 2 cup moth beans matki, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 2 tsp oil, 2 inch ginger, 1 onion, 2 clove garlic, 0.25 cup dry coconut kopra, 1 to mato, 0.25 cup water, 3 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp jeera cumin, few curry leaves, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli pwoder, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala goda masala, piece jaggery gud, 0.5 tsp salt, 5 cup water, 2 cup farsan mixture, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 6 pav, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 3 tbsp oil and splutter 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp jeera and few curry leaves. 2. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and 1 tsp garam masala. 3. saute on low flame till spices turn aromatic. 4. now add in prepared masala paste and saute well. 5. cook till the oil is released from masala paste. 6. add in cooked matki, small piece jaggery and ½ tsp salt. give a good mix. 7. also add 5 cup water and adjust consistency. 8. cover and boil for 10 minutes or till misal is cooked completely. 9. once the misal is cooked, oil starts to float indicating misal is ready. 10. in a serving plate, take matki usal add some farsan over it. 11. top with chopped onions and coriander leaves over it. 12. also pour ladleful of kat or gravy from sides. 13. finally, serve misal with pav and lemon wedges making a complete misal pav recipe.

title: mishti doi recipe | bengali sweet yoghurt or curd recipe | mitha dahi
ingredients: 3 cups milk, 2 tbsp sugar, 2 tbsp brown sugar, 1 tsp water, 2 tsp curd yogurt mishti doi, few dry fruits
instructions: 1. firstly, in a thick bottomed nonstick pan heat 3 cups of milk. 2. stir occasionally, and get milk to boil. 3. further add 2 tbsp of sugar and stir well. 4. boil the milk on medium flame till it thickens. 5. stir occasionally, till the milk reduces to half. 6. meanwhile, in a thick bottomed pan take 2 tbsp brown sugar. 7. add in a tsp of water and mix well. 8. keeping the flame on medium, stir till the sugar caramelises. 9. transfer the caramelised sugar into boiled milk. 10. stir well and get the milk to another boil. 11. now allow it to cool completely. 12. once the milk is cooled and yet slightly warm, transfer into a clay pot or any container. 13. add in a tsp of curd and stir well. 14. cover and allow it to set in warm place for 8 hours or till it sets completely. 15. then refrigerate for 2 hours to get nice creamy texture. also garnish with chopped nuts. 16. finally, serve mishti doi chilled garnished with few chopped dry fruits.

title: Mix Veg Paratha (Vegetable Paratha)
ingredients: 1 cup atta, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon ajwain, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 cup potatoes, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup cauliflower, 0.25 cup green peas, 0.25 cup methi leaves, 1 Handful coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Steam or boil veggies as desired until soft. I do over a pressure cooker. It just takes around 5 to 7 mins to steam the veggies. 2. Begin to mash them using a masher. They don’t need to cool down, you can do it right away when they are still hot. 3. Make sure there are no bits and chunks of veggies. The veggies must be mashed to very smooth. 4. Add finely chopped leafy greens. I used methi and coriander leaves. Also add ginger garlic paste, garam masala, red chilli powder, salt and ajwain 5. Mix up well, taste it and check if there is enough salt. It must be moderately salty. If needed add more. 6. Add whole wheat flour. 7. Begin to mix without using any water. If it doesn’t come together to form the dough, then sprinkle some water or the vegetable stock and form the dough. 8. The dough must be soft and non sticky. 9. Divide the dough to 5 to 6 balls. 10. Sprinkle flour over the rolling board and a ball. Flatten and begin to roll it. 11. Roll to a 6 to 7 inches paratha of even thickness. 12. Heat a griddle or tawa. When hot enough, transfer the paratha to the tawa. 13. Flip it when you see tiny bubbles. Drizzle few drops of ghee or oil. Spread it all over the paratha. 14. Cook on both the sides until well cooked with golden to light brown spots on them. Place a kitchen cloth or tissue in a plate. Stack the vegetable parathas over it to keep them soft. Continue to make the rest of the parathas and fry them. Serve veg paratha with chutney or pickle.

title: Mix Veg Paratha Recipe | Dhaba Style Mix Vegetable Paratha
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 carrot, 5 tbsp cauliflower, 4 tbsp peas, 5 beans, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 0.5 tsp kala namak, 1 tsp salt, 3 boiled potato, 1 cup paneer, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, wheat flour dough, oil, butter
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan, heat 2 tbsp oil, add 1 tsp cumin and a pinch of hing. 2. Add 2 chillies, 1 inch of ginger, and sauté well. 3. Also add 1 carrot, 5 tbsp cauliflower, 4 tbsp peas, 5 beans and saute well. 4. Mash the vegetables, making sure they're cooked uniformly. 5. Now add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, ½ tsp aamchur, ½ tsp kala namak and 1 tsp salt. 6. Sauté on a low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 7. Now add 3 boiled potatoes, 1 cup paneer, 2 tbsp coriander and 2 tbsp kasuri methi. 8. Mix well, ensuring all the masalas are thoroughly combined. Keep aside. 9. Now pinch a ball-sized dough and roll it about 4 inches in diameter. 10. Place a ball-sized prepared mix veg stuffing in the centre. 11. Take the edge and start pleating, bringing it to the centre. 12. Also, join the pleats together and secure tight pinching off excess dough. 13. Sprinkle some wheat flour and roll slightly thick. 14. On a hot tawa, place the rolled paratha and cook for a minute. 15. Furthermore, flip the paratha when the base is partly cooked (after a minute). 16. Also, brush oil/ghee and press slightly. Flip again once or twice till both sides are cooked properly. 17. Finally, serve hot Mix Veg Paratha with sauce, raita or pickle.

title: mix veg recipe | mix vegetable | mixed vegetable curry | mix veg curry
ingredients: 3 tsp oil, 12 cubes paneer cottage cheese, 2 tbsp almonds badam, 1 potato aloo, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 cup cauliflower gobi, 4 beans, 0.25 cup peas matar, 0.25 capsicum, 2 tomato, 1 inch cinnamon, 5 clove 5 clove, 2 pods cardamom, 12 almonds badam, 4 tsp oil, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 2 tsp kasuri methi, 1 green chilli, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 cup curd yogurt, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp cream malai, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly heat 4 tsp oil and saute spices. 2. saute 1 onion and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 3. further add spices and 1 tsp salt. saute well. 4. add tomato puree and cook well. 5. add ½ cup whisked curd. stir continuously. 6. now add roasted mix vegetables and mix well. 7. add ½ cup water and cook for another 10 minutes. 8. turn off the flame and add 2 tbsp cream, 1 tsp kasuri methi and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 9. finally, serve mix vegetable curry / mix veg recipe with hot roti.

title: Mixed Dal Dosa (Lentil Dosa)
ingredients: 0.25 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup green gram, 0.25 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup toor dal, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.625 cup water, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons oil
instructions: 1. Wash the lentils in a large bowl at least thrice. Drain the water and add fresh water. Soak for at least 4 hrs. You can also soak it overnight. 2. Drain the water completely. Add them to a blender jar along with ginger, chili, cumin and salt. Pour ½ cup water and blend to a smooth batter. 3. If you prefer crisp dosas then add in less water. Blend to a thick batter. If you prefer soft dosas add more water while blending and make a slightly runny batter. Taste test and add more salt if required. 4. Grease a dosa tawa or a griddle lightly. Heat it well on a medium flame. 5. When the pan is hot enough, pour ¾ cup ladle batter to the center. Quickly spread it to a thin dosa. 6. Cook on a medium heat. Drizzle some oil around the edges. 7. This step is optional. With the help of the spatula, gently scrape off the excess batter and spread it evenly. 8. When the dosa is cooked, the edges will begin to leave the pan. Flip and cook the dosa on the other side until brown spots appear. 9. Flip again and cook until crisp. Remove the dosa to a plate and make the next one the same way. 10. Serve mixed dal dosa with chutney or pickle.

title: mixture recipe | south indian mixture recipe | spicy kerala mixture
ingredients: 1 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, pinch of hing asafoetida, salt to taste, water as required, oil to grease mould and deep fry, 1 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, pinch of hing asafoetida, 0.25 tsp baking soda, salt to taste, water as required, oil to grease mould and deep fry, 0.25 cup peanuts groundnuts, 0.25 cup fried gram dal putani, few curry leaves, 0.25 cup cashews kaju, 0.5 cup thick poha flattened rice beaten rice, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, pinch of hing asafoetida, 1 tsp sugar, salt to taste
instructions: 1. firstly, crush the prepared sev / omapodi slightly. 2. also add in prepared boondi. 3. in the same oil fry ¼ cup peanuts, ¼ cup fried gram dal, ¼ cup cashews and few curry leaves separately. 4. also fry ½ cup thick poha till they turn crisp. drain them onto kitchen paper. 5. add the fried nuts and poha into the mixture bowl. 6. additionally, add in 1 tsp chilli powder, pinch of hing, 1 tsp sugar and salt to taste. 7. mix all the ingredients till the spices gets combined well. 8. finally, mixture is ready. store in a airtight container once cooled completely and serve for a month or more.

title: Modak Recipe | Ukadiche Modak With & Without Muold
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee, 2 cup coconut, 1 cup jaggery, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 2 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp ghee, 2 cup rice flour
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 tsp ghee and saute 2 cup coconut. 2. Saute until the coconut turns aromatic. 3. Now add 1 cup of jaggery and mix well. 4. Keep cooking on medium flame until the jaggery melts. 5. Continue to cook until the mixture thickens and still remains moist. 6. Now add ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. Stuffing is ready, keep it aside. 7. Firstly, in a large kadai take 2 cup water, ½ tsp salt, and 1 tsp ghee. 8. Mix well and bring the water to a boil. 9. Further, add 2 cup rice flour and mix gently. 10. Mix until the rice flour absorbs all the water. 11. Cover and rest for 3 minutes. 12. Now transfer to a large bowl and start to knead the dough. Wet your hand to prevent it from burning. 13. Knead for 5 minutes or until the dough turns soft. Wet your hand and knead if the dough seems dry. 14. Now take a ball-sized rice flour dough and roll. 15. With the help of both thumbs start pressing the edges and create a dent in the center. 16. Slowly press from the edges till it forms a cup. 17. Start creating pleats with your index finger and thumb. 18. Now scoop a tbsp of prepared coconut-jaggery stuffing. 19. Get the pleats together to form a bundle. 20. Close the top by pinching and making it pointed. 21. Grease the modak mould with ghee to prevent it from sticking. 22. Stuff the rice flour dough into the mould walls. 23. Also, place a small ball-sized mixture in the centre. 24. Close tightly and scrape off the excess mixture. 25. Unmould the modak gently without breaking. 26. Place the modak in steamer leaving gaps in between. 27. cover and steam the modak for 10 minutes or till the shiny texture appears on them. 28. Finally, of Ukadiche Modak to lord Ganesha and celebrate Ganesh Chathurthi. 29. Firstly, in a large kadai, heat 1 tsp ghee and saute 2 cups of coconut. 30. Sauté until the coconut turns aromatic. 31. Keep cooking on a medium flame until the jaggery melts. 32. Firstly, in a large kadai, take 2 cups of water, ½ tsp salt, and 1 tsp ghee. 33. Further, add 2 cups of rice flour and mix gently. 34. Mix until the rice flour absorbs all the water. Cover and rest for 3 minutes. 35. Now take a ball-sized rice flour dough and roll it. 36. With the help of both thumbs, start pressing the edges and create a dent in the centre. 37. Now, scoop a tbsp of prepared coconut-jaggery stuffing. 38. Place the modak in a steamer, leaving gaps in between. Cover and steam the modak for 10 minutes or till the shiny texture appears on them.

title: Modak Recipe With & Without Mould
ingredients: 2 cups coconut, 1 cup jaggery, 4 green cardamoms, 0.125 teaspoon nutmeg powder, 1 teaspoon ghee, 1 cup rice flour, 1 cup water, 1 pinch salt, 1 teaspoon ghee, 1 teaspoon ghee
instructions: 1. Add 1 teaspoon ghee to a hot pan. 2. Saute together jaggery and coconut until both blend well and the mixture turns thick. Do not overcook. There should be no excess moisture in the pan. (check video to know consistency) 3. Add cardamom powder and nutmeg. Turn off the stove and cool this completely. 4. Bring 1 cup water to a rolling boil along with salt and ghee. 5. Regulate the flame to low, then add the flour and give a good mix. Ignore the dough looking dry and crumbly at this stage. 6. Turn off the stove and cover the pot. Set aside and let the dough steam in the residual heat. Rest until the temperature comes down a bit. 7. While the mixture is still slightly hot, knead it to a soft non-sticky yet smooth crack-free dough. The mixture will look dry so sprinkle little hot or warm water and knead. I sprinkle about 2 to 3 tablespoons at this stage. (check video or step-by-step photos) 8. Divide the dough to make 11 equal sized balls. Keep the balls covered with a damp cloth until you finish making all of the modak. 9. Ensure the coconut filling has cooled down completely before you begin to make the modak. Take each ball to your palm and smoothen them to a crack-free and soft ball. 10. Grease the mould lightly with ghee. Close the mould and keep a dough ball inside. Press with your finger to spread the dough towards the sides of the mould. 11. Then stuff the sweet coconut and seal the modak carefully. (Check video or step-by-step pictures). Open the mould and transfer the modak to a greased steam basket. 12. Take a ball and make a dent in the center. Dip your fingers in water. 13. In a circular motion begin to spread the dough to make a thin disc using thumb and forefingers on both the hands. 14. Pinch off little dough on the outer side of the disc to make pleats at a distance of ½ to ¾ inch. (refer pics above). 15. If the edges break, keep dipping your fingers in water and moisten the dough. 16. Place 1 to 1½ tbsp coconut mixture in the center. 17. Bring together all the pleats and join them carefully. If needed moisten the fingers and join them. 18. Next place all the modak in a greased steam basket. You can skip greasing and just spread a moist cloth in the steam basket. You can also sprinkle some saffron if you like. 19. Place a rack/trivet in a steamer or pot or pressure cooker. Pour 1½ cups water & bring to a rolling boil on a medium high heat. 20. Place the steam basket inside the pot or cooker and cover with the lid. If steaming in a regular pot, your lid should have a vent for the steam to escape. If using a pressure cooker, remove the weight/whistle. Steam the modaks on a medium high heat for 10 minutes. 21. The steaming timer starts from the time you cover the pot/ cooker. After 10 to 12 mins, turn off the stove and let the modaks rest in the cooker for 5 mins. 22. Remove the steam basket and place on a cooling rack. 23. Pour few drops of ghee over the modaks. Offer them to Lord Ganesha and serve as prashad. 24. If using a instant pot, Press saute button and pour 1½ cups water in the steel insert. Place a long legged trivet. When the water turns hot, place the modak basket inside and press cancel. Cover the Instant pot. 25. Position the steam release valve to venting. Press Steam button. Since the timer won't work for this option, use an external timer. 26. After 10 minutes, press cancel. Wait for another 5 to 10 mins, remove the basket and pour a few drops of ghee.

title: Mohabbat Ka Sharbat Recipe | Mohabbat Sharbat Watermelon Juice
ingredients: 4 cup milk, ice cubes, 2 tbsp rooh afza, 0.25 cup sugar, 2 tbsp sabja seeds, 2 cup watermelon
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take 4 cup of chilled milk. Make sure to use full-fat cream milk. 2. Add ice cubes to make the milk extra chilled. 3. Also, add 2 tbsp rooh afza and ¼ cup sugar. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. Now add 2 cups chopped watermelon, 2 tbsp sabja seeds and mix well. The small pieces of watermelon give a nice crunch in the milk. 5. Further to make it extra flavourful, you can add more rooh afza depending on the flavour. 6. Finally, mix well and enjoy Watermelon Milkshake or Mohabbat Ka Sharbat.

title: Mohanthal Recipe | Halwai Style Mohanthal Mihai
ingredients: 3 cup besan, 0.25 cup ghee, 0.25 cup milk, 1 cup ghee, 0.5 cup milk, 1.5 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, pinch saffron food colour, 0.5 cup khova mawa, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, silver vark, dry fruits
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 cup besan, ¼ cup ghee and ¼ cup milk. 2. crumble and mix until the besan turns moist. 3. keep rubbing, until the besan turns grainy texture. 4. now sieve the besan using a large holes sieve. 5. the besan turns grainy texture. keep aside. 6. in a large kadai heat 1 cup ghee and add besan mixture. 7. keep roasting the besan on low flame. 8. after roasting for 20 minutes, the besan turns golden brown and ghee releases from sides. 9. now add ½ cup milk and mix continuously. 10. the mixture turns frothy forming a more grainy texture. 11. keep cooking the besan until the milk is absorbed completely. 12. transfer to a bowl and keep aside. 13. in a large kadai add 1½ cup sugar and ½ cup water. 14. boil until 1 string consistency of sugar syrup is attained. 15. add pinch saffron food colour and mix well. adding colour is optional, however, it enhances the colour of burfi. 16. further, add ½ cup khova and mix well. mix until the khova is well combined in sugar syrup. 17. now add roasted besan mixture into the sugar syrup. 18. mix well making sure the besan is well combined. 19. continue to cook until the mixture starts to separate the pan. 20. also, add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 21. transfer the mixture into the tray lined with baking paper. 22. tap and level up forming an even top. 23. rest for 4 hours, or you can refrigerate to set quickly. 24. once the burfi has set completely, unmould and decorate with silver vark. 25. chop into the desired shape and garnish with dry fruits. 26. finally, enjoy mohanthal for a week when refrigerated.

title: moist chocolate cake recipe in cooker | super moist eggless chocolaty cake
ingredients: 1.25 cup sugar, 0.75 cup oil, 0.5 cup buttermilk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 2 cup maida plain flour, 0.5 cup cocoa powder, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tsp coffee powder, 0.25 cup water, 2 cup heavy cream whipping cream, 1 cup icing sugar, 0.5 cup cocoa powder, 1 tsp vanilla extract
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1¼ cup sugar, ¾ cup oil, ½ cup buttermilk and 1 tsp vanilla extract. 2. whisk and mix well until sugar melts completely. 3. place a sieve and add 2 cup maida, ½ cup cocoa powder, 1 tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp baking soda, ¼ tsp salt and 1 tsp coffee powder. 4. sieve the flour making sure there are no lumps. 5. now using cut and fold method, mix the batter. 6. add ¼ cup water or as required and prepare a smooth consistency batter. 7. to prepare the cake in a pressure cooker add in 2 cup of salt and close the lid of cooker without keeping the gasket and whistle. heat for 5 to 10 minutes. as a result, gives a preheat oven atmosphere. 8. now transfer the cake batter to a cake mould (dia: 7 inch, height: 4 inch). make sure to line a butter paper at the bottom to prevent from sticking. 9. and also tap twice to level up uniformly and remove any air bubbles if present. 10. place the cake pan into preheated cooker. 11. cover and cook on a medium flame for 45 minutes. you can alternatively, preheat and bake at 180-degree celsius for 45 minutes. 12. insert a toothpick and check if the cake has baked completely. 13. cool the cake and then unmould the chocolate cake. 14. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup heavy cream. you can use whipping cream or any cream with 35% milkfat. 15. now add 1 cup icing sugar, ½ cup cocoa powder and 1 tsp vanilla extract. 16. beat on low speed until stiff peaks appear. 17. chocolate frosting is ready. keep it refrigerated until you use. 18. once the cake is cooled completely, level up the top. 19. spread the chocolate frosting generously on top and sides. 20. level up using an offset spatula. 21. finally, enjoy moist chocolate cake or refrigerate and consume within a week.

title: momo manchurian recipe | manchurian momos | veg manchurian momos
ingredients: 1.5 cup maida, 0.5 tsp salt, water, oil, 3 tsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 2 cup cabbage, 1 carrot, 1 tsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp spring onion, 2 tbsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp spring onion, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp carrot, 3 tbsp cabbage, 3 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tsp chilli sauce, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 cup corn flour slurry, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 cup water, 2 tbsp spring onion
instructions: 1. firstly, in a kadai heat 3 tsp oil. 2. add 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp spring onion. stir fry on high flame. 3. now add ½ onion, saute until the onions shrink slightly. 4. further add 2 cup cabbage, 1 carrot and stir fry. 5. additionally, add 1 tsp soy sauce, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ½ tsp salt. 6. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 7. finally, add 2 tbsp spring onion and mix well. vegetable stuffing for momos is ready. 8. firstly, in a large bowl take 1½ cup maida and ½ tsp salt. mix well. 9. now add water as required and start to knead the dough. 10. knead for 5 minutes, or until the dough turns non-sticky and soft. 11. grease the dough with oil, cover and rest for 20 minutes. 12. after 20 minutes, knead the dough slightly. 13. pinch a small ball sized dough and roll uniformly. 14. roll to slightly thin thickness. 15. now place a tbsp of prepared vegetable stuffing. 16. start to pleat from one side without damaging. 17. bring all together and twist to seal tight. 18. place the momos into the steamer. make sure to grease the plate to prevent momos from sticking. 19. steam for 10 minutes, or until a shiny layer appears. 20. finally, veg momos are ready. keep aside. 21. firstly, in a large wok heat 2 tbsp oil. add 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp spring onion and 2 tbsp onion. 22. stir fry until the onions shrink slightly. 23. further add 2 tbsp carrot, 3 tbsp cabbage and 3 tbsp capsicum. 24. stir fry making sure all the vegetables are cooked yet crunchy. 25. keeping the flame on high, add 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tsp chilli sauce, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ¼ tsp salt. 26. stir fry until the sauces are well combined. 27. now add 1 cup corn flour slurry and mix well. to prepare the slurry, i have mixed 2 tsp cornflour in 1 cup water. 28. keep cooking until the slurry thickens and turns glossy. 29. for that extra spice, add ½ tsp chilli powder and ¼ cup water. 30. mix well forming a smooth saucy texture. 31. now add steamed momos add 2 tbsp spring onion. 32. mix gently, making sure all the sauce is well coated to momos. 33. finally, enjoy vegetable momos manchurian topped with more spring onions.

title: Mooli Paratha (Radish Paratha)
ingredients: 2 cups wheat flour, 1 teaspoon oil, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 4 cups radish, 0.625 inch ginger, 1.5 green chilies, 3.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.25 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon ajwain
instructions: 1. Mix together grated radish with salt and let rest for 7 to 8 mins. Squeeze out the juices from the radish using your hands and place it in a mixing bowl. 2. To another mixing bowl, add the flour and oil. Start adding the radish juices little by little and begin to form a soft non-sticky dough. (You will only need about ½ cup+ 2 tbsps juices. Store the rest in the fridge to use later) 3. Knead the dough well until smooth and divide to 8 portions. Cover and keep aside. 4. Squeeze the grated radish once more to remove any more moisture in it. Add the grated radish to the bowl along with chili, ginger, coriander leaves, cumin powder, turmeric and garam masala. Mix everything well. The stuffing should be dry and not too wet. 5. You may taste test and adjust any other spice but do not add salt. Roughly divide the filling to 8 portions in the bowl. 6. Take 2 to 3 tbsps of flour to a small bowl. Dip a dough ball in the flour and shake off the excess. Sprinkle some flour on the rolling area. 7. Flatten the dough ball and spread it with your fingers to form a small cup (about 4 inches, check pics in the post) 8. Place one portion filling in the center, in the cup and press down the stuffing gently. Sprinkle chaat masala if you want. Bring the edges higher and seal the cup. 9. Flatten with your fingers and place it on the rolling area. Sprinkle a little flour on it and spread gently. Roll it to a 5 to 6 inch paratha. If the paratha breaks, apply little dry flour. This way make 4 parathas and cook them first, later follow the same steps. 10. To fry the paratha, heat a pan on a medium to medium high heat. Dust off the excess flour from the paratha and place it gently on the tawa/pan. 11. Wait until you see bubbles on the mooli paratha. With the help of a turner, turn it to the other side. Spread ghee and let cook until you see bubbles. 12. Reduce the heat to medium. Turn them again and spread more ghee. Fry them pressing down with the turner, until the paratha has some deep golden spots. Make sure they are cooked on the edges as well. 13. Remove and stack on a plate. It is important to dust off the uncooked paratha before placing on the pan. If required, wipe off the pan with a tissue after cooking each paratha. 14. Serve Mooli Paratha hot with yogurt, pickle or chutney. If you want you may sprinkle some chaat masala on the hot parathas.

title: mooli paratha recipe | radish paratha recipe | punjabi mooli ke paratha
ingredients: 2 cups radish muli, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 green chilli, 0.5 tsp kashmiri chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric powder, few coriander leaves, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.75 tsp dry mango powder amchur powder, 1 cup whole wheat flour atta, salt to taste, 1 tsp oil, water as required for kneading, 0.25 cup wheat flour for dusting, ghee
instructions: 1. firstly, take 2 cups of grated radish in a strainer. 2. mix salt and rest for 20 minutes. 3. squeeze the radish juice completely. 4. then add chilli, chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, dry mango powder and coriander leaves. 5. mix well making sure to blend spices well with radish. 6. firstly, in a large mixing bowl, take 1 cup of wheat flour. 7. also add required salt to taste. 8. now add water in batches and knead the dough. 9. finally, cover with moist cloth and rest for atleast 20 minutes. 10. firstly, pinch a medium sized ball dough, roll and flatten it. 11. roll it in a circle of about 5 to 5.5 inches in diameter. 12. place the prepared radish stuffing in the center. 13. also join the pleats together and press the pleats from center. 14. furthermore, sprinkle some flour and roll to of a chapati size. 15. firstly on hot tawa, place the rolled paratha and cook for a minute. 16. then add oil / ghee and press slightly. 17. flip again once or twice till both the sides are cooked properly. 18. finally, serve muli paratha with curd, pickle or curry.

title: Moong Dal Chakli Recipe | Moong Dal Murukku | Pasi Paruppu Murukku
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 1 cup water, 2 cup rice flour, 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp sesame, pinch hing, 1 tsp salt, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, rinse ½ cup of moong dal and put it in a pressure cooker. 2. Add 1 cup of water and pressure cook for 3 whistles. 3. Cool the dal and grind to a smooth paste. 4. Transfer the moong dal paste to the large plate. 5. Also add 2 cup rice flour, 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp sesame, pinch hing and 1 tsp salt. 6. Crumble and mix well, making sure everything is well combined. 7. Add water slowly and start to knead the dough. 8. Knead to smooth and soft dough adding water as required. 9. Now take the star mould and fix it to the chakli maker. 10. Grease the chakli maker with some oil. This prevents the dough from sticking to the mould. 11. Furthermore, make a cylindrical shape out of dough and place the dough inside the maker. 12. Tighten the lid and start preparing chaklis. 13. Press the chakli into the hot oil, keeping the flame on medium.0 14. Flip the murukku and fry on medium flame till they turn crispy from both sides. 15. Furthermore, drain over to remove excess oil. 16. Finally, once cooled, enjoy Instant Moong Dal Chakli or store in an airtight container for 2 weeks.

title: Moong Dal Chilla Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, 0.5416666666666666 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 cup water, 0.75 cup carrot, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 3 tablespoons ghee, 1 cup oats, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3 red chilies, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 cup water
instructions: 1. Add moong dal to a bowl and rinse them well at least thrice. Pour fresh water and soak them for 15 to 20 mins. 2. While the lentils soak, prepare the veggies. (use double the amount of veggies if using oats) 3. Optional - Add the optional ingredients - cumin seeds, red chilies & oats to a grinder jar. Powder them as fine as possible. Keep this aside in a bowl. 4. Rinse the lentils and add them to the grinder along with cumin, ginger, green chilies (optional), turmeric (optional) and ¾ cup water. (cut down to half cup if you soak the lentils longer). Grind to a smooth batter. 5. Optional - Stir in the powdered oats. Add more water as required and make a lump free batter that is of pouring consistency, yet thick. (see pics in the step-by-step photos) 6. Add the veggies and salt. Mix well. Taste test and add more salt if required. The consistency should be thick yet of pouring consistency. 7. Heat a pan/ dosa tawa until it is hot enough but not smoking hot. If using a cast iron pan, season it first and then heat. 8. Pour the batter in the center of the pan and begin to spread it to a round dosa, starting from the center. Make it as thin as possible. (You won't get as thin as a dosa) 9. Drizzle some ghee or oil and cook on a high heat (or medium high heat). When it is done, the edges will loosen from the pan on its own. 10. Turn them to the pother side with a spatula and press down gently all over the chilla and cook until you see brown to deep golden spots. Turn it back and cook on a low heat for 1 to 2 mins so it turns crisp. 11. Remove to a serving plate. To make the next moong dal chilla, make sure the pan is hot but not smoking hot. If it is too hot, the batter will clump up and won't let you spread. 12. Towards the end, you may feel the batter has become thick as it rests. So sprinkle some water if required. I usually sprinkle about 2 tbsps before I make the last 3 chillas. 13. Serve Moong dal chilla with Tomato chutney, Coriander chutney or garlic chutney or with pickle.

title: moong dal chilla recipe | moong dal cheela recipe | moong dal ka chilla
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp coriander, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tbsp water, oil for roasting
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup moong dal for 3 hours. 2. drain off the water and transfer to the mixi jar. 3. also add 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger and 1 tsp cumin. 4. blend to smooth paste adding water if required. 5. transfer the batter to the bowl. 6. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp coriander, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 7. mix well adding 3 tbsp of water forming thick flowing consistency chilla batter. 8. further, pour a ladleful of batter onto a hot tawa and spread gently. 9. pour ½ tsp of olive oil over chilla. 10. cover and allow cooking on a medium flame for a minute. 11. now flip the chilla and cook both sides pressing gently. 12. finally, moong dal chilla is ready to serve with green chutney.

title: Moong Dal Dosa
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 0.875 inch ginger, 1.5 green chilies, 2 red chilies, 0.875 teaspoon cumin, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.125 cup water, 2 tablespoons Ghee
instructions: 1. Rinse moong dal thrice and drain the water. Soak them for 15 to 20 mins. If your lentils are too old, use warm water. 2. Drain the water and rinse the lentils well. Add them to a grinder jar along with salt, ginger, green chilies, red chilies, cumin and 1 cup water. If you soak the lentils longer, use less water. 3. Grind to a smooth thick, pouring and spreadable consistency. If the batter is too thick, pour more water and blend. I use up ¼ cup more water. (check stepwise photos in the post for consistency) 4. If using cast iron pan, heat it well. Once hot drizzle a few drops of oil and spread it well with a cut onion or a kitchen tissue (careful! hot pan). Let the pan become smoking hot, then sprinkle little water and turn down the heat to low. To use a non-stick you just need to heat the pan until medium hot and not smoking hot. 5. Pour a ladle full of batter to the center of the pan. Spread the batter in circular pattern to make a thin moong dal dosa. 6. Sprinkle little ghee towards the edges. Let cook on a medium high heat until golden. When it is cooked you will see the edges come off the pan on their own. 7. Turn the moong dal dosa to the other side and cook for a minute or 2. Turn them back and cook on a low heat for a minute until crispy. Fold the dosa and remove to a serving plate. 8. To make the next dosa, make sure your pan is not too hot but it should be medium hot. If it is too hot, the batter clumps up in the center and won't let you spread it. So it is essential to reduce the heat during the last 2 mins while you are cooking the previous dosa. 9. Serve moong dal dosa hot with your favorite chutney.

title: Moong Dal Dosa Recipe - Instant High Protein Breakfast
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 2 tbsp rice flour, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp cumin, oil for roasting
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl rinse 1 cup moong dal and soak in water for 2 hours. 2. Drain off the water and transfer to the mixer jar. 3. Also, add 2 tbsp rice flour, 1 tsp salt and ½ tsp cumin. 4. Grind to a smooth batter, adding water as required. 5. Transfer the batter to a bowl and mix continuously for 2 minutes. 6. To prepare the dosa, heat the dosa pan and pour the moong dal dosa batter. 7. spread as thin as possible. 8. Once the dosa is cooked on top and well roasted. 9. Scrape the dosa from the sides and fold half. 10. Finally, enjoy Moong Dal Dosa Recipe with green chutney.

title: moong dal halwa | moong ki daal ka halwa | moong dal sheera
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, water, 0.5 cup ghee clarified butter, 1 tbsp rava semolina suji, 2 cup milk, 1 cup sugar, 5 cashew, 5 almond, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, rinse the moong dal and soak for 2 hours. 2. drain off the water and transfer to a blender. 3. blend to a slightly coarse paste adding water if required. keep aside. 4. in a large kadai heat ½ cup ghee and add 1 tbsp rava. 5. roast on low flame until the rava turns aromatic. adding rava helps to give good texture to halwa. 6. now add in prepared moong dal paste and mix well. 7. keeping the flame on medium, mix until the moong dal absorbs the ghee. 8. continue to cook breaking the lumps. 9. cook until the mixture turns crumble and grainy texture. 10. after 20 minutes, the mixture turns crisp. 11. now add 2 cup milk, few saffron. you can alternatively use water or milk and water combination. 12. stir continuously, until the mixture absorbs all the milk. 13. the mixture will start to hold shape leaving the kadai. 14. now add 1 cup sugar and mix well. 15. the sugar starts to melt and turns the mixture watery. 16. continue to cook on low flame breaking lumps in between. 17. cook until the mixture starts to separate the ghee and turns golden brown. take approximately 60 minutes. 18. now add 5 cashew, 5 almond and ½ tsp cardamom powder. mix well. 19. finally, enjoy moong dal halwa garnished with more nuts.

title: Moong Dal Halwa Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 6 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 tablespoon suji, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 cup milk, 1 pinch saffron strands, 7 tablespoons sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 6 almonds, 6 pistachios
instructions: 1. Rinse moong dal thrice and soak it for 30 mins. Drain the water completely and add it to a grinder jar. 2. Blend it to a almost smooth but very slightly coarse texture. Scrape the sides and blend. If needed you may sprinkle little water if your grinder gets jammed. 3. To a heavy bottom nonstick pan, add 6 tbsps ghee and heat it. 4. Add semolina and fry for about a minute until it gets roasted well. 5. Next transfer the ground moong dal and begin to incorporate it into the ghee stirring constantly. 6. Keep the flame to medium. Continue stirring & frying. 7. After 10 to 15 mins the dal mixture will turn crumbly and may have small lumps. 8. Begin to break down the lumps and continue to fry on a low flame. 9. When the moong dal is done frying, you will begin to smell it good and it turns to deep golden color. 10. Pour milk & water (both at room temperature) slowly stirring the dal. Also add the saffron. 11. Cook stirring and ensure there are no lumps. 12. The entire liquid will be absorbed soon. 13. When the mixture turns thick add sugar and continue. The mixture turns gooey and then turns thick like a mass. 14. Add 2 tbsps ghee and cardamom powder or rose water. Adding ghee at this stage makes sauteing the halwa easier. You may skip if you are able to do it. 15. Mix and cook till the moong dal halwa begins to leave the sides of the pan. 16. Toast the nuts until crunchy in a different pan. Garnish the halwa with the nuts. 17. Serve moong dal halwa hot or warm.

title: moong dal kachori recipe | moong dal ki kachori | moong ki kachori
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, water, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tsp fennel, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli, 1 coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur, 0.25 ginger powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup besan, 2.5 cup maida, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tbsp ghee clarified butter, water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl soak ½ cup moong dal for 2 hours. 2. drain off the water and transfer to a mixi jar. 3. pulse and grind to a coarse paste. keep aside. 4. in a pan heat 1 tsp ghee. add 1 tsp fennel, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing. 5. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 6. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli, 1 coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur, ¼ ginger powder and ½ tsp salt. 7. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 8. now add ¼ cup besan and roast on low flame until the besan turns aromatic. 9. add in coarsely ground moong dal. 10. mix and cook for 5 minutes or until the moong dal is well combined. 11. moong dal stuffing is ready. keep aside to cool completely. 12. firstly, in a large bowl take 2½ cup maida, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 13. pour in 3 tbsp hot ghee and mix well. 14. crumble and mix making sure the flour is moist. 15. now add water and start to knead the dough. 16. add water as required and knead to a smooth and soft dough. 17. grease the dough with oil, cover and rest for 30 minutes. 18. after 30 minutes, knead the dough slightly. 19. pinch a small ball sized dough and tuck well. 20. grease with oil and roll gently. 21. place a small ball sized prepared moong dal stuffing. 22. start to pleat and seal tight. pinch off excess dough. 23. now roll gently, having a uniform thickness. 24. drop in hot oil, keeping the flame on low. 25. do not touch until the kachori start to float by itself. takes approximately 3 minutes. 26. carefully flip over and fry both sides on low flame. 27. fry until the kachori turns golden brown and crisp. 28. drain off the kachori over kitchen paper making sure to remove excess oil. 29. finally, enjoy moong dal kachori with imli chutney.

title: Moong Dal Ladoo
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 0.75 cup sugar, 4 green cardamom, 6.5 tablespoons ghee, 2 split cashews, 15 raisins
instructions: 1. For this recipe you will need 1 cup organic moong dal. I prefer to rinse my lentils before using, this is optional but suggested. I wash it a few times and drain them completely to a colander. 2. Shake off the colander to remove excess water. Spread it on a clean cotton cloth, preferably white. Rub it with the cloth and leave it for 10 mins. You can also sun dry this. Dal need not be dry completely. This step is completely optional but highly recommended if making for kids. 3. Begin to roast the dal on a medium to low heat stirring often until the dal turns aromatic and golden. You can also roast the dal in 1 tbsp ghee if you want. 4. Dal turns crunchy when it is roasted completely. You can eat a few and check if it is still raw. There should not be even little raw taste in the dal. Add the cardamom seeds from 4 pods or the entire pod. 5. Cool completely and add to a blender jar. Make a fine powder but not like super smooth flour. We don’t sieve the flour since slight coarse texture gives crunch to the moong dal ladoo. If you make very fine flour, the ladoos will stick to your mouth. Set this aside. 6. Add ¾ cup sugar to the same jar and powder finely. 7. Set this aside as well. Mix both the sugar and flour. You can also store this mixture in a air tight jar for few months. Use this when ever needed to make laddu.

title: Moong Dal Pakoda (Moong Dal Bhajiya)
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 0.75 cup onions, 2 green chilies, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 2 red chilies, 3 sprigs curry leaves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons ginger garlic paste, 0.875 cup oil, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala
instructions: 1. Add moong dal to a large bowl and rinse them well thrice. Drain and soak in fresh water for 60 mins. For softer pakoras soak for 2 hours or soak them in slightly hot water for 1 hour. 2. Drain the water completely and leave the dal in the colander for a while until the water drains completely. 3. Add it to a blender jar along with red chilies. 4. Pulse the dal a couple of times in the blender until you get a coarse mixture. If you prefer soft moong dal pakoda, splash some water and blend it to a smooth yet thick batter. 5. Transfer this to a mixing bowl. 6. Next add in onions, ginger garlic paste, green chilies, coriander leaves, curry leaves, jeera, garam masala and salt. 7. Mix the dough and taste it to check the salt and spice. If required add more. 8. Heat oil in a deep pan. 9. To test if the oil is hot enough, drop a small portion of the batter to the oil. It should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning brown. This is the right temperature. 10. Take small portions of the pakora mixture and drop bite sized portions to the hot oil. 11. Do not disturb the bhajiyas until they rise up. Fry them stirring on a medium high flame until golden and crisp. 12. Drain them to a steel colander or kitchen tissue. Continue to fry the pakoras in batches. 13. Sprinkle chaat masala as desired. Serve moong dal pakora hot with green chutney or sauce. 14. If your air fryer requires preheating, preheat it at 350 F or 180 C for at least 10 minutes. 15. Place small flat portions of the mixture on the greased rack and air fry for 11 to 13 minutes at 350 F or 180 C. Adjust the timings as needed depending on the size of your pakoras. Air fry them until crisp and golden. 16. After air frying I brush the hot pakoras with ghee instead of spraying oil. Sprinkle chaat masala or serve them with chutney or chai.

title: Moong Dal Payasam (Paruppu Payasam)
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 1.5 cup water, 0.625 cup thin coconut milk refer notes, 0.625 cup jaggery, 0.5 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 0.25 teaspoon dry ginger powder, 1 cashews, 1 raisins, 2 tablespoons coconut slices, 2 tablespoons Ghee, 0.25 cup thick coconut milk
instructions: 1. Add moong dal to a cooker or pot. Dry roast on a medium heat till you get a nice aroma. 2. Then rinse the dal very well and pour 1½ cup water. If cooking in a pot you may need to add more water. 3. Pressure cook on a medium flame until soft for 2 whistles. 4. Optional - Skip this step if the jaggery is clean. While the dal cooks, grate and add jaggery to a small pot with 3 to 4 tbsps of water. Boil it on a medium heat until slightly thick yet runny & of pouring consistency. When done set this aside to cool down. 5. When the pressure releases, mash the cooked dal slightly. If you prefer smooth payasam then mash it well. 6. Dal must be fully cooked and should not be even slightly hard. If it is aldente then cook for some more time until soft adding more water if needed. 7. Pour the thin coconut milk or milk to the dal and bring it to a gentle boil on a low flame. 8. Add cardamom powder & ginger powder (optional). 9. Place a filter over the pot and pour jaggery syrup through it to the payasam. Mix it well. 10. Bring the paruppu payasam to a gentle boil. Optionally pour ¼ cup thick coconut milk. 11. Turn off the stove. Do not boil any longer. 12. If using regular milk - Cool the jaggery syrup completely before you add here. If you pour the hot jaggery syrup to boiling payasam, it will curdle most of the times. So make sure you cool it. Also turn off the stove and cook no longer. 13. Heat ghee in a small pan. Fry coconut on a medium high flame until slightly golden. Remove to a small bowl and add cashews to the ghee. Fry until light golden. Then add the raisins and stir until they become plump. 14. Pour this to the paruppu payasam.

title: moong dal puri recipe | moong dal ki puri | moong dal poori
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 0.25 cup water, 2 cup wheat flour, 2 tbsp rava semolina suji, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi, water, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, soak ½ cup moong dal for 2 hours. 2. drain off the water and blend to a smooth paste adding ¼ cup water. 3. transfer the moong dal paste to a large bowl. 4. further, add 2 cup wheat flour and 2 tbsp rava. 5. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp kasuri methi and ½ tsp salt. 6. mix well combing all the spices well. 7. further, add water as required and knead to a tight dough. 8. add 1 tsp oil and knead again forming a smooth dough. 9. now take a ball and roll using a rolling pin. 10. roll to slightly thick thickness. 11. drop the rolled dough into the hot oil. 12. press until the puri puffs up and splash oil to puff up fully. 13. flip over and fry until it turns golden brown. 14. finally, drain off the moong dal puri and is ready to enjoy with paneer sabzi.

title: Moong Dal Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 0.25 cup onions shallots, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chili, 4.5 curry leaves, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1.75 cups water, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.5 teaspoon amchur, 1 tablespoon ghee, 2.5 garlic cloves, 1 red chilli, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 7 curry leaves, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder
instructions: 1. Add moong dal to a bowl and rinse them thrice. Soak in 1¾ to 2 cups water until you chop and prepare the rest. (If you don't have a cooker, soak them in hot boiling water for 30 mins before you proceed with the below steps) 2. Heat oil in a cooker or pot and add cumin seeds. When the seeds splutter, add ginger, green chilies and 4 to 5 curry leaves. Fry until aromatic for about a minute and add onions if using. Saute until transparent for 2 to 3 mins. 3. Add tomatoes and salt. Saute for 1 to 2 mins. Stir in turmeric, red chilli powder and garam masala. To bring out the flavors, optionally saute for 1 to 2 mins. 4. Add the moong dal along with the soaked water. Mix well to deglaze the bottom of the cooker. Cover and pressure cook for 4 to 5 whistles on a medium flame. 5. When the pressure drops, open the lid and add kasuri methi and amchur. If you prefer you can mash the moong dal a bit. Taste test and add more salt if required. 6. Moong dal is ready at this stage and you can serve it as is. For extra flavour you may proceed to the next steps. 7. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee in a small pan, on a medium flame. Add the crushed garlic and dried red chillies. Let the garlic fry a bit but not brown. 8. Turn down the heat to low and add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and curry leaves. Turn off the heat and stir in red chilli powder and hing. 9. Pour this over the moong dal and mix gently. When the dal cools down a bit squeeze some lemon juice and serve. 10. Pour oil or ghee to the steel insert of the Instant pot and press the saute button. When the oil turns slightly hot, add cumin seeds & curry leaves or bay leaf, followed by ginger, green chilies and onions. 11. Saute onions for 1 to 2 mins. Stir in turmeric, red chilli powder & garam masala. Press Cancel. Add tomatoes, moong dal & pour water. Deglaze the pot well. 12. Optionally if you want, you may place a long legged trivet and place your rice bowl over that. You will use only short grain rice here. The cook time for basmati won’t match. 13. Secure the IP with the lid and position the steam release valve to sealing. Press the pressure cook button and set the timer to 10 minutes. 14. Let pressure drop naturally. Open the lid, add kasuri methi & give a good stir. Taste test and adjust salt. If the consistency is too thick, you may pour some boiling hot water and mix. 15. Though you can serve the moong dal at this stage, since this recipe is for a moong dal tadka, make an additional tadka (next step). 16. Heat ghee in a small pan on a medium heat. Add crushed garlic cloves and red chilies. When the garlic begins to smell good, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and curry leaves (optional). 17. Turn off the stove and stir in red chilli powder and hing (optional). Pour this all over the Instant pot moong dal. Before serving give a gentle mix and add some coriander leaves. Squeeze in some lemon juice.

title: Moong Dal Tadka Recipe - Restaurant Style & Pro Tips
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, 2 cup water, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 red chilli, pinch hing, 3 chilli, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tomato, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp ghee, 5 cloves garlic, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 red chilli, 0.25 tsp chilli powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup moong dal, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, and 2 cup water. 2. Pressure cook for 2 whistles. 3. Once the pressure releases, add 1 cup water and mix well. This helps to prevent moong dal from turning mushy. Keep aside. 4. In a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tsp cumin, 1 dried red chilli and pinch hing. 5. Saute on low flame until the spices are aromatic. 6. Further add 3 chilli, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger. Saute till they turn aromatic. 7. Now add 1 onion and saute well. 8. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder. Saute till the spices turn aromatic. 9. Add 1 tomato and saute till the tomatoes turn mushy. You can also add water to make the tomato mushy. 10. Furthermore, add cooked dal, 1 tsp salt and mix well adjusting the consistency as required. 11. Simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavors are well absorbed. 12. To prepare the tadka, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Fry 5 cloves garlic till they turn golden brown. 13. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp cumin, 1 dried red chilli, ¼ tsp chilli powder. 14. Pour the tempering on the dal and add 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 15. Finally, enjoy Moong Dal Tadka Recipe with hot steamed rice.

title: Moong Dal Tadka Recipe - Restaurant Style & Pro Tips
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, 2 cup water, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 red chilli, pinch hing, 3 chilli, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tomato, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp ghee, 5 cloves garlic, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 red chilli, 0.25 tsp chilli powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup moong dal, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, and 2 cup water. 2. Pressure cook for 2 whistles. 3. Once the pressure releases, add 1 cup water and mix well. This helps to prevent moong dal from turning mushy. Keep aside. 4. In a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tsp cumin, 1 dried red chilli and pinch hing. 5. Saute on low flame until the spices are aromatic. 6. Further add 3 chilli, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger. Saute till they turn aromatic. 7. Now add 1 onion and saute well. 8. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder. Saute till the spices turn aromatic. 9. Add 1 tomato and saute till the tomatoes turn mushy. You can also add water to make the tomato mushy. 10. Furthermore, add cooked dal, 1 tsp salt and mix well adjusting the consistency as required. 11. Simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavors are well absorbed. 12. To prepare the tadka, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Fry 5 cloves garlic till they turn golden brown. 13. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp cumin, 1 dried red chilli, ¼ tsp chilli powder. 14. Pour the tempering on the dal and add 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 15. Finally, enjoy Moong Dal Tadka Recipe with hot steamed rice.

title: moong dal toast recipe | moong toast | how to make dal toast recipe
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 2 chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp eno fruit salt, 6 slice bread, butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup moong dal in hot water for 1 hour. 2. drain off the water and transfer to the blender. 3. add 2 green chilli and blend to smooth paste. 4. transfer the moong dal batter to a large bowl. 5. now add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt, ¼ tsp pepper powder and pinch hing. 6. whisk and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 7. further add ½ onion, ½ carrot, ½ capsicum and 2 tbsp coriander. 8. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 9. further, scoop out 2 ladlefuls of batter to a small bowl. 10. add ¼ tsp eno and mix gently. make sure to mix eno in small batches. 11. now toast the bread applying the butter on both sides. 12. now spread 2 tbsp of prepared moong dal batter. 13. flip over and apply the moong dal batter on both sides. 14. toast on medium flame until both the sides are cooked well and turn golden brown. 15. finally, enjoy moong dal toast with butter and tomato sauce.

title: Moong Dal Vada Recipe | Moong Dal Pakoda - Street Style
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp ajwain, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp salt, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, soak 1 cup moong dal for 1 hour. Ensure that you do not over-soak the dal, as this makes the pakoda soft rather than crunchy. 2. Drain off the water and transfer to the mixer jar. 3. Add 1 inch of ginger and grind to a coarse paste. 4. Transfer the moong dal paste to a large plate. 5. Add 2 chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander. 6. Also add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp cumin, ¼ tsp ajwain, ½ onion and 1 tsp salt. 7. Squeeze and mix well, ensuring all the species are thoroughly combined. 8. Drop a small bite-sized mixture into hot oil. 9. Stir occasionally, keeping the flame on medium. 10. Fry till the pakoda turns golden brown and crunchy. Drain off. 11. Finally, enjoy the Moong Dal Pakoda Recipe with green chutney.

title: mosaru kodubale recipe | curd rings recipe | majjige kodbale
ingredients: 0.5 cup curd, 0.5 cup water, 1 tsp cumin jeera, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 chilli, 1 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup rice flour, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, take 1 cup buttermilk into large kadai. 2. also add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 10 curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 chilli, 1 tsp oil and ½ tsp salt. 3. get the buttermilk to a boil. 4. now add 1 cup rice flour. do not mix the flour. 5. cover and simmer for 3 minutes or until the rice flour are cooked. 6. now mix well making sure the water has been absorbed completely. 7. transfer the dough into a large mixing bowl and allow to cool slightly. 8. with wet hands start kneading the dough. 9. pinch a small ball sized dough, roll gently to form a thick and long rope. 10. cut the rope approximately 2 inches length and join the edges to form a ring. 11. now deep fry the kodubale in medium hot oil in batches. or bake in preheated oven at 180 degree celcius for 15 to 20 minutes. 12. and fry the kodubale on medium flame making sure they are cooked completely. 13. finally, enjoy mosaru kodubale with hot tea.

title: motichoor ladoo recipe | motichoor laddu | motichur ladoo
ingredients: 2 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rava semolina suji, 0.25 tsp saffron food colour, 1.5 cup water, oil for frying, 1 cup sugar, 0.5 tsp saffron food colour, 0.5 cup water, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 0.5 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 2 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. firstly, to the hot sugar syrup, add prepared boondi. 2. mix well and make sure to coat all the boondi with sugar syrup. 3. continue to cook for 2 -3 minutes or until sugar syrup thickens. 4. cover and keep aside for 10 minutes. 5. after 10 minutes, sugar syrup is absorbed completely by boondi. 6. add 2 tbsp cashew and 2 tbsp pistachios. mix well. 7. you can see the the mixture is dried and all the sugar syrup has absorbed. 8. prepare the ladoo taking a small amount of boondi. add milk if required, to make moist laddu. 9. finally, motichoor laddo is ready to be served or refrigerate for a week.

title: Mukhwas Recipe | Homemade Mouth Freshener & Digestive Aid
ingredients: 2 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp water, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 0.25 cup white sesame seeds, 0.25 cup black sesame seeds, 0.25 cup fennel seeds saunf, 0.25 cup pumpkin seeds, 0.25 cup sunflower seeds, 0.25 cup flaxseeds, 0.25 cup chia seeds, 0.5 tsp ajwain carom seeds
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 2 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp water, 1 tsp turmeric and 1 tsp salt. 2. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 3. Further add ¼ cup white sesame seeds, ¼ cup black sesame seeds, ¼ cup fennel seeds, ¼ cup pumpkin seeds, ¼ cup sunflower seeds, ¼ cup flaxseeds and ¼ cup chia seeds. 4. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 5. Allow the seeds to soak for 30 minutes so that the seeds turn moist from the inside. 6. After 30 minutes, the water was well absorbed by the seeds. 7. Transfer the soaked seeds to the heavy pan and roast on low flame. 8. Continue to roast on low flame until the seeds turn dry. 9. Once the seeds are almost dried, add ½ tsp ajwain. 10. Continue to roast until the seeds turn crunchy and dry off completely. 11. Cool the seeds and transfer them to the airtight container. 12. Finally, enjoy Mukhwas Recipe for a month.

title: Mullangi Curry (Radish Stir Fry)
ingredients: 25 grams radish, 1 medium tomato, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 green chilli, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 garlic clove, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts, 1 teaspoon Sesame seeds, 2 tablespoons Coconut
instructions: 1. Rinse 2 to 3 medium radish (about 250 grams) thoroughly under running water. If you prefer to use the leaves, open them up and rinse thoroughly. Peel the skin and rinse the radish well. If you are using organic vegetables then you may keep the skin on and just rinse them well. 2. Grate in a food processer or chop them finely. Shake off the water from the leaves and chop them. Keep them separately. 3. On a medium flame, heat 1 to 2 tablespoons oil in a fry pan. Add 4. 1 teaspoon urad dal (optional). Let fry until the dal turns golden. 5. Add 6. 1 slit or chopped green chilli 7. Next add 8. 1 medium tomato chopped (optional) 9. ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. Sauté till the tomatoes turn pulpy and dry. 10. Stir in half teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder or red chili flakes (optional) and saute for a minute. 11. Next add the grated radish. 12. Saute on a high flame till it is cooked, half cooked or full cooked to suit your choice. 13. Add radish leaves or coriander leaves, Fry till they wilt, for about 3 minutes or longer. 14. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons roasted crushed peanuts or 2 tablespoons raw or desiccated coconut or 1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds. Serve mullangi curry with rice or roti.

title: Multigrain Dosa Recipe | Mixed Dal Dosa | High Protein Dosa
ingredients: 3 cup sona masuri rice, 1 cup black chana, 1 cup lobia black eyed pea, 1 cup urad dal, 1 cup mung beans, 1 cup thin poha, 1 tsp methi, water, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take 3 cup sona masuri rice, 1 cup black chana, 1 cup lobia, 1 cup urad dal, 1 cup mung beans, 1 cup thin poha, and 1 tsp methi. 2. Rinse with enough water and soak for 6 hours. 3. After the dal have soaked well, transfer it to the mixer jar. 4. Grind to smooth batter adding water as required. 5. Transfer the batter to a large vessel and mix well with hand. 6. Cover and allow to ferment for 8 hours in a warm place. 7. After 8 hours, the batter would have doubled indicating it is well fermented. 8. Now mix the batter gently and use the batter to prepare dosa adding salt as required. 9. Spread the dosa batter on hot tawa. 10. roast the dosa adding little oil or ghee. roast until the dosa turns golden brown. 11. Finally, the Multigrain Dosa batter can be stored in the refrigerator for up to a week and used to make weekly breakfast.

title: Mung Bean Soup
ingredients: 1 cup mung beans, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic sliced, 0.5 cup tomato - diced, 1 carrot diced, 1 cup pumpkin, 4 cups baby spinach, 2 tablespoons sambar powder, 4 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 1 tablespoon ghee, 2.5 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Rinse 1 cup mung beans / green gram in a colander. Soak them overnight or a minimum of 5 hours in boiling hot water. This step not only cuts down your cook time but also prevents bloating & improves the texture. You can also do this ahead, drain well and refrigerate the mung beans for 4 to 5 days. 2. Prep up the following ingredients: 3. Slice or chop 3 to 4 garlic cloves to make 1 tablespoon 4. Fine chop 1 medium tomato to make ½ cup (I prefer to deseed) 5. Peel and dice a pumpkin piece to 1 inch portions to make 1 cup (or use French beans, sweet potatoes, butternut squash, potatoes or a mix of all) 6. Roughly chop 4 to 5 cups/ handfuls of baby greens like spinach/ kale/ chye sum (can double if you want) 7. handful of chopped coriander leaves to serve – optional 8. Heat 1 tablespon oil or ghee in a sauce pan or pot. Add the ginger garlic & saute for 1 to 2 mins till you begin to smell it good. 9. Add the vegetables including tomatoes (not leafy greens). Saute for 2 to 3 mins. (If you want your veggies firmer and not mushy, add them later when the mung beans are partially cooked (after 10 mins). But add only tomatoes now. 10.
