Use it as a topping on pancakes, poke cakes, ice creams or to make chocolate milk. 13. Oats Recipes f... Oats Upma Recipe Oats idli Oats Dosa Recipe Oats Chilla Recip...

title: oatmeal recipe | overnight oats recipe | oats recipes for weight loss
ingredients: 0.75 cup rolled oats, 1 tbsp chia seeds, 0.5 cup low fat milk, 2 tbsp curd greek yogurt, 1 tbsp honey, 0.25 cinnamon powder dalchi powder, 0.5 cup carrot, 1 tbsp raisins, 1 tbsp walnuts, 0.75 cup rolled oats, 1 tbsp chia seeds, 0.5 cup low fat milk, 2 tbsp curd greek yogurt, 1 tbsp honey, 1 tbsp choco chips, 0.5 tsp almonds, 1 tsp vanilla extract vanilla essence, 1 banana, 0.5 tsp pistachios
instructions: 1. firstly, in a glass jar add in ¾ cup rolled oats. 2. also add 1 tbsp chia seeds and ½ cup low fat milk. 3. furthermore add 2 tbsp curd, 1 tbsp honey and ¼ cinnamon powder. 4. add in ½ cup grated carrot and press gently. 5. add in 1 tbsp raisins and 1 tbsp walnuts. 6. finally, refrigerate overnight and serve for morning breakfast. 7. firstly, in a glass jar add in ¾ cup rolled oats. 8. also add 1 tbsp chia seeds and ½ cup low fat milk. 9. furthermore add 2 tbsp curd and 1 tbsp honey. 10. add in 1 tbsp choco chips and 1 tsp vanilla extract. 11. furthermore add in chopped banana and press slightly. 12. add in ½ tsp almonds and pistachios chopped. 13. refrigerate overnight and serve for morning breakfast. 14. finally, enjoy healthy overnight oatmeal with your family.

title: Oatmeal Soup
ingredients: 0.25 cup rolled oats, 1.5 cup vegetables, 1 tbsp butter, 1 tsp garlic, 1 tsp ginger, 0.375 pepper, 0.25 tsp red chili flakes, 1.5 to 2 cups stock, 0.5 tsp herbs, Salt, Lemon juice
instructions: 1. Melt butter and saute garlic until it turns aromatic. You can also use ginger if you like the flavor. 2. Add oats and saute for a while. If using steel cut oats, saute for about 3 mins or until they begin to smell nutty. 3. Add all the mixed veggies and saute for 2 to 3 mins. 4. Pour stock/broth or water as needed. 5. Cook it on a medium heat until the veggies are tender. We don’t need to add any thickeners here since oats makes the soup slightly thicker. Add more liquid if required to bring it to a consistency. 6. Add herbs and pepper. You can also use fresh herbs. I used mixed italian herbs. 7. Add salt as needed. Switch off. Add red chili flakes now or while serving. Serve oats soup warm. We prefer to add some lemon juice just before serving.

title: Oats Chilla Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup oats, 0.25 cup besan, 1.25 water, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 cup carrot, 2 tablespoons onion, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon oil
instructions: 1. Add oats to a grinder jar and made a slightly coarse powder. If the powder is too coarse the chillas may break. 2. To a mixing bowl, transfer the ground oats, gram flour, salt, turmeric, green chilies, cumin, ginger, carrot, onions & coriander leaves. 3. Pour 1 cup water and mix to a lump free batter. Pour another ¼ cup water and mix to make a thick batter. It must be of pourable and spreading consistency. 4. Heat a pan on a medium heat. If using a cast iron pan, Once the pan is hot enough drizzle few drops of oil and spread with a tissue or cloth. Let it heat up well. 5. When the pan is hot enough, stir the batter well and pour ¼ cup of batter in the center of the pan. 6. Spread it gently to a slightly thin pancake of about 6 inches. Drizzle half tsp to tbsp oil or ghee around the edges. 7. Let it cook on a medium heat until the base is set and cooked well. 8. Turn it to the other side and cook pressing down with the spatula. You will see brown to golden spots when the oats chilla is cooked. 9. If you prefer crisp chilla, cook them on a low heat for a minute or 2. Remove to serving plate. 10. To make the next chilla, stir the batter well and make sure the pan is hot enough but not very hot. Also the batter thickens as it rests, you can splash 1 to 2 tbsps of water if required as you make them. 11. Serve oats chilla warm or hot with chutney.

title: oats chilla recipe | oats cheela | how to make oats chilla recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 2 tbsp rava semolina suji, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp tomato, 0.5 tsp salt, olive oil
instructions: 1. firstly dry roast 1 cup rolled oats. take unflavoured oats as we are adding spices. 2. roast until the oats turn crisp. 3. cool the oats completely and blend to a fine powder without adding any water. 4. transfer the powdered oats into a large mixing bowl. 5. also, add 2 tbsp rava for binding. 6. now add ¼ cup curd and 1 cup water. 7. whisk and mix make smooth lump free batter. 8. additionally, add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, ½ tsp crushed cumin. 9. also add 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp tomato and ½ tsp salt. 10. mix well forming thick flowing consistency chilla batter. 11. further, pour a ladleful of batter onto a hot tawa and spread gently. 12. pour ½ tsp of olive oil over chilla. 13. cover and allow cooking on a medium flame for a minute. 14. now flip the chilla and cook both sides pressing gently. 15. finally, oats chilla is ready to serve with green chutney.

title: Oats Chivda Recipe (Oats Mixture)
ingredients: 0.75 cup rolled oats, 1 cup poha, 1.25 tablespoon oil, 3 tablespoons peanuts, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 3.5 garlic cloves, 2.5 tablespoons fried garm, 2 tablespoons coconut thinly sliced chopped, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.1875 teaspoon turmeric, 1 pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. Dry roast oats until slightly golden. They will not turn crisp. Set them aside to a plate. 2. Next roast poha in the same pan. Keep stirring and roast until crisp and light. Set them aside. 3. Heat oil and crackle mustard, cumin and red chili. Roast peanuts until golden & aromatic. 4. Add garlic, green chili and curry leaves. 5. When the garlic begins to smell good. add coconut and fried gram. 6. Fry until the coconut smells good. 7. Add turmeric, chili powder and salt. Switch off the stove. 8. Transfer poha and oats. Mix everything well. 9. When the mixture cools down, add more salt if needed and pour little coconut or olive oil. Toss them. 10. Store oats chivda in a air tight jar and use within 2 months.

title: Oats Dosa Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup oats, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup semolina, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 2 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoon onions, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 0.25 cup curd, 1.25 to 1 cups water, 2 tablespoon Oil
instructions: 1. Add half cup oats to a grinder jar and make a fine powder. 2. Transfer it to a mixing bowl along with salt, rice flour, semolina, cumin, curry leaves, ginger, green chilies, chopped onions and coriander leaves. 3. Add curd (optional) and pour 1¼ cup water. Mix all of them together to get a free flowing runny batter. 4. The consistency must be similar to that of the rava dosa batter. 5. If required add more water to get the required consistency. Keep this aside for 10 to 15 mins. 6. If using a non stick pan you can straight away use it. If using a cast iron pan, heat it first until hot enough. Drizzle a few drops of oil and spread it with a kitchen tissue or a cut onion. Heat this well until smoky hot. When you see the smoke turn off. This step creates a non-stick effect on your cast iron pan. Wipe off the excess oil with kitchen tissues or a cloth. 7. Just before you begin to make the dosas, check and adjust the consistency if needed by pouring little more water. 8. Heat the griddle until moderately hot enough. To check you can drizzle a teaspoon of batter on the pan, it should sizzle immediately. This means the pan is hot enough. 9. Stir the batter with a deep ladle as the solids settle at the bottom. Regulate the flame to medium, take a ladle full of batter and begin to pour from the sides /edges across the pan and then in the center. 10. Do not spread the batter. Just pour it and fill up the gaps. 11. Drizzle 1 teaspoon ghee or oil all over including the edges. 12. Cook the oats dosa on a medium heat until it turns golden on the base. 13. When it is cooked well, the edges will begin to leave the pan. Flip it to the other side and cook for 1 minute. If you have a trouble making these, then refer my pro tips mentioned in the post. 14. Again flip it back and cook until crisp and golden. Remove oats dosa to a plate and serve with chutney. 15. To make the next dosa, the pan has to be hot enough. if needed you can add few drops of oil and rub with a cut onion. Then make the next oats dosa. 16. Also stir the batter well before you pour it to the pan, as the solids settle at the bottom. 17. If you have some left over plain dosa batter, then powder some oats. For 1 cup dosa batter add ¼ cup powdered rolled or quick cook oats. 18. Stir the powder in a small bowl along with little water. Mix to make a lump free batter. 19. Mix the oats batter with dosa batter. 20. Bring it to thick dosa batter consistency by pouring more water if needed. 21. Heat a greased tawa and pour the batter spreading it well to make a thin and crisp dosas like we spread the traditional dosa. 22. Pour 1 teaspoon oil around the edges. Flip it and fry the oats dosa on the other side as well.

title: Oats Dosa recipe | Instant Oats Rava Dosa | Oats Onion Dosa
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 1 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup rava semolina suji, 1 tsp salt, 4 cup water, 0.5 onion, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp curry leaves, 1 tsp cumin
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan dry roast, 1 cup of rolled oats until it turns crunchy. 2. cool completely, and grind to a fine powder. 3. transfer the oats powder to a large bowl. 4. add 1 cup rice flour, ¼ cup rava, and 1 tsp salt. 5. prepare a watery battery by adding 4 cup of water. 6. now add ½ onion, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp curry leaves, and 1 tsp cumin. 7. mix well preparing a watery batter. 8. rest for 15 minutes for oars and rava to soak well. 9. after 15 minutes, you can see the batter is watery. you can adjust the consistency by adding water if required. 10. when the pan is super hot, pour the batter on the pan. 11. spread 1 tsp oil and allow to roast for 2 minutes or until the dosa turns crisp. 12. finally, enjoy the Crispy Oats Dosa Recipe with spicy chutney.

title: Oats Egg Omelet
ingredients: 2 eggs, 0.25 cup oats, 3.5 tablespoons milk, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 pinch turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon oregano, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon oil, 2 tablespoon onions, 2 tablespoon carrot, 2 tablespoon capsicum, 2 tablespoon tomato, 2 green chilies, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash and chop veggies as needed. I use 2 tbsp onions, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp grated carrot, 2 tbsp deseeded and chopped firm tomato. 2 green chilies chopped, cilantro (coriander leaves) chopped finely. You can also use spring onions and cabbage. Prepare and keep these aside. 2. To a bowl add oat flour, salt, turmeric, pepper and oregano or your preferred seasoning. To make oat flour process ¼ cup oats to a fine flour in a mixer or processor. You will get around ¼ cup (heaped) flour. 3. Add 3 tbsp milk to the flour and make a lump free batter. If you follow the ayurvedic food combining rules, then egg and milk do not go well as they are not compatible with each other. So you can use plant milk. Oat milk goes very well for this. 4. Break 2 eggs to the bowl. 5. Beat them all together with a fork until lightly frothy. The mixture must be of a pouring consistency. If your eggs are very small, you may need to add in 1 to 2 tbsp. more milk. 6. Add 1½ tsp oil, butter or ghee to a pan. Heat it. 7. Set the flame to medium. When the pan is hot enough, pour the egg mixture. 8. Mix all the veggies and layer them. Gently press the veggies with a spatula. 9. When the base is firm enough, flip it. Cook until the other side is fully cooked. 10. If you like you can sprinkle pepper and chaat masala. Serve oats egg omelette hot. 11. Oats Recipes f... How to Cook Oatme... Masala Oats Recip... Oats Chilla Recip... Savory Steel Cut ... Oatmeal pancakes ...

title: Oats idli
ingredients: 1 cup oats, 0.5 cup rava, 0.25 cup curd, 1 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon soda-bi-carbonate, 2 tablespoon carrot, 3 tablespoon water as needed for consistency, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon channa dal, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 4 cashews, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 pinch hing, 1 green chili, 6 curry leaves, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add oats to a blender or grinder and make a coarse powder. Set aside. 2. Heat oil or ghee in a pan. If using cashews (optional), fry them until golden. Remove and set aside. Add mustard, cumin, chana dal and urad dal. 3. Fry until the dals turn golden. Then add curry leaves, green chilies, hing and ginger. Saute for 30 seconds. 4. Pour rava and fry until aromatic. Add powdered oats. 5. Fry until the mixture turns aromatic. Add salt and mix. Cool this completely. 6. Once cool, pour water and curd to it. Mix to make a thick batter. Set this aside for 5 to 8 mins. 7. Heat a steamer with 2 cups water or as required. Meanwhile drizzle a few drops of oil over the idli plates and grease them. Place fried cashews optionally. 8. After 10 mins, the batter turns slightly thick. At this stage add lemon juice and more water if needed to bring it to a consistency like that of idli batter. 9. The batter must be thick and not of pouring consistency. But must be smooth and not lumpy. 10. Add lemon juice, carrots, coriander leaves. Mix a good mix. 11. Make sure the water is getting hot. Then sprinkle half teaspoon soda or 1 teaspoon fruit salt - eno to the batter. Gently mix it. Do not over mix. 12. Fill the greased plates with batter. This must go right away for steaming. Delaying will make the idlis dense. 13. When the water in the steamer begins to boil rapidly, place the oats idli stand in the cooker and steam for about 11 minutes. Switch off the heat. 14. If cooked properly then a skewer inserted in comes clean or with slight crumbs. 15. Allow them to cool down a bit. Remove them with the help of a spoon. 16. Serve oats idli hot with a tomato chutney or idli-sambar.

title: oats idli recipe | instant oats idli | steamed oatmeal idli
ingredients: 1 cup oats, 3 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, few curry leaves, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, 1 carrot, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 cup curd yogurt, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.75 tsp salt, 7 cashew, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a kadai heat 3 tsp oil and splutter 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, ½ tsp cumin and few curry leaves. 2. also, add ½ tsp ginger paste and 2 chilli. saute slightly. 3. further, add 1 carrot and ¼ tsp turmeric and saute for 2 minutes. 4. now add ½ cup rava and roast on low flame until it turns aromatic. 5. add powdered oats and mix well. 6. transfer the mixture and allow to cool completely. 7. add in ½ cup curd and ½ cup water. 8. mix well making sure there are no lumps. 9. further add 2 tbsp coriander, ¾ tsp salt and ½ cup water. 10. mix well making sure to form an idli batter consistency. 11. rest for 20 minutes or until the rava absorbs water. 12. just before steaming add a ½ tsp eno (fruit salt) and mix well. 13. steam the oats idli for 15 minutes on medium flame. 14. finally, serve instant oats idli with coconut chutney and sambar.

title: Oats Khichdi Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 1 cup rolled oats, 1 tomato, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.5 cup green peas, 0.3333333333333333 cup green beans, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 4 cups water, 2 tablespoon ghee, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. Rinse moong dal well a few times. Drain the water. 2. Rinse and chop tomato, carrot, green beans & green chili. Grate ginger. 3. Heat a pot or pressure cooker on a mdeium heat. When the ghee melts, add cumin seeds and bay leaf. As soon as the seeds begin to sizzle, add ginger and green chili. 4. Quickly mix and add hing, carrots, beans and peas. Saute for 1 to 2 minutes. Add tomatoes and saute for 2 minutes. Add salt and red chili powder. Give a good stir. 5. Pressure cooker: Add the drained lentils and oats. Pour water. Give a good stir. Cover and pressure cook for 3 whistles on a medium flame. Let the pressure release naturally. 6. Cooking in a pot: Add the drained lentils and pour water. Bring the water to a rolling boil and then simmer until the lentils are soft and tender. Keep stirring. When the lentils soften, add oats. If there is not enough water, pour some hot water. 7. Open the lid. Oats and lentils both must be softened and the texture has to be thick, mushy and of pouring consistency. I usually pour some boiling hot water if required at this stage and simmer for a minute becaue the khichdi becomes too thick as it cools down. 8. Taste test and add more salt if required and garnish with coriander leaves. Drizzle some ghee if you want. Squeeze some lemon juice and serve hot. 9. Perform all the same steps as done for the stovetop cooker. Pressure cook for 9 mins on porridge mode. Wait for the pressure to release naturally for 8 mins. Release the rest manually. Pour some hot boiling water for perfect consistency.

title: Oats Laddu (Indian Oat Balls)
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 0.25 cup peanuts, 0.25 cup pumpkin seeds, 125 grams jaggery, 0.5 inch Ceylon cinnamon pieces, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 cashews, 2 tablespoons raisins
instructions: 1. Add the peanuts to a heavy bottom pan and toast them on a medium heat until golden and aromatic. Transfer to a tray to cool down. 2. Add rolled oats & toast until aromatic, for 2 to 3 minutes. Transfer to another tray, (separate from the peanuts) and cool. 3. To the same pan, add pumpkin seeds and toast until they turn crisp and aromatic. Transfer to a tray to cool down. Optionally, rub off the peanuts to peel and separate the skins. 4. Add the rolled oats to a grinder or a food processor and grind/process to a fine powder. 5. Add the cinnamon, cardamom, roasted peanuts and pumpkin seeds, process until fine. Next add the grated jaggery and process/grind until well blended. Taste test and add more jaggery if you want. If it is too sweet you may add more nuts, seeds or oats. 6. You just need to process a little longer until the peanuts and seeds begin to release enough oil so the mixture can bind. To test, take a few tbsps of this to your palm and try to bind it, pressing down hard with your fingers. 7. If the mixture does not bind, process a little longer or add more jaggery. Bind to 10 to 12 balls. For a crunch I add 2 tbsps crushed skinned roasted peanuts before binding. 8. Heat ghee in a pan and fry the cashews until light golden. Add the raisins and fry until they turn plump. 9. Turn off and take the pan down from the stove. Let cool down for 2 mins and stir in the ground oats nut seed jaggery mixture. 10. It will all bind together. Take equal small portions to your palm and press down with your fingers to bind. You will be able to make 10 to 12 oat balls. 11. Store the oat balls in a air tight jar at room temperature and use within 3 weeks. These can also be refrigerated for longer.

title: Oats Pongal
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 0.5 cup moong dal, 2.25 cups water, 9 cashews, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper corn, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoon ghee, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 1 green chilli
instructions: 1. Dry roast oats in a pot or pressure cooker for 3 to 4 minutes and remove them to a bowl. This step helps to make less sticky oats pongal. 2. To the same pot or cooker, add moong dal and dry roast for 5 to 6 minutes on a medium heat until aromatic. This makes your oats pongal aromatic. 3. Pour water and rinse the dal well at least thrice. Pour 1½ cups water and pressure cook for 3 whistles. If cooking in a pot, cook until moong dal becomes soft and mushy. If needed add more water while you cook. 4. Once the pressure releases, open the lid and check. Dal has to be soft cooked. Pour ¾ to 1 cup hot water and add roasted oats. 5. Cover and cook on a low to medium heat until oats are softened. Rolled oats do not take too long to cook as they are pre-steamed. 6. Add salt as required and taste test it to adjust. 7. Heat a pan with ghee. Add ginger and green chilli (optional). Saute for 30 to 40 seconds, then add cumin seeds, cashew nuts, curry leaves and black pepper. 8. Fry until the cashews become light golden. Then add hing and turmeric. Turn off the stove. 9. Pour the tempering to the cooked oats and moong dal. Stir and cover for 5 minutes before serving. 10. Press the saute button and add ghee to the steel insert. Fry the cashews until golden and remove them to a bowl for garnishing later. 11. Add ginger, green chilies (optional), cumin seeds, black pepper and curry leaves. Saute until you begin to smell the curry leaves good. 12. Then add hing and oats. Roast for 2 to 3 mins. If the IP becomes too hot, press cancel in between and continue. 13. Add rinsed moong dal, turmeric and salt. Continue to sauté for 4 to 5 minutes. I found this step makes the dish aromatic and less sticky. 14. Press cancel. Pour 4 cups water and deglaze the pot. 15. Secure the instant pot with the lid. Press pressure cook and set the timer to 8 minutes. 16. After the IP beeps, wait for natural pressure release for 8 minutes. Release the rest of the pressure manually. 17. Stir oats pongal. I prefer to top with little ghee. Serve with papads, pickle or chutney.

title: Oats Scrambled Eggs
ingredients: 0.75 cup oats, 1.5 egg, 1 tbsp oil, 1 green chili, 1 tsp ginger, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.25 cup vegetables, 1 pinch Turmeric, Salt, 1 tsp Lemon juice, 2 tbsps Coriander leaves, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. Crackle cumin in hot oil. Saute onions, ginger and green chilies. If using ginger garlic paste , fry the onions until golden and then saute ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes away. 2. When the onions turn slightly golden or pink, then add mixed veggies. I used carrots, peas and corn. You can also use capsicum and beans. You can also skip the veggies. 3. Saute for a minute. Then cover and cook on a low flame until soft and tender. If needed you can also sprinkle little water and cook. 4. Add oats, salt, turmeric and garam masala (optional). I used quick cook oats. If using rolled oats, add them to a colander. Rinse them under running water. Drain completely. Then add to the pan. 5. Saute for 2 to 3 mins, they do not turn crunchy due to the moist veggies. 6. Mix everything well scrambling the egg. 7. Pour the egg to pan. If using quick oats, immediately sprinkle 3 to 4 tbsp water (about ¼ cup)evenly in the pan. Add more if it looks dry. You can also beat the egg and then add to the pan. Avoid sprinkling water if using rolled oats. 8. Cover and cook on a very low flame until the oats and egg are completely cooked. Just for 2 to 3 mins. Check by tasting little, oats must be soft now. If oats remain under cooked, you can also sprinkle little more water. 9. Sprinkle coriander leaves and lemon juice as needed. Lemon juice helps to remove the smell of egg, recommended for those new to eating eggs. 10. 1 green chili chopped 11. 1 tsp ginger chopped or ginger garlic paste 12. ¼ cup vegetables fine chopped mix 13. 2 tbsps Coriander leaves chopped finely 14. Heat oil in a pan until hot. Then add cumin and let it splutter. 15. Next stir fry the onions, ginger and green chili until the onions turn golden. 16. Next add the chopped vegetables and saute for a min. 17. Cook covered until tender. If needed sprinkle some water to help them cook. 18. Add in oats, salt, garam masala and turmeric. If using rolled oats, add them to a colander and rinse under running water. 19. Drain the water completely, then add to the pan. 20. Fry for 2 mins. Then beat the egg until frothy and pour it to the pan. 21. Sprinkle 3 to 4 tbsps of water if using quick oats. Avoid water if using rolled oats. 22. Scramble the egg. Cook covered on a low flame until oats and egg are cooked well. Check salt and add more if desired. 23. Add lemon juice and coriander leaves. Stir well and serve oats srambled eggs hot. 24. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > title: Oats Upma Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 1 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1 onion, 0.625 cup mix vegetables, 1.5 green chilies, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons coconut, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.5 teaspoon chana dal, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 2 tablespoons peanuts, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric
instructions: 1. Dry roast 1 cup oats in a wide pan for about 5 minutes until crisp. They do not become very crisp. Set this aside to cool. 2. Heat a pan with 1½ tablespoon oil. Add ½ teaspoon cumin, ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds, 2 tablespoons peanuts, ½ teaspoon chana dal & ½ teaspoon urad dal. 3. Fry them stirring often till the peanuts turn golden and aromatic. 4. Next add 1 pinch hing, ½ teaspoon ginger, 1 to 2 slit green chilies & 1 sprig curry leaves. Fry all of them till the curry leaves turn crisp. 5. Then add 1 small chopped onions and saute until golden. 6. Optional - If using veggies. Then add ½ to ¾ cup vegetables and fry for 2 mins. 7. Sprinkle 2 tbsp water. Cover and cook until the veggies are slightly soft. 8. Next sprinkle some ½ teaspoon salt and ¼ teaspoon turmeric. 9. If using rolled oats then pour 1 cup water. or if using quick cook oats use ½ cup water. 10. Mix well and then taste the water. The water has to be slightly salty. If needed add more salt. Bring it to a boil. 11. Transfer the roasted oats & give a gentle mix to incorporate the water with the oats. 12. With in 1 to 2 mins the water will be absorbed. Cover and cook on a low flame for 2 mins or until all the moisture is evaporated. 13. Add 2 tablespoons chopped coriander leaves. Mix well. 14. When the temperature comes down, pour 1 tablespoon lemon juice. You can top it with some fresh grated coconut if you have. It tastes good as it is. 15. Oats upma is best served with yogurt.

title: oats upma recipe | vegetable oats upma | oatmeal upma | oats for breakfast
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 1 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin, few curry leaves, 1 cashew kaju, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 5 beans, 0.25 capsicum, 2 tbsp peas matar, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp coconut, 1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp oil and splutter 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, ½ tsp jeera, few curry leaves and 10 cashews. 2. saute until the cashews turn golden brown. 3. now add 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli and ½ onion. saute well. 4. furthermore add ½ carrot, 5 beans, ¼ capsicum, 2 tbsp peas, ½ tsp turmeric and ½ tsp salt. 5. add 2 tbsp water, cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 6. now add 1 cup water and get it to a boil. if using instant oats, just add ¼ cup water. 7. once the water comes to a boil, add in roasted oats and mix well. 8. mix until the oats absorb all the water. 9. now cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until oats gets cooked well. 10. furthermore add ¼ tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp coconut and 1 tbsp lemon juice. 11. finally, enjoy vegetable oats upma as a healthy breakfast or pack to the lunch box.

title: Oats uttapam
ingredients: 0.5 cup oats, 0.25 cup rice flour, 2 tablespoons curd, salt as needed, water as needed, 2 tablespoons onions, 2 tablespoons carrots, 2 tablespoons tomatoes, 2 green chilies, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add half cup quick cook oats or rolled oats to a bowl along with ¼ th cup rice flour or sooji, salt. If using rolled oats, measure half cup first and then powder it. I used quickcookoats so did not powder. If using sooji instead of rice flour, then powder the oats. 2. Add 2 tbsp. curd/ yogurt or 1.5 tsp lemon juice along with a pinch of soda. Pour enough water to make a batter of pouring consistency. Stir well. Rest for 5 mins. 3. Grease and heat a pan/ tawa. Pour a laddle full of batter and lightly spread it. Usually uttapam are made thick, but here oats may lend a sticky texture, so make them medium thick. Add chopped onions, carrots, coriander and green chilies. You can also add deseeded tomatoes, thinly sliced capsicums. Press with a wooden spoon gently. 4. When the base is cooked firm, flip it and cook for a while. 5. Flip it to the other side, Fry for a while on a low flame for the uttapam to turn crisp. If you have any pickle or chutney podi, you can sprinkle evenly. I like to use flax seed podi. Serve with any chutney or pickle. 6. Add dosa batter to a bowl. 7. Add quick cookoats and little salt. 8. Add water as needed to bring it to the right pouring consistency. 9. Pour a ladle full of batter on a hot pan. Spread it to suit your liking. Add chopped veggies. I made this using only carrot. 10. Sprinkled sesame seeds spice powder. 11. ½ cup oats (rolled or quick cook) 12. 2 tablespoons onions fine chopped 13. 2 tablespoons tomatoes fine chopped 14. 2 green chilies chopped 15. 2 tablespoons coriander leaves fine chopped 16. Add oats to a blender jar and make a fine powder. 17. If you do not have rice flour then add sooji along with oats to the blender and powder finely. 18. Transfer the oats flour, rice flour or semolina, salt, yogurt or lemon juice + soda to a mixing bowl. 19. Mix together with water to make a batter similar to the dosa batter consistency. 20. Check salt and add more if needed. 21. Meanwhile rinse and chop onions, tomatoes, chilies and coriander leaves. 22. Mix together all the veggies on the chopping board and keep them ready. 23. Grease a tawa with few drops of oil and heat it on a medium flame. 24. Stir the batter well and adjust the consistency if needed by adding little water. 25. Pour a ladle of batter and spread a bit to make a thick oats uttapam. 26. Sprinkle the veggies as desired and gently press them with a wooden spatula. 27. Cook until the base is done well & slightly crisp. You can also cover and cook until the base is set. 28. Flip and cook well on the other side as well. 29. Flip again and fry until the base is slightly crisp. 30. Remove the oats uttapam to a serving plate. 31. Serve oats uttapam with any chutney, podi or pickle. 32. title: Okra Curry (South Indian)
ingredients: 25 grams okra, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 cup onion, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 4.5 garlic cloves, 1 medium sweet potato chop to inch slices, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 tablespoon sambar powder, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.5 tablespoon Tamarind paste chutney, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 1 cup water, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 0.29166666666666663 cup thick coconut milk, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves cilantro
instructions: 1. Gather the following ingredients: 2. Rinse, drain & chop 250 grams /9 ounce okra to 1 inch pieces (don’t need to wipe dry before chopping) 3. Rinse & pat dry 1 sprig fresh curry leaves (omit if you don’t have) 4. Fine chop 1medium onion to make 1 cup (120 grams) (red or yellow onion) 5. Chop 1 medium tomato to make ¾ cup (50 grams) (deseed if preferred) 6. Slice 4 to 5 large garlic cloves (10 grams) (don’t chop too small) 7. Optional – Rinse & scrub 1 medium sweet potato (150 grams). Chop to ½ inch thick slices & immerse in a bowl of water 8. ½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (adjust to heat preference) 9. 1 to 2 tablespoon Tamarind paste or sour tamarind chutney (or 2 tbsp tamarind soaked in ⅓ cup hot water) 10. optional – ¼ to ⅓ cup thick coconut milk ( or grind ¼ cup grated coconut with ¼ cup water or use coconut chutney, adjust to taste) 11. On a medium flame, heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. 12. When the seeds begin to pop, add the curry leaves, onions and garlic. Saute for 3 mins. 13. Add the okra and saute for another 5 mins. 14. Stir in the red chili powder, sambar powder/ curry powder and salt. 15. Add the tomatoes, tamarind and ½ cup water. (If using soaked tamarind, squeeze well & strain using a strainer/filter) 16. Also add the sweet potatoes. 17. The consistency should be like seen in the following image else the sambar powder/curry powder can burn at the bottom. 18. Mix well and cook covered for 5 mins. The curry gets thicker fast due to the ground spices, so keep an eye. This step is crucial to cook down the gooey texture in okra. 19. Pour 1 more cup water and cook covered till okra and sweet potatoes are fork tender. Add more water if required while they cook. 20. Mine takes about 15 to 20 mins. It may depending on the kind of cookware, intensity of the flame and the okra. Mature okra take longer to cook. 21. Stir in jaggery and coconut milk if using. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt and tamarind to your preference. It should be ideally tangy, spicy and lightly sweet. Bring to a boil and turn off the heat. 22. Garnish Okra Curry with coriander leaves if you want and serve with brown rice, millets or eat it on its own like a meal. Flatbreads like chapati, ragi roti, roti are also some options.

title: Omelette recipe
ingredients: 2 eggs, 1 tbsp onions, 1 tbsp capsicums, 1 tbsp tomato, 0.125 tsp Salt, 1 Green chili, 1 Pinch turmeric, 1 tbsp Oil, 1 tbsp Coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash and chop onions, tomatoes, chilies, coriander leaves and capsicum finely. 2. Mix together with salt and turmeric. 3. Break the eggs to the bowl and check for shells. 4. Beat the eggs until frothy. 5. Add oil to a pan and heat it on a medium flame. Do not let the pan turn very hot, it will brown the omelette without cooking well. 6. Pour the egg mixture, allow to cook until set on a medium flame. 7. When the base firms up, flip it to the other side and cook till done. Sprinkle crushed pepper or chilli powder if you like. 8. Serve egg omelette hot with rice, roti or bread.

title: Onion Bhaji Recipe (Better than Restaurant)
ingredients: 2 grams onion sliced, 1 green chili, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.75 cup besan, 0.3333333333333333 cup rice flour, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.125 teaspoon baking soda, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon carom seeds, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 2 tablespoons water, high smoke point oil to deep fry - about 1 cups
instructions: 1. Slice onions thin, of uniform thickness and add to a large mixing bowl. Add salt and mix well, squeezing gently to release the juices. Rest aside for a minimum of 15 to 20 mins, while you prepare the rest. 2. On a medium flame, heat oil in a deep pan for deep frying. 3. Meanwhile add all the ingredients except water and mix well with your hand for form a thick batter. If the batter is too dry and crumbly, sprinkle 1 tablespoon water and mix. (See images in the post for consistency) 4. I used 2 tablespoons in total. You may need more or less depending on the freshness or kind of onions. 5. Test if the oil is hot – drop a small portion of the batter to the hot oil. It should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning brown. If it sinks and not come up, it means the oil is not hot enough. 6. Take ¾ tablespoon portions of batter to your fingers and slide to the hot oil. Do not shape them. Alternately use a pair of spoons, one to pick up the batter and the other to help slide it down to the hot oil. 7. Avoid overcrowding. Do not touch them for 2 to 3 mins as they can disintegrate. 8. Fry them on a medium heat for 3 mins. Stir occasionally to fry until crisp and light golden. Remove to a cooling rack or a steel colander. Fry the rest of the batter in batches. I fried mine in 3 batches. 9. I like to refry mine for 1 to 2 mins for a extra crisp texture. 10. Onion bhajis are best served hot, immediately as they begin to lose their crispness when they sit.

title: Onion Chutney Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup onions, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons oil, 2 tablespoons peanuts, 0.75 tablespoon bengal gram, 0.75 tablespoon skinned black gram, 2 cloves garlic, 3.5 red chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon tamarind, 1 teaspoon jaggery, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon urad dal, 0.5 red chili, 1 curry leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. This step is optional but I prefer to soak my jaggery and tamarind in water. Filter and use as most times both of these have some debris. However you may add them directly. Soak 1 teaspoon tamarind and 1 teaspoon jaggery in half cup water. Set this aside. 2. Pour 1½ tbsp oil to a pan and heat it. Add ¾ tablespoon chana dal, ¾ tablespoon urad dal, 2 tablespoons peanuts and 3 to 4 dried red chilies. 3. Fry until them stirring often until lightly golden & aromatic. 4. Add 2 garlic and 1 cup cubed onions. Fry them for 3 to 4 mins until the onions are half done. 5. Next add jeera and curry leaves. Saute until the onions are light golden or turn soft. Cool completely. 6. Next add these to a blender jar along with ½ teaspoon salt. Squeeze soaked tamarind in the water. 7. Filter it through a small sieve or tea strainer. Blend all of these until smooth adding more water as needed. 8. Taste test and add more salt. If needed pour more water to the soaked tamarind & filter it back to the jar. Transfer the onion chutney to a serving bowl. 9. Pour ½ tbsp oil to the pan & heat it on a medium flame. When the oil turns hot, add ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds, ¼ teaspoon urad dal and ½ dried red chili. 10. Fry till the dal turns light golden, then add curry leaves and hing. 11. Pour this to the chutney. Serve onion chutney with breakfast or snacks.

title: Onion Chutney Recipe | Vengaya Chutney | Multipurpose Indian Onion Chutney For Dosa
ingredients: 4 red onion, 1 red chilli, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tbsp urad dal, 0.5 tbsp chana dal, 1 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp methi, 1 cloves garlic, 0.5 tsp salt, ball sized tamarind, 0.25 cup oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 1 tsp jaggery, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, soak 10 dried red chilli in hot water for 10 minutes. keep aside. 2. in a large pan heat 2 tbsp oil. add ½ tbsp urad dal, ½ tbsp chana dal, 1 tsp cumin and ¼ tsp methi. 3. saute until the dal turns aromatic. 4. now add 10 cloves of garlic and saute slightly. 5. further, add 4 sliced onions and saute until the onion shrinks slightly. 6. also, add ½ tsp salt and continue to cook until the onion changes colour slightly. 7. now add soaked red chilli, and small ball-sized tamarind and saute for 2 minutes. 8. cool completely, and grind to a smooth paste without adding water. keep aside. 9. in a large pan heat ¼ cup oil. add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, pinch hing and few curry leaves. splutter the tempering. 10. further, pour in prepared onion chutney and cook for 2 minutes. 11. add in 1 tsp jaggery and ½ tsp salt. 12. cook until the oil separates from the pan. 13. finally, enjoy onion chutney with idli, dosa, rice or roti.

title: onion kulambu recipe | vengaya kuzhambu | chinna vengaya kulambu | onion puli kulambu
ingredients: 1 tsp oil, 0.5 cup coriander seeds, 0.25 cup toor dal, 2 tbsp pepper, 2 tbsp cumin, 0.25 tsp methi, 2 red chilli, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp hing, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp oil, 0.25 tsp methi, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 onions shallots, 1 cloves garlic, 1.5 cup tomato puree, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 2 tsp masala powder, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp jaggery, 0.5 cup tamarind extract, 1 cup water, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tsp oil. add ½ cup coriander seeds, ¼ cup toor dal, 2 tbsp pepper, 2 tbsp cumin and ¼ tsp methi. 2. roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 3. now add 20 dried red chilli and a few curry leaves. 4. roast until the chilli turns crisp. 5. cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 6. further, add ¼ tsp hing and ½ tsp turmeric. 7. grind to a fine powder and homemade masala powder. 8. firstly, in large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add ¼ tsp methi, 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin and pinch hing. splutter tempering. 9. now add 10 small onions, 10 cloves garlic and saute on medium flame. 10. further, add 1½ cup tomato puree and mix well combining everything well. 11. add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 2 tsp masala powder, 1 tsp salt and 1 tsp jaggery. mix well. 12. also add ½ cup tamarind extract, 1 cup water and a few curry leaves. 13. cover and boil for 10 minutes or until everything is cooked well and oil separates. 14. add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well making sure the flavours are balanced. 15. finally, enjoy vengaya kulambu or spicy onion curry with rice, dosa or idli.

title: Onion Pakoda | Onion Pakora
ingredients: 2 cups onions, 1.25 cup besan, 1.5 tablespoon rice flour, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon ginger finely chopped, 0.5 teaspoon carom seeds, 2.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 mint leaves chopped, 0.25 cup cashews, Oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. Slice onions moderately thin and add them to a mixing bowl. 2. Add chilies, ginger, curry leaves, pudina and salt to the same mixing bowl. 3. Nicely squeeze the onions with your fingers few times without mushing them up. 4. Leave it aside for 5 minutes. This helps the onions to release some moisture. 5. Then sprinkle besan, ajwain, cashews and more salt if needed. 6. Mix everything well to coat the onions with the flour. The mixture will be fairly dry at this moment. 7. Add water 2 tbsps each time and mix the flour to make a moist mass of dough. 8. The dough must be tight yet very moist and not soggy like batter else they absorb lot of oil while frying. 9. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a kadai until very hot. 10. Be careful as it is too hot. Pour this to the dough. 11. Immediately the oil will sizzle. Mix the dough with a spoon and not hand as the oil is too hot. 12. Heat oil in the same kadai or fry pan for deep frying the pakoda. 13. Check if the oil is hot enough in the kadai by sliding a tiny piece of dough first. 14. It has to rise and not sink in the oil. Next it should not brown too quickly. This is the right temperature. 15. When the oil is hot enough, regulate the flame to medium high. 16. Take a small amount of dough and make small pakoda with your fingers and slide them gently in the hot oil. 17. Do not add too many pakodas in the pan. They should have some space in the pan to swim around & fry well. 18. While frying stir & if needed flip the onion pakodas occasionally for even frying. 19. Fry them till they turn crisp & golden. Then transfer them with a ladle to an absorbent tissue placed in a plate or to a colander. 20. Continue to make more onion pakoda in batches with the rest of the dough. 21. Serve onion pakoda hot with a cup of tea or coffee. You can also serve them with Coriander Chutney, Mint Chutney or Green chutney. 22. Or sprinkle some chaat masala or any ground seasoning of your choice.

title: onion pakoda recipe | kanda bajji | pyaj ke pakode | onion pakora
ingredients: 4 grams onion, 3 chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain, 1 tsp ginger paste, 0.75 tsp salt, 1.5 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, oil, 0.5 cup thick curd, 2 tbsp green chutney
instructions: 1. firstly, thinly slice the onions. make sure to slice of uniform thickness as it helps to fry easily. 2. transfer the sliced onion into a large bowl. 3. add 3 chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp ajwain, 1 tsp ginger paste and ¾ tsp salt. 4. squeeze and mix well making sure all the spices are well combined and onions release moisture. 5. further add 1½ cup besan, ¼ cup rice flour and mix well. 6. squeeze and mix without adding any water. 7. the water in the onion is enough to form the dough. add more besan if required. 8. now drop the dough into hot oil forming a random shape. 9. fry on medium flame stirring occasionally. 10. fry until the pakoda turns golden brown and crisp. 11. finally, drain off the pakoda and enjoy with hot tea. 12. firstly, in a bowl take ½ cup thick curd and 2 tbsp green chutney. 13. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 14. finally, enjoy dahi chatni with onion pakoda.

title: Onion Paratha Recipe - Dhaba Style | Pyaz Ka Paratha
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 onion, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp salt, 0.25 cup besan, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 2 cup wheat flour, 0.5 tsp salt, water, wheat flour, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil. Add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, and saute till the spices turn aromatic. 2. Add 2 onion, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, and saute till the onion softens. 3. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur, and 1 tsp salt. 4. Saute till the spices turn aromatic. 5. Also, add ¼ cup besan and saute till the besan turns aromatic. 6. Further add 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp kasuri methi and mix well. Onion stuffing is ready, keep it aside. 7. Add 2 cup wheat flour and ½ tsp salt to prepare the dough. 8. Add water as required and knead to a smooth dough. 9. Now pinch a ball-sized dough and make a cup by pressing from the sides. 10. Place a ball-sized prepared onion stuffing in the center. Take the edge and start pleating bringing it to the centre. 11. Also, join the pleats together and secure tight pinching off excess dough. 12. Sprinkle some wheat flour and roll slightly thick. 13. On a hot tawa place the rolled paratha and cook for a minute. 14. Furthermore, flip the paratha when the base is partly cooked (after a minute). 15. Also, brush oil/ghee and press slightly. Flip again once or twice till both sides are cooked properly. 16. Finally, serve hot Onion Paratha with sauce, raita, or pickle.

title: Onion Raita Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup onions, 1 cup curd, 2 green chilies, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, salt to taste
instructions: 1. Rinse onions under running water. peel off the skin and rinse them again. Rinse green chilies. Add coriander leaves to a bowl filled with water. rinse and repeat the rinse. Drain them. 2. Slice onions, chop chilies and coriander leaves, discarding the fibrous stalks. 3. Add yogurt & salt to a bowl. Whisk the yogurt until smooth. 4. Add onions, green chilies, coriander leaves to the bowl. Mix well. Taste test and add more salt if required. 5. Optionally add more yogurt if you prefer more. Add more sliced onions if you prefer more onions. Serve onion raita with biryani or pulao.

title: onion rings recipe | crispy onion rings | onion fried rings
ingredients: 1 onion, 0.5 cup maida plain flour, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup corn flakes crumb, oil for frying
instructions: 1. in a small bowl take ½ cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour, ½ tsp mixed herbs, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp salt. 2. prepare a smooth lump free batter adding more water if required. 3. now dip an onion ring into maida paste and coat well. 4. slowly drop the coated onion ring into corn flakes crumb and coat well. 5. now for double coating, drop the onion ring again to maida batter and wet completely. 6. further, roll in corn flakes crumbs and coat well. 7. fry until the onion rings turn golden brown and crisp. 8. finally, enjoy onion rings with sauce as an evening snack.

title: Onion Salad Recipe (Laccha Pyaaz)
ingredients: 2 medium onions, 1 green chilli, 2 sprigs coriander leaves, 1 lemon, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper
instructions: 1. Rinse onions under running water and peel them. Repeat the rinse and remove any outer layers that are bruised or dried out. Rinse lemon, green chili and coriander leaves. 2. Slice the onions to thin rings. You can also simply slice them. Deseed and fine chop green chilies and coriander leaves. 3. Separate the layers, discard if any thin membrane comes out. Add them to a bowl of very cold water. Leave for 10 mins. Drain the water from bowl. 4. Shake off the onion rings and add them to another serving bowl. 5. To spice up the onion salad, you will use red chilli powder, black pepper, chaat masala and salt. If you don’t have chaat masala, you may use a small amount of cumin powder and amchur powder (dried mango powder). But the flavors won’t be the same. You can start with smaller quantities and adjust towards the end. 6. Squeeze lemon juice and toss the onions. 7. Mix the ground spices and salt in a small plate. Sprinkle all over evenly. Also add chopped chilies and coriander leaves. 8. Toss and taste test. Add salt or spices or lemon juice if you want more. Serve the onion salad immediately. It is best eaten under 1 hour.

title: onion samosa recipe | patti samosa with samosa sheets | irani samosa
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 2 onion, 1 cup poha avalakki flattened rice, 1 chilli, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, maida paste, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour and ½ tsp salt. 2. add 2 to 2½ cup water and mix well using a whisk. 3. whisk until a smooth lump-free batter is formed adding water as required. 4. now grease the pan and pour a ladleful of batter on a hot pan. 5. swirl once making sure the batter is uniformly spread. 6. cook for a minute or until the sheet is cooked without browning. 7. now flip over gently and continue to cook. 8. finally, the samosa sheet is ready. you can cover and use it to prepare patti samosa or spring rolls. 9. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 onion, 1 cup poha, 1 chilli and 1 tsp chilli powder. 10. also add ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, ¾ tsp salt and 2 tbsp coriander. 11. mix well by squeezing onions. 12. onion stuffing is ready. 13. how to fold irani samosa: 14. firstly, take the prepared samosa sheet and cut in half. 15. fold into triangle sticking using maida. 16. stuff the onion stuffing. make sure to stuff well else the samosa will be bland. 17. seal the samosa using maida paste. 18. deep fry the samosa in hot oil keeping the flame on low. 19. stir occasionally, frying in between making sure to brown uniformly. 20. it will take approximately 15-20 minutes to get crispy golden coloured samosa. 21. drain off the samosa and enjoy irani samosa with tomato sauce.

title: Onion Tomato Chutney Recipe | Tomato Onion Chutney Recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 2 tbsp urad dal, 1 inch ginger, 3 cloves garlic, 1 red chilli, 0.5 onion, 3 tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp mustard, 2 red chilli, pinch hing, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil and roast 2 tbsp urad dal till turns aromatic. 2. Add 1 inch of ginger, 3 cloves of garlic, and 10 dried red chilies. Roast until the chilies puff up. 3. Further saute ½ onion, till the onion softens and raw smell is lost. 4. Add 3 tomato, ¼ tsp turmeric, and cook until the tomato softens and turns mushy. 5. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 6. Add 1 tsp salt and grind to a smooth paste adding water as required. 7. Transfer the chutney to the large bowl and adjust the consistency by adding water as required. 8. To prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add ½ tsp urad dal, 1 tsp mustard, 2 dried red chillies, a pinch of hing, and a few curry leaves. 9. Splutter the tempering and pour over the chutney. 10. Finally, enjoy Tomato Onion Chutney Recipe with dosa, idli, and rice.

title: onion tomato raita recipe | tomato onion raita recipe
ingredients: 1 cup whisked curd yoghurt, finely chopped onion, finely chopped tomato, few coriander leaves finely chopped, salt as per the taste, water to maintain the consistency
instructions: 1. initially, finely chop the onions and tomato. also take 1 cup of curd and whisk it properly.onion tomato raita 2. in a bowl onion and tomato. mix it well. now add the whisked curd and give a rough mix. 3. add salt as per your taste. be careful, while adding salt. you do not want your raita to be salty.onion tomato raita 4. add finely chopped corriander leaves anad mix it. garnish the raita with coriander leaves. 5. your raita is ready. serve it with biryani, pulao or paratha

title: Orange Juice Recipe
ingredients: 9 oranges, 1 inch ginger
instructions: 1. Rinse the fruit well under running water and pat dry with kitchen tissues. Cut the fruit to 2 parts. 2. Remove the seeds as much as possible with the help of a knife. 3. Squeeze the halved oranges over a glass, to extract the juice. Simply remove any seeds left in the juice. Please note this method works only with the freshest, thin skinned juicy oranges. 4. This is probably the most obvious way to juice oranges. With either a handheld juicer or an electric model, this method is quite effective. Start by washing the fruit and cutting it in half, exposing the fruit. 5. Another way to make this juice at home is with an electric blender. This is a great option because it utilizes the whole orange, minus the peel, providing more fiber that other methods that yield just the juice. 6. For this method, start by washing your oranges and removing the peel. 7. Remove the white pith or white membrane, as well. Orange pith is nutritious and is a rich source of fiber however it imparts a bitter taste so I try to remove as much as possible. 8. You will have to halve the oranges and then make a deep cut in the center as seen in the picture below. 9. You will see the seeds there. 10. Remove them gently with the knife. If you prefer cold juice, freeze your oranges for about an hour before juicing them. 11. Once your oranges are prepared simply add them to the jar of your electric blender. Pour water or any other juice just to cover them partially and blend until smooth and liquefied. Do not add a lot as it dilutes the taste. 12. When properly blended, optionally strain the juice through a fine mesh strainer to remove any pith and enjoy! 13. Juice made in blender has slight bitter taste from the pith. Also note that pith is healthy and has many nutritional benefits. To cut down the bitter taste, you may add a pinch of salt and 1 tablespoon of honey or maple syrup. 14. If you happen to have a slow juicer in your arsenal of kitchen tools, making juice is quite simple. Start by washing the fruit and peeling. Remove any white pith. 15. Turn on the slow juicer and add the whole peeled fruit to the chute/feeding tube. Collect the juice and the pulp in the respective containers. You can also add a small piece of ginger if you live in a cold place. We always add little mineral salt and a small piece of ginger to the juice since the cold pressed juice is very cooling to the body. 16. Serve orange juice immediately before if begins to oxidize. If you have any excess you can also freeze it to popsicles. Pour it the moulds and freeze.

title: orange kulfi recipe | kulfi orange in orange shells | orange kesar pista kulfi
ingredients: 5 orange, 1 litre milk, 1 cup milk powder, 1 cup cream, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp orange food colour, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. firstly, take 5 navel orange and cut in such a way you get the orange mould and cap for setting the kulfi. 2. scrape the pulp out carefully without damaging the oranges. i have used navel oranges as they are seedless and are thought to be one of the world’s best-tasting oranges. 3. keep aside the mould and cap aside. 4. now in a large kadai take 1-litre milk, 1 cup milk powder and 1 cup of cream. 5. mix well making sure everything is well combined without any lumps. 6. stir continuously and get the milk to a boil. 7. simmer for 10 minutes, stirring in between or until the milk thickens slightly. 8. now add ½ cup sugar and ¼ tsp orange food colour. 9. mix well and boil for at least 10 minutes. 10. stir in between and cook until the milk thickens. 11. transfer the milk into a bowl and refrigerate for an hour. 12. after 1 hour the milk has thickened well. 13. now add 1 cup orange juice and pulp which we collected while scraping oranges to make moulds. 14. mix well forming a smooth consistency. 15. also add 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp almonds and 2 tbsp pistachios. 16. mix well and the orange kulfi mixture is ready to set. 17. pour the kulfi mixture into the orange moulds. you can alternatively use any cups or kulfi mould to set the kulfi. 18. cover with the orange cap and freeze for at least 8 hours. 19. after 8 hours, the kulfi has set completely. 20. finally, cut into pieces of your choice and enjoy orange kulfi.

title: oreo milkshake recipe | oreo shake recipe | oreo smoothie recipe
ingredients: 5 oreo biscuits, 0.75 cup full cream milk, scoops vanilla ice cream, 0.25 cup whipped cream, 1 oreo biscuit
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take oreo biscuits. 2. furthermore, add ¾ to 1 cup of thick cold milk. add more milk if you are looking for thin milk shake. 3. also add 2-3 scoops of vanilla ice cream. ice cream helps to make milkshake thick and creamy. 4. further, blend to smooth milkshake. 5. pour the milkshake into serving glass. 6. also top with generous amount of whipped cream. 7. finally, serve oreo milk shake garnished with few crushed oreo biscuits.

title: Ottu Shavige Recipe | Instant Akki Vattu Shavige Karnataka Style
ingredients: 2 cup water, 1 tsp coconut oil, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup rice flour
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large kadai take 2 cup water, 1 tsp coconut oil, and 1 tsp salt. 2. Mix well and get to a rolling boil. 3. Once the water comes to a boil, add 2 cup rice flour and mix well. 4. Mix until the flour absorbs all the water. 5. Turn off the flame, cover it, and rest for 2 minutes. 6. When the flour is still hot, start to knead the dough. 7. You can sprinkle hot water if the flour is dry. 8. Prepare a soft and non-sticky dough. if the dough is too soft, then idiyappam will turn sticky once steamed. And if the dough is too hard, it will be difficult to press. 9. Now pinch a ball-sized dough and shape it into a cylindrical shape. 10. Place the dough into the steamer and steam for 10 minutes or until the dough is non-sticky. 11. Now grease the small holes mold with oil and stuff in the dough. 12. Press the shavige into a spiral shape. 13. Finally, enjoy the Instant Ottu Shavige Recipe or store it in the casserole if serving later.

title: Overnight Oats Recipe | Dates Nut Overnight Oats Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup rolled, Milk, 1 handful almonds, sweetener, 2.5 tbsp coconut
instructions: 1. Add half cup rolled or ¾ cup quick oats to the jar. Quick ones are precooked and dehydrated, so if possible use rolled oats. But the nutrition in rolled and quick variety is almost the same. 2. Pour milk just enough to immerse them. You can use any vegan milk too like organic soya, coconut or almond milk. You can use any fresh fruit juice too instead of milk. 3. I prefer to add in some nut since soaking enhances the nutrition. You can add any of your favorite nuts. I added few broken walnuts. 4. Add some chopped dates. Top with little more milk. You can skip dates and add any sweetener. 5. Cover and refrigerate over night or at least for 4 hours. 6. The next morning all the ingredients are soaked well. 7. You can add your favorite toppings. I like to add some fresh grated coconut. You can also use some frozen coconut. 8. Then lot of chopped nuts, seeds and then few more chopped dates. I used some pumpkin seeds, almonds and dates. You can even add some choco chips, chopped chocolate, sesame seeds or sunflower seeds.

title: paan kulfi recipe | instant paan ice cream | no cook kulfi recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup thickened cream malai, 0.5 cup milkmaid condensed milk, 2 tbsp dates khajoor, 0.5 cup full cream milk, 3 paan leaf betel leaf, 1 tbsp almonds badam, 1 tbsp pistachios, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder elachi powder, 1 tbsp cashew kaju, 1 tsp saunf fennel, 2 drops green food colour
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl add ¾ cup thickened cream. preferably use 35% milk fat. 2. beat or whisk for a minute or till stiff peaks appear. 3. further add ½ cup milkmaid and ½ cup full cream milk. add more milkmaid if you prefer it to be more sweet. 4. give a mix making sure everything is combined well. 5. additionally, add 3 paan leaf chopped finely. 6. add 2 tbsp dates, 1 tbsp almonds, 1 tbsp pistachios, 1 tbsp cashew, ¼ tsp cardamom powder, 1 tsp saunf. 7. also add 2 drops green food colour. 8. mix well making sure everything is combined well. 9. transfer the mixture to matka or kulfi mould. 10. cover and freeze for 8 hours or till it sets completely. 11. finally, serve paan kulfi recipe garnished with few chopped nuts.

title: pachi pulusu recipe | raw tamarind rasam | vankaya pachi pulusu
ingredients: ball sized tamarind, 3 cup water, 2 chilli, 1 onion, 2 tbsp curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp jaggery gud, 0.75 tsp salt, 3 tsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 red chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small bowl soak ball sized tamarind in 1 cup water for 20 minutes. 2. squeeze the tamarind and extract the juice. keep aside. 3. take 2 green chilli, make a slit in centre. 4. roast on direct flame until skin darkens all sides. 5. now peel the skin off by rubbing between tissue paper. 6. take roasted chilli into large bowl. 7. add 1 onion, 2 tbsp curry leaves and 2 tbsp coriander. 8. squeeze and mix well making sure the flavours are absorbed. 9. add in tamarind extract discarding the pulp. 10. also add 1 tsp jaggery and ¾ tsp salt. mix well. 11. prepare the tempering by heating 3 tsp oil. 12. splutter 1 tsp cumin, 1 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 13. pour the tempering over raw tamarind rasam and mix well. 14. finally, pachi pulusu is ready to enjoy with hot steamed rice.

title: Pakodi Recipe (Street Style Onion Pakora)
ingredients: 1.25 cup onions, 0.75 tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 green chilies, 1 mint, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 sprigs coriander leaves, Salt, 0.25 cup besan, 2 tbsps rice flour, 2 tbsps corn flour, 0.375 tsp red chili powder, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp ajwain, 1 tbsp oil, Oil
instructions: 1. Add thinly sliced onions, green chilies, ginger garlic paste, finely chopped coriander, mint & curry leaves to a bowl. Sprinkle salt all over. Gently mix for about 30 secs and set aside for 10 minutes. 2. You will see onions release moisture. 3. Add besan, rice flour and corn flour (corn starch). 4. Add red chili powder, turmeric and ajwain. 5. Mix well to make a tight dough. I have not used water at all. The mixture should look something like shown in the picture. But if it is too dry and doesn’t bind together then you may can sprinkle some water. The quantity of flours mentioned in the recipe works well for our taste. If you like more flour in your pakora, then you may also use up the flour mentioned as optional in the recipe card. 6. Heat up 1 tbsp oil in a kadai. It must turn very hot. Pour it to the dough. Mix it. 7. Heat oil in a kadai for deep frying. Test if it is hot enough by dropping a small portion of the mix. If the oil is hot enough the dough raises, make sure it is not smoky hot as well. 8. Take small portions and drop in the hot oil. Do not add the mixture in large lumpy portions. 9. Fry until golden on a medium flame stirring often. When they are done, bubbles will reduce. 10. Drain them on a kitchen tissue. 11. Serve onion pakoda hot or warm. These can also be reheated quickly in a oven at 220 C for 3 to 5 mins depending on the size.

title: Pakora Recipe | Vegetable Pakora
ingredients: 0.5 cup besan, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 3 green chili peppers, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.5 teaspoons carom seeds, oil for deep frying as needed, 1 medium carrot, 0.25 cup capsicum, 1 cup cabbage, 1 medium onion, 6 french beans, 1 cup spinach
instructions: 1. Wash the veggies and cut to thin 2 inch long strips. Check the pictures in the post. 2. Add them to a bowl along with ginger garlic, green chilies (or chilli flakes), salt, garam masala, mint or other herbs. Mix well & squeeze them gently to release moisture. Keep aside for 10 mins. 3. Add the gram flour, rice flour (or corn starch) along with ajwain (optional). Mix well to a slightly sticky dough. If the dough is too dry, sprinkle a few tablespoons of water and mix. It must be of a sticky dough consistency and not batter consistency. 4. Taste test and add more salt, garam masala or green chilies. 5. Heat oil in a deep pan on a medium heat. Test by dropping a small portion of dough, it must sizzle and come up but not brown. This is the right temperature. 6. Take small portions of dough and flatten with your fingers to 1½ inch size portions and gently slide to the hot oil. You won't shape it or drop it in lumps. 7. Regulate the flame to medium. Do not disturb for a minute or 2 until they firm up a bit. Then stir them and fry until golden, crisp and aromatic. 8. Remove the vegetable pakoras to a cooling rack or steel colander. To make the next batch, ensure the oil is hot enough but not smoking hot. Fry in batches until you finish all of the prepared dough. 9. Serve vegetable pakora hot with a cup of masala tea, Coriander chutney or green chutney. 10. To keep them crispy for longer, place them on a wired rack in a low oven setting. You can also reheat them in air fryer.

title: Pala thalikalu recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup rice flour, 0.5 cup water, 0.25 cup milk, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp ghee, 2 tbsp ghee, 12 cashews, 12 raisins, 2 tbsp coconut, 3 cups milk, 1 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 cup water, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 0.625 cup jaggery, 3 tbsp water
instructions: 1. Grease your murukku mould or janthikalu maker. 2. Mix 1 tbsp rice flour in ¼ cup water to make a smooth lump free mixture. 3. Set this aside. 4. Put the dough inside the mould. 5. When you press the mould gently the dough should come out easily without breaking. This is the right consistency of the dough. 6. Heat ghee in a wide pan. I used a pressure pan. Add cashews and fry until light golden. Then add dried coconut and raisins. When they turn golden remove to a plate. I forgot to use coconut, later I fried separately and used. 7. Pour milk to the pot and bring it to a boil. 8. When the milk comes to a boil, regulate the flame to medium. Make sure the milk is boiling, then gently squeeze in the dough from the mould. 9. I squeezed up all the dough at once in circular motion as my pressure pan was wide enough to hold the entire dough. If you are using a small pan then they will all clump up together due to less space. 10. Please note: Avoid using a spoon or ladle from here on. Just swirl the milk in the pan when needed to mix. This avoids breaking the pala thalikalu. 11. Do not stir them or disturb for 3 mins. They will break as the dough is still not cooked. After 3 mins, Swirl the milk in the pan gently a few times to disperse the thalikalu evenly in milk. 12. Allow to cook exactly for another 4 to 5 mins depending on the thickness of the thalikalu. So total cook time is 7 to 8 mins. Overcooking can make them hard and dense. 13. Stir the rice flour mixture in the cup with a spoon. Slowly pour it to the pan towards the sides of the pan. Swirl a few times to disperse the rice flour in the milk otherwise lumps can form. Cook until it thickens. Swril again if needed. With in 1 to 2 mins the mixture turns thick. Do not overcook until it turns too thick, slightly thickness is good enough as it turns more thick after cooling. 14. Add cardamom powder. Turn off and take it away from the stove. If adding grated jaggery then you can add it and stir until it dissolves. Cooking further may curdle the payasam. 15. Ensure the jaggery syrup is cooled completely. Filter that to the pala thalikalu. Swirl gently as many times as needed until the jaggery is blended well. You can also use ladle at this stage as the thalikalu firm up. 16. Pour some ghee if desired and add the cashews, coconut and raisins.

title: palak chaat recipe | palak pakoda chaat | spinach pakora chaat
ingredients: 1 cup besan, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp ajwain, 0.5 tsp salt, water, 1 bunch palak leaves, oil, potato, 2 tsp green chutney, 2 tsp tamarind chutney, 2 tbsp curd, chilli powder, chaat masala, cumin powder, aamchur, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp sev, 2 tbsp boondi, salad
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup besan and ¼ cup rice flour. 2. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp chaat masala, ¼ tsp ajwain and ½ tsp salt. 3. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. add water as required and prepare the batter. 5. prepare a smooth lump-free batter. 6. now dip the palak leaf in batter, covering completely. 7. deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 8. stir and fry on both the flame until it turns golden brown and crisp. 9. drain off the pakoda to remove excess oil. 10. in a plate, place fried pakoda. 11. top with boiled potato, 2 tsp green chutney, 2 tsp tamarind chutney and 2 tbsp curd. 12. also top with chilli powder, chaat masala, cumin powder and aamchur. 13. further, add 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp sev, 2 tbsp boondi and salad. 14. finally, enjoy palak patta chaat as an evening snack.

title: palak chakli recipe | palak murukku recipe | spinach murukku
ingredients: 2 cups rice flour, 0.5 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp roasted chana dal roasted bengal gram putani, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin seeds, pinch of hing asafoetida, salt to taste, 1 tsp butter, 0.25 cup water, oil for deep frying, handful of palak spinach leaves, 2 green chilli, 0.25 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare the palak puree by blending handful of palak, 2 green chilli and ¼ cup water. 2. blend to smooth paste and keep aside. 3. now in a large mixing bowl, take 2 cups rice flour, ½ cup besan, 2 tbsp powdered putani, ½ tsp jeera, pinch of hing, salt to taste and 1 tsp butter. 4. combine all the ingredients well, making sure to mash butter. 5. now add prepared palak puree and mix well. 6. additionally water as required and knead dough to smooth and soft dough. 7. now take star mold and prepare small spiral shaped chaklis by pressing. 8. take one palak chakli at time and slide it into hot oil. 9. flip the chakli and fry on medium flame till they turn crispy from both sides. 10. furthermore, drain over a paper towel to remove excess oil. 11. finally serve crispy and healthy palak chakli with masala tea or masala milk.

title: Palak Chicken (Chicken Saag Recipe)
ingredients: 1.1 pounds Boneless Chicken, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon black pepper, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 8.5 ounces Baby Spinach, 3 green chilies, 18 cashews, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.875 cup onions, 0.875 cup tomatoes, 2 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons whipping cream, 1 Indian bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3 green cardamoms, 3.5 cloves 3 to 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 1 black cardamom
instructions: 1. Add chicken to a large mixing bowl. Sprinkle ¼ teaspoon salt, black pepper, red chilli powder and lemon juice. Mix and set aside until we need it. 2. Pour 1 teaspoon oil to a hot pan and add cashews and green chilies. Stir fry until the chilies blister. Add spinach (cleaned, rinsed and drained) and stir fry on a medium heat until wilted and fully cooked, for 6 mins. Turn off the heat & cool this. 3. Pour ½ cup cold water to a blender and transfer the spinach, chilies and cashews. You may set aside some of the green chilies which you can adjust later. 4. Make a smooth puree and taste test. If you want more heat add back the green chilis that we set aside and blend. 5. Add the rest of the oil (along with ghee if using) to a hot pan. Add all the whole spices and let them sizzle. Stir in the chopped onions and saute until light brown & really soft. 6. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for a minute, until aromatic. 7. Add tomatoes and salt. Stir well and cook until the tomatoes lose their raw flavor. If you want you may cook covered at this stage. 8. Add the marinated chicken and stir fry on a medium high heat, until pale. Stir in the garam masala. 9. Pour ½ to ¾ cup hot water and mix well. Cook covered on a medium heat, until chicken is fully cooked through. 10. Stir in the Spinach puree. If the palak chicken gravy is too thick , you may pour little hot water. 11. Simmer until bubbling hot and cook for 3 to 4 minutes. Taste test and adjust salt. Take kasuri methi in your palms and crush it well. Sprinkle all over. Turn off the heat. Keep the gravy slightly runny if you want to serve it with rice. It thickens slightly upon cooling. 12. Garnish Palak Chicken optionally with cream. Squeeze lemon juice while serving. Serve with Steamed rice, Naan, Roti or Chapati. 13. This is not a part of the recipe but if you love authentic Indian flavors, make a nice tadka and pour it over the dish. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee on a medium heat. Add 2 to 3 sliced garlic cloves and 2 dried red chillies. Fry on a low heat for a minute, until garlic turns light golden. Add ¼ teaspoon hing (optional). Turn off and pour it over palak chicken.

title: Palak Corn Recipe
ingredients: 8 cups spinach, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1.5 cup corn kernels, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 2 green cardamoms, 4 green chilies, 1 inch ginger, 4 garlic, 1 cup yellow onion, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 1 teaspoon salt, 18 cashew nuts, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 2 teaspoons kasuri methi, 0.5 cup water to cook, Lemon juice, 2.5 tablespoons heavy cream, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon ghee, 2 red chilies, 3 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon hing, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 grams paneer
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pot and add the cardamoms, onions, green chilies, ginger, garlic, and salt. Saute for 4 to 5 mins until transparent. Add the cashews, tomatoes and water. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat. Reduce to medium and cook covered until very soft, for 10 mins. 2. Open the lid & reserve half of the green chilies aside so you can adjust the heat levels as you blend in the next steps. 3. Stir in garam masala, salt, spinach and coriander leaves. Saute until wilted and cooked through, for 3 to 4 mins. Turn off and stir in the rest of the water. 4. Blend using an immersion blender or a stand blender to a smooth or coarse puree, as per your preference. (cool down first, if using a stand blender). Taste test and adjust the green chilies/heat level at this stage. 5. Return the palak mixture to the pot and add corn kernels. Bring to a boil and let simmer for 3 to 4 mins. If you are using mature spinach, you may cook for another 3 to 4 mins. Taste test and adjust salt & garam masala. Crush kasuri methi in your hands and sprinkle. 6. Turn off and pour the cream. Corn palak is ready to serve at this stage. You may serve it with a squeeze of lemon juice if you don't want to make the tadka. 7. Heat 1 teaspoon ghee in a non-stick pan and place the paneer cubes. Fry until golden on a medium high heat, tossing them around gently. Stir in half to the corn palak and reserve the rest. 8. Turn off the heat and add the remaining ghee, cumin seeds and red chilies. When the seeds sizzle, turn on and add the garlic. 9. Let fry on a low heat until light golden. Turn off and stir in the hing, red chili powder and the rest of the fried paneer. Mix well and pour this immediately over the corn palak. Garnish with more heavy cream if you want. 10. Perform the first step on saute mode and pressure cook for 5 mins after pouring water. I cook basmati rice along with this, placed on a high legged trivet. Gently release the pressure immediately. Follow the rest as mentioned above.

title: palak cutlet recipe | palak tikki recipe | spinach potato cutlet
ingredients: 3 tsp oil, 3 chilli, 1 inch ginger paste, 1 bunch palak spinach, 3 potato, 0.5 cup paneer cottage cheese, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp corn flour, 1 tbsp maida, 0.25 tsp chilli flakes, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup bread crumbs
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 3 tsp oil and saute 3 chilli and 1 inch ginger paste. 2. add 1 bunch palak and saute until palak shrinks. 3. cool completely and blend to smooth paste without adding any water. 4. transfer the palak paste into lage bowl and add 3 potato. 5. also add ½ cup paneer and ½ cup bread crumbs. 6. now add 1 tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp aamchur, 2 tbsp coriander and ½ tsp salt. 7. combine well making sure the spices are well combined. 8. mix well making a non-sticky dough. if the dough is still sticky, add 1 tbsp breadcrumbs and mix well. this helps to absorb moisture. 9. now prepare maida paste by mixing, 2 tbsp corn flour, 1 tbsp maida, ¼ tsp chilli flakes and ¼ tsp salt. 10. make a smooth lump free batter by adding ½ cup water. 11. further, take a ball sized vegetable mixture, and roll cylindrical shape. 12. dip in maida paste coating all over. 13. then roll in breadcrumbs. make double coating to get a crispy outer layer. 14. now deep fry in hot oil or bake in preheated oven at 180 degree celcius for 15 minutes. 15. stir occasionally, until it turns golden brown and crisp. drain over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 16. finally, enjoy palak cutlet with tomato sauce.

title: Palak Khichdi Recipe | Dhaba Style - Palak Dal Khichdi
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 0.5 cup moong dal, 0.5 cup toor dal, water, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp ghee, 4 cup water, 1 bunch palak spinach, few coriander, few mint, 3 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 4 chilli, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 2 tomato, 1.5 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 1 tbsp ghee, 5 cloves garlic, 0.25 tsp chilli powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, take 1 cup basmati rice, ½ cup moong dal and ½ cup toor dal. Rinse and soak for 15 minutes. 2. Drain off the water, and transfer to the pressure cooker. 3. Add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp ghee and 4 cup water. 4. Cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles. 5. In another pan, boil enough water to blanch 1 bunch palak. 6. Once the palak is blanched, dip in cold water. This helps to retain the bright green colour of palak. 7. Now transfer to the mixer jar, add few coriander, few mint, 3 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger and 4 chilli. 8. Grind to a smooth paste. Keep aside. 9. To prepare the khichdi, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tsp cumin and pinch hing. 10. Add 1 onion and saute till the onion softens. 11. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp of cumin powder, ½ tsp of coriander powder, and 1 tsp of garam masala. 12. Saute till the spices turn aromatic. 13. Now add 2 tomato and saute till the tomato softens. 14. Also add prepared palak puree and cook well. 15.
