Now add cooked rice and dal, 1½ cup water, 1 tsp salt and mix gently. 16. Boil for 5 minutes, adjusting the consistency as required. 17. To prepare the tempering, heat 1 tbsp ghee. Fry 5 cloves garlic till they turn golden brown. 18. Turn off the flame, add ¼ tsp chilli powder and mix well. 19. Pour the tempering over khichidi and mix well. 20. Finally, enjoy Palak Khichdi Recipe with pickle and raita.

title: palak medu vada recipe | spinach medu vada | keerai vadai recipe
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, water for soaking, tbsp of water for grinding, 1 tsp rice flour, salt to taste, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.25 onion, 3 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 cup palak spinach leaves, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. soak one cup of urad dal with enough water. 2. soak for 3 - 4 hours. soaking for more than 4 hours makes the batter watery and absorbs oil. 3. grind to fine fluffy paste adding water as required. i have added 4 tbsp of water to make smooth thick paste. 4. transfer the urad dal paste / batter to a large mixing bowl. 5. beat and mix the batter in circular motion till they turn light. this helps to incorporate air into batter and make vadas soft and fluffy. 6. now add a tsp of rice flour and salt to taste. adding rice flour makes vadas crispy. 7. to test the consistency of batter drop a small ball of batter into bowl containing water. if the ball floats up then the consistency is rite. 8. add chopped ginger and green chilli. 9. also add in chopped onions and coriander leaves. 10. now add finely chopped spinach / palak leaves. 11. mix the batter well. 12. heat the oil in large kadai. wet your hands with enough water and take a small ball sized batter and make it round. 13. now slowly shape the edges and make a hole at the centre like you prepared in bread vada. 14. deep fry the palak vadas slowly on medium flame. 15. fry on both sides till they turn golden brown. 16. drain on paper towel to remove excess oil. 17. serve hot vada with chutney or sambar of your choice.

title: Palak pakoda (Spinach pakora)
ingredients: 2.5 cups spinach, 1 cup onions, 2.5 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup mint, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon carom seeds, 0.5 cup besan, 2 tablespoon rice flour, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, teaspoon garam masala, Oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. Pluck and rinse spinach leaves (not stalks) in a large pot of water. Drain the water and repeat rinsing a few times. Drain them to a colander. Shake off the colander in your kitchen sink to drain excess water completely. Set this aside & slice the onion. I used about 2 ½ cups of chopped spinach. You can use more or less as much as you have. 2. Meanwhile slice 1 large onion (1 cup slices). Slice them evenly and not too thin or too thick. If the slices are too thin pakoras will burn quickly before the besan fries. If they are too thick pakoras won’t turn crispy. You will have soft onions in your palak pakora. Add chopped spinach and sliced onions to a mixing bowl. Sprinkle salt over the onions. 3. Add 2 to 3 chopped green chilies, handful of mint leaves and coriander leaves. Skip if you don’t have mint leaves. But they make your palak pakoda super flavorful. 4. The add ½ teaspoon ginger garlic paste or 1 teaspoon fine chopped ginger, ½ teaspoon carom seeds (ajwain), ½ teaspoon red chilli powder and ¼ teaspoon garam masala or ½ teaspoon coriander powder. 5. Mix all of these well gently without crushing the palak. Set this aside for 10 mins. Meanwhile you can make your chutney or tea to with these fritters. 6. After 10 mins, begin to heat the oil on a medium heat & then make the pakora mixture. 7. To the bowl, sprinkle ½ cup gram flour & 2 tablespoons rice flour. 8. Mix to make a slightly tight but moist dough. If the mixture is too dry, sprinkle water and mix. Do not pour water, just sprinkle as we don’t want a lot of moisture in the mixture. If you feel there is not enough flour in your mixture add more in the same proportions. The amount of water to use depends on how much moisture the onions and spinach will release. 9. Taste test at this stage and add more salt if needed. Also add 6 to 10 cashew nuts. Split them and use. You can also break them to small pieces.

title: Palak Pakoda Recipe | Spinach Fritters - Street Style
ingredients: 1 bunch palak spinach, 1 onion, 2 chilkli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp ajwain, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, pinch hing, 1 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.5 tsp salt, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, rinse the palak leaves and take 1 bunch palak in a wide plate. 2. Add 1 onion, 2 chillies, a few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, ¼ tsp ajwain, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and a pinch of hing. Mix well, making sure all the spices are well combined. 3. Now add 1 cup besan, 2 tbsp rice flour and ½ tsp salt. 4. Squeeze and mix without adding any water. 5. The water in the palak is enough to form the dough. Add more besan if required. 6. Now drop the dough into hot oil, forming a random shape. 7. Fry on medium flame, stirring occasionally. 8. Fry until the pakoda turns golden brown and crisp. 9. Finally, drain off the Palak Pakoda Recipe and enjoy with hot tea.

title: Palak Paneer Recipe (Spinach Paneer)
ingredients: 15 grams paneer, 3.5 to 4 cups palak, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 green chilies, 0.75 cup onions, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 9 cashewnuts, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup water, 0.75 cup water, 3 tablespoons cream, 0.125 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((laung) (optional))
instructions: 1. Pluck only the young & tender spinach leaves and discard the stems as they may leave a bitter taste. If using baby spinach you can use the stems as well. 2. Add them to a large pot of water. Rinse them well few times & drain to a colander. 3. Allow the water to drain completely otherwise it will let out lot of moisture while cooking. 4. Heat half tablespoon oil in a pan. Saute green chilies, cashews and spinach for 3 to 4 mins until the leaves wilt off thoroughly & raw smell of spinach has gone away. [OR another option is to blanch the palak in 4 cups of hot water with ¼ tsp salt for 2 mins. Then immerse in ice cold water. Drain completely.] 5. Cool this completely. Blend this along with water to a smooth puree. The puree should be smooth and thick. You may add 1 to 2 tbsps more water to help in blending. 6. Heat 1 tablespoon butter and half tablespoon oil to the same pan, Once they melt, add cinnamon, cardamoms, cloves & cumin seeds. 7. When the spices begin to sizzle, add onions and fry till they turn transparent to golden. 8. Next saute ginger garlic paste for 1 to 2 minutes or until you begin to smell it nice. 9. Then add tomatoes with salt. Saute until they break down and turn mushy. 10. Add garam masala & saute until the masala smells good. This may take 2 mins. (for a smoother curry refer notes) 11. Pour water and cook covered until onions are completely soft. There should be some water left in the pan. (for consistency check video) 12. Lower the flame, add kasuri methi and pureed spinach. Mix well and cook until it begins to bubble for about 2 to 3 mins. If the curry is too thick you may add a few tbsps of hot water. 13. Avoid overcooking. Add paneer & mix well. Turn off and remove to a serving bowl. Optionally garnish with cream. 14. Serve palak paneer with naan, roti, Basmati rice or Jeera rice.

title: Palak Paratha (Spinach Paratha)
ingredients: 1.5 cups Whole wheat flour, 1.5 cups spinach, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 green chili, 0.5 tsp salt, 1.5 tbsp water, 1 tbsp Oil, 2 tbsp Ghee
instructions: 1. Wash spinach and drain completely. 2. Add them to a blender with chili, ginger or garlic and make a fine puree with the help of 1 to 2 tbsp water. Use water only if needed. 3. Add the puree to the flour along with salt and spice powders. 4. Knead together to make a dough. 5. If the dough turns sticky, just sprinkle more flour & knead to a non sticky dough. 6. If the dough turns too dry then, sprinkle water & knead. 7. Add little oil and knead well to make a soft pliable dough. 8. Set this aside for 20 to 30 minutes. Make 8 equal sized balls. Cover them. 9. Flour the rolling area and flatten a ball. Sprinkle flour. 10. Roll it to make slightly thick palak paratha. 11. Sprinkle flour over the paratha as needed to prevent sticking. It has to be slightly thicker than roti. 12. Heat a tawa until hot enough. Transfer a paratha to the tawa. 13. When the paratha begins to get bubbles over it. 14. Flip it to the other side. Gently press down the paratha with a spatula to puff it. Cook until golden spots appear. 15. Once done smear little ghee. 16. Place a kitchen tissue on a plate. Stack the palak parathas to keep them soft. 17. Serve palak paratha with chutney, dal or curry. Or use them to wrap as rolls with veggie or egg fillings.

title: palak paratha recipe | spinach paratha recipe | palak ka paratha
ingredients: 1 bunch palak leaves spinach, 3 cups water, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, 2 cup wheat flour, 0.5 tsp ajwain carom seeds, salt to taste, 2 tsp oil, water as required, 0.5 cup wheat flour, oil ghee for roasting
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare a dough with palak puree. 2. further pinch a medium sized ball dough, roll and flatten it. 3. also dust with some wheat flour. 4. and roll it in a thin circle like chapathi or paratha. 5. now on a hot tawa place the rolled paratha and cook for a minute. 6. furthermore, when the base is partly cooked, flip and cook. 7. also brush oil / ghee and press slightly. 8. finally, serve palak paratha with raita and pickle.

title: Palak Pulao Recipe | Palak Rice | Spinach Rice - Lunch Box Meal
ingredients: 1 bunch palak, 4 cloves garlic, 2 inch ginger, 3 chilli, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 carrot, 1 potato, 0.5 capsicum, 3 tbsp sweet corn, 3 tbsp peas, 2 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 cup basmati rice, 2 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a saucepan take water and get to a boil. Once the water comes to a boil, add 1 bunch palak and dip completely. 2. Boil for 2 minutes or until the palak is blanched. 3. Drain off the palak and rinse in ice cold water to retain the colour of the palak. 4. Squeeze the water from the palak, and transfer to the mixer jar. 5. Add 4 cloves garlic, 2 inch ginger and 3 chilli. 6. Grind to fine paste without adding water. Keep aside. 7. In a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Add 1 tsp cumin and splutter. 8. Add in 1 onion and saute until the onion softens. 9. Further add 1 carrot, 1 potato, ½ capsicum, 3 tbsp sweet corn and 3 tbsp peas. 10. Stir fry until the vegetables turn crunchy. 11. Now add in prepared palak paste and cook well. 12. Further add 2 cup water, 1 tsp salt and ½ tsp garam masala. 13. Mix and get to a rolling boil. 14. Once the water comes to a boil, add 1 cup basmati rice (soaked 20 mins) and mix gently. 15. Also, add 2 tsp lemon juice, cover and simmer for 20 minutes. 16. Once the rice is cooked well, fluff the rice gently. 17. Finally, enjoy Palak Rice Recipe with raita.

title: Palak Rice Recipe (Spinach Rice)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 bay leaf, 2 cups spinach, 2 green chilies, 2 garlic cloves, 1 onion, 1 medium potato, 0.625 garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 11 mint leaves, 1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Rinse rice and cook in a pot or cooker. Rice must be grainy and not mushy. 2. Wash palak thoroughly in lots of water. Drain completely. Add them to a small blender and puree to a smooth paste. You can also add few tablespoons of water to help blend smooth or alternately fine chop and use. 3. Heat a pan with oil, add cumin seeds and bay leaf. 4. When the cumin begins to sizzle, add garlic & saute until it turns fragrant for about 40 to 60 seconds. 5. Add onion and green chilli . Saute until the onions turn golden. 6. Then add potatoes. Saute until they become tender. If needed sprinkle some water and cook. 7. Sprinkle garam masala, salt and turmeric. Pour palak puree and saute until the raw smell goes off. 8. Add mint leaves and saute for a min or two. Switch off the stove. 9. Transfer the cooled rice, squeeze in the lemon juice. Mix everything well. Taste test and add more salt if needed 10. Serve palak rice hot or warm. 11. Heat oil in a pot. Add cumin and bay leaf. When the cumin sizzles, saute ginger garlic paste. 12. Then add onions and green chilies. Fry until transparent. Add potatoes or any other veggies you intend to use. Stir fry for 2 to 3 mins. 13. Stir in the garam masala, salt and turmeric. Transfer the pureed palak and saute until the raw smell goes away. 14. When the mixture turns thicker, add uncooked rice. 15. If using pressure cooker, pour 1¾ cups water for basmati rice. (2 cups if using normal rice). If cooking in a pot use 2 cups water. 16. Pressure cook on a medium high flame for 1 whistle for basmati rice. If using normal rice pressure cook for 2 whistles. 17. Release the pressure manually with the help of a spatula if using basmati rice. 18. Fluff up palak rice with a fork.

title: Palak Sabji Recipe | Dhaba Style Palak Sabzi Curry
ingredients: 1 bunch palak spinach, 2 tomato, 2 tbsp roasted peanut, 1 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 tbsp sesame, 2 tbsp oil, pinch hing, 2 tbps oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, 5 cloves garlic, 2 onion, 2 tbsp besan, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 1 tbsp ghee, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 tsp sesame, pinch hing, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 0.5 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a blender take 2 tomato, 2 tbsp roasted peanut, 10 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger and ½ tbsp sesame. 2. Grind to smooth paste. Keep aside. 3. In a pan heat 2 tbsp oil, add pinch hing and saute 1 bunch palak. 4. Saute for 5 minutes or until the palak is cooked well. Keep aside. 5. In a kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil, ½ tsp cumin, 5 cloves garlic and saute well. 6. Also add 2 onions and saute till the onions turn golden brown. 7. Further add 2 tbsp besan and saute till the besan turns aromatic. 8. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder and 1 tsp salt. 9. Saute until the spices turn aromatic. 10. Add in prepared tomato puree and cook till the oil separates. 11. Further add in water and adjust the consistency. 12. Now add cooked palak and mix well. 13. Cover and cook until the flavours are absorbed well. 14. To prepare the tempering, heat 1 tbsp ghee. Add ½ tsp cumin, 1 tsp sesame, pinch hing and ½ tsp chilli powder. 15. Pour the tempering over the curry, add 2 tbsp kasuri methi, ½ tsp garam masala and mix well. 16. Finally, enjoy Palak Sabji with roti or rice.

title: Palak Soup Recipe | Cream Of Spinach Soup
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 2 cloves garlic, 0.5 inch ginger, 2 tbsp onion, 1 bunch palak spinach, 2 tsp oil, 1 tbsp butter, 2 tsp maida, 1 bay leaf, 4 cup milk, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp sugar, 2 tbsp cream
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil. Add 2 cloves garlic, ½ inch ginger and 2 tbsp onion. 2. Saute until the onions shrink slithgly. Do not brown the onions as the flavour will change. 3. Now add 1 bunch palak and saute until the palak shrinks completely. 4. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 5. Add water and grind to a smooth paste. Keep aside. 6. In a large kadai heat 2 tsp oil, 1 tbsp butter and add 2 tsp maida. 7. saute on low flame until the maida turns aromatic. Do not brown the maida, just fry until the maida turns aromatic. 8. Now add 1 bay leaf and saute slightly. 9. Further, add 4 cup of milk and mix using a whisk. 10. Continue to cook until the milk thickens. 11. Also, add in prepared palak puree and cook well. Simmer for 2 minutes, or until the flavors are absorbed. 12. Add in ½ tsp pepper powder, ½ tsp salt, ½ tsp sugar, and 2 tbsp cream. Mix well. 13. Finally, enjoy Palak Soup Recipe garnished with cream.

title: Palak Tofu (Spinach Tofu)
ingredients: 2 grams extra firm pressed tofu cut to bite size pieces, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 15 whole cashew nuts, 1.5 tablespoons unrefined virgin coconut oil, 0.75 cup onions, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 inch ginger, 3 medium garlic, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 5 cups baby spinach, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.375 teaspoon toasted cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom, 1 teaspoon crushed kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon butter
instructions: 1. Marinate: In a medium bowl, mix together chili powder, garam masala, salt, lemon juice and water or yogurt. Add pressed tofu and gently coat the marinade. Cover and rest until needed later or in the refrigerator overnight, up to 2 days. 2. Heat oil in a pan and add onion, ginger, garlic and green chilies. Saute for 5 to 6 mins, until transparent. 3. Add tomatoes & sauté until mushy. Pour ¼ cup water. Cook covered until the tomatoes and onions become soft. 4. Stir in the spinach and saute until wilted & the raw smell is gone, for 4 to 5 mins. 5. Cool down and transfer to a blender. Add the soaked cashews with the soaking liquid or coconut milk. 6. Blend to a smooth puree, adding more water/ coconut milk to bring to a consistency (I used ¼ cup more). Return to the pan & stir in salt, cardamom, garam masala & cumin powder. Cook for 3 to 5 mins, until thick. 7. Add crushed kasuri methi and turn off. 8. Pan Fry: Meanwhile, heat oil in a pan and add the marinated tofu. Pan fry until golden, turn with a spoon and fry on all sides. (Takes me about 6 to 7 mins.) Optionally add butter and mix well to coat the tofu with melted butter. 9. Air Fry or Bake: If you want to air fry or oven bake, preheat the oven to 400 F or 200 C for 15 mins. Spread the tofu on a prepared baking sheet and air fry or bake for 15 mins, shaking after 9 mins. 10. Mix in 1 tablespoon lemon juice to the palak gravy. Layer the pan fried tofu over the curry. 11. Optionally garnish with coconut milk or whisked Greek yogurt. Serve Palak Tofu with flatbreads like roti, chapati, naan, bread or over rice.

title: Palakura Pachadi Recipe | Andhra Style Spinach Chutney
ingredients: 1 bunch palak spinach, 2 onion, 2 tomato, 1 cloves garlic, 0.5 cup oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 3 red chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp salt
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a small mixer jar take 2 onion, 2 tomato and 10 cloves garlic. 2. Blend to coarse paste, keep aside. 3. In a large kadai heat ½ cup oil. add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 3 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering. 4. Keeping the flame on low, add 2 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder and 1 tsp salt. 5. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 6. Now add in prepared onion tomato paste and cook well. 7. Cook until the oil separates from the mixture. 8. Add in 1 bunch of finely chopped palak leaves, 1 tsp salt and mix well. 9. Mix until the palak leaves are cooked well and the oil separates from the sides. 10. Finally, enjoy Palakura Chuteny with hot steamed rice, idli, dosa or chapathi.

title: Palakura Pappu
ingredients: 0.5 cup dal, 1.25 cup water, 1 grams spinach, 1.5 green chili, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 1 garlic, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2 teaspoons tamarind, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili, 1 green chili, 1 garlic, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 6 curd chilies
instructions: 1. Add dal to a large bowl and rinse it well. Pour fresh water to cover the dal. Add onions, tomatoes, chilies, garlic, tamarind water (start with less), salt, turmeric and chopped palak on top of the dal. Place it in the cooker. 2. Cover and pressure cook for 4 whistles or 10 to 12 mins in the Instant pot. When the pressure drops, open and mash slightly. Taste test, adjust salt and tamarind to taste. 3. Temper - Thalimpu: Pour ghee to a small tadka pan and heat it. Add cumin, mustard, red chili, chopped green chili (for extra hot) and garlic. When the spices splutter add curry leaves and hing. Turn off. (If you want you may add ⅓ tsp red chili powder for green chilies at this stage) Pour this to the cooked palakura pappu. 4. Add dal to a sauce pan or cooker. Rinse well a few times. Drain the water and pour fresh water. Add all the optional ingredients - onions, tomatoes, garlic and green chilies. Pressure cook for 2 whistles on a medium heat or for 10 mins in the Instant pot. 5. When the pressure drops, open the lid and add the chopped palakura. Let cook until it wilts completely. Stir in tamarind water, turmeric and salt. Mash it slightly. If your pappu is too thick pour little hot boiling water to bring to a consistency. 6. Make the thalimpu following the above instruction. 7. Rinse dal in a pot and pour ¾ cup water. Optionally add chopped tomatoes and green chilies. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat and reduce to medium. Cook until pappu is tender, hold shape and fully cooked but not mushy. 8. Stir in the spinach, turmeric and salt. While it cooks, heat a small pan with ghee or oil. Add the curd chilies (optional), fry till they turn crispy. Remove them immediately to prevent burning. Keep aside. 9. Next add mustard, cumin, garlic, red chili/green chili when they begin to splutter add curry leaves and hing. 10. When the spinach wilts and is cooked through, pour the tempering to the pappu & mix well. Taste test and adjust salt. Turn off and let rest for 5 mins. Squeeze some lemon juice and garnish with the fried curd chilies.

title: Palkova Recipe | South Indian Special Palgova
ingredients: 5 cup milk, 0.25 cup sugar
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 5 cup milk. use good quality milk for more creamy texture. 2. stir occasionally to prevent milk from sticking to bottom. 3. get the milk to a boil. 4. stir continuously, and the milk thickens in 8 minutes. 5. after 20 minutes, the milk turns creamy texture. 6. keep stirring on low flame, to prevent milk from burning. 7. after 40 minutes, thick texture of milk is formed. 8. now add ¼ cup sugar. add more sugar if you prefer. 9. keep stirring till the sugar dissolves. 10. continue to cook on low flame till the mixture separates from the pan. 11. finally, serve palkova hot / chilled or make peda and serve.

title: pan fry ghee rice recipe | nei choru recipe | ghee rice recipe |
ingredients: 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 cup basmati rice, 0.75 tsp jeera cumin seeds, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 2 cardamom elachi, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 star anise, 1 inch cinammon stick, 15 black pepper, 15 cashews, 0.25 onion, 2 garlic cloves, 0.5 inch ginger, salt to taste
instructions: 1. in wide pan, take a tbsp of ghee. 2. once the ghee starts to melt, add jeera. 3. also add spices like bay leaf, cardamom, cloves, star anise, cinammon stick and black pepper. saute till they turn aromatic. 4. now add cashews and fry till they turn golden brown. 5. once the cashews turn almost golden brown, add sliced onions, ginger and garlic. fry till the raw smell of ginger and garlic disappears. 6. finally, add cooked rice and salt to taste. 7. mix well without breaking rice grains. cook for a minute. 8. serve ghee rice with raita, saagu or as it is.

title: pan fry ghee rice recipe | nei choru recipe | ghee rice recipe |
ingredients: 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 cup basmati rice, 0.75 tsp jeera cumin seeds, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 2 cardamom elachi, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 star anise, 1 inch cinammon stick, 15 black pepper, 15 cashews, 0.25 onion, 2 garlic cloves, 0.5 inch ginger, salt to taste
instructions: 1. in wide pan, take a tbsp of ghee. 2. once the ghee starts to melt, add jeera. 3. also add spices like bay leaf, cardamom, cloves, star anise, cinammon stick and black pepper. saute till they turn aromatic. 4. now add cashews and fry till they turn golden brown. 5. once the cashews turn almost golden brown, add sliced onions, ginger and garlic. fry till the raw smell of ginger and garlic disappears. 6. finally, add cooked rice and salt to taste. 7. mix well without breaking rice grains. cook for a minute. 8. serve ghee rice with raita, saagu or as it is.

title: Panakam Recipe
ingredients: 4 tbsp Jaggery, 2 cups Water, Pinch cardamom powder, Pinch dry ginger powder, Pinch pepper, 0.25 cup moong dal, 0.5 cup jaggery, 1 cup Rice flour, few tbsp water, 0.25 cup ghee, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup rice, 0.25 cup coconut pieces, 2 pinches cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Mix jaggery and water and stir till jaggery melts. Strain to remove impurities. 2. Add cardamom, ginger powder and crushed pepper. Panakam is ready. This is traditional panakam offered to god. There are also variations where lemon juice is squeezed. If you prefer you can also add little lemon juice but not sure if it is offered to god. 3. Soak moong dal for one to 2 hours. Drain off the water completely. This is vadapappu. 4. Mix jaggery and rice flour well. Sprinkle little water and knead well to get a dough. Do not add too much water. Dough should hold shape. Pachi chalimidi is ready. 5. Wash 1 cup rice well. Soak it overnight in enough water. 6. Next morning, drain the water. Leave it in the colander until all the water is completely drained but the rice is still moist. 7. Add the rice to a blender and make a very fine powder or flour. 8. Sieve it using a very fine mess or sieve. Measure 1 cup of the flour for making chalimidi. Keep this ready. You will be left with little excess flour. You can use that for some other recipe. 9. Roast ¼ cup chopped coconut pieces in ¼ plus 1 tbsp ghee till you get a nice aroma. The coconut pieces have to turn slightly golden in color. Set the pan aside with coconut and ghee together. 10. Add half cup sugar along with half cup water to a large pot. 11. Boil the mixture until it reaches a soft ball consistency. Drop a spoonful of syrup in a bowl of water, you must be able to make a ball. 12. Immediately add ghee and coconut, give a quick stir. Move it away from the stove. Add cardamom powder. 13. Add ¾ cup sieved rice flour. Mix well to remove lumps. You must be quick in doing this. Depending on the consistency, you may need to add more flour, about 2 to 4 tbsps. You will need to stop adding if you feel the mixture is too thick. The mixture has to be slightly runny, it will firm after cooling. 14. Add another table spoon of ghee at the end. This prevents it looking dry like mine. Cool it thoroughly. 15. Enjoy chalimidi with family and friends.

title: pancharatna sweet recipe | pancharatna halwa | pancharatna barfi
ingredients: 1 cup dates khajoor, 0.5 cup raisins, 1 cup water, 0.5 cup cashew, 0.25 cup almonds, 2 tsp poppy seeds, 1 cup dry coconut, 1 cup jaggery, 0.25 cup water, 1 tsp ghee, 2 tbsp badam, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take 1 cup of dates and ½ cup raisins. 2. soak in 1 cup hot water for 30 minutes or until it softens. 3. transfer to the mixi and blend to smooth paste. keep aside. 4. in a heavy-bottomed pan, take ½ cup cashew, ¼ cup almonds. 5. roast on low flame until the nuts turn crunchy. 6. also, add 2 tsp poppy seeds and 1 cup dry coconut. 7. roast until the coconut turns slightly golden brown. keep aside. 8. in a large kadai, take 1 cup jaggery and ¼ cup water. 9. stir until the jaggery melts. continue to boil until 2 string consistency is achieved. 10. now add in dates raisins paste and cook. 11. cook for 3-4 minutes or until the mixture thickens. make sure to cook well, else the burfi will be sticky. 12. further, add roasted nuts and mix well. 13. also, add 1 tsp ghee and continue to cook until the mixture separates the pan. 14. additionally, add 2 tbsp powdered badam and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. 15. mix well, combining everything well. adding powdered badam helps to absorb excess moisture, adjust the quantity accordingly. 16. now transfer to the baking paper and shape to a log shape. 17. roll into roasted sesame seeds and roll tightly in butter paper. 18. refrigerate for 60 minutes, or until the burfi is set completely. 19. finally, cut into desired shapes and enjoy pancharatna halwa for 2 weeks.

title: panchmel dal recipe | pancharatna dal | rajasthani dal panchratan
ingredients: 0.25 cup toor dal, 0.25 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup moong dal, 0.25 cup masoor dal, 0.25 cup chana dal, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 bay leaf, 1 tsp ghee, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 bay leaf, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 black cardamom, 1 tsp cumin, 1 red chilli, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tomato, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 1 red chilli
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take ¼ cup toor dal, ¼ cup urad dal, ¼ cup moong dal, ¼ cup masoor dal and ¼ cup chana dal. 2. rinse well and soak for 30 minutes. 3. transfer the soaked dal into the pressure cooker. 4. add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt, 2 bay leaf, 1 tsp ghee and 3 cups water. 5. pressure cook for 5 whistles or until the dal is cooked completely. 6. once the pressure releases, open the cooker and check if the dal is cooked well. keep aside. 7. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee. add 1 bay leaf, 4 cloves, 1 black cardamom, 1 tsp cumin and 1 dried red chilli. 8. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 9. now add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until onions turn golden brown. 10. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and ½ tsp salt. 11. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 12. add 2 tomato and saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 13. now add cooked dal and mix well adjusting consistency as required. 14. cover and simmer for 10 minutes or until the flavours are well absorbed. 15. further add ¼ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 16. to prepare the tempering, heat 1 tsp ghee. add 1 tsp cumin, ¼ tsp chilli powder and 1 dried red chilli. 17. immediately pour the tempering over dal. make sure the tadka doesn't get burnt. 18. finally, enjoy panchmel dal or pancharatna dal with roti or jeera rice.

title: Paneer 65 Fry Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.5 teaspoon lemon juice, 0.1875 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons cornstarch, 2 tablespoons rice flour, 0.25 cup water, 0.75 cup oil, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 4.5 green chilies, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 tablespoon garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon sugar, 1.5 teaspoons vinegar, 3 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Dice paneer to ¾ inch pieces and add them to a mixing bowl. Add all the ingredients mentioned for marinade – ginger garlic paste, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, red chilli powder, turmeric, chopped curry leaves and salt. 2. Toss the paneer with all the ingredients we just added so the spices coat all the paneer well. Sprinkle lemon juice or vinegar and 2 to 3 tablespoons of water. Toss again and set aside as long as you like. (up to 48 hours in the fridge.) 3. Tempering paneer 65 is optional but this makes a lot of difference to the dish. If you want to skip you may skip this section and head over to frying paneer section directly. 4. Prepare the red chilli paste by mixing the red chilli powder, garlic paste, sugar, salt, vinegar (or yogurt) and water in a small bowl. Keep this aside. 5. Pour 1 ½ tablespoons oil to a wok and heat it. Add the chopped garlic, green chilies and curry leaves. Fry until the garlic begins to smell good. 6. Lower the flame. Stir and pour the sauce we made. Cook this on a medium heat until oil separates from the thick sauce. At this stage the sauce should be essentially thick to keep your final paneer 65 crisp and not runny. (check stepwise pictures in the post) Turn off the heat. 7. Add flour and salt to a small bowl and pour water. Mix together to make a thick batter, it should be of neither too thick nor runny. If you dip a spoon into the batter, it should coat the back of the spoon. (Check stepwise pictures in the post) To make the air fryer version, it needs to be more on the thicker side as this prevents the batter from dripping. 8. Pour this batter over the marinated paneer and give a gentle mix. If you feel the batter is too thick and does not spread, add a splash of water. It may need a few tablespoons at this stage. 9. Heat oil in a deep pot or pan for frying. If you want you may shallow fry in 3 tablespoons of oil. Test if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of batter to the hot oil, it should sizzle and come up without turning brown. 10. Regulate the flame to medium. Gently pick up each pieces of paneer, making sure it is well coated with the batter, gently drop it to hot oil. Make sure you put them away from each other so they don’t stick. 11. Do not crowd the pan by adding a lot of pieces. Do not disturb for 3 minutes, meaning do not stir. 12. Later gently turn them to the other side and fry until crisp and golden. Remove to a wired rack or steel colander. 13. Fry the paneer in batches until all of them are done. Turn off the heat and place the curry leaves (pat dry first) in the frying skimmer and gently down to the hot oil. They will fry and become crisp, remove to the plate. 14. Repeat frying by placing the slit and deseeded green chilies (pat dry first) in the skimmer and lower them to the hot oil. Fry them until blistered. 15. If you don’t want to temper, you may serve the paneer 65 fry as is. If you have made the spicy tempering, add your paneer 65 to it and toss until well coated. Serve it immediately. 16. Garnish paneer 65 with fried green chilies and curry leaves, Squeeze some lemon juice and serve alongside fresh cut onions. 17. Keep the tempering ready first. 18. Preheat your air fryer for 10 mins at 400 F or 200 C. Spray oil and begin to place each piece of paneer in the basket/tray, spacing them apart. For the air fry version, I have felt keeping the pieces slightly bigger and the batter a bit thicker works well. 19. Air fry for 6 to 7 mins. Turn them to the other side and continue to air fry for another 3 mins , until crisp and cooked through. Adjust the air frying time depending on the size of your fryer. Smaller fryer requires a bit lesser temperature and lesser cook time. 20. Toss the paneer 65 in the tempering and serve immediately.

title: Paneer 65 Recipe | Street Style Paneer 65 - Secret Tips
ingredients: 2 cubes paneer, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tbsp curd, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 3 tbsp corn flour, oil, 2 tbsp oil, pinch hing, 1 inch ginger, 5 cloves garlic, 2 chilli, 2 red chilli, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. Firstly, take 20 cubes of paneer in a bowl. Add 1 tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp garam masala, and ½ tsp salt. 2. Also add 3 tbsp curd, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 3 tbsp corn flour. Mix well. 3. Deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 4. Once the paneer turns golden brown and crunchy, drain off the paneer and keep it aside. 5. In a pan, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add a pinch of salt, 1 inch of ginger, 5 cloves of garlic, 2 chillies, and 2 dried red chillies. Saute well. 6. Also add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp salt, ½ tsp chaat masala and cook till the sauce turns glossy. 7. Now add a few curry leaves and fried paneer. 8. Mix well, making sure the sauce is coated well. 9. Finally, enjoy Paneer 65 Recipe hot as a starter.

title: Paneer Amritsari Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups paneer, 0.125 teaspoon carom seeds, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon amchur powder, 1 pinch black pepper crushed, 0.5 teaspoon Kasuri methi, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 to teaspoon salt, 1 Pinch turmeric, 3 tablespoons besan, 2 tablespoons Oil
instructions: 1. Add chilled paneer, red chilli powder, kasuri methi, ajwain, garam masala, salt, amchur powder, ginger garlic paste and besan to a bowl. 2. Sprinkle very little water and toss to coat the paneer well with this masala. 3. Heat oil in a pan. Add the paneer and begin to roast them well. 4. Paneer cubes may stick to each other, you will have to separate them gently while sauteing. Do not over cook. When the raw smell of the besan goes away turn off and move to a plate. 5. Garnish with coriander leaves and onion wedges. Skew them or put them on to picks. Serve warm or hot. Do not leave them in the pan else they may get cooked further & turn rubbery.

title: paneer bhurji gravy recipe - dhaba style | paneer ki bhurji gravy street style
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp butter, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 tbsp besan gram flour, 3 tomato, 0.5 capsicum, 1 cup water, 2 grams paneer cottage cheese, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp butter, and 1 tsp cumin. saute until the cumin turns aromatic. 2. now add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, and saute until onions turn golden brown. 3. keeping the flame on low, add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ¾ tsp salt, and 1 tbsp besan. 4. saute until the spices turn aromatic. adding besan gives nice thickness and body to the gravy. 5. further, add 3 tomatoes and saute well. 6. saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 7. also, add ½ capsicum and saute for a minute. 8. now add 1 cup water and mix adjusting the consistency as required. 9. further, add 200 grams of crumbled paneer and mix well. 10. cover and simmer for 2 minutes. do not overcook as the panner will turn chewy. 11. now add ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 12. finally, enjoy paneer bhurji gravy with pav or chapati.

title: Paneer Bhurji Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 medium onion, 1 green chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 medium tomato, 0.25 cup capsicum, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 2 tablespoon Coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Pour 4 cups milk to a pot and bring it to a boil. Add 1 tbsp vinegar or lemon juice and Stir. When the milk begins to curdle well, turn off the stove. If the milk does not curdle then add 1 tbsp more vinegar. 2. Drain it to a colander lined with a muslin cloth. Rinse it well under running water. Squeeze off the excess whey and make a knot with the cloth. 3. Hang the paneer for about 10 to 15 minutes to remove excess whey. Meanwhile chop the onions & tomatoes finely. 4. Pour oil to a hot pan and add cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add finely chopped onions and chopped green chili. 5. Saute until the onions turn golden to light brown. Add ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw smell has gone, for about a minute. 6. Add chopped tomato, salt and turmeric. Saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 7. Stir in the red chili powder, kasuri methi & garam masala. 8. Mix and saute until the raw smell goes off from the mixture & the masala leaves the sides of the pan. 9. Stir in peas or capsicum if using. Fry until capsicum turns slightly soft. Crumble paneer well and add to the pan. 10. Stir and fry till everything blends well just for 2 minutes. Taste it and add more salt and spices if required. 11. Turn off the stove. Add 2 tbsp chopped coriander leaves. Serve paneer bhurji with roti, paratha or bread. Sprinkle some lemon juice before serving.

title: paneer bhurji recipe | how to make dry paneer bhurji recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin seeds jeera, 1 medium sized onion, salt to taste, 1 green chilli, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste adrak lasun ka paste, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 1 medium sized tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp garam masala powder, 2 cup paneer cottage cheese, 1 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 2 tbsp coriander leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat oil and saute cumin seeds till they turn aromatic. 2. further add onions, green chilli and ginger-garlic paste. 3. saute well till the onions turn slightly golden brown. 4. now add tomatoes and saute till they turn soft and mushy. 5. additionally, add ½ tsp red chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp garam masala powder and salt to taste. 6. roast the spices on low flame for a minute. 7. furthermore add crumbled paneer / cottage cheese and mix gently. 8. mix gently without mashing out paneer. 9. cover and simmer for 3 minutes till the paneer gets cooked well. do not over cook paneer, as they turn hard and chewy. 10. also add crushed kasuri methi and coriander leaves. 11. mix gently, making sure everything is combined well. 12. finally, serve paneer bhurji hot with chapathi or paratha.

title: Paneer Bhurji Sandwich
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 2 tsps butter, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 tsp garlic, Salt, 1 Pinch turmeric, 0.625 tsp red chilli powder, 0.375 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp amchur, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 0.3333333333333333 cup paneer
instructions: 1. Smear softened butter or ghee over the bread slices and toast them until golden. If you are making 2 servings, Take a larger skillet and toast them all at one time or two. If you prefer to toast or grill the sandwiches then skip doing it on the tawa. 2. While the bread toasts, slice an onion and tomato. Keep aside all the toasted bread slices. 3. Add 1.5 tsp oil to the pan and saute chili, garlic and cumin. You can also use ginger in place of garlic. Saute until the garlic turns golden. 4. Add the crumbled paneer. Switch off the stove. 5. Add salt, turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala, amchur powder or lemon juice and chopped coriander leaves. 6. Mix everything well. Taste test and adjust salt and other spices to your taste. 7. Place this on the bread. 8. Then layer onions & tomatoes. You can also add very thin slices of yellow and red bell peppers. 9. You can place lettuce or coriander leaves. You can also add green chutney or chili sauce. 10. Cover with another slice of bread. Gently press down. You can also grill these now until golden if you have not toasted at step 1. Halve the paneer bhurji sandwich and serve.

title: Paneer Biryani Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 2 cups aged basmati rice, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons salt, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1.5 bay leaf, 2 star anise, 6 green cardamoms, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 2 black cardamoms, 1 strand mace, 325 grams paneer, 6 tablespoons ghee, 2 cup onions, 1.5 green chilli, 1.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri chilli powder, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons biryani masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.75 cup mint leaves, 0.75 cup coriander leaves, 1.25 cup yogurt, 3.5 tablespoons water, 2 cashews, 3.5 tablespoons raisins, 0.25 cup whole milk, 2.5 pinches saffron, 1 teaspoon kewra water
instructions: 1. I prefer to use 20 to 25 whole cashews (split open later) and 2 to 3 tablespoons sweet raisins for garnish. You may skip them if you want. 2. Heat ¼ cup milk in cup or sauce pan and add 2 to 3 large pinches of saffron strands. Optionally, also add ½ tbsp. of kewra or rose water to it and keep aside. Whisk 1¼ cup fresh yogurt (not sour) well with a whisk or fork and set aside. (To use Greek yogurt whisk 1 cup Greek yogurt with ¼ cup milk.) 3. Puree ½ cup tomatoes (sweet tomatoes) in a small grinder. Slice 2 cup onions thin and evenly so they fry evenly. I use 1 large onion, slice and then halve the slices so they fry faster. You will slice and then squeeze them gently to separate the layers completely, then measure. 4. Rinse & drain ¾ to 1 cup each of mint leaves and coriander leaves. Chop them later. Slice 1 green chili (optional, deseed if needed) & set aside 1½ tablespoons ginger garlic paste (or gratings). 5. You will need the following ground spices: 6. Rinse 2 cups aged basmati rice well and soak it in water for at least 30 minutes. You can soak up to 50 minutes but not more as the rice can easily break and turn mushy. 7. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee in a heavy bottom pan. When it is hot enough, place the paneer block/s (250 to 400 grams) gently and fry it on a medium high heat until golden on both the sides, turn it when one side is done. (If you put the paneer in cold ghee or cold pan, it will get stuck, so wait until hot) 8. Remove to a plate and cool down. Later dice to desired sizes. I dice each block to 10 portions. To garnish I dice to smaller pieces. 9. To the same pan, add cashews and fry until light golden. Stir in the raisins. If you want you may add a tsp of ghee while frying. 10. Let fry until they turn plump. Remove to a plate and keep aside. I prefer to wipe the pan with some kitchen tissues to prevent burnt bits of nuts or paneer. 11. Pour 4 tablespoons ghee and add the sliced onions. Begin to fry on a medium high heat. Keep stirring often & spread them in the pan. Continue to fry for a few minutes, then reduce the flame as needed to medium or low. 12. After about 8 to 9 minutes, onions begin to caramelize. At this stage turn down the heat to lowest so they don’t burn. If using dutch oven, turn off the heat. Saute until the onions caramelize and turn evenly deep golden. Be very attentive at this stage as they can burn in few seconds. Burnt onions taste bitter. It took me 12 minutes for this entire task, at varying heat levels. Turn the heat to low. Remove 2 tbsps of these fried onions to a plate and set aside. 13. Add the following spices 14. bay leaf 2 star anise 6 green cardamoms 2 inch cinnamon 2 Black cardamoms 1 large strand of mace / javitri 15. Quickly sauté 1½ ginger garlic paste & slit green chilli until slightly aromatic. Be cautious as this can burn in a flash. 16. Stir in salt, Biryani/ garam masala, Coriander powder, Cumin powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder & turmeric. 17. Pour the half cup tomato puree. 18. Cook until it turns thick & aromatic. 19. Make sure your yogurt is smooth and whisked well. To the yogurt bowl, stir in 2 tbsps of the prepared masala. 20. Turn the heat to low and add the spiced yogurt to the pot. 21. Stir well and cook on a low heat until the mixture turns thick and fats begin to separate. 22. Turn off the heat. Pour 3 to 4 tablespoons hot water and stir well. Taste test this and add more salt or garam masala if you want. Add ¾ of the mint leaves, ¾ of the coriander leaves you prepared and ¾ of the fried paneer (save some for kids or garnish if you want). Do not add all 400 grams here. You can add max 300 grams. 23. Cover and keep aside until the rice is prepared.

title: Paneer Biryani Recipe | Veg Paneer Dum Biriyani
ingredients: 1 cup curd, 2 tbsp biryani masala, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp ginger garlic, 2 tbsp mint, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 carrot, 5 beans, 5 florets cauliflower, 3 tbsp peas, water, 1 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp cloves, 1 tsp cumin, 2 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 1 javetri, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 chilli, 1.5 cup basmati rice, 1 tsp ghee, 2 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp raisins, 2 cubes paneer, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cloves, 1 tsp cumin, 2 bay leaf, 1 javetri, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 onion, 0.5 capsicum, handful coriander, handful mint, 2 tbsp fried onion, 1 cup saffron milk
instructions: 1. Firslty, in a bowl take 1 cup curd. Add 2 tbsp biryani masala, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp ginger garlic. 2. Also, add 2 tbsp mint, 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 3. Add in ½ carrot, 5 beans, 5 florets cauliflower and 3 tbsp peas. 4. Mix well, making sure everything is well combined. Marnate for 30 minutes. 5. To cook the rice, in a large vessel, take enough water, add spices and 3 chilli. Get the water to boil. 6. Now add 1½ cup basmati rice, 1 tsp ghee and 2 tsp lemon juice. 7. Boil for 5 minutes or until the rice is 80% cooked. 8. Drain off the rice and keep aside. 9. In a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Fry 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp raisins and 20 cubes paneer. 10. Fry till the paneer turns golden brown. Keep aside. 11. Add 2 tbsp oil and saute the spices till they turn aromatic. 12. Also, saute 1 onion till it turns golden brown. 13. Add in ½ capsicum and saute slightly. 14. Now add marinated vegetables and cook well. Cook till the vegetables are almost cooked. 15. Add a handful of coriander, a handful of mint, 2 tbsp of fried onion, and half of the paneer and cashews. 16. Now, layer with 80% cooked rice. 17. Also, top with coriander, mint, fried onion, and the remaining of the paneer and cashews. 18. Also, sprinkle biryani masala, and pour 1 cup of saffron milk. 19. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked well. 20. Finally, enjoy Paneer Biryani Recipe with raita.

title: paneer burger recipe | masala burger | tawa masala paneer burger
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp butter, 2 clove garlic, 1 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tomato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp pav bhaji masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 cup paneer cottage cheese, 2 tbsp coriander, 4 burger bun, 1 tsp butter, 0.25 tsp pav bhaji masala, 1 tsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil and add 1 tsp butter. 2. saute 2 clove garlic on medium flame. 3. now add 1 onion and saute well. do not brown the onions. 4. further, add ½ capsicum and saute slightly. 5. add 2 tomato and saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 6. keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp pav bhaji masala and ½ tsp salt. 7. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 8. now add 2 tbsp tomato sauce and mix well. make sure the mixture is of smooth consistency. 9. further, add 1 cup paneer and mix without breaking them. 10. add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well, paneer stuffing is ready. 11. to prepare the burger, slice the burger bun. 12. spread 2 tbsp of prepared paneer mixture on one side of the bun. 13. on the other half of burger bun spread 1 tsp green chutney. 14. close the bun and press gently. 15. now heat 1 tsp butter, ¼ tsp pav bhaji masala and 1 tsp coriander. mix on low flame. 16. now toast the burger bun both the sides. 17. finally, enjoy paneer burger with tomato sauce.

title: Paneer Butter Masala Recipe
ingredients: 1 tablespoon oil, 2 green cardamoms, 1 cup onions, 1 inch ginger, 5.5 garlic cloves, 2 cups tomatoes, 15 whole cashew nuts, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 1 teaspoon jaggery sugar, 0.875 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 cup cold water, 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, 1 bay leaf, 2 inch Ceylon cinnamon piece, 3 cloves 3 cloves ((laung, optional)), 275 grams paneer, 0.5 tablespoon kasuri methi, 3.5 tablespoons heavy cream, 1.5 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Pour oil to a pan and add cardamoms, onions, ginger and garlic. Saute for 4 to 5 mins, until the onions turn transparent. 2. Add tomatoes, salt and cashews. Mix well and cook covered until the tomatoes turn mushy and break down. (Takes me about 7 mins on a medium heat. If your pan is dry, add a splash of water). 3. Stir in the red chili powder, coriander powder and garam masala. Saute on a medium heat for 3 to 4 mins, until aromatic. Turn off and cool down. 4. Pour 1 cup cold water to a blender jar & transfer the onion tomato masala. Blend to a silky smooth puree. 5. On a low heat, melt butter in the same pan and add the optional whole spices if using – bay leaf, cinnamon and cloves. 6. When they begin to sizzle, stir in the optional red chili powder. Hold a strainer over the pan and pour the onion tomato masala. Stir with a spoon to strain. 7. Pour ½ cup more water & mix well. Cook covered on a medium heat until slightly thick, yet flowy. It will thicken upon cooling. (Takes about 10 to 12 mins, keep stirring every 3 to 4 mins). You will see traces of fat on the butter masala when it is ready. 8. Remove the whole spices to discard and taste test to adjust salt and garam masala. Stir in the jaggery, kasuri methi & paneer. Cook covered for 2 to 3 mins on a low heat and turn off. Stir in the cream (reserve a tablespoon to garnish). 9. Garnish paneer butter masala with cream and chopped coriander leaves. Best served with Basmati Rice, Jeera Rice, Butter Naan, Tandoori Roti or plain parathas.

title: paneer butter masala without onion and garlic | paneer jain recipes
ingredients: 3 to mato, 2 tbsp butter, 3 pods cardamom elachi, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 0.5 tsp jeera cumin, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 1 cup milk, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp sugar, salt to taste, 0.25 cup cashew paste, 9 cubes panner cottage cheese, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves
instructions: 1. firstly heat kadai with 2 tbsp butter. 2. saute in ½ ts jeera, 3 pods cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon and 1 bay leaf till they turn aromatic. 3. additionally add 1 inch ginger and 1 green chilli. saute well. 4. further add tomato puree and cook till oil releases from sides. 5. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. 6. saute on low flame for a minute or till spices get cooked well. 7. keeping the flame on low add ¼ cup cashew paste and 1 cup of milk stirring continuously. 8. additionally add 9 cubes panner. 9. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or till panner gets cooked well completely. 10. further add ¼ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp crushed kasuri methi. mix well. 11. finally, serve paneer butter masala with tandoori roti or aloo kulcha.

title: paneer capsicum recipe | paneer capsicum curry masala | paneer gravy
ingredients: 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, 1 tbsp butter, 15 cube paneer cottage cheese, 1 cube capsicum, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 4 cloves lavang, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 pods cardamom elachi, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 onion, 2 tbsp mint pudina, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 4 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.75 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 tomato, 2 tbsp curd yogurt, 2 tbsp cashew paste, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp cream, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. firstly in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp butter saute spices. 2. furthermore, add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 tbsp mint, 2 tbsp coriander leaves and saute well. 3. transfer the spice mix along with ¼ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder and ¾ tsp salt. 4. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. now add 1 tomato and saute till they turn soft and mushy. 6. furthermore, add 2 tbsp curd and 2 tbsp cashew paste. 7. mix till the oil is released from masala paste. 8. add 1 cup water and adjust consistency. 9. now add fried paneer and capsicum, mix gently. 10. add cream, cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 11. finally, serve paneer capsicum curry with roti / naan.

title: Paneer cheese toast recipe | Paneer cheese sandwich recipe video
ingredients: 5.5 bread slices, 1.25 to 1 cups Paneer, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 medium onion, 1 green chili, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 medium tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.625 tsp red chili powder, 0.625 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi, Salt, 1 tbsp Butter, 18 grams mozzarella cheese, Red chili flakes, 1 Handful coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add jeera to hot oil. 2. Fry the onions until golden. 3. Add the ginger garlic paste and fry till the raw smell goes away. 4. Add tomatoes, turmeric and salt. 5. Fry the tomatoes until mushy. 6. Next add in the chilli powder and garam masala. 7. Saute till the raw smell of the chili powder goes away. 8. Add paneer. I made fresh paneer the previous night. Mix up the paneer with the masala. 9. Add kasuri methi and coriander leaves. 10. Regulate the flame to low. Place the bread with butter on the pan. Spread paneer over it. 11. Sprinkle cheese, red chili flakes and any herbs or coriander leaves. 12. Cover with a lid and cook on a low flame until the cheese melts. If you do it on a high flame the bread can burn so keep it at lowest. You can also put this in the oven for 3 to 4 mins. Serve paneer cheese toast immediately. 13. Heat a pan with oil and add cumin. 14. When they begin to crackle, add onions and chilies. 15. Saute well until the onions turn golden. 16. Add ginger garlic paste and saute till the raw smell goes away. 17. Add tomatoes and sprinkle salt, turmeric. 18. Cook until the tomatoes turn mushy. You can also cover and cook at this stage to speed up the process. 19. Add garam masala and red chili powder. Mix well until well combined. 20. Add panir. Mix to spread the masala very well. 21. Add coriander leaves and kasuri methi. Saute for a minute and switch off. 22. Add very hot paneer bhurji over the bread and then the grated cheese over it. 23. Cover with a lid and toast on a low to medium heat until the cheese melts. Keep checking in between to prevent burning. 24. Just microwave it on high heat for 1 minute. 25. Put the bread along with cheese topped in a 170 C preheated oven. Grill for 8 to 10 mins or until golden. 26. Serve paneer cheese toast immediately.

title: Paneer Curry Recipe (Dhaba Style)
ingredients: 225 grams Paneer, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon crushed kasuri methi, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 teaspoon ghee, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 black cardamom, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 2 red chilies, 1 cup onions, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1.25 cup tomatoes, 1.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 1.5 tablespoon roasted besan, 3 tablespoons curd, 1.5 cups water, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Mix together salt & spices (mentioned under marinade) in a bowl. Place the paneer pieces and coat them gently with the spices. If it is too dry, add a splash of water. Keep aside until needed later. 2. Fine chop onions or mince in a FP. Begin to cook while you prepare the rest of the ingredients. 3. Heat oil in a pan and add the whole spices - bay leaf, black cardamom, green cardamoms, dried red chillies, cumin, cinnamon & cloves. 4. Once they begin to sizzle, add the onions and stir fry on a medium high heat for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring often. 5. Later reduce the heat and cook on a medium to low heat until the onions begin to caramelize & turn light brown but not burnt. 6. Reduce the heat to low and stir in the ginger garlic. Saute for a minute until the raw flavor is gone. 7. Stir in the dry spices, roasted besan and salt. Stir well and fry for a minute or 2 until the mixture turns aromatic. If you want you may add ghee at this stage. 8. Add the chopped or grated tomatoes and mix well. Cook until the onion tomato masala blends well and turns thick. 9. Take 1 tablespoon of this onion masala and mix it well into the whisked yogurt or cultured cream. Pour this into the pan and cook until you see traces of oil being separated from the masala. 10. Pour 1½ cups water to the same pan and bring it to a boil on a high heat. Reduce the heat and cook covered until you see traces of oil on top of the curry. Taste test and adjust salt & garam masala if you want. 11. While that cooks, in another non-stick pan heat a teaspoon ghee. Place the paneer pieces in the hot pan and coat them with ghee. Fry on a medium heat to bring out the flavors of the spices. Take a spoon and stir them. Do not over fry, it just takes a few minutes. 12. Add this paneer along with the ghee to the cooked curry. Make sure your curry isn't too thick. It has to be slightly flowy so the extra moisture liquid goes into the paneer keeping it soft. 13. Give a gentle stir and cook covered on a low heat for 2 mins. Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve with plain rice, roti, paratha, naan or with jeera rice.

title: Paneer Cutlet (Paneer Tikki Snack)
ingredients: 1 cup paneer, 0.75 cup potato, 2.5 green chilies, 0.25 cup bread crumbs, 1 teaspoon ginger grated, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 medium onion, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Boil veggies till soft cooked without making mushy. Mash them. If using sweet corn, boil them till al dente. Cool and pulse them in a blender/ grinder. 2. Add them to a mixing bowl. Also add paneer, bread crumbs, salt, cumin, ginger, turmeric, garam masala, coriander leaves and chilies. 3. Mix them well to make a dough. If the mixture is too soft, you can add more bread crumbs. 4. Take portions of this dough to your hand and shape them to patties. The patties should not be too thin or thick. 5. Grease a pan generously with oil and heat it. When the pan is hot enough, place these on the pan & fry until golden. Turn them to the other side, fry them until golden and crisp. 6. For baking in oven, preheat the oven to 220 C or 430 F for 15 mins. Prepare a baking tray, grease it well. 7. Make small thin patties (appr ¼ inch thickness), and smear little oil or spray oil on them. 8. Bake for 10 to 15 mins, turn them to the other side carefully. Repeat baking paneer cutlet for another 10 to 12 mins. 9. Serve paneer cutlets with Coriander chutney or ketchup.

title: Paneer Cutlet Recipe | Paneer Tikki - Party Snack
ingredients: 1.5 cup paneer, 2 potato, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp carrot, 0.5 onion, 1 inch gigner, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp black salt, 0.5 tsp dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp corn flour, oil, breadcrumbs, 0.5 cup corn flour, 0.5 cup maida, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, water
instructions: 1. Firstly, take 1½ cups grated paneer, 2 boiled & grated potatoes. 2. Add 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp carrot, ½ onion, 1 inch ginger, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp black salt, ½ tsp dry mango powder and ½ tsp salt. 3. Mix well, ensuring all the spices are thoroughly combined. 4. Add 2 tbsp corn flour and mix well, making sure the tikki holds its shape. 5. To prepare the slurry, add ½ cup maida, ½ cup corn flour, ½ tsp salt, ½ tsp chilli powder, and water. 6. Mix well, making sure the slurry is smooth and lump-free. 7. Shape the cutlet, dip it into the slurry, and coat it with breadcrumbs. 8. Deep fry the cutlet in hot oil, stirring occasionally. 9. Fry until the cutlet turns golden brown and crispy. 10. Drain off the cutlet to remove excess oil. 11. Finally, enjoy the Crispy Paneer Cutlet Recipe with sauce.

title: Paneer Do Pyaza Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams Paneer, 0.75 cup onions, 0.3333333333333333 cup tomatoes, 0.3333333333333333 cup capsicum, 7.5 cashews, 2.5 tbsps oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, 2 green cardamoms, 1.25 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp red chilli powder, 0.75 tsp garam masala, 0.75 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp Salt, 0.75 cup water, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi, 1.5 tbsp oil, 12 shallots, 2 green chilies
instructions: 1. Make a fine puree of capsicum, tomatoes and cashews. Set this aside. 2. Add oil to a pan and heat it. 3. Add jeera and cardamoms. 4. When they splutter, add chopped onions. 5. Fry until they turn golden. 6. Add ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell of ginger garlic goes away. 7. Pour the tomato puree and saute on a low to medium flame. 8. Within minutes the mixture begins to leave the pan. 9. Add salt, red chili powder, garam masala and coriander powder. 10. Saute till oil separates. After frying the mixture for few minutes, oil begins to ooze out. 11. Pour water and mix well. 12. Cook covered until the gravy thickens. Traces of oil would be seen on the gravy. 13. Lower the flame completely. Add paneer and sprinkle crushed kasuri methi. 14. Cook covered just for 2 to 3 mins. Turn off the stove. Do not overcook the paneer. 15. Meanwhile add 1 to 1.5 tbsp oil or ghee to a hot pan. Fry onions and green chiles on a high flame just for 2 to 3 mins. The onions must be partially cooked but must retain the crunch. 16. Pour this over the paneer gravy and sprinkle some corainder leaves. You can set aside few onions for garnish. 17. Paneer do pyaza is ready to serve.

title: Paneer Do Pyaza Recipe - Dhaba Style | Bhuna Paneer 2 Pyaza Gravy
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp butter, 12 cubes paneer, 0.5 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp butter, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 3 pods cardamom, 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp salt, 2 tomato, 0.5 cup curd, 2 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp butter. 2. add 12 cubes paneer, ½ onion, ¼ tsp turmeric and ½ tsp chilli powder. 3. roast on low flame until the paneer and onions are roasted slightly. keep aside. 4. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp butter. add 1 tsp cumin, ½ inch cinnamon and 3 pods cardamom. 5. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 6. now add 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste and saute until the onions turn golden brown. 7. keep the flame on low add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and 1 tsp salt. 8. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 9. further, add 2 tomato and saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 10. add ½ cup curd and mix continuously, until the oil starts to release from the sides. 11. now add 2 cup of water and mix well adjusting the consistency. 12. add in roasted paneer and onion, and mix gently. 13. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 14. also add 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp kasuri methi and ¼ tsp garam masala. mix well. 15. finally, enjoy paneer do pyaza with rice or roti.

title: Paneer Dosa (Street Style)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onion, 1 green chili, 1 heaped teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 cup tomato, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 225 grams paneer, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2.25 cups dosa batter, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons softened unsalted butter ghee oil
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan and add onions & green chili. Saute until golden for 6 to 7 mins and add ginger garlic. Saute for a minute and add chili powder, garam masala and salt. 2. Add the tomatoes & cook until they lose the raw smell, for 4 to 5 mins. 3. Stir in ¼ cup water and cook until the masala is thick & aromatic. Mix in the diced paneer. Cook for 2 mins, until heated through. Taste test and adjust salt to taste. 4. Mix in kasuri methi & turn off. Cool down slightly and sprinkle coriander leaves. 5. Bring your dosa batter to a thick yet of pouring and spreading consistency. (I usually add little water and salt at this stage) 6. Heat a dosa pan on a medium flame until really hot. 7. Pour a ladle full of batter in the center and spread it from the center to the edges of the pan, making concentric circles. 8. Add diced butter or sprinkle ghee across the dosa. Cook on a medium high heat until golden, crisp and the edges begin to come off the pan. 9. Scoop out ¼ portion of the prepared paneer to one half of the dosa. Cover it half way and remove to a serving plate. 10. Serve paneer dosa hot. You don't need any sides but coconut chutney or any other will go well. 11. Make the next dosa when the pan is hot enough, but not smoking hot. If it is too hot, splash little water to cool down slightly.

title: Paneer Frankie Recipe | Paneer Kathi Roll - Street Style
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp ghee, warm water, oil, 0.5 cup thick curd, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tsp kasuri methi, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 1 cup paneer, 2 tbsp oil, 1 inch ginger, 5 cloves garlic, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 tomato, green chutney, onion, cabbage, tomato sauce, chaat masala
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large plate, take 2 cups maida, ½ tsp salt and 1 tsp ghee. Mix well. 2. Add warm water and knead until you have a smooth dough. 3. Grease with oil, cover and rest for 20 minutes. 4. To prepare paneer stuffing, take ½ cup thick curd, 1 tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 2 tsp kasuri methi, ½ tsp salt and 2 tsp oil. 5. Mix well, ensuring all the spices are thoroughly combined. 6. Add 1 cup paneer, and mix well. Marinate for 20 minutes. 7. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a pan and add 1 inch of ginger and 5 cloves of garlic. Saute well. 8. Also, add ½ onion, ½ capsicum, ½ tomato and stir fry. 9. Now add the marinated paneer and cook until well done. 10. Cook till the oil separates, and the paneer stuffing is ready. 11. To prepare the wrap, once the dough has rested well. Kead again. 12. Pinch a ball-sized piece of dough and roll it out thin. 13. Spread oil and maida. 14. Now fold a zig-zag to create layers. Also, roll the spiral and tuck it inside. 15. Dust with maida and roll gently, slightly thick. 16. Cook in a hot pan by spreading a little oil. 17. Roast on both sides till they turn golden and slightly crunchy. 18. To prepare the wrap, spread green chutney on the roti. 19. Also, place 2 tbsp of prepared paneer stuffing. 20. Top with onion, cabbage, tomato sauce, chaat masala and coriander. 21. Finally, roll the Paneer Kathi Roll tightly and wrap it with baking paper.

title: Paneer fried rice
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.875 cup paneer, 2.5 tablespoons Thick curd, 0.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.375 cup spring onions, 0.5 cup carrot, 0.375 cup bell peppers, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper, 2 cloves garlic, 1 green chilli, 1.25 tablespoon soya sauce
instructions: 1. If you have precooked rice, you may skip this step. You will need about 2 to 2.5 cups cooked rice for this recipe. Soak 1 cup rice for 15 to 30 mins and cook al dente in lots of water. Do not overcook and make the rice mushy. Drain off the water and set this rice aside to cool. If you are a beginner, you can check my fried rice recipe post to learn about cooking perfect rice for fried rice. 2. To a bowl, add 3. ¼ to ½ teaspoon garam masala. Mix all of the ingredients. 4. Add ¾ to 1 cup cold paneer cubes. Mix well. and set aside for at least 15 mins 5. Meanwhile chop and prepare 6. Heat large pan with 1 tablespoon oil. Add marinated paneer and cook on medium high till the marinade dries up and clings on to the paneer. Once done remove to a plate. 7. Pour another tablespoon oil to the same pan. Add 1 tablespoon chopped garlic and 1 chopped green chilli. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds. Then add spring onion whites, carrots and bell peppers. Fry on a high heat until they are half done yet crunchy. 8. Add fried paneer and green onions, toss everything well. 9. Add cooled rice, sprinkle a pinch of salt and ¼ teaspoon crushed black pepper. Pour 1 tablespoon soya sauce around the sides in the hot pan. You will see the soya sauce sizzles and the entire paneer fried rice turns aromatic. You can also add any hot sauce at this stage if you want. Soya sauce also has salt so add salt with caution. 10. Mix and fry on high heat for 2 mins. Taste test and add more sauce or salt as required. Serve paneer fried rice hot.

title: Paneer Fried Rice Recipe with Secret Sauce - Street Style
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 2 cube paneer, 2 chilli, 2 cloves garlic, 0.5 onion, 5 beans, 1 carrot, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tsp schezwan sauce, 2 tsp vinegar, 2 tsp soy sauce, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 1 tsp salt, 3 tbsp cabbage, 4 cups cooked rice, 2 tbsp spring onion
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large wok heat 2 tbsp oil and fry 20 cubes paneer. 2. Fry until the paneer turns golden brown, keep aside. 3. In the same oil, add 2 chilli, 2 cloves garlic and fry on high flame. 4. Now add ½ onion, 5 beans, 1 carrot, ½ capsicum and fry on high flame. 5. Fry until the vegetables turn crunchy. 6. Keeping the flame on high, add 2 tsp chilli sauce, 2 tsp schezwan sauce, 2 tsp vinegar, 2 tsp soy sauce, ½ tsp pepper powder and 1 tsp salt. 7. Stir fry on high flame until the sauces are well combined. 8. Add in fried paneer, 3 tbsp cabbage and mix well until the sauce is coated well. 9. Now add 4 cups of cooked rice and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 10. Finally, add 2 tbsp spring onion and enjoy Paneer Fried Rice.

title: Paneer Garlic Recipe | Pepper Garlic Paneer Fry - Quick Starter
ingredients: 2 pieces paneer, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp corn flour, 2 tbsp oil, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 3 chilli, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tsp vinegar, 2 tsp soy sauce, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup water, 3 tbsp corn flour slurry, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp spring onion
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a plate take 20 pieces paneer, ½ tsp pepper powder, ½ tsp salt and 2 tbsp corn flour. 2. Mix well, ensuring the flour is evenly coated on the paneer. 3. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan and fry the paneer. 4. Fry till the paneer turns golden and crispy. Keep aside. 5. In the same oil, add 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger and 3 chilli. Stir fry. 6. Also add ½ onion and 2 tbsp of spring onion, and sauté well. 7. Now add ½ onion and ½ capsicum, and stir-fry until the vegetables turn crunchy. 8. Add an additional 2 tablespoons of tomato sauce, 2 tablespoons of chilli sauce, 2 teaspoons of vinegar, 2 teaspoons of soy sauce, ½ teaspoon of pepper, and ½ teaspoon of salt. 9. Cook until the sauces are well combined. 10. Add ¼ cup of water and bring to a boil. 11. Add in fried paneer, few curry leaves and 2 tbsp spring onion. Mix until the sauces are well-coated. 12. Finally, enjoy Garilc Pepper Paneer Recipe as a starter.

title: paneer ghee roast recipe | veg ghee roast recipe | how to make paneer roast
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 5 red chilli, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.25 tsp methi fenugreek, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, 0.5 tsp pepper, 4 clove garlic, 2 tbsp tamarind extract, 0.25 cup water, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 15 cubes paneer cottage cheese, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 0.5 onion, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp curd yogurt, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp jaggery
instructions: 1. firstly, to prepare masala paste heat 1 tsp ghee and roast 5 dried red chill, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin, ¼ tsp methi, ½ tsp fennel and ½ tsp pepper. 2. roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 3. further, add 4 clove garlic and saute slightly without burning. 4. transfer the spices into a blender and allow to cool completely. 5. also add 2 tbsp tamarind extract and ¼ cup water. 6. blend to smooth paste. masala paste is ready, keep aside. 7. in another pan heat 1 tsp ghee and roast 15 cubes paneer. 8. roast on low to medium flame or until it turns golden brown. keep aside. 9. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee and add ½ onion and few curry leaves. 10. saute until onions shrink slightly. 11. further add ¼ tsp turmeric and saute well. 12. now add in the prepared masala paste and cook well. 13. saute and cook well for at least 10-15 minutes or until ghee separates. 14. add in 2 tbsp curd and saute well until well combined. 15. furthermore, add ½ tsp salt and ½ tsp jaggery. mix well. 16. now add in roasted paneer and cook for a minute. 17. finally, garnish with few curry leaves and enjoy paneer ghee roast with roti or dosa.

title: paneer ghotala recipe | Surat Veg Ghotala | Paneer cheese Ghotala
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 2 tsp oil, 2 chilli, 2 clove garlic, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 to mato, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 1 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, grate 200 grams paneer and keep aside. 2. in a large pan heat 2 tsp oil, 2 chilli, 2 clove garlic, and 2 tbsp coriander. 3. add ½ onion and saute until the onions shrink slightly. 4. keeping the flame on low add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, and 1 tsp kasuri methi. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. now add 2 tomato and saute well. 6. mash smooth making sure everything is well combined. 7. further add grated paneer, ½ tsp salt, and mix well. 8. add 1 cup water and mix well adjusting consistency as required. 9. now add 1 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp coriander, and simmer for 2 minutes. 10. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 11. finally, enjoy paneer ghotala with buttery pav.

title: Paneer Hyderabadi Masala | Hyderabadi Green Paneer Curry
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 25 cubes paneer, 2 onion, 1 inch ginger, 8 cloves garlic, 7 green chilli, 2 tbsp cashew, 1 bowl palak spinach, handful mint, handful coriander, 1 cup curd, 1 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 cup oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp cream, 2 tbsp kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan and add cubed paneer. 2. Roast the paneer on medium flame until it turns light golden brown. Keep stirring so the paneer roasts evenly. 3. Transfer the roasted paneer into warm water and soak for a few minutes. This keeps the paneer soft and removes excess oil. 4. In the same pan, add onion and saute until it softens. 5. Add ginger, garlic, green chilli and cashew. 6. Saute until the onion turns golden brown. Cashew helps make the gravy thick and creamy. 7. Add palak and saute until it shrinks. 8. Cool the mixture completely. Transfer it to a mixer jar. 9. Add mint and coriander. This gives the special Hyderabadi flavour. 10. Blend to a smooth green paste, adding water if required. 11. In a bowl, take curd and add masala powder. 12. Mix well and make a smooth lump free curd mixture. This helps prevent the curd from splitting. Keep aside. 13. Heat oil in a kadai and saute cumin until aromatic. 14. Add the prepared green masala paste. 15. Cook well until the raw flavour disappears and oil starts separating. 16. Add the curd mixture and stir continuously. 17. Cover and cook until the oil separates from the sides. 18. Add salt, cream and the roasted paneer. Drain the water from the paneer before adding it to the gravy. 19. Cover and simmer for 2 minutes so the paneer absorbs the flavour. 20. Add kasuri methi and garnish with cream. 21. Mix gently and serve Paneer Hyderabadi hot with roti, naan or rice.

title: Paneer Jalfrezi Recipe (Restaurant Style)
