ingredients: 275 grams paneer, 1 medium onion, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 1 cup bell peppers capsicum, 1 green chili slit, 2 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 0.875 tablespoon vinegar, 1 teaspoon sugar jaggery, 1 teaspoon Kasuri methi, 2.5 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Cut paneer to square or rectangles of preferred sizes. I cut mine to ¾ * 2 inches long pieces. If using fresh tomatoes, chop and puree them in a blender. 2. On a medium flame, heat oil or ghee in a pan. Add cumin seeds and chilies. 3. Fry them for a minute and add the chopped/sliced onions. Saute until light golden, for 5 mins. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for 1 to 2 mins. 4. Turn down the heat to low, stir in the chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder (optional), turmeric and salt. Pour the tomato puree immediately. 5. Cook on a medium high heat until it becomes slightly thick, for 5 to 7 mins. (If using store bought puree it takes only 2 to 3 mins) 6. Add the bell peppers & diced onions. Stir fry on a medium high heat, for 4 to 5 mins until slightly tender, yet crunchy. If your pan dries out, add 1 to 2 tablespoons of water. 7. Taste test and stir in more salt, vinegar/ tomato paste (only if needed) and sugar if using. 8. Reduce the heat and add paneer. Crush kasuri methi in your hands and sprinkle. Gently stir in to coat the paneer with the jalfrezi. 9. Cover and cook on a very low heat just for 2 to 3 mins, until heated through. Sprinkle coriander leaves and serve paneer jalfrezi with rice or roti. It also goes well to make kathi rolls and sandwiches.

title: paneer jalfrezi recipe | how to make restaurant style paneer jalfrezi
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 1 to mato, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 2 tsp oil, 1 red chilli, inch ginger, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds jeera, 0.5 onion, 1 tbsp tomato sauce, 0.5 capsicum, salt to taste, 8 pieces paneer cottage cheese, 0.5 to mato, 0.25 tsp garam masala powder, 2 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. firstly, heat oil and saute onions. 2. further add ginger garlic paste and saute well. 3. additionally add tomato and saute till they turn soft and mushy. 4. furthermore add spice powder and saute. 5. blend to smooth paste without adding any water. keep aside. 6. now in the same kadai heat oil. 7. add in dried red chilli, ginger julienne and cumin seeds. saute well. 8. furthermore, add thinly sliced onion and saute for a minute. 9. additionally, add sliced capsicum and continue to saute. 10. add in prepare masala paste, tomato sauce and salt to taste. mix well. 11. now add in paneer pieces and thinly sliced tomato. give a gentle mix. 12. now add garam masala powder and lemon juice. mix gently. 13. finally, serve paneer jalfrezi with rice, roti or naan.

title: Paneer Kathi Roll Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams paneer, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons Greek Yogurt, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 cup onions, 1 cup bell peppers, 0.5 cup carrot, 4 or 6 medium chapati roti paratha, 2 tablespoons cilantro chutney, 0.25 cup onions, 2 green chilies, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, stir together all the ingredients mentioned for marinade except paneer. Mix well and taste test. Adjust salt and spice levels. 2. Add the paneer to the marinade. Mix well and rest until you prepare the rest. You can marinate this up to 24 hours in the refrigerator. 3. When you are ready to make the Paneer Kathi Rolls, slice onions, chilies, carrots and bell peppers thinly. 4. Add the sliced onions, and green chilies (called to assemble) to a small bowl. Sprinkle little salt and squeeze the lemon juice. Cover and rest aside until we need them to assemble later. 5. Cook the parathas following the instructions on your pack or make them from my post. Stack them on a rack or on a plate lined with tissue. This keeps them soft. 6. Heat oil in a skillet and add the onions, carrots and bell peppers. Sauté them on a high heat until they lose the raw flavor, for 3 to 4 mins. Remove them to a plate. 7. Add the marinated paneer to the same hot pan and spread them in a single layer. Let cook until the marinade no more tastes raw, for about 4 to 5 mins, stirring half way. 8. Add the bell pepper onion mixture and mix well with the paneer. Taste test and add a sprinkle of salt, garam masala and red chili powder for more heat if you want. 9. Mix well and turn off. Cool down slightly. 10. Place the paratha on a tray/plate. Spread the cilantro chutney. If you are making this ahead to serve later, drizzle the green chutney later, at the end, before rolling. 11. Add paneer in the center, followed by some sliced onions, green chilies. 12. Bring the sides to the center and wrap them in a parchment paper. If you don’t have a paper, simply spread the chutney on the edges and assemble the kathi roll.

title: paneer kheer recipe | paneer payasam | paneer dessert recipes
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 5 cashew kaju, 1 tbsp raisins kishmish, 3 cups milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.5 cup paneer cottage cheese, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder elachi powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 tsp ghee and roast 5 cashew, 1 tbsp raisins to golden brown. 2. add in 3 cups milk and stir well. 3. get the milk to boil keeping the flame on medium stirring occasionally. 4. simmer for 5 minutes or till the milk thickens slightly. 5. further add ¼ cup sugar and ½ cup crumbled paneer. mix well. 6. boil for 3 minutes or till the paneer gets cooked well completely. 7. add in ¼ tsp cardamom powder and stir well. 8. finally, serve paneer kheer hot or cold garnished with few dry fruits.

title: Paneer Ki Sabji | Dhaba Style Instant Paneer Curry
ingredients: 3 tbsp oil, 15 cubes paneer cottage cheese, 0.5 capsicum, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 2 tbsp gigner garlic, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp salt, 3 tomato, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp cream, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a wok, heat 3 tbsp oil. Fry 15 cubes paneer, ½ capsicum till they turn golden brown. 2. Once the paneer turns golden brown, keep it aside. 3. In the same oil, add 1 tsp cumin and saute it until it crackles. 4. Add 1 onion, 2 tbsp ginger & garlic, and sauté until the onions turn golden brown. 5. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder and 1 tsp salt. 6. Sauté on low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 7. Now add 3 tomatoes and sauté until the tomatoes soften and the oil separates. 8. Add 1 cup of hot water and cook, adjusting the consistency as required. 9. Also, add fried paneer, capsicum and 2 tbsp cream. 10. Mix well and cook for 5 minutes. 11. Further add ½ tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, and 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 12. Finally, enjoy the Paneer Sabji Recipe, which tastes great with roti or rice.

title: paneer kofta recipe | paneer kofta curry | stuffed paneer kofta masala
ingredients: 1 cup paneer cottage cheese, 1 cup potato, 0.25 cup besan gram flour, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 5 cashew kaju, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 3 tbsp corn flour, 0.25 tsp salt, oil for deep frying, 3 tsp oil, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom elachi, 1 tsp jeera cumin, 1 onion, 2 cup tomato pulp, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 0.25 cup cashew paste, 1 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. firstly, mix all the ingredients to make small round kofta. 2. roll them in corn flour for extra crisp out covering. 3. deep fry in hot oil or bake in preheated oven at 180 degree celsius for 20 minutes. 4. stir occasionally, and fry on medium flame. 5. fry till the kofta turn golden and crisp. 6. drain off the paneer koftas and and in tomato onion based curry. 7. cover and simmer for 8 minutes or till the kofta absorbs curry. 8. additionally, add ¼ tsp garam masala and 1 tsp crushed kasuri methi. stir well. 9. finally, serve paneer kofta curry with jeera rice , roti or naan.

title: paneer kolhapuri recipe | how to make spicy paneer kolhapuri gravy
ingredients: 1 tsp sesame seeds til, 1 tsp poppy seeds khus khus, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 3 cloves lavang, 1 inch cinnamon dalchini, 2 cardamom elachi, 0.25 tsp balck pepper kali mirch, 1 star anise chakr phool, 3 kashmiri red chilli, 2 tbsp coconut, 3 tbsp oil, 9 cubes paneer cottage cheese, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 1 onion, 3 to mato, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 cup water, 1 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan dry roast all the spices listed for masala on low flame for 2 minutes. 2. blend to fine powder and kolhapuri masala is ready. keep aside. 3. heat 3 tbsp oil and fry 9 cubes paneer. keep aside. 4. in the same oil add in 1 bay leaf, onion, ginger garlic paste and saute well. 5. furthermore add 3 tomato and cook well. 6. mash the tomatoes well with the help of potato masher. 7. add in prepared kolhapuri masala, ¼ tsp turmeric and 1 tsp salt. 8. saute on low flame till oil separates from masala. 9. now add 1 cup water and mix well adjusting consistency. 10. add in fried paneer. 11. cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 12. now add 1 tsp crushed kasuri methi and mix well. 13. finally serve paneer kolhapuri masala with tandoori roti or garlic naan.

title: Paneer Korma | Paneer Kurma
ingredients: 275 grams paneer, 1.25 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.125 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 mint leaves, 1 bay leaf, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 star anise, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 2.5 cloves 2 to 3 cloves, 1.25 cup water, 12 cashew nuts, 0.25 cup coconut, 7 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Skip this step if you want to use coconut milk in the recipe. Add cashews or poppy seeds to a grinder jar and make a fine powder first. Add fresh coconut and repeat grinding. Pour 6 to 8 tbsps water to make a smooth paste. If required add a little more water. 2. Heat a pan with oil on a medium heat. Once the oil is slightly hot, add whole spices - bay leaf, star anise, cumin, cloves, cinnamon and cardamoms. 3. When the spices begin to sizzle, add ginger garlic paste and fry till it turns slightly aromatic for about 60 seconds. Do not over fry, it can make the gravy bitter. 4. Add onions, sprinkle salt and fry till they turn transparent or golden. 5. Add tomatoes , salt and turmeric. Fry till tomatoes turn mushy. If required splash a bit of water and cook covered until the tomatoes turn mushy. 6. Next add red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder and mint leaves or coriander leaves. Carefully saute for two to three minutes without burning. 7. Add the smooth ground paste. Fry for three to four minutes on a medium flame stirring occasionally. It must begin to release a nice aroma of coconut and poppy seeds. [If using coconut milk stir it together with ¾ cup water] 8. Pour water (or diluted coconut milk) and mix well. Bring this to a boil and reduce the heat, cook till the gravy thickens & you see specks of oil floating on the korma. Keep stirring occasionally. 9. Once the curry reaches a desired thick consistency, add paneer and gently stir. 10. Turn off the heat. Let paneer korma rest for a while so it absorbs the flavors. Serve with rice, roti or naan. 11. Perform all the steps following the instant pot saute mode, except for adding the coconut paste or coconut milk. Reserve this for adding later. Also you will use only 1 cup water for cooking the gravy. 12. Pressure cook for 5 mins and wait for 2 mins. Release the pressure manually. 13. Stir in the ½ to ¾ coconut milk or the coconut paste. If you think your korma is too thick, pour little boiling water. Taste test and add more garam masala and salt if required. 14. Let cook on saute mode until aromatic and thick for 3 mins. 15. Turn off the IP and add paneer. Cover paneer korma with a lose lid and serve after 10 mins. Garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Paneer Lababdar Recipe (Instant Pot and Stovetop)
ingredients: 2 cups tomatoes, 0.5 cup water, 15 cashews, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 4 cardamoms, 4 red chilies, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.875 cup water, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, 1 green chilli, 0.625 teaspoon kasuri methi, 2 grams paneer, 2.5 tablespoon cream, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 3.5 tablespoon paneer
instructions: 1. Add tomatoes, dried red chilies, cashews, cinnamon stick, cloves and cardamoms to a pot or pan. 2. Pour water and cook covered until tomatoes turn soft and pulpy. 3. Cool this and add to a blender. Blend well till you get a smooth puree. The puree has to be smooth. If needed you may add little water and blend. 4. Heat oil in a pan. Then add add bay leaf and saute for a min. 5. Add finely chopped onions and saute until they turn completely and uniformly golden. 6. Next add ginger garlic paste and saute till the raw smell goes away. This takes about 1 to 2 mins. 7. Pour the tomato and spice puree to the pan. 8. Add red chili powder, salt, coriander powder and garam masala. 9. Mix and saute for about 2 to 3 mins. Cook covered for another 2 to 3 mins until the gravy thickens slightly. 10. Pour water and add sugar. Mix well. Cook covered on a medium heat until the gravy thickens and you see traces of oil over the gravy. 11. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Take kasuri methi to your palm, crush it and add it here. Also add grated paneer, cubed paneer and green chili. 12. Mix everything well and lower the flame completely. Cook just for 1 to 2 mins. Turn off the stove. 13. Pour cream and mix. Add coriander leaves and mix. 14. Keep the pan covered for 5 mins so paneer absorbs the flavors. Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with coriander leaves. You can also garnish with more grated paneer & cream. 15. Serve paneer lababdar hot with roti, garlic naan or rice.

title: Paneer Lababdar Recipe | Restaurant Style Creamy Paneer Curry
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp pepper, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 onion, 2 tomato, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp melon seeds, 1 tsp salt, 2 dride red chilli, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 2 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 2 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 4 tbsp panerr, 2 tbsp coriadner, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp cream, 1 tbsp butter, 18 cubes paneer, 0.5 capsicum, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi, pinch salt
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan, heat 2 tbsp oil and saute the spices. 2. Also add 5 cloves of garlic, 1 inch of ginger and 2 onions. Saute well. 3. Further, add 2 tomatoes, 2 tbsp cashew and 2 tbsp melon seeds, 1 tsp salt, and 2 dried red chillies. 4. Saute well till the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 5. Cool completely, grind to a smooth paste, and add water as required. 6. Now filter the base to get a silky smooth base gravy. Keep aside. 7. In a kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil and 1 tsp cumin. Saute till it turns aromatic. 8. Further add 1 onion and saute till the onion turns golden brown. 9. Keeping the flame low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 2 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, and ½ tsp cumin powder. Saute til the spices turn aromatic. 10. Now, add masala gravy to the base and cook well. Cook till the oil separates. 11. Furthermore, add 2 cups of hot water, ½ tsp salt and cook, adjusting the consistency. 12. In a pan heat 1 tbsp butter, add 18 cubes of paneer, ½ capsicum and stir fry. 13. Also add ¼ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp kasuri methi and a pinch of salt. 14. Saute till the paneer is cooked well. 15. Transfer the masala paneer 4 tbsp grated paneer and mix well. 16. Cook for 10 minutes, or until the gravy is cooked well. 17. Further add 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, ½ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp cream. Mix well. 18. Finally, enjoy Paneer Lababdar Recipe with roti.

title: Paneer Makhani Recipe
ingredients: 17 cashewnuts, 2.25 cups tomatoes, 2 cups cups paneer, 1.5 tablespoons butter, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional, but recommended)), 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, salt as needed, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup cream
instructions: 1. If using store bought paneer, then soak it in hot water for 15 to 20 mins. 2. Drain the water and use. This will keep the paneer soft. 3. Blend together tomatoes and cashews to a smooth puree. 4. If you do not have a powerful blender then just soak cashews for 10 to 15 mins in hot water. 5. The tomato puree has to be smooth. 6. If you find your puree coarse, then pass it through a strainer. 7. Heat a pan with butter. saute cloves, cinnamon, cardamoms and green chili. 8. Then saute ginger garlic paste for 2 mins until the raw smell has gone. 9. Transfer the tomato puree to the pan. 10. The tomato puree will begin to splash so be cautious. 11. Cover partially and cook until the puree thickens. 12. Add chili powder, turmeric and salt. 13. Saute until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan. 14. Next add garam masala and saute for 1 to 2 mins. 15. Pour 1 cup water and sugar. Adjust the amount of water as needed to make a gravy. 16. Cook until the paneer makhani gravy thickens & you see traces of butter or grease on top. 17. Add the paneer & kasuri methi to the makhani gravy. 18. Stir gently and cook covered for 2 mins on a low heat. 19. Pour the cream and allow to bubble. 20. Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with cream and coriander leaves. 21. Serve paneer makhani with roti, naan or jeera rice.

title: paneer makhani recipe | paneer makhanwala recipe | paneer makhani masala
ingredients: 1 onion, 3 tomato, 2 tbsp cashew, 1 bay leaf, 3 pod cardamom, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp butter, 2 cup water, 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp oil, 15 cubes paneer, 1 tsp cumin, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp cream, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, to prepare onion tomato base, in a pan take 1 onion, 3 tomato and 2 tbsp cashew. 2. add 1 bay leaf, 3 pods cardamom, 3 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 3. also, add 1 tsp butter and 2 cup water. 4. mix well, cover and boil for 10 minutes or until everything are cooked well. 5. remove the bay leaf and cool completely before grinding to smooth paste. keep aside. 6. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp oil and fry 15 cubes paneer. 7. once the paneer turns light golden, keep aside. 8. in the same oil, add 1 tsp cumin, 1 chilli and 1 inch ginger. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 9. keeping the flame on low, add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder and ¼ tsp garam masala. 10. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 11. pour in a prepared onion tomato base and mix well. keep mixing until the oil separates. 12. also, add 2 tbsp cream and ¾ tsp salt. mix well. 13. cover and boil for 5 minutes or until the base is cooked well. 14. add in fried paneer, 1 tsp kasuri methi and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 15. finally. enjoy paneer makhani with roti or rice.

title: paneer malai tikka recipe | malai paneer tikka | paneer tikka malai
ingredients: 0.25 cup cream malai, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 chilli, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp corn flour, 1 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 12 cubes paneer cottage cheese, oil for roasting
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take ¼ cup cream, ¼ cup curd and 1 tbsp lemon juice. 2. add ½ tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 chilli, ½ tsp pepper, ¼ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp cumin and 1 tsp kasuri methi. 3. further add 1 tsp cornflour, 1 tsp oil and ½ tsp salt. you can replace corn flour with roasted besan. 4. mix well making sure the spices are well combined. 5. now add ½ onion, ½ capsicum and 12 cubes paneer. 6. mix gently making sure the mixture is coated well. 7. cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes. you can marinate for overnight for more flavours. 8. after 30 minutes, mix gently. 9. now take a skewer and pierce in capsicum, paneer, onion, paneer, onion, paneer and capsicum. 10. roast on tawa adding oil as required. 11. turn around and roast until it turns golden brown. you can also bake in oven or tandoor. 12. finally, sprinkle chaat masala and enjoy paneer malai tikka recipe with green chutney.

title: Paneer Manchurian Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 0.25 cup all-purpose flour, 3 tablespoons corn flour, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder replace with pepper powder, Water, Oil, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.625 tablespoon garlic, 0.625 tablespoon ginger, 0.25 cup spring onion, 0.375 cup capsicum, 1 teaspoon corn flour, 0.5 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons soya sauce, 2 tablespoons tomato sauce, 0.875 teaspoon sugar, 0.625 teaspoon black pepper, 0.625 teaspoon vinegar, Salt, 2 tablespoons Spring onion
instructions: 1. Prepare all the ingredients for making the sauce and set aside. Heat oil in a kadai for deep frying paneer. 2. While the oil heats, add corn flour, maida, salt and chilli powder to a bowl. Add water just enough to make a free flowing yet thick batter. The consistency must be moderately thick, not too thick or too runny. 3. Test if the oil is hot by dropping a small portion of the batter to the oil. It should immediately sizzle and come up to the surface without turning brown. This is the right temperature. 4. Dip the paneer cubes in the batter and gently drop to the hot oil. Do not disturb them for a minute or 2 until the coating set firmly over the paneer. 5. Stir often and fry on a medium heat until golden and crisp. Remove them to a steel colander. 6. To a mixing bowl add 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 2 teaspoons ketchup (tomato sauce), little sugar and mix well. Add paneer and give a good stir. 7. To a small bowl, add 1 tablespoon corn starch, 1 tablespoon all-purpose flour and 2 to 3 pinches of black pepper. Mix together and sprinkle all over the paneer. 8. Coat the paneer well with the flour. If the mixture is too dry, sprinkle little water. Each piece of paneer should be well coated with a thick batter. 9. Heat a tablespoon of oil in a non-stick pan. When the oil turns hot, place each piece in the oil, each atleast an inch apart. Fry until crisp on both the sides, turning to the other after one side turns golden. 10. To air fry, place them on a sheet and air fry for 6 to 8 mins at 350 F or 180 C, turn the pieces and air fry for another 5 mins. 11. Add 1 teaspoon corn starch to a bowl and add ½ cup water. Mix and make a lump free slurry. Keep this aside. 12. Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a wok. Regulate the flame to high. Add ginger garlic and saute for a min for the nice aroma to comes out. 13. Next add spring onion whites and fry for a min or two. Add capsicum and saute for another minute. 14. Pour soya sauce, tomato sauce and corn slurry. Make sure you stir the corn slurry once before pouring to the pan. 15. Add more water if needed. Reduce the flame to medium and add sugar. Stir and check the taste test. 16. Add black pepper, salt and vinegar if needed. Let the sauce boil and cook down to a thick consistency. 17. Add the fried paneer and spring onion greens. Toss the paneer in the sauce well to coat evenly. 18. Serve paneer manchurian hot with noodles or fried rice.

title: Paneer Masala Recipe - Dhaba Style
ingredients: 2 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp pepper, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 2 pods cardamom, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, 2 cubes paneer, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 2 pods cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tomato, 0.5 cup curd, 0.5 cup water, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 20 cubes paneer. 2. also add ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, ¼ tsp salt. 3. mix well and marinate for 30 minutes. 4. now heat 1 tbsp ghee and marinated paneer. 5. fry for a minute making sure all the sides are cooked well. keep aside. 6. firstly, in a heavy-bottomed pan take 2 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin, ¼ tsp pepper, 3 cloves, 2 pods cardamom, ½ inch cinnamon and ½ tsp fennel. 7. dry roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 8. cool completely, and blend to a fine powder. keep aside. 9. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee and 2 tbsp oil. 10. add 1 bay leaf, 2 pods cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves and 1 tsp cumin. 11. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 12. now add 1 onion and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. saute until the onions turn golden brown. 13. further keeping the flame on low, add the prepared spice powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder and ¾ tsp salt. 14. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 15. now add 2 tomato and saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 16. further, keeping the flame on low add ½ cup curd. 17. stir continuously until the curd is combined well and oil separates out from the mixture. 18. add ½ cup water or as required adjusting the consistency. 19. now add fried paneer and mix well. 20. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 21. add 1 tsp kasuri methi, ¼ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 22. finally, enjoy paneer masala recipe dhaba style with roti, naan or phulka.

title: paneer momos recipe | paneer momo | veg paneer momos
ingredients: 1.5 cup maida plain flour, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp oil, water, 3 tsp oil, 3 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, 4 tbsp spring onion, 1 onion, 1 cup cabbage, 2 tbsp capsicum, 1 cup paneer cottage cheese, 1 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, knead the dough again slightly and pinch a small ball sized dough. 2. dust with maida and roll thin. 3. place a tbsp of prepared paneer stuffing in centre. 4. start pleating from one side. 5. pleat until you reach ¾ of the circle. 6. now seal the pleated part with other part and secure tight. 7. heat a steamer and arrange the momos in the tray without touching each other. 8. furthermore, steam momos for 12-15 minutes or till shiny sheen appears over it. 9. finally, paneer momos recipe is ready to enjoy with momos chutney.

title: paneer nuggets recipe | paneer bites | crispy cottage cheese nuggets
ingredients: 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp pepper, salt to taste, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 8 cubes paneer cottage cheese, 0.25 cup corn flour, 2 tbsp maida plain flour all-purpose flour refined flour, 0.5 tsp pepper, salt to taste, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 cup panko bread crumbs crushed corn flakes, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp pepper and salt to taste. 2. also add 2 tbsp coriander leaves and 1 tbsp lemon juice. mix well to form paste. 3. coat the prepared paste to paneer cubes. 4. cover and allow to marinate in refrigerator for 30 minutes. 5. meanwhile prepare maida batter by mixing, ¼ cup corn flour, 2 tbsp maida, ½ tsp pepper and salt to taste. 6. add ½ cup water and prepare lump free, flowing consistency batter. 7. dip the marinated paneer into maida paste and coat well. 8. now coat with bread crumbs covering all sides. use panko bread crumbs / crushed corn flakes as they give nice texture. 9. deep fry the paneer nuggets in hot oil. alternatively, bake in preheated oven at 180 degree celsius for 12 minutes. 10. fry on medium flame, stirring occasionally till it turns golden brown. 11. finally, serve crispy paneer nuggets with green chutney or tomato sauce.

title: Paneer Pakora Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp garam masala, Oil for deep frying, 0.5 cup besan, 2 tbsps rice flour, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, Salt as needed, 0.875 tsp ajwain, 1 tsp ginger green chilli paste, Water as needed
instructions: 1. Cut paneer to desired sizes. 2. Sprinkle red chilli powder, garam masala and chaat masala. 3. Toss them well in the bowl. Set this aside. 4. Heat oil in a deep kadai on a medium heat. 5. Add besan, rice flour, salt, ajwain, red chilli powder, ginger chilli paste to a bowl. 6. Pour water just enough to make a thick but free flowing batter. 7. When the oil is hot enough, dip each paneer piece in the batter, swipe off the excess dripping batter. 8. Gently slide to the hot oil. You can use your fingers or a table spoon to do this. 9. Do not disturb them for about a minute. 10. Gently keep stirring & flipping to fry evenly until they turn golden. 11. Drain them to a colander. Sprinkle some chaat masala. 12. Serve paneer pakora hot with chutney or a cup of tea.

title: Paneer Pakora Recipe | Masala Stuffed Crispy Paneer Pakoda
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 1 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp salt, pinch hing, 2 tbsp coriander, water, 0.5 tsp baking powder, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup besan, 2 tbsp rice flour, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp ajwain, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, ½ tsp salt, pinch hing and 2 tbsp coriander. 2. Add water and whisk smooth. 3. Prepare a smooth lump free thick batter. 4. Now add ½ tsp baking powder and mix well. 5. Take small pieces of paneer slices and dip them in batter. 6. Deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 7. Stir occasionally, until the pakoda turns golden brown and crispy. 8. Drain off and finally enjoy Paneer Pakoda Recipe with green chutney.

title: Paneer Paratha Recipe (Plain Layered and Stuffed)
ingredients: 2 cups wheat flour, 0.25 teaspoon sea salt, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.625 cup water, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 25 grams Paneer, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.625 teaspoon amchur powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon dry ginger powder, 0.375 teaspoon ajwain, 1 tablespoon Kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Make Dough: Add flour, salt, oil and ½ cup water to a large mixing bowl. Begin to mix, adding more water as required to form a soft dough, (not sticky). Cover and rest the dough until you prepare the filling or for 20 mins. 2. Take ¼ cup flour in a bowl and set aside to help you roll the parathas. 3. Make the filling: Mix together grated paneer, salt, herbs and all the spices in another bowl. Taste test to adjust salt and spices. Divide the filling roughly to 8 portions. 4. Roll the paratha: Divide the dough to 8 portions. Roll the dough in your palms to form balls. Keep them covered until used. Sprinkle little flour over the rolling area & take a ball and dip it in the flour. 5. Method 1 – Dust off the excess in the bowl & roll to a 5 inch disc or roti. Or shape the dough to a small cup with your fingers, starting from the center. (Refer stepwise pics in the post) 6. Place a portion of paneer stuffing in the center. Push down the stuffing & gently bring the sides of the roti up, shaping like a cup. Repeat the steps of pushing the stuffing inside with your finger and bring the dough up until the edges come over the stuffing. (Refer video). 7. Bring the edges together over the stuffing and join them to seal carefully. 8. Method 2 – Roll a 5 inch roti and place the paneer stuffing in the center, hold the edges and make small pleats, across the roti. Bring all of them on top and join them together to seal the stuffing. Remove the excess dough if any on top. 9. Dip the dough ball in flour, shake off the excess and flatten it gently to spread the stuffing evenly. This ensures the stuffing does not come out. 10. Sprinkle some flour on the rolling area. Place the paneer paratha disc on the rolling area with the sealed side down. Begin to roll evenly all over without putting pressure. 11. Do not roll the paneer paratha continuously. Instead rotate in clock-wise direction a little after every 4 to 6 rolls to ensure it does not tear or get stuck at the bottom. Sprinkle flour as needed and avoid using excess. If you see the stuffing coming out, gently smear some flour to seal it. 12. Roll the paneer paratha as thin or thick as per your preference without tearing. 13. Transfer the rolled ones to a dry counter space. Once you are done with 3 to 4 begin to fry them. 14. Method 3 – With this method, you will be able to make only 6 large parathas. So divide the dough to 12 and stuffing to 6 parts. Roll 2 rotis of 6 inches each. Spread the paneer mixture on one of the roti, leaving a quarter inch on the edges. Wet the edges with little water. 15. Place the other roti on top of it. Press down gently on the sides to seal both the rotis. Then sprinkle flour and roll it evenly as thin as you want. 16. Heat a pan/tawa or griddle to cook the paneer parathas. The griddle should be hot enough else the parathas will turn hard. 17. When the pan is hot, gently transfer a rolled paneer paratha over it. (Make sure you dust off the excess flour before frying) 18. Let cook for 2 to 3 mins, turn it to the other side and cook for another 2 mins. Turn it again. 19. You will see the paneer paratha begins to puff and cook well. Drizzle ghee or oil all over and turn it to the other side and cook. 20. Smear some ghee on this side as well and press down the edges to cook thoroughly, until you see golden to light brown spots. 21. Remove the paneer paratha to a plate and stack them. 22. Top paneer paratha with some butter and serve with coriander chutney, mint chutney, pickle or yogurt if preferred.

title: Paneer Pasanda Recipe
ingredients: 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1.75 cups onions, 0.5 inch ginger, 4 medium garlic, 1.5 cups tomatoes, 2 soaked cashews, 0.5 cup curd, 1.25 tablespoon butter, 1 green chili, 1 bay leaf, 1 strand mace, 1 black cardamom, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1.5 inch cinnamon, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 to 1 cups hot water, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.125 teaspoon nutmeg, 1 pinch saffron, 4 grams Paneer, 1 teaspoon ghee, 1.5 tbsps cashew nuts, 0.125 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.125 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon amchur, 0.125 teaspoon salt, 1.25 tablespoon coriander chutney, 1 tablespoon heavy cream, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a deep pot and saute onions on a medium heat for 10 minutes. Add the ginger and garlic and continue to saute on a low heat until deep golden (caramelized) but not burnt. 2. Cool this and add to a blender along with tomatoes, soaked cashews and curd. Make a smooth paste. 3. Add butter to the same pot and heat it on a low heat. Add the whole spices – bay leaf, mace, cinnamon, cloves, green and black cardamoms and green chilies. 4. Let them sizzle in the butter for a minute and stir in the red chili powder and garam masala, followed by the onion tomato puree. 5. Be careful as this can splutter. Quickly cover and cook for 8 minutes on a low to medium heat (leaving the spoon in & stir after every 3 minutes without uncovering). 6. Turn down the heat and stir in the hot water. Cover and simmer until the gravy turns thick and creamy. 7. Meanwhile, trim the edges of the paneer blocks to cut down about 35 to 40 grams ( ¼ cup crumbled). Cube the rest of the paneer to 8 portions. 8. Make a slit on the paneer portions, keeping one side intact (refer the pictures in the post). 9. Mix together the crumbled paneer with salt, coriander chutney, chopped cashews, red chili powder, amchur, garam masala and dried ginger. Taste test and add more spice to your taste. The mixture has to be moist. 10. Carefully fill the paneer sandwiches with this mixture, making sure to keep one side intact. (do not break). Keep them aside. 11. Check the gravy, it should be thick and creamy. Add nutmeg, kasuri methi, black pepper, sugar and salt. Taste test and adjust all the spices if you want. 12. Sprinkle saffron or rose water or kewda. Turn off and keep covered for at least 5 to 10 minutes. 13. Heat a pan with ghee or oil and place the paneer sandwiches. Fry them on a medium to medium-high heat until golden, using a wooden tong or 2 spatulas, hold the paneer sandwiches and turn them to the other side. 14. Fry them until crisp and golden. 15. The gravy becomes thick after cooling down a bit. Serve the gravy with Basmati rice, naan or roti. Place the sandwiches in the gravy and garnish with coriander leaves & cream.

title: paneer pepper masala recipe | paneer pepper dry | paneer pepper fry
ingredients: 0.75 cup maida, 0.75 cup corn flour, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, water, 2 grams paneer, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp fennel, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 4 garlic, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp water, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take ¾ cup maida, ¾ cup corn flour, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp pepper powder and ½ tsp salt. 2. add water and prepare a smooth lump-free batter. 3. dip paneer cubes in batter and coat uniformly. 4. deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 5. fry until the paneer turns golden brown and crisp. 6. drain off the paneer and keep aside. 7. in a large wok, heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp fennel, pinch hing and few curry leaves. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 8. add 4 garlic, 1 inch ginger, ½ onion and saute until the onions turn translucent. 9. also, add ½ capsicum and saute slightly. 10. keeping the flame on low and add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp pepper powder and ½ tsp salt. 11. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 12. further, add 2 tbsp tomato sauce and cook on high flame. add 2 tbsp water and adjust the consistency of the sauce. 13. add in fried paneer and mix well coating sauce uniformly. 14. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander, lemon and enjoy crispy paneer pepper fry.

title: Paneer popcorn
ingredients: 1.25 cup paneer, 0.25 cup flour, 1.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, Salt as needed, 0.625 tsp red chilli powder, 0.375 tsp pepper, 0.75 tsp garam masala, 0.75 tsp herbs, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, Salt as needed, 0.25 tsp red chili powder
instructions: 1. Cut paneer to one by half inch cubes. Keep these aside. 2. Add red chili powder, salt and bread crumbs to a wide bowl. Mix everything well and set aside. 3. Add flour, salt, ginger garlic paste, pepper, herbs, red chili powder and garam masala to a mixing bowl. 4. Add water little by little and make a thick batter. Too thick batter will make them hard so it has to be moderately thick. 5. Add the paneer cubes to this and coat them well with the batter. 6. Roll each of these batter coated paneer cubes in bread crumbs. 7. Gently press down the crumbs to the paneer. 8. Refrigerate for about 15 mins. 9. Heat oil in a kadai. When the oil is hot enough, add the bread coated paneer cubes in the hot oil. 10. Regulate the flame to medium and fry stirring until golden. 11. Drain them when golden and crunchy. To fry the next batch, discard the bread crumbs that settle at the bottom of the kadai. Then add the next batch. 12. Serve paneer popcorn with red chilli sauce or schezwan sauce.

title: Paneer Pulao Recipe (Paneer Rice)
ingredients: 1.5 cups basmati rice, 2.5 cups water for stovetop pressure cooker, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 onion, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 cup green peas, 0.5 cup carrots, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 13.5 mint leaves, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3.5 green cardamoms, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 star anise, 1.5 cup paneer, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.75 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, salt as needed, 1 tablespoon gram flour, 0.375 kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Prep-up paneer: Add all the marination ingredients including 1½ tablespoon oil to paneer. Mix and set aside. If it is too dry, sprinkle 1 tbsp water. Rinse and soak rice in water for 15 mins. 2. Make pulao rice: Heat oil or ghee in a pot or cooker. Add all the whole spices. When they begin to sizzle, add sliced onions & green chilies. Saute them until golden. 3. Saute ginger garlic paste till the raw smell is gone, for 1 minute. Add all the chopped veggies, mint and coriander leaves. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. Pour water and stir in salt. Taste test and adjust salt to make it slightly salty. 4. Bring it to a boil. Add soaked and drained rice and pressure cook for 1 whistle on a medium flame. If cooking in a pot, cover and cook on a low flame until the rice is done. 5. Fry Paneer: Meanwhile, heat a non-stick pan and place the marinated paneer cubes. Fry them until golden. If the pan looks too dry add some oil. 6. Assemble Paneer Pulao: When the pressure releases naturally, open the lid and fluff up. Remove to a serving plate and top the fried paneer. Serve paneer pulao with a raita or a salad.

title: Paneer Pulao Recipe | High Protein Pulav
ingredients: 2 tbsp ghee, 1 cup paneer, 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp cloves, 0.5 javetri, 0.5 tsp pepper, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 tbsp ginger garlic, 1 onion, 3 chilli, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 1.5 cup basmati rice, 2 tsp lemon juice, 3 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp ghee and roast 1 cup paneer. 2. Roast till the paneer turns golden brown. Keep aside. 3. In the same ghee, add spices 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp cloves, ½ javetri, ½ tsp pepper, 1 inch cinnamon. Sauté till the spices turn aromatic. 4. Now add 2 tbsp of ginger-garlic and sauté well. 5. Also, add 1 onion and sauté until golden brown. 6. Add 3 chillies and sauté till aromatic. 7. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp garam masala, ½ tsp cumin powder and 1 tsp salt. Sauté till the spices turn aromatic. 8. Pour in 3 cups of water and bring to a boil. 9. Add 1½ cups of basmati rice, fried paneer, 2 tsp of lemon juice and 3 tbsp of coriander. Mix gently. 10. Once the water comes to a boil, cover and simmer for 20 minutes. 11. Fluff the rice gently, making sure it's not sticky. 12. Finally, enjoy Paneer Pulao Recipe with raita.

title: Paneer Sandwich Recipe (3 Variations)
ingredients: 6 slices brown bread, 1 tablespoons butter, 0.5 cup paneer, 1 tablespoon sauce, 2 tablespoon tomatoes, 3 tablespoon sweet corn, 0.25 cup carrot, 2 tablespoon cucumber, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 green chilli, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 2 tablespoon green chutney, 6 slices bread, 1.5 tsp oil, 0.25 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 0.75 tsp ginger, 1 tomato, 1 Pinch turmeric, Salt, 0.25 tsp red chilli powder, 0.375 tsp garam masala powder, 0.625 cup paneer, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup capsicum, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl add crumbled paneer, carrots, cucumber, tomatoes, green chilies, coriander leaves and salt. 2. Add 1 tbsp sauce or green chutney or thick curd along with chaat masala. Mix well. 3. Taste this add more salt or sauce if needed. 4. Butter the bread slices on the outer side. Spread sauce or mayo or curd or green chutney on the inner side. 5. Spread the paneer mixture over the sauce and cover with another slice. Toast the paneer sandwich in a toaster or tawa until golden. 6. Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan. Add a pinch of cumin & fry until they begin to sizzle. 7. Add 1 chopped garlic and fry until fragrant. 8. Then add ¼ to ½ tsp red chili powder, ¼ to ½ tsp garam masala, ¼ to ½ tsp kasuri methi and salt. 9. Add 1/3 cup crumbled paneer and fry just for 1 minute. Sprinkle coriander leaves. 10. Butter 4 bread slices on one side. Spread the paneer on the other side. 11. Place some onion slices, tomatoes & cucumbers. You can also smear some green chutney or tomato sauce on the inner side of bread slices. Toast on the same tawa. 12. Heat oil and add cumin seeds. When they splutter add onions and fry until golden. 13. Add grated ginger & fry for a minute. Next put in tomatoes and salt. Saute untill soft. 14. Stir in red chili powder, garam masala, kasuri methi & amchur powder. Mix and fry for a minute. 15. Turn off the stove and add in crumbled paneer and coriander leaves. Check salt and spice add more if needed. Remove this to a bowl. 16. Grilling on tawa or griddle - Apply ghee or butter on one side of the bread slice. Toast bread slices till golden. Then place the prepared paneer. Layer with some grated carrots, beets & onions. Cover with another slice and press down the paneer sandwiches with a flat wooden spoon. 17. Grilling in toaster - You can also grill these in a toaster. Cut and serve paneer sandwich.

title: paneer sandwich recipe | how to make grilled paneer sandwich recipe
ingredients: 1 cup paneer cottage cheese, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp corn, 1 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 4 slice bread, 2 tsp green chutney, 2 tsp butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 1 cup grated paneer. 2. add 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp boiled corn and 1 tbsp coriander. 3. additionally, add ¼ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp salt and 2 tbsp tomato sauce. 4. mix well making sure all the spices are combined well. 5. now spread green chutney over a bread slice. 6. furthermore, spread 2 tbsp of prepared paneer stuffing. 7. cover again with bread slice spread with green chutney. 8. now grill golden or toast on tawa by spreading butter. 9. finally, cut half and serve paneer sandwich recipe.

title: Paneer Tawa Pulao Recipe - Lunch Box Special | Tawa Paneer Pulav
ingredients: 1.5 cup basmati rice, water, 1 tbsp salt, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp butter, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 chilli, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 capsicum, 1 carrot, 5 beans, 3 tbsp peas, 1 tomato, 1 tsp chilli powder, 2 tsp pav bhaji masala, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp schezwan sauce, 0.5 cup paneer, 0.5 potato, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, soak 1½ cup basmati rice for 20 minutes. 2. now take hot water in a large vessel. add 1 tbsp salt, 1 tbsp oil and ½ tsp turmeric. 3. once the water comes to a boil, add soaked rice and mix well. 4. boil for 8 minutes or until the rice is cooked well. 5. drain off and allow to cool completely. 6. firstly, in a large tawa or wok heat 2 tbsp oil and 1 tsp butter. 7. add 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 chilli and saute slightly. 8. also add ½ onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until onion shrinks slightly. 9. further add ½ capsicum, 1 carrot, 5 beans, 3 tbsp peas and 1 tomato. 10. stir fry on high flame until vegetables are cooked yet remain crunchy. 11. now add 1 tsp chilli powder, 2 tsp pav bhaji masala, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt and 1 tsp schezwan sauce. 12. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 13. further, add ½ cup paneer and ½ potato. mix gently without breaking. 14. add in cooked rice, 2 tbsp coriander and mix gently. 15. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 16. finally, enjoy paneer tawa pulao with raita.

title: Paneer Tikka Masala Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams paneer, 0.5 cup Hung curd, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon Kasuri Methi, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.5 bell pepper, 0.5 onion, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 green cardamoms, 1 cup onions, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 14 cashew nuts, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder less spicy kind, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.75 cup water, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 3 tablespoons Heavy cream, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl, add yogurt, red chili powder, ginger garlic paste, garam masala, turmeric, kasuri methi, oil, lemon juice & salt. 2. Mix well and taste test it. Add more salt if required. Add paneer cubes & gently mix well to coat the paneer with the marinade. 3. Optional - If using onions & bell peppers, dice them to the same size as that of paneer. Separate the layers in the onion. Add them along with bell pepper and marinate gently. Set this aside until the gravy is done. 4. Heat half of the oil and add the chopped onions. Saute them well until transparent or golden on a medium heat. 5. Add tomatoes, cashews, sugar and salt. Saute all of these well until the tomatoes turn mushy and soft. 6. Stir in red chilli powder & garam masala, Saute for another 2 mins, until the masala smells good & aromatic. 7. Cool this completely and add to a blender. Pour water and blend to a smooth puree. 8. Heat the rest of the oil in a pan on a low flame and add the whole spices. When they sizzle, add ginger garlic paste & saute for 1 to 2 mins, until the raw smell disappears. 9. Pour the onion tomato puree. (Careful as it may splash). At this step, taste test and add more red chilli powder if you want to achieve a brighter color. 10. Pour water to bring this to a gravy consistency. Cook covered until the gravy turns thick for about 10 mins and you see traces of oil on top. Keep stirring in between. 11. Once the gravy is thick yet of a pouring consistency, you may remove and discard the whole spices. Crush the kasuri methi in your palms and sprinkle it. (optional). Taste the gravy & add more salt if needed. Rest this aside. 12. Option 1 - Grill in oven. Preheat the oven to 430 F or 220 C for at least 15 mins. Line a baking tray with foil. Thread paneer, onion & bell peppers alternately on skewers. Place the skewers in the baking tray & grill for 8 to 10 minutes, turning them to the other side after 5 mins. Remove them when golden and crisp. 13. Option 2 - To make on griddle. Heat a tsp oil in a wide pan on a high flame, coat each paneer piece with the marinade & place on the griddle. Cook on a high flame till the marinade dries up and paneer turns golden. Similarly place the bell peppers and marinated onions and fry until the marinade dries up. 14. Option 3 - Air fryer. If your air fryer needs preheating, you may preheat it for 5 mins first. Thread paneer, onion, bell pepper on skewers. Air fry the skewers placed in a tray at 390 F or 200 C for 5 mins. Brush them with the marinade and then air fry for another 2 mins. The marinade has to partially dried up. Also adjust the air fryer time as needed. 15. Transfer grilled paneer tikka along with veggies & onions to the tikka masala gravy. 16. Pour the cream. Gently mix. Garnish with cream and coriander leaves. 17. Serve paneer tikka masala with Roti, Butter Naan, Basmati Rice or Jeera rice

title: paneer tikka masala recipe | paneer tikka gravy sabji restaurant style
ingredients: 0.75 cup curd, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp besan gram flour, 2 tsp oil, 2 tsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 onion, 14 cubes paneer, 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 1 tsp cumin, 2 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup tomato puree, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take ¾ cup curd, ¼ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ½ tsp garam masala. 2. also add ¼ tsp ajwain, ½ tsp salt, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp besan and 2 tsp oil. 3. further, add 2 tsp lemon juice and 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. now add ½ capsicum, ½ onion and 14 cubes paneer. 5. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 6. cover and refrigerate for 1 hour to marinate. 7. after 1 hour, insert into skewer and grill until golden brown. 8. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 bay leaf, ½ inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom, 3 cloves and 1 tsp cumin. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 9. add 2 onion and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. saute until the onions turn golden brown. 10. keeping the flame on low, add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder and 1 tsp salt. 11. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 12. further, add 2 cup tomato puree and cook well. 13. cook until the oil separates from the sides. 14. now add leftover marination mixture and cook on low flame. 15. make sure to stir continuously and cook until the oil separates from the sides. 16. further, add 1 cup water and mix adjusting the consistency as required. 17. add in prepared paneer tikka and mix gently. 18. cover and simmer for 2 minutes, or until the flavours are absorbed well. 19. now add ¼ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 20. finally, enjoy paneer tikka masala with tandoori roti or naan.

title: paneer tikka recipe | recipe of paneer tikka on tawa | dry paneer tikka
ingredients: 0.5 cup curd yogurt, 0.5 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder daniya powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi dry fenugreek leaves, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp ginger - garlic paste, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 2 tsp besan gram flour, 1 tbsp lemon juice, salt to taste, 3 tsp oil, 0.5 onions, 0.5 capsicum, 5 cubes paneer cottage cheese
instructions: 1. firstly, take ½ cup thick curd / yogurt. 2. further add in all the spices along with salt. 3. mix till all the spices are combined well with curd. 4. now add ½ onion petals, ½ cubed capsicum (red & green) and 5 cubes paneer. 5. also add 1 tsp of oil. 6. mix gently till all the vegetables are coated well. 7. furthermore, to marinate, cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes. 8. after marination, insert the marinated paneer, capsicum and onions into wooden skewers. 9. further, roast it on a hot tawa or grill in oven or tandoor. 10. finally, sprinkle some chaat masala and serve paneer tikka immediately.

title: Paneer Tikka Recipe in Oven, Air Fryer, Stovetop
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 6 tablespoons greek yogurt, 2 grams paneer, 1 onion, 1 red capsicum, 1 green capsicum, 1 tablespoon mustard oil, 1 tablespoon gram flour, 0.375 teaspoon carom seeds
instructions: 1. To make this recipe, we need greek yogurt or hung curd (thick curd). If you do not have thick curd, you can check this post on how to make hung curd . I have used thick curd here. If you use runny yogurt, it will let out lot of whey while grilling and all the masala will come off to the tray. 2. Cube 200 to 250 grams paneer to 1½ inch cubes. Keep aside. Then choose 1 large onion and bell pepper. Rinse and cube them to almost the same size of paneer. Do not chop them small as they shrink after grilling. Separate the layers of onions. 3. To a mixing bowl, add 4. tablespoons thick curd 1 to 1½ teaspoon red chili powder 1/8 teaspoon turmeric 1 to 1½ teaspoon coriander powder ½ teaspoon salt 1 teaspoon garam masala 1 teaspoon chaat masala 5. Next add 6. Optional: If making on a griddle or tawa add 1 tablespoon roasted besan or sattu (roasted gram flour). Dry roast besan until aromatic. It should lose the raw flavor, cool and add it. Do not skip using besan it helps to hold the curd well to the paneer tikka. It also brings out a good flavor and will avoid messing up the tawa. 7. Add 1½ tablespoon oil. I have used mustard oil, you can also use any other oil. 8. Mix everything well. The marinade must be thick. Taste the marinade and add more salt and spices if needed. 9. Add capsicum and onions. Coat them well with marinade. 10. Move the veggies to one side of the bowl and add 200 grams cubed paneer and gently coat them as well. Handle with care they may break. 11. Cover and rest them in the fridge for minimum of 30 mins or an hour. If you leave them for too long, sometimes they break when you thread them on to the skewer later. If you prefer to keep them longer, thread them first and then refrigerate. 12. Next begin to thread them on skewers. With the quantities mentioned in the recipe card you can thread them on 3 large or 4 medium skewers. Paneer tikka on tawa and oven were made at 2 different times so the number of skewers seen in the pictures will not match the recipe. 13. Pour 1½ tablespoons mustard oil to a pan. Add 2½ tablespoons of besan. I highly recommend using mustard oil here for flavor. But I have used regular cooking oil in the pictures. 14. Mix well to a smooth mixture. Turn on the stove and fry this on a low heat, stirring continuously until fragrant. The mixture begins to bubble up and turn frothy. Once you smell it good and fragrant, turn off the heat. 15. Add 16. to 1 ¼ teaspoon red chilli powder ½ teaspoon salt ¼ teaspoon turmeric 1 to 1¼ teaspoon garam masala ½ to 1 teaspoon coriander powder 1 tablespoon crushed kasuri methi / dried fenugreek leaves 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste ½ to ¾ teaspoon ajwain / carom seeds 17. Mix all of these and let them cool down completely. Then add 6 tablespoons thick curd or greek yogurt and add 1 tablespoon lemon juice or ¾ to 1 teaspoon chaat masala. Skip the lemon juice if your yogurt is even slightly sour. 18. Mix everything. Taste test and adjust any the seasoning. The marinade has to be thick as you see in the picture here. 19. Add 200 to 250 grams of paneer, 1 medium onion and 1 cup cubed bell peppers. Mix them gently. Cover and refrigerate in the fridge for at least 20 to 30 mins. To store longer I thread them on the skewers and then store. 20. To grill them on direct fire, in oven or air fryer, thread them on the skewers, alternating paneer, bell peppers and onions.

title: Paneer Tikka Sandwich Recipe | Paneer Tikka Grilled Toast Sandwich
ingredients: 0.75 cup curd, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tbsp oil, 2 tsp besan, 1 cup paneer, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 onion, 1 tomato, 2 tbsp oil, bread, butter, green chutney
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take ¾ cup curd. add 1 tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, and ½ tsp coriander powder. 2. also add ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp aamchur, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, ½ tsp salt, and 1 tbsp oil. 3. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. now add 2 tsp roasted besan and mix well. besan helps in binding. 5. further add 1 cup paneer, ½ capsicum, ½ onion, and 1 tomato. 6. mix well and marinate for 30 minutes. 7. heat 2 tbsp oil and marinated mixture. 8. saute and cook until the oil separates from the sides. 9. stir and cook until the mixture is cooked uniformly. 10. cool completely, and paneer tikka stuffing is ready. 11. to prepare the sandwich, spread butter on bread slices. 12. also spread green chutney on both slices of bread. 13. now spread 2 tbsp of prepared paneer stuffing. 14. now place the sandwich in the gas sandwich maker and close. 15. trim off the sides which have come out. also, make sure to butter the top and bottom of the bread before placing it into the sandwich maker. 16. grill both sides until the sandwich turns golden brown. 17. finally, the street-style paneer tikka sandwich is ready to enjoy.

title: paneer toast recipe | paneer cheese toast | chilli paneer toast sandwich recipe
ingredients: 1 tsp butter, 1 chilli, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 capsicum, 1 tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, pinch black pepper powder, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 cup paneer cottage cheese, 2 tbsp coriander, bread, butter, 4 tsp green chutney
instructions: 1. firstly, in a tawa heat 1 tsp butter and saute 1 chilli. 2. add ½ onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute well. 3. also, add ½ capsicum and saute slightly. 4. now add 1 tomato and saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 5. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, pinch black pepper powder, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp salt. 6. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 7. further, add 2 tbsp tomato sauce and saute until everything is well combined. 8. add 1 cup paneer and mix well. 9. now add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. paneer mixture is ready. 10. take the bread and spread butter on both sides. 11. toast the bread on tawa until it turns golden brown. 12. now spread 1 tsp green chutney on one side of bread. 13. spread 2 tbsp of prepared paneer mixture uniformly. 14. finally, cut the toast into half, and chilli paneer toast is ready to serve with tomato sauce.

title: pani wale pakode recipe | pani phulki | chatpate pani pakoda | pani pakora
ingredients: 1 cup coriander, 0.5 cup mint, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, 1 cup tamarind extract, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp aamchur, pinch hing, 1 tsp salt, 5 cup cold water, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp mint, 0.5 lemon, 0.5 onion, 2 cup besan gram flour, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, pinch hing, water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 1 onion, 2 tbsp coriander, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixi jar take 1 cup coriander, ½ cup mint, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli. 2. add ¼ cup water and blend to smooth paste. 3. transfer the green chutney into a large bowl. 4. add 1 cup tamarind extract. i have soaked a ball sized tamarind in water for 30 minutes, and squeezed out the juice. 5. also add 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp aamchur, pinch hing and 1 tsp salt. 6. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 7. further, add 5 cup cold water and mix well adjusting the spices as required. 8. now add 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp mint, half lemon and half onion. 9. mix well and refrigerate to keep the pani chilled. 10. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup besan, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt and pinch hing. 11. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 12. now add water as required and mix well forming a thick batter. 13. beat for 2 minutes, to make besan batter airy and light. 14. further add ¼ tsp baking soda, 1 onion and 2 tbsp coriander. 15. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 16. now take a small ball sized batter and drop it into the hot oil. 17. stir occasionally, until the pakoda is cooked uniformly and turns golden brown. 18. drain off the pakoda over the kitchen towel to remove excess oil. 19. now drop the hot pakoda into the water and soak for 1-2 minutes. 20. soaking pakoda in water helps to make them soft and also remove excess oil. 21. now squeeze the pakoda slightly, and drop it into theeka pani. 22. refrigerate for at least 1 hour to absorb all the flavours. 23. finally, enjoy pani wale pakode recipe topped with boondi.

title: Paniyaram Recipe | Kuzhi Paniyaram | Gunta Ponganalu
ingredients: 1.5 cups idli batter, 0.5 cup onion, 2 tablespoons carrot, 2 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 6 curry leaves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 pinch hing, 2.5 tablespoons oil, salt
instructions: 1. This section is optional and you can add all the ingredients directly to the batter. But tempering makes the paniyaram flavorful. 2. Heat a pan with oil and add mustard seeds. When they crackle, add curry leaves. Saute for a min. 3. Add onions, chilies and carrots. Saute until the onions turn pink or raw smell disappears. Add ginger and saute for a minute. 4. Then add coriander leaves and hing. Cool this and add to the batter. 5. Taste the batter and add more salt if needed. Batter must be of medium consistency not very thin or thick. If needed sprinkle some water. 6. Season the paniyaram pan and heat it with few drops of oil in each cavity. (Check notes to season your cast iron pan) 7. When the pan is hot enough, pour batter up to ¾ in each mold. Lower the heat and cook for 2 mins. If desired you can cover and cook. 8. When the base is golden fried, remove the lid and fry for another minute,. Turn them and cook until golden on the other side too. I usually check by poking a paniyaram with a fork or skewer to check if it is done inside. 9. Remove them to a plate. To make the next batch, reduce the heat and pour few drops of oil in each cavity. Pour the batter and cook the same way. 10. Serve masala paniyaram with chutney or sambar.

title: Papaya halwa (papaya dessert)
ingredients: 5 cups papaya, 2 tablespoons desiccated coconut, 0.25 cup sugar, 5 green cardamom, 0.25 cup cashews, 2 tbsps mixed dry fruits, 2.5 tbsps ghee
instructions: 1. Rinse papaya well and grate it either in a food processer or using a hand grater. I got about 5 cups of papaya gratings. If you have lesser gratings then reduce all the ingredients in the recipe proportionately. 2. Heat 2 tablespoons ghee and fry nuts until golden. Remove them to a plate and add raisins if using. Fry until they turn plump. Remove them as well. 3. Add grated papaya & saute until it begins to release juices. If you are using raw papaya, saute for 2 to 3 minutes and then cook covered on a low heat until tender and soft. 4. Skip this step if using raw papaya. You can use 2 to 3 tablespoons desiccated coconut, or 2 tsps rice flour or 2 tablespoons almond meal. Add it in batches so the dry ingredients don’t turn lumpy. Mix and continue to cook. 5. When the papaya turns tender, add cardamom powder and sugar. I added about 4 tablespoons. 6. Continue to cook until the papaya halwa becomes thick. Taste test and add more sugar if required. You may add 1 tablespoon more ghee at this stage. Papaya halwa is best served at warm or room temperature or cold. Let it cool down and garnish with nuts and raisins.

title: Papdi Chaat Recipe (Papri Chaat)
ingredients: 15 papdi, 1 medium potato, 0.5 cup moong sprouts, 0.5 cup curd, 1 tablespoon sugar, 1 medium onion, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 cup nylon sev, 0.375 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 2 cup coriander leaves, 0.625 cup mint leaves, 2 green chili, 0.25 inch ginger, 1 garlic clove, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 teaspoons Lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoons roasted chana dal, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons water, 0.25 cup tamarind, 0.5 cup jaggery, Water, Salt, 0.375 teaspoon ginger powder, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 13 red chilies, 3.5 garlic cloves, salt, 0.5 teaspoon sugar
instructions: 1. Add coriander, mint, garlic, ginger, chili, cumin, salt and lemon juice to a mixer jar along with fried gram (optional). 2. Pour water just as needed and blend to a fine thick chutney. 3. Soak tamarind, jaggery and dates (optional) in hot water. 4. Boil until they soften. 5. Add chili powder, cumin, coriander powder, salt, red chili or powder. 6. Cool this and blend well. If needed add more water. 7. Pass through a strainer. If you have not used dates you can skip blending and just pass through the strainer. 8. Taste it and add more or less jaggery to suit your taste. 9. You can skip this section and just sprinkle some red chili powder over the potatoes. 10. Add red chilies to a bowl and soak them in little hot water until they soften. 11. Blend them in a small jar with garlic & little sugar to a smooth chutney. 12. Use water as needed and blend to a thick paste. 13. Add curd and sugar to a bowl and beat well until smooth. 14. Pour water if desired to the chutneys to bring to a consistency. Thicker chutney will keep your papdis crisp. 15. Place papdis in individual serving plates. 16. Slice or chop the boiled potatoes and place them over the papdis. 17. Next add steamed moong sprouts (optional). 18. Add some garlic chilli chutney and then freshly chopped onions and then yogurt. 19. Add little green chutney & then the tamarind dates chutney. 20. Sprinkle a dash of cumin / jeera powder and chaat masala. 21. Sprinkle generous amount of nylon sev and then chopped coriander leaves. 22. Serve papdi chaat right away after assembling.

title: Papdi Recipe | Masala Papdi for Chaat & Tea Time Snack
ingredients: 2 cup wheat flour, 0.5 cup rava, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp fennel, 0.5 tsp ajwain, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, pinch hing, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp ghee, water, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large plate, take 2 cups of wheat flour, ½ cup rava, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp fennel, ½ tsp ajwain, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, pinch hing and 1 tsp salt. 2. Mix well, making sure all the spices are well combined. 3. Now, pour 2 tbsp hot ghee and mix well. 4. Crumble and mix till the flour is moist. 5. Knead the dough well, adding warm water as required. 6. Knead the dough well to make it soft. The rava will absorb water and become compact. 7. Now divide the dough into two halves. 8. Roll the dough uniformly, keeping it thin. 9. Prick the papdi with a fork to prevent it from puffing up. 10. Using a cookie cutter or cup, cut small round puri. 11. Fry the papri in medium-hot oil. Alternatively, bake in a preheated oven at 180 degrees Celsius for 15-20 minutes. 12. Fry on low flame till the papdi floats. This takes approx 1-2 minutes. 13. Continue frying on low to medium heat until they become crispy and golden in colour. 14. Drain off the papri over kitchen paper to absorb excess oil. 15. Finally, serve the Masala Papdi Recipe with evening chai, or store it in an airtight container for a month to prepare papdi chaat.

title: Pappu charu
ingredients: 0.5 cup kandi pappu, 1.25 cup water, 1.5 teaspoons tamarind, 1 medium onion, 1 tomato, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 sprigs coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 1 green chilli, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 Pinch hing, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 1 pinch methi seeds, 1 red chili, 1.5 cloves garlic
instructions: 1. Wash and cook 1/2 cup dal with 1 1/4 cup water till soft in a pressure cooker. Mash the dal and set aside. Soak tamarind in 1 cup water. squeeze it and set aside. 2. Pour ghee or oil to a hot pot. When the ghee turns hot add cumin, mustard and methi. When they begin to splutter add red chili, curry leaves, garlic and hing. 3. When the curry leaves turn crisp, add onions and fry for about 2 to 3 minutes. 4. Add chopped tomatoes, green chilies, turmeric and salt. Fry till the tomatoes turn mushy. 5. Add red chili powder and fry for a minute till the raw smell goes off. 6. Filter the tamarind water and add one more cup of water. 7. When the water comes to a boil, Add mashed smooth dal. I prefer to pass it through a seive to get nice smooth pappu charu. You can skip this if your dal is smooth. Stir well. Check salt, sour and chili. Adjust what ever you like. 8. When the pappu charu comes to a boil, switch off the stove. Add coriander leaves with stalks. keep it covered. Serve pappu charu with rice or idli. 9. Pressure cook dal till smooth. I usually use 1 cup of water for ½ cup of dal and pressure cook for 2 whistles on a medium flame. You can also cook following pot-in-pot method by placing the bowl of dal inside the cooker. In this case pressure cook for 3 to 4 whistles. When the pressure releases mash it well. 10. Soak tamarind in little water. Set aside. 11. Heat ghee in a pot. Add red chili, mustard, cumin and methi. When they begin to crackle add garlic, curry leaves & hing. 12. Add onions and sauté for a min. I like them to be crunchy. If you want them to be soft, fry till they are half done. 13. Add tomatoes, green chilies sprinkle salt and turmeric, fry till the tomatoes turn fully mushy. If you do not want to use tomatoes, just proceed to next step 14. Add red chili powder, mix well and fry for a min. Squeeze the tamarind and filter the water to the pot. Pour 1 cup plain water, bring it to a boil. 15. When the water comes to a boil, add mashed dal or filter the dal through a colander like me to remove coarse grains. Taste and adjust the salt as needed. Bring it to a boil and simmer for 1 minute. Add coriander leaves. 16. Serve pappu charu with rice, ghee and papad.

title: Paratha Recipe (Flaky South Asian Flatbread)
ingredients: 2.5 cups wheat flour, 0.875 cup warm water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon neutral oil, 3.5 tablespoons melted ghee
instructions: 1. Stir flour, salt & oil in a mixing bowl (or food processor). Pour water (start with lower amount) & mix to form a soft non-sticky dough, adding more water as required. 2. Knead/process until the dough is soft and pliable, for 3 mins. Smear a few drops of oil, cover and let rest for 20 mins to 1 hour. 3. Add ¼ cup flour to a medium sized plate or bowl & reserve aside to roll the parathas. Divide the dough to 8 portions. Smoothen and roll them to balls in between your palms. Keep covered until the end. 4. Sprinkle a tsp of flour over the rolling area and place the dough ball over it. Flatten it with fingers and coat with flour on both the sides. 5. Roll to a 5 to 6 inch paratha. Apply ⅓ tsp melted ghee/ oil all over and sprinkle 2 pinches of flour all over. 6. Triangle Parathas – Fold it to half, to make a semicircle. Apply ghee over the semicircle & sprinkle a pinch of flour. Fold it again to get a triangle. 7. Square Parathas - Fold the left ⅓ part over the middle ⅓. Fold the right ⅓ part over the middle. Apply a few drops of ghee all over the visible part & sprinkle a pinch of flour. Fold the top ⅓ over the middle ⅓ and fold the bottom ⅓ over the middle, to get a square. 8. Sprinkle flour on both the sides lightly and roll to a 6 to 7 inch square or triangle paratha. When you are done rolling 4 to 5, you may begin to fry. 9. Heat a tawa/ pan on a medium flame, until it is medium hot. Dust off any excess flour from the paratha & transfer to the tawa. 10. When you see bubbles on top (takes me about 40 to 60 seconds), turn it to the other side and drizzle with ¼ teaspoon ghee or oil. Gently spread the ghee all over with a spatula. 11. When it begins to puff (within a minute), turn it to the other side and drizzle more ghee. Press down gently with the spatula, until it puffs and light brown spots appear. Press down on the edges to cook well. 12. Remove and stack the parathas in a clean kitchen cloth lined in a casserole or on a wired rack. To fry the next paratha, make sure the pan is hot enough, but not smoking hot. 13. Serve parathas with your favorite curry. Store the leftovers in an airtight container and refrigerate up to 3 days. Heat up on a medium hot pan.

title: Pasta soup recipe
ingredients: 1 tbsp butter, 1 tbsp garlic, 1.5 cups veggies, 1.5 medium tomato, 1 cup pasta, 2 cups water, Salt as needed, 0.5 tsp Herbs, 0.5 tsp Pepper, Any other seasoning as desired
instructions: 1. Pour 6 cups of water to a large pot and bring it to a boil. Cook pasta until al dente. Drain and rinse them well in a colander. 2. Add butter to a hot pot. 3. Saute garlic until it smells good. 4. Saute the veggies along with garlic for 3 mins. I did not have potatoes so didn’t use, but I suggest using since they help to slightly thicken the soup. You can also use corn. 5. Puree tomatoes and add them to the pot. This step is optional. I used since there was no lemon at home. I usually add lemon juice to the warm soup just before serving. 6. Cook for 3 to 4 mins. I prefer to do this on a high flame until the tomatoes dry up. 7. Pour 2 cups of water. 8. Simmer covered with a small vent open to let out the steam. Cooking on a low heat makes the soup tasty. 9. Once the veggies are tender, add the cooked pasta and stir. Add more water if needed to suit your consistency. 10. Cook for 2 to 3 mins. 11. Add pepper. 12. Add oregano. Serve hot or warm with spicy roasted boiled eggs or toasted bread. You can also serve with Garlic cheese toast French toast egg bread toast 13. You can also skip this step and cook the pasta along with the veggies. To get rid of the excess starch I have preboiled them first. Bring 6 cups of water to a boil and add pasta. Cook until al dente. Drain to a colander and rinse them well. 14. Heat butter in a pot, saute garlic until you begin to smell the garlic good. 15. Saute the veggies for about 2 to 3 mins until you smell them good. 16. Add tomato puree and saute on high for 3 to 4 mins. If using lemon juice, skip using tomato. 17. Pour water and cook on a low heat until the veggies are soft and tender. 18. Add salt and pasta. Adjust water as needed. 19. Cook further for 2 to 3mins. 20. Add pepper and herbs. Serve pasta soup hot / warm. Add lemon juice just before serving. 21. Sprinkle your favorite seasoning if desired.

title: pav bhaji dosa recipe | how to make pav bhaji masala dosa recipe
ingredients: 1 cup dosa batter, 3 tsp oil, 1 onions, 3 tbsp capsicum, 1 to mato, 6 tbsp boiled vegetables, 6 tbsp tomato sauce, 3 tbsp coconut chutney, 6 tbsp chilli sauce, 1.5 tsp pav bhaji masala powder, salt to taste, 3 tsp butter, 2 tsp coriander leaves
