title: Pesara Pappu Charu | Hesaru Bele Saaru | Moong Dal Rasam | Pesara Kattu
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, 2 cup water, 1 tomato, 1 onion, 3 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp salt, few curry leaves, 0.25 cup tamarind extract, 2 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp oil, 8 cloves garlic, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 red chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup moong dal and rinse with water. 2. Add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil and 2 cup water. 3. Pressure cook for 2 whistles or until the dal softens. 4. Mash the dal slightly to have a creamy consistency. 5. Add 1 tomato, 1 onion, 3 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp salt, few curry leaves and ¼ cup tamarind extract. 6. Boil for 7 minutes or until the tomato softens. 7. Further, add 2 cup water and mix well adjusting the consistency as required. 8. Once the rasam gets to a boil, prepare the tempering by heating 2 tbsp oil and saute 8 cloves garlic. 9. Also add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 10. Splutter the tempering and pour over the chaaru. 11. Add in 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 12. Finally, enjoy Pesara Pappu Charu with hot steamed rice.

title: Pesarattu Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup whole moong beans, 0.75 inch ginger piece, 2 green chilies, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 tablespoon raw rice, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 medium onions, 2 green chilies
instructions: 1. Add green gram and rice (optional) to a large bowl and rinse them well a few times. 2. Pour fresh water and soak them in for about 4 to 6 hours. Drain the water and rinse them well. 3. Add moong dal, green chilies, cumin seeds, ginger and salt to a blender jar along with little water just enough to make a thick batter. 4. Blend the ingredients to a slightly coarse or smooth batter to suit your liking. I usually blend to a coarse fine semolina texture. 5. Pesarattu batter must be of pouring consistency yet thick & spreadable. 6. If needed pour more water to bring the batter to a consistency. Do not make the batter runny as the pesarattu will not turn crispy. 7. Optional - topping: heat 1 tablespoon ghee or oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, onions and green chilies. Saute them on a medium high flame until transparent yet crunchy. This will bring out the sweet flavor from the onion. They should not turn soft. Cool these completely. 8. Heat a tawa or non-stick griddle on a medium flame. 9. If using a cast iron pan, when it turns hot, drizzle few drops of oil or ghee and rub with a piece of sliced onion. Wipe off the excess. This prevents the pesarattu from sticking to the pan. If using non-stick pan you don't need to grease it. 10. Test by sprinkling little water. Immediately water should sizzle rapidly. When the water evaporates, reduce the flame to medium heat. 11. Pour ½ to ¾ cup of batter on the tawa. Then quickly spread it to a thin layer beginning from the center, moving towards the edges of the pan. 12. On a low to medium heat let the pesarattu cook for a few minutes. Pour 1 teaspoon ghee around the edges and the center too. 13. This step is optional for a thin pesarattu. Lightly scrape off the thick parts with a wooden spatula to the thinner parts making it even. (Check my step-by-step photos above) 14. When the pesarattu is done, you will see the edges begin to leave the pan. It also cooks to golden color & crisp on the outside. 15. Then flip and cook on the other side just for a minute or so. When you see the batter is no more raw, then flip back and roast just for a minute until crisp. Optional - Sprinkle 1 to 2 teaspoons of the fried onions here as much as you like. 16. Fold it and remove to serving plate. Do not over cook until it browns or discolors as the taste will alter. 17. To make the next pesarattu, ensure the pan is not too hot. Reduce the flame and cool down a bit. If using cast iron pan, you can rub it well with the onion and few drops of oil. 18. Stir the batter well before making the next one and then use it. 19. These pesarattu are best served hot with this ginger chutney. It is eaten alongside rava upma.

title: Phirni Recipe (Firni)
ingredients: 0.25 cup rice, 4 cups full fat milk, 7 tablespoons sugar, 1 pinch saffron, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom, 1 almonds, 1 pistachios
instructions: 1. Add rice to a colander and rinse it under running water. Drain the water completely by shaking off the excess water. 2. Spread the rice in the same colander and air dry them. You can also spread the rice on a cloth and leave until the milk is ready. 3. Meanwhile pour 3½ milk to a large pot and begin to heat stirring often so a layer of cream does not form on top. 4. Once the milk becomes hot, add the dried rice to a grinder and make a slightly coarse powder. We do not want fine flour here. If your rice is still moist it's just fine. 5. Pour ½ cup milk to the ground rice and mix well. This prevents any lumps of rice in the milk. 6. Pour this to the hot milk and mix well. Keep stirring and cook on a medium heat until the rice is fully cooked. Phirni also turns slightly thick at this stage. 7. Crush the saffron in your palm with your finger and add it. Also add the sugar and continue to cook stirring often. 8. Once the phirni has become thick, yet of pouring consistency, add cardamom powder and mix well. Dip the back of the spoon in the phirni and check the consistency. It should coat the back of the spoon. 9. Phirni turns thick upon cooling. Take down the stove and keep stirring every few minutes until it cools down. This step prevents a layer forming on top of the phirni. 10. Once it cools down, transfer to serving cups or clay bowls. Serve phirni chilled or at room temperature.

title: phirni recipe | firni recipe | phirni sweet recipe | rice phirni
ingredients: 2 tbsp basmati rice, water for soaking, 1 litre milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder elachi powder, 2 tbsp saffron milk, few dry fruits
instructions: 1. firstly, soak 2 tbsp of basmati rice for 30 minutes. 2. discard the water and blend rice to coarse paste. keep aside. 3. now in a large kadai boil 1 litre milk. 4. stir occasionally, and simmer for 10 minutes. 5. keep the flame on low or till milk thickens. 6. now add in prepared coarse rice paste. 7. stir continuously for 5 minutes. else, lumps might form. 8. simmer for another 5 minutes stirring in between. 9. boil the milk till the rice gets cooked completely. 10. further add ¼ cup of sugar and 2 tbsp saffron milk. 11. mix well till the milk thickens and turn creamy. 12. add in cardamom powder and mix well. 13. finally, serve chilled phirni garnished with few chopped nuts.

title: pin wheel sandwich recipe | pinwheel sandwich | pinwheel sandwiches
ingredients: 3 slice bread, 3 tsp butter, 0.25 cup eggless mayonnaise, 1 tsp green chutney, 0.5 capsicum, 3 slice cheese, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 3 tbsp cabbage, 0.5 carrot, 1 tbsp tomato sauce, pinch salt
instructions: 1. firstly, take 3 bread slices and trim of the sides. you can use sides to prepare bread crumbs. 2. now using a rolling pin, flatten the bread slices. 3. using butter, press and seal 3 bread slices together. 4. now spread 3 tsp of butter over the bread slices. 5. prepare a sauce by taking ¼ cup eggless mayonnaise and 1 tsp green chutney. 6. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 7. spread 3 tsp of prepared sauce over the buttered bread. 8. also, top with ½ capsicum. 9. place 3 slice cheese and sprinkle ¼ tsp pepper powder. 10. now top with 3 tbsp cabbage and ½ carrot. 11. garnish with 1 tbsp tomato sauce and pinch salt. 12. roll tight making sure all the layers are intact. 13. now wrap in cling wrap tightly. you can alternatively use aluminium foil. 14. refrigerate for 30 minutes or until it sets well. 15. after 30 minutes, slice the sandwich thickly. 16. finally, the pinwheel sandwich is ready to be served.

title: pineapple halwa recipe | pineapple delight recipe | pineapple sweet or meetha
ingredients: 2 cup pineapple, 3 cup water, 1 cup corn flour, 2 cup sugar, 0.5 tsp yellow food colour, 6 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp nuts
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixi jar take 2 cup pineapple and 1 cup juice. 2. blend smooth making sure the chunks are ground well. 3. now strain the juice extracting the pineapple juice. you can alternatively take 3 cup pineapple juice here. 4. now add 1 cup cornflour to the pineapple juice and mix using a whisk. 5. further, add 1 cup water and prepare a smooth consistency batter. keep aside. 6. in a large kadai take 2 cup sugar and 1 cup water. 7. stir well until the sugar dissolves and comes to a boil. 8. once the sugar syrup comes to a boil, add prepared pineapple mixture. 9. keep stirring continuously, keeping the flame on low to medium. 10. after 5 minutes, the mixture will start to thicken. 11. add ½ tsp yellow food colour and give a good mix. food colour is optional, it makes the delight attractive. 12. now add 2 tbsp ghee and continue to cook. 13. the ghee is absorbed completely. 14. further, add 2 tbsp more ghee and continue to cook. i have used a total of 6 tbsp of ghee in batches. 15. keep stirring until the mixture will start to hold shape and separate the pan. 16. also, add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and 2 tbsp nuts. mix well. 17. transfer the mixture to a box lined with parchment paper and topped with nuts. 18. level up making sure it is of uniform thickness. 19. rest for 2 hours or until the dessert is set well. 20. cut into the shape of your choice and garnish with chopped nuts. 21. finally, enjoy pineapple delight or pineapple halwa topped with more nuts.

title: Pineapple Juice Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups pineapple, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp black pepper, 0.5 inch ginger, 2 pineapples, 1 lemon, 1 inch ginger, 1 handful holy basil leaves, 0.25 tsp Himalayan salt
instructions: 1. Cut off both the ends of the pineapple. 2. Begin to cut down the skin of pineapple with a sharp knife from top to bottom. (Refer pics above). 3. Repeat peeling off the skin all around. 4. Keep the core intact. If juicing in a blender, cut them to cubes. 5. If making in a juicer, then cut to the size that fits in your juicer chute. 6. Add them to a blender jar or juicer chute along with all the other ingredients desired except lemon juice. 7. Blend until smooth. 8. If using a blender, then place a strainer over a bowl. 9. Pour the juice and filter it. Press down with a spoon to bring out the juice from the pulp. 10. Stir in the lemon juice. Serve pineapple juice fresh.

title: Pineapple Kesari (Pineapple Sheera)
ingredients: 1 cup semolina, 0.29166666666666663 cup ghee, 1 cup pineapple, 2.5 cups water, 0.875 cup sugar, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 pinch saffron, 1 whole cashews, 2 tablespoons raisins
instructions: 1. Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a pot. Fry cashews until light golden and stir in the raisins. Fry them until they turn plump. Remove to a plate and keep aside to garnish later. 2. Add rava/ semolina and fry on a medium heat until it turns aromatic & crunchy. Do not brown the rava, this step may take about 5 to 6 mins. Transfer to a plate and set aside. 3. To the same pot, add rest of the ghee and pineapple. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes & add sugar and saffron followed by water. 4. Bring to a rolling boil and reduce the flame to medium. With one hand, pour semolina to the pot in a slow stream, stir constantly with the other hand to incorporate semolina into the water. This avoids formation of lumps. 5. Lower the flame completely, break lumps if you find any. Cook covered until all the water is absorbed. Meanwhile cool down a little in a spoon and taste test. If you feel the need add more sugar and ghee. 6. Stir in the cardamom powder, turn off the heat & let rest for at least 5 mins before serving. 7. Garnish pineapple sheera with nuts and raisins. It is best served hot or warm.

title: pineapple kesari bath recipe | pineapple sheera | pineapple rava kesari
ingredients: 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 cashew, 2 tbsp raisins, 1 tsp ghee clarified butter, 1 cup rava semolina suji, 1 cup pineapple, 1 cup sugar, 3 water, 3 drops yellow food colour, 0.5 cup ghee clarified butter, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tbsp ghee and add 10 cashew, 2 tbsp raisins. 2. roast on low flame until they turn golden brown. keep aside. 3. in the same ghee, add 1 cup rava and roast well. 4. roast for at least 5 minutes or until it turns aromatic. keep aside. 5. in a large kadai heat 1 tsp ghee and add 1 cup pineapple, chopped. 6. saute for 3 minutes or until it turns aromatic. 7. now add ¼ cup sugar, 3 cup water and mix well. add more sugar if the pineapple is sour. 8. cover and boil for 3 minutes or until the pineapple softens. 9. add in roasted rava and stir continuously. 10. stir until the rava absorbs all the water without forming any lumps. 11. add ¾ cup sugar, 3 drops yellow food colour and mix until the sugar melts. 12. further add ½ cup ghee and mix until the ghee is well absorbed. 13. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until everything is well cooked. 14. now add roasted cashew, raisins and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. mix well. 15. finally, enjoy pineapple sheera or pineapple kesari bath garnished with nuts.

title: Pineapple Milkshake
ingredients: 2 cups pineapple, 1.25 cup chilled milk, 3 tablespoons sugar, 1 teaspoon vanilla extract, 4 scoops ice cream
instructions: 1. Use pineapple that has ripen well and is ready for consumption. I have the details on choosing perfect pineapples & preparing them on my pineapple juice post . Good tasting & flavorful pineapple is very essential for a shake. Do not use a sour fruit. 2. Peel the fruit and remove the eyes deep inside. 3. Quarter them and remove the core. Use them later for juicing. Chop the pineapple to cubes & chill them. 4. Add soaked 20 to 25 cashews or almonds. Soak cashews for 15 mins in warm water or almonds for 3 to 4 hours. Peel the almonds. Pour ¼ to ½ cup water or coconut milk. Do not add lots of liquid at one time unless you are using a high speed blender. 5. Blend until really smooth. We don’t want any grits in the shake. 6. Then add 2 cups pineapple cubes, 1 tsp vanilla extract or pineapple extract & 3 tablespoons sugar. 7. Pour more water or coconut milk. I added another 1 cup here. If you want you can also add 4 scoops of ice cream here. 8. Blend to a smooth shake. If it is too thick you may add more liquid. 9. Pour pineapple shake to glasses and serve them.

title: Pineapple Rice Recipe
ingredients: 0.875 cup precooked rice, 1.5 tbsp butter, 0.75 tbsp garlic, 9 cashews, raisins, 0.5 cup spring onions, 1 green chili, 0.5 tsp curry powder, 2 tsp soya sauce, salt, 1 cup pineapple, 1 tbsp coriander leaves fresh, oil
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a hot wok/pan. Fry cashews until light golden and add the raisins. Add garlic and fry till it turns aromatic and cashews turn golden. 2. Slide the cashews and raisins to a side. You can remove to a bowl if you want. Add spring onions, bell peppers and serrano peppers/ green chili. 3. Saute bell peppers until slightly tender yet crunchy. Add soya sauce, salt and curry powder. 4. Give a quick stir and add pineapples. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. 5. Add cooked rice, little more oil if you want and then finely chopped coriander leaves/cilantro (optional). Toss everything well and fry for 2 minutes. Taste test and add more salt if required. Serve pineapple fried rice hot.

title: Pinni Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup whole wheat flour, 3 tablespoons gond, 1 tablespoon semolina, 18 grams cashews, 18 grams almonds, 18 grams pistachios, 9 grams sweet raisins, 15 grams melon seeds, 0.5 cup ghee, 1 cup jaggery powder, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.5 teaspoon ginger
instructions: 1. Add jaggery powder to a grinder jar/ food processor and grind/process until fine. Transfer to a container and keep aside. 2. To the same jar, add nuts and pulse them only a few times - 2 to 3 times to get a coarse mixture. Transfer this to a plate. 3. Add gond to the same jar and pulse it only a few times to get a coarse mixture (not fine). 4. Pour 1 teaspoon ghee and add raisins and seeds. Fry them until the raisins become plump. 5. Add the coarse ground nuts and fry on a low to medium heat, until golden and aromatic, for a few minutes. Turn off the stove and transfer this to a plate. 6. Pour 2 tablespoons ghee and heat it. Stir in the gond and fry on a medium heat until they turn light and golden. If you find gond sticky, add another tablespoon ghee and fry. Break up any clumps. Transfer this to the same fried nuts plate. 7. Pour the rest of the ghee and stir in the semolina. On a medium-low flame, fry until it begins to sizzle and turns crunchy, for 2 to 3 mins. Add the atta and begin to saute until it turns golden and aromatic, but not burnt. If you overcook, it can burn and taste bitter. 8. Depending on the kind of kadai, flame and your room temperature, the saute time changes. It took me about 12 minutes, but this may not be the same for you. Cool little atta and taste test to make sure it is not tasting raw. 9. Turn off the heat and remove the pan to the counter. Stir in the fried nuts, seeds, raisins, ginger, cardamom and jaggery powder, (start with lower quantity). Taste test and add more jaggery as needed. 10. Grease your hands generously with ghee. Take portions of this slightly hot mixture in your hands and press down tightly in your fist to shape it like a ball, oval or oblong shape. 11. If your mixture cools down, it won't bind. In that case, simply add 2 tablespoons of hot ghee or add this to a grinder/ processor and pulse it a few times. This releases the ghee and helps to bind the pinni. You will be able to make 12 to 13 pinni. 12. Store Pinni in a air tight jar for 2 weeks at room temperature or in the refrigerator for longer.

title: Pistachio Ice Cream Recipe (Pista Ice Cream)
ingredients: 0.5 cup unsalted pistachios, 1 cup full fat milk, 2 cups heavy whipping cream, 0.75 cup sugar, 1.5 teaspoon vanilla extract
instructions: 1. If you do not have a ice cream maker, please follow this ice cream recipe without maker. Return back to the next section in this recipe 2. Chop pistachios finely and set aside some for garnishing. 3. Freeze the icecream maker bowl as per the manufacturers instructions, usually for 24 hours or so. 4. Whisk together milk, vanilla and sugar to dissolve the sugar completely. Pour cream and mix well. This has to be very cold otherwise chill this well for 12 hours in the refrigerator. 5. Start the machine and pour the mixture slowly into the churner. Churn for 15 to 20 mins, until thick. The timings may vary depending on the temperature and your ice cream machine. 6. If using a kitchenaid ice cream attachment it makes a click noise when the ice cream is thick and perfectly done. You are going to stop at this stage. It is soft set & is eatable. 7. Once the ice cream is ready add the chopped nuts. Fold gently to incorporate the nuts into the ice cream. Do not churn after adding nuts, they will get stuck at the bottom. 8. Transfer to a freezer safe container. Place a parchment paper over the ice cream and press down so there is no air in between the parchment and the ice cream. Cover and freeze until firm set or for 6 to 8 hours. 9. When you are ready to serve, take the container out of the freezer and leave it at room temperature for 5 mins. Scoop and serve pista ice cream. Garnish with your favorite toppings or with the pistachios we set aside. 10. Soak ¼ cup pistachios in half cup milk for about 1 to 2 hours so they soften. Chop the rest of the pistas & set aside. 11. Blend the soaked nuts well in a blender/ grinder, adding milk only as needed to make a very smooth & thick paste. It should be of peanut butter consistency and not too thick. 12. Scrape the sides and grind. It should have a smooth paste/butter like consistency as we don't want grits in the pista ice cream. 13. Transfer this pistachio paste to the rest of the milk, cream and sugar. Whisk until the sugar dissolves completely. If your pistachio is not smooth, simply strain the mixture before you make the ice cream as we don't want grits. 14. Pour the chilled mixture in your ice cream churner following the manufacturers instructions. It takes about 15 to 20 mins depending on your ice cream maker. 15. Gently fold the rest of the chopped pistas into the ice cream without deflating. Transfer this to a freezer safe bowl and freeze until pista ice cream is set.

title: Pithla Recipe | Maharashtrian Pitla Bhaji
ingredients: 0.5 cup besan gram flour, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 3 chilli, 1 tbsp ginger garlic, few curry leaves, 0.5 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl, take ½ cup besan, ½ tsp of turmeric, and ¼ tsp of chilli powder. 2. Add ½ cup of water and prepare a lump-free batter. 3. Now add 2 cups of water and mix well, forming a watery consistency batter. Keep aside. 4. In a kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin and a pinch of hing. 5. Add 3 chilli, 1 tbsp ginger garlic, few curry leaves and splutter the tempering. 6. Now add ½ onion and saute till the onion softens. 7. Add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, and sauté slightly. 8. Pour in the besan batter and mix continuously. 9. Stir until the mixture starts to thicken and turns glossy. 10. Cook till the mixture turns creamy. 11. Add 2 tablespoons of coriander and mix well. 12. Finally, add Besan Pithal Recipe with bhakri or roti.

title: pizza bread recipe | bread pizza recipe with instant pizza sauce
ingredients: 0.25 cup tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 3 slice bread, capsicum, tomato, onion, sweet corn, olives, jalapeno, mozzarella cheese, chilli flakes, mixed herbs, butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small bowl take ¼ cup tomato sauce, 1 tsp chilli sauce, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp mixed herbs. 2. mix well making sure everything is well combined. pizza sauce is ready. keep aside. 3. take a bread slice and spread 2 tsp of prepared pizza sauce. you can alternatively use store brought pizza sauce. 4. top with few capsicum, tomato, onion, sweet corn, olives and jalapeno. 5. also spread generous amount of mozzarella cheese. you can use cheese of your choice. 6. now sprinkle, chilli flakes and mixed herbs. 7. place the bread slice over tawa brushed with generous amount of butter. 8. cover and cook for 2 minutes on low to medium flame until the cheese melts. 9. finally, enjoy bread pizza sliced into half.

title: pizza burger recipe | burger pizza in kadai | pizza cheeseburger
ingredients: 3 tsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp chilli sauce, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 potato, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 0.25 cup cornflour, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp salt, oil, 3 burger bun, 1 cup cheese, few jalape o olives, 3 tbsp tomato sauce, 3 tbsp mayonnaise
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 3 tsp oil and saute 2 clove garlic. 2. add ½ onion and saute slightly. 3. now add 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp sweet corn and 2 tbsp capsicum. 4. saute for a minute without overcooking vegetables. 5. now add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp chilli sauce, ½ tsp chilli flakes, ½ tsp mixed herbs and ½ tsp salt. 6. mix well and cook for a minute. you can alternatively use 3 tbsp pizza sauce instead. 7. finally, pizza toppings are ready. 8. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 boiled and mashed potato, ½ tsp ginger paste and 1 chilli. 9. also, add ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp aamchur. 10. add ¼ cup cornflour, 2 tbsp coriander and ½ tsp salt. 11. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 12. form a soft dough. if there is a lot of moisture, then add 1 tsp cornflour and mix. 13. now grease hands with oil and prepare a ball sized patties. 14. shallow fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 15. fry on both the sides until the patties turn golden brown and crisp. 16. drain off over kitchen towel and aloo patties is ready. keep aside. 17. firstly, take a large burger bun and slice half at the centre. 18. spread 1 tbsp of prepared pizza toppings. 19. now add few jalapeño, olives and grate a generous amount of cheese over it. 20. place prepared aloo patties and garnish with 1 tbsp tomato sauce. 21. on another half slice of the bun, spread 1 tbsp mayonnaise. 22. cover the bun and press gently. 23. on top of burger bun, spread 1 tbsp pizza toppings and 1 tbsp cheese. 24. to bake the burger pizza in oven or kadai, make sure to preheat for 5 to 10 minutes. 25. place the burger pizza in the kadai and cover. 26. cook for 10 minutes, on medium flame. or you can bake at 180-degree celcius for 10 minutes. 27. finally, enjoy burger pizza with chips.

title: pizza cutlet recipe | cutlet pizza - kids snack recipe | pizza cutlets
ingredients: 3 potato, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 5 olives, 5 jalapeno, 0.25 cup pizza sauce, 0.5 cup cheese, 0.25 tsp mixed herbs, 0.25 tsp chilli flakes, 2 tbsp corn flour, 2 tbsp maida, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup panko breadcrumbs, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small bowl take ½ onion, ½ capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 5 olives and 5 jalapeno. 2. add ¼ cup pizza sauce and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 3. now add ½ cup cheese, ¼ tsp mixed herbs and ¼ tsp chilli flakes. 4. mix well and pizza stuffing is ready. keep aside. 5. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 potato, ½ tsp mixed herbs, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp salt. 6. also, add 2 tbsp cornflour and mix well forming a dough. corn flour helps to absorb moisture. 7. form a soft dough adding more cornflour if required. 8. now grease hand with oil and pinch a ball sized mixture. 9. flatten slightly and stuff in 2 tbsp prepared pizza stuffing. 10. seal tight forming a round ball. 11. now flatten slightly, to shape into a cutlet. you can shape the cutlet to your choice. keep aside. 12. to prepare the slurry, in a small bowl take 2 tbsp corn flour, 2 tbsp maida, ¼ tsp pepper powder, ¼ tsp salt. 13. form a smooth lump free batter adding ½ cup water. 14. now dip the prepared cutlet into cornflour slurry. 15. add coat with breadcrumbs. you can fry immediately, or freeze and prepare it up to a month. 16. shallow fry in hot oil. you can alternatively, pan fry or deep fry. 17. flip over and fry both sides until it turns golden brown. 18. finally, enjoy pizza cutlet with tomato sauce.

title: pizza paratha recipe | cheese paratha recipe | cheese stuffed paratha
ingredients: 2 cup wheat flour atta, 2.5 tsp oil, 0.25 tsp salt, water to knead, 1.5 cup mozzarella cheese, 2 tbsp onion, 0.5 capsicum, 5 olives, 5 jalapeno, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 cup wheat flour atta, 0.5 cup pizza sauce, 2 tsp chilli flakes, 2 tsp mixed herbs, oil for roasting
instructions: 1. pinch a ball sized dough, roll and flatten it. also dust with some wheat flour. 2. spread 2 tsp of pizza sauce leaving the space from sides 3. place 1 tbsp full of prepared pizza stuffing in the centre. 4. sprinkle ¼ tsp chilli flakes and ¼ tsp mixed herbs. 5. take the edge and start pleating bringing to centre. 6. also join the pleats together and secure tight. 7. sprinkle some flour and roll slightly thick. 8. place the paratha on a hot tawa and cook for a minute. 9. furthermore, when the base is partly cooked (after a minute), flip the pizza paratha. 10. also brush oil / ghee and press slightly. flip again once or twice till both the sides are cooked properly. 11. finally, serve hot pizza paratha or cheese paratha with sauce.

title: Pizza recipe | How to make pizza
ingredients: 2.5 cups all-purpose flour or, 1 teaspoon dry active yeast, 0.5 cup warm water, 1.5 tablespoon olive oil, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, 1 tablespoon cornmeal, 4 grams tomatoes, 2 garlic, 1 teaspoon red chili flakes, 1 teaspoon Italian herbs, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 teaspoon sugar, 2 grams Mozzarella cheese, 1.5 cups Mix vegetables, 0.5 teaspoon Italian herbs, 0.25 teaspoon red chili flakes
instructions: 1. Bring yeast, flour and sugar (or maple syrup) to room temperature. I generally keep all of these out of the fridge an hour before making the dough. 2. Firstly pour half cup luke warm water to a large mixing bowl. Dip your finger in the water to check if it is just warm & not hot. Hot water will kill the yeast and won’t let the pizza dough rise. 3. An ideal temperature of water is 40 to 43 C – 105 to 110 F. If you live in a very cold place, just warm up your bowl first. Next pour warm water. 4. When the pizza dough doubles in size, it will look like this. With longer proofing the dough will have lots of air pockets and will rise more. 5. Transfer it to the work surface & punch it down to deflate and remove the air bubbles. 6. Divide to 2 to 3 parts depending on the size you prefer. 7. Knead a bit and shape them to round balls. If the dough is too sticky then sprinkle some flour & knead. I made one 9 inch thin crust and then two 6 inch thick crust pizzas. Alternately you can make three 9 inch thin crust or a single 14 inch thin crust pizza with this recipe. 8. The flavor of the yeast dough gets much better with cold fermentation as it ages in the fridge. This keeps good for 3 days. To use the refrigerated dough let it to come down to room temperature before using. This may take 2 to 4 hours depending on where you live. 9. When you see the dough has relaxed and is ready to be stretched, preheat the oven to the highest temperature, 220 to 240 C or 470F for at least 25 to 30 mins. Place a baking tray or a cast iron pan in the middle rack and heat it as well in the oven. This baking tray/cast iron pan will help to achieve a golden and crisp pizza crust. To use a pizza stone refer the faqs below. If the dough is too sticky then sprinkle some flour & knead. 10. Sprinkle some cornmeal evenly on a tray. Place a dough ball and flatten it with your fingers. Add few drops of oil. 11. Begin to spread and stretch the dough on the tray with your fingers. Flatten to a 9 inch thin even base. Keep the edges slightly thicker than the centre. I do not use a rolling pin as sometimes the pizza base turns out dense as the rolling pin deflates the air bubbles. 12. Next spread pizza sauce as desired. 13. Lastly layer grated cheese. Then layer the veggies of your choice. Add more cheese on top avoiding the veggies. Sprinkle Italian herbs and chili flakes as preferred. 14. Transfer the pizza tray to the oven. Place it right over the hot baking tray/ cast iron pan in the preheated oven. Bake it for 8 to 10 minutes or until the cheese melts and the crust becomes golden and crisp. Adjust the baking time as needed. Let the temperature come down a bit and then slice it. Serve hot. 15. 2½ cups organic all-purpose flour (300 grams) or (1 cup all-purpose flour & 1½ cup wheat flour) 16. 400 grams tomatoes (fine chopped or pureed) 17. 2 garlic (cloves chopped finely) 18. 1½ cups Mix vegetables sliced or cubed (capsicum, onions, olives, tomatoes, mushrooms) 19. Pour ½ cup luke warm water to a large mixing bowl. If your mixing bowl is cold, first warm it up a bit by swirling some hot water in it. Discard the hot water and then pour luke warm water. An ideal temperature of warm water is 40 to 43 C or 105 to 110 F. 20. Dip your finger and ensure the water is warm and not hot. Then add yeast and sugar. Stir and set aside undisturbed for 10 to 12 minutes. 21. After 15 mins the yeast mixture should become bubbly or frothy indicating it is active and alive. If it doesn’t turn frothy by then, the yeast is inactive and cannot be used for the dough. In this case discard and start afresh with luke warm water, sugar and yeast. 22. When the water turns frothy, add flour, salt and oil. Next mix and make a soft dough adding more water if needed. 23. Knead the dough very well until soft & elastic for about 5 to 6 mins. 24. Let it rise at room temperature until it doubles or triples. An ideal temperature for rising dough is 26C (80 F) to 32C (90 F). Depending on the temperature it will take any where from 1 to 4 hours to rise. 25. High temperatures will kill the yeast so be cautious. Let it rise slowly for longer so it develops a good flavor. During winters, you may proof it in your instant pot on the yogurt settings (LOW). 26. Puree or chop tomatoes finely. To make sauce, heat oil in a pan and saute garlic until it smells good. Then add red chili flakes & mix. 27. Add tomatoes, salt and sugar. Stir and continue to cook covering partially as it splatters a lot. 28. Cook until the sauce thickens and is of spreading consistency (but not runny). Add herbs and crushed pepper. 29. Mix and taste the sauce. You may add more salt, herbs and pepper to suit your taste buds. Set this aside to cool. 30. Bring your choice of veggies to room temperature. Avoid using cold veggies from the refrigerator as they let out moisture on the pizza making it soggy. 31. Rinse and slice or cube the veggies. Separate the layers of onions. Avoid chopping them too small or too large. 32. If using bottled olives drain them to a small strainer. Grate or cube the cheese and set aside. 33. When the dough rises well, punch it a few times to deflate and remove air bubbles. 34. Knead it a bit and gently shape it to round balls. If the dough is too sticky then sprinkle some flour & knead. After this step you may make the pizza or cover the bowl with a cling wrap and refrigerate it for up to 3 days. 35. When you are ready to make the pizza, preheat the oven to the highest temperature, 220 to 240 C or 470F for at least 25 to 30 mins. Place a baking tray or a cast iron pan in the middle rack and heat it as well in the oven. 36. Sprinkle some cornmeal over the pizza tray. Place the pizza dough over the tray and flatten it slightly. 37. Add few drops of oil on the dough. Begin to spread and stretch the dough on the tray with your fingers. Flatten to a 9 inch thin even base. Keep the edges slightly thicker than the center. (with this recipe you can make three 9 inch thin crust pizzas or one 14 inch thin crust.) 38. Spread the pizza sauce as desired, leaving about ¼ inch on the edges. Layer the grated cheese and then the veggies. Lastly sprinkle Italian herbs and red chili flakes. To finish off top with more cheese if desired avoiding over the veggies. 39. Place the pizza tray right over the hot baking tray or cast iron pan in your oven. Bake for 10 to 12 mins or until the pizza base becomes crisp, golden and cheese turns slightly golden. 40. If your cheese doesn’t turn golden, you can move it to the top rack and bake for another 1 to 2 mins. Slice and serve pizza hot or warm. 41. If making a thick base & you own a small oven, then put the base in the oven without the sauce and toppings. Bake it for 5 to 7 mins. Later top with sauce, cheese, veggies and herbs. 42. title: Pizza Sandwich Recipe | Grilled Veg Pizza Sandwiches
ingredients: 0.25 cup paneer, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp tomato, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp pizza sauce, 2 tbsp eggless mayonnaise, 0.5 cup cheese, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, bread slices, butter
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a small bowl take ¼ cup paneer, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp tomato, 2 tbsp sweet corn and 2 tbsp capsicum. 2. Also add 2 tbsp pizza sauce, and 2 tbsp eggless mayonnaise and mix well. 3. Add in ½ cup cheese, ½ tsp mixed herbs and ½ tsp chilli flakes. Mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 4. Take the bread slice, and spread butter on it. 5. Stuff in prepared pizza stuffing and top with a generous amount of cheese. 6. Cover the bread slice and spread butter on top. 7. Grill the bread on the pan until the sandwich turns golden and crisp from both sides. 8. Finally, cut in half and enjoy Pizza Sandwich.

title: pizza sauce recipe | homemade pizza sauce recipe
ingredients: 6 ripped red tomatoes, 2 tsp oil, 3 cloves garlic, 0.25 onion, 2 tbsp basil leaves, 0.75 tsp sugar, 0.75 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, salt to taste, 0.5 tsp oregano, 0.25 tsp chilli flakes, 1 tbsp tomato sauce, 0.5 tbsp butter
instructions: 1. firstly, slit x on bottom of tomatoes. 2. furthermore, add 6 tomatoes (slitted at bottom) into boiling water and blanch. 3. close the vessel and boil for 3-4 minutes or till the skin starts to leave from tomatoes. 4. further, peel the skin of tomatoes once they cooled completely. 5. then take 3 peeled tomatoes and blend to smooth purees. 6. chop other 3 tomatoes discarding the seeds. 7. transfer to the plate and keep aside. 8. furthermore, heat oil in a non stick tawa or heavy bottomed pan. 9. add chopped garlic and saute till they turn slightly golden brown. 10. further, saute chopped onions till they change colour. 11. then add chopped - deseeded tomatoes and cook completely. 12. mash in between so that it turns mushy soon. 13. also add prepared tomato puree (3 balanced tomatoes). 14. mix well and add basil, sugar, chilli powder, oregano, chilli flakes and salt. 15. mix well and get the sauce to a boil. 16. further, cover and simmer the sauce for atleast 5 minutes or till the sauce turns thick. 17. also add tomato sauce and butter. 18. finally, mix well and pizza sauce is ready to prepare pizzas or pastas.

title: Pizza Sauce Recipe from Fresh Tomatoes
ingredients: 5 grams grams tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon red chili flakes, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 2 tablespoons Olive oil, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon oregano, 0.625 teaspoon basil, 0.375 teaspoon crushed black pepper
instructions: 1. Rinse and dice 500 grams tomatoes (1.1 pounds). Add them to a blender. 2. Make a smooth puree. 3. Heat a heavy bottom pan with 2 tablespoons of olive oil. When the oil becomes hot, stir in ¾ tablespoon finely chopped garlic. 4. Fry the garlic for a minute or two until it turns fragrant. Stir in ½ to 1 teaspoon red chilli flakes (optional). 5. Lower the heat and let the chilli flakes fry in the oil for 30 seconds. 6. Pour the tomato puree and stir. 7. Quickly cover the pan as it begins to splash. Cook for about 15 minutes until the tomato puree turns slightly thick. 8. Next add 1 teaspoon sugar & ½ to ¾ teaspoon salt. Sugar helps to balance the flavors. 9. Add ¼ to ½ teaspoon black pepper, ½ teaspoon oregano and ½ to ¾ teaspoon basil. You can add more herbs to your liking. 10. Give a good mix and continue to simmer until pizza sauce cooks down to a thick yet spreading consistency. This takes another 5 minutes. Turn off the stove. Taste test and add more salt if required. 11. Cool this completely and store in an air tight clean dry glass jar. It keeps good for 2 weeks in the refrigerator & for 3 months in the freezer.

title: Podi Idli Recipe (Podi Mini Idli)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons peanuts, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 tablespoon urad dal, 3 red chilies, 1.5 teaspoons sesame seeds, 1.5 teaspoons copra, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon tamarind, 2 pinches jaggery, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 9 idlis, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 1 pinch mustard seeds
instructions: 1. If you are new to making idli, then you can follow this Soft idli recipe. 2. While the idlis steam, dry roast peanuts along with red chilies until light golden. 3. Add urad dal and chana dal. Roast on a medium flame until they turn golden. Make sure peanuts, urad dal and chana dal all are roasted well to deep golden. 4. Add sesame seeds, coconut, cumin & curry leaves. Dry roast until the leaves turn crisp and sesame seeds smell good. 5. Cool these and powder finely along with enough salt, tamarind (optional) and jaggery (optional) if using. Check the salt and add more if needed. 6. Remove the idlis from the mould. Allow them to cool a bit. 7. Pour ghee or oil to a hot pan. Add mustard seeds if using. Let them splutter. 8. Add idlis and then switch off the stove. 9. Sprinkle podi over the idli and toss gently. Add more or less podi & ghee as desired. Coat the idlis well with podi. 10. Serve podi idli warm.

title: Poha Chivda Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams poha, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.25 cup peanuts, 0.25 cup cashews, 2 tablespoons roasted gram, 2 tablespoons raisins, 0.25 cup coconut, 4 medium garlic, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 3 green chilies, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds
instructions: 1. Heat a wide and large heavy bottom pan. 2. Add 1½ cups of poha. Do not add a lot of them at one time. You must be able to spread them evenly in the pan. 3. Roast them on a medium heat stirring often & gently till they turn crunchy & light. They barely take few minutes to turn crisp. Check the step-by-step pictures above. 4. Transfer them to a large wide bowl for cooling. Fry all of the poha the same way in 2 to 3 batches depending on the size of your pan. Scroll down for tempering instructions. 5. Heat 1 cup oil on a medium heat in a small deep fry pan. You will need a fine hole skimmer to remove the fried poha. Keep this ready. 6. When the oil is hot, test by dropping a flake of poha. It has to rise immediately without browning. This is the right temperature. Pour 1 cup poha in a stream to the oil. Fry them stirring constantly. 7. They puff up immediately. Then remove them using a fine hole skimmer or a perforated ladle. 8. Transfer them to a colander lined with kitchen tissues. Repeat frying the poha in batches. Ensure the oil is not extremely hot before frying the next batch. It has to be hot enough else they will soak up oil. 9. This deep fried oil is not good to use, so make tempering with fresh oil. Scroll down for tempering instructions. 10. To Roast in Built-in Oven: Spread thin poha in a single layer on a baking tray. Preheat the oven to 350 F or 175C. Bake for 14 to 15 mins, stirring them with a spatula, halfway after 7 mins. Remove them when they are crispy. Thicker poha takes a few mins longer. 11. To Roast in Medium to Small OTG: You don't need to preheat the oven. Roast thick poha for 12 to 14 mins, thin poha for 5 to 6 mins at 250 F or 120 C. Shake or stir the poha halfway. 12. If you are using paper thin flakes, then cut down the roasting time even more. 13. Pour 1 tbsp oil to a small pan and heat it. Add peanuts and roast them on a medium heat until lightly golden. 14. Next add cashews and fry until lightly golden. Add coconut and roasted gram. Fry briefly until the coconut turns light and golden. 15. Remove the fried ingredients with a spoon and add to the roasted poha. Retain the excess oil in the pan. 16. Pour another 1 to 2 tbsps of oil to the same pan. Add green chilies, garlic and curry leaves. Fry until the garlic turns crisp and golden. 17. Then add cumin seeds (optional) and raisins. Soon the raisins will swell up. 18. Turn off the heat and add hing, red chilli powder, salt, turmeric and jaggery. Give a quick stir to melt the jaggery. 19. Transfer this to the poha. Immediately add some roasted poha over the tempering. Begin to coat the poha while the tempering is still hot. This helps to coat all of the poha well with the tempering. Mix all of the chivda well. 20. Cool completely & store poha chivda in an air tight jar.

title: Poha Chivda Recipe | Poha Namkeen Mixture 2 Ways
ingredients: 4 grams thick poha, oil, 0.5 cup peanut, 0.5 cup roasted gram dal putani, 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp cashew, 3 tbsp raisins, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 3 red chilli, few curry leaves, 1 tbsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp kala namak, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 2 tbsp sugar, 2 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp hing, 3 cup thin poha aval avalakki beaten rice, 2 tbsp oil, 0.25 cup peanuts, 2 tbsp gram dal pottukadalai putani dariya, 1 cashew kaju, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 2 red chilli, pinch hing asafoetida, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tsp powdered sugar
instructions: 1. Firstly, fry 400 grams thick poha in batches. make sure to fry in hot oil, or else the poha will absorb oil. 2. Stir and fry uniformly until the poha turns crispy. 3. Drain off over tissue paper and keep aside. Fry all the poha in the same way. 4. Now fry ½ cup peanut, ½ cup roasted gram dal, 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp cashew, 3 tbsp raisins, and 2 tbsp dry coconut. 5. Transfer to the plate allowing it to absorb excess oil. 6. Also, fry 3 dried red chilli and a few curry leaves. Keep aside. 7. To prepare spice mix, in a bowl take 1 tbsp chilli powder, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp kala namak, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 2 tbsp sugar, 2 tsp salt and ½ tsp hing. Mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 8. Now in a large bowl or vessel take fried poha. Add in prepared spice mix and mix gently. 9. Also, add fried nuts and mix well. 10. Finally, enjoy Crispy Poha Chivda Recipe for up to a month when stored in an airtight container. 11. Firstly, in a tawa dry roast 3 cup of thin poha on medium flame. alternatively, microwave for 1 minute or keep under a hot sun for 30 minutes. 12. Roast until poha turns crisp. keep aside. 13. Now in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and roast ¼ cup peanuts to golden and crunch. 14. Also roast 2 tbsp pottukadalai, 10 cashew, 2 tbsp dry coconut on low flame. 15. Now add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 2 dried red chilli, pinch hing, and a few curry leaves. 16. Saute and splutter the tempering. 17. Now add ¼ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp salt, and 1 tsp powdered sugar. 18. Furthermore, add roasted poha and mix gently. 19. Finally, serve Crispy Poha Chivda Recipe or store it in an air-tight container for a month.

title: Poha Cutlet Recipe | Veg Poha Patties
ingredients: 1 cup poha avalakki, 3 potato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp kala namak, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 2 chilli, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, bread crumbs, oil, 3 tbsp maida, 3 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp salt, water
instructions: 1. Firstly, rinse 1 cup of poha and rest till the poha softens. 2. Transfer the soaked poha to the large plate. 3. Add 3 boiled and mashed potatoes. 4. Also, add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp kala namak, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp chilli flakes. 5. Further add 2 chilli, ½ carrot, ½ capsicum, ½ onion, 2 tbsp coriander, ½ tsp ginger garlic paste. 6. Mash and mix well, making sure all the spices are well combined. 7. Now prepare the maida-corn flour batter by mixing 3 tbsp maida, 3 tbsp corn flour, ½ tsp salt in ½ cup water. 8. Stir well to make a smooth, flowing consistency batter. 9. Prepare small ball-sized patties, greasing your hands with oil. Greasing oil prevents the dough from sticking. 10. Now dip in prepared maida-corn flour slurry. 11. Further coat with bread crumbs on all sides. Use panko bread crumbs / crushed corn flakes as they give a nice texture. 12. Now deep fry, or pan fry in hot oil. Alternatively, bake in a preheated oven at 180 degrees Celsius for 12 minutes. 13. Fry on medium flame, flipping occasionally till it turns golden brown. 14. Finally, serve Poha Cutlets Recipe with mint dip or tomato sauce.

title: Poha Cutlet Recipe | Veg Poha Patties
ingredients: 1 cup poha avalakki, 3 potato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp kala namak, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 2 chilli, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 capsicum, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, bread crumbs, oil, 3 tbsp maida, 3 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp salt, water

title: Poha Dosa Recipe | Instant Aval Dosa - Soft & Spongy
ingredients: 1 cup poha aval, 1 cup rava suji, 1 cup curd, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp baking powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take 1 cup poha, 1 cup rava, 1 cup curd and ½ tsp salt. 2. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 3. cover and rest for 20 minutes. 4. after 20 minutes, the rava has soaked well, transfer into the mixer jar. 5. add 1 cup water and grind to smooth batter. 6. transfer the batter to a large bowl. 7. add ½ tsp baking powder and mix well. 8. once the batter turns frothy, pour the batter over the hot tawa. 9. cook until the top of the dosa is cooked completely. i have not used any oil or ghee while roasting the dosa. 10. finally, enjoy poha dosa with coconut jaggery chutney.

title: poha idli recipe | instant poha rava idli | avalakki idli
ingredients: 1 cup poha aval avalakki atukulu flattened rice, 1 cup curd, 1.5 cup rice rava idli rava, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 0.75 tsp eno fruit salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small mixi grind 1 cup poha to a coarse powder. i have used thick variety poha, if you are using thin then increase the quantity of poha. 2. add 1 cup curd and mix well. make sure to poha absorbs curd. 3. further, add 1½ cup rice rava and mix well. you can alternatively, use upma rava and mix well. 4. now add ¾ tsp salt and 1 cup water. 5. mix well making sure everything is well combined adding water as required. 6. cover and rest for 30 minutes or until the rava and poha absorb water. 7. after 30 minutes, mix gently, making sure the rava has absorbed water. 8. now add ½ cup water and mix well adjusting the consistency. 9. just before steaming, add ¾ tsp eno and mix gently until the batter turns frothy. 10. now pour the batter into greased idli plate. 11. steam the idli for 15 minutes or until the idli is cooked well. 12. finally, enjoy poha idli with coriander mint chutney.

title: Poha Nasta Recipe | Healthy Steamed Vada - Perfect Breakfast Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup poha, 0.5 cup rava suji, 1 tsp cumin, 0.75 tsp salt, 1 cup curd, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 chilli, few curry leaves, pinch hing, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixer jar take 1 cup poha and ½ cup rava. 2. grind to a coarse powder. 3. transfer the powdered poha suji mixture to a large bowl. 4. add 1 tsp cumin, ¾ tsp salt and 1 cup curd. 5. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 6. now add ½ cup water or as required and form a dough texture batter. 7. cover and rest for 10 minutes allowing suji and poha to absorb well. 8. add 2 tbsp coriander, 1 chilli, few curry leaves, pinch hing and ½ tsp chilli flakes. 9. mix well combining everything well. 10. with your wet hands, pinch a ball sized dough and shape it. 11. flatten it slightly and make a hole in centre. 12. place in a steamer and steam for 15 minutes. 13. finally, enjoy steam vada with spicy chutney.

title: poha pakoda recipe | poha pakora recipe | easy & crisp veg snack
ingredients: 1 cup poha aval avalakki flattened rice, 0.5 onion, 1 potato aloo, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp peanuts, 3 tbsp besan gram flour, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, wash 1 cup thin poha with enough water. do not soak thin poha, just rinse in water. 2. drain off the water and rest poha for 15 minutes so that water is dropped down completely. 3. now transfer the washed poha into a large mixing bowl. 4. also add ½ onion, 1 boiled potato, ½ tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander and few curry leaves. 5. additionally add ¼ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp ajwain, ½ tsp aamchur and ½ tsp salt. 6. further, add 2 tbsp peanuts and 3 tbsp besan. 7. mix well by mashing potato. 8. also, add more besan if required to form a dough. 9. now grease hand with oil and prepare small ball sized. 10. deep fry in hot oil stirring occasionally. 11. fry until the pakora turns golden and crisp. 12. finally, enjoy poha pakoda with tomato sauce.

title: Poha Recipe (Indian Breakfast)
ingredients: 1.5 cups poha, 1 medium onion, 2.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 medium potato cubed to inch, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 3.5 tablespoons peanuts, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, Lemon juice as needed, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. To make poha, choose medium to thick flakes. Avoid thin flakes. Add them to a bowl or a strainer or colander. 2. Pour 4 to 5 cups of water and rinse them quickly. Drain the water completely. I rinse them twice. 3. Cover and set aside to soften. Meanwhile prepare the veggies. 4. Press a few flakes of poha in between your thumb and forefinger, they should be soft & break easily. If they don't then sprinkle little water mix and cover. 5. When they turn soft enough, add some sugar and salt. Run your fingers through the poha to break the lumps & spread salt. 6. Heat a pan with oil and fry the peanuts on a medium heat until golden and crunchy. 7. Remove the peanuts to a plate and set aside. 8. To the same pan add mustard and cumin. 9. When they begin to pop, add hing, onions, curry leaves and green chilies. 10. Saute until the onions turn lightly pink. 11. Optional - Add cubed potatoes or mix veggies and saute for a min. Cover and cook on a low heat until soft and cooked completely. If needed can sprinkle some water and cook. Once done, saute for another minute. 12. Add turmeric and poha. Mix well and cover the pan. 13. Cook on a very low heat until it turns hot. If you feel poha is too dry, you may sprinkle some water and continue to steam. 14. Turn off the heat when poha has softened & become hot. Remove from heat. Taste test and add more salt if needed & squeeze lemon juice. 15. Garnish poha with coriander leaves & roasted peanuts. You may top with sev or farsan or fresh coconut. Serve it hot. 16. Add 2 cups of poha to a wide plate along with ½ cup fine chopped onions, 1 cup fresh grated coconut, ½ teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon fine sugar and ¼ cup fine chopped coriander leaves. 17. Squeeze the juice out of 1 medium lemon and mix all of them with your hand. You may optionally add 1 to 2 tbsps of coconut water or plain water to soften them little more. 18. Cover and set aside for 5 minutes until they soften. Onions and coconut will release some moisture and make the dadpe pohe soft. 19. After 5 minutes, pour 1 tablespoon oil to a small tadka pan and heat it. 20. Add ¼ to ½ teaspoon mustard seeds, 2 sprigs of torn curry leaves, 1 to 2 chopped green chilies. Fry all of them until the curry leaves turn crisp. 21. Then add a pinch of hing and 1/8 teaspoon of turmeric. Pour this to the flattened rice mixture so it absorbs all of the flavors. 22. Meanwhile add peanuts to the same pan and roast them until golden and crunchy. Once done set these aside to cool. 23. Mix the poha well and taste test. If needed add more salt and lemon juice. If the poha is not soft to your liking you may add more coconut water or plain water and mix. 24. Garnish dadpe pohe with fried peanuts.

title: Poha Recipe | Maharastrian Aloo Kanda Pohe
ingredients: 2.5 cup poha, 2 tbsp oil, 2 tbsp peanut, 1 potato, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 lemon, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, place a sieve in a bowl and rinse 2½ cups of poha till the water runs clean. Do not soak the poha in water. 2. Keep the poha aside for 10 minutes, and it will become non-sticky and soft. 3. In a wok heat 2 tbsp oil and fry 2 tbsp peanut till they turn crunchy. Keep aside. 4. Also, fry 1 potato till it turns golden and crisp. Keep aside. 5. In the same oil, add ½ tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves and splutter the tempering. 6. Add 1 onion, and saute till the onions softens. 7. Now add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp sugar and saute till the spices turn aromatic. 8. Further add fried peanut and potato and mix well. 9. Now add in poha and mix gently. 10. Also add ½ lemon and 2 tbsp coriander. Mix gently. 11. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the poha is cooked fully. 12. Finally, enjoy Kanda Poha Recipe with chivda.

title: poha vada recipe | aval vadai | flattened rice vada | aval masala vadi
ingredients: 1 cup poha aval, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 0.75 tsp salt, 0.5 onion, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp rice flour, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, place a sieve and rinse 1 cup poha with enough water. 2. transfer the washed poha into a large bowl. 3. add ¼ cup curd, ¾ tsp salt and mix well. 4. rest for 10 minutes or until poha softens well. 5. mix and mash well making sure the poha is softened. 6. add ½ onion, 1 chilli, ½ tsp ginger paste, few curry leaves and 2 tbsp coriander. 7. mix well forming a dough. 8. further, add 2 tbsp rice flour and mix well absorbing excess water. 9. pinch a ball sized dough and flatten slightly. 10. deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 11. stir occasionally, making sure the vada is cooked uniformly. 12. fry until it turns golden brown and crisp. 13. drain off over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 14. finally, enjoy poha vada with tomato sauce.

title: Pomegranate Juice Recipe
ingredients: 1 kg pomegranate, 1 medium lemon, 0.125 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Before you begin deseeding a pomegranate, keep in mind that pomegranate juice stains. If you don’t want your kitchen to look like the set of a horror movie, be careful of what you’re wearing and the materials you’re using. Plastic cutting boards might get stained, so wooden cutting boards are recommended. Use vinegar or lemon juice to clean your wooden cutting board if things get messy and pink. 2. Luckily, this deseeding technique should help keep kitchen mess to a minimum. Once you know the technique, you can use pomegranate arils for all kinds of good cooking. 3. Wash pomegranates under running water. Cut off the crown, the top portion of the pomegranate. 4. Similarly cut down the bottom part of the fruit. This loosens the rind as it looses the strength on both the parts. 5. Remove the top of the pomegranate by cutting a shallow, angled circle around the crown. Wiggle it loose and discard. Make 4 shallow cuts as you see in the picture. 6. Hold 2 of the parts/segments in each hand and gently pull out to separate them. Without messing up, the fruit gets divided to 4 segments/parts. Pomegranates stain the work tops and even the clothes. A few disposable tissues or a kitchen cloth is helpful. 7. Separate each segment. 8. Deseed to a large bowl, they come off easily. Or You may immerse one segment at a time in a large bowl of clean water and remove the pomegranate arils under the water. Once you are done with all the segments, drain the water and remove any rinds. 9. Discard all of the yellow and white membranes from the bowl. 10. Making pomegranate juice in a blender is surprisingly easy. Add the arils to the blender. 11. Add a pinch of salt and lemon juice if you prefer. 12. Pulse briefly – 10 to 15 seconds, depending on the strength of your blender. You want to make sure the seeds are still mostly intact but the juice around the seeds is released. Crushing the seeds will make the juice gritty and requires to be filtered in a fine strainer like the one I used below. 13. Once the arils are crushed, pour the blender’s contents through a very fine mesh strainer into a bowl to separate the seeds and pulp from the juice. It is important to use a fine strainer if you do not want the juice to be gritty. 14. Don’t be afraid to press the pulp against the strainer with the back of a spoon or spatula to squeeze out every bit of fresh juice. 15. Because I crushed the seeds pomegranate juice looks frothy. If you want a clear juice, strain it again through a fine strainer or a thin cheese cloth. Consume pomegranate juice fresh before it begins to oxidize. Adding ice cubes dilutes the juice and reduces the taste & the nutrition values as well.

title: Poori Masala
ingredients: 45 grams potatoes, 1 cup water, 1 cup sliced onions, 0.625 cup tomatoes, 0.875 tablespoon ginger, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.75 tablespoon chana dal, 0.75 tablespoon urad dal, 1 sprig curry leaves, 3 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 pinch hing, 0.375 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.875 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.1875 teaspoon turmeric
instructions: 1. Wash potatoes under running water. Peel and dice them to 1 inch. Add them to a pressure cooker or regular pot and pour 1 cup water. 2. Pressure cook for 1 whistle on a medium flame, until soft and slightly mushy. If cooking in a pot, bring the water to a rolling boil on a high heat, reduce and cook until they are soft. If required add more boiling water. 3. While the potatoes cook, slice the onions, chop the tomatoes, ginger & green chilies, 4. Pour oil to a pan and heat it. Add cumin, mustard, chana dal and urad dal. Fry until the lentils turn golden and aromatic. 5. Stir in the curry leaves and green chilies. Fry till leaves turn crisp. Sprinkle hing. 6. Add onions and ginger. Saute them on a medium heat until they lose the raw flavor & turn pink. 7. Add tomatoes, turmeric and salt. Cook stirring often until the tomatoes turn mushy and the raw smell has gone completely. 8. Transfer the cooked potatoes along with the water (potato stock). Mash them slightly to make it a thick curry. If required pour some hot water to bring it to a consistency. 9. Taste test and add more salt and green chili if required. 10. Mix well. Cook covered for 3 to 4 minutes until the curry reaches a thick consistency. 11. Sprinkle coriander leaves. Serve poori masala with hot Pooris. If you want you may squeeze some lemon juice. 12. Pour oil to the steel insert of the Instant pot. Press the saute button. When the oil turns hot, add mustard, cumin, chana dal and urad dal. 13. Saute until lightly golden. Stir in curry leaves, ginger, green chilies & hing. 14. Add onions and saute for 2 to 3 mins until transparent. Add tomatoes, salt and turmeric. 15. Saute until the tomatoes lose their raw flavor for 2 to 3 mins. Press cancel. 16. Add potatoes and pour 1 cup water. Deglaze and pressure cook for 4 mins. Release the pressure immediately. Mash some of the potatoes to thicken the curry. (check pics in the post). Taste test and add more salt if required. 17. Poori masala is not a dry curry so it should be like a thick gravy consistency. If the consistency is too thick pour some hot water and mix well. 18. Sprinkle coriander leaves and lemon juice. Serve with Poori.

title: poori recipe | puri bhaji recipe | puri recipe | how to make poori | poori baji
ingredients: 2 cup wheat flour, 1 tbsp rava semolina suji, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, water, oil, 3 potato, 1.5 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 2 red chilli, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 1 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup wheat flour, 1 tbsp rava, ½ tsp sugar, ½ tsp salt and 2 tsp oil. 2. crumble and mix well making sure the flour is moist. 3. now add water and knead the dough. 4. knead the dough tight adding water as required. 5. now pinch a large ball size dough and roll to form a log. 6. cut into pieces and roll them to small balls. 7. place the balls into a small bowl and 1 tsp oil. grease the ball to prevent from drying. 8. now take a ball and roll using a rolling pin. 9. roll to slightly thick thickness. 10. drop the rolled dough into the hot oil. 11. press until the puri puffs up and splash oil to puff up fully. 12. flip over and fry until it turns golden brown. 13. finally, drain off the poori and is ready to enjoy with aloo bhaji. 14. firstly, take 3 cubed potato into the pressure cooker. 15. add 1½ cup water, ½ tsp salt and pressure cook for 4 whistles. keep aside. 16. now in a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 2 dried red chilli, pinch hing and few curry leaves. splutter the tempering. 17. add 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 1 onion and saute until onions change colour slightly. 18. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp salt and saute for a minute. 19. add pressure cooked aloo and mix well adjusting consistency as required. 20. cover and cook for 5 minutes or until flavours are absorbed. 21. now add 1 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 22. finally, enjoy aloo bhaji with hot and puffy poori.

title: Poori Recipe, How to Make Puri
ingredients: 1 cup wheat flour, 0.75 teaspoon semolina, 0.75 teaspoon oil, 0.25 cup water, 0.125 teaspoon salt, oil
instructions: 1. Mix together flour, salt, rava and oil in a large mixing bowl. 2. Pour ¼ cup water. Begin to make a little stiff & tight dough, adding more water as needed. (check video) 3. Smear ½ tsp oil, knead lightly and smoothen the ball. 4. Do not knead it a lot otherwise poori will turn soft and absorb oil while frying. 5. Keep the dough covered all the time until the last poori is rolled. Do not rest the dough for more than 3 to 5 minutes. The longer it rests quicker the puffed puri will collapse. They will turn oily after frying. 6. Divide the dough to 8 to 10 portions. Avoid kneading them. 7. Smoothen all the balls by rolling them lightly in between your palms. Keep them covered in the bowl. 8. Drizzle a few drops of oil on the rolling area and spread it. 9. Smear oil over the dough ball & flatten it. 10. Begin to roll it evenly from the center to a round puri. 11. It should not be too thick or thin. It must be moderately thick which helps the puri to puff. 12. Make 5 to 6 puris and set them aside. Do not stack them. 13. Begin to heat the oil on a medium high flame and continue to make rest of the pooris. 14. The oil has to be hot enough. Check by dropping a small portion of dough to the pan. It has to rise immediately without browning. 15. If it browns quickly then reduce the flame slightly and allow the temperature to come down. 16. Slide the puri carefully to the hot oil. Do not disturb until it rises half way to the surface. 17. Quickly but gently begin to press down with a perforated ladle. This helps the poori to puff well. 18. Once puri puffs, flip it carefully. Fry on the other side as well until golden and crisp. 19. You can fry flipping too and fro once more until slightly crisp & to get the desired color - light golden to dark golden. 20. Remove the puri to a steel colander. Fry the next poori the same way. 21. Make sure the oil is hot enough, but not smoking before frying the next puri. Serve poori with potato curry, chutney or kurma.

title: Poornam Boorelu (Poornalu, Burelu Recipe)
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 1.75 to 2 cups water, 1.5 cup jaggery, 2 tbsps coconut, 0.25 tsp green cardamom powder, 0.75 cup urad dal, 1.25 cup raw rice, 1 pinch salt, Oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. Wash and Soak separately urad dal and rice in water for 4 to 5 hours. 2. Option 1 - Cook dal in a pot (stovetop): Rinse chana dal well thrice and soak in water for a minimum of 2 hours and a maximum of 3 to 4 hours. Later drain the water and pour fresh water as mentioned in the recipe and bring to a rolling boil. Cook uncovered until soft but not mushy & they should still hold their shape. (add more hot water if required or evaporate the excess moisture while cooking). 3. Option 2 - Cook dal in a pressure cooker: Rinse the dal and add to a pressure cooker and cook for 5 to 6 whistles or for 10 mins in a instant pot. Lentils must be soft, hold shape but not mushy. If there is excess water in the dal, strain it. 4. Cool down, mash or blend the dal to a smooth or coarse mixture in a blender. I just mashed it. Put back the dal to the same pot. 5. Optional step - Skip this if your jaggery is clean & free from debris. To a large bowl, add grated jaggery and 45 ml of water (about 3 tbsps). Melt jaggery by placing this bowl on a low flame or microwave for 1.5 minutes. keep this aside. 6. If using sugar, add it to the mashed dal. If using jaggery , filter the prepared syrup to the mashed dal. Discard the debris. Mix them well. Add fresh coconut if using and cardamom powder. 7. Cook till the water evaporates completely and the dal looks dry and comes together like a mass. When it cools down, make 14 to 16 equal sized balls. 8. Drain the rice in a colander. There must be no dripping water. 9. Add urad dal, little salt and little water to a blender. Blend till frothy or smooth. Adding too much water will make the batter runny, so use as needed only. 10. Next add rice to the batter and repeat blending. Keep the batter slightly coarse , this helps the outer covering to turn crusty. The consistency should be like thick dosa batter. If the batter turns runny, add little dry rice flour. 11. Heat oil for deep frying. Check if the oil is hot by dropping a small portion of batter, it must come up. 12. Next drop one ball each time to the batter, take a tablespoon and coat it well and swipe off the excess drippings. Then drop it in the oil. 13. Do not disturb for a min or more to prevent breaking. Fry till golden on a medium heat. Stir them in between. Fry them in batches till you finish off all the balls. 14. Repeat frying poornam boorelu for a min or two for extra crust. 15. You can make punugulu with the left over batter. 16. Cool them completely and Offer Poornam Boorelu to Goddess Lakshmi. For serving - Heat up little ghee in a small bowl, make a small hole in the center. Pour little ghee and enjoy.

title: poornam boorelu recipe | how to make poornalu recipe | purnam burelu
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 2 tsp ghee, 2 cup water, 1 cup jaggery, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 0.75 cup urad dal, 1 cup rice, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup chana dal for 2 hours. make sure to rinse the dal and then soak. 2. drain off the water and transfer it to the cooker. 3. add 1 tsp ghee and 2 cup water. 4. pressure cook for 2 whistles or until the dal is cooked well. 5. mash the dal to smooth paste. you can also grind the dal in mixi if you prefer. 6. transfer the mashed dal into a large kadai. 7. add 1 cup jaggery and start to cook on medium flame. 8. the jaggery starts to melt and combines well with mashed dal. 9. continue to cook until the mixture starts to hold the shape. 10. now add 1 tsp ghee and cook for a minute, or until the mixture starts to separate the pan. if the mixture is undercooked, then the stuffing will be sticky and will be difficult to make a ball. 11. transfer to the plate and cool slightly. make sure not to overcook, else the stuffing turns hard. 12. now add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 13. grease hands with ghee and makes ball sized stuffing. you can store this stuffing in the fridge and prepare burelu when required. 14. firstly, in a bowl soak ¾ cup urad dal and 1 cup rice for 5 hours. 15. drain off the water and transfer it to the mixi jar. you can also grind the batter in the grinder. 16. now adding water as required, prepare a smooth thick batter. 17. transfer the batter to a large bowl. make sure the consistency of the batter is like idli batter, if the batter is thin then will be difficult to coat. 18. also, add 1 tsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. mix well. adding sugar will give a nice colour to burelu. 19. now dip the purnam into the batter and drop it into the hot oil. 20. deep fry on medium flame cooking unifromly. 21. fry until the burelu turns golden brown and crisp. 22. drain off the poornalu over the kitchen towel to get rid of excess oil. 23. finally, boorelu or burelu or poornalu is ready for prasadam.

title: Poornam Kudumulu (Sihi Kadubu)
ingredients: 0.75 cup rice flour, 0.75 cup water, 1 teaspoon ghee, 0.5 cup channa dal, 1 cup water, 0.75 cup jaggery, 1.5 tablespoons raw coconut
instructions: 1. On a low flame, melt jaggery with 1½ to 2 tbsp water until completely liquidy. Place a strainer over a bowl and filter it. Keep this aside. I omitted this step since my jaggery was clean, free from debris and stones. 2. Wash and cook chana dal with water until fully cooked but not mushy. I cook half cup dal with 1 cup water and evaporate the excess at the end. 3. When pressed in between your fingers, the lentils should get mashed easily. 4. Cool the dal and add to a grinder. Alternately you may mash it with a masher. 5. You should have a coarse mixture as seen in the picture. 6. Add the dal, jaggery and coconut to the same pot. 7. Begin to cook the poornam till it looks dry and becomes a mass. During this step, jaggery will melt and makes the mixture gooey but as you cook it will thicken. When thick, turn off and add the cardamom powder. Keep it aside to cool. 8. Take small portions of the poornam and roll to balls if you want to make round balls. The balls have be non-sticky and hold good round shape. If not it means the purnam is not cooked yet. 9. Grease your fingers and make a thin puri out of the dough. Place little poornam in one half of the puri leaving the edges. 10. Join the edges to seal the poornam. If required dip your fingers in water and press the edges. You can also make a design with your fingers or use a greased fork to press lightly on the edges. 11. You can also make balls, by rolling the dough around the poornam. 12. Bring a pressure pan or cooker or steamer to a boil with enough water. Grease a steam basket and place the prepared kadubu in the basket. When the water begins to boil rapidly, place the basket in the steamer. Steam for 8 to 10 minutes depending on the size of your steamer.

title: Popcorn Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 1 lb chicken breast, 0.25 cup cornstarch, 2 tablespoons buttermilk, 2 egg whites, 2 cups panko crumbs, Oil, 0.875 teaspoon Salt, 1 teaspoon onion powder, 1.5 teaspoons garlic powder, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon paprika, 0.25 teaspoon allspice, 2 teaspoon seasoning
instructions: 1. Cut chicken to tiny bite sized pieces. Make sure you cut them to uniform sizes as this helps to cook uniformly. 2. In a bowl, mix together seasoning and chicken well. Optionally add 2 tbsps buttermilk or yogurt and mix well. You can refrigerate this for 30 mins to 8 hours. 3. Pour the egg white or buttermilk and mix well. Sprinkle cornstarch in 2 batches and coat the chicken well. At this stage your chicken must have a thick and moist batter coated over it and should not be dry. 4. Add panko crumbs to a plate or bowl. Place the bowl of chicken and the plate of panko crumbs, side by side. 5. Pick up each piece of chicken & roll in the bread crumbs. Press down gently each piece with your fingers to coat the chicken with the crumbs. This gives results similar to double breading. 6. Place them in a wide tray. Repeat the same step until all the chicken bites are breaded. 7. Rest for 10 to 15 mins for the bread crumbs to stick well to the chicken. You can also refrigerate in a air tight container up to 48 hours for further use. 8. Pour oil to a deep pan and heat on a medium flame until hot enough. 9. Oil has to be medium hot and not smoking hot. Test by dropping a small piece of bread crumb into the oil, it should sizzle and come up to the surface without become brown or burnt. This means the oil is at the right temperature for frying. If the crumb sinks, it means the oil is still not hot enough. 10. 350 to 375 F is the right temperature if you are measuring. 11. Gently drop the breaded popcorn chicken to the hot oil one after the other. Do not stir or disturb them for about a minute or 2, as this is going to separate the bread crumbs from the chicken. 12. Do not crowd the pan with many chicken bites at one time as this will bring down the temperature of the oil. Fry popcorn chicken in batches. 13. Stir after 2 mins and fry evenly till golden and crisp on a medium flame. The internal temperature of the thickest piece of fried chicken should reach 165 F. If you don't have a food thermometer, simply cut down a popcorn chicken. It should not be pink inside any more. 14. Remove the popcorn chicken to a cooling rack placed over kitchen tissues or a tray. Or to a steel colander. 15. To fry the next batch of popcorn chicken, turn down the heat to low. Using a mesh skimmer, remove any bread crumbs that have fallen off to the oil. Bring the oil back to right temperature and fry them in batches. 16. Place the tray in a 375 F or 190 C, preheated oven in the middle rack. Bake for 14 mins or until the chicken is cooked through, turning them half way. 17. Place the breaded chicken in the air fryer basket and air fry for 10 mins at 400 F or 200 C. Turn them after 5 mins. Adjust the cook time as required as this significantly changes with marinade timings and the size of your pieces. 18. The internal temperature should reach 165 F or 74 C. Once done, cut down a piece of chicken to see if it is done. The inside should no more be pink in color. 19. You can sprinkle your favorite seasoning over popcorn chicken before serving. Sriracha or wasabi go great on these.

title: Pori Urundai Recipe | Murmura Laddu
ingredients: 1.5 cup jaggery, 0.75 cup water, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tsp ghee, 4.5 cup murmura puffed rice
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a heavy-bottomed pan take 1½ cup jaggery, ¾ cup water and mix well. 2. Once the jaggery melts, boil on medium flame. 3. Check the consistency by dropping syrup into a bowl of water; it should form a hard ball and make a crystalline sound. Else boil for another minute and check. 4. Also, add ½ tsp cardamom powder and 1 tsp ghee, then mix well. 5. Pour the jaggery syrup over 4½ cup murmura and mix gently. 6. Mix until the mixture is well combined and the jaggery syrup is coated well. 7. Grease your hand with ghee and prepare ladoos when the mixture is still warm. 8. Finally, enjoy Murmura Ladoo / Pori Urundai Recipe for a month when stored in an airtight container.

title: potato bites recipe | aloo bites recipe | chilli garlic potato bite
ingredients: 3 potato aloo, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.75 tsp garlic paste, 1 tsp chilli flakes, 1 tsp mixed herbs, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp corn flour, 2 tbsp maida plain flour, 1 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp garlic paste, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, other ingredients, 1 cup panko bread crumbs, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 boiled and mashed potato. 2. add ½ cup bread crumbs and 2 tbsp corn flour. they help to absorb moisture. 3. also, add ¾ tsp garlic paste, 1 tsp chilli flakes, 1 tsp mixed herbs, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ½ tsp salt. 4. squeeze and mix well forming a soft dough. 5. if the dough is still sticky, add 1 tbsp breadcrumbs and mix well. this helps to absorb moisture. keep aside. 6. further, take a small ball sized aloo mixture, and shape into cubes. 7. dip in corn flour slurry all over. 8. then roll in breadcrumbs. make double coating to get a crispy outer layer. 9. now deep fry in hot oil or bake in a preheated oven at 180 degree celcius for 15 minutes. 10. stir occasionally, until it turns golden brown and crisp. drain over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 11. finally, enjoy aloo bites / potato bites with tomato sauce.

title: potato chips recipe | aloo chips recipe | homemade potato chips
ingredients: 3 potato, 4 cup chilled water, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, oil
