instructions: 1. firstly, peel the skin of the large potato. make sure to use a large potato to get good slices. 2. slice the potato as thin as possible. 3. now transfer the potato slice to a large bowl and add chilled water. 4. rinse well and spread on kitchen towel. 5. rub off the excess water. this helps to prevent oil spluttering. 6. drop the sliced potato into hot oil without overcrowding. 7. stir occasionally and fry on medium flame. 8. fry until the chips turn crispy. 9. drain off into a colander. do not drain over kitchen paper as it turns chips soggy. 10. now add 1 tsp chilli powder and ½ tsp salt. 11. toss and mix well making sure all the spices are coated. 12. finally, enjoy potato chips recipe or store in an airtight container for a month.

title: potato curry for masala dosa | aloo baji for masala dosa
ingredients: 4 potatoes, 1 onion, 1 green chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 red chilli, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tsp mustard seeds rai, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp turmeric powder, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 stem curry leaves, 2 tsp lemon juice, 1 stem coriander leaves, 2 tsp oil, salt to taste
instructions: 1. boil the potatoes well and peel off the skin. 2. finely chop green chillies, ginger, coriander leaves. also thinly slice onion and keep ready. 3. in a pan heat a tablespoon of oil and add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, urad dal, red chilli and channa dal. wait till mustard seeds splutter. 4. then add curry leaves and hing. fry for a minute. 5. now add thinly sliced onion and fry till then turn translucent. 6. add in finely chopped chilli and ginger. fry till the raw smell of ginger goes away. 7. now add boiled potatoes and turmeric. 8. mash the potatoes well and add in 3 tbsp of water to have a smooth gravy paste. cook 3 mins 9. squeeze some lemon juice and add chopped coriander leaves. give a good mix. 10. serve aloo bhaji hot with masala dosa's, rice or chapathis.

title: Potato Curry Recipe (Aloo Curry)
ingredients: 1.1 lbs potatoes, 1.5 cup onions, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.5 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chilli, 11 curry leaves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves cilantro, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1.5 to 2 cup water, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1.125 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder
instructions: 1. Wash and fine chop onions. Chop or puree tomatoes. Peel and cube potatoes to ¾ inch pieces. Keep them immersed in a bowl of water until used. 2. Pour 2 tablespoons oil to a hot pan. When the oil becomes hot, add the optional ingredients - mustard, cumin seeds & curry leaves. 3. Add chopped onions & saute until they turn pink or transparent. 4. Stir in ginger garlic and saute for 1 to 2 mins, until a nice aroma comes out. 5. Next add the tomatoes, turmeric and salt. Saute until the tomatoes break down & turn soft and mushy. 6. Add red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder & cumin powder (optional). Saute everything well for 2 to 3 minutes until the masala begins to smell good. 7. Drain the potatoes and add them to the pan. Saute them for 2 to 3 minutes. Pour water or light coconut milk to partially cover the potatoes. 8. Bring it to a boil on a medium heat. Cook covered until the potatoes are fully done or fork tender. Stir the curry in between to prevent burning. 9. Taste test and add more salt if needed along with kasuri methi (optional). If the potato curry is runny, open the lid and evaporate the excess liquid. If there is not enough liquid pour some hot water and bring it to consistency. 10. Turn off and keep the pot covered for 2 mins for the flavors to be absorbed. Garnish potato curry with coriander leaves. Serve with rice or roti. 11. To make this potato curry in Instant pot, dice the potatoes to 1 inch pieces so they don’t turn mushy when cooked under pressure. 12. Press saute button on your Instant pot and pour oil to the steel insert. 13. When the oil turns hot, add mustard, cumin seeds, curry leaves and onions. 14. Saute well until the onions turn light golden & lose their raw flavor. 15. Then stir in ginger garlic and saute for 1 minute. Add tomatoes and salt. 16. Stir fry until tomatoes break down and turn completely mushy. 17. Then add all the spice powders – garam masala, chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder (optional), cumin powder (optional). Sauté for 30 to 60 seconds until aromatic. 18. Press cancel. Add the potatoes and pour 1 cup water. Deglaze the pot with a spatula to release any bits of food stuck there. 19. Optionally you can also cook your rice in your instant pot by placing your rice bowl over the trivet. Use hot water to cook your rice as the cook time for this recipe is only 4 mins. 20. Secure the Instant pot with the lid. Press pressure cook button and set the timer to 4 minutes. 21. When the Instant pot is done cooking, do a quick pressure release by moving the steam vent from sealing to venting. 22. Potato curry is ready at this stage. If you prefer to use coconut milk, then bring the potatoes to the center of the pot with the spatula. Optional - Pour only half cup coconut milk around the edges, inside the steel insert. Cook on saute mode until the curry thickens slightly. This just takes 1 to 2 mins. 23. Remove the pot from the IP and add coriander leaves or Kasuri methi. Serve potato curry with rice or roti, paratha.

title: Potato fry (Aloo fry)
ingredients: 1.25 cup potatoes, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 red chili, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Wash potatoes and peel. Cube to small bite sizes about half inch cubes. Keep them in a bowl of water until used. This helps to remove the excess starch and prevents them from sticking to each other while frying. 2. Crush or chop garlic. Rinse curry leaves and set aside. 3. Pour oil to a heavy bottom non stick or cast iron pan. When the oil turns hot, add cumin, mustard, red chili and dal (optional). Fry until the dal turns golden. 4. Add curry leaves and garlic. When the garlic is lightly fried, add hing. 5. Drain the water from potatoes and then add them to the pan. Sprinkle turmeric. Mix and fry potatoes for about 3 to 4 minutes on a medium heat stirring often. 6. For crispy aloo fry, keep stirring and fry them without covering until fork tender. You can also add more oil for better roasting. 7. For soft potatoes, lower the flame. Cover and cook till the potatoes are fork tender. Stir in between to prevent burning. 8. When the potatoes are done, prick them with a fork to check if they are cooked completely. You shouldn't feel them hard from inside. Otherwise cook them for a little longer. 9. Then sprinkle chili powder, salt and garam masala. Toss everything well and fry for a minute or two to blend well. I usually add a tsp of oil at this stage so the spice powders coat all of the potatoes easily. 10. Serve potato fry with rice and rasam, sambar.

title: potato garlic rings recipe | garlic potato rings with chilli garlic chatni | aloo rings
ingredients: 1 tbsp butter, 4 clove garlic, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.75 cup rava semolina suji, 2 potato, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup corn flour, oil, 7 red chilli, water, 2 tomato, 2 tbsp oil, 4 clove garlic, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp sugar
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tbsp butter. add 4 clove garlic, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp mixed herbs. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 2. add 1 cup water and ½ tsp salt. get to a rolling boil. 3. keep stirring and add ¾ cup rava. 4. continue to stir until the rava absorbs all the water. 5. sprinkle 2 tbsp water, cover and rest for 10 minutes. 6. after 10 minutes, the rava has absorbed the moisture. transfer to the large bowl. 7. also add 2 potato, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 8. since the dough is sticky, add ¼ cup corn flour and mix well. 9. mix well forming a non-sticky dough. 10. grease the dough with oil and roll slightly thick. 11. cut into ring shapes using a round cutter. 12. deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 13. stir occasionally, until the potato rings turn golden brown and crisp. 14. finally, drain off the potato garlic rings and enjoy with spicy tomato garlic chutney. 15. firstly, in a bowl soak 7 dried red chilli in hot water until it softens. 16. transfer the soaked chilli into mixer jar. 17. add 2 tomato and grind to a smooth paste. keep aside. 18. in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil and add 4 clove garlic. saute slightly. 19. pour in prepared tomato chilli paste and cook well. 20. also, add ½ tsp salt and ½ tsp sugar and mix well. 21. finally, enjoy spicy tomato garlic chutney with snacks.

title: Potato Kurma (Aloo Kurma)
ingredients: 3 potatoes, 2 tablespoons oil, 1.25 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon fennel seeds powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 tablespoons pudina, 3 tablespoons coconut, 2 tablespoons poppy seeds, 0.5 teaspoon Shahi jeera, 2 green cardamoms, 1 star anise, 1 inch cinnamon, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 1 strand mace, 1 bay leaf
instructions: 1. To make the kurma paste, add grated coconut and poppy seeds (or cashews) to a grinder jar. Pour little water and make a fine paste. Or If you want to use yogurt, add it to a bowl and whisk well with a fork. Keep this aside. 2. If using yogurt, you need to add the diced potatoes to a pot and cover them partially with water. Cook until 90% done. Skip this step if using coconut. 3. Pour oil to a hot pan. When the oil becomes hot, reduce the flame to low and add all the whole spices - bay leaf, star anise, cloves, cinnamon, green cardamoms, mace and shahi jeera or cumin. 4. When they begin to sizzle, add the fine chopped onions. Increase the heat to medium and saute them until golden. 5. While the onions fry, chop the tomatoes and the potatoes. keep the tomatoes aside & the potatoes in a bowl of water. 6. When the onions are golden, add ginger garlic paste. Fry until the raw smell goes away, for about 40 to 60 seconds. 7. Transfer the tomatoes and sprinkle salt and turmeric. Fry until the tomatoes turn completely soft and blend well with onions. 8. Next add mint and fry for a minute. 9. Add garam masala, red chili powder and crushed fennel. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes until the raw smell of chili powder goes away. 10. Add the coconut paste and mix well. If using yogurt in place of coconut, add 2 tablespoons of the onion tomato masala (from the previous step) to the yogurt and mix well. Lower the heat and pour this to the pan slowly. 11. Mix well and fry until the mixture begins to smell good. Add potatoes and saute for 2 minutes. 12. Pour 1½ to 1¾ water and stir. Bring it to a rapid boil. Lower the heat to medium and cook covered till the potatoes are fully cooked and oil rises to the top. You can also pressure cook on a low flame for one whistle. 13. If the aloo kurma is runny, increase the flame and cook uncovered to evaporate some of the water. Turn off the heat when the kurma reaches a thick gravy consistency. Taste test and adjust salt. 14. Add chopped coriander and Serve potato kurma with biryani, pulao or chapathi.

title: potato nuggets recipe | spicy potato nuggets | potato snacks recipes
ingredients: 2 potato, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 3 tbsp cheddar cheese, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.25 tsp pepper, salt to taste, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.25 cup bread crumbs, 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.25 tsp pepper, 1 cup bread crumbs, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 cup water, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 potato, 3 tbsp cheddar cheese, ½ tsp ginger garlic paste, ½ tsp chilli flakes, ½ tsp mixed herbs, ¼ tsp pepper, ½ tsp chaat masala, ¼ tsp salt and 2 tbsp coriander leaves. 2. mix well making sure the spices are combined well. 3. now add ¼ cup bread crumbs and combine well. breadcrumbs helps to absorb moisture and prevent from breaking in oil. 4. further prepare corn flour batter by mixing ¼ cup corn flour, ¼ tsp pepper, ¼ tsp salt and ¼ cup water. 5. prepare a smooth flowing consistency batter. 6. pinch a small ball sized potato dough and roll to cylindrical shape. 7. dip in corn flour batter covering all sides. 8. then roll in bread crumbs. 9. deep fry, shallow fry or pan fry the patties in hot oil. alternatively, preheat and bake at 180 degree celsius for 25 minutes. 10. flip and fry all the sides on medium flame till it turns golden and crisp. 11. finally, drain over kitchen paper to remove excess oil and serve potato nuggets with tomato sauce.

title: Potato Rice (Aloo Rice)
ingredients: 1 cup raw rice, 2 medium potatoes, 1 medium onion, 2 garlic, 12 mint leaves, 1 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon Garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon Red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons Oil, 1.25 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 bay leaf, 3 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1 star anise, 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Cook rice in whatever way you prefer. Here is how I do it - Rinse rice in a pot and pour 3 to 4 cups of water. Bring to a rolling boil and cook on a medium heat till fully cooked, yet firm. Drain to a colander, cover and let rest until used later. 2. While the rice cooks, wash, peel and dice potatoes. Add them to a bowl of water to avoid discoloring. 3. Pour oil to a hot wide pan / skillet and add the whole spices if using. When they begin to sizzle (in a minute), add the onions, garlic and chili. Saute until the onions turn transparent for 3 to 4 mins. 4. Drain the water from the potatoes and add them to the pan. Saute and roast them until tender and fully cooked. If the pan dries out, add a splash of water and cook covered. It should take you about 8 to 9 mins. 5. Stir in turmeric, red chili powder, mint, salt and garam masala. Saute for a minute and turn off the heat. Stir in the rice and squeeze some lemon juice. 6. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt. If you want more heat, add black pepper. 7. Serve potato rice hot with yogurt and a veggie salad.

title: Potato Sandwich Recipe (Aloo Sandwich)
ingredients: 1 potato, 1 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon ginger, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 1 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon ghee, 4 slices bread, 2 tbsp butter, 0.25 cup green chutney
instructions: 1. Wash and peel 300 grams potatoes (1 large or 2 medium). I prefer to quarter them and steam over a pot just until done. You can also boil the whole potatoes in a pressure cooker for 3 whistles or for 5 to 9 mins in the instant pot depending on the size of your potatoes. 2. To a mixing bowl, add ½ teaspoon grated ginger 1 green chili chopped (optional) 1 tbsp chopped coriander leaves ½ teaspoon red chili powder ¼ teaspoon salt (adjust to taste) 1/8 teaspoon turmeric ¼ to ½ teaspoon garam masala (adjust to taste) ½ teaspoon coriander powder. Pour 1 to 2 tsp ghee or oil over the spices. I prefer to use hot ghee as it helps to bring out the aroma of the other ingredients used. However it is optional. 3. Mix up everything. You can also add lemon juice or amchur now. 4. Add mashed potato. Mix up everything well. Taste test and add more spices, salt or lemon juice to taste. 5. Smear softened butter or ghee on one side of the bread. I prefer ghee. Turn the bread slices to the other side. If you want you can also smear some green chutney or schezwan sauce and then spread the aloo masala. Cover with another slice and smear butter or ghee on the outer side. You can also smear chutney or sauce on both the slices. 6. Heat the sandwich griller and grill the sandwiches for 3 to 5 mins until crisp. Alternately you can also toast them on a hot griddle on both the sides until crisp & golden. Cut aloo sandwiches to squares or triangles and serve with green chutney or schezwan sauce. 7. Add 1 tablespoon oil to a pan and heat it. Then add cumin sees. When the seeds splutter, add half teaspoon ginger paste or grated ginger and fry till you smell good. Add 1 medium sliced onion, 2 to 3 curry leaves and 1 chopped green chili. Saute till the onions turn lightly golden. While the onions fry, peel and mince 1 large raw potato or mash boiled potato, whichever you prefer. 8. Add minced raw potato or mashed boiled potato. Fry for 3 to 4 minutes. 9. Skip this step if using boiled potatoes. If using raw mined potatoes, add water just enough to cover the potatoes.Cover and cook till the potatoes are soft cooked and tender. Since they are mined they cook faster. 10. Add 1/8 teaspoon turmeric, ¼ teaspoon fennel powder (optional), ¼ teaspoon pepper powder(optional), ¼ teaspoon amchur (optional), ½ teaspoon garam masala and ½ teaspoon red chili powder. I added chili powder later, since I forgot. 11. Fry everything well till you get a nice aroma of the masala and the fennel seeds powder. Everything should blend well, potatoes soft cooked and fairly dry. 12. Smear butter on one side of the bread for crust. On the inner side, spread the curry in a thin layer, sprinkle some coriander leaves if you like. If you do not have a toaster, you can toast on a tawa on both the sides. Gently flip the bread & toast on the other side. The filling tastes so good on its own that you can simply spread it over toasted bread slices and serve. 13. If making in toaster. Cover with another slice and toast following the manufacturers guidelines. Serve potato sandwich with tea or with any of your favorite dips. 14. Sandwich Recip... Mayonnaise Sandwi... Vegetarian Club S... Veg Sandwich Reci... Bombay Sandwich R... Veg Grilled Sandw...

title: potato smiley recipe | mccain smiles recipe | potato smiles recipe
ingredients: 2 cups boiled potato, 0.25 cup bread crumbs, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, salt to taste, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cups boiled and grated potato. 2. further add ¼ cup bread crumbs, 2 tbsp corn flour, ½ tsp chilli powder and salt to taste. 3. combine all the ingredients well. add more bread crumbs if required. 4. form a soft non sticky dough. 5. cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes, this prevents smiley from breaking in oil and retain its shape. 6. now take a large ball sized prepared potato dough and flatten slightly thick over butter paper. butter paper avoids from dough to stick. 7. cut round using a cookie cutter or a glass. 8. also using a straw make eyes and use spoon to make a smile. 9. deep fry the potato smiles in hot oil. or brush with oil and bake in preheated oven at 180 degree celsius for 25 minutes or till it turns golden and crisp. 10. stir occasionally and fry till golden brown, keeping the flame on medium. 11. finally, serve potato smiles or potato smiley with tomato sauce or eggless mayonnaise.

title: Potato Wedges Recipe
ingredients: 5 grams potatoes, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon basil, 0.75 teaspoon thyme, 0.75 teaspoon oregano, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, 1 teaspoon onion powder, 0.375 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 tablespoons flour, oil
instructions: 1. Wash potatoes well and scrub them with a brush to remove dirt and mud. 2. Cut each potato to 2 parts. Then cut each part to 3 to 4 wedges depending on the size of your potatoes. Each potato can give you 6 to 8 wedges. 3. Add them to a bowl filled with cold water. Rest them for 30 mins. To speed up the process, you may rinse the wedges a few times in fresh cold water and soak for 15 mins. 4. Meanwhile prepare the seasoning. To a small mixing bowl, add all the herbs, garlic powder, onion powder, red chilli powder and flour. Mix and taste test. Add more salt if needed. Keep this aside. 5. Preheat the oven to 220 C or 400 F for at least 15 mins. Line a baking tray. 6. Drain off the water completely & wipe dry with a kitchen towel. I let mine air dry instead. 7. Add them to a bowl, pour oil and mix them well. Then add all the seasonings and mix well to coat all of the seasoning. 8. Place the potato wedges on the baking tray with the skin side down. 9. Bake them in a preheated oven for 30 to 35 minutes or until crisp and golden. Adjust the baking time as needed depending on the size of the wedges. 10. Serve potato wedges straight out of the oven. 11. Preheat your air fryer as mentioned in the manual. 12. Drain the water completely from the wedges and add 1 tablespoon oil. Coat them well. 13. Add all of the seasonings and flour. Mix everything well. If the seasoning is too dry you may sprinkle just a tbsp of water. Spread them in the air fryer basket. 14. Air fry them at 200 C or 400 F for 16 to 20 mins depending on the size of the wedges. After 8 to 9 minutes, shake them gently and continue to air fry. Alternately you can also air fry them on the fries setting and towards the end air fry at 200 C or 400 F until crisp. 15. Bring water to a rolling boil in a large pot and add salt. 16. Add the potatoes to the boiling water and cook until they are half done. Drain them immediately. 17. Cool them completely. Then add all the seasoning and flour. Gently coat them. Sprinkle little water to coat the seasoning and the flour evenly all over the wedges. All of the dry ingredients should coat the potatoes well and should not be dry. 18. Heat oil in a deep kadai. When the oil is medium hot, drop the wedges one after the other to the hot oil. 19. Fry on a medium high flame until lightly golden. Remove them to a colander. Finish frying all of these once. 20. Then refry in batches. Regulate the flame to medium and fry until crisp and golden. 21. Remove crisp potato wedges to a colander and serve them hot with your favorite dip.

title: potato wedges recipe | deep fried & baked potato wedges
ingredients: 4 medium sized potato, 0.75 tsp salt, 0.25 cup corn flour, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, oil for deep frying, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs
instructions: 1. firstly, in peel the skin of potato, use same sized potato preferably less starchy. 2. chop them to thick wedges cutting each potato to 8 wedges. 3. further, boil the potato wedges for 3 minutes adding little salt in the water. 4. drain the water and keep potatoes aside. cool completely. 5. coat the potatoes with 2 tbsp oil. 6. further, add ¼ cup corn flour, ½ tsp pepper, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp mixed herbs. 7. mix well making sure the corn flour gets coated uniformly. freeze the leftover potato wedges and use for atleast 3-4 month. 8. bake at 180 degree celsius for 15-18 minutes or till the potatoes are golden and crisp. 9. alternatively, deep fry the potatoes into hot oil. 10. stir occasionally, till the potatoes turn golden brown and crisp. 11. sprinkle wedges with salt and chilli powder before serving. 12. finally, enjoy spicy and crispy potato wedges with tomato sauce.

title: Potlakaya Perugu Pachadi | Snake Gourd Yogurt Pachadi
ingredients: 2 cups snake gourd pieces, 0.75 cup sour curd, 1 tbsp shredded ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 3 green chilies, 1 red chili, Coriander leaves, 1 Pinch hing, Salt
instructions: 1. Wash potlakaya under running water. With the help of a knife scrape off the whistish skin. Rinse thoroughly. Chop to cubes, remove the pith and seeds. Chop them to cubes. 2. Pressure cook the chopped gourd for 1 to 2 whistles, depends on how tender your gourd is. You can even cook them in a pot. 3. Once the vegetable is cooled, puree it. Can alternately skip pureeing cooked gourd and use them directly. 4. Heat oil in a pan, add curry leaves, green chilies, mustard, cumin and allow them to splutter. 5. Add ginger and red chili, fry to get an aroma. 6. Add turmeric and hing. 7. Pour the pureed gourd and fry for a min on high. 8. Once cool, transfer potlakaya perugu pachadi to the yogurt bowl and mix well. Add coriander leaves. 9. Ready to serve with rice or phulka

title: Praline recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup refined sugar, 0.25 cup almonds, 1 tbsp butter
instructions: 1. Add sugar to a dry heavy bottom pan. Begin to melt it on a medium flame. Within seconds you will see the sugar begins to melt. 2. Do not use a spoon or ladle to stir the sugar. Instead swirl the pan when ever needed to spread it evenly. Be attentive and reduce the flame to lowest. 3. You can gently push inside the sugar stuck to the edges. Be ready with a tbsp of butter. As soon as you see the entire sugar has melted completely, it will begin to smoke up. Quickly take off the pan from the heat. Add the butter. Do not let it smoke for longer. 4. I placed the pan over a cork pad. Note that the mixture is no more on the heat. Mix well. The mixture will bubble up a lot. 5. Add chopped almonds. 6. Pour it to a greased plate or parchment paper. Cool this completely. 7. The entire mixture will cool and come out as a brittle. Break it to pieces. 8. You can transfer this to a zip lock bag or a clean cloth. Cover it and crush with a rolling pin to a coarse powder. I used my mixer jar to powder it. If using a mixer then break them to small pieces and use a large jar. Using a small jar may damage the lid as the pieces bounce up to it. Just pulse the mixer to get coarse praline. Do not run for longer as the praline will become fine powder. 9. I removed the large chunks and ran the blender once more. 10. Transfer this to a clean glass jar and store it in the fridge. 11. Roughly chop the nuts of your choice. Set them aside. Grease a plate with butter or keep a parchment paper ready. 12. Add sugar to a heavy bottom pan. 13. Avoid stirring the sugar. Swirl the pan as needed for even distribution. 14. When the sugar melts completely and begins to smoke, take off the pan from the heat. Do not allow the sugar to smoke up for longer. 15. Immediately add butter and mix well. 16. The mixture froths up and reduces. 17. Add the chopped nuts. Mix well. 18. Pour this to the parchment paper or plate. 19. Cool it completely. Remove the brittle and break it to small pieces. 20. Using a rolling pan, crush it to a small pieces. You can add it to a blender and make a coarse powder.

title: pranhara recipe | bengali kacha golla | bengali mishti pranhara
ingredients: 2 litre milk, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 3 tbsp sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 0.25 cup milk maid condensed milk, 0.25 cup milk powder
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large vessel get 2 litre milk of milk to a boil stirring occasionally. 2. once the milk comes to a boil, pour 2 tbsp lemon juice and stir well. you can alternatively use curd or vinegar. 3. stir until the milk curdles keeping the flame on low to medium. do not boil further once water separates completely. 4. drain the curdled milk over a cloth-lined over a colander. you can use the leftover water to make soup or knead the dough as they are very nutritious. 5. rinse off the curdled milk with fresh water to remove sourness from lemon juice. 6. squeeze off the water completely. do not over squeeze as the moisture in paneer will be lost. hang for 1 hour making sure the water is drained completely, yet remain the moisture. 7. after 1 hour, cut the paneer into 2 equal half. 8. keep one half aside, and start to mash the other half. 9. crumble the paneer making sure there are no large lumps. 10. now add 3 tbsp sugar and mash until the sugar dissolves completely. 11. transfer the paneer sugar mixture into a pan and cook on low flame. 12. cook until the sugar melts keeping the flame on low to medium. 13. now add ¼ cup condensed milk and mix well. 14. mix until everything is well combined. 15. continue to cook, until the mixture thickens. 16. transfer the mixture into a plate and add reserved half of the paneer. 17. break the paneer and mix well. 18. combine both the mixture well. 19. now roll ball-sized ladoo and roll into milk powder. you can also use mawa to roll in. 20. finally, enjoy pranhara for a week when stored in the refrigerator.

title: Prawn biryani (shrimp biryani)
ingredients: 1.25 cups basmati rice, 1 bay leaf, 2.5 green cardamoms, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 25 grams shrimp, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon biryani masala, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon salt, 2.5 tablespoon oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinammon, 1 star anise, 2 green cardamoms, 4.5 cloves 4 to 5 cloves, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 onion, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 medium tomato, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 2 tablespoon mint leaves, 1 teaspoon biryani masala, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, salt, 0.25 cup curd
instructions: 1. Drain the water completely from prawns, add chili powder, turmeric, salt & masala powder. 2. Marinate and rub them gently for a while with the marinade. Set aside for at least 30 minutes. You can also do this the previous night and refrigerate. 3. Wash and soak rice for about 30 minutes. Drain off completely. Bring 3 to 4 cups of water to a boil with salt, a bay leaf, 4 green cardamoms, 1/2 tsp shahi jeera. Add rice to the pot and cook until fully done but al dente. It should remain grainy, firm and not mushy. 4. Heat oil in a pan, Add bay leaf, shahi jeera, cloves, cardamom, star anise and cinnamon. Use just an inch of cinnamon, i used ceylon cinnamon hence used a bit more. 5. This shrimp biryani needs a little more oil than the other non-veg biryani recipes since prawns do not release much fats when cooked. Stick on to the quantity mentioned in the recipe else biryani may turn dry. 6. When they begin to splutter, add sliced onions. Separate the layers of onions before adding. Fry until brown or golden. 7. Browned onions lend a good flavor, so don’t skip this. You can set aside a few for garnishing. Add ginger garlic paste and saute till the raw smell goes off. 8. Add chopped tomatoes, mint and coriander leaves. Cook until the tomatoes turn soft. 9. Next add turmeric, red chili powder, biryani masala powder . I also added a bit of coriander powder since my masala powder was very strong. 10. Cook until the oil begins to separate. 11. Add marinated prawns. 12. Fry just for a minute. 13. Let the flame remain low. Pour slightly thick coconut milk and stir. If you do not follow any food combining rules of compatibility you can use yogurt/curd as well instead of coconut milk. 14. Cook until the gravy thickens & prawns are cooked through. Prawns take just 3 minutes to cook. They curl up to C shape when cooked through. Off the stove and set the pan aside. The color of the gravy is dark due to the brown onions.

title: Prawn Curry (Prawn Masala)
ingredients: 2.25 tablespoons oil, 0.75 cup onion, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 cup tomato, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 cup raw coconut, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 green chilli, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 garlic, 25 grams prawns, 1.25 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 teaspoon tamarind paste optional
instructions: 1. Devein and wash the prawns well with running cold water. Drain the water completely. Optionally toss them in a pinch of salt, chili powder and garam masala if you want. I do not do that. 2. Heat a tablespoon oil in a pan and saute ¾ cup sliced onions (1 medium onion) until they turn pink, for 5 to 6 mins. Add ¾ tsp each chopped ginger and garlic (or 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste). Saute for a minute. 3. Add ¾ cup chopped tomatoes (1 large tomato), sprinkle ½ teaspoon sea salt and ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. Saute till the tomatoes turn mushy & break down completely, for about 5 mins. 4. Add ½ to ¾ teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, ½ to ¾ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder and ¼ cup grated raw coconut. (I used tightly pressed frozen raw coconut). You can also use ¼ cup coconut milk or 10 cashew nuts. 5. Saute on a medium heat till the mixture is nicely roasted, for about 3 to 5 minutes. Take care not to burn. Cool down completely. 6. Transfer to a blender with ¾ cup water. If you want add half to 1 tsp of tamarind paste (not concentrate) and blend to a smooth puree (or slightly coarse paste if you want). Be careful with tamarind as there is no way back. 7. Heat the same pan with 1 to 1½ tablespoon oil. This step is optional and you may instead cook the prawns directly in the gravy later. Place the prawns in the pan and cook just for 2 mins, 1 min on each side. I also added a few curry leaves with the prawns for flavor. Remove to a plate. 8. Add ¼ teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 slit green chili (optional) and 1 chopped garlic clove. Wait till the garlic is lightly roasted then add 1 sprig curry leaves. Fry till the garlic turns aromatic and the leaves turn crispy. 9. Pour the onion tomato mixture and mix well. If required add more water or coconut milk to bring to a slightly runny consistency. Bring to a rolling boil and cook until it is thick and traces of oil are seen on the curry. Reduce the heat completely, and add the prawns. Simmer just until the tails curl up to “C” shape, meaning they are fully cooked. 10. Overcooking will make them hard. Switch off the stove. Taste test and add more salt or ¼ teaspoon black pepper if you want. If you have not used tamarind like me, sprinkle some lemon juice for a tang. Garnish prawn curry with coriander leaves. Serve with rice or roti, naan or chapathi, Ghee rice or Jeera rice . 11. Then transfer them to a bowl and pour 2 to 3 cups of cold water. Drain and repeat rinsing them a few times. If using frozen prawns defrost them the same way and avoid thawing or defrosting in microwave or using hot water as the prawns lose their taste and texture. 12. Rinse each prawn well, drain the water & place them in a bowl.

title: Prawn pulao (shrimp pulao)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 2 grams prawns, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 Handful mint leaves, 1.5 green chili, 0.625 tsp Garam masala, 0.625 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp Red chili powder, 1 pinch turmeric, Salt, 2.5 tbsps coconut milk, Oil, Lemon juice, 1 bay leaf, 3 green cardamoms, 5 cloves 4 to 6 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon stick, 0.5 tsp Shahi jeera, 1 star flower, 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Wash and soak rice for about 30 mins. 2. Cook it in whatever way you like either in the pot, pressure cooker or electric cooker. 3. Cook rice till al dente. You can also add few drops of oil to the rice pot to keep the rice grainy. Rice should be grainy fully cooked but not mushy. 4. Devein and clean prawns. Rinse them well. You can also wash them in vinegar to remove the fishy smell. Drain them completely and set aside. 5. While the rice soaks and cooks, you can marinate prawns with ginger garlic paste, garam masala, coriander powder, red chili powder, salt and turmeric. 6. Heat a pan with oil and saute all the pulao spices until fragrant. 7. Add onions and saute until golden and cooked well. Transfer the prawns and saute for 2 to 3 mins or until the raw smell from the ginger garlic goes away. Add mint and saute for 2 mins. 8. Cover and cook on a low heat until the prawns are completely cooked. Add the coconut milk or water and allow to thicken. You must be left with a thick curry like prawn mixture. At this stage if the prawn are swirled it means they are cooked completely. If not sprinkle little water and cook further. Overcooking makes them hard. So be sure not to overcook. 9. Add cooked and cooled rice along with lemon juice. Mix well and serve prawn pulao hot with fresh onion, cucumber wedges and papad.

title: Prawns Fry Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams prawns, 2 teaspoons lemon juice, 0.5 tablespoon oil, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon sea salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon Indian red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 green cardamom, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 medium onion, 1 green chili slit, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon Indian chili powder
instructions: 1. Devein and clean prawns. I used about 10 tiger prawns (200 grams). Rinse them thoroughly. To remove fishy smell, they can be rinsed with water and little vinegar. You can also use lemon juice and salt to rinse. If using frozen prawns, you will need to soak in hot water for a while. This will help to soften them. Drain off water completely. Add 2 teaspoons lemon juice and ½ tablespoon oil. Mix well. 2. Add 1½ teaspoon ginger garlic paste, ½ to ¾ teaspoon red chili powder, ⅛ teaspoon turmeric, ¼ teaspoon salt, ½ to ¾ teaspoon garam masala and ½ teaspoon coriander powder (optional). Mix and set aside for at least 10 minutes, while you chop the onions and green chili. 3. Heat 1 tablespoon oil or ghee in a hot pan. Add ½ teaspoon cumin seeds and a green cardamom (optional). 4. When the seeds begin to sizzle, stir in the onions and slit chilies. Sprinkle a pinch of salt. 5. Saute until the onions turn transparent or light golden and add the marinated prawns. 6. Add curry leaves and stir fry until they swirl up to a C shape. 7. The prawns will swirl up completely, meaning they are cooked thoroughly. If you want add more red chili powder, salt and stir fry for just a minute. 1 tbsp fresh grated coconut can also be added at this stage. Stir fry until the coconut begin to smell good. 8. Serve prawns fry with rice. You can also add some cooked basmati rice, little more ghee, salt to the pan. Mix and stir to make prawn ghee rice.

title: Pudina Chutney (Mint Chutney)
ingredients: 1 cup mint leaves, 0.5 cup coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 inch ginger, 1 garlic clove, 1.5 green chilies, 1 cup yogurt, 0.625 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.625 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 cup coconut, 0.375 cup pudina, 1 tablespoon roasted bengal gram, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 inch ginger, salt as needed, 2.5 green chilies, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon Oil, 1 red chili, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 1 pinch hing, 1.5 cup mint leaves, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.5 inch ginger, 1.5 green chilies, salt as needed, 0.5 teaspoon lemon juice, 0.375 teaspoon chaat masala, 1.5 tablespoon water, 1.5 tablespoon fried gram
instructions: 1. Pluck 1 cup mint leaves (tightly packed, 15 grams). We don’t want to use stalks here. Add them to a bowl of water and rinse them very well a few times. Rinse half cup coriander leaves with tender stalks (tightly packed, 15 grams), a few times. Drain completely and add them to a grinder jar. 2. Add ¼ inch ginger, 1 small garlic clove and 1 to 2 green chilies. 3. Add ½ teaspoon sugar and 1/3 teaspoon salt. 4. Add 1 tablespoon chilled thick yogurt. Do not use runny yogurt. If you double or triple the recipe then add yogurt accordingly. Do not add too much yogurt at this stage. 5. Grind until smooth. Do not over blend. Over blending may cause the mint leaves to release oils and can taste bitter. Keep this aside. If you want you can serve this as is or even refrigerate at this stage for later. 6. Add the rest of the thick yogurt (1 cup minus 1 tbsp) to a bowl. Add ½ to ¾ teaspoon roasted cumin and ½ to ¾ teaspoon chaat masala. Skip chaat masala if you don’t have. If you want you can reduce the quantity of yogurt but this is what we like at home. 7. Whisk very well until smooth. 8. Add the mint chutney to the yogurt. 9. Mix well and taste test. Adjust salt and spice to taste. Serve mint chutney as a side. 10. Wash pudina in a pot filled with clean water. Repeat the wash a few times and drain them in a colander. 11. Dry roast ¾ tbsp each of chana dal and urad dal on a low flame until golden. Add ½ tsp cumin to the hot pan and switch off. Cool these. If using bhuna chana skip roasting. You can also saute pudina in oil for 2 to 3 mins first and use. You can also use peanuts. Just roast the peanuts until aromatic and golden. 12. Add ½ cup chopped coconut, 2 to 3 green chilies, ½ inch ginger or 2 small garlic cloves, pudina, roasted dal, cumin and salt. You can also add a tsp of tamarind paste if you like the tang. 13. Blend them all together first for pudina chutney. 14. Add water as needed and blend to a smooth or coarse chatni to suit your liking. I prefer to use lemon juice now. Transfer the pudina chutney to a serving bowl. 15. Pudina chutney can be served without tempering too. To temper heat a small pan with little oil on a low flame. Add red chili, ¼ tsp mustard and ½ tsp urad dal. When the dal turns golden, add hing and switch off the stove. Pour this to the chutney. Mix well. Serve pudina chutney with south Indian breakfasts like idli dosa evening snacks

title: pudina paneer tikka recipe | mint paneer tikka | green paneer tikka
ingredients: 1 cup mint pudina, 0.5 cup coriander, 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 0.5 cup curd yogurt, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp lemon juice, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp salt, 9 cubes paneer cottage cheese, 9 cubes capsicum, 0.5 onion, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare pudina chutney in a small blender take 1 cup mint and ½ cup coriander. 2. also add 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger and 2 chilli. 3. blend to smooth paste without adding any water. 4. transfer the pudina chutney to a large bowl also add ½ cup curd. 5. further add ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ¼ tsp cumin powder and ½ tsp aamchur. 6. also add 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 tsp lemon juice, ¼ tsp, 2 tbsp oil and ½ tsp salt. 7. whisk and mix well making sure all the ingredients are well combined. 8. further, add 9 cubes paneer, 9 cubes capsicum and ½ onion. 9. mix gently without breaking anything. 10. cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to marinate well. 11. after 2 hours mix gently. 12. now take a skewer and pierce in capsicum, paneer and onion alternatively. 13. roast on tawa adding oil as required. 14. turn around and roast until it turns golden brown. you can also bake in oven or tandoor. 15. finally, sprinkle chaat masala and enjoy pudina paneer tikka recipe with green chutney.

title: Pudina Rice (Mint Rice Pulao)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 0.75 cup mint leaves, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 0.75 inch ginger, 2 medium garlic, 0.3333333333333333 cup raw coconut, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon Oil, 1.25 to 2 cups water, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 cup mix veggies carrots peas potatoes beans, 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3.5 cloves 3 to 4 cloves (/laung), 1 inch cinnamon, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Wash 1 cup aged basmati rice or any short rice. Soak it for about 15 minutes, until you pluck and prepare the mint and coriander leaves. Pluck ¾ cup mint leaves (without stalks) and ¼ cup coriander leaves (optional, can include tender stalks). Wash them in plenty of water, a few times. Remove and place them in a colander, shake off to drain as much water as possible. 2. To a grinder jar, add the coriander and mint leaves along with 3. ⅓ cup grated raw coconut (or ¼ cup chopped or ⅓ cup yellow onion cubed) 4. Grind to a fine paste, as fine as possible. Avoid adding water. 5. Heat 1½ to 2 tablespoons ghee or oil in a pan or cooker. If using Instant pot, cook on a saute mode. Add the following 6. small bay leaf / tej patta 1 star anise /chakri phool ⅓ teaspoon cumin seeds or shahi jeera 3 to 4 cloves /laung 1 inch cinnamon /dalchini 2 to 3 green cardamoms /elaichi 1 small strand mace /javitri (optional) 10 to 15 split cashews (optional) 7. Add the paste and saute for 3 to 4 minutes, until it turns aromatic and loses the raw flavor. If you want you may add 1 cup mixed veggies, such as potatoes, beans, carrots, peas etc & saute for 2 to 3 mins. These are optional and I have not used it here. 8. Firstly pluck mint leaves, add them to a large pot filled with water. Rinse them well in more water. Drain them in a colander. 9. Soak rice for at least 15 to 20 minutes. 10. Cook it and set aside to cool completely. You can cook rice in electric cooker, pressure cooker or pot. The rice must be grainy and not mushy. 11. While the rice cooks, add the mint leaves to a small blender jar. Blend it little coarsely without adding water. Set this aside. 12. To make pudina rice, heat oil in a pan. Next add peanuts, chana dal and urad dal when they are lightly golden, add mustard and red chili. Lower the heat. When mustard splutters, add curry leaves, ginger, green chilies. 13. Ginger may get burnt, be quick. Add the pudina. Saute for 1 minute. Switch off the stove. 14. Add turmeric to the pan and add this to the cooled rice. Lastly sprinkle salt and squeeze off lemon all over the rice. Mix well. I also like to add some extra virgin olive oil before the rice is mixed.

title: Pudina Rice Recipe | Mint Pulao - Pudina Pulao
ingredients: 1 bunch mint, 0.5 bunch coriander, 3 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 4 chilli, 1 black cardamom, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 javetri, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 1 onion, 2 tbsp peas, 1 carrot, 0.5 capsicum, 5 beans, 1 potato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 2 tsp biryani masala, 2 tsp salt, 1 cup basmati rice, 2 cup water, 2 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar, take 1 bunch of mint, ½ bunch of coriander, 3 cloves of garlic, 1 inch of ginger, 4 chillies, 1 black cardamom, 5 cloves, 1 inch of cinnamon and ½ javetri. 2. Grind to a smooth paste, adding water if required. Keep aside. 3. In a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp ghee. Add 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom. 4. Sauté until the spices turn aromatic. 5. Add 1 onion and sauté until the onion turns golden brown. 6. Further add 2 tbsp peas, 1 carrot, ½ capsicum, 5 beans and 1 potato. 7. Stir fry till the vegetables turn crunchy. 8. Also, add ½ tsp turmeric, 2 tsp biryani masala, 2 tsp salt and saute till the spices turn aromatic. 9. Add the prepared masala paste and cook well until the oil separates. 10. Now add 2 cups of water, 1 cup of basmati rice, 2 tsp of lemon juice, and mix gently. 11. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes. 12. Fluff the rice gently, ensuring it is non-sticky. 13. Finally, enjoy Pudina Pulao Recipe with raita.

title: Puffed rice upma | Uggani | Pori upma
ingredients: 25 gms puffed rice, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 3 tbsps fried gram, 2 tbsps Lemon juice, 2 tbsps coriander leaves, Salt, 2.5 tbsp oil, 3 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp jeera, 3 tbsp peanuts, 1 tbsp channa dal, 1 tbsp urad dal, 2 tbsps grated coconut, 0.25 cup capsicum, 0.25 cup carrots, 0.25 cup green peas
instructions: 1. Add them to a blender jar. 2. Make a fine powder. Set aside. 3. Fill a large pot with lots of water. Add puffed rice and keep stirring for 30 seconds. They will turn softer and soak up water. If you are using the thicker variety, then soak it for 5 minutes to soften. The airy variety just needs a quick wash otherwise they turn mushy. 4. Squeeze off the water from with both your palms. After squeezing they should be just moist and no excess water. 5. Heat oil in a pan. Then add mustard and cumin (optional). Also add chana dal, urad dal and peanuts. Fry peanuts and dals till golden & crunchy. 6. Add curry leaves, green chilies, onions and fry till onions turn lightly pink. 7. Add tomatoes, turmeric and salt. 8. Cover and cook till the tomatoes turn mushy. Then fry till the oil begins to leave the sides of the pan. 9. Lower the heat completely. Add puffed rice and mix well. 10. Cover the pot and heat on a low flame until the puffed rice upma turns hot. Do not do it for longer it will get burnt. 11. Turn off the heat. Add fried gram powder and coriander leaves. Mix well. Taste and add more salt if needed. Then squeeze the lemon juice. Do not add all the powder at once. Add little by little and adjust to suit your taste. Serve pori upma hot or warm with mirchi bajji, any fried snacks or even with plain yogurt. 12. Pour lots of water to a large pot and add puffed rice to it stirring with your hand. 13. If you are using thicker variety, leave it in water for around 5 mins. If you are using the very airy variety, just dunk them in water and squeeze up the water immediately else they turn mushy. 14. Add fried gram to a blender and make a fine powder. Set this aside. 15. Heat a wide and deep pan with oil. 16. Add mustard, cumin, dals and peanuts (if using). 17. Fry until the peanuts turn golden. Then add green chilies and curry leaves. Saute for a minute. 18. Add onions and fry for 2 to 3 mins until they turn pink. 19. Next add tomatoes, turmeric and salt. 20. Fry till the tomatoes turn mushy. If needed cover and cook on a low heat. 21. Saute the mixture till it begins to leave the sides. 22. Lower the flame completely. Add the rice puffs and mix well. 23. Cover and cook on the lowest heat until the puffed rice turns hot. Do not leave for long time as they burn quickly or stick to the bottom. 24. Turn off the stove and add 2 tbsps fried gram powder & coriander leaves. Mix well. 25. Taste it & add more fried gram powder, salt if needed and then lemon juice. 26. Mix well and serve puffed rice upma with mirchi bajji or plain yogurt.

title: Pulao Recipe | Veg Pulao | Vegetable Pulao | Pulav
ingredients: 1.5 cups basmati rice, 2 tablespoons oil, salt, 2.5 cups water, 1.5 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 1 medium onion, 2 green chilies, 1 medium carrot, 4 medium french beans, 0.5 cup green peas, 1 potato, 3 tablespoons mint, 1 bay leaf, 0.75 teaspoon shahi jeera, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 2 inch cinnamon, 1 star anise, 1 strand mace, 1 pinch nut meg, 2 pinches stone flower, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds powder
instructions: 1. Add basmati rice to a bowl and rinse it a few times until water runs clear. 2. Soak it for at least 20 minutes. After 20 mins, drain off the water completely and set aside. 3. While the rice soaks, rinse carrots, beans, peas, potatoes, onions, chilies & mint leaves. 4. Peel the carrots and potatoes. Nip off both the ends of beans and chop all of them to bite sized pieces. 5. Slice onions and slit green chill. Fine chop the mint leaves. (If you want you can also make a fine paste of ginger garlic, green chilies and mint.) Set all of these aside. 6. On a medium flame, heat 2 tablespoons ghee or oil in a pot or pressure cooker. 7. When the oil is slightly hot, add all the whole spices. 8. When the spices begin to sizzle, add the onions & chilies. Saute until onions turn golden. 9. Next saute ginger garlic paste, for 30 to 60 seconds, until the raw smell goes off. 10. Add all the veggies and mint. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes. 11. Pour water and add salt too. Taste the water, it has to be slightly salty. 12. Bring the water to a rolling boil. Next add drained rice and stir. 13. If cooking in pot, cover and cook on a low heat until all the water is absorbed and the rice grains are cooked. When the rice is done, turn off the stove. Cover and let the veg pulao rest in the pot for 10 to 15 mins. This helps the rice grains to become fluffy. 14. If making in pressure cooker, cover the cooker with the lid. Then cook on a medium high flame for 1 whistle. 15. Switch off the stove. When the pressure releases, remove the lid and fluff up the veg pulao rice with a fork. 16. Serve veg pulao hot or warm with a simple raita or gravy. 17. Press SAUTE button on the instant pot. Pour ghee or oil to the inner pot. Saute the spices in the hot oil for 30 seconds. 18. Fry onions & green chilies until transparent for 2 mins. Then fry ginger garlic paste just for 30 seconds. 19. Next add in all the chopped veggies and mint. Saute for 2 mins. Add the drained rice (1 ½ cups) and salt. Press CANCEL button. 20. Pour 1¾ cups water and mix. Taste the salt level and add more if needed. 21. Scrape the bottom gently with the spatula to release any bits of food stuck. This prevents a burn notice. Secure the instant pot with the lid. 22. Press PRESSURE COOK button (high pressure) & set the timer for 5 mins. Position the steam release handle to sealing. 23. Instant pot beeps when it is done. Let the pressure release naturally for 5 mins. 24. Release the rest manually by moving the steam release handle from sealing to venting with a spoon. 25. Fluff up the vegetable pulao with a fork & serve with yogurt raita.

title: Pulihora Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups aged rice, 1 tablespoon sesame oil, 0.25 teaspoon Turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 1 handful tamarind, 0.75 teaspoon Jaggery, 0.5 teaspoon mustard powder, 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 1 tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon channa dal, 0.25 cup peanuts, 2 red chilies, 2 Green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon Ginger, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Cooking rice - Add rice to a bowl & wash it well a few times. Pour 3 cups water. Place the bowl in a pressure cook and cover with a lid. Pressure cook for 2 to 3 whistles on a medium flame. When the pressure drops remove the bowl and transfer to a wide plate. Do not mash it. 2. Preparing rice - The rice has to be grainy and not mushy. Sprinkle salt and turmeric over the rice. Then pour 1 tbsp sesame oil. Let cool down and mix well. If needed add more turmeric to get the color you desire. 3. Making mustard powder (optional) - If you wish to use mustard powder, roast the seeds until slightly hot and cool. Powder and set aside. or You can roast sesame seeds until they begin to splutter. Cool and powder. Do not use both, use either of them. 4. Soaking Tamarind - Add tamarind to a small bowl and rinse it. Pour hot water just enough to immerse it. When it turns soft, squeeze and make a thick paste. Pass it through a filter, discard stones and pulp. 5. Heat a pan with oil. Add mustard, peanuts, chana dal & urad dal. Fry till the peanuts turn golden & aromatic. Add curry leaves, green chilies, red chilies and ginger (optional). Fry till the red chilies turn crisp 6. Add hing, followed by tamarind pulp, jaggery and little more salt. 7. Mix everything well & cook till the mixture turns thick & begins to leave the oil on the side. If you take off the mixture a bit early your pulihora will turn very moist & mushy. Turn off the stove. 8. The rice must be cool by now. Sprinkle mustard powder or sesame powder all over the rice. Then transfer ¾ of the cooked pulihora mixture to the rice. 9. Gently mix and add more if needed. If offering to God do not taste it. Else you can taste and add more salt as needed. If the pulihora is too dry add more oil & mix. 10. Serve pulihora with fresh homemade curd.

title: Pulihora Recipe | Chintapandu Pulihora | Andhra Style Tamarind Rice with Pulihora Premix
ingredients: 25 grams tamarind, 5 chilli, handful curry leaves, 3 tbsp salt, 2 tbsp jaggery, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp sesame, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp pepper, 1 tsp mustard, 0.25 tsp methi, 0.75 cup oil, 1 cup peanut, 1 tbsp mustard, 2 tbsp urad dal, 2 tbsp chana dal, 1 tsp hing, 5 red chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, soak 250 grams of tamarind in 2 cup of hot water for 30 minutes. 2. squeeze the pulp of tamarind and filter it out. 3. add more water to squeeze all the pulp from the tamarind. 4. concentrated tamarind pulp is ready. 5. transfer the tamarind pulp to a large kadai. 6. also add 5 chilli, a handful of curry leaves, 3 tbsp salt, 2 tbsp jaggery, and 1 tsp turmeric. 7. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 8. cover and boil for 20 minutes stirring in between. 9. cook until the tamarind is cooked well. 10. to prepare the pulihora spice mix, in a pan take 2 tbsp sesame, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp pepper, 1 tsp mustard, and ¼ tsp methi. 11. dry roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 12. cool the spices completely, and grind them to a fine powder. 13. transfer the pulihora masala powder to the tamarind mixture and cook for 1 minute. 14. to prepare the tempering, heat ¾ cup oil and fry 1 cup peanut until it turns crunchy. 15. add 1 tbsp mustard, 2 tbsp urad dal, 2 tbsp chana dal, 1 tsp hing, 5 dried red chilli, and a few curry leaves. 16. splutter the tempering and pour over the tamarind mix. 17. mix well and pulihora pulusu is ready to store in an airtight container for 6 months in the refrigerator. 18. to prepare the temple-style pulihora, mix 3 tbsp of tamarind rice instant mix with 3 cup of boiled rice. 19. finally, pulihora is ready to enjoy or pack to lunch box.

title: Puliyogare Recipe | Tamarind Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 4 tablespoons tamarind, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 2.5 tablespoons peanuts, 0.625 tablespoons chana dal, 0.625 tablespoons urad dal, Salt, 1.5 green chilies, 1 red chili, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.75 tablespoons chana dal, 0.75 tablespoons urad dal, 2.5 red chilies, 1 tablespoons coriander seeds, 0.5 tablespoons cumin, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 0.25 teaspoon pepper corn, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 1.5 tablespoons sesame seeds, 2.5 tablespoons coconut
instructions: 1. Wash rice a few times and cook to keep the rice grainy. Mushy rice doesn't go well to make puliyogare. 2. I usually cook rice with 2 cups of water in a pressure cooker for 3 whistles following the pot-in-pot method. 3. Spread the rice and cool it completely. 4. Soak tamarind in ½ to ¾ cup water. Add jaggery and stir until it dissolves. 5. When the tamarind turns soft, squeeze it well with your fingers and extract juice. 6. Taste test this and if you want it slightly more sweeter, add a bit more of jaggery. The sweetness reduces as you cook down. I prefer to pass it through a filter. Set this aside. 7. Heat a pan on a low to medium flame. Dry roast chana dal, urad dal and red chilies until lightly golden. 8. Add pepper corn. methi seeds and coriander seeds. 9. Continue to roast stirring often until methi begins to smell good. 10. Add cumin, mustard, sesame seeds and coconut. Continue to roast stirring continuously. 11. When the coconut begins to smell good. Add hing. 12. Switch off the stove and cool this completely. 13. Powder this as finely as possible in a blender. Set this aside. 14. Heat the same pan with oil and add mustard seeds. 15. When they begin to crackle, add dals and peanuts. 16. Roast them until lightly golden. Then add curry leaves, green chilies and red chilies. 17. When the peanuts and dal turn completely golden, add hing. 18. Pour the tamarind and jaggery water and stir. 19. Add turmeric and salt. Stir and begin to cook until slightly thick. 20. Then add the powder little by little and mix. 21. If the mixture becomes too thick, then add some more water to the tamarind and squeeze. Pour this water back to the pan. 22. Stir and cook on a medium heat until it thickens. 23. You can add 1 tbsp oil at this moment. 24. Cook on a low heat until the mixture turns thick and begins to leave oil. Add more salt if needed. 25. Add this to the cooled rice and mix. Do not add the entire mixture at one time. 26. Add little by little as needed and mix to prevent too much of puliyogare gojju in your rice. 27. You may be left with some extra puliyogare mix, which you can use up the next few days. 28. Allow puliyogare to absorb the flavors for about 30 mins then serve. 29. Usually tamarind rice is served without any accompaniment. You can serve it with some plain curd.

title: Pumpkin Curry Recipe
ingredients: 35 grams pumpkin, 1 cup chickpeas, 1.25 cup onions, 1.25 cup tomatoes, 1 green chilli, 0.5 tablespoon ginger paste, 0.5 tablespoon garlic paste, 3 tablespoons oil, 1.25 teaspoon salt, 1.25 cups water, 0.75 cup coconut milk, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1.5 teaspoons Kashmiri Red chilli powder, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1.5 teaspoons coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon fennel powder, 1 tablespoon Kasuri methi, 1.25 teaspoon amchur powder, 1 teaspoon jaggery, 1 Indian bay leaf, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 1 star anise
instructions: 1. Heat a pot with oil and add bay leaf, cinnamon and star anise. 2. When they begin to sizzle in the oil, add onions and green chilies. 3. Stir fry them until deep golden, switching between medium to low flames & vice versa as required. 4. Lower the flame, add ginger garlic and saute for about a minute, until the raw smell has gone. 5. Stir in ½ tsp salt and ground spices – red chilli powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric and fennel powder. 6. Quickly add the pumpkin and mix well. Stir in the tomatoes and stir fry for 2 to 3 mins. 7. Cover and cook on a medium to low flame until the tomatoes turn mushy. This takes about 5 to 7 mins. 8. Pour 1 cup hot water and add chickpeas (if using). Pour Coconut or almond milk. 9. Rise the flame and bring the curry to a rolling boil. Reduce the flame and cook covered until the pumpkin is fork tender and about to get mushy. As you cook, if required add more boiling hot water, this depends on your pumpkin. I had to add another ½ cup hot water. 10. Mix well and taste test. Add the following ingredients according to taste – ½ to 1 teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon garam masala, kasuri methi, jaggery (sugar) and amchur (or tamarind paste). Crush the kasuri methi before adding to the pan. 11. Pumpkin curry should be thick, yet of pouring consistency as it thickens further after cooling. If your curry is runny, cook uncovered on a high heat for a few minutes so some of the liquid evaporates. 12. Turn off and rest the pumpkin curry covered until serving. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. If you want you may squeeze in some lemon juice while serving. 13. Serve with Poori, Roti, Paratha, Naan, plain steamed rice, quinoa or millets.

title: Pumpkin Dal Recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup dal, 1.5 to 2 cups pumpkin, 0.875 cup tomatoes, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chilli, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2.25 cups water, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 teaspoon jaggery, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Add dal to a bowl and rinse it well at least thrice. Drain the water. If you don't have a pressure cooker, you may soak them for 30 to 45 mins so they cook faster in a pot. 2. Pour ghee or oil to a pot or pressure cooker and heat it. Add cumin, ginger, garlic, green chilli and curry leaves. 3. Saute until ginger garlic begins to smell good. Turn down the flame to low. Then add red chilli powder, turmeric, pumpkin and tomatoes. 4. Saute for 1 to 2 mins and add dal. Pour water and deglaze the pot. 5. Stovetop Pressure cooker - Cover the pressure cooker. Pressure cook on a medium heat until you hear 3 whistles. When the pressure drops, open the lid. 6. Instant pot - Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position the steam release valve to sealing. Set the timer to 11 mins. When the pressure drops, open the lid. 7. Regular sauce pot - Bring the water to a boil and simmer. Let the lentils cook slowly until done and tender. If required add more hot water as you cook them. 8. When the lentils are tender and fully done, add garam masala, kasuri methi and jaggery. Mix well, taste test and adjust salt. (if using spinach, cook further until the spinach is tender) 9. Turn off the heat and let cool down slightly. Then add coriander leaves and lemon juice. 10. Serve dal pumpkin with rice, roti, quinoa or naan.

title: Pumpkin Halwa | Kaddu Ka Halwa
ingredients: 2 cups pumpkin, 0.25 cup sugar, cashews, 1.5 tbsp ghee, 1 pinch cardamom powder, 0.25 cup milk
instructions: 1. Rinse pumpkin and cut to two. 2. Peel the skin and grate it or steam cook the entire pumpkin until soft. 3. Add ghee or coconut oil to a hot pan and fry the nuts till golden. Keep them side. 4. To the same pan, add grated pumpkin or cooked pumpkin (refer image step by step one). Fry for about 4 to 5 minutes till the raw smell goes off. 5. Pour the milk if using and add cardamom powder. Cook until the milk is absorbed. 6. Add sugar and cook till the sugar melts and the halwa thickens. 7. Add fried cashews and garnish. Serve pumpkin halwa hot or warm.

title: Punugulu Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups idli dosa batter, 2 tablespoons poha, Salt, oil, 2 green chilies, 0.25 cup onion, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.75 teaspoon ginger
instructions: 1. You can skip steps 1 to 5 if using idli dosa batter. Rinse urad dal and rice separately multiple times till the water looks clear. 2. Soak them for at least 4 hrs separately. Later drain and rinse the dal and rice. Add dal to a grinder along with 3 to 4 tablespoons water. If you want you can also add green chilies and cumin to the grinder at this stage or add them chopped to the batter directly later before frying. 3. Grind dal to a smooth thick batter. Do not make it runny. Transfer this to a mixing bowl. 4. Grind rice to a thick and slightly coarse batter, adding water only as required. Mix it well with the dal batter. Check the consistency now, it has to be thick of dropping consistency. 5. If you want you may ferment the batter for 4 to 6 hours. This is optional. 6. If the batter is runny, add powdered poha/ attukulu or rice flour or semolina. Set this side for 10 mins. It becomes thicker after it is soaked. Now adjust the consistency, if required, you may add very little water or can proceed as it is if the consistency is right. Check by making few fritters, if they are hard you need to add little water. 7. Add cumin, chopped chilies, ginger and onions to the batter. 8. Heat oil in a kadai on a medium flame. 9. To test drop a small portion of batter to the hot oil, the batter has to sizzle and rise to the surface but not sink. This is the right temperature. 10. Shape the batter to round balls with your fingers and drop them in the hot oil. 11. Do not disturb them for a few minutes. Later using a slotted spoon, stir and fry till golden and crisp. Remove punugulu to a cooling rack or steel colander. 12. serve them warm with your favorite chutney. 13. Mix 3/4 cup maida, 3 tbsp rice flour, 3 tbsp curd, 1 green chili, 1 small onion, 1/2 tsp. chopped ginger, salt as needed and 1/4 tsp soda. Set this aside for 30 to 45 minutes. 14. Heat oil and drop small portions of the batter in hot oil. Fry until golden.

title: Punugulu Recipe with Leftover Idli Dosa Batter
ingredients: 2 cup idli, 0.5 cup maida, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 onion, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, pinch baking soda, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl, take 2 cups of idli or dosa batter. Make sure to use leftover batter, as it's more sour. 2. Add ½ cup maida, 2 tbsp rice flour and ½ tsp salt. Mix well. Add more maida to adjust the consistency. 3. Also, add ½ onion, a few curry leaves, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp cumin, a pinch of hing and a pinch of baking soda. 4. Mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined. 5. Wet your hands with water, and pinch a ball-sized dough. 6. Drop in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 7. Stir occasionally, until the bonda turns golden brown and crisp. 8. Drain off to remove excess oil. 9. Finally, enjoy Punugulu Recipe with coconut chutney and tea.

title: Puran Poli Recipe (Bobbatlu)
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 2.5 cups water, 1 cup jaggery, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.1875 teaspoon nutmeg powder, 2.5 cups flour, 1 pinch salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 3 tablespoons oil, 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour, 3.5 tablespoons ghee
instructions: 1. Rinse and pressure cook chana dal until soft. If cooking in traditional cooker, cook for 5 to 6 whistles. If cooking in instant pot, pressure cook for 10 mins. 2. While the dal cooks, make the dough by mixing together flour, salt, turmeric and ¾ cup water. Make a soft dough adding more water as needed. Then drizzle oil and knead until all of the oil is absorbed. Cover and rest until the puran/ filling is ready. 3. When the pressure in the cooker drops, open the lid and strain the dal. Cool slightly and blend the dal to a fine powder or paste. 4. Add the smooth dal back to the same cooker pot along with grated/powdered jaggery. Begin to cook on a medium heat. Jaggery melts and the entire mixture becomes gooey. If using instant pot, cook on saute mode. 5. Keep stirring and cook until the puran turns thicker & much of the moisture evaporates. 6. Then add ghee. Saute until it leaves the pan and becomes thicker. Check step-by-step photos for the consistency. Add cardamom powder and nutmeg powder (optional). Cool this completely. 7. Divide the dough to 12 equal portions and the puran as well to 12 equal portions. Keep the dough covered. 8. Take a dough ball and dip it in all-purpose flour. Dust off the excess. Press down in the center spread the dough to a 4 inch cup or disc. You can roll it with a rolling pin or do it with your fingers. 9. Place the puran in the center and press it down inside. Gently bring the sides over the stuffing by tapping the cup on your other hand & pushing the puran in with your finger. 10. Bring the edges together and seal them carefully. Flatten the ball and dip it in flour. Dust off the excess. Sprinkle some flour on the rolling area and place the ball. Begin to roll evenly all over without putting pressure. 11. Roll to a 8 to 9 inch puran poli. Take care not to tear off the covering. If you see the poli becoming too thin in certain places, sprinkle some flour and gently apply it. You can make about 3 to 4 polis before you begin to cook them. 12. Heat a pan on a medium high flame. When it is hot enough, dust off the excess flour from the puran poli and gently transfer it to the hot pan. 13. Soon you will begin to see bubbles. Flip it to the other side and cook gently pressing down with a spatula. 14. Puran poli will puff soon, then drizzle a generous amount of ghee about 1 tsp. Flip it to the other side as well and drizzle more ghee. 15. Press down with a spatula as needed and cook well until you see golden to light brown spots on it. The edges also should get cooked well. 16. Transfer to a serving plate and serve with melted ghee or milk. Ensure you smear enough ghee before you stack them. 17. Serve puran poli with ghee, katachi amti, milk or this badam milk.

title: puran poli recipe | puran poli sweet | maharashtrian pooran poli
ingredients: 1.5 cup chana dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp ghee, 3 cup water, 1.5 cup jaggery, 1 tsp ghee, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 2 cup wheat flour, 1 cup maida, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, water, maida, ghee
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1½ cup chana dal for 1 hour. 2. drain off the water and transfer it to the cooker. 3. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp ghee, 3 cup water. 4. pressure cook for 3 whistles on medium flame. 5. now drain off the water separating dal and water. 6. transfer the dal to a large kadai. 7. add 1½ cup jaggery and mix well. 8. once the jaggery melts, start to mash the dal. 9. mash until the dal turns smooth paste. you can alternatively, grind the dal and jaggery into the mixi jar. 10. keep cooking until the mixture thickens and hold the shape. 11. now add 1 tsp ghee and ½ tsp cardamom powder and cook for a minute, or until the mixture starts to separate the pan. if the mixture is undercooked, then the stuffing will be sticky and will be difficult to make a ball. 12. transfer to the plate and cool slightly. make sure not to overcook, else the stuffing turns hard. 13. grease hands with ghee and makes ball-sized stuffing. 14. finally, puran is ready. keep aside. 15. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup wheat flour, 1 cup maida, ¼ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp salt and 2 tbsp oil. 16. mix well making sure all the ingredients are well combined. 17. now add water as required and start to knead the dough. 18. knead to a smooth and soft dough adding water in batches. 19. grease the dough with 2 tsp oil, cover and rest for at least 1 hour. 20. after 1 hour, punch and knead the dough again. 21. pinch a ball sized dough and make a smooth ball. 22. form a cup by pressing the dough from the sides. 23. place a ball sized prepared puran (dal stuff). 24. start to stuff inside by pulling the dough without having any pleats. 25. now seal the dough without any cracks. 26. dust the ball with maida and flatten slightly. 27. now roll the holige gently, without putting much pressure. 28. roll as thin as possible and making sure the puran is distributed uniformly. 29. now cook the puran poli on hot tawa keeping the flame on medium. 30. flip over once the base is half cooked. 31. spread the ghee on both sides and cook well. 32. puff the puran poli, making sure to cook uniformly 33. finally, enjoy puran poli with ghee or milk.

title: puri recipe for pani puri | gol gappe puri recipe | golgappa recipe
ingredients: 1 cup sooji semolina rava, 2 tbsp maida plain flour all purpose flour refined flour, pinch of baking soda, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 0.25 cup water, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 1 cup sooji and 2 tbsp maida 2. also add pinch of baking soda, ¼ tsp salt and 1 tsp oil. 3. combine all the ingredients well. 4. further add ¼ cup water and start to knead. 5. knead to smooth and soft adding more water if required. 6. grease the dough with oil. cover and rest for 20 minutes or untill the rava absorbs moisture. 7. now knead the dough again making sure the dough is stiff absorbing moisture. 8. divide the dough into half and dust with maida. 9. furthermore, roll the dough as thin as possible using a rolling pin. 10. take a small cup and start to make small puris. 11. drop the prepared puri and press with the spoon to puff up. 12. once they puff, it will automatically turn over. if not, once the bottom side is golden brown, turn over the puri. 13. and fry the puri till golden brown and crisp. 14. remove the puri and drain over tissue paper to remove excess oil. 15. finally, golgappa puri is ready to prepare pani puri or bhel puri.

title: pyaz ki kachori recipe | onion kachori | jaipuri pyaaz kachori recipe
ingredients: 3 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 2 onion, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.25 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup besan gram flour, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 cup maida plain flour, 1 tbsp rava semolina suji, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp ghee clarified butter, water to knead, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, pinch a small lemon sized ball and flatten it. 2. now scoop 1 tbsp of prepared pyaz stuffing and place in the centre. 3. get the edges together and form a bundle. 4. close the top by pressing and flattening. 5. furthermore, gently press the edges and flatten to form a poori size. 6. when the oil is medium hot, add prepared kachori. 7. do not touch it for a minute or till they float. then press with the spoon to puff up. turn around and fry the kachori till it turns golden brown all over. 8. finally, serve pyaz kachori as it is or with green chutney and tamarind chutney.

title: Quinoa Fried Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup quinoa, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrots, 0.3333333333333333 cup green beans, 0.3333333333333333 cup Bell peppers, 3 sprigs spring onions, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chilli, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper, 1.25 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon Soya Sauce, 0.5 teaspoon rice vinegar, 1 teaspoon oil, 125 grams paneer extra firm pressed tofu, 2.5 eggs
instructions: 1. So to get rid of the saponins, it is essential to rinse them well before you cook the quinoa. Pre-rinsed quinoa is also available in the markets. You may use that for convenience if you prefer not to do it. 2. To rinse quinoa (1 cup for this recipe), put it in a fine mesh strainer. Wash it under cold running water, gently agitating it with your fingers, until the water runs clear. Alternatively, you can add quinoa to a bowl and cover it with cold water. Rub them around with your fingers and discard the water carefully. 3. The water should change color because of the saponins. Pour off the water and repeat the previous steps until the water runs clear. 4. When you’re ready to cook the quinoa, add your rinsed quinoa and water or broth to a pot or bowl. You’ll want about 1 ¾ cups of liquid for every 1 cup of dry rinsed quinoa to cook in a pot. To cook in instant pot, you will need 1¼ cups water. 5. Bring your water or broth to a boil over high heat and then immediately turn the heat down to a simmer. Let the quinoa cook until it absorbs most of the liquid. When you see a few tablespoons of liquid, cover the pot and let cook on the lowest heat for a few more minutes until all the liquid is absorbed. 6. Turn the heat off and let the quinoa steam in the covered pan/pot for 10 minutes. Fluff the quinoa with a fork and set it aside to cool while you prepare the ingredients for the quinoa fried rice. 7. Chop ⅓ cup carrots (1 medium), ⅓ cup green beans (8 to 10), ⅓ cup bell peppers, 1 tablespoon chopped garlic, and one green chili and 3 spring onions, if using. Combine the tablespoon of soya sauce and ½ teaspoon of rice vinegar (optional) in a small bowl. Whisk 2 or 3 eggs, if using, or crumble 125 grams of paneer or tofu if using those instead. 8. To cook the quinoa fried rice, heat 2 tablespoons oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Fry the chopped garlic, green chili, and the whites of the spring onions (optional) for a minute or until aromatic, without burning them. 9. Add the chopped vegetables and ¼ teaspoon salt. (If using mushrooms, cabbage or frozen veggies, avoid salt here). 10. Stir fry on the highest heat until just tender before reducing the heat to medium. 11. If you’re adding eggs, move the vegetables to the side and pour them in. 12. Scramble the eggs until soft. 13. Don’t overcook them or they’ll be rubbery by the end! 14. Pour in the cooled quinoa and soya sauce mixture. 15. Add ¼ teaspoon black pepper, and more salt. Stir fry everything together until the quinoa heats up. 16. Once the quinoa is hot, add the spring onion greens (optional). Turn off the heat, and season to taste if necessary. Your quinoa fried rice is ready to serve hot or warm!

title: Quinoa Pulao (Vegetable Quinoa Recipe)
ingredients: 1 cup quinoa, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 1 onion, 1 green chili pepper, 0.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.375 cup carrots, 0.25 cup green peas, 2.5 green beans, 1 tomato, 11 mint leaves chopped finely, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1.25 cups water, 2 cups water
instructions: 1. Pour quinoa to a large bowl and pour 3 to 4 cups of water. Rub them well for a few seconds and drain the water. 2. Pour more water and rinse them again. I rinse them thrice. Lastly drain them to a colander and set aside. Rinse and chop all the veggies. 3. Press saute button and pour oil to the steel insert. Add all the whole spices. When they sizzle, add onions and chilies. Saute until transparent for 2 mins. 4. Saute ginger garlic paste for 30 seconds and stir in turmeric, salt, red chili powder, garam masala, mint and mixed veggies. Saute until the tomatoes breakdown. 5.
