Add quinoa and saute for 30 seconds. Press Cancel and pour water. Mix well to release any bits of food stuck. Cover the IP and position the steam release valve to sealing. 1¼ cups water 6. Add quinoa and saute for 30 seconds. Press Cancel and pour water. Mix well to release any bits of food stuck. Cover the IP and position the steam release valve to sealing. 7. Pressure cook for 1 min in new IP or 2 to 3 mins in old IP (read notes). Let the pressure drop naturally before opening the lid. 8. Let the quinoa pulao cool down slightly. Taste test and add more salt and lemon juice before serving. Do not stir a lot when it is hot. 9. On a low to medium flame heat oil in a pot. Add all the whole spices. 10. When they begin to sizzle add sliced or chopped onions and chilies. Saute until they turn golden or transparent. 11. Add ginger garlic and fry until you get a nice aroma. Do not burn. Reduce the heat and stir in salt, garam masala, turmeric and chilli powder. 12. Add the chopped veggies and mint. Stir fry for 60 seconds. If using tomato then saute until they break down and turn mushy. 13. Transfer drained quinoa and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. Pour water and give a good stir. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 2 cups water 14. Transfer drained quinoa and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. Pour water and give a good stir. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 15. Bring the water to a rolling boil on a high heat & then reduce the flame to medium. Cook until the quinoa turns tender and absorbs all the water. 16. When quinoa is cooked fully they look transparent. Cover and reduce the flame to low. Let the quinoa pulao rest on the stove for 5 to 7 mins. Squeeze some lemon juice if desired. Serve with raita.

title: Quinoa Upma Recipe (Instant Pot and Stovetop)
ingredients: 1 cup quinoa, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.3333333333333333 cup onion, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1.5 green chili, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 lemon, 0.5 cup carrot, 0.25 cup green beans, 0.25 cup green peas, 1.25 cups water to cook in Instant Pot, 2 cups water to cook on stovetop in a pot, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon channa dal, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 1 pinch hing, 13.5 cashews
instructions: 1. Add quinoa to a bowl or strainer and pour water. Rub them very well and discard the water. Rinse them at least thrice and drain in the strainer. shake off gently to get rid of the excess water. 2. Rinse and chop the ginger, carrots, beans and onion (optional). Slit the green chili. 3. Press saute button and pour oil to the steel insert of the Instant pot. 4. When the oil turns slightly hot, add mustard, cumin, chana dal, urad dal and cashews. 5. Fry them until slightly golden. Then stir in curry leaves, ginger and green chili. 6. Stir in the onions & saute until transparent. Add the vegetables and saute for a minute. 7. Add turmeric, salt, quinoa and water. 8. Press CANCEL button. Secure the lid of the instant pot. Position the steam release handle to sealing. Press PRESSURE COOK button & Set the timer to 2 minutes. 9. It takes time for the pot to build up pressure and cook. Once the pressure builds up, it cooks for 2 minutes. When it is done cooking the Ip beeps and goes to keep warm, press cancel. Wait for the pressure to release naturally for at least 8 mins. You may release the rest manually. 10. Open the lid and fluff up the quinoa upma. Sprinkle some lemon juice and serve hot. 11. Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, chana dal and urad dal. Fry them for a minute and add the cashews. 12. Fry them until light golden and add curry leaves, green chilies and ginger. Stir fry for a minute until aromatic and add the onions. Saute for 2 minutes. 13. Stir in mixed veggies & saute for a minute. Add salt, turmeric and quinoa. Pour water and mix well. 14. Cook uncovered on a medium flame until quinoa turns tender and absorbs all the water. Cover and Turn off the heat. Let rest for 5 to 6 minutes. 15. Fluff up the quinoa. Mix and taste test to adjust salt. Squeeze lemon juice before serving quinoa upma.

title: Rabri Recipe (Rabdi)
ingredients: 1.5 liter full fat milk, 4.5 tablespoons sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 strands saffron, 1 teaspoon rose water, 1 almonds, 17.5 pistachios
instructions: 1. Grease or rinse a heavy bottom pot and pour milk to it. 2. Bring it to a boil then reduce the flame to low. Let the milk simmer until it reduces approximately to half the original quantity (to 3 cups). 3. While the milk simmers, bring 1 cup water to a boil in a small pot. Add almonds and pistachios. 4. Turn off the heat and rest the nuts until the skin loosens (for about 10 to 15 mins). Drain them and peel the skin. Chop them finely and keep aside. 5. Transfer 2 to 3 tablespoons of hot milk from the pot to a small bowl. Add saffron strands and leave them to soak. 6. If you do not want to have layers of cream in your rabdi, you can skip this step. After simmering the milk for a while, a layer of cream forms on top of the boiling milk. Gently move that to the sides of the pot and stick up. (check the step-by-step pictures above or video) 7. Next stir once to prevent the milk from getting scorched at the bottom. 8. Continue to simmer the milk, slide the layer of cream to the sides of the pot every time it forms and repeat sticking it up to the side. Stir again once. This process of sliding and sticking to the sides should happen every time a layer of cream forms until the milk reduces to half the original quantity. I.e 3 cups in this case. 9. Do not forget to stir at the bottom every time you slide the cream. 10. When the milk reduces to half, add sugar and then scrape off the layers of cream from the sides of the pot. If the milk cream has dried up, pour some hot milk (from the pot) over the sides. This will soften it and loosen. Add it back to the milk. 11. Simmer until the quantity reduces to 2 cups. Add nuts, saffron milk, cardamom powder & rose water. Turn off the heat. 12. Chill rabri and garnish with nuts just before serving. It can be served with gulab jamun, malpuas & jalebi.

title: rabri recipe | rabdi recipe | how to make malpua rabdi
ingredients: 1 litre milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp saffron milk, 7 pistachios, 5 almonds, 1 cashews
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large nonstick pan heat thick cows or buffaloes milk. 2. furthermore, stir occasionally and get to a boil. 3. also reduce the flame to medium. and once a layer of cream is formed over the milk, stick it to sides of vessel. 4. further, get the milk to boil again. 5. and repeat the gathering of cream to sides of kadai. 6. repeat the process for atleast 5 times or till milk reduces to one-third. 7. further, add sugar. 8. also add cardamom powder and saffron milk. 9. give a good mix and get the milk again to boil. 10. also scrape off the collected cream from sides. and give a good stir. 11. transfer to a bowl and let it attain to room temperature. 12. further, serve garnished with chopped dry fruits or use it to prepare rasmalai. 13. finally serve rabdi / rabri chilled or hot.

title: Radish Chutney Recipe | Mullangi Chutney
ingredients: 1 radish, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp chana dal, 1 tbsp urad dal, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin, 5 red chilli, 3 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, piece tamarind, 2 tbsp water, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 3 clove garlic, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. Firstly, peel the skin of 1 radish and chop it well. Keep aside. 2. In a pan, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tbsp chana dal, 1 tbsp urad dal, 1 tsp coriander seeds, ½ tsp cumin and 5 dried red chilli. 3. Roast on a low flame till the spices turn aromatic. Keep aside. 4. In the same kadai, add 3 cloves of garlic, 1 inch of ginger and saute slightly. 5. Now add 2 cups chopped radish and saute well. 6. Also, add ½ onion and saute till the onion softens. 7. Now add 1 tsp of salt, a small piece of tamarind, and 2 tbsp of water. 8. Cover and cook for 5 minutes. 9. Cook till the radish softens slightly. Cool completely. 10. Now transfer the reserved masala into the mixer jar and grind to a fine powder. 11. Also, add in cooked radish and grind to a smooth paste. Transfer into the bowl. 12. To prepare the tempering, heat 2 tsp oil. Add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, 3 cloves of garlic, and a few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering and pour over the chutney. 13. Finally, enjoy the Radish Chutney Recipe with hot steamed rice or as a breakfast treat.

title: Ragda Chaat Recipe | Matar Chaat Recipe | How to Make Ragda Chaat
ingredients: 1 cup white matar safed matar, 2 potato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp black salt, 1 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp garam masala, onion, tomato, green chutney, tamarind chutney, sev, chilli powder, coriander, chaat masala
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup white matar, 2 potato, ½ tsp turmeric and 1 tsp salt. 2. Pour in 3 cup water, pressure cook for 5 whistles. 3. Take the boiled potato and mash it smooth. Keep aside. 4. In a kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. Add ½ tsp cumin and pinch hing, splutter on low flame. 5. Add 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger and saute well. 6. Also add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp black salt, 1 tsp chaat masala and 1 tsp garam masala. 7. Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 8. Add 2 tbsp water and cook the masala. 9. Now add in the mashed potato and mix well. 10. Pour in boiled ragda and mix well. 11. Adjust the consistency, and boil for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 12. To prepare the chaat, pour in ragda into the plate. 13. Top with onion, tomato, green chutney, tamarind chutney and sev. 14. Also, top it with chilli powder, coriander, and chaat masala. 15. Finally, enjoy Ragda Chaat Recipe.

title: Ragda Patties Recipe (Ragda Pattice)
ingredients: 1 cup white peas, 1.5 teaspoon oil, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 green chilli, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 2.75 to 3 cups water, 1 tablespoon tamarind paste, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 5 grams potatoes, 0.5 cup poha, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chilli, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.625 cup tamarind chutney, 0.25 cup green chutney, 2.5 tablespoons red garlic chutney, 0.25 cup onions, 0.25 cup tomatoes, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 3 tablespoons nylon sev, 1 teaspoon chaat masala
instructions: 1. Rinse white peas well under running water and soak them for atleast 8 hours to overnight. Later drain the water and give a good rinse. 2. Heat oil in a cooker and add ginger, garlic and green chilies. If using Instant pot, follow the same steps on the saute mode. 3. Saute them until aromatic for about 1 minute. Then add hing, peas, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder and garam masala. 4. Saute for 1 minute & pour water. Mix well. 5. Pressure cook on a medium heat for 6 whistles. If using Instant pot, press pressure cook button and set the timer to 11 minutes. 6. When the pressure releases completely, open the lid and check if the peas are done. They should be soft cooked & not al dente. If they are undercooked, pressure cook for a little longer. 7. Take 3 to 4 tbsps cooked peas to a small bowl and mash them very well. Then add back to the ragda so it turns thick. 8. Add tamarind & jaggery (or 1 to 2 tbsps sweet tamarind chutney). Mix well and taste test. If you want you can also add more garam masala & salt at this stage. 9. Simmer ragda for a few more minutes until it turns thicker. Turn off when it is of pouring consistency as it thickens a bit after cooling. 10. Boil potatoes in a pot or pressure cooker just until fork tender. Do not over cook them. I boiled the potatoes along with the white peas in the instant pot for 11 minutes. (check my step-by-step photos above for more details) 11. Peel the potatoes while still hot or warm. Crumble or mash them well and cool completely. Then add chaat masala, ginger, green chili, salt, coriander leaves & 4 to 6 tbsps bread crumbs (or poha powder). 12. Mix all of them and taste test. Add more salt if needed. The mixture must be non-sticky. If it is sticky then add more bread crumbs or poha powder. 13. Divide the dough to 8 portions and make balls. Then flatten them. If you want to make them extra crisp, roll the pattice in poha powder and press down so it sticks to the patties. 14. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a non-stick pan and spread it. When the oil becomes hot, place the patties and fry them on a medium high flame. 15. When the base turns golden and crisp, invert them and fry until golden. Set these aside. 16. Assemble only when you are ready to eat else the patties become soft. 17. Pour 2 ladles of ragda to a serving plate. Pour 1 tablespoon of sweet tamarind chutney & some green chutney. Then place the patties on it. 18. Top with all the 3 chutneys as much as you want. Then sprinkle coriander leaves, onions and tomatoes. 19. Sprinkle some sev and chaat masala. Serve immediately.

title: ragda patties recipe | ragda pattice | ragda petis chaat recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 2 bay leaf, 0.5 tsp cloves, 3 pods cardamom, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp fennel, pinch hing, 1 onion, 1 chilli, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup white peas, 3 cup water, 2 potato, 1 tsp chaat masala, 3 potato, 0.25 cup thick poha, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp salt, oil, green chutney, tamarind chutney, chaat masala, salad, sev
instructions: 1. firstly, in a cooker heat 2 tbsp oil. add 2 bay leaf, ½ tsp cloves, 3 pods cardamom, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp fennel and pinch hing. 2. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 3. now add 1 onion, 1 chilli and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. saute well. 4. further, keeping the flame on low add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp aamchur and 1 tsp salt. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 5. further, add 1 cup white peas and 3 cup water. 6. cover and pressure cook for 4 whistles or until the peas are cooked well. 7. adjust the consistency by adding water. the ragda thickens once cooled. 8. further, add 2 potato and 1 tsp chaat masala. 9. mix well and get to a boil adjusting the consistency as required. 10. finally, ragda is ready to make chaats. 11. firstly, in a bowl take 3 potato, ¼ cup thick poha. adding poha helps to absorb the moisture. 12. add ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp garam masala and 2 chilli. 13. also add ½ tsp ginger paste, 2 tbsp coriander and ½ tsp salt. 14. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 15. grease hands with oil and prepares a small pattice. 16. shallow fry on medium flame or you can pan fry or deep fry. 17. fry on both sides until it turns golden and crisp. 18. aloo tikki is ready. keep aside. 19. firstly, on a plate place 2 aloo tikki. 20. top with green chutney and tamarind chutney. 21. further, pour a ladleful of prepared ragda over it. 22. garnish with chopped onions, sev and chaat masala. 23. finally, enjoy ragda pattice with evening chai.

title: Ragda Recipe - Multipurpose Curry for Chaat Recipes
ingredients: 1 cup white peas, 2 potato, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp kala namak, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 2 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp tamarind chutney
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup soaked white peas, 2 potato, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp turmeric and 3 cup water. 2. Cover and pressure cook for 5 whistles or until the peas are cooked well. 3. Once the pressure releases, keep the boiled potato aside. 4. In a kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing and splutter tempering. 5. Also, add 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger and saute well. 6. Further, keeping the flame on low add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp aamchur, 1 tsp chaat masala, ½ tsp kala namak and ½ tsp coriander powder. 7. Saute on low flame, until the spices turn aromatic. 8. Mash the potato and cook well. 9. Add in boiled peas and mix well. 10. Adjust the consistency, adding 2 cup water, 1 tsp salt or as required. 11. Simmer and boil for 10 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 12. Also, add 2 tbsp tamarind chutney and mix well. 13. Finally, Ragda is ready to prepare chaats.

title: Ragi cake
ingredients: 0.75 cup ragi flour, 0.75 cup wheat flour, 2 tbsps cocoa powder, 1 cup sugar, 0.3333333333333333 cup curd whisked, 0.6666666666666666 cup butter, 0.75 cup milk, 0.5 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp vanilla extract, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.5 tsp baking soda, 0.125 tsp salt, 1 cup full fat milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 2.5 tbsp cocoa powder, 0.25 cup sugar
instructions: 1. Pour milk. You can use any milk. 2. Add vinegar & vanilla extract. 3. Pour melted butter. You can also use oil, I had used virgin coconut oil before it works well without the flavor of oil in the cake. 4. Whisk the curd and pour it. 5. Begin to mix gently. 6. There should be no lumps. Do not over mix. 7. Transfer this to the greased cake pan. Knock a few times to the counter. 8. Preheat the oven to 170 C for at least 15 mins. If using a fan forced oven, preheat at 160 C. 9. Fluff up the flour in the jar with a fork. Spoon it to a measuring cup. Level the flour. Do the same while you measure the cocoa. Sieve together ragi flour, wheat flour, salt, baking powder, baking soda and cocoa. Sieve thrice. 10. To the same bowl, add sugar or jaggery. 11. Pour milk, vinegar, vanilla, curd, and butter to the bowl. Whisk the curd before adding it. 12. Mix everything well just until combined. Do not over mix. 13. Pour the mixture to the greased tray. 14. Bake for 25 to 30 mins. Mine was done at 30 mins. When the cake is done a skewer inserted comes out clean. 15. Pour milk to a pot or pan. 16. Add cocoa and sugar. Mix well and boil stirring often. 17. Within few minutes the mixture turns thick. Add vanilla extract and turn off the heat. 18. Place the cake on a deep serving plate. Pour the chocolate sauce over the cake. 19. Gently spread with a spatula. Sprinkle some white chocolate or sprinkles. 20. Slice ragi cake and serve.

title: Ragi Chilla Recipe | Instant Healthy Ragi Cheela
ingredients: 0.75 cup ragi flour finger millet flour, 0.75 cup besan gram flour, 0.75 cup curd, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 1 tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, few curry leaves, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 carrot, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp sesame, pinch hing, ghee
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take ¾ cup ragi flour, ¾ cup besan, ¾ cup curd and 1 tsp salt. 2. Mix well using a whisk. 3. Also add 1 cup water and mix forming a flowing consistency batter. 4. Now add 1 tsp ginger paste, 2 chilli, few curry leaves, ½ onion, 2 tbsp spring onion, ½ carrot, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp sesame and pinch hing. 5. Mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined. Feel free to add vegetables of your choice. 6. Now heat the pan, and pour the batter. 7. Spread gently making sure the chilla is as thin as possible. Spread half tsp ghee or oil. 8. Cook on medium flame, making sure the base is cooked well. 9. Flip over and roast both sides. 10. Finally, enjoy Ragi Chilla Recipe with pickle and raita. 11. Now heat the pan, and pour the batter. Spread gently, making sure the chilla is as thin as possible. Spread half tsp ghee or oil.

title: Ragi Cookies (Ragi Biscuits)
ingredients: 0.5 cup ragi flour, 0.5 cup wheat flour, 5 tbsp sugar, 0.625 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 7 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp milk
instructions: 1. Firstly powder 4.5 to 5 tbsp sugar. It will give you about half cup. Measure and use it. 2. Add ragi and wheat flour to a mixing bowl. Next add in baking powder or cardamom powder. Mix them well until combined. 3. Melt the ghee and cool it. Pour 6 tbsp of ghee to the flour mixture. 4. Begin to mix well until combined. If the dough doesn't come together, then pour 1 to 2 tbsp of more ghee or milk. Use just as needed. I have used only 7 tbsp ghee. 5. Bring the dough together and make a ball. Cover and keep in the fridge for 30 mins. You can also keep in the freezer for 10 mins. 6. Preheat the oven to 170 C or 350 F for 15 mins. Divide the dough to 14 to 16 balls. Flatten them slightly and press down with a fork. 7. Place them on a baking tray, 1 inch apart from the other as they become big after baking. 8. Baking in Oven - Bake for 12 to 14 mins depending on your oven and thickness of the biscuit.Baking in cooker - Preheat the cooker with salt at the bottom. Remove the gasket and weight. When the cooker turns hot, place the tray in the cooker and bake on the lowest flame for 12 to 15 mins. You can bake in 2 to 3 batches. 9. When done the biscuit top will be non sticky. They will be slightly soft in the center but when they cool down they will firm up. 10. Transfer to a wire rack and cool completely. Store ragi biscuits in a air tight jar and use up to 3 weeks.

title: Ragi Dosa Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup ragi flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 2 tablespoons semolina, 2 cups water, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger, 5.5 curry leaves, 2 tablespoons onions, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 tablespoons ghee oil, 0.5 cup rice, 0.5 cup ragi, 0.5 cup urad dal, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, salt as needed, 2 tablespoon oil
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, stir together ragi flour, semolina, rice flour, onions, chilies, curry leaves, coriander leaves, cumin, salt and ginger. Pour half of the water and begin to mix to a batter. Pour the remaining water in batches and mix to a lump free batter. 2. The consistency of the batter should be very runny for a thin dosa, slightly thicker for a thicker dosa. Rest aside for 10 to 15 mins while you make your chutney. 3. Grease your pan and heat it on a medium high flame until really hot. A few drops of water sprinkled on the pan should sizzle and evaporate. 4. Stir the batter very well as the solids settle at the bottom. Regulate the flame to medium high and begin to pour the batter, moving forward - starting from the edges in a circular pattern to cover the entire pan. 5. You will have some unfilled pockets, fill them up with the batter. (Check the pictures) 6. Drizzle 1 teaspoon ghee or oil across the edges & over the dry spots. Let cook for 3 mins on a medium high until the edges begin to leave the pan. 7. Gently turn with a spatula to the other side and cook for another 1 min. Lastly turn it again and cook until the base is crisp, for another minute. This is just a guideline for timing, it may vary depending on the kind of stove and cookware used. 8. Remove to a serving plate. Before you make the next ragi dosa, make sure the pan is really hot (not smoking) and give the batter a good stir else the solids settle at the bottom. 9. Rinse ragi and rice together and soak them for 8 hours. Later wash urad dal & methi seeds thoroughly and soak for at least 4 to 5 hours. 10. Drain them and rinse well. Blend the urad dal with water as needed until frothy and thick. Transfer this to a bowl. 11. To the same jar, add ragi (or ragi flour) and rice. Blend well with water to a slightly coarse medium batter (not too thick or too runny). Mix this well with little salt. You can avoid salt now and add after fermenting. 12. Ferment this batter for 6 to 8 hours depending on the climate. The batter must rise at least slightly. 13. Pour water and bring to a desired consistency. To make crisp dosas keep the batter slightly thick. For thin dosas make the batter slightly thin. 14. Grease the pan and heat up. The pan must turn hot enough. 15. Pour 1 ladle of ragi dosa batter and spread it as you like thick or thin. Drizzle some oil or ghee. When the sides leave the pan, flip it and cook on the other side.

title: Ragi Dosa Recipe - Instant & Healthy
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour, 0.5 cup ragi flour, 0.25 cup rava semolina, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 2 chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 inch ginger, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 1 cup rice flour, ½ cup ragi flour, and ¼ cup rava. 2. Also, add 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt and 3 cup water. 3. Whisk and mix well making sure there are no lumps. 4. Now add ½ onion, ½ carrot, 2 chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander and 1 inch ginger. 5. Also, add 1 cup water and mix well adjusting the consistency. 6. When the pan is super hot, pour the batter on the pan. 7. Spread 1 tsp oil and roast for 2 minutes or until the dosa turns crisp. 8. Finally, enjoy the Crispy Ragi Dosa Recipe with chutney. 9. notes: 10. Firstly, adjust the consistency of the batter by adding water as required. If the batter is thick, then the dosa will be soft. 11. also, the chutney tastes great when prepared spicy. 12. Additionally, you can add maida for the batter to help with binding. 13. Finally, the crispy Crispy Ragi Dosa Recipe tastes great when served hot with spicy chutney.

title: Ragi idli recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 1.5 cups ragi, 1 teaspoon methi seeds, 0.625 teaspoon non-iodized salt
instructions: 1. Add urad dal and methi seeds to a bowl and rinse them well thrice. Soak for 5 hours. 2. To another bowl, add ragi and rinse them a few times very well. Add fresh water and soak them for 5 hours. 3. If using poha then soak them 30 minutes before blending. 4. After 5 hours drain the water. Add the urad dal, methi seeds (or soaked poha) to a blender jar. 5. I always add salt while blending. If you live in a hot place, then you may add after fermenting. 6. Pour chilled water during summers and blend the batter until thick, fluffy and frothy. The batter should be thick yet of pouring consistency. 7. Transfer this to a large bowl. 8. Add ragi to the blender and add little water. Blend to a slightly coarse paste. I do not make it very fine as this gives the texture of rava. 9. Next add more water as needed while blending. 10. Pour this to the blended urad dal batter and give a good mix. The batter should be thick like the regular idli batter. If needed sprinkle some water at this stage. 11. Cover and keep it in a warm place. Leave overnight to 14 hours until it ferments and rises well. 12. When fermented, you should not have the raw smell of urad dal or ragi in the batter. But should have a mild fermented flavor, yet should not have a sour smell. This is the correct fermentation for our taste. 13. When the batter is ready, pour 1 cup water in your steamer or cooker. Bring it to a boil on a medium heat. 14. Meanwhile grease the plates and pour the batter in the moulds. 15. When the water begins to boil rapidly, open the lid and place the ragi idli stand in it. 16. Cover and steam for exactly 10 to 12 mins. You may need to adjust the timing based on your pot. 17. Switch off and allow to cool slightly. 18. Dip the edge of a spoon in water and remove the ragi idlis using it. 19. Serve ragi idli hot with sambar or chutney.

title: Ragi Idli Recipe | Instant Finger Millet Idli - Diabetic Recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger paste, few curry leaves, 1 cup rava, 1 cup ragi flour, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup curd, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 cup water, 1 tsp eno fruit salt
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing and 2 tbsp cashew. 2. Fry on low flame until the cashew turn golden brown. 3. Now add 2 tbsp carrot, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger paste and a few curry leaves. 4. Saute slightly until it turns aromatic. 5. Further, add 1 cup rava and roast for 2 minutes. Roast until the rava turns aromatic. 6. Add 1 cup ragi flour and roast for 1 minute. 7. Cool completely, and transfer to a large bowl. 8. Add 1 tsp salt, 1 cup curd and 2 tbsp coriander. 9. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 10. Furthermore, add 1 cup water and mix well adjusting the consistency. 11. Cover and rest for 20 minutes, for rava and ragi flour to soak well. 12. Now add 1 tsp eno fruit salt and 2 tbsp water, and mix gently till the batter turns frothy. 13. Pour the idli batter into the greased idli moulds. 14. Steam for 15 minutes, or until the toothpick inserted comes out clean. 15. Finally, enjoy Ragi Idli Recipe with chutney.

title: Ragi laddu
ingredients: 1 cups ragi flour, 0.25 cup peanuts, 0.375 cup almonds, 0.25 cup desiccated coconut, 4 green cardamoms, 15 grams jaggery, 2 tbsp Ghee
instructions: 1. Heat ghee and fry the flour on a low heat till you get a nice aroma of ragi. Set this aside to cool. 2. Dry roast peanuts, cool, remove the skin. It is optional to remove the skin. 3. Roast almonds or sesame seeds until you smell them lightly roasted. Set aside ½ tbsp. of seeds aside (optional). 4. Dry roast desiccated coconut lightly. set aside to cool. 5. Add peanuts, sesame seeds, coconut and cardamoms to a grinder and make a fine powder. You can even powder each of these separately if doing in large quantity. 6. Add the cooled ragi flour and jaggery to the grinder. 7. Blend for about 1 to 2 minutes for the oil to ooze out from the mix. Make balls while the mix is still warm. If needed add melted warm ghee and make ragi ladoo. (check video) 8. Tip: If making in large quantity, you can mix the blended nut powder, jaggery, ragi flour and sesame seeds in a mixing bowl. Blend this in batches. 9. Store ragi ladoo in a air tight jar.

title: Ragi Malt Recipe (Ragi Java, Ragi Porridge)
ingredients: 4 tablespoon ragi flour finger millet flour, 1.5 tablespoon sugar, 1.5 cups Water, 1 cup Milk, 4 drops ghee, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 6 almonds, 4 tbsp finger millet Ragi flour, Salt, 1.5 cup Water, 1 cup Buttermilk, 1 tsp Oil, 1 pinch Cumin, 1 sprig Curry leaves, 1 tbsp Coriander leaves, 1 Green chili
instructions: 1. Add 4 tablespoons ragi flour to a heavy bottom pan or pot. 2. Pour 1½ cups water. Remember the stove is off yet and we are not cooking it at this stage. 3. Mix well and ensure there are no lumps in the ragi flour mixture. Turn on the stove and begin to cook stirring often on a medium flame to prevent lumps. 4. Keep stirring continuously and cook until the mixture turns thick and glossy. You will see there are no lumps. 5. Then add jaggery or any other sweetener. Mix well and turn off the stove. 6. I went ahead using 1 cup milk here. You can use even lesser. Slowly incorporate the milk to the ragi malt and mix well. I used preboiled milk here. If you use cold milk, slowly bring it to a boil and turn off. 7. Stir well to incorporate the milk well with the ragi malt. Add almond powder or mixed dry fruits powder if using. You will see smooth ragi malt. 8. For salted version – After cooking it in water, cool it completely and season it or add butter milk to bring it to the desired consistency and season it. You can find the detailed recipe in the recipe card. Serve ragi malt warm or cold. We usually top the ragi malt with ghee and nuts. 9. Most times I cook the ragi malt with water as it turns too heavy with milk. 10. Cooking ragi with milk may hinder the absorption of iron from ragi. So it is good to cook and consume it without any milk products. But it is up to you to judge. 11. You can skip the sweetener and add chopped dates or maple syrup to sweeten the ragi malt. 12. To enhance the nutrition you can add dry fruits powder. I usually blanch almonds, dry them completely and make almond meal for my toddler. Add a tsp of this to the ragi malt. 13. You can also blend together mixed nuts and few dates in a small chutney jar. Add this to the cooked ragi malt. 14. Consuming ragi with fruits high in vitamin C helps in absorption of iron from ragi. So adding an apple or banana to your ragi malt helps in absorption of iron. Avoid using milk in this case. 15. Aged or elderly : For the aged or elderly people with digestion problems, soak ragi overnight and discard the water. Give a good rinse and blend well until smooth with water. Cook this to a thick porridge with water. Ragi porridge rec... Ragi laddu | Nac... Ragi Cookies (Rag... Ragi Dosa Recipe Ragi idli recipe ...

title: Ragi Milk Recipe | Healthy Ragi Juice 2 Ways Summer Drinks
ingredients: 1 cup ragi grain, water, 1 badam, 2 tbsp coconut, 3 tbsp jaggery, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, water, 2 tbsp sabja seeds, 2 tbsp ragi flour, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp jaggery, 3 anjeer, 1 badam, water
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 1 cup ragi grain and rinse with water. 2. Drain off the water and spread it on a cloth to dry. 3. Once the ragi has dried, transfer to the pan and roast on low flame. 4. Roast until the ragi grains lose their moisture and turn aromatic. 5. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 6. Add 10 badam, 2 tbsp coconut, 3 tbsp jaggery, ½ tsp cardamom powder and water. 7. Grind to smooth consistency. 8. Sieve the milk using a muslin cloth. you can again grind with more water to extract the milk further. 9. Also, add 2 tbsp sabja seeds and ice cubes to make it more cool. 10. Finally, enjoy Ragi Milk Recipe chilled with ice cubes. 11. Firstly, in a saucepan take 2 tbsp ragi flour and mix it with 1 cup water. 12. Once the mixture is well combined cook on low flame. 13. Cook until the ragi mixture thickens and turns glossy. 14. Cool the mixture and transfer the the mixer jar. 15. Add 2 tbsp jaggery, 3 anjeer, 10 badam and water. 16. Grind to smooth consistency. 17. Finally, enjoy Ragi Milk Recipe chilled with ice cubes.

title: Ragi Mudde Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup ragi flour, 2 cups water, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 teaspoon ghee, 1 tablespoon ragi flour, 3 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. In a small bowl, mix together 1 tablespoons ragi flour with 3 tablespoons water. The mixture should be free of lumps. Set this aside. 2. To a heavy bottom pot, pour 2 cups water. Add ⅓ teaspoon salt and ½ teaspoon ghee. Both these are optional. 3. Bring the water to a boil, then stir the ragi mixture with a spoon and pour it straight into the boiling water. 4. Give a good mix with the spoon and let it come to a boil again on a medium heat. 5. You will see the mixture begins to thicken and bubble, then lower the flame completely and add the flour. 6. Mix with a sturdy wooden stick or a spatula to incorporate the flour into the water. Within 60 to 90 seconds you should see no streaks of flour. 7. Continue to mix until no lumps remain. Scrape off the stick with a spoon to loosen all of the ragi stuck to it. 8. Cover the pot and let it cook on the lowest heat for 5 mins. 9. After 5 minutes, give a mix again for 30 to 60 seconds. Remove the stick and cover back. Turn off and let it steam in the residual heat for another 5 minutes. (with the stove turned off) 10. Perfectly done ragi mudde will be non-sticky when you touch it with your fingers or a spoon. 11. Grease or moisten a wide plate with few little water. This helps the dough not to stick to the plate. 12. Transfer the dough to the plate. Grease your fingers with ghee or dip your fingers in a bowl of water. 13. Begin to knead the dough for 30 to 60 seconds. Then divide to 2 to 3 equal portions and roll to small balls. 14. Serve ragi mudde hot with a gravy or soupy lentil dish. 15. We will need ½ cup flour for this recipe. Stir in 1 tbsp ragi flour in 1 cup water. Also add little salt to taste and bring the water to a rolling boil. 16. Add the rest of the flour in 2 parts. If you are making more ragi mudde then add it in 3 to 4parts. 17. Take the pot away from the stove and place on the kitchen counter. Stir well with the wooden stick or spoon to prevent any lumps. Put it back to the stove and cook until it turns thick. Scrape off the sides and get the dough to the center of the pot. 18. Cover and cook on a lowest heat for about 2 to 3 minutes, making sure it does not burn. 19. Turn off the stove and let the dough rest for few minutes. Keep the pot covered at the steam helps to cook it fully. 20. When the temperature comes down, either grease your hand with ghee or moisten with water and roll it to balls. 21. Serve ragi mudde hot with any gravy or sambar.

title: Ragi porridge for babies
ingredients: 1 tsp to tbsp ragi flour, 1.5 tbsp ragi flour, 2 tbsp ragi flour, 3.5 tbsp ragi flour, milk, jaggery
instructions: 1. Method 1 1. Wash and soak ragi for at least 8 to 10 hours. While soaking they can be refrigerated or water needs to be changed a couple of times to prevent odor. 2. Drain off and blend them in a blender jar with little water. Add water as needed. 3. Place a cloth over a colander and keep a container under it to collect milk. 4. Pour the blended mixture to the cloth and then squeeze off to extract milk. You can repeat blending and then filter once more. 5. Cook this milk on a low flame till the mixture thickens as needed. If using milk, cool it completely. Add formula powder to a cup and little water make the milk and add it to the ragi. Stir well. 6. Always serve warm. Warm foods are lighter on the tummy and digest well. 2. Add 1 tsp of flour to a pot or pan. I used 3 tbsp flour with 1 cup of water, to make porridge enough for a 4 year old. 3. Pour ½ to ¾ cup of water. Break the lumps. If using jaggery, add it to the warm water, melt it. Filter to the pan. 4. Cook till it reaches a semi thick consistency. 5. Cool and then mix formula milk as needed. 6. If you intend to use jaggery, add it to water. Stir until it melts off. Filter it to the pan. 7. Stir in the flour. Break up the lumps. 8. Cook on a medium to low flame until it thickens. 9. Add milk as needed to bring it to the consistency. 10. Serve immediately.

title: Ragi Porridge Recipe For Babies | Homemade Ragi Cerelac Powder
ingredients: 0.5 cup ragi, 0.25 cup rice, 0.25 cup moong dal, 1 badam
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take ½ cup ragi, ¼ cup rice, ¼ cup moong dal, and 10 badam. 2. rinse 3 times with clean water. make sure to rub and rinse well. 3. drain off the water and spread it on a clean cloth. 4. sun dry until it gets dries well. 5. now dry roast on low flame until it turns aromatic. 6. cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 7. grind to a fine powder. 8. now sieve the powder using fine mesh. 9. ragi cerelac powder is ready, can be stored in an airtight container, and used for 3 months. 10. to prepare porridge, in a steel saucepan, take 1 tbsp ragi cerelac and 1 cup water. 11. mix well making sure there are no lumps. 12. keep on flame on and cook on low flame. 13. keep stirring until the mixture thickens and turns glossy. 14. finally, once the porridge turns warm, serve the Ragi Cerelac Recipe or Ragi Porridge Recipe for 8 months plus baby in a silver bowl.

title: Ragi Rava Upma
ingredients: 1 cup ragi rava, 2.5 cups water, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 green chilies, 1 pinch hing, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.25 cup onion, 1 cashew nuts, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.5 tablespoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. Wash and soak ragi rava for about 30 mins. Drain completely and squeeze off the excess water using your hands. Set this aside. 2. Heat a pan with oil. Add cumin, mustard, chana dal, urad dal and cashew nuts. Fry them until the lentils turn golden. 3. Add hing, onions, curry leaves, ginger and green chilies. Fry them until the onions turn transparent. 4. Transfer the rava and saute for a while for about 3 to 4 minutes until you begin to smell the rava good. 5. Meanwhile stir water and salt in a pot. Taste test the water. It should be slightly salty. Bring this to a boil. Turn down the heat and slowly pour the water with one hand, while you stir the rava with another hand. Be careful as the water can splash at this stage. 6. Cover and cook on a low to medium heat until all the water is absorbed and cooked through. If you think it is still uncooked, simply add more boiling water. Stir in between a few times. Turn off and let rest for 10 to 15 mins. 7. Sprinkle some lemon juice and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve ragi rava upma hot.

title: Ragi Roti Recipe - Ragi Rotti | Nachni Roti
ingredients: 2 cup ragi flour, 1 onion, 1 carrot, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp dill leaves, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp sesame, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coconut, water, ghee oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large plate, take 2 cup ragi flour, 1 onion, 1 carrot, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp dill leaves, few curry leaves,2 tbsp sesame, ½ tsp cumin, pinch hing, ½ tsp salt and 2 tbsp coconut. 2. Squeeze and mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined. 3. Now pour hot water and mix well. 4. Squeeze and mix, making sure everything is well combined. 5. Now take a wet cloth. You can alternatively use butter paper or a banana leaf. If the banana leaf is not tender, heat it slightly and then grease it with oil. 6. Take a ball-sized dough and tap gently to a thin thickness. 7. Make four holes; this will help roast as we can add oil in the centre. 8. Now flip over to hot tawa and press gently, peel the cloth off. 9. Now add oil and roast both sides until they turn slightly golden. 10. Flip over once the base is cooked. 11. Finally, enjoy Ragi Rotti Recipe with coconut chutney.

title: Ragi Roti Recipe (Ragi Rotti)
ingredients: 0.5 cup ragi flour, 0.5 cup water, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoons onion, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons carrot, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Pour water to a pot and add salt & oil. Bring to a rolling boil and add the flour. 2. Turn off the heat and using a sturdy spoon or a silicon spatula begin to incorporate the flour into the water until no more dry flour is visible. If it is too dry, splash 1 to 2 tablespoons of water. 3. Scrape off all the dough from the spoon and add to the bowl. Cover and let rest until the temperature cools down to a slightly hot or warm temperature (not cold). Add little flour to a wide small plate and keep aside. 4. Remove the dough to the rolling board and mix well with your hand until it looks very smooth. Divide to 4 equal portions and keep them covered in the same pot. 5. Smoothen each portion & roll to a ball, flatten it and dip in flour. Shake off the excess and roll to a 6 inch moderately thick ragi roti, moving it often to prevent sticking. (check pics or video) 6. If the dough sticks, sprinkle some flour lightly as you roll. Transfer to the counter and make the rest. 7. Heat a tawa/griddle on a medium high heat. Dust up the excess flour if any from the surface of the roti and transfer to the pan. 8. Let cook for 30 to 35 seconds, turn/flip it to the other side and press down gently all over with a spatula. Turn it again and cook until puffed. If it does not puff, press down again all over until it begins to puff. 9. Remove to a wired rack and spread ghee if you want. Repeat toasting the ragi roti the same way. Serve them hot or cold with a vegetable curry or a meat curry accompanied by plenty of veggies. 10. To make Masala roti, make the dough as mentioned above and add the other ingredients and mix well. 11. Divide the dough to 3 portions. Place a dough ball over a greased parchment paper or banana leaf. Begin to spread with your fingers to a thin roti of 6 to 7 inches. If you want make 3 holes across the center. 12. Heat a pan and invert the ragi roti over it (parchment paper up). Press down gently with your hand and pull off the parchment carefully. 13. Pour 1 teaspoon ghee or oil all over including into the wholes. On a medium heat cook covered for 4 to 5 mins, turning to the other side after 3 mins. Remove when they have golden spots.

title: railway cutlet recipe | train cutlet recipe | how to make railway veg cutlet
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 cup carrot, 1 cup beans, 1 cup peas, 1 cup beetroot, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 potato, 2 tsp lemon juice, 0.5 cup breadcrumbs, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 cup maida, 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup panko breadcrumbs, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. saute 2 chilli and 1 inch ginger for a minute. 2. add 1 cup carrot, 1 cup beans, 1 cup peas and 1 cup beetroot. 3. saute for 2 minutes, or until vegetables shrink slightly. 4. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp pepper powder and ¾ tsp salt. 5. saute until the spices are well combined and vegetables are almost cooked. 6. transfer into a large bowl and cool completely. 7. furthermore, add 2 potato, 2 tsp lemon juice and mix well. 8. squeeze and mix the mixture well. 9. now add ½ cup breadcrumbs and 2 tbsp coriander. 10. mix well forming a soft mixture. breadcrumbs help to absorb the moisture and holds shape without breaking while frying. 11. grease hands with oil and pinch a ball sized dough. 12. roll and shape to a leaf-shaped or shape of your choice. keep aside. 13. to prepare the slurry, in a small bowl take ½ cup maida, ¼ cup corn flour, ½ tsp chilli flakes and ½ tsp salt. 14. adding ½ cup water or more and prepare a smooth lump-free slurry. 15. dip the cutlet into the slurry and coat it with bread crumbs. you can double coat for crisper coating. 16. deep fry in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 17. stir and fry both sides until the cutlet turns golden brown and crisp. 18. drain off the cutlet over the kitchen towel to remove excess oil. 19. finally, enjoy railway cutlet with green chutney or tomato sauce.

title: Raita Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup curd, 0.5 cup cucumbers, 3 tablespoons onion, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.5 green chilies, 1 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder
instructions: 1. Wash cucumbers, onion (if using) and green chilies under running water. Peel the cucumbers if you prefer and chop them to bite sized pieces. Deseed chilies if you prefer low heat and chop them. Fine chop coriander leaves and onions. 2. Add yogurt and salt to a mixing bowl and whisk well until smooth. Add cucumbers, onions, coriander leaves, chilies, salt and cumin powder. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt. If it is too thick for your liking add more yogurt. 3. Garnish with cumin powder or black pepper. Serve raita with biryani, pulao, kebabs or paratha.

title: raj kachori recipe | how to make raj kachori chaat recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cup maida plain flour refined flour, 0.5 cup rava semolina suji, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 cup water, oil for frying, 1 potato, 0.5 cup chickpea chana, 0.5 cup green moong dal, 1 cup curd, 0.25 cup green chutney, 0.25 cup tamarind chutney, 1 tsp kashmiri chilli powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 cup sev, 0.25 cup boondi, 0.5 cup pomegranate, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp onion
instructions: 1. firstly take a poori and make a hole. poori turns crisp once cooled completely (after 2 hours) 2. stuff with 1 tbsp boiled potato, 1 tbsp boiled chana and 2 tbsp boiled moong dal. 3. also pour 2 tbsp curd. 4. now pour a tsp of green chutney and tamarind chutney. 5. also sprinkle chilli powder, cumin powder, chaat masala, aamchur and salt. 6. further drizzle half tsp of curd, green chutney and tamarind chutney. 7. garnish with 2 tbsp sev, 1 tbsp boondi, 1 tbsp pomegranate, 1 tsp coriander and 2 tsp onion. 8. finally, enjoy raj kachori immediately as a chaat.

title: rajbhog recipe | rajbhog sweet | kesar rasgulla recipe
ingredients: 1.5 litre milk, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp rava semolina suji, pinch saffron food colour, pinch cardamom powder, 2 tbsp crushed dry fruits, 1.5 cup sugar, 8 cup water, 2 tbsp saffron kesar water
instructions: 1. firstly, knead fresh paneer till it turns out smooth texture without any grains of milk. 2. now add a tbsp of suji, pinch saffron food colour and pinch cardamom powder. 3. knead further for 5 more minutes or until a soft dough is formed. 4. pinch a small ball sized ball and flatten it. 5. place ½ tsp crushed dry fruit in the centre. 6. get the edges together, and make a round ball without any cracks. keep aside. 7. sugar syrup recipe: 8. firstly, in a deep vessel take 1½ cup of sugar. 9. furthermore, add 8 cups of water and 2 tbsp saffron. 10. boil the syrup for 10 minutes on medium flame. 11. after that, drop the prepared paneer balls into boiling sugar syrup. 12. cover and boil for 15 minutes. the paneer balls will have doubled in size. 13. furthermore, keep aside till it cools completely and then refrigerate. 14. finally, serve rajbhog / kesar rasgulla chilled or at room temperature garnished with few saffron strands.

title: Rajma Recipe (Rajma Masala)
ingredients: 1 cup rajma, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 cup onions, 1 green chilli, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 cups tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons coriander powder, 0.875 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.75 tablespoon kasuri methi, Few Ginger juliennes, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoons heavy cream
instructions: 1. Add rajma to a large bowl and rinse them thrice. Soak them uncovered in 4 cups water for at least 6 hours to overnight. Discard the water and rinse them well. 2. Transfer them to a stovetop pressure cooker or instant pot. Pour 1½ cups water and pressure cook until soft, for 4 to 5 whistles. Or in the instant pot for 12 to 16 mins, depending on the age of rajma. 3. Once cooked they should be soft enough yet hold there shape. If you mash a rajma with your fingers it should get mashed easily. 4. Heat ghee or oil in a pot. Add cumin seeds and bay leaf. When they begin to sizzle add the onions and green chili. 5. Saute on a medium heat until golden. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for a minute, until aromatic. 6. Pour the tomato puree & cook until the onion tomato mixture turns thick, for about 3 min. 7. Stir in the spices – red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder and turmeric. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes, until the masala begins to smell aromatic. 8. Add the rajma along with the stock (rajma cooked water). Pour more hot water as required (about ½ to 1 cup, only as required) to make a gravy. (If using canned beans, drain the liquid from the can and rinse them before adding). 9. Mix well and simmer for 10 to 15 mins, until slightly thick. To make the gravy thick, if you want mash a tbsp of the rajma and add back. 10. Taste test and add more salt, ¼ teaspoon garam masala if required and crushed kasuri methi. Optionally you may stir in cream and garnish with coriander leaves and ginger juliennes. You can also make a dhaba style tadka garnish or a restaurant style creamy finish from the post. 11. Squeeze some lemon juice before serving. Serve rajma with chawal (rice). 12. Press Saute button and pour ghee to the steel insert. When the ghee melts, add cumin seeds, bay leaf, chopped onions, green chili and salt. 13. Saute for about 5 minutes stirring constantly to cook evenly until golden, about to caramelize but not burnt. 14. Add ginger garlic & saute for 30 seconds to 1 minute. 15. Add all the spice powders – chili powder, garam masala, turmeric, coriander powder and cumin powder. 16. Press cancel. Add rajma and pour 2 cups water. Deglaze to release any bits of food stuck at the bottom. 17. Place a trivet and keep the bowl of tomato puree over that. Secure the IP with the lid and pressure cook for 18 mins (for softer beans, if you prefer al dente – 16 mins). 18. When the pressure drops, open the IP. Rajma should super soft, yet hold shape. Pour the tomato puree and cook for 2 to 3 mins on saute mode. For a thicker consistency, mash some of the rajma. (If required pour about ½ to ¾ cup water to bring to a runny consistency.) 19. Taste test and add more salt, ¼ teaspoon garam masala, cream (optional) and crushed kasuri methi. Press cancel. 20. Garnish with coriander leaves and ginger juliennes. Serve rajma with chawal. Drizzle some lemon juice.

title: Rasam Powder Recipe | Rasam Podi
ingredients: 0.75 cup Coriander seeds, 0.25 cup Toor dal, 0.25 cup Chana dal, 2 tbsp Cumin, 1.5 tsp Pepper corn, 5 red chilies, 0.375 tbsp Fenugreek seeds, 0.5 tsp hing, Handful fresh curry leaves
instructions: 1. Dry roast chana dal and toor dal on a medium heat. Since I made in small quantity I have roasted both the dals together. If making in larger quantity, dry roast them separately. 2. Keep stirring and fry until golden to light brown and aromatic. 3. Pour the coriander seeds and red chilies. 4. Keep stirring and roast until the coriander emits a nice aroma. The red chilies will also turn crisp. Set them aside to a plate. 5. To the same pan, add methi seeds and roast until they turn aromatic. 6. They will turn slightly dark in color. 7. Add jeera and pepper. Very soon jeera turns aromatic. Transfer these as well to a plate. 8. Add the curry leaves and roast. You can also fry them in little oil. I always avoid using oil for any spice powders including for rasam powder. 9. When the leaves turn crisp, remove them. 10. Cool all these completely. 11. Clean all the ingredients first and roast them one by one on a low to medium flame until aromatic. Do not combine 2 ingredients and roast. 12. When done in small quantities like I have done below, they can be added one after the other & roasted. But when made in large quantities they have to be roasted separately as the roasting time is different for each ingredient. 13. Cool the ingredients in a large wide plate . Make sure no moisture from steam is trapped in them. 14. Send the entire mixture to a flour mill to make the rasam powder. Do not cover the container until the rasam powder is completely cool. 15. For many years I used aluminum foil to pack all the foods I would bring from India. In the recent years I have stopped that as well and began to use washed & sun dried small cloth bags. Pack the foods & then wrap the bag in cling wraps so that the rasam powder does not come out.

title: rasam powder recipe | udupi saaru pudi | rasam podi
ingredients: 2 tsp coconut oil, 1 cup coriander seeds, 0.25 cup cumin seeds, 2 tbsp methi, 0.5 cup curry leaves, 1 gram red chilli, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp hing asafoetida
instructions: 1. firstly, in a heavy-bottomed pan heat ½ tsp coconut oil and saute 1 cup ((75 grams)) coriander seeds. 2. saute on low flame until the coriander seeds turn aromatic. 3. transfer to a large plate and keep aside. 4. in the same pan heat ½ tsp coconut oil, add ¼ cup ((25 grams)) cumin seeds and 2 tbsp ((20 grams)) methi. 5. roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 6. now add ½ cup ((10 grams)) curry leaves and roast well. 7. roast until the curry leaves turn crisp. 8. transfer to a large plate and keep aside. 9. further heat 1 tsp coconut oil and saute 100 gram dried red chilli. 10. saute until the chills puff up and turn crisp. 11. transfer to a large plate and cool completely. 12. now take all the roasted spices to the mixi, add 1 tsp turmeric and 1 tsp hing. 13. grind to a fine powder. 14. finally, udupi style rasam powder or saar pudi is ready to prepare udupi saar.

title: Rasam Recipe | South Indian Hotel Style
ingredients: 2.5 medium tomatoes chopped, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2.5 to 3 cups water, 1 teaspoon Tamarind, 1.5 tablespoon jaggery, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 3.5 cloves garlic, 0.25 teaspoon fenugreek seeds, 0.375 teaspoon pepper corn, 1.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili
instructions: 1. To a pan, add ¼ teaspoon methi seeds & one dried red chilli. On a low flame, dry roast them until methi turns slightly aromatic. Then add 1½ teaspoon cumin seeds (jeera) and ¼ to ½ tsp pepper corn. 2. Roast all of them until methi seeds turn slightly dark in color and aromatic. If making for baby or toddler skip red chili and use less pepper. Cool them and add to a grinder jar. 3. You can also coarsely crush it in a mortar pestle. Set aside. You can also skip this roasting part & just powder the spices directly but the aroma is great when roasted. 4. Add 1½ tablespoon oil to a hot pot. When the oil turns hot, add half broken red chili, ½ teaspoon cumin seeds and ½ teaspoon mustard seeds. 5. Soon they begin to splutter, then add 3 to 4 crushed garlic, 1 sprig curry leaves and then a pinch of hing. Saute well until the leaves turn crisp. 6. Add 1 to 1½ cups chopped or mashed tomatoes or pureed (about 2 to 3 medium tomatoes). Sprinkle ½ teaspoon salt and 1/8 teaspoon turmeric. 7. Cover and cook or saute until the tomatoes turn mushy. 8. Add spice powder or rasam powder from step 1. 9. Mix and saute for 2 to 3 minutes. The spice powder must smell good. 10. Pour 2½ to 3 cups water. Add 1 teaspoon tamarind. Less is fine but don’t a lot. 11. Add jaggery to taste. I use about 1 to 2 tablespoons to balance the flavors. So adjust to your taste. 12. Bring it to a boil & let simmer for 5 minutes. Rasam boiled on a low flame tastes good. Stir well and take little rasam in a tablespoon. Cool it and taste test. Add more salt, tamarind and jaggery if needed. 13. Add ¼ cup chopped coriander leaves with stalks. Cover and turn off the stove. Serve Rasam with steamed rice and some papad, stir fry veggies. 14. Pour ½ cup water to a pot and heat it. Add a lemon sized ball of tamarind and let it soften. Then add another 1½ cups water and extract the juice. Discard the pulp. 15. To the same bowl, add the spice powder we made in the recipe along with 8 well crushed medium garlic cloves. Add jaggery and salt to taste. Bring this to a boil and then reduce the flame to low. Simmer it for 5 minutes. Add coriander leaves and turn off. 16. Make a tempering in 1 tsp hot ghee by adding ½ teaspoon mustard seeds, 4 crushed garlic cloves, 1 red chilli & 1 sprig curry leaves. When the garlic cloves turn slightly golden, add 1/8 tsp hing. Pour this to the garlic rasam.

title: Rasgulla recipe
ingredients: 1 liter milk, 2.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 liter ice water, 1.5 cups refined white sugar, 4 cups water, 3 green cardamom pods, 0.5 teaspoon rose water, 1 pinch saffron stands kesar for garnishing
instructions: 1. Bring milk to a boil in a pot. 2. If using non-homogenized milk (milk from milkman), then keep the pot aside. Wait for 5 mins. 3. If using homogenized milk from packets or cartons then reduce the flame to low. 4. Add 2 tbsp lemon juice & stir until the milk curdles. 5. If it doesn't curdle, add more & stir. 6. When you see the milk curdled completely, Switch off the stove. Rest for 2 mins. 7. Then pour cold water to the pot. This will stop the chena from cooking further & keeps it soft. 8. Place a colander over a large bowl & line with a thin cloth. Drain the curdled milk. 9. Rinse the chenna under running water to remove the acidic flavor. 10. Tie the cloth. Squeeze it well to remove the excess whey as much as possible. 11. Hang it for 1 to 1½ hours. Chenna should not have any excess whey. It must be crumbly. 12. Add sugar, cardamoms & water to a wide pan or pot. 13. Make sure you use a wide pot good enough to hold all the rasgullas. 14. The balls will cook & almost double in size only if there is enough space in the pot. 15. Stir to dissolve the sugar. Bring it to a boil. 16. Knead the chenna well to make it a smooth dough for 3 to 5 mins. I do it just for 3 mins. 17. Do not over knead to the extent that the chenna turns greasy or soggy. 18. When you see the mixture turns uniformly smooth and no more grainy, then stop kneading. 19. Take small portions of this and roll to tiny balls. 20. They should be tiny and not big as they expand in size after boiling. I made about 16. 21. Add rose water to the sugar syrup. (optional). 22. Bring the sugar syrup to a rolling boil on a medium flame. 23. Remove the cardamom pods. Then add the balls one after the other gently. 24. Cover the pot immediately with a lid. 25. The syrup must be boiling steadily at a constant heat. 26. Cook on a medium flame for 9 to 10 mins . The syrup must be bubbling & boiling steadily through out this time. So adjust the heat as needed depending on the kind of pot & stove used. 27. After 5 mins gently stir the sugar syrup once with a skewer without touching the rasgullas. Cover immediately. 28. This ensures even cooking and puffing. During the cooking time if you feel the heat is too much reduce the heat slightly, but ensure it is still bubbling & boiling. 29. After 10 mins, remove the pot immediately from the stove to prevent rasgulla from cooking further. 30. Keep the lid closed always otherwise they will shrink or fall flat. Do not open at least for 20 mins. 31. They double in size and also sink in syrup when cooked completely. 32. Allow rasgulla to rest and cool completely. Serve them chilled. If desired garnish with saffron. 33. Once the rasgullas have rested for a while, taste the sugar syrup and check for sweetness. 34. If you prefer more sweeter rasgullas or syrup, then add ¼ cup sugar to a small pot. 35. With the help of a ladle transfer about 3/4 cup sugar syrup to the pot and make a sugar syrup. Cool this and pour to the rasgulla pot or pan.

title: Rasmalai Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 liter milk, 1.5 tbsp lemon juice, 1 liter ice water, 1 cup refined white sugar, 3.5 cups water, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 0.75 liter milk, 0.5 cup sugar, pinch saffron, 2 tbsp pista
instructions: 1. Pour 3 cups milk to a heavy bottom pot and boil. 2. Add saffron and sugar when the milk comes to a boil. 3. Reduce the flame to medium. Stir every 2 to 3 minutes to prevent the milk from scorching at the bottom. 4. When a layer of cream forms, just move it aside. 5. Boil until the milk thickens and reduces to half the quantity. Set this aside to cool. 6. Cool half of this and chill in the fridge. Keep the other half warm. 7. Bring 5 cups milk to a boil in a pot. Turn off the stove. 8. Pour lemon juice and keep stirring until the milk has curdled completely. 9. If it doesn't curdle then add some more and stir. 10. Next if it fails to curdle then turn on the stove & regulate to a low flame. Stir until it curdles. 11. Turn off the stove. Pour ice water or add ice cubes to the pot and set aside for 2 mins. 12. Drain to a muslin cloth lined over a colander. 13. Pour fresh water over the chena and rinse well. You can also do it under running water for the sour taste to vanish. 14. Wrap the chena in the cloth. Squeeze off excess whey and make a knot. 15. Next hang it for about 45 minutes to 1 hour for the excess whey to drain off. 16. Chena should not have any excess dripping whey & should not be completely dry. It must be moist and grainy but not very sticky or dry. Please check the images above the recipe card. 17. Transfer the chenna to a plate. At this stage chenna should not have any excess whey in it. 18. Knead well for about 3 to 5 minutes until smooth. Chenna should not be grainy but must be smooth. Do not go by the time but focus on the texture - smooth, non grainy and non-sticky texture is required. 19. Make 12 equal balls and flatten them to make discs. 20. If the discs are slightly cracked on the sides, roll the sides gently. Set these aside. 21. Bring 3½ cups of water to a boil with 1 cup sugar. 22. Stir until the sugar dissolves completely. Then add cardamom powder. 23. When the sugar syrup begins to boil rapidly, then add the discs gently. 24. Cook covered for about 9 to 10 minutes on a moderately high flame. 25. Turn off the stove. Remove the pot from the stove to avoid cooking further. 26. Keep the lid closed for the next 20 mins to avoid them turning flat or shrinking. 27. When done they will sink in the syrup. 28. Take off the discs to a plate and cool them. 29. Squeeze them gently in between your palms to remove sugar syrup. 30. Add these to the half of the warm thickened milk or rabri. 31. Rest them for 3 to 4 hours. Pour the other half of the chilled rabri to this just before serving. 32. Garnish rasmalai with chopped nuts.

title: Rasmalai Recipe | Soft Rasamali & Rabdi Halwai Style 9 Secret Tips
ingredients: 2 litre milk, 2 tbsp vinegar, 1.5 cup sugar, 3 pod cardamom, 7 cup water, 1 litre milk, few saffron, pinch saffron food colour, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp nuts
instructions: 1. firstly, get 2 litre to a boil stirring in between to prevent it from burning. 2. add 2 tbsp vinegar and give stir. you will notice the milk starts to curdle. 3. add 1 more tbsp of vinegar and stir until the milk curdles fully separating water. 4. drain off the water over the cheesecloth. you can use any clean cloth here. 5. rinse with cold water to remove sourness and stop cooking. 6. squeeze the paneer gently and hang for 30 minutes. 7. now take the moist paneer and crumble gently. 8. using the palm, start to knead gently. 9. knead until the paneer mixture turns smooth without any grains. do not over-knead here as the rasgulla will turn hard. 10. pinch a small ball sized chenna and prepare smooth crack free balls, flatten slightly. 11. keep the flattened paneer ball aside and cover with a moist cloth. 12. firstly, in a large vessel, take 1½ cup sugar, 3 pods cardamom and 7 cup water. 13. stir and dissolve sugar. 14. now boil the water for 5 minutes or until the syrup turns slightly sticky. 15. drop in a prepared flattened paneer ball keeping the flame on high. 16. cover and boil for 7 minutes or until the size of the ball doubles. 17. chenna has been cooked well. keep aside. 18. firstly, in a large kadai heat 1-litre milk, few saffron and pinch of saffron food colour. 19. stir and get the milk to a boil. 20. continue to cook until the milk thickens slightly. 21. now add ½ cup sugar and continue to boil. 22. do not make very thick milk as it will be difficult for chenna to absorb milk. 23. also, add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and 2 tbsp nuts. mix well. 24. squeeze off the sugar syrup from the cooked paneer balls. 25. place them in a tray and pour in prepared rabdi. 26. rest for at least 4 hours or until the rasmali is soaked well. 27. finally, enjoy rasmalai slightly warmed or chilled. 28. drain off the water over the cheesecloth. you can use any clean cloth here. rinse with cold water to remove sourness and stop cooking.

title: rasmalai recipe with milk powder | eggless milk powder rasmalai recipe
ingredients: 4 cup milk, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, few threads saffron kesar, 0.25 cup sugar, 2 tbsp dry fruits, 1 cup milk powder, 2 tbsp sugar, 0.25 cup milk, 0.5 tsp ghee clarified butter
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare the rabdi by boiling 4 cup milk. 2. stir occasionally, to prevent milk from burning at the bottom. 3. now add ½ tsp cardamom powder and few threads saffron. 4. also add ¼ cup sugar. add more sugar if required. 5. stir well and simmer for 5 minutes. 6. scrape off the cream from sides and allow milk to thicken. 7. add in 2 tbsp dry fruits like cashew, almonds and pistachios. 8. simmer for another 5 minutes stirring occasionally. 9. finally, the rabri is ready once the milk thickens. keep aside. 10. prepare the rasmalai from milk powder by taking 1 cup milk powder in a large kadai. 11. add in 2 tbsp powdered sugar and ¼ cup milk. 12. kepping the flame on low, stir continuously till the milk combines well with milk powder. 13. scrape from bottom till the milk thickens and no lumps present. 14. now add ½ tsp ghee and mix well. 15. mix till the mixture starts to separate from the pan and forms a lump. 16. further, grease hands with ghee and prepare small balls and flatten. 17. finally, pour rabdi and serve instant milk powder rasmalai.

title: Rava Burfi Recipe (Suji Barfi)
ingredients: 1 cup rava, 1 cup coconut, 0.5 cup besan, 2 cups sugar, 1 cup milk, 0.75 cup ghee, 0.625 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. Grease a tray and set side. 2. Pour ¼ cup ghee to a heavy bottom pan and heat it on a medium flame. 3. Add rava and saute for 2 mins until it blends well with ghee. 4. Then add besan and roast them together until a nice aroma comes out. Keep stirring constantly to prevent burning. Make sure the besan doesn’t get discoloured. 5. Add coconut, sugar and milk. Regulate the flame to low for a while and mix up everything to prevent lumps. 6. Begin to cook on a medium heat stirring often. The mixture turns gooey while the sugar melts and begins to bubble up. 7. When the mixture thickens slightly add ¼ cup of ghee and cardamom powder. Stir well until the ghee is absorbed completely. 8. Add another ¼ cup of ghee. Stir well until absorbed. 9. Cook on a low to medium heat until the mixture thickens completely and turns to a halwa consistency. The mixture begins to leave the pan slightly. At this stage add 2 tbsps. Ghee and stir well until it is absorbed. 10. The burfi mixture will soon begin to leave the pan and turn to a single mass. 11. Be quick and transfer this to the greased tray. Level it well with a spatula. Sprinkle some sliced nuts on top and press down gently with a greased spoon. 12. Allow to cool down. When the burfi is still hot but not very hot, cut them to pieces. 13. Cool them completely and store in an air tight jar. 14. Rava burfi keeps good for 7 to 10 days at room temperature and for over a month if refrigerated.

title: Rava Burfi Recipe | Suji Barfi Recipe | Sooji Ki Katli
ingredients: 1 cup ghee, 2 cup rava suji, 0.5 cup dry coconut, 0.25 cup milk powder, 2 cup sugar, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp nuts
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 1 cup ghee and roast 2 cup rava on low flame. 2. Roast until the rava turns aromatic and sandy texture. 3. Now add ½ cup dry coconut, ¼ cup milk powder and roast for a minute. 4. Do not brown the rava else the colour of burfi will change. Keep aside. 5. In a large kadai take 2 cup sugar and 1 cup water. 6. Stir and boil till you attain 1 string consistency. 7. Keeping the flame on low, add roasted rava and mix well making sure there are no lumps. 8. Now add ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. 9. Cook on low flame until the mixture turns non-sticky and starts to hold the shape. 10. Transfer to the tray greased with ghee. 11. Top with nuts and dry coconut. Rest the burfi for 10 minutes to set. 12. When the burfi is still warm, cut into pieces. 13. Finally, enjoy Rava Burfi Recipe for a month when stored in an airtight container

title: rava dhokla recipe | instant sooji dhokla recipe | suji ka dhokla
ingredients: 1 cup rava semolina, 1 cup rava semolina, 1 cup curd, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 tsp sugar, 0.75 tsp salt, pinch hing, 0.5 cup water, 1 tsp eno fruit salt, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 2 tbsp sesame, 2 chilli, few curry leaves, pinch hing, 0.25 cup water, 1 tsp sugar, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coconut, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup coarse rava, 1 cup fine rava. 2. add 1 cup curd, 2 chilli, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 tsp sugar, ¾ tsp salt and pinch hing. 3. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 4. now add ½ cup water and mix well. 5. cover and rest for 20 minutes or until the rava is soaked well. 6. further, add 1 tsp eno fruit salt and mix gently. 7. the batter turns frothy. 8. pour the batter immediately into a vessel. make sure to grease and line with butter paper to prevent from sticking. 9. place in the steamer and steam for 20 minutes or until the toothpick inserted comes clean. 10. to prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp oil. splutter 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, 2 tbsp sesame, 2 chilli, few curry leaves and pinch hing. 11. add ¼ cup water, 1 tsp sugar, ¼ tsp salt and 1 tsp lemon juice. 12. mix well and get to a boil. 13. pour the tempering over the dhokla. 14. also, top with 2 tbsp coconut and 2 tbsp coriander. 15. finally, enjoy instant rava dhokla with green chutney.

title: Rava Dosa Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup rava, 0.5 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup all-purpose flour, 2.5 tablespoons onion, 1 green chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.375 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 6 curry leaves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2.5 cups water, 1 tablespoon curd, 2.5 tablespoons oil
instructions: 1. Add rava, rice flour, all-purpose flour, salt, ginger, green chilies, coriander leaves, curry leaves to a bowl. 2. Next add cumin, crushed pepper, onions and yogurt (optional). 3. Pour 2½ cups water and begin to make the batter. Add more water and bring it to a runny consistency. 4. Taste the batter and add more salt if needed. Rest the batter for 20 mins. 5. Heat dosa pan on a medium high heat. If using cast iron pan, then drizzle few drops of oil over the pan and spread it well with a sliced onion or kitchen tissue or cloth. Wipe off the excess oil & let it heat. 6. While the pan heats, stir the batter well. Ensure it is very runny and is pourable. At this stage you can add more water to bring to the desired consistency. 7. When the pan is hot enough, stir the batter very well and begin to pour it from the edges across the pan in a circular motion. 8. Then fill up the gaps if any by pouring more batter. 9. Reduce the flame to low. Pour 1 tsp oil across the edges and a little over the rava dosa. 10. When it is done cooking, it will begin to peel off the edges on its own. Do not pull the dosas forcibly. 11. If you make very very thin (paper thin dosas) then you don't need to cook on the other side. 12. When the rava dosa base becomes crisp & golden, remove it to a serving plate. 13. Optionally If the rava dosa has browned at the bottom but still moist on top, then you can also flip and toast the dosa on the other side. And back again. 14. Ensure the pan is hot again. 15. Stir the batter well before pouring the batter every time. If needed add more water to bring it to consistency. 16. It is best served hot as it turns soft when it cools down. Serve with any chutney.

title: Rava Dosa Recipe | Instant Crispy Suji Ka Dosa
ingredients: 0.5 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup maida all purpose flour, 1 tbsp curd yogurt, 1 tsp salt, 2.5 cup water, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 tsp pepper, 1 tsp cumin jeera, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 onion, 1.5 water, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take ½ cup rava, ½ cup rice flour and ¼ cup maida. 2. also add 1 tbsp curd, 1 tsp salt and 2½ cup water. 3. whisk and mix well making sure there are no lumps. 4. further add 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, ½ tsp pepper, 1 tsp cumin, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander and 1 onion. 5. add 1½ cup water and prepare a watery consistency batter. 6. rest the batter for 20 minutes making sure the rava has absorbed water. 7. mix well making sure the batter is thin watery consistency. 8. now carefully pour the dosa batter over very hot tawa. 9. lower the flame and cook until the dosa turns golden and crisp. 10. finally, fold the dosa and serve rava dosa immediately with tomato chutney or coconut chutney.

title: Rava Idli Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 1 cup semolina, 2 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 tablespoon chana dal, 1 tablespoon cashews, 1 curry leaves, 2 green chilies, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 2.5 tablespoon coriander leaves, 0.75 cup runny curd yogurt, 0.5 cup water, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon Eno, 1 teaspoon oil, 4 whole cashewnuts, 1 teaspoon carrot
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a pan or kadai. Fry 8 split cashews until golden. Remove to a plate and set aside. To the same pan, add ½ teaspoon mustard seeds, ¼ teaspoon cumin seeds, ½ tablespoon chana dal and 1 tablespoon chopped cashews. Saute until the dal turns golden. 2. Lower the heat. Add 1 sprig chopped curry leaves, 2 chopped green chilies and ¾ teaspoon grated ginger. Saute for 30 seconds until the ginger smells good. 3. Immediately add 1 cup rava/ semolina and begin to mix well. On a low flame fry the rava until crunchy and lightly aromatic. Do not brown the rava. Cool this completely. You can also store the cooled rava idli mix in a jar for further use. 4. Heat oil in a pan and add mustard and cumin. When they splutter, add hing, curry leaves and green chilies. Add onions and fry until transparent. Do not brown them. 5. Add turmeric and salt. Pour water just to bring it to a consistency. I use the ¾ cup water in which I boiled the potatoes. Bring it to a boil. 6. Meanwhile stir in besan with ¼ cup water. Break the lumps and make it a smooth mixture. When the water begins to boil, pour this gently and give a good stir. You can add more water as needed to cook this. Let the mixture cook and thicken. 7. Meanwhile mash the boiled potatoes keeping small chunks here and there. Add it to the sagu and mix well. Add more water if needed. When it comes to a boil, turn off. 8. The consistency should be slightly runny as it will thicken when it cools down. Rava idli sagu is not very thick. It should also have some water in it so it is suitable to serve with rava idli. Add coriander leaves and taste test it. Add more salt if needed. 9. Notes: This sagu should have more flavor of aloo and less of the besan. However if you prefer more, you can stir in more besan with some more water and pour it. You may add ½ tsp grated ginger to the tempering but most times you won’t find that flavor in the hotels.
