title: Rava Kajjikayalu Recipe | Suji Karanji - Gujiya
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp rava, 2 tbsp ghee, 0.5 tsp salt, warm water, oil, 1 tsp ghee, 1 cup rava, 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp cashew, 1 tsp poppy seeds, 1 cup dry coconut, 1 cup powdered sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, to prepare the dough, in a large plate take 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp rava, ½ tsp salt. 2. Pour 2 tbsp hot ghee and mix well. 3. Crumble and mix till the flour gets moist. 4. Now add warm water and start to knead the dough. 5. Knead the dough well, ensuring it is smooth and tight. 6. Cover and rest for 10 minutes. 7. To prepare the stuffing, heat 1 tsp ghee in a pan and roast 1 cup rava. 8. Roast on a low flame till the rava turns aromatic and golden brown. 9. Add 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp cashews, 1 tsp poppy seeds and 1 cup dry coconut. 10. Continue to roast till the coconut turns golden brown 11. Transfer the mixture to the bowl and cool slightly. 12. Now add 1 cup powdered sugar, ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. Make sure to add sugar once the mixture has cooled slightly, or else the sugar will melt. 13. After the dough has rested well, knead it slightly and pinch off a small ball-sized piece. 14. Take a small ball-sized dough and flatten it slightly. Roll the dough slightly thin, ensuring it is of uniform thickness. Place a tbsp of stuffing in the centre and spread water around the corners. 15. Fold in half, and seal by pressing gently. 16. Now you can decorate by folding with your hand, with a pastry cutter, or with a knife. 17. Deep fry in medium-hot oil. Make sure to drop them gently. 18. Stir occasionally, keeping the flame on low. Fry until the karanji turns golden brown and crisp. 19. Drain off the karanji over a kitchen towel to drain excess oil. 20. Finally, enjoy Rava Kajjikayalu for up to 10 days when stored in an airtight container. 21. Firstly, to prepare the dough, in a large plate take 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp rava, ½ tsp salt. Pour 2 tbsp hot ghee and mix well. Crumble and mix till the flour gets moist. Now add warm water and start to knead the dough. Knead the dough well, ensuring it is smooth and tight. Cover and rest for 10 minutes. To prepare the stuffing, heat 1 tsp ghee in a pan and roast 1 cup rava. Roast on a low flame till the rava turns aromatic and golden brown. Add 2 tbsp almonds, 2 tbsp cashews, 1 tsp poppy seeds and 1 cup dry coconut. Continue to roast till the coconut turns golden brown. Transfer the mixture to the bowl and cool slightly. Now add 1 cup powdered sugar, ½ tsp cardamom powder and mix well. Make sure to add sugar once the mixture has cooled slightly, or else the sugar will melt. Now you can decorate by folding with your hand, with a pastry cutter, or with a knife. Deep fry in medium-hot oil. Make sure to drop them gently. Stir occasionally, keeping the flame on low. Fry until the karanji turns golden brown and crisp. Drain off the karanji over a kitchen towel to drain excess oil. Finally, enjoy Rava Kajjikayalu for up to 10 days when stored in an airtight container.

title: Rava Kesari Recipe (Kesari Bath)
ingredients: 1 cup semolina, 0.875 cup sugar, 2.5 cups water, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 pinches natural kesari color, 5.5 tablespoons Ghee, 12 Cashews, 12 raisins
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp ghee in a heavy bottom pot and fry cashews until light golden. 2. Then add raisins and fry until they swell up. Remove to a plate and set aside. 3. In the same ghee add the rava and fry on a low to medium heat until aromatic and crunchy. 4. Do not brown or discolor the rava as the aroma of the rava kesari will change. 5. While the rava is getting roasted, pour water to another pot and bring it to a boil. 6. When the water turns boiling hot, slowly pour it to the rava by consistently stirring to prevent lumps. 7. The water will be absorbed by the semolina soon. Keep stirring and make sure there are no lumps. 8. When you see there is no excess water in the pan, then cover and cook on a low flame for 2 to 3 mins. This is just to ensure rava is cooked completely and turns fluffy. 9. Add sugar and mix well. Sugar will dissolve and the entire rava kesari mixture turns gooey. 10. Keep stirring and cook until the mixture thickens and the moisture is evaporated. 11. Then sprinkle cardamom powder, saffron or kesari and ghee. I used about 3 tbsp ghee. 12. Mix well and cook for 2 to 3 mins until the rava kesari leaves the pan. Cover and rest for 2 to 4 mins. 13. Grease a mould or round cup and then scoop some rava kesari to it. Level the top. 14. Invert the bowl or mould to a plate. Gently shake off. Garnish rava kesari with cashews and nuts. 15. You can also fluff up the rava kesari with a fork to make it fluffy.

title: rava kesari recipe | kesari bath recipe | kesari recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp cup ghee clarified butter, 1 cashews kaju, 1 tbsp raisins kishmish dry grapes, 0.5 cup bombay rava sooji semolina, 1 cup water, 0.75 cup sugar, 2 tbsp saffron water kesari, 0.25 tsp cardamom power elachi powder
instructions: 1. firstly, heat 2 tbsp ghee / clarified butter and roast 10 cashews, 1 tbsp raisins on low flame. 2. once the cashews turn golden brown, keep aside. 3. now in the same ghee roast ½ cup bombay rava. use fine / coarse variety rava. 4. roast on low flame for 5 minutes or till it turns aromatic. keep aside. 5. in a large kadai boil 1 cup water. 6. keeping the flame on low, add in ½ cup roasted bombay rava. 7. stir continuously till the rava absorbs water and no lumps are formed. 8. further add ¾ cup sugar (add ½-1 cup depending on sweetness you prefer). 9. keeping the flame on low, stir well till the sugar dissolves. 10. furthermore add 2 tbsp saffron water or kesari food colour. (soak few treads of saffron in 2 tbsp of warm water for 15 minutes) 11. also and ¼ cup ghee and stir continuously. 12. make sure the mixture is combined well and no lumps are present. 13. cover and simmer for 2 minutes or till rava gets cooked well completely. 14. additionally add roasted cashews, raisins and ¼ tsp cardamom power. 15. mix well till the rava kesari separates from pan. 16. finally, serve rava kesari / kesari bath as a dessert.

title: Rava Laddu Recipe | Rava Ladoo
ingredients: 1 cup rava, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.25 cup desiccated coconut, 0.25 cup ghee, 1 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 raisins, 1 cashews
instructions: 1. Add semolina & desiccated coconut to a pan and dry roast it on a medium to low flame stirring constantly for 6 to 7 mins. 2. It has to be evenly light golden without browning or burning. When it turns aromatic, light and crunchy, turn off the stove. Transfer this to a plate & cool down. 3. To a grinder jar, add sugar and cardamoms. Grind it to a fine powder and rest it in the jar itself for 2 to 3 mins. 4. When the semolina cools down, add it to the same grinder jar. 5. Grind to a slightly coarse powder. Taste test this. It has to be slightly coarse and not very coarse. If it is too coarse the ladoos will not bind.Transfer this to a mixing bowl. 6. Pour ghee in a hot pan. Add cashews & fry on a medium flame until light golden. 7. Then add the raisins and stir for 30 seconds. Turn off the heat. 8. Pour this hot ghee along with nuts and raisins to the rava laddu mixture. 9. Stir it with a spoon and not use your hand yet as it is too hot. 10. Once the ghee incorporates well, use your hand and mix everything well. 11. Take small portions of this mixture to your palm and press down hard with your finger to form a ball. (Check video). 12. Form the ladoos while the mixture is still hot. You will be able to make 10 to 11 rava laddus. If the mixture does not bind well, heat more ghee or milk in the same pan and add it to the mixture, 1 tablespoon at one time until you are able to do it. I did not use any more. 13. Store them in an air tight jar at room temperature and use up within a month.

title: rava modak recipe | suji ke modak without mould | suji modak recipe
ingredients: 0.25 cup ghee, 1 cup rava semolina suji, 2 cup milk, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, few raisins
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat ¼ cup ghee. 2. add 1 cup rava and roast on low flame until the rava turns aromatic. make sure to take fine rava, else the texture of modak will be different. 3. now add 2 cup milk and stir until the milk is absorbed well making sure there are no lumps. 4. further, add ¾ cup sugar and stir until the sugar melts. 5. cook until the mixture starts to hold the shape. 6. cover and simmer for 2 minutes, or until the rava is cooked well. 7. now add ¼ tsp cardamom powder and 1 tsp ghee. mix well. 8. transfer the mixture to a large plate and cool slightly. knead the dough forming a smooth and soft dough. 9. to prepare modak without mould, pinch a ball sized rava mixture and flatten slightly. 10. place 2 raisins in the centre and seal tight. 11. form a cylindrical shape and then shape to modak by pressing down gently. 12. using a toothpick, design the modak. 13. you can also prepare modak using the moulds. to prevent it from sticking, make sure to grease the mould with ghee. 14. stuff with dry fruits and shape the suji modak. 15. finally, garnish with saffron and enjoy rava modak for ganesh chaturthi.

title: Rava Pongal Recipe | Sooji Ka Pongal
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp ghee, 1.5 cup water, 0.25 cup ghee, 2 tsp pepper, 1 tsp cumin, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp cashew, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 1 cup rava, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1.5 tsp salt, 3 cup water
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a cooker take ½ cup moong dal, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp ghee and 1½ cup water. 2. Cover and pressure cook for 3 whistles. 3. Once the pressure is released, mash the dal into a smooth paste. Keep aside. 4. In a large kadai, heat ¼ cup ghee. Add 2 tsp of pepper, 1 tsp of cumin, and splutter. 5. Also add 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp cashew, pinch thing and few curry leaves. Roast till the cashew turn golden brown. 6. Add in 1 cup rava and roast for 3 minutes. 7. Now add the cooked moong dal, ¼ tsp of turmeric, and 1½ tsp of salt. Mix well. 8. Add 3 cups of water and mix well, making sure there are no lumps. 9. Cover and simmer on a low flame for 3-5 minutes or till rava is cooked well. 10. Finally, serve Rava Pongal with coconut chutney topped with ghee.

title: rava toast recipe | sooji toast or suji toast recipe | bread rava toast
ingredients: 0.5 cup bombay rava semolina sooji, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 1 green chilli, 0.25 cup water, 2 tbsp onions, 2 tbsp tomato, 3 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp carrot, 1 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp sugar, salt to taste, 4 bread slices, 4 tsp green chutney, 2 tsp butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small mixing bowl take ½ cup rava, ¼ cup curd and ¼ cup water. 2. combine all the ingredients well and make a batter. 3. further add 1 green chilli, 2 tbsp onions, 2 tbsp tomato, 3 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp carrot and 1 tbsp coriander. 4. also add ½ tsp sugar and salt to taste. 5. combine all together. 6. further rest for 10-15 minutes or till rava turns soft. 7. now spread green chutney over bread slice. 8. furthermore spread prepared rava batter. 9. toast it over hot tawa. 10. also spread butter on the other side of the bread. 11. once the rava side bread roasts well, turn over. 12. roast the butter side bread till it turns golden and crisp. 13. finally, cut the rava toast and serve for breakfast.

title: rava toast recipe | sooji toast or suji toast recipe | bread rava toast
ingredients: 0.5 cup bombay rava semolina sooji, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 1 green chilli, 0.25 cup water, 2 tbsp onions, 2 tbsp tomato, 3 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp carrot, 1 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp sugar, salt to taste, 4 bread slices, 4 tsp green chutney, 2 tsp butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small mixing bowl take ½ cup rava, ¼ cup curd and ¼ cup water. 2. combine all the ingredients well and make a batter. 3. further add 1 green chilli, 2 tbsp onions, 2 tbsp tomato, 3 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp carrot and 1 tbsp coriander. 4. also add ½ tsp sugar and salt to taste. 5. combine all together. 6. further rest for 10-15 minutes or till rava turns soft. 7. now spread green chutney over bread slice. 8. furthermore spread prepared rava batter. 9. toast it over hot tawa. 10. also spread butter on the other side of the bread. 11. once the rava side bread roasts well, turn over. 12. roast the butter side bread till it turns golden and crisp. 13. finally, cut the rava toast and serve for breakfast.

title: Rava Uttapam (Sooji Uttapam)
ingredients: 1 cup semolina, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 cup curd, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon unflavored fruit salt, 1.25 tablespoon oil, 1 onion, 1.5 green chilies, 2 tablespoons carrot, 1 tomato, 2 tablespoons capsicum, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add semolina, water, salt and yogurt to a mixing bowl. Mix well to a batter and cover it. Rest for 20 mins. 2. Meanwhile chop the veggies and season the pan or tawa with oil. Wipe off excess with a kitchen tissue. 3. After 20 mins the batter turns thicker as it absorbs moisture. The consistency should be medium thick, of pouring consistency (not runny) 4. If the batter is too thick you may splash a bit of water - 1 to 2 tablespoons & stir. (Check pics in the post to know the consistency) 5. Heat a pan on a medium high flame. Drizzle a few drops of oil on the pan and smear with a kitchen tissue carefully. 6. Optional - if you want you may stir in eno or baking soda at this point. 7. When the pan is hot, pour ⅓ cup of batter and spread a bit gently. Do not spread it a lot. It has to be thick. Sprinkle the veggies and press with a spatula. 8. Add ½ to ¾ teaspoon oil or ghee around the edges and cook on a medium heat until you see the edges loosen from the pan. 9. Turn the uttapam carefully to the other side with a spatula. 10. Cook until the veggies are partially done or brown to your liking. Turn back and cook until the rava uttapam turns lightly crisp. Transfer to a serving plate. Before you make the next uttapam, make sure your pan is hot enough. 11. If you want you can also sprinkle some Podi or cheese. Serve rava uttapam hot or warm with chutney or masala tea.

title: Rava Uttapam Recipe | Instant Sooji Uttapam
ingredients: 1 carrot, 1 onion, 2 tbsp capsicum, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp curry leaves, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp salt, 1.5 cup rava suji, 0.5 cup curd, 0.5 tsp salt, water, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, to prepare the topping, in a bowl take 1 carrot, 1 onion, 2 tbsp capsicum, 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp curry leaves, ½ tsp cumin and ½ tsp salt. 2. Mix well, making sure all the vegetables are well combined. Keep aside. 3. In a small bowl, take 1½ cup rava, ½ cup curd, ½ tsp salt and water. 4. Mix well, ensuring the batter is thoroughly combined and adjusting the water as needed. 5. Rest the batter for 5 minutes. 6. Now add ½ tsp eno fruit salt and 2 tbsp water. 7. Mix gently till the batter turns frothy. 8. Heat the griddle and pour a ladleful of batter. Spread out in a circular motion, slightly thicker than a regular masala dosa. 9. Top with the mixed vegetable mixture uniformly and pat slightly. 10. Also pour 1 tsp oil around the edges. 11. Roast the dosa to a golden brown on the bottom. 12. Flip over and roast both sides. 13. Finally, enjoy Mixed Vegetable Rava Utappam with chutney.

title: Rava Vada Recipe (Instant Suji Vada)
ingredients: 1 cup rava, 0.5 cup yogurt, 2 tbsp water, 0.25 cup onions, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 2 sprigs coriander leaves, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 green chilies, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 0.25 tsp soda-bi-carbonate, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Mix together yogurt and water in a bowl. Add rava and soak it for 10 minutes. The dough must bind well if it is dry you can sprinkle some water. 2. While the rava soaks, chop onions, chilies, curry leaves and coriander leaves. Grate ginger. 3. After 10 mins, add onions, chilies, leaves, cumin, salt and ginger to the soaked rava and make a dough. Beat the batter very well for 30 seconds to a minute with your hand in clock wise direction. This helps to make the rava vada fluffy. Lastly add soda and mix it gently. 4. The dough must bind well and must be sticky and easily spreadable. If it is dry you can sprinkle some water. If the dough is too stiff then they will turn hard. 5. Heat oil in a deep fry pan. Grease a foil or cling wrap. Divide the dough to 12 portions and make balls. Place them on the foil. Flatten and make a whole in the center. 6. When the oil is just hot enough, test by adding a bit of the dough to the hot oil. The dough has to rise without browning. This is the right temperature. 7. Grease your fingers, transfer the vada to your fingers. Then gently shake off the fingers. Rava vada will drop in the oil. Do not disturb them for a while until they turn firm. 8. Fry until the rava vadas on a medium high flame by stirring often. Drain them on a kitchen tissue when they turn crisp & golden. Fry all the vadas in batches similarly. Serve rava vada with chutney.

title: rava vada recipe | instant rava medu vada | suji vada | sooji medu vada
ingredients: 2 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp oil, 1.5 cup rava semolina suji, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp pepper, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp lemon juice, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 2 cup water, ½ tsp salt and 1 tsp oil. 2. mix well and get to a boil. 3. once the water starts to boil, add 1½ cup rava stirring continuously. 4. keep stirring until the rava absorbs the water. 5. cover and simmer for 2 minutes or until the rava is cooked well. 6. transfer the cooked rava to a large bowl. 7. also add 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp pepper, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander and 2 tbsp lemon juice. 8. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 9. now grease hand with oil and pinch a large ball sized mixture. 10. roll and flatten slightly, also make a hole in the centre. 11. deep fry the vada in hot oil keeping the flame on medium. 12. stir occasionally, until the vada turns golden brown and crisp. 13. drain off to remove excess oil. 14. finally, enjoy rava vada with tomato sauce or chutney.

title: Raw Banana Fry (Aratikaya Vepudu)
ingredients: 3 Raw banana, 2 pinches turmeric, 0.25 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp Cumin, 1 tsp Urad dal, 2.5 Green chilies, 0.5 inch ginger, 4 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.625 tsp garam masala powder, 2 tbsp Oil
instructions: 1. Peel raw banana. Cut to half vertically. Slice and transfer them to a bowl full of water. The pieces should not be too thin or too thick. 2. Make a paste of green chilli, ginger and garlic. Set this aside. 3. Coarsely crush or pulse onions in a blender. 4. Heat a deep Kadai or pan with oil. 5. Add urad dal, mustard and cumin. Let the dal fry till lightly golden without burning. Add ginger garlic and chili paste. 6. Fry till you begin to get a nice aroma. It should begin to turn slightly golden. 7. Drain the water from raw banana and add them to the Kadai. Add turmeric. 8. Mix and fry on medium flame till they are almost cooked. Stirring constantly to prevent burning. 9. Sprinkle salt evenly and fry on a medium flame till they turn soft, cooked and tender. Do not add salt until the banana is almost cooked, otherwise it will not get cooked further. 10. Add the coarsely crushed (pulsed) chili onions paste. Fry on high till the onions begin to caramelize. Before adding onions make sure the banana is cooked well. 11. Add garam masala, red chili powder (optional) and fry on a low flame till they firm up. Since there is green chili used you can use very little red chili powder for the color. But I haven’t used since we like the flavor of green chili in this. Raw banana fry Vazhakkai Fry (Po... Bajji Recipe (Ara... Banana Curry (Pla... Green Banana Reci... Eggless chocolate...

title: raw banana fry recipe | vazhakkai fry | balekai fry | aratikaya fry
ingredients: 4 grams raw banana balekai vazhakkai kacha kela plantain, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1.5 tsp sambar powder, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, 1 red chilli, pinch hing asafoetida, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp water
instructions: 1. firstly, peel the skin of 2 raw banana (approx. 400 grams) 2. slice the raw banana to slight thick size. you can alternatively cut in cubes. 3. add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1½ tsp sambar powder, ¼ tsp pepper and ½ tsp salt. 4. coat the spices well to raw banana. keep aside. 5. now in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and splutter 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp fennel, 1 dried red chilli, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 6. further, add spiced raw banana slices. 7. stir-fry for 2 minutes till raw banana gets coated well with oil. 8. spread the raw banana uniformly over kadai. 9. now sprinkle 2 tbsp oil. 10. cover and cook on medium flame for 10 minutes. 11. stir occasionally to prevent raw banana from burning. 12. now spread the raw banana again and cook for 2 minutes, without covering to make raw banana fry crisp. 13. finally, serve raw banana fry / vazhakkai fry with hot steamed rice as a side dish with rasam.

title: red chutney for mysore masala dosa | red chutney for dosa recipe
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 2 tbsp chana dal, 4 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 onion, 3 whole kashmiri red chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, salt to taste, water to grind
instructions: 1. firstly, heat a pan with some oil. 2. further saute 2 tbsp chana dal to light golden brown. 3. add in 4 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger and 1 onion. 4. saute well till the onions turn translucent. 5. additionally add in 3 whole dried red chilli, ¼ tsp turmeric and salt to taste. 6. saute well till the chillis turn crisp. 7. cool the mixture completely, and transfer to the blender. 8. blend to smooth paste adding water as required. 9. finally, serve red chutney with mysore masala dose or cheese dosa. 10. finally, serve red chutney with mysore masala dose or cheese dosa .

title: red chutney for mysore masala dosa | red chutney for dosa recipe
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 2 tbsp chana dal, 4 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 onion, 3 whole kashmiri red chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, salt to taste, water to grind
instructions: 1. firstly, heat a pan with some oil. 2. further saute 2 tbsp chana dal to light golden brown. 3. add in 4 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger and 1 onion. 4. saute well till the onions turn translucent. 5. additionally add in 3 whole dried red chilli, ¼ tsp turmeric and salt to taste. 6. saute well till the chillis turn crisp. 7. cool the mixture completely, and transfer to the blender. 8. blend to smooth paste adding water as required. 9. finally, serve red chutney with mysore masala dose or cheese dosa. 10. finally, serve red chutney with mysore masala dose or cheese dosa .

title: red coconut chutney recipe | red chilli coconut chutney | idli red chutney
ingredients: 1 cup coconut, 1 tbsp putani roasted gram dal, 3 kashmiri red chilli, ball sized tamarind, 1 inch ginger, 1 clove garlic, 0.75 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 3 tsp oil, 0.75 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 1 kashmiri red chilli, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a blender take 1 cup coconut, 1 tbsp putani, 3 dried red chilli. 2. also add small ball sized tamarind, 1 inch ginger, 1 clove garlic and ¾ tsp salt. 3. blend to smooth paste adding ½ cup water. 4. in a small kadai heat 3 tsp oil and splutter ¾ tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, 1 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 5. pour the tempering over chutney. 6. finally, enjoy red coconut chutney with idli or dosa.

title: Red Lentil Curry Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup red lentils, 2.5 cups water, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 3 inch cinnamon piece, 0.875 cup onions, 0.75 tablespoon garlic cloves, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 1 green chili, 1.75 cups Tomatoes, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 1 tablespoon curry powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 2 teaspoons kasuri methi, 0.3333333333333333 cup thick coconut milk heavy cream, 2.5 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add red lentils to a pot and rinse them thrice. Drain and pour water. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat and reduce to medium. 2. Let cook until tender (al dente), for 10 mins. If you want you may skim the froth. Older lentils take longer to cook. While the lentils cook, chop and prepare the other ingredients. 3. Heat oil/ghee in a medium pan. Add the whole spices, onions, green chilies and curry leaves. Saute until golden, for about 8 mins. 4. Stir in ginger garlic and saute for a minute, until aromatic. 5. Stir in the curry powder, garam masala and Kashmiri chili powder, followed by tomatoes & (a splash of water if using fresh tomatoes). Cover and cook until the tomatoes break down. 6. Saute for 1 to 2 mins until the mixture turns aromatic. 7. Add the red lentils along with the leftover stock/ water. Mix well and more water as needed. You will need about ½ cup. Bring it to a boil and simmer for 2 to 3 minutes. 8. If you want mash some of the lentils with the back of the spoon. Stir in kasuri methi and coconut milk or cream. Simmer for 2 minutes. 9. Taste test. If you want, add more salt, garam masala or black pepper for heat. 10. Garnish with coriander leaves and squeeze some lemon juice. 11. Serve red lentil curry over rice or with Naan, Chapati, Roti or any other flatbreads. 12. You don't need to cook the lentils separately. Make the masala first, add the lentils and pressure cook for 7 minutes. Wait for natural pressure release and add kasuri methi and coconut milk or cream. 13. You don't need to cook the lentils separately. Make the masala first, add the lentils to the cooker. Pressure cook for 2 to 3 whistles on a medium heat. Wait for the pressure to drop and add kasuri methi and coconut milk or cream. If you want make a extra tadka.

title: Red Pepper Chutney (Red Capsicum Chutney)
ingredients: 1 red bell pepper, 0.5 cup onions, 0.875 tablespoon channa dal, 0.75 tablespoon urad dal, 1.5 garlic cloves, 2.5 Kashmiri dried red chilies, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon oil, 1 red capsicum, 2 teaspoons oil, 0.3333333333333333 cup raw peanuts, 0.3333333333333333 inch ginger, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3 Kashmiri dried red chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, Pinch mustard, 1 pinch urad dal, 1 red chili, Oil, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Heat 1 teaspoon oil in a pan. Add the 2 dried red chilies, ¾ to 1 tablespoon chana dal and ¾ tablespoon urad dal (or red lentils). On a medium heat, toast until the lentils turn deep golden, Add 1 to 2 garlic cloves or ¼ inch peeled ginger and ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. Fry them for a minute. 2. Add 1½ cups diced bell peppers (1 large), half cup diced onions (optional) and sprinkle ¼ teaspoon salt. Saute until they turn slightly soft & lose the raw flavor. Turn off and cool completely. 3. If using tamarind, soak ¼ teaspoon tamarind in 2 tablespoons hot water until soft. Squeeze and use only a little – ¼ teaspoon in the next step. 4. Transfer all the cooled ingredients to a small grinder/blender jar along with 1 tablespoon lemon juice or tamarind. If you want you may set aside 1 red chili to adjust later. Blend to a smooth or coarse pepper chutney to your preference. Taste test and adjust salt as per your preference. Add the red chili that we set aside & grind for extra heat. 5. Pepper Chutney does not require any tempering. If you want you may do that following my instructions in the recipe card below. 6. On a medium flame, heat oil in a pan, add the dried chilies and fry just until crisp. Remove to a plate. To the same pan, add bell peppers and sprinkle salt. Roast on a medium heat until soft. Add the cumin and ginger or garlic. Fry for a minute. Remove to the same plate. 7. Add peanuts. Fry them on a medium heat until golden and aromatic. Remove to the plate to cool. Add all the cooled ingredients to a small grinder and pour a few tablespoons water. Grind until smooth, adding more water as needed. Taste test and adjust salt if required. Serve pepper chutney with any snacks or breakfasts.

title: Red Sauce Pasta Recipe (Red Pasta Recipe)
ingredients: 1.5 cups fusilli, 2.5 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 3.5 garlic cloves, 0.25 cup shallot, 25 grams tomatoes, 0.5 cup red bell peppers, 0.75 teaspoons red chili flakes, 0.5 teaspoon mixed Italian herbs, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 6 cups water, parmesan
instructions: 1. Spread tomatoes and bell peppers on a baking tray and add a tbsp. of oil. Bake for 15 mins at 425 F - 220 C, until the tomatoes are softened. Or alternately, pan fry the tomatoes and bell peppers on a medium high heat until blistered & soft, for about 5 to 6 mins. Or Air fry for 9 mins at 390F / 200 C. 2. Cool down and blend to a smooth sauce with chili flakes. 3. Bring water to a rolling boil and add the pasta along with salt (if required, check the package). On a medium high heat, cook al dente following the timings on the pack. Mine takes 11 mins. 4. Reserve half cup pasta stock and drain to a colander. Cover and keep aside. 5. Heat a pan with oil and add garlic. Saute for a minute and add the onions. 6. Saute until transparent. Stir in the chili flakes and pour tomato puree. Saute for 3 to 4 mins and pour the reserved pasta stock. 7. Cook for another 3 to 4 mins until thick (check pics) and add the herbs, black pepper (optional) and salt. Taste test and add sugar if your sauce is too tart. 8. Stir the pasta into the red sauce. Garnish with parmesan and black pepper if you want.

title: Reshmi Kabab | Chicken Malai Kabab
ingredients: 25 grams chicken, 2 tablespoons cream, 2 tablespoons greek yogurt, 1.25 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon oil, 2 tablespoons almond meal, 2 tablespoons cheese, 1 tablespoon butter, 0.5 cup Mint Chutney
instructions: 1. Add chicken to a bowl and pat dry any excess moisture. This prevents the chicken malai tikkas from letting out lots of moisture. 2. In a separate bowl, mix together almond meal, fresh cream, thick yogurt, cheese (optional), lemon juice, oil, salt, black pepper, ginger garlic and coriander leaves. 3. Mix them well and taste test. If you want you may add more salt and black pepper to adjust to your taste. This mixture should be ideally thick. 4. Pour this over the chicken and mix well. Cover and set aside in the refrigerator for atleast 4 hours to overnight. 5. Preheat the oven to the highest. At 240 C or 470 F for at least 15 mins. 6. Thread the chicken on the skewers and place them on a prepared baking tray. Grill for 15 mins. 7. Turn them to the other side and grill again for 7 to 8 mins. Check after 22 to 23 mins of grilling to prevent burning. 8. The last 2 minutes grill them on a broil mode for charring. Remove the grilled chicken malai tikkas from the oven and brush butter over them. 9. Serve chicken malai kababs with mint chutney.

title: reshmi paneer recipe | paneer reshmi recipe | reshmi paneer masala
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 capsicum, 1 tomato, 1 tbsp butter, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 1 cup tomato pulp, 0.25 cup cashew paste, 0.25 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 15 pieces paneer cottage cheese, 2 tbsp cream malai, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute ½ onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 2. now add ½ capsicum, 1 tomato and saute for a minute. 3. do not overcook, keep aside. 4. in the same kadai, heat 1 tbsp butter and add 1 tsp cumin. 5. keeping the flame on low and add ½ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder and ¼ tsp cumin powder. 6. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 7. further, add 1 cup tomato pulp and saute well. 8. also, add ¼ cup cashew paste and saute on medium flame. 9. cook until the mixture thickens and oil separates. 10. furthermore, add ¼ cup water and ½ tsp salt. mix well, adjusting consistency as required. 11. add in sauted onion, capsicum and tomato. 12. also, add 15 pieces paneer and mix gently without breaking paneer. 13. add in 2 tbsp cream and mix gently. 14. simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 15. now add ¼ tsp garam masala and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 16. finally, enjoy reshmi paneer recipe with roti or naan.

title: Ribbon Pakoda (Ribbon Murukku, Aaku Pakodi)
ingredients: 0.25 cup gram flour, 0.25 cup fried gram, 1 cup rice flour, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 1.25 tablespoons white sesame seeds, 2 teaspoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon salt, oil, 2 green chilies, 0.5 inch ginger
instructions: 1. Powder ¼ cup fried gram in a mixer grinder jar. Measure and use only ¼ cup powder for the recipe. 2. Place a sieve over a large bowl. Measure the flours including the powdered fried gram powder. Add all of them to the sieve. Sieve them together. 3. Crush cumin seeds in a mortar pestle. Or using a rolling pin crush them on the chopping board. Add it to the flour, along with red chilli powder, sesame seeds and salt. Mix them. 4. Then heat up 2 tsps oil in a kadai and pour to the flour. Using a spoon mix well. Then use your fingers and incorporate the oil well. Rub the flour well in between your palms to incorporate the oil 5. This step is optional. Make a fine paste of ginger and chili if using. Add 3 to 4 tbsp water to this and filter it to a bowl. Pour this to the flour. 6. Pour more water as needed and make a dough that is non sticky but smooth. If the dough is dry then sprinkle more water. If the dough turns sticky, then sprinkle little rice flour. Taste test the dough and add more salt and chilli if you prefer. 7. Pour oil to a deep fry pan or kadai. Heat it on a medium flame. 8. Meanwhile grease the mould and fit the ribbon pakoda plate to the mould. 9. Fill the mould with the prepared dough. Cover the rest of the dough to avoid drying. 10. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of the dough in hot oil. The dough has to rise steadily without browning and must not sink. 11. Press the dough in the hot oil, moving the hand in circular pattern to make a disc of ribbon pakoda. 12. Fry until golden and crisp on a medium high heat. Keep stirring and fry. Towards the end if you want you may fry them on a low heat for even crispness. Once crisp remove to a steel colander. 13. Make more ribbon pakoda and fry in batches until all of the dough finishes. Just ensure the oil is just hot enough and not too hot. 14. Cool ribbon pakoda completely. Break them as desired to pieces and store in a airtight jar.

title: rice bath recipe | karnataka style vegetable rice bath | rice bhath recipe
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 inch ginger, 4 clove garlic, 2 chilli, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp fennel saunf, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 pod cardamom, 4 cloves lavang, 3 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp mint pudina, 0.25 cup coconut, 0.25 cup water, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom, 4 cloves lavang, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 1 carrot, 0.5 potato aloo, 5 beans, 3 tbsp peas matar, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp mint pudina, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup sona masuri rice, 2 cup water, 1 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small pan heat 2 tsp oil and saute 1 inch ginger, 4 clove garlic and 2 chilli. 2. also add 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp fennel, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 pod cardamom, 4 cloves and saute until spices turn aromatic. 3. further, add 3 tbsp coriander and 2 tbsp mint. saute until it shrinks. 4. turn off the flame and add ¼ cup coconut. 5. saute until the coconut warms up. 6. cool completely and transfer to a small blender. 7. blend to a coarse paste adding ¼ cup water. keep masala paste aside. 8. in a pressure cooker heat 1 tbsp ghee and 2 tsp oil. 9. saute 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 pods cardamom and 4 cloves. 10. add 1 onion and saute until onions turn golden brown. 11. also, add 1 tomato and saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 12. now add the prepared masala paste and saute until the raw flavour is gone. 13. add 1 carrot, ½ potato, 5 beans, 3 tbsp peas, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp mint and ½ tsp salt. 14. saute for 2 minutes or until masala paste is combined well. 15. add 1 cup sona masuri rice. make sure to soak rice for 20 minutes. 16. saute for a minute without breaking rice. 17. now add 2 cup water, ½ tsp salt and 1 tsp lemon juice. mix well and get to a boil. 18. cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles. 19. finally, enjoy rice bath with raita and cucumber.

title: rice cutlet recipe | leftover rice cutlets | chawal ke cutlet | chawal ki tikki
ingredients: 1 cup cooked rice, 1 potato, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp corn, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp lemon juice, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, 0.25 cup maida plain flour, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 cup panko bread crumbs, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a bowl take 1 cup cooked rice and mash smooth. 2. add 1 boiled and mashed potato. pressure cook potatoes for 3 whistles adding little salt. 3. also add 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp corn, 2 tbsp cashew. 4. further add ½ tsp ginger paste, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp garam masala, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ¾ tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander and 1 tsp lemon juice. 5. now add ½ cup bread crumbs and mix well. 6. squeeze and mix until a soft dough is formed. 7. now prepare slurry by mixing ¼ cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour, ½ tsp pepper powder and ¼ tsp salt. 8. grease hands with oil and pinch a small ball sized cutlet mixture and shape to diamond. 9. dip in maida paste coating all over. 10. then roll in breadcrumbs. make double coating to get a crispy outer layer. 11. now deep fry in hot oil or bake in preheated oven at 180 degree celcius for 15 minutes. 12. stir occasionally, until it turns golden brown and crisp. drain over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 13. finally, enjoy rice cutlet with tomato sauce.

title: Rice Murukku Recipe | Rice Chakli | Rice Flour Murukku | Rice Flour Chakli
ingredients: 0.75 cup roasted gram dal, 3 cup rice flour, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp black sesame seeds, 0.5 tsp ajwain carom seeds, pinch hing, 0.75 tsp salt, 2 tbsp butter, water, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixer jar take ¾ cup roasted gram dal and grind to fine powder. 2. transfer the roasted gram dal powder to a sieve. 3. add 3 cup rice flour and sieve the flour. 4. also add ½ tsp chilli powder, 2 tbsp black sesame seeds, ½ tsp ajwain, pinch hing, ¾ tsp salt and 2 tbsp butter. 5. crumble and mix well making sure the flour is moist. 6. now add 1 cup hot water and mix well. 7. press the flour with your hand making sure the flour is moist. 8. cover and rest for 5 minutes. 9. after 5 minutes, mix the flour again. 10. add water as required and start to knead the dough. 11. knead to a smooth and soft nonsticky dough. 12. now take the star mold and fix it to the chakli maker. 13. grease the chakli maker with some oil. this prevents the dough from sticking to the mold. 14. furthermore, make a cylindrical shape out of dough and place the dough inside the maker. 15. tighten the lid and start preparing chaklis on the steel plate. or you can use wet cloth or butter paper make small spiral shape chaklis by pressing. seal the ends so that it doesn’t fall apart while deep frying. 16. take one murukku at a time and slide it into the hot oil. 17. flip the murukku and fry on low to medium flame till they turn crispy from both sides. 18. furthermore, drain over a paper towel to remove excess oil. 19. finally, once cooled enjoy instant chakli or store in an airtight container for 2 weeks.

title: Rice Payasam (Paramannam Recipe)
ingredients: 4 cups whole milk, 0.5 cup rice, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom, 0.875 cup jaggery, 0.5 cup water, 2 tablespoons Ghee, 12.5 cashews, 1.5 tablespoon sweet raisins
instructions: 1. Wash rice several times and soak it in enough water until milk comes to a boil. 2. Pour milk to a rinsed heavy bottom pot. Bring it to a boil on a medium flame. 3. Add grated jaggery to a pan along with water. Boil on a medium flame until slightly thick and sticky. It doesn't need a string consistency but has to be sticky. 4. Keep this aside to cool completely. 5. When the milk comes to a boil, drain the water from the rice and add it here. 6. Cook until the rice grains are mushy and soft. Stir every 4 to 5 minutes during this process to avoid the milk solids from getting burnt at the bottom. 7. When the rice is soft cooked, gently mash some of the rice grains to thicken the payasam. Turn off the heat when it reaches a slightly thick yet of pouring consistency, (Check video) 8. Add cardamom powder. Filter the cooled jaggery syrup to the cooked rice. (If using clean jaggery you can add the gratings now. Stir well until dissolved.) 9. Heat ghee in another small pan. Fry cashews till light golden, stir in the raisins & fry until they puff. Pour this all over the rice payasam. 10. Serve rice payasam warm or cold. 11. Wash and soak rice and dal in enough water for at least 15 mins until the milk comes to a boil. 12. Add jaggery and water to a pan. Bring it to a boil and simmer until bubbly and slightly thick, but of pouring consistency. Stir in the cardamom powder. Keep this aside to cool down completely. Leave out this step if you want and add the grated jaggery directly but jaggery syrup brings out a lot of flavor. 13. Pour milk to a heavy bottom pot and bring it to a gentle boil. Stir in the rice, dal and hot water. Cook until tender and mushy. Keep stirring every 15 mins as you cook. 14. Turn off when the pongali turns thick, yet of pouring consistency. It will thicken a lot when it cools down. 15. Stir in ¾ of the cooled jaggery syrup or powdered jaggery and cardamom directly. Start with lesser jaggery here, you can add more as required. 16. Heat ghee in a small pan, add cashews and fry till light golden. Add raisins and fry till they turn plump. Turn off and pour this to the bellam pongali. 17. Make sure your sealing ring is clean and sterilized. Pour ghee to the steel insert and fry the cashews on a sauté mode until light golden. Turn off and stir in the raisins. Remove them to a plate. 18. Transfer rice, moong dal, water and milk to the steel insert. Cover the IP and position the vent to sealing. Press the porridge button and set the timer to 12 to 14 minutes. 19. Let the pressure drop naturally, open the lid. It should be thick and fully cooked. Let cool down for 2 to 3 minutes. 20. Stir in the jaggery/ jaggery syrup and cardamom. Start with lesser amount of jaggery and add more to taste later. 21. Garnish with cashews and cardamoms.

title: Rice Rava, How to Make Rice Rava
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 3 cups water
instructions: 1. Wash the rice thoroughly in lots of water. Drain the water and soak for at least 1 to 2 hours in enough fresh water. If you do not have time, you can skip this step. 2. Later drain off to a colander. Let it rest for a while for the water to drain completely. Transfer the rice to a clean dry cloth. 3. Spread it evenly and fan dry or air dry for about 2 hrs. Alternately you can sun dry till completely moisture free. 4. Add the rice to a blender or grinder. Run the machine to powder the rice to rava. (The texture should be of semolina). 5. Transfer this to a sieve/filter and pass the rice rava to remove large grains. This is optional, if you don't have large grains you can skip it. 6. If using new harvest rice, sieve and separate the flour as it makes your food sticky after cooking. Use only the rava for undrallu and store aside the flour for making chalimidi or for dosas. This must be done to avoid undrallu from becoming sticky. * this step is not required if using aged rice 7. Make sure the rava is dry fully before you move it to a jar.

title: Ridge Gourd Recipe
ingredients: 5 grams ridge gourd, 1 tbsp Oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 Pinch hing, 2 garlic cloves, 2 onions, 1 green chili, 0.5 cup tomatoes, Salt, 0.125 tsp turmeric powder, 0.5 tsp red chili powder, 0.75 tbsp chana dal, 0.75 tbsp urad dal, 1 garlic, 1 tbsp coconut, 1 tsp sesame seeds, 0.5 tsp jeera, 1 red chilli, 1 pinch methi seeds
instructions: 1. Dry roast red chilli, chana dal and urad dal until the dal turns golden and aromatic. 2. Then add garlic, sesame seeds, jeera and coconut. Fry until coconut turns aromatic. 3. Cool completely and make a fine powder. Set this aside 4. Wash ridge gourd well, then peel off the skin. If using organic gourd, then you can use the peel to make chutney. 5. Chop the ridge gourd to half inch pieces and set aside. 6. Wash and chop onions & tomatoes finely. I do not use onions for this recipe just to cut down the cook time. 7. Heat the pot with oil, then add mustard and cumin. When they begin to crackle, add hing and curry leaves. Then add garlic and chilli. Saute for 1 minute. 8. If using onions add them and fry till transparent. Then add the ridge gourd and fry on a medium flame for 3 to 4 minutes. 9. Add tomatoes, salt, red chili powder and turmeric. Mix and fry for about 2 minutes. 10. Cover and cook on a low flame. After a while the gourd will begin to ooze out moisture. Keep stirring occasionally to prevent burning, continue cooking till the tomatoes and gourd are soft cooked. 11. Some gourds do not let out any moisture in that case sprinkle 3 to 4 tbsps water and cook. I do not use water at all. 12. When the ridge gourd is finished cooking, you should be left with little thin gravy. 13. Sprinkle the spice powder made earlier. 14. Cover and cook until the curry turns slightly thick for about 4 to 5 mins. Taste the curry and add more salt as desired. 15. Serve ridge gourd curry with rice or roti.

title: Roasted Cashews Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup whole raw cashews, 0.75 teaspoon extra virgin olive oil, 0.125 teaspoon sea salt, 0.125 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon garlic powder, 1 pinch ground dried ginger, 1 pinch black pepper, 1 pinch chaat masala
instructions: 1. Add the cashews to a heavy bottom pan and roast on a medium heat, stirring constantly until crunchy, for 5 to 6 mins. Turn off the heat. 2. Optional – Add the oil. Coat the nuts with oil well. Mix all the spices and sprinkle. 3. Spread the cashews in a prepared baking tray and place in the center rack. Turn on the oven and roast/bake at 340 F (170 C) for 13 mins, stirring well after 8 mins. 4. Optional – Spicing: Add all the spices to a small bowl and mix well with oil. Add it over the toasted cashew nuts and mix with a spatula to spread the spices well. 5. Spread the cashews in 2 prepared trays. Place the first one in the center and the other below it. Turn on the oven and roast/bake them at 340 F (170 C) for 13 mins, stirring well after 8 mins. 6. Transfer all the roasted cashews to one tray. Mix together all the spices and oil in a small bowl. Pour over the cashews and coat them with a wooden spatula until well coated. 7. I usually put back the nuts in the oven (turned off), with the door left azar so they turn extra crisp. Transfer to an air tight container. Store at room temperature in a dry place and use up within a month. 8. Rinse and fine chop curry leaves or any herbs of your choice. Preheat oven at 360 For 180 C for 15 mins. Grease a baking tray or line it with a parchment paper or foil. 9. Add cashews to a mixing bowl and rinse them well under running water. Drain the water completely. 10. To the cashews, add salt, ginger garlic, chaat masala, curry leaves, ajwain and red chilli powder. 11. Mix them well. If you want to omit the flour, just spread them on the baking tray and put them in the oven at this stage. 12. Then add gram flour and rice flour. Begin to coat the cashews with the flour. At this stage there will be a lot of dry flour. 13. Sprinkle 1 to 2 tablespoons water and mix well adding more only as needed. All of the cashews should be coated well with the dough. 14. Avoid adding too much water as the dough comes off the cashews. Check step-by-step picture. 15. Taste test the dough and sprinkle some salt if you prefer. Lastly spray or pour oil and gently mix. Do not overmix else the dough will begin to clump & come off the cashews. 16. If all of the cashews are not coated well with the masala, then you can sprinkle more flour on top and mix gently. 17. Spread them gently on a baking tray. If any of the cashews aren’t covered with the masala, coat them with the dough. Roast them at 180 C for 7 mins. 18. After 7 mins, give a gentle stir and continue to bake for another 8 to 9 mins until crisp and golden. If you are making without flour, check after 14 mins. Adjust the timing as needed depending on your oven. If they are still soft, bake them a little longer. Keep checking after baking for a total of 15 mins. 19. Heat ¼ cup oil on a medium heat in a small deep kadai. When the oil turns hot, loosen the cashews from one other and add a few at a time. Fry on a medium heat until golden and crisp. Towards the end, fry on low heat for a golden color. Remove to a colander. 20. Place them in the air fryer basket and air fry at 180 C for 5 mins. After that gently separate any large clumps and continue to air fry for 3 mins. 21. They are done when they turn deep golden. They will be slightly soft when done but will firm up and become crunchy after cooling. Adjust the timing as needed, mine is done at 8 mins. 22. Cool masala cashews completely and store in an air tight jar.

title: Rogan Josh Recipe (Lamb Rogan Josh)
ingredients: 1.1 lbs bone-in mutton, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.5 cup curd, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1.25 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 cup water, 1.25 cup sliced shallots, 1 bay leaf, 3.5 cloves 3 to 4 cloves ((or ¼ teaspoon ground cloves)), 1 inch cinnamon, 3 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 2 teaspoon fennel seeds powder, 1 teaspoon ginger powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. This step is optional. But it helps to thicken the rogan josh. Heat 1.5 tablespoons oil in a pan and saute shallots/onions on a medium heat until golden, about to caramelize but not burnt. Cool them and blend with 2 to 3 tablespoons of yogurt to a smooth paste. Keep this aside. 2. Heat oil or ghee in a heavy bottom pot and add bay leaf, green cardamoms, cinnamon, cloves and black cardamoms. (If you opt for the ground spices, add them later.) 3. When the spices begin to sizzle in oil, add mutton and sear it for 1 to 2 minute on a high heat. 4. Reduce the heat to medium and fry for another 4 to 5 minutes until the meat is slightly brown. 5. While you fry the meat, add yogurt to a mixing bowl. Also add ground spices - red chilli powder, fennel powder, dried ginger powder and salt. 6. Whisk all of them well. If your yogurt is too thick (like Greek yogurt), add 3 to 4 tablespoons of water or stock and whisk it well until smooth. 7. Once the meat is light brown in color, add hing to the oil. Take the pot off the stove and slowly stir in the spice and yogurt mixture. 8. Mix well and place the pot back on the stove. Cook covered for 3 to 4 minutes on a medium heat. 9. Pour 1 cup hot water or stock. Cook covered on a low heat until tender, adding more hot water if required. 10. Stir rogan josh every 7 to 8 minutes. When it is done, the mutton pieces should be soft, tender and fall off the bone. (Optional - During the last 20 minutes of cooking add the blended onion paste and mix well.) 11. The cook time depends on the kind of pot, source of heat and the intensity of heat. Do not forget to add more hot water (if required) as you cook. It takes me 2 hours for the mutton to get tender. 12. The gravy in Rogan josh is neither too runny nor too thick but is slightly thick as the some of the bone marrow seeps into the liquids. This results can be achieved only by slow cooking the dish. 13. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala if required. Turn off the heat and rest for few hours before serving. This helps to develop the flavors. 14. Serve rogan josh with plain steamed basmati rice, Saffron Rice, Tandoori Roti, Butter Naan, Paratha or Roti.

title: Roti Pizza Recipe | Pizza Roti with Leftover Chapati
ingredients: roti chapathi, cheese slice, onion, capsicum, corn, tomato, paneer, mixed herbs, chilli flakes, cheese
instructions: 1. Heat a pan and add a little ghee. Place one roti on the pan. 2. Once the roti is slightly warm, flip it and place cheese slices on top. Spread the cheese to cover the roti completely, creating a cheesy crust. 3. Place another roti on top. Apply a little ghee and roast until the bottom turns slightly crisp. 4. Flip the roti stack carefully. 5. Spread pizza sauce evenly on top. (You can use homemade or store-bought sauce.) 6. Add a layer of grated cheese. 7. Top with vegetables of your choice, such as onion, capsicum, corn, and tomato. Add paneer cubes if desired. 8. Sprinkle mixed herbs and chilli flakes. 9. Add more cheese on top for an extra cheesy texture. 10. Cover and cook on low flame for about 5 minutes, or until the cheese melts completely. 11. Remove from the pan, slice, and serve Roti Pizza hot.

title: Royyala iguru
ingredients: 25 grams prawns, 2 medium onions, 1 green chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 tsp cumin, Salt, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, 1 tbsp Lemon juice, 1 tsp garam masala powder, 1 tbsp Coriander leaves, 1.5 tbsp oil
instructions: 1. Clean and wash prawns. Drain water thoroughly, marinate with ½ tsp. ginger garlic paste, ¼ tsp. red chili powder, little salt and lemon juice. Set this aside till you prepare the rest. 2. Heat a pan with oil, add cumin, and curry leaves and green chili. Sauté till the curry leaves turn crisp 3. Add onions, sprinkle salt and fry till they turn golden, reduce the flame to minimum (sim), continue to fry till they caramelize. Take care not to burn them. 4. Add ginger garlic paste and give a quick stir. Since the onions are already caramelized the ginger garlic can get burnt. Take care 5. Add the marinated prawns and fry for 2 to 3 mins 6. Add the rest of the red chili powder, turmeric and garam masala. Give a good stir and fry for a min 7. Sprinkle some water just enough to bring in some moisture for the prawns to get cooked. I used almost 90 ml water. 8. Cover and cook on a medium flame till they are completely cooked. I understand them to be cooked when they curl up and turn pink or orange. If there is more water left, you can evaporate by cooking further without a lid 9. Garnish royyala iguru with coriander leaves

title: Saag Aloo Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams potatoes, 15 grams baby spinach, 0.5 cup onion, 0.3333333333333333 cup tomatoes, 1.5 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon medium garlic, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon Garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.5 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Use only the leaves and avoid stems of the leafy greens. If using kale, destem them as well. 2. Rinse your greens in plenty of water and drain them to a colander. Shake off to drain the water completely. Keep aside to use later. Prepare the other ingredients. 3. Heat oil/ghee in a wide pan. Add cumin seeds. When the seeds begin to sizzle add onions and saute for 4 to 5 minutes. 4. Add potatoes, ginger, garlic and green chilies. Saute for another 1 minute. Stir in salt, chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder and garam masala. 5. Pour water and cook covered until the potatoes are al dente, not fully cooked or mushy. If required add more water as they cook. Taste test and add more salt or spices to taste. While the potatoes cook, chop the greens roughly & keep them ready. 6. Add tomatoes and cook on a medium high heat until they lose the raw flavor. Avoid over stirring here. 7. Optional - Add chopped fenugreek leaves/kale/mustard greens if using and stir fry until they wilt completely. 8. Add spinach in batches and saute until wilted. Add crushed kasuri methi, microgreens if using and turn off. Mix well to wilt the microgreens. 9. Let cool down a bit and squeeze in lemon juice and garnish with coriander leaves/ microgreens if you want.

title: Saag Paneer Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams paneer, 4 cups baby spinach, 4 cups mustard greens, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon ghee, 0.75 cup onions, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 inch ginger, 3 medium garlic, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrot, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.875 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 cup yogurt, 0.25 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 cup heavy cream, 1 lemon, 1 tablespoon unsalted butter, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 1.5 garlic, 1.5 red chili, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder
instructions: 1. Rinse the spinach and mustard greens at least thrice in plenty of water. Drain them to a colander. If using kale prepare reading the notes below. 2. Add ½ tablespoon ghee to a non-stick pan, spread and heat it. Place the cold paneer on the pan and fry on a medium high heat, until the bottom turns golden. Turn the pieces to the other side and repeat frying until golden. Keep this aside 3. Heat the rest of the ghee in a pan and add onions, green chilies, ginger and garlic. Saute until the raw flavor from onions disappear, for about 7 to 8 minutes. 4. Stir in coriander, turmeric and cumin. Add tomatoes and yogurt. Mix well. Cover and cook until the tomatoes break down to a mush. The onions should completely become soft. 5. Add carrot and saute for 2 mins, to bring out the sweet flavors. 6. Add spinach and mustard greens. Let them steam/cook or saute until wilted & cooked. It should take you about 5 to 6 minutes. 7. Pour water & reserve a green chili aside. Blend to a slightly coarse puree using an immersion blender or a blender. If using a blender, cool down first. If the mixture is too thick, use a splash of water. Taste test and if you want add the reserved chili & blend. 8. Mix together saag puree and salt in the pan. Bring it to a gentle simmer and cook on a medium heat for 6 to 7 mins, or until it no more smells raw. If it is too thick to your liking, add some hot water during this process. 9. Add garam masala, fried paneer (retain some to garnish) and sugar (if you need). Mix together very well first and then stir in the cream. Cook for another 3 to 4 minutes, until aromatic. 10. To Temper – Add butter to a pan. Turn on the stove & regulate to low heat. Before the butter melts, add garlic and let cook for 2 minutes on a very low heat to infuse the flavors. Do not brown the garlic, add dried red chili & fry until crisp. 11. Turn off the heat. Add hing and red chili powder or flakes. Pour this over Saag Paneer & garnish with fried paneer. Serve with Tandoori Roti or Basmati Rice. Squeeze lemon juice while serving.

title: Sabji Masala Recipe | How to Make Multipurpose Sabzi Mix Powder
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 7 cloves garlic, 1 cup peanut, 2 tbsp dry coconut, few curry leaves, 0.5 cup coriander seeds, 2 tbsp cumin, 1 tbsp fennel, 0.25 cup sesame, 0.25 cup chilli powder, 1 tbsp turmeric, 2 tbsp aamchur, 1 tbsp garam masala, 0.25 cup kasuri methi, 2 tbsp sesame, 2 tbsp salt
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil and roast 7 cloves garlic until it turns golden brown. 2. Add 1 cup peanut and roast until it turns crunchy. 3. Now add 2 tbsp dry coconut and few curry leaves. 4. Keeping the flame on low, roast until it turns golden brown. 5. Transfer to a plate and allow to cool completely. 6. In a pan take ½ cup coriander seeds, 2 tbsp cumin, 1 tbsp fennel, and ¼ cup sesame. 7. Roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 8. Cool completely, and grind to a coarse powder. 9. Transfer the powder to a large bowl. 10. Add ¼ cup chilli powder, 1 tbsp turmeric, 2 tbsp aamchur, 1 tbsp garam masala, ¼ cup kasuri methi, 2 tbsp sesame, and 2 tbsp salt. 11. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 12. You can use this sabji masala mix to prepare any dry sabzi or bharwa sabji. 13. Finally, store the Sabji Masala Recipe in an airtight container.

title: sabudana falooda recipe | sago royal falooda | sabudana dessert | sago drink
ingredients: 4 cup water, 0.5 cup sabudana sago, 3 cup milk, 2 tbsp rooh afza, 1 tbsp rooh afza, 2 tbsp sabja basil seeds, 2 tbsp strawberry jelly, 3 tbsp nuts, 3 tbsp tutti frutti, 1 scoop vanilla ice cream, 1 cherry
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 4 cup water and add ½ cup sabudana. 2. stir and boil for 10 minutes stirring in between. 3. continue to boil until the sabudana turns translucent. 4. drain off the sabudana once it is cooked completely. 5. now rinse with cold water. this helps to rinse off excess starch and prevents from sticking. 6. sabudana pearls are ready. keep aside. 7. firstly, in a large kadai take 3 cup milk. 8. stir and boil until the milk reduces slightly. 9. cool the milk completely. recommend chilling the milk as it gives a good flavour. 10. now add 2 tbsp 2 tbsp rooh afza and mix well. 11. finally, rose milk is ready. you can adjust the concentration of rooh afza to your choice. 12. take a tall glass, and add 1 tbsp rooh afza and 2 tbsp sabja. 13. now add 2 tbsp prepared sago pearls and 2 tbsp strawberry jelly. 14. further add 2 tbsp nuts and 2 tbsp tutti frutti. 15. pour in 1 cup chilled rose milk and stir gently. 16. further scoop in 1 scoop vanilla ice cream. 17. garnish with chopped nuts, tutti frutti and cherry. 18. finally, enjoy sabudana falooda or sago falooda with more rooh afza.

title: Sabudana Kheer Recipe (Sago Kheer)
ingredients: 0.25 cup sabudana, 3.5 tablespoons sugar, 1 cup water, 1 cup milk, 0.125 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 tablespoon nuts
instructions: 1. Rinse ¼ cup sabudana very well at least twice. Then pour 1 cup water and begin to cook stirring often to prevent burning. You can also soak the sabudana first in hot water for 30 mins to overnight to reduce the cook time. Refrigerate soaked & drained sabudana in the fridge for up to 2 days so you can cook anytime easily. 2. When the sabudana turns transparent check if they are cooked through. You can bite a few and check. They should not be hard or chewy. All of the pearls look transparent this is the first sign they are well cooked. Pour 1 cup milk and mix. 3. Continue to cook until they turn completely soft and light. The mixture also turns thick & looks starchy. Then add 1/8 teaspoon cardamom powder. 4. Add 3 to 4 tablespoons sugar. I used brown sugar. You can also use jaggery. If you add jaggery to the boiling kheer it will curdle. So to use jaggery turn off the stove, cool down a bit & add the grated jaggery. Or make a jaggery syrup following the recipe notes and cool it completely. Then add it here. If using jaggery to kheer, you should never cook it further after adding it to avoid curdling. 5. Since I used sugar I boiled until the sabudana kheer turned thick. It will thicken further upon cooling so turn off when it is of pouring yet thick consistency. Serve sabudana kheer chilled or warm. Garnish with chopped nuts and saffron if you desire.

title: Sabudana Khichdi Recipe (Fluffy & Non-Sticky)
ingredients: 1 cup sabudana, 0.25 cup peanuts, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 green chili, 1 medium boiled potato, 0.5 teaspoon sendha namak adjust to taste, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 1 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 teaspoon ginger, 2 tbsp coconut
instructions: 1. Add sabudana to a wide bowl and pour 2 to 3 cups of water. Rub them well with your fingers and drain the water completely. 2. Repeat this steps of adding fresh water, rubbing and then draining the starchy water completely. Do this at least thrice. 3. Then pour ¾ cups fresh water and soak them for 4 to 6 hours or until softened. You can also leave them overnight. 4. After 4 to 6 hours, check if the sabudana is soaked well and softened by pressing a few pearls in between your thumb and fore finger. Well soaked sabudana will get mashed well and completely. 5. If they are hard, cover and set aside for few more hours. You can also sprinkle some water if needed at this stage. 6. When the sabudana is soaked well, transfer them to a colander to ensure there is no excess water left. 7. Dry roast 4 tbsps peanuts until golden and aromatic. Set these aside. 8. Optional - If you prefer to add some crushed or powdered peanuts, you can powder half of them & set aside. Crushed peanuts coat the sabudana and help to keep them grainy without turning sticky. 9. Heat ghee or oil in a pan. Then add cumin, green chilies, ginger and curry leaves. 10. Saute for a minute and then add cubed boiled potatoes. Saute them for 1 to 2 minutes. 11. Meanwhile mix together sabudana, salt, sugar and peanut powder (optional). 12. Regulate the flame to high. Add sabudana to the pan along with coconut (optional). 13. Stir and saute for 2 to 3 minutes or until transparent. Overcooking can make them sticky. 14. Immediately remove from the heat. Squeeze in the lemon juice, add coriander leaves & roasted peanuts. 15. Mix the lemon juice lightly. This helps to release any sabudana stuck together. 16. Serve sabudana khichdi with yogurt.

title: Sabudana Khichdi Recipe | 5 Tips For NonSticky
ingredients: 2 cup sabudana, 1.5 cup water, 0.25 cup peanut, 2 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp peanut, 1 potato, 1 tsp cumin, few curry leaves, 3 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp senda namak, 1 tsp sugar, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 lemon juice
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup sabudana and rinse in water for 3 to 4 times. Make sure to rub the sabudana to remove excess starch. 2. Add 1½ cup water. Dip the sago completely, and rest for 5 hours. Soaking time varies depending on the quality of Sabudana. 3. Once the Sabudana is soaked well, ensure there is no water at the bottom. Otherwise, the khichdi will turn mushy. The Sabudana must be able to mash easily when pressed between fingers. If it is still hard, sprinkle some water and soak for an additional 30 minutes. 4. Now, in a heavy-bottomed pan, roast ¼ cup of peanuts. 5. Roast on a low flame until the peanuts turn crunchy. 6. Cool completely, peel the skin and transfer to the mixer jar. Pulse and powder to a coarse peanut powder. Keep aside. 7. Now, in a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp ghee. 8. Fry 2 tbsp peanuts till they turn golden brown, keep aside. 9. Also, fry 1 potato till it turns golden brown and crispy. Keep aside. 10. In the same ghee, splutter 1 tsp cumin and a few curry leaves. Add 1-inch ginger, 2 chili and saute well till the spices turn aromatic. 11. Furthermore, add the soaked sabudana, 1 tsp senda namak, crushed peanut powder and 1 tsp sugar. 12. Mix well, ensuring they are thoroughly combined. 13. Cover and cook until the sago pearls turn translucent. 14. Now add fried peanuts, potatoes, ½ lemon and 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 15. Finally, enjoy Sabudana Khichdi or pack it in your lunchbox.

title: sabudana papad recipe | sabakki sandige | javvarisi vadam | sago fryums
ingredients: 1 cup sabudana sabakki tapioca, 4 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp chilli paste, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup sabudana in enough water for 3 to 4 hours or till it gets mashed up easily. depending on quality of sabudana increase or decrease the soaking time. 2. after 4 hours drain off the water and transfer to a large kadai. 3. add 4 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp chilli paste and mix well. 4. boil for 20 minutes stirring occasionally. 5. cook till the sabudana pearls turns translucent and swells. 6. turn of the flame and add pinch hing, 1 tsp cumin and 1 tbsp lemon juice. 7. mix well and cool for 10 minutes. 8. cool till the mixture turns slightly thick flowing consistency. 9. line a greased baking paper over the tray or use large plastic sheets and pour a tbsp of prepared sabudana mixture making a small rounds. 10. sun dry them for 2 days or till it dries out completely turning translucent. 11. now the sandige / sabudana papad is ready to store for atleast 6 months when stored in airtight container. 12. or deep fry in hot oil over both the sides. 13. keep stirring till the sabudana papad or sabakki sandige doubles in size. 14. finally, serve sabudana papad / sabakki sandige as a side dish or enjoy with chai.

title: sabudana tikki recipe | sabudana cutlet recipe | sabudana patties
ingredients: 0.5 sabudana sago tapioca, 1 potato, 0.25 cup peanuts groundnuts, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, salt to taste, 6 tsp oil
instructions: 1. firstly, take soaked sabudana in large mixing bowl 2. add in 1 large boiled and grated potato. 3. also add ¼ cup crushed peanuts, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp aamchur and salt to taste. 4. combine, mix and mash well with your hand. 5. now prepare small flat patties. 6. roast the sabudana tikki (patties) on medium hot tawa. 7. spread few tsp of oil, for even roasting. 8. flip over once one side turns golden brown. 9. spread oil again and roast both sides to golden and crisp. 10. finally, serve sabudana tikki with tomato sauce or green chutney.

title: Sabudana Vada Recipe (with Air-fryer option)
ingredients: 0.5 cup sabudana, 1 cup potatoes cubed, 0.25 cup peanuts, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 green chilies, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon rock salt, 1.5 teaspoons lemon juice, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon flour kuttu ka atta if fasting, 1 teaspoon ginger
instructions: 1. Add ½ cup sabudana to a wide bowl. You can optionally rinse them well twice. 2. Then pour ½ cup water and soak sabudana for 4 to 6 hours depending on the kind. You can also soak overnight. 3. Some need only 2 hours of soaking while some need at least 5 hours. 4. Once they are soaked, take few pearls in between your thumb and forefinger. 5. Press down to check if they have softened. The pearls must get mashed well. 6. Make sure they are soaked well & softened otherwise unsoaked sabudana may burst in oil. 7. You can also bite and check. It should not be hard from inside. If you feel they are hard, soak them for a little longer. 8. Drain them completely to a colander and set aside. 9. Meanwhile roast peanuts until golden and aromatic. Cool completely. 10. Pulse them in a blender only once to get coarsely crushed peanuts. Keep aside. 11. Wash and peel potatoes. Boil them just until done and not too mushy or soggy. 12. Mash them. If the potatoes are mushy then the vadas may break. So cook them just al dente. 13. Mix together sabudana, mashed potato, peanuts, green chili, ginger, salt, cumin and coriander leaves. Add lemon juice (optional) and mix well. 14. If the dough turns sticky, then just grease your finger lightly. 15. Mixing well helps in binding the dough well and prevents the sabudana vada from breaking while frying. 16. Make 10 to 12 balls and then slightly flatten to make the patties or vada. 17. Do not make them too thin as they may break. Making then too thick will not cook them from inside. 18. Heat oil in a kadai until hot enough on a medium flame. 19. Drop a small portion of the dough to check if the oil is hot enough. The dough has to rise without browning quickly. 20. Next take 1 tsp of the mixture in your hand and roll it to a small vada. 21. Slide it to the hot oil to check if it doesn't disintegrate. 22. If the ball breaks, then add 1 tablespoon flour to the mixture and mix it well. Then shape them to vadas. 23. Slide the sabudana vada to hot oil one after the other. Do not crowd them all at one time. Slide only 2 to 3 depending on the size of your pan. 24. After adding the vadas, let them deep fry on a medium flame for a few minutes. Do not disturb them or touch them as they may break. When they firm up and become slightly golden, flip to the other side. Fry until golden and crisp. 25. The vadas will puff well and turn golden & crisp on the outside. 26. Drain them to a kitchen tissue or colander. Serve sabudana vada with green chutney. 27. I prefer to shape our sabudana vada thinner so they won't remain chewy and undercooked inside. Shape the mixture to not very thin or very thick patties. 28. Place them in the air fryer basket. Air fry at 360 F or 180 C for 14 to 16 minutes, turning after 10 minutes or until crisp and golden. Adjust the timing depending on your air fryer. 29. I brush them with ghee as soon as they are out of the air fryer. 30. Place them on a prepared tray with parchment paper and preheat your oven for at least 20 mins. Bake at 390 F or 190 C for 30 to 35 minutes or a little longer until crisp.

title: Sabudana Vada Recipe | Sago Vada | Sabakki Vada
ingredients: 1 cup sabudana, 2 potato, 0.5 cup roasted peanut, 4 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 lemon juice, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, to prepare a non-sticky sabudana, in a bowl, rinse 1 cup sabudana and soak in ¾ cup water for 8 hours. 2. Transfer the soaked sabudana in to the large plate. 3. Mash 2 boiled potato. Potato helps to bind the vada well. 4. Also add ½ cup roasted peanut, 4 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt and ½ lemon juice. 5. Mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined. 6. Squeeze slightly, till the mixture binds well and holds its shape. 7. Prepare small patties from the sabudana mixture. 8. Deep fry the patties in hot oil. Or preheat and bake at 180 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes. 9. Fry on medium flame until they turn golden brown, flipping in between. 10. Drain off excess oil over a kitchen towel. 11. Finally, serve Sabudana Vada Recipe hot along with masala tea.

title: Sabudana Vada Recipe | Sago Vada | Sabakki Vada
ingredients: 1 cup sabudana, 2 potato, 0.5 cup roasted peanut, 4 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 lemon juice, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, to prepare a non-sticky sabudana, in a bowl, rinse 1 cup sabudana and soak in ¾ cup water for 8 hours. 2. Transfer the soaked sabudana in to the large plate. 3. Mash 2 boiled potato. Potato helps to bind the vada well. 4. Also add ½ cup roasted peanut, 4 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt and ½ lemon juice. 5. Mix well, ensuring everything is thoroughly combined. 6. Squeeze slightly, till the mixture binds well and holds its shape. 7. Prepare small patties from the sabudana mixture. 8. Deep fry the patties in hot oil. Or preheat and bake at 180 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes. 9. Fry on medium flame until they turn golden brown, flipping in between. 10. Drain off excess oil over a kitchen towel. 11. Finally, serve Sabudana Vada Recipe hot along with masala tea.

title: Saffron Rice (with Basmati)
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 1.5 tablespoons unsalted butter, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon saffron stands, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom, 0.25 teaspoon cinnamon, 0.25 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.25 cup toasted pistachios, 0.25 cup sweet raisins
instructions: 1. Add rice to a colander or bowl and rinse well thrice. Drain completely. Mix together cardamom, nutmeg and cinnamon in a small bowl. Keep this aside. Crush the saffron in a mortar pestle to a fine powder. 2. Melt butter in a saucepan and add raisins. Fry them on a medium heat, until plump. Remove to a small bowl, retaining most of the ghee in the pan. 3. Stir in the rice gently to coat it with the rest of the butter and let toast on a high heat for 2 mins. Stir gently only 1 to 2 times (taking care not to overdo else the grains can break) and toast for another 1 to 2 mins. 4. Add crushed saffron, ginger, salt and pour water. Give a gentle mix and bring the water to a rolling boil, on a high heat. 5. Reduce the heat to low and cook covered until the water is fully absorbed and the rice is cooked through. (It takes me about 13 to 15 mins but can vary depending on the cookware and intensity of heat) 6. Sprinkle the ground spices all over and cover back the pot. Let rest for 10 mins. Later fluff up the rice gently with a fork to spread the spices all over. 7. Garnish Saffron Rice with chopped toast pistachios, raisins or pomegranate arils. Serve with curry, lentils, kababs, grilled veggies or kofta.

title: Saggubiyyam payasam
ingredients: 0.5 cup saggubiyyam, 1.5 cups water, 1 cup milk, 0.25 cup jaggery, 1 pinch cardamom powder, 1 tablespoon ghee, 4 cashews, 4 raisins
instructions: 1. If your jaggery/ palm jaggery is clean or you prefer to use sugar you can skip this step. But if you are unsure whether clean or not, melt it in a hot pot with just little hot water. Filter it through a coffee strainer, boil it until it reaches a thick consistency. Set this aside to cool. To cool this, i transfer to a wide bowl and cool it inside a bowl of cold water. 2. Wash and cook half cup sago in 1.5 cups of water till they are cooked and look transparent. You can also use a handful of pesara pappu or moong dal to this. Make sure pappu/ dal is cooked well. 3. Add 1 cup milk and cook on a medium flame. Keep stirring. 4. The payasam turns thick and sago is cooked completely. 5. Add cardamom powder / elaichi powder. Cook for a while. 6. Add the cooled and thickened jaggery syrup or sugar. Stir and switch off. Cooking further may curdle the payasam. 7. Add ghee to a hot pan. Roast nuts and raisins until golden. 8. Pour this to the payasam. 9. Grate the jaggery and add it to a pan with 3 to 4 tbsps water. Heat it until the jaggery dissolves. Place a filter over a glass and pass the jaggery syrup through it. Rinse the pot and add back the jaggery syrup to it. Boil this until the syrup thickens a bit. Cool this completely and set aside. 10. Add saggubiyyam to another pot and rinse them well twice. Pour 1½ cups water and cook them until they turn transparent. You can also bite them and check they must be fully cooked and not be hard inside. If needed add more water and keep stirring until done. 11. Pour milk and cook until it thickens. Stir in the cardamom powder and cooled jaggery syrup. Do not pour hot jaggery syrup to the cooked saggubiyyam otherwise the payasam will curdle completely. 12. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee in a small pan. Add split cashews and fry until they turn lightly golden. Then stir in the raisins and fry until they turn plump. Pour this to the saggubiyyam payasam. 13. Always cool the foods before offering to god. Sagubiyyam payasam is served after cooling completely to have the cooling effects on the body. 14. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > title: Sagu Recipe
ingredients: 2 potatoes, 0.25 cup green peas, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2.5 green chilies, 0.25 inch ginger, 0.25 cup fried gram, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 0.25 teaspoon cumin, 1 pinch hing, 1 pinch turmeric, Salt, 2 tablespoons Oil, 2 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Peel and chop potatoes to small cubes. The smaller, the quicker the curry gets cooked. I chop them to almost half inch. Heat oil in a deep pan, add mustard, cumin and curry leaves. When the spices begin to crackle, add hing, ginger, slit green chilies, potatoes and peas. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes. Make sure you add enough green chilies since there is no red chili powder used.
