The heat comes only from green chilies. If you want to a use onions, add them before the potatoes and saute for 4 to 5 mins. 2. Pour water to cover the potatoes and bring the water to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat to medium and cook until the potatoes are fork tender/ fully cooked. 3. While the potatoes cook, make a fine powder of the fried gram. 4. Add very little water to make a thick paste but of pouring consistency. Add salt and turmeric to the paste. 5. Once the potatoes are soft cooked, pour this mix and let it simmer till the gravy thickens. If your gravy becomes runny, you can mix some more fried gram powder with very little water and pour it to the gravy. When it is thick, yet of pouring consistency, tun off the heat. It will thicken further upon cooling. Taste test and adjust salt. 6. Add lemon juice and stir well. Garnish with coriander leaves if you want.

title: Sagu Recipe | Mixed Vegetable Saagu - Hotel Style
ingredients: 1 carrot, 0.5 cup peas, 1 potato, 1 beans, 1 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 2 cup water, 0.5 cup coconut, 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, 4 cashew, 1 tbsp poppy seeds, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 0.5 tsp pepper, 1 inch ginger, 2 cloves garlic, 0.5 onion, handful coriander, 5 chilli, 0.25 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 0.5 onion, 1 tomato, 0.5 capsicum, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. Firstly, take 1 carrot, ½ cup peas, 1 potato and 10 beans in a saucepan. 2. Add 1 tsp salt, ¼ tsp turmeric, and 2 cup water. 3. Boil for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are half cooked. Keep aside. 4. To prepare the masala paste, in a mixer jar, take ½ cup of coconut, 2 tbsp roasted gram dal, and 4 cashews. 5. Also, 1 tbsp poppy seeds, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves, and ½ tsp pepper. 6. Further add 1 inch ginger, 2 cloves garlic, ½ onion, a handful of coriander, 5 chilli, and ¼ cup water. 7. Grind to a smooth paste. Keep aside. 8. In a kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil. Splutter 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, pinch hing, and few curry leaves. 9. Add ½ onion and saute well. 10. Also, add 1 tomato, ½ capsicum, ¼ tsp turmeric, and 1 tsp salt. 11. Saute for 2 minutes or until tomatoes soften. 12. Now add in prepared masala paste and cook till the oil separates. 13. Add in cooked vegetables and mix well. 14. Adjust the consistency by adding ½ cup water or as required. 15. Cover and cook for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are cooked well. 16. Finally, enjoy Veg Sagu Recipe with poori or roti.

title: Salmon Curry Recipe
ingredients: 1 lb salmon, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 2.5 garlic cloves, 1 cup onion, 1 green chili slit, 0.25 cup tomato, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.125 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1.125 teaspoon coriander powder, 1.5 tablespoons tamarind, 1.25 cups water, 0.5 cup coconut milk, 2 sprigs coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Bring salmon to room temperature. Rinse it well under running water and pat dry with a kitchen tissue. 2. If using tamarind, soak it in half cup hot water. 3. Pour 1 tbsp oil to a nonstick pan and heat it. 4. When the oil turns hot, place the salmon with the skin side down. 5. Sprinkle 1 pinch of each - salt, red chilli powder and garam masala. Fry it for 1 to 2 mins & then carefully flip. 6. Repeat sprinkling salt, red chili powder and garam masala on the other side. Add curry leaves towards the sides in the oil. 7. Fry just for another 1 to 2 mins depending on the thickness of the fillet. 8. Salmon should be partially cooked, not fully. Gently remove it to a plate along with crispy curry leaves and set aside. 9. Pour 1 tablespoon oil to the pan and heat it. 10. Add mustard seeds. When they pop, add curry leaves and garlic. 11. Saute until the garlic smells fragrant. 12. Then saute onions and green chilies until onions turn golden. 13. Then add tomatoes, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala and coriander powder. 14. Saute all of these until tomatoes break down and the masala turns fragrant. The mixture should leave the sides of the pan as well. 15. Pour 1 cup water and ¼ cup tamarind water. Mix well and cook until the onions turn completely soft. The curry should thick yet slightly be runny at this stage. 16. Taste the curry and add more salt and tamarind if needed. 17. Optional - Pour coconut milk and stir. 18. Gently place the salmon (skin side down) and pour some of the curry over the fillets. 19. Cover and cook on the lowest flame until the salmon is cooked completely. When it is done you can see the color changes. Press down the fillet gently, if done it flakes. 20. Sprinkle coriander leaves. Rest the salmon curry for atleast 2 hours before serving.

title: Salmon Rice Bowl
ingredients: 35 grams salmon, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon paprika, 0.75 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.75 teaspoon onion powder, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon sugar, 1 teaspoon soya sauce, 0.5 teaspoon rice vinegar, 1 cup basmati rice, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.625 tablespoon butter, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper crushed, 1 cucumber, 1 medium ripe tomato, 0.25 cup parsley, 1 red onion, 1 teaspoon extra virgin olive oil, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, salt if required, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 cup yogurt, 0.75 cup mint leaves, 0.5 cup cilantro, 1 garlic clove, 0.25 inch ginger, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.375 green chili pepper
instructions: 1. Rinse rice at least thrice and drian the water completely. To a pot/pan, add butter and melt it on a low flame. Stir in turmeric and salt, followed by water/stock. 2. Cover and bring the water to a rapid boil. Add the rice and increase the heat to bring to a boil. 3. Reduce the heat completely and cook covered until all of the water is absorbed by the rice. This takes about 12 to 14 mins depending on your stove. 4. Once the water is absorbed, open the lid and sprinkle the garlic powder, black pepper and garam masala. Do not stir. Cover and let rest for 15 mins. Later taste test and add more salt and seasoning if required. 5. While the rice cooks, pluck the mint and coriander leaves, rinse, drain and add them to a small grinder/blender. Add ginger garlic salt pepper or chili and 1 tablespoon yogurt. 6. Blend until smooth and stir in the rest of the yogurt. Mix well with a whisk, do not blend after adding more yogurt. Taste test and adjust salt. 7. Wash and chop the cucumbers, tomatoes, onions and herbs. Transfer them to a bowl. Sprinkle lemon juice, oil and black pepper just before serving. 8. Mix together soya sauce and vinegar in a bowl. Keep aside. Add all the seasoning spices to a bowl and mix well. Keep aside. 9. Pat dry the salmon cubes. Heat a non-stick pan with oil and spread it all over the pan. Place the salmon cubes (with the skin side down if it has skin). 10. Let cook for 3 to 4 mins on a medium to medium high heat. Using a spatula or a tong gently turn them to the other side and pan-fry until cooked through, for another 3 to 4 mins, depending on the thickness of the salmon. 11. Salmon is considered fully cooked when the internal temperature of the thickest part reaches 145 F. If you do not have a meat thermometer, you will know it is done when it flakes easily with the touch of a fork. 12. Pour soya sauce/tamari and vinegar all over the salmon. Immediately sprinkle the spices all over. Turn off the heat and gently turn the pieces if required to coat the sauce and spices in the pan. Taste test and adjust the seasoning. 13. Fluff up the rice in the pot with a fork and divide the rice to 2 large serving bowls. Place the salmon, salad and drizzle yogurt mint sauce as preferred. 14. Serve salmon rice bowls hot or warm.

title: sama ke chawal pulao recipe | sama rice pulao | farali recipe | upvas recipe
ingredients: 1 cup sama rice samo rice moriyo samvat barnyard millet, water, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 3 pods cardamom, 0.5 tsp pepper, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 0.5 potato, 0.5 carrot, 2 cup water, 0.5 tsp sendha namak rock salt, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 2 tbsp peanut, 5 almond, 1 cashew, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 0.5 cup makhana fox nuts, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup sama rice for 20 minutes. 2. in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp ghee, add 1 tsp cumin, 3 pods cardamom and ½ tsp pepper. saute slightly. 3. now add 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli and saute well. 4. add ½ potato, ½ carrot and saute for 3 minutes or until they are half cooked. 5. further add soaked sama rice. make sure to drain off the water completely. 6. pour in 2 cup water, ½ tsp sendha namak, ½ tsp pepper powder and mix well. 7. cover and simmer for 15 minutes or until the sama rice is cooked completely. 8. now in a pan heat ½ tbsp ghee, add 2 tbsp peanut, 5 almond, 10 cashew, 2 tbsp dry coconut, ½ cup makhana 9. roast on low flame until they turn crunchy and golden brown. 10. transfer the roasted nuts over pulao. 11. add 1 tbsp lemon juice and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 12. finally, enjoy sama ke chawal pulao with curd.

title: Sambar Powder Recipe (Sambar Masala)
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoons bengal gram, 1.5 tablespoon black gram, 1 tablespoon toor dal, 1 red chilies, 0.25 cup coriander seeds 4 tbsps of 22 to 25 grams, 0.75 teaspoon methi seeds, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 2 teaspoon cumin, 1 teaspoon pepper corn, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric
instructions: 1. Heat a pan. If making sambar powder in large quantity then dry roast each ingredient separately as the cooking time is different. Since this recipe is to make little sambar powder I have roasted them together but in batches. 2. Pour 1 ½ tablespoon chana dal, 1 ½ tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon toor dal and 8 to 12 dried red chilies. You can also use more red chilies than mentioned in the recipe. But with the amount I mentioned it was too hot for my kids. So if you have kids do adjust. Dry roast them on a low flame until the dal is golden in color & begin to turn aromatic. 3. Add ¼ cup coriander seeds and ¾ teaspoon methi seeds. Roast until coriander seeds turn crunchy and the methi seeds are deep roasted & aromatic. 4. When you smell the roasted methi seeds, add 2 sprigs curry leaves and roast until they turn crisp. By now the methi seeds will turn slightly darker in color. 5. Add 2 teaspoon cumin seeds and roast till crunchy. You will smell the cumin good very soon. Turn off the stove. If you prefer to use pepper corn, you can add 1 teaspoon black pepper corn along with cumin. We use pepper only for rasam powder. 6. Add ¼ teaspoon turmeric and 1/8 teaspoon hing to this. Turmeric is used here as a preservative to keep the sambar masala good. Transfer all these to a wide plate and cool completely.

title: sambar powder recipe | sambar masala | sambar podi | sambhar masala at home
ingredients: 2 tsp coconut oil, 1 cup coriander seeds, 0.25 cup cumin seeds, 2 tbsp methi, 2 tbsp urad dal, 1 tbsp chana dal, 0.5 cup curry leaves, 1 gram red chilli, 1 tsp turmeric
instructions: 1. how to make udupi sambar pudi or sambar powder: 2. firstly, in a heavy-bottomed pan heat ½ tsp coconut oil and saute 1 cup ((75 grams)) coriander seeds. 3. saute on low flame until the coriander seeds turn aromatic. 4. transfer to a large plate and keep aside. 5. in the same pan heat ½ tsp coconut oil, add ¼ cup ((25 grams)) cumin seeds and 2 tbsp ((20 grams)) methi, 2 tbsp ((30 grams)) urad dal and 1 tbsp ((15 grams)) chana dal. 6. roast on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 7. now add ½ cup ((10 grams)) curry leaves and roast well. 8. roast until the curry leaves turn crisp. 9. transfer to a large plate and keep aside. 10. further heat 1 tsp coconut oil and saute 100 gram dried red chilli. 11. saute until the chills puff up and turn crisp. 12. transfer to a large plate and cool completely. 13. now take all the roasted spices to the mixi, add 1 tsp turmeric. 14. grind to a fine powder. 15. finally, udupi style sambar powder or sambar pudi is ready to prepare udupi sambar.

title: Sambar Recipe, How to Make Sambar
ingredients: 0.75 cup toor dal, 2 cups water, 1.5 tablespoon tamarind, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 0.75 tablespoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1 medium onion diced, 1 tomato, 1 medium carrot diced, 1.5 vegetable drumsticks, 2.5 bhindi, 1 green chili, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 Pinch methi seeds, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 1.5 red chili, 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 0.375 teaspoon methi seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 4.5 red chilies
instructions: 1. Wash toor dal a few times in cooker or pot until the water runs clear. 2. Pour 2 cups water & pressure cook for 2 to 4 whistles depending on the brand of cooker. If cooking in Instant pot, press the pressure cook button & set the timer for 9 mins. If you do not have a pressure cooker, it can be cooked in a pot until soft adding more water as needed. 3. Rinse all the veggies. Scrape the drumsticks lightly & rinse. Chop them to 2 inch pieces. Peel the shallots as well & rinse. 4. Dice tomatoes & chop okra to 1 inch pieces. If using pumpkin, peel the skin & dice. Keep all these aside. 5. While the dal cooks, make the sambar powder. Dry roast red chilies, urad dal and chana dal until golden & crunchy. Add coriander seeds & fry till aromatic. Remove to a plate. 6. Next add methi seeds and saute until slightly dark. Then add cumin, fry for a minute. Cool these and powder finely in a spice jar. 7. Heat 1 tsp oil in a pot. Saute onions for 1 minute. Add all the veggies & saute for 2 mins. This saute step can be skipped but sometimes onions, tomatoes and chilli powder don't smell good when boiled without sautéing. 8. Stir in the chilli powder (optional) and pour 4 cups water. 9. When the water turns hot, take ¼ cup hot water with a ladle to a bowl. Add tamarind & jaggery (optional). Set aside to soak. 10. When the veggies are cooked al dente, add turmeric, sambar powder & salt. Squeeze the tamarind and pour the tamarind water. I prefer to filter the tamarind water while pouring. 11. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes. When the pressure releases, mash the dal to smooth with a masher or ladle. 12. Stir it to the boiling sambar. Adjust salt and tamarind if needed. If it is too thick to your liking, add little hot water. Bring it to a gentle boil and add coriander leaves. 13. Heat a small pan with ghee. Add mustard, cumin, methi & red chillies. When the spices pop, add curry leaves. 14. The leaves will turn crisp, then add hing & pour this to the simmering sambar. 15. Serve sambar with rice, idli, vada or dosa. 16. Set aside the desired portion to refrigerate in smaller containers. 17. Cool completely and refrigerate immediately. To retain the taste of sambar, refrigerate within two hours of cooking. 18. It keeps good for about 2 to 4 days. Bring it to a boil & make a fresh tempering.

title: Sambar rice
ingredients: 0.25 cup toor dal, 0.5 cup rice, 2 cups water, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1.5 tbsp ghee, 2 pinches turmeric, 0.25 tsp red chilli powder, 9 shallots, 1 drumstick, 1 carrot, 1 tomato, 1 tbsp tamarind, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 1 red chilies, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 1.5 tsp coriander seeds, 0.75 tbsp coconut, 0.125 tsp methi
instructions: 1. Add ½ cup rice and ¼ cup plus 2 tbsp dal to a pressure cooker. I have used a mix of toor and moong dal. Wash and soak for about 15 minutes. Cook with 2 cups water for 1 to 2 whistles on a medium flame. You can use a combination of white and brown rice or white rice and fox tail millet. If using millets, soak at least for 2 hours. 2. If using brown rice, the texture turns out perfect even when the dal and rice are cooked together. Since brown rice doesn’t turn very mushy but the dal turns very soft and hence adds a smooth texture to the sambar rice. Mash the dal and rice. Dal must become soft and blend well with rice. 3. If you have ready sambar powder, skip this one and the next 2 steps. But i suggest making this powder to get a very flavorful dish. Dry roast the ingredients for making the powder. You will find double the quantity of ingredients as i doubled it to make this recipe twice. First roast dals with red chilies until lightly golden, add coriander seeds. When they get roasted, add methi, cumin. Switch off the stove when you begin to smell the methi good. 4. Add coconut and toss in the hot pan for a while. You can also skip coconut if you do not have. 5. Cool these and powder finely. 6. Heat ghee or oil in a pan. Add red chili, mustard and cumin. When they splutter add curry leaves and hing. When the curry leaves turn crisp, i set aside half of this seasoning to use at the last step. 7. Add shallots, carrots, beans and drumsticks. If you are short of time skip drumsticks since they lot of time to cook. Chayote/ chow chow or mullangi can also be used. 8. Saute well for 2 to 3 minutes. Add tomatoes, red chilli powder (optional) and turmeric. You can also skip tomatoes. Saute for 3 to 4 minutes. 9. Add 1 cup water and cook until the veggies are soft cooked. 10. Make sure the veggies are cooked completely before adding tamarind. so check the drumstick & then proceed. 11. Add salt. 12. Add the sambar powder. 13. Simmer well for few minutes until the spice powder begins to smell good. Check if there is enough sour, salt and spice. You can adjust now. 14. Add the mashed rice. 15. Stir well and simmer until dal rice blends with the sambar masala. Taste test and add more salt, tamarind or sambar powder if needed. To add more sambar powder, simply stir it with little hot water and pour. Add more hot water if needed to bring it to a consistency. 16. Add the tempering that was set aside. This doesn’t give any special tadka flavor but i still prefer. Serve sambar rice with papad and ghee.

title: sambar rice recipe | sambar sadam recipe | sambar rice & curd rice combo
ingredients: 1 tsp oil, 1 tbsp chana dal, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.25 tsp methi, 0.5 tsp pepper, 6 red chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coconut, 0.5 cup water, 1 cup rice, 0.5 cup toor dal, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil, 4.5 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp mustard, 2 red chilli, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 6 shallots, 1 potato, 1 carrot, 15 piece drumstick, 6 beans, 1 tomato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup tamarind extract, water, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a thick bottomed pan heat 1 tsp oil. add 1 tbsp chana dal, 1 tsp coriander seeds, ¼ tsp methi and ½ tsp pepper. 2. roast until the spices turn aromatic. 3. now add 6 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. roast until they turn crisp. 4. turn off the flame and add 2 tbsp coconut. roast until the coconut turns aromatic. 5. cool completely, and transfer to the mixi jar. 6. add ½ cup water and blend to smooth paste. 7. firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup rice and ½ cup toor dal. make sure to soak the dal for at least 20 minutes. 8. add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp oil and 4½ cup water. 9. cover and pressure cook for 5 whistles. 10. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp ghee. splutter 1 tsp mustard, 2 dried red chilli, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 11. add 6 shallots and saute for a minute. 12. also add 1 potato, 1 carrot, 15 piece drumstick, 6 beans and 1 tomato. 13. further, add ½ tsp turmeric and 1 tsp salt. 14. saute for a minute or until the vegetables turn aromatic. 15. now add 1½ cup water, cover and boil for 10 minutes or until the vegetables are cooked well. 16. furthermore, add 1 cup tamarind extract and boil for 3 minutes or until the raw flavour of tamarind goes away. 17. add in the prepared masala paste and mix well. 18. once everything comes to a boil add in cooked rice and dal. 19. mix well and add water as required adjusting the consistency. 20. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 21. finally, garnish with coriander and enjoy sambar rice or sambar sadam with curd rice and boondi.

title: sambar vada recipe | how to make south indian sambar vadai
ingredients: 0.5 cup toor dal, 1 to mato, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1.5 cup water, 3 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard rai, pinch of hing asaofetida, 1 red chilli, few curry leaves, 2 green chilli, 0.5 onion, 0.5 cup tamarind extract, 0.5 tsp jaggery gud, 1 tsp salt, 1.5 cup water, 1 tbsp sambar powder, 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 cup urad dal, 2 green chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tbsp dry coconut kopra, pinch of hing asafoetida, 2 tbsp rice flour, 0.5 tsp salt, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, pressure cook ½ cup toor dal, 1 tomato, ¼ tsp turmeric and 1½ cup water for 5 whistle. 2. in a kadai heat 3 tsp oil and saute 1 tsp mustard, pinch of hing, 1 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 3. also saute 2 green chilli and ½ onion for a minute. 4. now add ½ cup tamarind extract, ½ tsp jaggery and 1 tsp salt. mix well. 5. boil for 10 minutes or till tamarind extract gets cooked well. 6. add in whisked dal and tomato. mix well. 7. also add 1½ cup water and adjust consistency. boil well. 8. furthermore add 1 tbsp of sambar powder and stir well. 9. add in 1 tbsp ghee and boil for 2 minutes. 10. finally sambar is ready to serve with vada or idli.

title: Samosa Bites Recipe | Samosa Pockets - Tea Time Snack
ingredients: 2 cup maida, 0.5 tsp ajwain, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup ghee, 0.5 cup water, oil, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 3 potato, 0.5 cup peas, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup maida, ½ tsp ajwain, ½ tsp salt and ¼ cup ghee. 2. crumble and mix well until the flour turns moist. 3. now add ½ cup water and start to knead the dough. 4. knead to a tight dough adding water as required. 5. cover and rest for 20 minutes. 6. to prepare the stuffing, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil. add 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli and 1 inch ginger. 7. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 8. keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur and ¼ tsp pepper powder. 9. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 10. further add 3 potato, ½ cup peas and ½ tsp salt. 11. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 12. add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. potato stuffing is ready for samosa. 13. to prepare the puff samosa, knead the dough again. 14. roll the dough to a slightly thick thickness. 15. now cut into pieces. 16. place a tsp of prepared aloo stuffing in the centre. 17. cover with another piece of the pastry sheet and stick using some water over the sides. 18. deep fry in medium hot oil. 19. stir on medium flame until the puff samosa turns golden brown and crispy. 20. finally, enjoy puff samosa or samosa bites with chutney.

title: samosa chaat recipe | how to make samosa matar chaat recipe
ingredients: 1 cup ragda, 1 samosa, 1 tsp tamarind chutney, 1 tsp green chutney, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp tomato, pinch kashmiri red chilli powder, pinch aamchur, pinch cumin powder jeera powder, pinch chaat masala, 3 tbsp sev, few coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, break 1 samosa roughly. you can use homemade samosa or store brought samosa. 2. pour in 1 cup of ragda. i have already shared how to prepare ragda in my prev post. 3. top with 1 tsp tamarind chutney and 1 tsp green chutney. 4. also, add 2 tbsp onion and 2 tbsp tomato. 5. further, sprinkle pinch chilli powder, pinch aamchur, pinch cumin powder and pinch chaat masala. 6. additionally, top with 3 tbsp fine sev and coriander leaves. 7. finally, enjoy samosa chaat immediately topping with pomegranate if required.

title: Samosa Recipe (Punjabi Potato Samosa)
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour, 0.25 cup oil, 6 tablespoons water, 0.75 teaspoon carom seeds, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 4 medium potatoes, 0.5 cup green peas, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1.5 green chilies, 1 pinch hing, 4 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon fennel powder, Mint Chutney, Tamarind Chutney
instructions: 1. Peel, halve and boil potatoes in a pot of water, until fork tender (not mushy). Or (pressure cook unpeeled potatoes for 5 whistles on a medium flame or in the instant pot for 8 mins, placed over a trivet. When the pressure drops, cool & peel them.) 2. Crumble the potatoes, don't mash them. Keep aside. 3. Mix together flour, carom seeds, salt and oil in a mixing bowl. 4. Rub the flour well with your fingers or in between your palms for 3 to 4 mins to incorporate the oil. It should look like breadcrumbs and this is very important to make flaky samosa. 5. Take handful of flour and press down with your fingers. It must hold shape and not crumble. This is an indication that the oil is well incorporated. (check video or photos in the post) 6. Add water little by little and mix the flour to form a slightly stiff yet pliable dough. (Check pictures in the post). It should not be very soft like roti/naan dough. Cover and rest for 25 to 30 mins. 7. Heat a pan with oil. Add cumin seeds. 8. When they begins to sizzle, add ginger & green chilies. Fry until ginger smells aromatic, for about 30 to 60 seconds. 9. Stir in the green peas and saute for 2 mins. 10. Add red chilli powder, garam masala, cumin powder, chaat masala (optional) & fennel powder. Saute for 30 seconds. 11. Stir in the potatoes and sprinkle salt. Mix and saute for 2 to 3 mins. Mix in coriander leaves & set aside to cool. 12. Taste and add more salt if needed. If using lemon juice, add it now. Divide the mixture roughly to 10 portions. 13. Knead the dough gently to smoothen a bit. Divide it to 5 portions and roll to balls. Cover the dough balls until the end, to prevent drying out. 14. Grease the rolling area and then flatten a ball. Drizzle a few drops of oil. 15. Begin to roll to a circle or oval shaped disc. (Around 8.5 inches long by 6.5 inches wide). It should be neither too thick nor too thin. (Watch video) 16. Using a knife cut it to two semicircles. The 2 parts make 2 samosas. If the edges are too thick, gently roll it to thin down. 17. Work on one part - apply water over the straight edge (towards the cut side). Join the edges to make a cone. Press down gently to seal the cone from inside as well. (Check video or photos above) (If you have trouble sticking them, make a paste of flour and water, smear that lightly & then stick) 18. Fill the cone with one portion of potato masala and press down with your finger to push it inside the cone. Smear water generously on both the other edges. 19. Bring the edges together and seal them by pinching off the edges together. If you prefer to make a standing samosa, make a pleat on one side. Bring back the pleat and seal it by pinching off the edges together. (Check video or photos above) 20. Make sure the samosa is sealed well. Cover them with a cloth to prevent drying. 21. After you make 5 samosas, begin to heat the oil until medium hot. 22. Oil has to be medium hot and not very hot or cold. A piece of dough dropped in the oil should not sizzle or rise immediately. Instead you should see tiny bubbles in the oil & the dough should take a while to come up to the surface. This is the right temperature. 23. Gently add as many samosas as you can to the oil and fry them on a low flame, undisturbed for few minutes (about 10 to 12 mins). 24. When the crust becomes firm, increase the flame to medium. Turn them to the other side and fry until crispy and golden. 25. While the first batch of samosas fry, make the rest of them. Remove the golden fried samosas to a wired rack or a steel colander. Let the temperature of oil come down slightly before you fry the next batch. 26. Serve samosa with mint chutney, sweet tamarind chutney or tomato ketchup. 27. Preheat the oven at 180 C or 360 F for about 20 mins. Brush each samosa generously with oil all over and place them on a prepared tray. Bake these for about 35 to 40 mins. 28. Spray your samosa with oil or brush with ghee. Preheat your air fryer for 7 mins at 360 F or 180 C if required. 29. Place the samosa in the air fryer basket or tray spacing them at least an inch apart from each other. 30. Air fry for 12 mins, turn them to the other side and air fry for another 5 to 6 mins, or a little longer until golden and crisp. (In non-preheated air fryer air fry at 370 F or 190 C for the same time) 31. Brush them with ghee or oil as required.

title: samosa recipe | how to make samosa | aloo samosa
ingredients: 2 cup maida plain flour, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup oil, 0.5 cup water, 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp coriander seeds, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, 0.5 cup peas matar, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.5 tsp aamchur dry mango powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.75 tsp salt, 4 potato aloo, 5 cashew kaju, 2 tbsp raisins kishmish, 2 tbsp coriander, water, oil
instructions: 1. pinch a ball sized dough and grease with oil. 2. roll the dough into oval shape. 3. now cut it horizontally, diving into 2 equal portions using a knife. 4. grease with water and make cone. 5. stuff 2 tbsp of prepared samosa masala into the cone. 6. grease little water on the edges. 7. close and seal tightly by pressing firmly. 8. deep-fry the samosa on a low flame. alternatively bake at 180 degree celcius for 40 minutes. 9. stir occasionally, frying the samosa on low flame for atleast 15 minutes. 10. once the aloo samosa turns golden and crisp, drain off over kitchen paper. 11. finally, enjoy aloo samosa with green chutney and tamarind chutney.

title: Sandesh Recipe (Bengali Sondesh)
ingredients: 0.5 cup chenna, 2.5 tbsp powdered sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tbsp blanched almond powder, 2 tbsps nuts
instructions: 1. If you are a beginner or making paneer the first time, then read this detailed post on how to make soft paneer . However I am also sharing briefly here. 2. Bring 3.5 cups full fat milk to a boil. 3. Add 4 to 5 tbsp curd or lemon juice diluted with water to the milk. Stir well. When you see the milk begins to curdle, switch off the heat. Make sure it is curdled well. Allow to settle just for a minute. 4. Place a muslin cloth on a colander. Drain off the paneer. If you have used lemon juice, Rinse the paneer under running water. If you have used curd, you can just skip rinsing. 5. Squeeze off the excess water from paneer. Make a knot of the cloth, hang it for sometime to drain up excess dripping whey. We just need moist paneer, so make sure there is no dripping whey or water from paneer otherwise sandesh will turn grainy. 6. Add paneer to a plate along with almond powder. Knead it very well until smooth. 7. Move the chenna to a non stick pan. Add sugar (powdered) and cardamom powder begin to heat on a low flame. Fry for 4 to 5 mins. 8. Cool down completely. Then knead lightly. 9. Roll them to balls and flatten a bit. Tuck whatever you wish to garnish in the centre. Refrigerate for 2 hours and serve.

title: sandesh recipe | sandesh sweet | bengali sweet sondesh recipe
ingredients: 2 litre full cream milk, 3 tbsp lemon juice vinegar curd, 0.5 cup powdered sugar, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 3 tbsp tutti frutti
instructions: 1. firstly, boil 2 litre full cream milk in a thick bottomed pan, stir occasionally. 2. turn off the stove add add 3 tbsp of lemon juice / vinegar / curd. 3. mix well till the milk curdles like while preparing paneer. add more lemon juice if required. 4. now drain off the curdled milk using cloth. 5. rinse with cold water, making sure the paneer remains soft and removes lemon flavour. 6. squeeze and drain off the water. 7. now hang the cloth along with curdled milk for 30 minutes. do not hang for more time, as they loose moisture completely. 8. after 30 minutes, mash well for 10 minutes. 9. mash till the curdled milk turns smooth and soft. else it will be difficult to prepare balls. 10. further add ½ cup powdered sugar and combine well. 11. transfer the prepared paneer dough onto the kadai. 12. cook on low flame by spreading and mashing well. 13. cook for 5 minutes or till the curdled milk is cooked well. over cooking, makes paneer crumbly loosing out all moisture. 14. now add in cardamom powder and mix well. 15. allow it to cool for 5 minutes. 16. further, prepare small balls once its lightly cooled and make a dent in center. 17. finally, garnish sandesh with tutti frutti, almonds or pistachios and serve.

title: Sauteed Okra Recipe
ingredients: 1 lb okra, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1.25 tablespoon apple cider vinegar, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 7 garlic, 1 teaspoon red chili flakes, 0.375 cup paneer, 1.125 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.75 teaspoon chaat masala, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse 1 lb. (450 to 500 grams) okra under running water and wipe dry with a clean kitchen napkin or tissues. Chop them to ¾ inch and keep aside. Also peel and slice 6 to 8 small garlic cloves to make 1 tablespoon. Do not slice them too thin, they will burn. 2. Below are the spices I prefer to use. You may leave out a few if you don’t have or simply omit and use salt to keep them plain. 3. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a heavy bottom pan or wok or a non-stick pan. Add the garlic cloves and saute for 30 seconds. Then add the chopped okra and begin to saute on a medium high heat. 4. Pour 1 to 1½ tablespoon apple cider vinegar and continue to saute until the okra wilts and all the slime has gone away. If required reduce the heat and saute. 5. If your pan is too dry, splash a tablespoon water around the pan and mix the okra. But make sure you add liquid only after the okra is not slimy anymore. Once the okra is done to your liking (taste test to make sure they are done), turn down the heat. Add salt and red chili or flakes. 6. If you love more flavors, add the other spices and herbs too – garam masala, cumin powder, kasuri methi and chaat masala. 7. Saute on a low heat for a minute, until aromatic and turn off. Taste test and add more spices, salt and lemon juice. 8. If you want, add fine grated paneer & saute for a minute. You can use ¼ to ½ cup (100 grams) paneer. 9. Mix sauteed okra with paneer and garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve sauteed okra with rice-dal, roti-dal or with flatbreads. Okra Curry Bhindi Masala Rec... Bhindi Fry Recipe... Aloo Bhindi Recip... Bhindi Do Pyaza Vendakkai Poriyal...

title: Savory French Toast (Spicy Masala French Toast)
ingredients: 4 slices thick bread slices, 1.5 tablespoon unsalted butter, 2 medium organic eggs, 0.25 cup milk, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2.5 tablespoons onion, 1.5 green chilies, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon chili flakes, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon garam masala, 4 tablespoons mint chutney, 0.25 teaspoon chaat masala
instructions: 1. Gather the following ingredients 2. 2 to 3 tablespoons onion or shallots or green onions – chopped finely 3. 1 to 2 green chilies chopped (optional, deseeded for low heat or use black pepper) 4. 2 tablespoon coriander leaves fine chopped (or your favorite herbs) 5. Break eggs to a mixing bowl. Pour milk and add onions, chilies, coriander leaves, turmeric, chili flakes, cumin powder, garam masala and salt. Whisk together until frothy and combined. 6. Heat a non-stick pan with butter on a medium heat. Optional – Spread chutney/sauce on one side of the bread slice. If you don’t like this you may omit. 7. Place it in the beaten eggs. I don’t prefer to soak my bread in eggs for longer, but if you want you may do that for 2 mins. This results in a more eggy/ custard like texture inside. 8. Turn it to the other side with a fork 9. Remove, shaking off the excess to the same bowl and place in the hot pan. Follow the same steps and toast as many slices as you can in one batch. 10. If you want you may spoon a tablespoon of beaten eggs on each slice. 11. Cook on a medium flame until the base is cooked through. Turn it to the other side with a turner. Press down gently and cook until golden and crisp. 12. Remove to a serving plate and cook the rest of the slices. Sprinkle chaat masala and serve hot. Savory French Toast is best served hot.

title: Savory Steel Cut Oats
ingredients: 0.25 cup steel cut oats, 0.25 cup moong dal, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1 bay leaf, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 2 tablespoon onions, 1 medium tomato, 0.25 cup mix vegetables, 2 cups water, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. Rinse steel cut oats and lentils thrice and soak them until you chop and prep up the ingredients. 2. Heat ghee in a pressure cooker or pot. Add cumin and bay leaf. When the cumin splutters, add hing. Add onions and saute until pink, stir in ginger and saute for 1 minute. 3. Add tomatoes, veggies, turmeric and salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn mushy. Stir in the red chili powder, garam masala, oats and lentils. 4. Saute for 2 mins and pour water. Give a good mix and pressure cook on a medium heat for 2 to 3 whistles. To cook in a pot without cooker, bring to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat and cook until the lentils and oats are tender. Add some hot water if it is to thick. 5. Open the lid when the pressure drops and give a good mix. Taste test and adjust salt. Serve steel cut oats hot or warm topped with a tsp of ghee or lemon juice 6. Press saute button on the IP. When it displays hot add ghee or oil. Add cumin and bay leaf. Saute for 30 seconds and quickly add onions. 7. Saute for 2 mins and then add hing, ginger, tomatoes and salt. Saute until the tomatoes break down and turn soft. 8. Saute veggies, turmeric and chili powder for a minute. Then add dal, steel cut oats and pour water. Mix well. 9. Secure the insta pot lid and set the steam release handle to sealing. 10. Press the porridge button. It is set to 20 mins. You can also pressure cook on high for 10 mins. 11. Wait for the natural pressure release. When the pin drops, open and stir the steel cut oats. Taste test and adjust salt to taste. Add little ghee and lemon juice.

title: Schezwan Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams grams boneless chicken, 1 egg white, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon crushed pepper, 1 teaspoon soya sauce, 3 tablespoon Cornstarch, 0.25 cup oil, 1 tablespoon sesame oil, 3 red chilies, 1 teaspoon Sichuan peppercorn, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 teaspoon ginger, 2.5 sprigs spring onions, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 1 tablespoon rice vinegar, 2 tablespoon red chilli paste, 1 tablespoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 0.375 teaspoon pepper
instructions: 1. Make the red chilli paste by mixing red chilli powder with 2 tbsps water. Or Soak red chilies in hot water for 30 mins and drain. Blend them with little water to a smooth paste. Set aside. 2. To a large mixing bowl add chicken, salt, pepper, soya sauce & flour. 3. Add egg white & mix everything well until the chicken is well coated. (refer picture below). 4. Heat oil in a pan for deep frying. 5. When the oil is medium hot, gently slide the chicken pieces one after the other. (fry them in batches) 6. Fry them on a medium high flame until crisp and golden. Remove to a plate. 7. Turn off the stove & remove the oil to a small bowl, retaining 1 tbsp in the pan. 8. Cool down the oil a bit and then fry the red chilies until crisp without burning. 9. Add the ginger garlic. Saute them on a high flame for 30 seconds. 10. Then add the spring onions whites & fry for 1 min. 11. Add the ground sichuan peppercorns & saute until aromatic for 30 seconds. 12. Next add red chili paste, soya sauce, sugar, vinegar and very little salt. Stir well. 13. Pour 3 to 4 tablespoons water or oil and allow the sauce to bubble & thicken slightly. 14. Taste the sauce and add more chilli paste, sichuan peppercorns, sugar, salt, vinegar etc to suit your taste. 15. Stir in the fried chicken and toss on the highest flame for 1 minute until the sichuan sauce coats the chicken well. 16. Sprinkle ground pepper and spring onion greens. 17. Garnish schezwan chicken with more spring onion greens. Serve hot with noodles or fried rice or as a appetizer.

title: Schezwan Chutney Recipe | Street Style Szechuan Sauce - Tips & Tricks
ingredients: 5 grams kashmiri red chilli, 5 grams spicy dried red chilli, water, 0.5 cup oil, 1 cup garlic, 0.5 cup ginger, 3 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp sugar, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tsp soy sauce, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 1 tsp salt
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take 50 grams kashmiri red chilli and 50 grams of spicy dried red chilli. Make sure to deseed the chilli to reduce the spice level. 2. Soak in hot water for 30 minutes. 3. Cool completely, and grind to a smooth paste. Keep aside. 4. In a pan heat ½ cup oil, add 1 cup garlic and ½ cup ginger. 5. Fry until the garlic and ginger turns aromatic. make sure not to brown the garlic. 6. Add in prepared chilli paste and mix well. 7. Continue to cook until the oil separates. 8. Now add 3 tbsp tomato sauce. 2 tbsp sugar, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tsp soy sauce, ½ tsp pepper powder, and 1 tsp salt. 9. Mix well making sure everything is well combined and the oil separates. 10. Finally, cool completely, and Schezwan Chutney Recipe is ready to enjoy for up to a month.

title: Schezwan Fried Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups rice, 0.25 cup spring onions, 0.75 cup carrots, 0.75 cup french beans, 0.75 cup capsicum, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 medium onion, 2 tablespoons celery, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1.5 tablespoons soya sauce, 1 tablespoon tomato ketchup, 0.625 tablespoons vinegar, 0.875 tablespoon red chili powder, 0.5 tablespoon sugar, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon Sichuan pepper corn, 3 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Wash rice and soak for 20 to 30 mins. Bring 6 cups water to a boil. 2. When the water begins to boil rapidly, then add rice and 1 teaspoon oil. Cook on a medium high flame till al dente. 3. Al dente rice is firm but fully cooked. Make sure rice is not over cooked. 4. When the rice is cooked al dente, drain it to a colander. 5. While the cooked rice cools completely, chop the vegetables. 6. Pour oil to a hot wok. Fry ginger & garlic till they smell good. 7. Next add onions and fry until golden. 8. Skip this step if you have the sauce ready. Stir in red chili powder with water. Add this to the pan along with soya sauce, vinegar, ketchup, sugar, salt and schezwan pepper. Cook until it thickens. Pour 3 tbsps more water and cook till it thickens, Remove to a bowl. To the same pan, add 1½ tablespoon more oil. 9. On a high flame, fry spring onion whites, carrots, beans & capsicum until half cooked yet crunchy. 10. Next add Schezwan sauce, cooled rice and spring onion greens. 11. Mix and stir fry schezwan rice for 2 minutes. Taste test and adjust salt and spice if needed. 12. Serve schezwan fried rice hot or warm with any Indo chinese side.

title: schezwan fried rice recipe | schezwan rice recipe | szechuan fried rice
ingredients: 25 grams red chilli, 5 grams spicy dried chilli, water, 0.25 cup oil, 7 clove garlic, 2 inch ginger, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp pepper, 1 tsp salt, 0.75 tbsp sugar, 2 tbsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 yellow capsicum, 5 beans, 0.5 green capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp schezwan sauce, 3 cup leftover rice, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp spring onion
instructions: 1. firstly, take 25 grams dried kashmiri red chilli and 5 grams spicy dried chilli. to reduce the spice level, i have removed the seeds. 2. soak in hot water for 20 minutes. 3. transfer the soaked chilli into a blender with some water. 4. blend to smooth paste. keep aside. 5. in a large kadai, heat ¼ cup oil. add 7 clove garlic, 2 inch ginger and stir fry on high flame. 6. now add prepared chilli paste and cook well. 7. keep stirring and cook for at least 10 minutes, or until the oil separates. 8. further add 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, ¼ tsp pepper, 1 tsp salt and ¾ tbsp sugar. 9. mix well and cook for a minute. 10. finally, schezwan chutney is ready. you can store it in an airtight container for up to a month. 11. firstly, in large wok heat 2 tbsp oil. saute 2 clove garlic on high flame. 12. add 2 tbsp spring onion, ½ onion and stir fry until the onions shrink slightly. 13. further add ½ carrot, ½ yellow capsicum, 5 beans, ½ green capsicum, 2 tbsp sweet corn and ¼ tsp salt. 14. stir fry without overcooking the vegetables. 15. make some space in the centre, add 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar and 2 tbsp schezwan sauce. 16. stir fry until the sauces are well combined. 17. add 3 cup leftover rice and ½ tsp salt. mix gently and stir fry without breaking the rice. 18. finally, add 2 tbsp spring onion and served schezwan fried rice.

title: Schezwan Noodles
ingredients: 16 grams noodles, 3 tablespoons oil, 1.25 cup mixed vegetables, 2 sprigs spring onions, 1 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.25 tablespoon tomato sauce, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 0.25 cup onion, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 1 teaspoon vinegar, 0.375 teaspoon sichuan pepper
instructions: 1. Bring 6 cups of water to a rolling boil in a large pot. Add 160 to 180 grams (6 ounces) noodles (or 2 servings). Cook al dente following the instructions on your pack. While they boil, 2. prepare 1¼ cup chopped mixed vegetables (1 medium carrot, 1/3 cup capsicum, 8 to 10 green beans). You can also use cabbage or mushrooms if you want. 3. Also fine chop 3 to 4 garlic cloves and half inch peeled ginger. 4. chop ¼ cup onions from 1 small onion 5. fine chop 2 sprigs spring onions (optional, greens and whites separated) 6. When the noodles are cooked perfectly, drain to a colander. If you want you may rinse them and spread a teaspoon oil. 7. Make a red chili paste by mixing 1 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder with a few tsps of water. Please note that the red chili powder must be of good quality and less hot. You can also soak 15 deseeded Kashmiri red chilies and blend them to a paste. 8. Heat a pan with 2 tablespoons oil. Saute ginger and garlic until they turn aromatic. Add finely chopped onions. Saute until they turn golden. 9. Add the red chili paste, 1 tablespoon tomato sauce (optional) and very little salt. 10. Pour 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 1 teaspoon vinegar and add 1 teaspoon sugar. 11. Add 2 tbsp water and mix well. Add ¼ to ½ teaspoon crushed Sichuan pepper. If you don’t have them replace it with regular pepper. 12. Cook until the oil begins to separate. Set this aside.

title: Schezwan Noodles Recipe with Homemade Sauce - Street Style
ingredients: 6 cup water, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp oil, 25 grams noodles, 2 tbsp olive oil, 3 clove garlic, 4 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 0.5 carrot, 4 tbsp cabbage, 4 beans, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp schezwan sauce, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp olive oil and saute 3 clove garlic and 2 tbsp spring onion. 2. also, saute ½ onion on high flame. 3. further add ½ carrot, 4 tbsp cabbage, 4 beans and ½ capsicum. 4. stir fry on high flame without overcooking vegetables. 5. now add 2 tbsp schezwan sauce and ½ tsp salt. you can also use homemade schezwan sauce or schezwan chutney. 6. stir fry for a minute making sure sauce is well combined. 7. furthermore, add in cooked noodles and stir fry on high flame. 8. mix until the sauce is well combined. 9. finally, add spring onions and enjoy schezwan noodles.

title: Schezwan Paneer Recipe
ingredients: 2 tablespoons red chili paste, 0.5 cup water, 3.5 tablespoons corn flour, 3.5 tablespoons all purpose flour, salt, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper, water, 2 grams paneer, oil, 1.25 tablespoons oil, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.5 teaspoon schezwan peppercorn, 2 tablespoons spring onion whites, 2.5 tablespoons celery, salt, 0.875 teaspoon sugar, 0.75 tablespoon soya sauce, 0.625 tablespoon vinegar, 4 tablespoon water, 0.25 cup capsicum, 0.625 cup water, 1 teaspoon corn flour
instructions: 1. Deseed & soak red chilies in hot water. Set aside until soft. 2. Blend them well with just 2 tbsps of water to a smooth paste. Discard the rest of the soaked water. 3. You may skip this entire section and simply add the paneer without frying to the schezwan sauce. To a mixing bowl, add corn flour, maida, pepper and salt. 4. Pour water just enough to make a free flowing batter of slightly thick consistency. 5. Add the paneer bites and coat them in the batter well. 6. Heat oil in a kadai for deep frying or shallow frying. 7. When the oil turns hot, gently drop the batter coated paneer in the hot oil, spacing them apart so they don't touch. Do not disturb them for 2 to 3 mins. 8. Stir and Fry them on a medium high flame until the paneer bites turn crisp. To pan fry turn them to the other side and fry until crisp. Remove to a cooling rack or steel colander. 9. Heat oil in a pan or wok. Fry ginger garlic for a minute. 10. Add spring onions, celery (optional) and schezwan peppercorn. Saute for 2 mins. 11. Add red chili paste, salt and sugar. Fry for 2 to 3 mins. Remove the schezwan pepper as many as possible (optional). 12. Pour water and cook until the mixture leaves oil. Pour soya sauce and vinegar or tomato sauce. 13. Mix and saute for a minute. Add capscium and fry for 2 mins. 14. Mix corn flour with water. Pour that to the pan. Mix well and cook until the sauce thickens. Taste test and adjust the spice and vinegar as desired. 15. Turn off the stove. Cool this sauce a bit. Add the paneer and mix well to coat the sauce. 16. Serve the schezwan paneer hot with noodles or fried rice. 17. To air fry paneer, make the batter slightly thicker. These won't turn golden but will be white in color. If you want you may add some chilli powder to the batter for color. 18. Place the batter coated paneer in a greased, preheated air fryer basket or tray. Air fry for 6 to 7 mins at 400 F or 200 C. Turn them to the other side and air fry for another 3 to 4 mins until crisp. The timing may vary depending on your air fryer.

title: schezwan paneer recipe | paneer schezwan | schezwan chilli paneer
ingredients: 0.5 cup maida plain flour, 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp schezwan sauce, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 11 piece paneer cottage cheese, oil for frying, 3 tsp oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 chill, 4 tbsp spring onion, 0.25 onion, 0.25 capsicum, 2 tbsp schezwan sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 0.25 tsp sugar, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 3 tsp oil and saute 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger and 1 green chilli on high flame. 2. also, saute ¼ onion and 2 tbsp spring onion on high flame. 3. further, add ¼ capsicum and continue to saute till they slightly change colour. 4. additionally add 2 tbsp schezwan sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce, ¼ tsp sugar and ¼ tsp salt. 5. mix well and saute for a minute. 6. add cornflour slurry. to prepare corn flour water mix 2 tsp of corn flour with ¼ cup of water. 7. give a good mix until the gravy slightly thickens and turns glossy. 8. additionally, add fried paneer. 9. mix gently making sure the sauce has coated well uniformly. 10. finally, transfer schezwan paneer to a serving bowl and garnish with chopped spring onions green. serve with fried rice.

title: schezwan rice recipe | triple schezwan fried rice | triple schezwan rice
ingredients: 3 tsp oil, 1 tbsp garlic, 4 tbsp spring onions, 0.5 cup carrot, 0.5 cup cabbage, 1 capsicum, 2 tsp schezwan sauce szechuan sauce, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp tomato sauce, salt to taste, 2 cups basmati rice, 1 cup hakka noodles, 2 tsp oil, 1 tbsp garlic, 2 tbsp spring onions, 0.5 cup cabbage, 0.25 capsicum, 1 tsp schezwan sauce szechuan sauce, 1 tbsp tomato sauce, salt to taste, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp corn flour, 1.5 cups water
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat oil and saute garlic on high flame. 2. further saute chopped spring onions till they sweat. 3. also add carrot, cabbage and capsicum. 4. and saute for a minute. 5. additionally add schezwan sauce, soy sauce, tomato sauce and salt. 6. mix well and saute for a minute. 7. furthermore, add cooked rice and cooked noodles. 8. mix gently, and add spring onions. 9. firstly, in a large kadai heat oil and saute garlic and spring onions. 10. also add cabbage and capsicum. saute for a minute 11. additionally add schezwan sauce, tomato sauce, soy sauce and salt. 12. mix well and saute for a minute. 13. further pour cornflour water slowly, mixing continuously. 14. stir till the gravy thickens slightly. 15. and also slowly turns shiny and translucent. 16. finally, serve hot triple schezwan fried rice recipe with prepared gravy and fried noodles.

title: Schezwan Sauce Recipe
ingredients: 26.5 red chilies, 1 cup water, 0.25 cup oil, 2 tbsps garlic, 1 tbsp ginger, 9 sichuan peppercorn, 3 tbsps onions, 0.5 cup water, 1.25 tbsp sugar, 0.875 tsp salt, 0.875 tbsp soya sauce, 0.875 tbsp vinegar, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 cup celery, 2 tbsp tomato sauce
instructions: 1. Add the red chilies to a bowl and soak them in hot water for 30 mins. 2. To reduce the heat you can deseed and use them. 3. Meanwhile chop onions, ginger and garlic. 4. After 30 mins, add the red chilies to a blender jar. 5. Make a fine paste. If needed you can add 1 to 2 tbsp water. Set this aside. 6. Heat oil in a pan. Saute garlic, ginger and sichuan pepper for 2 mins. If using celery, add it now. 7. Add onions and fry until they turn transparent. Do not brown the ginger garlic as they may lend a bitter taste. 8. Stir in the chili paste, sugar, salt and pepper powder. Saute for 2 mins. Pour half cup water and mix well. 9. Cover and cook on a medium heat till the sauce turns thick. 10. Open the lid and cook further stirring often until oil begins to separate. 11. When you see the mixture begins to leave the sides of the pan, add soya sauce and vinegar. The sauce must taste hot with hints of sour and sweetness. 12. Optional - If using tomato sauce add it now. 13. Mix well and cook just for 1 to 2 mins. Taste it and add more salt or vinegar if needed. 14. Cool schezwan sauce completely and store it in a glass jar. Refrigerate for better shelf life. 15. The prepared sauce can be enjoyed as a side dip with snacks. It can be used to make noodles, fried rice or schezwan snacks. 16. To make fried rice or noodles, heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok. Fry onions and bell peppers on a high flame. Then add the rice or noodles and sauce as desired. Mix and fry on a high flame for 2 mins.

title: Semiya Kesari
ingredients: 1 cup semiya, 0.625 cup Sugar, 0.125 tsp cardamom powder, 1 Pinch saffron, 1.5 to 1 cups water, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 cashews, 1 raisins
instructions: 1. Bring water to a boil in a pot on stove or microwave. 2. Heat a heavy bottom pot or pan with ghee. 3. Next add cashew nuts and fry them till light golden. 4. Then add raisins and fry until they swell up. Remove these to a plate. 5. Add semiya to the pan and roast them lightly on a medium flame. 6. Do not let them brown. If using roasted semiya skip this step of roasting. 7. Pour hot water to the pan and increase the flame till the water begins to boil rapidly. 8. Next add saffron and cardamom powder. 9. Reduce the flame to medium and cook till the semiya is fully cooked. 10. If the semiya is undercooked and all of the water has evaporated, simply add more water. 11. Add sugar and stir gently. Sugar begins to melt and release moisture. 12. Cook till all the moisture evaporates and comes to a semi dry consistency. 13. Garnish semiya kesari with nuts and raisins. If desired can add a tsp of ghee.

title: Semiya Payasam Recipe (Vermicelli Kheer)
ingredients: 0.75 cup semiya, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.5 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 4 cups milk, 2 tablespoons ghee, 13.5 cashew nuts, 9 sweet raisins
instructions: 1. Heat a heavy bottom pan with 1 tablespoon ghee. 2. Add 12 to 15 cashew nuts and fry till lightly golden. I split the cashews and check before using. 3. Next add 1 to 2 tablespoons sweet raisins. We don’t prefer a lot of raisins in payasam so I just add 1 tablespoon raisins. 4. Saute till they turn plump. 5. Remove these to a plate and keep them aside. 6. Add another tablespoon ghee (optional) and add ¾ cup semiya to the same pan or pot. 7. Roast the semiya till slightly golden on a medium flame. You can skip roasting if using pre-roasted semiya. Do not brown them. 8. Press saute button on the instant pot. Pour 1 tablespoon ghee to the steel insert. When the ghee melts add, cashews and fry them until golden. 9. Remove them to a plate. I have not used raisins here as I did not have. If you want you may stir them in the ghee at this stage. They will become plump soon. Remove them. 10. Pour another tablespoon ghee (optional). Add semiya and roast them stirring continuously until light golden. If using roasted vermicelli, just fry for 30 seconds. 11. Pour milk and 3½ cups milk and ½ cup sugar. If you want to use jaggery read making jaggery syrup section below. 12. Press cancel and stir well. Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position the steam release handle to sealing. 13. Press porridge button and set the timer to 6 minutes. Once the Instant pot is done, it beeps wait for the pressure to release naturally for 6 minutes. 14. Then gently release the rest manually by moving the vent from sealing to venting. You will see the semiya is clumped up slightly. Add cardamom powder and give a gentle stir. 15. Add the nuts and more ghee as you prefer. Serve semiya payasam hot or chilled. If you double the recipe, let the pressure release naturally. Do not do a manual release as the milk can come out from the vent. 16. Grate ¾ cup jaggery and add it to a small pot. Pour 3 tablespoons water. Boil this until sticky & slightly thick. The jaggery syrup will become bubbly and begin to froth, just give a good stir often. 17. Turn off when it is slightly sticky. No string consistency is required. Cool this completely. 18. Once the semiya is soft cooked with the milk. Turn off the stove and cool it for 5 to 6 minutes on the counter. 19. Then fiter the cooled jaggery syrup into the semiya paysam. Add only as much as required. The amount to add depends on the kind of jaggery. So add as needed. I use up the entire syrup. 20. Adding hot jaggery to hot payasam will curdle it instantly. So 2 things worth to note here: Cool the jaggery syrup completely. Bring down the temperature of paysam slightly before adding the cooled syrup. This way your semiya payasam will turn out perfect.

title: Semiya Upma Recipe (Vermicelli Upma)
ingredients: 1 cup semiya, 1.5 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 onion, 1 green chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon channa dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 pinch asafoetida, 2.5 tablespoon peanuts, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.75 cup mix vegetables, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 2 tablespoon coconut
instructions: 1. Skip this step if using roasted semiya. Dry roast 1 cup vermicelli (110 grams) on a low to medium flame stirring often until light & slightly golden. Do not brown the vermicelli as the flavor alters. Transfer this to a plate. 2. Pour ½ tablespoon oil to a hot pan. Add 2 to 3 tablespoons peanuts. On a medium heat, roast them until golden and crunchy. Remove to a plate and set aside for garnishing. 3. Roast the vermicelli if required. Set aside. For this recipe you will require 110 grams. Pour half tablespoon oil to a pan and roast the peanuts on a medium heat until golden and crunchy. Remove to a plate and set aside for garnish. 4. To the same pan, pour 1 tablespoon oil and add – 5. Fry until the dal turns lightly golden. Add – 6. ½ teaspoon chopped ginger(optional) 7. 1 chopped green chili. 8. Add 1 small sliced onion and saute until transparent. Optional – You can also skip the vegetables. Add ¾ cup mixed vegetables – carrots, peas and french beans. Ensure the veggies are fine chopped otherwise they won’t cook fast. I sprinkle little salt so the veggies release moisture and cook easily. 9. Cover and cook on a low flame till they turn tender and almost cooked. If they don’t cook well, then sprinkle little water and cook. 10. Add salt and pour 1½ cups water. Stir and taste test the water. It has to be slightly salty. If using thin vermicelli 1 ¼ cup water would be sufficient. 11. Bring it to a rolling boil on a high flame. 12. Add the roasted vermicelli. Stir well to prevent lumps. 13. Cook on a medium high flame till all the water is absorbed. By then the semiya should be cooked well. (Troubleshooting tip: If the semiya does not cook well then boil little hot water and pour it here. Read tips below.) 14. Cover and cook on a low flame for 2 to 3 minutes. Turn off and rest for 5 minutes. 15. When the temperature comes down a bit, sprinkle lemon juice and add coriander leaves. Mix and garnish with roasted peanuts or cashews. Serve it hot with peanut podi . This is the semiya upma I made without veggies. It turns out good even without veggies.

title: Semiya Upma Recipe | Vermicelli Upma - 15 Mins
ingredients: 1 cup vermicelli semiya, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp urad dal, pinch hing, 2 tbsp peanut, 2 tbsp cashew, 5 chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp beans, 2 tbsp peas, 2 tbsp caspicum, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1.5 cup water, 0.5 lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, roast 1 cup of vermicelli on a low flame till it changes colour. I recommend roasting, even if you are using roasted vermicelli, to get a non-sticky texture. 2. Keep the roasted vermicelli aside. 3. Heat 2 tbsp oil, add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp chana dal, ½ tsp urad dal and pinch hing. Splutter the tempering. 4. Add 2 tbsp of peanuts and fry till the peanuts turn crunchy. 5. Also, add 2 tbsp of cashews and continue to roast till the peanuts turn golden brown. 6. Now add 5 chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves and splutter the tempering. 7. Furthermore, add ½ onion and saute slightly till the onion softens. 8. Add 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp beans, 2 tbsp peas, 2 tbsp caspicum, 1 tsp salt and ½ tsp turmeric. 9. Sauté till the vegetables turn crunchy. 10. Now add 1½ cup water and roasted rava. Mix gently. 11. Once the water comes to a boil, cover and simmer for 10 minutes. 12. Add ½ lemon juice and 2 tablespoons of coriander, and mix gently. 13. Finally, enjoy Vermicelli Upma Recipe with chutney.

title: Set Dosa Recipe (Sponge Dosa)
ingredients: 2 cups raw rice, 1 cup poha, 0.5 cup urad dal, 1.5 teaspoons fenugreek seeds, 0.5 teaspoon salt, oil
instructions: 1. Soaking : To make set dosa recipe, firstly add rice to a large bowl and wash very well a few times. Then soak in ample amount of water for about 5 hours. Add urad dal and fenugreek seeds to another bowl and rinse them a few times. Then soak in ample amount of water for about 5 hours. Wash poha just before grinding the batter. 2. Making batter: Drain the water from from both the bowls. Add urad dal, salt, fenugreek seeds and poha to a blender jar along with 1 cup water. Blend them till bubbly. If needed add more water to grind the batter smooth & frothy. Do not add too much water, batter should not be very runny. 3. Transfer the batter to a large batter bowl. Add rice to the jar along with 1/4 cup water. Grind to a smooth batter. Pour this to the urad dal batter. Mix everything well till well combined. 4. Fermenting : Set aside in a warm place for 8 to 12 hours for the batter to ferment and rise well. 5. Add few drops of oil to a tawa. Using a tissue or cut onion spread the oil very well. Heat the tawa. Mix the batter gently. Taste test and add more salt if required. 6. Pour one ladle full of batter and dab it gently to spread the batter do not spread like a normal dosa. Add oil across the edges. 7. Cover and cook for a while until it is cooked inside. You can flip and cook it for a minute on the inner side as well. 8. To get a crispy set dosa, lower the flame and cook for a while. Sprinkle idli podi if you have it or serve hot with chutney. Repeat making more set dosa on the tawa.

title: Set Dosa Recipe | Hotel Style Super Soft Sponge Dosa
ingredients: 2 cup idli rice, 0.5 cup urad dal, 0.5 tsp methi fenugreek, 1 cup poha aval flattened rice, water, 2 tsp salt, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup idli rice, ½ cup urad dal and ½ tsp methi. 2. add enough water and soak for 5 hours. 3. drain off the water and transfer to the mixi or grinder. 4. blend to smooth paste in batches adding water as required. 5. in a bowl take 1 cup poha and rinse well. 6. blend to smooth paste adding water if required. 7. mix the rice-urad dal batter and poha paste well. 8. cover and ferment in a warm place for 8 hours. 9. after 8 hours, the batter has doubled in size. mix gently without disturbing the air bubbles. 10. further add 2 tsp salt and mix well. 11. heat the griddle and pour a ladleful of batter. 12. spread out in a circular motion slightly thick than regular masala dosa. 13. also pour ½ tsp oil around the edges. 14. cover and roast the dosa to a golden brown from the bottom and completely cooked from the top in presence of steam. 15. finally, enjoy set dosa with chutney.

title: Sev Puri Recipe (Sev Batata Puri)
ingredients: 14 papdi, 1 potato, 1 onion, 4 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 cup nylon sev, 0.375 teaspoon chaat masala, 14 red chilies, 4 garlic, salt, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 1.5 to 2 cup coriander leaves, 0.625 cup mint leaves, 2 green chili, 0.25 inch ginger, 1 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, Lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoons fried gram, 1.5 tablespoons onions, Salt, water, 2 tablespoons tamarind, 0.25 cup jaggery, Water, Salt, 0.25 teaspoon dry ginger powder, 0.25 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon coriander powder
instructions: 1. To make red chilli garlic chutney, soak red chilies in some hot water. When they soften add them to a blender jar along with garlic, salt and sugar. Make a fine paste adding more water as needed. You can use the chilli soaked water to blend the chutney. Set these aside. 2. Making green chutney – Add all the ingredients to a blender jar. Add very little water and make a fine chutney. Set this chutney. You can also check this detailed post on Coriander Chutney or Green chutney . 3. To make tamarind chutney, place tamarind and jaggery in a bowl and pour very hot water just enough to cover them. Add red chili powder,salt cumin and coriander powder. When the mixture cools down, squeeze well & pass it through a strainer. Add ginger powder and salt. Mix well and set aside. 4. Arrange puris in individual serving plates. 5. Place sliced potatoes, you can also just crumble and add them. 6. Add a generous amount of garlic chili chutney. I prefer to make the chutney thick. This prevents the papdis from turning too soft quickly. 7. Sprinkle some onions on each puri. 8. Top with some tamarind chutney. Same here I prefer the tamarind chutney to be slightly thick. You can add more water if you desire. 9. Then some green chutney on top. Sprinkle chaat masala. 10. Top with a generous amount of nylon sev. Sprinkle some coriander leaves. Serve sev puri immediately.

title: sev puri recipe | save puri | how to make sev batata poori chaat
ingredients: 1 cup white peas vatana safed matar, 1 potato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 3 tsp oil, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli, 1 to mato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp salt, 2 tsp coriander, crispy puri, potato aloo, green chutney, tamarind chutney, onion, tomato, kashmiri red chilli powder, cumin powder, chaat masala, sev, coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup soaked white peas (vatana), 1 potato, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt and 3 cup water. 2. pressure cook for 5 whistles or until white peas get cooked completely. 3. now in a large kadai heat 3 tsp oil and saute 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 1 chilli. 4. also, saute 1 tomato until it turns soft and mushy. 5. furthermore, add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp garam masala and ¼ tsp salt. 6. saute until the oil is released from the sides. 7. now add boiled peas and mix well. 8. boil for 5 minutes or until the spices is absorbed well. 9. mash the peas and potato slightly, to thicken the consistency. 10. also, add coriander leaves and mix well. ragda is ready. 11. first, make a hole in the centre of puri. 12. place small pieces of potato into puri. 13. add a tbsp of ragda into each puri. 14. also spread green chutney and tamarind chutney. 15. further top with onions and tomato. 16. top with more ragda and sprinkle chilli powder, cumin powder and chaat masala. 17. garnish with a generous amount of sev and coriander. 18. finally, enjoy sev puri immediately.

title: Sev Recipe | Omapodi Recipe | Karapusa
ingredients: 2 cups besan, 0.5 cup rice flour, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 tablespoon ajwain, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 2 cups oil
instructions: 1. If making plain sev, skip this step. For flavoring, add ajwain powder to half cup boiling hot water. Cover and keep aside for 5 to 10 minutes. Filter the water and use for kneading the dough. If you like green chili or garlic flavour, blend 3 garlic cloves or 3 green chilies and add to cold water. Strain and use only as needed. 2. To a large bowl, add gram flour, rice flour, salt, chili powder, turmeric and hing. Mix well. 3. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a kadai until hot. Pour this to the flour. 4. Using a spoon mix it well. Then use your hand to incorporate the oil well to the flour. Rub the flour with your palms for a while to incorporate the oil evenly. Break up any lumps of flour and oil. 5. Add water only as required and make a non sticky dough. You can use plain water if you don't prefer the spiced water. 6. Heat oil in a deep frying pan on a medium flame. 7. Meanwhile grease the sev maker/ mould with little oil using a brush. Fill the dough in the mould. Fit the plate with the smallest holes to your sev maker. 8. Fill ⅓ of the prepared dough or as much to fill your sev maker. 9. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of dough. The oil is ready when the dough rises without browning. 10. Regulate the flame to medium. Press the mould gently over the hot oil in circular motion to release the dough in circular discs. 11. If you are a beginner, then grease a perforated ladle with little oil. Squeeze the dough on to the ladle and keep the ladle in the oil. Within a few minutes the sev will release on its own to to hot oil. 12. Fry the omapodi until crisp and golden. Remove them to a colander or kitchen tissue. 13. To make the next batch of sev, ensure the oil is medium hot and not too hot. If the oil is too hot they will burn. If the oil is not hot enough they will soak up the oil. 14. Cool all of the sev and transfer to air tight jars. I usually break them and store in separate containers so they remain crisp and fresh for longer. 15. Serve sev as a tea time snack or garnish your chaat snacks with it.

title: Sev Recipe 2 Ways | Omapodi Mixture Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, pinch of hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup water, oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 1 cup besan and ¼ cup rice flour. 2. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, pinch of hing and ½ tsp salt. combine well. 3. now add ¼ cup water (or as required) and prepare smooth dough. the dough will be slightly sticky, thats normal. 4. grease the small holed mould with some oil and stuff dough into it. 5. further, press and spread the sev in hot oil forming a circle in the oil and make sure you do not overlap. 6. after a minute, flip over and fry the other side. 7. finally, drain over kitchen paper to absorb oil and repeat making more sev with the remaining dough.

title: Sev Tamatar Nu Shaak Recipe | Sev Tamatar Ki Sabzi
ingredients: 3 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 1 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 cup tomato puree, 0.5 cup curd, 3 tomato, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup water, 1 tsp sugar, 1 cup sev, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 0.5 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. First, heat 3 tbsp oil in a kadai. Add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin and pinch hing. Splutter the tempering. 2. Add 5 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli and saute well. 3. Also, add 1 onion and fry till the onions turn golden brown. 4. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder. 5. Sauté on a low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 6. Add 1 cup tomato puree and cook until the oil separates. 7. Keeping the flame on low, add ½ cup curd. 8. Cook on a low flame till the curd gets well combined and turns silky smooth. 9. Now add 3 tomatoes, 1 tsp salt and mix well. Cook till the tomato softens. 10. Add 2 cups of hot water, 1 tsp of sugar, and bring to a boil. 11. Just before serving, add 1 cup of sev, 2 tbsp of coriander, 2 tbsp of kasuri methi, and ½ tsp of garam masala. Mix well. 12. Finally, enjoy Sev Tamatar Recipe with rice, roti or phulka.

title: Seviyan Recipe (Sweet Vermicelli)
ingredients: 15 grams Seviyan, 0.625 cup sugar, 3.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1.5 cups milk, 4 tablespoons nuts, 1 teaspoon rose water, 3 tablespoons mawa khoya
instructions: 1. Heat ghee in a heavy bottom pot and fry the vermicelli until golden. 2. Wheat vermicelli are very delicate and break quickly so be gentle while stirring. If using roasted seviyan, then just fry for few seconds. 3. In another pot, add sugar and pour water or milk. Bring it to a boil and dissolve the sugar completely. 4. When the sugar syrup comes to a rolling boil, then pour it to the roasted vermicelli. 5. Stir gently and allow to cook until the water/ milk has absorbed completely. 6. Then add cardamom powder and kewra water. 7. When the seviyan is cooked completely it will begin to leave the sides. If you have mawa add it at this stage. 8. Turn off and let it cool a bit. Do not cover. When the temperature comes down, fluff it up with a fork. 9. Garnish meethi seviyan with nuts and serve hot or warm.

title: Shahi Korma Recipe
ingredients: 1 kg bone-in chicken, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tablespoon garlic paste, 1 tablespoon ginger paste, 1.5 teaspoon korma masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.29166666666666663 cup oil, 2.75 cups onions, 1 cup yogurt, 1.125 tablespoon korma masala, 0.25 cup heavy cream, 1 almonds, 15 whole cashew nuts, 0.29166666666666663 cup water, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.25 teaspoon saffron, 0.5 teaspoon rose water, 0.25 cup ghee, 0.75 cup onions, 1.5 tablespoons toasted almond flakes
instructions: 1. Soak almonds & cashews in hot water for 25 mins. Later, drain and add to a blender or grinder. If you want you may peel the almonds. Pour fresh water. Make a smooth, thick & creamy puree. Make korma masala from the recipe notes. 2. Rub chicken with salt, lemon juice and ginger garlic paste. Add chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder and korma masala. Marinate well, cover and keep aside for a minimum of 30 mins to max of 60 mins. 3. Heat ghee or oil in a wide pan and add the chopped onions. Fry them on a medium heat, until transparent for 10 mins. Reduce to medium low, continue to saute until golden & light brown. It takes me another 10 mins. Deglaze with 2 tablespoons water and cook until jammy, for 2 mins. 4. Add the chicken and saute on a medium high heat, for 5 to 6 mins. 5. Lower the heat and stir in the whisked yogurt in 2 batches. Mix and cover the pan. (It’s alright if the yogurt splits.) Cook for 5 to 6 mins. 6. Pour hot water as needed, depending on how much liquid your chicken released. (I use ¾ cup). Cover and cook until the chicken is fully cooked. (Your meat thermometer shoud read 175F / 79 C.). (It takes me another 8 mins) 7. Stir in the nut puree and cook until thick, for another 4 mins. Stir in rest of the korma masala and cook for 3 mins. Taste test and adjust salt. 8. Turn off the heat & stir in the heavy cream & nutmeg. Sprinkle the kewra/rose water & saffron water all over. Keep covered. 9. In a small wide pan, heat ghee and fry the sliced onions until golden and light brown, but not burnt. Remove to a plate, spread to cool down, for 2 mins. 10. Pour the hot ghee all over the shahi korma. Once cooled, separate the fried onions and drizzle them on top. 11. Serve Shahi Korma with Butter Naan, Tandoori Roti, Parathas, pulao or with saffron rice.

title: Shahi Paneer Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 12 whole cashew nuts, 1 almonds, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1.5 cup yellow, 1 inch ginger, 4 garlic cloves, 1.5 tomatoes, 1 green chili slit, 0.3333333333333333 cup water, 237.5 grams paneer, 3 tablespoons ghee, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 cup yogurt, 0.875 cup water, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper, 1 pinch saffron strands, 3 drops Kewra water, 3 tablespoons heavy cream
instructions: 1. On a medium flame, fry cashews, almonds and cardamoms in 1 tablespoon ghee or oil, for 2 to 3 mins (read notes 1). Stir in onions, ginger, garlic and green chili if using. 2. Saute for 5 to 6 mins, deglaze with 3 to 4 tablespoons water and cook further for 3 to 4 mins. Remove the green chili for a low heat option. Cool down and blend with water to a smooth puree/paste. 3. On a medium flame, heat the rest of the ghee in the pan and add bay leaf, jeera, cinnamon and cloves (if using). When they begin to sizzle, add the puree along with chili powder, garam masala, turmeric and salt. 4. Saute for 3 to 4 minutes. Meanwhile whisk yogurt in a bowl and add 1 to 2 tbsps of the sautéed paste. Mix well to a smooth mixture. 5. Reduce the heat to low and incorporate it to the rest of the sautéed paste/sauce in 2 batches, mixing between each addition. 6. Saute on a medium heat for 3 to 4 mins and pour water. Mix well and cook covered until thick, creamy and traces of fats are visible on top. (takes me about 10 mins, depends on the cookware) 7. Optionally, remove the whole spices (except jeera) at this stage and taste test to adjust salt. Add the paneer and black pepper for spice/heat or more garam masala for flavor. Cook for a minute and turn off the heat. Stir in the heavy cream if using. 8. Sprinkle crushed saffron, kewra/ rose water and cover. Rest for a minimum of 5 to 7 minutes. 9. Shahi Paneer goes well with Saffron rice, Turmeric rice, Jeera rice, plain basmati, roti, naan or plain parathas.
