title: Shahi Paneer Recipe: Creamy and Delicious Dish
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp pepper, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp cumin, 1 inch cinnanmon, 0.5 tsp cloves, 3 pods cardamom, 1 inch ginnger, 4 cloves garlic, 2 onion, 2 tomato, 2 tbsp badam, 0.25 cup curd, 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 cup cream, 1 tsp salt, 2 cubes paneer, few kesar, 1 tsp sugar, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. Take spices and saute on low flame. 2. Add 1 inch ginger, 4 cloves garlic and 2 onion. Saute until the onions turn golden brown. 3. Also add 2 tomato, 2 tbsp badam and cook till the tomatoes soften. 4. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 5. Add ¼ cup curd, and grind to smooth paste. 6. Now sieve the puree to get a silky smooth texture. Keep aside. 7. In a Kadai, take 2 tbsp butter and 2 tbsp oil. Add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and ½ tsp cumin powder. 8. Saute on low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 9. Add in prepared puree and cook well. 10. Also add ¼ cup cream, 1 tsp salt and cook till the oil separates. 11. Now add 20 cubes paneer, few kesar, 1 tsp sugar and mix well. 12. Simmer for 5 minutes, or until the flavours are well absorbed. 13. Add 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 14. Finally, enjoy Shahi Paneer Recipe with roti and naan.

title: Shahi Tukda | Shahi Tukra Recipe
ingredients: 6 slices bread, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.3333333333333333 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.5 teaspoon rose water, 3.5 cups milk, 4 tablespoons sugar, 1 Pinch saffron, 2 Almonds, 0.25 cup pistas, 3 tablespoons khoya mawa
instructions: 1. Air dry or sundry the bread for 3 to 4 hours. You can also toast them on a pan. Do not use moist bread or bread from the fridge directly as they tend to absorb lot of ghee and have to fry longer to make crispier. 2. Soak saffron in 2 tbsps hot milk. 3. Method - 1 Bring milk to a boil in a wide pot. Reduce the flame to low and continue to boil until the milk reduces to half. Then add sugar and saffron. Boil for another 5 mins. Set aside & cool this. 4. Method - 2 Grind peeled almonds with 3 tbsps. Sugar, cardamom powder and ¼ cup milk to a very smooth paste. Pour to the 3 ½ cups milk and boil on a low flame till it reduces to almost 2 cups. Set aside to cool. 5. You can also chill the rabdi until you serve. 6. Mix sugar with water. Boil and bring to a 1 string consistency. Switch off the flame. Add rose essence. 7. Remove the brown edges and cut the bread diagonally. Each slice into 2 or 4 as you desire. 8. On a low flame deep fry the bread evenly till they turn golden and crispy. Alternately you can smear ghee over the bread and pan till they turn crispy. Set aside. 9. Immerse these bread slices in the sugar syrup one after the other and place them in a serving plate. 10. Just before serving pour the cooled rabri over the bread slices and garnish with toasted pistas. You can also sprinkle koya over it. 11. Serve shahi tukda warm or chilled.

title: shahi tukda or shahi tukra recipe | hyderabadi double ka meetha recipe
ingredients: 1 litre milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp saffron milk, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.5 cup water, few saffron threads, 6 slice bread, 3 tbsp ghee, few dry fruits
instructions: 1. firstly, cut the sides of bread and cut them into triangle. 2. fry the bread slices in hot ghee till the bread turns golden brown. 3. fry the bread both the sides till they turn golden brown. 4. and dip both sides of bread in sugar syrup. 5. furthermore, in a plate, pour in ½ cup of prepared rabri. 6. and place in fried bread slices. 7. finally, garnish with few chopped nuts and serve shahi tukda.

title: Shami Kabab (Shami Kebab)
ingredients: 45 grams chicken, 0.3333333333333333 cup chana dal, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 inch cinnamon stick, 2.5 green cardamoms, 2 red chilies, 1 inch ginger, 3 garlic cloves, 1 teaspoon roasted cumin, 1 teaspoon garam masala, Water, Salt, 1 onion, 2 eggs, 2.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 1 green chili, Oil
instructions: 1. Wash and soak chana dal for about 2 to 3 hours. Drain the water and add them to a large pot/ Dutch oven along water. 2. Add ginger, garlic, salt, turmeric, red chili powder, whole red chilies, garam masala, cumin, cinnamon stick, green cardamoms and meat. 3. Bring this to a gentle boil, Cover the pot partially and cook until lentils and the meat is completely tender. Add more hot water, only as required as you cook. 4. Once the meat and lentils are tender, evaporate the excess water completely. 5. Cool this completely and discard the whole spices and bones (if any). 6. Add this to a food processor or an onion chopper and grind to a slightly coarse mixture. 7. Add green chilies, onions, mint and coriander leaves. Taste this and adjust salt and heat levels to your preference. 8. Break an egg to a bowl and whisk with a fork to a uniform mixture. Add it to the mixture in batches and mix well to form a non-sticky dough. 9. Divide to 10 equal parts and shape them to balls. Flatten them on your palm, to shape like kebab/patty. Transfer to a tray. You can also freeze these at this stage. 10. Stovetop: Heat oil for shallow frying or pan frying in a wide skillet. Dip one kebab each time in the egg and lift it with a fork. 11. Gently shake off the excess egg and gently place these in the hot oil. Fry on a medium heat until golden, turn them to the other side & fry until crisp. Transfer to a wired rack or to absorbent tissues. 12. Air Fryer: Grease your air fryer basket and preheat it. Air fry the kebabs for 8 to 10 mins at 370 F/ 185 C, until crisp. 13. Bake in Oven: Place the egg washed shami kabab on a prepared tray (with parchment) and bake at 400 F - 200 C for 14 to 16 mins, turning them halfway. 14. Serve chicken shami kabab hot with mint chutney.

title: Shankarpali Recipe (Shakarpara)
ingredients: 0.25 cup water, 0.25 cup sugar, 2.75 tablespoons ghee, 1 pinch salt, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 cup wheat flour, 1 cup oil
instructions: 1. Heat together sugar, ghee and water in a pot, until the sugar dissolves completely. Cool down to slightly hot temperature, not cold. 2. In a mixing bowl, mix together flour, salt, cardamom powder and the hot syrup. 3. Bring the flour together with a spoon until incorporated. Use your hand to form a stiff, smooth and non-sticky dough. If you are short of liquid, sprinkle water & proceed. 4. Divide the dough to 4 portions and smoothen them in your palm to form balls. Keep them covered in a bowl. 5. Grease a rolling board and flatten a dough ball. Begin to roll to a 6 to 7 inch roti which is neither too thick nor too thin. 6. Make straight cuts, spacing ¾ to 1 inch apart vertically. Then repeat horizontally to get sweet diamond cuts/shapes. (refer pictures in the post) 7. Separate the shankarpali and transfer to a plate or a parchment paper. 8. Heat oil in a kadai on a medium flame. While the oil heats, keep rolling the dough and making more shankarpali. 9. Check if the oil is hot by gently sliding a small piece of dough. It should rise slowly without browning. This is the right temperature. 10. When it is hot enough gently slide them one by one to the hot oil. Do not crowd them in the pan. 11. Ensure the flame is medium to medium high and not low. Keep stirring & fry until golden & crisp. Remove to a steel colander. 12. Repeat frying the next batches. 13. Cool sweet shankarpali completely and store in a air tight jar.

title: Shankarpali Recipe | Sweet Shakarpara - Shakkar Para
ingredients: 0.5 cup sugar, 2 tbsp rava semolina, 2 cup maida plain flour, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup ghee, water, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar take ½ cup sugar and 2 tbsp rava. Grind to a fine powder. 2. In a large bowl, take 2 cup maida, ½ tsp salt and powdered sugar. Mix well. 3. Heat ¼ cup ghee and heat it well. 4. Pour the ghee over the flour and mix well. 5. Crumble and mix making sure the flour is moist. 6. Now add water slowly and start to knead the dough. 7. Just bring the flour together, do not over-knead. 8. Cover and rest the dough for 30 minutes. 9. Further, roll it slightly thick like paratha. 10. Cut in a square shape, size of your choice. 11. Deep fry in medium hot oil, or bake at 180 degree celsius for 15-20 minutes. 12. Stir occasionally keeping the flame on medium. 13. Fry till the sweet shankarpalli / shankarpara turns golden and crisp. 14. Finally, drain over the kitchen towel and serve / store Sweet Shankarpalli / Shankarpara in an airtight container once cooled completely.

title: Sheer Khurma Recipe
ingredients: 4 cups milk, 1 cup thin vermicelli, 0.25 cup sugar, 8 dates, 2 tablespoons chironji seeds, 15 almonds, 15 cashew nuts, 2 pistachios, 3 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon edible rose water, 0.375 cup mawa
instructions: 1. Bring 2 cups water to a boil. Pour half of the water to another small bowl. Soak almonds, pistachios and cashews in one bowl. Soak chironji seeds in another bowl. 2. After 30 mins, drain the hot water and pour cold water in both the bowls. 3. Meanwhile chop the dates to bite sized pieces and set aside. 4. Drain the water completely and peel the almonds and pistachios. Discard the skin and soak them in fresh room temperature water for at least 1 to 2 hours. 5. Drain the water from the chironji seeds and transfer them to a clean cloth. Pat them dry and rub gently. The skin begins to peel off. Discard the skin and any brown seeds that seem to be rancid. Keep the seeds in a small bowl and set aside. 6. When the nuts are soaked well after 1 to 2 hours, chop them very finely. Keep them aside. Ensure the nuts are dry before frying them. 7. Fine vermicelli is usually long and not broken. You will need to break them a bit and measure 1 cup (50 grams) and keep aside. 8. Heat 2 tbsps ghee in a heavy bottom pot. Fry cashews and almonds in hot ghee for about 1 to 2 mins. 9. Then add pistachios and chironji seeds. Fry all of them until crisp and golden. This takes about 5 to 6 mins as the nuts are soaked. When done, remove to a plate. 10. To the same pot, add dates and fry until they begin to smell good. Remove to a plate. 11. Pour 1 tbsp more ghee and add the seviyan. Saute on a low to medium heat until golden. Remove to a plate and keep aside. 12. To the same pot, pour 4 cups milk and bring it to a boil on a medium to low heat. 13. When the milk begins to boil, simmer for 5 minutes stirring often. Later add the roasted vermicelli, half of the fried nuts, chironji seeds and dates. 14. Stir and cook until the vermicelli is cooked. You have to keep stirring every few mins to avoid the milk scorching. This took about 10 mins on a medium heat. 15. Add cardamom powder & sugar or condensed milk (optional). If using mawa (optional) add it now. 16. Simmer for a few more minutes until slightly thick yet runny. Sheer khurma will thicken upon cooling so turn off when it is of runny consistency. Add cardamom powder & rose water (optional). 17. Serve warm or chilled. Garnish with fried nuts, chironji seeds and dates.

title: Sheera Recipe | Suji Halwa
ingredients: 1 cup suji, 1 cup sugar, 0.5 cup ghee, 2.5 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 15 cashew nuts, 15 raisins
instructions: 1. Pour 2½ cups water to a pot & begin to heat it. 2. Meanwhile on another burner heat ghee in a pot. When the ghee turns hot, add cashews and fry until light golden. 3. Then add the raisins and fry until they swell. Remove to a plate. 4. Now add rava to the ghee and begin to fry on a medium heat stirring constantly. 5. Fry until the rava turns golden. If you prefer deep roasted suji halwa, then continue to fry for few more minutes. 6. Bring the water to a rolling boil. 7. Pour the hot water to the hot rava pan stirring constantly. Be careful as it splatters. 8. Keep the flame to medium and keep stirring until all the water is absorbed. Sheera is thick at this stage. 9. Cover and cook on a low flame for 1 to 2 mins. 10. Next add the sugar and cardamom powder. Mix well to dissolve the sugar. It dissolves & makes the entire sheera gooey. 11. Within a minute the gooey sheera turns thick again. Keep stirring until it begins to leave the pan. 12. When it cools down fluff up with the spatula. Transfer to serving bowls and garnish with cashews and raisins. 13. You can also press it down in a small bowl and invert on serving plates.

title: Shorba (Biryani gravy)
ingredients: 2 tbsp Oil, 1 onion, 2 medium tomatoes, 2 green chilies, 0.75 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 4 tbsp coconut, 3 tbsps mint, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp biryani masala powder, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 1.5 tbsp curd, 1 bay leaf, 1 strand mace, 3 green cardamoms, 2 petals star anise, 3 cloves 3 cloves ((laung)), 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp shahi jeera
instructions: 1. Heat 1 tbsp. oil in a pan, add onions and chilies , fry till onions turn slightly golden. 2. Add ginger garlic paste and sauté till you get an aroma. 3. Add tomatoes and salt, fry till they turn completely mushy. 4. Add grated coconut. Fry for 3 to 5 mins without burning the mix. Set this aside to cool. Grind this to a smooth puree with 1 ½ to 2 cups of vegetable stock. You could use ice cold stock to grind the hot mix if you want to cut down the cooling time. Pour the ice cold stock in the jar first and then add the hot mix. 5. Heat the same pan with some more oil, add the dry spices and fry till you get a nice smell for a min.Do not burn the spices. 6. Add pudina / mint, sauté for a min. 7. Pour the ground mix, add red chili powder, masala powder, turmeric. Mix well. Adjust the quantity of water to bring it to the consistency you desire. 8. When the mix begins to boil, add yogurt and simmer for at least 8 to 10 mins. If your gravy becomes too watery, you can increase the heat to high and evaporate the water, so it comes to the desired consistency. 9. If you wish to refrigerate it, for best taste, refrigerate it immediately after it cools down and before 2 hours of cooking. Serve biryani shorba or sherva with hot biryani or pulao.

title: Shrikhand Recipe (Flavored Yogurt)
ingredients: 3 cups curd, 1 pinch saffron strands, 0.375 tsp green cardamom powder, 0.25 cup sugar, 2 tbsps almonds, 1 tbsps pista
instructions: 1. Place a colander or strainer over a large tall bowl to collect the whey. Spread a clean muslin or cheese cloth over the strainer. 2. Add curd to the cloth. Bring all the edges together. Squeeze out the excess whey from the curd. 3. Wrap the curd in the cloth and tie it with one of the edges to make a knot. 4. Method 1: Place the bundle of yogurt over the strainer/ colander with the tall bowl beneath. Place an heavy object over the yogurt so all of the whey drains to the bowl beneath. Ensure the colander does not touch the drained whey in the bowl. 5. Method 2: Place the bundle of curd in between a thick large absorbent towel or cloth. Transfer this to a plate and place a heavy object over the curd. I use my cast iron pan. 6. Transfer this to the refrigerator. For the first method you have to leave it in the fridge 15 to 18 hours. For the second method, leave it for 1 hour. 7. Drained super thick hung curd or chakka will be ready after the whey has been drained completely. 8. Soak saffron in 1 tbsp hot water or hot milk. 9. Powder the sugar and set aside. If you do not have cardamom powder, add the cardamom pods to the grinder along with sugar and grind to a fine powder. 10. Remove strained yogurt/ chakka from the cloth and transfer to a mixing bowl. Most of the whey would have been drained. Save this for later use, you can use it to make roti dough, bread dough or pancake batter. 11. Add saffron infused milk, powdered sugar and cardamom powder. 12. Whisk it well with a metal whisk until really smooth. If you are making in smaller quantities, then a tablespoon works too. If you are making this in large quantity then you can use a electric beater/ whisk. 13. Serve shrikhand with puri or chill and enjoy as a dessert. 14. Garnish shrikhand with chopped nuts.

title: shrikhand recipe | shrikand sweet | how to make kesar shrikhand
ingredients: 8 cup milk, 2 tbsp milk powder, 0.5 tsp curd yogurt dahi, 2 cup curd dahi yogurt, 0.5 cup powdered sugar, 2 tbsp saffron water kesar water, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 pistachios
instructions: 1. firstly, take 1 cup milk in a heavy-bottomed vessel. 2. stir in 2 tbsp milk powder to get more creamy curd. 3. stir well making sure there are no lumps. 4. now add 7 cup milk and mix well. 5. get the milk to boil on medium flame stirring occasionally. 6. turn off the flame and allow to cool slightly (lukewarm). 7. transfer the milk into another vessel. you can use the same vessel to set the curd. 8. now stir in ½ tsp curd into lukewarm milk. use whole red chilli (with its stalk/head) if you want to make a curd without culture or without using curd / dahi. 9. cover and rest in a warm place for 8 hours. you can also use instant pot / yogurt setter to set the curd if you are living in cold places. 10. after 8 hours, the milk will be set into curd. 11. now refrigerate for 2 to 3 hours to have a very thick and creamy curd. 12. in a large bowl, place a sieve and spread a muslin cloth. 13. pour in 2 cup of prepared curd. you can also use store brought curd here. 14. tie the cloth and allow to rest for 2 hours or until the water drops completely. refrigerate, to prevent curd from turning sour. 15. the curd turns thick and creamy. you can alternatively use store brought hung curd or greek yogurt and skip these steps. 16. take the hung curd into a large bowl and mix well. 17. add in ½ cup powdered sugar, 2 tbsp saffron water and ¼ tsp cardamom powder. 18. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 19. finally, garnish with 10 chopped pistachios and serve shrikhand chilled. 20. turn off the flame and allow to cool slightly (lukewarm). transfer the milk into another vessel. you can use the same vessel to set the curd.

title: Shrimp Curry Recipe
ingredients: 45 grams Shrimp, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onion, 1 teaspoon ginger grated, 1.5 teaspoon garlic, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 4 ml 13 5 oz canned coconut milk, 0.5 cup stock water, 1.5 tablespoon tamarind paste, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil (30 ml) in a pan and add 1 cup fine chopped onions (1 medium, 130 grams). Optionally If you have fresh curry leaves you may add 8 to 10 leaves. 2. Saute till golden, for 6 to 7 mins and add 1 teaspoon grated ginger and 1½ teaspoon pressed/grated garlic. (or alternately use 1½ tsps ginger garlic paste). Saute for 1 to 2 mins, until aromatic. 3. Stir in 4. 1 to 2½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (I use 2½, or use paprika for low heat) 5. Deglaze with 3 tablespoons water and add 1 cup chopped tomatoes (2 medium, or ½ cup tomato puree). 6. Saute until they become mushy and break down. 7. Pour 400 ml/ 13.5 oz canned coconut milk (1⅔ cups, or use raw grated coconut, read notes in recipe card at the end of the post) 8. If you are using small shrimp or any frozen shrimp, you won’t need any extra liquid. But if you are using jumbo/large size fresh shrimp, stir in ½ cup stock/water with 1½ tablespoon tamarind paste/pulp (or 1 tsp concentrate). Pour it to the pan. 9. Bring to a gentle boil and simmer for 3 mins for the flavors to develop. Taste test to adjust the seasoning. I felt mine needed a bit of sweetener to balance the tamarind so I added a tsp of jaggery powder (optional).

title: Shrimp Pakora (Shrimp Fritters)
ingredients: 3 tablespoons gram flour, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, 1 onion, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 green chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, oil, 1 shrimp, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric powder, salt
instructions: 1. Peel, devein and rinse the shrimps or prawns very well. Drain water completely. Add red chili powder, half of the salt and turmeric. I also added a bit of lemon juice. 2. Mix well and keep aside for only 10 minutes, no longer as the shrimp can turn mushy. 3. On a medium flame, heat oil in a deep pan to fry. Add ginger garlic paste, green chilies, curry leaves, sliced onions, flour, garam masala and a rest of the salt. Mix gently to coat the prawns well with all the ingredients. If you are using small prawns, omit the onions and fine chop chilies and curry leaves. 4. I forgot to add garam masala at this step so I added later. I did not add water, the moisture from the prawns and lemon juice was sufficient to coat the prawns well. But if your mixture looks dry, just sprinkle a tsp of water and mix. 5. When the oil is hot, turn the flame to medium high. Take each prawn along with some onions and curry leaves batter and drop it to the hot oil. Do not add too many at one time. 6. To Bake: Preheat the oven to 200 C / 400 F. Place the shrimps on a prepared baking tray and spray with oil. Bake them for 10 mins, turning after 6 mins, spray with oil again. Largest ones take about 13 to 14 mins. Keep an eye after 12 mins. They can get overcooked and turn chewy very soon. They are done, when the tails are curled. 7. Prawns tend to get burnt faster, so be attentive. Flip them to the other side and fry till golden. Fry all the prawns in batches. 8. This is an optional step. When you finish frying all the prawn fritters, add them back to hot oil and repeat frying them for a minute, this gives a good crunchy texture to the pakora. Remove to a cooling rack or steel colander. Serve them with a sprinkle of chaat masala or with your favorite sauce or mint chutney. 9. 1 sprig curry leaves or 8 mint leaves finely chopped 10. 1 green chilli fine chopped 11. oil to deep fry 12. Marinate prawns: Devein, peel and clean prawns/ shrimp. Rinse well and drain them completely. Coat them with red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala and salt. If you want add a tsp of lemon as well. Leave them aside for 10 mins while you prep up the rest. 13. Prep up to fry: Heat oil in a deep pan to deep fry. Coat the prawns with ginger garlic, green chilies, gram flour, cornstarch, more salt and curry leaves. If it looks too dry, add a tsp of water to coat the prawns well with all the ingredients. 14. Test if oil is hot: When the oil is hot enough, to test drop a small portion of the dough to the oil. It should rise steadily and not brown too quickly. This is the right temperature. Your thermometer should read 350 F. 15. Fry shrimp pakora: Regulate the flame to medium high, hold each prawn along with some onions, curry leaves batter and gently slide to the hot oil. Let fry until golden and crisp. You know they are cooked when the tails are curled. 16. Remove them to a cooling rack or steel colander. Fry all the shrimp in batches. If you want refry for a minute, for extra crisp texture. 17. Shrimp fritters are best served hot, they begin to lose the crunch upon cooling. 18. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > title: Shrimp Tikka Masala (Shrimp Masala)
ingredients: 1 cup onions, 1.25 cup tomatoes, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1.125 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon Kasuri methi, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 cup whipping cream, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 325 grams shrimp, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri Red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon cumin powder, 2 teaspoons lemon juice, 0.25 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.25 teaspoon kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Heat a pan with oil and add onions. Stir fry until deep golden to light brown. 2. Add ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute. 3. Reduce the heat to lowest. Stir in Kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder and salt. 4. Immediately pour 3 to 4 tablespoons water and cook until the water evaporates. 5. Add the tomatoes and saute on a medium high heat for 3 mins. Cover and simmer until the tomatoes break down to a mush. 6. Reduce the heat and stir in the nut paste (optional) and cook until you see traces of oil on the top or sides of the pan. (If using cream wait until the last step) 7. Pour water and bring it to a rolling boil. Cover and simmer until the gravy turns thick and the onions are softened. 8. Add sugar and kasuri methi. Taste test and add more salt if you want. Mix and turn off. Your tikka masala is ready. 9. For this recipe, it is essential to cool down the sauce a bit before cooking your shrimp with the sauce. Else your shrimp will be bland. 10. Optional - If you want a smooth sauce, cool this and blend to a smooth gravy, adding more water if required. If you want some texture in the gravy, blend only a part of it. 11. Mix together shrimp, lemon juice, red chilli powder, salt, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric, garlic powder (optional) and kasuri methi. Rest aside for 5 to 8 mins. 12. Add 1 tablespoon oil or ghee to a wide skillet or pan. Spread the oil and begin to heat on a medium heat. 13. When the pan is medium hot, begin to layer the marinated shrimp and cook just for a minute. Quickly with a tong, turn them to the other side and cook for another minute. Do not burn. 14. Immediately pour the cooled or warm tikka masala to the pan. Stir and cook on a low heat just until the shrimp transform to a “C” shape, which means they are cooked. It takes only about 2 to 3 mins depending on the size of your shrimps. Do not overcook. 15. Take the pan off the stove and stir in cream if using. Keep covered until served, 16. Turn off and garnish shrimp tikka masala with cream and chopped coriander leaves.

title: Shrimp Vindaloo
ingredients: 1 lb shrimp prawns, 0.25 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 inch Ceylon cinnamon piece, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 5 black pepper corn, 0.75 tablespoon coriander seeds, 1 cardamom pod, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 red chilies, 3 tablespoons vinegar, 5 garlic cloves, 0.75 inch inch ginger, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onions, 1 tomato, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. Deseed the chilies , break them to smaller portions and add them to a small bowl. Bring vinegar and 3 tablespoons water to a rolling boil in a small pot. Pour over the chilies and let soak until softened, 10 mins. 2. Add cumin, cinnamon, cloves, coriander, cardamom, pepper, salt, turmeric & mustard to a small spice grinder and grind to a fine powder. Transfer ginger, garlic, soaked red chilies, vinegar and tomato. Make a smooth vindaloo paste. 3. Marinate shrimp with salt and red chili powder. Cover and reserve aside. 4. Heat a pan and add oil. Saute onions until golden, for 8 to 10 mins. Deglaze with 2 tablespoons of water and cook down until jammy. 5. Add the vindaloo paste & saute for 5 mins. Pour water and bring to a boil. Cook until it becomes a thick paste. Taste test to add more salt, vinegar & sugar/jaggery (optional). Remove to a plate. 6. Heat the same pan with 1 teaspoon oil and add curry leaves and the shrimp. Cook on each side for 1 to 2 mins on a high heat. 7. Add the vindaloo paste & pour little hot water (start with lower amount). Mix well and cook covered on a low heat until the shrimp takes on a “C” shape meaning they are cooked through. Avoid overcooking. 8. Serve Shrimp vindaloo with plain rice, saffron rice, chapati or bread. It tastes best the next day.

title: Singapore Fried Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrots, 0.3333333333333333 cup green beans, 0.3333333333333333 cup Bell peppers, 3 sprigs spring onions, 1 tablespoon garlic, 0.875 tablespoon ginger, 1 green chilli, 1 red chilli, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 star anise, 0.375 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1.5 teaspoon curry powder, 0.875 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon Soya Sauce, 0.875 tablespoon red chilli sauce, 1.25 tablespoon tomato ketchup, 0.5 teaspoon rice vinegar, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 1 tablespoon oil, 125 grams paneer extra firm pressed tofu, 1.5 eggs
instructions: 1. Add 1 cup basmati or jasmine rice to a large pot and rinse well a few times. Drain the water completely. Pour 2 cups of water and add a few drops of oil (optional). Bring the water to a rolling boil and reduce the heat to low. Cover and cook on a low heat until al dente (cooked fully but not mushy). Drain any excess water to a colander and add back the rice to the same pot. 2. Rice is a little moist at the bottom. Using a spoon, turn the rice from the bottom to the top. Cover the hot rice and place aside for 5 to 6 mins. Later fluff with a fork. 3. While the rice cooks, Prepare the veggies. You will need: 4. ⅓ cup carrots (1 medium carrot, chopped finely) 5. ⅓ cup green beans (or French beans, 8 to 10 chopped finely) 6. ⅓ cup Bell peppers (chopped, any color) 7. 1 tablespoon garlic (5 to 6 medium cloves, fine chopped) 8. ¾ to 1 tablespoon ginger (1 inch, peeled fine chopped) 9. 1 green chilli chopped (optional) 10. You won’t have time to look around for the ingredients. So you need to set aside everything before you begin to stir fry the dish. To a small plate add: 11. star anise (optional for flavor, skip if using Malay curry powder) ¼ to ½ teaspoon red chilli powder (Kashmiri or paprika) 1 ½ teaspoon curry powder (or sambar powder or ¾ to 1 teaspoon garam masala) ¾ to 1 teaspoon coriander powder ¼ to ½ teaspoon crushed black pepper ¼ teaspoon turmeric 1 to 1 ½ teaspoon salt (adjust to your taste) 1 to 2 dried red chillies 12. Pour 1 tablespoon soya sauce, ¾ to 1 tablespoon red chilli sauce, 1 to 1½ tablespoon ketchup, ½ teaspoon rice vinegar (or apple cider vinegar) and ½ teaspoon sugar to a small bowl. Set this aside. 13. Dice your paneer or extra firm pressed tofu and set aside. To use eggs, chicken or shrimp, refer notes section in the recipe card.

title: singapore noodles recipe | veg singapore noodles | singapore mei fun
ingredients: 4 cup water, 1 pack rice vermicelli, 2 tbsp sesame oil, 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, 4 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 1 carrot, 0.25 cup cabbage, 0.25 capsicum, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 0.5 tsp sesame seeds
instructions: 1. firstly heat 2 tbsp sesame oil and saute 2 clove garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli and 2 tbsp spring onion. 2. further, saute ½ onion until it slightly changes colour. 3. additionally add 1 carrot, ¼ cup cabbage and ¼ capsicum. 4. stir fry on high flame without overcooking vegetables. 5. furthermore add ¼ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp pepper and ½ tsp salt. 6. saute on low flame without burning the spices. 7. now add 2 tbsp soy sauce and mix well. 8. add in cooked noodles and mix well. 9. finally, enjoy singapore noodles topped with spring onions and roasted sesame seeds.

title: snake gourd recipe
ingredients: 2 cups snake gourd, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 0.25 teaspoon cumin, 1 tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 Pinch hing, 1 garlic clove, 1 dry chili, 0.25 teaspoon chilli powder, 2 tablespoon Oil, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 0.125 teaspoon Turmeric
instructions: 1. Cleaning : Scrape off the whitish layer on the snake gourd with a knife. Take some salt and rub it on the snake gourd and wash off under running water thoroughly. 2. Chopping : Trim both the ends. Cut to 2 to 3 parts. Cut length wise each part and remove the seeds as well the white layers inside. Chop to small pieces that are uniform in size, this ensures even frying. 3. Optional step : You can sprinkle some salt over the snake gourd pieces and rest for 15 mins. Take a handful of pieces and squeeze off to drain all the moisture. This step is done to remove the strong smell of the snake gourd. Some amount of nutrients are lost so I do not do this. 4. Cooking : Pour oil to a hot pan. When the oil turns hot, add mustard, cumin, chana dal and urad dal. Stir and fry until the dals turn golden. Then add red chili, curry leaves and chopped garlic. Fry till the leaves turn crisp, then add the hing. 5. Add chopped snake gourd and fry on high for 3 mins stirring often. Reduce the flame completely. Cover and cook until it is soft and tender. Keep stirring in between to prevent snake gourd curry from burning. 6. Sprinkle salt, turmeric, & chilli powder. I use this vepudu karam or idli podi or peanut podi to flavor up the snake gourd. You can also use pav bhaji masala or sambar powder. 7. Stir fry until the raw smell of chilli powder goes away. You can also sprinkle 2 tbsps coconut if you have. Mix well and off the heat. Serve snake gourd curry with rice and rasam.

title: Soft Ghee Mysore Pak
ingredients: 1 cup Besan, 1 cup sugar, 0.5 cup ghee, 7 tablespoons ghee, 6 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Turn on the flame and heat a wide heavy bottom pan. Dry roast the besan stirring continuously for 5 minutes on a low flame. 2. Be attentive as we don't want the color of the besan to change but want it to loose the raw flavor. So keep stirring. When you smell it good turn off the heat. Transfer to a wide plate to cool down. Sieve it directly to the same wide heavy bottom pan. 3. Pour 4 to 6 tablespoons ghee and mix well to a smooth paste. Then add another 2 to 4 tablespoons and mix well. There should be no lumps in the flour mixture. If your ghee is solid, put the pan on a low heat on the stove until the ghee melts & then mix well. Set this aside. 4. Grease a tray or line with a parchment paper. You can use a 6 or 7 inch cake tray. 5. Add sugar and water to a heavy bottom pan and cook until you see an almost 1 string consistency, but not a full 1 string consistency. It is sticky and you see the string breaks when you check a drop of syrup in between your thumb and index finger. 6. Pour the besan and ghee mixture all at once to the sugar syrup and mix continuously non-stop until it becomes a uniform mixture. 7. Cook this stirring continuously on a low flame for 2 minutes. Then pour ¼ cup ghee and incorporate it into the Mysore pak mixture again. 8. When you see all of the ghee is absorbed, add another 2 tablespoons ghee and incorporate it again. 9. You should see no ghee separating at this stage. Then add the last 2 tbsps of ghee in batches ensuring it is absorbed by the mixture. 10. Keep stirring all this constantly to avoid burning. At the last stage you will see the mixture turns thick and will leave a trail in the pan. Turn off the heat. 11. Testing - You can test by taking a small portion of the mixture into a spoon. Cool quickly and roll to a ball. It should be non-sticky and soft. Be quick at this stage. 12. Pour all of the mixture at once to the greased and lined tray. 13. Let it cool for 5 to 6 mins on a wired rack so it won't sweat at the bottom. 14. After 5 to 6 mins or when it firms up a bit, draw lines. If using a 6 inch tray cut to 9 pieces. If using a 7 inch tray slice to 12 pieces. 15. Cool it fully for another 30 mins or so. Slice the Mysore pak and store it in air tight container.

title: Soft multigrain roti
ingredients: 0.5 cup rolled oats, 0.5 cup millet flour, 0.5 cup whole wheat flour, 1 tbsp oil, 0.75 cup water
instructions: 1. Add rolled oats to a blender jar and powder it finely. 2. Heat water in a bowl, add the ground oats to the hot water and mix. Set this aside for about 5 minutes. 3. To this add millet flour, wheat flour and mix well using additional hot water to make it a soft pliable dough. 4. Knead for few minutes till it turns sticky. Add oil and knead it again. 5. Smear oil all over the dough and wrap it in a moist cloth. Keep the ball in a bowl, cover it and set aside for at least 30 minutes. 6. Make 8 to 10 equal sized balls and knead them again, roll to balls and keep them covered in the cloth to prevent drying. 7. Dust lightly the rolling area with flour. Take each ball and roll to chapathi. Do not put pressure while you roll, delicately roll the roti. Set aside separately, do not stack them. 8. Finish rolling all the balls and heat a griddle 9. Prepare a small bowl half filled with water and roll a clean cloth to a ball and dunk it in the water. I usually use handkerchief which I use for draining the paneer. 10. When the griddle becomes hot, transfer the roti with floury side on top, immediately squeeze off excess water from the cloth to the bowl and smear water all over the roti including the edges with the cloth. Do not pour water on roti.The roti must become moist all over. By now the roti firms up on the other side.Could not shoot better pictures. 11. Immediately flip it to the other side and repeat dampening the other side of the roti and cover it. Flip again to the other moist side, press the roti randomly to puff it. 12. Transfer to a plate and cover the roti again to keep them soft. An insulated casserole works too. 13. Finish cooking all the rotis, stack them one over the other.Place kitchen tissues (an absorbent kitchen towel)beneath the last and above the first roti to prevent them from becoming moist.Keep covered till you serve to retain their softness. 14. These can be refrigerated in an airtight box and used within 48 hours. Just microwave for 15 to 20 seconds on high.They turn very soft. 15. Powder oats finely in a blender. 16. Add the powder to 3/4 cup of hot water and stir well to break lumps. keep this aside for 5 minutes 17. Add whole wheat and millet flours to the same bowl and mix with hot water. Knead till it becomes a soft, pliable and sticky dough. 18. Pour oil and repeat kneading. 19. Divide the dough to 8 to 10 equal sized balls and knead them again, roll to smooth balls, put them back to the moist cloth and cover. 20. Sprinkle wheat flour lightly on the rolling area. Lightly roll each ball to a chapathi . If needed sprinkle flour. Do not make them very floury. 21. Heat a griddle. Add water to a bowl and roll a cloth to a ball and dunk it in the bowl. 22. Transfer the roti with floury side facing towards you, With the damp cloth apply water to the roti including the edges.Do not pour the water. 23. Repeat the process abd finish cooking all the rotis, Stack them. 24. Keep multigrain roti covered till you serve.

title: Solkadhi Recipe | Sol Kadi - Kokum Curry
ingredients: few dried kokum punarpuli bhirand muragalu kaatampi, 1 cup coconut, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, soak dried kokum in hot water for 2 hours. 2. Squeeze the juice of kokum and keep it aside. 3. In a mixer jar, take 1 cup of coconut, 1 inch of ginger, 2 chillis, and 1 tsp of cumin. 4. Add 1 cup of water and grind well. You can also add garlic for authentic flavour while grinding. 5. Squeeze the milk from the coconut using a cloth and a strainer. 6. Add in extracted kokum juice to the coconut milk. 7. Also, add ½ tsp salt and 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 8. Finally, enjoy Sol Kadhi Recipe chilled.

title: Sooji Masala Sticks Recipe 2 ways | Masala Rava Fingers Tea Time Snack
ingredients: 2 cup rava semolina suji, 2 tbsp sesame, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, warm water, oil, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp mix herbs, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp black salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixer jar take 2 cup rava and grind to fine powder. 2. transfer the suji powder to a large bowl. you can also add half cup maida at this stage to make the binding easy. 3. also add 2 tbsp sesame, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 4. pour in 2 tbsp hot oil and mix well. 5. crumble and mix making sure the flour is moist. 6. further, add warm water and start to knead the knead. 7. knead the dough adding water as required to form a soft dough. 8. cover and rest the dough for 20 minutes or until the suji absorbs the water. 9. after 20 minutes, knead the dough gently. 10. pinch a ball sized dough and roll. 11. roll to a slightly thick thickness. 12. cut into thin strips or the shape of your choice. 13. deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 14. stir and fry on medium flame until the suji sticks turn crispy. 15. drain off the sticks to remove excess oil. 16. in a bowl take ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp mix herbs and ½ tsp pepper powder. mix well. 17. add in fried suji sticks and coat the spices uniformly. 18. finally, chatpata flavour suji sticks are ready to enjoy. 19. firstly, in a bowl take ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp black salt. mix well. 20. add in fried suji sticks and coat the spices uniformly. 21. finally, masaledar flavour suji sticks are ready to enjoy.

title: Sorakaya curry
ingredients: 2 cups bottle gourd, 0.25 cup channa dal, 1 green chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 onion, 1 tomatoes, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 3 tablespoon Coconut milk, 2 tablespoon Oil
instructions: 1. Optional: If using chana dal soak it for at least 30 minutes in warm water. Drain and set aside. 2. Pour oil to a hot pan. When the oil turns hot add mustard, cumin and curry leaves. Allow them to splutter. Then add hing. 3. Add onions and green chilli. Fry until the onions turn golden. Then add tomatoes, salt and turmeric. Fry till the tomatoes break down and turn mushy. Then add the bottle gourd and fry for about 3 mins. 4. Add chana dal (optional) & red chili powder. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes. Pour about ¼ to ½ half cup water. Cover and simmer till the chana dal is just cooked thoroughly. Do not over cook the dal. Or alternately you can pressure cook for 1 whistle. 5. Taste the curry and add more salt if needed. Pour coconut milk or coconut if using. Mix well. If the curry is runny just evaporate the excess moisture by cooking on a slightly higher flame. Serve sorakaya curry with rice or chapati.

title: Sorakaya Pachadi (Bottle Gourd Chutney)
ingredients: 2 cups bottle gourd, 3.5 garlic cloves, 2 teaspoons chana dal, 2 teaspoons urad dal, 2 teaspoons white sesame seeds, 0.625 cup tomatoes, 2 red chilies, 3 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon Tamarind paste, 2 teaspoon Oil, 1.5 cloves garlic, 1 red chili, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. Dry roast red chilies, chana dal and urad dal to golden and aromatic. To the same pan, add sesame seeds and cumin. Roast until the sesame seeds begin to splutter. Transfer to a wide plate and cool. 2. Add a tsp of oil to the same pan, fry green chilies and garlic or ginger until the chilies are blistered. Remove to a plate. Add the peeled and chopped bottle gourd. Saute on a high flame for 3 to 4 mins. Cover and cook until they turn completely soft and lose the raw flavor. I have sauteed the gourd and tomatoes separately but if you want, you can do them together. 3. Transfer the cooled dal to the chutney jar and move the bottle gourd to the plate. 4. If you don’t like to use tomato, you can omit this step. Add chopped tomato to the pan and sprinkle turmeric and salt. Saute till the tomatoes turn completely mushy. Move this to the plate and cool down. 5. Powder the dal to a fine powder. 6. Scrape off the sides and add cooled bottle gourd, tomato, green chilies, and ginger/ garlic. 7. Grind to a coarse chutney. Heat oil in the same pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, urad dal, broken red chili, crushed garlic and curry leaves (pat dry to avoid splatters). When the dal turns golden, turn off the heat. Add hing, tamarind paste and the sorakaya pachadi. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt and tamarind. If you want you may add 1 tablespoon coriander leaves but they are not used traditionally. Serve sorakaya pachadi with rice and ghee or with dosa , chapathi.

title: Sorakaya Pappu
ingredients: 0.25 cup toor dal, 0.5 cup moong dal, 2 cups bottle gourd, Salt, 2 tbsp tamarind, 1.5 tbsp Oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp mustard, Pinch asafoetida, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 3 green chilies, 1 red chili, 0.25 tsp ginger, 1 clove garlic
instructions: 1. Wash dal and add 1 ½ cups water to a pressure cooker or a pot. 2. Wash and peel the skin off from sorakaya. Cube them. Add them to the cooker or pot. If you wish you can add green chilies too. 3. On a medium flame, pressure cook for 2 whistles if cooking directly in the cooker or for 3 to 4 whistles, if you are cooking in a bowl and placing it in the cooker. The dal and bottle gourd has to be cooked until soft. 4. When the pressure goes off, add salt and mash the dal with the back of the ladle. Set aside. 5. While the dal cooks soak tamarind in ¼ cup hot water. Squeeze the tamarind pulp. Set this aside. 6. Heat a pan with ghee or oil. Add mustard, cumin and red chili. 7. When they splutter, add grated ginger, crushed garlic, green chili and then the curry leaves. Fry till you begin to get a good aroma. 8. Add hing and turmeric. 9. Pour the tamarind water to a filter and add it to the pan. If you don’t mind you can add the pulp as it is. Let it begin to bubble. 10. Pour this to the mashed dal. Stir well. Turn off the heat. 11. Serve sorakaya pappu with rice or roti.

title: Sorakaya Perugu Pachadi Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups bottle gourd, 1.25 to 1 cups yogurt, Salt, 1 tbsp oil, 2.5 green chilies, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp cumin, 0.25 tsp mustard, 1 Pinch hing, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 1 pinch fenugreek powder, Coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash and peel bottle gourd, chop them to pieces. 2. Cook them till tender in a pot with very little water, you can sprinkle more if needed. Try not to use lot of water since we don’t want to drain the nutrients. Set this aside. 3. Heat a pan with oil, add cumin, mustard and chana dal and urad dal. Fry till the dals turn crunchy. Add sliced ginger, sliced or chopped green chilis, red chili and curry leaves. Fry till the curry leaves turn crisp for about 2 minutes. 4. Add hing, turmeric and fenugreek powder to the oil, stir. Add cooked bottle gourd and salt. Transfer this to a plate and let it cool completely. 5. Meanwhile, whisk yogurt with a fork to smooth 6. When the seasoned bottle gourd cools down, mix it with the yogurt and add chopped coriander leaves

title: Sorakaya Pulusu Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups bottle gourd, 1 medium potato, 1 cup onions, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons Tamarind, 0.75 tablespoon Jaggery, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 tablespoons Oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 Pinch methi seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder
instructions: 1. Rinse sorakaya under running water. Peel and chop off both the ends. Taste test to make sure it is not bitter. Bitter bottle gourd is not edible. Dice to 1½ inch pieces and chop onions and slit green chilies. 2. You may skip this step and use store bought tamarind paste instead. If using tamarind, add it to a small bowl and pour ⅓ cup hot water. Let soak until soft. Later squeeze the tamarind and pass through a strainer. Keep aside. 3. Heat oil in a pan, add mustard, cumin and methi seeds. When they splutter add curry leaves and hing (optional). 4. Add onions and green chilies. Sprinkle some salt. Saute till they turn pink. Takes around 3 to 4 mins. 5. Add the bottle gourd and potatoes (optional). Fry for another 3 to 4 mins. 6. Stir in the red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder and cumin powder (optional). Saute for a min. 7. Pour water just enough to immerse the veggies (about 1½ to 2 cups). Bring to a rapid boil. Reduce the heat and Simmer till veggies are soft cooked. 8. If it is too thick for your liking pour more hot water. If it is too runny, cook for a little longer on a high heat to evaporate the water. Pulusu should be runny consistency. 9. Add jaggery and tamarind. Mix well. Cover and cook till the pulusu reaches the desired consistency. Turn off when the veggies are soft cooked. Taste test and add more salt, jaggery or tamarind. Add coriander leaves. 10. Serve with sorakaya pulusu with plain dal, rice, ghee & papad or its variations.

title: Sorakkai Kootu (Bottle Gourd Kootu)
ingredients: 1.5 cups bottle gourd, 0.5 cup moong dal, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.75 tablespoon Tamarind, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.75 red chilli, 1 Pinch hing, 3.5 tablespoon coconut, 3.5 red chilies, 0.625 teaspoon cumin, 1.25 tablespoon chana dal
instructions: 1. Soak tamarind in a bowl with ¼ cup hot water and set aside to soak. Squeeze and strain it later. Set aside. 2. Add dal to a pot or pressure cooker and rinse well till the water runs clear. Pour 1½ to 2 cups water. Begin to heat on a medium heat. 3. Meanwhile rinse the bottle gourd, cut it to 2 parts. Taste test to make sure it is not bitter. Peel the skin and cube. 4. Add the cubed gourd to the dal and bring it to a rolling boil. Turn the heat to low. 5. If using pressure cooker, cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles on a medium heat. When the pressure drops open the lid. 6. If cooking in a pot, cook uncovered on a low to medium heat, adding more hot water as required. 7. To make sorakkai kootu, lentils and the gourd both must be soft cooked. 8. While the lentils coo, in a small tadka pan, dry roast red chilies and chana dal until deep golden and aromatic. Add cumin seeds and fry for 30 seconds. 9. Cool these and add to a grinder along with coconut. Pour little water and make a thick paste. 10. When the dal and gourd is cooked completely, add the ground paste, tamarind and turmeric. 11. Stir well. Add more hot water if required to bring it to the desired consistency. It has to be thick yet of pouring consistency. cook for about 3 to 5 minutes, until bubbling and aromatic. 12. Meanwhile, heat the tadka pan with ghee or oil. Add mustard and cumin seeds. 13. When they begin to crackle, add curry leaves, red chili and hing. 14. Switch off the stove and add the tempering to sorakkai kootu. Serve with rice or chapathi.

title: South Indian Egg Curry
ingredients: 4 hard boiled eggs, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.625 teaspoon cumin, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 1 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 2 tomatoes, 9 whole cashews, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.875 cup Water, 3 tablespoon Coriander leaves, 0.25 cup thick coconut milk
instructions: 1. While the eggs boil, prep up all the ingredients like chopping and gathering your spices. Heat a pan with oil. 2. Add the cumin seeds and mustard (optional). When the seeds begin to splutter, add curry leaves or mint. 3. Add onions and chopped green chilies. Saute well on a medium heat till they turn golden. 4. This step is optional. If you are okay with chunky masala then just use chopped tomatoes. While the onions fry, add tomatoes and cashews (optional) to a blender jar. You can also substitute cashews with fresh coconut or omit them. Make a smooth puree. Keep this aside. 5. When the onions turn golden, add ginger & garlic. 6. Saute well till the raw smell has gone, for about a minute. 7. Add the tomato cashew puree. 8. Saute well for about 3 to 4 mins. The onion tomato mixture blends well. 9. Add chili powder, salt, turmeric, garam masala and coriander powder. 10. Saute for a minute or 2 until the mixture turns aromatic. 11. Make 3 to 4 slits on the eggs and add them here. Saute for 2 mins gently (don’t break them). If you want roasted eggs, move the masala aside and add little oil to the pan. Fry the eggs until golden. 12. Pour water just enough to make a gravy. I used about 3/4 to 1 cup. 13. Bring the curry to a rolling boil. 14. Cover and cook until the curry turns thick. 15. When the curry is done, you will see the sauce/gravy turns thick with traces of oil on top. If you want you can add ¼ cup thick coconut milk at this stage. Taste test and adjust salt and garam masala to taste. Do not over cook as the eggs can turn hard. Garnish with coriander leaves. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve egg curry with rice, roti or chapathi. 16. 4 hard boiled eggs 17. 1 cup onions finely chopped 18. 1 green chili chopped 19. 2 tomatoes finely chopped or pureed 20. 3 tablespoon Coriander leaves or cilantro chopped 21. Preparation: Peel the boiled eggs and make 3 to 4 slits over them. You can use chopped tomatoes in the recipe. Or make a smooth puree of tomatoes and cashews (or coconut). Set this aside. 22. Temper: Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin, mustard and curry leaves. When they splutter, add onions and green chilies. Saute until golden and add the ginger garlic. Saute for 1 minute and stir in the tomatoes. Cook until they break down. 23. Make the curry: Stir in the red chili powder, turmeric, salt, garam masala and coriander powder. Saute for a few minutes until aromatic and add the eggs. Saute for 2 mins and stir in water to make a curry. 24. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat, reduce to medium. Cover and cook until the curry becomes thick and traces of oil is visible on top. Taste test to adjust salt or garam masala. If you want, add coconut milk, warm through and turn off. 25. Garnish with South Indian Egg Curry with coriander leaves. title: south indian rasam recipe | no dal rasam | no lentil rasam
ingredients: 1 tbsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp pepper, 3 clove garlic, 2 tbsp coriander stem, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 2 red chilli, few curry leaves, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 1 chilli, 1 cup tamarind extract, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a small blender take 1 tbsp cumin, 1 tsp pepper, 3 clove garlic and 2 tbsp coriander stem. 2. blend to a coarse paste without adding any water. keep aside. 3. in a large kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil and splutter 1 tsp mustard, 2 dried red chilli, few curry leaves and pinch hing. 4. add in the prepared masala paste and saute for a minute. 5. now add 1 tomato, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt and 1 chilli. 6. saute for a minute or until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 7. further, add 1 cup tamarind extract and 3 cup water. mix well. 8. cover and boil for 8 minutes or until raw flavour disappears. 9. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy south indian rasam with hot steamed rice.

title: Soya Burger with Soya Cutlet
ingredients: 1.25 cup potatoes, 0.75 cup carrot Optional, 0.5 cup baby beans, 1.5 cup soya granules, 1 tablespoons Oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.875 tsp red chili powder, 1 green chili, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 2.5 tbsp Oil, 3 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 cup water, 0.625 cup bread crumbs, 6 buns, 0.5 cup mint chutney, 1 medium onion, 1 tomato, 6 lettuce
instructions: 1. Steam potatoes, carrots, beans or peas in a steamer just until soft cooked. Do not overcook them. We don't want them to turn soggy. Mash them well with a masher. 2. Boil soya in hot water for 2 to 4 mins. Drain them. Squeeze of the excess water and rinse well few times. Squeeze and drain up the water. 3. Heat a pan with oil and add cumin seeds. When they splutter and ginger garlic & saute until it smells good. 4. Then add red chili powder, green chili, turmeric and salt. Mix quickly and add potatoes, carrots, peas or beans. Mix well and saute for a min. 5. Add soya and coriander leaves. Mix well. Cover and cook on a low heat for 3 to 4 mins. 6. Taste the mixture and add more salt if needed. 7. The soya patty mixture must bind well at this stage. If it is doesn't bind well mash the mixture a bit more. Divide the mixture to 6 to 8 portions depending on the size of your burger. 8. Make round balls and gently flatten them. 9. Make a slurry by mixing the rice flour and water. For batter consistency check the pictures below. 10. Dip each of these patties in the slurry and then coat with the crumbs. Set aside for ten minutes. 11. Heat a wide pan with 1½ tbsps oil. When it is hot enough place the patties in oil and fry on a medium heat until golden on both the sides. If needed add more oil for the other side. 12. Slit the buns horizontally and toast them on a pan. Then spread mint chutney and place the soya patty. Place the onions, tomatoes and lettuce. Add a generous amount of chutney over the veggies or spread it on the other half of the burger. 13. Place it over the soya burger and serve.

title: Soya Chaap Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 kg Soya chaap, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon Garam masala, 1.5 tablespoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 medium yellow onion, 2 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 cup water, 2 tablespoons ghee, 4 garlic, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 2.5 green chilies slit, 12 whole cashew nuts powdered, 0.625 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Drain and squeeze out all the liquid from soya chaap. Immerse them in a bowl of water for 10 mins. Later drain, remove the stick, squeeze out and cut them to 1 inch portions. 2. Pulse the onion only twice or thrice in a small grinder/ processor or onion chopper. (do this when you are ready to cook, don't let the onion sit) 3. Cook: To a pan/pot add the soya chaap and all the marinade ingredients including crushed onion & water. Mix well & cook for 8 mins. Cool down for 5 mins. (read notes for options) 4. Roast: Heat ghee in a wide pan and add green chilies, garlic and curry leaves. Let fry until the chilies are blistered. Spread the cooked soya chaap and add the nut powder. 5. Roast until aromatic for about 5 mins, stirring occasionally but not vigorously. Stir in salt, black pepper, fennel and garam masala as needed. Roast for another 4 mins, until you begin to see some fats releasing at the bottom of the pan. 6. Garnish with chopped coriander leaves and sliced fresh onions. Squeeze some lemon juice if you want and serve as an appetizer, or as a side with flatbreads or rice.

title: soya chunks 65 recipe | soya 65 recipe | mealmaker 65 | soya vadi nasta 65
ingredients: 2 cup soya chunks, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 cup curd, 0.25 cup corn flour, 0.25 cup maida, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large vessel take enough water, add ½ tbsp salt and get to a boil. 2. once the water comes to a boil, add 2 cup soya chunks. 3. boil for 5 minutes or until the soya chunks are cooked well and softened. 4. drain off the chunks and rinse with cold water. 5. squeeze off the extra water and transfer it to a large bowl. 6. add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander and 1 tsp ginger garlic paste. 7. mix well making sure all the spices are well coated. 8. now add ½ cup curd, ¼ cup corn flour and ¼ cup maida. 9. mix well and make sure the batter is well coated. 10. deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 11. stir occasionally, until the soya chunks turn crispy golden brown. 12. drain off the fried soya chunks to remove excess oil. 13. finally, enjoy soya chunks 65 with spicy green chutney.

title: Soya Chunks Biryani | Meal Maker Biryani Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 0.5 cup soya chunks, 2 tablespoons curd, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 2 cups water, 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 2.5 green cardamom, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon, 1 strand mace, 0.25 teaspoon shahi jeera, 4 black pepper corn, 0.5 cup mint leaves, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 1 inch ginger pieces, 3 garlic cloves, 1 onion, 0.25 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Soak soya chunks in warm water till they soften, squeeze off the excess water, 2. Wash with cold water and squeeze to remove excess water, repeat this twice. Make sure there is no water left in the chunks. 3. Add mint leaves, coriander leaves, onion, ginger, garlic, green chilies to a grinder. Grind to a smooth paste. 4. Marinate the soya chunks with this paste and garam masala. Set aside for at least 15 mins. 5. Soak rice for 15 mins and drain off the water and set aside. 6. Heat oil in a pot or cooker. Add whole spices and sauté till you get an aroma for about a minute. 7. Add the marinated soya chunks along with the ground paste. Saute on a low heat until the raw flavor in ginger garlic goes away. 8. Add curd or coconut milk and turmeric. Mix well. Cook till the mixture leaves the sides of the pan. The moisture evaporates and the masala turns thick. 9. Pour water and add salt. Taste test and more if required. Bring water to a rolling boil in a high heat. 10. Reduce the heat and add rice. Mix well. 11. Cover the pot or cooker. If cooking in a pot, cook covered on a low heat until the rice is fully cooked. If cooking in a pressure cooker, Regulate the flame to medium. Turn off the heat when you hear one whistle. Rest soya chunks biryani for 15 minutes and then fluff up with a fork. 12. Serve soya chunks biryani with any raita of your choice.

title: Soya Chunks Curry Recipe (Meal Maker Curry)
ingredients: 1.25 cup soya chunks, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch mustard, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.25 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 0.25 cup raw coconut
instructions: 1. Bring 3 cups water to a boil, switch off the stove. Add soya chunks and leave them in hot water till they soften. 2. Debris settles down, discard the water and squeeze off the water from the chunks. Rinse them in cold water twice and repeat squeezing off the excess water. You will get frothy water from the soya chunks, it is just normal. Set this aside for later use. 3. If you are using large chunks you can cut each one to two. Grind the tomatoes and coconut to a smooth paste. 4. Heat oil in a pan and add mustard & cumin seeds. When they begin to splutter add curry leaves, onions and green chilies. 5. Saute until the onions turn golden. Add ginger garlic paste and fry till the raw smell disappears, for a minute. 6. Add the tomato and coconut puree. Cook till thick and begins to leave the sides of the pan. 7. Stir red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, and salt. Saute everything well until the spices begin to smell good, for about 3 mins. 8. Next transfer the soya chunks. Saute them for 3 minutes stirring often, till the meal maker are coated well in the masala. 9. Pour hot water and mix well. If the curry is too thick add more water. Bring it to a boil on a high heat & simmer. 10. Cook on a low flame for about 5 to 8 minutes, till soya chunks are cooked well. The oil begins to float on top when the curry is done. Add coriander leaves and mix. Turn off the heat & let rest for at least an hour so the soya chunks soak up the flavors well. 11. Serve soya chunks curry with plain rice, variety rice or roti. We usually squeeze some lemon juice and serve with raw onions, cucumber and carrot slices on the side.

title: Soya Chunks Cutlet Recipe | Soya Tikki - High Protein Tikki
ingredients: 1 cup soya chunks meal maker, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp chilli ginger garlic paste, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 cup roasted besan, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take 1 cup soya chunks and add 1 tsp salt. 2. Soak in hot water for 10 minutes. 3. Squeeze the water out of the soy chunks. 4. Transfer the soya chunks into the mixer grinder and coarsely pulse. 5. Take the coarse texture of soya into the large plate. 6. Add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp aamchur powder and ½ tsp salt. 7. Also add ½ onion, 2 tbsp chilli, ginger, garlic paste, 2 tbsp spring onion, ½ cup roasted besan. 8. Squeeze and mix well, ensuring all the spices are thoroughly combined. 9. Grease your hands with oil and prepare ball-sized tikki. 10. Shallow fry in hot oil. You can also roast or deep-fry. 11. Roast on a low flame until it turns golden brown. Flip over and cook on both sides. 12. Finally, enjoy Crispy Soya Tikki Recipe with dahi green chutney.

title: Soya Chunks Dry (Fry)
ingredients: 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 0.875 cup soya chunks, 2 medium tomatoes, 1 onion, 1 Pinch hing, 1.5 green chilies, 0.875 tsp ginger garlic paste, Salt, 0.5 tsp red chilli powder, 0.625 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsps Oil, Coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Bring 6 cups water to a boil. Add soya and cook for about 3 to 5 minutes till they soften. Leave them aside for some more time for the dirt to settle down. 2. Rinse them off in water and squeeze to remove frothy water inside the chunks. Repeat the rinse. Squeeze off the water and keep them side. If you are using large chunks, you may chop one chunk to 2. You can also marinate soya chunks with 1/2 tsp of ginger garlic paste, 1/4 tsp chili powder and 1/2 tsp of garam masala. Keep this aside for 15 mins. 3. Slice onions and chop tomatoes. Heat a pan with oil. Add curry leaves and cumin seeds. When they begin to splutter, add onions and green chilies. 4. Saute till the onions turn transparent. Add grated ginger garlic. Saute till you get a nice smell. Do not burn. 5. Add tomatoes, sprinkle salt and turmeric. 6. Saute till the tomatoes turn mushy. Now you should be left with a moist onion tomatoes mix that is sufficient to coat the nuggets well. Add red chili powder, garam masala powder and mix well. 7. Add soya chunks and mix everything well. 8. Cover and cook till they turn soft, moist and flavorful. This takes about 5 minutes. Sauté for 2 mins until it smells good. Taste test and adjust salt or more garam masala to your taste. Garnish soya chunks fry with coriander leaves. If you want sprinkle little lemon juice.

title: Soya Chunks Kurma
ingredients: 0.5 cup onions, 1 tomato, 1.5 green chili, 1 tbsps coriander leaves, 1 tbsp mint leaves, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.375 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, Salt, 2.5 tbsp Oil, 1 cup Soya chunks, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, Salt, 1 tbsp Lemon juice, 2 tbsp Poppy seeds, 2.5 tbsp coconut, 0.5 tsp saunf, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 star flower, 0.5 tsp shahi jeera, 1 cinnamon stick, 4 cloves 4 cloves (/ laung), 2 green cardamoms
instructions: 1. Heat 3 to 4 cups water, add soya chunks. Switch off the stove. Leave them to soften for about 10 minutes. 2. Squeeze off the water and rinse them in lot of water. Repeat squeezing them. Make sure there is no water left in the chunks. If you desire you can cut the chunks to half or use them as it is. 3. Marinate them with 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1/4 tsp garam masala, 1/4 tsp red chili powder, salt and lemon juice. Set this aside until needed. 4. Chop onions, tomatoes, set them aside. 5. If using poppy seeds, dry roast them until crunchy. Powder them first with saunf and then add the coconut with very little water to make a thick paste. 6. Heat a pan with oil, add the dry spices. 7. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and green chilies. Fry till the onions turn golden. 8. Add tomato and fry till the tomato turns mushy. If needed sprinkle water to cook tomatoes. 9. Add marinated soya chunks, coriander and mint leaves. Fry everything well till the raw smell of the ginger garlic goes away. 10. Stir well to prevent burning. If needed use little more oil. 11. Add the ground coconut paste or coconut milk. (If using yogurt, mix a few tablespoons of onion tomato masala to the yogurt, whisk it well and pour it to the pot.) 12. Then add red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder. Mix and fry well for about 3 to 5 minutes, till you begin to get a nice aroma. 13. Pour water just enough to make a good amount of kurma gravy. Adjust salt as needed. 14. Simmer and cook till you get the desired consistency of gravy, for about 5 minutes. You will see traces of oil over the kurma when the gravy is done. 15. Garnish soya chunks kurma with coriander leaves. Serve with rice or roti.

title: Soya Chunks Manchurian
ingredients: 0.75 cup soya chunks, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 0.625 tsp red chili powder, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 2 tbsp corn flour, Salt, Oil, 1 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp garlic, 0.5 tbsp ginger, 0.25 cup spring onions, 0.25 cup capsicum, 1 tsp soya sauce, 1 tbsp chili sauce, 0.625 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tsp vinegar, 0.5 tsp sugar, 0.375 tsp red chili powder, 0.75 tsp corn flour, 0.5 cup water, Salt, 0.25 tsp pepper, spring onion greens
instructions: 1. Bring 3 to 4 cups water to a boil and add soya chunks. Allow to rest for some time until they turn soft. 2. Squeeze off the excess water (froth) from the chunks. Rinse them well in a pot full of water and repeat squeezing them to remove excess water. 3. Add them to a mixing bowl along with salt, ginger garlic paste, red chili powder and corn flour. Mix everything well, if needed sprinkle little water for the flour to bind the chunks. 4. Set these aside for 10 mins, until you prepare the garlic, onions and capsicum for sauce. 5. Heat oil in a kadai and fry these on a medium heat until golden and crisp. Remove them to a kitchen tissue. Set these aside. 6. Mix ¾ tsp corn flour in ½ cup water. Make sure it has no lumps. Set aside. 7. Heat oil in a pan. Add garlic and saute for a min. Add spring onions and saute on a high flame till they smell good, for about a minute. Next add capscium and saute for 2 to 3 mins until the aroma comes out. 8. Add soya sauce, vinegar, chili sauce & tomato sauce. Stir the corn flour well with water and pour it. Add chili powder if you prefer a deep color. 9. Lower the heat and stir well. Cook until the sauce thickens. Taste the sauce and check if needed add sugar and salt. Sauce has to be slightly sour, sweet and hot. 10. When the sauce thickens, add pepper and mix. Allow the sauce to cool down a bit. Add the crisp fried soya chunks and mix well. 11. Garnish soya chunks manchurian with spring onions.

title: Soya Fried Rice Recipe | Soya Rice - Meal Maker Fried Rice
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 5 cloves garlic, 2 tbsp spring onion, 3 chilli, 1 onion, 3 tbsp peas, 3 tbsp sweet corn, 1 carrot, 5 beans, 0.5 capsicum, 1.5 tsp salt, 1 cup boiled soya, 3 cup cooked rice, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, add 5 cloves garlic, 2 tbsp spring onion and 3 chilli. 2. Stir fry on high flame until the garlic shrinks. 3. Add 1 onion and stir fry till the onion shrinks. 4. Further add 3 tbsp peas, 3 tbsp sweet corn, 1 carrot, 5 beans, ½ capsicum and ½ tsp salt. 5. Stir fry till the vegetables turn crunchy. 6. Also, add 1 cup boiled soya and continue to fry for 2 minutes. 7. Add in 3 cup cooked rice, ½ tsp pepper powder, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp chilli sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar and 1 tsp salt. 8. Stir fry until the sauce is well coated with the rice. 9. Add in 2 tbsp spring onion and mix well. 10. Finally, enjoy Soya Fried Rice Recipe with sauce.

title: Soya Keema Recipe (Soya Granules Recipe)
ingredients: 0.5 cup soya mince keema granules, 0.5 cup cauliflower, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.125 cup bell peppers, 0.125 cup green peas, 0.375 cup onions, 1 green chili, 2 tbsp Oil, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp meat masala, 0.25 tsp amchur powder, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi, 2 tbsp Cashew paste, 1 bay leaf, 0.25 tsp cumin seeds, 3.5 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves (or crushed black pepper corn), 1 inch cinnamon stick
instructions: 1. If you do not have soya keema, here is how to make it from soya chunks. Soak soya chunks in hot water till they soften, discard the water and rinse in cold water, squeeze off the excess water and set aside. Pulse the chunks in a grinder to coarse keema or mince. Set aside. 2. To use the store bought soya keema bring 3 cups of water to a boil. Add the mince and turn off the stove. Let soak well soft. Then discard the water and squeeze the mince well. 3. Rinse it again 2 to 3 times in fresh cold water, squeezing off the absorbed water. 4. Heat a heavy bottom pan with oil, add curry leaves or bay leaf, cumin, cardamoms, cloves and cinnamon stick. Sauté for a minute. 5. Add onions, sprinkle salt and fry till golden. 6. Next add ginger garlic paste and fry till the raw smell goes away 7. Add tomatoes, salt and turmeric. fry till tomatoes turn fully mushy 8. Next add red chili powder, fry for a min 9. Add soya keema , green peas, cauliflower and capsicum and fry for 3 to 4 mins 10. Pour little water to bring to a consistency. 11. When it comes to a boil, add kasuri methi, amchur and garam masala. 12. Add the nut paste or melon seeds paste. Cook till it comes to a desired consistency. 13. Off the heat and serve soya keema with rice or paratha

title: Soya Manchurian Recipe | Dry Soya Chunks Manchuri
ingredients: 2 cup soya chunks, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp chilli paste, 2 tsp soy sauce, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 cup maida, 0.25 cup corn flour, oil, 2 tsp oil, 1 inch ginger, 0.5 onion, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 0.5 tsp chilli paste, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 cup corn flour slurry, spring onion
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large saucepan, bring the water to a boil. Add a generous amount of salt to make the water salty. 2. Add in 2 cup soya chunks. Mix well. 3. Boil for 5 minutes, or until the soya is cooked well. 4. Squeeze off the water and drain off. 5. Add 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp chilli paste, 2 tsp soy sauce, ½ tsp salt, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ cup maida and ¼ cup corn flour. 6. Mix well, ensuring the masala and flour are well-coated. 7. Deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 8. Stir occasionally, till the soya chunks turn golden and crispy. 9. Drain off the soya chunks. Keep aside. 10. In a pan, heat 2 tsp oil. Add 1 inch of ginger, ½ onion and stir fry well. 11. Add ½ cup of capsicum and ½ onion, and stir-fry until the onions turn crunchy. 12. Now add 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, ½ tsp chilli paste, ½ tsp salt. 13. Cook till the sauces are well combined and cooked well. 14. Now add ¼ cup of corn flour slurry and cook until the slurry thickens and turns shiny. 15. Add in fried soya and mix gently. 16. Finally, garnish with spring onion and enjoy the Soya Chunks Manchurian Recipe.

title: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams spaghetti, 1.25 tablespoon garlic, 1 teaspoon red chili flakes, 2 tablespoons olive oil, 2.5 tablespoons parsley, 1 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons Parmesan cheese
instructions: 1. Making aglio e olio traditionally begins with spaghetti. Begin by bringing a large pot of generously salted water to a boil. While you wait for this to happen, gather the finest olive oil you can find, a few cloves of chopped garlic, salt and crushed red pepper. For this step bring 8 cups of water to a boil with 1 teaspoon salt. 2. Once the water has begun to boil, add 2 servings of spaghetti. About 250 grams. 3. Let it boil on a medium high flame and stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Set your timer according to the package instructions, keeping in mind you are looking for perfectly al dente pasta. 4. After testing for proper doneness (generally 7-9 minutes), reserve about a cup of the pasta cooking water before straining. 5. Over low heat, gently warm 2 tablespoons of olive oil. To the pan, add 1 to 1 ½ tablespoon minced chopped garlic, making sure not to allow it to take on too much color or burn. As soon as garlic begins to shrink, add 1 teaspoon chilli flakes. For spicier version use up to 1½ teaspoons chilli flakes. Give a quick stir. 6. To the saute pan, carefully add about half cup of stock (pasta cooked water) and ¼ teaspoon salt. Allow it to simmer. 7. Let it reduce slightly before adding your spaghetti. I let mine to reduce to half of the original quantity (¼ cup). Taste test both the cooked spaghetti and the sauce so you know how much to add at this stage. Add more salt if required. 8. Turn off and add the spaghetti. Toss well to coat the spaghetti with the garlic oil and chili flakes. 9. At this point, taste the pasta and adjust the seasoning if necessary according to your taste. For more heat, add chilli flakes. If it is too dry, you may splash a bit of the stock. Serve aglio olio hot with a vegetable salad , green salad or fried eggs.

title: Spicy Bread Masala Recipe
ingredients: 8 slices Bread, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.75 teaspoon cumin, 1 medium onion, 1 green chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 2 medium tomatoes, salt as needed, 0.75 teaspoon pav bhaji masala, 0.625 teaspoon chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 3 tablespoon water, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi optional, 3 tablespoon coriander leaves, chaat masala, 3 tablespoon onions
instructions: 1. Cube the bread and keep aside. Chop onions and tomatoes finely. Reserve some onions to garnish. 2. Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and let sizzle for 30 seconds. Add the onions and chilies. Saute till they turn golden. 3. Add ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw smell is gone, for a minute. Stir in the tomatoes, salt, garam masala, pav bhaji masala and chili powder. 4. Saute until the tomatoes break down and the masala turns aromatic. Pour 3 tbsp of water and cook till the masala begins to bubble & turns thick. Crush kasuri methi in your palm and add it 5. Taste test and adjust salt or spices to your taste. Stir in the bread and coat gently with the masala. Garnish with coriander leaves, sprinkle chaat masala and reserved onions. 6. Spicy Bread Masala is best served immediately.

title: spicy buttermilk recipe | majjige huli recipe | masala chaas recipe
ingredients: 2 cups curd yogurt, 1 green chilli, 2 springs coriander leaves, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp lemon juice, 2 cups cold water, 1 ice cubes, salt to taste, 1 tsp oil ghee, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds jeera, 1 red chilli, pinch hing asafoetida
instructions: 1. roughly chop the ginger, green chilli and coriander leaves. keep aside. 2. now in a small chutney blender, blend coriander leaves, ginger and chilli along with 2 tsp of thick curd / yogurt. 3. blend to a smooth paste. 4. in a large blender add 2 cups of yogurt / curd and 1½ cup of cold water. blend well. 5. combine the ground masala with blended yogurt. add some salt and give a pulse. so everything gets mixed well 6. for tempering, heat oil / ghee in a small pan and add mustard seeds, urad dal, cumin seeds, red chillies and hing. when the seeds crackle pour the tempering over the buttermilk. 7. squeeze some lemon juice. garnish with coriander and lemon.

title: spinach corn sandwich recipe | spinach and corn sandwich recipe
