ingredients: 1 tsp butter, 2 tbsp onion, 1 cup palak spinach, 0.5 cup corn, 0.5 tsp mixed herbs, 0.5 tsp chilli flakes, 0.25 tsp pepper, 0.25 tsp salt, 4 slice bread, 4 tsp green chutney, butter for toasting
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large tawa heat 1 tsp butter and saute 2 tbsp onion. 2. also, add 1 cup palak and saute for 2 minutes or until palak shrinks. 3. now add ½ cup corn and saute until corn is cooked well. 4. further add ½ tsp mixed herbs, ½ tsp chilli flakes, ¼ tsp pepper and ¼ tsp salt. 5. saute and mix well. the mixture is ready, keep aside. 6. now take 2 slices of bread and spread 1 tsp green chutney on each slice of bread. 7. spread 2 tbsp of prepared palak corn mixture over bread slice. 8. further place a slice of cheese. 9. cover with another slice of bread and press gently. 10. roast the sandwich with butter on both sides. 11. finally, cut the bread in half and serve spinach corn sandwich.

title: Spinach Curry Recipe (Palak Curry)
ingredients: 2.5 cups spinach, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 cup onion, 1.5 green chilies, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 tablespoon besan, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1.25 cup water, 0.625 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1.25 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.75 tablespoon ghee, 2.5 garlic cloves, 1.5 red chilies, 0.125 teaspoon hing
instructions: 1. Heat 1½ tablespoons oil in a pot and add ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. 2. When they begin to splutter, add ½ cup fine chopped onions and 1 to 2 chopped green chilies. Saute them well until the onions turn golden. Then add chopped 1 tablespoon chopped ginger garlic, ½ teaspoon salt and ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. 3. Saute for 2 minutes to bring out the aroma of ginger garlic. Then add 1½ tablespoons besan (gram flour) and ¾ teaspoon garam masala. If you do not have gram flour, you can use 4 tablespoons almond meal or 6 tbsps (¼ cup + 2 tbsps) grated fresh or frozen coconut. If you do not have both then, skip it and use thick coconut milk later as mentioned below. 4. Regulate the flame to low and saute all of these for about 4 to 5 mins without burning. When done you will begin to get a nutty aroma.The raw smell of the gram flour/ besan should disappear completely. If the flour begins to stick to the pot, add a bit more oil and cook covered. 5. Add 2½ cups chopped spinach. 6. Saute again till the leaves wilt completely. Cool this slightly. 7. Pour water to a blender jar and add this mixture. Blend to a smooth or coarse puree to suit your liking. I do it smooth as my kids don’t like the chunky curry. 8. After transfer the puree to the pot along with 1¼ cup water. Give a stir and bring it to a boil. 9. Cook it for 3 to 4 mins adding more water if needed. The spinach curry begins to smell flavorful when done. Crush ½ to ¾ tablespoon kasuri methi and add it. Turn off when the curry turns slightly thick yet is of pouring consistency. It thickens further after cooling.

title: Spinach Omelette Recipe
ingredients: 2.5 eggs, 1 cup spinach, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 green chili, 0.25 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon butter, 0.25 teaspoon Oregano, 0.125 teaspoon black pepper, 2 tbsp cheese
instructions: 1. Rinse spinach and coriander leaves in lot of water and drain completely. 2. Fine chop green chilies and spinach. If using ginger mince it or grate it. 3. Break the eggs to a bowl. Add chopped spinach, coriander leaves, onions, salt, ginger, turmeric, and green chili. 4. Beat all of them together until the mixture turns frothy. 5. Heat a pan and add oil or butter on a medium heat. Spread it well to coat the pan evenly with oil. 6. When the pan is hot enough, pour the mixture evenly. 7. If required spread the spinach over the egg. Cook on a medium heat until the base is set and firm. 8. Flip it and cook until the eggs are cooked completely. Sprinkle Italian herbs and crushed pepper. 9. Serve spinach omelette hot. 10. Break eggs to a bowl. Add salt and beat the eggs until frothy. 11. Heat 1 tsp oil or butter in a 6 inch non-stick pan. Saute ginger until fragrant. 12. Then add spinach and green chilies, saute just until spinach turns tender. Add cilantro, black pepper and turmeric just to allow the flavors to combine. 13. Remove from the pan and set aside. 14. Add 1 teaspoon butter or oil to the same pan & heat on a medium flame. 15. When your pan is hot, add beaten eggs, stirring and pulling them away from the sides of the pan until the egg is just cooked. 16. At that point add your spinach filling, then fold your spinach omelette. Gently slide it onto a plate and garnish as desired.

title: Spinach Soup Recipe (Palak Soup)
ingredients: 1 teaspoon oil, 12 grams spinach, 2 garlic cloves, 2 tablespoons butter, 1.5 inch cinnamon, 1 bay leaf, 2.5 tablespoons oats flour, 2.5 cups milk, 1 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 2.5 tablespoons cream, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli flakes
instructions: 1. Pour 1 teaspoon oil to a pot and saute sliced garlic for 1 minute. 2. Add the spinach and saute until it wilts and the raw smell has gone completely. This will take about 5 to 6 minutes. 3. Cool this and blend to a smooth puree with ½ cup water or stock/broth. 4. Melt butter in the same pot and reduce the heat to low. Add 1 bay leaf and cinnamon. Let them sizzle in the butter for a minute. 5. Add flour and saute until it loses the raw flavor, for 2 to 3 minutes. Do not brown the flour. 6. Pour milk slowly and mix well. Simmer and let it cook until slightly thick. 7. Then add sugar, black pepper, spinach puree and ½ cup more liquid (stock/broth or plain water). 8. Bring this to a gentle boil and add cream. 9. Let it simmer until the soup thickens and all the raw flavor of spinach is gone. (for about 3 to 4 mins). Taste test and adjust the seasoning. 10. Turn off the heat and cover the spinach soup. Bay leaf and cinnamon will continue to infuse their flavors so keep them in the soup until you serve. 11. Pour spinach soup to serving bowls and garnish with cream, black pepper and red chili flakes. Serve hot or warm with croutons or cheese toast.

title: Split Chickpeas (Senaga Pappu Kobbari)
ingredients: 1 cup split chickpeas, 1.75 to 2 cups water, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 4.5 garlic cloves, 2.5 red chilies, 2.5 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 pinch turmeric, 1 pinch hing, 1 cup coconut
instructions: 1. Add split chickpeas to a bowl and rinse it well thrice. Pour 1¾ cups water and soak them for 2 hours. 2. Begin to cook by bringing the water to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat and cook them on a medium high heat. 3. You will see a lot of froth coming up, if you want you may skim it off using a skimmer or a slotted spoon. If you skim the froth, you will need to pour some boiling water. 4. When there is very little water at the bottom (about 3 to 4 tablespoons, check pics in the post), add ½ teaspoon salt and mix. Reduce the heat completely and cover. 5. Cook until the lentils are tender & all of the water is evaporated. Take care not to burn them at the bottom. 6. Test if they are soft cooked by mashing a few split chickpeas, they should be soft but not mushy. When you eat, it should have a bite to it and fully cooked. 7. If they are still hard, pour some hot boiling water. At the end, you should have lentils that are perfectly cooked, soft enough, have a bite to them and not mushy. 8. This step of cooking lentils just takes you about 18 to 20 mins. Prepare the tempering ingredients while the lentils cook. 9. Heat oil or ghee in a pan. When it turns hot, add mustard, cumin, urad dal, crushed garlic and broken red chillies. 10. When the lentils are light golden, add green chilies and curry leaves. Fry them until the chilies are blistered and curry leaves turn crisp. 11. Optional - If you want you may add ginger, hing and turmeric at this stage. Fry for 30 seconds until the ginger turns aromatic. 12. Add the cooked split chickpeas and mix well. Taste test. Add coconut and more salt. Turn off the stove and mix. 13. We love to sprinkle some lemon juice before serving. Serve with rice-rasam, pulusu/stew or plain as a snack.

title: sponge cake recipe | eggless sponge cake | plain cake recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup butter room temperature, 1.5 cup sugar, 2 cup milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 3 cup maida plain flour, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.5 tsp baking soda, 0.25 tsp salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take ¾ cup butter and 1½ cup sugar. 2. beat well until the sugar and butter turn creamy and soft. 3. now add 2 cup milk and 1 tsp vanilla extract. 4. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 5. further, place a sieve and add 3 cup maida, 1 tsp baking powder, ½ tsp baking soda and ¼ tsp salt 6. sieve the flour making sure there are no lumps present. 7. mix well until the batter is a smooth consistency. 8. transfer the cake batter into the square cake mould. 9. place the cake tray into the preheated oven. bake the cake at 180 degree celsius or 356 degrees fahrenheit for 40 minutes. 10. finally, enjoy eggless sponge cake with frosting or as it is.

title: Spot Idli Recipe | Hyderabadi Instant Spicy Idli On Tawa
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 0.75 cup chana dal, 1 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup sesame seeds, 1 tsp oil, 2 red chilli, handful curry leaves, lemon sized tamarind, 2 tsp jaggery, 1.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp oil, 2 tbsp onion, 0.5 tsp ginger, 1 chilli, 1 tsp coriander, 2 tbsp tomato, 1 tsp curry leaves, 2 tsp milagai podi, 1 tsp butter, 1 bowl idli batter, butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil. roast ¾ cup chana dal, 1 cup urad dal, and ¼ cup sesame seeds until it turns aromatic. 2. once the dal turns golden brown and aromatic, transfer it to a plate and cool it completely. 3. heat 1 tsp oil and add 20 dried red chilli, a handful of curry leaves, and lemon-sized tamarind. 4. roast until the chilli puffs up and turns crunchy. 5. transfer to the plate and cool completely. 6. once everything is cooled completely, transfer to the mixer jar. 7. add 2 tsp jaggery, 1½ tsp salt, 1 tsp turmeric and 1 tsp hing. 8. grind to a fine powder without adding water. 9. finally, milagai podi is ready to enjoy. 10. in a large cast iron pan, heat 1 tbsp oil. 11. add 2 tbsp onion, ½ tsp ginger, 1 chilli and 1 tsp coriander. saute until the onion softens. 12. further add 2 tbsp tomato, 1 tsp curry leaves and saute until the tomato softens. 13. now add 2 tsp milagai podi and 1 tsp butter. roast until everything is well combined. 14. divide into 4 parts giving good space in between. 15. pour in a ladleful of idli batter. 16. also sprinkle some milagai podi, butter and coriander. 17. cover and simmer for 10 minutes or until the idli batter is cooked well. 18. now carefully flip over without breaking the idli. 19. roast on both sides until it turns golden brown and crisp. 20. finally, enjoy spot idli with chutney and milagai podi.

title: spring dosa recipe | schezwan dosa recipe | chinese dosa recipe
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 2 cloves garlic, 3 stem spring onions, 1 capsicum, 1 carrot, 0.5 cup cabbage, 1 tbsp schezwan sauce, 1 tsp soy sauce, salt to taste, 1 tsp vinegar, 1 rice noodles maggie any noodles, 4 cup dosa batter, 3 tbsp butter, 3 tsp schezwan sauce
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai or wok heat oil. 2. further add garlic and saute well. 3. also add spring onions and continue to sauce. 4. now add vegetables and saute for a minute or till they shrink slightly. 5. additionally add salt, schezwan sauce, soy sauce and vinegar. 6. also add cooked rice noodles or any noodles of your choice. 7. mix gently without breaking the rice noodles and making sure the sauce has covered uniformly. keep aside. 8. pour a ladleful of the dosa batter on it and spread it in a circular motion thin circle. 9. add a dollop of butter add schezwan sauce 10. and mix schezwan sauce and butter together and spread. 11. also spread 2 tbsp of prepared schezwan noodles at one side. 12. cook till the bottom of dosa turns golden brown. 13. then roll the dosa along with noodles. 14. finally, cut half schezwan noodle dosa or spring dosa and serve immediately with tomato sauce.

title: Spring Roll Recipe | Vegetable Spring Rolls
ingredients: 3 cups cabbage, 1 medium carrot, 0.25 cup bell pepper, 2 sprigs spring onions, 1.5 teaspoons oil, 1.5 teaspoons soya sauce, 1 teaspoon Rice vinegar, 0.375 teaspoon Black pepper powder, salt, 1 cup cooked noodles, 6 spring roll wrappers, 1 cup oil
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a wok or pan. The entire filling has to be cooked on a high flame. Saute spring onions just for a minute. 2. Next add shredded cabbage, carrots & capsicum. You can add bean if you like and can even skip capsicum. The restaurant rolls I ate in Singapore never had beans in them. 3. Saute until the veggies are half done yet crunchy. Add soya sauce. 4. Pour vinegar. 5. Next add spring onions, pepper and very less salt. There is also salt in soya sauce and even in wrappers as well. So take care not to add too much. Turn off the stove and set aside to cool. 6. Heat a pan with oil. Add cumin, garlic or ginger garlic and sauté till it becomes fragrant. Add all the veggies and stir fry on high for 3 to 4 mins. 2. Add all the other ingredients mentioned under stuffing and mix well. The stuffing is ready. Preheat oven at 220 C at least for 15 mins. 3. Place a damp cloth over the frozen sheets for 5 mins. They begin to come out easily when you pull out. Replace the damp cloth on the rest of the sheets. 4. Next place a sheet and add the stuffing as shown in the picture. 5. Roll one edge towards the filling. And continue to roll till the center. 7. Bring the other 2 sides to the center and place them. 7. Continue to roll. Apply the flour mix over the edges and gently press it over the roll to seal. 8. This way, finish rolling all. Brush them with a generous amount of oil evenly. Sprinkle some sesame seeds and place them on a parchment paper or greased foil. 9. Bake them for 12 to 15 mins. I do not flip them to the other sides, mine gets baked evenly on all the sides. These can also be shallow fried or deep fried too. Serve vegetable spring rolls with your favorite sauce or dip.

title: spring rolls recipe | veg spring roll recipe | veg roll with spring roll sheet
ingredients: 2 cup maida plain flour, 2 tbsp corn flour, 0.5 tsp salt, 2.5 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 3 clove garlic, 2 chilli, 2 tbsp spring onion, 0.5 onion, 1 carrot, 2 cup cabbage, 5 beans, 0.5 capsicum, 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tsp chilli sauce, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup maida paste, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup maida, 2 tbsp corn flour and ½ tsp salt. 2. add 2½ cup water and mix well using a whisk. 3. whisk until a smooth lump-free batter is formed adding water as required. 4. now grease the pan and pour a ladleful of batter on a hot pan. 5. swirl once making sure the batter is uniformly spread. 6. cook for a minute or until the sheet is cooked without browning. 7. now flip over gently and continue to cook. 8. finally, spring roll wrapper is ready. you can cover and use to prepare patti samosa or spring rolls. 9. firstly, in a large wok, heat 2 tbsp oil. saute 3 clove garlic, 2 chilli and 2 tbsp spring onion. 10. add ½ onion and stir fry on high flame. 11. now add 1 carrot, 2 cup cabbage, 5 beans, ½ capsicum. 12. stir fry without losing the crunchiness of vegetables. 13. further, add 2 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tsp chilli sauce, ¼ tsp pepper powder and ½ tsp salt. 14. mix well making sure everything is well combined. stuffing is ready. 15. firstly, take a prepared wrapper and place a tbsp of prepared veg stuffing. 16. rub a tsp of maida paste around the edge. maida helps to seal the roll. 17. now roll and fold the sides making sure the roll is sealed tight. 18. deep fry in hot oil, keeping the flame on low. 19. stir occasionally, until the roll turns golden brown and crisp. 20. drain off removing excess oil. 21. finally, enjoy veg spring roll with sweet chilli sauce.

title: Sprouted Green Moong Stir Fry
ingredients: 1.25 tablespoon ghee, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1.5 red chilies, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 0.625 inch ginger peeled minced, 3.5 medium garlic cloves minced, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 3.5 tablespoons water, 3 cups sprouted green moong, 0.25 cup raw coconut, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves chopped, 0.875 tablespoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Add 3 cups/ 250 grams sprouts to a large pot/ bowl and rinse very well with clean water. Drain completely. 2. On a medium flame, heat 1 to 1½ tablespoon ghee or oil in a wide pan. Add 3. Fry until the dal turns light golden, for a minute. Stir in 4. 3 to 4 chopped medium garlic cloves 5. Saute for 1 to 2 mins, until ginger garlic begins to smell aromatic. Stir in 6. Immediately pour 3 to 4 tablespoons water. (Avoid adding too much water) Bring to a rolling boil and turn down the heat to the lowest. 7. Stir in the sprouts & cover. 8. Pan-steam for 3 to 4 mins, until the sprouts are partially cooked (to your liking), yet crunchy. If the pan dries out and they are still undercooked, add a splash of water and continue to steam for another 1 to 2 mins. 9. Turn off and stir in ¼ cup raw grated coconut. Taste test to adjust salt. 10. Don’t overcook. This is the texture when it cools down. They should remain crunchy but not chewy or too soft. When you are ready to serve, stir in ¾ to 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 2 tablespoons slightly crushed roasted sesame seeds (optional) and 2 tablespoon chopped coriander leaves (optional). Serve stir-fried sprouted green moong on their own or with roti, parathas or plain steamed rice.

title: Sprouts Salad Recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup mung bean sprouts, 1 tablespoon onions, 1 tomato, 0.25 cup cucumber, 1 carrot, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, salt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.25 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 cup cornflakes, 1 tablespoon coconut, 0.25 inch ginger, 3 mint leaves
instructions: 1. I sprouted the green gram at home following this Mung bean sprouts recipe. Since these are homemade I use them raw. If using store bought, Rinse the sprouts well under running water. Bring 2 cups water to a rolling boil and add the sprouts. Give a good stir and turn off the heat. Immediately drain them to a colander and pour 2 cups clean drinking water, not tap water. Leave them in the colander until you are done with the other preparation. 2. Rinse onion, tomatoes, carrot, green chili, cucumber, lemon, ginger and coriander leaves under running water. If you want you may also add 2 to 4 young mint leaves (optional). Peel and fine chop onion, tomatoes, cucumber and chili (skip for kids). Grate carrots and ginger (optional). Cut the lemon. Fine chop coriander leaves too. 3. Make the salad only when you are ready to serve as the veggies will let out moisture. Add the sprouts to a mixing bowl. Add all the prepared veggies, ginger, chilli (or crushed black pepper, herbs and salt. Squeeze in the lemon juice and mix well. Taste test and adjust salt. Transfer to serving bowl and top with corn flakes or roasted poha chivda. Serve sprouts salad immediately. If you want you may add little chaat masala or fresh coconut.

title: Steamed Cabbage Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams cabbage, 1 tbsp oil ghee, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch asafetida, 0.75 tsp ginger, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp red chilli powder, 0.75 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. Wash and shred or chop cabbage as desired. Drain the water completely, if you have rinsed it.. 2. Add it to a steam basket. Sprinkle some salt and turmeric. Lightly mix it. Spread evenly in the basket. Steam in a pressure cooker (without whistle) or in a steamer until tender. It takes around 5 to 7 minutes. 3. Heat a pan with oil. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, add red chilli and hing. My Mom also adds a pinch of urad dal (skinned black lentils) for a nutty flavor. 4. Add curry leaves. 5. Keep the flame to very low. When the leaves turn crisp, saute ginger garlic until the raw smell is gone. It takes about 40 to 50 seconds. Do not burn. 6. Add red chili powder and garam masala. Taste the steamed cabbage to test the salt levels. If you want more add it here. 7. Add steamed cabbage. 8. Stir and saute on a medium heat for 2 mins. Switch off the stove. It can also be sauteed further to make it more tasty, if you want. Serve steamed cabbage curry with rice or roti. If you want you may squeeze some lemon juice. Cabbage Curry Cabbage Stir Fry ... Cabbage Rice (Cab... Cabbage Sabzi ( C... Veg Manchurian Re... Cabbage Kootu

title: strawberry jam recipe | homemade low sugar strawberry jam
ingredients: 75 grams strawberry, 1 cup 250 grams sugar, 1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 750 grams strawberry and 1 cup sugar. add 3 cups (750 gm) of sugar if you are not diet conscious. 2. keeping the flame on medium, stir well till the sugar dissolves. 3. now add 1 tbsp lemon juice to prevent sugar from crystallising. 4. stir well and boil for 5 minutes. 5. keep stirring occasionally to prevent burning of strawberry. 6. continue to boil for another 10 minutes or till strawberry turns soft. 7. mash the strawberry well and till you get smooth consistency. 8. cook on medium flame till the mixture thickens and the foams disappear completely. 9. to check the consistency, dip a cool metal spoon into the mixture and make sure the mixture doesn't drop off. else continue to cook further. 10. transfer the jam into glass jar and cool completely. 11. finally, spread homemade low sugar strawberry jam over bread / chapathi and enjoy.

title: Stuffed brinjal fry
ingredients: 4 grams brinjals, 2 onions, 0.25 cup coconut, 0.25 cup peanuts, 2 tbsp sesame seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1.5 tbsp oil, 1.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.25 tsp red chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, salt, lemon juice, coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Dry roast ¼ cup peanuts on a low to medium flame until they begin to smell good. 2. Add ½ tsp cumin, 2 tbsp sesame seeds and ¼ cup chopped coconut and saute lightly without burning until the sesame seeds smell good. Transfer this to a plate to cool. 3. Add 1 ½ tsp oil to the hot pan. Fry ¾ cup sliced onion to golden. 4. Add 1 ½ tsp ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell goes off. You can also use 1 inch ginger and 2 to 3 garlic cloves. Saute them lightly without burning. 5. Switch off the stove. To the hot pan, add 1 to 1 ½ tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and salt as needed. You can also add half tsp garam masala or skip coriander powder and use only ¾ tsp garam masala if you like it. 6. Cool all the ingredients and add them to a blender. You can also add tamarind paste and very little jaggery. I prefer to use fresh lemon juice at the next step. 7. Blend it to a slightly coarse paste. If you have not used tamarind, add lemon juice and mix well. Taste this mixture and adjust the spice and salt levels. 8. Wash brinjals thoroughly. Keeping the stem intact, slit each of them partially. Keep them in water until used. Stuff each brinjal with the ground paste. Heat a pan with 1 tbsp oil, add curry leaves, hing and then the stuffed brinjals. Toss to coat them well with oil. 9. Cover and cook on a low flame. Flip or toss them to the sides that remain uncooked. If needed, you can sprinkle 1 to 2 tbsp water, if your brinjals are not tender. 10. When completely cooked, they will wilt off and the skin looks loose. Add coriander leaves. Keep them covered until you serve. Serve with rice, ghee, sambar or simple rasam .

title: Stuffed Capsicum | Stuffed Bell Pepper Recipe
ingredients: 3.5 medium capsicums, 3 medium potatoes, 1 medium carrot, 0.25 cup green peas, 2 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 garlic, 1 onion, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon chaat masala powder
instructions: 1. Dice & steam potatoes, carrots & peas until fork tender, without making them mushy. 2. Chop off the bell pepper tops and set aside. Deseed them as well. If you want you may level the bottom too so they stand well while cooking but make sure the bottom is still closed to hold the stuffing. 3. Cut & save the edible portion of the capsicum tops to use in the stuffing. 4. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and garlic. Let the garlic fry for 30 seconds. 5. Then add the onions & chopped bell pepper tops. Saute until golden. 6. Add chili powder, garam masala, kasuri methi. Mix & saute for 1 min. 7. Next add the steamed veggies and mash them. Mix together salt and coriander leaves. 8. Stuff the bell peppers with this potato mixture. Fry them in 1 tbsp oil stirring often. You can also cover and cook for sometime. 9. Baking: To bake them preheat the oven at 200 C or 400 F and bake for 8 to 15 mins depending on the size of capsicums. 10. Serve stuffed bell peppers as a appetizer or side in your meal.

title: stuffed idli recipe | aloo masala stuffed rava idli | potato stuffed idli
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 tsp chana dal, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 0.5 onion, 2 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 potato, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tsp oil, 1.5 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.75 cup curd, 1 cup water, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tsp oil and splutter 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp cumin, 1 tsp chana dal, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 2. add ½ onion, 2 chilli and ½ tsp ginger paste. 3. saute slightly until the onions shrink. 4. now add ½ tsp turmeric and ½ tsp salt. saute for a minute until the raw flavour of turmeric goes away. 5. add 3 potato and mix well. 6. mix and mash until everything is well combined. 7. further, add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. aloo masala stuffing is ready. keep aside. 8. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil and roast 1½ cup rava. 9. roast until the rava turns aromatic. 10. cool completely and transfer to a large bowl. 11. add ½ tsp salt and ¾ cup curd. mix well. 12. add 1 cup water or as required and mix to form idli batter consistency batter. 13. rest for 10 minutes, or until the rava absorbs the water. 14. further, add ½ tsp eno and 2 tbsp water. 15. mix gently, forming a frothy idli batter. 16. fill half the greased idli mould with idli batter. 17. place a small ball sized aloo masala. make sure to adjust the size of aloo masala based on the size of idli stand. 18. cover the aloo masala with idli batter. 19. cover and steam for 15 minutes or until the idli is cooked completely. 20. finally, enjoy stuffed idli with chutney or as it is.

title: sugar free modak recipe | no sugar no jaggery modak
ingredients: 2 tsp ghee clarified butter, 2 tbsp cashew kaju, 2 tbsp almonds badam, 2 tbsp raisins kishmish, 1 tsp poppy seeds kush khus, 0.5 cup dates khajoor, 0.25 cup milk, 0.5 cup milk powder, 0.25 cup coconut, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1.25 cup water, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp ghee clarified butter, 1 cup rice flour
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 1¼ cup water, ¼ tsp salt and ½ tsp ghee. 2. get the water to boil on medium flame. 3. keeping the flame on low and add 1 cup fine rice flour. 4. mix the flour well with water. do not worry if the dough is dry. 5. turn off the flame, cover and allow to rest for 3-5 minutes. 6. now transfer the rice flour mixture into large mixing bowl. 7. when the flour is still hot, knead with your hand to form a smooth and soft dough. 8. prepare the modak and place in steamer leaving gaps in between. 9. cover and steam the modak for 10 minutes or till the shiny texture appears on them. 10. finally, offer no sugar - no jaggery modak to lord ganesha and celebrate ganesh chaturthi.

title: suji besan cutlet recipe | rava besan cutlet | suji besan ball snacks
ingredients: 0.5 cup suji rava semolina, 0.5 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp salt, 1.5 cup water, 2 tsp oil, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 onion, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp lemon juice, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take ½ cup suji, ½ cup besan, ¼ tsp turmeric and ¾ tsp salt. 2. add 1½ cup water and mix using a whisk. 3. prepare a smooth lump free batter. 4. now in a large kadai heat 2 tsp oil and saute ¼ tsp ajwain and pinch hing. 5. pour in prepared suji besan batter and mix well. 6. stir continuously until the mixture thickens. 7. continue to stir until the mixture holds shape and starts to separate from the pan. 8. transfer the dough into a large mixing bowl and allow to cool slightly. 9. further add ½ onion, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, ½ tsp ginger paste, 2 tbsp coriander and 1 tsp lemon juice. 10. mix well forming a soft dough. 11. now grease hand with oil and prepare small ball sized. 12. deep fry in hot oil stirring occasionally. or preheat and bake at 180 degree celcius for 25 minutes. 13. fry until the pakora turns golden and crisp. 14. finally, enjoy suji besan cutlet / suji besan balls with tomato sauce.

title: suji ka paratha recipe | sooji paratha | rava paratha
ingredients: 2 cup water, 1 tsp oil, 0.25 tsp salt, 1 cup rava suji semolina, 0.5 cup wheat flour atta, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain carom seeds, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, oil for roasting
instructions: 1. firstly take cooked cooked rava into a large mixing bowl along with ½ cup wheat flour. 2. also add ¼ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp ajwain, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, ¼ tsp salt and ½ tsp ginger paste. 3. knead the dough for 5 minutes to a smooth and soft dough. 4. pinch a ball sized dough and flatten slightly. 5. dust with wheat flour and roll gently. 6. now on a hot tawa place the rolled paratha and cook for a minute. 7. furthermore, when the base is partly cooked, flip the suji paratha. 8. also, spread ½ tsp oil / ghee and press slightly both the sides. 9. flip again once or twice till both the sides are cooked properly. 10. finally, serve suji ki paratha / rava paratha with dal, raita and pickle.

title: Suji Ke Namak Pare Recipe | Crispy Semolina Salt Crispies
ingredients: 2 cup rava semolina suji, 0.5 tsp ajwain, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, 2 tsp sugar, 2 tbsp ghee, water, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixer jar take 2 cup rava and grind to a fine powder. 2. transfer the powdered rava to a large bowl. 3. add ½ tsp ajwain, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, 2 tsp sugar, and 2 tbsp ghee. 4. crumble and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 5. now add water as required and start to knead the dough. 6. add water in small batches as rava absorbs more water. 7. prepare a smooth and soft dough. 8. rest the dough for at least 20 minutes. 9. now pinch a ball-sized dough and dust it with flour. 10. roll them into round / square shape making slightly thick than chapathi. 11. take a knife / pizza cutter and cut them into diamond shape. 12. drop the namak pare into the hot oil. or bake in preheated oven at 180-degree celsius for 15-20 minutes. 13. flip it over so that it cooks on both sides. 14. fry them to nice golden brown on low-medium flame. drain them on a kitchen towel to remove excess oil. 15. finally, allow suji namak pare to cool to room temperature and store them into an airtight container.

title: suji pakora recipe | instant rava pakoda | semolina pakoda fritters
ingredients: 0.75 cup rava semolina suji, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 2 tbsp onion, pinch hing asafoetida, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, oil for frying
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take ¾ cup rava. 2. add ¼ cup curd, 1 chilli, ½ tsp ginger paste, few curry leaves and 2 tbsp coriander. 3. further add 1 tsp cumin, 2 tbsp onion, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 4. mix well making sure the batter is slightly watery. 5. rest for 10 minutes, or until the rava absorbs and swells. 6. after 10 minutes, the batter will turn slightly thick. 7. now add ¼ tsp baking soda and mix gently. 8. drop a small ball sized batter into hot oil. 9. stir occasionally, frying on medium flame until the pakoda turns golden and crisp. 10. drain off the pakoda over a kitchen towel to absorb excess oil. 11. finally, enjoy suji pakoda recipe with tomato sauce or coconut chutney.

title: suji roll recipe | suji ke roll | aloo paneer rava roll nasta | sooji ke roll
ingredients: 1.5 cup rava semolina, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp chilli flakes, 0.25 cup curd, water, 1 cup paneer cottage cheese, 1 cup potato aloo, 1 carrot, 3 tbsp cabbage, 2 tbsp capsicum, 0.5 onion, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 chilli, 0.75 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 1 tbsp green chutney, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.75 tsp cumin, 1 tsp sesame, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a mixi blend and powder 1½ cup rava. 2. transfer the powdered rava to a large bowl. 3. add ¼ tsp baking soda, ½ tsp salt, 1 tsp chilli flakes and mix well. 4. also, add ¼ cup curd and combine well. 5. now add water as required and start to knead. 6. knead to a smooth and soft dough. 7. cover and rest for 20 minutes. 8. meanwhile, to prepare the stuffing, in a large bowl take 1 cup paneer and 1 cup potato. 9. also, add 1 carrot, 3 tbsp cabbage, 2 tbsp capsicum, ½ onion, 2 tbsp coriander and 2 chilli. 10. additionally, add ¾ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp chaat masala and mix well. 11. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. stuffing is ready. 12. after the dough has rested for 20 minutes, knead it again slightly. 13. now roll to slightly thick thickness. 14. spread 1 tbsp green chutney uniformly. 15. top with prepared aloo paneer stuffing and spread uniformly. 16. roll tight making sure it is well secured. 17. now cut into half and place in the steamer. if you have enough room in the steamer, then you can place the whole log. 18. steam for 15 minutes, or until the rava layer is cooked fully. 19. now slice into thick pieces and keep aside. 20. prepare the tempering, by heating 2 tbsp oil. 21. splutter, 1 tsp mustard, ¾ tsp cumin, 1 tsp sesame and few curry leaves. 22. place the sliced roll and roast on medium flame. 23. flip over and roast both sides. 24. finally, enjoy suji roll with green chutney or tamarind chutney.

title: Suji Sandwich Recipe | No Bread Masaledar Aloo Stuffed Sooji Nasta
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 1.5 cup rava semolina suji, 1 cup curd, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.5 tsp eno fruit salt, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 0.5 onion, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 potato, 2 tbsp coriander, green chutney, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 2. splutter the tempering. 3. now add 1½ cup rava and roast until the rava turns aromatic. 4. cool completely, and transfer to the large bowl. 5. add 1 cup curd, ½ tsp salt, and mix well. 6. also, add ½ cup water and mix forming a lump-free thick batter. 7. cover and rest for 10 minutes or until the rava absorb well. 8. now add 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp carrot, and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 9. further, just before steaming add ½ tsp eno fruit salt and 3 tbsp water. 10. mix well forming a frothy batter. 11. transfer the batter to a tray lined with baking paper. 12. steam for 30 minutes or until it is cooked fully. 13. cool completely, and cut into pieces. 14. firstly, in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, pinch hing, and 2 chilli. 15. splutter the tempering. 16. add ½ onion and saute well. 17. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 18. further, add 3 potatoes and mix well. 19. now add 2 tbsp coriander and mix. aloo stuffing is ready. 20. slice the suji cubes and spread green chutney. 21. also, place a ball-sized potato masala and spread it uniformly. 22. cover with another slice of suji base. 23. roast on the pan making sure both sides are golden and crisp. 24. finally, suji aloo sandwich is ready to enjoy with tomato sauce.

title: sukha puri recipe | stuffed sukha poori chaat | sukha masala puri
ingredients: 2 potato aloo, mashed, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 1 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp dry mango powder aamchur, 1 tsp green chutney, 0.25 tsp salt, 7 puri golgappe puri, pinch of chaat masala, pinch salt, handful sev, pinch of kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 1 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.25 lemon juice
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 boiled and mashed potato. 2. add ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp aamchur, 1 tsp chaat masala, 1 tsp green chutney and ¼ tsp salt. 3. mix all the ingredients well. aloo mixture ready. 4. now in a small plate take 7 puri and make a hole in the centre. 5. stuff in prepared aloo mixture. do not overstuff the mixture. 6. also sprinkle, chaat masala, salt and chilli powder. 7. add in fine sev into each puri. 8. further, garnish with chopped coriander leaves and squeeze ¼ lemon juice. 9. finally, serve sukha puri immediately after pani puri or enjoy as it is.

title: Sundal Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup chana, 0.625 cup water for cooking, 1.5 tablespoon Ghee, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 red chilies, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 Pinch hing, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 2 tbsps coconut
instructions: 1. Wash and soak half cup chana in lots of water for 8 hours or overnight. Drain the water and pressure cook for 3 to 4 whistles on a medium flame using ½ to ¾ cup water. If using instant pot pressure cook for 18 minutes. Chana needs to be soft, tender and hold the shape. 2. Add 1 ½ tablespoon ghee to a hot pan. When the ghee becomes hot, add ½ teaspoon mustard seeds,½ teaspoon cumin seeds and 1 teaspoon urad dal. We use desi cow’s ghee for tempering especially if offering to Goddess Lakshmi. 3. Allow mustard, cumin to crackle. Saute dal until golden. 4. Add 1 sprig curry leaves, ½ teaspoon ginger (optional) and 1 broken dried red chili. Saute until the curry leaves turn crisp. Add a pinch of hing. I do not use ginger. 5. Drain water/stock completely from cooked chana to a bowl and add the chana to the pan along with ⅛ teaspoon turmeric and ¼ teaspoon salt. 6. Add 2 to 4 tablespoons fresh grated coconut. I blended coarsely few freshly broken coconut chunks in a chutney jar and used. We use freshly broken coconut and do not use the one which is already offered during pooja or one used for regular cooking purposes. 7. Mix every well and switch off.

title: Sunnundalu Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 1 tablespoons sugar, 4 green cardamoms, 0.25 cup ghee, 1 cup urad dal, 0.75 cup jaggery, 4 green cardamoms, 6 tablespoons ghee
instructions: 1. Optional – You may skip this and the next step. Add urad dal to a bowl and rinse well thrice with lots of water. Quickly drain to a colander. Shake the colander well to get rid of any excess water. 2. Spread it on a clean cloth and pat dry. Alternately you may spread it on a tray and fan/air dry it for 10 mins. 3. Without Rinsing Lentils: If you did not rinse the lentils, start on a medium low heat & roast for 5 mins. Later reduce to completely low and roast until aromatic, deep golden and crunchy. Turn off the heat. 4. With Rinsed Lentils: Add the rinsed urad dal to a heavy bottom pan and begin to roast on a high flame, stirring often for 5 mins. Then reduce the heat to medium and roast for 2 mins. Later reduce the heat to low and roast for another 3 to 4 mins. 5. Urad dal should turn golden but not burnt. (Check the pictures). You should smell it nutty, aromatic and the dal should be crunchy. Cool the lentils completely. 6. Test the lentils: Eat a few lentils to make sure they are cooked well. You should not feel them raw or undercooked. 7. Later add to a grinder jar and make a powder that is slightly coarse like fine semolina or chiroti rava. Make sure it is not too coarse nor too fine. Take 1/8 teaspoon of this mixture and taste it. You should feel a very fine crunch but not too coarse. 8. With Sugar: Transfer the urad dal flour to a mixing bowl. Add sugar & cardamoms to the same grinder. Make a super fine powder and add that with the urad dal powder. You may want to start with lesser quantity of sugar, taste test and add more. Mix and taste test, adjust the sugar as required. You can also store it in an air tight jar for several months and make ladoos later. 9. With Jaggery: Keep the urad dal powder in the grinder. Add chopped or grated jaggery (start with lesser quantity) to the grinder and grind for a few minutes until well blended. Taste test and add more if you want. Transfer to a bowl. 10. Heat ghee in a small pot until it melts and becomes slightly hot. Start by adding 4 teaspoons to the sunnundalu mixture. Mix only a portion of the flour with the ghee until you get a fine wet sand like texture. 11. Take 3 to 4 tablespoons (a small handful) mixture with your fingers and begin to press down into your palm with your fingers to shape the ladoo. It takes a few minutes to form a ladoo. If the ladoo doesn’t bind, it means you should add more ghee. If it is too soggy and loosely shaped, it means it requires more flour. 12. Add a few teaspoons of ghee at one time to the mixture and form balls. The jaggery version takes a little more ghee than the sugar ones. 13. Cool them completely and if you want you press them again to shape properly and smooth. Store sunnundalu in a steel or glass jar. Store at room temperature for a month and in the refrigerator for 3 months.

title: Sweet Appam Recipe (Banana Appam)
ingredients: 1 cup whole wheat flour, 1 banana, 2 tablespoons rice flour, 2 tablespoons coconut, 0.5 cup jaggery, 0.5 cup water, 0.125 teaspoon soda, 2 pinches cardamom powder, water, 3.5 tablespoons ghee
instructions: 1. Heat half cup water in a pan. Switch off and add grated jaggery or karupatti. I used raw jaggery. 2. Stir and dissolve it completely. 3. This is the ripe banana I used. It weighed about 110 grams (with skin on) and was 6 inches long. We are adding banana only to enhance the sweet flavor. Using too much banana may make them very dense, so giving you more details about size and weight. You can reduce the quantity of banana. 4. Mash the banana very well. 5. Filter the jaggery syrup. 6. Add coconut and cardamom powder. 7. Add rice flour, wheat flour and pinch of baking soda (optional). Mix very well and make a thick batter using more water as needed. 8. Make sure the batter is of thick but pouring consistency and is lump free. Whisk very well for a minute. 9. Grease your paniyaram pan very well. Add ghee as desired and heat it. 10. Fill the moulds upto 3/4 level with batter. 11. Cover and cook for few minutes. Check if the base if firm and is cooked to golden. Then flip them with a skewer, chop stick or wooden spoon and cook until completely cooked or the edges turn brown. 12. You can flip them again to make them slightly crisp. Serve sweet appam hot or cold as desired.

title: Sweet Corn Chaat Recipe - 2 Ways
ingredients: 4 cup sweet corn, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, water, 2 cup sweet corn, 2 tsp butter, pinch salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 cup sweet corn, 0.5 tsp butter, pinch salt, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.25 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp chaat masala, 0.25 tsp kala namak, 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp cucumber, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp tomato, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a vessel, get the water to a boil. Add 4 cups of sweet corn; you can use either fresh or frozen sweet corn, the boiling time will change accordingly. 2. Add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt and boil for 5 minutes or until the sweet corn softens. 3. Drain off and keep aside. 4. To prepare the butter corn chaat, take 2 cup boiled sweet corn. 5. Add 2 tsp butter, pinch salt and 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 6. Finally, enjoy Butter Corn Chaat when it's warm. 7. To prepare the masala corn chaat, take 2 cup boiled sweet corn. 8. Add ½ tsp butter, pinch salt, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ¼ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp chaat masala, ¼ tsp kala namak. Mix well, making sure the butter melts. 9. Also add 2 tbsp carrot, 2 tbsp cucumber, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp onion, 2 tbsp tomato, 1 inch ginger, 1 chilli, finely chopped and 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 10. Finally, enjoy Masala Corn Chaat when it's warm.

title: Sweet Corn Soup Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups sweet corn, 0.5 cup sweet corn, 3 sprigs spring onions, 0.29166666666666663 cup carrots, 0.25 cup French beans, 3.25 cups water, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.625 tablespoon garlic, 0.625 tablespoon ginger, 1 green chili, 1.5 tablespoon corn starch, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon crushed pepper, 1 teaspoon vinegar
instructions: 1. Pour 1 tsp oil to wok and heat it on a high flame. Add ¾ tablespoon fine chopped ginger, ¾ tablespoon fine chopped garlic and 1 small deseeded and chopped green chilli. If you don’t prefer your corn soup to be hot, they skip the chilli. 2. Let the ginger garlic fry for 30 seconds. Do not burn. Then immediately add spring onion whites and saute for 1 minute. 3. Add 1/3 cup fine chopped carrots and ¼ cup fine chopped French beans. Saute for 1 minute. 4. Add sweet corn and sweet corn paste made from half cup sweet corn kernels blended with ¼ cup water. 5. Pour 3½ cups water and mix well. If you prefer thicker soup cut down the water to 3 cups. Add salt and sugar. Bring this to a rolling boil on a medium high heat. 6. Meanwhile add 1 ½ tablespoon corn starch to a bowl. Pour ¼ cup water to it. 7. Stir and make a smooth lump free corn slurry. 8. When you see the soup boiling, Let it boil for 2 mins. Then stir the corn slurry with a spoons and pour it to the boiling soup. Mix well. 9. Reduce the heat to medium and Let it simmer until the sweet corn kernels are soft and tender. As the soup boils you will begin to see some froth on top. If you prefer you may skim this with a spoon and discard. 10. After a few minutes you will see the sweet corn soup begins to turn thick. It will thicken upon cooling. So when it is of pouring consistency and slightly thick turn off. This is the consistency I turn off. 11. Taste test and add more salt if required. Add 1 teaspoon vinegar (optional) and ½ teaspoon crushed white or black pepper. Turn off the heat and add 2 tablespoons of spring onion greens. Serve sweet corn soup hot or warm with a bowl of noodles, pasta or bread toast. 12. I used carrots, beans, peas, spring onions and sweet corn kernels. I used potato as well to thicken the soup. It is optional and you may skip if you prefer the Indo Chinese style. 13. Wash, peel and chop carrots. Also chop spring onions, beans, garlic and ginger. You will need ¼ cup carrots, ¼ cup French beans (4 to 6), ¼ cup green peas, 1 small potato, 2 sprigs of spring onions, greens and whites separated. ½ to ¾ tablespoon each ginger & garlic. 14. Heat 1 teaspoon butter or oil in a pot. Add ginger garlic and saute until it turns aromatic. You can also skip ginger if you do not prefer. 15. Then add spring onion whites, peas, sweet corn, potatoes (optional) & beans. Saute them on a high flame for 2 to 3 mins.

title: Sweet Corn Soup Recipe | Chinese Corn Soup
ingredients: 1 cup sweet corn, 2 tsp olive oil, 1 inch ginger, 3 cloves 3 cloves (finely chopped), 2 chilli, 2 tbsp onion, 0.5 carrot, 0.5 capsicum, 3 tbsp sweet corn, 1 tsp salt, 5 cup water
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a blender, take 1 cup of sweet corn and grind it to a smooth paste. Keep aside. 2. In a large kadai, heat 2 tsp olive oil. Add 1 inch ginger, 3 cloves, 2 chilli and saute well. 3. Also, add 2 tbsp of onion and saute slightly. 4. Further add ½ carrot, ½ capsicum, 3 tbsp sweet corn and 1 tsp salt. Stir-fry till the vegetables turn crunchy. 5. Also, add sweet corn paste and cook well. 6. Pour in 5 cup water and mix well. 7. Boil for 5 minutes, making sure to take off the foam. 8. Add in corn flour slurry and cook till the soup thickens. 9. Now add 2 tbsp spring onion and mix well. 10. Finally, enjoy the Sweet Corn Soup Recipe piping hot.

title: Sweet Paniyaram (Bellam Ponganalu)
ingredients: 2 cups idli batter, 0.25 cup jaggery, 0.25 cup coconut, 2 pinches elaichi, 3 tbsp rice flour, Ghee
instructions: 1. I used 2 cups of fermented thick idli batter which was prepared in 1:2 ratio (dal:idli rava). You can use idli or dosa batter made with any ratio of dal, lentil, poha and methi. Do not use sour or runny batter. 2. Add ¼ cup grated or powdered jaggery. You can also use 2 tbsps more if your batter is thick enough. I have used thickened jaggery syrup made from raw jaggery. Note that thin jaggery syrup cannot be used here. You can also use palm jaggery, coconut palm sugar or sugar or raw jaggery. Make sure your jaggery is free from stones and debris. 3. Add ¼ cup grated coconut. 4. Add 2 generous pinches of green cardamom powder. Mix well. 5. If your batter turns thin or your batter is made of only 1:2 (dal:rice) ratio, then add 2 to 4 tbsp rice flour. I used 4 tbsps rice flour. Adding too much rice flour can make them hard, use only as needed. Using rice flour helps to make them crisp and thicken the batter slightly. 6. Mix well. The batter consistency must be thick but of pouring consistency like shown in the picture.

title: Sweet Pongal | Chakkara Pongali | Sakkarai Pongal
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 0.75 cup rice, 4 cups water, 1 cup jaggery, 0.5 cup water, 0.625 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 15 cashew nuts, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((crushed, optional)), 2 tablespoons coconut, 2 tablespoon raisins, 1 pinch edible camphor
instructions: 1. Add ½ cup moong dal to a heavy bottom pan or pressure cooker. 2. Dry roast it on a medium flame stirring often till the dal turns fragrant & light golden. This takes about 5 to 7 minutes. 3. Next add ¾ cup rice to the same pan. Pour water and rinse both of them at least 2 to 3 times. Be careful as the pot is hot. Drain the water completely. 4. Pour 4 cups water. Cover the cooker and pressure cook for 3 whistles on a medium flame. If cooking in pot, pour more water. 5. You can also put the rice & dal in a bowl and pour water. Place the bowl in a pressure cooker. Pressure cook for 4 whistles. 6. To make jaggery syrup, transfer ½ cup water & jaggery to a small pan. You can also skip this step & add clean jaggery or sugar directly to the mashed rice. But the flavors won't be so good. 7. On a low flame stir and melt it. Boil until slightly thick & sticky. Do not over cook as this turns too thick after cooling. Set this aside. 8. When the pressure releases, open the cooker and mash the rice & dal lightly. Both should be soft cooked. 9. Next filter jaggery syrup directly to the cooked rice & dal. If you prefer less sweetness in your sweet pongal you can add less syrup check and add later if needed. Add ½ to ¾ teaspoon cardamom powder. 10. Mix and cook on a medium flame till the jaggery syrup blends well with rice. It should begin to bubble up well. Turn off the stove. 11. Heat a pan with 2 to 3 tablespoons ghee on a medium flame. 12. Add 2 tablespoons coconut pieces (optional) and fry them till they turn golden & aromatic. Transfer only the coconut pieces to a plate. 13. Add 15 cashew nuts to the ghee. Slightly crush 2 cloves & add them. Crushing prevents them from spluttering. Fry until the cashews turn golden. 14. Then add 2 tablespoon raisins and fry until they turn plump. 15. Off the stove and add 1 small pinch edible camphor (optional). Pour this to the sweet pongal & mix well. 16. Chakkara pongal is ready. Offer to the Goddess Lakshmi or Durga following your family customs. 17. Rinse moong dal and rice separately in lots of water. Drain them & set aside. 18. Press saute button & pour 1 tablespoon ghee to the steel insert of your instant pot. 19. When the ghee becomes slightly hot, fry cashews until slightly golden. Press cancel and add raisins. Fry them in the residual heat so they don’t burn. When they become plump remove them to a small bowl. 20. Press saute button again and transfer drained moong dal. Saute for 3 to 4 minutes until you begin to get a nice aroma. Do not let the dal brown. 21. Press cancel & add drained rice. Pour 4 cups water (or 3 cups water & 1 cup milk). Give a good mix & deglaze if needed. 22. It is important to use a long legged trivet here. Place it in the IP and then a medium sized bowl over it. Pour ¾ cup water to it. Cover the bowl. 23. Close the Instant pot with the lid. Move the steam release handle to sealing position. 24. Press pressure cook button and set the timer to 10 minutes. Once done, let the pressure release on its own. 25. Open the lid and remove the bowl of water carefully. Immediately add finely grated jaggery to it and stir well until it dissolves completely. Use fine jaggery so it dissolves faster. 26. Rice and moong dal has to be soft cooked & mushy. I don’t mash it at this stage since it is already mushy. 27. Strain the jaggery syrup through a strainer to the soft cooked rice & dal. If your jaggery syrup is clean then you may pour it directly without filtering. 28. Also add cardamom powder & give a gentle stir. Press saute button on the IP. Cook for a few minutes until all the ingredients blends well. This only takes around 1 to 2 minutes. 29. Then add 2 to 3 tablespoons ghee and press cancel. Garnish your Sweet Pongal with cashews and raisins.

title: Sweet Pongal Recipe Temple Style | Chakkara - Sakkarai Pongal
ingredients: 1 tsp ghee, 0.5 cup moong dal, 1 cup sona masuri rice, 4 cup water, 0.5 cup ghee, 2 tbsp cashew, 2 tbsp raisins, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 2 cup jaggery, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Firstly, heat 1 tsp ghee in a pressure cooker and roast ½ cups moong dal until it turns aromatic. 2. Add 1 cup sona masuri rice and continue to roast on low flame. 3. Add 4 cup water and pressure cook for 4 whistles. 4. Once the pressure is released, make sure to make the dal and rice mushy. Keep aside. 5. In a pan heat ½ cup ghee. Add 2 tbsp cashews, 2 tbsp raisins, and 5 cloves. 6. Roast until the cashew turns golden brown. Keep aside. 7. In a saucepan take 2 cup jaggery and 1 cup water. 8. Stir and dissolve making sure the jaggery is well combined. 9. Boil till the jaggery syrup turns a slightly sticky consistency. 10. Add in fried nuts and boil for 2 minutes. 11. Now pour in the mixture and mix gently. 12. Mix until everything is well combined adjusting the consistency as required. 13. Finally, enjoy Chakkara - Sakkarai Pongal Recipe or Sweet Pongal Recipe offering to God.

title: Sweet potato baby food
ingredients: 0.5 lb sweet potatoes, 0.25 cup rice, 1.25 cup water, 0.5 cup sweet potatoes, 0.5 teaspoon ghee, 0.25 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 1.5 tablespoon jaggery
instructions: 1. Rinse the sweet potatoes well and boil them until tender. If using an Instant pot, pour water inside the steel insert and place the sweet potatoes on the steam rack. Pressure cook them for 5 mins if using medium sized one, 10 mins for larger ones. 2. When the sweet potatoes are done, cool and peel them. Puree in a blender with some water or breast milk. Stage 1 baby food should be soft. 3. Rinse rice well in a bowl. Soak for about 30 minutes. Drain the water and pour fresh water. 4. If using instant pot, Pour 1 cup water to the steel insert. Place the bowl over a trivet and cover the bowl. Press pressure cook button and Set the timer to 10 mins. If cooking in a traditional pressure cooker, cook for 3 whistles on a medium flame. 5. When the pressure releases, remove the bowl and mash the rice well. If needed you can add water or soup to bring it to a consistency. If you prefer a smoother texture, then puree the sweet potato baby food. 6. Add jaggery to the hot food along with ghee and mix well. If your jaggery has debris, then dissolve it in little boiling or hot water. Then pour this to the cooked sweet potato baby food. 7. Cool the baby food first and then serve.

title: sweet potato chutney recipe | genasina chutney recipe
ingredients: 1 sweet potato, 3 tsp oil, 1 tbsp urad dal, 1 tbsp chana dal, 3 red chilli, few curry leaves, 0.5 cup coconut, ball sized tamarind, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 cup water, 3 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, peel the skin of the sweet potato. you can use white or brown sweet potato. 2. grate the sweet potato uniformly. keep aside. 3. in a pan heat 3 tsp oil and saute 1 tbsp urad dal, 1 tbsp chana dal, 3 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 4. add in grated sweet potato and saute for 5 minutes. 5. saute until the sweet potato is cooked well. 6. cool completely and transfer to the blender. 7. add in ½ cup coconut, small ball sized tamarind, ½ tsp salt and ½ cup water. 8. blend to smooth paste adding water as required. 9. now prepare the tempering by heating 3 tsp oil and splutter ½ tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal and few curry leaves. 10. pour the tempering over the chutney and mix well. 11. finally, enjoy sweet potato chutney with hot steamed rice or dosa.

title: Sweet Potato Curry Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups sweet potatoes, 1 cup onions, 1 cup tomatoes, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 cup thick coconut milk
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a pot. Add ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds & ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. When they splutter add 1 sprig curry leaves & a pinch of hing (optional). When the leaves turn crisp, add 1 teaspoon grated ginger (or paste) and fry till aromatic, for about 40 seconds. 2. Add 1 cup fine chopped onions, 1 slit green chili and ¼ teaspoon salt. Saute the onions on a medium heat. Meanwhile, wash, peel and cube sweet potatoes. I have used 2 heaped cups diced sweet potatoes. Add them to a bowl of salted water (2 cups water with half teaspoon salt) till used else they get discolored. 3. When the onions turn transparent add chopped tomatoes or tomato puree. Sprinkle ¼ teaspoon turmeric. Saute well for 2 mins & simmer until the tomatoes breakdown and become mushy. 4. Add ½ to 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 to 1 ½ teaspoon coriander powder & ½ to ¾ teaspoon red chili powder. Stir and saute for a minute. 5. Add cubed sweet potatoes to the pot. 6. Stir well. 7. Add the water we used to soak the sweet potatoes just enough to immerse them. Bring the water to a rapid boil. 8. Reduce the heat. Simmer and cook till sweet potatoes turn tender and cooked fully but not mushy. Keep stirring in between so they cook evenly. Taste test and add more salt if required. If you want less watery curry, evaporate the excess moisture on a high flame. If you want you may add 4 tablespoons thick coconut milk. Turn off the heat after adding the coconut milk. Serve sweet potato curry with rice & ghee or with roti, paratha or dosa.

title: Sweet Potato Stir Fry
ingredients: 2 cups sweet potato, 1 onion, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric
instructions: 1. Peel the sweet potatoes. If you have large ones cut them to half lengthwise. Then slice them thin like potato chips. Add them to slightly salted water immediately. This step avoids them from discoloring and burning. Chop onions and ginger. 2. Heat a nonstick pan with oil. Then add mustard & cumin. When they splutter add grated ginger and curry leaves. Fry till the ginger smells good. 3. Add onions, sprinkle salt. Fry till they turn lightly golden. Don’t need to brown them. 4. Drain off the water completely from sweet potatoes and add them to the pan. Sprinkle turmeric. 5. Fry for about 3 to 5 minutes, stirring evenly. Cover and cook on a low flame till they are cooked fully and turn tender. Keep stirring in between. 6. Ensure the sweet potatoes are cooked fully before sprinkling red chili powder, garam masala and little more salt. Adjust spices and salt now. 7. Stir and fry till the masala is blended well. If the sweet potatoes look too dry then you may add little oil at this stage. Do not over fry otherwise the masala gets burnt. Garnish sweet potato stir fry with coriander leaves. This time we served it with plain rice and tamarind rasam .

title: Tamarind Chutney Recipe (Imli Chutney)
ingredients: 0.5 cup tamarind, 0.5 cup jaggery, 0.5 cup dates, 0.75 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon roasted saunf powder, 0.5 teaspoon roasted jeera powder, 0.5 teaspoon ginger powder, 0.5 teaspoon kala namak, 0.75 teaspoon roasted coriander powder, 2 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon ghee, 0.125 teaspoon hing
instructions: 1. To a bowl, add tamarind, dates, jaggery, 2 cups water and red chilli powder. 2. Pour 1 cup water to your pressure cooker, place a rack/ trivet. Keep the bowl of ingredients inside. Cover the bowl. Cover the pressure cooker. 3. Pressure cook for 3 whistles on a medium heat. If using Instant pot, press the pressure cook button and set the timer to 9 to 10 minutes. 4. Once done, wait for the pressure to release completely. Remove the bowl and add all of the spice powders. Mix and cover immediately so the flavors will be absorbed. 5. Cool this completely. Using a masher, mash it very well or blend in a blender. Place a strainer over a large bowl and add the cooked tamarind paste. Pass it through the sieve. Taste test and add more salt and spice if needed. 6. Store tamarind chutney in a clear jar and refrigerate. 7. Optional: Heat half tsp ghee in a pot. Add hing to it. 8. Pour water to the pot. Deseed the tamarind and dates if any. Add them to the pot. Also add jaggery. 9. Boil all the 3 ingredients until dates & tamarind turns very soft. If the mixture turns too thick then you can add up another half cup water. Some kind of dates and tamarind need more water. 10. Add red chili powder, salt, saunf powder, coriander powder, ginger powder and jeera powder. Mix all of these and continue to cook further for 2 to 3 mins until the flavor of the spices comes out. 11. Cool this mixture completely. Check if any tamarind or dates seeds are left in the pulp. Discard them. 12. Add the entire mixture to a blender jar and blend until smooth. 13. I added about half cup boiled and cooled water at this stage to help blend smoothly. 14. Place a sieve or colander over a large bowl. Then transfer the pureed dates tamarind chutney to it and pass through. 15. You will get smooth thick tamarind chutney. Add this to a clean moist free glass bottle and store in refrigerator for up to 6 weeks. 16. This chutney turns thick due to the addition of dates. So you will need to bring to consistency before using in chaats. Scoop a few tbsps from the bottle to a bowl and add some hot water. Mix up well. This will refresh your tamarind chutney.

title: tamarind chutney recipe | imli chutney | sweet tamarind dates chutney
ingredients: 0.5 cup seedless tamarind imli, 0.5 cup seedless dates khajoor, 2 cups water, 0.5 cup jaggery gud, 0.5 tsp fennel powder saunf powder, 1 tsp coriander powder dhaniya powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder jeera powder, 1 tsp kashmiri red chili powder lal mirch powder, 0.5 tsp dry ginger powder sonth- saunth powder, salt to taste
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take equal proportion of tamarind, dates and jaggery (½ cup each) 2. further pour 2 cups of water. 3. mix and boil the mixture for atelast 10 minutes. 4. also add ½ tsp fennel powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp kashmiri red chili powder, ½ tsp dry ginger powder and salt to taste. 5. mix and mash tamarind and dates. 6. furthermore, simmer the mixture for 2 minutes. 7. later allow the mixture to cool completly. 8. now transfer the dates, tamarind mixture to the mixer and blend smooth puree. 9. further strain the puree. 10. and transfer the prepared khajoor imli ka chutney into airtight jar and store it in the refrigerator.. 11. finally, use the prepared tamarind date chutney in preparing sev puri, bhel puri or pani puri.

title: Tamarind Paste Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams tamarind pulp, 3 cups water
instructions: 1. Firstly clean tamarind to remove any unwanted particles if you find any like shells or strings. Heat 2 to 3 cups of water in a pot. Water should be hot not warm. Layer your tamarind in a wide bowl and pour half of the hot water. It must be fully immersed in water. If not pour more as needed. Do not use excess water else you will end up with hot splashes while simmering. 2. Leave it for about 45 minutes to 1 hour. Tamarind turns soft. 3. A lot of people blend this with additional water. However I prefer not to blend. So showing you how I do it. Squeeze the tamarind well with your hand. 4. Pass this through a strainer to another heavy bottom non reactive pot. you might find stones or shells of the tamarind or seeds, discard them. Do not use a wide pan. 5. Add the pulp back to the tamarind pot, add more water that was set aside and squeeze it again and repeat passing the left over pulp through the filter. You can do this once more if you wish. 6. Do not overdo, you might get mud or stones, just discard the pulp. or you can even make thin tamarind water and refrigerate to use the next 2 days.

title: Tamarind Rasam without Tomato
ingredients: 1 tablespoon ghee, 3.25 cups water, lemon sized tamarind, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 1 pinch Hing, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 4.5 garlic cloves, 2 tablespoons jaggery, 1.5 tablespoons coriander seeds, 0.5 tablespoons cumin, 0.5 teaspoon pepper corn, 0.25 teaspoon methi, 1 dry chili
instructions: 1. Heat a pot and dry roast on a low to medium flame - methi seeds, coriander seeds, pepper corn and red chilli until the methi smells good. 2. Then add cumin and fry for 30 seconds. 3. Cool and make a fine powder in a blender or hand pestle. Set aside. 4. Soak tamarind in ¼ cup warm water. Squeeze the juice and set aside. You can filter to remove debris. You can also add tamarind directly. If your tamarind is clean you may add the pulp as well. 5. Heat oil in a deep pan. Add cumin, mustard, red chili. Fry till you get a nice aroma. 6. Add curry leaves, hing and fry till crisp. Add the powder and saute for a minute. 7. Pour water, add tamarind, crushed garlic, turmeric, salt, jaggery . 8. Let the tamarind rasam boil for 5 minutes on a medium flame. 9. Add coriander leaves and switch off. 10. Taste the tamarind rasam and add more salt or tamarind if needed. 11. Serve tamarind rasam with rice and vegetable stir fry.

title: Tandoori Aloo Tikka Recipe (Potato Skewers)
ingredients: 25 grams Baby potatoes, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 teapoon garam masala, 0.25 cup hung curd, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1.25 tablespoon besan, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon salt for the marinade, 1 tablespoon butter, 0.25 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Wash potatoes under running water. Prick each potato twice with a toothpick so they absorb the flavors. 2. Add them to a pot along with 3 to 4 cups of water. Also add red chili powder, garam masala and salt. Boil them till al dente, fully cooked but not mushy or too soft. Remove them and cool completely. 3. While the potatoes cool, to a mixing bowl add all the ingredients - yogurt, oil, salt, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder, chaat masala, kasuri methi and gram flour or sattu. 4. Mix well and taste test. Adjust salt and spice to suit your taste. Also taste test the potatoes so you get to know how much more salt you need to add. Marinade has to be thick and not runny. 5. Gently stir the potatoes into the marinade. Set aside for 35 to 40 mins. 6. Preheat the oven to 460 F or 240 C for 20 mins. Line a baking tray or place a wired rack over a baking tray. Thread the potatoes onto the skewers, shake off gently to remove the excess marinade and place them on the pan or on the rack. 7. Grill them for 15 minutes. Turn the skewers to the other side and grill for another 10 minutes or so until the marinade forms like a crust. 8. Move the rack to the top rack and broil for the last 2 to 3 minutes to get a slightly charred effect. 9. Remove and quickly brush the tandoori aloo tikka with some soft butter. 10. Sprinkle some chaat masala and serve with this Mint Chutney.

title: Tandoori Chicken Recipe (Smoky Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 0.75 kg chicken, 0.5 cup Greek yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper crushed, 1.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.5 tablespoon oil
instructions: 1. Greek yogurt also known as hung curd is the key ingredient used in the marinade. It is nothing but curd that has been strained of all the whey/liquid. If you do not have Greek yogurt, please check this link to make hung curd . To a mixing bowl, add the following ingredients: 2. 1 to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (or use paprika or a smoked hot chili powder, use 1 tsp for low heat and 2 tsps for high heat) 3. Add 1 tablespoon lemon juice or 1 teaspoon amchur powder/mango powder or half teaspoon chaat masala. Be careful not to add a lot. Use as required depending on how sour your yogurt is. 4. Pour 1½ tablespoon oil as well. Any cooking oil or neutral oil like avocado, grapeseed, refined coconut oil works. In India, mustard oil is used for a unique flavor. If you want you may use it or any other cooking oil as per your preference. 5. Mix well and taste test the marinade. Add more salt or spices to your taste preference. The marinade has to be thick as shown in the picture and should not be runny. A runny marinade will let out moisture to the pan and the chicken won’t hold the spices well. 6. Heat a pan/ skillet. Spread 1 to 2 tbsps oil. 7. When the oil turns hot, place the chicken on the skillet/pan. Regulate the heat to medium. 8. After 3 to 4 minutes turn the pieces. Apply the marinade and turn them after 3 to 4 mins. 9. Meanwhile add red chili powder to a small bowl. Mix it with the oil. This is optional. Smear that to the tandoori chicken. 10. Cook them, regulating the flame as required until they are cooked through. When the chicken is cooked through, prick it with a fork to see the meat fall off the bone easily (a sign of chicken being cooked). An instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part should read 165 to 170 F (75 C).

title: Tandoori Fish Tikka Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 kg fish fillets, 4 tablespoons hung curd, 1.25 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 tablespoon Lemon juice, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.75 tablespoon fenugreek leaves, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 green, 0.5 red bell pepper, 1 onion
instructions: 1. Please make sure your yogurt is not runny. It should be thick with no whey in it like Greek yogurt. (Check the pics in the post) 2. Add all the marinade ingredients to a mixing bowl. Oil, Yogurt, salt, turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala, cumin powder, coriander powder, black pepper, lemon juice, lemon juice & kasuri methi. 3. Mix well and taste test the marinade. You can adjust the spice and salt levels at this stage. The marinade should be thick and smooth. Keep this aside. 4. If using bell peppers and onions, dice them to 1½ inch sizes and keep aside. 5. Pat dry the fish with kitchen tissues/paper and dice equally to 1½ inch pieces each, the same size as that of vegetables. 6. Add the fish pieces to the marinade and coat them well. Let the fish rest in the marinade for a minimum of 15 mins to a maximum of 1 hour. Leaving it for too long in the marinade can make your fish mushy. 7. Keep the veggies aside and add them to the marinade only before cooking the tikkas else they release juices and ruin the marinade. 8. Soak your wooden skewers in water for 30 mins to prevent burning or simply use metal skewers. Line your baking tray with a parchment paper and grease it again or spray lightly. 9. Position your oven rack in the middle/Centre of the oven. Preheat the oven to 375 F or 190 C for at least 15 mins. 10. When you are ready to cook the tikkas, place the fish pieces to one side of the bowl. Add the diced veggies and marinate them with the rest of the marinade. 11. Thread onion, bell pepper & fish/salmon onto the skewer spacing ¼ inch apart. Repeat the same order again on each skewer. I could thread on to 5 skewers. 12. Place the Fish tikka skewers on the prepared tray. 13. Bake them for 8 to 9 minutes. Later using tongs turn them to the other side. Bake for another 4 to 5 minutes or until cooked through. If you want to slightly char the tikkas, move the tray to the top rack and broil for 2 mins. Adjust the timings depending on your oven as each is different. 14. The internal temperature of cooked salmon should reach 145 F in the thickest part. When it is fully cooked, fish flakes easily. 15. Squeeze lemon juice if required. Serve Tandoori fish tikka with Mint Chutney or Cilantro Chutney. Turmeric rice, kachumber & raita also go well. 16. Line the air fryer drip pan/basket with aluminum foil for easy clean-up. If your air fryer requires preheating do it for 2 mins at 390 F or 200 C. 17. Air fry the fish tikka skewers for 5 mins, if required brush them with little marinade. Turn the skewers to the other side and repeat brushing lightly. 18. Air fry again for 3 to 5 mins (until the internal temperature reaches 145F), depending on the thickness and size of fish. Adjust the timings depending on your air fryer as each air fryer is different. 19. Fish easily flakes when you poke a fork into it. This is an indication that it has cooked through well. 20. Squeeze some lemon juice over the fish tikkas and serve with mint chutney, coriander chutney. It also goes well with Turmeric rice or lemon rice and a vegetable salad like this kachumber.

title: tandoori gobi recipe | tandoori gobhi | tandoori cauliflower tikka
ingredients: 2 florets gobi cauliflower, 1 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 0.5 cup curd yogurt, 1 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp lemon juice, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 2 tbsp besan gram flour, 1 tsp oil, oil for roasting
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 20 florets gobi and 1 tsp salt. 2. pour 3 cup hot water and rest for 5 minutes to blanch. 3. now drain off the water and keep aside. do not overcook gobi. 4. in a bowl take ½ cup curd, 1 tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi, crushed, ½ tsp salt, 1 tsp lemon juice, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 2 tbsp besan. 5. mix well making sure all the spices are well combined. 6. further, add blanched gobi and 1 tsp oil. mix well coating uniformly. 7. refrigerate for 30 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed. 8. now heat 2 tbsp oil in the pan and spread in marinated gobi. 9. roast on all sides until they turn golden brown. alternatively, you can cook in tandoor or oven. 10. now take the skewer and slide in roasted gobi. 11. to get the tandoori flavour, roast directly on the flame brushing oil. 12. finally, enjoy tandoori gobi sprinkled with chaat masala along with green chutney.

title: Tandoori Mushroom Tikka
ingredients: 25 grams mushrooms, 1 onion, 1 cup bell peppers, 0.5 cup Greek yogurt, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoons coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons kasuri methi, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 1 lemon, 1.5 tablespoons butter
instructions: 1. To a large mixing bowl, add all the ingredients mentioned under marinade except lemon juice. Mix well. 2. Taste test and add more salt and lemon juice. If the marinade tastes sour, skip lemon juice. 3. Add the mushrooms, bell peppers and onions. Mix well. 4. If using wooden skewers, rinse them under running water. 5. Preheat air fryer or oven to 200 C or 400 F for atleast 15 mins. If using stovetop, heat a non-stick griddle with 1 tablespoon oil. 6. Thread the veggies on to skewers - onion, bell pepper & mushrooms. 7. Place the skewers on a prepared baking tray. Place them in the oven or air fryer or on a griddle. 8. Oven - Grill them for 10 mins & then brush the skewers lightly with some marinade. Turn the skewers to the other side and brush if needed. Grill them again for 6 to 7 mins depending on the size of the mushrooms. 9. Air fryer - Air fry for 8 mins, then brush them with little marinade. Turn the skewers to the other side and repeat brushing lightly. Air fry again for 5 mins. Adjust the timings based on the size of mushrooms. 10. Stove top - Place the skewers and keep turning them to all the sides and cook on a high heat. Some of the marinade comes to the griddle, you can use a wooden spatula to bring it together. When the veggies wilt, place a wired rack on the stovetop and place the mushroom skewers over direct flame and grill until done. 11. To char the tandoori mushrooms, brush some butter or mustard oil and place the skewers back in the oven. Broil or air fry for another 2 mins. 12. When done perfectly, grilled mushroom tikka & onions will begin to smell so good. Brush with more butter or oil if needed and serve immediately.

title: Tandoori Roti Recipe (Butter Roti)
ingredients: 2.25 cups whole wheat flour, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon baking soda, 0.5 cup yogurt, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 cup water, 1 tablespoon butter
instructions: 1. Mix together atta, salt and baking soda in a mixing large bowl. Make a well in the center. Pour yogurt and oil, followed by half of the water. 2. Mix together to form a soft dough, adding more water as required. Smear a few drops of oil to the dough. Cover and rest aside for 30 mins. 3. After resting knead the dough well with both your hands until really soft and smooth but not sticky. Divide the dough to 8 equal sized portions and roll to smooth balls. Keep them covered until you finish rolling. 4. Dust the rolling area lightly and sprinkle little flour on a dough ball and flatten it, 5. Roll a dough ball to 6 inches roti. It should be slightly thick (thicker than roti) and not thin. So don't roll bigger than 6 inches. Roll all the rotis the same way and transfer to a dry kitchen counter. 6.
