Heat a tawa (iron or steel pan, not non-stick) until really hot on a medium heat. 7. When the tawa is very hot, apply water on one side of the roti with a brush or your fingers generously all over the roti. [Basically you are going to wet the roti so it sticks to the hot tawa.] 8. Place the roti on the tawa with the wet side down, so it sticks to the tawa. Make sure the pan is hot and the flame is at medium high. 9. It should begin to puff immediately with a few large bubbles. 10. After 25 to 30 seconds of cooking, hold the handle of your tawa (wear mittens if required) and turn the tawa upside down, showing direct fire /heat to your tandoori roti. (check video) 11. Keep moving your hand all over so the roti cooks evenly and shows up brown spots or chars slightly. Do not take the roti too close or on to the flame as it can burn instantly. Turn back the tawa to normal position. 12. Gently remove the tandoori roti with a wooden spatula. Place it on a serving plate and brush with butter immediately. 13. Make sure there are no bits of roti/dough stuck to the tawa before you throw in the next one. Gently rub your tawa/pan with the spatula to remove any bits stuck & wipe with a wet cloth/kitchen tissues. Place your next roti only when the tawa is really hot. 14. Same way toast all the rotis and stack to keep them soft. If you want them to remain crisp and puffed spread them in a serving tray instead. 15. Serve tandoori roti with your favorite curry, dal or stew. This vegetable curry, dal tadka, chole and dhaba paneer curry are some choices.

title: tandoori roti recipe in toaster | tandoori roti maker for home
ingredients: 2 cups wheat flour atta, 0.5 tsp sugar, salt to taste, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 1 tbsp oil, water as required, wheat flour atta to dust, 3 tbsp butter
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl take 2 cups of wheat flour. 2. further add in ½ tsp sugar, 1 tsp baking powder and salt to taste. 3. also add in ¼ cup curd and 1 tbsp oil. 4. combine all the ingredients well. 5. additionally, add in water as required and knead the dough. 6. knead 5 minutes or more to get smooth and soft dough. 7. cover with the moist cloth and rest for 20 minutes. 8. again knead slightly, making sure the dough is smooth and soft. 9. pinch a small ball sized dough. 10. make a ball and flatten it slightly. 11. dust with wheat flour before rolling. 12. roll the dough slightly thick to 5 inch diameter. (depending on the size of toaster) 13. now roast the roti on hot tawa. 14. half cook the roti both the sides. 15. now transfer the half cooked roti into the toaster. 16. and turn on the toaster. i have kept the setting to meter 2. however, adjust the meter accordingly and it takes the same heat as required to toast the bread. 17. wait till the tandoori roti pops up and puffs up. 18. now using the forceps place the tandoori roti on to the plate. 19. brush with generous amount of butter. 20. finally, serve tandoori roti prepared from toaster along with palak paneer. 21. notes:

title: tandoori roti recipe on tawa | butter tandoori roti at home | tandoori roti naan
ingredients: 3 cups wheat flour, 2 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.5 cup curd, 2 tbsp oil, Warm water, Coriander leaves, Butter
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large bowl take wheat flour, sugar, salt, baking powder, curd and oil. 2. Mix well, making sure all the ingredients are combined properly. Baking powder helps the roti turn soft and fluffy, and gives a light tandoori texture without using yeast. 3. Now add warm water slowly and start kneading the dough. 4. Keep adding water as required and knead to a smooth and soft dough. Knead the dough for at least 5 minutes. This helps the roti turn soft and gives a good texture. 5. Tuck the dough, grease with oil, cover and rest for 1 hour. 6. After 1 hour, knead the dough again to make it smooth and soft. Pinch a ball sized dough and dust with wheat flour. 7. Roll gently into an oval shape or any shape of your choice. Keep the roti slightly thick while rolling, as thin roti will not give a soft tandoori texture. 8. Sprinkle coriander leaves on top and roll again gently. 9. Flip the roti and spread water on the back side. 10. Heat an iron tawa and place the water side down on the tawa. 11. Press gently, making sure the roti sticks properly to the tawa. Use an iron tawa for best results. Non stick tawa will not allow the roti to stick properly. 12. Once the roti starts to puff slightly, it means the bottom side is cooked. 13. Flip the tawa and cook the roti directly on the flame. 14. Cook until the roti is evenly roasted and gets a tandoor like texture. 15. Once cooked, scrape the sides gently and remove the roti from the tawa. 16. Brush with butter while the roti is still hot. This keeps the roti soft and adds flavour. 17. Finally, serve hot tandoori roti with paneer curry or dal.

title: Tandoori Salmon Recipe (Indian Salmon Recipe)
ingredients: 1.2 lbs Boneless Salmon fillets, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 tablespoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder, 1.125 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon cumin powder, 1.125 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.25 teaspoon ginger powder, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.75 teaspoon Chaat masala, 1 tablespoon Kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Gather all your spices first and make the marinade. You can make the wet marinade ahead and refrigerate for up to 4 days. Alternately you may combine the ground spices and store in your cabinet for several weeks. Here is what you need for the marinade: 2. tablespoon oil (olive/mustard/avocado or any other of choice) 1 tablespoon lemon juice (or lime juice) ½ teaspoon garlic powder (or 1 teaspoon garlic paste) ¼ teaspoon ginger powder (optional or ½ teaspoon ginger paste) ⅓ to ½ teaspoon salt (adjust to taste) ⅛ teaspoon turmeric ½ to 1 tablespoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder (adjust to taste) 1 to 1¼ teaspoon garam masala ½ to 1 teaspoon cumin powder 1 to 1¼ teaspoon coriander powder 3. The following spices add another layer of flavors to the tandoori salmon. If you don’t have you may skip. For chaat masala you can use half teaspoon amchur and ¼ teaspoon more cumin powder. 4. tablespoon Kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves) ¼ teaspoon ground black pepper (powder) ¾ teaspoon Chaat masala (I also prefer ½ teaspoon fennel powder , optional) 5. Add all these to a prepared baking tray (if preferred line with a foil). I usually marinate and grill directly on the baking pan/tray in baking in oven. 6. Mix all the spices and pour the oil and lemon juice. 7. Begin to mix, adding more water only as required. I use about 3 to 4 tablespoons. 8. Mix well to make a thick marinade. We don’t want the marinade to be runny as it drips off the fish. Taste this and make your own adjustments like adding more salt, lemon etc. 9. Place your salmon on the baking tray/pan and pat dry with kitchen tissue. 10. Smear the marinade all over on both the sides. If you are baking it in the oven, put the tray as is in the preheated oven (salmon skin side down in the pan). Bake at 390 F or 200 C for 10 to 12 mins. Later broil for 3 to 4 mins. Adjust the baking time depending on the thickness of the salmon fillet, size of the oven and the kind of pan used. These timings work for me in a metal tray. If using a otg, cut down the time. (Read my pro tips for more details)

title: Tandoori Shrimp Recipe
ingredients: 45 grams shrimp, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon sea salt, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri Red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.75 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 3 tablespoons Greek yogurt, 2 teaspoons lemon juice, 0.75 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon ginger powder, 0.625 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.75 teaspoon kasuri methi, 2 tablespoons oil
instructions: 1. Preheat the air fryer at the highest heat for a minute. Or Preheat your oven at 400F or 200 C, for at least 15 mins. 2. Marinate: In a mixing bowl, stir together all the ingredients except shrimp and lemon juice. Taste test and adjust salt and heat levels to preference. If your marinade is thick enough and is not too sour, add lemon juice. You should have a thick marinade. 3. Add the shrimp and coat them well with the marinade. You may rest for 5 to 8 mins, no longer. 4. Spray/ brush the air fryer basket or prepared baking tray with oil. Place the tandoori shrimp in the basket/ tray, spacing them ½ inch apart. 5. In the Air Fryer: Air fry for 7 to 9 mins, depending on the size. I do not feel the need to turn them to the other side. But if you want you may do that around 5 mins mark. 6. In the Oven: Broil for 8 to 10 min. Adjust the time depending on the size of your shrimp. (I use extra large shrimp/ tiger prawns) 7. To cook them in a pan: Spread 1 teaspoon oil in a cast iron pan or a non-stick pan and heat it. When the pan is hot, regulate the heat to medium high and place the marinated shrimp one by one, spacing them apart. 8. Let them cook for 2 mins and turn them quickly with a tong. Cook until they swirl to a C shape, for another 3 to 4 mins, depending on the size. Avoid overcooking, they can become chewy. 9. Test: If you are in doubt, put your meat thermometer into the largest shrimp, in the thickest part. It should read 145 F (or 63 C). 10. Serve Tandoori Shrimp hot with mint chutney or as a side with yellow rice, raita and vegetable salad.

title: Tangdi kabab (restaurant style tangri kabab)
ingredients: 4 medium chicken drumsticks, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon pepper powder, 0.5 red chilli powder, 2 tablespoons cream, 4 tablespoons greek yogurt, 1 tablespoon cornflour, 0.75 inch ginger, 3.5 garlic cloves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 tablespoons oil
instructions: 1. Drain the moisture well from the chicken drumsticks & make gashes or slits. 2. Rub them well with lemon juice & pepper & or chilli. 3. Cover and rest for 15 to 30 mins. 4. Please ensure you use only strained yogurt, greek yogurt or hung curd only. 5. In a separate bowl, mix together yogurt, cream, ginger garlic, flour, garam masala, coriander leaves, salt and cardamom powder. 6. optional - If using cashews, then blend them well to smooth in a small blender along with yogurt. 7. Mix everything well and taste the marinade. If needed add more salt. 8. Transfer this mixture to the chicken and marinate at least for 4 hours to over night. 9. Preheat the oven to 460 F or 240 C for at least 20 mins. 10. Meanwhile prepare a baking tray and line with a foil. 11. Place a wire rack over the tray. Place the chicken tangdi over the rack. 12. Grill for 13 to 15 mins depending on the size of the chicken. 13. Once done, towards the end broil for 3 to 4 mins until slightly charred. 14. Remove from the oven and brush butter or oil. 15. Serve tangdi kabab as an appetizer with mint chutney or green chutney. 16. Transfer the entire marinated chicken in a pan or kadai and begin to cook on a medium heat. 17. Chicken will ooze out a lot of moisture, just cook until all the marinade dries up and the chicken is fully done. 18. Place a small bowl in the chicken pan or tray. 19. Burn a woodcoal piece until hot and red. 20. Place the coal in the steel cup and drizzle 1 tsp ghee. Quickly smoke begin to release. 21. Cover the pan and smoke the chicken for 5 to 7 mins depending on how strong you want the smoky flavor to be.

title: Tawa Paneer Masala Recipe | Paneer Tawa Fry - Dhaba Style
ingredients: 0.25 cup curd, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp oil, 16 cubes paneer, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 2 tomato, 1 capsicum, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp kasuri methi, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a bowl take ¼ cup curd, ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp garam masala, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp kasuri methi, ½ tsp sal,t and 2 tsp oil. 2. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 3. Add 16 cubes of paneer and mix coating the marination well. Marinate for 30 minutes. 4. In a pan heat 2 tbsp oil and splutter ½ tsp cumin. 5. Also add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute till the onions turn golden brown. 6. Keeping the flame on low add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp coriander powder. Saute on low flame till the spices turn aromatic. 7. Now add 2 tomato and cook well till the oil separates. 8. Further add 1 capsicum and stir fry till the capsicum turns crunchy. 9. Also, add in marinated paneer and cook well. 10. Add in ½ cup water, ½ tsp salt, and mix adjusting the consistency as required. 11. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the curry is well cooked. 12. Now add 2 tbsp kasuri methi, ½ tsp garam masala, and 2 tbsp coriander. Mix gently. 13. Finally, enjoy Tawa Paneer Recipe with roti or wrap.

title: Tawa Paneer Sabji (Paneer Tawa Masala)
ingredients: 2 cups paneer, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onions, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.75 cup tomato puree, 0.25 cup capsicum, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Pour 2 tablespoons oil to a hot tawa or pan. Sprinkle ½ teaspoon cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add 1 teaspoon grated ginger or ginger garlic paste. Saute till it begins to smell good. Do not burn it. 2. Add 1 cup fine chopped onions and ¼ teaspoon salt. Keep stirring and fry evenly on a moderately medium flame till they turn golden. 3. While the onions are frying, chop 1 cup tomatoes (about 3 medium) to a grinder/blender jar. If you wish to add few cashews to make the dish rich, you may add. Grind to a fine puree. 4. When the onions turn golden, pour the tomato puree. Cook till the raw smell of the tomatoes vanishes. Keep stirring to prevent burning. 5. Add 6. ¼ cup capsicum chopped 7. Mix well and fry on a medium high flame till the mixture smells good. This usually takes 4 to 5 minutes. 8. Pour 3 to 4 tablespoons of water to deglaze the pan. Add 200 to 250 grams (2 cups) paneer and 1 teaspoon kasuri methi. Mix well, taste test and add more salt or masala if you want. 9. Cook covered for 2 to 3 minutes, until aromatic. Garnish tawa paneer masala with coriander leaves. Serve with rice or roti.

title: tawa pizza recipe | veg pizza on tawa without yeast | pizza without oven
ingredients: 0.25 cup curd yogurt, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp baking powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, 2 cup maida plain flour, 0.75 cup water, 4 tsp pizza sauce, cubes onion, few sliced capsicum, few sliced olives, 3 slice tomato, few jalapeno, 1 cup mozzarella cheese, 0.25 tsp chilli flakes, 0.25 tsp mixed herbs, 1 tsp oil
instructions: 1. place the rolled pizza base into the tawa. 2. further, leaving a cm or more and create a dent. this helps the crust to fluff slightly. 3. then prick with a centre of dough with the help of a fork to prevent pizza base from fluffing up like chapati. 4. spread 4 tsp of pizza sauce uniformly. 5. further top with onion, capsicum, olives, tomato and jalapenos. 6. also, spread 1 cup mozzarella cheese. 7. further, sprinkle ¼ tsp chilli flakes and ¼ tsp mixed herbs. 8. now cover and simmer for 8 minutes or till cheese melts completely. 9. finally, slice the tawa pizza and serve hot.

title: Tawa Pulao Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup basmati rice, 1 tablespoon butter, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 cup onions, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.3333333333333333 cup tomatoes, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.875 tablespoon pav bhaji masala, 0.5 tsp red chili powder, 0.25 cup capsicum, 2 tablespoon water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 potato, 0.5 carrot, 3 green beans, 0.25 cup green peas
instructions: 1. Wash rice well a few times until the water runs clear. Soak the rice for 30 mins and drain the water. 2. Cook the rice with 1 cup water in a pot, on a low heat until fully cooked - al dente. If you want you can also cook in a cooker. Cool the rice completely. 3. Wash and chop onions, tomatoes & capsicum. Set these aside. 4. Wash and cube potatoes, beans and carrots. 5. Boil or steam potatoes, beans, green peas and carrots until fork tender and not mushy. (Pour 1 cup water to a cooker or steamer, add the vegetables to a bowl and place them over a steel rack in the cooker. Cover and let steam for 15 to 20 mins) 6. Heat butter/oil in a pan, add cumin and let them sizzle. 7. Add onions, fry them till transparent. Add ginger garlic paste, fry till a nice aroma comes out & the raw smell disappears. 8. Next add tomatoes and salt. Fry till tomatoes turn mushy. Add pav bhaji masala and red chili powder or chili paste. 9. Next add capsicum and saute for 2 to 3 mins. Pour 2 tbsp of water and cook the masala until water evaporates. 10. Add the boiled vegetables. Mix well. Stir in the cooked rice. Taste test and add more salt if required. 11. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve tawa pulao hot.

title: Tawa Pulao Recipe | Mumbai Tawa Pulao | Pav Bhaji Pulao
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp butter, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 capsicum, 3 tbsp peas, 1 carrot, 2 tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 3 tsp pav bhaji masala, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp water, 1 potato, 2 tbsp coriander, 5 cup cooked rice, 2 chilli, 0.5 lemon
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp butter and splutter 1 tsp cumin. 2. Add ½ onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and saute until onion softens. 3. Now add ½ capsicum, 3 tbsp peas, 1 carrot, 2 tomato and stir fry until the vegetables turn crunchy. 4. Keeping the flame on low, add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 3 tsp pav bhaji masala and 1 tsp salt. 5. Saute until the spices turn aromatic. 6. Also, add 2 tbsp water and cook until all the masala are cooked well. 7. Add in 1 potato, 2 tbsp coriander and mix until the masala is coated well. 8. Further, add 5 cup cooked rice and mix gently. 9. Now add 2 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander and ½ lemon. Mix well making sure everything is well combined. 10. Finally, enjoy Tawa Pulao Recipe with raita.

title: Tehri Recipe (Tahri)
ingredients: 1 cup aged basmati rice, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 medium onion, 2 potatoes, 1 tomato, 0.5 cup cauliflower, 6 beans, 1 carrot, 0.25 cup green peas, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 cups water, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon, 1 black cardamom, 4 green cardamoms, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. Rinse rice well at least thrice. Soak for about 15 to 20 mins. Later drain it and set aside. 2. While the rice soaks, wash and dice the vegetables. 3. Heat oil or in a pot or pressure cooker. 4. Add cumin, bay leaf, green cardamoms, black cardamoms, cloves and cinnamon. 5. When the cumin begins to splutter. add onions and green chili. Fry until the onions turn golden. 6. Add ginger garlic paste. Fry just for a min. 7. Lower the flame. Stir in coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala, cumin powder, turmeric and salt. 8. Add all the vegetables including tomatoes. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes on a medium heat. 9. Add the drained rice and mix gently. On a slightly high flame fry the rice for 2 mins. Occasionally stir & do not over do else the rice will break. 10. Pour water and mix well. Taste the water and add more salt if needed. 11. Bring the water to a boil on a high flame. Reduce the flame to low. Cook covered until the rice is fully cooked but not mushy. 12. If using a pressure cooker, cover the lid and cook for 1 whistle. The flame has to be medium. When the pressure drops, open the lid. 13. Add chopped coriander leaves. Gently fluff up the rice with a fork and mix the coriander leaves. Keep covered for 5 mins. 14. Serve veg tehri with raita and papad. 15. Perform all the steps on saute mode until you pour water to the steel insert of the Instant pot. Later cover the Ip and pressure for 5 mins. Release the pressure immediately after 2 mins. Fluff up with a fork. 16. If the rice is too dry on the top, fluff up well with a fork. Remove the steel insert from the Ip and keep covered for 5 mins.

title: Thakkali Sadam
ingredients: 1 cup uncooked rice, 1.25 cup tomatoes, 0.5 cup onion, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons oil, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.625 teaspoon cumin, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon mustard, 0.75 tablespoon chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 2 cloves garlic, 1 cashewnuts, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon saunf powder
instructions: 1. Wash rice in lot of water and cook the rice. I soaked basmati rice for 30 mins. Then cooked in a pressure cooker with cups water for 1 whistle on a medium heat. While the rice was cooling I went ahead making the tomato masala. Heat a pan with ghee or oil. Add mustard and cumin. They will begin to pop soon. 2. Add chana dal, urad dal and cashews. 3. Fry until the dal turns golden. Add hing, slit green chili and curry leaves. Omit green chili if you have young kids at home. Fry until the curry leaves turn aromatic. 4. Add onions. 5. Saute until they turn slightly golden or pink. 6. Add chopped garlic or ginger garlic paste. Fry until the garlic losses the raw smell for a minute. 7. Stir in the chopped tomatoes and sprinkle salt. 8. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. 9. Cover and cook until the tomatoes turn completely mushy and soft. 10. Stir in red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder and saunf powder (optional). 11. Saute this mixture until it turns slightly dry, aromatic and begins to ooze out oil. Taste test to adjust and spices. If you want add more. Reserve 2 to 3 tbsp of the tomato masala aside. 12. Transfer the cooled rice and mix gently until the rice blends with the tomato masala. If you want you may add the reserved masala and mix. 13. Add some chopped coriander leaves. Serve Thakkali Sadam with thayir pachadi or papad or plain curd.

title: Thalassery biryani
ingredients: 1 star anise, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves (/ laung), 2 inch cinnamon sticks, 2 strands mace, 0.25 tsp nutmeg, 0.5 tsp shahi jeera, 1 tsp fennel seeds, 1 red chili, 0.5 tsp pepper corn, 3.5 tbsps ghee, 1 Handful cashews, 2 tbsps raisins, 0.625 cup onions, 0.5 cup onions sliced for gravy, 2 tbsps ginger garlic chili paste, 0.5 kg chicken, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 cup tomatoes, Salt, 2 tbsps curd, 0.25 cup mint, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 2 cups Jeerakasala rice, 1.5 tbsp ghee, 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 4 green cardamoms, 4.5 cloves 4 to 5 cloves (/ laung), 1 inch cinnamon stick, 0.375 tsp fennel seeds, Salt, 3.5 cups water, 0.375 tsp kerala garam masala, 0.5 Handful pudina, 0.5 Handful coriander leaves, 1 tbsp ghee, 0.5 tsp rose water
instructions: 1. Clean the spices and add them to a blender jar. I have not roasted the spices as the powder gets roasted along with the chicken. 2. Make a very fine powder. Set this aside. 3. Add 3 to 4 tbsps of ghee to a biryani pot. Fry cashews until lightly golden. Then add raisins. When they turn plump, transfer these to a plate. We will be using these for garnishing. 4. To the same pan, add onions and fry them evenly until golden or light brown. You can skip this step if you are using store bought fried onions. But the ones fried in ghee lend a delicate aroma. What you are seeing in the picture is half done. I fried them a little more. Set them aside along with cashews and raisins. We will be using these as well for garnishing. 5. Heat a pot with 1 to 2 tbsps ghee and saute the spices. 6. Add drained rice and saute for 2 to 3 mins. Add salt. 7. Add hot water. Note that cold water makes the rice sticky. Stir gently. 8. Cook on a medium heat until the entire water is absorbed but still very wet. 9. Cook covered and lower the flame completely and cook until the rice absorbs all the water and turns dry. At this stage rice is cooked to 90% meaning al dente. If the rice is under cooked, you can sprinkle some warm water and continue to cook. 10. By then the chicken would have completely cooked. A thick gravy would be left. Add mint and coriander leaves. Level the chicken properly.

title: Thandai Recipe for Holi 2025
ingredients: 4.5 cups milk, 2 almonds, 1 cashews, 15 pistachios, 1.5 tablespoon melon seeds, 1.5 tablespoon poppy seeds, 1.5 tablespoon fennel seeds, 12 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 7 black pepper, 5.5 tablespoons sugar, 1 pinch saffron, 2 rose petals, 2 tablespoons sliced nuts, 6 Ice cubes
instructions: 1. Add almonds, cashews & pistachios to a bowl. Pour water to immerse and soak them for 4 hours. To quicken this process you can also soak them in hot water for 1 hour. In another small bowl, soak the poppy seeds and lemon seeds too. 2. Peel the almond skin and add them to a blender jar along with cashews and pistachios. You can also rub off the pistachios to remove the skin easily. 3. Drain the water from melon seeds & poppy seeds. Add them to the same grinder jar along with fennel seeds, cardamoms, cinnamon, black pepper, rose petals (optional) and saffron to the blender jar. 4. Blend all of these to a coarse paste. Then pour half cup milk and blend to a very smooth paste. If you want you may add more milk as needed while blending. 5. Bring 4 cups of milk to a gentle boil in a pot. Then add sugar and the thandai masala paste we just made. Taste test and adjust the sugar to taste. 6. Turn off the stove and stir well to incorporate the ground paste well with the milk. 7. Cover this and refrigerate for at least 4 hours. Resting overnight gives the best flavors. 8. Place a filter/strainer over a pitcher. Pour the thandai and pass it through the filter. Press down the solids to extract all of the milk. 9. Add 1 to 1½ teaspoon gulkand to serving glasses and stir in the thandai.

title: Thattai Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 teaspoon urad dal, 1 cup rice flour, 1 tablespoon butter, 2 tablespoon fried gram, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 tablespoon sesame seeds, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 1 sprig curry leaves, Water as needed, Oil for deep frying as needed
instructions: 1. Wash and soak chana dal for 1 hour. Drain and set aside. 2. Dry roast urad dal or peanuts until lightly golden and cool them. If using peanuts, remove the skin. 3. Add urad dal to a blender jar along with fried gram. Make a fine powder. If using peanuts, then powder fried gram very fine, then add skinned peanuts and make a coarse powder. You can also powder sesame seeds. 4. To a mixing bowl, add rice flour, urad dal & fried gram powder, red chili powder, hing, salt, sesame seeds & chopped curry leaves. 5. Drain the soaked chana dal and add it to the bowl. 6. Also add soft butter & mix all of them well to incorporate the butter. 7. Pour little water and begin to mix everything to make thattai dough. 8. Add more water as needed and make the dough. Dough has to be non sticky, stiff and smooth. 9. Too moist dough will make your thattai greasy as the excess moisture will soak up oil. 10. Next too little moisture in the dough will make the thattai crack a lot while spreading the dough and make them hard. 11. Taste the dough and add more salt if required. Divide the dough to 14 to 16 balls. Keep them covered. 12. Heat oil on a medium flame in a wide kadai. 13. Spread a dry cloth, grease your fingers. Place a ball on the cloth or greased sheet. 14. Begin to spread them moderately thin. Very thin thattai will break when you remove from the cloth. Very thick ones will not turn crisp. 15. If the edges begin to crack then join them together. Make as many thattai as possible till the oil heats up. 16. Check if the oil is hot by dropping a tiny flat piece of dough. It should rise and not sink. 17. Transfer one thattai at one time to the hot oil. 18. You can fry more than one thattai at one time if there is space in your kadai. But do not crowd them as they will not fry properly. 19. Make sure the flame is set to medium high. Low flame will make them oily and high flame will brown them. 20. Flip them when fried on one side. Flip as needed to both the sides and fry until evenly golden & crisp. 21. When they are fried fully, bubbles will begin to reduce in the oil. Remove them to a cooling rack or colander. Repeat frying them in batches. 22. When they cool down, transfer them immediately to a tight air tight container. 23. Serve thattai as a snack. They keep good for about 2 weeks if fried in fresh oil.

title: thatte idli recipe | tatte idli or plate idli | how to make thatte idli
ingredients: 2 cup idli rice dosa rice sona masuri rice, 1 cup urad dal, 0.75 cup thin poha avalakki aval, oil for greasing
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large mixing bowl soak 2 cup idli rice for 5 hours. alternatively, use dosa rice / sona masuri rice. 2. in another bowl soak 1 cup urad dal for 3 hours. 3. drain off the water from urad dal and blend to smooth paste adding water as required. 4. transfer the smooth and fluffy batter of urad dal into a large bowl. 5. in the blender take soaked rice and add ¾ cup of washed thin poha. 6. bend to slightly coarse paste adding water as required. 7. transfer the rice batter to the bowl of urad dal batter. 8. mix well making sure everything is combined well. 9. now cover and rest in warm place for 8-10 hours or till the batter ferments and doubles. 10. after 8 hours, batter doubles indicating well fermented with air pockets present. 11. add 1½ tsp salt to the batter and mix well. 12. mix gently without disturbing the air pockets. 13. now grease the thatte idli plate with oil. 14. scoop the batter into idli plate greased with oil. 15. arrange the idli plate into the stand. 16. steam for 20 minutes on medium flame or till a tooth pick inserted comes out clean. 17. finally, thatte idlis are ready to serve along with chutney and sambar.

title: thayir vadai recipe | mosaru vade recipe | south indian curd vada
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 1 chilli finely chopped, 1 inch ginger finely chopped, few curry leaves chopped, 2 tbsp coriander finely chopped, 0.5 tsp pepper crushed, 2 tbsp dry coconut chopped, 0.75 tsp salt, oil for frying, 3 cup curd yogurt, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp sugar, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 red chilli, few curry leaves, 2 chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 tbsp coriander, pinch kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 cup kara boondi, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl soak 1 cup urad dal in water for 2 hours. 2. drain off the water and transfer to a mixi or grinder. 3. blend to smooth thick and fluffy batter adding 1-2 tbsp of water. 4. transfer the urad dal batter to a bowl. 5. beat in one direction for 2 minutes or until the batter turns smooth and fluffy. 6. now add 1 chilli, 1 inch ginger, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp coriander, ½ tsp pepper, 2 tbsp dry coconut and ¾ tsp salt. 7. combine and beat for a minute. 8. now dip your hand in the water, and start dropping them one by one to hot oil. 9. keep the flame in lower to medium stir occasionally. 10. also, fry the vada till they turn golden brown and crisp. 11. drain off the vada on to kitchen towel to absorb oil. 12. drop the vada into large bowl and pour 4 cup hot water. 13. dip and rest for 5-10 minutes. this helps to make vada soft and juicy. 14. once the vada is soaked well, squeeze gently and keep aside. 15. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 cup curd, ½ cup water, 2 tbsp sugar and ½ tsp salt. 16. whisk well to have smooth flowing consistency curd. add more water if required. 17. in a pan heat 3 tsp oil and splutter 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, ½ tsp cumin, pinch hing, 1 dried red chilli, few curry leaves, 2 chilli and 1 inch ginger. 18. splutter the tempering and pour over curd mixture. 19. add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well making sure everything is well combined. 20. further, pour the curd mixture over vada. 21. cover and refrigerate for 2 hours or until curd is well absorbed. 22. for serving, in a small plate place a dahi vada and pour ladleful of curd mixture. 23. sprinkle little chilli powder and top with 2 tbsp boondi and few coriander. 24. finally, enjoy mosaru vada / thayir vada chilled.

title: Thecha Paneer Tikka Recipe | Paneer Thecha Tikkis
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 5 chilli, 1 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp cumin, 0.25 cup peanut, 1 bunch coriander, 1 tsp salt, 12 pieces paneer
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan, heat 2 tsp of oil. Add 5 chilli, 10 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp cumin and ¼ cup peanuts. 2. Roast on a low flame till the chilli turns aromatic. 3. Also, add 1 bunch of coriander and saute till the coriander shrinks. 4. Cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 5. Add 1 tsp of salt, 2 tbsp of water, and grind to a smooth paste. Thecha is ready. 6. Now take 12 pieces of paneer, thecha, and coat well. 7. Marinate for 30 minutes, allowing the masala to be well absorbed by the paneer. 8. Further, roast it on a hot tawa or grill in an oven or tandoor. 9. Finally, sprinkle some lemon juice and serve Paneer Theecha immediately.

title: Thecha Recipe
ingredients: 1 teaspoon oil, 8 green chilies, 12 grams medium garlic cloves, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3 tablespoons roasted unsalted peanuts, 0.5 cup coriander leaves, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt
instructions: 1. Add oil to a pan and fry the green chilies and garlic cloves, until blistered. Add cumin seeds and let toast for 30 seconds. 2. Cool down and add to the grinder/ stone mortar. Also add the roasted peanuts, coriander leaves and salt. 3. Crush to a coarse paste. Taste test to adjust salt. If it’s too spicy for your preference, may sprinkle little lemon juice. 4. Serve thecha as a side to spice up your meal. It goes well with flatbreads, dal-rice or any one pot dishes like khichdi.

title: Thenkuzhal Murukku Recipe | Thenkuzhal Recipe | How to Make Thenkuzhal
ingredients: 2 cup rice flour, 0.5 cup urad dal flour, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tbsp cumin, pinch hing, 2 tbsp melted butter, water, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large parath take 2 cup rice flour, ½ cup urad dal flour, 1 tsp salt, ½ tbsp cumin, pinch hing and 2 tbsp melted butter. 2. Crumble and mix well, making sure everything is well combined. 3. Add water slowly and start to knead the dough. 4. Knead to smooth and soft dough adding water as required. 5. Now take 5-hole plate and fix it to the chakli maker. 6. Grease the chakli maker with some oil. This prevents the dough from sticking to the mould. 7. Furthermore, make a cylindrical shape out of dough and place the dough inside the maker. 8. Tighten the lid and start pressing to spiral shapes. 9. Drop the chakli into the hot oil, keeping the flame on medium. 10. Flip the murukku and fry on medium flame till they turn crispy from both sides. 11. Furthermore, drain over to remove excess oil. 12. Finally, once cooled, enjoy Instant Thenkuzhal Murukku or store in an airtight container for 2 weeks.

title: Thepla Recipe (Methi Thepla)
ingredients: 1.5 cup wheat flour, 0.25 cup bajra, 3.5 tablespoons besan, 1.125 cup methi leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1.5 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.375 teaspoon turmeric, 4 tablespoons curd, 1 tablespoon oil, 2 tablespoons oil
instructions: 1. Skip if making plain thepla - Rinse methi leaves in lot of water and drain them well. Chop them finely and add to a mixing bowl. 2. Add all the ingredients except oil to the mixing bowl. Begin to mix well all the ingredients to bring out the moisture from methi leaves. 3. Sprinkle water just as needed and knead to a soft non sticky dough. Pour 1 tbsp oil and knead it well. Taste test and add more salt if you want. 4. Keep it covered and rest the dough for 20 mins. Divide the dough to 8 to 9 portions. Make balls and keep covered. 5. Lightly dust flour over the rolling area. Place a ball over the flour and flatten it slightly. Smear flour on both the sides. 6. Begin to roll to a 8 inch thepla of medium thickness. Similarly make about 5 and keep them aside on the platform. If you want you can cut them with a 8 inch lid. 7. Heat a pan or tawa on a medium high heat until hot enough. Transfer a thepla to the tawa. 8. Cook on a medium heat until half cooked on the bottom, then turn it and cook until golden spots appear on both the sides. 9. In between press down the thepla with a spatula for even cooking. 10. Drizzle about half tsp oil or more on each. Stack them on a cloth or kitchen tissue to keep them soft. 11. Serve methi thepla with curd, pickle or aam ka chunda.

title: thondekai palya recipe | dondakaya fry | kovakkai fry | tindora fry
ingredients: 0.75 cup coconut, 1 tsp mustard, 5 red chilli, 3 tsp oil, 0.75 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 red chilli, few curry leaves, 3 cup thondekai tindora ivy gourd, 0.5 cup cashew kaju, 1 tsp jaggery, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 0.25 cup water
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare masala by taking ¾ cup coconut, 1 tsp mustard and 5 dried red chilli. 2. blend to coarse masala paste without adding any water. 3. in a large kadai heat 3 tsp oil and splutter ¾ tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 dried red chilli and few curry leaves. 4. add 3 cup sliced thondekai, ½ cup cashew and stir fry for a minute. 5. further add 1 tsp jaggery, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt and mix well. 6. additionally add ¼ cup water, cover and cook for 10 minutes on low to medium. 7. cook until tindora is cooked well yet retain its shape. 8. add in the prepared masala paste and mix well. 9. mix until the masala is coated well. 10. finally, enjoy thondekai palya with hot steamed rice.

title: Tikki Chaat (Chole Tikki Chaat)
ingredients: 1.25 cup chole, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 1 inch ginger grated, 1 tablespoon garlic minced, 1 green chili chopped, 0.75 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.75 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.75 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.75 teaspoon black salt, 1 tablespoon tamarind paste, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves chopped, 1 pound potatoes, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1 green chili chopped, 0.875 tablespoon ginger, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 cup onions fine chopped, 0.5 cup coriander leaves, 0.75 cup tamarind chutney, 0.5 cup coriander chutney, 0.25 cup radish, 1 teaspoon chaat masala
instructions: 1. Rinse chickpeas well thrice and soak them in 4 cups of water overnight. Later drain the water and rinse them. 2. Heat ghee or oil in the instant pot / pressure cooker. Add bay leaf, black cardamom, ginger garlic and green chili. Saute for a minute and add the soaked chickpeas, red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder, crushed fennel and salt. 3. Pour water and mix well. Cover and pressure cook for 18 mins. When the pressure drops, open the lid and transfer ¼ cup chickpeas to a small bowl. Mash them very well until smooth or grind them in a grinder with little water. Add back to the chickpeas. 4. Add tamarind paste & jaggery or tamarind chutney and black salt. If your pot is too dry, add little water. Mix and simmer for 3 to 4 mins until you have a thick masala. Add kasuri methi and coriander leaves. Taste test and adjust salt, garam masala and tamarind. 5. Boil potatoes in 3 to 4 cups water, until fork tender, not mushy. It should take about 20 mins, depending on the size. I pressure cooked mine (medium sized) in a 3 qt Instant pot for 10 mins. The timings may vary depending on the size of your potatoes. 6. Cool down, peel and grate them to a bowl. Add garam masala, chaat masala, salt, green chili, ginger, coriander leaves and half of the bread crumbs or poha powder. Mix well. If the mixture is still too soft and wet, add the remaining bread crumbs. 7. Mix well and taste test to adjust the spices. Divide the mixture to 8 portions and roll to balls. Place them on a parchment paper, placed over the kitchen counter. Flatten them slightly to make thick or thin patties. I made mine thin (about 3 inches). 8. Heat 2 tablespoon oil in a non stick pan. Place the tikkis and fry on a medium high heat, until golden. Turn them to the other side. If your pan is dry, add more oil and fry until crisp. Remove to a wired rack. (Takes about 5 mins on each side). You may fry them in batches. 9. Mix the chole well and divide among 4 serving plates. Place 2 tikkis (each serving) over the chickpeas. (you can also do it vice versa, like the street style) 10. Pour 1 to 2 tablespoons tamarind chutney and 1 to 2 tablespoon green chutney all over the chole and the tikki. Top with onions, coriander leaves, grated radish or sev. Sprinkle with chaat masala. Serve tikki chaat immediately.

title: Til Ke Laddu (Sesame Laddu)
ingredients: 1.5 cup sesame seeds, 0.75 cup jaggery, 0.75 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.25 cup roasted skinned peanuts, 1 tablespoon ghee, 15 grams sesame seeds, 15 grams jaggery, 3 tablespoons water, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder
instructions: 1. Dry roast sesame seeds on a low to medium heat till you begin to get a nice nutty aroma. Keep stirring to toast them evenly. Good quality sesame also splutter/ sizzle. 2. Avoid over roasting as they tend to turn bitter quickly. Remove to a plate and set aside. 3. Crush or break down the roasted peanuts slightly with the base of a bowl/rolling pin or in a small hand mortar pestle. (Don't powder, check pic in the post) 4. Reserve 2 tbsps sesame seeds in a small bowl. Add the rest to a grinder or food processor. You can pulse/ grind/process to a fine powder depending on your machine. Do not over do. 5. Add grated jaggery and cardamom powder. Grind or pulse them until the mixture begins to release oils. If the mixture is still dry, add a tablespoon ghee and process again until the mixture looks greasy. 6. Mix-in the crushed peanuts and the reserved sesame seeds. Take portions of this to your fingers and press down against your palm to shape them to ladoos. You will get about 12. 7. Store sesame seeds ladoo in an air tight jar and handle with moist free hands. 8. Take a small bowl with ¼ cup of water and keep it ready aside to check the syrup consistency. 9. Heat jaggery in the same pan along with water. 10. Optional - Once dissolved you can also filter it to another bowl to remove the dust particles. Rinse the pan and pour back the syrup to the pan. 11. Boil the jaggery syrup on a medium flame stirring often until it reaches a ball consistency. 12. The syrup reduces, turns to deep color & turns frothy. To check add a tsp of the syrup to the bowl of water we prepared earlier. 13. The syrup should stay in there in the bowl without dissolving. Gather the jaggery and make a ball. You should get a soft ball that turns firm upon cooling. It should not be too soft and should not turn to a brittle. 14. If the syrup hasn't reached that consistency yet, continue to cook for few more minutes. 15. When you get the correct ball consistency, turn off the stove. Add cardamom powder and sesame seeds. Mix well. 16. Let the mixture cool down a bit. Then quickly dip your palms in water just to moisten and scoop out 1 tbsp of the mixture to your palm and shape to ladoo. 17. Be quick at this stage and keep dipping your hand in water as needed to avoid burns. 18. Roll the entire mixture to balls when the mixture is still hot. Cool the til ladoos completely and store in air tight jar.

title: Til Ladoo Recipe | Sesame Peanut Ladoo
ingredients: 1.5 cup sesame til, 0.5 cup peanut, 1 cup jaggery, 0.5 tsp cardamom powder, 2 tbsp ghee
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pan, dry roast 1½ cups of sesame on a low flame. 2. Dry roast till it turns aromatic and golden brown. 3. Transfer to the large plate to cool completely, 4. Also, dry roast ½ cup of peanuts till they turn crunchy. 5. Peel the skin and transfer to the large plate to cool completely. 6. Once cooled, transfer to the mixer jar. 7. Also, add 1 cup of jaggery and grind to a coarse powder. Make sure the mixture is moist. 8. Transfer the mixture to the large plate, add ½ tsp cardamom powder and 2 tbsp ghee. Ghee helps the ladoo bind. 9. Mix well and shape the ladoo. 10. Finally, enjoy Sesame Ladoo Recipe for a week.

title: Tirupati Vada
ingredients: 1.5 cups whole urad dal, 1.25 teaspoons cumin seeds, 1.25 teaspoons black pepper corn, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2.5 tablespoons water, 0.25 cup Ghee
instructions: 1. Wash black gram a few times and soak in lot of water for 4 to 5 hours. Drain it in a colander. 2. Make a fine powder of cumin and pepper along with salt. 3. Add dal and blend without water. Then sprinkle 2 to 3 tbsps water and blend to a coarse paste. 4. The mixture will be thick and sticky. 5. Grease a foil or banana leaf or clean moist cloth for making the vada. Dip your fingers in water. 6. Divide the dough to 12 equal parts and shape them to balls. 7. Place them on the cloth and spread them to thin vada with moist hands. Since the dough is sticky it sticks up to the hands. Make sure the vada are very thin else they will not cook well. 8. Heat ghee in a kadai or fry pan. Transfer the vadas to your greased fingers and drop in hot ghee. 9. Flip them and fry on both the sides on a medium flame until golden and crisp. 10. If the urad dal vadas puff up then press them down with the spatula. 11. Drain them on a kitchen tissue. 12. Tirupati vada are ready.

title: Tofu Bhurji Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 onion, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 tomato, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon cumin powder, 2 grams firm tofu, 2 tablespoons yogurt, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil butter ghee, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat a pan and add 2. 1 small onion fine chopped ( about ½ cup) 3. 1 green chili chopped (omit for low heat) 4. Sauté for 6 mins on a medium heat, until light golden. Stir in 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste or ¾ tsp each grated/ pressed. Saute for a minute until aromatic. 5. Add 6. 1 small tomato (⅓ cup fine chopped, deseed if preferred, optional) 7. Cook until completely broken down & mushy. Add 2 tablespoons yogurt if using and cook until evaporated, for 2 to 3 mins. 8. Crumble 200 grams (7 to 8 oz) firm tofu roughly with your fingers and add it. Don’t break down too much. Also note larger pieces won’t soak up the flavors well. 9. Saute on a medium heat for 6 mins until cooked through. It should still be slightly moist. Do not overcook else it will dry out. 10. Sprinkle Kashmiri red chili powder, garam masala, cumin powder and crushed kasuri methi, all over the tofu. Saute for 1 to 2 mins until the spices begin to smell nice. Turn off. 11. Taste test to adjust salt. Add ½ tablespoon olive oil or unsalted butter if it looks dry. I always add at this stage because it helps keep the tofu bhurji moist & enhances the flavor. 12. Optional – I usually add ¼ to ½ cup microgreens or 1 cup baby spinach at this stage. It’s not for flavor but for nutrition. Adding greens won’t change the flavor. If adding microgreens make sure your stove is off and do not cook them. They will wilt slightly in the residual heat. If using baby spinach cook for 2 to 3 mins until they wilt off. I don’t like using regular more mature spinach here. 13. If you are not using leafy greens, simply garnish with 2 tablespoons chopped coriander leaves. 14. Sprinkle ½ teaspoon chaat masala, kala namak or lemon juice if you want. Taste test first, before adding the above ingredients. I usually don’t add anything as it tastes good even without them. Serve tofu bhurji over toasted bread, roti, paratha or lemon rice.

title: Tofu Curry Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 2 grams extra firm tofu, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri Chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup onion, 45 grams tomatoes pureed, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 green chilli, 0.75 cup green peas, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 2 cashew nuts, 1.5 teaspoons Kashmiri chili powder, 0.25 teaspon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.25 teaspoons garam masala, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 cup water, 1 teaspoon maple syrup, 1 bay leaf 2 inch cinnamon 2 cardamoms - optional whole spices
instructions: 1. Wrap the tofu in a few kitchen towels to pat dry and dice them to 1 inch cubes. To a mixing bowl, add the spices to marinate - red chilli powder, salt, garam masala and kasuri methi. 2. Mix up the spices and coat each piece of tofu with the dry spices. Keep aside until used. 3. To use coconut milk, read notes below & leave out this step. If using cashews (or almond flour), add them to a small grinder and powder first. Pour half cup water and grind to a smooth puree. Keep this aside. 4. Heat oil in a pan and add the whole spices if using. When they begin to sizzle, add the onions and saute on a medium high heat until deep golden. 5. Reduce the heat to low . Add the ginger garlic and green chili. Saute for a minute, until aromatic. 6. Quickly stir in salt and all the ground spices - red chilli powder, garam masala, cumin powder and coriander powder. Take care not to burn. 7. Stir in the tomato puree and cook partially covered on a medium high heat until the tomatoes cook down and lose the raw flavor. 8. Pour the cashew/ almond along with hot water. To use coconut milk, omit/cut down the water. Mix well and bring to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat to medium and cover partially. Cook until traces of oil are visible over the curry. 9. Add the peas or any other grilled veggies and cook until soft and tender. 10. Meanwhile, heat a pan with 1 tablespoon oil. Spread the oil. When the oil is hot, reduce the heat to medium and place the tofu pieces. Fry them for 2 minutes, turn them gently to the other sides and fry. Turn off. Over frying can burn the spices. 11. When the curry turns thick, add maple syrup/sugar and taste test. Adjust salt if required and add the fried tofu. 12. Mix well and turn off the heat. Let rest for at least 15 mins before serving. Garnish tofu curry with some coriander leaves & a teaspoon of cashew cream or coconut milk.

title: Tofu Tikka Masala Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams extra firm pressed tofu, 0.25 cup thick yogurt, 0.5 teaspoon ginger paste, 0.5 teaspoon garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 teaspoon Kasuri Methi, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 tablespoon oil, 1 onion, 0.75 cup carrots, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 2 green cardamoms, 1 cup onion, 1.75 cups tomatoes, 0.3333333333333333 inch ginger, 2 garlic, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder less spicy kind, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 14 cashewnuts, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 cup water, 0.875 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoon cream, 1 green chili
instructions: 1. For best results, freeze extra firm tofu for 2 days and then defrost in the refrigerator. Drain the water, rinse & shake off. Wrap the tofu in kitchen towels, gently press down to remove any excess moisture, weigh it down with a cast iron skillet for 10 mins. Then dice them to 1 inch to make 9 portions. If you want to pan fry the tikkas, make thin slices of larger sizes. 2. You will require the following ingredients for Tofu Tikka: 3. grams (7 ounces) extra firm pressed tofu (cubed to 9 pieces) ½ teaspoon ginger paste or grated (or use 1 tsp ginger garlic paste) ½ teaspoon garlic paste or minced/crushed ½ tablespoon lemon juice (omit if your yogurt is sour) ¼ cup thick yogurt (dairy or plant, has to be thick enough to hold the tofu) ½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder or smoked paprika (less spicy kind) ½ teaspoon garam masala ½ teaspoon coriander powder ½ teaspoon chaat masala (or ¼ tsp ground cumin & ¼ tsp amchur) ½ teaspoon Kasuri Methi (dried fenugreek leaves) ½ tablespoon oil ¼ teaspoon salt (adjust to taste, as per your tofu) 4. Mix together all the ingredients except tofu. Taste test and adjust salt, depending on your tofu. 5. Remove 2 tbsps of marinade to a small bowl and add the tofu to the rest of the marinade. Gently coat them and spread in a single layer. Cover and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours and maximum 12 hours. 6. These are the ingredients you need to make the tikka masala: 7. 1¾ cups tomatoes sliced/ chopped – (225 g, 2 medium) 8. While the sauce simmers, preheat the oven to 460 F or 240 C for at least 15 mins. Line a deep baking tray with foil. To a mixing bowl, add the reserved marinade, ½ cup onions diced & layers separated and ¾ cup carrots thinly sliced or bell peppers diced. Keep the size of onions and bell peppers the same as tofu. Marinate them. 9. Thread onion, carrots/bell peppers & tofu alternately on the skewers. Place them in the tray. Make sure your tofu does not touch the baking pan at the bottom as they can easily break while turning the skewers. Transfer to the middle rack. Bake for 10 minutes, turn them to the other side and bake for 5 mins more (15 mins total). Keep the skewers close to one another, after turning them else tofu can rotate. Broil 1 to 2 mins for charred spots. 10. If you want to serve them as an appetizer, brush them generously with melted butter or ghee. Sprinkle some chaat masala or lemon juice and serve. 11. Spread a teaspoon oil/ghee in a wide pan. Heat on a medium high flame, coat each tofu piece with the marinade & place on the HOT (important) griddle. Cook on a medium flame on both the sides till the marinade dries up and tofu turns golden. Similarly place the bell peppers and marinated onions and fry until the marinade dries up.

title: tomato-onion rasam recipe
ingredients: 2 tomatoes, 1 medium onion, pods garlic, 1 tsp sugar, 0.5 tsp turmeric powder haldi, 1 curry leaves kadi patta, 2 strads coriander leaves dhania patta, 2 and tsp rasam powder, 1 chilli, 1 tsp mustard seeds, pinch of hing, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 red chilli, 5 tsp oil
instructions: 1. finely chop tomatoes, onion, green chilli, garlic, coriander leaves and keep them aside. 2. in a pan add oil. add mustard seeds, curry leaves, red chillies, urad dal and hing. 3. once mustard seeds splutters, add garlic. fry them for a minute or two till the raw smell of garlic goes away. 4. now add chopped onion and fry till they turn golden brown. 5. now add tomatoes, chilli and fry on medium flame. 6. add sugar and cook tomatoes on a medium flame. 7. now add salt to taste. 8. cook tomatoes till they turn mushy. now add water till you make thin waatery or rasam consistence. 9. now add 2 tbsp of rasam powder and mix well. 10. add chopped coriander leaves and turn off the flame. 11. your tomato onion rasam is ready. serve hot with steamed rice along with papad.

title: tomato bajji recipe | stuffed tomato bonda | stuffed aloo tomato bonda
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 onion, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 tbsp peas, 2 potato, 0.5 tsp aamchur, 2 tbsp coriander, 1.5 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 0.25 tsp ajwain, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 tsp salt, water, 6 tomato, oil, onion, chaat masala, sev
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, add ½ onion, 1 chilli and ½ tsp ginger paste. 2. saute slightly, until onions shrink. 3. now add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala and ½ tsp salt. 4. saute on low flame until the spice turns aromatic. 5. further, add 3 tbsp peas and saute for a minute. 6. now add 2 potato and cook until everything is well combined. 7. further, add ½ tsp aamchur and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 8. cool completely and aloo stuffing is ready. keep aside. 9. to prepare the besan batter, in a small bowl take 1½ cup besan and ¼ cup rice flour. 10. add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ¼ tsp ajwain, ¼ tsp baking soda and ½ tsp salt. 11. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 12. now add water and mix to form a lump-free batter. 13. prepare a thick flowing consistency besan batter. 14. to stuff the aloo mixture into the tomato, scoop out the seeds of tomato carefully. you can reserve the pulp and use to prepare the curry. 15. now tuff the prepared aloo mixture into tomato and level out. 16. dip the stuffed tomato into besan batter coating uniformly. 17. deep fry in hot oil, making sure there is enough space in kadai. 18. stir occasionally, and fry on medium flame. 19. fry until the pakoda turns golden brown and crisp. drain off over kitchen paper to remove excess oil. 20. now for serving, cut the bonda in the centre forming quarter. 21. top with 2 tbsp onion, pinch of chaat masala and 2 tbsp sev. 22. finally, enjoy aloo stuffed tomato bonda with tomato sauce or as it is.

title: Tomato Bath Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 3.5 tbsps fresh coconut, 0.75 inch ginger, 3 garlic cloves, 3 red chilies, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 6 tablespoons water, 0.625 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 1.5 tbsps ghee, 1 star anise, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1 onion, 1.25 cup tomatoes, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 cup green peas, 1.75 cups water, 1.5 tbsps mint leaves, 2 tbsps coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse the rice well under running water and drain completely. Chop tomatoes, onions, mint, coriander leaves and set aside. 2. To a grinder jar, add fresh coconut, red chilies, fennel seeds, ginger, garlic cloves, green cardamoms & red chilli powder. Pour 4 to 6 tbsps water and grind to a smooth paste. 3. Pour ghee or oil to a pressure cooker or a pot and heat it. Lower the heat and add all the whole spices – star anise, green cardamoms, cinnamon, cloves, cumin seeds and bay leaf. 4. When the spices begin to crackle, add onions and saute until transparent and the raw flavor has gone. 5. Add tomatoes and sprinkle salt. Saute till the tomatoes break down and become soft. 6. Throw in garam masala, coriander powder and the ground coconut paste. Saute this until aromatic and the oil begins to ooze out from the mixture. This takes about 5 to 6 minutes. 7. Add green peas, mint leaves and coriander leaves. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. 8. Pour water and mix well. Taste test and adjust the salt. The water has to be slightly salty. 9. Add drained rice and mix well. Cover the cooker with a lid and cook until you hear 2 whistles. If using a pot, cover and cook until the water is absorbed by rice and the grains are soft and fluffy. 10. When the pressure drops, open the lid. You will see all the masala on top of the rice. This is the coconut masala, mix it gently with the entire pot of tomato bath. 11. Serve tomato bath warm or hot with onion raita. 12. Wash and soak rice for at least 20 to 30 minutes. Drain and set aside later. 13. Cube potatoes and add them to a bowl filled with water. Chop other veggies too. Peel and Cube beetroot. set this aside, slice onions and chop tomatoes. 14. Pour oil to a pressure cooker or pot. Saute whole spices and fry till they turn fragrant. Add onions and fry till transparent. 15. Next add ginger garlic paste and fry till the raw smell goes off. Add tomatoes, salt and turmeric and fry till they turn mushy. 16. Then add pepper powder and red chili powder. Fry for 2 minutes or until the raw smell goes away. Add all the other veggies, fry for another 2 to 4 minutes. 17. Drain the water from the rice and add it along with coriander leaves. 18. To cook in pressure cooker , for basmati rice pour 2½ cups water. For normal rice use the same amount as you would use to cook it normally. Cook on a medium heat for 1 whistle. For aged rice and non-basmati, let the cooker whistle twice. 19. To cook in pot: Cover and cook on a low heat till the rice is fully done. If it is under cooked, you may pour little hot water and cook. 20. When the pressure releases, open the lid and fluff up tomato bath with a fork. Serve with yogurt or raita & papad.

title: Tomato Bath Recipe | Tomato Pulao Recipe | Tomato Rice Bath | Tomato Bhath
ingredients: 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 5 cloves 5 cloves, 2 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 3 chilli, 1 onion, 0.25 cup peas, 3 tomato, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp sat, 2 tbsp coriander, 2.5 cup water, 1 cup sona masuri rice
instructions: 1. Firstly, take 2 tbsp ghee in a pressure cooker. Add 1 tsp cumin, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, and 5 cloves. Saute until the spices turn aromatic. 2. Add 2 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 3 chilli and saute well. 3. Also, saute 1 onion till it softens. 4. Add ¼ cup peas and saute 2 minutes. 5. Now add 3 tomato and cook till tomato turns soft and mushy. 6. Further add ½ tsp turmeric,1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, and saute till the spices turn aromatic. 7. Pour in 2½ cup water. Mix and to a boil. 8. Add in 1 cup sona masuri rice and mix gently. 9. Cover and pressure cook for 3 whistles or until the rice is cooked well. 10. Once the pressure settles down, fluff the rice gently. 11. Finally, enjoy the Tomato Bath Recipe with raita.

title: tomato biryani recipe | thakkali biryani | tomato biryani in pressure cooker
ingredients: 1 tbsp ghee clarified butter, 1 bay leaf tej patta, 5 cloves lavang, 1 inch cinnamon dalchini, 1 star anise, 2 pods cardamom elachi, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 green chilli, 1 cup tomato puree, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, 1 tsp biryani masala, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 carrot, 2 tbsp peas matar, 1 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 cup coconut milk, 2 tbsp mint pudina, 1 cup water, 1 cup basmati rice
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pressure cooker heat ghee and saute spice. 2. add 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste and 1 green chilli. saute well. 3. now add 1 cup tomato puree and cook till it releases oil. 4. further add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp biryani masala and 1 tsp salt. saute well. 5. additionally, add vegetables of your choice. 6. now add 1 cup coconut milk and 1 cup water. mix well. 7. furthermore, add 1 cup basmati rice, soaked 20 minutes and give a good mix. 8. cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles on medium flame. 9. finally, serve tomato biryani with onion tomato raita or as it is.

title: tomato cheese sandwich recipe | cheese and tomato sandwich
ingredients: 2 bread slices, 1 tsp garlic mayonnaise eggless mayonnaise, 5 slices tomatoes, 3 tbsp mozzarella cheese, few lettuce leaves, 1 tsp mustard sauce, 2 tsp butter
instructions: 1. firstly, spread 1 tsp garlic mayonnaise over a bread slice. 2. further place 5 slices tomatoes, 3 tbsp grated mozzarella cheese and few lettuce leaves. 3. on another bread slice, spread 1 tsp mustard sauce. 4. grill the sandwich spreading butter. 5. finally, sever tomato cheese sandwich hot for breakfast.

title: tomato chitranna recipe | tomato rice recipe | tomoto chitranna
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil, 2 tbsp peanuts, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, pinch hing asafoetida, few curry leaves, 1 onion, 1 inch ginger, 2 tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp salt, 2.5 cup rice, 2 tbsp coconut, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and roast 2 tbsp peanuts on low flame. 2. roast until peanuts turn crunchy, set aside. 3. in the hot oil add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, ½ tsp cumin, pinch hing and few curry leaves. 4. splutter the tempering without burning. 5. now add 1 onion, 1 inch ginger and saute well. 6. also add 2 tomato and saute for a minute. 7. cover and cook until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 8. further, add ¼ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder and 1 tsp salt. 9. saute until spices turn aromatic. 10. add in 2½ cup cooked rice and mix gently. 11. cover and cook on low flame for 5 minutes or until flavours are absorbed. 12. add 2 tbsp coconut and 2 tbsp coriander. mix well. 13. finally, enjoy tomato chitranna with raita.

title: Tomato Chutney (Restaurant Style Recipe)
ingredients: 2 cups tomatoes, 0.75 cup onions, 3.5 garlic cloves, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 tablespoon chana dal, 1 tablespoon urad dal, 7 red chilies, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoon oil, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon black mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon urad dal, 1.5 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. Prep up the following ingredients 2. Chop 3 medium tomatoes to make 2 cups (330 grams, after preparing) 3. Rinse & pat dry 2 sprigs curry leaves (1 for the chutney + 1 to temper) (or for a different flavor use 10 mint leaves or 2 to 3 tender stalks of coriander leaves to make the chutney) 4. Measure ½ tablespoon chana dal (6 grams, Bengal gram or sub with 2 tbsp roasted peanuts. If using raw fry them and reserve aside) 5. You also need 6 to 8 low to medium heat dried red chilies (12 grams, I use Byadgi, or use ¾ to 1 tsp mild red chili powder) 6. To temper the chutney, you need 7. Heat 1½ tablespoon oil (22 ml) in a pan. Add urad dal, chana dal & red chilies. Fry them on a medium flame until light golden & aromatic, for 2 to 3 mins. 8. Add cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, sliced ginger and garlic. Fry them for a minute. 9. Add the onions and saute until they turn transparent, for 4 to 5 mins. Your lentils can burn if you cook on a high heat at this stage. If you feel so, you may deglaze the pan with a splash of water. I never do that though. 10. Add the chopped tomatoes, ¾ teaspoon salt and tumeric. 11. Saute until they break down for 5 to 7 mins. If you want you may cook them covered after 2 to 3 mins of sauteing. If your pan dries out, add a splash of water.

title: Tomato Cucumber Sandwich
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 1.5 tablespoons butter, 0.25 cup Greek yogurt, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli flakes, 0.625 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 1 tomato, 1 onion, 0.5 cucumber, 0.5 teaspoon Chaat masala
instructions: 1. Thick and firm slices of bread is good for making these sandwiches. Spread soft butter first over the bread slices as it is going to prevent the bread from turning soggy. If you plan to carry these to work, then toast the bread until crisp without applying butter. When it cools down smear the butter. This way the sandwich will keep good for few hours. 2. Spread hung curd or mayonnaise or cheese spread or cream cheese as desired. You can follow this post on how to make hung curd if you do not know how to make it. Alternately add half cup of homemade curd or dahi to a muslin cloth and squeeze off the excess whey. 3. Add herbs and spices. I used hot red chili flakes, black salt, pepper powder and chaat masala. Gently spread the spice powders evenly. 4. Place the slices of onions, tomatoes and cucumber. 5. Repeat sprinkling spice powders and herbs. I have used pepper powder, black salt and chaat masala powder. Make sure you add enough salt and spice at this stage. 6. Cover with another slice of bread and press down gently. Cut to squares or triangles. Serve tomato cucumber sandwich.

title: Tomato Curry Recipe
ingredients: 3 tomatoes, 0.875 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1.5 tablespoons Oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.375 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.25 cup coconut milk, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons almond meal
instructions: 1. Optional step: Boil tomatoes for 3 to 4 minutes in 2 cups water. This will loosen the skin. Once cool, remove the skin and discard. Discard the core as well and chop the tomatoes. If you want you can also deseed completely. 2. Optional: Skip this step if using almond meal. Toast the sesame seeds or peanuts on a low flame until aromatic. Cool and make a fine powder. If you do not have sesame or peanuts, replace with almonds or roasted gram. Set this aside. 3. Add oil to a pan and heat it. Add cumin and mustard. 4. When they begin to sizzle add curry leaves, green chili, garlic (optional) and hing. Let them fry for a minute or 2. 5. Add chopped onions and salt. Saute till they turn pink, not necessarily golden. 6. Add the chopped tomatoes and turmeric. Saute for about 3 to 4 minutes. 7. Cook covered till the tomatoes turn soft and completely mushy. 8. Next add red chili powder and almond meal (or sesame seeds powder or fried gram powder). Mix and cook for 2 to 3 minutes until the curry turns thick. 9. Add coconut milk and stir. Taste the curry and add more salt if needed. 10. When the tomato curry begins to bubble, Turn off the heat. Cooking for longer may coagulate the coconut milk if using homemade. 11. Serve tomato curry with rice & ghee or with roti.

title: Tomato Curry Recipe | Tomato Onion Curry | Pyaaz Tamatar Ki Curry | Thakkali Curry | Tomato Kura
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 3 onion, 2 tomato, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 bay leaf, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 3 cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 1.5 cup tomato puree, 2 tbsp cream, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil. add 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp kasuri methi, 1 bay leaf, 4 cloves, 3 cardamom and 1 inch cinnamon. 2. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 3. keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and ½ tsp garam masala. 4. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 5. now add 1½ cup tomato puree and mix well. 6. cover and cook until the gravy starts to release oil. 7. further, add 2 tbsp cream and mix well. 8. now add 1 tsp salt and 1 cup water. mix well adjusting the consistency as required. 9. meanwhile, in a pan heat 2 tsp oil and add 3 onions. 10. saute until the onions shrink slightly. 11. further, add 2 tomato and cook for 1 to 2 minutes or until the tomato softens a little. 12. transfer the fried onion and tomato to the gravy and mix well. 13. cover and simmer for 2 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 14. now add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 15. finally, enjoy onion tomato curry with roti.

title: Tomato Dosa recipe | Instant Tomato - Thakkali Dosai
ingredients: 2 tomato, 1 cup rice flour, 0.5 cup rava suji, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp salt, water, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 1 inch ginger, 1 tsp cumin, oil
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar take 2 tomato, 1 cup rice flour, ½ cup rava, ½ tsp chilli powder and 1 tsp salt. 2. Grind to smooth batter adding 2 cup water. 3. Transfer the batter to a large bowl. 4. add 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 1 inch ginger, grated, 1 tsp cumin and water. 5. Mix well preparing a watery consistency batter. 6. When the pan is super hot, pour the batter on the pan. 7. Spread 1 tsp oil and allow to roast for 2 minutes or until the dosa turns crisp. 8. Finally, enjoy the crispy Tomato Dosa Recipe with chutney.

title: Tomato Oats Recipe | Healthy Weight-Loss Oats Recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli, 1 onion, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.75 tsp chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, 2 tomato, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp peas, 4 cup water, 1.5 cup rolled oats, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp ghee. add 1 tsp cumin, 1 inch ginger, 2 chilli and saute until they turn aromatic. 2. Add 1 onion, 1 bay leaf and saute until the onion softens. 3. Further keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp garam masala and saute until the spices turn aromatic. 4. Now add 2 tomato, 1 tsp salt and cook till the tomato turn soft and mushy. 5. Add 2 tbsp peas and 4 cup water. Mix and get the water to a boil. 6. Add in 1½ cup rolled oats and mix well. 7. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the oats soften completely. 8. Add in 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 9. Finally, enjoy Tomato Oats Recipe with raita and pickle.

title: Tomato Pachadi Recipe (Andhra Style)
ingredients: 3.5 red chilies, 2 cups tomatoes, 0.75 tablespoon chana dal, 0.75 tablespoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon oil, 2 cloves garlic, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 red chili, 1 pinch cumin, 1 pinch mustard, 1 pinch urad dal, 1 pinch hing, 1 pod garlic
instructions: 1. Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan. Add red chilies, chana dal and urad dal. Roast them until they turn golden & aromatic. Red chilies should turn crisp. If using sesame seeds add them now and toast slightly. 2. Then add cumin & roast just for few seconds. Remove them and set aside to cool. 3. To the same pan, add tomatoes, garlic, salt and turmeric. Fry till the tomatoes turn completely soft and mushy. If needed cover and cook. Set aside to cool. 4. To a grinder add the roasted chilies, lentils and cumin. Powder them fine. 5. Add tomatoes and grind. If you want a traditional pachadi consistency, pulse a few times, scraping the sides. 6. Check the salt and spice now. if needed add more. 7. Add oil to the same pan and heat it. Add mustard cumin, red chili, curry leaves and hing. if you wish you can add a garlic pod too. when the curry leaves turn crisp. Turn off the stove. Add the pachadi to the pan and mix well. 8. Serve tomato pachadi with rice, any Indian breakfasts or bread.

title: Tomato Pappu Recipe (Andhra Tomato Dal)
ingredients: 0.5 cup split pigeon peas, 2 medium tomatoes, 1.5 green chilies, 1 cup water, 0.75 tablespoon tamarind, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tbsp Ghee, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon cumin, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 2.5 cloves garlic, 1 red chilli, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Add toor dal to a cooker or a large bowl. Rinse at least thrice. Drain the water. Pour 1 cup water to the dal. 2. Then add tomatoes, tamarind, green chilies and turmeric. 3. Pour 1½ cups water to the pressure cooker or instant pot and place the rack or bottom plate. 4. Place the dal bowl inside in your cooker and cover. If you want you can also cook the rice in the same cooker, for this simply rinse your rice in another bowl, pour water and place it over the dal bowl. 5. Pressure cook on a medium heat for 3 whistles. If you are coking directly in the cooker, then cook until you hear 2 whistles. If using Instant pot set the timer to 10 minutes. 6. Once the pressure drops open the lid. Add salt and mash the dal to slightly coarse or smooth texture to suit your taste. To adjust the consistency you can very well add more hot water. 7. Heat a pan with ghee or oil. Add cumin, mustard. When they begin to splutter, add red chilies, garlic and curry leaves. If needed you can add more green chili as well. 8. Fry till the leaves turn crisp. Add red chili powder & hing (optional). Quickly stir in the pan and pour the mashed dal to the tempering. Alternately you may pour the tempering to the dal. 9. Mix well and do not cook further to avoid splatters. Serve tomato pappu with with rice, ghee and avakaya. 10. Rinse and Soak the dal for 30 minutes. Drain the water. Pour 1 cup water and bring to a rapid boil. 11. Lower the heat and cook on a medium to low flame for 3 to 4 mins. Remove any froth that arises on top with a spoon and discard. Cook until the pappu is soft and completely cooked 12. If your pappu is undercooked, simply pour more hot water and cook until soft. 13. While the dal cooks, In another pan heat ghee or oil. When hot add mustard, cumin, red chilies and garlic. When the garlic turns aromatic, stir in curry leaves. 14. Stir in red chilli powder, turmeric & hing. Add the tomatoes and cook until they turn soft and pulpy. Add this to the soft cooked pappu. Mix well and taste test. Adjust salt as needed.

title: Tomato Pappu Recipe | Tomato Dal - Andhra Style | Pappu Tomato Curry
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 1 tsp ghee, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 onion, 3 tomato, 4 chilli, 0.5 cup tamarind extract, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 1 cloves garlic, 2 red chilli, few curry leaves, pinch hing
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a pressure cooker take 1 cup toor dal. You can even soak the dal for 15 minutes to hasten the cooking time. 2. Add 1 tsp ghee, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt and 3 cup water. 3. Cover and pressure cook for 5 whistles or until the dal is cooked well. 4. Once the pressure settles down, mash the dal slightly and keep aside. 5. In a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute ½ onion until the onion softens. 6. Add 3 tomato and saute for 2 minutes or until the tomato softens. 7. Further add 4 chilli, ½ cup tamarind extract, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder and 1 tsp salt. 8. Cover and cook for 5 minutes or until the tomatoes are soft and mushy. 9. Add in cooked toor dal and mix well. 10. Adjust the consistency, and boil for 5 minutes or until the flavour is absorbed well. 11. Now to prepare the tempering, heat 2 tbsp ghee. Add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 10 cloves garlic, 2 dried red chilli, few curry leaves and a pinch hing. 12. Splutter the tempering and fry until the garlic turns golden. 13. Pour the tempering over the tomato pappu and add 2 tbsp coriander. Mix well. 14. Finally, enjoy Tomato Dal or Tomato Pappu with hot steamed rice.

title: Tomato Pickle Recipe
ingredients: 5 grams tomatoes, 0.25 cup red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 27.5 grams tamarind, 15 grams garlic, 1 teaspoon methi seeds, 1 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 tablespoon oil, 2 tablespoons salt, 0.29166666666666663 cup oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.625 teaspoon mustard seeds, 5 cloves garlic, 1.5 red chilli, 0.25 teaspoon hing
instructions: 1. Make sure all your ingredients and cutlery are dry and moist free. Wash tomatoes under running water and leave them aside. 2. Skip this step if using tamarind pulp or want to add the tamarind directly to the pickle. Bring 6 tablespoons water to a rolling boil and add tamarind to it. Turn off the stove and let the tamarind soak in it. 3. On a very low flame, dry roast fenugreek seeds on a low flame till they turn deep golden but not burnt. They must emit a nice aroma. Transfer to a plate and cool. 4. If using mustard add them and dry roast on a low flame just for a minute or until they begin to pop but not burnt. Transfer to a plate and cool. 5. Once cool, powder the seeds to a fine powder together or separately (mustard & fenugreek) . I prefer to do them separately as it is easy to adjust the quantities while using. 6. Heat a heavy bottom pan with 1 tablespoon oil. Add the chopped tomatoes saute for 3 to 4 minutes and then cook covered until soft and mushy. 7.
