Meanwhile squeeze the soaked tamarind fully with your fingers. You should see most of the pulp in the water and the fibers separated or loosened. 8. When the tomatoes turn mushy and completely soft, place a strainer/colander over the pot and pour the tamarind pulp to it. 9. Using a steel spoon, press down the pulp, so much of the extract goes into the pan. This way pass all of the tamarind to the pan. Discard the fibers and the leftover pulp in the colander/strainer. 10. Cook this mixture until it becomes thick and begins to leave the pan. Turn off the stove. 11. Optional: If you want you may cool this completely and blend to a smooth paste along with garlic in a grinder jar. This step gives you smooth pickle. 12. Add chili powder, salt and turmeric to the tamarind and tomato mixture. Mix all of these until well blended. 13. Heat oil in a pan until hot enough. Turn down the heat to low, add mustard seeds. 14. When you hear the seeds splutter, add broken red chillies, garlic and curry leaves. 15. Let the garlic sizzle and change their color slightly then add hing. Pour this to the tomato pickle. 16. Mix well until the oil incorporates into the tomato pickle. Then continue to cook on a low heat for 5 to 8 mins. 17. Add methi and mustard powder. You may add in batches to see if you require all of the powder we made. I add all of it plus more salt at this stage. Turn off the heat. 18. Cool the tomato pickle completely. Store this in a air tight glass bottle. Keep it at room temperature for 2 to 3 weeks or up to 6 months in the refrigerator.

title: Tomato Rasam Recipe (Andhra Tomato Charu)
ingredients: 1.5 to 2 cups tomatoes - 300 grams chopped, 2.5 to 3 cups water, 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch methi seeds, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1 red chili, 4 cloves garlic, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 1 teaspoon tamarind, 1 Kashmiri red chili, 1.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 1.5 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1.5 tablespoons chana dal
instructions: 1. Skip this section if you have ready rasam powder. To make instant powder, on a low heat dry roast chana dal till deep golden & aromatic. 2. Then add coriander seeds, methi seeds, red chili and pepper corn. 3. Dry roast until aromatic. Add cumin seeds to the hot pan and fry till aromatic. 4. Cool them and make a fine powder. Keep this aside. 5. Heat a pot with oil. Add cumin, mustard & methi seeds. 6. When they begin to splutter, add red chili and hing. Add curry leaves and fry for a min. 7. Next add pureed or chopped tomatoes, salt and turmeric. Salt helps to cook tomatoes faster. 8. Fry for about 2 minutes. Cover and cook. Tomatoes should turn mushy and soft. 9. Next add the crushed garlic, tamarind, rasam powder and jaggery. Then mix well. 10. Pour water & bring it to a boil. Then add coriander leaves with tender stalks. Simmer for 3 to 5 mins or until slightly thick. 11. Taste the rasam and add more jaggery, tamarind, salt as needed to suit your taste. 12. I make it sweet, sour and slightly hot. To add more heat sprinkle some crushed pepper. 13. Add few more coriander leaves for fresh flavor. Turn off the stove. Keep the pot covered to retain the flavors. 14. Serve tomato rasam with rice or just serve it as an appetizer in your meal. 15. Place the whole tomatoes or halved in a bowl and cover the bowl. Place it over your rice bowl in your cooker. Pressure cook as you would cook your rice. Later remove the bowl. The skin loosens so you may remove it if you prefer. 16. Mash the tomatoes with a masher. 17. Follow all the instructions as mentioned above for method 1 - tempering, adding rasam powder, tamarind, jaggery and water. Let the water come to a boil and then pour all of the mashed tomatoes along with the liquid. 18. Let simmer for a while, 3 to 4 mins. Add coriander leaves. Serve tomaro rasam with rice.

title: Tomato Rice Recipe (Indian Style)
ingredients: 1.5 cups aged basmati rice, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 medium onion, 1 green chili, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon Turmeric, 2.5 cups water for cooker, 3 tablespoon coriander leaves finely chopped, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 12 whole cashewnuts, 1 bay leaf, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 3 green cardamoms, 3 cloves 3 cloves ((laung) )
instructions: 1. Add 1½ cup rice to a bowl and rinse it well a few times until the water runs clear. Pour fresh water and soak the rice for 30 mins. Then drain to a colander & set aside. 2. Heat a pressure cooker or a pot with 2 tbsps ghee or oil. 3. Add cashews and fry until golden. Remove to a plate & set aside. 4. Saute 3 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon piece, ¾ tsp cumin, 3 green cardamoms and 1 small bay leaf. 5. When they begin to crackle, add 1 finely chopped onion and 1 slit green chili. 6. Saute until the onions turn golden. Next saute 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste until a nice aroma comes out for 30 seconds. 7. Next add in 1 cup tomatoes, salt as needed and ¼ tsp turmeric. 8. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes. Cover & cook until the tomatoes break down & turn mushy. 9. Add ½ to 1 tsp red chili powder and ¾ to 1 tsp garam masala powder. 10. Mix and cook until the tomato masala turns completely soft and mushy. 11. At this stage the masala leaves the sides of the pan. Pour water & stir well. Taste the water and add more salt if needed. 12. Bring the water to a rolling boil. Add the drained rice. 13. Cook until the water has almost absorbed. When the rice is still soggy, lower the flame completely and cover with a lid until the tomato rice is cooked completely. 14. Sprinkle chopped coriander leaves and squeeze in some lemon juice. Tomato rice is ready to serve hot or warm with a raita. Garnish with cashews. 15. Stir and cover with the lid. Cook on a medium high heat. Allow to whistle once. When the pressure goes down, open the lid. Fluff up the tomato rice. 16. Soak rice for 30 mins. Press SAUTE button & pour oil to the inner pot of the instant pot. Then fry cashews until golden and remove them. Stir in the whole spices and then add onions. 17. Saute them until transparent. Add the ginger garlic paste & saute for 30 seconds. Add tomatoes and salt. Saute them until the tomatoes break down and turn mushy. 18. Add chilli powder, garam masala & turmeric. Saute for just 1 to 2 mins untilthe moisture from tomatoes almost dries up. 19. Next pour 1¾ cups + 2 tbsp water. Press CANCEL button. Scrape off any bits of spices stuck at the bottom. This prevents from getting a burn notice. 20. Stir & check the salt. Add more if needed. Then add the rice. Then secure the lid. Set the steam release handle/ value to sealing. Press PRESSURE COOK button (highpressure) for 5 mins. 21. When the tomato rice is done, Instant pot beeps. Wait for 2 mins & release the pressure manually with the help of a spoon by moving the steam release handle to venting from sealing. If you like softer rice then let the pressure release naturally for 7 to 8 mins. Then release the rest manually. 22. Sprinkle coriander leaves & fluff up the tomato rice with a fork. Garnish with cashews.

title: Tomato Rice Recipe | Instant Tomato Rice with Premix Paste | Thakali Rice
ingredients: water, 8 tomato, 1 red chilli, 0.25 cup oil, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 bay leaf, 3 pods cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 star anise, 0.5 tsp hing, 1 inch ginger, 4 cloves garlic, 1 chilli, 3 tbsp cashew, 0.5 onion, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder, 2 tsp salt, 2 tbsp mint, 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tomato
instructions: 1. firstly, in boiling water add 8 tomato and 10 dried red chilli. 2. boil for 5 minutes, or until the tomato softens and the skin separates. 3. cool completely, and grind to a smooth puree. keep aside. 4. in a large pan heat ¼ cup oil and 2 tbsp ghee. 5. add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 1 bay leaf, 3 pods cardamom, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 star anise and ½ tsp hing. 6. also add 1 inch ginger, 4 cloves garlic, 1 chilli and 3 tbsp cashew. 7. saute until the cashew turns golden brown. 8. now add ½ onion and saute until the onions soften slightly. 9. keeping the flame on low, add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder and 1 tsp salt. 10. saute until the spices turn aromatic. 11. further add 2 tbsp mint, 2 tbsp coriander and saute well. 12. add in prepared tomato puree and cook well. 13. also add 1 tomato, 1 tsp salt and mix well. 14. cover and cook for 5 minutes or until the oil separates from the sides. 15. tomato rice mix is ready, cool completely and store in a glass jar refrigerated upto a month. 16. to prepare tomato rice, in a large bowl take 4 cup boiled rice and add 2 tbsp instant tomato rice paste. mix well. 17. finally, enjoy tomato rice with raita and papad.

title: Tomato Saar Recipe | Konkani Style Tomato Curry
ingredients: 3 tomato, 4 cloves garlic, 4 red chilli, 1 cup coconut, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch hing, few curry leaves, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp jaggery, 4 cup water, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. Firstly, in a mixer jar, take 3 tomatoes, 4 garlic cloves, 4 dried red chillis, 1 cup of coconut, ½ tsp cumin and 1 tsp coriander seeds. 2. Grind to a smooth paste and keep aside. 3. In a kadai, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add 1 tsp of mustard, ½ tsp of cumin, a pinch of hing, and a few curry leaves. Splutter the tempering. 4. Add in the prepared masala paste 5. Add ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp salt and ½ tsp jaggery. 6. Cook till everything is well combined. 7. Add an additional 4 cups of water and mix well. 8. Boil well until the flavours are infused. 9. Now add 2 tbsp of coriander and mix well. 10. Finally, enjoy the Tomato Saar Recipe with hot steamed rice.

title: Tomato Salan Recipe | Bagare Tamatar Salan | Hyderabadi Tomato Curry
ingredients: 2 tbsp peanut, 2 tbsp sesame, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 cup water, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, pinch hing, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 onion, 1 cloves garlic, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, 2 cup water, few curry leaves, piece jaggery, 2 tomato, 2 chilli, 0.5 cup tamarind extract, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan dry roast 2 tbsp peanut until it turns crunchy. 2. add 2 tbsp sesame, 2 tbsp dry coconut, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin. 3. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 4. cool completely, and transfer to the mixer jar. 5. add ½ cup water and grind to smooth paste. keep aside. 6. in a pan heat 2 tbsp oil. splutter 1 tsp mustard, pinch hing and 1 tsp cumin. 7. add ½ onion, 10 cloves garlic and saute until onion softens. 8. further add ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala and 1 tsp salt. 9. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 10. also, add the prepared masala paste and cook until the oil separates. 11. now add 2 cup water, few curry leaves and small piece of jaggery. 12. mix well adjusting the consistency as required. 13. boil for 10 minutes, or until the oil separates. 14. now add 2 tomato, 2 chilli and ½ cup tamarind extract. 15. cover and boil for 5 minutes or until the tomato softens. 16. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy tomato curry with biryani or roti.

title: tomato sandwich recipe | tomato cheese sandwich | pav bhaji sandwich
ingredients: 1 tsp butter, 0.5 onion, 2 tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp pav bhaji masala, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tbsp coriander, bread, butter, green chutney, tomato slice, cheese slice, chaat masala
instructions: 1. firstly, in a pan heat 1 tsp butter and saute ½ onion. 2. add 2 tomato and saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 3. now add ¼ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp pav bhaji masala and ½ tsp salt. 4. saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. 5. add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. tomato stuffing is ready. 6. to prepare the sandwich, spread butter on 2 slices of bread. 7. also spread green chutney to make it masaledar. 8. further top with 1 tbsp of prepared tomato stuffing. 9. and place 2 slice of tomato and 1 slice of cheese. 10. sprinkle some chaat masala and cover with bread slice. 11. grill the sandwich to golden brown on both sides. 12. finally, enjoy tomato sandwich with tomato sauce.

title: tomato sauce recipe | tomato ketchup recipe | homemade tomato sauce
ingredients: 6 ripped tomatoes, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chili powder lal mirch powder, 3 tbsp sugar, salt to taste, 3 tbsp vinegar
instructions: 1. firstly, blanch the tomatoes in enough water. take well ripped tomatoes. 2. further cover and boil for 3 minutes or till the skin of tomatoes starts to separate. 3. cool down the tomatoes completely, and transfer to the blender. 4. puree the tomatoes without adding any water. 5. using a filter, extract the tomato puree separating seeds and skin of tomatoes. 6. further, stir and mix well. 7. boil the mixture well. 8. make sure to stir occasionally, to prevent from burning. 9. further continue to boil for 10 minutes. 10. add in ½ tsp chili powder, 3 tbsp sugar and salt to taste. 11. keep stirring till the tomato puree thickens. 12. also cook the mixture till the water is evaporated completely, and turns thick. 13. and in 3 tbsp vinegar, as they act as preservative. 14. mix well and check for seasoning. 15. finally, transfer the tomato sauce or tomato ketchup once cooled completely.

title: Tomato Soup Recipe with Fresh Tomatoes
ingredients: 5 grams Tomatoes cubed, 1 tablespoon olive oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 medium onion, 2 garlic, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon Salt, 1.5 cup water more if needed, 1 tablespoon sugar, 0.75 tablespoon corn starch, 0.5 teaspoon pepper, 0.875 teaspoon herbs, 2 stalks Basil, 2 tablespoon cream, 1 carrot
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan. Add bay leaf, garlic and onions. Saute all of these on a high flame for 3 to 4 mins. 2. Then add in tomatoes and salt. Stir and cook until soft. 3. Next add in basil and cook until tomatoes turn completely mushy. Turn off and cool. 4. Discard the basil & bay leaf. 5. Add the mushy tomato mixture along with the juices to a blender jar. 6. Pour 1 cup water or stock and blend smooth. 7. Place a sieve over a pot & transfer the blended tomato soup to it. 8. Filter and discard the leftovers. Bring it to a boil on a medium heat & simmer for 2 to 3 mins. 9. Meanwhile stir in corn starch in half cup water and add it gradually to the boiling tomato soup. 10. Stir constantly and boil until slightly thick. 11. Add sugar & more salt if desired. Simmer until it reaches a desired consistency. 12. Next add herbs & pepper. Cover the soup & turn off the stove. 13. Allow it to cool down a bit before adding the cream. If you prefer to use cashew nuts, soak them in ¼ cup hot water for 30 minutes. Add them to a small chutney grinder along with the soaked water and puree until you get a thick and smooth cream. If needed add a few tablespoons more water for consistency. Use this to top your tomato soup while serving. 14. Transfer tomato soup to serving bowls. Garnish with herbs & cream. Enjoy with croutons. 15. Spread butter on the bread slices and toast them on a medium heat until crisp. 16. Then cut them to bite sized pieces.

title: Tomato Thokku Recipe
ingredients: 5 grams ripe tomatoes, 1 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 tablespoon Ginger, 2.5 tablespoons Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon mustard powder, 0.25 teaspoon roasted methi seeds powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 1.5 tbsp oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large pan and add ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds and 1 tablespoon fine chopped ginger. Let fry until the ginger turns fragrant without burning. 2. Immediately add chopped tomatoes and salt. Saute on a high heat for 2 to 3 minutes. Cover and cook until the tomatoes have softened completely. 3. Later remove the lid and cook until most of the juices evaporate and tomato mixture turns thick. (Check the stepwise pics in the post for consistency) 4. Stir in jaggery, turmeric, red chilli powder, mustard powder & roasted methi powder. If you don't like the flavors of mustard and methi, skip them. 5. Taste test and add more salt & red chilli powder if you want. Cook the tomato thokku for 2 mins and turn off. 6. In a small pan heat 1 to 2 tablespoons oil. Add mustard seeds and half tablespoon ginger or garlic (optional). Let the ginger turn fragrant, add curry leaves and hing. 7. Pour this over the hot tomato thokku. Give a good stir and cool completely. Store in a glass jar . Tomato thokku tastes best the next day after developing the flavors. 8. Store in the refrigerator for a couple of months, but tastes best for a month. It keeps good at room temperature for 2 days.

title: Tomato Upma
ingredients: 0.5 cup semolina, 1.25 cup water, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.25 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 cashews, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 Pinch hing, 1 onion, 2 pinches turmeric, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 2 tablespoons Coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Dry roast rava on a medium heat until it turns crunchy & aromatic. Stir constantly to prevent burning. 2. While it toasts, Wash, deseed and chop tomatoes. Slice onions and green chilies. 3. Transfer the rava to a bowl. 4. Heat the same pan with oil or ghee. 5. Add cumin, mustard, dal and cashews. Saute all together until the lentils become golden. 6. Then add onions, curry leaves and green chilies. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. Sprinkle hing. 7. Add chopped tomatoes, turmeric and salt. Saute for 2 mins. Cook covered on a medium heat until they turn mushy. Open the lid and saute on a medium high heat until all of the raw flavor vanishes from tomatoes. 8. Pour water and bring it to a boil. Taste the water and adjust the salt. The water must be slightly salty. 9. When the water comes to a boil, turn the fire to medium. Slowly pour the rava in a stream stirring with the other hand using a ladle. Stir well to check there are no lumps. 10. Cook covered on a medium heat until the water is absorbed. 11. Stir well. Add some more ghee (optional) & chopped coriander leaves. 12. Serve tomato upma hot or warm.

title: Toor Dal (Arhar Dal) Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 2 cups water 1 cup more to adjust, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.875 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 red chilies, 0.25 teaspoon hing, 1 sprig curry leaves, 3 garlic, 1.5 green chili, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add toor dal to a strainer and rinse under running water, rubbing with your fingers. Drain completely & add to a bowl. Pour fresh water and add turmeric. Place the bowl in the cooker and cover the bowl & cooker. (no cooker? check recipe notes) 2. Pressure cook on a medium flame, for 3 whistles in a stovetop cooker or in an instant pot for 10 mins. When the pressure drops, open the lid. 3. Mash the cooked toor dal lightly with the back of a ladle or a masher or a whisk, until the dal looks thick and creamy without water separation. Add ¾ cup hot water (if pressure cooked) to bring to a consistency. 4. Heat ghee or oil in a small pan and add mustard, cumin & red chili. When the seeds begin to pop, add hing, curry leaves, ginger, garlic & green chili. 5. Fry until the garlic smells aromatic, for a minute or 2. Add red chili powder, tomatoes & salt. Cook until the tomatoes break down and become mushy. 6. When you see fats separating from the tomatoes, pour ¼ cup water and cook until the water evaporates. Transfer this to the toor dal and mix well. Add coriander leaves if you like. Adjust salt to your preference. 7. Sprinkle lemon juice if you want and serve toor dal hot with rice or roti. Store the leftovers in the refrigerator for 2 days. Reheat until bubbling hot with a splash of water.

title: Turmeric Latte Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup whole milk, 1.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon cinnamon, 1.5 pinches black pepper, 0.25 inch ginger, 0.25 teaspoon vanilla extract, 1.25 tablespoons maple syrup
instructions: 1. To a sauce pan, add ginger (optional), black pepper, cinnamon, cardamom, black pepper and turmeric. 2. Optional step for a stronger flavor: Pour ¼ cup water. Slowly bring it to a boil and simmer for 3 to 4 mins. 3. Pour milk and whisk well. Add maple syrup or sugar and vanilla extract. 4. On a medium flame, heat until the milk begins to steam. Do not boil. When you see the steam coming from the milk, turn off. Remove from heat. Ideally temperature should be 145 to 155 F or 62 to 68 C. 5. You can froth the milk in 3 ways depending on your choice. 6. Froth in the Sauce Pan: Using a hand whisk, begin to whisk the milk until frothy and foamy. To use a electric mixer, start on a low speed and then switch over to medium speed. Make sure your sauce pan is deep enough or use a tall jar, else it can get messy & the milk can spill all over. Leave out the turmeric that settles at the bottom. 7. Froth in a Mason Jar: Strain the warm milk to the jar and secure it tightly with a lid. Shake the jar continuously until you see froth on top. This is a bit of arm work and takes about 5 mins. 8. Using a Milk Frother: Strain the turmeric infused milk into a deep/tall container or jar. Froth the milk as desired. It only takes about 25 to 30 seconds. 9. Serve your Turmeric Latte immediately.

title: Turmeric Milk Recipe (Haldi Doodh)
ingredients: 1 cup whole milk, 0.875 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper 1 to 2 pinches for kids, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.125 teaspoon green cardamom, 0.125 teaspoon cinnammon, 1 pinch dried ground ginger, 1 clove 1 clove ((crushed))
instructions: 1. Traditional turmeric milk is made by bringing Cow's milk to a boil first. Pour milk to a pot and bring it to a gentle boil. If you want to use all the other optional spices, add them and boil for 2 mins. (read my notes below) 2. Turn off and cool down the milk to a slightly hot temperature. Add ground black pepper (powder) and turmeric. Mix well. 3. Transfer turmeric milk to a cup and consume while still warm. You may filter/strain the milk if you use other whole spices. Add sweetener if required. If using honey add it only when the milk comes down to warm temperature.

title: Turmeric Rice Recipe (Indian Yellow Rice)
ingredients: 1 cups aged basmati rice, 1 tablespoon unsalted butter, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1.5 to 2 cups stock, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon pepper, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 2 inches cinnamon, 1 strand mace, 1 star anise, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.25 cup nuts raisins
instructions: 1. Rinse rice well a few times. Soak it for at least 15 mins. Drain the rice and set aside. 2. On a medium to low flame, heat butter in a pot. Add ginger garlic and saute for 30 to 60 seconds without burning or browning the garlic. 3. Add all the whole spices or garam masala, pepper and turmeric. 4. Add drained rice and regulate the flame to high. Fry the rice for about 2 mins stirring occasionally. Avoid stirring often as the rice grains will break. 5. Pour the stock or water & add salt. Taste test the water and add more salt as required. Bring the water to a rolling boil. 6. Simmer and cover the pot. Cook on a low heat until the rice is fully cooked. 7. When done fluff up the turmeric rice with a fork and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve with a curry, dal or a raita. 8. Press saute button and add butter to the steel insert of the instant pot. 9. When the butter melts, add all the whole spices and saute for 30 seconds. 10. Saute ginger, garlic, turmeric and pepper for 30 to 60 seconds. 11. Then add the drained rice & fry for a minute. 12. Pour 1¼ stock or water along with salt. Stir and check salt. 13. Press cancel button & secure the instant pot with the lid. Position the steam release handle to sealing. 14. Press pressure cook button (manual in older Ips) & set the timer to 5 minutes. 15. When the instant pot beeps, press cancel and let the pressure release naturally 10 mins. 16. Fluff up the turmeric rice and garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Turmeric Shots Recipe
ingredients: 17.5 grams turmeric root, 0.25 cup water, 6 tablespoons water, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper corn, 4 teaspoons honey, 0.25 cup lemon juice, 0.75 teaspoon cold pressed avocado oil
instructions: 1. Rinse fresh turmeric root under running water. Scrub it well with a brush to get rid of any mud deposits. Rinse well and peel it with a spoon or a butter knife. Slice the root. 2. Optional - heating turmeric enhances the bioavailability. So if you want add the turmeric slices to a pot along with ½ cup water. Boil until the water reduces to half, for about 5 to 7 mins and cool completely. 3. Add to a blender jar with ¼ cup water and black pepper corn. Blend until smooth. 4. Place a fine mesh strainer over a bowl and strain the turmeric puree. OR You can also use a cheese cloth. Using a wooden spoon, extract the liquid to the bowl. You will see a lot of smooth turmeric makes through the strainer. Use the leftovers in the strainer in your curry, soups, smoothies etc. 5. To make the turmeric shots, pour 1½ tablespoons slightly hot water to each shot glass and stir in 2 tablespoons of the liquid turmeric. 6. Mix well and make sure it is luke warm. If it is cold, you may add little more hot water. Stir in honey until well combined. Consume turmeric shot immediately. 7. Optionally you may add ⅛ teaspoon avocado oil and 1 tablespoon lemon juice or 1 teaspoon lime juice to each serving. If you prefer orange juice you may add as much as you want. 8. Pour 1½ to 2 tablespoons luke warm water to each shot glass. Stir in half teaspoon ground turmeric, ⅛ teaspoon freshly crushed black pepper and 1 teaspoon honey. 9. Stir very well and consume turmeric shots immediately. If you want you can add lemon juice and healthy fats.

title: Turmeric Tea Recipe (Golden Tea with No-Brew Option )
ingredients: 0.375 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon black pepper, 0.75 teaspoon ghee, 1.25 cup water, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon honey
instructions: 1. You can make turmeric tea either with ground turmeric or with fresh turmeric rhizome, which you will peel to cut down the bitter taste. Slice or chop 1 inch root or use ¼ to ½ teaspoon ground turmeric/ powder. Crush or crack 3 to 4 black pepper corn. Choose your fats – ghee or cold pressed oil like extra virgin coconut oil, olive oil or avocado, depending on your diet. Pepper and ghee help in better absorption. I prefer to use ghee for flavor and fats prevent dehydration. 2. Slice or chop half inch fresh ginger (or use ¼ teaspoon dried ground ginger). I am using lemon juice here but you can also use ¼ orange juice. 3. Mix together 1¼ cup of water, turmeric, black pepper, ginger and fats. If you want you can also add the ghee/oil later just before straining the tea. 4. Mix well. 5. Optionally If you want you can use crushed spices like – 1 cardamom, 1 clove, 1 inch True cinnamon (not cassia), ¼ teaspoon each of cumin, carom seeds and fennel seeds. All these spices work on the digestive and respiratory system. They also help to detox the body. You need to coarsely crush the spices using a mortar pestle before adding to the pot. 6. Bring the mixture to a boil and reduce the heat to medium. Ground turmeric requires only 4 mins, and chopped turmeric requires about 8 mins of boiling. If using spices, you need to boil for 10 mins. 7. Remove from the heat and strain the turmeric tea. 8. Press down the ingredients to extract all of the turmeric goodness. For those who are put off by the idea of drinking nothing but turmeric & spices because of its strong flavor, you are in luck. There are plenty of add-ins that both enhance the taste and the nutritional content of the tea. 9. You may add jaggery or palm jaggery while boiling. Squeeze in some lemon juice for a citrusy flavor and tang. This tea is best served hot or at least warm. You can also stir in organic honey to the warm turmeric tea (not to the hot tea).

title: tutti frutti burfi recipe | tutti frutti sangam barfi | tutti frutti sweet
ingredients: 3 cup milk powder, 0.5 cup ghee, 1 cup sugar, 0.75 cup water, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 0.25 cup tutti frutti
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large bowl take 3 cups of milk powder. i have used unsweetened full cream milk powder. 2. add ½ cup ghee and mix well using a spoon. mix gently making sure there are no lumps in the mixture. keep aside. 3. in a large kadai take 1 cup sugar and ¾ cup water. 4. stir well until the sugar dissolves completely. 5. continue to boil till the 1 string consistency of sugar syrup is attained. 6. now keeping the flame on low, add milk powder mixture. 7. keep stirring until the milk powder is well combined in sugar syrup. 8. continue to cook on low flame until the mixture turns silky smooth consistency. 9. further, add 1 tsp vanilla extract and ¼ cup tutti frutti. 10. mix well until the mixture starts to hold the shape and separates the pan. you can also check by making a small ball and it is perfect if the mixture is non-sticky and holds shape. 11. now transfer the mixture into the tray lined with baking paper. 12. top with 4 tbsp tutti frutti and press to level it up. 13. rest for 1 hour or until it cools completely. 14. now cut into pieces and ready to serve. 15. finally, enjoy tutti frutti burfi for a week when stored in the refrigerator.

title: Tutti frutti cake recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups all-purpose flour, 1 tablespoon all-purpose flour, 0.5 cup tutti fruiti, 1.25 teaspoon baking powder, 0.5 teaspoon baking soda, 1 pinch Salt, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.5 cup melted butter, 27 ml milk, 1.25 tablespoon vinegar, 0.875 tablespoon vanilla extract
instructions: 1. Grease a 8 by 4 inch loaf pan or a 7 to 8 inch cake tray. 2. Dust with flour evenly or line a parchment paper. Set this aside. 3. Preheat the oven to 170 C or 340 F for at least 15 mins. If using an OTG, top and the bottom heating elements must be On. 4. Add tutti frutti to a bowl and sprinkle 1 tbsp flour. 5. Coat them well with flour. Set this aside. 6. Measure milk to a bowl. I warmed up the milk and used. It is good not to use very cold milk. 7. Add lemon juice or vinegar to the milk. Stir well and set aside. 8. Place a sieve over a mixing bowl. 9. Fluff up the flour in the jar with a fork. Spoon it to the measuring cup and level the top with a straight edged knife or spoon. Add it to the sieve. 10. Add baking powder, baking soda and salt to the sieve. 11. Sieve it to the bowl. Sieve the flour again. 12. Make a well in the center & Keep this aside. 13. To the milk bowl, add sugar and vanilla extract. 14. Pour melted butter or oil. Mix everything well until the sugar has dissolved. 15. Pour this to the flour. 16. Gently mix with a spatula or whisk until the flour is just combined well. Do not over mix the batter. 17. The batter will be of ribbon consistency at this stage. 18. Add in the tutti frutti. Gently stir in only a couple of times. Do not over do this as the tutti fruttis will settle down. 19. Pour this to the cake pan. Knock it a few times to the counter. If desired you can sprinkle some more tutti fruttis on top. 20. Brush with milk on top to get a golden top. 21. Bake for 40 to 45 mins if using a 8 by 4 inch loaf pan. 22. A cake tester or skewer inserted comes out clean when it is baked completely. 23. Cool it for 15 mins on a wire rack and then invert it on to the same rack. 24. Cool completely before slicing. Eggless tutti frutti cake is ready to serve.

title: tutti frutti recipe | how to make tutti frutti | tutty fruity
ingredients: 5 grams raw papaya papita, 7 cup water, 2 cup sugar, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 4 drops food colour
instructions: 1. firstly, take raw papaya and peel the skin of it. 2. cut the papaya into small cubes 3. boil the papaya cubes in 4 cup water for 5 minutes. 4. cook until the papaya cubes turn into semi-transparent. 5. drain off the water and keep aside. 6. now in a large kadai take 2 cup sugar and 3 cup water. 7. further, add semi cooked raw papaya cubes and stir. 8. boil for 20 minutes stirring in between. 9. make sure to check for 1 string consistency of sugar syrup and papaya to turn soft yet retain its shape. 10. turn off the flame and add 1 tsp vanilla extract. mix well. 11. divide the cooked papaya cubes into 3 parts along with sugar syrup. 12. add 4 drops of red, yellow and green food colour to each part and mix well. 13. allow to soak for 12 hours or a day making sure papaya absorbs all the colour. 14. now drain off the sugar syrup and allow them to dry over kitchen towel. 15. once the tutti-frutti dries off completely it will not be sticky. mix the colours together. 16. finally, tutti frutti is ready to consume immediately or refrigerate for later use.

title: udupi chitranna recipe | kayi sasive chitranna | masala chitranna recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup coconut, 0.5 tsp mustard, 2 kashmiri red chilli, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 1 tbsp coconut oil, 1 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 1 kashmiri red chilli, few curry leaves, 2 tbsp peanuts, 2 tbsp tamarind extract, 0.5 tsp jaggery gud, 0.5 tsp salt, 3 cup cooked rice
instructions: 1. firstly in a blender take ½ cup coconut, ½ tsp mustard, 2 dried kashmiri red chilli and ¼ tsp turmeric. 2. blend to a coarse paste without adding any water. 3. now heat kadai with 1 tbsp of coconut oil. 4. add 1 tsp mustard, ½ tsp urad dal, 1 dried red chilli, few curry leaves and 2 tbsp peanuts. 5. saute and splutter on medium flame. 6. further, add 2 tbsp tamarind extract, ½ tsp jaggery and ½ tsp salt. cook well. 7. now add prepared masala paste and saute for 2 minutes 8. further, add 3 cup cooked rice and mix gently. 9. finally, serve udupi chitranna / masala chitranna hot or pack to your lunch box.

title: udupi saaru recipe | udupi tomato saaru | udupi rasam recipe
ingredients: 2 tbsp coconut oil, 0.5 tsp methi fenugreek seeds, 0.25 cup coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 25 red chilli, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp hing asafoetida, 1 to mato, 1 chilli, 1 tbsp jaggery gud, 0.5 tsp turmeric, few curry leaves, 1 cup tamarind extract, 1 tsp salt, 4 cup water, 2 cup toor dal, 3 tsp udupi saaru pudi, 2 tbsp coconut, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 tsp coconut oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 red chilli, pinch hing asafoetida, few curry leaves
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai add 1 tomato, 1 chilli, 1 tbsp jaggery, ½ tsp turmeric, few curry leaves, 1 cup tamarind extract and 1 tsp salt. 2. cover and boil for 10 minutes. boil until the tomatoes turn mushy and tamarind is cooked well. 3. further, add 4 cup water and 2 cup toor dal. 4. mix well adjusting consistency as required. 5. further, add 3 tsp prepared udupi saaru pudi and mix well. 6. boil for a minute making sure the flavours are well absorbed. do not over boil once the saaru pudi is added. 7. add in 2 tbsp coconut, 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 8. pour the tempering over saaru and mix well. 9. finally, enjoy tomato saaru with hot steamed rice.

title: Ugadi Pachadi
ingredients: 1.5 cup water, 2 tbsp green unripe mango, 3 sprigs neem, 1 pinch salt, 3 tbsp Jaggery, 1 pinch pepper powder, Tamarind, banana, Putnalu, Cashews, Raisins
instructions: 1. Wash tamarind and soak it in ½ cup warm water till it softens. 2. Traditionally only fresh neem flowers are used to make ugadi pachadi. But if you live in a place where you don't get fresh flowers then use the dried ones. 3. While the tamarind soaks, pluck the flowers from neem sprigs. Or alternate quick method: add the neem sprigs to a thin cloth, bring the edges of the cloth together so that they don’t fall off from the cloth. 4. Hold the edges tightly and hit the cloth on to the kitchen counter several times. Neem flower or petals get separated from the sprigs and fall of in the cloth, collect them and set aside. 5. Add grated jaggery to another one cup water, stir till it melts off. Filter it through a coffee strainer to another bowl, filter the tamarind pulp or water as well to the same bowl. 6. Next add rest of the ingredients. Mix well. 7. Do not taste it if you intend to offer it to god. Offer ugadi pachadi as naivedyam. 8. Share ugadi pachadi with your family members & friends.

title: ukkarisida akki rotti | akki ubbu rotti | soft akki roti | soft rice roti
ingredients: 2 cup water, 1 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 cup rice flour akki hittu
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai take 2 cup water, 1 tsp oil and ½ tsp salt. 2. get the water to a rolling boil. 3. now add 2 cup rice flour and mix gently. do not worry if there are lumps. 4. once the water is well absorbed, cover and simmer for 2 minutes. 5. now the mixture is super moist. 6. transfer the rice flour mixture to a large bowl. 7. when the mixture is still hot, start to knead by dipping hand in the water. 8. knead to form a smooth dough. dipping hand in water prevents them from burning. 9. form a smooth and soft dough. 10. now pinch a ball sized dough and knead again. 11. dust the rolling board with rice flour to prevent from sticking. 12. also, dust the dough with rice flour and start to roll gently. 13. roll to uniform thickness dusting rice flour if required. 14. now transfer the rolled rotti to a hot tawa. 15. wipe off the flour using a wet cloth. note, you need to wipe off only one side. 16. once the base is partially cooked, flip over and cook. 17. cook both sides partially as we will be cooking directly on the flame. 18. now place on the flame directly, and allow to puff up. 19. finally, ukkarisida akki rotti recipe or ubbu rotti is ready to enjoy with ennegayi.

title: Ulavacharu Recipe
ingredients: 1 kg horse gram, 3 liters water, 5 grams tamarind, 0.625 cup water, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons oil, 0.875 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.875 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 red chilli, 9 garlic cloves, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 onion, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon Red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.875 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Add horse gram to a large pot and pour lots of water. A lot of times I use my cooker for this. I rinse, soak and cook in the same cooker. Rinse them well rubbing with both your hands. Drain the water. 2. Repeat rinsing the same way at least 4 to 5 times. Pour 3 liters of fresh water and soak them overnight. 3. While you soak, cover the pot with a clean cotton cloth and not with a lid. This helps the air to circulate and won’t make the horse gram slimy later. 4. The next morning transfer the horse gram along with the water and pressure cook for 13 minutes in an Instant pot. If using the traditional cooker, cook for 2 whistles on a medium heat and then lower the heat. Continue to cook for 50 mins. 5. When the pressure releases, open the lid and check if the lentils are well cooked. They should be soft. Strain the lentils using a strainer and separate the stock and the lentils. 6. While the lentils cook, bring half cup to a boil and add 50 grams tamarind. If using tamarind with seeds you will need more. Set this aside. When it cools. Squeeze the pulp well. 7. Heat a pan with oil and add mustard, cumin and dried chilli. When the spices splutter, add green chili, curry leaves and cloves. Saute for 2 mins until aromatic. 8. Add onions and saute until transparent. The onions should lose the raw flavor. 9. Pour the strained lentil stock. Add turmeric, coriander powder and red chilli powder. Mix and boil until it reduces to half. This takes about 20 mins. 10. Then add the thick tamarind paste/ pulp as required and mix well. Add salt and mix well. 11. Let simmer until thick. The total time takes about 1 hour for me to reach the right consistency. This may vary depending on your stove and pot used. 12. Taste test and adjust salt and tamarind as required during the simmering. 13. Turn off when the ulavacharu is thick yet of flowing and pouring consistency. It will thicken further upon cooling. 14. Serve ulavacharu hot topped with butter or cream. Goes well with only plain steamed rice. The lentils are usually fed to the cattle. It also makes a good compost. The lentils can also be tempered and served as a side.

title: ulta vada pav recipe | street style inside out vada pav recipe
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 2 clove garlic, 0.5 tsp ginger paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 2 potato, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 tsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp coriander, 2 cup besan gram flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp chilli powder, pinch hing, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 cup water, 0.25 tsp baking soda, pav, green chutney, garlic chutney, oil
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tsp oil. add 1 tsp mustard, pinch hing, 2 chilli, 2 clove garlic and ½ tsp ginger paste. 2. saute until the mustard splitters. you can also add curry leaves in tempering. 3. now add ¼ tsp turmeric and saute until the turmeric turns aromatic. 4. further add 2 potato, ½ tsp salt and mix well. 5. turn off the flame and add 2 tsp lemon juice and 2 tbsp coriander. 6. mix well and aloo stuffing is ready. 7. firstly, in a large bowl take 2 cup besan, ¼ cup rice flour, ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, pinch hing and ½ tsp salt. 8. mix well making sure everything is well combined. 9. further, add water as required and prepare a smooth lump-free batter. 10. now add ¼ tsp baking soda and mix well. besan batter is ready. 11. firstly, slice the pav in half. 12. spread green chutney on one side and garlic chutney on the other. 13. also, place a ball sized aloo masala and press gently. 14. cover the pav and is ready to coat with besan batter. 15. now dip the pav in besan batter and deep fry in hot oil. 16. make sure to place on oil for 5-6 seconds and then flip over. this helps the top to fry a little and hold the batter. 17. fry on both sides until it turns golden brown and crisp. 18. now drain off over kitchen towel and cut it into half. 19. finally, enjoy ulta vada pav recipe with fried chilli and chutney.

title: Upma Recipe (Rava Upma)
ingredients: 1 cup Rava, 2.25 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon split urad dal, 1.5 teaspoon chana dal, 2 tablespoons peanuts, 1 cashews, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida, 0.25 cup onion, 1.5 green chili, 0.5 inch ginger, 3 cups water, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2 teaspoon ghee, lemon juice, 0.5 cup mixed vegetables
instructions: 1. Roast rava: On a medium heat, dry roast rava in a pan, stirring often until crunchy and aromatic. Do not brown it. Remove to a bowl and keep aside to cool down. 2. Optional - Roast peanuts to garnish - Fry peanuts in 1 teaspoon oil until golden, aromatic and crunchy. Reserve aside to garnish. Soak dal - If you like your dal softer, you may soak them in ¼ cup hot water for 10 mins, drain & pat dry before using. 3. Temper the spices: Pour oil to the hot pan & add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, urad dal & chana dal. Fry until the dal turns light golden. Add cashews and fry until they turn golden & aromatic. Stir in hing, green chilies, ginger and curry leaves. 4. Saute for a minute & add chopped onions. Saute until transparent & add mix vegetables (optional) if using. Stir and cook covered until slightly tender. 5. Boil water: Pour water and add salt. Mix and taste test the water. It has to be slightly salty. If required add more and bring the water to a rolling boil. 6. Make upma: Lower the heat. With one hand pour rava in a slow stream to the boiling water, while you incorporate it into the water with the other hand. (check video). Be quick at this stage. Stir everything once to check any lumps. Break up if any. 7. Cover and cook on the lowest flame until the water is completely absorbed. This just takes 2 to 4 mins depending on the kind of pot or pan used. Add ghee and give a quick stir. Turn off and cover back to rest for 5 mins. 8. Stir in the lemon juice and serve rava upma plain or with spiced peanut podi, chutney, pickle or curd. Garnish with roasted/fried peanuts.

title: Urad Dal Recipe (Black Dal)
ingredients: 0.5 cup black urad dal, 0.5 cup chana dal, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 1 black cardamom, 1 bay leaf, 0.75 cup onion, 1 green chili, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 1.25 cup tomatoes, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 3 cups water, 2.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 tablespoon kasuri methi
instructions: 1. The process of making this dish starts by rinsing and soaking the lentils overnight or for a minimum of 7 to 8 hours. This helps them cook faster, cuts down the phytic acid in the lentils and is easier for digestion. You will add ½ cup (100 grams) urad dal and ½ cup (100 grams) chana dal to a large bowl, rinse them well, rubbing with your hands. Rinse at least 4 to 5 times and soak them in plenty of water. 2. It is normal for the black dal to lose some of the black color and begin to look brown, dark green etc. Rinsing them well before and after soaking prevents the dish from turning too dark. 3. When you are ready to cook, prep up the following: 4. ¾ cup onions chopped from 1 medium 5. 1 green chili chopped, more if you want or omit for low heat 6. 1¼ cup tomatoes (deseed & chop 3 medium tomatoes, or ⅔ cup store bought tomato puree/ passata) 7. Pour oil or ghee to a hot pressure cooker and add bay leaf, cumin seeds, cinnamon and black cardamom. 8. When the seeds begin to sizzle, add onions and saute for 5 mins, until light golden. 9. Add ginger, garlic and green chili. Saute for 2 mins. 10. Stir in turmeric, salt, red chili powder and garam masala. 11. Add the tomatoes and saute for a minute or 2. 12. You don’t really need to cook down the tomatoes here because we are going to cook the lentils for longer and the tomatoes will eventually become soft. 13. Add the dal and pour 3 cups (720 ml) water.

title: Uttapam Recipe (Uthappam)
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 2 tablespoon chana dal, 2 cup rice, 0.5 teaspoon methi seeds, 0.25 cup thick poha, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.875 cup water, 0.5 cup water, 2.5 tablespoon oil, 1 onion, 1 medium tomato firm ripe deseeded chopped, 0.25 cup carrot, 2.5 green chilies, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add urad dal, chana dal and methi seeds to a large bowl and rinse thrice. Pour fresh water and soak them for 4 to 5 hours. 2. In another bowl, rinse rice and soak it well for 4 to 5 hours. 3. Add poha to a small bowl & rinse it once. Then soak it in ¼ cup water at least 15 mins before blending. 4. Drain the water from dal and add it to a grinder jar along with soaked poha. 5. Pour ¾ cup water and grind to a thick & frothy batter. It has to be of pouring consistency and not runny. If the batter is too think pour another 2 to 4 tbsps water and grind. Transfer this to the bowl. Ensure the batter doesn't become hot or warm while blending. 6. Drain water from the rice and add it to the grinder. Pour ½ cup water. 7. Blend until smooth. Transfer to the same urad dal batter bowl and add salt. 8. If the weather is too hot then avoid adding salt at this stage. Add it after fermentation. 9. After mixing the consistency of the uttapam batter must be thick & pourable but not be too thick or too runny. 10. Cover and rest in a warm place overnight or until the batter rises. If you live in a cold region, then you may ferment the batter in a instant pot with the yogurt setting (low). Depending on the weather it takes anywhere from 8 to 16 hours. 11. Do not over ferment else the batter turns sour. After fermentation the batter rises and aerates. You will see bubbles over the batter much like the dosa batter. 12. Wash and chop the veggies needed. Also finely chop coriander leaves. Mix all of the topping. Prepare these when you are ready to make the uttapams else the veggies will release juices. 13. Grease a griddle pan or tawa. Heat it well. 14. Bring the batter to consistency by adding little water if very thick. The batter must be of thick yet of pourable consistency. Check the salt and add a bit more as we are using the veggies. 15. When the pan is hot enough, pour a ladle full of batter in the center of the hot pan. We usually do not spread it as it does a bit on its own. If needed spread a bit gently. 16. Lower the heat and sprinkle the toppings all over the uttapam. Sprinkle oil around the edges and over the onions. 17. When the base is firm, flip it and cook on the other side until it is cooked well. Cook on a medium flame at this stage as it needs to cook from inside. 18. Cook the toppings to suit your liking - caramelized or crunchy. Set aside and make the next one. 19. Serve onion uttapam with chutney or sambar or sprinkle podi.

title: vada curry recipe | vadacurry recipe | vadakari recipe
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, 1 chilli, 0.5 tsp cumin jeera, 2 tbsp coriander, few curry leaves, 0.5 tsp salt, oil for frying, 2 tbsp oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 0.5 tsp fennel saunf, pinch hing asafoetida, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli, few curry leaves, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tomato, 1 cup water, 0.5 cup coconut milk, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and saute 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves, ½ tsp fennel, pinch hing until it turns aromatic. 2. further add 1 onion followed by 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 chilli and few curry leaves. 3. saute well until it changes colour. 4. keeping the flame on low add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp chilli powder, ½ tsp coriander powder and ½ tsp salt. 5. saute on low flame until spices turn aromatic. 6. further add 1 tomato and saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 7. add 1 cup water and ½ cup coconut milk. 8. add in pieces of chana dal vada and mix well. 9. cover and boil for 5 minutes. 10. add 2 tbsp coriander and mix well. 11. finally, enjoy vada curry recipe with idli and dosa.

title: Vada Pav Recipe - Street Style | Mumbai Batata Vada Pav
ingredients: 2 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard, pinch of hing asafoetida, few curry leaves, 1 inch ginger, 2 clove garlic, 1 chilli, 2 tbsp coriander, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 2 potato aloo, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 0.75 cup besan gram flour, 1 tbsp rice flour, 0.25 tsp turmeric haldi, 0.25 tsp kashmiri red chilli powder lal mirch powder, pinch of hing asafoetida, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp baking soda, 0.5 cup water, oil for deep frying, 6 ladi pav dinner rolls, 7 green chilli, 6 tsp green chutney, 3 tsp tamarind chutney, 3 tsp dry garlic chutney
instructions: 1. firstly, take ball sized aloo mixture and dip in besan batter and coat well. 2. deep fry in hot oil stirring occasionally. 3. now slit the ladi pav and spread 1 tsp green chutney, ½ tsp tamarind chutney and ½ tsp dry garlic chutney. 4. place the prepared vada in center of pav, chura and fried chilli. 5. finally, press the vada pav and serve immediately.

title: Vada Pav Recipe (Mumbai Street Style)
ingredients: 1.25 cups coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green chilies, 2 garlic cloves, 1.5 teaspoons Lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoons water, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 1.5 tablespoons coconut, 7 garlic cloves, 8 red chilies, 0.75 cup coconut, 1 tablespoon peanuts, 0.375 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 3 medium potatoes, 2.5 green chilies, 0.75 inch ginger, 2.5 cloves garlic, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.375 teaspoon mustard seeds, 2 pinches hing, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.125 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon Salt, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 cup gram flour, 2.5 tablespoons rice flour, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 1 Pinch asafoetida, Tiny pinch soda-bi-carbonate, 1 pinch Turmeric, 0.125 teaspoon red chilli powder, oil for deep frying, 0.25 cup Tamarind chutney, 7.5 pav, 7.5 green chilies
instructions: 1. Pluck coriander leaves, you can keep the tender stalks. Rinse them well in lot of water a few times. Drain them well in a colander. 2. Add all the ingredients to a blender jar. Make a thick paste by adding 1 tbsp water at one time and blending until smooth. 3. Adjust salt, lemon juice and green chili to suit your taste. The chutney must not be very runny otherwise it makes the pav very soggy quickly. 4. Store this in a clean dry airtight glass jar. Refrigerate until used. 5. Skin the garlic and dry roast them for 2 to 3 mins. 6. Add red chilies and roast until crisp. Transfer them to a plate and cool. 7. To the same pan add coconut and just allow it to turn hot. Transfer this to a plate and cool. 8. If using peanuts you can dry roast them on a medium heat until golden & aromatic. 9. First powder red chilies with red chili powder and salt. Then add garlic and blend once. Then add coconut and peanuts. Powder them little coarsely. Set this aside. 10. Boil potatoes until just done. They must be firm, fork tender and not mushy. You can boil them either in a pressure cooker, steamer or in a pot. Cool and crumble them lightly. 11. Make a coarse paste on green chilli, garlic and ginger. 12. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan on a low heat. Add mustard, when they begin to crackle. Next add curry leaves and fry until crisp. 13. Add ginger garlic and green chili paste. Fry on a low heat until it turns fragrant. 14. Next put in hing, salt, turmeric and red chili powder. Mix well and add potatoes and coriander leaves. Turn off the stove and mix everything well. 15. Cool this mixture. Divide to 7 to 8 portions and shape to balls. 16. Heat oil for deep frying in a kadai. 17. Add besan and rice flour or corn flour to a mixing bowl. Next add salt, hing, turmeric and red chilli powder (optional). Mix everything well. 18. Pour 4 tablespoons water and begin to mix to make a thick lump-free batter, adding more water as required. Add more water as needed and make neither too thick nor too thin batter. Adjust salt and spice. 19. When the oil is hot enough, add a tiny pinch of soda to the batter. Mix well. This is optional. 20. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small amount of batter in oil. The batter should sizzle and rise to the surface, without turning brown. 21. Regulate the flame to medium. 22. Dip each ball in the batter, coat the potato balls with batter and drop in oil. You can use your fingers or a tbsp to do this. You can drop 3 to 5 balls in each batch depending on the size of your kadai. (Check video to see how I do) 23. Do not disturb them for 2 minutes, until they firm up. Later gently stir and fry until golden. Remove them to a cooling rack or a colander. 24. Wipe dry & Deseed green chilies to prevent them from bursting. Place the green chilies in a mess strainer or colander. In the same hot oil, fry the deseeded green chilies lightly. Do not over fry them. 25. Slit each pav bun to sandwich it. Make sure one side is intact. 26. Smear green chutney on both the inner sides of the pav. Sprinkle garlic chutney. If you want smear tamarind chutney of the other side of the pav. 27. Place a batata vada. You can also flatten the vada first and then place it. 28. Serve vada pav with fried chilies, tamarind and green chutney on the side.

title: vangi bath recipe | brinjal rice recipe | how to make vangi bath powder
ingredients: 5 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp clove, 5 pods cardamom, 1 mace javitri, 2 tsp poppy seeds khuskhus, 2 tsp oil, 0.25 cup coriander seeds, 0.25 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup chana dal, 1 tbsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp methi fenugreek, 0.5 cup dry coconut, 2 red chilli, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin jeera, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 2 tbsp peanut, few curry leaves, 1 red chilli, 2 brinjal baingan, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.5 cup tamarind extract, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp jaggery, 3 cup cooked rice, 2 tbsp coriander
instructions: 1. firstly, in a heavy-bottomed pan, dry roast 5 inch cinnamon, 1 tsp clove, 5 pods cardamom and 1 mace. 2. roast until the spices turn aromatic. keep aside. 3. in the same pan dry roast 2 tsp poppy seeds until it starts to pop up. keep aside. 4. in the pan heat 1 tsp oil, add ¼ cup coriander seeds, ¼ cup urad dal, ¼ cup chana dal, 1 tbsp cumin and 1 tsp methi. 5. roast until the lentils turn golden brown. 6. now add ½ cup dry coconut and roast slightly. 7. cool completely, making sure all the spices are not burnt. 8. now heat 1 tsp oil and roast 20 dried red chilli until it puffs up. 9. cool completely and transfer the roasted spices to the blender. 10. blend to coarse powder without adding any water. vangi bath masala is ready. 11. firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil, add 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 2 tbsp peanut. 12. saute and splutter on medium flame. 13. now add few curry leaves and 1 dried red chilli. saute well. 14. further add 2 brinjal and saute for 2 minutes. 15. also add ¼ tsp turmeric, ½ cup tamarind extract and ½ tsp salt. 16. mix well, cover and boil for 5 minutes. 17. now add 2 tbsp of prepared vangi bath masala powder, ½ tsp jaggery and mix well. 18. add 3 cup cooked rice and ½ tsp salt. 19. mix well making sure the masala is well combined with rice. 20. cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well. 21. finally, add 2 tbsp coriander and enjoy vangi bath with raita.

title: Vangi Bath Recipe With Homemade Powder
ingredients: 2 cups raw rice, 3 tablespoons Oil, 1 onion, 1 green chili, 25 grams brinjals, 0.5 cup green peas, 1.5 tablespoons Lemon juice, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 4.5 red chilies, 2 tablespoon Coriander seeds, 1 tablespoon channa dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.375 teaspoon methi seeds, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 1 cinnamon stick, 1 tablespoon Dry coconut
instructions: 1. Add rice to a pot or cooker. Wash it well at least thrice and pour water. 2. Cover and pressure cook for 2 whistles. If using basmati rice then just cook for 1 whistle. (refer notes for details) Do not make the rice mushy. Fluff up the rice and cool this completely. 3. Dry roast coriander seeds until crunchy and aromatic. Transfer them to a plate. 4. On a medium to low heat, toast chana dal, urad dal and red chilies until the dal turns golden & aromatic. Lastly add methi, cumin, cloves, and cinnamon. Saute till the methi seeds turn aromatic. 5. Cool all these completely. Powder them finely in a mixer or grinder. 6. Add ¼ tsp salt to a bowl of water. Wash brinjals, chop & discard the crowns. Dice them and add to the salted water. 7. Pour oil to a hot pan. Add in mustard, cumin, chana dal and urad dal. 8. Fry them until the lentils turn golden. Add the curry leaves, green chilies and onions. Saute till they turn golden or transparent. 9. Add chopped brinjal, green peas if using and sprinkle salt, turmeric. Fry them for 2 to 3 minutes stirring often. Cook covered on a low to medium heat until fork tender. If the pan is too dry, add a splash of water. 10. Add vangi bath powder and saute till it begins to smell good. Stir in the tamarind and a few tablespoons of water if the mixture is too dry. Cook for 1 minute and turn off. 11. Add the cooled rice and squeeze in some lemon juice (if not using tamarind). Mix well to coat the rice with vangi bath masala. Taste test and adjust salt or lemon juice to preference. 12. Garnish with roasted peanuts if you want. Serve vangi bath hot with some papads and yogurt.

title: vangibhath masala powder recipe | vangi bath powder recipe
ingredients: 1 tsp oil, 3 kashmiri red chilli, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 0.25 tsp methi seeds fenugreek seeds, 1 tsp coriander seeds, 0.75 tsp poppy seeds khas khas, 1 inch cinnamon stick, 3 cardamom elaichi, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 2 tsp coconut
instructions: 1. firstly, in a large kadai add a tsp of oil and fry red chillis. 2. fry the red chillis till they puff up. keep aside. 3. furthermore, add urad dal, chana dal, coriander seeds, methi, poppy seeds, cinnamon stick, cardamom, cloves and coconut. 4. roast them on medium flame till they turn aromatic. this may take around a minute. 5. once the spices are cooled transfer the roasted spices to a small blender. 6. blend them to smooth and fine powder. 7. finally, vangi bhath masala is ready. use it to prepare vangi bhath or store in a airtight container.

title: vanilla ice cream recipe | homemade ice cream recipe
ingredients: 2 cups thickened cream, 1 cup condensed milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, waffle cones, few choco chips
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare the whipped cream by using hand blender / whisk or mixer. 2. pour 2 cups of thickened cream or 35% milk fat into a blender. 3. further, blend for 40-50 seconds till the cream turns light and fluffy. do not over blend, as they turn to butter. 4. transfer the prepared whipped cream to a large mixing bowl. 5. furthermore, pour 1 cup of condensed milk. make sure the condensed milk is cold or refrigerate for atleast 30 minutes. 6. also add 1 tsp of vanilla extract or any flavours of your choice. to prepare chocolate ice cream, use coco powder. 7. now fold gently without breaking the air incorporated into whipped cream. 8. further, transfer the mixture into a airtight container. 9. make sure to cover the box and freeze for atleast 5 hours or till they set completely. 10. now take a scooper and scoop up the vanilla ice cream onto a waffle cones. make sure to wet your scooper in hot water before scooping. this helps to scoop ice cream easily. 11. finally, garnish with few coco chips or choco syrup before serving.

title: vanilla ice cream recipe | homemade ice cream recipe
ingredients: 2 cups thickened cream, 1 cup condensed milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, waffle cones, few choco chips
instructions: 1. firstly, prepare the whipped cream by using hand blender / whisk or mixer. 2. pour 2 cups of thickened cream or 35% milk fat into a blender. 3. further, blend for 40-50 seconds till the cream turns light and fluffy. do not over blend, as they turn to butter. 4. transfer the prepared whipped cream to a large mixing bowl. 5. furthermore, pour 1 cup of condensed milk. make sure the condensed milk is cold or refrigerate for atleast 30 minutes. 6. also add 1 tsp of vanilla extract or any flavours of your choice. to prepare chocolate ice cream, use coco powder. 7. now fold gently without breaking the air incorporated into whipped cream. 8. further, transfer the mixture into a airtight container. 9. make sure to cover the box and freeze for atleast 5 hours or till they set completely. 10. now take a scooper and scoop up the vanilla ice cream onto a waffle cones. make sure to wet your scooper in hot water before scooping. this helps to scoop ice cream easily. 11. finally, garnish with few coco chips or choco syrup before serving.

title: Vankaya pachadi (Brinjal chutney)
ingredients: 4 brinjals, 3.5 green chilies, 1 tomato, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.875 teaspoon tamarind paste, 1.5 garlic cloves, 1 pinch turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 sprig coriander leaves, 1 pinch mustard, 1 pinch cumin, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 Pinch urad dal, 1 red chili, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Wash and cut brinjal to cube sizes. Add them to a bowl of salted water to prevent discoloring. Slit and fry chilies in oil. Set aside. You can also fry 1 tsp channa dal and 1.5 tsp urad dal until they turn golden. These are optional but give a nutty taste. 2. Add brinjal and sprinkle salt. 3. Fry until they wilt off and get cooked. You can also cover and cook until tender. 4. Set aside the eggplants to a plate. Fry tomatoes in the same pan. 5. Preferably fry the tomatoes instead of cooking until soft. When the tomatoes turn mushy, add cumin. 6. Cool the ingredients completely. Blend them coarsely along with a garlic and tamarind or lemon juice. An onion chopper gives the best texture to a chutney. 7. Halfway through i prefer to add a small garlic clove. Add more oil to the pan and fry the seasoning ingredients. I prefer to use the same pan used for frying veggies as this lends a nice aroma to the chutney. I did not add curry leaves, but i suggest using them. 8. Switch off the stove and add chutney and coriander leaves to the pan. Stir well. Taste test and add more salt and lemon juice Serve brinjal chutney with rice or phulka. 9. Pour 1½ cups water to a bowl and add ¼ teaspoon salt to it. Rinse and chop brinjals to 1 inch cubes. Keep them immersed until used. 10. Heat a heavy bottom pan with 1 tablespoon oil. 11. Add the slit green chilies & fry for 1 to 2 mins until they blister. Remove them to a plate and set aside. Remove them to the plate. 12. To the same pan, add brinjal pieces, sprinkle salt and fry till for 2 to 3 mins. 13. Cover and cook them until tender. Remove them to the plate. 14. To the same pan, add tomatoes and turmeric. Fry till fully mushy and almost dry. Then add cumin. Do not cover and cook as this doesn't give a good taste to the brinjal chutney. 15. Add green chilies and all the other ingredients along with tamarind or lemon juice to a onion chopper or blender. 16. Run the machine to make a coarse chutney. Taste the chutney and add more salt and tamarind or lemon juice. 17. Heat another small pan with 1 tsp oil. 18. Add mustard, cumin , red chili and dals. When the dals turn golden, add curry leaves and pinch of hing (optional). 19. Off the heat and add the chutney to this. Mix well and garnish brinjal chutney with chopped coriander leaves.

title: Vazhakkai Fry (Poriyal)
ingredients: 2 raw banana, 2 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 red chili, 1 pinch asafoetida, 0.875 tbsp sambar powder, 0.5 tsp red chilli powder, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp salt
instructions: 1. Wash the raw bananas and chop off both the ends. Peel the skin completely. High amounts of pesticides are used in the cultivation of bananas, so it is good to peel the skin completely unless you are using home-grown produce. 2. Slice the bananas and add them to a pot of water. Slice them evenly and not too thick or too thin. 3. Heat a wide pan with oil. Add mustard, cumin, red chilli and urad dal. You can also omit the dal. 4. When the dal turns golden, throw in the curry leaves and sprinkle hing. 5. Lower the flame and add the turmeric, sambar powder and red chili powder. 6. Mix it well. 7. Drain the banana slices and add them here. 8. Mix well and fry for 2 minutes. Then sprinkle 1 to 2 tbsps of water. Mix well. 9. Cover and cook on a very low to medium flame until the banana slices turn tender/soft. If needed sprinkle more water and then stir and cook covered again. 10. Check if the vazhakkai has cooked properly. 11. Sprinkle salt evenly. 12. I sprinkle another tbsp of water since I use pink salt that is coarse. This way the salt dissolves and coats the banana slices well. I also cover and cook for 2 to 3 mins. Serve vazhakkai fry with rice or variety rice. 13. Raw Banana Fry (A... Banana Curry (Pla... Sambar Powder Rec... Beans Poriyal (Gr... Carrot Poriyal (C... Vendakkai Poriyal...

title: Veg Biryani | Vegetable Biryani Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup aged basmati rice, 0.5 cup onion, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tomato chopped, 1.75 cups water, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons Oil, 3 tablespoon curd, 0.125 teaspoon salt for veggies, 0.3333333333333333 cup potato, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrot, 0.3333333333333333 cup green peas, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves 3 cloves (( laung)), 3 green cardamoms, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 star anise, 1 fried cashews, 1 tablespoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Soak rice for at least 20 minutes. Drain the water and set aside. 2. Chop all the veggies, coriander and mint leaves. Keep these aside as well. 3. If using cauliflower keep them slightly larger. 4. Add oil to a hot pressure cooker or a heavy bottom pot. 5. Next add bay leaf, star anise, shahi jeera, cloves, cardamoms & cinnamon. Saute for 1 to 2 mins. 6. Add sliced onions and slit green chilies. Fry them stirring often until the onions turn evenly golden or light brown. 7. Next add ginger garlic paste and saute for about a minute or until the raw smell has gone off. 8. Add all the chopped vegetables and fry for about 2 minutes. 9. Next add tomatoes, ⅛ teaspoon salt, yogurt, coriander leaves, mint leaves,red chilli powder, turmeric and garam masala. 10. Mix all of these and fry on a high flame for 2 to 3 mins. The tomatoes should break down completely and the raw smell disappear. 11. Reduce the flame to low. Drain the water from the rice completely and spread it in a layer evenly over the veggies. 12. Measure the water or coconut milk to a separate bowl & add ¼ tsp salt. Mix well and taste the water. It has to be slightly salty. Bring the water to a boil. 13. Pour the salted water across the sides or edges of the pot. 14. If you do not mix up everything then the veg biryani will cook in layers. (However you can also mix up everything and cook just like it is done normally.) 15. Optional – Sprinkle 1 tsp ghee, 2 tbsps chopped mint leaves, handful of fried onions & saffron soaked milk. (refer notes) 16. For pressure cooking : Cover the cooker and pressure cook for 1 whistle on a medium high flame.Turn off the stove. For al dente non mushy veg biryani, I release the pressure manually after 1 to 2 mins using a wooden spatula. When the pressure releases, open the lid and fluff up veg biryani with a fork. 17. If cooking in a pot , bring it to a rolling boil. Cook covered on a low to medium flame until the water is absorbed and the veg biryani is cooked fully. Turn off the stove & rest for 15 mins. Serve veg biryani with raita or salan. 18. Optionally squeeze lemon juice while serving. 19. Press the SAUTE button on your IP and pour oil or ghee to the steel insert. 20. Add thinly sliced onions. Spread them to a single layer and saute occasionally until uniformly deep golden. Remove half of these and set aside for later. 21. Then add the whole spices and green chili. Then add the ginger garlic paste and saute until a nice smell comes out. 22. Add tomatoes, chopped veggies, mint, coriander leaves and sprinkle ⅛ teaspoon salt. Saute until the tomatoes breakdown. This takes about 1 to 2 mins. 23. Stir in the garam masala and red chilli powder. 24. Press CANCEL button & add yogurt. Deglaze by scraping the bottom of the pot with a spatula. This releases any bits of food stuck there. 25. Spread the veggies to a single layer. Next layer the drained rice and level it. 26. In another bowl, stir in ¼ teaspoon salt & 1¼ cup water and taste it. It has to be slightly salty. If needed add more. 27. Pour the water starting from the sides and then in the center. Do not mix. Ensure all of the rice is under the water. 28. Secure the IP with the lid. Position the steam release valve to sealing position. 29. Press pressure cook/ manual (set to high pressure) & set the timer for 5 mins. If you double or triple the recipe then set to low pressure with the same cook time. (Alternately you can pressure cook for 4 mins on high pressure setting and wait for 8 mins. Then release the rest manually.) 30. When the IP finishes, for aldente cooked vegetable biryani quick release the pressure manually. For softer rice, wait for 5 to 7 mins (natural pressure release) and then release the rest manually with the help of a spoon. Garnish with fried onions. Serve with Raita . 31. Rinse rice well and soak in water for 30 minutes. 32. Heat ghee in a heavy bottom pot and fry cashews until light golden. Stir in the raisins and fry until they swell up. Remove to a plate, retaining most of the ghee in the pot. 33. Add the sliced onions and spread them in a single layer. Let fry on a medium heat for 5 mins, stirring often in between. Reduce the flame to low and fry for another 3 to 4 mins until caramelised but not burnt. Remove these to a plate and set aside. Turn off. 34. While the onions fry, on another burner bring 7 to 8 cups of water to a rapid boil with salt, oil, lemon juice, green cardamoms, cloves, shahi jeera, cinnamon and bay leaf. 35. Drain the rice and add to the boiling water. Cook on a high to medium high heat until the rice is just 90% cooked (ensuring the water does not over flow but keeps boiling rapidly). When the rice slightly undercooked and have a bite to it, drain to a colander. 36. To the same pan of ghee (you used to fry onions), add bay leaf, star anise, cloves, cardamoms, cinnamon and shahi jeera. 37. Regulate the heat to low and quickly saute ginger garlic until slightly aromatic, for a minute. 38. Drain the water from potatoes and add them to the pan. Also add carrots, peas, green chilli and cauliflower. Saute on a medium heat for 3 minutes. 39. Pour ¼ cup water and cook covered on a medium heat until the potatoes are fork tender but not mushy. (evaporate any excess water) 40. Stir in red chilli powder, biryani masala, salt and turmeric powder. Saute for 2 minutes & reduce the heat to low, 41. Deglaze with 2 tablespoons water and stir in the whisked yogurt. Cover and cook on a low heat for 4 minutes, until heated through. 42. Add ¾ of the fried onions and 2 tablespoons each of mint & coriander leaves. Turn off the heat. Taste test your gravy and adjust salt to taste. 43. On another burner, heat a griddle or a pan on a medium heat for dum process. Avoid using non-stick. 44. While the pan heats, layer the hot rice over the biryani curry. Pour rose & saffron infused milk. Sprinkle the rest of mint leaves, fried onions, raisins and nuts. Reserve half of the nuts and raisins to garnish later. 45. Seal the pot with aluminium foil and press down to the sides of the pot carefully so the edges stick to the pot. This ensures the steam is trapped well. Place a heavy lid. 46. Transfer this on to the hot griddle and cook for 22 minutes on a low heat. You can also cook this over direct flame for 12 minutes at the lowest heat (only on a small burner). Once done remove the pot from heat and let it rest for at least 20 minutes. 47. Oven Baked – Alternately bake it in a preheated oven at 350 F (180 C) for 20 minutes. Later rest it in the oven itself for another 15 to 20 minutes. 48. Serve vegetable biryani with a flat serving spoon, from the bottom of the pot such that every serving gets a portion of the curry and rice. Mirchi ka salan or shorba and a raita make great sides.

title: Veg Burger Recipe (Veggie Burger)
ingredients: 25 grams potatoes, 2 medium carrots, 0.5 cup beans, 0.5 cup peas, 1 inch ginger, 2.5 green chilies, 3.5 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1.25 teaspoon Garam masala, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup flour, 0.3333333333333333 cup water, 1.25 cup bread crumbs, 0.5 cup mayonnaise, 1.5 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, 1.25 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.625 teaspoon mustard seeds powder, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper powder, 0.5 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.5 teaspoon red chili flakes, salt, 5 burger buns, 1.5 tablespoons butter, 5 lettuce leaves, 1.5 medium tomatoes, 1 onion
instructions: 1. To a small mixing bowl, add mayonnaise or Greek yogurt, mustard, olive oil, garlic, chilli flakes and black pepper. Taste test and add salt if needed. If your mayo is not sour add lemon juice as well. Mix and refrigerate until needed later. 2. Pour 2 ½ to 3 cups water to a steamer/ cooker or a pot. Place a trivet or a rack inside the pot. Cover and bring the water to a boil. 3. Meanwhile rinse potatoes & French beans. Trim off both ends of French beans and fine chop them. Peel and cube potatoes to 1 inch pieces. Add them to a steamer basket and place them in the steamer. 4. Steam them until half done for about 5 to 7 mins. Meanwhile, peel and chop carrots to 1 inch pieces. 5. When the potatoes are half done, transfer carrots & peas to the steamer. Cover and steam them until potatoes are done completely and fork tender. Even peas and carrots should be done by now. 6. Cool all these steamed veggies and transfer to a mixing bowl. Once cool mash them well and then add garam masala, coriander powder, green chilies, coriander leaves and salt. 7. Mix all of them. You will have a mushy and sticky mixture. To this add, bread crumbs or powdered poha or flour in batches only as needed. 8. The mixture for the patties must not be too soggy or too moist. Slight stickiness is alright but it has to be firm. If not add more bread crumbs. Check pictures in the step by step photo section in the post. 9. Taste test the mixture and add more salt or any seasoning if you prefer. Divide the dough to 4 to 6 portions depending on how thick or thin you want the patties to be. 10. To a small mixing bowl, add gram flour or corn starch to make the batter. Add a pinch of salt & pour water just enough to make a fairly thick batter. Do not make the batter runny as the excess moisture will be soaked up by the patties making them too soft. 11. Pour bread crumbs or panko crumbs to a wide bowl or plate. 12. Take one portion of the vegetable patty mixture and flatten it to the same size as your burger bun. If the mixture is sticking. Place the patty in some flour or corn starch. This way make all the patties and place them aside in a plate. 13. Dip each patty in the batter completely and then place them in the bread crumbs. Coat the patty well with the crumbs including the sides. 14. This way coat all the patties and set them aside. Rest these for 10 mins so the crumbs stick well to the patties and don’t mess the oil/ pan while frying. 15. Rinse each lettuce leaf separately under a slow stream of running water. You may also sprinkle a solution of 1 part vinegar mixed with 3 parts of water. Rinse well and let them sit of kitchen tissues so all of the water drips away. 16. Slice onions and tomatoes to round thin slices. If needed deseed the tomatoes. 17. Refer notes section to bake or air fry the patties. To pan fry, heat a pan with 1½ to 2 tablespoon oil. Let the oil become hot, then place 2 to 3 patties as many as your pan fits. Rotate the patties with your fingers gently to coat the patties with the oil. 18. Fry them on a medium high heat until the base turns golden and crisp. Flip them gently and pour 1 ½ to 2 tablespoons oil as needed. Fry until crisp & uniformly golden. For a proper golden to brown crust, increase the flame the last 2 mins. 19. To shallow fry, pour ½ cup oil to a small pan and heat it. When the oil becomes hot, gently place the patties in the hot oil & fry until golden. 20. Gently turn them to the other side and fry until golden & crisp. Remove them to a wired rack to cool slightly before assembling. To make cheese burgers, place cheese slices over the very hot patties. 21. Once done with frying the patties, transfer the oil to a cup. Slice each bun to 2 parts and spread some butter on the buns if you prefer. Toast them in the same hot pan. Remove them and set aside. 22. Before assembling please recheck - Lettuce should not have any water dripping, patties not too hot and the burger bun is also not hot. 23. Place the toasted bun in a serving tray and then the veggie patty. Optionally spread the burger sauce we made earlier or some ketchup to keep the patty in place. However this is optional & skip if you want your patty very crisp. 24. Place the lettuce, then the onion and tomatoes. Optionally drizzle some sauce over the lettuce and onions if you like.
